text: We had the Lorraine quiche, super good. The pastries are are great.  Service is a bit slow but good taste.

The bar at Paradise Cafe has always been one of my favorites!  The staff is so friendly and attentive! The menu changed recently and it was definitely for the better (food and drinks)! The ingredients are so fresh and you can really taste the difference. I look forward to frequently visiting Paradise Cafe!

Amazing pizza but the people who answer the phone are horribly rude. Called for pickup and the guy was clearly annoyed (sorry for calling to order pizza from a pizza place???) and rushed me off the phone, literally like hung up before I was done. Called back, the lady who answered was the same way. I needed to clarify that that guy got my order correct. So ridiculous. I get you're busy, but come on. I've always loved this place but for the price I'm saying, this service is not cutting it.

Super sad where this once great place has ended up. Used to be such a cool place with great friendly staff especially at the deli area, now if you can get help your lucky and when you do the attitude is not pleasant. If the location in Summerland is the same owners I don't understand the problem here. Summerland much better, although you can tell the new owners are a bit frugal Raise the prices a bit, and offer the old school Cantwell extras !

One of the best places, right around the corner from wine country (Los Olivos) to grab a tasty tri trip sandwich on Sunday afternoon 3-6 pm.   There's always music, dancing and a great biker crowd in this woodsy mountain landscape. This is one of my favorite places in SB.

Great atmosphere. Cozy, intimate, cool. Yummy drinks. Would go back.  Would not recommend the vodka-infused cucumber lemonade...very clumpy. But, when I wanted to send it back, they were very nice about it and it was replaced with a delicious cocktail.
My husband and I came here for dinner and to try the famous oyster shooters and we were not let down! Not only was the shooter absolutely delicious but their clam chowder was too!

Food is great. Bartender is always friendly. Then... The airport bar stories continue at SB Airport. Who is this manager and how does he not have hostile work environment claims against him. He thinks he's funny. He's not. Who hired this guy?

This is a great spot for breakfast. The servers work really hard, and are good at making sure your coffee cup is never left empty. The atmosphere lends itself to the name, as it is definitely old fashioned, but it goes with everything else.   The food was great. Really good greasy spoon food, that will leave you full for most of the day. I left here very happy, and very full. I definitely recommend stopping in for a good plate of blueberry pancakes and some hash browns.

All I can say is the pastries here are to die for.  So unbelievable in flavor and amazing portions! You are definitely getting what you paid for.  Definitely expect to pay $4 per croissant but it's worth it!! Plus it's Montecito, what do you expect?

This is the best Mexican lunch we have ever had in a very long time. We got on Yelp wait list while doing wine tasting nearby. Turned out we didn't even have to wait. All the tables were on sidewalks with umbrellas so there was enough shades to my relief.   I started off with a very pretty and the most delicious Mezcal cocktail. For $13 each craft cocktail it was inexpensive and amazing! The house Mezcal shot I had at the end of the meal was yummy too. For food we had queso fundido which was definitely the best I have ever had. I have made this Mexican cheese dip my go to appetizer at any Mexican restaurants anywhere. Chips and salsa was so fresh and delicious. We especially liked the red salsa so hubby asked for more of it to go with his tacos. I had octopus ceviche and hubby had two steak tacos as entrees. I'v come to really like raw octopus for some reason. This ceviche was so refreshing with tender pieces of octopus. Hubby liked his steak tacos a lot too.   The service was very friendly and attentive. And I realized this is hotel Indigo, wish we could have stayed there. We have stopped staying at hotels ever since COVID. For one thing that I don't know how to disinfect an entire hotel room. And we don't like to breath recycled indoor air for long period of time.   This was a very pleasant Mexican meal on a beautiful Saturday. We wouldn't mind coming back every time we are in Santa Barbara!
Ate here and was pretty amazed at how bad it is.  Tried the chicken soup and the tandoori lamb.  It was the worst I've ever had! Avoid this place at all costs.

This actually is the best steak in town!  Tee Off is a place like none other in Santa Barbara.  Sure their steaks are the amazing (prime rib and bone in rib-eye) but it's the little things that make the short list for date nights, birthday dinners, family in town and just Tuesday.   First off it's dark. REALLY dark. There are no windows and it was years before I realized how green the place is. I mean, it's golf themed but this place is all green.   A long bar, which is where you'll find me, is tended mostly by Todd and Matt. Both will shake up a killer martini while a bottle of big, bold red wine sits ready for the main event. The dining room is split with high backed booths up front with a very loungey feel and tables in a back room where there is more light. Also a great room for a surprise birthday party (thank you Sarah S).   Bring your appetite! Enjoy a cocktail while you pursue the menu. As mentioned, I suggest red meat but more on that in a moment. The meal starts with a cold veggie tray then it's feeding time!  'Big Bertha' $49 is a massive bone-in ribeye (I leave off the garlic marinade) that comes with Soup (prime rib soup!!!) or Salad, Rice or Potato, Fresh Seasonal Vegetables & Fresh Baked Sourdough Rolls. This is pretty much the only thing I order. Perfect for 2 and you'll have leftovers. Through Covid I've called in a take out a few times and it travels well too. Buyer beware. You'll need a big cutting board to carve this one up.   Prime Rib $35-54 is the house specially, while they last. Multiple cuts for multiple appetites and again, I dare say, the best in Santa Barbara. Some pork, chicken and a fish or two too but come on. You know why you're here...COW. Moo.   This is a locals joint where you and team know each other by name and the kitchen crew says 'hi' when you walk by. It's on the small side and people tend to linger and enjoy their time so make a reservation on weekends and holidays to ensure you have a seat. If you're lucky, you can slide into the bar and make some new friends.   Cheers and I'll see you at the Tee Off.

My friend was visiting from LA. If there's any vegan spot that you NEED to visit, its this one! Mesa Verde has exotic menu selections that won't let you down.  Yum yum yum!' Pretty atmosphere, outdoor seating and pleasant service. Bring your friends and family for a healthy and delicious plant based meal!!! Located on the Mesa in Santa Barbara hidden next to the 711!

Everything it lived up to be and more. They say you must go when in Santa Barbara. I concur and highly recommend this small authentic Mexican restaurant. The specials change about the quality is outstanding! As your ordering there pressing drag corn tortillas that soon go on the cook top for your eating pleasures. I had the super rica especial, quesadilla with chorizo, tacos de bistec, frijol super-rica and a cold cup of horchata. The wait time varies but well worth it. Quaint and local ambiance. Don't mind the pics as we waited just about 25 minutes so when it came down to quality pics or snap shits to eat well you be the judge. Highly recommend a visit if your in the area. Oh yeah. CASH ONLY! love slots like this...

This place is perfect for brunch. We ended up sitting outside since it was such a nice day. We ordered the Norwegian Benedict and Challah French Toast to share.  Norwegian Benedict: The benedict came with smoked salmon, cucumber, tomato, capers, dill, two soft-poached eggs, and hollandaise sauce. There was a lot of salmon which I really appreciated and the potatoes that come with it are seasoned very well.  French Toast: These were incredible. We couldn't stop eating them. Lol. Loved the bread that's used and the berries were so fresh!  Bummed out that their brunch items differ from weekday to weekend. I really wanted to try their lemon poppyseed pancakes, but they are only available sat/sun. I still loved the food here and will definitely be back for their weekend brunch.

We stopped in the restaurant for lunch. They had 3 different types of half sandwiches were just right with salad. The burger was done perfectly. Looking at the beach and water. Protected by glass and overhead sails.

Hands down best restaurant ever! From the amazing food & menu choices, the attentive staff, easy valet parking, awesome atmosphere - everything about it made for a perfect evening. Oysters on the half shell with a bit of grapefruit, flat iron streak that melted like butter - terrific menu!

if you're in Santa Barbara this place is a must!!! Family friendly, entertaining, tasty! Great team work and sushi bar is so perfect for when in a rush. The hibachi dinners are standard price & always love how the cooks perform for your dinner! This place is always a convenient 'go-to' spot!!

Nothing but positive things to say about Sandbar! To start off, the ambience provides such a refreshing and relaxing setting. The service is always great, very friendly and attentive servers and the food and drinks are delicious! You can't go wrong with any of their margaritas but if you're not a margarita person they have a huge menu of other tasty cocktails to try! Every time I go to Sandbar I will myself to try something different to eat and I haven't been let down yet! (and don't expect to!) Everything is so flavorful and cooked to perfection.  I would definitely recommend checking out Sandbar for a relaxing lunch/dinner or a fun night of drinking and dancing!

Seven Bar and Kitchen serves upscale bar food with a twist. The food was good as was the ambiance. I enjoyed the casual vibe and music.  The only issue I had was an up charge on alcohol I did not authorize but it was a night out so I said nothing which is atypical so check your bill.

LOVE, LOVE, LOVE  this place!!! I normally order the Pitaya and go soy. The service is friendly and fast; which makes me want to go back even more. I really appreciate the variety of blenders they offer and the nutrition information is useful if your trying to watch your lady figure. It's a nice place to stop and revitalize yourself to continue your epic shopping spree!

Very happy I tried this restaurant. Have ordered delivery 2x now. Food is Excellent. The first delivery was the chile verde breakfast that was out of this world 5 star breakfast. This last was a mushroom burger on sourdough with potato salad, another great meal. Truly a treasure

Love this place since the new remodel. Excellent bbq. Probably the best in town. The chicken and waffles are to die for. The candied bacon is a must. The smoked wings with white sauce is something I've been trying to perfect for years and they do it perfectly. The salads, especially the kale (we ask for burrata instead of goat cheese) and the smoked beets are so damn good. Great beers but they REALLY need to get some hazys. Kind of weird the staff after two different times we went didn't know what hazys were. The smoked old fashion is off the charts. Ps I miss the stripper pole . Love you guys. Get some Hazy ipas on tap.
Went again recently and I thought it couldn't get any better but it did! Food is phenomenal as usual and the service just went to a whole noter level...

Jeannine's is a nice enough place for breakfast, especially if you're okay with paying $30 for a breakfast for two. What I can vouch for on the menu is the generously sized breakfast burrito, which is moist and buttery on the outside so it's more of a knife-and-fork dish if you're so inclined. It's stuffed with eggs, bacon, tomatoes, and is accompanied by a zesty salsa. It may or may not be served with a side of roasted potatoes as the menu would lead you to believe. It certainly evokes French bakery more than taco shop but is pretty tasty nonetheless. The American breakfast, meanwhile, is your standard eggs-and-meat platter with a biscuit.   Attention to detail isn't necessarily paramount at Jeannine's. Their website contains a heartwarming love story about a lady named Eleanor, yet they named the restaurant 'Jeannine's.' You might find that they've forgotten to bring you a side dish that you ordered, and you'll also want to verify while ordering that the burrito's usual accompaniment, the roasted potatoes, are available, as they might just give you fresh fruit instead. Not that there's anything wrong with fresh fruit, but burritos and berries aren't as natural a duo. The staff was very welcoming and accommodating however, promptly rectifying our side dish issues and keeping my iced tea topped off, so I'd say it's still a pretty worthwhile breakfast joint if you've got some extra coin.

Decent GF pizza, but I prefer Ca Daria. This pizza's crust was rather hard to saw through with my butter knife. We asked for generous amounts of cheese and were assured that they always put a lot of mozzarella on their pizzas. Not.   Got a pepperoni and it was fine, but nothing I'd come back for, nothing special, just okay.   Some of the other non GF pizzas looked huge and delicious, but I didn't get to try those.
Delicious hidden Gem behind the laundromat. Hearty authentic Mexican fare. The tortas, tacos and burritos are delicious, good fresh salsa bar. Worth giving a try
Totally crappy overpriced food. But what did you expect? Location, location, location! Soak it up!

For some reason Santa Barbara just doesn't have a good vegetarian/vegan restaurant scene. I was really excited when Mesa Verde opened, and hoped they could help fill this gap. We have eaten here three times now, and sadly there won't be a fourth visit.   The overriding tastes of the meals we've had here are bitter and salty. I could get over the fact that prices are fairly high for how small the portions are, but when the food just doesn't taste very good that is a hard thing to excuse. Each time we ate there we ordered a lot of plates just so we could leave feeling full, and really there was no dish that I loved. Most were decent, and some left a lot to be desired. My boyfriend is vegan and I'm about 70% vegetarian, so its not like we are people who aren't into vegetables and grains.   I've worked enough service jobs to cut people a lot of slack when I eat out, but frankly the last time we were here the service sucked. I'm not an eater that needs to be coddled, but having my water glass refilled once in an hour period would have been nice. I had to physically get up and interrupt waitstaff chatting in the kitchen to ask for more. We definitely have encountered a couple friendly people who work here, but  there is a young and pretentious vibe that was off-putting to this 30 year old.  It makes me sad to write this, as SB really needs a decent sit-down, vegetarian/vegan-friendly spot.  I don't think this is it.

Great food, lousy service.  I like Lure, and we go there pretty often, but this time, the service was about as bad as I've ever had.  Our waiter introduced himself and said he'd take 'excellent care of' us. Well, that became the running joke of the meal, because he did quite the opposite.  First:  He said he'd bring our drinks and bread immediately:  I had ordered hot black tea and a glass of ice, since the iced tea had passion fruit in in and I don't like fruit flavored tea.  What I got was a cup of hot water and assorted herbal teas - no black tea among them, and no ice, either.  A while later, my husband's wine was brought over by the barkeep, and I asked him for some black tea.  He brought one bag of Lipton a while later.  Second:  There were 4 different kinds of fresh oysters on the menu, and we decided to start with one of each to share.  What we got was 6 oysters, two of each of THREE kinds.  Third:  Still no bread, but our meals came eventually.  Both the Cioppino and the fried shrimp were excellent, but the french fries with the shrimp were luke warm, like they'd been sitting around waiting to be brought out for a while.  Still no bread.  We asked a passing busboy for bread, and eventually, our waiter (whom we had not seen in a while) brought some, and said 'I hear you've been asking for some bread!'. Fourth:  Our waiter did bring me another pot of  hot water, but no tea bag.  I asked for a bag of black tea, and it took about 15 minutes for it to come, by which time the water had started to cool off.  Not the finest experience.  UPDATE:  shortly after posting this review, I was contacted by Rob, the manager, who thanked me for the review, and for the opportunity to counsel his staff. He invited me to return so that he could make it right. I did go back with a friend and he greeted me warmly, explained and apologized, and again thanked me for the opportunity to counsel his staff. We had delicious food, great service with him checking on us frequently, and after 'forcing' the key lime pie on us for dessert, he comped our meal. Kudos.

I started eating here when they were behind the mall on Chapala St. When Amy the owner was still coming up with the menu along with her amazing chef. At that time bib overalls would be what they were wearing when they took your order, cleared your table or cooked. There's nothing even close to the menu Amy has come up with.. oh and breakfast. Better plan on 2 for the stuffed French toast. They moved to State St. Right next to the Granada Theater. If you're going there for a show or if you're just here for a visit. Make sure this place is at the top of your list. You would really miss out on something truly special

Such a great meal on a perfect sunny santa barbara day. Our lunch was complete with a nice breeze and lots of laughs shared among my family. I had my mom join my hubby and daughter for a lovely brunch.   Our waitress was constantly checking up on us even though I bet she had lots of tables! This place is always PACKED!  I ordered the grilled tofu with mango sticky rice and coconut sauce. Reminded me of authentic thailand cuisine-- very reminiscent of the good ol honeymoon days  My mom got the lobster mac n cheese which I tried a bite of and was in awe! So yum and filling!  Thank you Boathouse for a lovely meal to remember!

My new favorite vegan restaurant in the SB and Montecito area. We are sorely lacking in high quality vegan food that is more than just salads- and now Olivers is helping mend that problem! My partner and I came here and had the avo toast, the potato wedges with AMAZING green garlic sauce, the squash ravioli, the mushroom taco plate, and the chocolate torte. It was all absolutely delish!   Service was great and the decor/ambience is lovely. Beautiful patio and nice bar.   We will definitely be back to Olivers! Thanks for making veganism an even easier choice!
This place keeps calling me back every time I go to SB.  Our last two meals here were fantastic.  Service was very sweet and friendly.  I just wish Chef Mudge would write a cookbook!

I have yet to find a sandwich in Santa Barbara, much less anywhere else, that even comes close to touching those found at South Coast. Despite a recent increase in price, I've found that both the quality and the quantity of food found in a normal order at South Coast might quite possibly make it the best deal for a delicious breakfast or lunch in Santa Barbara.

The service was fine but I remember the food being pretty expensive here for some standard pub grub (see $14 buffalo wings).   It seemed like an ideal place to catch a game, and a fine stop on our pub crawl, but I don't know that I'd be back on my return to SB. I do recall a DJ, music, and some dance space so if anything that would be the draw for me.

I go through periods when I'm here several times a week.   It's that good.  I order the Bangkok Street Noodles and they are absolutely delicious every single time.  I've tasted a bunch of other things from the menu, and everything is good.  My noodles with a beer or glass of wine hits the spot every single time.   I wish this was a restaurant because it's not a place to go if you are a party of three and want to have a conversation.  I often go alone, so it's perfect.   One guest works, but sometimes there is a bit of a long wait.   It's not the cheapest, but well worth the price in my book.

I really liked the space. I would love to come here for a nice Sunday brunch. Maybe next time. My wife and I recently ate here and enjoyed the French Onion Soup (Excellent), Pistachio Crusted Brie on Sourdough (Excellent), Escargot (Can't go wrong here) and Duck Burger (Super Tasty but a but too Well for me...asked for Medium Rare).  The service was excellent and the staff really went out of their way. I enjoyed the food and I look forward to trying out this space again.

I love this place. My daughter ate her 2 nights in a row. We think if is delicious, nutritious, and great quality for the price! Everything is fresh. The sauce is great. I have gotten take out there and I think the owner is delightful. I highly recommend it.

Came in w/ my bff   We built up an appetite & came in hungry. Although our server was clearly exhausted(yawning) she was awesome.  I had a glass of Prosecco & the bff something on tap.  We shared the avocado egg rolls & Spicy Chorizo Pizza.  Avo Egg rolls sounded odd to me but were good.   The Pizza was a hard pass for me. Wouldn't have it again.

Came here months ago for a late night drink with some girl friends and wasn't particularly impressed with the place.  At the time it seemed busy and overpriced.  I recently stopped by for happy hour on a Sunday afternoon and was pleasantly surprised.  My opinion completely changed.    Beautiful patio, good service.  They have a pretty decent happy hour tapas menu, both in terms of options and prices.  We ordered the Ahi Poke (a Hawaiian tartare) with taro chips and a brie flatbread 'pizza'.  Delicious and a good price at $6 each.  The signature cocktail list was only so-so for me but the signature margarita was pretty tasty.    I'll definitely come back for another happy hour.

My favorite place when in Santa Barbara. It's the best combination of good food, great scenery, ambiance and wonderful service. I recommend the tortilla soup (which they'll serve upstairs as an exception) and steak Diane (flambeed at table side). Soufflées or banana fosters are great option for dessert. This place also has the best old fashioned I ever tried. One thing they don't do well: their foie gras (it's small and over cooked)
Good services, good food (give the parm fries a try!), and gotta love the outdoor patio seating. A little on the expensive side, but worth the patio.

In less than seven days I have visited three Mexican Restaurants which is three more than I have had a meal at in five years. Yes, I did love the Mexican Restaurant choices that my friends were introducing us to for lunch while in California. La Playa Azul Café was one of my favorites  but that might have been because I am a seafood lover and they had many selections on their menu to choose from.   Our party wanted to be seated at their outdoor patio along the main road. The patio had high umbrellas with comfortable seating.   For lunch, we all shared and enjoyed the crab salad, chicken salad and Chile Relleno while washing everything down with their well-known potent margaritas. Everything was wonderful including the service.
Great food, as usual. They keep the line moving at a good pace. There's a decent size indoor dinning area and plenty of parking.
The Mole Burrito was amazing and gigantic.  Don't expect anything healthy, but if you want a heavy and delicious burrito for an average price, this is the right place.
Consistently excellent food for years. Open late, excellent atmosphere. My favorites include the Tomato basil soup, caprese salad, and pasta with filet mignon meatballs.
This is the BEST! I love these gourmet' burgers! Each bite is tasty, juicy and filled with amazing flavor!

Burgers are great!!!  Fries were served cold, that didnt make me very happy.  Beer taster was a great option to try several types.  pool tables are fun.   pretty good prices on shots (don julio 1942)! :-)

Overall, nice restaurant. The service was good.   Tuna cones: Unique. Pretty good Kid's Caesar salad: delicious (we ate it bc kid's said, no thank you) Kid's pepperoni pizza: Son ate 6 slices of the pizza - that's a lot Kid's meatball and pasta: Daughter who is very picky ate all her pasta with pieces of meatball! Lasagna: Good - great portion Cioppino: Great portion of seafood - I couldn't finish it but it had great flavor   The reason why we picked this place was because they had a great kid's menu. Kid's definitely enjoyed their meal and so did we! Great place if you are in the area. Try to request a window seat so you can view the ocean!

I think this is one of the more creative restaurants in town. Their salads are delightful and unusual.  The warm dishes are just a step above traditional Asian fair. Overall, nice atmosphere and reasonable prices. (I mean it's SB, so it ain't cheap.) reservations recommended!

The food here is delicious. I would recommend trying the puttanesca. They have a great wine and beer selection for very reasonable prices. They also have a happy hour menu which includes some delicious meatball sliders and a variety of other options to choose from. Rosemary, the owner, is very friendly and the service is prompt and accommodating. They have a great front patio as well. Overall an enjoyable dining experience.
Decent food, bit over priced, marginal Bloody Mary's.

Natural Cafe is my go-to for a healthier take-out or dine-in meal. The food is always fresh and is decently priced (expect to spend around $9-12). I am obsessed with the Old Town salad. It's one of my favorite salads in Santa Barbara. I usually get it with a side of sautéed tofu for protein. I've tried the pesto pasta, the portobello mushroom sandwich, a couple of the veggie and rice sautés and they are all very good.   It's always clean inside and the service is great. I usually do take-out orders and they are almost always ready when I arrive ten minutes after calling.   I definitely always recommend Natural Cafe.
Stopped by for a coffee.  Nice staff and decent service and drinks.  The restaurant looks nice.  I would try that next time.

I called in an order to eat there, Bow helped us and was a wonderful host. Our food was ready when we got there and he even chose some good brews for us to drink. The food it great and the staff are spectacular!  Well worth the visit!

The Palace has become one of those restaurants where we HAVE TO visit every time we're in Santa Barbara.   The Cajun / Creole dishes are fantastic - try the gumbo appetizer, a bowl of intense Louisiana flavor. The blackened red fish is cooked properly, flaky and moist. Both the crawfish etouffee and jambalaya are good, but I prefer the former.   We love the atmosphere of the restaurant, especially when everyone sings. The first time is especially magical, especially after a drink or two and the fresh muffins.  End the evening with the proper dessert: the banana foster and key lime pie are both fantastic; try the soufflé if it's a special occasion.
These folks know what they're doing. Generous portions splendidly prepared. This is one of the best breakfast places in town!

This place will do the job  if you're craving Boiling Crab but the depth of flavor is not nearly as good.   The service is great. Very attentive and they will let you sample their sauces if you tell them it's your first time. The shrimp are pretty big and they offer you some plastic gloves if you like to keep your fingers clean but I think they make it more difficult to eat.   We ordered shoestring fries well done and it was still pretty soft. Kind of disappointed with that. We wouldn't mind waiting a little longer for it to be crispy but it came out pretty fast and it wasn't worth it.   The calamari is decent. The portion is so small for the price. I don't think I would get it again for that price.   The shrimp was also heavily overcooked. I've been there twice now, and it's happened both times. When I go to boiling crab, their shrimp are cooked thoroughly and the temperature is cool enough to peel them immediately. Here, the sauce is so hot that I can't even touch them, which sucks because the shrimp will continue to overcook and it gets wrinkly to the point where the meat is adhered to the shell.

So my family came here for my Mom's Birthday last week and the experience was not good.    The New York strip steak was too oily, because they slathered oily mushrooms all over the top of it.  I'm confident a steak from my grill would have been better.  The fresh fish was also caked in spices that was too overpowering.  I'm not sure if the choice was to celebrate the spices, or the fish.  Lastly, the icing on the cake or, in this instance, lack there of - is that although we told the waitress it was my Mom's birthday (and granted we didn't order desert because they were already upset with the meal) the waitress brought back a Plank of wood with a candle on it.  What restaurant brings a plank of wood?  It's like at least substitute a cookie or something.  Ugh *facepalm.
Still wonderful! TBH, I haven't been here in a while as I rarely trek downtown lately. I stopped in and had a great lunch. It was nice to see the expanded menu and as always, the service was great.

Outstanding tonight-management was more than helpful-our server was extraordinary.  Oh yea, the brews and food?  '10' out of '10'...   We are there almost every weekend-being the old folks ordering beers, Brussel sprouts, fries and protein salads...  Kudos to the kitchen staff too, that nobody ever sees....  totally make for a great experience-we'll keep coming back..........

Been here before and had a great time. For a very popular spot they always seem to be surprised by the work load. Told many times that they were very busy. I kinda dont care. On our first night, it took 2 hours to get our bags. Thats after three calls letting them know we had a dinner engagement to get to. We missed it. On three occasions, I asked the desk to have a manager contact me. Never happened.  At the Bistro, (while waiting for bags) it took almost 30 minutes to get a drink. The drink was delicious.  On a more positive note, the valet team is great. Very helpfull and up beat. We love the facility, but for a Ritz, the service needs an upgrade

On Friday nights, their website says they close at 11pm, Yelp says 11pm, and last Friday they closed before 10:15pm. We just wanted dessert and a martini, but were turned away. I've been coming there for 20 years for birthdays, office parties, wedding rehearsal  dinners, dates, and just because 50 other times. Business is that good that you can turn away one of your best customers??? Sorry, never again. Go to Julienne instead.

Hands down some of the best Ramen I have ever had! I am a Ramen snob and I come hear at least 1x a month as their food and service are really just fantastic.  This spot has the VIBEZ, that's for sure. Plenty of outdoor seating, half of the tables are covered for shade and the other half provide a little sun. Very comfortable and casual. The decor is subtle but they have a great neon ramen sign and the outdoor space is very clean and spacious.   Service: 5 stars! They are super on top of it here. They are perfect for a quick lunch as they really stay on top of taking your order quickly and the food literally comes out within minutes of ordering. I also appreciate that as fast as they are, they never make you feel rushed and are appropriately attentive. Everyone that works here is always super friendly and efficient. The service has kept me coming back for sure.  Now for the food. Again, this is genuinely some of the best ramen I have ever had. Their broth is so rich and creamy. The meats are perfectly seared and have a little bit of a Smokey taste to it. The additions like their perfectly cooked egg or chili oil topping does the trick for me. I haven't had a bad dish here, great menu and a wide variety of ramen options to meet anyone's needs. They also recently incorporated a sushi menu, will try their sushi soon and report back.  My recommendations for ramen choices would be their signature ramen or their spicy red ramen. Their pork belly is phenomenal so I would highly suggest that being your protein if your a carnivore like me.  Santa Barbara Craft Ramen is such a gem and one of my favorite restaurants in SB hands down. Please give them a try and support a small business!
The fried chicken sandwich was delicious, and the Caesar salad was very large. The bartender was really cool and the restaurant was very calm and enjoyable.

I love how they are professionally and properly maintaining the social distance in their restaurant. They have a variety of vegetarian option. I got all vegetarian options. My favorite was the pineapple curry it has a lot of flavor. The sticky mango rice is an absolute favorite of mine- I get it atleast once a month.  The spring rolls come with peanut sauce that is absolutely delicious for the health conscious.  I got an order of fried tofu and was very pleased that it was very well made - comes with sweet and sour sauce which pairs well. I also topped my pineapple curry with it. Will definitely keep coming back to this gem of a place. I cannot wait yo try the bbq chicken next.

Terrible customer service. I was a regular at this place and one time when I placed a big order they forgot an egg roll order. I figured no big deal I'll just tell them the next time to comp me the missing order.  But when that time came a week later, I did, but the manager got mad and accused me of lying and wanting to get 'free food'. When I explained that I order all time and even tip the guys big he said he did care and kept on being awfully rude. I explained that it was about the principle and not really about the five bucks or whatever the egg roll cost. He asked if I want to cancel the order which I did and haven't been back in years.

Showed up on graduation night.  What a mob!  Enterprise Fish was very organized and handled this large crowd quite well.  Interesting décor - kind of a fixed up fish processing plant look.  The main kitchen is in the center of the restaurant behind glass and under huge industrial vents that were painted red.    Our service was excellent.  Got the popcorn shrimp and great Hawaiian poke appetizers to split with our large group.  The artichoke appetizer was good, but not something easily split apart.    My grilled scallops were excellent.  Big scallops tasted great with the grilled veggies and rice pilaf.    Parking was a bit strange.  Although the address for Enterprise Fish is on State Street and the restaurant does face State St., it's above an underpass for the freeway and you can't get there directly from State St.  In fact, you can't get there from the nearby municipal pay lot next to the train and bus station.  You have to drive around the lot to get to the west side of Enterprise Fish and park in their free lot off East Montecito.   When it's crowded and you have to wait, you'll get a buzzer so you can hang outside.  Pretty warm inside, but it cooled off as the sun went down.

This place just opened a few days ago and I wanted to try it. Let's just say it did not disappoint. The cocktails are amazing and the food is even better! They make Cuban sandwiches and plates. I ordered the pollo y ajo sandwich. It was great! I will try the Cubano sandwich next.

A dining experience at Bouchon is always top-notch because the family that owns it treat customers like friends coming to their home. The chef uses quality, farmer's market ingredients. The food, wine and service are all excellent. On this last visit, we enjoyed a Brut Rosé with our mushroom ragout and crab cake appetizers. The crab cakes were exceptionally plump and tasty--I recommend them! I had lovely lamb chops cooked to perfection. Other family members had the lobster pot pie (with a buttery crust) and the short rib which was the best I've ever tasted--seared nicely on the outside but tender and moist from long braising. Also recommended: the cake with berry sauce and McConnell's Marion Berry ice cream. Really a flawless experience!
Burger is great! Drinks are so good and service is great!! The cheese board was just okay but everything else was spot on! Will come back

This place is a favorite for my husband and I when we visit Santa Barbara! This place just reminds of of a home cooked meal at yours moms... or something like that! During the Christmas season I love their decor. It's just so lovely and feels like home.   The food is good!  A great place to wine and dine and get some yummy Italian food... you won't be sorry!   Their music is also just lovely.
Very cute place with excellent service! Exciting but approachable Moroccan cuisine with a California spin.

We picked this place for a light lunch take out. this place is the real deal if you are looking for good tacos at reasonable prices. We had an assortment of beef, chicken and fish and they were all very good. I would have liked a little more pico de gallo but this is being finicky. The corn chips with guacamole were not overly oily and the avocados very fresh. Mony's , well done!
Great service and amazing food! Loved the attention to detail on each plate!

I cleaned my plate...that's how delicious it was. I am so glad I finally tried Opal, one of my new favorite restaurants in Santa Barbara. I came here on a Friday night. Party of two. It was a busy night and we did not have reservations, but we were quickly seated at the bar. The staff was friendly, bar tender started pouring refreshing drinks. The food was top notch.

This is a simple, straight forward restaurant. They make big huge burritos and there are lots of salsas to choose from. While their menu has a few categories, they can make almost anything you want. Prices are reasonable and they accept cash and credit. There's plenty of free parking in their private lot and a nice dining area. It's old school to say the least. I was instantly transported to the college days walking in here. It just has a very homey nice vibe. Rudy's is conveniently located near the freeway and other shops.   Staff here is very friendly. The kitchen is open and you can see the chefs preparing food. They have authentic Mexican bottled beverages from natural sugar sodas to imported cervezas. The vibe is super casual, so feel free to stroll in here after a hike or on your way to the beach, but obviously dress appropriately.

This place seems to be the area where everyone books their wedding after parties haha. Well each time I came here that was the case. This was a total of two times in the same week.   I came here initially with a small group of friends and I liked their back area, but their drinks are on the more expensive side. The previous yelpers are correct. You are paying for the atmosphere. If you want to have a great time on the cheaper side it's not necessary to come here. This place is basically for a little more upscale crowds. I do have to point out that the service is nice and the staff is extremely polite and accommodating. I guess I will change my three star rating to a four star because of that. Good job Blush. I guess you are catering to the upscale niche. I'll be elsewhere though ;]  -Tej

Cute breakfast lunch place in Montecito. It serves fresh French pastries and hot/cold sandwiches. I got there late morning and enjoyed a croissant, sugared brioche bun, and a egg cheese bacon brioche breakfast sandwich. The brioche breakfast sandwich was spectacular! The filling was made perfectly, eggs cooked soft and creamy- all served on a fluffy buttery brioche bun. I could eat 10 of those!   Sadly the pastries wasn't to my liking. Both the brioche bun and croissant was dry and had a staleness to them. Disappointing.

What a delight! I came for their rebranding, and have popped in a few more times. It keeps getting better and better. I enjoy the wine 'machine' that lets you do tastings, half glasses, or full ones. Their wines are from unique small-batch wineries that make 500 cases or less, so it's a great place to taste some fabulous boutique wines. The owner made a recommendation of a spicy red, and I really enjoyed it. The food is really nicely presented, tastes great, solid portions, and priced reasonably. The service - well it feels like a place locals will haunt because they're very friendly and treat you like you've been a regular for ages. I am so happy to have this great place in the neighborhood!

Farmer Boy is a staple in Santa Barbara. Been coming here for years and the service as well as the food is always consistently tasty and great!! I highly recommend this place always!! To anyone and everyone. It's easy to make a plate vegan and or vegetarian. They will work with you.
Just super simple Mexico City style tacos.  My fave was bacon and cheese.  Simply outrageous.  The poblano pork and cheese was great as well.  All house made tortillas.  Must visit.
Cute little sandwich shop with great service and excellent sandwiches.

A solid happy hour kept the Fishouse near the top of the rotation for my friend's and I over many years, but unfortunately, they've fiddled with the menu and the restaurant and this spot has become less appealing.  First of all, happy hour here is typically packed and seating is 'first come first served', so it could be like the hunger games when you and your friends are staring down another group from across the dining area.  Once seated, the happy hour menu is extensive with a wide selection of food and drink options.  My biggest gripe is that the menu staples like the fish / shrimp tacos are now significantly smaller with a higher price and drinks have increased in price more rapidly than expected.  As a capitalist, I understand that prices typically increase over time, but when price / quality take a significant hit, I'd rather they increase their prices and not mess with food quality. Having said this, I still enjoy happy hour at SB Fishouse, it's simply not as compelling as it used to be.   Dining in the restaurant area is nice - you're presented with a view of the waterfront and the full dinner menu is decent, though not particularly innovative.  Service is typically good but, for some reason I've almost always walked away saying my experience was 'OK' given the price.  Overall, the SB Fishouse is nice, you won't be disappointed but you won't be wow'd either.  Tourists will like the location, but you can do better in SB if you're looking for more than a view.
Wonderful little cafe attached to a hotel. It is surprisingly delicious! Very nice staff, great prices and portions, and everything is so yummy. Enjoy!

My husband and I love this place! It's always considered a special treat because it feels like a lush oasis with great, unique food in a very unassuming spot. I feel like we've nailed which combination dishes we should order in order to enjoy the excellent cuisine and leave full (without spending an arm and a leg).  First up: get that Halloumi Cheese. There is *no* doubt that this should be on your must-order list. A grilled slice of that sandwiched between watermelon slices, basil, mint, and cucumber. If I could order just this with some skewers, I'd be a happy camper.  Next: get the Batata Harra. These are spicy potatoes with a ton of flavor from the added cilantro, citrus, and garlic.  For the main dish, we've loved splitting the Mashawi Combo. It comes with all the classics you want from a Lebanese restaurant such as kebabs, tabouli, hummus, and baba ghannoush. It is abundant, too, so you should have plenty of food!  The only downsides to me can be the overall prices and the occasional belly dancing entertainment (personally not a fan). Their drink prices are not ideal, and I have found it hard to justify a glass of wine or a cocktail when an after-dinner drink nearby would be a much better value. But other than that, I do enjoy the amazing food and atmosphere!

I'm updating my review to reflect the outstanding quality of cakes and pastries available at Renaud's. A family favorite is the flourless chocolate torte. We recently purchased one to freeze and bring to family living out of town. It was just as beautiful and delicious after 2 weeks in the freezer as it is fresh.

Fried rice with beef and poached eggs - yummy! Lots of meat, just the way I like it.  Well seasoned and unique concept. Asian fusion food with options of full salad or brown rice or gluten free.  Casual atmosphere and eclectic decor with several couches to sit and eat.  It's a blend of things that on  the surface doesn't seem to go together but somehow they make it work.  Definitely will be visiting this place next time we're in Santa Barbara.

Highly highly recommend this place. Your taste palettes will thank you. My favorite was the crispy duck salad but the wings are some of the best wings I have ever had. For a little table munchie the chips were really yummy and super light. You will want to order a few things because the plates are for sharing. But that's what I love about this place is you can order a bunch of different things and have fun with your meal! Once again definitely a must try here in Santa Barbara!!!!!!!

Love the pizzas, love the subs, the salads are always fresh... Everything is always fresh and yummy. I love the sausage and pepper sub. It has melted cheese and grilled onions and the bread is toasted perfectly. My husband loves the Italian sub and it is GOOD. The chicken Parmesan sub is one of my favorites too. The Siciliana pizza is yuuuuummmmyyyy. It has sausage and roasted red peppers and very thinly sliced tomatoes and cheese. Simple, flavorful and very satisfying. Delivery or takeout it's worth it.

Can't say enough great things about this place! 4 stars because their hours are weird and sporadic, but they've improved on them over the years. But, their food is so good, it's worth it trying to find out when they're open. The Thai Basil Burger is literally one of the most juiciest and flavorful burgers I've ever had - it surprises me every time I order it. Their Fried Rice is also pretty dang amazing, and garnishing it with chili garlic sauce is a great touch. Blue Owl is one of the few late-night Asian places around SB, so I'm super glad they're here!

You CANNOT miss out on this place for breakfast! There are tables in the sand so you can eat breakfast or lunch with your shoes off while you watch the beach and the locals enjoy the sun. Great omelettes and homefries! Gorgeous surroundings and great food.

The food here was great and the customer service was excellent. I got the chicken and shrimp chow fun, scallion pancake, and the chicken with yellow chive. The chow fun was really good and the meat to noodle ratio was good. The scallion pancake was disappointing I felt as if their was still flour dust on it and it wasn't the best I've had as it had an odd taste. The chicken with yellow chive was the star of the show as I did not know what to expect flavor wise and was happy with the dish that I finished the whole plate. I definitely recommend coming here as they were all so nice and patient!

Location is amazing and the view from the balcony is beautiful. The service were off their game today and it took over 20min to just get a drink. Matcha mule had a mild flavor but good. Poke bowl's fish selection was fresh but the amount wasn't worth the price and overly deep bowl made it a bit awkward to eat. Firecracker shrimp wasn't too oily or heavy.

Right off the bat, I have to start by saying that Sean is one of the best bartenders I've ever come across. He's attentive, personable, courteous, and best of all, he makes the effort to remember you. Everything you could ask for in a bartender. He alone is a good enough reason to come back time and again.  The Benchmark Blonde is hands down my favorite beer on tap up and down State Street, and I don't even normally like blondes.  The shrimp tacos are so incredible, they're the only thing I ever order.  Happy hour is the best time to go, as most of their best items are discounted, including the drafts and the shrimp tacos.

Came here for a late night snack because it was recommended by the locals. Ordered the things they were known for and have to say my only disappointment was the Mac and cheese, it was bland. Everything else was delicious. Those pub loaded fries were outstanding. Fries perfectly cooked with the right amount of sauce and meat - so good. The cheeseburger egg rolls served with the pickles on the side and this special sauce - taste like you are eating a real cheeseburger - I liked it! It was something I never had before. The tikka chicken masala was like eating at an Indian restaurant - they hit that one out of the park. I don't know if I would visit this place that late again, it was next to a wild pub and you can't use their restroom at night which is no bueno but kudos on the delicious food!

So last week I left a review of a Margherita pie I had at Revolver. That pie was phenomenal! Go try one for yourself. Like I stated in the review of that pie, I was going to try a Pepperoni pie today. I did and It was the best pepperoni pie on the West Coast. I will debate this with anyone at any time, any where! You would have to travel to NYC to get a pizza to rival this one. My credibility is very important so I'm going to score today's pie fairly and accurately. Before I do this, I want to put out a disclaimer. I want everyone to know that I broke a cardinal rule when I picked up my pie. Earlier today When I called I asked that my pie be ready at 3:30.... I showed up at 3:15. I should have waited outside until it was 3:30. The interior of the restaurant is very small and the chefs noticed that I was waiting for my pie. As a matter of customer service, I'm sure my pie was removed from the oven and placed in the box and given to me. So, the rating for this pie is lower than what it would have been if the pie was left in the oven longer as I'm sure it would have been had I showed up at 3:30. Not their fault! I rate today's pie a 8.2.... still an extremely good score, but not reflective of what it should have been had I not ruined it for them. I'm going to order another pepperoni pie next week and rate it. I won't show up early to pick it up. All being said, this is the best pizza in Santa Barbara.... until next weekend.

Was there on 1/12/13 and I will not be back. By far the worst customer service I've ever experienced in my entire life. Just like everyone else writing about,  this place has no idea how to run a restuarant and the most shocking of it all is the owner is the most rude disrespectful owner I've ever encountered in a restuarant or a business. She should do herself a favor and not deal with the customers. Save your $ and don't support nastiness from the owner/servers.

Please don't use the pandemic as an excuse for poor and inexcusable customer service!!! Every other establishment we visited that day had outstanding employees. The only trying times was us enduring our visit to your bakery!!!!

Tons of beers on draft and they're all local, also had a margaritas that was really good.  The place is open and big so you don't feel cramped at all.  It's my new go to spot since they have the NFL package and awesome seating. At some point ill try everything on the menu, but for now their crispy chicken sandwich and some loaded fries are the way to go.  Would definitely recommend come checking out this place.  I had to fight the urge to want to sit on their patio that has a giant fountain full of turtles, and when we came, live music out there too. Looks like they're all day Sunday happy hour will have me as a regular.

The bartender graciously poured me a couple of tastes before I decided on my wine . The Albariño was my reluctant choice (I had to order SOMEthing) but it was weak at best. Then I saw the price on the bill of $15 and I was shocked! A very small glass as well! Ridiculous.

Grabbed drinks here for happy hour with co-workers.  They were divine.  Strawberry Tequila??? I'm in heaven.  Ordered a cheeseplate and flatbread, both were great.  Can't wait to try dinner.  I took a star off for service, the manager yelled at the bartender right in front of us.  'ARE YOU F***ING KIDDING ME YOU DIDN'T ID THEM YET?? ARE YOU FU***ING STUPID??!!'  He was SO rude, not only to his bartender, but to us.  He kept glaring at us like we were there to cause trouble...I just wanted a cocktail and snack! For the record, we were all over 21, and we hadn't even ordered drinks yet.  We had simply sat down.  He should have handled the situation in a much more professional manner...it really turned me off.  The lack of manners and professionalism is intolerable.  Food was fab though, so maybe if Cadiz can hire a better management team, it can be a 5*.

I'd probably come back here. It was interesting. We didn't drive up onto the Wharf like most. We parked our bikes at the sidewalk bike rack and walked up. We almost had dinner here because there's about five restaurants, i think. There's a sea life museum or aquarium or something like that for $8 adult entry. There's a fourtune teller, candy store, a few gift shops and fish and chips place. Parking can be tricky so, use the valet. No bare feet and no high heels. The wood planks are difficult to walk on in some areas. Watch your step so you don't trip.
Wow. Loved everything we ordered Falafel and olives and hummus were so good Green goddess salad was incredible  Everything fresh and delicious

We stopped by here because they opened a little later than other places. We sat down outside and there were plenty of heat lamps to keep us warm. The waitress was very nice and amused us because we were already a little topsy. We had the pretzel and the shrimp and it was very tasty! The pretzel came with three dipping sauces (cheese, caramel, and one more) and they were all good. Maybe the best pretzel I've ever had. The shrimp was decent but I'd come back for the pretzel for sure.

stearns wharf wow a cool spot with great seafood restaurant bars and they got a good candy shop that sell salt water taffy and a great selection of home made candy to pick from and there is a wine taste room wow stearns wharf  good place to check out when u are in santa barbara yelp on yelpers

Updaaaaate!  An additional Star for the following reasons, yay!  1) DJ Zeke and DJ Jerry are always rockin' the house with the latest beats.  And they will always stop to hug and kiss you.  Especially if you're crowding the booth and screaming at them.  2) The bartenders still know how to make awesome LCs, Long Island Teas and 7&7s.  So awesome, you might accidentally tip one of them $17 for three drinks in your drunken stupor.  3) The couches on the third floor are extremely comfortable.  So comfortable, that one can easily pass out,.. before midnight.   4) The bouncers are kind enough to 'ask people to leave.'  And yes, there is a difference between that, and being kicked out.   5) There is a quick getaway to the back parking lot through the patio.  But be aware of the cracks in the pavement.  You just might fall and bust both your knees, only to find yourself bleeding in the backseat of your friend's car.  And no, all of that wasn't me by the way.   Okay fine,.. it was me.    But at least we all made great memories for my brother, Steve H.'s 28th birthday!  Unfortunately, I can't remember any of them.

The difference between 'good' and 'great' is the waitstaff NOT the food. The food is absolutely outstanding except for a few things which I will tell you about.  Went for lunch. Had a baldish older guy try and seat me inside and was lying about reservations. Bad vibe on this guy which made for an uncomfortable meal.   Ice tea- not brewed strong enough.  Olive appetizer seemed old.  Pasta dishes were outstanding.  The olive oil was a bit 'spicy' and not what they have had in the past which was disappointing.     An absolute must for the pasta dishes which are inspired, original and incredible.

Usually like this place. But last night I ordered take-out of rad na (noodle dish) and what i got was a mostly-liquid, soggy, tasteless, mess. I wasn't expecting this soup. i doubt they would serve this atrocity inside the restaurant, but I won't be ordering take-out from them again.
Gelsons is great... it may not be able to get all its produce on time right now.

Great food. Great drinks. Great service. I've been here several times and everything has been delicious! And the owner Hakan has the best customer service. He remembers people, takes care of everyone and is generally very kind! Highly recommend!

I have been dining here for years but the service as of late has been awful. Last week we went for breakfast and sat at our table. For a good 30 min before being greeted by a server. Their excuse was change of shifts. Other restaurants can manage this seamlessly. This week we went for dinner and my husbands order was served wrong and my dinner was served cold. I really love this place so I hope it will be corrected.

The wait was definitely not worth it on a Saturday evening.  Our group of six wanted a nicer seafood spot that could accommodate those who were craving fresh fish and ones who didn't want seafood. Brophy Bros was one of the first ones that popped up on yelp. We called ahead and they said it was first come first serve for spots, so we walked upstairs and were handed a buzzer and told that the wait would be 30-45 minutes. There's a beautiful harbor where all the boats are docked, so there's some view...but once the sun goes down it's huddle up and beat the cold! There are other eatery spots in the same area, but it's not a lot of diversity--there's sushi, and small tokens shop that helped us kill some time. We ended up waiting for an hour, and by then we all felt like we were students again pulling all-nighters to stay awake by 10PM.  Immediately after being seated, we were given complimentary sourdough bread and butter--SUCH a lifesaver! We mauled through baskets of them, and the servers were all so friendly and kept on offering us more. I ordered the fish sandwich with fries, which was ahi for that night. I wish I could have asked for it medium cooked through, with a bit of rare ahi in the middle, but it's alright. The ahi was fresh and yummy...as well as the fries. I had a taste of my friend's clam chowder which was the best consistency--not too thick or creamy! It seems like those who ordered the seafood pasta loved it the best. My friend who ordered the crabcakes did not like it and ended up sending it back. We think she got food poisoning because right when she got home she threw up. However, none of us had seafood problems and our stomachs were fine.  The space itself is great! It's homey and warm, and the service was very friendly and quick. They probably know how long we have to wait to get seated, so I really appreciate their service. Shoutout to the staff! The portion sizes are huge, and they really let us go ham on the complimentary sourdough bread. However, at that point in the night...for a large group of six, we were just done with waiting. I think the experience would have been better if we arrived earlier and didn't have to sit out in the cold for over an hour. Overall, a long wait...but not worth it for our party of six.

This is my favorite place to get açaí berry bowls! Best I've ever had. Recently, I ordered the Green bowl. Kind of tastes similar to Naked Juice's Green Machine. I liked that it tasted more 'green' than overly sweet. Strawberries add a nice and sweet touch. This is very filling, even if you get the smallest size. About 25% of the bowl will have all the toppings and granola. At the end when you pick up your bowl, you get to choose however much honey you want.  I've ordered almond butter with every bowl I've gotten here (2 bowls total), and each time the almond butter is forgotten. I've had to walk all the way back to retrieve it. So, heads up, if you are ordering a side, I have a feeling that it will be forgotten so you should just make sure you remind them or else you will walk out without the side that you paid for. Still think this is a great establishment! Just want you to get what you paid for.
What a gem!!! We had a fantastic mushroom pizza and chicken salad sandwich.   Great place to stop while wine tasting in the funk zone. Looking forward to returning!

Ha an early dinner at Mizza on Friday. Good food and service. We had a small table facing State St. enough room for our small dog, but it would be tight for a larger dog to be out of the way.  The Prosciutto and grilled peaches with fresh burrata is a wonderful combination, but very small to share.  Hubby had the Chicken Marsala. Chicken was a slightly tough, but a large, thick fillet. The sauce was absolute killer, I think the mushrooms were Porcini...meaty and flavorful.  I had the Butternut Squash Ravioli. It's in a cream sauce, very good. I'd of loved a bit more filling.  Would we go back.....Yes....they're still fairly new.
Good food for the money. Good place to take the family for decent food without breaking the bank. Service is good.

Usually I love this place but went to happy hour tonight and the bartender was very rude to me and my friend. When she asked if we could keep our tab open for happy hour he put a finger in her face (mind you we were the only two people there) and then said 'ya I'll give you a drink if you're old enough' ... we are 23. I believe his name was Scotty? (Tall older light hair) The finger in the face and the attitude made us feel like we were an inconvenience to him which is not the experience we are looking for at a restaurant like that.

I make it a point to visit Los Agaves any time I'm in the SB area. There is almost always a wait out the door, but that's a true testiment to how great their food is. Parking in the area is a bit of an issue. Mostly street parking so you  have to turn around the block a few times, but again, worth it!   Fresh meat and ingredients, very flavorful, plus super cheap. Can't go wrong!  My personal favorites are their nachos and carne asada plate is to die for!
What a Gem in our neighborhood; we will definitely make Louie's a regular spot.  Fine dining and excellent service, ambiance galore.
Nice family owned place. Love their ceasar salad, their pizza, their pasta, and their italian sub. I've tried most of the items on the menu, and i keep coming back year after year.

Apna goes beyond good, it's exceptional. The flavors are bright and unforgettable, after finishing a meal here we are always left thinking about when we will be coming back. If you are craving Indian food in Santa Barbara, it's undoubtedly the best it town.  Every single dish is a knockout. It makes choosing which to order a conundrum every time. The Butter Chicken is the clear center piece and something that shouldn't be missed. If you have kids, I'd say this is the safe bet to introduce them to Indian curry, especially if you get it mild. The Korma though, is mind-blowing, and something the leaves me feeling like I've found the meaning of life. Yes, it's that good. The Saag and Dal of the day are phenomenal and always balance well to any of the curry choices. The curry doused french fries are a crowd pleaser and something you won't see here in California, definitely worth a try. In the end, it all comes down to the naan and rice, and both are freaking amazing!  All the glowing reviews highlight a restaurant that takes its time and care crafting dishes from scratch with fresh ingredients and spices. I read one negative reviewer saying the food was delicious but they had food dye in it, which is hilariously misguided. Indian food is colorful due to the spices used, and that shows at Apna. You won't be disappointed giving Apna a chance.

I've gone to Killer B's about 6 times in the past and I've always had a great experience.  This time around, the service was horrible and the food wasn't prepared right.  Our lunch order 'got lost' and once we got it,  my pulled chicken had numerous bone fragments.  I won't be returning for a long while, if at all.

Perfect place to go out to eat during Covid. Plenty of outdoor dining on a quieter side street downtown. This restaurant is under rated for its culinary spectacularism! (Did I just invent a word?). We did the chef's course, highly recommended. Our waitress Julia and expeditor Doug were fabulous. Will be back soon.
It was a let down.  It's hard to support microwaved eggs at a French bakery.   You can do better.

What a great little unique place attached to an equally unique hotel.  The restaurant has some great indoor and outdoor seating options.   We elected to sit inside as it was a bit cool outside but both are great options.   The staff were friendly accommodating and customer focused.  My wife was still a bit chilly inside and they provided a blanket straight away.  She elected to just use her sweater but they were right on top of things.    The server was attentive and helpful.  I would put this on my shortlist anytime I am in Santa Barbara.

Perhaps the best burgers I have had in a long time. Super juicy - like run down your elbows juicy. Every burger I have tried I have liked. I love the sweet potato fries. I think my favorite burgers are Funk Zone and Montecito.
Great spot for a celebration. The restaurant is beautifully set up and peaceful. Will definitely be returning!
My favorite Mexican place!! The food is authentic, delicious each visit!  I can't help get food from here each time I visit SB.

The place has a 'jazzy' feel but fell a bit short for me. Now it was lunch which maybe that was the problem   Pecan Chicken Picatta was a decent plate, the veggies where way undercooked and oily lacking any kind of real flavor.  The cornbread muffins were tiny and the flavor was ok.     The bottom line is I guess this is 'Cajun' for SB, but if you have ever had the real deal this is pretty dam far off the mark.

The burgers are yummy. We loved the more extensive beer selection. The mac n cheese has changed. The images are not accurate. This place was good enough for us to look and see if there was once closer to home. There is!

First time trying this place out we got the homemade chicken mole enchiladas (my fav), steak fajitas and shrimp ceviche which we were skeptical to order ,but were pleasantly surprised. Washed it all down with homemade horchata. Our server was very polite and attentive will definitely be back!

So you are going to Santa Barbara and figure you want some yummy Mexican food. Milpas is the street you want, forget the hordes of people milling around State street. You can hit Supa Rica, which is consistently good street food. You can go to Los Agaves, great salsa choices and the authentic flavors are great. But you should go to Taqueria El Bajio. This place is bomb ass delicious.  Had the carnitas gordita, filled with meat and other tastiness it is served between two masa tortilla that is as big as a small child's head. No skimping on the meat which was tender and perfect.  The wife had a spicy chicken taco and we shared a spicy pork one that was as tasty as any restaurant is serving. Everything on the menu looks good and next times am trying the shrimp caldo which looks simply amazing. Go for the food not the ambience.
Mesa pizza has always been a solid choice when I have a craving for pizza in sb. I have never had a single issue ordering through them, and they are always very friendly and courteous.

Huge selection of artisan cheeses and cured meats! There's also an attached eatery that sells yummy-looking food and desserts.   My boyfriend and I spent over 20 minutes sampling different cheeses and meats with the ever-so-patient Laila. We told her what kinds of meats and cheeses we like (basically everything) and she made some awesome recommendations! She was very knowledgeable and super friendly despite our indecisiveness.   We walked away with 3 different kinds of meat (prosciutto san daniele, soppressata salami, mole salami), 4 kinds of cheese (Humboldt fog, Ewephoria, Fromage d'Affinois, Taleggio latte crudo) and some plain crackers, since they were out of baguettes. All of their cheeses are amazing quality from different countries all over the world. The also have such a wide variety so we tried to get one from each category (hard, soft, goat's milk, cow's milk, etc). We asked for about a $5 amount of each item and with the number of items we chose, this was more than enough to build a meat and cheese board for 2 that filled us up (with a bottle of wine, of course). The amount shown in my photo is 1/2 of what we purchased. With a few dried fruit, nuts, and honey it could have been even better!  Everything came out to about $28 excluding tip. Amazing price, amazing quality, and amazing service. You can't go wrong at C'est Cheese!
They have a great veggie burger, service seems to be good whenever I am their. I'm lot a meat eater anymore, but when I was at had one of Their burgers and it was great.

Called breakfast...and guess what...no breakfast. Just another coffee spot...in the midst of 8 other places that serve coffee and espresso. Wish they would close. The neighborhood needs a family restraunt...not another place to buy coffee...and stale pasteries...oh they have art.  Who cares...and vegan donuts? if you want a donut, you go to spudnuts. This place is a joke and after reading other similar reviews, you would think the owner would make some quick changes...nope
Fun little place to eat at. Got the fish and chips which were fresh and delicious but definitely felt heavy because they were fried. Couldn't have more than 1-2. The fish was amazing and fresh

We did a catering order for an afternoon lunch for our anniversary, and it was wonderful. Kathryn, one of the owners, was very responsive to my questions and helped me choose exactly the right menu and amount of food for the size of our party. She was also able to easily help me choose a vegetarian menu at my request.  The whole process was streamlined; it was easy to communicate my questions/get help with my order, place my order and pay securely online, pick up, and serve our guests. Beautiful presentation, AMAZING FOOD that our guests and we loved...   Highly recommended.  Thank you, C'est Cheese!

Absolutely fantastic breakfast. We had to wait about 20m before being seated (no reservations allowed), but ordered coffee to sip while in line.   Ordered the lobster Benedict and smoked salmon scramble on everything bagel and both were perfection. Would absolutely recommend hands down.   Coffee was pretty darn good too.
I've been eating here since they opened. The whole is awesome. If you like Italian it is the place to go.  bring the whole family and have a blast.

Hard to place Backyard Bowls between a 3 and 4 star review. I REALLY like their smoothie bowls, and overall l enjoy frequenting this location when I'm in the area. Their fruit is fresh, and their granola is as well; they also have an excellent variety of smoothie bowls and smoothies. I wouldn't necessarily call them true açaí bowls, as the base is a blend of fruits, fruit juice, and açaí, but regardless they are filling and rejuvenating yum yum meals. Some of the bases for the bowls are quite tasty, with strong tartness and sweetness; others are a bit more subtle and mild.   But, the wait for your bowl is INSANE. We are talking 30-40 minutes from ordering to finally getting your bowl. And this is during a quiet hour in the morning. I have never waited that long at any other smoothie bowl/açaí bowl/Jamba Juice-like place. Further, there were multiple other individuals waiting who were waiting just as long, if not longer for their bowls. Unfortunately, the last time we were here (at this location specifically), the wait was also strangely long. I know they are making my bowl from scratch, but it's also starting to feel like they are going out and picking the fruit that day as well. Meanwhile, a bunch of people are picking up their online orders with seemingly no wait at all. We couldn't figure out what the issue is, because there were a gazillion people working in the back.

Old Santa Barbara, love the atmosphere. Very friendly, wonderful patio in the back where you can play Bocce ball. Pasta dishes are very good, my favorite is the meat and tomato sauce pasta cooked perfectly. This is a very charming and authentic restaurant.

This was one of the best burgers I've had in a long time and I love the vibe of the whole place.  The manager on duty, Macy, really went out of her way to make sure we newbies knew our options and she gave us a few tips that were spot on.  My only warning is to keep the food over the table because this was one very tall, juicy, yummy burger.  We'll definitely be back to try some of the other dishes.
This is one of my all time favorite Mexican restaurants. The Rajas con Queso are absolutely amazing. I would recommend this to anyone. The Flan and churros are also fantastic for dessert.

My wife and I had lunch here today after taking a drive through the mountains.  The restaurant had been highly recommended to us by friends and the limo driver who took us wine tasting a couple of weeks ago.  We were not disappointed - the food was great - the tri-tip sandwich is the best I have ever had and my wife had the spicy corn chowder which she said was very delicious.  The building is authentic, rustic and just a fun place and the staff was very accommodating and friendly.  Great lunch!

** Funk Zone** They have beer tasting samplers here. It comes on a wooden carved California. But who knew that the food was going to be that delicious? It is not your average bar food.   Those chicken skewers were juicy and perfectly spiced!! It came with tzasiki sauce. This was easily one of the best chicken skewers I have ever been served. Mmm... We also ordered a side of potato cake with it.  They will cut your Brass Burger in half if you want to share. There are fries on the side. We ordered the burger to be safe but if I had known that the chicken skewers were going to be ultra delicious, we would have tried other items.  The brewery itself was a lot smaller than I expected. If you have a big group, you probably can only fit if no one else was in there. They do have a babysitting area for kids in a room next to the Bistro but it was not open at the time. There are toys, etc but I would say that it is for 3 years and under.   Just a skip and a hop away from the beach.

I am a big customer during the winter months because their soup is a hit with my family. Actually all their food is home style traditional Mexican food and the family that works their couldn't be more hospitable. Theirs soups are so flavorful and have big helpings of veggies and meat and come with piping hot, steaming corn or flour tortillas.  The ONLY reason I didn't give this restaurant five stars is because I can never find parking in the area but it's not their fault. The restaurant actually looks like a residence with tables on the porch area as well as inside.  It's located in a residential/commercial area semi hidden and I usually order for pick-up anyway so I sometimes park where I shouldn't which I do not recommend.

This place has it all!  Great location with a view of the water and the wharf, classy indoor and outdoor eating areas, rooftop bar and friendly, welcoming service. Generous portions of delicious food and a wide variety of beverages.  Be sure to order the Crispy Korean Cauliflower!

This one star is for the eggplant parmigiana only. The service is great, 5 stars for that.   Other dishes we have tired are amazing: chicken parmigiana, ravioli and lasagna... All 5 stars.  However, I love my eggplant and let me tell you-I was not sure if it was eggplant or jerky. It was so hard and over fried that I couldn't eat more than a bite. I hate returning dishes, so I just drank tea and took the dish home to throw it away :(

This place was really just okay. We went on a weekday so it wasn't very crowded, which was nice. The service was good until we were done eating and then it took way too long to get the check. The food was okay, nothing exceptional.  We ordered:  Mimosas - Freshly squeezed orange juice (yay); very little champagne/sparkling wine (boo!). Overall, meh.  Apple cinnamon beignets - They were more like donut balls than beignets. I've been to the South too many times to call these beignets but they were solid for what they were (whatever they were).  French toast - Made with baguettes, it was just way too much bread and not nearly enough french toast 'batter.' When one makes french toast, they dip it in the egg-based batter and they needed to dip these baguettes for much longer. It was basically like eating a baguette. I like baguettes but I wanted french toast.  One of their benedicts (I forget which one) - It was good but very forgettable (as denoted by me forgetting the name of the benedict.  There are so many good brunch places on this block alone. I don't think I will be choosing this one ever again. Just so average (and mildly expensive).

My other half surprised me with a dinner here for my birthday. We drove over from a visit of Santa Barbara. I was getting more and more confused the deeper we got into a residential area. The drive was beautiful as was the driveway. The property looked amazing at night, I can't begin to imagine what it looks like during the day. The restaurant wasn't awfully busy, and was very intimate with very very dim lighting.   We were seated on the heated patio. The service was attentive, and pushy. We ordered drinks, and settled on multiple appetizers and one entree. The sea urchin was delicious. I forget what it was enveloped in, creme fraiche and something else. The combination was wonderful and it cut through the richness of the urchin very well. I was a bit sad the urchin spines were not moving and thus the shell was used as a serving bowl.  The consumme was great and probably my second favorite. The bone marrow was decent as was the sashimi salad. The presentation exceeded the taste.   Sadly we were pretty stuffed at this point since we ate at a few places prior to this dinner. But we shared the spaghetti carbonara. Why would one order spaghetti carbonara at a restaurant like this? Because it had jamon iberico! It was probably the best carbonara I've had.   Overall, impressive restaurant. It is apparent a lot of care and detail went into everything from the grounds, to the decor, to the food presentation. I had slightly higher hopes in terms of food taste though.

Ok we were a bit skeptical on a sandwich shop with such high reviews but man once you go and turn the corner to see the line waiting to pick up orders, you start to salivate! Then you finally get the sandwiches and OMG a the bread is amazing, the spicy flavors kick you in the pants and the uniqueness of things so simple is on point! Definitely making this a usual stop in SB!

I have been here a few times now and left feeling different each time about the food. What was consistent is very little fish on the rolls and almost paper thin slices at times.
