The rolls are also on the smaller side. I ordered the spareribs twice. The first time was delicious; therefore I came back and ordered it again, but the meat was chewy and not good the second time. Staff is friendly.

Our friends like this place so much they insisted on having breakfast here on our way out of SB. We found it by accident. It looks like a converted old Denny's but it's clean. Our first visit was for lunch and the service was impeccable. I ordered the shrimp po boy. It was ok but the fries were awesome. The kid ordered a BLT without the T . He enjoyed it. Breakfast food was better. The kid ordered eggs Benedict with bacon instead of Canadian ham. He loved it but he also loves the Knorr Benedict sauce from a packet best so . . . I liked their pancakes, coffee was hot and refilled often. My husband enjoyed his scramble and hash browns. A great place to fuel up before the drive home.
I always order from here cause thier food is awesome, large portions, quick service and they are nice. Highly suggest them if u are in the mood for some chinese.

BIG shout out to management who responded to my first review!  They asked us to come back, and we did yesterday before Happy Hour.  Wandered into the bar unannounced and the bartender, Megan, was busy, but she said 'hi' right away and came over to get our drink orders in just a few minutes. We let her know about our 'situation' after we ordered,and she assured us that this would be a different experience....and it was!  We enjoyed our drinks (Cadillac marg was not a mix...I watched her make it from scratch...), warm chips, salsa, and we ordered the chicken taquitos, which were crisp, yummy and a large portion. The staff who wandered in & out while working (and I never saw Megan tell them that we were to be treated special...) struck up friendly conversation. My hubby and I appreciated the fact that a poor review got attention and there is a sincere effort to recognize problems and improve.  And I guess the bartender who wiped his face, the bar and then the glasses is no longer there.
Really good Classic Benedict here and the potatoes cooked just right - definitely recommended. The staff were also helpful positioning the baby stroller which is always appreciated when alone.

Came here for a lunch break during a full day of being a tourist in Santa Barbara.  The FisHouse is across the street from the pier and the wharf, so there is something of a view but mostly you'll see traffic from the busy highway that is directly in front of the restaurant.  But on the bright side, this is an actual restaurant with parking, indoor/outdoor comfortable seating and real service.  Plus a parking lot and no fishy smell like the restaurants right on the pier and wharf.  So we definitely thought it was a better choice than some of the more casual places right on the water.  Also, we had a Groupon - $50 worth of food and drinks for $30.  It's a good deal in that it can be used anytime - lunch or dinner...  and you actually get a lot more bang for the buck during lunch.   We chose an indoor table, which was still located next to front facing windows and afforded us a view of the traffic and off in the distance, the waterfront.  Service was fast and friendly with no issues whatsoever with the Groupon.    My SO started with the grilled artichoke ($9.95) and moved on to the Strawberry Fields Salad ($9.95).  NOTE - she is a vegetarian and while there aren't a ton of choices at FisHouse, there are a few.  Anyway, she really liked both of her dishes and the portion sizes were more than generous.  The salad was lovely with some candied walnuts and bits of goat cheese.    I went with a bowl of clam chowder ($6.25 and HUGE).  The soup was creamy and thick - just as I like it.  A good rendition...  but if you're not starving you can definitely make do with a cup.  They also serve the clam chowder in a bread bowl, and that sounds like a filling lunch.  I also ordered a Lobster Roll ($17.95) - because 'when in Rome...' right?  The lobster roll wasn't the New England version I was expecting - it's more like a lobster salad as opposed to big buttery chunks of lobster meat.  But it was still good.  I do prefer my lobster rolls more traditional but as a lobster salad, this still contained plenty of meat and was again, a large sandwich.   The onion rings were very good though...  definitely get yourself an order of these.  They have a full bar so you may need to get a cocktail or two to make your lunch hit the $50 mark...  but hey, no judgement here.  FisHouse was better than the average tourist trap you expect in Santa Barbara.  Recommend - especially if you can snag a deal.
Pretty tasty BBQ spot. Must try the Frito Pie appetizer!

Great atmosphere and top quality food and service.  This is just what East Beach has needed.   Ordered the Dads Mai Tai (ahh.. reminded me of Maui Westin Kaanapali!) and Grapefruit Martini... both excellent, and definitely had a good kick of booze in them.  (4.5, ~$15)  We selected the Asian Crispy Ribs appetizer, which were excellent with the salad complimenting the ribs nicely. (5/5, $17)  For lunch we split the Classic Cheeseburger, 1/2 pound beef, pickles, tomato,  toasted onions, cheese, on a light bun.  Cooked medium rare - - it had that perfect charred flavor that I think makes it one of the best in town.  (5/5, $17)  As for those wanting their East Beach Grill back... why?  That place was a dump.  Nice job City of SB and Reunion Kitchen!  The Cabrillo Pavilion is now a classy, beautiful beach location for all of us to enjoy again in Santa Barbara.  And I have a feeling our Montecito neighbors will have found their replacement for Cafe del Sol.

Santa Barbara needs more restaurants like The Black Sheep. The menu is adventurous and the food is delicious. The bone marrow bruschetta can't be missed. The risotto, beef cheeks, and carpaccio are sensational. The added bonus is that the owners and staff create a friendly vibe that makes it feel like you are dining at the home of a good friend.

My very large family had an excellent dinner here on a Labor Day Sunday.  We had made a reservation for 22 a week ahead and even confirmed it the day of dinner.  When we got their, they didn't have our reservation. (The manager later explained that the worker who had both made the reservation and later confirmed it was not authorized to do so.)  Remarkably, they were able to seat us together (excellent job on their part).  The place has lots of different types of seating from couch-type booths, to standard tables, bar-stool height tables.  The food and drinks were varied, reasonably priced, and very good.  If they can get a 22 person group to be happy with their food and service, they would have no trouble with regular-sized groups.  I would go back were I to return to Santa Barbara.

Pricey?  yes, but also my favorite sandwich shop, hands down.  Stoked they're in SB.  A massive menu for all types (meat-eaters, vegetarians, and vegans), and you can order off menu for sandwiches that are not on the SB menu but are on the menus of their other locations (my favorite is the Tom Brady [vegetarian]).  I agree with the prior reviewer who said it isn't the same experience without the Dutch crunch and the sauce, although I did enjoy my wife's gluten-free bread from Ike's, too.

Top notch decor and ambiance. Great for a date place or just an evening out with friends.   You got to love tapas because even if one dish isn't great, you still have several more. And I found that to be the case here - several fantastic plates and a few that left something to be desired. This is alright as the good dishes were memorable enough to trump any that may have fallen short.   I thought the Pulpo (octopus) was AMAZING. The Vieiras (scallops) were really good as well. The Patatas Bravas were fantastic (one of the most unique potato dishes I've ever had). The Broccolini was great too. The dishes that I wasn't a huge fan of: Coliflor, Tortilla Española, Gambas al Ajillo.   The service was fast and friendly.   Definitely a memorable meal. Pretty pricy though!  I'd suggest reservations for busy times.

Simple, friendly and delicious. An absolute must for their seafood; the fish tacos.  You will come back for those, it's sublime with a cold drink.  Pro tip: if you like spicy get the bright-green jalapeño salsa. Not for amateurs.   Enjoy, and say hello to the staff.

There were six people working behind the counter. We had to wait about 15 minutes for them to finally order my salad even though we were the only ones in line. They were very disorganized and we're not well trained. They didn't know there was a fundraiser that night (the reason we were there). Their beer was not cold enough. Another lady returned her beer for that reason. There were flies everywhere. The pizza is just OK.

This might have been the best bread I've ever had... I'm normally not one to gravitate towards focaccia but this loaf was out of this world. Paired with their artichoke dip... just heaven!  There's always a line and they tend to sell out of their staples, so show up early! Their service is top notch and their employees are very knowledgeable about their products.  Although literally just a hole in the wall, worth making it a destination during your time in SB.

This place does not disappoint. The food is delicious and the drinks are a sight to see. My go to is the Mitla which includes smoked rosemary and mezcal. There is a slight kick to this drink. They have different variations of plates that have mole. If you want mole this is the place to come. They have mole enchiladas, with halibut, tacos just to name a few. I had the mole verde with halibut that included glazed carrots and mashed cauliflower. The potion was perfect and filling but not heavy and the mole was flavorful. Most of the group ordered the enchiladas Oaxaca that have the dark red mole sauce and chicken as the protein with cheese and sour cream.  The restaurant was able to accommodate our big group just make sure to call ahead. They get very busy on the weekends so plan accordingly. The people there are always accommodating and hospitable and make sure that the experience is nothing short of amazing.

After eyeballing this place for almost fifteen years I decided to try these place out last week. There Tacos kind of reminds me of Tacos La Bonita in Oxnard but there meat is juicier and so is there salsa and it's in a homemade corn  tortilla. Great service from the staff. Parking lot is for three cars and can be kind of tight during busy hours. There is parking on the street. But definitely worth the drive.

Stayed here for 2 nights and everything was great. Hotel was clean and the balcony was a nice touch. I have to say I was a little scared cause the other reviews were sketchy but it was a super cute hotel they have 2 bars and the one outside is perfect the bartender Gabby is such a sweet girl. I will be returning to the hotel.

I've been an avid Crushcakes Lover since I first sunk my teeth into a delicious Strawberry Cupcake in 2013, at that time there was a location in Isla Vista as well that I would also frequent. While their Cupcakes are still yummy, and their coffee is always fresh, the food just didn't seem to hold the same standard. I was disappointed today when I came in with my family and the Red Velvet Pancakes just tasted like plain hotcakes that were dyed Red. While the girls that served us were excellent and sweet, the iconic red velvet pancakes where not up to bat. No matter how busy it gets (and it gets pretty busy) the girls are so fast and orderly. So overall great experience as usual. Will definitely be giving them and my beloved pancakes a second chance since they have always been my go to breakfast place.
What a  little gem in Santa Barbara. A lot of choices of European and South part of former USSR food and drink choices. People who run this store always pleasant and helpful.

Finney's was strongly recommended by our hotel staff for a late lunch. Certainly I've had my share of gastropub fare so I wasn't expecting much- but YUM. We had the usual: nachos, pretzel, tacos and flat bread. It was all lickety-finger good with a decent amount of flair.  And it was fast. The carnitas on the nachos was 'I'll stab you with my fork if you eat mine' good. Our vegetarian friend couldn't resist the sausage on the flat bread. Our group had a variety of dietary restrictions and pain-in the butt-edness requests- all of which were handled well by Braeden and the kitchen.  We had planned on meeting another couple after the meal but they arrived early. We asked for a couple of extra chairs so they could squeeze in at our bar top table. Instead of being squished Braeden instead moved us to a larger table so we had more space. Even though that meant double-bussing, moving our food order and losing a better table upfront. Unfortunately his shift ended a bit after that- and that's where the 5th star was lost. Our replacement server disappeared on us for long lengths at a time. At this point Finney's was half as full- and she took twice as long to do anything. We were anxious to make it out for the sunset and service just ground to a halt. However overall it was a great meal, the drinks were good, the ambience was fun and we'll be back.
Food portions reduced a lot.  Order take out 40 minutes in ahead and then wait for another 1+ hour inside of the restaurant when went to pick up.

Finch & Fork and the Lark were definitely the best 'foodie' finds in Santa Barbara.  We couldn't decide which we liked better; we liked them both for entirely different reasons.  The atmosphere and food options at each are so different; however, they both share the same level of quality and thought behind their food preparation.  You will get fantastic flavors and fresh ingredients at each locale.  If you're in town and have time, I'd definitely recommend trying out both.   At Finch & Fork, the ambiance in the dining room is a bit more upscale, yet nothing too 'fancy pants' such that it feels stuffy.  The bar also offers a more relaxed atmosphere that allows for a more casual experience (particularly great for a casual dinner, snacks at the bar, or pre/post-dinner drinks.  Last weekend my husband and I dined for a Friday night dinner in the dining room, and also stopped back in on Sunday evening for a happy hour / snack hour.  On Friday we tried the following items:  Complimentary pickled veggies: 3.5 stars - Yummy treat; nice touch.  Salt & Pepper Chicken Wings: 4 stars - The addition of the honey and chili oil drizzle definitely made these wings.  We were surprised to see that they were fried (we weren't expecting them to be battered); however, all the flavors blended so well together the fact that they were fried did not disappoint us.  Market Fish Taco: 4 stars - This dish was one of my favorites; we loved them so much, we decided then and there to come back to have them again before we left town.  Loved the fact that they were not creamy like a lot of other tacos (which in my opinion ruins fish tacos).  They were so fresh and light, and the vegan sauce served with them is scrumptious!  We asked the waitress to find out what was in it so we could try to recreate it at home :)  Pulled Chicken & Plobano Flatbread: 2.5 stars - My husband and I were not a fan of the flatbread.  It was just way too bland and lacked flavor; the crust was decent.  We agreed we would not get another flatbread upon our next return.  Wasn't terrible and/or didn't ruin our experience, but there are so many other delicious items on the menu we wouldn't want to waste one of our selections on the flatbread again!  Lamb Burger: 5 stars - This lamb burger is neck and neck with our favorite lamb burger that we love in SF.  Couldn't say one was better than the other, because each was so well done, but different styles.  The one at Finch & Fork is amazing; perfectly cooked, and the hint of sweetness to balance out the savory is perfect.  The aioli (which we got on the side) was so delicious, we were dipping the perfectly cooked fries in it (vs. ketchup) in a swine-like style ;)  And no, we didn't feel guilty about it, it was oh so worth every calorie!  On Sunday when we came back, we had a round of drinks along with some of their fish tacos.  If possible, we liked the tacos even better the second time.  Will definitely be back for those again!  Overall, the Finch & Fork is a great restaurant with a charming atmosphere, great service, and delicious food.  Definitely try stopping in for dinner, lunch, or brunch.  We will definitely be back, and are particularly excited to try out their brunch/lunch (the menus are slightly different, so we're excited to try out some of the other items they offer).

Blew in to town and needed lunch asap. Had scouted Los Agaves on Yelp prior to arrival and man did we make the right choice. Al Pastor was excellent as was the Alambres... really really good. Crowd was locals, tourists, working class joes, a great mix. Staff was friendly and efficient and the joint was spotless. We had to go back the next day to be sure it wasn't a fluke. Wise choice because this time we went straight to the ceviche which was fresh and incredible but the piece de resistance, the reason why I will return time and again was the Land And Sea Molcajete. Oh. My. God. Incredible. People: run don't walk. You can get tacos anywhere but Molcajete you can't. The sauce/broth was alone worth the money: thick, rich, flavorful. Three giant butterflied shrimp with a healthy chunk of fresh halibut mixed with chicken and beef and a couple of slices of killer chorizo sausage. Add a little cheese and some clever cactus on top.... sigh. I Can't stop thinking about it. Do you deliver to LA?
Very dog friendly, but our two orders of poached eggs were each hard boiled instead of runny.

Pros Good shrimp, cooked perfectly, nice & spicy. Big portion of mahi on the sandwich. Good service at the bar. Some beer choices. Axxess card deal.  Cons Many empty beer taps. They could feature some craft beers! Only one server in the entire restaurant (hence us sitting at the bar).

Been waiting to try this place out, as I love seafood and SB has surprisingly little choice around really good seafood.  We both ordered the green salad with blackened salmon / opah and it was excellent!  Fantastic, homemade dressing, not over dressed, fresh... the fish done perfect.  Outside seating and friendly staff.  Going back soon for the ceviche!
Wow! The veggie wraps are so fresh and yummy! Freshly made kettle chips are a pleasant bonus :) service is pleasant and prompt. Thank you.
My famous Asada fries are the best. And the tortilla soup is amazing. I highly recommend. Tim  & Lonnie are awesome

Ali with Sugar Cat did the cupcakes for our wedding a couple of weeks ago and I can't recommend her enough. From the start, Ali was great to work with - she asked my (now) wife and I what kind of flavors we liked and set up a consultation for us to try the flavors and select our favorites. We ended up liking pretty much everything and selected our 5 (6 if you include our cake topper) favorite flavors to have as mini-cakes at the wedding. From salted carmel to red velvet to key lime cheesecake, all the cupcakes were delicious and a huge hit at the wedding. We're still getting comments about them weeks later, and people actually contacted me after the wedding to ask if we had leftover cupcakes (as if we'd share). Thanks, Ali!

Wow, what a disappointment. The only reason this isn't a two-star review is because the service was friendly. I have been missing Yanni at McKenzie Market and hoping to find a new spot for gyros...couldn't deny my craving today, so I went into Luna Grill hoping for a good one. After waiting nearly 20 minutes for a to-go order I was told would take 5, I arrived home to find that my sandwich had only been partially wrapped and then placed upside down in the bag, so that when I removed it, all of the contents of the wrap were left on the bottom of the paper bag. Some 're-assembly' required, and even then a disappointment on temperature, flavor, sauce, etc. Anyone have another recommendation for a decent gyros sandwich in Santa Barbara?

Weirdly their food gets better every time I get it. No delivery but food is always ready super fast for pickup. Inside of the restaurant is really nice, but I always order to go. Tons of vegetarian options and I love that their packaging has a lot less plastic than most for to go food. Love this place

I am a foodie, I travel for work and have been fortunate enough to have amazing food all over the world. That being said this is one of the most underrated restaurants in Santa Barbara I can easily say this is one of the top three restaurants in Santa Barbara.  My recommendation would to be to order anything with the burrata on it! The 24 hrs burrata, the pere brushchette , and the stratacciatella brushchette were all amazing! The passion fruit margarita is also to die f  Thank you for upping the food game in Santa Barbara Aperitivo!   Side note, my other two favorite restaurants in Santa Barbara are the Black Sheep and Bouchon

We went in the late afternoon.  This place was packed.  Sat at the bar facing the water. While waiting for a table.  Excellent service and drinks.  Sat on the deck facing the harbor view.  Our server Laura was excellent in every way.  Had a burger.  It was prepared perfectly.  My wife's meal was excellent as well.  The service was perfect. When we paid with the CC.  She made an error and put the whole amount as cash.  The server came us within a few minutes and explained the error.  Laura is a gem.  Honest with integrity.  You are the best.

I love this place!! Everything tastes amazing, the staff were friendly and talkative with us. I have never had a more flavorful poke bowl. Their wasabi is on point! I have been craving it ever since I went there and can't wait to go back.

Incredibly delicious, moderately priced, sunny & cheery dining room, friendly and relaxed vibe. I'm not entirely sure if you're supposed to order at the bar or the table, and neither is the staff. The communal tables mid-room are great for eating with friends or making new ones. The small tables at the perimeter are better if you're just drinking, because they're awkwardly low compared to the benches you'd sit at. There's a patio and an upstairs too apparently.  OK, food. Basically anything here involving grilled bread, olive oil, veggies and cheese is amazing...and lucky you, that's most of the menu.  The salads are great. I like the panzanella. They'll ask you if you want burrata with it, and the answer is yes -- they need to make burrata a standard part of that dish, because between the heirloom tomatoes and the dressing, it would be way too acidic without the fattiness of the fresh soft cheese to cut it.  The grilled bread chunks and flavorful greens are heavenly.   The farmer's market pizza is HIGHLY recommended. It's made with whatever's seasonal.  The 'garlic bread' is actually a small pizza of mind-bending richness. A thick pillowy crust with a golden salty crunch surrounds a well of burrata and olive oil seeded with countless cloves of roasted-to-perfection garlic.  For some reason, this is a hard spot for restaurants to make it in. Go, go now, while you still can.
The Shop Kitchen!!! Is a hidden gem. If your a foodie I would urge you to visit this spot while in Santa Bárbara. Yummy  chilaquiles, eggs Benedict and chicken n waffles just to name a few.
Love this place! Honestly grew up eating it and it's just such a homey vibe. The taquitos and crispy tacos are the way to go. Oh and the cheese enchiladas

I wish I had looked at yelp before ordering from this place.  Today was our second time ordering. The first time it took an hour and 15 minutes for it to be delivered. We ordered a cheese burger, fried rice, wonton soup and 2 desserts. A cheesecake and a chocolate mouse cake. The fried rice was incredibly dry and the soup was so salty we didn't even eat any. The cheeseburger was okay. The dessert seemed like it had sat out for far too long.  Now today, I decided to order again because they recently dropped a flyer off at my door and I was craving soup.  The trip tip was literally grey in color and my daughter couldn't even eat it. She had ordered it with a side of mashed potatoes and it came with a baked potato.  The pu pu tray was actually a poo poo tray. Not one thing it came with was edible. Every single thing was burnt beyond what a decent restaurant would EVER send out.  The chicken strips and French fries were the exact same. So burnt that when my friend tried to strip the breading off the chicken was so dry and over cooked that it wouldn't detach.  I ordered the sizzling rice soup for myself and it wasn't bad, so we all shared that and the two cheese cakes we had also ordered and were actually okay.  This time the order took an hour to be delivered. Which I understand the time, but not when this is the quality of food being delivered.   I would seriously avoid ordering food from this place. Out of our $70 order maybe $55 was thrown in the trash. So disappointed.

Good food & service at reasonable prices. I can't think of a better interior layout in SB...plenty of room for single seaters, couples or families.   Most restaurants in SB serve up overpriced, average food with lousy service in a cramped & loud atmosphere. Save your money - come here and have a relaxing conversation.  This place has been around a long time. The owner is a nice guy which is one of the reasons it's a locals favorite.

I find it frustrating, sometimes, when restaurants get much better ratings for things that are no where near food related. Ambiance is definitely part of a good meal but you're at a restaurant to eat. Not to gaze adoringly at the fire pit.   Zaytoon's food was honestly not memorable at all. We went there for the hookah, dinner being an after thought. The only thing I remember about my meal was the dry lamb. I feel that a middle eastern/Mediterranean restaurant should be ashamed to serve DRY lamb. Not to mention a hefty price for the terrible meat. Firepit, cool. But just because this is the only restaurant of its variety in tiny Santa Barbara does not mean you should settle.   2 Stars for their apps

Very poor all around. Watery Bloody Mary's. 2* out of 10 for the buffalo wings. Simply not crispy. And a margarita flatbread pizza that was oddly missing basil - what? Goodness knows it had 12 lbs of unmelted cheese on it though. Now the servers were rushed off their feet with a bid private party. Nevertheless, low on character, low on quality.

I have heard such great things about this place and am hoping my visit was not the norm. I came in December but just noticed my review didn't post then.  Nothing is worst than Burnt pizza! Shame on us for not sending it back but my husband believes you shouldn't do that, plus after we got our pizzas there was a very long line. They also forgot to cut one of the pizzas so I had to go back and they ended up cutting it in all different uneven sizes. I threw my pizza away after the first slice since I could not get past the burnt taste.  The restroom was completely disgusting. The men's was out of order so there was only one restroom. The floors were completely soaked, toilet paper and paper towels were all over the ground. It was like the place exploded! When we were leaving I felt for the gal who was cleaning the restroom.

Good sign that I was the only gringo in the place! My marinated pork taco salad sans shell was delicious. Included chips and salsa bar very tasty.  Minus one star for not offering healthier black beans instead of retried, and for water tasting terrible.

Had lunch here with a couple of friends.  The food was good.  We had fish tacos and clam chowder in the bread bowl.  We sat outside on the patio and watched the ocean and the people going by.  The service was adequate, but nothing to write home about.  One big upside to this restaurant is that it has its own free parking lot.

I eat at this location frequently and am amazed at the wonderful staff this location has.  Always friendly and attentive to you right when you walk in.  Order the market salad with lite balsamic dressing, you can't go wrong it's the best salad for under 9 bucks.   I am hooked for life!!!!!

Very delicious and great ambiance. Love the low ceilings and the comfy yet airy feel. Had the mushroom tacos and they were just delicious. Even the corn tortillas were great. Only thing I'd comment negatively about was all the sauce that wasn't on the taco but on the wood serving block it was delivered on. Is this a style? Found it kind of annoying when it was hard to scrape into the taco. Next was the peach and black lentil salad. It was very delicious  but VERY SMALL and also having the sauce served on a wood block didn't work well. Block was also too small even for the small portion size so parts of our salad were always falling on the floor. Next we had fig, hummus and honey flatbread. Really good. Seemed kind of pricey to add that very light sprinkling of cheese for $2. But tasted great. For desert we had the cheesecake. Maybe I don't like vegan desserts, because every one--no matter how people rave about it--I find average, or 'good for vegan,' which I think means desserts should't be vegan.   Service was excellent.   Overall: Very delicious, portion too small, too pricey, some of the saucy stuff needs to not be served on that block.

I just love le cafe' Stella! Second time this month that I have been in for Lunch!!.  Started with their Pistachio crusted Brie with sourdough toast and blackberry jam!!  It's unreal!! Then their Ceviche!! Absolutely delicious, then we shared the Salad Nicoise absolutely perfect!!  I brought a friend in for the first time!! She was so impressed that she is coming in for Mother's Day!!. Thank you Markie for providing such wonderfully superb service!!  I will be back!!

The best sandwiches in Santa Barbara!... wonderful vegan options like 'Mellow Kitty ' Vegan orange chicken I like it on sourdough, with the works, including their ' dirty sauce' and add avocado However! Their phone number doesn't work on this yelp page. Here is the correct phone number if you want to place an order or have questions  Ike's phone number is: 1 (805) 837-8857
Our favorite hangout, Islands,  now has Impossible Burger! Fabulous!

Well, I have never been more disappointed in service, and that says a lot because I eat at many different restaurants, breweries, etc. I have been here before to sit at the bar and eat a quick lunch. A couple of friends and I decided we wanted to have an actual lunch here and eat at a table - worst mistake we could have made.   It started off with our server, Ashley, being somewhat rude and off in another land I should say. The entirety of our time spent at this spot she was unhelpful and texting over by their computer.  We continuously would ask for things such as a beer sample, beer pint, water, sauces, which would either come about 10 minutes later or not at all. After we received the food it only got worse as the daily soup I had ordered had to be a can from section 2 or maybe 4 at the nearest grocery store. The only food that seemed to be enjoyed was the Western burger and sweet potato fries (which isn't that difficult to mess up in the first place).   I am just extremely disappointed that the waitress did not care whatsoever about her service. There are many people who need jobs in SB so her spot could easily be filled - great service is a job so treat it like one!  I really dislike writing terrible reviews, but unfortunately this place just didn't cut it in any fashion. I only give it two stars because it's a neat looking place good for watching sports and usually the bartenders are great, just not on the particular day we came to chow down. Maybe come here for a beer and then walk a few feet for some REAL food and REAL service.

Great views. Good mud pie, but the menu choices and food are just pretty poor. My wife had the fish and chips, I had the surf and turf. It was cut up beef pieces, on mini shrimp - on fries. None of it tasted very good. Our server was great. But missed opportunity to have that location with subpar food.

This place is amazing! I've been a couple times. I love getting the veggie plate & the kebab combo. The meat is marinated so well and so tender.  The ambiance is amazing, like an enchanted garden. The service is impeccable. Highly recommend this restaurant if you're in Santa Barbara!

Decent sushi, not the most flavorful I'd had... but above average with a few exotic concoctions.  However they have a very, very extensive menu of Japanese appetizers, salads, udon, desserts.  Servers are very attentive.
Very good food, great beer and wine selection. I had the pork ribs. They were fantastic along with a very good salad. I will be back!

This restaurant must be in trouble. We have eaten here many times but tonight was really, really poor. We ordered the pork belly sliders, which we have had a number of times in the past. This time you needed a magnify glass to find the meat! On top of that the slider buns were blackened so bad that we had to use a knife to scrape off the worst part.

Wabi Sabi is a wonderful addition to the Public Market. I have gone here once a week at least since they have opened. Super fresh, melt in your mouth sushi. Great customer service as well as delicious food. My top Favorites are Seared Salmon belly, Uni, All tuna options, & Yellowtail. Favorite roll is the Please Don't cry special. Spicy and I'm obsessed with it & the Snake Roll. I've been probably 50 times and the quality & service have always been great. We also get it to go sometimes and that's been awesome as well.
Amazing pizzas and friendly staff. Love love love the Sopressata pizza and their pepperoni. The crust is incredible! Highly recommend!

Our favorite spot in Santa Barbara. Delicious food, amazingly friendly waiters, and a really nice atmosphere. We come here anytime we're celebrating, or any time we want a really good meal. We've had amazing experiences with the staff, and we've loved everything we've eaten. If you go on the Farmer's Market nights (Tuesday and Sunday..I think), it's even better!
Love the food here as others have properly glowed about. But hey, Peerless Coffee? Ugh. Such a bummer they don't serve a stronger coffee blend.

This place has the BEST PHO in SB! It has the intricate authentic flavors that many other pho places lack. I've tried all the pho restaurants in SB, Goleta and even Carpinteria and this place blows it out of the broth! ;) They also do not use MSG which is also very rare to find at other pho places. This sweet restaurant is also family owned and it feels so homey when you're here! This will definitely be my regular pho restaurant in SB!

I love love it here to be honest everything on the menu is amazing! from their sandwiches to their salads.  I usually like to order online during lunch hours just so I don't have to wait in line because it gets pretty busy during lunch. I have gotten pretty much everything on the menu that i not like to pretty much make my own salad! But let me tell you that every time i get my salad its always on point! to the things I subtitue to the things I add. The atmosphere is great the people working there have great energy! nothing but good vibes!

I DREAM ABOUT THEIR MACAROONS. I miss their desserts, but then again its good I am in SD because I would be breaking my bank and fat from eating their desserts all the time. If you are coming for their desserts or pastries, then I would be giving a five star; however, their service makes it lose a star.   Food: The desserts are DROOL worthy. They offer a variety of pastries and desserts; yet my heart will always call for the macaroons. My favorite flavors are Pistachio, Passionfruit, or Coffee. For a small dessert that can easily by finished in one bite, they cost around 1.95 and make me cry from how much I could easily spend. The almond croissant is another amazing option since its so flaky and delicious with its buttery filling and all the almonds on top.   Service: If you are eating lunch or when its super busy with a line out the door, the service can go downhill quick. The server or cashier doesn't seem very friendly at times when its busy or when its close to closing and just want to hustle everyone out. I have come here and the tables haven't been bused so there are piles of plates still on tables.   Location: The plaza it in can get very busy since its a popular place for grocery shopping and other shops. I just end up parking farther from the store.

I had a couple cocktails and appetizers at the bar with coworkers and had a good time. The prices are a bit expensive, but the quality more than makes up for it.  The drinks are well made and do the trick, I'd highly recommend trying one of the specialty cocktails.  For appetizers we had the Carrots (yum), Octopus (best I've had, they braised it so was not rubbery at all), Figs (really delicious and sweet) and Flat Bread (it was good, wouldn't get it again as it was pretty basic).  The restaurant is part of the new Hotel Californian which is absolutely incredible and a much needed luxury hotel in Santa Barbara. The grounds are gorgeous and you almost feel like you're in a different place when you are walking around the plaza.  I would highly recommend checking this place out.

In all my years of living in Santa Barbara I have never headed to the shop kitchen for brunch. This morning my boyfriend and I decided to check it out, he had been there before and he and his mom both rave about the Mac on crack. There was a bit of a line we ended up waiting to order for about 20 minutes but we were seated right away.  I ordered the chilaquiles my boyfriend ordered the Mac on crack, and we shared the sunshine salad. I also had a Horchata latte with my meal. The mac & cheese was by far the best thing that we ordered. It was very creamy very flavorful and the green apple was a really great touch that I hadn't expected to like. The salad was also really good, it comes with a poached egg that made the whole dish really come together. I really liked the potatoes though I kind of wish that they had been warm or a bit more seasoned. The Mandarins were really good, I'm not sure if they're in season but really enjoyed them in the salad.  The chilaquiles were good, I really liked the salsa Verde and the avocado was perfectly ripe. However I see what other reviewers mean when they say that it's a bit mushy. I wish that some of the tortilla chips were a bit crisper to break up all of the soft food. Also most of it was a bit cold once it arrived at the table. This dish definitely could've used some texture.   The Horchata latte was also good but a bit sweet so I didn't end up finishing it. All in all it was quite a bit of food I think we could've just done the salad and the mac & cheese and been fine. Since the chilaquiles were good but not amazing, I'm wishing that we had done that instead. I would definitely come back here but I will probably order the mac & cheese or try something new.
Good bbq. Nice location. Outside patio dining under a nice big tree. Not the best, not the worst. It was on the drier side, not to juicy.

The order of Brussels Sprouts was not good. It said it was a balsamic reduction and they just put straight balsamic over the top it seems like. They definitely didn't try it before sending it out because I can't imagine the taste they are going for to be straight vinegar. These were $12 dollars, steep as is for Brussels sprouts, no bacon or anything else. Super disappointed, but not worth sending back. Ate with ranch which made it tolerable to get down, but not good.   Also their selection is pretty limited. The seltzer is 5/10

Loooove this place! The noodles are super authentic and service is quite amazing! The servers are very attentive, always making sure that everything is alright The Long Tail Boat Noodles are absolutely amazing!!! The soup is sooo brothy and meaty tastin and pairs so well with the yummy noodles. The meat was also super juicy! The northern thailand curry noodles are also super good, loved how they put crunchy noodles in it haha all in all a place that people must check out! btw, theres usually a line since theres only bar seating but we came on a sat. afternoon and only waited about ten to fifteen min.!

I was invited to Blush by some friends who are local.  They suggested it since it was close to my hotel downtown Santa Barbara.  It was a nice restaurant but a bit on the pricey side for what you get.  We sat in a booth which was comfortable and I did appreciate that it wasn't loud and you could talk easily. The food was good but not amazing.  I had the herb roasted chicken served over truffle risotto. I also had the vodka mule which was great. The service was good and friendly staff.  I would go back but I know there are many nicer restaurants in Santa Barbara.  It's just conveniently located downtown close to many hotels and shops.
Was unable to delete the previous review I posted. Am pleased with the food, and customer service. Recycle please, container near the door.

We really enjoyed the food and experience here.  The food was eclectic, which enables us to taste numerous types of food at one sitting.   From Ramen to roasted watermelon to duck confit tacos, it was delicious.  The servers and owner were very pleasant, and we loved the artwork on the walls.  We will be back!

We arrived at 5:30 & were told it would be about 20-30 minutes.  Went for a walk & came back & we were once again told it would be at least another 30 minutes.  Waited in front & saw customers leave but no wait staff making any attempt to clear the tables.  What we did see was a procession of customers come in & get to-go food.  The front desk person needs to be honest with her time assessments because we left & 20 minutes later we got a call that our table was ready.  Really bad service.  Moral of the story, if you expect to eat at this place order to go food cuz you won't be seated any time soon

In a town with not too many pizza options I'd go running to, Lucky Penny is most likely a top contender.   Crust airy, chewy, yet crispy (does that even make sense) and interesting toppings each time, Lucky Penny is a great place to grab some easy, casual pizza in the beautiful Funk Zone. They usually rotate certain pizzas, so be sure to check the menu before committing to go!  Each pizza is unique, but most certainly not for everyone.   Downsides? As good as this pizza joint is, they have pretty limited option, the price for the size of the pizza is overpriced, and the crust can definitely come out burnt on some occasions. It's for sure a place I would offer as an option if in the area, but never go out of my way to run back to.

This was such a magical dining experience! I came to celebrate and celebrate I did! The filet mignon was like 'butter', the service by Carol was extraordinary, and the ambiance was devine! The location is also perfect!
We absolutely love this restaurant!  Friendliest neighborhood feel to the eatery and we now have our favorite spot at the bar!  The burger is a must and make sure you get the fries on the side

I've been going to Mezza for a while now and they have never failed to surprise me. I just had my birthday celebration there and I failed to go through their event coordinator. 20 people showed up (I didn't think I had that many friends ) in addition to the usual crowd and they handled us like champs. (Please go through the event coordinator of you want to have a party there, don't be a jerk like me) Luis, one of their bartenders, is fantastic at his craft - Daisy, whom I believe is the GM, will always make you feel welcome and ensure your visit is going smoothly and the rest of the staff is nothing short of fantastic. I highly recommend Mezza if you're looking for great cocktails and some of the best hummus you may ever consume. (Also, everything else on the menu is fire). Thanks Mezza Staff for killin' it! Can't wait for my next visit.

Belmond El Encanto is a beautiful hotel, with a not so exceptional indoor wait staff. I've had the pleasure of eating at their scenic patio dining area, and also inside.  For my indoor experience, it started a sunny afternoon, and on arrival there were plenty empty tables that had not been cleared off; nor did my boyfriend want to wait 20 plus minutes to order food, so we were stuck choosing one of the very small tables on the inside near the bar. We decided that since the tables were so small we'd only order a few cocktails and one plate, of very tiny portioned yellowtail crudo, which was pretty expensive -- $18?  I don't feel like we got what we paid for. I felt as though the drinks were very watered down. I had ordered a cocktail and there was a huge cube in there, which then melted not even 10 minutes later. And although the dining room's ambience was great, the lack of food, beverage and service definitely fell flat.  For my patio experience, I went for my Birthday, and we had a wonderful experience, away from the dreaded indoor dining area. I ordered the Thai Inspired Crab soup, their exceptional squid ink linguine & crab, and their Enchanted Floating Island, with the Tables Creek 'Sacrerouge' pairing which cannot be forgotten.    THE STAFF -  Although the El Encanto dining room was a bit short-staffed at the times I've dined here, I would think they could have had someone cleaning tables for new arrivals, since they accept walk-ins. The servers were knowledgeable of the menu, but the bartenders inside seemed like they were pretty clueless, which hindered our experience a bit.  Definitely recommend making a reservation here for eating on their patio dining area during a sunset, you won't regret it.

Amazing !! It's all of your favorite food trucks finds in a restaurant setting. The tacos are super tender high quality meat & produce. I feel bad about not taking pics . But it was so good we just dove in . Tacos & pizzadilla is a must have.

No no no. Ha, Julia Child probably went here only because she didn't know about Lilly's Taqueria on Chapala. This super rica place is ONLY so popular because Julia Child ate here.  If you want your ho-hum menu with busy foo foo tacos, gorditas, and quesadillas and fajitas that are all Americanized, definitely go to the over-rated tourist trap--- super rica. But don't look for things like fresh steamed lengua. WHAT, no lengua??? I feel really sorry for all these tourists gushing over this place. It's food is the same you'd get at El Torito/Baja Fresh/Sharkey's. Truly, Super Rica has more customers than it merits, only so because Julia sometimes ate here in her final years. But it's not like she ate nowhere else but here, and get real--Julia was a master chef in many cuisines, but Mexican was not one of them. Worshiping her opinion on tacos, like mindless sheepies is dumb.Gosh, going there, I felt just like I was in Santa Barbara on Milpas St.  Now, if you want simply delicious street tacos and only tacos like carnitas, beef heaven-ojo, lengua, cabeza, cachete, tripas, and just minced onions, cilantro and lime with bomb salsas, then go straight to Lilly's. A Coke or Corona completes the meal. You'll feel like you're at Avenida Revolucion, Tijuana.  And read this very fitting article by David Rosengarten on Julia and Super Rica:  https://www.forbes.com/sites/drosengarten/2013/06/27/santa-barbara-tacos-and-julia-child-how-americas-most-famous-french-chef-caused-a-mexican-food-sensation/#141e39474af1

I was recommended to stop by Blenders in the Grass by a good friend who's from Santa Barbara. She really hyped it up and said it's the best smoothies place so I went in with expectations. It did not disappoint at all. We split a 24 oz strawberry colada and it was not only really cheap at less than $6 but also a HUGE portion. We could not finish between 2 people. Our drink had a great blend of strawberry, pineapple, and bits of coconut mixed perfectly. I should have had more forethought and tried two drinks of the 12 oz size instead just to get a chance to taste something else. Will have to return on my next visit to Santa Barbara!

Barf.   Is Chef Cora even involved anymore?   I certainly hope not if she's ok with bread that falls apart when you grab your burger, pink tomatoes and fries so salty you have to chug a liter of water for your mouth to feel like you didn't just jump into the Dead Sea. There aren't many places you can get a vegetarian burger in SB, and the past few times keeps getting worse.

Definitely touristy but that's expected with the location of this place. Goat tree is beautiful and I've found it to be a good spot to meet for coffee & a snack. Haven't had the food but I enjoy coming in the afternoon between lunch and dinner crowds. Sit on the patio!

Great food. Great drinks. Great service. Great location. Parking is restricted and there is usually a wait to be seated, so go early. Also, I have to point out, the bathroom signage favors one gender over the other. In today's society I think such things are not acceptable. I suggest management provide equal bathroom access for both (all?) genders.

I feel like the food has not been the same since covid. Not sure if there is a new cook or what, but noticeably less meat in meals and the food seems more watery and less flavorful. Hopefully everything is ok with the family that usually runs this place. I would not recommend this restaurant at the moment for dining. Donations, maybe.

Beautiful location & atmosphere. It just feels like a special occasion kind of place (I had a 21st birthday here a million years ago). Stopped by for lunch last weekend & enjoyed it--just a beautiful SB spot to enjoy on a nice day. Split an ahi salad & burger between two, & it was as enjoyable. The ahi was served rare and was a tad bit sinewy. The burger was delicious.

I went to Arnoldi's again for a third time and realized this was my first time reviewing the place. How could that be?  This is a classic 'checkered tablecloth' Italian restaurant in Santa Barbara. Tasty pastas, decent calamari, and good red wine. Nothing was too expensive and the al fresco atmosphere was great as usual. My only disappointment on my latest stop here was that the bacci ball court was out of commission due to recent rains... guess I'll have to go back again soon!

LOVE this deli! While I'm still upset Whitefoot closed, this is my new go-to place for lunch. They have a great eggplant sandwich I have to order every time I am there. My husband gets the buffalo chicken sandwich and absolutely loves it. We fight over which sandwich is better. These bad boys will fill you up too.

If I wasn't mainly a food guy, I would have given it a zero.  To dispense with...food was better than average, and my french toast was tasty.  Wait - you'll be doing this if you go in on a weekend morning.  Denizens - pharmacy crowd, but seem pleasant and happy to be there.  Service - what is with these people? The service was just rotten each of the four times we tried it. Four times and each time was like they wanted us out of there. Not sure what the deal is, but  we won't go back there to find out.

the first time I ate here, I was surprised at how good it was.  I've been back several times now and it has become by favorite tacos place in SB.  Really fresh and tasty.  Love the battered cod, ahi poke, spicy crispy shrimp.  Haven't even gotten to the traditional tacos yet.  Love it.
I grew up on Rusty's and order it whenever I'm home. Is it authentic? No. Is it delicious? Yes.

We had lunch here the other day and I can't believe that we have missed this place so many times before! I absolutely love Benchmark and so did my friends who joined me for lunch. We walked up to the counter to order and were very impressed by the menu. The selection of specialty drinks, beers, and wines offered was also amazing! It would be safe to say that there is something for everyone at Benchmark. There are tons of appetizers including crispy Brussel sprouts, sliders, and coconut shrimp, not to mention lots of others!  There are homemade soups, salads galore and amazing burgers. We enjoyed the State Cobb, the BBQ Chicken, and the Sesame Salmon salads. But it doesn't stop there. There are many sandwiches, entrees, and flatbreads plus tacos and burritos too. We can't wait to go back! Also, the service is so friendly, professional, and efficient. Needles to say, we love Benchmark!
This is authentic Mexican restaurant. Hand made tortillas being made. Prices very reasonable. Chili relleno, corn tamales and fresh salsas and pico de gallo! Plenty of seating but very popular place.

Our women's group meets here once a month. You can't beat the view, and the food is reasonably priced and delicious. We really appreciate the staff because they're willing to give individual checks to each of us. We don't have to scramble to figure out who owes what at the end of the meal. Ample parking, a wide selection of good food, and beautiful scenery. What more could anyone want?

What a great find! Stumbled across this place on Yelp when another place that looked good had a phone number that went straight to voicemail when I called for reservations. This place got similar stars and we were able to get 8:30 reservations at 7pm for tonight.  It's hard to remember all the great things we tried and loved thanks to Kelly's expert recommendations. He recommended so many wonderful dishes we couldn't keep track. Service was great as was the ambience. Will be back for sure.

This a great place to eat something that's staring at you from a tank! If you're and uni fan this is the place to get it. They have a great dish that is still moving when it comes out. Don't expect this place to be fancy but for what it lacks in looks it makes up in taste!

Service was good and food was delicious.  The reason for the 4 star rating was my wife asked for sour cream on side because she doesnt like it and I do.  After waiting for a good amount of time our meals arrived (seafood enchiladas).  Sour cream was on both plates when it should have only been one.  The server did ask us if she wanted it remade but we said no since it took so long to get our food in the first place.  Another thing was the beans and rice...the serving size was so small it literally took is only 4 forks full and it was gone.  Never been to a mexican restaurant where they skimped on beans and rice.  The atmosphere was awesome and our server was good.  If my wife and I are ever back in town we would definitely come back.

Incredibly fresh tasty food.  Certainly one of the best restaurants in Santa Barbara.  The servers are attentive and knowledgeable.  The restaurant is beautifully designed.  I loved everything about it

We loved visiting the Public Market and this restaurant is by far one of its best offerings. We came by for a quick late lunch and left more than satisfied.  Items we tried: Ahi Nigiri Hamachi Sashimi Crab Handroll Oysters  The fish was so fresh and delicately prepared. The service was friendly and impeccable. If I had a little more room in my stomach I would have tried one of the Poke bowls everyone is raving about.   Big Eye gets a BIG thumbs up in my book!

I have to change my rating unfortunately. There's always an issue at this location lately. We ordered a mobile order the other day, and it took over 20 minutes. This time they're down 1 espresso machine. But of course! If it's not the espresso machine, the oven is broken, they're out of all teas, they're out of espresso all together... The list goes on and on with the mesa location. I miss the staff from last year!!

Wow! Having recently undergone a major remodel, The Creekside is my new favorite place. The atmosphere is family-freindly, the service is impeccable, and the free valet parking makes it easy to visit. The food was delicious, and our server was on the ball. The patio is wonderful, and the bar area very well done...note to bar top made of an old bowling alley lane! I'm in Goleta almost every month, and intend to return here whenever I'm in town.

clean and modern. I'd come back to Santa Barbara for these tacos!!! great fusion food. Great idea. We tried the paneer cheese and lamb shoulder tacos, both of which were fantastic. Great Service, I just love it!!!!!

Opal was incredible. What a class act. My date and I arrived and we were immediately seated in the cozy heated outdoor dining area, given a basket of warm herbed bread and butter, and a watermelon margarita and a Corona beer to start off the night.   First off, the calamari was incredible; it was the most tender calamari I've ever eaten (perhaps slow-cooked?), glazed with a delicious sweet and tangy sauce. It almost reminded me of chicken, instead of the usual chewiness of crispy calamari. I loved it.   The salmon was also fantastic. Opal really thinks outside the box, creating concoctions and fusions I've never had the pleasure of encountering the likes of. The salmon lay atop a bed of orange zest basmati rice, surrounded by a moat of Thai curry and topped with thin strips of veggies. I can confidently say that fish itself was the best I've had the pleasure of eating thus far in life; it was beyond soft inside, with a delicate crackly crispy outside, and marinated once again in the lovely lemongrass flavors. It was amazing; definitely definitely coming back just to order the same thing (although other menu items looked promising as well; my date's wild mushroom pizza was super tasty). Thanks to Opal and to Paul, our awesome waiter, for a great night!!

We checked into a Best Western adjacent to this little restaurant. We (8 of us, 3 kids and 5 adults) came here for lunch. We all really enjoyed our meals the salads that were ordered were very fresh with a variety of veggies. The calamari appetizer was good. My BLT Sriracha Sandwich and Jalapeño Soup of the day was just enough for lunch. The Jalapeño soup is not spicy at all. All the sandwiches ordered and soups and salads were really good. They have good bloody marys too. Overall, we all enjoyed a good lunch meal and the service was good. The restaurant is observing covid-19 guidelines and they're wearing masks and enforcing social distancing too. We were all very pleased. We checked out their breakfast menu and it looks like they have good options. We may return for breakfast another day before leaving town.

Breakfast review - Opens at 7 but plan to wait til 7:15 for service.  Once they arrive, coffee is very good, food portions are very large and fruit is extremely fresh.  Buffet to please any discriminating guest and then the view and relaxing atmosphere make this a great start of the day.  Very good food quality and service.

My husband heard about this place on the Food Network for their famous Cinnamon Roll and I've had it bookmarked for quite a while, but every time we were in the area and wanted to go, they were closed. Please note, they are open from 9:00 am - 2:00 pm every day. It's also a little tricky to find. The restaurant is set back from State St. inside Victoria Courtyard. The best place to park is in the parking lot which is free for 75 minutes. You can dine inside or outside. The inside is very welcoming and super cute with succulents on each table and expensive horse paintings on the walls.   Obviously, we ordered the Yukon Gold Cinnamon Roll ($8). It was good, but we were expecting something gooey and hot out of the oven. We also were hoping it would taste better than Cinnabon. It wasn't better than Cinnabon, but it was good. I'd order it again, it's not too big but good enough for my husband and I and we also shared with our kids.   Aside from the Cinnamon Roll, we also ordered the Blueberry Pancakes ($8) and a Bloody Mary ($14). The Blueberry Pancakes were yummy, the Bloody Mary was okay, the best part of it was the pickled asparagus. Yummy!   I'd definitely come back to try the Dirty Fries, the Lemon Ricotta Pancakes, the Brioche French Toast and the Banana Nutella Croissant, sounds good!

Thank you for bringing somewhere different to the SB scene. Best curries since we left UK and the best in town by a mile. Service is superb - friendly and approachable whilst remaining ruthlessly efficient & attentive. The barman was stolen from Sama Sama and makes a real difference - knowledgable and welcoming. We've been a few times and will keep coming.
This has a beautiful space. Unique and different. Almost all seating is outside.  The food is fabulous!

Been here so many times and we love dining in as much as we love ordering at the window to go. They have picnic benches right nearby so it's easy to just grab the food and eat real quick. Either way, we've always been able to bring our dog and they can enjoy the nice ocean view with us. The seafood here is always delicious, we love getting the rock crab special. The clam chowder and lobster bisque was very good!
Always a good time here. Lots of energy, good staff and great food. Always looking forward to dining here.

I saw the eight 5-star reviews and thought I'd try it, even though the reviews sounded suspiciously identical.  I love Korean food.  I go all the way to Sura in Oxnard to get my fix.  This place is just offering their previous AYCE lunch for dinner now, at a cost of $27.  They no longer offer bibimbop or their other a la carte Korean dishes.  The meats are good but it's just meat cooked on the grill.  The only spices included was salt, black pepper, and tempura sauce for the pot stickers.  Korean food is all about the spices.  None of those spices were present, and they were also lacking when it was called iGrill.  Rice was not included either, tho I didn't specifically ask for it.  Maybe I need to ask for the sauces to make my Korean dinner taste Korean?  I think the owners should go to Sura in Oxnard and see what a Korean Menu should look like.  I would love to have a real Korean restaurant here.  This isn't it.

Always good good.  The seafood soup was extremely tasty and the staff is very accommodating for people with food allergies.  I mostly have take out but occasionally eat in the restaurant.  You can count on the food always being good with no surprises.

Hot waitresses and dank food. Seating a little crowded and sometimes wait for table is long, but once your in everything is great. Pretty fast, and they have really good appetizers, salads, and drinks while you wait. highly recommended

If you appreciate delicious, healthy, locally sourced, gluten-free and superfoods, this is soon to be your favorite coffee shop and breakfast or lunch spot! The menu is hiding so many gems, just go and try it today!!

This review is coming off of a phenomenal New Year's Eve date night dinner. They had a special menu for the holiday, which differed from the everyday dinner menu. We had a salmon starter, which was delicious and so unique. I had the New York Strip and my partner had a Mahi Mahi Tuna plate, both of which were enjoyed heartily. The apple strudel dessert was perfect to finish off the meal.  We've been here for brunch as well and it didn't disappoint. Graham has been our server both times and has been so kind and attentative. The food, the service, the cute atmosphere, there's nothing not to like here! Definitely would recommend.

This place has the best clam chowder I have ever had, so far. It's thick and creamy, but just the right amount. There's  great clam flavor, nice chunks of clams and potatoes. Just delicious. We also tried the fish tacos and shrimp taco, also delicious. It's a casual place with a nice patio, reasonable prices considering the high quality.

Came here twice for happy hour, and it was always packed in pre-COVID times! The store looks like a page from an interior design magazine. So nautical and cute. Both the indoor and outdoor dining areas look cozy. Would totally recommend it as a date spot.  - Oysters - You can ask the server for more info on the different oysters that are available that day. They're fresh and have changing varieties. I honestly forgot what my oysgters were but we got three different types. One of them was super small so if you're not too picky with oyster tasting, you should ask for more info on sizing and such.  - Charbroiled oysters - The charbroiled topping was BOMB. I don't often eat American-style oysters so my first time having them like this was here. The garlick-y breadcrumbs are so savory but they definitely mask the oyster flavor; the oyster becomes a squishy vehicle for something that tastes like garlic bread. And who doesn't love garlic bread?  - Crab cakes -  Delicious! They were crispy, not soggy, and I liked the corn bed that they came on.  - Calamari -  It's hard to mess this up. The calamari here was light and flaky, just how I like it. Goes great with dip.  - Mixed Berry Mojito -  Whew! This cocktail is tasty but it packs a punch. I don't know if it was because we had empty stomachs but we were buzzin' after this.  - Lure Bloody Mary -  This was my first ever Bloody Mary! I felt like if I liked micheladas, then I should like Bloody Marys, but now I'm not sure if savory drinks are my thing. This drink came with a celery stick, a shrimp, a cucumber and olive garnish, and citrus wedges. Based on other Yelpers' photos, there seems to be a variance in toppings.  As Asian college students, my friends and I definitely looked out of place in the times we went LOL. The service was nice. I haven't had the chance to come here for a regular, non-happy hour meal so I would like to come back to try their pastas or other seafood fare.

I had high expectations for this place because it's a Four Seasons, and typically you can't get better than that for customer service.  Boy was I wrong. Service was slow.  I think they miscalculated staffing.  We arrived at 645pm and didn't leave until 825pm.  The place is beautiful and has an amazing view of the ocean and that's why I chose it as the place for dinner for my partner's 50th birthday dinner.  We had reservations via Opentable and I even called that afternoon to to re-confirm the reservation and a request for 'birthday candle' in his dessert.   We had finished our dessert and there was no birthday greeting or candle, so I pretended to go to the bathroom and told the hostess.  She assured me it would happen, that 'sometimes it's brought out after dessert'....... Huh?  Well, they brought the check, I paid, and still no birthday greeting.  We left.  For $211 for a dinner for two, I expect more than that and they dropped the ball big time. Don't think I'll ever go back. The food was good, but I don't even want to talk about it because the attention to detail - and my special occasion - was not there.

Food was remarkably fresh and had delicious flavors.  The Farm Grain Bowl (sauce mixed in or on the side) is a great vegetarian lunch - will be my go to for a while.  My friend had the ham sandwich and said it was really good as well.  Hours are a little random, would be a great lunch place on State.

This place is the best.  Been coming here for a few years just for their Almond Croissants.  Recently my husband and I have been going here for a weekly weekend brunch with our dog and we love it.  Their breakfast is great.  We usually have the ham and cheese croissant or the breakfast sandwich.  Both are so good! Add the breakfast potatoes and a few chocolate and salted caramel macarons to go and we are always satisfied eating there.

Though a little pricey, this is a great store when I need staples.  The produce and the vitamin sections, where I usually spend most of my money, are small.   For most of these things I go to Lazy Acres; their sister store.  The staff are always freindly.

Great ambiance!  A belly dance comes and dances around the table.  The tables also has a firepit in the middle, which is nice - but a little strange since you cant cook anything on it and reduces the amount of space for plates and glasses.  Also, our firepit was super high and heated up our wine that was on the table.  It was also a little hard to do a toast.    Food:  The food overall was decent, but nothing really stood out except for the grape leaves.  I have had better Middle Eastern food at divey spots, but I think you pay for the ambiance.    Drinks: You can bring your own bottle (two bottle max) which is an A+.  its way better to bring in a couple good bottles of wine!  Service; Really good an attentive.  A+

I went out to dinner here with some friends on 5/6/21. It was the most awkward and uncomfortable service I have ever received.   We wanted to hold on to a menu because we were unsure with what we wanted and wanted to start with some appetizers before ordering a fee entrees. We didn't want to over-order any food.   The waitress refused, scolded us, and had a really weird excuse and was complaining about how busy it was starting to get with other tables. She basically forced us to order everything all at once. It was so uncomfortable and awkward. They had no tap water only bottled water and didn't give us any cups. The waitress rushed us the entire time.   I work in customer service and it is tough! I totally understand that and empathize... however, I don't think its fair to customers to make them feel uncomfortable by complaining about something in your job that just is part of it and out of your customers control.   My friend wanted to order a small bowl of soup during the meal and felt uncomfortable doing so. Our other friend continued to order but the waitress came off as very hostile and like she didn't want us there. That is a shame. We are younger so it may seem that we do not tip very well when we do. We gave them a very small tip because we couldn't justify paying more when the waitress was extremely unwelcoming and at times even hostile. You would think that during such a hard time for many restaurants in Santa Barbara, they would be more welcoming and encouraging us to order more because its better for business.   The food made myself and my friend sick for 2 days.   We will never be coming back. Muen Fan (on the Mesa),  Zen Yai (downtown), & Your Choice (Upper State) are much better options.

So in love with this place and the one in Montecito. The BEST breakfast/brunch food ever (lemon ricotta pancakes!) and the BEST salads ever (Asian chicken and a fabulous kale salad with pistachios, apples and pomegranate seeds are my faves. Bakery has scones to die for, among other offerings, and I usually but some of their granola to take home. I am a regular and will remain a devoted fan.

Pickles and Swiss is my favorite sandwich place in Santa Barbara! The service in the store is excellent and their manager Owen is always the nicest! I highly recommend the Prosciutto and Brie sandwich. They are always fast and beyond friendly.   What else can you ask for? Great owner. Great manager. Great food.

The food here is sooo good! I have come here twice now and have been able to try an array of dishes (yay, friends!) and they have all been so delicious. My favorite is the Tikka Masala and the curry fries are a must! Naan is always tasty, too. Service has been exceptional and I look forward to returning and recommending Apna to friends.

There are some decent bakeries in Santa Barbara, and what is especially good at Andersen's Bakery are the pastries with custard which are seldom found here, seems as if all the pastry goods are excellent and the old-fashioned service is refreshing.   Charlotte and her Mom have put in decades of hard work to provide consistently good fare at SB reasonable prices.     Am comfortable there with the menu items and that there's a environment which is free of trendy looks.   Long may Andersen's Bakery continue well.    Sometimes in the afternoon there are special rates/ discounts for some items.  That's always a plus.      Parking is close by, there's pleasant outdoor dining as well as inside.     Kudos to the staff.

We still come here for breakfast, maybe once every few weeks (we used to come more when we lived in SB), usually in huge groups, and even after years we are still treated the same- this place is amazing :)  Our same amazing waitress works here and always remembers us, and our order, drinks are still so good, food is awesome, this is a great place to enjoy a lively morning.  So thankful this place is still around.  One of the few gems on State Street that has outlived most other places.

I'm not into bakeries as most just throw together flour water and butter and shape into different forms calling themselves bakeries.  Well one day I was in a juice shop in town and ordered Avo toast which came on a bread that came from this bakery and ever since I'm a converted bakeriette if such a thing exists.  In the old days you could walk in this place almost empty and smell all of the wonderful breads stacked up next to each other to throw your nostril at. But now that's all changed, you know  like the toilet paper and soap thing in 2020 -- so even if I try my best to show up at 9 am I'm looking at lines of people eating to get some of their goodies. Like a 20-30 minute wait on a Saturday morning.   So all this writing talk but what the deal with why this bread is so good.  First off it's made freshly every day and they are not afraid to use a lot of of whole grains and gluten free flours so that anyone can find something interesting.  Most are vegan too except for a few that have cheese.   My favorites here are the superseed. That bread lasts for weeks as it's so dense. Great triple  toasted  with nut butter on top GF. They are gluten free they also make a superseed cookie that is pretty phenomenal too.  I also like their sage garlic in either gluten free or regular, the shiitake shallot.  I'm more on the savory spectrum but if you like more sweeter option they have those too. Heck they even have a charcoal bread for those needing a bit of a detox;)  Prices are crazy high here I see people throwing out $100 bills without a hitch.  I suck it up and throw out the CC and then they will ask you what kind of tip if any you would like to give to the bakers of course not the servers.  Bravo to the bakers.    Don't mess around folks. Get here early, be patient,  bring the wallet and when you are at home biting into your sliced triple toasted slice of bread you will realize that the smile it gave you was worth every dollar. Thanks
amazing superb excellent meal. love the nom tok burr. go her if you want the best tasting food! great service and cool atmosphere. no place like it in sb. hamds down my favorite! love you blu owl!
You must come to the Brat Haus and yet the Rubens sandwich.  The lean pastrami and homemade sauerkraut is a delish.  I would show you a picture but it was so good i ate the whole thing.

this place is hilarious, its all seinfeld themed and even the mean ol Korean lady that works the counter is strinkingly similar to the soup Nazi- coincidence or not? You must be prepared on what you want to order or shell be on you. My good friend used to study the menu online cuz the lady scared him so much. haha,  the sandwiches arent that great but id go for the pure entertainment of seinfeld being played non-stop on the tv.   when I went i did get hassled by the lady but she ended up forcing me to choose the jalapeno pastrami which was exactly what i wanted.... 'you like spicy, you like spicy? you get the jalapeno pastrami!'  well alrgiht then

Chocolate pistachio baklava. Do I really need to say anything else?  Ok, I will anyway. I went in after running an errand in SB and saw more varieties of Turkish delight than I knew existed. I tried a tiramisu flavor and a one with chocolate, banana and nuts. Both good, I got a piece of the later to go along with a strawberry chocolate and a couple of pieces or the aforementioned chocolate pistachio baklava. I waited until I got home to try that.  Amazing. That alone is worth the trip. Go here, eat that and try the cornucopia of Turkish delight.  Very friendly service as well. Can't wait to take more time and try the coffee as well.

This is a lovely way to spend an afternoon or evening. We've eaten here several times and enjoyed it each time. Overall the menu is very solid - California Bistro is a good name for it. The setting is lovely, I recommend the outdoor seating which is along a wraparound porch that's nicely sheltered from the street by trees.  The food is good, we've had salads and main courses and each time they've been very good. It's not a 'foodie' place, more the type where you go for an anniversary or with family. My only downside was the dessert - I had creme brulee ...which was so 'bruleed' it was seriously burnt, my husband had the lemon meringue pie, which wasn't that great - just ok. Otherwise, it's a solid 8 out of 10.

Order carefully when you want to have the sundae!  Their charburger is always great with melt-y  cheese and grilled onion. I have never gotten disappointed by it. Besides, the sundae is sometimes yummy! Why sometimes? Because some staff here don't know what a  sundae is. If you order vanilla sundae, you will expect the vanilla ice cream with chocolate sauce, nuts and whipped cream, right? However, when I ordered the vanilla sundae last time, the staff asked me what I want  for my sundae. First, I didn't understand what she was saying. Then, she asked me, 'Do you want chocolate?' -- 'Of course, yes.' 'Do you want nuts?' -- 'Of course, yes.' 'Do you want whipped cream?' -- 'Of course, yes.' If I didn't want these things, I would ask about that when I ordered. Anyway, I eventually got what I wanted and it was super yummy.   Today, I ordered the vanilla sundae again. This time, although the cashier didn't ask me anything about sundae, she asked me if I needed chocolate sauce when she almost finished making my sundae. As you can guess, she added a little chocolate sauce on the top of the whipped cream. Therefore, I had to eat only vanilla ice cream after I finished whipped cream. This is not sundae! This is just vanilla ice cream!!
Lilly's is legendary. Highest quality at the best prices.  Widest selection in town.  And consistently great year after year.  Don't miss the salsa

Loved this trstaurant when we discovered it in Ventura, then were elayed when it came to S.B. Their charbroiled oysters were LEGEND! But our past 3 visits have been so disappointing! The service was galacially slow, and the oysters just not the same.. small and dry .  Have tried back 3 times, but are no longer counting it as a favorite. Very slow service on a very slow nite, as well.

We were able to walk up and get a table on a Saturday night.  Foods not that great.  Service was a little slow, but friendly.  Limited parking.  If you ride share go off the main street for the pick up.
First and foremost our server was amazing. She was so friendly and on top of everything. The food was delish. We both got the deal of that day-lobster. Fresh, tasty and friendly. Check them out!

Today's lunch sealed the deal, this is 5 star food.  The broccoli stem salad, the caprese, the soup, the bread, cookies, service....everything was so fresh and so good.  If only I lived closer.  A must-do!

The ambiance is great as it is always lively. The food left a lot to be desired for me.   The muffins they serve you are frictions, my favorite is the Rosemary variety of it. I ordered the gumbo ya ya and it was ok. For my entree I had the Louisiana soft shell crabs and it was lackluster.
