I was happy to discover that several of the dishes here have vegan options.  We had the Banh Mi sandwich made vegan with tofu, and it was excellent.  Great combination of flavors and textures.  Also had the lotus root summer rolls, also terrific.  Everything was very fresh.  The service was friendly and attentive.  Loved the pretty decor too.  Finished things off with popsicles (vietnamese coffee and lychee lemongrass) from the adjacent ice cream place.  Highly recommend a visit here, we'll be back soon!

My business partner and I love Mr B's! We have a space in the mall and we love getting lunch here! They even deliver our food sometimes when they are slow. Great people, Great food and Great location!

Tacos and batting cages....what more do you need?   This stopover on a busy street was an-ok joint to get your standard fare of tacos. From surf & turf but nothing exotic it does the job as the tacos are good sized and filled with plenty of toppings but fell short on flavor.   The fish taco was just as good at a Rubios and the Carne Asada was cooked well but didn't not have that char that taco trucks are known for.  Overall, if you are hitting some fast balls and need a snack its not bad.
Best Indian in town hands down and very lovely owners. Highly recommend the coconut curry dish and the lamb saag!

Hands down the best restaurant in the 805 in terms of flavor, ambiance, fun, family friendly, indoor and outdoor seating, bar and more.  I recently learned about Chef Damon Murphy taking over and I couldn't be more pleased.    I stop at Cielito every time I'm in SB.  If I'm there for 2 days I'm there at least twice, 3 days maybe 4 times.  It's a little bit of everything, they have a raw bar, a classy bar (literally the bar dates back quite some time), and the food is progressive and most importantly for my body gluten free (naturally).  GF might sound like a fad but really it means less processed and less fillers and you are eating the good stuff.  Everything that I've consumed here has been above my expectation.    IF you don't believe me check out their happy hour.  Bites and drinks, affordable in a casual but somewhat upscale and welcoming atmosphere.  They make a mean margarita and offer a solid wine list and I've yet to have an average experience.  I can't  wait to go back!
We love this place, their steak taco is to die for. The owner is always greeting us at the register with a smile that goes the extra mile!

review is for red devil pancakes...smaller than regular stack...red...kinda doughy almost not cooked enough...came with same icing as the cupcakes banana and strawberries...(fruit is only part i liked) i loved the nonfat mayan mocha light chocolate...cupcakes looked great have to try them...i should have ordered a cupcake and not the pancakes...
Really great taqueria right in the Funk Zone next to train station, Shrimp tacos were amazing, so was their salsas and guacamole!

First impression of the place: cool little picnic style hometown feeling. I think some parts of the business could use updating and modernizing.   Hungry and always passing this place I stopped in to get a slice of pizza. $5 for a slice of pizza, I thought it was expensive until I got my slice. HUGE. so satisfying and a great portion size.   My review on the pizza itself, it was a tad bit greasy and almost half of my cheese fell off. But yummy nonetheless. Will I return? Definitely.

The last time we went there, we had a party of 5. I guess they automatically add 18% gratuity with a large party, but since we all had the buffet, adding that much gratuity was entirely inappropriate. I won't go there again.  貪心
Jorge was awesome and accommodating! He made amazing drinks and we couldn't be more happy with the experience. Highly recommend!

I love Indian food and feel great about giving this place five stars just after getting takeout.  Unfortunately, the takeout menu is super short (butter chicken and bainghan bharta were the curries), but the flavor of the curries was awesome and I liked how the food just came spicy.  Sometimes butter chicken is too sweet for me, but this was perfectly savory.  The eggplant was fantastic, too.  The rice and naan were really good.  I cannot wait to go back for a proper dine-in dinner to try more of the menu.

When I'm in SB I try and hit this spot at least once for a sandwich. One of the best sandwich spots in town hands down. Everything I've had was bomb. Highlights for me are the gyro and the metro firehouse. Gyros are only offered on thurs-sat so don't be disappointed if you go on Sunday-wed and they don't have them. My ultimate fav tho is the metro firehouse. I always get the extra spicy version which comes with bacon also. Bomb!

Stopped by on Friday for some sushi and had a great time. Volcano roll was amazing! I could eat it everyday and will be back for this haha. Ask for Chef recommended sushi, they give u the most fresh fish they got.

Great food at great prices! Super casual so don't expect a restaurant with Mexican maps or photos on the wall. Most customers are takeout.   Only negative is the wait. You can stand in line for almost an hour on the sidewalk at lunch time.   Food is absolutely outstanding so plan early or late to avoid the huge lines.

The food is delicious and the location is so perfect you couldn't ask for better. The one thing I've noticed on the 3 or 4 time I've gone is the hostesses always overtime your wait time almost to try discouraging you to stay. On Mother's Day I went here, and def expected to wait a long time. Was quoted over an hour way and thought ok no problem. Looked around and noticed maybe one other party waiting but the place was packed but still counted maybe 5 empty tables. Figured it was for reservations so that's fine. Went and ordered drinks at the bar and literally got our drinks and then got buzzed to be sat. How can they justify telling me id wait well over an hour and seat me 10 minutes later. Owners please take note! This isn't good business when the first people they see tell them this. BUT- I continue to return because this place is wonderful. Mojitos are a must, I always get at least two ha! Everything from the ahi tuna to the seafood pasta and the banana fosters desert. Everything taste fantastic.

If you want a sand which, hot or cold, then Metropulos fine foods is the place to go to. Right near the funk zone awesome little place with a lot of other fancy selections to choose from. Great customer service, great awesome crew that is always busting there ass!
Love their salad bar! Warm bread and blue cheese dressing! But I'm not a fan of their steaks! Even steaks is the whole point of coming here..:)
These folks are doing a great job keeping us fed with great food during this trying time!

Vegan restaurant in Montecito. Parking in the street. Nice atmosphere, friendly service. I ordered the 'crab' cakes. They were pretty good, small around the size of a scallop. I loved the spicy aioli sauce, that was the best part for me!  Pros Clean food Extra flavor  Interesting menu Cons Small operations Charge for plain soda water ($4) Pricey Only a few gluten free options

The service here is terrible.  I've been here four times since they've re-opened and every time I think it's going to be better, but I'm wrong.   Each time I've been there in the afternoon for high tea.  The presentation of the food is beautiful and it tastes good too.  The portions are very small for the price too. I am comparing it with places in San Francisco to be honest.  There are many choices in SF and they are expensive but the service and portions are very good.   It's almost like the wait staff doesn't want to serve us.  The tea selection is limited as well.  I don't understand why they offer high tea if they can't do it right.   I always say I won't be back, I end up there after a year or so though.   I would not recommend this place at all.

Best happy hour in Santa Barbara.   Local's tip... always ask for a seat at the bar/happy hour tables so you can enjoy a beverage while you wait for a table.  The wait is never as long as they say it will be.

This is a tough one. The pros and cons are pretty neatly stacked against each other.   Pro: Giant goblets of alcohol for relatively little dough. Con: Takes forever to get one if the place is busy and you're not blonde and busty.  Pro: Club-like atmosphere is isolated to the dance floor and the patio out front allows a nice retreat from all that crap with big screen tv's playing funny or nostalgic music vids. Con: Undergrads (sorry).  Pro: Karaoke on Wednesdays features a nice mix of earnest amateurs and impressive performers. Con: Officially you have to order a drink to sing (even if you have brought other people with you who are drinking and you happen to be on antibiotics and don't want to pay $3 for a coke).  PS: I hear this place is also often a sports bar, if you're into that kind of thing. And they offer 2-for-1 steaks that are pretty decent.

This review is specifically about the salt water 'adults only' pool at the Bacara. Incredibly serene and completely relaxing and enjoyable.  The staff there is five star fantastic-- friendly and accomodating.  The low volume piped in ambient music is perfect for me and just adds a little extra goodness to the proceedings.  A mix of well written modern songs and Frank Sinatra, Louis Armstrong (Louis Armstrong?!!-- I didn't know anybody else still listened to his music besides me) The Bacara charges a $50 resort fee above their room rate and this is one of the reasons. On a warm sunny day this is the place to be. But get there early to stake out a good spot.  At least during my recent Covid era visits, there weren't a lot of lounging chairs to be had.    The Bacara overall is a terrific property. Excellent rooms and the beachside property is well planned, well built, well maintained, well landscaped and overall really idyllic.  Yeah, it's expensive but, for me, there is no air fare involved in getting this luxury vacation experience and so I can spend a little more on just being there.
Trio app is a really great Scooby snack, healthy, and the garlic humus is kray kray good.  Reminder, check-in first to get a free app.

So I'm giving 1 star based on service not the food. The food is amazing but service lacks. It's very disappointing. I have been here multiple times in the last few years but every time, I have a rude or non existent server. I only come back for the great food but with new restaurants opening in the funk zone I may have to take my business elsewhere from now on. Last night my husband and I went with a groupon and It was my birthday. I wanted some great food. The hostesses were lovely and we were seated promptly. However, it took 10 minutes for our server to finally greet us. I understand it was busy but I saw her check on the table next to us after we were seated, (I didn't know she'd be our server at the time) but she failed to acknowledge us. Once she did, she seemed annoyed and very rushing. Pretty much through the whole meal it was as if we were being rushed. She seemed very complacent and it made the experience awful. I tried to enjoy the evening but the service killed it. Once we finished dessert she brought the bill and before I said anything, she says 'groupon?' With an annoyed tone. Sorry did I offend you? After she checked us out, I barley had my receipt to sign for gratuity, for a minute, she comes back like 'so? Can I take this now?' Attitude again. I was beyond over it at this point and wanted to get out of there. I did still tip her 18% even though she was extremely rude and didn't even acknowledge my birthday. I'm also in the service industry so I would hope my generous tip would smarten her up. Her name was Arwen and the reason I'm giving her name and this review is so hopefully management takes care of educating their team on great service. We have a ton of friends and family coming out for an event and I'd like to come back but that won't be happening.

I went to this place on the suggestion of some of the nurses I was working with. Since I had eaten habit 2 days in a row I thought that it would be okay to switch it up. The line was really long but it moved fast. I ordered the rbc panini roastbeef, sundried tomato may, carmalized onion and cambozola cheese it was delicious!

Time has not been kind or maybe it was just our experience b/c others around us seemed to be having a good time  Mr & I brought Mom here for her 80th birthday & I thought this would be the perfect treat but we started off getting seated on the patio, in the corner, where I had to literally climb over the stroller of the table beside us.....it was freezing out there & Mom had no jacket.  I asked to be moved to a table inside but the answer was no.  Umm, okay.....  The waitress kept calling Mom 'honey' which was very condescending and then she kept trying to talk us out of our order saying it was too much food.  Well, if it's too much, we'll get it to go & you still get more money.  Seems like a win-win to me. Stop telling us how to order - especially when you advised the table beside to order even more dishes than we ordered.....that made zero sense!  We shared the Brussels sprouts & they were heavenly but then the small hiccups above turned to downright falling on their faces.  Mr & I both ordered the mushroom pasta. We wanted our own b/c I was sick & wanted to keep my germs to myself if there were any leftovers.  They brought out the pasta & then we sat for 20 min while we waited on Mom's chicken.  It was only after someone walked by & asked how was everything & we said we didn't know b/c we were waiting on our Mom's dish that she went & tracked it down....10 min later.  Cold pasta.....yup, no offer to heat it back up  I get it that this is a family / shared experience but when the 3 of us are ordering our own dishes then maybe one could infer that we each wanted our own plate.  A quick question could have solved that   There was no offer of any dessert options. Our waitress said a birthday cake was being brought out for Mom. But the Mr & I wanted dessert too....but not after waiting another 20 min b/c we seemed to have been forgotten, out there in the cold, dark corner. The table beside us who also had a birthday got the full range of dessert options made available to them.  Umm, okay....  Then the bill comes out & the Mr & I asked for the 2nd boxed up portion of pasta that the Mr couldn't finish & the waitress tells us she combined everything into the same box!  First of all, since when does one EVER assume all leftovers should be in the same box!?  So I tell her I'm sick & we'd been expecting separate boxes & she apologized & said she could have the kitchen make another portion and then split it up for us....sorry but no.  We'd already been there 2 hours for 1 shared appetizer and 1 main course.  I wasn't about to wait another hour for another serving of pasta.  She did remove the pasta from our bill for the inconvenience but overall, this was an awful birthday experience for our entire family. Too many other great restaurants around to have to deal with this again!  We won't be back
The entrance to the restaurant always reeks of feces, but it dissipates once you go to the seating areas. Weird.  But the food is really good and I've had no problems with the service.

Nice looking donuts, and decent matcha latte. The taste of both were just 'ok.' A dozen donuts is $29, and the latte was close to five dollars. Parking was a bit difficult to obtain. Overall, if you're in the area... i can see it being worth a try... (as novelty?), but it's not awesome enough for me to do it on the regular. Maybe once a year if I thought a client really likes donuts?   I do think it would be nice to provide some sort of bags to help carry large orders out. The girl behind the counter even looked a little surprised when I asked her to tape the boxes... and looked like she never heard someone asking for a bag. I could have easily dropped the three boxes of donuts, plus drink, plus phone and wallet while I crossed the street. I actually dropped one donut and it landed face down on the street.   I guess I'm a little spoiled with Porto's : staff helps you bring food to the car for you if you can't carry it all, much cheaper, huge selection, good quality items.

We tried the fisherman's stew and the cioppino. Both dishes are in the same tomato base and absolutely loaded with seafood. The stew is all fish, while the cioppino is all shellfish. The seasoning was pretty good. My husband and I both agree that a little more broth would be great since it's a very chunky dish with hardly any 'stew'. Attentive service and very fair prices. We'll definitely be back.

Fantastic! The service is a real highlight here. They are attentive and you feel like the whole team is watching out for what you need. The Miso Noodles are great and their Mai Tai has just the right kick. Finally, the view. Go just before sunset and have an experience that not many restaurants in Santa Barbara can compete with.

VEGAN EATERS HERE.....To be honest, the tag might better read 'Should have been better'.  If you know me, there is no bigger fan of restaurants in general. I rarely write a bad review, I just don't say anything. This is something that needs saying and, there will be no photos this time.  We've been going to Mesa Verde for almost seven years now and at times considered it our favorite (V) restaurant on the Central Coast. The food was spectacular, the presentation was always equal to the food, the creativity and  thought given to each element of the eating experience was simply remarkable.  Then, a while back, things started to fail and yes, I'm taking into consideration covid19. The quality started getting spotty, the service erratic, the ambience less inviting and the food, always the mainstay was inconsistent.  While in Santa Barbara the other day we decided to take a chance and revisit for lunch. To be honest, we were simply stunned. The menu was much smaller and literally dirty, and the prices had jumped to an alarming degree. The vibe was so flat, the only welcome came from the busser.  We ordered the street tacos and the green bowl to share between us. The prices were shocking, both $22.00 nearly double the previous prices, the presentation was beyond lackluster with the tacos arriving on a plain white plate, not the wooden planks they used to use, no garnish, just three tacos which comes to over $7.00 each?   The bowl wasn't much better but we figured we bought it so eat it. Both dishes were so salty as to be nearly inedible. We mentioned this to the server who shrugged his shoulders and walked off. Water was never refilled, no dessert offered, not that we would have ordered one at this point and, they range from $13-14.00 each.   To say that Mesa Verde is no longer what it once was isn't even close to the disappointing experience. This place used to be a Mecca for vegans and non-vegans. The food/service/ambience/quality was remarkable. Now it is simply sad, even the restroom was dirty.   I prefaced these comments by saying I am fully aware that Covid19 has created countless problems for restaurants, some have made it and some have not. Some have flourished and some have not which is the case here. It would take a lot to get us to return or bring any of the many friends and family we've introduced to MV over the years.  It gives me no joy to say any of this but it's not my restaurant, I have been a very good customer for many years so not one of the malcontents here who never even ate at a restaurant and gave it 1 star for not getting in. Nope.   I sincerely hope they wake up and give up the ghost or pull it together. Everyone deserves better.
The char broiled oysters are the best this side of New Orleans! Absolutely amazing. The service is always on point and it is one of our very favorite places to be!

This is my new favorite place!! Went there with a group of friends to celebrate a birthday and it was a really special celebration because of the warm, welcoming atmosphere and the DELICIOUS food. We ordered a few salads and each ordered an entree and every single bite of food was like a party in your mouth. The owner came over to deliver a few of our entrees and each time chatted with us. He is charming and clearly very talented, as is his partner/chef. The food is all farm to table, everything was super fresh and tasty. Can't wait to plan our next adventure at The Middle Child!
The outdoor dining patio is absolutely lovely! I enjoyed the scallops, chicken entree and cocktails were ok. The service and ambiance of this place are what really makes it special and worth the $$$.

We came here for dinner tonight (2/20/14) for our wedding anniversary and we had such a lovely meal! First of all, I wish I could remember our servers name, he was an all-around good guy, worked his butt off and was also training a new server. We were there at about 6.30. He was very attentive and it was clear that he loves making people happy. As soon as he knew it was our anniversary he offered us a drink on the house (so sad I had so say no, this pregnant girl would have loved a glass of bubbly!) but my husband gladly accepted. We ordered the baked Mac and Cheese, husband got the chilli caramel ribs, and I got the sea bass.   We could have eaten the Mac and Cheese all day! Creamy and delicious, but not too rich. The ribs fell off the bone, and had a great balance of spice and sweetness. The sea bass was delicious, but the crumbled garnish on top tasted a little bad to me. Honestly though, the dish overall was great, so I ignored it happily. Especially because it had baked Brussels Sprouts, which I love!  Our server threw us a dessert on him our way, which we were happy to accept. Order the Banana Milk Shake! It's such a decadent mix of salted caramel, banana, graham crackers and meringue. And way fun to share!  Have to share this funny moment we had with our server.....he spotted me making a chicken out of my cloth napkin (yep, I'm a huge dork in nice restaurants) so he walks over and put a ghost made out of a paper napkin on our table, and it started floating around by itself! We were laughing so hard I thought I busted a rib! He was nice enough to share his little party trick with us. Good guy!!  We will be back here for sure, so many more dishes I want to try, and their service is too good to not experience again! I hope our sever sees this and knows he made our night!

Knocking it down to two stars.  They've been slip sliding downhill lately.  I ordered items including a Big Mac and a few bites in, I noticed there wasn't any burger on the top part of the sandwich.  I lifted the bun and saw that only half the patty was there.  Nice.  Also, they had some tattooed gangsta wannabe working the counter for picking up orders.  He called my number and I picked it up and said thank you.  The A hole just looked off to the side and said 'yep'.  Not loving so much.

Always loved going here as an undergrad at UCSB. Definitely one of my favorite restaurants and was definitely a treat, getting to eat there again recently. Everything was just how I remembered it. I ordered my usual order, Duck breast and duck confit, and it was perfect as always. My partner got the veal dish, and it was cooked to a perfect medium rare. I always recommend this place to anyone visiting Santa Barbara.

Closed, after 39 years!  RIP Woody's. Bodacious!  Always a fun eclectic atmosphere and great energy and bountiful comfort food . I'll miss Woody's. And I'm vegetarian! Yummy fries.   A lot of history. I used to go to the location on Montecito Street. They were always at festivals, too.   Do you remember when the Goleta location was Flapper Alley? The old logo is still on the windows.   The owners are opening a new restaurant at the old Timbers location

I've been here twice now and both times have failed to impress me. I went during fiesta and had the chorizo, there was nothing special about it. They did have attentive servers who constantly checked on us, but it just isn't my kind of place. Lots of pastries, so if that's what you're into then this is your place.

Okay food with a pretty high price point. I came during Labor Day weekend so I expected the long wait, so that's not what made me give 3 stars.   I ordered the cioppino, crab cocktail, and the calamari appetizer. My fave dish was definitely the calamari because it was so crispy and tasty! The crab cocktail was pretty disappointing because there was very little crab on top of a full cup of cabbage with very little cocktail sauce. I think I definitely would have enjoyed the shrimp cocktail better. In my opinion, the cioppino wasn't reduced enough because it still had a prominent wine taste that wasn't the most pleasant. The cioppino was full of uncracked crab, scallops, mussels, and clams. The scallops were 100% my favorite part of the dish! I wish the crab was pre-cracked or if they gave us a cracker because mid meal we had to go back up to the window and ask for a cracker. The bread bowl unfortunately soaked up a lot of the broth so I it started to fall apart and make a mess on the tray. But nonetheless the bread soaked in the soup was pretty tasty.   A pretty cool place at the end of the pier that you should try at least once. I'm glad I checked this place out, but I don't think I'll be back anytime soon.

i haven't been here in a while, but i don't remember the food being that bad the last time that i was here.  we ordered takeout this evening, and i was seriously disappointed in the quality of the food. the lemon chicken was dry and tough as leather.  the chicken did not taste fresh, and possibly cooked the day before (or even more!).  the lemon sauce was bright yellow, yet it did not taste like lemon at all.  the fried wontons were cold and hard...again, did not taste fresh and probably cooked earlier that day. the sweet and sour sauce for the wontons had the consistency of jello...like they had packed it the day before and just let it sit.  gross.  chow fun had no flavor. beef broccoli hardly had any broccoli and the beef was chewy.  not worth the $50 we spent on an appetizer & 3 main dishes.  if you have no choice but to eat here, you will have better luck eating from the buffet. definitely will not eat at yen ching again. ever.

I was taken here for a free lunch so I can't complain too much. I'm not a vegetarian or health-nut, so most of the menu was unappealing to me. I had the eggplant sandwich and carrot juice. Both were good, but a bit overpriced. The outdoor seating was very close to the busy road and in direct sunlight.

I'd love for this review to be 5-stars and highlight the games, food, drinks, and general ambiance that the Cruisery has to offer. I'd heard pretty good things about the place, and my wife, friends, and I agreed that we should head there for a bit of a Sunday funday.  Well, our visit was cut short and there was no fun to be had...at least at this stop.   We walked into the back room of the Cruisery where there were 15 people present, along with 10 large screen TV's, a bunch of empty tables, and some empty couches. A few football games were on and we figured we'd pull up a couch and watch the U.S. Open tennis finals at one of the many unwatched TV's. There are at least a few of us who'd rather watch a U.S. Open tennis finals match instead of the first game of the football season.   We asked the bartender if he'd put the tennis match on one of the TV's that no one was watching. He left, and a few minutes later returned, saying that the general manager said all TV's were reserved for football games.   'Really? ALL the TV's?' we asked.   'Yup.'  That was it. No apologies, no alternate solutions or suggestions...kinda weird. You'd think that maybe they could have offered one (or 4?) of the non-viewed screens to other non-football sports. Especially with no one else there.   But alas, it was not to be. We walked out less than three minutes after walking in, taking our business to Paxti's up the street for some food, brews, mimosas, pizza, and tennis in what is arguably a better environment (read: indoor/outdoor bar) and a pleasant atmosphere.   Yes, I'll probably try the Cruisery at some point again in the future, but so far they're at one strike...and 1 star.

OMG! Their food is awesome. Love their Salmon and my son always orders their Diner burger with fries. The meet has so much flavor.  Great customer service and the price is right.  Go for breakfast or lunch.

I'd definitely come back here. Located right on State Street that has now become closed to car traffic, the seating is right on the side walk so if people is your jam, you'll have a prime seat for viewing.   Parking: Paid lot in the back with rates that are more reasonable and plenty of time to enjoy your meal and browse the nearby shops in Downtown Santa Barbara. A lot of restaurants are located nearby, so the parking is very convenient.   Food: 3 Lengua tacos were my choice for that night and they were very good. Juicy, flavorful and filling and doused with hot sauce and a squeeze of lime and cilantro, delicious!!! Didn't even care about the raw onions (not a fan) You have your choice of fillings, so get what you like but you really can't go wrong with any meat choices. Nothing fancy, but very filling and the meat cooked perfectly. Ask for hot sauce. Chips and guac are freshly made and the pricing is better than any place in LA (trust me).  Summary: Sadly, I no longer have reason to visit Santa Barbara anymore as family have moved away from the area. However, if you happen to be in the area on your way to Solvang, stop by for a bite. There are a number of taco shops in Santa Barbara that you can go to seeing as the area is part of the Spanish exploration era, but come here if you're in the area. Chips and guac must be shared because the portion is not small. Happy eating!!

If you're in the mood for a truly delicious burger in Santa Barbara, this is the place to go.  It's not as inexpensive as some other options, but the ingredients are better quality than any of the other burger joints here that come to mind.  Also, they have a lot more options.  The Fig Marmalade burger is my favorite.  They have quite a few interesting combinations and options of burgers and within their other entrees as well.  I also really appreciate the environment.  It's a bit loud and can be hard to talk inside, but it's always lively.  It's a modern space, but comfortable.  They also have a big selection of beer and good well drinks.  The food, the drinks and the environment make Eureka an awesome downtown dinner option, especially with friends.  It's a fun place to go with excellent food and not terribly expensive for downtown/late night dining.

We absolutely love this place everyone is friendly and the food is great. Biscuit sampler is a must!!! We love their mocha coffees. But if you wanting to drink get the mimosas to share. You get a whole bottle of brut and orange juice to pour yourself.

Best Italian restaurant in Santa Barbara.  I've tried them all!  finally a lasagna just the way I love it. The salmon putenesca pasta with its generous chunks of fish was to die for!  And wait till you try their pizzas, made with fermented dough!  If you love traditional Italian food, in a lovely restaurant with its gracious host and owner Guido this is for you....
Super delicious! We had the deviled eggs, pork chop and rainbow trout and they were all fantastic.

I came here a last week, and I can't stop thinking about how good their Tonkatsu Ramen is. Visiting from the SF bay area where I frequently eat Japan Town Ramen, I was shocked at how good this place is.   Their broth is absolutely amazing. It had a ton of flavor, depth and a touch of spice.  The pork had a nice crust on the outside and super tender on the inside.  If they ever decide to make some sort of taco with that pork, please sign me up.  It also had a perfectly cooked egg. This place is an absolute gem in Santa Barbara and a MUST try.    I'll make sure I eat here before I fly back to SF tomorrow.  **update** I am glad I stopped to enjoy some ramen before flying back to SF. Second time was just as pleasing. Friendly staff and an amazing Duck ramen.  My Dad enjoyed their Soba noodles and asked me to bring him again. I'll be back in a few weeks.

This is the best Indian food in Santa Barbara. Period. We love this place so much! Delicious flavors, good portions, vegan options. We have done take out only so far (saw the other reviews on poor service) but we haven't had a mistake yet with our order. HIGHLY recommend.

As many reviewers have said- decent food, terrible service. My fiance and I have eaten here a few times, most recently on Christmas day around 11:30. The restaurant was pretty full, but not overly crowded. After sitting at our table for 10 minutes, we had to get up to ask someone for menus. Another 15 minutes go by with no water on the table before we have to flag a server down. We ordered Eggs Benedict and an Italian sandwich. Pretty straight forward, right? WRONG. We waited for 45 minutes for my Eggs Benedict to arrive. I patiently wait for my fiance's sandwich to show up before eating my food. Waiting... waiting... waiting... By this time it's 12:45, we are both starving, my eggs are cold, server is ignoring us, still no sign of our second plate. We are over it. We let the server know we want to leave and he so graciously 'took off the sandwich' WOW! Thank you for not charging us for food that we never received, so kind of you! Any other day and this experience probably wouldn't have upset us so much, but this was Christmas day. It is really disappointing that this restaurant would chose to open on a day that should be enjoyable for diners when they are incapable of handling a table of two. We will absolutely not be returning to Caffe Primo.

Five years after my first posted review and roughly a dozen meals at Black Sheep later, I can happily report this place is still churning out some of the best quality, tastiest eats in the city.            For over a decade I was a concierge in town.  During a good portion of those years I watched while other restaurants have come and gone & Black Sheep has held it down.  They never fail to garner a good review from a hotel guest sent their direction and in my own experience, Ruben and crew are top-notch.        Thank you for always coming through!

Why can't we have a place like this in Norcal? Ensenada beer battered fish tacos were really great, but the standout was the tuna ceviche with pomegranate seeds and watermelon, the cauliflower taco was incredible and the Octopus and shrimp taco was so gooood. Every taco had so much depth of flavor that we came here two nights in a row and ordered the exact same thing. Just wow.

Nice quiet spot in the heart of downtown Santa Barbara. They have tables all over the front of the restaurant, some right on State street creating a very unique experience. Their iconic Italian decor and friendly atmosphere, makes them a family favorite. I had their famous bacon cheeseburger. It was grilled to perfection; AMAZING!  Their tiramisu is really awesome as well.   PLUS! Parking is super easy since the city parking lot is literally on their back entrance.

Stearns Wharf encompasses a lot businesses. The business is run up and down the pier and there's a little bit of everything. . If you want breakfast, they're ready to serve you breakfast in several locations. If you want history, there are placards and history up and down the pier. Now I was there very early. So most of the restaurants we're really not even open yet. But I studied the plaques along the way and learned about Stearns Wharf and this guy Stearns who came and built a really solid pier and made Santa Barbara come to life. The story Is it there was another Pier down on Chapala . That was a little rickety. So this guy Stearns built a real solid Pier? And everybody was able to do business then and ship things in and out in a really good way. So that made all the difference. The investment in infrastructure is always something that every city needs to make. Now back to the pier. It's a nice walk out there and you can drive out and park out there and if you come early, it's really empty. Later in the day. It will fill up and they will charge you to come out and or charge you when you leave. And I think it's worth it. But for me. I'd rather walkout the pier and enjoy everything along the way. Be sure to take your time and look at it and read. The placards along the way it's quite revealing about Santa Barbara its history. And Mister Stearns in the wharf

A friend invited me today for dinner at this restaurant.  The waitress Nicky was so courteous, pleasant and very helpful, she is a plus plus to the place. Wish they had broader and more high end wine selections. Salad was great, baba ghanouch was decent, falafel was abit salty, hummus was abit acidic. Ambiance was wonderful, music blends in with the environment. Customer service is good.
Amazing, flavorful, and fresh vegan and vegetarian options.  Friendly staff, but can sometimes be a bit slow with the service. Worth a go, just not if you're in a hurry.

After tirelessly shopping around on State street on a Tuesday night, I was extremely excited to try this place because of amazing reviews. It's also conveniently located by all the shops :) Although I enjoyed the environment and beer, the food was a little underwhelming.   The environment is quite casual with outside seating, wooden benches, and open-viewing bars. It's the type of place where you grab a menu and seat yourself.   To my delight, the brew choices lived up to the expectations of a 'brat haus.' The pilsner and lizard mouth beers were both supple and a perfect pair for our pub grub. However, beware of the sizes; a single is a 16 oz. pint, whereas the stein is a massive 1 liter mug!   As for the food, the garlic parmesan fries bucket  ($10) was tasty but incredibly dry/ brittle when they were first brought out. My friend complained about them destroying the roof of her mouth . Thankfully our server took them back to the kitchen and returned with fluffy yet VERY CRISPY, thick cut fries. There were no complaints, however, about the complementary sauces (you choose 3) which were creamy and savory. The chipotle aioli is a MUST try!   My reuben sandwich (~$10) was tasty but somehow incredibly heavy. Yes, pub food isn't known for being very healthy but I could have lived without all that grease! The cole slaw was realllly good though. It was well seasoned but not wilted; moist but not drowned in mayonnaise .   My partner ordered the spicy brat (~$9) which was recommended to be paired with the lizard mouth. However, we both expected the brat to be much juicier. For some reason we were missing the SNAP, that is indicative of a good sausage. The texture was a disappointment. Nonetheless the slow burning heat, good fennel flavor, and caramelized onions saved the bite.   Honestly, I was underwhelmed by the food. However, I hope that it was just a fluke and hope to give this place another try.

I love the fajitas but I have to tell the waiter twice to not burn them or overcook them -and only then do they always come out perfect. If I don't remind the waiter -they come out with a lot of charcoal on them. It's worth reminding them! The Quesadillas have rubbery fake American cheese in them but I think other than the Quesadilla -dish-the food is really good! I love that they have their own easy parking really special and cool to not have to hunt for parking downtown! The location is amazing great people watching if you sit outside!   The live music is wonderful BUT WAY TOO LOUD! Such a small place and they use the speakers that r blasting music pretty much 510 feet from your face, so you can't hear the person you're with talking!  It would be much better if they didn't use speakers- it's such a small patio

It pains me to leave a poor review for Lilac. I have a gluten allergy and have been eating, almost exclusively, here for years. We order cakes for occasions and buy bread by the loaf. I take my out-of-town guests without gluten issues here. I'm not sure what happened to Lilac recently, though.   While I am very aware that restaurants are facing challenges this year, Lilac chose not to open for quite awhile after the shutdown, despite the fact that the majority of their menu is soups, salad, and sandwiches.   When they did reopen, with very short hours, the price of the quiche that I ordered in January went from $35 to $75, and many other items jumped in price drastically, as well. As a loyal customer, that felt more like a slap in the face than it felt like they appreciated my patronage.   Today, I finally agreed to go eat at Lilac and give it another try. They have a fraction of the baked goods that they used to have. My partner was in line, second to a party of four. I went to sit at the only available table outside, which was a two-top.   A man who worked there approached and told me that the table was reserved. There was no reserved sign. I asked what he meant. He said it was reserved for people ordering indoors. My partner was the next in line, ordering indoors for both of us. I told him that, and he asked me to leave the table anyway. There were no other parties of two ahead of us.   Had I brought my parents with me, as I often do, my dad would not have been able to go into the restaurant to order and would've had to sit and wait at a table, as he is disabled. Would he be asked to leave, as well? I can't take that chance.   There was no sign indicating that all guests needed to stand until they ordered. Had I seen one, I wouldn't have asked my partner to wait in line in the first place. I'm not willing to spend the upwards of $50 per person that I'd planned and then gamble as to whether there will be an available table. Nor am I willing to support a business that treats its guests for Sunday brunch with no hospitality or, frankly, respect.  I stopped my partner from ordering and do not plan to return. I'm very sad to lose a place that I had really enjoyed, but I don't need another message from the establishment about how little they value our choice to spend our money at their business and how quickly they become willing to treat loyal customers poorly.  As an update, I did bring my concern to the restaurant directly over five days ago. I didn't even receive a response.

First time here and I was satisfied.  The service was good even though they gave away our garlic cheese bread to a different table. They immediately corrected it by refunding us our money back and still giving us the bread for free. Food was Delish
As a woman of the South, I was disappointed.  But for Santa Barbara, it's alright.  And it keeps your kitchen clean since you don't have to fry the chicken yourself.

Yaaaahhhooooo....my 100th revieeeeeeeewwwwwww!  This was a new experience for me. I'm not going to lie...I was a little nervous, but hey....aren't we all a little nervous the first time?  So my brother and his wife had tried this place a few years back and fell in love with the food, atmosphere, and service. It wasn't just a restaurant, it was like being invited into the home of some friends....and these friends know how to have a good time. I think Chef Karim let them get married in their restaurant initially....I'll have to check on that.  Don't plan on making this a fast meal...they fill up the restaurant and keep it that way. It's like a 7 course meal followed by dessert and dancing. His wife is an amazing belly-dancer who isn't afraid to grab people from their seats and get them to jiggle their bellies.  The food...simply amazing.   Give this place a try. It's a great place for a first date...especially if you want to help ease the nerves. If the date is bad, at least you'll have a good meal and make some new friends out of Chef Karim and his family.
Very casual but at the same time very romantic if you sit outside with the lights. Very good food, reasonably priced. Good selection of beers and wine. Does not disappoint.

Tried this place for the first time tonight. The reviews were way too generous if you ask me. First off the place was very hot and stuffy and the Mexicali roll I had was sub-standard. My date had the vegetable udon which was bland and flavorless. Lastly 3 out of five of the faucets in the bathroom were out of order. No second chances for Yume.

The great thing about this place is that they have happy hour like ALL THE TIME. It's kind of away from the hustle and bustle of State St., but not too far away. It's trendy, and modern and I'm sure the dinner entrees are wonderful, but I wasn't too impressed with their happy hour.  Compared to most restaurants, Arch Rock Fish actually has a good selection of food items on their happy hour menu. My boyfriend and I ordered the Spicy Tuna and Salmon Roll, Lobster Slider, Grilled Fish Taco, and Fried Calamari (which was actually free since there was a Yelp check-in offer). I thought the Spicy Tuna and Salmon Roll was good especially since this place wasn't a sushi restaurant. The Lobster Slider was alright, but for $6 during happy hour for only one I don't think it was worth it. The Grilled Fish Taco was good, however, when I inspected my taco the fish was the size of a chicken nugget. It was tiny, and there was so much tortilla left over after I ate the itty bitty nugget of fish. The Fried Calamari is just like calamari anywhere else, it's almost hard to mess it up.   Overall, I like the atmosphere, but I think they need to make their happy hour portion sizes a little more on a few of their items. When people order a fish taco, they want a decent sized piece of fish. At least one that fills up the whole tortilla!

This is not like the Sambos I grew up with, it's SOOOO much better.  Everything is fresh and homemade, with great care!  We ordered the 911 omelette that was flavor infused and the portions are huge!  Pancakes fluffy and fresh!  The corned beef hash Benedict  was so tasty.  Fresh corned beef, diced potatoes with greens,  poached eggs and fresh Benedict sauce that was spot on!!

Great staff. Ordered chimichanga rolls, popcorn shrimp, chicken tenders and bean burgers. C.rolls are tasty, came with mash potatoes & BBQ sauce. Sauce overpowered the rolls. We loved the chicken fingers, actual pcs of chicken not processed:-)  Bean burgers were good & came with half fries & salad.  Overall the place is good. Didn't go in the back but they had pool tables. Could be a fun place to hang.

This place is delicious! I came here for brunch and ordered an omelet. The ham inside the eggs were cooked with nice browning on the side and juicy insides. The eggs were soft and light. The toast was soft and puffy (not like the thin toasts you get in other places) with some burnt edges. Some people may not like the burnt edges, but I thought they were fine. The butter had just the right balance of hardness and softness. The butter was hard enough that it was actually butter, and not some creamy margarine. Yet, it was just creamy enough that it spread perfectly on the toast. The blueberry jam was my favorite! It tasted fresh and sweet and had none of that tart flavor. From how everything tastes, I can only assume that everything, including the butter and jam, was home-made. What a delight!   The atmosphere felt French. Many of the customers spoke French.   I was too full to try their collection of desserts, but they looked beautiful. Their contents also sounded interesting. I will definitely go back for some gluten-free desserts.
Hole in the wall and great for groups.  Best authentic Mexican food I've had in SB. Complimentary chips/salsa and cheese quesadilla-type thing(!!!).

Fabulous venue for wedding of about 100, but they could handle twice that.  We worked with Tyr (event manager) and Alex (chef), but they knew exactly what to do and when.  The reception was a no brainer and everyone enjoyed everything without any worries.  I would not hesitate to give the El Paseo five stars for helping to make wonderful memories with very little stress.   They have an amazing system and experience doing lots of events.    I would suggest you check their website to see what times are available.  They do book up.   As me details if you need to.

Fantastic Thai food. I think it's the best Thai food in Santa Barbara. I have been coming here for over 25 years. I have never been disappointed. I especially love the pumpkin curry. Also the chicken satay is excellent.  So is the chicken with basil curry, and the pineapple curry. They have a large flat noodle that's outstanding. But order it with extra peanut sauce (A slight additional charge well worth it). People are super nice, great service, a real family run institution.

Okay, so you like old school? This just may be the place! Once again, I owe knowing this place to my Italian in-laws that apparently know/knew the owner's family. Thusly, we got the royal treatment the first time we stopped in for a family lunch. But, it turns out that you, too, can be treated like royalty! Because the next time we dined for early dinner, we just got more of the same despite remaining anonymous! When I say 'old school' I mean authentic, in this case. The food is so true to tradition, but not heavy-handed in any way. It proves that authenticity doesn't mean Italian meals can't be fresh, bright, simple, and yet hearty. This casual spot produces dignified dishes with care, and boy does it show! The menu is often refreshed I'm told, but you can't go wrong with just an espresso and gelato, either. We'll be back!

Loved this place and would come back many times to try everything. Great carnitas burrito, delicious rice and beans, well put together nachos, and unique salsa bar. Pretty quick service but not much seating. Very small, super nice staff, highly recommend eating here!

Nice for the view... cannot talk about their food because we had already eaten food. It is a casual dining experience, though. Just enjoyed a glass of wine and took in the horizon.  Great views from Stearn's Wharf...   ~your constant companion,   Deirdra

We live a few blocks away on the Mesa so we were excited to have a new spot to walk to. Sat at the bar, service was friendly and the food and drinks were great! We tried the carne asada flatbread, wings and jalapeño poppers. The food was excellent! Hot and fresh ingredients. They lose a half a star because the acoustics in the main dining area need work, it is SO loud we couldn't really hear each other even sitting right next to each other at the bar. Overall it was great and we will make it a regular spot! (also please put a few more kid friendly options on the menu)

No good places in all of  Santa Barbara county to get a good pastrami sandwich but here! Even have the great pickles to go with it! Four stars because they do not put quite enough meat. Should be overflowing.

LOVE THIS PLACE!! The Molcajetes are the bomb! I've tried the chicken and the shrimp and wasn't disappointed either time. This is such a unique dish with deep, delicious flavors. The steak picado was also really good. It wasn't saucy like most steak picados that I've had in the past but it had great flavor and tons of veggies. On our last visit we started with the Los Agaves salad which was loaded with tomatoes, mandarin oranges, mango, avocado, and cranberries. I really like this salad as well but didn't thought the dressing was too sweet so I was happy we requested it on the side. The salad didn't really need dressing. Other great perks include the salsa bar and fresh tortillas. The portions are very large here so you can definitely share and everyone will still leave with happy, fully bellies!

I made a point to visit this restaurant before leaving Santa Barbara. I had their Burrito Mojado. It's a wet burrito topped with cheese, sour cream, and guacamole with a side of rice. The marinaded meat was tasty and tender. The burrito was smaller than what I'm used to, but still enough to leave you full and satisfied.   I also ordered their new sopaipillas to-go. Since it's a take-out order, they give you 2 salsa cups to pack your choice of salsas to-go. I'm not sure if they charge extra if you request for more salsa cups.  The last time I had sopaipillas was in a well known southwestern restaurant in Colorado's oldest town. It was one of the best desserts I've ever had. I still remember despite the fact it was 10 years ago!   The one in Los Arroyos is actually an entree with shredded chicken and vegetables similar to a tostada. It was nothing special. I wish they actually made dessert sopaipillas.  My search for real sopaipillas continues...

We had already visited a few tasting rooms and needed some food to help absorb the tasty wine. This place is right near the bulk of the tasting rooms on the Urban Wine Trail so it is conveniently located.  It was a beautiful day so we enjoyed the patio space. It is a large space with a lot of large picnic bench style seating. You walk up to the counter to place your order. Then you grab a seat and they bring you your food. I opted for the Oak Smoked Trip Tip Sandwich. We also ordered the Street Corn Off The Cob.  The food came out fairly quickly. The corn on the cob was tasty. I enjoyed that it was off the cob so I could eat it without making a mess. The portion is good to share for two to three people. The sandwich was pretty tasty too. It is good sized but not overwhelming. The horseradish cream gives it a nice punch. The meat was tender and pretty good on its own. The barbecue sauce tasted good. It was hard to get a good read on it because of the horseradish cream though. That was fine for me, but I am not sure how the sauce would be on its own.  If you are in the area this is a great place for a casual lunch between tastings or even for some drinks with friends.
Amazing cole slaw - sometimes I stop in just to have that for dinner! And the Picnic Tuna sandwich is fantastic, too!
Awesomeness!!!!! The service was great, food on point I was not wowed by the signature drinks.  Otherwise,  I'll be back for happy hour

I've been here a few times for drinks with friends and I've always had a great time. Their cocktails are pretty delicious if I do say so myself. I had previously never come to eat food other than fries, and I always wondered why people would eat here especially later on when it gets so crowded and being that it's so dark. However, I found my answer.  My friend I went wine tasting in the funk zone, and we were looking for a good food place, and since Seven's had an Axxess card deal, I figured, why not try it out? It was a very very good decision! I had the Envy sandwich, which was one of their veggie options, and my friend had the chicken and waffles, and both were pretty incredible! Not at all what I was expecting for a bar kitchen, for sure. I think I'd like to go back when it's a little less crowded and try out some of their appetizers because they also look great. Also, if you want a good drink, i recommend the Pomegranate Impression or the American Mule!

Came in after last call and asked what they'd recommend - ended up with one of the best, albeit messy, philly cheese steaks I've ever had.   Staff was  was awesome and friendly.  Looking forward to trying more of their menu - great addition to the SB late night scene.

UPDATE the tap water is no longer delicious. It kinda tastes like the schmutz you find between your gums and the inside of your cheek when you wake up from a nap, and are known to eat a lot of cheese.
Pizza was terrible, looked like somone sat on it and it was cold. And it took over an hour for delivery 3 blocks away. What a shame.

Thank you Embermill staff for provider such a pleasant and enjoyable eating experience last night. It was my first Time there for me and my friend and we were both blown away by the flavor of the food and the over all cleanliness of the restaurant. Water glasses were spotless and silverware were obviously sanitized and wiped down. We sat outside on the patio and the heaters worked perfectly. I will most definitely return to eat here again soon. May I suggest the plantains....they are to die for. Thanks again
When you are at Bacara, you only have a few choices. This place is pretty good. It is pricey, but the portions are decent...and you have a view of the ocean. That is pretty nice!

This could be it. The best Cajun food in the West Coast. (or at least California since I have not yet explore much of Oregon and Washington) After moving away from the South, I have yet to have any decent Louisiana fix. A simple bowl of gumbo or just  some good old jambalaya is harder to come by than a kaiseki meal.   From the moment I approached the Palace Grill, I know they are onto something. The ambiance, the hospitality and the genuine of the staff seemed all so familiar. That was the reason I waited patiently for 45 minutes on a very empty stomach after a full day of hiking. I know I would regret it if I just walk away.  I am glad I have stayed. This place is the real deal. Nothing fancy or extravagance just good old fashion authentic Cajun cooking. Seafood Gumbo was very comforting. Everything on the 'all the best' platter was highly enjoyable. The Soft Shell Crab, the Crawfish Etouffee and the Blackened Salmon.... all well prepared, fully flavored and completely gratifying. They have the signature boldness of Southern food but not overwhelming heavy or oily. The Bread Pudding Souffle that a lot of people raved about was indeed quite good. Tried both the dark chocolate and the original version. They are rich with just the right amount of sweetness. The whiskey cream sauce and chocolate bourbon cream sauce were wonderful. Be warned though, they are not for the weak of the heart or those with small appetite.  Even the miniature version is huge. The normal version would be a challenge to finish especially after such a fulfilling meal.   Did I mention the service was spectacular? Live music while you wait. Staff were very efficient and organized. Ambiance was great. it was so full of energy and warmth. Highly recommend this place to anyone looking for some Southern comfort.

This place is badass. Love coming here. Long longtime local bar and grill in an iconic location. Awesome deck for outside dining street side, seating inside with TV, and of course a bar counter to belly up to. The food is fantastic, with a ton of choices for breakfast (all day) lunch and dinner. Drinks are STIFF. And the prices are too cheap to believe! Great hospitality too. I'm ready to stop in here again, and I just left there two hours ago! A+

This is great Indian cousin in Santa Barbara. It's located on state street right by all the shopping. This was my second time here and I was just as pleased as the first. It was me, my brother, and my boy and we ordered some vegetable samosas to start, garlic naan, butter chicken, lamb vindaloo, and one of their specials with chicken and coconut. Everything was delicious. I can't really say much more. For dessert we ordered milk balls in sweet cardamon water. I don't remember the name because it was in hindu but they were little doughnut holes soaked in sugar water and served warm. Quite tasty!
This is the perfect place to grab a hotdog! Cheap, fast, easy, and convenient. Check this place out!

The food is always good and fresh. The service is good except when they try to rush you down the line ( even when there is no one behind you! Before you can finish saying 'Flour' they quickly ask 'what type of beans'! Annoying. The tables are usually wiped off and clean. They had a gas leak about 6 months ago and didn't bother to put a sign on the door so a bunch of us were all waiting outside for about 10 minutes at 10:30( when they open) and the workers just walked by and looked at us. I left scrambling to find somewhere else to get lunch. I was pretty upset and complained but never got a response to my complaint. I later found out from a worker what happened that day.   Also there is a gym across the street and sometimes smelly dudes come over and stink up the place. I had to pack up quickly one time when some gross smelly guy sat down next to me. Gym= 2 hours, deodorant= no, smell= putrid. It was a huge turnoff and I think about it every time I pass by and usually eat at Panda instead or get my food to go!

Passing through SB, and Yelp led us to Olio. We had a good experience.  The pizza with the black truffle was the bomb--addicting!  Just thinking about it now, is causing me to crave it.  Their margerita was lacking in toppings--needed more tomato and they hardly had any basil on the pizza.  And one of the pizzas, I can't remember which...I think it was the black truffle one, was soggy.  Pizza should never be soggy. They had the right idea with the crust.  They just need to avoid the 'sog' factor.  Personally, I think they need to cook their pizza a bit more to get some char on the bottom.  Maybe that's a preference thing, but that's how I like it.  Would I go back?  Yes, for the black truffle pizza!
Hadn't been here in ages, but heard they had to-go specials while dining rooms were closed. Grabbed dinners for the household and we all really enjoyed our meals.

We stopped in to Bella Vista before dinner one night and I'm glad we did as it was a lovely, relaxing spot to have a cocktail.  Service, as you'd expect from a Four Seasons property, was wonderful and friendly.  The location was serene and it was nice to hear the waves lapping on the beach.  My only complaint (if you can call it a complaint) is that the water isn't lit (or wasn't the night we were there), so while you can hear the waves, you can't see them.  Perhaps I was in a bad spot (not sure if a different area of the patio has visibility at night?), but it would have been nice to look out to the water (which I'm sure is great during the day).  Prices are high as you might imagine, but the drink list is varied and does allow for almost any guest to be happy here.  I would definitely visit again if I am in the area.  I'd love to stay at the hotel as it looks amazing as well!

Giving 5 stars because this was my second time here and the food/ambience is amazing.   I wanted to take off a star because they brought me the wrong order, took it back, but by the time I got my correct food my group was finished eating. I didn't ask for a discount, coming from the industry I thought my $3 dollar sprites would at least be free. Barley got a sorry from the waitress. Just not the best customer service/impression from her.  Other than that, great spot, good food and better vibes.

Rude, dismissive and not accommodating to a party of five returning from a previously good experience just two days ago. Today? Long wait though fewer people. Then made to sit in the sun with no shade.  When requested to sit elsewhere where told to get up and wait outside in a very rude and demeaning way. What the hell? Could you be a little bit less bothered and more interested in the $150-$200 I was about to drop at your joint. A-holes.----- Your reply is full of falsehoods sir. The wait was longer than 5. I was wearing a mask else you wouldn't have sat me in the sun. Lastly, the table requested was already vacant and just needed cleaning. Making us go back outside would have been ok had you asked us to, rather than 'ordered' me as if I was a child. You were rude and demeaning. Like I said, as if we were inconveniencing your Tuesday afternoon rush hour, masses that were waiting-- not. Your dishonest reply deserves your boss fire you. Moby Dick is a great place with great food. You sir, are an idiot.

To start off the New Year of 2022, I figured I would write a review for a place I regularly visit and appreciate. Given the bags under my eyes and slight fatigue due to being up past my normal bedtime of 9 pm, I could go for a cup of their joe right about now! While I do enjoy many coffee shops in town, Java Station often has a few perks that places it toward the top. - Shorter line for friendly service - Better food offerings - Decent amount of parking and seating - High speed Wi-Fi (not sure how this is possible in town) - Welcoming to all (they celebrate Pride, Halloween and beyond) - They have games available to play - It brings a sense of community to an odd little neighborhood (What the heck is it considered?! Noleta? Or Hope Ranch Annex? ...Now we are just getting particular...) I enjoy their Klatch Coffee Co. espresso which is bold without being acidic or underbearing. I often order the Oat Milk Vanilla Latte (yah, I'm basic), yet recently ordered a fall/winter special of Maple Bourbon Latte which I'm a big fan of. I will note that their 'small' coffee is truly small, so order a medium if in doubt. Now that I'm rambling due to a lack of sleep, I will adjourn...until a Review Update comes along!

Lure really took great care to make my Moms 87th birthday special. Made a reservation the day before for 11 and they set us up the the private room. The waitstaff was great and the food and drinks were even better. Every dish we ordered was fresh and delicious. Thank you Lure. We will be back.

Great food!!!!! Inside feels a little too much like a subway and I'm just not into it. Other than that though it is amazing. Hoping the ownership has changed since the scandal... I don't really like financially supporting would-be murderers but the nachos are bomb.

Yum, yum, yum is all I can say! What an amazing restaurant in Montecito. So happy to have found true Napoleon style food and pizza. The heirloom tomato with burrata and focaccia was to die for. Stunning taste and freshness. Followed by 2 fantastic pizzas- summer seasonal pizza with peaches and peppers was delicious and my fav, plus the rossa pizza which was simple and delicious. Definitely gets crowded- make reservations definitely.  Staff was friendly and patio is super cute.  Totally worth it and going back!

Love love love. The Natural Cafe is absolutely amazing. I love the ability and freedom to be able to replace ingredients with vegan options. Definitely recommend to anyone who is vegetarian or vegan as well as really anybody looking for a nice healthy meal!

Big shout out to the crew working tonight - what a great team! Especially the lady that took my order and the guy with camo pants making some of my food - great multitasking! I can truly say not only is the food super delicious and decently priced, but the people that work here are amazing! Makes me feel like my money was well spent lol :)
Wonderful on the beach restaurant. The food is excellent & it is a great place to gather with friends for breakfast, lunch or dinner. Try & get an outside table with a view of the beach & ocean.

This is the best Chinese food you will find in Santa Barbara. Although that means it is mediocre compared to the likings of LA, it deserves the name of THE BEST because it is in SB. The crab is pretty good - especially in garlic sauce (though the crab is not too meaty). The Singapore noodles are awesome - has a tingle of spice! Love the walnut shrimp!   A really good place to come with a good size crowd because that will make the meal price worth it and you will have a lot of selections to choose from bc you will be eating family style!

My introduction to Super Cucas started in Isla Vista in the summer of 2005(?) maybe 2006? when I believe the sons of the original Super Cucas opened up the #3 location.  For the first week, every item on the menu was .50!!!  I ate there breakfast lunch and dinner for the whole week and they had me every since.  When they introduced their breakfast menu, I fell in love all over again!  I then moved out of isla vista and in to downtown SB - In my search for local eateries, I came across Super Cucas again on Micheltorena, I believe this is the original location.  Same great food - same great breakfast options - its all here and its just as good as the IV location (of course it is - its the original!).  Every now and again, the chicken can be a little dry - but when its not, its delicious!  I usually judge the mexican places in town for their chicken - this place definitely has a unique method of preparation to give it a flavor only found in town - I love it.
Cute new place. We ordered a chowder bread bowl to share. It was good and my husband was satisfied.
Gotta try their cheesecake. You will thank me later Lol. Great job on the desserts! Their quesadilla was pretty good too.

First off, the food is great. So why 3 stars?  I've been coming to Renaud's on lower State St. for almost two years precisely because the food is so good. But frankly, the attitudes that seem to trickle down from Renaud to the rest of the staff have consistently colored my experience toward the negative.   It may seem like little stuff: a generally unfriendly staff, weird rules about bus staff not helping with drink refills ('if I do it for you, I have to do it for everybody.'), or the odd choice to strictly limit menu items based on time of day or day of week (you can have a ham & cheese croissant in the morning but not in the afternoon when you have to order the French Cubano sandwich with no pickles to get the same thing, and you can't have it on the weekends unless you order the ham & cheese & EGG croissant and tell them to hold the egg -which you have to still pay for. Really?  I guess if you're looking for an authentic French breakfast or lunch complete with weird rules snippy attitudes from a prickly staff and lots of grief if you want something basic not currently on the ever changing menu of the moment... then you'll be in for a treat. Because the food really is good.   But you might also walk away with a bad taste, if not in your mouth, then in your heart.

Happy hour is awesome. Get the nachos and watermelon lemonade. My absolute favorite thing on their menu has to be the blended mojito! I don't know why it's so good but I will make a trip just to sandbar for the blended mojito!

We made a reservation, when we showed up we found out someone had taken our reservation using our name. The hostess went to check with the couple and they admitted it. She sat us at another table- seems fine. We waited on a friend to show up with her baby so we ordered a bit after we sat down. We were in the middle of our shared plates and a manger came over saying, I'm so sorry but you have to leave in 10 minutes- we have another party coming who made a reservation. Lots of empty apologies but an unacceptable experience. We were all so disappointed and won't be going back. We had 5 different servers come over trying to take our plates away, rush us out before we could even finish our meals and order dessert- they just brought the check then tried to take it several times before we had cards down. The worst experience eating here with girlfriends trying to catch up. This could have easily been avoided if they told us we had a certain amount of time to eat from when they sat us down, or given us our original table and made the couple who took it leave instead. Terrible management.

Last meal before check out and it was great.  They open at 7AM which is perfect for a post gym workout meal to replenish. Spacious dining area with indoor and outdoor seating available. You can order a la cart or do the breakfast/brunch buffet for $75/person. Carving stations have prime rib and chicken which were moist, tender and juicy. Omlette station allows you to customize it to your preference and portion was huge. There's also a variety of cured meats, oatmeal station, fresh fruits and delectable pastries/desserts. Lastly, there's bottomless mimosas if you want some morning bubblies.  Service was great - staff is welcoming and attentive.

Loved the tuna cones, tomatoes with burrata, and rigatoni! Highly recommend making a reservation, I think we made ours a week in advance. Service was impeccable. Solid Italian food, nothing crazy, but definitely tasty. A good place to take family and friends since everyone will enjoy it.

I'm a fan of Lure! We ordered salads, lobster roll, calamari, and the tequila seafood pasta. The calamari got soggy taking it to-go but that is understandable. Everything else was spot on and packaging was great.  COVID-19: We ordered our food online for curbside pick up which was a great experience! Make sure to indicate whether or not you want utensils and/or complimentary bread. They have plenty of outdoor seating as well. A few booths are quite sectioned off which is nice.

Not sure if they are understaffed or still figuring it out but the service was terrible. Sat there for 30 minutes waiting for our server and nothing. Went to tell the hostess then suddenly the same server we saw assisting everyone in our section (bread, drinks and full entrees) came over and asked us if we had been helped? Hello who would have helped us in your section? Anyway the service was terrible overall -- slow, no refills, forgotten orders, like I said initially not sure if they are understaffed or still figuring it out but in the service industry you should know your role especially in a venue that can make it and keeps failing -- you need all the customers you can get. First and last time, I'm not going back.

wow! what a difference from a year ago! new head brewer totally knows what he's doing and the beers are excellent and well crafted. a new chef and a new menu make this a place to revisit, delicious! lots of happy hour & daily specials, i was there on a Monday and got a burger & brew for $10, a great deal. I enjoyed chatting with the bartender who was very knowledgeable about beer styles best brew company in SB!

mesa verde is an absolutely lovely gem of a place, tucked away far from the hustle of downtown santa barbara. it's also super dog friendly, so our pooches joined us on the patio for our supper!  i ordered the Picadillo Empanadas (butternut nacho cheese. cacao black beans. chimichurri. harissa. pickled savoy cabbage and carrots. V GF). i would give these 3 stars. i was super curious about the butternut squash nacho cheese, but honestly, there was so little of it in the center of the empanada that there was nothing really to try.  the pickled cabbage was great, and i really liked the chimi churri part too, but the dish overall was just too much empanada crust on top of black beans. i had been hoping for a plumper empanada experience, if you know what i mean. :)  the mister consumed the Moroccan Fez (seven vegetable tagine with traditional seven spices. served over couscous. carmelized onion. fried shallot. harissa. pomegranate. aleppo pepper. cilantro. V 17). this was beyond stellar, an absolute 100% 5 star dish. usually i find moroccan dishes a bit sweet, but this was just packed with fresh, bright flavors. his dish was the total star of the meal.  yes, we were full, but yes, we had dessert, which proved to be another 5 star decision. we tried the Bruleed Panna Cotto (almond cream. candied orange peel. orange segments. black betty. macadamia brittle. maca V GF 14). this was some serious alchemy here. so rich and light and creamy and flavorful!   definitely hit this place up, and definitely bring your furry friends!
Good beer & a fun hip atmosphere. The vegan chili fries are delicious. You could honestly not tell any of the food is vegan and definitely satisfies the California pub food itch. Beers are great!
Best almond croissant ever.  Good thing I don't live in Santa Barbara or I would get fat on these things.  Boys liked the chocolate croissants.  Macaroons were excellent.
Great empanadas from a local business. I personally like the prosciutto and the classic beef empenadas. The chimichuri sauce is great.

Amazing pastries! We happened to be next door at Handlebar getting coffee the day they opened and we picked up a few croissants (almond, chocolate and plain) and they were so good! Very tasteful French patisserie style cafe.
Ordered delivery form here.  Very average.  In fact, maybe not even average.

Came in super last minute, normally they are so rude. My fiancé and I were craving McFlurrys ;) you all know... normally the ice cream machine is broken/out of order.... She was so sweet she made me my shakes anyways and told me about all the bogo deals! She's the kind of gal you want representation your business :) she even got me on another fries and e em a 2 for 6 deal!  Thanks again, Anabelia!

If you like quality, unlimited toppings, with the ability to customize your acai bowl to your liking and intricately layered, this place is for you! (If you are willing to pay the price).   You can choose from a 12, 16, or 24 oz. You get unlimited toppings and they have quite unique toppings like chia pudding and even collagen unlike many other places. Extra toppings like almond butter or matcha powder is however, an additional cost. I definitely appreciate that because it's in a taller cup, it's easier to hold and eat and to compensate, the acai bowl is also layered with the acai base and the toppings.   However, as someone who likes a large portion for my acai bowl, this is really expensive. I completely understand the reasoning behind the pricing, but I personally can't justify the exorbitant cost of a 24 oz bowl for $17 with potential add ons so that is the only thing I would be wary of if you go here. Otherwise, I really enjoyed it!

My sis&bro suggested this spot for a casual weekday breakfast. It's nestled in a corner of a shopping plaza but has ample outdoor seating.  The guy who took our order was friendly and  my breakfast hit the spot.   I enjoyed a custom built breakfast croissant and a Chai latte. There were various baked goodies, omelets, and even lunch offerings available in the morning. I'm sure there is something for everyone at this little spot.

You will be impressed with everything created by this bakery. Pastries. Coffee. Breakfast. Lunch. Desserts. Breads.   Here you'll find the expected with special touches (salted brown butter cinnamon roll) as well as creative offerings (Elvis style banana bread.) Breakfast or lunch, the sandwiches are excellent too. And don't leave without a loaf to-go; we're partial to the sourdough or cherry and pistachio, which has the same sourdough base.   Don't hesitate, just go; open daily 8 to 2.   The Helena Avenue Bakery is reason alone to move to Santa Barbara.

Wow!  Really, really good Italian food.  I want to say it tasted authentic, but I've never been to Italy, so how would I know?  But I'm guessing it tasted authentic.  I had the Farfalle alla Ligure (that's bow-tie pasta with pesto sauce).  Soooo good.  And the service was friendly and personable.  I'm definitely going back!

I wasn't as impressed with this coffee shop as I expected. The staff seemed rather cold and although the shop itself was cute and decorated nicely, it was not very inviting. There were a few coffees brewed to select from, which I always appreciate since I am a fan of dark, rich roasts. The bagel/egg/cheese sandwich was not good at all (the bagel was rock hard and zero flavor for a jalapeno cheese bagel). The bran muffin was huge, but also somewhat dry and lacking in flavor. I would probably only go back if I needed a quick coffee on the run.
Best coffee ever. Locals love it! Beautiful, delicious burrito. Friendly servers. Casual. Outdoor patio with many canine pals.

I can honestly say as a child I always remember enjoying this donut house. Times have definitely changed!
