Tonight we came here at 11pm to satisfy a pregnant craving expecting fresh donuts since it's open 24hrs, there was no selections just a couple of left overs and as I go to ask the employee if there was a batch coming out soon he gestured with an attitude and said '4am' I don't understand how a donut house that is suppose to be open 24hrs doesn't make fresh batches through out the night. Telling all our friends to stop purchasing from this place.

Dinner here last night for the first time really blew me out of the water. Every single thing we ordered was delicious, and the service was impeccable! I can honestly say that last nights dinner was one of the most well rounded and memorable dinners I've had in a long time, and I eat out pretty often in LA, SD, and SB. Brett, our server, was a delight and incredibly helpful with his recommendations and very knowledgeable about the menu. We really enjoyed his company and service! We could not have had as amazing of an experience here last night if it wasn't for him.  We started our meal with the sunchoke shmear appetizer which had the best smoked salmon I've ever had topping it, and I don't even like smoked salmon usually. Our table ordered the pork chops, sea scallops, buttermilk fried chicken and the duck, all of which were phenomenal in their own ways. Here's a little on each dish:  The buttermilk fried chicken was the crispiest fried chicken I've ever had, and it was so juicy and well seasoned, with the bonus of being a huge portion! Lots to share.   The pork chop was amaaaaazing, juicy, tender, and with all the flavors complementing each other perfectly. This one was a table favorite.   Next were the sea scallops, big, cooked perfectly, and served with ruby red grapefruit which paired surprisingly very well with the scallop! Yum yum, I would order this again.   And last but not least, was the duck, which is rendered to tender, flavorful pieces that I honestly wish I had gotten more of.   To top off the meal, we all shared the fire roasted banana milkshake which if you get nothing else at this restaurant, GET THIS. Your life will be complete and you will have sweet dreams for the rest of your life with how good this is. Seriously, you get gooey toasted marshmallow, crunchy graham cracker clusters, and banana milkshake. I'm drooling just thinking of it again.   Finch and fork is seriously underrated, and I think their food alone (although service is phenomenal too) is leagues above popular places in SB like The Lark or Bouchon. Can't wait to be back!

Finally made it over to Sama Sama, and we weren't disappointed. Food was absolutely delicious albeit a bit pricey; I think you get what you pay for. Here, you get great seasonal ingredients and tons of flavor. You also get something that's not a repeat of every other restaurant in town. Ordered the fried rice, the noodles, the duck, and the scallops and green beans. Not a single criticism or disappointment among them.   Lots of tantalizing drinks on the menu, and my Manhattan was outstanding. I asked the waitress for a suggestion among 3 cocktails. She recommended the Ojai Rancho over the Manhattan or the Sazerac. I didn't get the feeling that she was really into cocktails, so I told her 'bartender's choice'. He made the Manhattan, and then delivered it with the explanation that it was his dad's drink and he'd grown up making it. You could tell, because it was spot on perfect. My husband had the blue-ginger margarita and was underwhelmed. Makes me sympathetic to another reviewer who thought the drinks were watered down -- it just depends on your palette and what you are ordering.   Our waitress was helpful and explained the whole 'food will come out when it's ready-this isn't your traditional kind of restaurant where each person orders their entree' thing, but avoided eye contact after taking our order -- not great if you need something  (which we did). Wound up moving our table to the outdoor patio (before food was coming up), because it was nice and cool on a very warm afternoon. Our waiter on the back patio was very attentive and friendly, making sure we had everything and checking in at appropriate intervals without being intrusive. Decor is nice and patio is a lovely bonus. Seating inside or out would depend on weather; both options have a place in my book. We will definitely be back.   In summary: -not cheap but worth it for quality and flavor -food sharing experience for the table -ask the bartender for drink recommendations and be specific about what you really want -service was overall pretty good but definitely better with our second waiter. I would give it a 4.5, while my husband said 3.5-4. So I settled on a 4-star to reflect experience for both of us.

Love the sandwiches and love the salad even more especially the Fowl Play salad, serving are huge which is great for college students as it will last at least two meal for price of one. If you go early like 11:30 am for lunch, there is no line. Very friendly staff and very good food, I would suggest a branch in San Jose or Berkeley.
I emailed David B after receiving his response and disappointed once again, he never responded to my email. It's unfortunate that this place with so much potential has such poor customer service.

+1 (949) 310-7016 is Lauryns Cel number by the way! Good luck getting your refund!   Jeff was the best part of that place . I guess I should've listened to the other 1 star helpers when they said it was hard to get into contact with anyone at the spa, but of course I had Lauryns personal number and she always used to get back to me, until shit started to hit the fan with her spa. I did a 5 star review for Lauryn because she asked me to, because she was going through legal shit with the spa and and her ex partner. Lauryn took $225 for a package of 3 massages and didn't refund me when I asked about it. She said she would have her travel table and travel to me and give me my massages but when I text her, she always has an excuse. Hopefully she's using our money for something good.

I have always have an amazing time here! The servers are always so incredibly sweet and conscientious. When my parents came to visit they demanded we have dinner here every night because the food is so good!   I have enjoyed coming here with friends to pick up or have a quick dessert, and also have made reservations for their high tea. I would definitely recommend the high tea, everyone in the room was amazed by how beautifully presented it was and I can't wait to do it again someday.

Food & service is impeccable! It's rare to find a restaurant these days that can deliver both. We love it here so much that our friends and I make it point to come at least once a week! I love that my friends can enjoy the entire menu and I, as a vegan, have many options as well! Can't go wrong with the Edamame, shishito peppers, (my partner loves the garlic noodles), and I am convinced they have the best espresso martini in town! Do yourself a favor and make a reservation.

What a breath of fresh air! Something different and mouth watering! For someone who is comepletely bored with the 'usual' spots and flavors I'm utterly thrilled to have chose this spot today!!! My favorite dish the stuffed grape leaves! Come and taste for yourself! I dare you!

Prime rib ravioli, DO IT. Lingini vongole was good but probably  wouldn't order again. If for no other reason as to explore other options. Service was excellent as is the interior space. Great local owners.  Super cool interior.

I've only been to Casa Blanca twice so far for Happy Hour so I cannot say much about the quality of food..  But, the Happy Hour Standard margarita for $5 seems like a good deal and tastes great and doesn't skimp on the tequila.    The view from the outside patio is also a great way to sit back and watch the tourist cruise on by and get some sun.  The chips and salsa were a bit of  a disappointment though.  The two times I went the chips tasted old, stale or just off and the salsa is average at best.  I think they're still refining their service because both times I was there I had the feeling they forgot about us...

Yum! This place rocks! Good hotdogs, tacos, taters and onion rings! I tried them all and enjoyed all my bites!  Good prices too!    Limited seating inside/out. Large parking lot out front.   Didn't see public restrooms?

Do not order takeout food from here. While the food is okay the service was horrible. Ordered a to go order because we were in a hurry. Arrived 15 minutes later and paid for our food.  20 minutes later i find out the food has been ready the whole time!!! Unbelievable. I had to chase them down and ask evem though i was across from the register the whole time i waited.  Oh and if you get a sandwhich it will be soggy from sitting in the side salad dressing. Poor execution poor service.

food is good and kind service but food to price ratio is kind of insane. specialty rolls are expensive and half the size of other places. think nobu sizes and portions..... but definitely not nobu ambiance. if u feel like dropping $200 for 2 people without alcohol this is your spot. not sure why this place is so popular.

As a kid, my family went to our local Sambo's fairly often. I loved it since my did would always tell me the story of Sambo and the tigers. It was like story time and breakfast all in one! So when I had a chance to go to the original (and only remaining?) Sambo's I was pretty excited.  It's a good breakfast spot. Love the mini muffins you get before you even order. Service was good and our breakfasts were tasty. The restaurant has a modern look and always seems to be very busy. I may not have gotten the nostalgic vibe that I had been hoping for - but I was definitely fueled for another day of sightseeing in beautiful Santa Barbara.
Love it love it!!! Food so authentic! Tom yum koong! Soooo Good!!! I just can't stop. Mango sticky rice. Ummm yum!!! I totally be back soon for more. :)

Atmosphere: Indian Decor  Service: Service was average, nothing special  Food: Chicken Tikka Masala, Plain Basmati Rice, and Plain Naan. Nothing special. FYI Spicy is pretty spicy almost on the boarder of inedible.   Cost: Portions and quality seem to match the price  Overall: Would go back if in the area but definitely had better indian food.

WHERE'S THE BEEF???This place has always been a favorite of mine, but lately the meat serving size in their food has been nearly nothing.  It's been exactly the same weak size for a few months now and I keep hoping it's been a fluke.  I can see now that it's not.  When you charge someone $14.95 for Bangkok street noodle you better put meat in it!  That's not exactly cheap and I'm not paying extra for you to make it satisfying.  For that price you better make it filling for us carnivores or you're going to lose our business.  Double the meat, please!

The best item is not even on their menu.  Toasted cheese bread.  If you ever come to this restaurant just eat the free bread and water.   I ordered the 'better than your mama's' You call that Lasagna?  Girlfriend order a Chicken piccatta, it arrived cold. When we told the waitress, she looked at us as if we wanted a free meal. How rude, we just wanted a hot meal. Never going back, i'll just take the bread.

Every time we visit Santa Barbara we order Rusty's!! The drivers are great, they deliver to our hotel room.  Located right on State Street the pizza always arrives piping hot. This pizza is our favorite, wish we had one in Vegas!! The thin, edgeless crust with extra cheese and toppings, yum!  Pizza Heaven.  Perfect after drinking wine all day.
If you're thinking about stopping here to grab a quick bite because it's a sandwich shop, think again. We waited almost 20 min for two sandwiches. Both delicious! Lemonade was great too.

Beautiful restaurant. If I could give 3 & 1/2 stars I would, but since I'm generous with reviews I'll round up instead of down. The service was impeccable, view was amazing but food left much to be desired.   What we ordered: Pasta experience  Seafood platter Uni butter pasta  Scallops Jidori chicken   The only dish we thoroughly enjoyed was the scallops. Everything else was so lackluster. The jidori chicken was quite confusing. It looked like we got some boiled chicken on a plate. Lacked seasoning completely, and wasn't what I was used to having with this dish. All of the pastas were also quite unseasoned. If I came back, it wouldn't be for the food. Thank you for the amazing service though! Everyone was so helpful and accommodating!

Definitely a must try in sb. Great food and a giant beer selection. I tried their new chipotle chicken sandwich and also had a bite of the fish tacos. Fiiiire.  Only thing is the atmosphere is typically pretty loud on the weekends though so you have to talk pretty loud for anyone to hear anything.

Ate there last night with 7 people. Service was beyond terrible. It took  20 minutes to get water and 10 more to get menus. Waiter did not come back to take our order until we got up and flagged him. Food then came out piecemeal. I was finished with my meal at the time two others at our table got their meals.  Dinner took 2 hours but it became big joke for us as the waiter was just oblivious. No apology or explanation. The food was good but not worth it.

We've recently revisited, and the service was exceptional!  We actually got to meet the manager, he was friendly & accommodating! The burgers were tasty & as always go great with beer. We took our family here while visiting, and they loved it too.   For a full experience, we recommend going to McConnells after for dessert. Burgers, beer, ice cream

Very good food, at eminently reasonable prices. I'm adding this review only to commend our waiter (Dillon) who noticed that their system had added a gratuity (I had no problem with that - we were a large party and he told us that the tip had been added), but that the system added another 20% to the amount of the original gratuity. (Not the whole bill - we would have noticed that - but another 20% of the first 20% or, for the math-challenged, a total tip of 24%.) So, why not 5 stars? Because I called to make a reservation (didn't want to use Open Table because the restaurant pays a fee for that), and left a voicemail asking for 6:30. We get a return voicemail, confirming our reservation at 6:00. I call back, get a live person this time who says that she'll change it to 6:30. The night of the reservation, we call to ask if it's OK if our friends bring their (small) dog, since we're eating outside. They tell me that they don't have a reservation for us. I mean, really??? You're running a restaurant (in the case of Arnoldis, for decades now) and you can't figure out how to run a reservation book? They did find us a table, so all's well that ends well, but my advice: Use Open Table if you want to make sure that they're going to have a place for you.

This place was the best Mexican food so far in Santa barbra.  Not the best I've had but best in SB.  The meat had good flavor. Nothing is simple about this place. It's a huge plate full of food.  It was good but it was a lot of food. It should be for how much you're paying.  I also wish they would have more selections with out all the sea food. There was only a few things I could get cause I don't eat sea food.  Try this place and see how you like it

Was recommended by a friend to try out this place when visiting santa barbara. The portion were okay size. I finished it and was full for 2 hours. Pretty small place, but they have another area to eat at about 2 building to the side.   I got the breakfast with sausage, egg and blueberry pancake. It was decently good. I would definitely come back and try other food they have when im back in santa barbara.

Decent place for breakfast. Hubby and I were lucky to beat the Saturday brunch rush and get seated right away. We shared the crab Benedict and the Kahlua Bananas french toast. Both were pretty standard fare. The challah bread in the french toast was a little soggy, which tends to happen with soft bread like that, but I think it also needed a few more minutes on the grill. The potatoes served with the benedict could have also used some more time on the grill. Although they were nicely seasoned, they were positively anemic looking. Hubby's comment upon trying the french toast was 'Your bananas foster french toast is better than this.' Awww, thanks honey, but you want the food you go out and pay for to taste better than the stuff you make at home. I can tell this place is a local favorite, but I think I will keep looking for a slightly better breakfast spot.
Best hand-made tacos I've ever had: made from scratch with fresh ingredients and authentic sauces. My favorites are Norteño (flank steak) and La Corriente (grilled shrimp).

My Wife Julie and I had a wonderful dinner last night at Jane. The setting was very nice, we were given a nice, quiet table upstairs, the service was excellent (Sierra was great) and the food absolutely delicious. Julie had the Filet Mignon and I had the Petrale Sole. The whole experience was very nice and we look forward to going back!

Another great visit!  Dined here today with 5 others for my birthday.  This was my first visit back since the pandemic.  Indoor dining was closed but outdoor dining was thriving.  Social distancing was in place and employees were masked.    The menu was as extensive as it was pre-pandemic.  This time were ordered 3 Artichoke Crab Cakes, 2 Mushroom Tostadas, Brussel Sprouts, 3 Impossible Burgers, a bowl of Butternut Squash Soup, Spinach and Artichoke Flatbread, Ravioli, 2 cabs and a Billy Billy.    As usual, everything came out quickly and was amazing!  We ordered our favorites so we knew what to expect.  The only new items we ordered was the ravioli and the flatbread.  The flatbread was my favorite dish. The ravioli was really good but filling.   Service was up to par and outstanding.  Our server, Lyle S., was friendly, helpful and attentive.  Oliver's is a solid, consistently good destination restaurant.

We came during covid and there was indoor and outdoor seating. There were a lot of pasta options to choose from. My friends got the shrimp scampi and it looked like it was just sitting in a pool of butter - not very appealing and it didn't seem like they enjoyed it very much. I got the chicken parm with fettucine alfredo. The chicken parm was good and crispy. The pasta was decent and didn't seem special to me, it just felt very heavy because of the creamy sauceThe meal came with garlic bread which was pretty good. The portion was pretty big in my opinion and the food is very filling. The service was good and the staff were really friendly as well. I think the food was mediocre but would be good if you want large portions if food - it was too heavy for me.
The endive salad might be the best salad I've eaten in SB... and the lamb lollipops are delicious.

I can't even find the right words for this place. Officially found our new favorite restaurant in SB! The food is to die for, the service (Victoria) is hands down the best you could ask for, and the whole vibe is welcoming. We had the numero due meal, the spicy chicken Alfredo, and the chicken Piccata. We can't pick a favorite. Try everything. Oh & the cheese garlic bread with the marinara! Omg the marinara in itself will making you drool!!! The portions are huge and the prices are very fair!! We are taking a ton of food home and still can't wait to come back!! Thank you Petrinis and Victoria!!!

We went to Live oak Cafe on a friend's recommendation. We loved the healthy choices, really positive and helpful staff, and loved their soups! Next time will try one of their Grain Bowls. Nice semi-outdoor patio and indoors, beautiful local art on the walls. Will go again for sure.

Cute date spot. Clean and quaint. Tables fill up fast! Try to make reservations beforehand, especially if you have a large group!  When we entered, there was no one to greet us at the door so we stood there waiting for the hostess for about 5 minutes which is a definite no-no in my book of restaurant etiquette! Hence, the 3 star (and not 4 star review). Service the whole time was also just mediocre and on the cold side. Hmph.  However, the food made up for a lot of the blah service!  FIRESTONE RED RINGS! This has got to be the BEST onion rings I have ever had in my life! SO GOOD! Even without the spicy herb cream dipping sauce, it's good just on its own. What's their secret? I have no clue. But trust me on this one and get this! Definitely not your average onion rings!  HALF PAPAYA & DUNGENESS CRAB SALAD BOWL - I enjoyed this salad bowl but it was a littleeeee bit too sweet for me with the papaya and the honey lime dressing. The toasted almonds were a nice touch though! I also expected the dungeness crab to be in bigger pieces, but it was shredded up kinda like California Roll imitation crab style. :( Boo. Not what I expected. Overall, the salad was fresh and yummy but I wouldn't get it again.

If you're looking for something that's fresh and different this place is for you. They have vegetarian options that are delicious. However, I am vegan and I will tell you the staff there was amazing! They helped me figure out what I can and can't eat. They opened up their ingredients book for everything and they have a menu behind their counter if you tell them you're vegan that says everything that can be substituted to make anything vegan. The staff there really went above and beyond to help me and I really appreciated it. It's on state st so there isn't parking in front just like anything else on state st and the bathroom was clean.
Friendly staff, fresh ingredients, charming vibe. I got the pad Thai and loved it, wasn't oily but was full of flavor. My daughter got the gyoza (potstickers) and LOVED them. We will be back!

Even though Corazon was our intended destination, we did a quick lap around the Santa Barbara Public Market to see what other options were around the eatery. Not only did Corazon have the longest line, they practically had the only line. We took it as a good sign and patiently stood in the queue.  The woman who took our order was super helpful letting me know what items are vegan or can be veganized. Here's what vegan items we got:  - Cauliflower taco http://bit.ly/2GD5qy9 - Butternut Squash taco http://bit.ly/2J5HHFw - Fried Plantains & Black Beans - Guacamole http://bit.ly/2GT1042 - Horchata http://bit.ly/2GgHcuh  Aesthetically, I absolutely adored the colors and presentation of the tacos. There's something about watermelon radish that makes anything look so pretty!  Flavor-wise, I totally love the flavor combinations. The Cauliflower taco was not just a cauliflower taco but tempura-battered cauliflower with almonds, Medjool dates, sunflower sprouts, and salsa verde. The sweetness of the dates and crunch of the almonds were a nice texture.  Just thinking about the Butternut Squash taco has me drooling! The roasted squash had this beautiful caramelization. Avocado makes any dish awesome and I loved the pops of color, and tanginess, from the pomegranate seeds. And if this wasn't already enough color on the plate, this features gluten-free blue corn tortillas. Yum!  The Fried Plantains & Black Beans didn't really do it for me but the Guacamole and Horchata is legit good!  Totally worth another visit!
Excellent strong drinks & tasty food. Not pretentious. Reasonably priced. What more can you ask for?

I love this place! Its delicious and the owner (im assuming) is so funny and sweet. He had a ball showing us photos of his native Greece and was just so nice. The hummus sampler is amazing. The lamb kebabs are amazing. Try the watermelon and honey salad. It's exquisite. Yay for Petros

The best Thai BBQ chicken I have ever had.  One appetizer is a whole meal in and of itself.  Tender, well seasoned, and cooked perfectly every time.  Also enjoyu the Tom Kah Gai soup (one of their specialties).  I've yet to order something here that I didn't enjoy.

I got a message from the management at Eureka asking me to give em another shot.  Super polite, super professional. I finally got a chance to head back up, and it was great! The blue cheese/mushroom burger was unreal.  Total 180 from last time.  My issue last time was the rare burger and the lack of good service.  Neither were an issue.  In fact my burger was a PERFECT medium, and our server was great. Habit used to be my go to burger in SB, but I think I'm making a move. Always good to see solid management taking care of business.

Favorite Mexican restaurant is Santa Barbara. They have such a nice outdoor patio (with heaters) and life music on the weekends! The service is top notch as well.  Let's talk about the chips and salsa. I think my table went through 6 buckets, they are amazing. The bean/corn salsa is my favorite salsa in the world. You really can't order wrong here, get the guac - it's super good. I love the combination plates,they had GREAT tacos and enchiladas. I love their chicken salad, sounds boring but it has amazing jalapeño dressing. Their burritos are great too. Honestly, you can't go wrong!   Get their mango margarita. Just do it.  Overall one of the best restaurants in Santa Barbara and it's right on State Street. I would recommend to anyone visiting the area and of course locals who have never tried!

Freshest coffee in Santa Barbara. They literally a roast it in front of you. Must try their homemade Yerba  matte, its my favorite. Staff is always nice and they have the best pastrys. Mom best coffee in sb.
Great place for groups. Get the big drink! It's 20 dollars and you can get it in a cosmopolitan or magarita good for sharing for up to 5 people. Great deal!!

We came back twice in a span of a month. This time around we were a little disappointed with their lobster bisque that had way too much cream. We even asked if they made any changes and the server whether real or not said they did.   All one has to do is take a look at the color of the bisque versus my past pictures. It's lobster bisque not cream of bisque.

I went in for lunch today and ordered their classic cheeseburger off their lunch menu. Comes with a HUGE basket of fries that were cooked perfectly! For under $10, they've got a winning combo here. Friendly service, and the a/c was at just the right temp.  The only downside was their free WiFi - it's slower than USPS. Seriously! Tim, the manager on duty said they're working on it, so that shouldn't be a deterrent. Starbucks next door has great WiFi, so it's probably just a faulty router. No biggie!  5 stars for a great lunchtime experience at a very reasonable price.

A very unique cafe experience, like a cocktail bar for coffee and tea drinks!  Yes the drinks are on the pricier side but you're paying for the complexity and aesthetics!  ---bella rose: I don't usually care for matcha but this drink was delicious and beautiful ---burnout: a great coffee based drink, perfect balance of sweetness that doesn't over power   Will be making this a regular visit when I return to SB!

One of my favorite places to have a nice dinner in SB. The service and vibe is great. This is also where you'll have the best Mai tai and salad bar in town. Their salad bar is so impressive that I've contemplated coming just for that sometimes. Their pickled cabbage, home made croutons and pumpernickle bread are to die for. I always end up with a generous entree meal and take home left overs. I've had the steaks, scallops and trout and they have never disappointed. Highly recommend.

Came back here after moving away last year. I went for a quick lunch so I opted for the lunch buffet since it seemed pretty popular. unfortunately the food was total crap. The only saving grace was the naan. I don't know if it's because they have caught on that a bunch of old retirees pile in at noon for a 8 dollar lunch and proceed to load up their plates multiple times with the crap they're serving up... Probably. It was pretty depressing and I feel for the staff having to deal with those customers and having to serve that embarrassing food.

It seems that certain people fetishize the 'authentic' and that causes them to taste something that really isn't there. It's all good stuff but definitely does not live up to the hype.  There are four or five places on the same street that turn out the same quality but without the long line.

We had a great lunch at Joe's! I haven't been in so long and when the kids asked to go there for New Year's lunch it took me a second. It's a place I always went when people visit town or to get a stiff drink. If it been so long but I figured why not. I was immediately drawn to the Cuban sandwich. And totally happy with that choice. It was delicious! The kids cheeseburger was huge yet she didn't have a problem making that go away. And the cheese ravioli totally satisfied my vegetarian child. And our server, Jana, was so nice and helpful!! The outdoor seating with heaters were perfect for us. So it was all around a positive experience! Thanks!

Let me start by saying that I can't wait to come back to Jeannine's restaurant! This small little neighborhood bakery/restaurant is simply amazing all the way around.  As you make your way in there is a super chipper and welcoming hostess at the door to hand you the menu and ask you where you would like to be seated. She informs you that you'll be ordering at the register and that your table will be ready for you when your done ordering.  When I came in I had asked if it was okay for me to bring my dog to sit out on the patio with me while I have my breakfast and she said yes. So without a doubt now you've been informed that this place is dog friendly.  I was happy to find out that my pooch could sit with me instead of in a hot car. After ordering I tied my pooch to the table and within less than 5 minutes my  healthy breakfast came out.  I ordered an egg white veggie scramble with sliced tomatoes and their homemade butter biscuit, which was simply delicious. Some of the dishes on the menu like the egg white scramble are a tad pricy, but worth if your having to watch what your eating.  I'm super glad that I found this place on Yelp and I highly recommend it to anyone who's looking for a breakfast place in this particular area. Jeannine's is by far my favorite breakfast place in Santa Barbara and I'm sure if your give it a try you'll love it too!  Keep up the good work Jeannine's!!!!

It was a really Yummy Thanksgiving Day meal with my parents!! The chicken and corn soup was so fresh, the chicken and peanuts was really good. Plenty to take home for dinner! Thank you Madame Lu! You are funny and hard working! Open 7 days a week!  Sally, Ron & David
I really enjoy Thai food and this small place is one of my favorite. I really enjoy the vergetarian options such as spicy vegetable soup. i would recommend this place.

Location. Location. Location. Great menu. Good food. Smack dab on the beach. Watch people with their dogs on the dog friendly beach. Fun waves. Good beach for a long stroll.   The wait was 1- 1 1/2 hours today. Go to bar and get a drink.   Service was excellent.

We came up to Santa Barbara to celebrate New Years 2022. This was our last meal before heading back to reality/home. What  a cool little sports bar that wasn't even crowded. I bet everyone else was at crowded breakfast/brunch locations that had a wait. Don't sleep on going to a sports bar for a meal and fun.  They have TVs galore featuring all of your favorite teams, Food, and an excellent selection of drinks. It has a sugar skull theme and I simply adore sugar skulls. They serve Mexican food and other type of American food. Plenty of choices for everyone. I had shrimp tacos with my favorite 'black beans'. My hubby had boneless chicken wings and a carne asada burrito. Our friends had tacos and nachos. Overall all the food was good but the chicken nachos were so bomb especially the presentation. All in all we had a great time and once again I totally love the sugar skull decor/theme. I'm not even a fan of sports but I enjoyed myself immensely. I also saw families with young children and everyone was chill so I do recommend it for families. Feel free to come here and chill and dress up in your favorite team attire. So long till next time

I seriously wish I could give this place a 4.5 but since I can't, they will get 4 stars. Their food is delicious and tastes fresh. I tend to order the gyro pita and have never been disappointed. I will say that I ask for extra tzatziki sauce because it can be a bit dry for my taste. The salad is nothing special but a nice side. The fries are a solid choice as well. I appreciate the indoor and outdoor seating and parking is plentiful. The reason for not giving five stars is the wait. I really feel like they need another cook in the back. If you order in-person, be aware that you will be waiting for some time. I now order online and they are usually pretty accurate with pickup time. Otherwise, great food!
Covid was tough in businesses  soo glad this is are looking up. Thst most survived  thank you.

Why did it take me so long to finally come to Flor De Maiz? This is a great place to meet friends, bring family and for date nights!    I love that the location is near the water and has both beautiful indoor and outdoor seating! They have an extensive drink menu with SO MANY margarita choices and signature cocktails.  We started off with the Margarita Sauylita which was a tasty pineapple mango margarita and it came it with a cute pineapple glass and garnish. 10/10! My boyfriend got the Jamaica which was also good! I would love to go back and try more of their drinks because there are so many great options!  As we waited, they brought out chips and salsa. Now the green salsa served here was by FAR the greatest salsa I have ever had in my entire life. It was so creamy...I just need a bottle of that to go.   As for food, I had the Mole Negro con Pollo which the waiter said was one the customers favorites and it did not disappoint!!! The chicken and the rice was amazing, the mole was rich and creamy and the plantains were PERFECTLY ripe and sweet. I loved this dish so much.  The service was amazing as well, shout-out to Adrian! I need to come back here on my next visit to Santa Barbara!

Where do I begin? 1. The hotel was under construction and amenities were not available (pool, hot tub, restaurant, bar). VERY UPSET - I would not have booked this hotel had I known it was under construction!! This should have been disclosed at time of booking!!! 2. I asked for an upper-level room and got a room on the lower level, NEXT TO THE INTERIM LOBBY. 3. The bed was TERRIBLE - squeaky and terribly uncomfortable. 4. The pack and play brought to us by the hotel staff was incredibly dirty and gross. 5. We decided to have a snack at the 'interim restaurant'. I ordered Mac and cheese for my 18 month-old and a couple things for my husband and I. The mac and cheese sauce was NACHO CHEESE SAUCE. WTF TERRIBLE EXPERIENCE and NOT worth what I paid at all. Thankfully, we only had to endure 2 nights. Why did I not bring this up at the time of my visit? I was way too upset and did not want to be rude to someone who had no control over my experience.

First off, this place is way better than the place before it. I can see myself coming here regularly in the future. Beer was surprisingly very good and reasonably priced. The ambiance is a little strange but in no offensive way. It's warm, they've got games and what not, but when I came (close to opening), the place was packed.   Service was a little disorganized but that's to be expected of a new restaurant. The seating is a bit of a mess on the long table which only compounds the service issue, but I'm not too concerned. Staff was friendly, and I could see no problems when we were actually ordering and what not.  Now for the big thing: Food and drinks. The whole menu is very creative, and frankly I'm very thankful to have a bolder restaurant on State Street. Almost every restaurant on this street does the same old same old style of American. Even the nicer ones, while they taste great, are culinarily dull. The menu items and cocktails at Cruisery are just different enough to be refreshing.   My only problem was that when you finally tasted it, it either was a bit under seasoned, lacked depth, or just needed a little bit of a finishing touch conceptually. For example, I got the steak flatbread, and while the steak was great, bread was crisp, balsamic glaze great, but the amount of ingredients felt rather small, and combined with the lack of sufficient binding (like cheese), it just rolled around more or less. I felt like I was eating steak, and then bread, but never a steak flatbread. I also ordered a seared ahi tuna burger which came with pineapple in it. I loved the idea when reading all the ingredients, but when I tasted it I felt that the flavors dropped off quickly.   I think the guys and girls running this place have a great head on their shoulders food and business wise, but it needs a little time to work out the kinks.
I went to this restaurant for the first time yesterday. My girlfriend and I split salmon dinner plate and it was very tasty. I just wish that they had charcuterie in their appetizer menus.

I initially avoided this place for a long time because of poor reviews about the management and staff. I finally ate here and have to be honest....the food is very good. Not the best pad Thai I have ever had, but the yellow curry is legit. And I don't like curry!!! The peanut sauce is outstanding and the food is not terribly overpriced. Also....very clean.

We always have a good time when we dine here. The atmosphere is lively and the food is done right..there's something for everyone. Also note, portions can be larger than average.  I love the veggies as a side always.
A great place for some eclectic food- definitely an 'older' crowd, not young and hip by any means, but  gourmet and unexpected merge of different tastes, flavors and textures.... even influences!
Fun place.  Friendly staff. Food was grEat.  Parking is thE only challenge.   We will return.
I got the Fast Burger and it was hands down best burger I've had in Santa Barbara. The meat is perfectly seasoned and the buttered bun is delicious. The fries were really good too.

I love the atmosphere. It's great all year long, but coming here has been a holiday tradition in my family for decades. They have simple, affordable but very tasty comfort food. Some of the staff have worked here longer than 20 years. This place just gives you a happy vibe. The drinks are quite strong, so only have one or get a DD. Cheers! Stay safe everyone. Happy holidays

We had a late lunch here.  The food was incredible.  I had the pork schnitzel and my partner had the chicken schnitzel sandwich.  The quality of the food was superb.  It was so good that we ordered dessert, which is rare for us.  These were also fantastic.  The service was very good and attentive.

We had nothing but a fabulous time here at the sandbar.  This place just seems like it is packed all the time- it was a good thing we made a reservation!  Our server, Kristen, was so cute and just so....nice that husband and I adored her immediately.  She kept the margaritas coming, and was just a great server all around.  She let us know when happy hour was ending (I.e. Shall I bring over more margaritas before it ends?) umm yes.  Onto the food: we thought it was pretty darn good! The guac tasted really fresh, and my husband inhaled his fish tacos.  My veggie fajitas were really good, but the portions are huge! The whole time we were here, we felt constantly looked after.  The place was packed, and the manager checked in with us no less than 3 times to make sure everything was cool.  We really enjoyed our time here at the sandbar!  Why not 5 stars, you ask?  Not very many vegetarian options :( other than that, a great place!!!

Went here on a week day evening with two others. I had the Uni with fish roe spoon, Uni and scallop sahimi, yellow tail sushi, and the wagyu beef. We also split an order of edamame and had a small bottle of the house sake. Overall, everything was excellent. The Uni was delicious. But the bill can get expensive. The wagyu alone was $60 for a single portion. But it was very tasty. The ambiance was busy but not overly noisy. Not sure what a weekend evening would be like. It could become a popular, trendy restaurant. It probably shouldn't be considered a 'budget' family style restaurant. If you're concerned about finances you may want to check out the prices first.  There were a couple of hiccups with the service but nothing major. It is a new restaurant and a few kinks are too be expected. It was an enjoyable experience. I would definitely eat here again and recommend it.

Fantastic!! Reservations are usually a must, but we hit the opening and sat at the community table. We started w/ the Charcuterie board, excellent. Followed w/ the Duck Liver Mousse... sinful. The last course was the Texas Redfish platter, the freshest fish prepared in a mild spicy Creole fashion, we are discriminating fish people, and this more than met our expectations. All this followed by some amazing desserts, never thought we would be able to eat the desserts, but duty called. Our server Louie was very helpful, knowledgeable, and attentive, without hovering. Lark is the king of the Funkzone

All male staff the day I picked up a doordash order and they were not on time, even served 3 customers before beginning the doordash order. Not sure if these young guys are keeping up with these orders. Expect your door dash order to arrive late.

I love this place, and I think the staff is amazing, and the food is some of the best Mexican cuisine here in Santa Barbara. Daniel is my favorite server, and made sure our work group was taken care of when we came in 2 weeks ago. I have absolutely nothing but love for Ryan behind the bar! The place is small, but always at capacity. The location alone lends to the crowd, so I think people should be mindful of that before complaining about the service. These guys work super hard, and I will definitely return.
I enjoy the veggie ramen at this place.

Ohhhhhhh I could eat from this place every single day. Get the long tail boat noodle! Super fresh sure it's a bit pricey (not really, you are in downtown Santa Barbara, this is actually cheap... but relative to like a sandwich shop its pricy) it's totally worth ever penny!  Sit at the bar, it's fun to see one of the guys chop off some veggies as you eat!

Good food. I'm not a big fan of sweet cornbeef and theirs is on the sweet side but I still enjoyed the make your own hash breakfast and that you could go Paleo. Good coffee, fresh OJ, made a table for us since it was packed. All in all nice people, good food, great spot on state but service was slow. They seemed a little overwhelmed even though they had sufficient staff. Also side note, the bathrooms were clean (huge plus to me) you can have 6 star food but a gross bathroom kills it. And they had old skateboard deck pieces as tile work on the bathroom walls. I want it in my bathroom ha! cool as shizy

We learned about this amazing GF bakery and had to check it out. The pastries, cakes and pies looked amazing. I had a hard time believing that the stuff was GF because everything was gorgeous and delicious. The atmosphere was welcoming and fresh. I was able to see the salads and quiche as they were being delivered to others and I was sorry that I had already eaten lunch. I am looking forward to going back for a meal.

So terrible... the hot pot place that was here before was SO much better.  They false advertise 'organic free range chicken' when it is rubbery cheap probably presliced frozen 'chicken'.... and you pay the price too.  Bring back the old menu my god.

BRUNCH at Loquita was amazing! We had the patatas and the Serrano jamon Benedict! The gazpacho Bloody Mary was soooo good! Stoked they will be open every weekend! Oh and patio is on point! Love love love it all

This place had good food but not amazing Italian pasta. We went to this place because they were known for Italian and I would say the food was good and I would go back if I wanted a very fancy sit down restaurant but it was not the best Italian. I had the penna pasta with a chipotle cream and it tasted like Chili and not a pasta.

I found this on Yelp.  I am from out of town, and I'm so happy we decided to visit this place on vacation.  The inside of this joint is so cute and kinda farm/industrial/chic.  The staff and patrons are so nice!  Maybe it's just SB people.  I love you, SB locals!  My favorite food was the pork belly bao.  Yummy!  My kid actually liked the salmon one too.  Before we even sat down, my friends were drinking their beer before noon.  Great ambiance.  Lots of nice, old ladies there for some reason.  Maybe it was just that day.

A very high class experience at Loquita, and one that I will remember as a high point during my trip to Santa Barbara.  Had a group of 4, and for this restaurant you 100 percent need a reservation, however after my friend went to talk to the hostess, and she told her the wait would be an hour and a half, I went up a bit later to give it another try, and she had us at our own table in a private area within minutes, big win for persistance!!  The mood/ambience is dark and intimate. Gorgeous fire pits and candles light the area. Very mellow mood.  The menu has a great selection and we went in on a few things, including Paellla, Octopus, and another incredible appetizer I don't remember the name of (helPful right?)  Either way the place is off the scales in terms of a sexy memorable dinner, highly reccommend!!

My friends and I went to a funny-looking little turquoise blue taco shack called La Super Rica Taqueria for dinner. Super Rica was the late Chef Julia Child's favorite Mexican restaurant in Santa Barbara. The Super-Rica Especial is the item to get. Roasted chile peppers stuffed with cheese and marinated pork on top of three fresh corn tortillas. The tortillas are made by hand just before they are placed on the grill. It doesn't get much better than that! I also got a pork tamale, but it wasn't as good as the one I got from a little truck called Grandma's Tamales.
Nicely updated from its previous condition. Breakfast all day! Burgers, salads, soup, sandwiches, and pies!

THIS PLACE IS AMAZING. A little pricey but worth every penny. I got enchiladas enchiladas which was absolute perfection. The salad compliments the spice of the chipotle sauce on the enchiladas! Enchiladas were filled with cheese, shrimp, and fish. My boyfriend got the steak burrito and he said it was delicious. We ordered two margs at the restaurant and bought 2 XL margaritas to-go. I am currently drinking the to-go margs at home. So good.  So grateful and happy that this was my first meal out after quarantine! I could not have asked for better service, food, or margaritas. Already excited for my next visit at this restaurant!

Good food and good lunch combos (about $10 each and includes rice, miso soup, and a few other sides).  Originally gave this place 5 stars based on the lunch combo price, but updated after getting their rolls.  If you're looking for rolls, I recommend Goleta Sushi House. Their rolls are about the same price but twice as big.

Best tacos in town! I love this place its so good and the staff is so nice.  I can't say enough good things you can count on the tacos tasting just as great as the first time you tried even if you come back after a year.  The line moves super fast and menu is simple.
We back for another breakfast. Denver omelet outstanding. Still waiting for my toast, 2 days later. Service Wayy better. Yes I will go again when in area.

Deliciousness!! Great atmosphere, superb food and good service. We ordered Halloumi Cheese, Batata Harra and Tzatziki as appetizers. Halloumi should have been fried more; it was a bit hard, cold and tasteless, but presentation was awesome. The Batata Harra coupled with Tzatziki was soooo good, and filling!   We split Mashawi Combo; more than enough for two people. It was full of taste and great presentation as well! The steak was a bit too bloody for me, but the lamb we couldn't get enough of. The entree came with hummus, tabbouli and baba ghannouje. Overall strongly recommend.   Spring fling was not 'cucumbery' at all... had to switch for the Mule... the music was fun! Love the lights and fire. Come with a big group to enjoy the fire pit, small groups don't get seated around the fire anymore   Come visit! You won't be disappointed!!

I just ran in here for some cilantro to make dinner with and not only did they not have cilantro but their vegetables were pretty low across-the-board. I was here at about 4:45 PM and can't imagine what the store's going to look like by 7 PM and no one was working in that section. I couldn't help notice that broccolini for $6.99 for a small package... Trader Joe's sells it for 2.99 the store is a rip off and they gouge their customers ... don't shop here! I buy wild caught grass fed and organic but I won't shop here because they gouge their customers and have been reprimanded for it because as I read in the news! Whole Foods prices are considerably better and I do recommend and buy my meats online through butcher box and other online meat sellers.

Tried this place for the first time. The food was great, tasty, good quality. I especially liked the beet salad, hibiscus tea, and potato tacos. Yummy! The dinner menu was pricey for my budget but I will try this again sometime in the future. The parking was tight in the evening, and some of the heating lamps weren't working outside.

Fresh, delicious bread. I tried the date almond, garlic sage, and a hygge - not all at the same time :-).All were delicious alone, so didn't need to try the spreads, but they looked good. Someday maybe. Anyway - they sell out, so get there early.

I love coming up to Santa Barbara and I always love going to the Sand Bar. The service is great, the setting is great and the food is really good.  The owner must know what he's doing because it truly is a well run restaurant. And there are so many restaurants to choose from. We always go to Sand Bar, every trip up!  Wish we had one closer to Orange County and LA county. Just appreciate the fun people there. Management is great and it shows. We have always had great customer service. Thanks Sand Bar!!
Happy hour goes all week and weekend long. Clam chowder is good, calamari is mad spicy, great drink specials, tacos are o-k. Super fast service, friendly.

First off, I want to say all of the staff were amazing but we loved our server Autumn. She was so sweet and really knew the menu. We ordered everything she recommended.   We got a bottle of wine to start, very crisp and light. Then for appetizers we ordered a dozen oysters which came in fresh from Baja, CA. I love that they provided a large amount of lemon, hot sauce and this amazing house made sauce. I could have eaten 100. One of the best selection of oysters we had. The quail and side salad was phenomenal. Just watch out for the little bones.  For dinner, I got the halibut which was so rich and tender I could barely scoop it up. It was very flavorful. Bae got the filet and potatoes which was cooked to perfection. We got a side of the cauliflower and it was the perfect side.   We opted for the chocolate mousse cake since chocolate is my favorite but to be honest wouldn't recommend. I wish they would expand their dessert menu but overall great night sitting by the water!
Sakana is our home away from home...We are there 1-2 times a week and I must say EVERYTHING is spot on! Great service! Best sushi in town! Friendly and prompt staff! Nobody comes close to this place!

We tried this restaurant today and all we can say is a big wow! We tried the shrimp, fried chicken, mushroom, goyza and the secret bow. I think the mushroom was my favorite, but I tried everything and loved everyone of them. I would highly recommend this restaurant for a flavorful exotic experience!

We picked this hotel because we wanted something on the beach with a view.  And we got that.  But not much else.  The only reason it's getting three stars instead of two is because the staff could not have been more friendly, helpful, and accommodating.  Our first room felt like a motel.  The building wasn't meticulously maintained, the bathroom looked old and rundown, and our room was EXTREMELY noisy.  We could hear the TV from the room next to us and people coming up and down the stairs.  They moved us to a different room the next night which was much more private but the new building we were in reminded me of The Shining and the bathroom was the same.

okay, they DO still have calidogs. but when i tried to order one, i got something with sour cream and bacon.  can't really find it in my heart to complain about bacon, but i will dock a star for the mix-up.

Great neighborhood spot that never disappoints when we come to town. Always listen to recommendations from locals at the bar- they steered us right last night on the Mai Tai IPA and the Saison. We shared the seasonal apple, walnut and kale salad and the mand arugula pizza. Fantastic! I've previously had a fantastic burger there, too.

After driving by here a couple of times I finally went ahead and gave it a try. It's a simple taco stand next to a batting cage, with outdoor seating only. Their menu contains a variety of tacos including seafood, beef, chicken, and pork. I tried their three taco special and had the grilled fish, spicy crispy shrimp, and 'Gangnam style' taco - which were served with chips and salsa (spicy.) All of the tacos were tasty and the seafood was fresh! Great value and a unique menu. Now I understand why there's always a line!

We ate here while visiting the beach In late April 2020. Ordered to go during covid season and sat at the wall above the beach area, for some views and rest. We ordered: 1) Clam chowder - good! 2) fish & chips - good!  Decent place to grab a bite when you are already at the beach and you're having a good time.

As I stand in my kitchen (I brought it home) eating the amazing grilled cheese sandwich for the second day in a row, I think....why did I wait so long to try this place! Yes, I ate the same one yesterday! :-)  It is so delicious!  This isn't your typical grilled cheese.  And it comes with a little salad that compliments the sandwich so well!  Take my advice and go get one.  You will definitely thank me!  And as an added bonus, the people working there are so wonderful!  In my opinion The Blue Owl on Canon Perdido, just off State street is my latest 'best of the best' in Santa Barbara!  Ask for Nadia, she will be thrilled to have you as a new customer after such a tough year! Tell her Beckie sent you!  Now what should I try next on the menu! :-)

This place is so much fun and one of the most unique places to have dinner in Santa Barbara.  If you have a party large enough you can even get a fire pit table which is always a blast. At night they have these really cool lights that hang above the outside patio making the restaurant shimmer in the light I love it!   The food here is always so delicious and the service is always amazing! I love coming here and sharing things with my friends that way I can get a little bit of everything. There are many appetizers that I like to share around the table. My favorite appetizers are the hummus, cheese sambosik and tabbouli. Everything is so crispy, fresh, and delicious!! :)  Both the greek salad, and the watermelon salad are good too! Everything is so fresh and tasty I love these salads! :) They have some amazing entrees including chicken kebab, chicken shawarma, and the salmon! All of their entrees come with rice, salad, and you can add fries too for an extra cost.   Overall this is a great place for birthdays, girls night out, graduations, and more! If you have a big party make sure to make reservations ahead of time. I love the food, service, atmosphere, and more here! Looking forward to coming here again soon for more tasty delicious fresh food! :)

I come here because it's close to my house and I like supporting local... Easy stop for a quick beer, tea, or a snack. I like that there are some more natural brands and local options as far as products but other than that I'm not super impressed with anything -- the prices are high, the deli stuff is 'ayight,' and the service is usually fine. I used to get breakfast burritos here hoping that the awful ones I was getting were just one-offs, but when one turned into every time, I just stopped ordering them. Overall, fine for a corner market but definitely leaves a lot to be desired. I know that sounds dramatic since it's a market for crying out loud, but it's the only way I know how to describe it. There's a young gal checker who is super nice though, so I'm happy when I happen to go in when she is there.
Very friendly and great atmosphere! Creative and tasty offering of cocktails, wine, beer, and non-alcoholic drinks

Cielito's is pretty much my ideal dining experience in every way. Think modern, trendy restaurant like you would see in a Sex and the City episode.  In Santa Barbara.  I would expect this kind of restaurant to be in more of a Los Angeles or New York kind of city, and never expected anything like it in here.   Atmosphere aside, the food and drinks were delicious and inventive. I highly recommend the blackberry margarita and Enchiladas de jaiba y Camaron.

Delivery for the wiiiiin! It was easy to order and honestly the wait wasn't bad. I got a burger and cheese sticks. Took just under an hour to be made and get to my house. The guys that made my food actually read my comments and spared me from tomatoes..I feel like that is usually half the battle. The driver was super nice he had his mask plus gloves and all that stuff if you're worried about that. The only reason I gave it 4 stars was because I honestly wasn't a fan of the burger I chose. It was a very dryyy pattie..

I walked past this place so many times but had never tried it before. The place is small but quite cozy. Because we went on weekdays, we got our food served pretty fast. We enjoyed larb, green curry, roti, and poormans noodles. Our waitress was attentive and we all enjoyed our food. Solid 3.5 stars, an additional half star for the hand lotion in the clean restroom.

Yoooooo I heard these mohfukkas got French dips shaped like labias so you know your boi gotta try that shit out. They got bread and meat and dip, so bomb. I asked Andrew if they got any glizzys or other sexually suggestive foods and sadly they didn't. Consider adding glizzys and oysters though Holdren! Summer 2021 let's get freakkkyyyyyyyy!!!!!!   P.s. make sure to ask Porter for a sex on the beach.

Went to Rudy's last week. Went to Super Cucas on the Mesa today. Wow- what a stark contrast. Rudy's now charges $6+ for one taco, which is mostly filled with shredded lettuce. Super Cucas charges $6.95 for (3) tacos. Same with burritos. For bean, rice and cheese burrito, Rudy's charges $10.95 and for same burrito, Super Cucas charges $7. Rudy's is stingy with salsa, they don't want to give you more than two. Super Cucas will give you as much as you want, all with a friendly smile. Which leads me to the last thing- we have found the staff at Rudy's to be unfriendly, no smiles. Super Cucas is the opposite, with a friendly greeting, and a 'nothing is too much trouble' attitude. Lesson learned. Don't go to Rudy's, go to Super Cucas!

When i went to Lilly's I wanted to try the lingua or cabeza tacos, but i was really hungry that day and preferred something familiar since i really wasn't in the taking chances kind of mood. So I got the adobado and the carnitas and i just wan't that impressed. It's pretty cheap and it's authentic, but to me they are just average street tacos. I've actually had better in Tijuana although that could have been the booze talking. Nevertheless, i'll give the place a second chance one of these days because it's really tough to judge any place after just one sit-down, but i'm not itching to do so.

Oh boy where do I begin? This is a MUST stop for foodies. They offer a vast selections of cheeses and meats but also things like preserved lemons, and the best Bloody Mary elixir you've ever tried! Anything and everything you'd want for a picnic can be found here. Their breakfast is wonderfully satisfying  and the staff are all so nice, too. Love their Niman Ranch bacon. No doubt we will DEFINITELY return!!

I love the cave-like atmosphere, decor and the romantic table near the fireplace. Great ambience.   My $32 fish was a small portion and not very interesting. One of the sides was volcanic hot pickled veggies. Why would you pair something so strongly flavored with mild fish? My entree was just weird. Not inedible. Just weird.   The Hub liked his ribeye steak. He had truffled polenta that was to die. I was still hungry so I scooped that up.  Too pricey for what you get.  I probably wouldn't choose it weren't it for a gift certificate.

Once you walk into the doors, you feel like you're transported simultaneously back in time and to the future! You'll see a robot waitress meandering around with a tray of food, but also a small stage with some old school videos projected on the wall. The vibe in general is quite funky and eclectic which I didn't expect.   There are a good number of Sichuan dishes - we tried the toothpick lamb (my favorite of the night), twice cooked pork (decent/a little salty, and I'm probably a tough judge because I grew up eating this), mapo tofu (glad to report that they don't hold back with the mala), and dan dan noodles (a generous serving!). It was so nice to find a restaurant that offers authentic Chinese food in Santa Barbara of all places.   We also tried the desserts (sesame balls and fried rice cake) which were alright, although I would not recommend (unless you're with non-Asian friends who otherwise wouldn't be able to try) because they're really overpriced for what they are.

The Santa Barbara Fish Market supplies fish to many local restaurants, a seafood market, and now they have great small salads to go! The Shrimp Ceviche is a great grab & go on a nice day in the harbor.   As a seafood market, they have a large inventory, even some special oysters in the back if you ask! I commend them for the consistently high quality of their fish. I have never been disappointed! They have a small selection of marinades/dry spices/soy sauce/wasabi, etc. to fill-out any missing ingredients for dinner. Speaking of dinner, they now have a seafood blog with recipes, and specials. It's a great go to for dinner ideas and more.   A few favorites: Black Cod Sea Bass Lobster Oysters  Thanks, SB Fish Market!

Wow. Just wow. I really loved my first time at Sun Sushi. My friends and I came in around 6, and there wasn't too many people (which was nice for us, because we didn't want to wait to get seated). The menu definitely has a good variety, but if you don't want to spend too much money on sushi, this might not be the best place. However, the prices are set for good reason- the quality of sushi here is absolutely amazing.   I ordered the Blue Fin Tuna 6 ways which came with miso soup, and salad. The tuna though- oh my goodness. Everything tasted so fresh and the tuna would melt in my mouth- so good. I also got two scotty salmon sushi, which were also delicious.  My favorite appetizer here is the crispy spicy tuna, which is spicy tuna with avocado sitting on a fried piece of rice (kind of like a rice tater tot). So delicious, I could order 4 of them.  Parking was easy for me, but that's also because places were closed around there (like the brunch place within the same plaza).   I almost don't want to recommend this place because I'm selfish and don't want people to flood my new favorite restaurant in SB, but it's honestly the best sushi I've had in my 8 years of living in California.   Update: August 17.  I wanted to add to my review, as I've been to Sun Sushi many times after the first review. I am leaving California tomorrow and I think that my saddest moment will be that Sun Sushi is not going to be near me anymore.  The chefs and all of the employees are very very professional, friendly, and also very funny! They all have a great sense of humor, which is what I really love and appreciate. The servers are great, the people who come around to refill waters are great! Max has been excellent in his responsiveness on Yelp, and his service. I am very grateful that I have met everyone here, because I always have a great time here.  I would highly encourage everyone to try their specials on Thursday. And some rolls. And their appetizers. And their ice cream. Basically, you should try everything here, because everything is amazing. This is my favorite restaurant in California. I will miss it so much, but when I come visit, Sun Sushi will be the first place I will go to.  Thank you Max, and everyone else for such an amazing experience. I will miss you all and hopefully I will see you all soon!

This is a hidden gem of a location hidden in the heart of the Santa Barbara mountains. Both times I went during the weekend and was not disappointed. They have live bands playing and they serve some amazingly delicious but simple smoked tri-tip sandwich.   The first time we ate at the restaurant and it was an extended wait as there is a sizeable weekend crowd. While waiting you can head to the bar and get a draft or liquor (located in the other building)  The last visit we skipped the restaurant and headed straight to the bar for a draft on tap and ordered the famous tritip sandwich. You get a ticket per sandwich and head over to the area where you smell delicious smoked bbq (really hard to miss but it's outside between the two buildings)  Each ticket will get you several freshly smoked and sliced pieces of tritip in between freshly toasted bread. They have a condiment stand where your choices are bbq sauce, horseradish sauce and a salsa like blend(super spicy but well worth it)  There's usually plenty of seating outside while you enjoy listening to music, eat your sammy and enjoy a draft.   Btw don't be frightened of the hordes of gruff looking bikers and equally dusty cyclists. It seems everyone is there for a good time of music and great food.

Doesn't seem like much when you walk by, but...  Wait no that was the Yelp boilerplate:)  Actually looove this place! Who could not love the California roll, it was the best! And the Omega roll has to be experienced do not get FOMO!   This is my #1 SB Sushi place now!

We've had Lilac Pâtisserie in our home for the last couple of months because 5 out of the 7 people in our house are gluten intolerant, but today was the first time I was tasked with grabbing the fresh bread. I was impressed with how clean and fresh the decor was. I personally don't eat gluten free products as I don't tend to like them, but this place was different. I've always really enjoyed the cakes and bread from here at family occasions and today when I saw the fresh breads I couldn't resist! They were light, fluffy, and I couldn't even tell they were gluten free. The staff was nice and when I didn't see the item I was looking for they checked the back before they helped me pick out a substitute. If you're like me and don't normally enjoy gluten free options, I would recommend coming here and giving them a shot, you'll be pleasantly surprised!

They always make such great acai bowls! More importantly, their customer service has me coming back because if they don't get it right, they are quick to own up to the misunderstanding!   Thanks for always delivering my somewhat 'complicated' order
Service is good, the burgers are pretty bad, go to Mesa Burger. Tacos were ok, I would not come back. Cheap ingredients.
The most refreshing Whiskey Sour that I have ever had.  Such a cool hipster place int eh hustle band bustle near State Street in Santa Barbara.  You should go.

I love the concept of giving back to special needs!!! Please keep that up and consider decoding dyslexia California!  Most of the food was great. I'm not happy about the grilled cheese for kids. My kid wouldn't eat it. I'd like for them to also have gluten free bread too. Ideally it would be nice to have feedback forms in the restaurants too. I would have preferred to fill out a form than leave feedback here. Overall good experience

Best Italian Sub in Santa Barbara.  The quality is top notch, only surpassed by the quantity of ingredients their sandwiches come with. The best part however is their price point, which makes you feel like you're getting a good deal every time. It's rare I don't finish a whole sandwich in a sitting, but this places subs push my limits.  Simply delicious!

This place is my favorite Italian restaurant in Santa Barbara. Their prime rib ravioli is to die for. It may be small if you're a big eater, but it's filling for me and leaves a little room to take the bread and soak up the yummy sauce.  The lasagna is also another go-to.  The pasta is fresh and homemade. Restaurant itself is cozy, highly recommend reservations.  The burrata appetizer is amazing too. So fresh and delicious. And the prosciutto is perfect. Not too fatty or chewy.  The vanilla gelato is great, and same with the tiramisu. I am not usually a tiramisu fan but this was creamy and delicious. So maybe if you're a traditionalist for tiramisu, this might not be what you want.... or maybe I have had just terrible tiramisu before this.

This is a cute place to grab a hearty breakfast and a respectably-sized mimosa.  The beignets were alright.  Meal portions are generous and pricing is moderate.  If this sounds like the perfect place to nurse the morning-after blues, that is because it is.  I found the parking lot across the street behind the shops quite helpful, too.
Good specials at happy hour. Food good but French fries need improvement. They must be pre cooked and heated. Not good to have poor fries at a burger place.

I love this place. The ambience is great. Casual but romantic. Great for dates with the wife. The food is great. Gluten free pizza that doesn't taste gluten free. Which I'm sure you also can appreciate if your spouse / girlfriend / boyfriend / significant other / etc is gluten free. Service is always great as well.

This is a review for The Hungry Cat.  Backstory: I lived around the corner of this place and would come with my gay boyfriends and good girlfriend alike for some years.  Things have changed. keep reading.  Atmosphere: Urban eatery. Small, clean sparse decor. I like the minimalist ambience.  Service: We were promptly seated and we did come late on a sat night.   Food: We came for drinks, specifically cocktails. What happened HUNGRY CAT? Where are the drinks with a variety of seasonal (fresh) fruits and herbs? They all sounded the same, yes I agree with the below reviewer: Mostly with Gin.  I had no choice but to get a mojito. 13.00!! Yow. Nothing special. We ordered shrimp. It was okay.  Summary: I miss the 'ol' Cat I used to know years ago. Would I go back? No, not even if I had money to blow. Sigh.
Food is good, pizza is good but the rude cashier don't cut it

Honestly, I'm fond of this place.  I have seen the parking get horrendous here, but I don't mind a nice walk in warm, ocean air.    More than a year ago, my sister and I were chatting out on the patio about how we had never seen a whale in the wild.  Yup.  Beach living for decades, and never a whale sighting to speak of.  Less than 15 seconds after we changed the subject, two whales breach the water.  We were then given quite the show, highlighted by thunderous tail-slapping and 10 meter tall spray jets.  The whales were so large and magnificent, it felt like they were less than 100 meters away.  Since that amazing moment, I've associated the Boathouse with the magic and magnificence of Mother Nature and continue to return here for strong mimosas and delicious brunches when I'm in the area.  What a memory making experience.

I tried the Salmon special with potatoes and bed of spinach.  We had a group of 7 and we all ordered different plates.  Everyone was very happy and satisfied with their dish and the service was good.  Outside seating had good energy and included mountain views.  No complaints and will definitely be back soon.

Pretty decent Thai food and with a little spice from the jars it can be livened up. My husband and I came here based on the Axxcess card and I am sure we will be returning. We ordered the Tap rolls. I would be careful of these if you don't like the taste of seeweed. My husband can't stand it so I ate most of these. The papaya salad, though more subdeud than most Thai places is a real winner. I liked the coconut rice that went with it. Try them together and dont forget to add more spice. The Green Curry was really good and it was loaded with veggies. My husband got the basil chicken and although it wasent amazing, it was fresh and good. Our waitress was good and overall I will return one day when needing that salad again!

I was so iffy about eating at a Mexican restaurant in Santa Barabra, not sure why.  However, I read that this restaurant was sister restaurant to Los Agaves, and I've been to Los Agaves and loved it, so decided to give this a shot, plus this one is fancyyy :)!  We started with some drinks.  I got the Flor de Maiz margarita, which is basically a cadillac....wowzers!  What a well made margarita!  Didn't disappoint.  Next they brought out the Panuchos, which is like sope, but a little different.  DELICIOUS!  The cochinita was amazing, the masa on the Panucho was so light and fluffy and the picked onions, a must on every bite.  Next my enchiladas de mole con pollo (chicken) came out, what a beautiful plate, two over filled enchiladas draped a delicious authentic mole, topped with micro radishes greens,  cheese, sour cream and cilantro.  It was a beautiful sight, but an even more delicious taste.  My entrée came with a side of green rice, which was okay.  Great experience and the view was also beautiful.  Great service and servers too!  Don't miss out!   I wish they had a cochinita pibil entree, that would be amazing!

These guys have it down for sushi! We didn't have reservations bc it was a Monday evening and I assumed it wouldn't be packed. Wrong. We thankfully got a cute high top table in the bar area. The rooftop was completely booked and downstairs inside a well was reserved. I highly suggest reservations. As soon as our server came over and I mentioned Celiac he had a crazy long amazing list of rolls and edits to normal dishes. I got the Carpaccio with a Gf garlic Tamari sauce, the mushroom garlic rice noodles (to die for) and a yellowtail roll that had peppers and was rolled in jicama. It was all delicious! Side note and huge perk, they have valet behind the restaurant.

I tried Finney's because I heard how amazing it was. I had told my friend that I wanted to try the impossible burger and she said they had one of the best ones in town.  True, the impossible burger was pretty good, however, I wouldn't say that it was great.   Sadly the service was less than mediocre.  Staff was friendly when we got to the hostess stand.  They told us it would be a 15 minute wait which it was. We got sat on the patio table right next to where the sidewalk menu was located so during our 1 hour dinner there probably 30 people with their cell phones and flashlight on leaned in (towards me) to look at the menu which was really intrusive and uncomfortable.   When you order a sandwich you have to order your french fries à la cart for a few dollars more. Not a big deal had they actually come with the meal. We had asked for them twice before having them serve the fries to us 15 minutes after our burgers which were over 1/2 way eaten and cold at that point.  When the server came by to ask if everything was everything OK we let her know about the french fries. Her response was that  she had to get her manager to come talk to us she did not have the authority to handle it herself.  After that servers were all over us ready to bust her food which we were not ready to have taken and nobody offered to refill her drinks.  Even though it was 930 at night it seem like they were wanting to get us out of there and clean the table as soon as possible.   I would rather spend my 30 something dollars on a dinner elsewhere. I felt like I was at an overpriced Islands. Meh.
Great people, great drinks, and great food.  The smoked beet salad is so delicious. It's like bacon with no meat. Make sure not to miss the chimichurri fries!

The best location out of all the restaurants  in Santa Barbara. They have a great late night happy hour ( why isn't that more popular.) I would also say it's the best brunch in town! Icing on the cake they always play reggae music! This is my go to spot when entertaining friends from out of town.

What a disappointment! Sad to say but ....truly awaist of money!! We got a sandwich that was obviously made in a rush~no care and NO LOVE~and defiantly wasn't special at all~NOT WORTH $10!! Hardly any pesto on one half of the bread...hardly any tomato~and a sad peace of lettuce. VERY SAD because we LOVE the Panino in Santa Ynez~the sandwiches are AMAZING at the Santa Ynez Panino~you can taste how much they care and honestly the quality is FANTASTIC....or wait...maybe the owner visits Santa Ynez Panino enough to keep the quality high?! My friend and I actually make special trips to Santa Ynez to enjoy the #12 sandwich with pesto chicken and pine nuts....mmmmmm amazing! DO NOT WAIST YOUR MONEY or time going to the Panino in SB. Very SAD! ): P.S. they have the food open really close to the bathroom....seems sorta gross and dirty..

Great place we found, usually always have good beer selection and food is great too. Their wings and fries are some of our favorites, as well as the pizza. Good place to go and get some food and drinks.

To be completely honest, I was thoroughly let down. My friend had come here once before and really liked it, but her experience from back then was quite different.  I ordered a combination plate with a chicken soft taco, rice and black beans. When my food came out, I was shocked. By their definition, I guess combination plate = a combination of dry, flavorless, overpriced food items. My chicken taco was extremely dry and flavorless. It was literally just a mound of dry chicken on a tiny plain tortilla, nothing else. They gave me a tiny scoop of rice (also pretty much flavorless) and a scoop of black beans in a crispy tortilla shell. The beans were very mushy and I couldn't eat more than a couple of bites.   They do offer complimentary chips and have an extensive salsa bar though, which is nice. I ended up eating a lot of these as I was still hungry [and disappointed] after eating my plate.   After paying more than $12 for this plate, I feel extremely ripped off. I've honestly had way better tacos from cheap, casual restaurants or even taco stands.  If you are in the area, skip this place. There's a lot better food in downtown SB.
Delightful atmosphere on the patio. Great service, great prices, and absolutely delicious sole picatta! ($16).

It is the new stone age, moved after all those years. Sucks they took out the BBQ options, but everything else is pretty much the same, I think even the cooks are the same as before. Not a bad place to have friends over for a quick lunch.
