Awesome cupcakes! Great flavors; I chose 4 different types, so my family could have an assortment. Compliments all the way around.
Second time here and I like it.  I eat a fair amount of Indian and so far I've enjoyed each dish I've tried.  Also very clean and courteous set up.

My favorite Mexican restaurant! The flavors at Los Agaves is very unique and special. I love their Los Agaves salad, Los Agaves Enchiladas, and shrimp taco.  Pretty much anything on their menu is excellent.

Today we had a nice breakfast with friendly service. Hostess remembered my husband and what he normally orders. Sal our waiter was good. My reduction in a point was the music today. Loud rock music. Not appropriate for a relaxing brunch frequented by a older crowd.

Had the popular Chilaqulies with carnitas, it was very good. Drizzled some cholula sauce over it and it set it over the top. Had plenty of outdoor seating and shade. The parking is very limited and may have to drive around the block to park. But nothing to out of the way. Definitely would like to try the variety of other items on the menu.

The food was absolutely delicious and so very nice to have another variety of cuisine added to downtown.  The only minus was the Classic Jerk Chicken that was under cooked (ie, still a little pink and didn't fall off the bone), but when it came back a second time, it was perfect.  The other minus was the short staffing, which is understandable with Covid19 restrictions that are impacting a lot of businesses, especially mom/pop ones, so we extended our understanding and told the server that it was not a problem.  He was apologetic knowing he neglected a handful of things including missing side dish that came with the entree.  We also had the Braised Oxtail, the Clams/Mussels in Coconut Ginger broth, the Beet Salad that was sliced paper thin with all the fixings, offering a delicate texture.  So, 4 stars it is because the ambience was that authentic rustic Jamaican casual sweet, the food was absolutely delicious when it's all said and done, and the server was very nice even under the pressure of having to be the greeter, the cashier, the runner, the busboy, the server.  That's quite a feat tending to at least half a dozen tables plus take-out orders.  When those couple of minuses are back on tract, it'll be an easy 5 stars for sure.  In the meantime, we're still gonna return.

It's inside Venus in furs.   So no happy hour anymore and it sounds like they are shutting down after June 2021.  So there will be a new food option there.     They are only offering 3 of their pies right now.  $15 for a half and $29 for a full pizza.  It's a fun bar interior though and the drinks and food were delicious.  Honestly the pie was one of the best I've eaten in years.  Saint Milo.   I hate saying any pizza is worth $28.   I mean I hate it.  But this pizza was WORTH IT.    I will be back before they close.  And I really hope they get their own restaurant someday.     If you see this review in time, run, don't walk and get yourself some pizza.   Honestly two people can share a half as a light meal.  And for $15 for two that's one hell of a deal.    Best food we've had in Santa Barbara.

Black bird came highly recommended from Another great restaurant so we decided to try it.  Made reservations and was seated very quickly. The place is alittle dark . They do have outside seating which I regret not using.  Menu is a little difficult to understand. We went with the citrus and the flat bread as starters. Citrus was a little weird , pretty much some fruits and yogurt. I would recommend. The flag bread was great.   My wife went with cauliflower soup. They make Table side and is very creamy! I chose the the spaghetti and it was decent. Maybe I'm just not used to ultra fancy food.   Staff was very attentive and helpful. Although people in other reviews are complaining about price we found the price to be just about why you would expect for a main spot on state street. Two people without drinking was about 100 plus a tip.   If I'm being honest food was about a 3 but service , facility and location make it an over all 4

If you like when a bouncer treats you poorly for trying to enter and the bar tenders to treat you like dirt for trying to order a drink then you found the perfect place.  I've never had such a chilly reception entering a new bar or restaurant.  The bartender was so rude to us that the 4 of us immediately just walked out and went to the next bar.  We are all local and will never be back, good luck trying to survive when there are plenty of better places.

The freshest and best burrito in town. Family owed and operated for years. Thank you for always having great food and service. My go to is Bean, Rice, Cheese and Guac but everything is fantastic. Locals fave spot!!!

My family has been coming here for years... continued excellent service and great food. Never been here in the evening; so I can't vouch for the nicely stocked bar - but breakfast and lunch have never dissapointed. Menu selection is not large - typical Tex-Mex-California cuisine; everything we've tried has been over-the-top excellent.  Highly Recommended.
Had the short ribs, very good. Wife had gnocchi (GF) & shrimp, she loved it. Wine recommendation: Rosso DiMontalcino , Ciacci Piccolomini d'Aragona, 2019 fit the moment perfectly.

I went for my friend's birthday dinner and don't like pizza so I got the greek salad instead so this review is purely based on service. The salad was yummy but pretty small for the price and I got the full size. But our waitress was REALLY awesome and helpful! I think her name was Amanda.  We had a pretty big group so we expected to wait for a while for the pizza's since they are deep dish and take longer to cook, but our waitress was really attentive and kept bringing us water and refills for our drinks and kept updating us on how much longer the pizzas would take. Then they accidentally burnt one of the pizzas as it was coming out of the oven, but  they took off the charge of that pizza and brought out a thin crust pizza of the person's choice to hold him over until the deep dish was cooked. So in the end he got two pizzas for nothing because of the mistake. We really appreciated that and Patxi's really great customer service make the whole experience awesome! I love when restaurants and their staff are this great. Thanks Patxi's!    Tip- the thin crust pizza is pretty small, whereas the deep dish is huge! The people in my group basically split the deep dish pizzas between two of them and ended up full, so I would definitely go with the deep dish!

Been here a couple of times and never disappointed. Just was here with some friends for Cinco de Mayo with no reservation and got seated in a fair amount of time. Staff is extremely friendly and helpful. The food is delicious and I should mention the massive amount of guac! I recommend the asada burrito. The margaritas there are decent but a little too sweet for my taste, perhaps im ordering the wrong ones? Haha. But neways the vibe is good and definitely a chill place to relax. The live music is an extra touch and not too overpowering like some places.

I prefer to visit when a crowd is barely visible. The juke box music collection is on fire. Although, it took us a moment to operate the juke. Bartender is a doll...very down to earth and comical. I ordered a long island because it fit the mood.

It's pretty noisy and the service is a bit slow, but their food is delicious!   There's always a bit of a wait and the tables are so close so forget about trying to have a private conversation -- not only will you be able to hear the next tables conversation, but you'll also bump elbows with them.   When ordering, I highly recommend the truffle cheese fries and their Fresno fig burger. They're my favorite!   *Side note: they used to have 'steal-the-glass' nights where you could take a beer glass home if you ordered whatever beer was being featured that day. I'm not too sure if they still do it, but I think that's pretty cool.

Underwhelming.   I have not been to Panera back east. I assumed people who have, come to ours because it is familiar and reminds them of another place.   Maybe the Paneras back east are different. A few people have implied as much.   Drinks and pastries are about the same quality as a Starbucks. Not bad. Some nice enough. But not great. (Realize, this city is full of Peet's and CB&TL, plus awesome independent pastry shops and cafés, boasting top notch sweets and java.)  So, think quality like Starbucks...but will be crowded and no super perky staff, and you will sit at a sort of Denny's style table or booth in a very well lit but bland atmosphere.   Food matches the atmosphere. Not bad at all, but just ok. In fact, if Starbucks did serve meals, I think they might be like this.   Why go here? Well... it is in a very popular shopping center so you might want to get some coffee during your day. The center has lots of parking, and you can grab lunch with coffee.   The Starbucks in this center is tiny, very limited seating, whereas  Panera is large and you likely will be able to sit right away. It is family friendly if you have kids.   They have a loyal following. I have been there a few times because friends wanted to go. Staff was pleasant though busy, as there is a huge volume of people to serve.   Nothing really wrong with my drink or food. Clean environment.   Three stars because it is clean and consistent. And nothing really bad about it.  But, in a city loaded with great pastries and truly delicious coffee, I have too many great choices... and never come here on my own.

What can I say, it's IHOP!  Eggs over medium with hash browns and a side of pancakes is what I got.  Hash browns were overly crispy, but everything else was just OK.  Hub E. got served cold sausages and eggs cooked the wrong way.  Waiter thought we said sunnyside up when we asked for scrambled, how you get that mixed up I don't know.  Anyway, we will never worry about bringing fussy Bay B. here since these types of places are havens for screaming children.

We found this gem from the Axxcess card. It's a high vibe place with incredibly friendly and helpful staff and the pizza is sooooo fresh! They have whole wheat crust and amazingly, lots of vegan options. We've been twice in one week after discovering Pizza Guru. Such a treat to be able to enjoy plant based pizza!! Even the kids love it!

I've been wanting to check this place out for a little while now, and I finally had the chance to eat brunch here with a couple of friends (before the covid-19 closures). The food was delicious and I personally really loved their pastry basket. I can't wait until it's safe to eat in restaurants again because this is definitely going to be one of the first places I go to! Their pastry case looked amazing and they had so many menu items that intrigued me!

The shrimp pesto pizza is to die for.  The sauce on these pizzas is fresh, light and delicious. One of my favorite restaurants. They have the best Chicken Marsala of anyone in Santa Barbara. My husband loves the Chix Parm.

came here because of zagat. cool little place with good pizza.  it was good but im not rushing back, mostly because I live +2hrs away.   We got the mushoroom pizza. it was probably a 3.5 star but im giving it a 4 because of the manager's service.  My parents got a different pizza and they both liked it a lot.  The pennys on the outside are very cool.

Not a bad spot if you really want some bar style food. Its not amazing, its not healthy, but its pretty satisfying and their covid response has been consistent. Definitely try the sb location over ventura which was awful

I had the vegetarian taco option, and it was so delicious and incredibly flavorful. Usually the vegetarian taco option(if they even have one) is like the scraps of bell peppers and onions inside a tortilla. But this place's vegetarian taco was THE best vegetarian taco I've ever had. My fiancé got the steak taco, fish taco, and pork belly taco- He loved all three and said the meat was tender and juicy. We also got the queso fundido and chips & guacamole appetizers. The fundido was like the best guilty pleasure food-it's just grilled onion, mushrooms, and roasted pepper slices lost inside a sea of melted cheese and served with homemade corn tortillas....so good! The guacamole was just simple and had clean flavor-perfect pairing with the tacos. Definitely will be going back!

Came here with my family for dinner. It was a very comfortable and clean environment. We ordered the KFC and Pork Belly. Both were delicious... I don't usually eat fried foods but the KFC was amazing and it didn't leave me feeling icky. Fried rice was okay.. The sweet potato glass noodle dish was bomb and I still wish I had ordered it for myself! Overall, this place is great and I would come again!
You must come in to try the food and cocktails. The view from the patios are outstanding. We will definitely be back soon. Outstanding food and service.

Had the pleasure of checking out this brand new bakery today with some friends.  The interior is beautiful, classy, and clean.  Right away, the bakery case filled with the most beautiful cakes caught my eye, and I had such a hard time deciding what to order!  I finally settled on a slice of Carrot Cake and a Toffee Nut cookie, both of which were delicious in that 'you'd never know it was gluten free' kind of way.  Decadent, moist, and so flavorful!  The icing on the cake was so light and fluffy.  I also got to try the Berry, Birthday, and Chocolate Almond Praline cakes, and oh man- I can't wait to go back and try more!  They also have great looking coffee/espresso drinks.  I hear they will have lunch service in the future-yes!  Each staff member was welcoming and friendly; service was prompt.  I'm hooked.

I didn't even want to write a review for this place, but yelp keeps telling me i'm the duchess so I suppose I will.  2 stars because the only thing we wanted was not available. We settled for fish and chips and were not amused. Their margs are super strong, which pretty much means it was a glass of tequila, and for some, that may be all you ask for, but I ended up giving mine away.   So BLEH, you La Rumba. Bleh, you!

Food and the service is great. Been living in SB for a while and never noticed this place because of its 'hidden' location next to Casablanca. My wife and I agreed that our favorite part of our experience was the service. Servers were very kind and attentive. The restaurant was also very clean and had a great vibe.  We will definitely visit again!

This is the number one place we Repeatedly come too. I highly recomend the Mother clucker, the Gobbler and chicken strips!!!!! Brooke H is always so helpful and has great Customer service!!! I highly recomend this place!

As an Australian who has been craving a good old Aussie meat pie for goodness knows how long, I was excited to hear that the next best thing had come to Santa Barbara.   I'm very sad to report that my beef and onion pie was quite flavorless and the pastry stiff, burnt, and inextricably stuck to the tinfoil. The chips were average, but at least there was vinegar on the table for me to drown them in. Granted, I wasn't as disappointed as the first time my craving (erroneously) took me to Outback Steakhouse, but this was a close second.   The prices are unbelievable. $6.50 for a lone pie? $8.50 for one piece of fish and some chips?   Unless they improve the quality of their fare or drop their prices, I don't think this place will last.
BEST cupcakes in SB!  I love every single one of their cupcakes. It's worth the price. The whole decor is super cute. If you have time to kill, definitely get something from their cafe menu.

Delicious wings!! I went with a big group so we ordered a bunch of flavors. My favorite were the thai peanut, buffalo, and lemon pepper! Great flavor! The service was great as well. We sat outside on the patio and it was perfect. Good selection of beer, kombucha and sours, as well. Try it out, you won't be disappointed!

I really enjoy the ample seating and general ambiance of the outdoor patio area. Its a great place to go after a bike/run on the nearby Obern path. The prices are also very reasonable for what you get! I almost always opt for the avocado bagel, which comes served over a layer of cream cheese, and topped with lemon pepper seasoning. I'm not a huge fan of the coffee, though. It's a very dark roast and the americano is often too watery. There is definitely much better coffee in SB, so I would not come here solely for that. However, the food is solid and a really great value!

The Empty Bowl is one of my favorite Thai restaurants in town! The curry noodles is my favorite dish. It's creamy but not too rich that you can't eat the whole bowl as a meal. I also love the papaya salad. It is fresh and just the right amount of sourness. I usually order the curry noodles but this time I ordered the hang over noodles and they were extremely spicy for some reason. I had to trade meals with my boyfriend who had curry noodles. This Thai restaurant has more interesting dishes than many of the Thai places in Santa Barbara. I would definitely want to try their chicken wings and their pad see ew.

Authentic Italian in town. The arugula and prosciutto pizza tastes just like Rome. Their desserts are always to die for. I like how they rotate them out sometimes but if they have the tarte tartin get it. You won't regret it.
This place is a must if you're going to the public market. Norteno taco is absolutely delicious. High quality ingredients, great chips and salsa, always busy which is a great sign. Try it out!

This is one of my favorite spots since moving to Santa Barbara. The food is excellent, service is fast, and there is plenty of seating. I suggest trying the classic 'Mesa Burger' if you aren't sure what to get. If you are going at night, there is a nice patio area with heat lamps!

This place was fantastic every step of the way! My wife made a reservation for my birthday and when we showed up we were seated right away. Our waiter even mentioned that he knew it was my birthday which means that the staff has great communication. On top of that Billy was on top of his game when it came to service always making sure our drinks were taken care of and giving solid recommendations including the amazing chocolate caramel bar for dessert!  Will definitely be back!
Delicious.  Great staff.  Hugs to Brad and JR. I have zero complaints for the many years we have been to Chuck's.
Delicious pizzas with fresh ingredients! We love the new humus dish on the menu.  Friendly staff! Cute decor! The aperol spritzers are fun!

Love their tacos. We ordered a bunch of different things so I was able to try a variety. All really good. The guacamole was excellent, delicious salsas, all with a good kick. Keep in mind, these are all gourmet, this is not traditional Mexican, and not cheap, but I think they do it really well. I really liked the pastor taco, the meat is so well marinated, you don't need much on the taco. The veggie quesadilla was outstanding. The baja fish style was not my favorite, but everything else I liked. I see why this owner is a star, really great creative menu with super flavors. I liked hanging out at this food hall, it's a cool huge space with lots of delicious choices, the tacos are definitely solid if that's your vibe.

As I'm writing this review, the guy walks up to my table to refill my coffee and says 'Did you drop this $10 bill?'. Yes, I did. Dude!  What a rad place. I love breakfast, okay?  I'm a breakfast person.  This is MY FAVORITE local spot for breakfast.  If you like fresh, flavorful, and healthy California food, get here quick.  If there's a line, wait!  Once you're in here though, there's no rush to leave. The vibe is laid back, the music sounds niiice, and even the bathrooms are fun to be in (ever seen a wall made up of chopped up skateboard decks?!).  Breakfast solo or with a close friend can be found no where better than this.

After a bunch of trendy Italian restaurants have come and gone, Arnoldi's stands tall.  It's not nouvelle.  There are no faux Italian waiters.  Prices are reasonable.  And the tiny kitchen kicks out some absolutely first-rate dishes that taste like they're right out of the boot-shaped old country.  We love this place.  On Wednesdays in the summer, you can watch the bocce tournaments from your table.  It's not exactly FIFA level competition, but it fits the vibe for sure.  Try it three times.  Order something different every time.  You'll agree.
The best restaurant in town, bar none. Haute cuisine, good portions, wonderful retro ambience, kitchen open until midnight.

Sometimes I Life to feel fancy, and this place seemed like a chill spot to eat out at, even though it's a tiny place, it never felt too cramped, the atmosphere was nice, and there was nice suttle music for the ambience. Their wine selection was quite impressive, and their quality of food is Devine, only to be topped by their deserts! The chocolate banana bread pudding was a perfect end to a great meal. The staff are very friendly, and when they suggest food to us we had to try it! If you want a fancy dinner date and feel a little Italian come and try their great and amazing food!

We originally wanted to try Bibiji, but it was too early. We stumbled upon Apna Indian Kitchen. The staff are very friendly and attentive. We ordered the samosa, latke and chana masala.  The latke was ok; it definitely benefited from the sauce served with it.  My favorite of the meal was their chana masala and garlic naan. If we go back again, it will because I am craving chana masala.  They have indoor and outdoor seating. Thank goodness for the patio umbrellas.  It was a hot day.
De-lish tacos. Super duper good. And an impressive array of sauces and salsas. Altogether, a really good experience. The only thing is that I've come by a couple times and its closed by 4 or so :/

This place is busy! I would def call ahead to make a reservation. However with that said, I called at about 5 pm on a Friday to see what might be available for around 615 and they were able to save me a table! Really nice service, our guy made a great wine recommendation as well. We had one of the jamon plates to start with, followed by scallops, brocoli, croquettes, and one of the pork dishes. Everything was GREAT. Also had some cocktails from the menu that were good as well! Will def be back.

A top tier chick fil a that only appears once in a lifetime. Love the food and service at this location specifically. I know there are many different chick fil a's but this one in particular nails customer service. When they mess up on an order (which they rarely do) they always make sure to make it right! That's customer service which is pretty hard to find in the fast food industry.

Went here for my brother's graduation from UCSB! We planned in advance given we had a large party and had the preset menu deal with basically unlimited food and alcohol given it was prepaid by person. Great deal for the money and everyone had an amazing time! Amazing ambience and well serving staff!  Belly dancer and fire-pit tables only added to the already amazing restaurant! Love how all the seating is outside in the garden with a fountain and twinkle lights!  We had to wait for about 40 minutes even though we had the reservation 4 months in advance but it wasn't even a problem given the happy and polite staff that was more than willing to keep the drinks flowing in the bar area! Great, fun, seasonal cocktails!  The wine list was lacking, but the cocktails and tea selection more than made up for it.

I cannot say enough good things about this amazing sandwich joint. I've been coming here since I was a kid and no other place can do sandwiches this perfect. Always so fresh, so fast and ALWAYS delicious. The owner is great, he knows his shit and is definitely the king of sandwiches :) Better then five stars!

I really enjoyed this resturant. I live very close to here and whenever I walk by, I get hints of thai chili, and thai basil, and garlic. It smells so good. I'm glad i finally gave them a try. I've been here twice now and the food has consistently been delicious and aromatic. The service is very friendly and inviting, the whole resturant just feels very warm and homey. The waitress is so sweet and gave me a little complementary 'rice crispy' treat and dessert to take home while I wait for my to go order. I'm from the San Francisco Bay Area, where they have AMAZING Thai food and this place just reminds me of home. Definitely give them a try
Nice courtyard atmosphere. Gourmet Mexican food with very good service. The bar offers a contrasting atmosphere and also classy.
Wonderful lunch food, shaded patio and excellent service! A good find on state street )reasonable prices too). Great staff and vibe

Rustic French furnishings with a glorious atrium for dining.  I went there for lunch and had the escargot appetizer and the potato leek soup.  Both were good enough to rub in my hair. (That's a mighty GOOD thing.)

We grabbed the Maitake mushroom and Salty surfer by takeout. Tip - online ordering may estimate an hour plus wait, but when we called it took just 15 minutes. The pizzas were delicious and full of flavor. The crust chewy and just the right amount of salty. Appreciated that the staff were all wearing masks and had a plexiglass guard in front of the register.

I had really high hopes for Stella Mare's and really wanted to love them (especially since I am smitten with their other business, McConnell's Ice Cream...). I was pretty amped up from all the great Yelp reviews too!  I actually called in on Tuesday morning to make reservations for a group of 5 for dinner that Saturday, just to be safe since it was Labor Day weekend. I asked the gentleman on the phone whether I could reserve a table around 7:30 pm and he told me that they were fully booked, except for a slot at 6:30 pm and one at 8 pm. I decided to take the later 8 pm slot.  Although we were kindly greeted by the hostess when we arrived (extremely promptly, might I add), we were informed that our table was not quite ready and that it would be another 5 minutes or so. Shrug, oh well I thought, maybe they were that busy! But while we were waiting near the entry, I did find it very odd that the dining areas were all fairly empty and few tables were actually occupied. I thought they said it was going to be a packed evening!! GRRR!  When we were led to our table in the 'greenhouse' dining room, I also noticed the same. There were only a handful of occupied tables, and PLENTY of empty ones. I thought perhaps they would fill during our dinner, but other than for a couple of tables, no new guests arrived!  Our server was cold and not very friendly. We waited a very long period of time before he returned to take our orders. We waited even longer for our food to arrive! However, I will say that the food was well-prepared and tasty: filet mignon, duck, and trout were all very good. I must also add that when the food arrived, the runners did not know who ordered which dish and we had to correct them twice on giving dishes to the wrong people. Dessert was also very good, especially the warm apple tart. The lavender creme brulee was a bit too thick and dense for me, and the sugar on top was cold when it arrived =(  The decor and setting were adorable! I absolutely loved the cuteness of the French cottage look. Nonetheless, it could not make up for the extremely subpar service. Perhaps I have grown accustomed to the quality of service at similar types of restaurants in LA and San Diego, but this experience was quite a disappointment. Being a native of SB, I wish our standards for service were higher and more competitive with the rest of other nicer Southern California dining establishments, so that we wouldn't need to rely on the 'charm' of SB to entice our patrons.

It's great!   Nice little location on Coast Village Road in Montecito, serving up pre-made juices and custom ordered smoothies and to-order juice combos.  Greeted by 2 super helpful, friendly girls. I asked for an all-green juice and they suggested the 'Yoda' which, as it turned out, tasted nothing like an unbathed, furry Star Wars character. They pulled a bottle out of the refrigerator and a minute later I was being restored, revived and refreshed by an ultra-fresh splash of amazing liquid greenery. My wife enjoyed a fruit/vegetable combo and our friend ordered a smoothie.   Everyone was super happy with their drinks.   Warning: This place is expensive-----sort of like Kreation in Los Angeles.

Dave's Dogs is D-LICIOUS! This is there new location and their second in Santa Barbara County. They offer a wide array of hot dog lunch options and all dogs are creative, unique and tasty! Today, my lunch combo included the mac + cheese dog with fries and a drink all for under $9! They are fast and friendly and great for a quick lunch stop! And, with all the options, I challenge you to try them ALL! I can't wait to go back for my next Dave's Dog!

We went from almost 5 star to a 2.5 star (sorry can't do point five star so looks like just 2). Breakfast deal is gone. Now there is no drink included. 9 bucks for breakfast and 9 bucks for a drink. Such a bummer that they changed their deal of breakfast and a drink for 7 bucks. Great chips (got an extra star for chips), but lost it again for the hung over/high staff with inappropriate clothing on. I never want to see ass cheeks hanging out of my waitresses shorts.   *With all that said they have great Mexican food and margaritas! The best breakfast deal in town is now over

I came here for the first time this weekend and I was very impressed! They also have almost two pages long of appetizers! You have a lot to choose from at this place. They have great service and the sushi is delicious! They do not overdo it on the rice in the rolls, which I liked. I also ordered a hand roll, which was packed with fish! They are definitely not stingy with their fish and their prices aren't expensive. Great place for sushi!

So about 8 years has gone by between visits... If your in the SB area and spent any part of your 20/30's there you will know the CK. They are pretty simple food with good portions This location is one of the more locals spots as it is not in the tourist trap. I used to live a few blocks from here so just look for street parking. Even better McConnel's Ice Cream is across the street for a little sweet bite to top off your eggs n bacon.
One of my all time places to eat. Love the steak and eggs with a liquid breakfast to go with it. The food taste so fresh and perfectly cooked

Too pricey and their service sucks! The service is horrible! Food is decent. We ate here three times on different occasions just to see if the service will change but it was a disappointment. We'll never eat here again. Be warned! They had better service two years ago but now.. it's so bad not even the food will bring us back.

Ordered to-go: large hot n sour soup and moo shu pork. Brought home to SLO only to discover the soup was a cup (not large) and this was the saltiest moo shu I've ever had. Also the mushrooms weren't the traditional black, they were generic button. Overall disappointing (and way to take advantage of to-go customers). Pffft.
Delicious basic tacos. Fresh salsa, marinated meat....and all this for under 2$ a taco....what more can u ask for?? Definitely glad I stop by tonight for a quick light flavorful dinner
Wonderfully prepared Mexican cuisine.  We go the De la Vina location. The line moves fast. Burrito Mojado is delicious!  I like the selection of Salsas as well.

I used to go here for cheap delicious Italian food when I was living in Santa Barbara 10 years ago. I always order the same dish - the California Pasta. This past weekend, I decided to order the same dish and it tasted exactly the same as I remembered. I love this place! Will definitely order again during my next trip!

Pretty disappointing, Casa Blanca Restaurant.  Monday night arrive 7:05 PM.  We and at least three other couples who were standing in front of the hostess were all told it would be at least 10 minutes before anybody could be seated (despite dozens and dozens of empty tables inside and outside).  Seated at 7:15, we were then essentially abandoned on the patio.  Finally just walked away at 7:40 PM.  Thanks for nothing, Casa Blanca.  Go to the Sandbar Restaurant, everyone.  We did and were served drinks and dinner promptly!

Clearly the best Indian (-ish) buffet in town. Oddly the Flavor of India restaurant seems to get good reviews in the Independent, but in my opinion it does not hold a candle to the Himalayan Kitchen.  I get a sense that the food is, as the name implies; 'Himalayan' (i.e.Nepalese) more than it is classical Indian... but when we are in the mood for 'Indian' food...it certainly hits the spot.  It's one of our favorite restaurants in Santa Barbara.  Actually the food has been improving since we've been coming here.  It's now more flavorful with a mor appropriate level of spice.  The food here is very good in my opinion. I recommend this place and I think you'll like it!

Honestly, only making this review because I used to love what you guys were doing but I just see this place going downhill fast.  Wingman was definitely popping off when y'all first opened on Milpas.  It was new, it was fresh and the flavors were phenomenal.  The problems started when you guys started to change how the seasonings were done. (Which I feel is one of the very main and important factors that differentiated this spot from any other wing spot) But cutting to what's the MOST important part of this review... I have not been to wingman in a long time for the facts that the seasonings changed and also the inconsistency of how the wings are cooked. It wasn't worth it, having gmo sized wings that aren't cooked all the way in the inside. (Which can be pink sometimes) But now I see y'all got a Goleta location.  And what I find insane is now it costs for 30 wings, $75?????!!!?!!  Like that makes no sense at all.  I'm assuming this change in price was made to keep up for the rent now that y'all have the Goleta location.  In all honesty, wingman should have strictly stayed in Milpas.  I don't think anyone would want to pay that amount for only wings ? You can go to a high end restaurant with that money.  I'm not trying to hype up any other business but you can get 3 or 4 combos at wingstop with that price.  And it saddens me because I want to see this business strive and rise back.  But seriously, considering that the seasonings aren't the same, which the biggest disappointment that the sichuan isn't the same anymore and again the inconsistency with how the wings are cooked, it just does not seem worth it to eat here.  I would be beyond mad getting bad food here for the prices y'all are using now.  Again not bashing this business because I used to love it. But I just don't like what it's become now.  Y'all used to do 100 wings for 99$ why is it now 250?
This is a warm and intimate place where the food is European good.  I personally love the quiche Lorraine.  The baked goods are to die for.  Nice outdoor patio as well.

Do you like to wait 45 minutes for your food?  Do you prefer your food flavorless? Are you a woman who likes to be called Sir?   If so this is the place for you!  Pretty much everything about this experience was terrible. The food was bland and pale in color and it took almost an hour to get it. Feels like this place caters to the taste of Santa Barbarians, aka there's no flavor at all. The thai tea is basically orange water. A lot of the food in this town suffers from this and unfortunately TAP is one of those places. The servers were nice but inattentive, the restaurant wasn't very busy and I had to flag someone down every time I needed anything, including the two glasses of water I drank while I waited almost an hour for my food. I watched several parties arrive, order, finish their meals and leave before I got my food. Also, one of the servers called me sir, which as a woman with short hair I get that it happens, but no apology or even acknowledgement of his mistake which on top of waiting, annoyed me.  I'll stick to my other Thai choices in town that serve palatable dishes for less money.
You're probably not going here for the food which isn't bad enough to ruin a great location and nice experience.

It's been over five years since we visited Cadiz and we are thrilled about our return.   We loved this beautiful restaurant, its environment and food when it first opened but we were admittedly flustered with what Cadiz' intent really was (initially) as it seemed to waffle between a fantastic (and much needed) Santa Barbara upscale restaurant and that of a hip/happening bar.  We shared our feedback constructively via YELP in 2011 and hadn't returned since, however we had been recently lured by dear foodie friends who recently had an amazing experience at Cadiz and raved about the food, service and atmosphere.     We arrived for our reservation to be greeted immediately by the hostess.  She led us to our table and we were quickly enveloped by the lovely, warm interior just as we remembered it from our multiple visits years before.   Justin arrived to introduce himself as our waiter, ensure we were settled, walk us through the menu and suggest a few starter drinks and signature dishes to marinate on.    I savored the amazing San Sebastien (with the perfect kick of spice) and my husband was thrilled to find a local Pure Order IPA.  We were both extremely excited to see a LOT of local choices on the menu and Cadiz' creative collaborations with other complementary artisans around our local community (more on that later).  Justin took our order and was such a talented and gifted server.  He reminded us of the type of folks we've interacted with while frequenting brilliant restaurants in metropolis cities like LA, SF, NYC, Hong Kong, Sydney and Tokyo.  He did a fantastic job of balancing all facets of his role - he educated, advised, had fun, was serious and throughout it all, his professionalism exceeded Santa Barbara in the most perfect of ways (a great thing).  Justin is an extremely talented and passionate expert and a complete joy to interact with for our two+ hour date.  We nibbled on the house marinated olives (my mouth is watering again as I type this) until our starters arrived.  We thoroughly enjoyed the albondigas meatballs and beet salad starters followed by the cioppino and mushroom risotto main courses.  All were both perfectly prepared, sized, presented and consumed.  While a bit on the full side (Cadiz offers nicely-sized plates - a great value for the price) we did decide to splurge on a tiramisu made using (one of our favorite and local) Handlebar coffee beans.  Super yummy, light and nontraditional in its approach, the tiramisu was splendid and was aptly accompanied with a piping hot Handlebar Americano.  This was a great close to a thoughtful and truly well prepared meal from start to finish.  Owner Messina came by to introduce herself, check in and also admitted in our conversation that an earlier graduation reservation of 28 people had brought in an additional 41 people......no, that is not a typo - over 70 people for a 28 person reservation (!!!!!)  Even more impressive is that this previous event wasn't evident during our time there as the restaurant was well organized and our drinks and courses arrived in a timely fashion.  We let her know that Justin was incredible and although we'd been away for awhile that we'd most certainly be back in the short term.  Clearly they've taken some of the feedback to heart and are heartily owning the role of a long-standing and great Santa Barbara restaurant.  We couldn't be happier!  We'll be back soon.  Thanks again!

very decent fish and chips. the fish had a nice batter, and it was crispy and delicious. the tartar sauce was great. i think it had dill in it. the boyfriend had bangers and chips and also enjoyed his brittish meal. the staff, who are brittish, are friendly and helpful. it's a small cafe, and the tv's play soccer which adds to the experience. it's places like these that keep me craving the good stuff. next time we're in santa barbara we will definitely go here again.

Excellent service at the Mex Authentic. We came in to try it out and used our Axxess Card (buy one entree, get one free). The spicy cucumber jalapeño margarita was refreshing but a little sweet for my taste. Nice spice to it though. I had the chicken enchilada rancheras, which were good, but I wish had gone with one of the spicier options. Not bursting with flavor by any means. All of the staff were exceptionally helpful and on the ball. Excellent beer selection, including my favorite brew from Ballast Point, Grapefruit Sculpin (bottles, not draft). Great place overall. Looking forward to coming back and trying some more of their food.

I was wrong when I said is just another McDonald:)))))  The food is really a good quality, their veggie burger is delicious, the choices you can have with it are super great. Fires and fried green beans are super sides!   Try them!

The impossible burger is SO GOOD, and I'm not even a vegetarian! The only drawback is how crowded this place gets, but the food is good and they have lots of beer and drink options. Everyone leaves happy!

One of my favorites in Santa Barbara!  The decor and atmosphere is fantastic. Drinks are creative, I definitley recommend going during happy hour. The food is best part though!  Beef cheek stew was awesome. Desserts are really creative and I can never pass them up. This restautant is a must see!

Le meilleur que je n'ai jamais eu! I was very surprised to discover this little French gym right downtown. It reminded me of places in France I had been. I ordered the French onion soup and a half grilled cheese sandwich with aged cheddar and gruyere, onion jam (caramelized) on  sourdough.   It was so delicious I ordered one to go! It really was such a generous portion that I could barely finish the soup but I 'forced' myself and left a token sip of the broth in the .tureen. Full of onions, cheese, bread, and delicious savory broth. No skimping on their portions or the recipe.   The grilled cheese with onion jam was on toasted buttery sourdough and melted in your mouth! It was not too large or too much bread but just right to go along with the soup. It really was the tastiest grilled cheese I've ever had in my 71 years of life, and that's not an exaggeration!   I cannot wait to return here with friends to try several items i saw as the staff were serving others. It was quite a spectacle actually has several of them paraded by together with plateful's of fantastic looking foods presented beautifully. It reminded me of a fashion show but he might see on television in France showing there new creations off.   I commented to my friend that it looked like a parade! My kind of parade for sure! I found the price is reasonable and so will not be prohibited by that factor.   Another nice added touch is they bring your bill to the table in a little machine and you don't have to go check out somewhere or wait for the staff to go process your credit card and then come back. It's done right at the table and your CC never leaves your sight. I regret to inform you that   I did not try any of their pastries as I am on a New Year's resolution to cut them out till I takeoff my Covid weight and then some but I promise you as you will see in my pictures everything looks fantastic!   My friend ordered two eggs bacon and berries. She loved it but I can't imagine ordering some thing that ordinary in a place like this! However it was prepared well and generous portions as you can see by the picture. Great for low-carb eaters!

This review gives five stars for the continuity of the Rose Cafe into 2018. It is motivated by the review by M J. on 4/1/2018. We have been coming to the Rose Cafe for a number of years. The cook is the same and the waitress that comes in at 9:00 is the same. The building has been freshened, and the credit payment system modernized. The breakfast meals are the same as before. My in-laws know all the people and know Mexican food, and they see no substantial change or any degradation in quality of the food. As I stated the cook has not changed, so if you liked the meals before, you should like them now.   To many of us the tradition is important, and to repeat myself I give five stars for that.

Very very good Mexican food!! I enjoyed the shredded tacos and almost tried to steal my husband's burrito with halibut and shrimp. ..OMG!! Only downside was the food poisoning I got from the salsa bar (although very tasty Pico pre food sickness!). Very excited they are coming to my hometown of Thousand Oaks!

Today's order was soooo perfectly done, I must give this subway experience a five star!!   First time for us to order online, and I wish I knew the sandwich prep persons name to compliment them in their awesome job, but came out perfect! Online you can add extra portion size for some things, and/or order light on others.       Anyway, perfect job, ready on time....WONDERFUL zoo lunch for us!!! Thank You

We went here recently on suggestion by a local friend. We were in the area on way to Socal and wanted a nice Family Friendly place for dinner. The weekend we were there was an unusually busy weekend because of an Annual Tex Mex celebration they hold in SB every year. We luckily got in without reservation. The wait staff was friendly and accommodated us. They were very informative and place was busy but pleasant. We had started with Crawfish fried appetizer  and some of their bread. I had a Redemption old fashion. The Creole inspired menu was good and the portions were generous. I had ordered the Shrimp etoufee and Wifey had ordered the Gumbo. The Pasta my son ordered was vegetarian and had a spicy kick. My only complaint if I had one was that they took my drink away when I was paying the check. I was only 1/2 way through my drink. We will definitely be back to try more of their Cajun food when we are down in the Santa Barbara area.
Ive had better bagels than here. I give them credit for having alot of variety but i think the bagels could be a little bigger. The egg sandwhiches are good but the italian sausage is not.

Went there with visiting relatives for weekday lunch.  Inside the music was so loud it was painful.  Outside the glass walls amplify the voices and sound system to the point that we had to shout at each other.    Food is still good and the setting spectacular.
Hands down the best pozole and menudo in town, and one of the few places that has it 7 days week!!  Service is always quick and to go orders are smooth as well.
There s'mores Stout was really good. Normally I don't like stouts but they were very good. Will definitely have to come back.
Excellent food and excellent service. We had the seafood ceviche and the Queso Fundido.   Portions can be shared, as we did. Highly recommend and definitely coming back.

Amazing service ALL THE TIME! This is definitely our go to, there can be many restaurants but the service is ALWAYS ON POINT! Our server today, we have had her before, super nice and super attentive,Summer. Shout out! As she is great always has a great attitude.  Management take note as this employee is hard to come by!! We come here for the service and Summer has always been great!

This brunch spot is one that needs to be in history books. Everything is delicious. Need I say more? The eggs Benedict is definitely one of my favorites but everything that I've had is definitely a delicious treat. This place is great for a birthday or a cute brunch. The food is always tasty, the waitresses are super nice, and they have a good amount of outdoor seating. I will say that's there's always a line and reservations book up fast so book it early if you anticipate grabbing a bite here!

There are 31 choices on the menu and the prices are comparable to other AYCE Korean restaurants. Very good customer service as the staff works as a team and they are very attentive. They are very quick and there's not much wait between orders. If you're in SB, you should check this place out.

Amazing food. Beautiful location. Excellent service. Crista the owner takes great pride in making sure everything is perfect. Breakfast, lunch and private dinner parties. All top notch.  5 stars for sure

There for the first time toinight, with my friends.....was concerned we would be smushed against some people, but got a great table in the corner.  The service was PERFECT. Our server could not have been better. Perfect. Friendly,helpful, attentive...could not have been better.  We had Prosciutto di Parma with garlic, amazing fries and fried artichoke hearts....  then the Margerita Pizza with Buffalo Mozarella and the Umbra pizza with truffles......Umbra was best, very very rich......  then chocolate-hazelnut panna cotta and some great Italian coffe..  great food and the place is absolutely beautiful, just beautiful.   This is worth it!!

Pricey (this is Montecito) but excellent. The service here is top notch too.   I was a little skeptical because I don't like when pizza is charred - I think pizza aficionados call it 'leopard spotting' - but I asked for it without that and it was perfect. You can taste the care that was put into the pizza and I love that.
If your looking for a flavor explotion look no further!!! Dave's has everything you will ever want at an unbeatable price!! The best hot dog joint I have ever come across!

I had Renaud's as an official part of our Santa Barbara agenda because my wife had gained a recent appreciation for macaroons and Yelp helped me find this spot.  Before digging into the desserts, we started with a quick breakfast: a croissant for my wife and sausage, eggs, and toast for me (with salad too).  The croissant was very good - slightly smaller than the one we had at Andersen's on State but flakier and fluffier.  My dish was good... eggs were a little underdone but the whole thing was delicious when I combined it with bites from the croissant.  I should have tried the croissant breakfast sandwich!  We tried a couple cookies (crispy, tender, and sweet) and a macaroon (more like a brownie and less delicate than the ones we've had from the east coast).  All around nice blend of sweets and breakfast eats.

Your table is on the beach....feet in the sand...  I don't care if they served me refried beans that was 3 days old (which is parallel to the food I consumed here)....this place just killed it with the view...

I loved everything about my wedding cake from Crushcakes. We ordered the one tier 6' round and had it decorated with a simple white vertical bands design. Vanilla bean cake with vanilla buttercream frosting inside and a super white cheese cheese frosting outside. It was delivered on time, no issues or worries at all. Emily was super easy to work with. And pricing-wise they were by far the most affordable. Highly recommend!

Came here with a friend for breakfast. We both ordered the wheat germ pancakes; mine with strawberries and his with blueberries. Both were amazing. I literally had bits of strawberries in every bite, yet it didn't overcome the pancake. The scrambled eggs and potatoes on the side were mediocre, but the pancakes... oh my goodness, the pancakes! were so moist, tasty, and therefore enough to compensate. I liked that most of the tables were still shaded by the building in the morning. The view of the ocean wasn't spectacular, but it didn't matter. I just want to go back for more wheat germ pancakes! The only downside is parking. Bring a few dollar bills.

Love Hook and Press. We get three to share every month or so and have a little tasting party :) I am glad that they are moving into a larger space and really appreciate their involvement with local non-profits and local businesses. I think this establishment is a great example of something really special about SB

Wow~.This is a phenomenal new spot for fresh sashimi and sushi at top of the pecking order in town. Excellent quality and great service!   Sushi chef Buck helps guide you which fish are esp good and will make recommendations if you ask.  But all great quality.  I have not been disappointed with any orders yet.  A special mention for Chu-Toro, for sure !   Lastly, I'm anti-roll person ... and really avoid trying to order any rolls.  Period.  But my wife ordered the 'Don't cry' roll, given its growing urban folklore.   I tried it... and Bam !  I'm fan. Unique and sooooo good.  Warning though.  It's very spicy.. but it's tempura'd lightly and to perfection.. And just unbelievably that good.  Must try at least once, esp if u have a palate for spiciness.  Few caveats. Sushi bar seating only, which I enjoy and prefer.  No formal tables but if u need a table, rest of Public Market tables are available.  No alcohol license but one can get it anywhere else in Public Market and have it with the food

I could give 10 starts if I could!!!!! It took me back to my vacations in La Toscana. It's authentic delicious Italian food. Prices are good and the service is excellent. The outdoor dining area is really comfortable and cozy, and pet friendly!!! I love this place, I'll be back for sure.

We had reservations to eat at Toma on a busy Saturday evening. We arrived on time, and the restaurant was full, with a warm energy of people out on a Saturday night enjoying a good meal. The hostess greeted us with a smile and took us to our table which was ready right away. We were soon greeted by the owner, Tom, who recommended a cocktail - which we split and the bar was kind enough to serve into two glasses. We ordered the ahi tuna cones and the grilled octopus. The waiter brought a special steak style knife for the octopus but we didn't need it because it was perfectly cooked, very mildly charred and very tender.  The cones are delicious, served in a custom designed rack, and the toasted black and white sesame seeds a perfect compliment to the sweet ahi. We switched from cocktails to a 2012 Barolo recommended by the house, and it was a perfect compliment to our food. I am particularly fond of Barolo's, but this one was from a small family owned winery the owner personally visits. My partner ordered the shrimp gnocchi and I ordered the beef rib with mashed potatoes. The rib came with a super rich, intense sauce, that was fantastic with the potatoes.  The portion of rib was tender, gently cooked and more than enough to share. The gnocchi were like soft, down filled pillows of flavor....and carried the flavor of the shrimp perfectly. We finished off the wine, and had the local Mconnells ice-cream (three different flavors) and strawberry phyllo pastry desert. Service throughout the meal was on-time, but never intrusive. All in all, one of the best meals to be had in Santa Barbara. Toma is a little off the beaten track and has a loyal local clientele, but well worth the effort to find if you are visiting Santa Barbara.
Good food, but not my favorite atmoshpere. A bit trendy & yuppie, but if you're looking for that kind of social crowd before a nice dinner, you'd like this place.
Everyone loved this restaurant.  The fried rice on the breakfast menu was AMAZING! . The bowls were flavorful and a good size. I would have not expected this little gem in the best western hotel!

Just left Eureka and thoroughly enjoyed the service. Our waiter Max had to host our wild table of 14 and he was awesome. He was on top of every order and every special request (which you can imagine is a lot with 14 people). He kept all our drinks refilled and was pleasant and kind the whole time. In a season where it's hard to find good staffing, it was a pleasant surprise to have a great waiter with a great attitude. :-) oh, and the food is always a hit with our family! We'll be back!

Today(Sunday) I was craving for Albacore Tuna sandwich and I went to Natural Cafe near Costco in Goleta ( they are open for take out only amid this Corona Virus pandemic).  I have been their loyal customer for more than twenty years and as usual, their albacore tuna satisfied my cravings. They are so generous in their servings. The staff are nice and respectful. I highly recommend Natural Cafe to all.

I heard about this restaurant because the former chef at Sakana opened a new place. In short, this place exceeded expectations. The sushi and pressbox (yum) style is similar to Sakana, but chef Teru has more experience with hot dishes and it shows.  A highlight is the baked spicy eggplant, which is amazingly soft with a caramelized and spicy crust. Another is the sautéed mushrooms, which has a great umami flavor and good texture. One of the few places you can find enoki mushrooms in SB.   You can't go wrong with the press boxes. We came here and got all five on the menu. You'll love them.   Can't wait to come back here, my fiancée and I are smitten.

I was super excited to try this place. The were sold out of a lot of items and understaffed. The bathroom was so gross. The food was good. The Elvis was tasty even tho the banana bread was overcooked. The fingerling potatoes were fantastic. The biscuit with jam was great but would have been delicious hot or warm. The blueberry jam was delicious. Unlikely to return.
Great coffee and treats in a fun, local atmosphere. This is Santa Barbara at it's best! We've been customers for over 20 years and the quality never disappoints.

Well went back different experience these next visits I had been raving to brother Inlaw how great this place was first day nobody acknowledged is sat people that cut in front of us still no acknowledgement then sat us inside and forgot about us all four of us got up walked out next day went back much the se wow but food was still great service needs a little help

I came here last Sunday with some friends for my 21st birthday brunch and it was the best setting for this kind of celebration. There is an open dining area outside with tables, benches, and umbrellas that scan sit large parties perfectly. Bathrooms and more seating exists inside too. This place was very convenient because you order before you take your table, making it very accommodating for multiple people and their separate tabs.   This place also serves Handlebar coffee roasters and they make a bulletproof coffee which I plan to try sometime. But the Kombucha Sangria, made local in Santa Barbara by Conscious Kombucha (MY FAVORITE), made my birthday 5x better than any mimosa could have.  The food was fantastic, from the eggs benedict to the various flavors of toppings for toast. I cannot wait to come back.   I think it is important to mention that the drinks come out immediately so you can drink it while you wait for your food.  Although my food didn't arrive until 45 minutes after the fact and all of my guests had eaten already, I was still very happy with my experience here because everyone had a good time, the drinks were good, the service was friendly, and the food was bomb.

Here for a horse show and anticipated shack-type of food. No, this was REALLY good. Had a breakfast bagel with salsa. It was great! The salsa was awesome. By the way, the coffee was pretty good too.  Thanks!

We almost always go to the Lark for dinner, but these last 2 times we had bites here before heading to the big sister right behind it.  Right up front, I will say that I wish the wine list was better.  There are so many great SB wineries, I mean they are right there in the funk zone, they could show off the region.  Instead it was a simple choice of (3?) a couple of varietal options that were underwhleming (I would knock a 1/2* off for this, but not a full one).  Next time I will just pop open a bottle myself.  As for the food, they bomb.  We had the arugula salad this time and the artichoke (I think) last time to start.  Last time was a bit hazy because we were also tasting at the distillery.  Then we dove into the cheese pizza and it was perfect.  Not a margharita, style, more like a NY style, but just perfect with a thin crust and just the right amount and blend of cheese.  You even get the light sheen of grease on top.  Delicious.  We took most of it home (we did head into the Lark for post pizza bites) and the pizza heated up perfectly on the cast iron flat-top on the stove.

My first experience with this pizza place started off to a Rocky start, but the issues got resolved. Thanks to the owner Joe, he was very professional in handling the situation. Although, I have yet to try this parlor I am giving this place 3 stars. Only because of the way the problem was handled. But as to referring to taste, I will update my rating when I get to try it out. See you soon Persona's!

Was expecting a hole in the wall ...far from it in terms of atmosphere! Really nice ambiance, plenty of space and tables, and nice view of the street.  Got the beef taquitos and they were delicious and came so prettily arranged! The outside was nice and warm and you could tell it was handmade. My friend got the burrito mijado and loved. It. Cleaned his plate. He scarfed that shit down so fast I thought he was going to throw it right back up ...but no, and he had the hugest smile on his face afterwards!  Clean bathrooms with a 'vacant/occupied' sign. I appreciate these because I hate when people knock repeatedly or jerk the door handle when I'm in a public bathroom :(  Must try!

All the cookies were fresh this time.  I got the Chocolate Walnut Toffee cookie and the Atomic Cookie.  Both were delicious.  The Atomic Cookie this time seemed slightly different and better than the one I had at my last visit; I don't know if the recipe changed or because the cookie was stale last time.  I strongly recommend buying the Atomic Cookie if you love chocolate cookies and have trouble deciding on flavors.  The Okie Dokie Artichoke sandwich is still amazing!

I found this new super cute & inviting clean, cutely decorated Pink & White place on the edge of the trendy, ever-growing Funk Zone thanks to Yelp!!  It was lunchtime on a Sunday & I to eat something I craved!  It seems like every time I go to Santa Barbara which is pretty often, there's one or two new places to eat!   By the looks of the pics on this site from my fellow Yelpers & the great reviews, I thought I found just the place annnnnd, it was brand new! Open for only 2 months.  Of course I hadn't eaten all day and I was starving!  The thing about me is I rather not eat anything at all if I can't eat something that I exactly CRAVE at the moment! And I craved Asian fusion!  Tyger Tyger, according to Yelp looked exactly like my respite & it is right off of the 101 FWY, even better! I was driving up the Central Coast food shopping every Farmers Market & Gourmet store from Hollywood through SB & SLO County for a Thanksgiving gig I'm catering in Big Sur from LA (my first hometown) back to my newest hometown, Cambria, I had to stop in Santa Barbara, my last hometown of 6 years, (also half way home).  I parked in the adjoining lot and saw some happy customers seated outside devouring & sharing their colorful & fresh looking Eats, I couldn't wait to get in the door!  Luckily I made it in between rushes.  I had anticipated ordering the Bahn Mi Sandwich at first- that's what I actually typed in Yelp Search , then according to the rave reviews on Yelp, couldn't decide between the Crispy Rice Salad w/seared albacore & the Weeping Brisket Salad...  I ended up ordering the the Northern Thai Pork Curried Noodle & a Hibiscus Iced Tea.  They serve great local wine & beer on tap too by the way!  The service was friendly & the entire staff was smiling!  The good Energy of this place made me happy and  I sat at the first stool next to the now, busy entrance.  While waiting for my food to arrive I went over to Monkey Shine, a super California & NY/Vegas FOOD (as in Momofuku's Milk Bar, etc.) trend soft-serve Asian ice cream bar (w/vegan options) This is in the opposite corner, all within the same space (room) that also includes a Coffee Roasting facility & a Tea  bar.  Monkey Shine serves flavors like Toasted Rice & Thai Spiced Chocolate, Ginger & Black Sesame, w/several choices of toppings served in a pressed lucky fish  shaped cone &/or Chinese little to-go box.  I tasted the toasted rice & the black sesame, both were delicious  and not at all too sweet, a lot like the stuff they serve at the new super trendy milk cake/'crack pie' etc., 'Milk,' on Melrose I had just sampled 2 days before. All in all Tyger Tyger I give a big !  Bravo  Chef Daniel Palaima! You hit it on point! I cannot wait to revisit this place w/friends & try everything!

Great restaurant!  Brought my GF here for dinner and had a great experience.   Service was awesome.  Server was very friendly and had the right amount of friendly banter, not too much to make it awkward, not too little to seem unfriendly, perfect. Also, the manager checked on us several times to make sure everything was to our liking.  Cocktails: GF had the Toma Manhattan and I had the Italian Stallion.  Both very good.  Very strong drinks.  We each enjoyed our cocktails more than the other.  Food: Started with the Marrow which was really good, but heavy.  It's served on crostini with beef cheek which is super savory.  Fresh peeled grapefruits are served on top to lighten it a bit.  And of course the bone marrow, super fatty, greasy, deliciousness.  This got messy and heavy but was really good.    We then split the Black Spaghetti.  This was definitely the highlight.  Spicy, buttery deliciousness.  Good portion size as well.   Finally, we ended the meal with the Warm Chocolate Torte.  Basically a chocolate lava cake, super rich, sweet, with Vanilla bean ice cream and an almond thing in the middle.    Price is fair, location is great, service is fantastic, and food is amazing!  2 thumbs up!

I was hesitant on writing this review but knew people needed to be aware. We had placed an order to be picked up. We wanted to use our Axxess card but the lady stated they were not accepting Axxess. Why offer a coupon if we aren't allowed to use it? I was going to pay for the food without being allowed to use my Axxess BUT, then noticed the server that was bringing out the food was not wearing a mask or any PPE to serve the food!! Bummed I saw this action by the server because we will not be returning. I wanted to support but will not support if not wearing a mask while serving food!!
Delicious food fun place to hangout! Great service, easy to park! I recommend the the chicken wings.

My man and I came to Jill's Place for the first time to celebrate his birthday...and we were very pleased!!  First off, you get a lot of food for the price of an entree. It comes with garlic bread, delicious split pea soup, sauteed veggies, and your choice of potato/spaghetti/rice. My bf got the rib-eye, and it was delicious and cooked to perfection. My filet mignon was also really good.  The menu is pretty extensive, boasting a long list of appetizers and a good wine list. My bf, though surprised that there weren't more beers on the menu, really enjoyed the beer he had.  For dessert, we tried the Shalhoob Banana, which was SO good. I highly recommend this amazing dessert.  Also, our service was perfect! Our waitress tended to us when needed, but otherwise, let us enjoy our meal. Friendly and welcoming.  We will be back.
Wow, this is the best sandwich in Santa Barbara (Order the #1) -- I love the vibe of this place, and the food simply blew me away.
Tried their breakfast sandwich, bread, peanut butter cookie and iced tea = all delicious! Worth the wait.

My visit was pre COVID.  Perfect. This place is SOOOO good. Their beers are certainly above average, the decor is out of this world BEAUTIFUL, service is so attentive and friendly and it's got the perfect beach vibes. Came here early enough that there was no wait and my husband and I snagged a seat outside after getting our sampler. They also have food (vegan!) and it looked deliciously though we didn't indulge. I've had their food at Modern Times Dankness Dojo in DTLA and it's super good so if it's anything like that here I imagine it's def worth trying.
I am saddened that Buenos Aires closed it's doors for good. Good luck to all the most excellent staff that made us feel at home when we would visit. Been going for 15 years. God Bless

Just when I thought I couldn't find a spot that was over a hundred years old, in the middle of nowhere, that had juicy tri-tip, and cold beer, I stumbled into this place. Not really. We've wanted to come here forever. Fellow Yelpers had been posting the place, the food, their experiences, and we finally got to go! It did not disappoint. Great detour off the beaten 154 will get you all filled up to drink way too much wine.  If you're not sure where it is just follow the windy canyon down to where all that smoke is coming from. Yep. That's it. The century old building that used to serve as a stop on an old stagecoach line. There is a restaurant, bar/music shack, craft shop, and a head on the complex. We grabbed a few beers from the shack to hydrate before ordering.   We walked into the restaurant and were greeted by the bar. Ordered there and stepped outside to find a table. Apparently, you can order sandwiches at the shack and get a ticket you can take to the guys on the grill. I was unaware, so we waited for our names to be called as the keep stuck his head out the front door of the restaurant. Tri tip sandwich with slaw and an order of onion rings. Both great choices as they were devoured quickly until we were down to the last ring, and then it's a standoff. Crispy and crunchy without being too greasy. I forget who won. The tri tip was juicy and moist. They have a horseradish ranch and bbq sauce next to the grill. If the horseradish ranch was bottled and sold, I would have grabbed a dozen. So good. Slaw was also tangy and tasty.  Parking looks like it can be a problem, escpecially on the weekends. A short walk may be necessary, unless you luck out like we did. Great place to finally visit. It won't be our last!

Seafood on the pier!! If you want a view, you better sit outside. They have live crab and lobsters in a tank and prices depend on heir specials at the time.   To be honest, I feel like the lobster is a bit overpriced, but I'm from LA so I'm used to cheaper lobster at Chinese places.   Either way, the food here was good. Lobster roll did not have butter like the ones I've had at other places, but this actually makes it much better because you don't get the overwhelming and heavy butter taste. Crab sandwich was good too and has a lot of crab meat. The clam pasta was good too but got a bit too heavy towards the end.   Overall, enjoyable meal.

Heard about Mr B through social media and decided to check it out both because their menu sounded really interesting and because they offered curbside pickup which is an important option to have during these times.  It was a bit hard to find out information about them, but they have all of their info up on their Google page (I uploaded the menu that they posted over here).  The woman who took my order over the phone was very nice, and everything was very easy to order.  She gave me detailed instructions on where the restaurant is since you have to go to the back of La Cumbre Plaza (the parking lot on Hope close to the south side of the plaza).  Curbside pickup went really well, and everyone wore masks to deliver the food.  The people who work here seem really nice.  The food was pretty good!  I personally ordered the chicken shawarma tacos, shawarma bread, baba ganouj, baklava, kunafeh, and basbosa.  They were all very flavorful.  The chicken shawarma is well-seasoned and flavorful, although somewhat different than what I was expecting.  The baba ganouj was excellent, and they included homemade pita chips to go with it.  The shawarma bread was nice, and I liked the dough a lot.  The three desserts (baklava, kunafeh, basbosa) were all very tasty.  Some french fries came with the tacos, but they were really undercooked and didn't seem to be fried.  Also, I should mention that everything was really thoughtfully packaged and survived the 15-ish minute trip back to Goleta.  Overall, I think this is a great start for a restaurant that has a lot of potential.  I think that Mr B fills an important gap in the Middle Eastern food scene in Santa Barbara, a city that has many restaurants but only a couple of Middle Eastern restaurants.  I'm looking forward to ordering from them again in the future (I'm eyeing the Moroccan breakfast for next time).

I enjoy the Mesa Cafe and always have. It's a great locals spot, good food and nice bar. I like to get myself a spicy bloody mary on the cold days and sit outside on the patio under a nice heater! I prefer the lunch/dinner compared to the breakfast. They have a delicious Mediterranean pasta with yummy artichoke hearts, tomatoes, olives and chicken that comes with garlic bread. (It's filling!). My favorite dinner appetizer are the avocado egg rolls that come with a chipotle dipping sauce and a spicy wasabi sauce, they're delicious! The prices here reasonable and the staff are always very friendly. I recommend that you check them out for yourself if you have never been.

Excellent food. Really neat vibe and atmosphere.  Cool hundred year old olive trees in back courtyard.  Fried olives were awesome. I had pork tacos, excellent. We also had the rock cod with avocado butter. What s beautiful presentation and flavors!

First visited Institution Ale in Camarillo a couple of years ago. I was so happy when I heard they were opening on State Street!  Their pizzas are pretty good! They usually have a new special every month. Their soft pretzels are freaking phenomenal + spicy cheese sauce and IPA mustard! Seriously, best soft pretzels in Santa Barbara.  Beers are good, too. Love their selection. Only complaint is that it gets so loud in their. Hard to hear yourself sometimes, lol.  They're open late and usually always preeeetty busy so plan accordingly.
My favorite breakfast place in town, a real dinner feel! I recommend getting the Monti Cristo sandwich, it is divine!
Had dinner with a couple of friends and let me tell you the mimosas are amazing! Our server Maja was the best and we loved the space !!

Stonehouse restaurant is nestled on the San Ysidro Ranch in beautiful Montecito, CA in Santa Barbara County. It is just about 45 minutes away from the Oxnard/Ventura area & about 1 1/2hr way from LA.   The drive from Ventura County has a beautiful ocean & mountain view just before going through Carpinteria, where there is a lot of construction for the expansion of the 101 freeway. This expansion will make it possible for many more people to come to Santa Barbara with ease & alleviate traffic issues they have.  The San Ysidro Ranch is located off of the San Ydidro exit on the 101 & you need to take a right to get there, but if you accidentally make a left, you will find a beautiful beach among massively stunning Santa Barbara beach homes. So, if you aren't in a rush, GO EXPLORE! You won't regret it.  Once you get to the STONEHOUSE signage you will enter a beautiful brick-paved rode. There will be lavender lining the roads on either side & you get the feeling that something very special is about to happen. All I can say is it feels like there is a lot of history here & there is!  Presidents, celebrities, millionaires/billionaires, famous people have dined here! I've done research that confirmed what was told to me & President Kennedy & Jackie have stayed at the hotel here for their honeymoon, so you know you're in for an interesting experience.   When dining at Stonehouse you will valet your vehicle at the round about just at the entrance of the restaurants & gardens & WOW!!!   There was a stunning arch way with greenery above. And after you pass that there is a wall of green & beyond that an outdoor dining area, just as beautiful as the pics I've seen!   We grabbed a drink in a dining space that I can only describe as the most elegant looking cave. Trust me, it's far from a cave, but it dimly lit & has a feeling of intimate dining. You're transported in what you'd feel to be a space in Europe & not here in Santa Barbara, but it is. There was a bar at the back with a very well informed bartender. It looked to be a bar stocked with very exotic & rare spirits & liquors. Both bottles that were familiar & unfamiliar to me lined the walls elegantly. And although the room was inviting, we weren't to dine there. We were headed upstairs with our drinks (805 beer for my husband & a strawberry mojito for me) ordered at the bar.  The outside of the building (house) is quaint & looks to be just any ranch house you'd expect, but once you walk in you see a lot of wood. It's built in a craftsman style, which has a pronounced style featuring the architecture & most likely local wood. There are comfortable looking old leather chairs, a fire place & styled elegantly.   Walk past this room & you enter an indoor dining area, that leads to an outdoor dining area,  that has a beautiful view!!! (See my pics)  We were seated but I could not sit! I was walking around, drinking everything in. My eyes & senses were scanning everything & soaking it in like a sponge! The outdoor dining space was covered in a canopy of bamboo & twinkle lights that turned on once the sun started to go down. Each table was covered in white, starched table cloths & old school oil lamps were on each table.   Finally, once we were seated, they gave us a platter of olives & radishes (from their garden) & we were also given bread. We ordered. My husband had the Steak Diane (beautiful presentation at the table with the cognac mushrooms) & I had the potato-crusted halibut. The food was outstanding! I did find the parsnip purée to have a natural sweetness that I liked very much, but I needed a little bit more salt on the fish because of it (just my personal preference).   If they'd let us, I think we would have licked our plates, but we refrained.   We ordered a lemon Meyer tart first & shared that, then ordered the ricotta cheesecake with blueberries & that was equally decadent! OH MY GOODNESS! What an experience!  Dining here is a wonderful experience! We even walked around the property to take pics & see more of this famous estate.   I will be returning for brunch one day.   Before I do, I want to share my tips & suggestions for making this a great experience.  * Make reservations * Research the menu & decide ahead of time what dishes & drinks you'd possibly order * If you have questions about the food, call the restaurants * If you can afford to, stay at the hotel.  * If you cannot afford the hotel, there are less expensive options, but keep in mind they are still expensive * Dining here has a 'smart casual' attire (no jeans & flip flops) but is not inforced * Dark jeans & button up shirts are ok * Have cash to tip valet. * Bring a camera or a camera phone with plenty of memory * Bring enough cash to dine or credit card! Two people can easily be $200 as it was for my husband & I. * To avoid a steep bill avoid ordering drinks, appetizers & side salads ($16/person).  * Our bill, include tip was $265.

I know there is often a line to get into this place, so I went right at opening on a weekday. Still had to wait a bit to be seated, but it wasn't long.
