The waterfront view here is great, so I can see why people come here. Ordered the oysters Rockefeller, the garlic baked clams, and the clam chowder. I preferred the oysters over the clams but only by a thin margin. The food was solid but I wasn't blown away, and the service was friendly. Even without the place being filled, the servers seemed a bit harried. Parking can be found in the nearby lot.

The menu prices on Yelp are completely incorrect.  EG, Eggs Rose, listed here at $10.75 is $15.50.  This seems terribly dishonest to me and potential diners should be forewarned.  The food here is good, but it is far more expensive than Yelp and the restaurant owner have shown.

Dave's dogs is the best place in town for comfort food/American style food. The hot dogs are unique, and quite filling. It's also the only place in town that managed to take care of my pregnant fried pickle craving!  The prices are fair, and I love taking my kids here for a bite to eat where I know everyone will clear their plate. This location on Turnpike is across from a high school, so depending on the day/time there are sometimes quite a few teens in here.   A fun bonus is they carry the sweet/spicy candies at the register as well for purchase!

Came for the Kaiseki a few weeks ago, and overall had a really great meal for two.   Service is very attentive as it should be, and the explanations the waitresses give for each dish helps to appreciate and understand what you're eating next.  Every dish was well made, my personal favorites were the Black Cod Soup and Sashimi course. The portion of the full course is well planned out - we were both nicely full after dessert. Though some courses are smaller, the steamed and grilled dishes tend to be larger.  Still, I would say they left space to improve purely in the sense that for charging 135/person, people expect to be mindblown. I thought the oysters inside an orange was the most creative dish of the night but nothing particularly surprised me.  I will be back in the future when they change up the menu for seasonality again!

This is a great mexican food place.   Everything is tasty.  Family owned & delicious.  It's a real santa Barbara classic.  If you're looking for old-school mexican food then this is a perfect spot.  I hope you like it.

I thought this was going to be a tourist trap, but it's actually pretty good! The seafood here is very fresh, clean, and delicious. I recommend trying their shrimp cocktail, I don't know how but they have a juicy and tender, yet firm/snap quality to it that I haven't had in any other shrimp cocktail before. The cocktail sauce with the horseradish mixed in is also veryy good, I even dipped my fries in them. Also there's a lot of places/piers all claiming to have some of the best clam chowder, but this place definitely does it right.

A wonderful and delicious new restaurant!   We've been to Mollie's twice now in the last couple weeks.  Totally a 5 star experience on all fronts.   Service was excellent, the food was fabulous - fresh pasta bolognese, I loved so much I had it the second visit, too - and the Veal was awesome.  A simple Mista salad, split between the two of us, was fresh and delicious.    They were waiting for their liquor license approval, and offered us glasses of wine on the house - much appreciated and also delicious.   Bravo!  Mollie's is top of our list for dinner in Santa Barbara.

Always a class act, this is the new kid on the block that has been good since day one and has just gotten better. I have been here many times and have not had a bad experience yet. The food is delicious, though the menu might be a little bit limited, they seem to focus in on making every dish perfect. The ambience is wonderful. It is amazing how they can be so bustling yet not feel tense and still there is a mellow vibe that is maintained. The last time that we were there we were seated in the bar area for the first time and usually I do not like to be seated in the bar area because it is noisy. However, to my pleasure it was very relaxed, the service was wonderful and it was almost communal seating. On one side of us was a happy fun family and on the other side a mother and daughter from Huntington Beach. It was a very fun atmosphere and we met some very cool people. The restaurant staff is always professional and friendly and you always feel like you are being taken well care of. I highly recommend. If you have a large party there are options in the main restaurant where there is a private room that is quite lovely with a closed door option for meetings.

We eat here all the time and the food, service, etc is great. Had dinner last Friday and ordered the prime rib, asked for medium and it was almost raw. Very disappointed, usually a great dinner but not last night!

Taffy's is great! They've been in SB for a while, and consistently have awesome pizza. I've had the pepperoni, cheese, and Canadian pizza, and they've all been great. We also really like their bread sticks as well. They have a great game area inside (during non-COVID times), and a really nice patio in the back.   Their service is also awesome. I picked up our food and totally forgot to bring a mask to wear inside, but they let me pay over the phone and brought the food outside.  Overall, I've had a great experience every time I've visited, and definitely recommend it!

This sushi restaurant is seating both inside and out. Wonderful service. Excellent selection of both fish and vegetable options. Don't forget dessert! Red bean ice cream tempura was to die for. We'll definitely be back. I can't tell you what took us so long to get here.

It's quite good.  Try the fire roll.  It's the best thing they have.  It's spicy and wonderful.  No other sushi place I've been to has anything quite like it.  Seriously, it's piled on fish and spicy goodness.  You'll want to eat it in 2 bites, but open wide like the slut you are!  It's rude to eat sushi in 2 bites.  I love the little rooms if you have a big party.  Volcano roll was weird.

Super crowded with rude drunk patrons...but it's a cool space with good BBQ fare.   I recommend the off the cob street corn which was delicious and the tri tip tacos. Really did the trick...filled the belly with tasty tidings.  Drink some water too.  You'll thank me when you wake up without a headache.

Location - This classy location on upper state street has gorgeous architecture and new buildings. Very clean! Parking is plentiful and close by.  Atmosphere - the outdoor patio, where we sat, was covered, heated, and very comfortable. It was beautifully decorated, I wish there had been a little more light so I could appreciate all the colors in the amazing food.  Food - the complimentary bread was authentic French bread. So yum! The three scallops trio was a fun taste adventure. I'd never had raw scallop before and had some hesitations, but it blew me away! So fresh and delicious. My fiancé favored the risotto preparation and my dad, the fava bean one. A house divided! Just goes to show how good each one is. I came here to try duck for the first time and it exceeded my expectations. Perfect cook, juicy, delicious flavor and OMG the veggie mix underneath was to die for. The wine reduction was executed flawlessly. Best sauce pairing I've ever tasted. My fiancé tried lamb for the first time here and loved it. When I asked him what came with it he said, 'I don't even know what that beauty in my mouth was but it was uhggggg so good'. I think that says it all. But it didn't end there! The molten lava cake with raspberry (my favorite flavor convo) was gooey goodness and even though I'm not a huge fan of lemon, the lemon blueberry cake was so quality even I could get into it.  Service - our server, Adam, was on top of everything, knew the menu in and out, and gave us a great intro to Bouchon's pride in their local drink list.

A pretty good place. I ordered the all American breakfast with french toast. A basic choice all things considered but I really did enjoy the my french toast. The bread was soft and still stayed moist after pouring syrup over it, not getting soggy. It looks to get pretty busy but I was up early enough that I missed the crowd. It's a good place to also grab a pastry and a coffee if you're not in the mood for a big b-fast in the morning hours.

Super tasty and reasonably priced too. I love the asada and the el pastor. The tortas and chicken tinga gorditas are delicious too. The salsa bar has some kick too. Try the green and the smoky red one if you like the heat.. The only drawback is parking so I usually end up a block or more down.
This place is the funnest bar in Santa Barbara  county without a doubt! They have something for everyone. The atmosphere can not be beat. The food and drinks are good for the prices.

Stopped in for happy hour. Showed up around 3:20 because after searching 'happy hour' in the reviews here they said 3-6pm. I'm thinking maybe they recently changed it, because everyone we encountered asked if we were there for happy hour & told us it started at 4. We decided to have some wine & wait. Once happy hour came around we ordered the shrimp cocktail, fried calamari, and crab cakes. Glad we waited, everything was $5-7 cheaper than regular price. And wine by the glass for $4.75 is great! Loved everything we ordered. The only complaint would be the sauces that came with the calamari just didn't go with calamari, so we asked for tartar sauce which was tasty!   Service started out great but then pretty much took a nosedive. Our waiter didn't come back at 4 like he said he would, once happy hour started. Others near us were waiting until 4 also. He checked on other tables randomly & we had to flag him down to order at 4:15.   We love Lure (first time at this location & first time coming for happy hour). The decor is lovely & feels light & beachy. The food is always great. We will be back.

This place is hidden on the Eastside of Santa Barbara, it's one of the best Mexican style seafood places in town. Their cóctel de camarón is tasty and full of shrimp, definitely recommend, also they a great satisfying packed in ceviche tostada. Parking can be a hassle but so are a lot of places in Santa Barbara unfortunately.

One of the absolute best Mexican restaurants in SB! Santo Mezcal offers classic dishes with a modern twist, giving it quite the flare. Service was excellent, food was delicious, and drinks were tastefully bold (the blood orange margaritas are bomb). This place definitely lives up to its raving reputation and I will be coming back soon!   Pro tip: Because they don't offer reservations, be sure to put your name in at the front before you are starving!

REBAR, WHAT IS GOING ON!!!!!   You had the best cold brew in town!  I was willing to spend $4+ dollars every weekend morning after yoga.  The Iced Vanilla Lattes were amazing, and then the last two times it tastes nothing like vanilla or latte or even coffee.  I actually threw both away.  The first time I thought it was a fluke, but then it was exactly the same the next time. Let's fix whatever is happening, but until then, I guess I have to find a new place for my weekend coffee.

I am sorry to leave this review! I had the crispy chicken tacos. I did not appreciate the dead lettuce! The food was extremely bland. I had to add jalapeños for flavor!  My friend on the other hand, ordered the omelette. She said it was a 3/5. Food arrived cold. Maybe that was a Dasher issue. The containers in which the food arrived, was flimsy, and falling apart.

Best steak nachos in SB. Would give it 5 stars but the service tends to lack when busy. We usually order multiple items and usually they don't come out together. One being steak nachos and the other being Caesar salad. The salad take a while to come after the nachos.

We enjoyed our food but unfortunately we had to wait over 30 mins to get it. We were ready to go next door to Harry's and get some bread and salsa to keep us occupied. Will be back just to get dessert.
Excellent ramen - rich broth.  I had shoulder, generous portion and really flavorful meats

Not sure why it took me so long to finally try this place out, but I'm very glad I did!! The Stella Burger may not look like much in the photos and some of you may not be fans of blue cheese as I was, but I'm now converted and hallelujah!! I don't normally order burgers at nice restaurants and I avoid all blue cheese things, but this burger came highly recommended by a very credible source---a professor in company.   You guys, it's absolutely the best burger I've ever had. Umami Burger has nothing on this place. Don't change anything about it, choose medium rare, and be ready for some juicy chin dribblings. Also, no guilt. Be selfish and don't share.  Ok another note, this place is super adorable on the outside. A little cottage with lovely romantic exteriors, but the dining interiors could use more touches of rustic charm and cozier furniture. It was rather somber and dark inside, even on a beautifully sunny day.

Best Secret in Santa Barbara!!!  You feel like you left the modern world and stepped back in time to a simpler more peaceful world.  I love the rustic feel this place has and was blown away at how great the food is I honestly was not expecting it but boy was I wrong.   You honestly can't go wrong with anything you order.   If you are in Santa Barbara and just want to check out for a bit alone or with friends and escape this is the place...

Amazing options, tastes, experience and pairings. Very spoiled by the service .. Sam is fun! And the food, my tongue is in heaven .. can't say enough about my dining experience  Thank You Black Sheep .. I will return soon
Yum. We always have good food here!  Friendly fast waiters quick service.  Worth a visit!
Great authentic Japanese restaurant! The service is good and the food is delicious! My favorite dish is the neb... udon soup.

This has become our favorite place for a great meal at a good price. We had regarded it as mediocre in years past, but then decided to try it with the Axxess card one evening and went Wow! We keep coming back now because of the excellent Italian cuisine, and especially the handcrafted pastas.

It's hard to find a Vegan restaurant in Santa Barbara, but Mesa Verde fulfills this niche by offering quality and tasty foods. Although the prices are a bit high (especially for dessert), the food tastes great. The service can be improved as it was below my expectations (for the amount of price you're paying). I would definitely come back to this place again. Oh yeah, Make sure to try the pizzas, they're fantastic.

Unless you are a regular, they are very aloof. I sat at the bar and had to beg for the bartender's attention. He was too busy serving and talking to his regular customers.   The food is good, but I don't like being made to feel like I should feel privileged to be allowed to eat there.

Keto brownies for the win!!! I used to eat here at least 4 times a week and i stopped when I started my keto journey. Since they have paleo dessert options I thought maybe they'd start accommodating keto at one point in their pastry case and I finally called yesterday and THEY DO!! So, so happy  thank you savoy

Classic delicious breakfast sandwiches. The only bummer is their iced coffee is more like iced tea- too watery. And I will say it is slightly overpriced- I don't come here that much because of the price but so enjoy their sandwiches when I do.
Had a bad experience with the oysters there. They were about a 2/10 and the server laughed when I said I wouldn't eat them.
I only come for their happy hour. Its mon-fri 3-5 I believe. And its $1 tacos and $3 margaritas and $2 tecate cans. Perfect for a broke college student. Minus a star for ambiance.

I've been to both Jane locations on multiple occasions and am quite the fan.  The new location in Goleta is beautiful, but the downtown location has its own charm.    The food has always been top notch.  It's hard to get in on the weekends downtown at least but I've been lucky for the most part.  There isn't really a place to wait downtown, so as a walk in it can be difficult to wait for a table.    The red rings are interesting and I always order them for the table.  The winelist is very good and a bottle of Justin has always been available so far.  The desserts are amazing. I've had their steak a few times and it is always perfect.    The noise level can reach staggering heights so I like to sit upstairs if I can.  Jane is my go to SB restaurant and I'm so happy that they have the Goleta location now.  Open for lunch and dinner, you can't go wrong.

OMG Tri Tip Sandwiches!!! That's all you need to know about this place.  To be fair, it's a great hangout and people watching spot. Entertainment abounds here! Crowd pleasing bands perform so you can line dance and shake your booty or co-mingle outside. Beer selection is good. Figueroa Mountain Brew on tap? Yes please!

Opal is one of my favorite restaurants in SB! In fact, I have been enjoying their food and service since they first opened. I love that they have a spectacular wine list that is equally as diverse as their menu. I have never had a bad meal in all my years visiting this local gem.. In fact, every year my husband and I share our anniversary at Opal for it holds a special place in our hearts and an even more loving moment in our relationship. No matter what happens, Opal will always maintain be a special lace in our hearts and our stomachs. For American Fusion or that picky eater you hate eating out with, Opal is perfect for you!

It's good not as amazing as the reviews say but a good meal. My husband liked his regular sushi. Really beautiful ambience. Not a ton of non fish items. I would love to see more vegetarian options. The vegetarian rolls were eh - nothing to return for. The chicken skewers were really bland and dry. Very disappointing and would not recommend.

I am so thrilled to find Uncorked during the pandemic. We have ordered gourmet takeout dinners twice and they are phenomenal: perfectly tender salmon, abundant, smoky brisket, incredible ahi salad, chocolaty desserts. The amazing thing is the price, which is about the same as pizza takeout for a five-star meal. I received excellent advice at the wine counter about a local Pinot noir to go with scallops. The service is wonderful. These are creative local businesspeople working tremendously hard during a challenging time. Let's support them enthusiastically.

Beautiful, beautiful, beautiful, simply beautiful.  If your looking for a place that best illustrates California's coastline, this is the place. It's like a vacation from a vacation, but it's in our backyard. When you arrive on the property, it can easily feel like you're in Hawaii or on some tropical oasis. Bacara definitely has a special feel about it as soon as you drive into the property. We decided to take a daycation trip to Goleta and I'm so glad that we found ourselves at Bacara. We decided to have lunch and a few cocktails at the restaurant over looking the pool and ocean. Our drinks were refreshing. I ordered the refreshing cucumber drink and the hubs ordered some fru-fru drink, both very good.   I really enjoyed the vibe while being on the hotel premises. One would think it was really ritzy-pritzy but I thought it was a fun-mixed crowd. The pool area is breath-taking, please refer to my pics. It's almost out of a movie, with the view that your presented standing at the top stairs of the pool. So serene.   After our lunch we walked around the outskirts of the hotel. There is one trail that walks you around the side of the hotel along the ocean and it's just gorgeous. It was nice to see the many hotel rooms, who's views face directly into the Pacific Ocean, it'll be nice to stay in one of those someday. I'm looking forward to the next daycation to Bacara...

This place is a gem! They have amazing locally inspired creative dishes unlike any other restaurant in Santa Barbara. They have a beautiful patio that is great on a nice day and a cozy indoor dining room for cooler evenings. Their staff is friendly and knowledgable and they have a great selection of quality Beers and wines to choose from.  They are always changing up their menu, but I highly reccomend starting with the chefs plate ( sample of all of their amazing appetizers) and ending with the 'movie snacks' which is their version of buttered popcorn creme brûlée and homemade milk duds.
What a gorgeous property. Always seems to be weddings going on here. I can see why. The spa is amazing and I highly recommend getting lost amongst the property. Can't wait to return!

Great location, good service, but food is missing something, maybe a good chef? The first time I came around this place was because I wanted to try something different in Santa Barbara. It was recommended by one of the hotels on State Street. And since I always wondered about this place, I dared to try it. I didn't like it.  The fish I ordered was fresh, the salad was fresh, my husband's burger was okay, and my mom's pasta was average. Prices were not bad either. Environment was meh.  I know they have entertainment on the patio sometimes, but it is a bit small compare to the rest of the restaurant.  I went to Eladio's a second time, not by choice. I tried another salad. It was better than the one I had before. Would I go back to eat there? No, if I want to eat good, I'll go somewhere else. It is a bit of a tourist trap in my opinion.  If someone suggest let's go have some Margaritas for happy hour, I am there. But I haven't heard any of my friends say that yet.

This rating is based on service only   Very simply, I stopped by Yellow Belly on my way home for work. A customer of mine had remarked about a particular menu item and Friday evening, on my way home from work, I dropped in (no reservations are taken). It was late so  I asked when their hours were. When the hostess said that they opened  at 2:00 on the weekends, I said I'd be coming through on Sunday. Making a special trip from the Mesa, I got to the door at 3:50 and was told nicely but firmly  that they were no longer serving- there was a staff party about to start. With customers still eating, it seemed to be an arbitrary decision. No mention on Friday evening, no sign that was evident - this suggests either sloppy  attention to detail and/or disregard for the customer. Is this worth a special trip? Having been turned away, I simply don't know.

Great restaurant!  We come to Santa Barbara often, but never make it this far up state street. We sat on their patio with our dog, they have heaters so we were very comfortable.  We came during happy hour, so I had the happy hour mai tai. Not the best, but good for the price. We had some happy hour apps: buffalo chicken mini quesadillas - good but very spicy, and the Kalua sliders - very good! They also brought us a plate of fries because we were first timers. At first I though ok fries, cool, but there's fries are SO GOOD! Highly recommend!   Then we were still a bit hungry so we shared the break point burger. AMAZING. Then to finish we shared the kalua our, very good!   On top of the great food and atmosphere the best part was the service! From the host to the waiter, to the multiple managers who checked in with us, everyone was incredibly friendly and accommodating! I highly recommend.

Best steakhouse in town! For the quality of food you get the price is right. The best surprise you get is when they bring the housemade split pea soup. I don't even like peas but this stuff is perfection. The staff is always nice and professional and the meat is delicious.

Yona special deluxe is the way to gooooo. Three different types of queso tacos with consomé. They have beer and sangria as well. You can enjoy a cold one on the patio they have downstairs as well as an upstairs balcony. This is a local business with an owner who is hands down the nicest and most down to earth person you will meet.

Great Ambiance, Great Food. We happened to go on a night there was live music in the brewery next door. So we had live music, beer and great food! I ordered the tri tip sandwich and my friend ordered the spicy fried chicken sandwich. The tri tip was so tender, amazing flavor and the bread was so soft. My friend ordered the fried chicken spicy sandwich and it was a little too spicy for me, but perfect for her! We ordered the jalapeños poppers stuffed with goat cheese and bacon. Soooooo good! Would highly recommend for a friends meet up or casual date. We also ordered the churro for dessert - it was delicious and perfect to share for two people.

Typically your food is great. Your fries, meat, and salad are all fantastic. Tonight the mac and cheese was bad. The cheese was clumpy and covered in pepper, and the pasta was naked. I didn't even eat it after the first bite. I did enjoy my chicken strips and my husband liked his food. It just may have been a bad night for mac & cheese, but if not, you might want to take a look at your mac & cheese game...

My family stayed at the Bacara for 6 days to get away for a little while.  Overall, our stay was great and we really enjoyed ourselves.    Pre-check in: We received an e-mail message days before our arrival providing the details on what was and was not available due to Covid 19.  This was helpful to set our expectations in advance.  Check in / Parking: The check-in on process was really smooth. When you pull in, they ask if you want to self-park or valet park.  Here is the deal, 1) valet parking is $70/day and self parking is $50/day, 2) the parking area is very close to the villas so self-park if you want to save $20/day and 3) Unless you plan to eat every meal at the hotel restaurants, you will likely be needing your car often (we have a 5 & 7 year old and I was either running to get takeout or driving the family somewhere).    Room: The room was very nice and was large enough for the 4 of us.  The beds were very comfortable. They provided plenty of bottled water.  We did not even miss the coffee maker because my wife and I rarely use the in-room coffee makers.  We appreciated that they had tar removal wipes in the bathroom, which you will need it you spend any time in the water at the beach.  The housekeeping service was great and was prompt with providing us with extra towels, water and anything else we needed.    Property: The grounds and buildings are beautiful.  It was the most beautiful and peaceful hotel property that I have stayed.  Since this was during the pandemic, the hotel has plenty signs throughout the property detailing the social distancing and mask policy.  The pool was very nice and they did manage the number of people that were in the pool area.  A few times they were at max capacity, and we put ourselves on the wait list and they called us when there was space.  There are trails with beautiful views on the property.  The beach is just a few hundred feet from the pool area.  There were not many people on the beach so it felt like we had the beach to ourselves.    Restaurants: We ate at the Bistro the first day we arrived. It was good but very expensive and had very little in terms of variety for a 5 & 7 year old so we got takeout, or ate out, for the rest of our stay.  There are lots of restaurants 8 minutes away in Goleta.  Based on the number of empty pizza boxes outside of other rooms it looks like others were doing the same.  If you do want to eat at the hotel restaurants, they are taking reservations to maintain social distancing.  Don't expect to walk up and be seated.    Fees: there is a $25/day resort fee and either $50/day or $70/day for parking (as mentioned previously).    Staff: The staff was very courteous and helpful throughout our stay.  We really felt well taken care of.

It's hard to give a cupcake place anything less than 5/5 stars as long as the cupcake is moist, and the flavors are on point. I got the peanut butter chocolate cupcake, and it was heaven! The cake was moist, and the icing was delicious. What was even better was that it was buy one get one today! My boyfriend got the strawberry cupcake, which was not as good as mine since the cake was a little dry. I would still give this place 5/5 stars for my excellent cupcake.
Solid local pizza joint. Inside decorated slightly rustic with a trendy air. Staff super friendly and efficient. This spot is most definitely worth a visit.

Definitely my favorite of all Jeannine's locations. I love their changing menu, the service, and the general atmosphere. They can be creative with some great egg dishes and sandwiches with a nice twist. If I find myself wanting to meet an old friend for lunch or brunch (and we don't mind the minor splurge), Jeannine's is our place!

10/10 recommend!  Our server Nelson was attentive to our requests and dietary restrictions. Our glasses were never empty (we had a party of 8!) and he made sure that each order was correct before bringing it our.   Try the carnitas tacos paired with the tater tots and you won't have a bad time. I highly recommend backstage to anyone who has been in the area for a while or is just driving thru.   Their location on state street makes it confident to access the rest of town and even head to the waterfront if you so please.   Next time you're around look no further than backstage!
This place was fantastic. Nice ambiance, tasty drinks & great food. The servers were really nice too & they even treated us with a complimentary dessert for my friend's birthday!

I've been back several times to Benchmark since my original review about a  year ago.  I must say that the place seems to be in rhythm and no longer feels as if it is disorganized.  The wait staff was attentive and friendly, the service was prompt and accurate.  Though I did not order again the fish and chips, I have tried several other offerings on the menu and found them to be very tasty.  I'm happy they were able to turn things around and I compliment both the manager and the ownership team on striving to improve the restaurant.  Nice job!

One of my fav spots for tapas. On the Mesa which is a nice change up of neighborhoods when it comes to dining in sb. Really great cocktails and nice service.   They don't only have tapas, they also have entree sized plates. When i go I usually get the enchuladas which are really tasty and filling. Def an sb staple for me. This is a sister restaurant is milk n honey which is also a really good tapas spot but it's downtown. This is a good change up h en u r looking to get away from the masses of ppl a little bit.

My main course was passable, but a bit heavy for my taste (Sauerbraten). The apple fritters for dessert on the other hand, were practically inedible.   The proprietor is absolutely nuts. Some may enjoy the *extremely*-attentive service. However, if you prefer to be left in peace or talk with your party without constant interruption, this is not the restaurant for you.

Went to breakfast june 17 first time back since the remodeling. It is nicer looking with retro style metal behind counter and cute neon sign. Nice pictures on the wall but food disappointed. Had French toast that was just ok. A scramble was the choice of the other person. He complained that the hash browns were just like steamed soft potatoes.  Pricing on the menu was good but the food was barely adequate.  Cute waitstaff were more interested in a single gentleman at the counter than other customers.  Sorry but I don't think we will go back.

My co-workers have been coming here for years, yet I have not come here until this week. I have been missing out!  They have plenty of options for chicken or tri-tip. I wanted to try both so I picked the combo plate which comes with plenty of chicken, tri-tip, beans, rice and tortillas. Oh....and also your choice of many different kinds of salsa on the side. For less than $13 you're getting an amazing deal (especially by Santa Barbara pricing standards).   I enjoyed it and ended up going back the next evening....just because. The cuts of meats are tender and juicy, and bbq'd to perfection.

This is a charming joint with quality food.  I didn't realize it was there, but it's actually been open for a couple of years according to my friendly server.   It's near the creepy metal statues of a man and a little girl on a bench where Bogart's used to be.    The salads are a really great value (I devoured my watermelon salad) and my scallops were delicious and plump.  My partner mentioned that it seemed like a professor bar.  They have a lot of wines by the glass and the service is really chill but also on top of things.    Fancy food for a reasonable price.

I would give this place four or even five stars if it weren't for the price to portion ratio being totally off. I came here with my husband and grandma for a casual lunch. The place was awesome - order at the window, grab a beer from Lama Dog and sit down. Really cool vibe, and even better sounding menu.   We ordered the albondigas sandwich, the fish tacos, one of the specials, the pretzel and a side of truffle fries.  My fish tacos were teeny tiny, unfortunately. Glad we got all that extra food with those. They were tasty, but I couldn't get over how small they were. My husband liked the waffle special, and my grandma loved her meatball sandwich, esp. the coleslaw.  The pretzel was unfortunately either a day or two old, or over cooked. Either way, it was a let down. The fries were delicious, but again, super small basket (probably for the better though, ha).   All in all, great spot for some food while drinking at Lama Dog, but man those prices. I hope they fix.

Came here a second time and ordered the same Dungeness Crab Cake & Potato Hash w/Avocado Salsa, Poached Eggs & Lemon Butter Sauce, which was YUM (see below).  It was so good, in fact, that I rewarded it by ordering the Spicy Smoked Chipotle & Crab Bloody Mary, which was SO yummy....spicy without having too strong a chipotle taste and so tasty that I dangerously forgot that there was vodka in it!  The Bloody Mary comes with a crab claw and pickled string beans in it.  All very wonderful!    Husband had the Vanilla Dipped French Toast w/ Homemade Berry Syrup & Fresh Whipped Cream, which was quite good.  The whipped cream was rich and fluffy and the french toast was not at all dry, but instead custard-like.  The berry syrup was more like a raspberry jam/preserve, but it went well with the whipped cream.  All in all, another great breakfast at Tupelo Junction!  Again, not the cheapest breakfast on State Street, but still my favorite!
They have yummy food , late night eats . Full bar with drinks and a lovely atmosphere. Mediterranean themed food , I recommend!

My team came here for a lunch and batting cages and it's impressive how good the tacos are here.  Their tacos have an insane range between traditional beef tacos to korean tacos and ahi tacos. If you were to bat here, it's convenient to have such a great taco shop right next to it.  Parking is a bit limited in the area and they don't take American Express.

The Brewhouse is the best for causal amazing food ! The service is good and the people couldn't be friendlier ! The Huevos rancheros are the best I have ever had ! Highly recommnded to come down to montecito street and enjoy some great food !

Great, fast, cheap tacos. Meats are a little oily, but the tortillas are soft and delicious. Wish they had tortilla chips! But the simplicity of their menu is obviously a secret to their success. Close enough to the beach to pick up a few tacos and walk over.

Santa Barbara is full of great taco places--this is not a taco place. This is high end Mexican that previously did not exist here. Not only is the food incredibly good, but it is also of the highest quality. All ingredients are organic and sourced from the finest sources. What I really appreciate about this place is attention to details. There is thought given to everything. Here's an example--I ordered my daughter a sprite. It came out in a bottle from Mexico because in Mexico the they use cane sugar in sprite!  By the way--best tres leches I've had in my life. Hands down! Worth every calorie!
This is an excellent sushi spot. Fast, fresh, delicious. They let you customize which I appreciate. Check out Wabi Sabi! Probably the best sushi I've had in SB.

This unassuming shop in the Paseo Nuevo mall is one of my absolute favorite sandwich shops in Santa Barbara! It's family-owned, the ingredients are fresh, and the employees are so sweet and really take pride in what they're doing. I work nearby, so I will often come here for the prosciutto and brie hot sandwich - it's delicious, and the apricot spread is the perfect little touch to take it to the next level.   This past weekend, I had a few friends in from out of town, and we wanted some nice and easy to take to the courthouse sunken Gardens to enjoy the Saturday night fiesta dancing events. We were lucky enough to be waited on by Jome, (I hope that's spelled right,) who is an absolutely phenomenal sandwich artist!   Jome took the time to tell us which sandwiches would work really well in a picnic situation (ie, still be crisp and delish after sitting in a cold picnic basket for a few hours) versus which options would get soggy and less-than-perfect after a while. He pointed us to the chipotle turkey club and a few other options, and we could not have been happier. He was super awesome, and we will be back to make sure that our next picnic basket is stuffed with goodies from pickles and Swiss!

Great, unique Santa Barbara spot.  As a local, I had walked by it many times after work and it always looked so inviting.  I was not disappointed.    Amazing food (the chicken tajine), a fun chat with the owner and excellent fellow guests.  The lemon curd desert was astonishing.  Great live music, not too loud... instrumental and jazzy on the night we were there.  Be aware if you are a party of 2-4 people book outside seating: some of the tables are communal, though the guests we shared with were delightful. Great for parties of 6 or more. Worked find for the two ofus - everyone engaged with each other here and there, but also left you to enjoy your meal with your party.  Will certainly return time and time again.

Undoubtedly the best pizza in Santa Barbara! If you're an East Coast transplant like me, this is where you want to get your pizza. Definitely order a 42' pie or by the slice to get the most authentic NY style pizza. One or two slices will fill a grown man. I've been enjoying Uncle Rocco's for over 20 years! From an after bar must have snack to weekly poker parties, this is an incredible option. Out favorites are The Godfather with no onion, pepperoni, tomatoes and basil (Margarita), or plain cheese. So thin and crispy with the right amount of sauce, cheese, and oil, this is real any style pizza!

nice!  finally an asian place that serves a good amount of tasty food for a decent price!  Had the large chicken bowl - had nice fresh veggies and grilled chicken with teriyaki sauce. will definitely be coming back in the future!

Uh-oh.  Um.  Wow.  Okay, let's start here: I've been waiting two years to write this review.  Why?  Every single time I go to Loquita my experience is moving and I haven't been able to put my finger on why...until now.  To tell of this transcendence requires delicacy and time, something few have these days.  If you are in a hurry I encourage read another review.  If you want to join me on an all-encompassing journey through the truly unimaginable, nearly indescribable, experience that is a dinner at Loquita...then let's jump in.  It's a Wednesday night and you want to make Friday night plans with friends.  Great idea!  You start thinking about where you could go, what vibe you are looking for: Bar scene?  Sit-down?  Street food?  Pizza?  Then it hits you, 'Oh...or we could go to Loquita.'  You start thinking about the cocktails, the shared plates, the Paella... Three minutes later you find yourself sitting in the same spot, staring at the same place on the wall, feeling warm, content, like everything is right in the world.  The decision is made.  You call and make a reservation.  Because you've put your name in a few days early, there is an open table for four.  You wake up Thursday morning and can't shake Loquita; the thought of it enhances  your day.  7pm on Friday comes around.  The host walks you inside and you teleport to a different country: Spain.  The design and attention to detail is phenomenal.  The countertops were custom built and installed by local craftsman.  The tile-work, timeless.  The lighting, perfect ambiance.  Because of COVID you can't sit inside, which you are fine with because every table outside is either next to a fireplace or a heater, and the vibe is just as perfect.  You are seated and the staff explains the menu, the hand-crafted cocktails inspired by the bar manager's travels through Europe, the tapas (shared plates), the paella...the paella, the harmony of everything good in the world (we'll get there).  The cocktail menu isn't overwhelming and is well balanced.  Some smokey, some sweet, some fruity.  You find something just right for you but you need clarification on an ingredient.  Turns out the Vermut has been mixed with something beautiful to create a foam that is applied to the drink as it is served (see photos of the Maravilloso in the following images).  Your eyes grow large and the words, 'I'll take it,' barely escape your mouth.  Your friends order different and equally wonderful sounding drinks which the waiter, at your request, has meticulously described and recommended as pairing well with your tapas.  Oh...the tapas.  The shared plates.  The flavors.  Spices. Meats. Veggies. Texture. Color. Beauty.  You decide on 3 or 4 tapas (food is always served with a utensil for sanitary distribution but sometimes we can't help ourselves and dive head first onto the plate of mushrooms (see the photo of the champiñones).  Speaking of mushrooms, I actually teared up at the taste of one of Loquita's mushroom dishes a year ago and not because of a memory/nolstalgia/spice/onions being cut nearby.  No, I was truly moved by the experience of tasting such a delicious, well balanced, thoughtful, piece of food.  I will never forget it.  The tapas are brought out and served.  The ingredients and cooking processes are explained to you in a way that brings you into each dish, highlighting each bite you take, making you conscious of each flavor and the time put into the experience happening to your taste buds and nostrils.  After three tapas with varying degrees of adventurous flavor, shared amoungst you and your friends, it comes time for the Paella.  The Paella, whether veggie, chicken, prawn and short rib, or marine inspired, doesn't matter.  They are all mind-bending, and for their own unique reasons.  The smell of the paella, the presentation, the temperature, the texture, the taste, all combined, create a unique and immersive experience into a traditional culture that is unparalleled by any other restaurant in Santa Barbara.  Your heart skips a beat with every bite...you know your life will be different from here on out.  You have just experienced one of the best dinner dishes of your life.  Dessert?  You and friends know there is some room to spare.  You look at the menu.  You ask the waiter their recommendation.  After trying 4 or 5 different dishes (tapas and paella) you know you can't go wrong...so you order one of each plate.  Your taste buds know the end is coming, when the last bite of grainless chocolate cake (see photos) disappears from the plate.  You all sit there, speechless.  3 hours have gone by that felt like 30 minutes.  You've laughed, you've teared up, you've had good conversations, hard conversations, you've gotten to know the people around you in a new way.  Loquita has given you more than good food, it's given you connection.  Connection the people around you, to a cuisine seldom experienced, to the present moment.  It will move you.  Receive it with open hands.

Great food and great prices!  The kids menu is a life-saver in a town where restaurants often have nothing to offer for younger guests.  $3.50 for a full-size kids burger?  Yes please!  We've had a number of things here (fish and chips, chicken club wrap, BLAT), and they've all been excellent.  The thing that has us dreaming of them on a regular basis: the waffle sundae!  Amazing!  Another great kid deal: get their $2 cone in a cup and they'll put chocolate sauce on it for free!  A $2 ice cream sundae!  And it's delicious!    Only note is that it can be cool sitting outside on the benches, as it's in the shade for a good part of the day, so bring a sweater.

Ibspent 50 dls on food including 3 drinks when I asked for cup holder the lady on the front she just said nope we don't have cup holders,really ? How I can carry my drinks and my car has only one cup holder? Not good not coming back any more

This is my second time at the restaurant and will now be my go to spot.  The service is great! The food is great as well. Inexpensive and good coffee!  It's a bit small and so far, I've parked on State St.

Our server Rogelio is one of the best servers we have met, also a nice friend. He is so professional, friendly and kind. The food is consistently delicious. We have been coming here for years. COVID SAFE, outdoor sitting and a beautiful view of the harbor and the mountains. I am a Santa Native ! Come enjoy.

Me and my boyfriend love this place and we keep coming back to it. Sun sushi has become our go to sushi spot in SB.   I think that everything's reasonably priced for sushi. The fish is pretty good. Not mind-blowingly amazing, but pretty good. This is the kind of place where I would order nigiri at, and I can't say the same about other sushi places in the area. (The quality of the fish has to surpass a certain level before I'm willing to order nigiri rather than rolls.)

Some seriously awesome BBQ at public market! This place is amazing, definitely more down to earth BBQ than their last venture.   We got 1/4 rack of ribs, 8oz of brisket, large Mac and cheese, and large coke slaw. This was for two people and we were stuffed. The ribs had an amazing smoky flavor, absolutely fall off the bone. Brisket was to die for juicy and tender and a crust that was divine. Mac and cheese was solid, could have been a little warmer. Cole slaw was excellent, cabbage sliced super thin and not too sweet.   Next time would do same meat order but small sides for two. Can't wait to go back because they are putting out great food and can't wait to have more!

Decided to give them another shot during restaurant week, which was definitely a mistake. The customer service here is terrible. I have a food intolerance to the side that came with the entree. Eating it is the equivalent of having horrendous food poisoning for 24 hours, so it's not even an option I can consider. I asked if I could have a different side instead, which they refused. They asked the chef, who apparently said they would remove the side from my order, but would not let me replace it with a different side. Cool, so I'll spend the same amount of money for a dish without a side? No thanks. I can't believe they think that is an acceptable response! I've never been so disgusted with a restaurant in my life.

If you like breakfast by the breakers and are visiting Santa Barbara then you should make a point to enjoy at least one breakfast at Boathouse at Hendry's Beach.  Alll seating is first come first serve.  You can either put your name on a a list to wait for an indoor or patio table to open up or you can linger by the bar where there is no wait list and you just fend to find two seats open up.  You can put your name on a list for a table and then try your luck at the bar.  If you order food once you have a seat at a bar table or bar you'll relinquish your name for the dining table lists; but you can order drinks while you hold your place on the list.  The food was fine. I had a take on huevos rancheros.  The bloody mary sucked. However, I came here for the experience of watching the waves while I sipped coffee so I'm giving Boadhouse 5 stars.
We were staying at the hotel and didn't want to leave. Beautiful scenery! Great service. Good food. The seafood tower was fresh, pasta was excellent, and the steak was comparable to a steakhouse.

First time trying this place and everything is disorganized. They lost my online order. Some other guy who's order they couldn't find was found by the customer sitting out on a back table with no explanation of how it got there. The workers just said 'sorry about that' without even looking up from what they were doing. Maybe it's just because this place is new but if this keeps up this place won't be in business long.  Update - I tried the food. It seems like they leave the wings sitting out under a hot lamp after cooking, then just grab some and throw sauce on it when an order is up. The wings were over cooked/ tough.
Straight up best tacos in town great service and not to mention hand made tortillas.will be visiting often thank u very much for the great food and service

I got two sandwiches for my wife and I on Wednesday 1/30 for lunch.  Both were create your own.  My wife is vegan and only got a wrap with lettuce, tomato, pickles and avocado and they had the audacity to charge the same price for it as my turkey sub which also had provolone cheese, bacon, lettuce, tomatoes, pickles and avocado.  $22 for two average sandwiches.  I used to like this place but that's an insult for a tiny veggie wrap.  I also asked for my sub toasted with mayo and mustard and it didn't have any mayo and it wasn't toasted.  I asked the cashier why the veggie wrap was so expensive and he said it was because it was a create your own. I guess you get penalized at this place if you want a tiny veggie wrap that literally costs under a $1 to make.  Nice 11x profit margin there for you but we felt totally ripped off!

I mean, it's Los Agaves, need I say more. This one has been a long standing SB favorite and probably my favorite Mexican spot in town.   Things you don't think about normally but worth the mention:  their sodas are always the best. (This may sound insignificant to some, but if you are a coke drinker, this is important). They have a ton of great salsas.

Neat patio bistro with an interior that's bigger than it looks at first blush (because you don't immediately notice the upstairs dining area until you check the place out more carefully).  A great server who knew his stuff pointed us in the direction of their wonderful grilled asparagus salad, followed by their roasted organic chicken (a 'whole half chicken' which was enough to feed me and my evil twin brother), and their mahi mahi special for the evening. Everything was excellent. As was the neat Tocai Friulano wine the server suggested, and which went perfectly with what we ordered.  Finished with one of the best plum tarts I can remember ever eating and walked away happy, food-satiated tourists.

We were wary of its limited appeal gleaned from other reviews but fell in for dinner after being unable to agree on lucky's or tre lune. We loved the food, the wine and the service. The place is a total winner in spite of its limited menu...or maybe because of it.

These guys make some great sandwiches.  I had the grilled cheese and it was really delicious it was on sourdough bread has several different cheeses mixed in.  I actually ended up buying two at 5 bucks a piece they were a deal.  My friend got a couple of pulled pork sandwiches and they were awesome too.  This is a great food truck, nice people and good prices
Great high quality Grocery store! From the bakery section, sushi bar, and the Poke bar...then the deli! It's a beautiful grocery store with all you could imagine!

I usually like eating here, the burgers are typically outstanding. Took family to lunch and found the menu had changed so I was excited to try something new. Ordered the $5 bloody Marys ( always a home run) and decided to try the  new short rib Kim Chi burger, loved the new plates, fries were great but this wasn't a short rib burger it was a mix of Angus beef and pork I think, the menu say short Rib. The description is pork and Angus. The texture not great, definitely not ground short rib and yes I've had burgers made from short ribs and they were great. This was a pork blend and just so so. The ' spicy' Kim Chi wasn't spicy at all. The fries were perfect And the truffle dipping sauce was great. Disappointed with the new burger. Only other not great comment is  the music was way too loud when we arrived so I assume the staff likes it loud to work? I literally couldn't hear my waiter when he talked, finally asked them to turn it down. Hoping this isn't going to be typical moving forward.
Mulligans is an awesome place to grab a delicious sandwich or wrap. Always get the best service from Sevy- he is so attentive, never forgets anything and is hilarious!
Tasty food. Slightly marked up with prices but that's to be expected since it is on Stearn's Wharf. LONG WAIT!!

Called in for pick up and they said my order would be ready in 20 mins.  I forgot what pizza I got but it was sausage cheese and basil. But oh man was this simple thin crust pizza sooo good! Reminds me of secret pizza in Vegas  I am a huge pizza lover and I loved everything about this pizza! The crust was just cooked to that crisp but still has a good chewy crust to it. That fresh basil and the seasoning on the sausage!  Will definitely have to come back for more!!

Love this place. Stop here all the time, since it is down the street from my aunts house. Very friendly, and if they can't answer your question, they will find someone who can! Great drinks and a chill attitude

What has happened to Your Choice? I ordered Panang Curry and pineapple rice for pick up. The portion size is so small for the price. Add egg rolls for $39 and it was barely enough to feed two people. I'm very disappointed in such small portions....not worth the value. And the panang was very salty as well. Think I will try Galanga downtown again but too bad as I live nearby Your Choice. Mentioned this to the woman at the counter who barely cared. Good-bye.
Always a favorite. Sit at the bar or order in line to take it to go. Hangover noodles are a must try. Love the ice tea too!
Best tacos in SB.  No rice, burritos, chimichangas.  Just simple tacos. And they're perfect.
Are you looking for a reasonably-priced hole-in-the-wall spot that serves up menudo and posole with a decent michelada on a Sunday morning? Look no further.

If I could leave 0 stars for this place I would! I like mcdonalds don't get me wrong! But the last 3 out of 4 times I have gone through the drive thru, they have given me the wrong order!! I know when it's busy things get hectic in there, but It wasn't even busy!! Honestly, horrible service and I know a thing or two about good service so when I don't receive it, I feel irked.  So in conclusion, please save your self the time, money, and sanity, and do not go to this awful McDonald's with it's incompetent workers..

I've been going here for over 6 years and it's always good!! Great value for money and good service every time. The yellow curry, the Tom yum, pineapple fried rice, and drunken noodles are to die for! Your place never disappoints.

Wow! Yes, I should have expected this level of luxury, but I was in continuous awe of how impressive yet cozy this location felt. The friendliness and helpfulness of the staff was commendable and the amenities exceptional. For a special experience, I can't say enough good things about this spot. I truly cannot wait to come back!

3.5 stars.  I'll keep it short.  Great food, great atmosphere, good hang out for not college kids.    Notoriously slow service.  Had a group dinner there on an average night and it took 2.5 hours because of how slowly everything was served.  Maybe it's hit or miss on the service.

This place gets five stars as you can get some good grub and get your booze on at the same place. I ordered some carne asada fries and the cook loads them up with pico de Gallo, steak, cheese and sour cream. It is a place I would come back again for some eats especially late night.

Café Luck is the latest addition to the Montesano Empire of Santa Barbara eateries - a factoid you can actually see and feel upon entry.  (For reference, try Buccatini, D'Angelos, Lucky's, Tré Lune or Joe's)  The restaurant is nicely designed, but I could easily imagine it getting quite loud.  We were spared the noise by dining on a Monday.  The premise is French Bistro, but the reality reminds me of the scene in 'Better Off Dead' in which John Cusack's mother prepares a French meal for their exchange student.  'Fraunch' Fries are included with a number of the meals.  (I didn't have the foie gras, the escargot, or any number of other seemingly authentic dishes, though...)  Still, the fries are draw-joppingly perfect.  Ditto the French onion soup and the polenta.  The olive bread mentioned in any number of these reviews is worth each rave review.  My Old-Fashioned was also memorably tasty.  (Someone behind the bar is a pro.)  My 'Market Fish' - salmon with truffle butter - was a bit rare, but plausible.  One of my dinner companions ordered a lamb dish rare and it came out still 'baaa-ing.'  The ratatouille was absolutely spot on - with pearl onions that will haunt me all week.  For desert, we ordered the Jack Daniels gelato with chocolate-covered pretzels.  I attribute its inclusion on the menu to the recent explosion in marijuana dispensaries in our fair city.  We finished it, but I wouldn't order it again.  The skinny:  with a half a bottle of wine, a cocktail, a soup appetizer, a side of fries, and the desert, we spent $130 for two including tip. Thus, the modestly expensive reference on Yelp and OpenTable are a bit misleading.  Café Luck is very good, but it ain't cheap.

Hungry? Go here - you will find something you like.  Awesome food choices.  We had fun just walking around looking at all the different offerings (wine bar, several coffee/baked goods, meat and cheese cones (!), fresh pasta, etc).

Amazing location, good portions, delicious food and despite all those great things, the prices are really good.  I came here one weekend for a Santa Barbara trip with friends.  I ended up with the Macadamia Nut Mahi Mahi and I couldn't be happier.  Everything was cooked beautifully and the fish wasn't overcooked like it usually is at other restaurants.  I also was able to enjoy some raw oysters which were equally as delicious.  Just as a sidenote- if you visit Santa Barbara, take advantage of all the seafood options you can- their raw oysters have been good no matter where I've gone.    My Mahi Mahi was around $25.00 but with that fantastic view and the restaurant built RIGHT. ON. THE. BEACH, I think that the price is more than fair.  Nothing gets better than watching the sunset and the beach while eating a delicious meal.  The service was great, and the other entrees my friends had that I tasted were equally as tasty as well.

Carlito's Way or the Highway? Take the highway! Our plans did not include coming here, but several places we thought of going to were inexplicably closed. This place is laughably bad, given that it is SO overpriced and so tasteless. I could easily stumble upon ANY Mexican restaurant in my hometown and they would all have better tasting food than this place. Yes, this includes Del Taco and even Taco Bell. A Taco Bell soft taco would kick this place's ass, like Nunes vs. Rousey kick ass. It makes me wonder if ANYONE is Santa Barbara knows what good Mexican food should taste like.  Anyways, the specifics...I guarantee you that they just pour the beans out of a can on the combo plates. For $18 a plate you could do more than that. The cheese enchilada was covered in random crap to make up for the fact that the cheese was this gross, inauthentic off-white tasteless cheese. As someone else said, they overcompensate by putting Mexican flags in their 'food.' The beef taco, was yes, a beef taco. No more, no less. For that price, it better be the tastiest god damn beef taco I've ever had.  It's so easy to get good or great Mexican food for half the price of this crap. I hope that Santa Barbara has better to offer than this place, for the sake of the Republic. If they don't, California should kick them out! Stay away!

Writing an update to this review based on the owner's response to my first review. I would like to clarify, I know what I ordered (El Capitan Salad) which had in the description something about taco salad. The meat on the salad could not be considered taco meat by any standard I am familiar with and it was served over a bed of plain lettuce. The meat was was buffalo-like: saucy, burning spice and bright orange.   They approached my negative review by blaming me for 'not knowing' what I ordered. I can't respect a business that tries to excuse their mediocre food by blaming it's customers.   I didn't lower their rating with this update, I still think the food is just fine and they did replace the salad that I didn't like.   The issue with this place, obviously, other than the annoying and pretentious management response, is that the word Greens is in their name but the menu lacks a real presence of greens and sells junky food as health food. It's misleading and our experience was very disappointing.
I would only go during happy hour when the rolls are half off. Even still, I've had cheaper and better sushi elsewhere.

Well I had been wanting this for a long time. But they close early so I have never been able to get one til now. The flavors were great. I ordered to eat there then realized I needed to leave so I asked for it to be in a Togo cup. But they forgot which is not problem and offered to repack it in a to go after they brought it out to me. However when I left I quickly realize there was only like 1 cup of smoothie and the rest was fresh fruit etc. which is awesome. The ratio was just slightly off. I would have liked a bit more smoothie in my bow. So I'm wondering if during their 'repack' of my Bowl Togo they lost some smoothie. I'll be back another time to check It out because it was great. We'll see I'm sure it was a fluke instance. Staff was super  nice and helpful.

An absolutely lovely place to dine in Santa Barbara.  We stopped in for a business lunch and immediately liked the elegant vibe of this place.   The lunch menu is quite extensive and full of variety, but we started with calamari and sweet potato fries appetizers to share (couldn't hold back on the fries).  For mains, there's chicken, steaks, salads and flat breads galore but we chose to chow down on the Presidio Burger full of blue cheese, fresh arugula and shallot jam.  Incredibly moist, yummy and dangerously gets you sleepy after lunch due to feeling so satisfied.  I really look forward to returning for dinner, as their menu looks fabulous and includes chef tasting menus.  And with the lovely ambiance of Wine Cask, you're in for a nice romantic evening.
My new favorite spot in Santa Barbara!!! Amazing that's all I can say!!! And the service was absolutely phenomenal!!

Small local mom/pop shop and that's what tipped it from a 1.5 star to 2 star. I really want it to succeed.  I think as a deli it's probably successful - unfortunately what drove me to to try it out is the recent posting on Restaurant Guy's blog about featuring Vietnamese pho and sandwiches - note they're careful to not state banh mi.   For a small pho and a half sandwich, it was $12.42. The bread either overwhelmed the filling or there simply wasn't enough meat/pate. Perhaps 1 slice of each. I should have taken  a photo. The pho wasn't very remarkable or memorable either. I should try giving this place another chance and will then post photos. Here's to the next time!

We loved the foods we ordered.  The Jalisco cuisine is distinctly delicious.  Our tacos and burritos were fabulous. It's hard to describe the difference of the flavor, but we're definitely coming back again.    The burrito shell was a little more bread like than the normal tortilla and seasoned, creating a wonderful flavor.  The multiple choices of salsas provided delightful options that we happily sampled.    This is a tiny little restaurant carved out of a space shared with a florist.  Just a couple of tables and a one man kitchen.  His daughter greets patrons, seats them, and also serves as cashier.   The regional flavors, the sense of the exotic differences and there authenticity make this a splendid treat. A mini vacation to Jalisco for the span of a meal. Across he parking lot is eve a panaderia, as well.

Did you know they serve dinner Thursday - Saturday until 10:30pm?!  Delightful, as was the whole experience!!!  I called around 8:30pm on a Thursday to see when the kitchen would close, and was pleasantly surprised to hear '9pm normally, but 10:30pm Thursdays - Saturdays'.  Great!  We were seated on the outside patio and received WONDERFUL service from Amber.  She immediately brought my girl friend and I fried chickpeas and water with a smile, then got us amazing drinks, took our dinner order and provided full disclosure when I inquired about using the SB Axxess card, 'they take the lower priced option off'.    The details of the drinks (Pim's cocktail had a sliver of rolled cucumber! along with a strawberry and mint), the sauce and veggies & roasted potatoes with the salmon I got was mouth-wateringly delicious, and the entire experience was so much more than I expected.    Thank you to the staff of Benchmark eatery - you guys rock!!!!! :)

Great day on the course!! Love Rancho. It's just over the hill from SB and well worth it. The course is in great shape for this time a year.  Yes it's brown in spots but use them to your advantage...roll baby roll! The animals were out today...turkeys, deer and tons of birds singing.  Ended up eating lunch after our round and the Caesar Salad was delicious...add grilled chicken it's tasty!  Had such a great we'll be back next Tuesday!

Birthday day dinner at one of my favorite places with some of my favorite people! Arigato has been a family favorite for years- for special occasions and anytime we want to enjoy a great atmosphere and amazing sushi - the seafood is always fresh and excellent and even the basics like California roll and shrimp tempura roll are superior to the quality you will find elsewhere- of course they use real  Dungeness crab used in the California roll- so how could it not be amazing?!?! The service is always great- efficient, helpful, Friendly- overall it's just an awesome dining experience!

So yes, the rooms are tight. You are snug as a bug in a rug here but boy that location ! We're beach people so close proximity to sandy paradise always attracts our attention.  We used to visit Santa Barbara quite regularly and when Covid struck, there was a tug pulling us to return.  SB is just so relaxing.  The room is small but comfy. The pool was shut on our visit but the new renovations feel great.   We had a sliver of a beach view but it was lovely.   I can imagine this beach haven will be a blast in summer. Give them a try!
Best Mexican street tacos in SB! You won't find tacos more authentic than these. Even the champurrado is home made and delicious.

This is what state street needed!! Such unique ideas for tacos. Instead of tortillas, they use paratha bread. The bread is so flaky and buttery, I can't believe someone hasn't thought of this sooner. So far I have tried the pork belly tacos and the paneer cheese and both have amazing and vibrant flavors with their veggie and sauce choices for each. The flavors combine and blend together so beautifully and the pork belly melts in your mouth. I would 10/10 recommend this place and already have to most of my friends! They have wine and beer and even kombucha on tap! Also give their 'churros' a try, perfect dessert.

This my second experience here and it's quite like the first- bland food. I definitely would hit up Brophy's and the Santa Barbara Shellfish Company before here.   While the atmosphere is second to none- the food needs some uplifting.   The crab and artichoke dip had cream cheese filling with very few artichoke hearts. I give that a 5/10.  The fish of the fish and chips was dry. So dry that I had to use all of the tartar sauce. This is where the shellfish company and Brophy's reign.   They are definitely feeling the Covid blues with lack of staff and supplies.   We will try something new next time. I do recommend happy hour here during sunset. Can't beat the few at the upstairs bar. No where else on Cabrillo has their view.

The simple dilapidated beauty of this old-time establishment will fool all but the most discerning of food adventurer. Decades and decades old, this little German inspired joint is singularly unique in all of restaurantdom in Santa Barbara. 20+ true imported German beers grace a beer menu. Less than 10 perfectly executed German dishes grace a small but wonderful food menu. A hot plate (schnitzel is my fav.) includes sauerkraut, hot potato salad, perfectly cooked carrots, and some stout bread. This hearty fare cannot be had anywhere else, not would it be served on the outdoor beer garden, or at the rickety counter with small revolving original stools. A trip back in time, where the chef/owner pleases one and all from his tiny grill sour rounded by steaming and bubbling pots of true German fare. Jawol!!!

Hi, I just had a pizza delivered.   I usually enjoy the pizzas I get at domino's, but when I opened this box, I noticed a long black hair on it.  I thought maybe it was mine, so I pulled it, but it was trapped under cheese and pepperoni.. it was definitely not mine. YUCK!  I decided to eat a slice on the other half of the pizza to avoid it, but noticed a short hair on that slice.  I subsequently noticed another short hair on a different slice. i pulled them out and threw them away and decided to just avoid those slices rather than make a big deal and call customer service or something.   So finally I pick up a 'clean' slice to eat.  I made it most of the way through, but as my mouth approached the crust, I felt a tickle on my lip.  Another short hair!! I tried not to gag because I really want to continue to enjoy the cheap pizza that dominos has, but this was too much!!!  ..I had to feel it in my mouth D:  I am not sure what to do and don't think I can get myself to finish the rest of the pizza or any dominos food I ordered today.  One of your preparers needs to wear some kind of hat!!  Also, I ordered cheesy bread with only jalapeno in it and they put bacon in it.  I love bacon, but I've eaten about 1/2 pound of bacon in the last 24 hours (it was good bacon). So I didn't want any cheap bacon in my mouth anytime soon!!  I feel bad giving this review but I dunno what else to do.

I found myself at Sushi 29 after I found out that they absorbed Yume.  I have to say, I don't quite like it as much as Yume, the menu is good, but not super vast.  Most of the rolls are variations of popular fish.  They do have some specials that are noted on the big board.  Plenty of seating for large and small groups.  Overall, nothing overly spectacular, but a reliable delicious meal
One of My favorite place in SB! I order the Milano usually with butter leaf lettuce salad. I love how the kids menu is  very reasonable. 3.99  The garlic bread is to die for.

Lure is by far my favorite seafood restaurant for happy hour. Hands down the most decent price for quality seafood on the Central Coast. I drive an hour just to eat here on Sunday's for their all day happy hour (11:30-6pm). I've basically tried everything on the happy hour menu and NOTHING, I REPEAT, NOTHING disappoints. The portions are big enough for sharing (even though, you won't want to). The services is always impeccable.   I don't know what else to say to convince you to try this place out. Maybe this will help: My very first Lure review was for the Camarillo location and a few days later, they mailed me a Lure shirt to show their gratitude. It wasn't a cheap shirt either!! One of my favorite tees to wear on a hot summer day now. I really should wear it next time I eat there! :)   Update:  After my SB, review, I was offered another shirt! Not that I'm reviewing for the shirt, but it's really nice to know your patronage is appreciated by this restaurant. :)

The best spot to eat in Santa Barbara! They serve authentic French food. The service here is top notch. They have lots of great wine choices. Whether you want a local wine or some European wine, they'll have something for you. The deviled eggs are the beat I've ever had. The coq au vin is so tender and flavorful. And the scallop puff pastry is bomb, the puff pastry is very well made and comes with plenty of scallops. We got a nice table with a lake view and it was very romantic. I absolutely love this place; it was the best part of my visit to Santa Barbara.
Was expecting more. Food was good but not my favorite in Santa Barbara. Could use more heat

Joes is a great local place. Consistently good drinks, great bar vibe with a local flavor. Doesn't feel super touristy. Has a ton of history and so many choices. It was a rainy day and I had the chili which was amazing! Must visit!

I thought D'Angelos was just okay. The boyfriend and I went for breakfast on a Saturday. There was a wait but it wasn't super long. I liked the atmosphere of the open kitchen/warehouse vibe but it made it pretty loud for early morning conversation.   Since we were on vacation we decided to splurge and ordered the salmon eggs benedict and the bananas foster French toast. The salmon eggs benedict was good, I liked the citrusy flavor of the hollandaise sauce, it was a nice combo with the salmon. But for $17 a side would have been nice - potatoes or something! The bananas foster French toast was also on the skimpy side - 3 small pieces of bread. Except for the sauce - there was way too much of that. It was overly sweet and overpowering.   The service was meh as well. They weren't rude, they just weren't much of anything - nonexistent really. I can't say I even remember our waiter. Kinda like our overall experience - a bit forgettable.
Went to a pop up dinner and had an amazing experience! The food was all fresh and locally sourced. Can't wait to come check out the full menu.
flip flopping- Ok - so even if I avoid eating breakfast here, I weekly buy their dense and delicious Sunflower rye bread.

Date Night! We started off with the Ceviche de Mariscos and Empanadas de Cameron. The ceviche was loaded with seafood, avocado, tomatoes, cucumber and cilantro; all marinated with just the right amount of lime juice = 4 stars. The deep-fried empanadas were filled with the right combo of shrimp, cheese, and sauce BUT the crust is not as flakey and buttery as I would have liked. I get it, every country has it own style of this dish. Overall, I give the empanadas 3 stars. Then my wife and I shared the Cazuela Pibil. This is a pork dish marinated in their special adobo sauce and served with tortillas. I found the pork a little dry however, the spicy, savory and richly smoky flavors are there. So, for this dish, I give it 3.5 stars. Nice fun, festive ambience. This restaurant is located 2 blocks from the wharf near the waterfront.

I'm shocked I haven't written a review for Bluewater! It's one of my go to seafood restaurants in town. The location is perfect for anyone who wants to dine in the heart of our beautiful water front.   There's plenty of seating available but I recommend dining upstairs on their balcony or at the bar, if you are lucky to grab a spot.   My go to drink is always the Mai Thai. It really packs a punch and always made perfectly.   I've tried many items on the menu, but my favorites are the ceviche and ponzu sashimi sea scallops to start. Both are excellent choices and light so you won't be stuffed before your main meal. I love how the ponzu sashimi sea scallops are served in a beautiful sea shell. The ceviche is also well presented and served on top of a crunchy tostada, but the tostada shell is small. I recommend asking for some tortilla chips on the side to help scoop up the ceviche.   It's hard to pick a main meal that stands out to me, but the shrimp tacos are pretty good. I was surprised at how generously filled the tacos were. I was full after eating one taco. I recently tried their Angus cheeseburger, which was delicious! My son had the crab cake benedict from their brunch menu and loved it. His plate was wiped clean, so that says how good it was.   Overall, a great place to dine at for seafood and drinks along the waterfront.
Fabulous place! Great location, plenty of parking, yummy flatbreads and starters, good selection of beers and wine and friendly staff.

Holy moly! Korean chicken sandwich and fries were delicious.  I'm only sad that we were going to the Bowl for a concert afterwards, and I had to leave my leftovers behind.  A friend had the burger with fries and also raved.  I can't wait to go back.  It took a little while for the food to come out, as they seemed to be busy with pickup orders.  Absolutely worth the short wait.

My boss just ordered from here today and bought me the flank steak salad. It was really good but the chipotle dressing was pretty overpowering. I wish that they had toned it down just a smidgen.  *I figured out just now  how to fix it: leave it in the fridge for a few hours, then it's less spicy, haha.
LOVEEE. The food is soooo good, and they have a good selection of chips and drinks. Donnie, Owen, Sabrina, and the rest of the crew is just awesome!!

Service is great, my French toast was amazing and the chai cookie was fabulous. I got the gluten free mint cookie and corn muffin. They were dry BUT gluten free foods are tough to get right. The coffee was good and the restaurant was cute. I have to say the staff and service there was amazing even though they were so busy.  I enjoyed this restaurant allot and would come back and try the other goodies.  Ohhhhh the Bacon was Sooooooo gooooooood
My apologies for the misunderstanding in my earlier post. This is a fine establishment.
Brilliant tuna cones, fabulous staff, excellent location! Walk across the beach for an after meal stroll. Breathe fresh air as you will be dining outside here.

Best pastas in town hand down! The salads are bomb as well, their burrata is a killer, and their salad with prosciutto is delicious! The pizzas are really good too actually, small but decent price, and really good daugh. All in all this place is just amazing!   Tip: Go get yourself some cupcakes on your way out ;)
Friendly. Great food. Awesome lunch specials. Consistently professional.  I love the mushroom crostini.
One of my favorite seafood spots in all of SB. Lobster is always cooked perfectly and sides are delicious as well. Love the kale salad and the presentation.

I have never written a review without actually being to an establishment or eating the food until now. So this is certainly not a review on the food at all. I have actually been dying to eat this pizza. However, I have tried numerous times to order pizza from them and every time they tell me over the phone 'Sure, but I do have to tell you...we have a gigantic wait tonight'. The first time, it was Labor Day weekend so I felt like good for you, glad you're busy as a new business. The second time (same response) was midweek. The third time I called right at 5 after they had just opened and thought to myself, wow I'm actually going to eat this pizza tonight.
