The same guy answered and his same opening response followed as 'we do have a gigantic wait tonight and it's looking like 2.5 hours'. After weeks of this, I got so frustrated and asked how this could be possible?! I mean I'm calling you when you open!! He then informed me that people actually start calling at 11am for orders...and their pizzas are so 'special' it takes awhile to make with the oven blah blah blah. By the way, this guy lacks any sense of urgency or empathy. If you start taking orders at 11, then start telling your eager customers so they can plan accordingly!! Why was this the first I'm hearing of this?? Being from the east coast, I am just accustomed to better customer service. Places get busy all the time. It's about the way you handle it. Instead of saying 'sure' and telling me about your stupid 'gigantic' wait, you should say....'wow thanks so much for supporting a new business, we have actually been doing so well people are calling in really early to place their orders. I'd be happy to put it in for you now, but it'll be 2 hours but I promise it'll be worth the wait! If not, call tomorrow early and I promise we will get that cooking for you'.   Again, just a review on customer service. Happy to change this once I actually taste the food. Wonder when that will be?

I've been coming here since they opened and am glad they have remained in business throughout the pandemic. I and always get the same thing: the Spicy Crispy Chicken Black Garlic Ramen. The broth is full bodied and very flavorful, and has a nice spicy kick to it but still mild for someone (like me) who likes super spicy things. One tip to get the most bang for your buck if you have leftover broth: if you have instant noodles or ramen/udon/etc at home, you can add it to the broth to get another delicious meal out of it. 10/10 highly recommended.

My personal favorite for fine dining in Downtown Santa Barbara!  I came here for a small birthday celebration with my friend and everything was just delicious! If I didn't live in San Francisco, I would come back here very soon!  Bouchon offers quite a large dining area with a very intimate, upscale atmosphere. Service is very attentive and polite.  My friend and I ordered 3 courses each. (Their menu may have changed slightly, as this is a late review) I started with the pork belly, my entree was the maple glazed duck, and my dessert was a creme brulee.  My friend had the scallop trio, rack of lamb, and strawberry shortcake. Needless to say, everything was delicious!  I highly recommend the maple glazed duck! The demi glace is AMAZING. In addition, definitely try the scallops as well as the rack of lamb. My friend mentioned the wagyu was a bit sub-par the last time.   Note: -Make reservations in advance! -There's nearby parking which is free for the first 75 minutes!

Good range of food to pick from, our fave is the Santa Barbara omelet !!  Waiters are always so nice and tentative. I have yet to have a bad experience with Cody's. BETTER THAN CAJUN KITCHEN! Boom. I said it!

The place struck me as a chain restaurant, and I didn't know what to expect out of the food, so I was surprised with the fig burger.  Paired with bacon, it's a surprisingly good combo!    I tried to strike a healthy(ish) balance with broccolini.  Not recommended - hardly anything was done to it.  The portions of french fries are also too small - more healthy(ish) balancing on their part?  It seems like service is hit-or-miss and was a clear miss on this day.  After trying for quite a while to catch the attention of any waiter in eyesight from our outdoor table until one clearly not assigned to us had to intervene on our behalf, we were served by no less than four or five different people throughout the night.  I'm not sure if this is an intentional system on their part, but let's consider it a failed experiment? The wait to pick up the check was also substantial.  Their beer selection is good, but overall, you might do better going to one of the local fast-food stands unless you're really looking for a restaurant atmosphere.

Who would've thought that there would be mochi donuts in this little town we call Santa Barbara??! Wow, I had MOR doughnuts for the first time last week when my friend bought some for us to share. Let me just say we devoured it in probably less than 5 minutes. I've had my share of mochi donuts (had them in Hawaii and Japan) & I have to say these donuts were in par or even better than the ones I've had before. The texture is where its at and MOR does it so right. It's chewy and light and there's consistency with all the doughnuts. Now the flavors are just another level. So creative and the execution always hits the spot. Last week, I had their mango, Thai tea Oreo, fruity pebbles and dalgona coffee. All were delicious but mango had to be my favorite. So innovative and worth trying. Thanks for bringing mochi donuts to SB!

The wait for inside seating is always going to be super packed on a Saturday but I'd suggest opting to take the food to-go to eat on the outdoor benches, assuming that you're not afraid of birds! Those seagulls can be pretty vicious...(I'm terrified of birds so thank goodness we found a little bench off to the side of the restaurant safe from those monstrous creatures..)  My boyfriend and I wanted to grab a snack and try something from this famous restaurant. The rock crab seems to be the house favorite but we wanted something more than just crab so we opted for the cioppino in a bread bowl ($18.95).   We expected it to come with all the seafood out of their shells. Boy, were we terribly wrong! It literally came with a leg of rock crab, and mussels and clams in their shells. At least the shrimp was ready to eat. Thus, it was a bit hard to eat the bowl but still delicious. The bread bowl itself was a little stale but still yummy once drenched in the sauce.  I'd be back to try the lobster tacos and maybe the rock crab once I'm in the mood!
This place is good, their views from the patio are gorgeous especially at sunset. The clam chowder and oysters were delicious. Service was great!

We had a great experience at barbareno! Food service ambiance were all fantastic. I brought a bottle of 2005 red blend from ZA. And the server seems to know a lot about wine...I had the vegetable crudités and Brussels sprouts and my friend had the tri tip. The highlight of the whole meal was actually the eucalyptus dessert. It's made with coconut milk Italian ice cream and fruit. Not too sweet which makes this non sweet tooth surrendering. Finally impressive reading about the story of the owners- cheers for being young and successful. Keep it up guys!
Stopped by after 9pm and was nonetheless treated to a nicely prepared daily special, a pleasantly light dessert, and a dependable beer/wine list. Husband liked his duck legs, too.
Shabu shabu is not great and real. It just comes with boiling water, some cabbages and a few pieces of seafood. Sushi is ok, not very decent. The service is not worth a great tip.

Methinks not indeed. This place is so gross. The food has small, cold portions. The wait staff is a bunch of bus boys, who speak very poor English. It's as if they've fired their regular wait staff to save money, and then dragooned the dish washer into serving.  Don't be penny wise and pound foolish, and this nonsense includes the tip.  One of the buffets was an $18 TERRIBLE hamburger and cold fries. Why?  I had a grilled chicken sandwich that was apparently taken out of the ice box minutes before it found its way to my plate. It may have been left over from the previous day. Soooooo gross. The hotel is undergoing a renovation, according to the manger. START WITH THE RESTAURANT.

I was in Montecito for tennis, and we stopped by for some juice, actually.  Then, when we saw the selection of baked goods, we also got some of the macaroons.  I also got a large Chai tea, which was exactly how I drank it growing up.  The staff was very friendly, and courteous, and happy to help.  The place isn't huge, but it's got a French cafe sort of look (same sort of thin-legged chairs, and marble-topped tables).  I'd recommend it to anyone interested in good food and drink over a great conversation.

Hats off to these guys!   Can we all just take a moment to appreciate how much time it takes to make a good donut?  You have to make the dough and kneed and kneed and kneed, let it rise. Roll out and let rise again and then fry. Add on top of that each individual topping and the care and quality that goes into it. You can tell these recipes have been tweaked and perfected for years before they opened.   Spudnuts and ellers shouldn't even be part of the conversation. There is no comparison!   $3.25 is a fair price. These are quality ingredients, a hard working husband and wife team and lease space on state street. How much do you pay for a frozen, dried out scone that probably travels from a warehouse in the Midwest at Starbucks? at least that much or more.   The only constructive feedback I have is that the interior could feel a little more lively, welcoming and festive inside. It's a little quiet and muted, staff is a little slow and cautious, but I think that will come in time.   The donuts are perfect and that's the most important part. They have the opportunity to make some lifelong memories with Santa Barbara families. This could become a special place, a quintessential Santa Barbara stop!!!!

Went here for a friend's birthday brunch on a Sunday and it was AMAZING! We sat outdoors where they could accommodate the 10 of us (fresh air and great ambiance) - no complaints!   Most of us got the bottomless mimosas ($15/pp - what a steal!) which were abundant and delicious. Brandon was the BEST server and made sure we were taken care of! The food was delicious as well. I highly recommend this place :)

We ordered clam chowder, lobster sandwich, fish and chips, guacamole chips and salsa and a dessert of chocolate chip cookie with ice cream on the top. Awesome food and amazing view. The only issue was of a lot of pigeons on the deck. But totally understand since inside seating was not open.

Me and my boyfriend went here sober one night because we were starving and mac n cheese sounded dank. We arrive at the hole in the wall greeted by two sweet women who were ready and willing to help! It was hilarious seeing all the drunk people downtown excited to eat mac n cheese i think that was the best part. And then after waiting a few short minutes we had a giant meal infront of us. Could even split it next time! Great cute quick place! Could be cheaper but hey drunk people will buy anything so it's smart!

My daughter loves empanadas, so we had to stop at Buena Onda after touring the Santa Barbara Art Museum across the street.   The shop is nice and has a local beer vendor in the shared space. There are several tables in the doorway outside under the arched entrance that looked really nice, but they were taken, so we sat at a table on State Street in an outdoor dining area. We ordered several types of empanadas, which were all very good. The dough was light and fluffy. We ordered the carne, quinoa vegan, Serrano, and caprese empanadas. All were good and the caprese was very good. Next time we're in town we'll stop by for a beer and a caprese empanada!

We are local and were obviously excited to try a 'new' restaurant here in lovely Santa Barbara. My wife and I sat outside on the patio with a excellent view of the beach tonight. Had a few appetizers, a pizza (mushroom and wonderful onions) and (homemade in house) pasta dish. Food was excellent with unique and creative flavors. Service was good. We shall return soon :)

Came down to SB for my SO birthday. Read reviews on Yelp about Black Sheep and wanted to try it.  A lot of the dishes on the menu looked really good so we decided to go with the tasting menu. Chefs choice. The waitress asked if we had any dietary restrictions (thank you) and I said no pork or shellfish.  The first dish that was brought out was white anchovies on a crostini with orange zest and mixed cocktail olives. Pretty good. The anchovy taste wasn't too overpowering.  Second dish:  the burrata salad. It had pickled persimmon, roasted lip stick peppers, endive, ruby stripes mustard greens, xeres vinegar dressing black pepper. So good! This is a must try! Third dish:  rock scallops. (Didn't I say no shellfish?). I didn't try this one but my SO loved them!  Said they were super fresh.  Fourth dish: veggie spring rolls with sambal dip. So yummy!   Fifth dish: black cod with ramen pasta. This was interesting.  I was surprised how much I liked the ramen pasta.   Sixth dish (yes it keeps going): Panko beef bone marrow on crustini with whole grain mustard. Very creamy and buttery. Good mix of flavors.  Seventh dish: baby back ribs. (Again, didn't I say no pork?). I also didn't try this one but again, my SO said they were excellent, with the meat literally falling off the bone. Eighth dish: Duck confit with roasted cipollini onions and seasonal veggies. I'm so stuffed by this point I'm just tasting. I don't usually like duck. Too fatty for me but this one was good. I wish I had more room in my belly. The carrots were my favorite. Roasted to perfection.  My SO loved it but said it was on the salty side. Ninth dish: last one! Yay! Rice pudding with seasonal fruit. Rice pudding?  Who eats rice pudding?  I tasted and I didn't like it. But I don't like any rice pudding. It was served with jam and strawberries.  Overall the tasting menu was the way to go. You get to try different things. Make sure you go hungry!  I wanted to give this place a 5 star but since they ignored my dietary restrictions I have to go with a 4 star. Good thing my SO eats anything! :)

MOST delicious!!   From the moment we arrived we were greeted with such kindness...   We ordered the ranch fries (more like spuds SOOO GOOD!), ranchero salad(fresh, crunchy, FLAVOR!), halibut (holy delicious!) and our favorite...the cheese and hominey casserole, don't you dare skip that one! We can not wait for another night spent here. Thank you, La Paloma!!

Great food and great waiter. Food came out quickly considering how busy they were. I'm still giving 5-stars because I have worked in restaurants for many years and I believe in constructive criticism and I know they run a great  establishment for many years. But there was a big party in their separate dinning room (wedding I think). We all shared the patio but it was divided by chairs. The party had multiple people smoking the whole time. While I understand it's a wedding and they're having fun. Smoking in SB is now illegal outside before 10 pm and it's not the best smell when eating your food. A few of us complained and I think an employee went over and told them to move away from the building but the smoking continued shortly after. I have asthma so we decided to leave quickly after eating instead of sitting around and enjoying. Again, no hard feelings. Will still go eat there again. They have great pizza and pasta! Just wish more was done about the half a dozen people smoking.

Tamira, is literally one of my most favorite places of all to eat in Santa Barbara. I probably eat here about 3 times a month. I love how Remy and the staff here they treat my boyfriend and I and my family like we are their family. Their lunch buffet is one of the reasons I love coming here so much they have a wonderful variety of fresh fruits vegetables, vegetarian options and I always love the chicken masala or the chicken Korma. I'm a huge fan of their Biriyani, pakora, aaaaand the fresh naan bread. My boyfriend loves their lamb Vindaloo. Their staff is always so attentive and genuine and most importantly we never feel rushed. This is the perfect casual place to enjoy a meal with family and friends and to feel like you're amongst friends.

The view is great, everybody knows that. The problem is the owners rely on the view being great, and do not care about the food at all. My family just had $18 burgers that were dry, with meat that tasted like a middle school cafeteria, and an onion slice. If you're going to charge higher prices, at least make the food decent. Boathouse is on the sand, charges higher prices and the food is great. That's a restaurant that makes me happy to pay for dinner. Shoreline café is not.

We just tried it over Graduation weekend at UCSB and we got it without a long wait twice not just once.  We have wanted to try the place for a long time, but never got there.  This time while in town we went and tried the standard fajita and wet burrito.  Both were great.  The next time we decided to explore some of the other parts of the menu.  We tried the chile relleno burrito and the Tampiquena.  Both were out of this world.  The best Mexican we have every had.

This is still one of our favorite casual places to go, especially if we've got a show to go to at the Granada or the Arlington.  The good news is that not long after they removed the fried green tomatoes and goat cheese stack from the menu, they brought it back again!  The bad news is that Tupelo Junction is no longer on OpenTable, which reduces the convenience of going there a bit.  We've had breakfast here a few times now, and the vanilla dipped french toast is phenomenal. It's very rich, though: I've never been able to finish much more than half of the plate.  The pumpkin oatmeal waffle is also very good, with a more subtle flavor than the vanilla french toast.

Told you I would be back.. What I didn't tell you was I'm coming back the same week.. Christmas Eve, me being late to make food choices. I called to see if Ginos was open.. Score! They were.  I tried the 'Chicken Pesto panini ' They told me I got the last sandwich bread used to make it. Not sure if me getting the last one was worthy of a medallion. But hey, I enjoyed the sandwich  And I can see I will be back to taste it again Next time I seek to be the first to get the sandwich bread The taste of the pesto was good, above average.  Here are some things that would have made this sandwich better. Ask for more pesto ( I liked the flavor) Ask if the chicken can be cut into smaller pieces  ( definitely real chicken breast, it was a little on the dry side) I don't mind that. But most will. That's it.... I recommend others to try it.. Would like to know what they think  Customer service was excellent. Gave the feeling I was dealing with mom and pop And not a corporate automated strategy

I don't understand the rave reviews for this place. It's ok, it serves average Mexican food for an above average price.  HOWEVER there is simply no excuse for how filthy this place is. There are food wrappers all over the floor, tables aren't cleaned and the service don't give a damn. There's often not even any way of drying your hands in the stagnant restroom.  Seriously, people...this is the same price as Los Agaves, who serve excellent food, in a spotless restaurant and the service is excellent. Why bother?
I have been back to Stella mare's, the service was good, and so was my food. I ordered the duck burger it was delicious. Thank you, Sunny

I don't go to vices & spices as often as I would imagine given how much I love it but I think that's why- I love savoring how good it feels to visit.  The coffee shop and options have the feel and service of a local coffee bar where everyone remembers you & I love that!  They do have a wonderful selection of teas, coffee - & even cardamom to sprinkle on your coffee next to the half & half, love that!!  The store is so cute and always has an interesting selection of gifts and decor for friends or yourself.  I'll be back...soon-ish!

Long wait but it's right on the water and near state street, so it's to be expected. Food is really good and service was very kind and helpful. We had a large party with lots of vegetarians and there were no issues. Portion sizes are  huge- we ended up taking a bunch of stuff home. Did not have the mimosa flight but it looked good. Overall if you are on/near State street, this is a great place to go in Santa Barbara!

Their wings are delicious. So far we haven't tried a flavor we don't like but our top 3 are: cali bbq, lemon pepper and buffalo. The bone in are great but the boneless are my favorite and a big hit with the kids. Not a bad deal either, $45 bucks for 40 wings will feed about 5-6 people. So glad this place came to town. Santa Barbara was missing a good wing spot but not anymore.

I stop by Yellowbelly every now and again. My favorite dish is the simple grilled cheese! Comes with a tomato soup dipping sauce that is delicious and I always want more! Used my Axxess card for the buy one get one to top off the lunch.

If you're vegetarian, this is not the place for you. There are minimal options and none are interesting. I did get the filet mignon with angel hair pasta (minus the filet mignon) and it was good, but it was nothing to go back for. There is one vegetarian polenta dish that is so bizarre. It was super flavorless and just too weird for me.   I didn't have a cocktail that night but ordered a shirley temple. It was the worst shirley temple I'd ever had! The reviews of the cocktails by the rest of my table were super mixed. So I would probably advise sticking to a wine or beer if you come here.    The service was great and our waitress was very friendly. The new outdoor patio is very nice. But overall, the lack of menu options for me and the mediocre food would be the reasons I won't be back.

I'm giving this place 4 stars for the the tacos.   I had an al pastor and a carne asada with all the fixings that they supplied.   They were Tijuana style, as was Cuernavaca (which I tried immediately before).    The tortillas at Lilly's tasted much better to me than those at Cuernavaca taqueria (Lilly's tasted like the corn tortillas I get in TJ, while the one's at Cuernavaca had a flavor / consistency that just seemed a bit off to me).  Service was fast and friendly.    The only downside to Lilly's was the following: - they don't have any guacamole - all of their indoor seating is closed during Covid - they had zero tables or chairs outside ...... you either had to sit on the curb, or take it to go ;  they have the perfect location to put a couple of tables and chairs... I think their customer's would appreciate it, and they might even get more customers (not everyone wants to sit on the curb).

I've been to Los Arroyos many times, although I've never managed to order anything other than the Burrito Mojado.  I also just ordered a large takeout order (graduation party with 25 people), and I am very happy with the experience.  The food was ready on time and my guests were happy with the food.  The manager was helpful on the phone (she asked if we were going to eat right away so they knew whether to put the cheese and lettuce on the food or on the side).  The only downsides were that I had to call a few times in order to catch the manager when she was there, and the beef tacos were labelled as chicken tacos, which caused a little confusion.  However, I would definitely order party food from Los Arroyos again.

Great food, great ambiance and great service from the valet to your table host. I was impressed with the way every dish even the most basic ones like the grilled Brussel sprouts were accentuated with a happy marriage of flavors (in this case the Brussel sprouts were grilled with fish sauce and garnished with caramelized dates) A flavorful twist! They start you off with seasoned popcorn, also unexpected. I tried the cheese plate, and the scallop dish paired with Israeli couscous. Very flavorful and cooked to perfection. The place is trendy, and perfect for a group dinner or date.
Okay, first of all. If this chicken, not bad. If it's fish ~decent~  Great meal and I had a blast, but what's the deal with all this mud under my food??

I like to sit outside, where it is less noisy and you can look out over the harbor. Unless you get in early you usually have to wait, but that's an opportunity to stroll around and work up an appetite for the delicious fat oysters. Following those, I usually have a beer and a salad - the tuna poke is absolutely delicious and very filling - so remember, come with a healthy appetite!

In chronology of order.  1. Taking the order  We sat at the bar for 25 minutes and the bartenders never looked up.  After being fascinated about this, we started trying to get their attention with waving and eventually yelling. Finally they took the order.  2. Lost the order Actually, the order was delivered to the wrong table, but we were at the bar..how can you confuse a different Ning table from the bat.  3. Post event lost order So they said, non problem we'll deliver your lost order in a minute..we reordered the lost order through the kitchen, after waiting we left. (We paid but we left, that's how bad it was)  4. At 8:00 pm it's freezing I used to live in Sweden, this is the coldest bar I have ever experienced, it was 54 degrees Fahrenheit sitting at the inside outside bar  5. Never come back We will never come back to this establishment ever, it is still standard in every conceivable or imaginable dimension  Overall as an American I'm embarrassed of my experience, although the young men working are good people ..they are clueless or stoned or both to provide an experience we had. I believe you have to work really hard at sucking this bad.  OVER N OUT

Made a return visit to Santa Barbara, can't get enough of this picturesque city! Decided to stop at Sunshine Cafe, Yelp pics of their food items looked cool! Ordered breakfast burrito, they use whole pinto beans which gives the burrito an authentic taste. Potatoes on side with green onion topping were very good. BLT sandwich very hearty, and coffee nice and strong! Recommended!

Wow, insanely good. This place was needed in Santa Barbara and it's a perfect addition to the Public Market. The Albacore bowl had the yummiest flavor combination (anything with watermelon wins in my eyes) and the shishito peppers were cooked perfectly and so tasty. Can't wait to go back and try each of the handrolls! I almost stole one off of my table neighbor's plate.

Bouchon would've received a 5 Star from me but I needed to deduct a point for a couple of things.    We had reservations for 8:30pm.  We arrived and waited for about 20-30 minutes before we were sat.  Unfortunately, some tables took longer to turn. The next negative is the restroom that is located next door in their private dining room area.  The restrooms are dirty looking.  :( If there's a wait for the downstairs restroom go upstairs but beware because there's a shower up there too that is not in use because there was dirt all over and wide open.   There was a private party going on and while we stood on the patio for that long period of time we watched the servers walked out into the street to turn right next door to serve the dishes to the patrons.   Now the last negative is there no waiting area. That's fine that there's no bar but nowhere to sit or stand other than on the patio seating where others are also eating.  Brr it was cold so a lot of people who were waiting for their table began to congregate on the patio as the patrons on the patio began to look at us.   Now the positive.  We sat at our table and the service was absolutely impeccable.  We were given complimentary champagne due to our wait.   My HANGRY moment started to calm down when I looked at the menu and drank my drink.  :)    Food:  Tutti Frutti Farms  Heirloom Tomato Salad- This was just alright.  I wish we ordered the Pork Belly after seeing our neighbors dish.  But the salad was refreshing.   Grilled Snake River American Wagyu Center Cut Ribeye.  I ordered it medium.  It was cut in half like a burger and the top half was perfect but the bottom half was RARE.  I had to return it back to cook a bit more and they ended up making it medium well.  :(  I still ate it cause I was not about to wait again for another one.  It was seasoned well and nice and tender.   Lastly, my friend ordered the Salmon that was dressed and cooked well.  I had a bite of it and it was delicious.    It was our birthday so they put a candle on our dessert.  We had the lava cake.  The lava was not hot but very luke warm. It didn't not ooze out when we tried to break it apart.   Overall, it was a nice dinner.  It is not a huge restaurant but more intimate setting.  I would return back because the food is good and would recommend it to all.

Amazing. We got roast beef sandwich and a grilled cheese and shared. It was yummy but it took a long time. Close to 25 minutes. But it was super tasty. After that we did cheese and salami tastings. They let us try everything we wanted. This was great and really helped us figure out what we wanted to buy. They have cheese and wine plates too. Definitely worth going to.
solid mexican food. nothing spectacular. good atmosphere. has been empty the couple times i've been, but nice staff!

I've been getting what I think is called (it's been a while) the bellagio mmm! It's smoky and so tasty!  I've had the same waitress a few times and she is amazing!  I highly recommend this restaurant and try the bellagio!
You pay for the view...food is OK but overpriced. Bahn mi flatbread was meh, Arctic chard was good.

Half full or half empty you decide. I stopped by Backyard Bowls on Saturday afternoon to pick up a 12oz Pitaya Smoothie. The smoothie includes: Pineapple Juice, Coconut Milk, Bananas, Mangos, Pitaya, Honey. The smoothie itself is actually really good. For the first attempt I was unable to drink it and asked them to blend it again, after all this is a smoothie not a bowl. When I got it back I ended up with a half empty smoothie. For $6.95 this is not acceptable and really false advertisement. I will not be returning to this location.

Flavor explosion!!! So delicious beyond words.... The manager is always super friendly and everything I have had has been phenomenal. I appreciate everything this place has to offer. The food is fresh and vibrant and so different than anything else in town. Please stop by and support this gem, we need more places like this to survive and not fade away. Please do yourself a favor and support this business.   Today I tried the Kale Burger subbed for the spicy falafel. My fiancé ordered the sweet potato burger and even though this time the sweet potato falafel was taking a temporary hiatus (back tomorrow) we both left super satisfied and so happy with our food. We got the sweet potato fries with our rewards points and they were delicious as well. Thank you Fala Bar, we shall see you again soon :)

This a Halloween edition type review, for I have truly met the biggest witch this Halloween morn. I know she wasn't playing a role because she always is. I don't appreciate my food with a side of attitude. If you don't like the customer service industry please make yourself available somewhere else.  I'm glad  I did not finish this because the husband of the wicked witch just found my number & threatened me for telling his wife about herself. If you enjoy this type of treatment by all means eat here, at your own risk
Love this place, especially before a show at the Granada!  Great date night location!

We have used La Colmena 3 times to cater our corporate events and everyone raves. The pricing is great for large groups and there are always leftovers. We get the big option with three kinds of meat, plus rice and beans, guac and all the fixings. It's the easiest event I have to organize!

They have to hand us our order just like the other restaurant amazing place the people who make the sandwiches is fast the stars are for the staff they don't care maybe they'll learn something from other restaurants

The location: ON THE BEACH, about 20 minutes from downtown Santa Barbara.  As you would and should expect, gorgeous views of the Pacific Ocean.  The grounds: beautifully manicured very good imitation of a tropical paradise and if it wasn't for the winter chills at night, I would buy that.  The hotel is also very pet friendly - with little bird kiosks for poop bags and enough garbage cans so you don't have to walk to find one. There is a self-washing station just off the path from the beach so you can rinse off sandy footwear, and sandy pets!  Very convenient.   The rooms: Aged, but well kept, clean and with charm.  Nespresso readily available - our ground floor unit also opened up to a small, yet private patio overlooking a garden.    The pricing: For a Ritz, it is not bad but there are deals going around, just make sure you know you can reason with registration employees - some have more power than you think.  The pool: during Covid times, you have to compromise.  At least they allow each family to experience the hot tubs in 15 min increments.  You have to make appointments to ensure a lounge chair on the deck - not a big deal, just plan early (1-2 days in advance)...   The restaurants/food - this is probably the most delightful surprise - whether it was the convenient coffee bar near valet, the poolside dining, or the rooftop bar, all our experiences were next level.  Read my other reviews...  The spa/fitness center: CLOSED (no minus points though)  Golf carts:  ugh, the golf carts.  I get it, it helps employees get around from one cluster to the next... but they go too fast, they are too many of them, and they run ALL HOURS OF THE NIGHT.  This probably had something to do with the fact that we were placed in Bldg 15, adjacent to the frontage road and we think, right next to housekeeping.  Most rooms did not require daily changes because of COVID and it didn't look like they were anywhere near capacity so why all the activity?  Would definitely stay here again, maybe not in peak time, and would try to change to a room in a different bungalow.

Service is horrible. Went hers last week for lunch. Got my bread and asked for some balsamic vinegar. The waitress took at least 10 minutes before she got it to us. She walked around the restaurant at least three times, serving other customers before she came around with the vinegar. Then our batch of bread had a long hair in it. Ewww so disgusting. I ordered the Milan  fetticine. It was just okay. I would rather eat at Olive Gardeen than this place. Waitress took my plates after I was done and just made me sit there. Didn't ask if I wanted dessert, which I did, and got the. Choclate creme brûlée
The Manager gets 5 Stars for his appreciated and warm response. I can tell this is a person who honestly has a great moral compass who was raised right!!! Bless you kind sir!!!

What a great place!  The setting is beautiful.  You eat under a pergola with lots of vines around and with a view of the beach.  The bar is good and the bartender knows what he is doing.  We had spritzes and negronis and they were great.  Had the pesce crudo and a sampling of the vegetable offerings as small plates for appetizers.  Very creative.  I never would have put some of the ingredients together, but they were great.  The carrots were really something special.  For dinner we had the ribs, and two kinds of pizzas.  The ribs were amazing.  The pizzas creative and good.  The prices are fair, especially for the quantity and the quality.  Some people gave negative reviews of their experience with the service.  I would say that our server was friendly and nice and could have checked in a little more frequently.

A local steered us this way. After a long morning of museums and walking, we were all hungry and ready for a big lunch. We sat down in a booth and after waiting for a few minutes, we realized that we had to get up and order in the room next door.  Oopsies.  Then they brought our order out to us in the dining room, but the salsa bar was near the kitchen in the other room, so we had to get back up and get the condiments there.  On to the food - I ordered the nachos, which were really filling and hit the spot. The tortilla chips were perfect, nicely sized. Overall, the dish was just fine, A-OK as Yelp says, just nothing that I haven't tasted before a thousand times.

We dined here a week ago and it was delicious! Even my super picky 6 year old loved it and she dislikes 90% of the food we give her. We ordered the fried okra, beet salad as appetizers and they were delicious. My husband ordered the gumbo, I had the pork shoulder and my daughters shared the jerk chicken. The food was soul satisfying. I can't wait to return.

i spent a lot of time here last winter doing my studies. its a bit strange at first,why?? many ah different people come in this coffee shop and it makes for good conversation. the staff is a bit laid back( like cali is) and a bit strange. but they are a good bunch of people( real). coffee was great and the people were even better to chat with. so this was my fav coffee place in SB .. btw the fireplace was nice but not often needed, lol.....
One of the best meals I've ever had. Someone give this place a Michelin Star. Hello?!

I had a better experience last time while we dined at Eureka than before, service was a tad better at least in the beginning.  Our first waiter, Jose was nice and attentive. He did finish his shift, so the new waiter was hard to get his attention, I waved to get the bill, finally after various failed attempts, I had to walk to him and ask for it. I just couldn't get his attention for the life of me and didn't want to get extreme!!!  The food is great as always, unfortunately they did change the HH menu and the great osso bucco riblets are gone off this new menu. But you can get them from the regular one, which we did, at regular price (sigh...). The beer selection is still good, the HH menu is not as good. But still a decent Hoppy Hour as they call it. Service - depends on who you get. Solid place for a casual meal in downtown Santa Barbara, overall.

Bottom Line:  The best live music venue in Santa Barbara, without question.  I think we are in House of Blues territory now!  Toe's Tavern, The Hibar, Yukitan, The Coach House, The history of Santa Barbara music venues is nearly as colorful as the history of the bands that came out of this town; Toad the Wet Sproket, Dishwalla, Big Bad Voodoo Daddy, The Ataris, Lagwagon, No Use for a Name, Snot, Nerf Herder......the SBCC Lunch Break band.  Nearly every single live music venue is gone from the golden era of music in this town.  Back in the 1990's I used to be on the local 'sound guys' that would turn up and try and make sure people could hear the bands.  Soho was absolutely one of the places where I used to do sound and sit against the wall behind what was basically a TV tray with a Mackie 1604 on it for $40 a night plus free drinks.  It was dark and kind of dingy but thats what they were all like back in the world of analog.  How times have changed in 20+ years (which is essentially the amount of time its been since i have been to Soho).  Ive heard people talk about it recently but never put much thought into it as I used to work there - still had the idea of a dark, hole in the wall type place in my brain.  Well, last night the wife tells me that we have a date night.  Sweet!   It had been some time since we had been able to get out together.  I didnt know that local hero Chris Schiflett was going to be playing so having dinner with the wife was going to be icing on the cake for sure.  We had the hamburger and the raviolis both of which were great.  The burger was excellent which frankly is really saying something with the burger game being what it is these days.  Tender, moist, lots of flavor - steak fries done properly which is a little crispy and not doughy and hot enough to burn my mouth!   Yes!! The raviolis are very similar to the ones I have had at Ca'Dario and were excellent.  Over drinks we had a Creme Brule as well and that was also delicious.  Into the room for the show and do you know how you can tell how the sound is pretty good?  When you dont notice it.  While getting my audio engineering degree years ago, I realized that the best sound people are ones that you never notice.  Which is kind of sad but it absolutely true - Ive always said that a good sound are like good windshield wipers: if they are doing their job properly, you'll never know they are there.  Half way through the show, I realized that I had just been enjoying the show and not critiquing how it sounded and it hit me - thats because the sound was good!  How refreshing and thank you kind engineer, whomever you are.   I was properly blown away last night at our visit to Soho.  Food was great, the venue is great, the staff are helpful, the bathrooms are modern and functional, the sound was great....  I may not have even recognized it had I been teleported - they have done an incredible job right down to the super modern and very fancy bathrooms.  Ill be back here just for dinner - not even for a show but I will absolutely be back for both.   Well done, Soho!  You're keeping the local music scene alive

Back again for our 1 year anniversary!   This place never disappoints. Our server was amazing! Food was amazing! Ambience in front of the fireplace outside was perfect.   Oh and yes we took home a WHOLE key lime pie..  we unfortunately lost the top tier of our wedding cake so we were unable to enjoy that for our 1st anniversary but that's okay because the key lime pie from here is so good!   Appreciate you guys so much for always having such top notch service and food!

This is just for the bar. The first few times I came here they had the best MaiTai in town. They are hit and miss on their specials. They used to also have free appetisers during happy hour. The last couple of times they have had new bartenders who didn't know how to make the Mai Taj listed on the menu.this current one is the worst one I have had. Not at all like the menu. I guess check before you sit down if the bartender is new. I am sorry I recommended the Mai Tai to people. The food is supposed to be good.

Love it.  I come here abut once a week.  Not tons of items on the salad bar but they are super fresh and high quality.  And their Blue Cheese dressing is off the hook.  Their steaks are awesome.  Their FISH is amazing.  Try the Ahi Tuna steak.  Best I have had anywhere.

Well I have to say I was pleasantly surprised! I absolutely enjoyed every moment at this place. Upon arriving there was a parking lot if their own! Which after spending the weekend in SB, I really appreciated... as most places you need to use valet or park in a common area parking lot and walk.... which is fine for me as well. But again a nice and easy way to start my Sunday!  Once parked we headed over to put our name on the list and was notified about the 1.5hr wait! Wow! Fortunately, we arrived right as a couple was leaving in the bar area.... which is a first come first serve area! Lucky for sure!  The views are amazing! It's ON the beach! I could've just stayed there all day long. It was warm on the outside seating patio area with heaters and a glass enclosure. Really any spot out there would be great for views!  They were crowded, but the fellow diners and restaurant overall had a great vibe. The bar was full and all tables inside and out, several families were dining for brunch.  We shared the lobster and shrimp omelette, a new one for me and delicious! We also had the French toast with berries(which included strawberries, blackberries, raspberries, AND blueberries) all of the good ones!!!   Tip:Generous breakfast portion sizes served here, you can definitely share.   Both meals were amazing,  one of my favorite breakfast experiences ever!  My sister enjoyed her bloody Mary and my mimosa was tasty. The paradise coffee....perfect!  I will be back to try this place again.  First experience was amazing!

Horrible experience here, both the food and the service were awful.   Food: Once we saw the menu (It's HUGE) you could tell that things would be frozen, not fresh. I ordered a burger to play it safe thinking that it would likely be fresh (I mean, come on, it's ground beef) and unfortunately it was very obviously a frozen burger patty like the ones you get from Costco. Only plus was that the fries were good.  Service: we were seated and completely ignored by the staff from that point on. The dogs around us received more attention then we did (no joke - refilled water bowls, treats, etc) We had to flag a waitress down after 10 minutes just to put a drink order in. We were never once approached by a waitress for anything and then their attitude was like they were annoyed we were bothering them! We just wanted to order food!   Won't be back.

Really love the sandwiches here, and the other local products. There's a  great range of vegetarian and meat-loving choices. Plus the service is very friendly. I've been 4 times now and will definitely keep coming.

Snuck into this busy busy restaurant thanks to the very cute hostess(by the way thanks )and were promptly seated.We were met by two very capable servers Mariel being the lead,well we wanted everything on the menu being that their were only three of us Mariel suggested that maybe we had a bit to much(albeit very tactfully)food ordered.So reluctantly we took her very goood advice and eliminated a few items.Sooo we widdled it down to four items Ribeye(OMG),Saffron Couscous,Hamachi Crudo w/ gazpacho(a must have),and the (not your mamas)Corn Bread.Generally theirs a week link in the chain yah not so here ALL I MEAN ALL WERE AMAZING.And then dessert Chocolate Creme Brûlée with Passion Fruit Ice Cream.Something Amazing for everyone.We had to share the dessert,the dishes we ordered were huge.Cant say enough about this restaurant come and enjoy.

Wow, what a treat! You would never know this bakery cafe was gluten free! Such a wonderful selection of cakes, desserts and breads. Since having to go gluten free I have been missing fluffy, fresh baked bread and moist, crumbly cake. No mas!! I know where to go from now on. Can't wait to come back.

My first time staying at a Ritz-Carlton resort and it was simply amazing. This one in particular had a Spanish style vibe with the archways, the tiles, the open door patio, French doors to the bathroom, etc. I loved that bathtub was open to the bedroom, it had a romantic feel. The valet service is beyond accommodating and super friendly. They are attentive and nice. They do have an overpriced coffee shop next to the lobby area. What I did not like was the amount of insects (which I know is expected) but just on the walls chilling, made me anxious. But it has a beautiful pool, nice towel service, walk to the beach and hiking trails. It is serene and a great escape.

This place is a small deli and they sell sandwiches and sushi. I ordered a tri-tip sandwich from here. The sandwich came with a half-foot French bread with red onions, tomatoes, lettuce, and tri-tip with mayo. It was mostly bread and there was barely anything in the sandwich. Overall, it tasted okay and the service is quick.

Fantastic hole in the wall place - great price and fantastic sushi, especially for a city like Santa Barbara.  The sushi is fresh tasting and at a great price and definately affordable for college students.  Definately a hidden gem!
Breakfast is ok, but it starts at like $16.  4 stars for being the only downtown bread bakery, but simple breakfasts shouldn't be so pricy.

One of our local watering holes we come in for ice cold chief peaks after work! The staff is amazing and Blair goes above and beyond to create a great experience with great service! Great job benchmark! And your clam chowda is awesome!

My last review was for the restaurant when it was called Cielitos. It has now changed to Viva and under the new ownership I am still very happy with food and drinks. The menu is much more casual, the food is worth the price especially during happy hour! I gave 4 instead of 5 because I have only been there for happy hour, not dinner yet..but I can see it climbing up to 5 soon!!

I been to this taco place wen it first opened I ordered 2 asada 1 lengua, they where ok at the time... i live really close by and  ran for some  tacos got their 2 minutes before they close the sign still said open they denied me service i was really upset but noting i could do about it ...months later got over it decided to give it another try I was very hungry didn't want to drive anywhere so i walked their and got some tocos notice that the price wen up, i still order because i was hungry. I was  disappointed i got the same thing as My last visit. The meat was greasy and had no taste the only thing going for this place is their salsa.

First of all, this review is for the biz, pre-coronavirus.  I started this a while ago and never finished.  Why bother? Well, even a year later I remember the night I was there, it was that good.  Service was excellent and the cocktails fantastic.  I kept it tame and ordered a mojito and my friends ordered cuba libres. Both drinks were amazing and went down way too easily.    I will definitely be returning asap. Idk if they're open during Covid but give them a call and please give them your biz if you can. No, I was not paid to say that. ;)  Basically, if you're in Santa Barbara, you've got to give this place a try!
I don't know what else to say other than AMAZINGLY AMAZING !! I don't want to leave. I want to die here please put Pacific Crepes in my casket with me, lol

My family and I decided to drive up PCH and we ended up in Santa Barbara.  It was a good thing we did because this place was so delicious!  Everything we got here was so good and fresh.  I will be coming back when I'm in the area!    Yay: -Clam chowder: probably one of the best I've had and I've had a lot from different states!  Creamy with a good amount of ingredients -Grilled oysters: this was my favorite! Tons of butter garlic goodness! -Sally Sand Dabs: I had no idea what sand dabs were but the waitress recommended it and I'm glad she did.  The lemon and capers sauce complimented the fish so nicely.  I will be ordering this again! -Tequila shrimp pasta: this was a good dish but since everything else was so flavorful, this was just okay.

A few hours ago, I ate dinner at Pascucci's with a friend, who is a SB local. This was one of her favorite SB restaurants.   I enjoyed every component of my meal, but I have to pick the salad as the stand out. I had a house side salad with gorgonzola dressing and it was outstanding. All of the salad dressings are house made and I think is part of what made it so stellar.   The other reason that the salad was so delicious, is Pascucci uses local ingredients from local farms. The salad really tasted like the ingredients had been picked this morning. Everything was super fresh and delicious.  The garlic bread is great. It's really garlicky though.  I had meat lasagna as my main entree. It was very good, very meaty and huge. I could only eat half, so I have lunch for tomorrow!  The service was attentive and even though the restaurant was packed, we never felt rushed.

We got a couple delicious IPAs while we decided on food. The specials were great- Tri tip salad, curry chicken wings and flatiron steak- all perfectly grilled and prepared, with sauces on the side. Staff is knowledgeable and friendly. We always enjoy dinners at Yellow Belly.

*note: this is not meant to harm the company whatsoever, this is the truth from the perspective of a ex-employee* I've worked here for the past three months and I can truthfully say that the constant harassment of one of my coworkers forced me to resign for the sake of my mental health. When I was hired three months ago, I was really excited because of the wonderful staff and good quality of food. Unfortunately, this took a deep turn for the past few weeks. One of my coworkers constantly told me that I wasn't good enough, looked down on me, pushed me aside and was unprofessional. I've heard them a couple of times yell at the chef for no reason. That's no way a business should be running. The owner yelled, screamed and called me multiple explicits over the phone in front of customers several times. I won't go into details and this is no way harming the restaurant but I'm just telling the truth of my experience working here. I had a severe incident on Friday 5/3/19 and my manager has not taken any immediate steps to resolve an ongoing issue. My advice to perspective clients, there's many more places where there is a better & ethical place for workers in SB. This place happens not to be one. I will miss working here & I wish the company the best of luck at the SBPM

Our dinner was lovely. We ordered by phone and had it delivered. The delivery man was masked, kind, and fast. The food was fresh and hot. Excellent mu shu, mock orange chicken, tasty vegetables. Fabulous!!
They were out of half of the food I wanted to order from the menue and it really sucks that they don't have WiFi! I mean It's the 21st century!
Disappointing.  Food was way too spicy even though we didn't order anything marked spicy on the menu. Kung pao shrimp had very few shrimp.  Staff was friendly.

Over the years we have seen changes from what originally was a nice quaint Italian eatery where you could enjoy a nice romantic dinner which has changed due to greed or the requirements to push massive amounts of guests through  the system.  Our reservation was right on, and where seated at one of their minuscule tables which are not large enough for two plates, a bottle of wine and the salt and pepper service. If you want water glasses on the table something has to go, your comfort. Jammed next to my wifes chair was a 4 set which they were able to squeeze in, very uncomfortably. No longer is the comfort of guests important, being able to turn tables no matter what the comfort level both sound from screaming children at their birthday party or having to excuse ones self when needing to move your chair. Sadly we will remember the better times at Ca Dario, and try to forget the recent and last visit here.

Unfortunate experience. As a follow up, I emailed the Stonehouse @  guestservice@sanysidroranch.com on April 12, but received no response.  On Tue, Apr 12, 2016 at 10:11 AM, Michael wrote:  To whom it may concern:  I write this email to describe my unfortunate experience at The Stonehouse restaurant on Friday, April 1.  It was a special dinner for my wife's birthday. We live locally but had never been to The Stonehouse. Upon arriving and walking the amazing grounds, my wife joked, 'I wondered if this is going to be like one of those waterfront restaurants that has mediocre, overpriced food because they are in such a great location.' I laughed and assured her that was not the case based on reviews I had read and the many awards bestowed upon the property.  After having a drink at the bar downstairs, we were seated at a lovely table on the terrace. We looked over the extensive wine list and had a few questions for the sommelier to finalize our decision. The waiter, a gentleman named [...], informed us that the sommelier was not available but he could tell us what he liked. We decided to take the easy route and selected a wine from the sommelier list, although we found it somewhat odd that the sommelier who represented such an extensive wine list would not have some type of in-person representation on a Friday evening.  Our waiter then took our appetizer and main course orders. He returned about 10 minutes later asking to take our main course order that we had already placed, but we laughed it off and moved along. Another 10 minutes later, the waiter came back to inform my wife that the chef's special trout dish she ordered was no longer available, but he would be happy to serve her salmon using the same preparation. We declined the offer, finding it odd that two different fish types could so easily be interchanged with a 'special' preparation designed by the chef.  Given this news, my wife decided on the halibut and I ordered the lamb loin. I joked with my wife about the odds that we would receive what we ordered, which unfortunately turned out to be the case. The server's assistant then brought us two orders of halibut. One was well prepared and the other was completely dried out and had obviously been waiting under the heat lamp for far too long. We asked the server's assistant to have the waiter come by to explain the mix-up. The server then offered to place my wife's halibut under the heat lamp while the chef prepared my lamb loin. While I might expect this approach from a casual dining establishment, I was shocked by this proposal. I explained to the waiter that we would enjoy the halibut as served and move forward. He then abruptly picked up my halibut and explained that we could share my wife's halibut while my lamb loin was prepared, and then we could share the lamb loin. After 15 minutes, the lamb loin came out, cooked well beyond my medium rare request (which the waiter came back to clarify yet another time), and seemingly the victim of waiting under the heat lamp.  We then ordered dessert, and as an apology, the waiter decided to bring us each a glass of champagne. I told him that we appreciated the gesture and left it at that. Unfortunately, it was brought while we were still enjoying our red wine and eating a chocolate souffle -- not exactly a pairing that I imagine would have been approved by the sommelier had he or she been present.  My wife's request of me was to take all of the mistakes in stride and enjoy the ambience, which we did.  I rarely provide feedback but feel the need to do so in this case. I've had worse dining experiences in my life, but unfortunately The Stonehouse ranks as the worst at a price point and assumed level of prestige for which my expectations were simply not met.  Regards, Michael

For some reason I was craving Alcazar on a weekday evening a couple of weeks ago, and I convinced the Ms. to go.  We hadn't been there in well over a year, and the dish I was craving was the Puntas de Filete con Champinones.  I remember it so well, it was pieces of filet mignon in a mushroom, butter, sherry sauce served with toasted bread.  But it was no longer on the menu :-(  We did end up getting both the Filet con Championes and the Filet con Crema...but for me, it just didn't cut it...I was still disappointed...plus it was so loud that we could barely have a discussion at our table.  The only plus of the evening is that they were playing old episodes of 'I Love Lucy' that kept our 7-year old entertained.  Alcazar, you went from a 4-star to a 2-star in my book.  Go back to your roots, and I'll be back again...and again.

I loved this place-- the views over SB as you're eating is enough to bring me back! The food was pretty good as far as I remember-- I think I got sliders of some sort. The service was good but the waiter was really gunning for tips and kept coming back to check on us-- even between ordering and us getting our food. Still he kept our drinks topped up and we were happy.  I find it strange that when you ask to take food home, they just give you a box, in the States. When I was working I would usually at least offer to do it for them, and people who preferred to could do it themselves-- don't know why, I mean, I brought you the food in the first place, I'm not any less clean now!?  So it was a bit of chaos trying to get our food into the doggy-box at the end, we were all covered in sauce and mess. But no worries.

We had reservations for four at their fancy prix fixe Valentine's Day Dinner last month. The service was superb and the waiter put up with my husband's sarcastic jokes very well. He also did a great job clarifying some of the fancy vocabulary on the menu. The food was wonderful, especially the ahi tuna dish. I was shocked how delicious the uber-healthy sounding fruit and nut dessert bar was, I even traded it for my Warm chocolate chip cookie pie. Avoid the beet puree soup though, it tasted like barbecue sauce.
We've been here a few times and each time has been delicious! The food is consistent and the staff are very attentive and friendly. Great cocktails! Highly recommend the veggie pizza!!

Food is very tasty, but not worth the absolutely horrendous service we received.  I used to love this place but will not be returning.  I'm paying you for good food, great service, and ambience.  Not for all your stank ass attitudes.  If you don't want to service your paying customers, don't work in customer service.

The owners Remi and Tamira are super friendly, the customer service is the best in town. Food is good, its authentic, the lunch buffet has more varieties,  Enjoy!, don't forgot the tasty desert (Kheer and some fruits) at the lunch buffet :)

The salad lyonnaise and French onion soup were recommended by the server and they were ok. Their bread was good. I had the sea bass dish. The sea bass was tasty but the dish as a whole was a little odd with the little shrimp, pesto,  fingerling potatoes, fava beans and pickled pearl onions. The seared tuna dish my boyfriend had I was not a fan of. Chewy fish and the herb crust I didn't care for.  The dish was also too sweet for my liking. He ate it all because he was hungry even though I told him to ask for something else he hates wasting things. When he mentioned to them the fish quality was not so good and chewy they immediately removed it from the bill so they do care about what they make.  The dessert I chose was baked Alaska which looked cool when they lit it on fire but I blew it out before it burned all the Bacardi 151. 151 soaked into the cake which tasted awful. It definitely looked better than it tasted. The lemon cake description got my hopes up.   Since their menu is seasonal I would give it another go.
Really quite good sushi rolls.  The press boxes are excellent- hard to describe, just enjoy selecting which ones and try them. Great service. Small dining room, so likely will wait for a table.
They forgot items in our order and the rude manager said, what do you want me to do about it!!!!!! Horrible customer service and sub-par food.

I have been back several more times since my last review. The homemade potato chips are well seasoned. They took the horrible spinach salad off the menu and replaced it with a shredded kale salad with parmesan vinaigrette. Much better. They do need to diversify their salad menu more often. Or perhaps add one more.

What a quaint find just off of State St!  PARKING: Look for the 75 minute free lots in the area or street parking.  FOOD: -Dirty Fries: Yes, I have fries for breakfast. They count when you add the egg (YOU MUST ADD THE EGG). As french fries come, these are stellar. Great medium thickness, crisp exterior with a warm, soft interior. The spicy aoli isn't quite as spicy as I thought it would be, but still delicious. Add the bacon and the perfectly poached egg, and you have a one way ticket to a happy heart attack.  -Shrimp & Grits: LOVED these. Generous portion of nicely cooked shrimp on a bed of fluffy grits. For those with smaller appetites, I would share this dish and add a salad or other green to break up the grits, shrimp, sausage, an egg. It can get quite heavy after a while.  -Burger: Hubs had this and basically wolfed it down in 3 minutes. Delicious brioche bun.  DRINKS:  -They don't mess around with brunch. Come prepared with pedialyte and try their craft sodas!  SERVICE: Friendly. Make sure you make a reservation on the weekends as they are quite popular.

Everything was under or over seasoned.   The best dishes were the appetizers (mussels, chowder, oysters)... The other food was forgettable and what you would expect from a harbor restaurant.   The view is gorgeous tho. The upstairs is the full restaurant. There was a wait but their buzzer allowed us to walk around the marina until our name was called. I would stick to the fresher seafood and I think you'll be ok.

Great service, great ambiance and amazing food. The drinks are fantastic and not over-priced. I could live off of their sour dough rolls and margaritas. I have never had a bad experience at any of the Lure Locations. This one by far has the best outdoor dining and is a must eat at while in SB. I love Lure!

This place was ...ok. Really not a place for the gluten free crowd. I had the scramble with wild mushrooms, asparagus, and truffle cheese, had them switch out the bread for home fries. I really wanted to try their famous bloody mary, but I couldn't confirm that the vodka was wheat free. I did try the yummy rum and peach drink which was delightful! The day I tried this place out was a Saturday around noon while it was storming out, and I had no trouble getting a table without a wait.  3 stars for: ok service, not great/not bad; not a good selection for those who are gluten free or vegan. The prices were 'ok' nothing special about the decor, the jelly jar glasses and cloth napkins were the only nice touches. The scramble had a good amount of ingrediants and was tasty. The salad was ok, and the home fries weren't so great...on the soft side and looked as though they came from a bag of frozen.  Next time I'll go back to Cajun Kitchen instead.

I give Oku 3.5 stars.  I'd been wanting to eat here for months and today was the day.  We ordered the Crazy Rich Asians Roll $33, the Black Garlic Filet Mignon with Whipped Potato $34, and a couple other things.  The food took a while to come out but they were rather busy.  Everything was good but not great.  I read rave reviews about the CRA roll and it did *not* live up to the hype.  I'm glad I tried it but I don't recommend it, not for that price.  The mushrooms that came with the steak were extremely salty.  Like squeeze-your-eyes-shut salty.  Service was good and the right amount of attention.  In the end, you pay a premium for the location, which got very noisy with traffic going by.  We were in the patio next to the street at lunchtime.
Cuban approved! Not from me....but from an actual Cuban! Cuban sandwich was perfectly put together and pressed...flavors were just amazing. Highly recommend!

If we were somewhere in the deep south, this place would probably only get 2 or 3 stars, but they are the best thing going in Santa Barbara barbecue. I do have to give them props for the spicy barbecue sauce and fresh fries.   They get an extra star for having sandwiches called the 'muleskinner' and 'bullwhacker' and for all the cool stuff on the wall.

This is place is awesome to have food delivered to your place. They are so helpful when ordering and super cool when the deliver. This is a local place and the quality of the food, the service, is worth every penny!   And they also have great dessert!! The chocolate chocolate dessert is amazing!!!   If you're going to order food....order from them....because we will continue to do so!!!
This place is so bomb! I love the food, atmosphere, and drinks. The prices are good and the drink selection is on point.

Consistently mediocre, occasionally horrible. Today was one of their bad days and I'm consistently disappointed that I keep trying but the location is great and the atmosphere is nice and every time I go I think it can't be that meh...  but today I had a fish/styrofoam lettuce wrap that was inedible and equally as bad as my wife's who ordered the same thing. The kids each ate half of their Cheeseburger and grilled cheese but told us they would have rather gone to the Habit.   The worst part is they really just don't care. The woman who took my order was flippant and after waiting a ridiculous amount of time (for them to over cook my fish apparently) we finally got our food. A little while later one of the servers came over and asked how the food was, 'Everything is delicious, right?'  When I replied 'no' that the fish was dry and hard and inedible he chuckled and said, 'Not your favorite, Ha!' and walked away. Just a big missed opportunity for Santa Barbara.

On the first Saturday after Spring Equinox, we decided to visit the El Encanto for High Tea. Arrived late - packed 101 of course - but had called the restaurant twice from the road and there was no problem getting seated. Beautiful view, casually elegant patio/terrace, service was impeccable. We chose the Bellini version of high tea and their version of this summer aperitif did not disappoint - perfect, not too sweet. We decided on oolong tea, served in individual pots, and were quite taken aback when the tea arrived, made with teabags. Teabags at a high tea service - unheard of. I stuck it out with mine, but wifey immediately changed hers to loose leaf 'Halo,' upon the recommendation of the pleasant female server. The 3-tiered stand then arrived, together with a separate plate of jams, jellies and clotted cream. The little finger sandwiches were rather bland and underwhelming; salmon was farmed, cucumber sandwich was not vibrant, egg salad was without any specific flavor, topping the most boring squares of white Wonderbread imaginable - they could not even muster a pumpernickel. The scones were tiny but perfectly fine. The dessert items were very good, and made in-house. We had a very nice discussion with the manager on how to change things around for them, as the staff clearly tries hard, but their tea service is very basic - not commensurate with a hotel that holds itself out as a luxury property. For us, we would rather have paid double and had more plentiful fare, more vibrancy and flavor of the food items, and absolutely no tea bags in sight. For comparison, the Peninsula in Beverly Hills still holds top place as best tea service in SoCal.

Beer was good but kind of annoying you have to buy an entree per beer you order. So we wanted to get only carne fries to share with a beer each, but they made us order another entree. And when Guac is $11, it becomes two very expensive beers.

Large Variety to Choose from.  Excellent Quality and Always Fresh and Flavor Full.  Donuts, Bagels, Croissants to name a few.  Many Flavors of Coffee to Choose from.  They Make Everything on the Premise.  Excellent Sandwiches.  The Best Donuts in Santa Barbara and  Located at 3629 East State Street.  The Owners Care About their Product.  Making the Freshest, Quality Donuts.  They are the Nicest, Kind, Hardworking People. Which makes purchasing the Donuts most Enjoyable.
We've tried every bakery that makes bread in Santa Barbara and nothing else comes close.  I know it sounds weird, but the charcoal bread is intoxicating.  We go through a loaf a week.

Not bad.   Service was great; our server was attentive, prompt, and kind. The food was pretty good but not the best I've had. We ordered the following for two people:   - brussel sprouts - vietnamese wings - hot chicken bao - mie goreng   The brussel sprouts were the highlight of the meal. They were packed with flavor and very well cooked! Loved them. The Vietnamese wings were pretty good but a little too spicy for my taste. My husband, a spice fiend and also Vietnamese, thought they were pretty tasty.   The hot chicken bao was pretty good - good flavor, great sauce and the bao itself was great. The cucumbers on the bao definitely added a much needed-freshness to the meal. Mie goreng was a bit of a disappointment. We spent some time in Bali the past year and comparatively, this Mie goreng lacked  flavor.   Overall, it's a good place if you're craving south east/javanese/vietnamese cuisine, this is a good stop. Although the food is A-OK to me, the service is great!

The food is not bad, but the service could be a little bit better. If you have kids that need a booster chair then I don't recommend coming here as they have none. The cleanliness of this restaurant is on point, it is always spotless whenever I come in.
Great vibe! Love sitting on patio and facing State St for people watching. Nice drinks and good Pizza. Will come again for more of a meal. Like the decor and feel a lot!

Get their beyond/ impossible burger!! Highly recommend with the mushroom and truffle toppings. Also loved their buffalo cauliflower. The only problem is it's so insanely packed on the weekends and most days.

Not a bad choice for breakfast at all. I suppose the big draw is the red velvet pancakes; partly because they taste very good, and partly because they're not something you see on every menu. I would recommend them solely on that. Get them because you're on vacation and you should eat extravagant high calorie meals.

Arguably my favorite authentic Thai restaurant in Santa Barbara. I am a big fan of all of their soups and their Pad Thai as well. Fairly priced with ample portions that will not only make your stomach happy but also your wallet as well. I find Your Place being a Top5 choice whenever I find myself not wanting to make dinner throughout the week.
Generous portions, friendly staff, great food. The Asian chicken salad is spicy and fresh and HUGE and the best one in town.

We were walking on State Street and looking for restaurants to get lunch on a Wednesday when we discovered Eleven14.  We were able to get a table outside next to the huge fountain that houses many, many turtles.  It was delightful to watch the turtles sunning and swimming while we enjoyed glasses of Lincourt chardonnay and pinot noir.  We liked the wines so much we made a point of stopping at the Lincourt winery the next day for more wine tasting.  We shared an excellent margherita flatbread and a small salad.
The food is amazing!  Perfect size plates for sharing. It tends to get very loud if you're seated inside.  I'll definitely be back!

Searching for a glass of (real) champagne and or wine and something lite to eat we choose Intermezzo and it was exactly that and more. We sat at the bar and Trevor was our server while he also tended bar with talent and skill. The ambiance was soothing with the music selection tastefully done (another talent by Trevor I was told) and not too loud or soft.  We choose a bottle of Roerder (spelling?) which was fantastic. That was followed by a glass of wine each. Small selection of wine by the glass but it covers a wide range of pallets. I know because I didn't know what I was in need of so Trevor had me taste several wines till I settled on a Rosso and was excellent! The bar food was in nice small sizes served fresh and hot and promptly. We had the Caesar salad then a pepperoni flat bread, then the steak bites with the awesome truffle Parmesan fries! Oh what a night we had!  This will be one of me regular spots now when we return to SB. I'm sure Huell Houser would have done an episode on this place if he were still alive for not only what I've mentioned but also the entire staff is pleasing, accommodating, and knowledgeable. The som for instance went out of his way to inform us of fine local wines to try, whether or not they offered them.  Thank you staff of cafe Intermezzo
This place is awesome! Great atmosphere and fantastic food. On Sundays, they have happy hour price all day. It is definitely upscale with plenty of space and great selections of wines and beers.

Everything is amazingly delicious at this place.  Breakfast is just as good as dinner.  They have a rotating menu which is great unless they stop serving the best meatball sandwich I've ever had in my life!!

My husband and I visit Santa Barbara at least every year or two, and we always make a stop at Jeannine's! We usually go to the one in Uptown-State St, but this one was much closer to where we stayed this time around. We didn't even think of looking for a closer location, but was glad to run into it one afternoon!  Much bigger location, with lovely outdoor seating! The breakfast was great as always - I got my usual Challah French Toast, and my husband ordered his usual Breakfast Club Sandwich. Both were very delicious & filling!! Also, very friendly & attentive staff, and very clean restaurant!  We're never disappointed visiting Jeannine's, and are looking forward to our next visit back!

I did a door dash order for pick up and they didn't have it confirmed. After forty mins of waiting and going up to the restaurant they didn't have anything in their system even though I had proof that I paid and was waiting for them to confirm the order after FORTY MINS!

Great neighborhood pizza spot! I've been coming here for so many years, and the quality has been consistent. Their pesto breadsticks when dipped in marinara sauce are also a great addition to their pizza! The people are always very nice when I go in there, and they usually get your pizza ready very quickly. I usually get take out, but I did stay and eat here once in their courtyard eating area, which was very nice! They also serve beer to enjoy with your pizza.

Dee-licious! Servings are ample, food is fresh.  Up to date menu.  Super friendly counter service and table servers.  Prices are great.  When you're looking for a really good, big salad- this is the place.  I wish they had restaurants close to where I live so I could go regularly.  you will enjoy!

For the simplicity and price of these baby's how could you not give this little side car gem a 5 stars with how amazing the food taste and how much you get for your money compared to paying  $16.00 for tow taco as at the sit down joints.     Grab 10 tacos at under $2.00 a pick and have a fiesta.

Great place.  The tart cherry danish could use some more cherries but very good.  Loved the poached eggs with the toast and poached eggs with ham and toast.  All fantastic.   The strawberry preserves very also very good.  The Americano, the Latte, and the fresh squeezed OJ were tasty. Would go back for sure.  The service was above average, the place was clean, and it had a nice family restaurant/bakery feel to it. Very good bakery and restaurant.
Had a really good meal here tonight. Good service, clean restaurant. Plenty of room to eat inside restaurant.
I just had the best fajitas burrito! The salsa was soo good! Ive been coming to El Citio for about 10 years.

Upon arrival, my friend and I found out that the hotel was fully booked and there were no more rooms with 2 beds. We booked through Hotwire so the super helpful and sweet Bella at the front desk informed us that it's based on availability. For the first night, we stayed at the building across the street from the main building that has a parking lot (tight spaces) on the ground level. To be able to park there was really convenient because you could just take the elevator to your floor. We were told that the room had a king sized bed. Although we both would fit, we still requested for the rollaway bed.   Room pros: HUGE restroom. This looked to be a wheelchair accessible room so the bathroom was huge and the shower was one where you can just walk in. It was gorgeous. We didn't get to use it since we both just came from the spa and had already showered. I should have taken a picture. Sorry! The rollaway bed is not the typical twin size but a full!   Room cons: The 'king' bed is actually a queen. The TV remote didn't work - we had the it changed twice. The heater was underneath the table which was really strange placement so I had to crawl under the table to turn it on/off.   The second night we stayed in the main building, first floor (yay! didn't have to walk to our room in the cold! Plus, it's closer to the restaurant and pool). Although this time we had 2 beds, the restroom was tiny. The sink and shower were separated. I missed my gorgeous bathroom from the night before. Directly across from the sink was a bar which I thought was really strange placement. It was just odd to have a bar right next to the restroom but maybe that's just me. On the plus side, we had 2 fridges in the room!  Overall, this hotel was pretty good.
