I'm not a huge fan of the hotel being separated by different buildings because it was a really cold night and to have to walk to another building to get your room was not so fun. Also, the coffee in the lobby was really good (to me - I don't drink coffee much)!

We went there 1 hour before they closed. They had a happy hour Cupcake deal! Buy 3 and get 1 free. Unfortunately we were only 2, and my love for cupcake has its limits... I took the Chocolate-Chocolate. It was a good cupcake. The frosting was very good. The cake was just a little too dry, but not too sweet. They are pretty large.  The only thing is they had already taken back all the outside seating! An hour before 6. We couldn't enjoy the cupcakes outside.
the breakfast croissants with cheese & turkey are yummy ! The coffee & lattes are pretty good but if your staying local Java Station is definitely better. The iced coffee is horrible.

My family and I went to Mirò at the Bacara this past Sunday for the champagne brunch to celebrate my graduation. It was $65/per person with unlimited champagne and food...   It took at least 20 min before we were even given a glass of water. About an hour after we arrived I asked a waiter for champagne - he told me it wasn't included... I had to speak to the manager who said it was included and we were finally given a glass of bubbles. Service was SLOW... I've been to the champagne brunch at The Biltmore multiple times now and you can barely put your glass down before it is filled again. (Also at The 4 Seasons they serve better champagne -- I guess this is why it's $85/pp and not $65/pp).  The food was good at Miró, but the sausages were cold. Again, they were pretty understaffed. We were stacking our dirty plates on our table because no one was coming by to pick them up.   Also - and I'm not being funny - some umbrellas or something would've been nice out on the patio... The tablecloths are SO white we were being blinded by the glare of the sun. I felt really awful for those in our party who didn't have shades on.   On a better note, the view from the Bacara puts the Biltmore to shame... You can see the ocean from the patio - it's stunning!!   Overall, we really didn't get our money's worth in champagne. I also think it's ridiculous that the 3 people in our party who are under 21 were charged full price... They said under 12s get child's price of $35 (I think)... They need another price for over 12s under 21s because it was a pretty big waste of $$ for them.   Definitely won't go back for the brunch! But would definitely go back for a meal, I hear great things about the restaurant overall so was pretty disappointed

When putting my name down I asked if there were any vegan options for my friend. One server had no idea, the other one said yes and gave a few items. When seeing the menu there was not one vegan option that he mentioned. I felt really bad for my friend, she ended up only eating french fries. I think they said the beans are mixed with lard so if you are vegan this might not be the place for you.  I got the birria tacos they were very tasty and juicy but the tortilla was not packed with meat. I also got a side of rice and it was the smallest side ever but I still enjoyed my meal.  My friend ordered the shredded beef tostada but they gave her this gross mush meat with a very distinct flavor. None of us were able to eat that.  It is a fun bar vibe though!

Looking for a place to grab dinner with a friend while visiting him in SB, we wandered around downtown and checked out our options.  Blush looked promising, but when we got in we ended up simply doing happy hour drinks, which were expensive at that, once we saw the prices on the dinner menu.  It was just simply out of our price range - in particular, a $25 veggie pasta dish - for what we thought was going to be an upscale casual spot.  I know, I know, it's Santa Barbara, but still - can't some friends go out to a trendy looking spot and not break the bank?  Blush is beautiful inside, but we just weren't feeling the dinner menu.

Good tacos! Homemade tortillas and generous serving of meat. The salsas were tasty too. Must try the frijoles charros (bean soup)! It was a bit pricy for a taco,  but they have to be able to pay State Street rents (don't let this be a factor to your decision to try). I would recommend stopping by, al pastor and asada are amazing but you must try the frijoles charros (if you like beans).
Tasty food. Tasty tunes. Tasty people.  After a long thunderous ride on the hog across the desert, a cold one and righteous BBQ here is all that's left to fuel your manifest destiny.
Small menu, but everything sounded really great. The bun on the burger wasn't my favorite, but otherwise enjoyed my meal. They have outdoor seating available during covid. No kids menu.

took a little time to get food.  once food got to table, we had forks in hand, no words spoken, ate good food, empty plates - sat back with full bellies.  our table was on the patio, the view of the pacific was beautiful and engaging.  we sat just looking at the beach and ocean as we had casual conversations.  Servers were cool and happy. We'll be back.

In my opinion, Galanga is the best Thai food in town, hands down! LOVE the baby clam hot pot, really filling and delicious. I've also enjoyed the red curry, bamboo and basil stir fry, drunken noodles, pad thai, fried tofu appetizer with peanut sauce. We wish we worked closer because their lunch specials are really a great value. Next I'm going to try the tom kah soup and the spicy fried rice!   The service here is very attentive and kind. They will make sure to leave out things you don't like (for me, it's cilantro), a lot of places mess up 'special' orders like that. I'm really surprised that folks on here have mentioned that the service isn't that great, because it's a hell of a lot better than the other Thai places in town (looking at Your Place....). Also, the atmosphere is a HUGE step up from the competition. Spacious, nicely decorated, but not over the top. The prices are good, and they use great ingredients. Lots of options for vegetarians, vegans, gluten-free too. It's an enjoyable experience and I have always left happy and more than satisfied.
Great food. Everything was tasty, fresh. And homemade. Service terrible...almost like they were trying to be rude.

Was out on a Friday night in downtown Santa Barbara for the first time. Went here just for a quick drink but it was a very cool place. If hadn't been traveling all day probably would have stayed longer.  The crowd did get younger as the night had gotten later but was a very cool place.

wowowow. this place was upsetting. everything looked nice but it was all a trick! the food was gross. salty chow mein, disgusting sesame chicken. my friend ordered a coke and got a glass 3/4 full and 90% of that was ice.  his coke was a light light brown. it was saddening. +1 for nice setting +1 for helpful service +1 for decent dumplings -1 for gross chow mein -1 for gross sesame chicken +1 because i'm nice.

I definitely do not get my fill of casual Mexican food back east, so I was stoked to come here on our last morning and get a load of well-reviewed grub before we headed for the airport. Let me first start off by saying that the portions here are gigantic(!), and that if you err on the side of ordering more food, you will end up like me - nearly keeling over from how full I was. But hey - I was about to get on an airplane for several hours, so I had to take it all down - no leftovers! We got there right when it opened at 11 AM, and even then there were several others who beat us in...a good sign that there was already decent business as soon as the doors opened on a rainy Monday. We purveyed the menus in line, as well as the specials board up top, before going with the carne asada (for her), the wet chicken burrito (for me), and a fish taco and guacamole to split. The beer selection isn't huge, but I took the Hoppy Poppy IPA when I saw it - a very pleasant IPA to sip on while chowing down my meal. There are loads of different salsas at the side (all delicious; I liked the medium-spiced tomato based one the best). Once seated, our food was brought out as it was finished, although the guacamole (thankfully) came first, along with a large helping of tortilla chips.  And that's where we start this adventure - the tortilla chips were relatively light, freshly cooked from the look of the oil sheen on each chip, but it was perfect for the salsa (especially the mild salsa labeled as 'chip salsa'), and it worked very well for the guacamole too. While the guacamole didn't have as much cilantro as I'd like (I'm a bit of a cilantro fiend when it comes to making my own guacamole at home), it is still fresh and is a very generous portion for only $5. We made our way through most of it before our entrees arrived, and we recognized the scope of the task at hand. The plates are huge, and everything comes with beans and rice, along with the main course. My wife's carne asada was a thin but very well-flavored steak - not the most succulent piece of meat, but one that was very flavorful. With a couple tortillas on the side, you could definitely make your own fajita if you wanted. My burrito - well, it was eye-poppingly huge, and I got a bit full just looking at it. The chicken was great, as was the slightly hot sauce it was drenched in, and after a lot of time, I managed to pack the entire thing away. A note: this burrito is not like other burritos, where you may have lettuce or beans or salsa in it. There is a TON of grilled chicken (very good, I might add), and the sour cream, guacamole, and lettuce are on the side to add to each bite. The fish taco (which we ate before our own dishes) was excellent - such bright flavors from the chopped peppers, and the fish itself is very lightly battered, giving you the juiciness of the mahi mahi without being over-breaded.  This probably facilitated our trip to the airport a little, as we were simply too stuffed to contemplate doing much else besides sitting still and letting the food digest. That said, holy cow - what a delicious meal, and I will definitely be back here if I am in town again. Excellent authentic Mexican cuisine at a great price - what more could one ask for?

I love Velvet Jones. One of the few places in town I actually enjoy going out to. First of all, Newcastle on tap. love it! Secondly, one of the only clubs in town that plays actual live bands. What a concept! Not playing 'top 40' hits all night!     This is where you come to punk rock it up in Santa Barbara. They have punk rock karaoke, which, if you've never gone there, is one hell of a good time. A live band (I think mainly Code 415, but I'm not sure)  plays punk songs, and you get to go pretend you're a lead singer! good times! A few times I saw signs that said no moshing, but whenever they have a punk band here...I still mosh...I've slipped on that tiny little floor lots of time....but that's where the fun is!  Generally, I've always, always had a good time here. They used to have an after hours poker game too...but that got shut down fairly quickly.  Also, they have Too Live Crew play here, you gotta love that!   One the places I miss most since leaving Santa Barbara, with out a doubt.

Wow - what a lovely surprise!! I had heard good things about this place, but never actually tried it until last night for my best friends B-day. The 2 of us sat at the bar, and I have to say, they were pretty busy for a gloomy Monday eve (which is a good thing!) Anyways, the negroni cocktail was on pointe and the food was plentiful (even though it is a 'small plates' tapas kind of restaurant) We had the chicken liver pâté, beef carpaccio, pan con tomate, octopus and chicken croquettes. Everything was beautifully presented and very flavorful! Marc, our server/bartender was professional and so nice, as was the rest of the staff. Great job!!!! I will be back with hubby!

Tried Pizza Rev because I liked the concept. The crust was okay but all was spoiled by the sauce. It is dark red, dominated by tomato paste---very , very salty. As someone else I was with said---   You cannot taste any of the toppings because some are salty and all is dominated by salt and the thick paste.I will check if they changed their sauce, and if not, I will not be back. Too many good pizzas in town with a much better sauce.

Perfect for date nights and celebrations. From the ambiance, service and great selection of food, you are sure to have an amazing time. Most of the outdoor tables have fire pits in the middle which are great are perfect for SB nights. Highl recommend!

Why on EARTH did I never try Renaud's when I lived in Santa Barbara?! Who was holding out on me and why? I came here with my boyfriend and two other couples on my most recent visit, and it was one of the best brunch experiences I have had in a loooong time! Not one person left unsatisfied.  The line was a bit of a disaster, but it was prime time (noon-ish) on a Saturday so it didn't surprise me. The restaurant itself was cute but tiny, and we opted to sit outside given the crowds inside and the beautiful weather outside. It took quite a while to get our food after ordering, but it was worth it in the end. My whole group ordered sandwiches and pasta dishes that were to dieeee for, with the hands down favorite being the Croque Madame. Gorgeous presentation and tasty on top of that! As for my order, I just had to try a pastry. Two words: ALMOND CROISSANT. RIGHT out of the oven and into my mouth. And wait, another three words: SALTED CARAMEL MACAROON. These paired with their dark Kona blend coffee completely made my day.  Can't wait to go back and try out breakfast (we had JUST missed it), a sandwich or two, and some more pastries and macaroons!

I ate at El Torito for lunch on New Years Eve for lunch, I was CRAVING their sweet corn cakes. When I arrived there was McDonalds trash shoved between two pots at the entrance and the tile floors looked like they hadn't been cleaned in months. When I entered the restaruant it looked as though they had a huge party the night before and didn't clean up afterward. Total mess. This did not kill my craving... So, we sat down and ordered food, which wasn't too bad, the sweet corn cake didn't disappoint. The service was quick, probably because two tables cleared out while we waited for drinks because they weren't getting service. The service is the only reasons why I give two stars. In the end I found myself searching the web for Sweet Corn Cake recipes so I wouldn't have to return to the filth to get my fix.

Shoreline Beach Cafe, located literally ON Leadbetter Beach is a delightful spot to catch some food-- while you catch some rays.  The service was helpful, knowledgeable, and prompt; particularly helpful on our short lunch break.  I had the Fish and Chips which was decent, though the breading did not cling well to the fish-- on the plus side, it was not as greasy as Fish and Chips tends to be!  I also tried the fish taco which suffered from the same problem, leading to the breading separating and becoming soggy in the sauce before you've had a chance to take a bite.  The atmosphere was placid and allowed for conversation at a normal level-- though we were more focused on the lapping waves to our side.  Generally, the food is decent, but I recommend coming here at least once for a fish taco and corona with your feet in the sand!
Unbelievably good tacos. I enjoyed these tacos better than Michelin star restaurant  Great food and wonderful new twist on already popular tacos.
Love this place. Their cowboy burger is amazing. High quality food, awesome kids menu.

I've been here twice and both times they've tried to up sell me so much that it made me and the other guests of my party feel uncomfortable. The food is pretty underwhelming compared to other Indian food options in the area.
Loved this place! Great location, great service.   The seafood pasta was absolute perfection!   And the space and environment is fantastic.   Highly recommend.

Today was my first time here. Friends have been telling about it and it's getting good reviews from my friends so I decided to check it out. The location is OK I wish it were in a better spot but they did the best they could with the space. It feels very clean and open. Since it was my first time I ordered a lot of items to try everything.  They were serving carrot ginger soup dairy free and vegan. This soup is a very hearty soup super thick and a huge bowl that could've been a meal in itself. . I prefer thin soup versus thick but I have to say it was very tasty and would probably order it again for just a meal. I ordered the hummus which was amazing the bread it came with was perfectly toasted and seasoned and the hummus was beautiful it had all kinds of seasonings and just was delicious probably in my top favorites of all the hummus in Santa Barbara.  I ordered the little gem salad and again super delicious I wish I would've added avocado but I loved the salad very fresh. Lastly I ordered the brisket sandwich and this was also very amazing I skipped on the garlic aïoli but had everything else on it and it was super super good probably my favorite thing. They had a great selection of menu items. I can't wait to go back!!!  Hoping Downtown gets cleaned up a bit more and Daisy stays around for a long time. I will be supporting this one!!

Super fun atmosphere and good food. We sat up on the bench today and it was really comfy. We've come another night and sat around the fire pit and made friends with people from all over the world. You might have a hard time getting a drink sitting over there though. The frozen Margs are amazing! We had lunch today and were disappointed there was no kids menu. We ended up ordering an a la carte taco for the youngling, the supreme burrito with chicken (massive but nothing special) and mahi mahi tacos (yummy but literally came with 3 small slivers of fish...don't come hungry for that dish. Kind of a rip off if you ask me. Bloody Mary's were pretty good. Would be back for fun atmosphere and super delicious chips and salsa!
I really love Adama! This is literally the best vegan food I have ever had!  I love everything here and I really, REALLY love the hostess chocolate cake. YUM!  Loving Adama!

Okay so I was back here this Sunday for my sisters birthday. The service was fantastic!! They specially made a number of items for our table and brought out so many extras that were requested. However the food was overall pretty mediocre. Nothing extraordinary. And the Brunch selection was dismal. They probably had less than half of the selection as they had my last visit. I understand that times are tough but really the buffet looked pathetic. It was so disappointing.

Unfortunate.... I've been here once before and loved THE FOOD, but the time matters to me as a person who works on state, and I know what customer service means so the worker bees is great the young man is always cool . But I have a Business as well let's move it.

Santa Barbara is known for its great Mexican food but somehow I never heard of this place until I read a small article about it. After reading the article I was on my way to give it a try and see what the fuss was about. Wow! How the hell have I not heard of this place before? The many great Mexican restaurants in the area and this one jumps to the top of my list. In one word to describe this place-Authentic.   For breakfast I ordered the chilaquiles and the wife ordered the molcajetes. We also ordered some corn cake dish to give it a try. These are by far the best chiliquiles I ever ate. Everything tasted fresh and home made. The portion was large and I was completely satisfied with this dish. My wife's dish came in a steaming stone pot(molcajetes) and was bubbling with an amazing aroma waiting to be tried once it cooled down.   As far as quality this place is one of my favorite restaurants after just one visit. The food is authentic as a fine restaurant in Mexico. The portions and the price were very reasonable with the service and the quality of the food. This is a must try for any local or Mexican food lover! I will be back!

Ugh, I don't understand what is supposed to be so good about this place!  Ordered the roasted turkey and artichoke panini. Summary: There was barely any of the spread on the sandwich that was supposed to have artichoke, the turkey was hella skimped, the baby spinach and roasted red peppers (which had no flavor) were very sparse, and there was a crap-ton of these gross caramelized onions on it!  The sponge-y no-flavor bread was wack too!  Since Panera is supposed to be better for soup & salad, and I am admittedly not much of a soup & salad guy, I think I just need to never, ever, ever go to Panera Bread again.  I'd much rather get a sandwich from South Coast Deli.... hell, even Quiznos!

This place is great. Every bit as good as Montecito's Cava without the price.. I had mango guac and tacos de camaron. It was beautifully presented and the food was fresh and delicious. La Arcada is a quaint center and the turtle fountain is a real focal point. The service was great which for some reason is rare in S.B. i even give the restroom a B+. I was there for lunch and just about everything is $11 or $12. It was four star dining without the price. I will definitely be back.
I love this place! Chicken burrito is where it's at. You get a ton of food for the price. Get your meal to go and eat in the park a few blocks away overlooking the ocean. I love Cali...

Meh.   Not bad. The place is clean and well maintained. The staff is polite and efficient. The food is fresh and the service is quick but the food is just really bland. It is just uninteresting to eat but if you are just hungry and not in the mood for an experience then this place will suffice.   It is just a shame this place is so highly rated because there is much better Mexican food in the area and I feel like this place kind of gets in the way and almost gives it a bad name. It was too much a fast food place. On the one had, consistency is good but not when quality suffers.   So not bad, but I have better things to do
Ordered a few Cappuccinos with Carmel, a pink drink for everyone and a pupaccino for my puppy. Each was made very well and my dog loved his little pupaccino.

Montecito Location Needs to improve their customer service, I didn't have my mask on right at the door, And the girl was rude. No hello , good afternoon, just, sir u need a mask on . I turned around and walked out. I get it but you don't have to come off abrupt like that.

The ambience of this place, especially the patio is really charming. They had a variety of sauces to use - favorite being the jalapeno style one. Had the perfect sweet flavor with a kick at the end.  Came here with a few coworkers so we tried a few different options. We tried the chefs plate so we could sample a few items. The pulled pork was good, as was the ribs, but the brisket was so dry it was like eating chewy meat. It was terrible. Even the sauce couldn't help how poorly cooked that meat was. Coming from Texas, I know what good brisket was and sadly, this was a major let down. Everyone at the table couldn't even eat it so it sadly was left untouched after a few bites. The mac and cheese was a great comeback though. It was super gooey and really had great flavor including the bread crumbs on top. We also had the Locals only sampler which everything there was really good if you want to jump off traditional bbq. And of course, the ribs. They actually were quite a great surprise and cooked almost perfect. Meat was delicious and had great flavor across the board.  I may have been a little more picky than most people from the south. However most items were great with just a few really missing the mark.

A small elegant restaurant with dim lighting and thoughtful service. Reservation via open table was harder to get few days before the weekend but we lucked out and saw a last minute cancellation while checking for reservation an hour before dinner. Despite last minute reservation, my comment to request for a high chair was noted and our table was set up by the time we got there.   Service was friendly, prompt and thoughtful.   My favorite of the night was the duck breast which was super tender and moist. We ordered one entree of the duck and wanted to share so they were thoughtful to split into two plates. Even after splitting the entree, the potion size appears to be big.   I also enjoyed the scallop trio and creme brûlée. I was a little disappointed at the French onion soup and thought there could've been more cheese and better flavor.   Overall we had a relaxing time at Bouchon!

The food overall was good and satisfying.  Their menu has moderate creativity and fun choices that are different.  This makes this place fun to go to.  (3.5 stars)  The service was unfortunately poor.  The server was kind when we interacted, however the service itself was poor.  There were couple of times I needed some service and I couldn't flag down my server.  I also noticed that the servers were talking and laughing, so I asked the bus-boy for help.  (1 Star - based on the pricing scale)  Sounds like a common experience for those that didn't give high reviews.  Sometimes, the food is good enough to compensate the lack in service.  However, in most cases, the service can diminish the value of the food (almost completely).  The opposite works as well, where the service can compensate a lesser food experience.

Zaytoon is great. It's a frequent for my friends, family and I. We celebrate special occasions here. The ambiance is fantastic and the service is always good. It's beautifully lit, especially at night. This is a must if you're looking for middle eastarn food and happen to be in Santa Barbara.

Good thing the bartender and waitress at the bar were very sweet! The manager and waitress were rude!!!! I asked for a table and they got my table and then let someone else go before me! Then could care less about us, and were rude. So we sat at the bar. But the waitress handling the bar was very sweet.
Another Santa Barbara tradition. Great steaks and fresh seafood. Their Hawaiin Chicken is a fave, and love their ribeyes with a baked potato. Expensive, but not overpriced.

Came in during the SBIFF.  This establishment is close to the movie theater and it was raining, so we stopped in for breakfast.  It took 15-20 minutes for us to be seated.  Our waitress was really nice.  They don't have a huge menu like I had anticipated, but they do have some options for vegetarians (slim pickings for vegans).  I ended up trying French toast (I haven't had French toast in like 15 years) and it was good.  The person I am with did not like their omelette and said that there was goat cheese inside of it instead of feta.

My name is also Kat but if you need anything talk to CAT! She is the absolute best server. Helped me out twice tonight and is absolutely lovely human being and a great server. Ask for Cat, and your night cannot go wrong. Thank you again!

Best Mexican food in town!! Totally lives up to its reputation!!! I didn't go for a long time because there was always a line around the block. Finally I went during the week and realized why there is a line around the block every weekend. It is worth the wait!! The pastor torta was the best I've ever had. And the prices are legit!!!

Blah.... meh, that's what I think of Casa Blanca. The exterior and interior might catch your attention as you drive by State Street but that's it. The menu was very limited, I found it real  difficult to decide what to order, I had to rely on yelp review Images to have an I idea what to eat. Ended up ordering Mahi Ahi tacos with a side of rice and black beans. And let me tell you, it seriously lacked flavor & was very bland. If someone wants authentic mexican food, stay away from state street and instead head for  Milpas for hole in the walls but the best, rich in flavor food

Great vibe at Finney's. It was the ideal place to hang out on our Friday night.  Menu offers some of the standard gastropub items such as chicken wings, beer and burgers but what stood out for us were the lobster sliders.  What can I say except I could have ordered 3 plates!

This is a special spot. Different from any other tacos I've had in this area. Definitely worth a try (and returning over and over). Love how filling one taco can be. Their veggie options are amazing also. Don't miss out on this spot!

i would have to say i was very un-impressed with my meal first of all i got a burger and a side salad... i asked for ranch they gave me like a 1/2 teaspoon of ranch i asked for more and the guy gave me a dirty look like are you serious....  then the burger was nothing to brag about at all. and to top it off they charged over $11 for that meal and was completely not worth it what so ever... i would not recommend this place
Wow! I went here and hands down had the greatest hotdogs I have ever had in my life!! What an amazing little spot!! Great service too!! I highly recommend.

Ordered some tortilla soup which also had chicken in it a few weeks ago I enjoyed it so much I just ordered some more today when i I was eating it thank God I noticed it had a bunch of small bones in it I'm so glad I noticed the bones before I actually ate one a small one which it could've damage my teeth cut my mouth my gums even damage my intestines that is not OK I will not eat here again and I recommend to anyone else do not get the soup if you're going to eat here.

We love FisHouse & been here several times in the past.  We had a wonderful dinner outdoor patio dining.  Chelsea was absolutely wonderful, she was a doll.  She was so sweet, informative and helpful with the menu and answered my questions.  We absolutely LOVED our meals my husband had had the scallop & shrimp ceviche & I had the petrole Sole.  Omg we finished everything on our plate and our drinks.  It was a perfect dinner for our perfect day trip taking Amtrak from GLENDALE.  We love Santa Barbara.  Dinner is always perfect when service is positive and pleasant.  Thank you Chelsea for your kindness.
Delicious food!! And AWSOME SERVICE!!! Chill birthday brunch, deinks and food were delicious. Friendly service and great variety of good and drinks. Cute outside seating on a beautiful sunny day.

We waited over 30 mins when they said 10-15 mins. Understand that it's a weekend mid afternoon but don't tell your customers one thing and do another. Also, our waitress was nice but the food was disappointing. I would not even feed it to my dog. The fish tacos drenched in sauce with a small piece of fish. It tasted spoiled. We also ordered calamari. You would think it's hot and fresh but no it's cold almost frozen. I don't know how a restaurant could serve its customers something like this. And to top it off. The sauce looked like it's been sitting out for days. Possibly recycling their sauces? But the picture is worth 1000 words. You can see the 2 tone color and texture. I was so disgusted and sick to my stomach. I paid and left. No manager no apology as if they didn't do anything wrong. People beware! I would never go back even if they paid me.

Came here with the Axxess card - I ordered the French Toast with strawberries & bluberries & my g.f. ordered the 2-egg breakfast with home fries. Serivce was good friendly & fast but food was just fair & a little overpriced. I expected much better given the 4.5 star rating

Came here for an early dinner and overall service was good! Our dishes came out quickly, I had the Panang Curry with Tofu and boyfriend had the Spicy Drunken Noodles. Curry had a yummy flavor and wasn't too spicy but just spicy enough. The rice wasn't very fresh. Overall the food was yummy though!

This is an update to my existing, very positive review. I've been back several times since. The experience is always excellent. What I love is the genuine care and quality of the food and the service. For instance, the chicken enchilada is much more than the Taco Bell variety. The enchilada sauce is more like a mole sauce than the usual fire engine red stuff. Their Fish tacos are delightfully prepared and stuffed into two corn tortillas. They ere a little difficult to handle because they are overflowing. I had their chorizo breakfast burrito this morning and some fresh squeezed orange juice. It doesn't get better!

I've been here four or five times and the food and service is consistently excellent. I ignored this place for a long time thinking it was just another hotel coffee shop, but I have not realized that it is the perfect place for me to come for the quiet, moderately priced and extremely tasty lunch. I love the physical space because it is comfortable, has Wi-Fi, the music is not too loud, easy parking, and I feel completely comfortable staying for a couple of hours when I need to do some work. The staff is friendly and the vibe is chill. But what really brings me back is the food. Imaginative, tasty, diverse, and beautifully presented. And... moderately priced! I also love the aviary full of finches near the front door. This is a great place to become a regular at.
I came here throughout my college career! lol! I LOVE INDIAN FOOD! Their naan is always toasted right, curries (meat and veggie) are yummy, tandoori chicken is amazing, and desserts are delectable!

A chance to transport myself to Turkey was  exactly what I needed. This coffee shop has been thoughtfully designed to invite all your senses to enjoy Turkish delights, baklava, coffee and/or tea. Sit, sip, and nibble your way through colorful, tasty, and rich lokum or baklava pieces. The highlight of our evening was getting to know the family who owns and runs the shop, they welcomed us and made us feel like family. Will definitely visit often.

Really, no one has reviewed this place yet?  Avoiding State St recently, I popped in here for the first time last week when it so happened to be the 1st Thursday the art-walk. The place was packed. Unfortunately the crowd tonite was the yuppie, stroller, baby types and my companions weren't so pleased. Therefore we had to retreat.   However, I know many of the fabulous people involved in making this place happen and I can't wait to come back and eat some *real* frozen yogurt. Hopefully someday Pinkberry will die a slow, painful, death.

Seriously the best burgers and flatbreads in town, the wait is long, but trust me. SO WORTH IT! Really great prices for their qualities, I got their strawberry mojito and it was amazing; you can really taste the mint, but that burger. I'll be having dreams of that fried egg Jalapeño burger.

Amazing food! Amazing atmosphere! Menu is small but they have perfected each of them wow   I would skip the avocado. It was pricy for literally a grilled avocado.  Highly highly recommend the especial. It was cooked to perfection with so many flavors coming together   For the mains, the meats were all cooked amazingly and it was nice to have the pasta to mix up the palate!!   Must come here! Call and get a reservation early

I've been familiar with Crushcakes for years now, and have stopped in many a time for a cupcake or pastry when I'm in Santa Barbara. When we began planning our wedding and settled on a Montecito reception venue, Crushcakes was at the top of our list for cake options. After doing a tasting with another bakery, and then Crushcakes, it was an obvious choice for us! Emily guided us through the whole tasting process on their outdoor patio, and we settled on a lemon cake with cream cheese frosting. Since we were doing a full dessert bar, we decided to go with a smaller wedding cake, so we did the two tier, which ended up looking wonderful among the other desserts. Not only was the process enjoyable, but the pricing was more than fair, and best of all, the cake tasted AMAZING. So fresh, great lemon flavor, but not overpowering. Our guests definitely enjoyed it, and we enjoyed the top tier with friends and family when we returned from the honeymoon, and it still tasted great! I would highly recommend Crushcakes for all of your wedding cake needs, they made our vision come to life and we are so grateful!
Mole Mole Mole Mole Mole Mole Mole Mole Mole Mole  Mole Mole Mole Mole Mole Mole Mole Mole Mole Mole Mole Mole Mole  Mole Mole Mole Mole Mole Mole Mole Mole Mole Mole Mole Mole Mole  Mole Mole Mole

Some amazing gluten free options. I've never thought to use the cheese tapioca bread as a bun for burgers. Holy cow that was amazing. The burger really was the winner for our lunch. The place is cute and staff were nice. I'm excited to try using the tapioca bread for sliders at home!

I reviewed Mesa Verde years ago, but I don't see my review, so I'm posting again.    The food is still excellent, and many of my favorites are still on the menu.    One standout entree is the polenta fries.  My favorite dessert is the creme brulee.   I take non-vegetarian friends here and they love it.   They make everything from scratch,  all the way.  The Arnold Palmer, they squeeze the lemons to make lemonade. The candied nuts on the creme brulee, they candied the nuts. If something is smoked on the menu, they smoked it.   That attention to detail and quality-  it's part of what makes each plate delicious.    Service is friendly and cheerful.   One of best vegetarian restaurants I've been to, in any city.

Great place for happy hour that you can make a reservation for! We went here and I had my doubts because Santa Barbara isn't exactly well known for Asian food. But my oh my everything was sooo good. We ordered 2 cocktails, the gado gado, egg noodles, and chicken wings during happy hour.  The gado gado was my favorite and I'm not a big fan of salad either. The avocado and the fried onions were perfect in this. The chicken wings were perfectly saucy and amazing. The egg noodles were quite delicious too and the perfect filler. Currently inspired to try to recreate the market gado gado for my personal lunch. Care to share the recipe Sama Sama Kitchen?   We were seated in the back outside where you go past the kitchen where there are heated lamps to keep people warm. They also mentioned they have blankets that people can borrow in case they get cold.   The hostess was especially kind and a student. Good luck with all those units!    Overall, would come back and excited to come back when in Santa Barbara again.
Consistently great Mexican food.  The family packs during the pandemic have been wonderful & great value.  Terrific enchiladas & salsas.
Excellent. Everything from their delicious breakfast to the baristas creations. Frequently get the platter breakfast. One of my favorite breakfast spots to eat.
Great breakfast food. I usually get their New Orleans omelette with avocado but have tried other stuff and I've never been disappointed.

Incredibly charming atmosphere if you visit during the weekday. A loud biker scene on the weekends. Rich with local history, as it was once a stagecoach stop. However, the food is terrible. If you can't cook eggs... Best bet is to visit the bar.

The Ensenada is my favorite.  It's refreshing.  Be sure to get the spicy pineapple for sauce.  The buzzers do not always work when it's busy.  The food is normally fast so you may want to check with the staff if your buzzer doesn't ring.
came here to support small business during these testing times and had the best donut in town!  blt & kfc sandwich were also delicious!

3 star for service 5 star for food  We made a reservation days ahead for a party of 5 for my mom's birthday this Saturday. The only place to sit comfortably is in the front of the restaurant due to airflow (no ac here)... they sat us completely in the back corner (last table) of the restaurant where there were zero other guests and we were immediately sweating. They informed us that we could see if there are any bar tables open to sit at. We did and thankfully found a partial table open for us to join. Here is my problem - if we made reservations ahead of time, should they not reserve us a good table?? We reserved for 4:45pm so I don't see that being a problem.  Our bar waitress was nice and the food came out quickly but we got our food and no plates or silverware... we had to wait for her to come check on us to ask for these items. It wasn't busy so I just don't see why these things were overlooked. I am mostly disappointed that they don't prioritize their reservations, esp since we noted it was for a birthday. When we left, still nobody had been seated all the way in the back like we had... aka they know it is uncomfortable...  The food was delish - our faves were the beef satay, popcorn shrimp, lobster bisque, fish tacos and the poke! Happy hour is the way to go!

I had the most incredible dessert pizza from 4 Eggs and Pizza this weekend. If that's any inductor of how the rest of the food is, I am thrilled to try their whole menu!!! The dessert pizza we had tasted like a cinnamon roll - so innovative. The owner was there and he was so kind and generous. I would 100% recommend this place! The five stars are no lie!

Had an excellent experience at Playa Azul this weekend. Our party of 12 was helped without a reservation, had excellent food, service was fantastic, and an overall great experience. Look forward to going again next time we're in SB.
My husband and I LOVE the Creekside!!! The food is always good and service is the best.  Did I mention the drinks, spot on as well.

Great Rock Crab, loved the clam chowder, excellent service. And who can say no to onion rings?  Didn't really like the fried seafood - batter didn't stay crisp very long. Nothing was over cooked, just didn't like the batter.

I wish a different proprietor would take over this place! Some of the servers are okay, but the manager that was there tonight was not keen. I called at 4:30 to make a reservation, but was informed they don't take them. I said okay, when can I come to put our name on the list if we want to have a group of 10-15 people outside at 5:30. He said a half hour before.  So I arrived at 5 and said we are just hanging out on the beach and want to have two tables. He said they might close the kitchen at 5:30 or 6 if they don't have enough people there.  Okay, so first of all, I had called previously and made plans with friends to bring their kids to the beach to play and have an early dinner... he didn't inform me of this when I called. I didn't feel comfortable having friends meet their with kids in tow if he was going to close the kitchen.  Meanwhile a couple came to ask if they could sit at the stools out back by the sand, but he said that was only for people who wanted to have a drink and wait for a table.... okay, you just got done saying you weren't busy. How unaccommodating can this guy be. Overall people aren't friendly there (except one waitress who was super nice when I left saying she didn't mind staying to help guests even if it wasn't busy).  I rarely come here because it's a disappointment each time. We don't have many beachfront options in our Santa Barbara beach town, so I keep trying to give it a chance. The food is average to poor and the general vibe is not that friendly. Such a bummer because it would be so great to have a semi casual, beachside eatery with tasty food that you can meet friends and family at, come to after playing in the water and sand or take a walk on the beach after a good meal...  I keep hoping it will meet expectations some day.

So the positives, Scarlett Begonia provided a comfortable outdoor dining atmosphere for our family's Easter Sunday brunch with all the coffee, tea, and champagne you could take down.  That said, the food left much to be desired considering the very hefty price of $65/person.  To begin, I was rather surprised to see only two pastry baskets (maple bacon and buttermilk biscuits) and two cinnamon rolls for a party of 6 adults and a toddler.  Sadly, they were all cold and overly dense.  From the second courses, the avocado toast was uninspired as the avo was just thinly sliced and seasoned.  Same for the organic deviled eggs.  Thankfully, my kale caesar was plentiful and well-dressed.  The third courses underwhelmed across the board.  My Scarlett cheeseburger was overcooked and painfully bland especially considering the inclusion of bacon marmalade and garlic aioli.  I barely got two bites down before I just stopped out boredom and disappointment.  Similarly, the chilaquiles' bold red mole sauce looked far more flavorful than it tasted.  The only decent dish was the smoked salmon, but it was literally just smoked salmon served with dilled cream cheese and bagel chips...none of which involve any cooking skill.  Ultimately, the best part was the coffee and champagne.  Quality time with the family for sure, just unfortunate that the food itself was so horribly overpriced and lacking in execution.
Me and my brother come here for first time the guy at reception so rude to us he shut down the door and said you have to call ?  So call for what ?  So bad never get back here again !
I frequent this place at least twice a week....I just gotta say....you haven't lived until you tried the spicy yellowtail sushirito.
Kale falafel burger was absolutely incredible. I've never really actively sought out vegan food, but this has made me start rethinking that. Super great flavor, and so nice to look at!

After hearing locals rave about Woody's I gave it a try back in '03 and really disliked my meal.  The bbq sauce was not very good and the fries were mushy and tasteless.  I gave it another try a few years later with the same result.  Although not bbq, if you find yourself in the Magnolia shopping center and need to eat immediately you should hit Del Pueblo.

Bluewater Grill, the eighth outpost in a small chain of seafood restaurants, opened near Stearns Wharf in Santa Barbara in March 2018. It's surprising, this being a beach town, that there are relatively few seafood specialists in the city. There's Enterprise, and also the nearby Fishhouse and Boathouse, which has common ownership with Fishhouse. I must say that Bluewater will now be my fave seafood destination. Their menu is expansive, the service is first-rate, and both the presentation and quality of the food are top notch. They have a full bar and what appears to this non oenophile to be a classy wine list. While the prices aren't inexpensive, they're on a par with other similar restaurants. I certainly plan on visiting often.
love this place! Roy never disappoints. I'm having the organic salmon, hubby has the meatballs with hand made pasta. Delish!

I love going to this place! Fantastic selection of local wines, and the staff is very knowledgeable and helpful if you have questions. Their selection of organic produce is amazing, I always leave with bags of stuff when I shop here.

Great nachos. Nice salsa bar. Mole sauce was flavorful but too spicy for mole. Fajitas were decent.   Service is mediocre; lead by an employee named E* who bless her heart, takes her job way too seriously at the expect of customer service.

The micheladas are bomb! The Vuelve a la Vida cocktail is bomb! The ceviche is good and the salsas are good too! Service is good but and food comes out quick when it's mariscos !   Really enjoy going to this place after a night of drinking
Employees are 5 star, you'll never meet more warm and understanding staff. The owner and his policies however lost him a paying customer.

The pizza tracker stated Johnathan left at 8:07 its now 8:38 and he just arrived, the company needs to do something so the drivers don't get dinged for unhappy customers ~ Dominos is doing a dis-service to the employee. Now for a note to Dominos corporate.  #pizza #sad

Awesome spot - especially on a Sunday afternoon.  Celebrating our anniversary and have watched this place grow from a small pick up window chill and eat to a destination spot.  Live band, fantastic good (cuz who would come for mediocre) great wine and beer. We are loving our Sunday afternoon in SB thanks to Shalhoob's.  We shared tri tip nachos and the cowboy salad.  LOVE this spot!  Now I just need to return during the week for happy hour

So, you cannot review this place without mentioning the brussel sprouts with dates. We thought about another order of those all day. The sweetness of the dates and unami of the soy sauce/white wine vinegar was to die for. Those are worth five stars and everyone knows how good they are. They continued to impress with a perfectly cooked bronzino and the special BBQ brisket satisfied my meat craving. With the BBQ, the cornbread with some sort of light (pimento cheese?) was a knockout. Great wine selection.

Great bbq spot in the Funk Zone! Loved the outdoor seating- perfect for those warmer days. They have great beer and food- the bacon jam sliders were so absolutely delicious. They also sell meat, jerky, seasoning, novelty items.. etc. All around great place to grab good food and good beer! Our visit was last year before the pandemic and we can't wait to go back soon!

Amazing. So good. Seriously what Santa Barbara needed. Everything, EVERYthing is made in house. When you look around you see the jars of what they are pickling or canning. The beautiful meat slicer is stationed right next to the bar so you can literally sit there watching them slice the meat they have cured themselves. Everything is clean and classy. Service was attentive. Chefs were all very friendly when we asked them questions. They thoroughly explained the wood fired pizza oven and the different heating pockets in it which was really interesting. People say it's a pricey, but you get what you pay for. People don't go to bouchon or julienne a couple times a week for happy hour. They are more so special occasion places. This is one of those. Great food. Great service. This is the same kind of feel. Truly what Santa Barbara should have and needed. Can't wait to come back when I am in town again!

We only visited this tourist restaurant because we had some coupons from our children's school.  I had expected mediocre microwaved chain restaurant food, but what I did not expect was the completely unsanitary condition of the restaurant.  In the back corner half-booth where we were sitting, the fabric of the booth looked like someone tried to clean a murder scene.  Next to our table was a loud humming portable AC unit, which made sense because the vent above us was black with mold.  I can only imagine the condition of the kitchen if this was what they thought was acceptable for their customers to see.  Service was adequate, food was standard 'salt substituting for flavor' chain food.

Their $5 beer + pizza made me wander in here on an idle Sunday afternoon this past Summer.  From that day on, I was a fan.  There is one instant where I had to wait 50 minutes at the restaurant to pick up an order, but It's okay though Mesa Pizza.  I forgive you.  Your pizzas make up for the wait.  The pizza served here is glorious!  I am salivating just thinking about their cheese pizza and I never eat plain cheese pizza!    If I had to order my enjoyment of pizza (taste only not price):  1.  Mesa Pizza 2.  Costco Pizza = Pizza Hut  3.  Rustys  4.  Pizza Guru (hit or miss)  5.  Round Table  6.  Dominos  After finishing writing this review, I'm going to order some pizza right now from them.
Not too shabby. Burritos are pretty good. Affordable prices. Friendly service. Tamales could use some TLC

Fan favorite.... the only issue is parking... half the time there is none. What does that tell you? Good is awesome.  My go to is the fish and chips, definitely upgrade to the tater tots.  I recently discovered the egg roles. They are the bomb especially with the sauce it comes with.  It s a must go if you are in town.
This place slaps! I've lived in SB for 5 years and I hit Blue owls all the time. 10/10 would recommend. Fav is the Rice with pork and spicy chili sauce!

They're always so fast and courteous! Excellent customer service and their food is delicious!  Everything I've tried there is delicious. All of my coworkers agree. I can tell they use high quality ingredients. Must try their cob salad, those pickled red onions and giant croutons take it to the next level!

hands down the best breakfast place to sit and relax for a while in sb. the inside has a greasy spoon feeling and outside is perfect for sunny days. only breakfast place i can think of with a full bar. its me and my fiancees new Sunday breakfast place every single Sunday from now on! been here my whole life. why am i just discovering this place now?!

I always like this place. The food is fresh, the drinks are good, staff is friendly. Low lighting, private booth seating & cute servers make for a sexy ambiance. Perfect date spot (request upstairs or make a reservation) or just a cool stop for catching up with friends before a night out. They have DJs & live music sometimes too.

SB Muchiez has been around a loooong time. I've been Ordering from them for years.. Thanks for keeping me fed at 2 am bros. Their fries are legit.  Menu was a bit bloated last time I checked it out, don't try getting a panini or omelette from them.. Stick to burgers and fries.

It was a wonderful and scenic dinner at the Boathouse after an afternoon at the beach. We knew we would have to wait, so we planned to be at the beach first, after putting our names on the list for an outside beach-view table. We 'waited' about 2 hours for our table. Then we ordered crab cakes, a bowl of clam chowder, the seafood pasta, cioppino, and the Scottish salmon. Every dish was delicious, more than we expected because it is a touristy kind of place. The flavors were all fresh and the dishes hot, as they were served promptly from the kitchen. Some of the Yelp reviews mentioned subpar quality seafood so it was worrisome to see what the food would really be like. Grateful that it all came out delicious. Service was efficient and lukewarm from our waitress who was much friendlier at the end of the meal when she gave us our bill. We can see why it's a popular place and we'd return again to be able to have dinner right next to the ocean and the view the incredible sunset colors.

Been here about 4 times this  past weeks trying out all their  different types of tacos .   Amazingly great value for their triple play tacos. 3 tacos for $ 9.00 .   Take out only !  Great TO GO food to be eaten on the beach which is a short walking distance.
Always happy with their to go dinners. Really picky about pre made food in grocery stores, but always enjoy what I get. Wish they had crab cakes more often!

My husband and I, with a small group of friends, returned to Monarch last night. We had a 10 course dinner and I can honestly say we were overwhelmed with every new dish that was served to us, family style.  Each course was beautifully prepared with delicious wines to complement, waitress and servers were very professional and friendly, and there was more than enough food for all of us. It was definitely the 'Wow' factor, it could not have been any better. We had several dessert courses in the main restaurant, and then followed up with drinks and homemade bon bon's in their intimate bar next door, Chaplins.  Thank you Monarch for hosting a wonderful evening at your restaurant.  Of course we will be back.

Wonderful market if you're looking for middle eastern foods. They have plenty of fruits and vegetables and a meat case as well. It's fun to look around and find new and interesting foods to try. The owners are always friendly. They also have some nice liquor a and a great assortment of chocolates!

Stopped by for a quick mid afternoon bite on a film watching day during the Happy Hour. It was a beautiful mild winter day and I sat outside on the patio in the warm sun. Friendly service.  Really enjoyed the vodka and grapefruit juice (Spring 44 Vodka, which I'd never heard of) with freshly squeezed grapefruit juice.  The app, chicken nachos. However wasn't something I'd recommend.  The tastes of the toppings were good but the chips were very greasy. Order something else.

I will preface this review with: I have never eaten dinner here; I have only had happy hour. However, the food and drinks were great! I was pleasantly surprised. I was with a few girlfriends and we split a few appetizers. The Honey Grilled Cheese was to die for! Extremely delicious (and I don't even like blue cheese). The Calamari and Bloody Mary Oysters were really impressive, albeit small. As for the drinks, there are well cocktails, margaritas, house wine, house beer specials, but also there were featured cocktails and featured wine for a slight price increase. Between the five of us we essentially tried everything. I was on wine for the night, and even the $3 house red was great! The price was right and the drinks were fabulous.  My main complaint was that the bar was crowded, and we didn't get as much attention as we would have liked from the wait staff. We had to beg for water.  Overall, it is a place I can't wait to visit again.  Oh and the Happy Hour times are great! 9PM to Close on Saturday night and all day Sunday!

As everyone says, get a cup of the Lobster Bisque. It is super rich so it was ok to split with my wife. The crispy brussel sprouts were amazing. I got blackened swordfish which was super juicy & delicious. We ate too much & didn't have room for dessert :-(

We had a really wonderful meal sitting outside on the patio looking at the ocean, everything we got was tasty and the service was great. Ended up getting a second olive oil cake, it was so light and not super sweet, highly recommend.

I'm a huge fan of wings. Wingman rodeo, does an ok job with them but not fantastic. I ordered multiple flavors. From their 'atomic' down. Atomic is a 6/10 for spicy. Price point is a bit high 'per wing'. Wings came out a with skin a bit undercooked on half of the order. I'd probably skip this place in the future based on price point, and delivery. Id expect perfection for a place with wing in the name.

I love Modern Times! I've had friends come to visit the last several weekends, and I've taken all of them here. It's such a great atmosphere, and the food and drinks are delish. My friend and I split the Chile verde fries and were blown away! We still had half the plate left, and we were so full. I don't eat meat, so the vegan menu is awesome. My friend does though, and she said she couldn't even tell the difference from their fries and regular Chile verde. A different friend brought her dog with her, and modern times was a perfect place to bring her pup! There were lots of other dogs there, it's super pet friendly! I'll continue to return here!

Kind of disappointed. We are locals and have been here before. It's overpriced (even for Santa Barbara) and service is slow, food is mediocre. Took us forever to even get an order in and a lot of the egg dishes were undercooked with runny white and such small portions. No music or ambiance this time around. I'm a little embarrassed I brought my out of town friends here. I expect way more at these prices and I'm no stranger to paying over 20 dollars for avocado toast in this town. Jeanines down the street is better

The pizza is really really good. They offer fresh, organic ingredients which I really love.   The reason I am giving this place a 3 star rating is because of the customer service. Every time I walk in there it seems like all the staff are either high or depressed. They are unfriendly, no one generally greets you when you walk up to the counter. They treat you like you're not even there. None of the staff cracked a smile any time I was there. I can only imagine how the management is, mean? harsh? Maybe that's why all the employees seem to be hating their job.   They would probably thrive better as a business and a team if their attitudes improved a bit.

Opal is a great restaurant for a nice meal with friends. Nestled in the bustling State Street, it's one of the standouts.   What I've eaten here:  Pan seared Crab Cakes: good but portion is very small Homemade Basil Fetuccine w/ Sauteed Tiger Prawns: It's good but can be a little too rich. This would be good to share rather than for just one person.  Chile Crusted Filet Mignon: This is my favorite! The sauce is too die for. Perfectly cooked. HMMM. HMMM.  Herb Grilled Filet Mignon: It's delicious but I would pick the Chile Crusted over this one.   Great food, nice ambiance, good service. Nothing to complain about this place. It's a must try in SB.

I went back tonight and the 'waiter / cashier' was playing guitar in between orders. The salsa was horrible, it's not consistent anymore. Used to love it...tables were dirty. Yet I continue to come back here so maybe I am the problem! This place is mediocrity defined.  If you eat at los Agaves there's no way you can take this shit-hole seriously. I wish the owner gave one shit about this place. It's like I stumbled into my young nephews first apt and I asked him to make me dinner.

I recently stumbled on this place and I'm glad I did, sadly I feel not too many people know about it, mainly because of the other two very famous and hyped up  Mexican restaurants down the street. I usually don't write a good review until I've actually tried the food more than 1 time. At this point I've eaten there 5 times and feel confident about my recommendation. The Fajitas were delicious, the carne asada plate was good,the breakfast burrito was amazing!! the carne asada tacos are yummy, the shrimp cocktail was good as well I've tried all the salsas and they are all good and authentic . The place it self is very clean, the service can be a little slow sometimes apparently  but I don't mind it since I'm on Latin time anyway so I suggest ordering a beer and chilling out. I highly recommend giving this place a shot for all the skeptics out there !

I used to live next to the truck but I didn't ever try it until I saw they had opened this store! I'm vegetarian so I'm obviously not a huge hot dog eater so when I found out they had veggie sausage I had to try. For all my vegetarian pals--it's so good. Like incredibly good. I had to get someone to take a bite to make sure it wasn't real sausage. Their corn is the best thing I've ever tasted. Their avocado is fresh and they have so many other treats to choose from. Also, you don't have to go for just a hot dog. They have tons of other types of food to choose from that's just as good. They're so affordable but it wouldn't matter if they were expensive bc I'd eat here all the same.

My husband and I love this place for Chinese delivery.  We love it, but we don't indulge often, as the bill usually comes to at least $50 for the two of us.  Granted, we always have left-overs, but still, pricey.  We have never eaten at the actual restaurant, but the delivery is pretty fast, and it's true, they use awesome reusable containers.

Another great hidden gem - GREAT FOOD, GREAT SERVICE!  Can't beat that combo tbh. Their happy hour was amazing! The scallops sashimi style was so delicious and fresh that it melted in my mouth. Also their menu has changed so call in advanced if you're looking for anything in particular.   Good people working there since we had an almost two year old with us and they totally accommodated us with patience.   Will definitely return here often when we are in town! Cheers!
I just really like their regular coffee and the people are nice. What else do i need?

We are locals and have been coming to Finch & Fork for years. One of the best desserts and reasons for coming was the Barrel of Monkeys Banana Shake served in a mason jar and layered, not blended. Perfect blend of banana, liqueur, chocolate, etc. with a different taste in every bite.   Much to our disappointment, desserts have been unceremoniously simplified.  The Barrel of Monkeys Shake is now blended and served in a cheap looking old style diner shake glass.  While it tasted okay, the experience was subpar and the glass looked so out of place on our table.   To add insult to injury, we were given two different dessert menus, which showed how much desserts have been dumbed down.  Sophisticated desserts are a thing of the past at F&F.  Hence, I have to deduct two stars. At the least, management needs to ditch the shake glasses and bring back the mason jars.   The entrees we ordered were fabulous. The scallops were the best and a fresh salad with a chicken breast on the side was delightful.   Service is great.   In the future, we will go here for dinner, but will skip the dessert. We'll go for a walk down to McConnell's and satisfy out sweet tooth with a Santa Barbara icon and at a fraction of the price too.

How do I even begin to say how amazing Arnoldi's made my wedding night. Serving and catering to 39 people is never easy but the team made us feel so welcome and gave us an experience I'll remember for the rest of my life. Sara the event manager was so down to earth, a joy to talk to, and made the whole planning process easy. The prices are reasonable, especially for Santa Barbara.   My guests are still raving about the food. And the family style we had at our tables was so fun, everyone loved being able to try each entree. They put a curtain up for privacy and it felt like we had the whole restaurant to ourselves.   I've also never received such amazing service anywhere ever. I'm easy to please but the servers and bussers were so on it, helpful, attentive, every good word in the book. Especially the head waiter (kicking myself for not getting his name) but I've seen him there in the past. His personality was so warm and fun. He immediately greeted my husband and I to champagne just for us. Then throughout the night made sure my wine glass was never empty. He and the team helped us cut and serve the cake, he had a rag ready for our faces for the inevitable cake smash haha.   My nieces and family got to enjoy some bocce ball after dinner. Nobody ever made us feel rushed when the food was done (which honestly if I was still working in a restaurant I'd be so ready for us to leave haha). I could seriously go on and on.   Long review short: Arnoldi's Cafe checks all the boxes you could want. Whether it be for a romantic dinner, a casual hang out and drinks, or an event like a wedding. It's the perfect local restaurant and event space for all occasions with amazing food and even more outstanding service. Thank you Arnoldi's crew for making my wedding so special!

The food is not the problem. It's the service. I came here for dinner, on a Tuesday night. I waited 12+ mins for a server to finally come to my table. Yes after 5 mins I did start my timer.  After my initial beverage order, I struggled to flag someone down for water. My second beverage order never showed up. My third water request showed up with my fourth water request. By this time I was done with my meal and wanted to get out of there.   I had been there for almost 2 hours!!! I planned on getting dessert and espresso, however, after waiting so long for the water, I had lost my patience and wanted to goooooo home. The place looked fully staffed so I don't find this as an excuse. Disappointed, to say the least.

Came here with a co-worker the other day, we sat outside on the patio. The sitting arrangement is really nice and quite for being near the street. I ordered the grilled chicken sandwich. The service was great and good was really good! I definitely recommend this place!!

I usually enjoy Thai food so much. This place was such a disappointment. The egg rolls were good and so I thought I was off  to a great start. My friend took me there and was treating me .  We ordered the coconut soup with chicken and tofu--the chicken was dried out and they forgot to put the tofu in. We tried with no success to get the owners attention- he also was the only waiter on the floor at the busy lunch time hour. There was one bus girl ,very pleasant, however too much for her  to handle .The owner- waiter enjoyed talking and socializing with regular customers- very nice ,although it was  at the expense of helping the other customers. I ate the soup and was not hoppy at all. I had to pick out all kinds of hard pieces of uncooked ginger and other spices. The portion was very small for the price compared to other restaurants of the  same ilk on State street. The other restaurants serve much  larger and far superior versions of this soup, Tap  for example is far superior to this Café. I explained this to the waiter and he said  'Why didn't you tell me the tofu was missing when you received it?'  I laughed and said 'How could  I ? ' I tried to get your attention for fifteen minutes to no avail and when I finally did I finished it --I was starving  And you came by our table  with both  of your hands loaded with dishes,,juggling them to talk to  us. I was astonished at his lack of professionalism in every aspect of my experience. He was fine as long as people were complimenting him and schmoozing with him about his wife's trip to Thailand. If there was one ounce of concern or criticism  from a customer he turned into another human being- one of horror and viciousness . He became so self defensive as if he was being attacked - it was ridiculous He acted like a third grader My friend wanted to leave at that point .She felt horrible she had subjected me to this silly behavior and awful food.  Also the noise level was unbearable,  the acoustics were awful --children screaming and people talking loud- the restaurant couldn't accommodate this loud noise.  I was still hungry. so we ordered a Main dish  on goof faith foolishly and it was beyond horrible. Believe me when I tell you we wish we had loved it so we would not have  had to deal with  what occurred from that time after. We had shared the egg rolls and the small portion of soup so I said lets share a main dish .The main dish was horrible and unlike no other I have ever had in a Thai restaurant .I told him it was not of our liking the spices had an awful taste we couldn't eat  The minute we saw it we knew there was something wrong with it-- it was so orange like a pumpkin and no bean sprouts --,this dish is known for bean sprouts . And I told him not everyone has the same taste buds. we cannot eat it --it tastes horrible to us and we have had this dish in almost every Thai restaurant in this City and all over the world --so please have the courteously to believe us when we tell you it is not to our taste buds and liking even if every single one of your adoring customers love it --we do not and can not eat it. Would You please  be so kind as to replace it with another dish . He then went ballistic and said this is our number one dish everyone loves it here there is something wrong with the two of you .I didn't know whether to laugh or cry at his point .His behavior seemed  insane  and childish to both of us and  beyond ridiculous . I have never encountered such  unprofessional behavior by a manager or waiter or owner in a restaurant in my entire life and remember I am from New York so that says a great deal here.He should have said I am so terribly sorry about the tofu in the soup and we are so busy now please allow me to take this away and  bring you another dish that would-be to your liking.  Well he didn't and insisted we pay for a dish we couldn't eat and we insisted we would not and were literally thrown out of the place --imagine such rude, crude behavior.  This owner-waiter lacked class in every single aspect of this experience shame on him.  Another customer was saying his meal was ruined by all this  and I apologized to him because I have class. so we left and went to the excellent Thai restaurant on upper state street to ordered the exact dish and enjoyed it tremendously--. not Tap because they open only for dinner we went to another one and loved it as it was prepared properly beans sprouts and all. I suggest anyone who goes to this Café never complain and if you do not like the dish lie and say it is  delicious or the big, bad man will attack you. verbally.    He lashed out at us in self defense .I have never encountered a Mr. Jekyll  and Mir Hyde in a business like setting behave like him before in my entire life.

On our weekend here - my hubby & I came here 3 times in 2 days.  Twice for cupcakes at CrushCakes - the adjoining shop.  And once for a meal (breakfast).  Everything we had from here was amazing - delicious & fresh. The chai latte BLOWS Sbux out of the water.  Their breakfast bagels are delish.  And the cupcakery - forget about it! Every one we had (2 each - which we shared - so 4 different in total) were amazing. The service was also extremely friendly at both counters & they had some great reggae going with all the doors and window open for the breeze to pass through.  My hubby & I both agreed if we lived in S.B (which we hope to one day) - we would def be regulars! =D

Patxi's has a great location in the middle of State Street. Great open atmosphere inside, and a solid setup for watching sports if you want to sneak peeks during dinner. The deep dish pizza is great, and the happy hour choices and pricing are solid.

One of my favorite places for breakfast. The pancakes with fresh berries are excellent (and gluten free!). The bacon is excellent. They have the best selection of loose leaf teas in Santa Barbara. Tea is served in Japanese cast iron teapots with decorative China cups and saucers. It's also the best place in town for cookies. They are baked multiple times a day and I've often gotten one still warm from the oven. They also have some of the best iced tea in town, including a caffeine free blueberry tea. Sometimes they have house made specialty lemonades including lavender lemonade and basil lemonade. Lastly, this is a family owned restaurant and the owners are there daily, always friendly and helpful.
Everything closes at 7pm?! Everyday. Even weekends. Tried to do some shopping after work but nope. Amazon it is I guess.  No wonder none of the businesses make it.

Lovely Italian restaurant mid-town Santa Barbara. Good wine list. Good menu. Outdoor seating available. Covid precautions observed. Service was acceptable but needs improvement. Definitely nice dinner.

Whoa! Didn't someone get stabbed here back in 1999? Well, the 'Funk Zone' sure has changed this part of SB, that's for sure. We couldn't get a rezzie, but we managed to mosie up to the standing-room only table just in the nick of time.  The four of us shared everything but the standouts were the beets and the pork shank. The wine list is extensive and they accommodate you if you want to try a few different things. It was all wonderful and I hope to return soon.  Our little piece de resistance was running into 'Elaine' from Sienfeld, and unfortunately, out friend isn't above going to talk with her. Sorry Elaine; for your sake, I hope we don't run into each other again.  Auf.

I honestly don't remember thinking this place was just okay. But the next few times after, everything is amazing. My favorites tacos. Love the ahi poke taco and spicy crispy shrimp taco. I'll never get tired of this place. It's just ALWAYS so packed.

First we had them as a gift to our office and I had to try the real deal.  So we went to their location on Haley st. We ordered their combination of three; The beef one chicken one and a special that they had with potatoes was amazing. It took about 15 minutes but the wait was worth it everything was so hot and delicious definitely recommend this place. They have a small parking lot and street parking is a little crazy. I can now say that this restaurant will be added into our rotation of good eats in Santa Barbara.

I don't know if it is because I went here later in the evening (after 10pm) but the food was mediocre at best. Definitely does not deserve the 4 star ranking it gets. The service was ok and like I said, the food does not live up to the hype. I would have rather bought food from McDonalds as it is cheaper and better tasting!

Service is really bad.  Not in an unfriendly way... more of a general incompetence and perpetual aloofness kinda feel goin on.  I've been four times.  Brought the horrible service to the attention of management who gave a generic generally uninterested reply and the service still seems to be very bad.  Example - me and buddy ordered app and fries, ordered entrees, ordered drinks, ordered waters.  One beer and one app comes out, ask about fries, beer and water  'Fries, beer and water on the way', one water arrives and no fries and no beer, entrees and beer arrive and no app fries (entree that comes with fries has fries but additional order for my cohort does not come) and no water, order beer, fries finally come and no water no beer.  Whole time fries are just streaming by us to other tables.  Paying didn't go smoothly either.  Experience is representative of everytime I have been.  Summary - location fine, good food, bad bang for your buck, incompetent servers and management in my experience.

Location is great, you get to see the crowd enjoying the outdoor whilst eating. The staff were attentive, service were great. I love their sushi, tasted fresh. The ramen I had was spicy,  not so much but I enjoyed it. Fast service, I did not feel rushed. I won't mind stopping here to have a date with the wifey. They are prepared for outdoor, tables were spaced out enough for social distancing, heater is available for the cold days, or night. We used the qr app to see their menu, but you cannot use it to order and pay. Still better than maintaining a clean menu each time someone uses it. Come and support the local businesses.

Super fresh seafood. Their clam chowder was sooo good - huge fresh chunks of clam. I got the bread bowl though and didn't like the bread at all and there wasn't too much soup in it, so just stick with a regular bowl.   Also got the medium sized local crab - again very fresh and yummy  Their garlic baked clams were good too - a little greasy for me but had good flavor.   Posted pictures of everything. Can be pricey but overall would definitely return if visiting the pier again.  Oh and the view isn't too shabby either!

Stood in line this morning for take away.  Line was really long (Saturday morning so it is expected) but couldn't wait for my sourdough toast lol!  As always, the strawberry peppercorn jam is just amazing!  Ordered also a breakfast sandwich on brioche, french toast, avocado toast with a 7 minute egg ($3 extra), an olive loaf, and a pomegranite iced tea.  I would have given this a 4 but the wait was ridiculous after ordering.  The people behind me in line were waiting for 4 coffees for over 20 minutes and my food took 1/2 hour.  With nowhere to sit, only a place to stand by the dirty dish drop off, 1/2 hour is an eternity.  Got home and no egg on the avocado toast to boot.  The brioche sandwich is a bit bland... I only ate 1/2 of it and left the rest on the counter for anyone else to eat and it is still there lol.  My son LOVED the French toast and my daughter said the avo toast was good.  Bummed about the egg though.  Will try again and hopefully it will be not so long... the kids in the kitchen area have no sense of urgency lol but again, good.  (Also, the guy taking the orders was SUPER sweet!)

Food is consistently good - love the salsa bar.  Unfortunately, we're 0 for 3 on togo orders.  We've tried through the website and calling.  They run out of things and don't tell you until you show up.  They could apologize, offer chips and a drink while you wait, or discount your bill... but they don't.  Maybe they're so used to screwing up togo orders they don't think anything if it..
