Update: Now, a year later and after they've been doing togo orders primarily for 9 months, the service still just doesn't care.  Just a few days ago I showed up and told them my name and my order.  They told me that the order wasn't done yet.  They proceeded to continue calling my name out for 10 minutes while also telling me that my order wasn't ready.  No idea what was going on in their head.. no apology when I insisted they were talking about me and insisted they check the order.  Sure enough.. my food.  'Oh yah, here you go'

Fun spot to grab a bite to eat with a small group of friends as the menu has a nice variety of types of food to choose from. I arrived in town late Thanksgiving night and while intrigued and somewhat excited by the multiple options on State Street that were open and serving special Thanksgiving meals I really just wanted to sink my teeth into some standard American fare like a burger or pizza. I'm always hungry for a pizza. The menu has tons of unique burger options, salads, cleaner entree options like fish, tacos, lots of interesting appetizers, tortilla wraps and a big section of pizza options. I loooooove garlic with white sauce pizzas but my man is not such a fan so typically we end up sharing marinara sauce pizzas. Since I was here sans man I went with my gut instinct, ordering the White Out Pizza and was not disappointed! Grilled chicken, mozzarella, garlic, TRUFFLE oil. Yummmmmmm.   I'm still not much of a drinker anymore so I didn't sample any of the brews but some guys next to us were having a grand old time with the samplers and I saw some cool sounding beers on the menu board that I would definitely want to try if I didn't have to be careful with belly bloat before an event!  Service was average. Seat yourself at any table and hope that a server notices your presence. We sat a good 15 minutes before were even able to make eye contact with someone who worked there to flag them down to take our orders.

The black mussels were in a spicy red sauce were great....the highlight of the meal.*   Pizza was only average - mainly due to the crust which just wasn't crispy or chewy or flavorful enough..nothing special...The pizza sauce was unmemorable. The toppings definitely were high quality, which I appreciated, but I have never paid $83 for pizza and drinks...okay, and those mussels...but still - $100 including the tip, and the pizza should be stellar.   Had one of the drink menu specials with lemon vodka, Limoncello, etc - it was much sour-er than a traditional Lemon Drop, which at first I didn't love but it grew on me. The sour factor made it easier to sip so at least it lasted more than 5 minutes.They had another interesting lemon drink made with lemon sorbet which I didn't try.  Servers, bartender, bussers were all efficient and helpful. Tables are way too close together and it is noisy. So glad we got a seat right in the middle of the bar so we could watch the bartender make the fancy drinks and the pizza guy spin the dough. They have purse hooks under the bar! (why doesn't every restaurant have these?)   At this price point, it will be interesting to see if Olio Pizzaria thrives.   *As much as I liked my Olio mussels, the next night I had mussels at Relais de Paris on State St, which for just a few bucks more were plumper, juicier, a larger serving AND came with French bread, a delicious salad and huge serving of pomme-frites (fries), served in a quieter more elegant atmosphere.

Dave is the best. He continues to work hard to bring great food to his community. Lots of delicious options on the menu but the pizza dog is my go to. Just when he thought it couldn't get better he brought in a great burger and chicken sandwich menu. Stop by and support local business.

I came here with my dad on a Thursday evening and luckily didn't have to wait for a table. We were allowed to choose a table of our liking in the open seating area. It took a few minutes for a server to greet us but it wasn't a major issue. We decided to start off with the charbroiled oysters upon reccomendation by our server and other yelpers. It took around 20-25 minutes  for the oysters to arrive and another 10-15 for our mains to arrive. I ordered the grilled wild pacific sea bass and my dad ordered the grilled wild swordfish. The swordfish was super tasty and moist, but unfortunately my sea bass was overcooked. It was pretty chewy and flaking from being dried out. I was hoping to inform our server aware of the problem early in the meal but she was difficult to track down. You can choose 2 sides with each main, my dad and I both ordered the sweet potato fries which were deliciously complemented by a chipotle aioli. Our server suggested I tried the kale salad which I wasn't particularly impressed by. And my dad's asparagus was slightly undercooked and a little woody at the ends. When our server finally made it to our table to check on us, I told her about my subpar meal. A few minutes later the manager came by to apologize and offered us a complementary dessert to make up for the unsatisfactory order. We were really pleased by this gesture and decided to go with the blueberry cobbler. It was a decently sweet end to our otherwise mediocre experience at Lure.

Forgive the brief review!  I've been very busy recently and haven't had enough time to keep up.  But I will be releasing a number of these 'brief/summary' reviews to catch-up to my recent dining experiences.  If anyone has any questions or would like to know any additional specifics, please do not hesitate to ask!  Cheers!  My stars...(5=epic 'best of')(4=really tasty and want to go again and again)(3=good and will go back once in a while)(2=fair, might return for a visit if there was some glimmer of hope)(1=avoid at all costs)  Overall:  3  Why I went:  It's a classic, old school greasy spoon.  It used to be a chain that ran up & down the coast, but now SB sports the last original one.  Ordered:  Bacon & Eggs breakfast for my wife.  A burger and fries for me.  We went around 11am...  The breakfast was done to order and just what one would expect from a good solid greasy spoon.  For anyone who ever used to like Sambo's back in the day, the nostalgia is worth the visit.  The food itself is really nothing to go for.  It's simply the ambience...and the scenery of the ocean isn't half bad.  The coffees were OK.    Service:  Great.  The Christina was very nice and accommodating.  We were treated very well.    Hints/Final thoughts:  The reason to go is for the nostalgia.  There is tastier food to be had elsewhere.  But, if you're feeling a 'comfort' breakfast or early lunch, it does the trick nicely.  Plus the view is quite nice.

Came here for breakfast, was way better than I expected. Their home made sausage was amazing, not too fatty, lots of flavor. They also make great fluffy omellettes. For pastry, they make the most amazing almond tart. It's crispy on the edges and the top, filled  with a creamy almond paste filling. So damn good & its actually cheaper to buy the whole tart then a single slice.

Quick service, nice staff, nice ambience. They have weekend $5.all day bloody marys & mimosas. good Happy Hour Menu 2pm-6pm Bison burger, B.Marrow burger, Blue cheese burger where all good!   I paid $26.43 for a bison burger w/side of fries, mac&cheese & ice tea. Good

RESTAURANT: I paid $18 for food that looked and tasted like it was a frozen prepared meal that you buy at the store for $7. The 'Impossible meat' was extremely dry, did not taste at all like Impossible meat, and looked like dog food. On top of that, it took forever to get any service and there were no forks for me to eat with.   BAR/POOL: Again, took forever to get any service. There were also no pool tables open as the employees were playing on one and another drunk couple was hogging one while not actually playing. When I tried asking the couple if anyone was actually playing or if I could play next with my friends, they very rudely said that they were playing although I only saw them shoot another 2 shots and talking to each other the entire time.

Wonderful guilt free tasty food! We returned two days in a row. Great juice and smoothie options, avocado toast, açaí bowl and sandwiches. They have 6 tables outside and are open during COVID days. Wonderful staff as well

Fantastic Fare!!!  Started off with One dozen Charbroiled Oysters, best in town!!!  Bread is freshly baked, excellent go-along!  We love the Sand dabs, so tender, flaky and perfectly cooked! Highly recommend!! Cocktails are average, unless Kevin is working on the bar then we recommend the Patron Perfection, which he creates perfectly as the name implies! :) Delicious salads, and such a lovely atmosphere, inside and out!!!

My fiancé and I ate here last week, really great food and the service was excellent! We sat up top on their balcony, great view of State street. We had the calamari for starters, filet mignon, and chicken; the meal was great. I highly recommend dinner here if you are looking for a place to eat in downtown Santa Barbara.

This was becoming my new favorite Mexican food spot--and then they completely changed the menu. They've primarily gone to some make your own bowl/burrito thing with a handful of other options. The old menu had killer entrees like chili verde and carne asada. These sadly, are no more. They added a burger to the menu...a burger for God sakes!  On a Friday night, the place was maybe 1/4 filled. The restaurant used to be packed with a wait outside. Perhaps they changed the menu to salvage the business? I'm not sure...but there's little on the menu that really interests me any more. If I want a bowl, I'll just head down the street to Chipotle.  **UPDATE**  Somebody from the restaurant chose to reply below and all they did was get defensive and ENCOURAGE me to go dine at a different restaurant. Perhaps that's the reason for the slump in business? The menu has changed and although there's 'chile verde' still, it only comes in the form of a burrito or what they call a bowl. You can't just get things as an entree anymore. The menu has cheapened, it feels more fast-foodish.

Recently had a surprise party here in their tatami room for my daughter. The service and food was excellent. Although we had more than ten people, our server was able to attend to our needs and kept on checking in with us. Really personable service.

This is one of my must-visit places when I'm in the Santa Barbara area!  Right now seating is limited and with the current situation they do have part of their dining room available, but only about 5-6 tables that can only handle parties of about 4 people. I've gotten take out from Los Agaves many times before and their attention to quality is so good that the experience is equally as good whether its take out or dine in!  I usually order:  AGAVES BURRITO - The BEST seafood burrito I have had anywhere! - The burrito is filled with shrimp and fish, lettuce, rice, beans, etc. - The real winner is the sauce that comes with the burrito SO GOOD - If it's your first time here and you like seafood this is definitely a must try!  2 ITEM COMBO - CHICKEN ENCHILADA & BEEF CRISPY TACO  - The combos come with a big cup of beans and a little pyramid of rice! - I usually order the enchilada and crispy taco for a little variety! - The meat is always super tender and flavorful  - This plate gives you a little taste of everything if you can't decide on just one thing  STEAK NACHOS - This makes a great appetizer or if you're really really hungry, it could be an entree too! - This pile of nachos is huge! I have never once finished it on my own  - It comes with all your normal nacho toppings (beans, cheese, guacamole, salsa, etc.) - The steak is sooooo good!   Overall, my favorite place in the area that I will always go back to!

I am so grateful for Sachi Ramen! With a big selection of food, delicious flavors, and great pricing I am never disappointed eating here. Their ramen is yummy. Craft ramen has way less of a selection and higher pricing. I always feel more welcomed at Sachi then at SB Craft Ramen. The staff are very friendly and hospitable. My food is always ready quickly and is piping hot, which is important when getting ramen for takeout. Highly recommend!
For the yelp check in they do not honor this - one of my worst YELp pet peeves...  came here for the lobster bisque it was also overpriced and not worth it, especially taking it to go.

Absolutely love the food here, especially the fact that it is all organic. One aspect they could definitely improve upon is their service. The girls at the registers aren't very friendly, and don't like answering questions. They are a bit slow in preparing the food, so make sure you have extra time when heading here. They are still fairly new and seem a bit dis-organized. Hopefully they can figure it out sooner rather than later because this place has so much potential!

Two words: Agaves Burrito. No matter what you think you want to order, don't! If you come here, you must order the agaves burrito. You won't regret it. It's the best thing your mouth will ever taste.  The atmosphere is a little crazy. The place is busy packed so it gets hectic. You have to get in line first and order what you want. The line goes pretty fast so you better grab a menu and make your selection quick. But you should already know to order the agaves burrito so you're all set! When it times come to sit down, you won't have the easiest time finding a seat but the staff is really good about knowing who came in before who and will find a seat for you. Once you get your meal, the big crowd and the waiting for a seat will all be worth it. Once you have some of that burrito in your mouth, you will forget about anything else. I'm making myself hungry as I type! Now if you're in SB, what are you waiting for? Go get that burrito!
Found this gem bopping around the neighborhood, thankfully we did! It was really good food food and the woman making it was gem! I'll be more than happy to go back!

Had dinner here last night with my wife.  The food was perfect and beyond what we expected.  She had the pear and bleu cheese salad, diver scallops and shrimp in lobster bisque with grits; I had the cheddar cheese hush puppies, fried chicken, and we ended with the chocolate beignets which were fantastic.  During the meal, we concluded that this is our favorite restaurant not only in the art district, but in all of what State Street has to offer.  Our server, Corrie, was awesome.  Great personality, made great recommendations; and even though we showed up right at closing, we were never rushed to get out of there.  We will definitely be back!

I've been here 3 times now, first two times I got fish & chips(for me) & chicken & chips (for my better half) & the largest order of the fried pickles that they will let me leave with!  This time it was just me. Strolling down state street, and there I stood........ Glaring at my reflection in the window outside the resturant when it hit me! I LOVE PIES! SO MUCH THAT MY MIND TOOK OVER MY BODY AND SENT ME HERE! I got the chicken till a masala haggis and daaaaaauuummmmmm girl dat be off the chains foooo sheeeezzy

I usually skip on Thai food because of the excess grease, salts, and sugars. This Thai Cafe is different because the food is cleaner and freshly prepared, and you won't leave feeling sick, or feeling sick the next morning. I'm so impressed with their high quality, fresh, piping hot food, for a reasonable price. There are some outdoor tables for those who are distancing. I'm going to put this restaurant on my rotation of tried-and-true places from now on.
darn good breakfast burrito. Each is easily enough for 2 normal eaters. Packed with eggs and meat, yummy.
Stiff drinks, great historic atmosphere, good food, and convenient location. Been a family favorite for generations!

I love their chicken strip and lemonade. They have a lot of sauce but I think the best one is chick-fil-a sauce. Their staffs are all friendly and the place is clean. They offer a family challenge for you to get a free ice cream.

Outstanding! You want Cajun, you get Cajun! Warm service. ...actually , legendary!!!! The flavors at re all there and they do  not come short.  Huge quantity in my stAndards. Live the  surprise mini muffins served wH Hime waiting. I loved the banana walnut! Definitely coming back!

This place is so cute! Service was wonderful, our waitress Audrey was exceptional. The food was absolutely delicious, as were the Bloody Mary's. I got the breakfast fried rice at Audrey's referral and it was so scrumptious. Will be back definitely!!!
The Thai curry noodles were a miracle in my mouth. I was sad when the meal ended and licked the bow clean.

I went to longboards for lunch on a hot and sunny Saturday.  The location on the wharf is great, and the deck upstairs is very sunny and has a great view of the water.  You're definitely paying for the view here, as the food is just average and it's not cheap.  The food is fine, but nothing special.  It's a fun place to go and have a beer and lunch with friends though.  I would definitely only go if it was nice out and you could sit on the deck, otherwise it's not worth it.

After trying 4 places to park and seeing all the hate signs about not parking there, I as a local was wondering how they had pissed a off all their neighbors so bad, and if it was even worth it after triple circling and ending up in the city lot by Amtrak.  Next came the trying to read the paper menu in the very dimly lit hall way, and trying to figure out why there were so many high priced up sells, like and egg for $2, or adding spice for $.50-$2. I opted away and wanted to see what I would get just ordering the bowl of ramen, the Tonkatsu ramen with sliced pork and fish ball. Great, two sources of protein to go with my noodles, bamboo and bean sprouts.   After filing through the barred off area for ordering, I picked up utensils and plunked down at the VERY uncomfortable seating at the picnic style benches. The benches are too far under the table, and you have to sit hunch back. Oh, and it is all communal seating. Why?  Then the real dissapointment set in as the emo girl dropped off our ramens.  The broth was great, but the noodles were very over cooked and mushy. The bamboo was plentyful, but the meat was not. There were 2 small razor thin slices of delicious pork and one razor thin slice of fish ball. Yes, not a fish ball, but a slice of fishball. You know, like a garnish. Why even put the meat on the menu if you are not really going to out any in the bowl?  I asked for some sriracha and was happily given the whole bottle. I wanted to walk around and offer people a squirt for a quarter, that would have been half off the menu price. Maybe I could sell eggs for a buck and make money too!   The moral of the story is, this is a tourist trap meant to hussle visiting cruise ship people in and out fast and not need to hire wait staff.  Too bad, because the flavors were good, just not pleantifil nor enticing to keep the local coming back.

My husband and I were in Santa Barbara for a job yesterday and decided to check this place out for lunch. La super rica taqueria was closed for the day. Just a few blocks up is this place, with nice interiors, really epitomizing the California feel with the Spanish tiles, open airy seating, bright colors. It was all very lovely.    We ordered the wet steak burrito and the special, gringa al pastor and two horchatas. Salsa bar was fresh and plentiful. The pico de gallo and salsa verde were my favorites, though the pico de gallo was way spicy! It burned my mouth after a while. The horchata was a little too milky for my liking. Wish there was a bit more cinnamon in there. My burrito was pretty standard, nothing too amazing. Good quality meat and it was really filling. It came with rice, guac and sour cream as well. My hub's al pastor special, which was like a quesadilla with pork chunks and pineapple was really delicious. We were stuffed by the time we finished. Huge portions with good prices.  Definitely will be coming back to this restaurant next time we're in Santa Barbara.

Tried Mesa Verde with some vegan friends visiting from out of town, and we were all totally impressed. Delicious, highly flavourful food that was hearty and satisfying without being too 'heavy' or rich, like some vegan restaurants.   I especially liked the ambiance and decor - most vegetarian/vegan restaurants are super casual and basic, but Mesa Verde, while still casual, classes it up a notch or two, and makes for an inviting spot for a night out.   My own constructive criticism is that most of our dishes came out at once, and weren't served in any regular dining order (ex. appetizers, then salads, etc). They warned us about this, but it was a bit overwhelming to get so much food at once, without any leisurely pacing to the meal.

Easy parking in the back.  Very friendly staff and good service.  Lots of good lunch special.  We got the Slice/salad/and drink for under $9.  The place is clean, has a TV, and some video games.  The salad was fresh.  Just lettuce, a slice of tomato, and lots of cheese.  The Italian dressing was yummy.  The slice of pizza was large.  It was not tasty.  The crust tasted like a frozen pizza crust.  The cheese had no flavor.  And I could not taste the sauce.  Very disappointing when everything leading up to it was good and it looked so good.  Toppings were $.50 each.  If I had ordered so flavorful toppings, I think it would have saved the pizza.

Stella Mare's will always have such a special place in my heart. It's such a beautiful spot for brunch or dinner, you really can't go wrong with the time of day. If you want to be in the greenhouse for brunch, make sure you make a reservation and request it, otherwise you'll probably not get it and it's definitely worth it!   The food is delicate and delicious, not as heavy as a lot of French food places. All their fish dishes are fabulous; the fish isn't overcooked, and the sauce doesn't overpower the flavor of the fish. My personal favorite is the Truite Rouge Almandine. The burger is also super good and unique--I can't even think of another place to get a duck burger! But you definitely can't get it well-done, because it becomes quite tough and ruins the duck.   As far as cocktails go, I would say skip them unless you're celebrating something--they're nothing special and a bit pricey, though I suppose not out of line with the prices of the food. Just run of the mill flavor with pretty garnish, which actually is where they lose a start for me. Overall though, the whole dining experience is beautiful and refined, and I definitely 10/10 recommend the food and atmosphere!

Usually a place like this with such an amazing view will be pretty mediocre when it comes to food.  Shoreline actually had a decent selection of good quality Mexican food and standard cafe fare.  Not bad service either and a good stop for a bite.

I have been to the location a couple of times.  Last night I ordered online for delivery.    I places the order at 545pm.   The website said delivery by 620pm.   At around 7 I had not received my order.  I called the location a couple of times.   No answer.  I called my credit card company to stop payment.  We made other arrangements for dinner.  At 8pm I got a knock on my door from the delivery guy.    I told him I ordered this pizza over 2 hours ago.  He said he just started his shift.   I said I did not want the pizza.   He said keep it anyways.    I checked and it was cold.  It was probably made at 6 and sat around for 2 hours.   I would be cautious using this service.   I would also suggest they provide a number where someone answers the phone    Your response to my review is not very useful.   You have a  non working number.   I emailed this address.  3 days later and not even the courtesy of a response.  I had to call my credit card company to dispute this charge  I did get a response from PizzaRev customer service.  They sent me a certificate for a free pizza.  The second time i ordered, they delivered on time and everything was perfect.  I appreciate the offer of a free pizza.  I probably would not have ordered from them again, without this offer.

Finally got the chance to return to the most wonderful restaurant in Santa Barbara. The wine, food, and service here is absolutely impeccable from start to finish. It is rare indeed to feel simultaneously so pampered/like royalty and so welcome/like family. The scallop trio shared appetizer is not to miss (get it as an entree of no one will share!).
We loved them so much we asked them to cater our wedding and it was definitely the right choice!

A great venue that could stand some minor improvements.   Pros - They book good bands that you normally wouldn't be able to see on a small stage. That is reason enough to LOVE this venue. The staff is friendly, the sound system is awesome, and they sell pizza in the back. Nuff said.  Cons - it feels like they oversell their shows. It's impossible to move in this joint when they have a full house. Also, 18+ ... Really? I can't take a beer to the front of the stage cause of these kids? Pack em in the balcony where they belong or don't let them in at all. Sorry kids, I'm getting old and grumpy. Papa needs some elbow room for him and his dranks.
Great service!  Our waiter was super nice and attentive!  He was super nice to my boy!  Thank you to the manager for getting awesome seats!

Long Line and Long wait.. i really wanted to like the tacos here because they have a great salsa bar. Unfortunatly the tacos and burrito i ordered were pretty bland. I also ordered sour cream on everything and they decided that I would get none even on the things that it should have came on. The great thing about this place and what stops it from being a 2 star with the chips and salsa bar. Unfortunately the rest of the food or service was not that impressive. Im  sticking with Lilys tacos and coming here for chips!
My favorite sandwich and salad shop in Santa Barbara  Reasonable prices great service

These guys must be killin' it here on Yelp...but that's not hard to do if you know how to push social media buttons. The food is quite good, the prices are high, the stand-in-line-to-order-at-the-counter thing is ridiculous, as the guy taking the order refuses to make eye contact and with the insane noise level in the place, you don't know if he's actually heard you clearly or not.  The table water service is just plain weird...and it's lukewarm tap water anyway, so what's the point of the 'special bottle' weirdness?  The coffee is excellent, the restroom decor is cool.  If you want to meet tourists (who are obviously sourcing the place through Yelp) and avoid bumping into locals, this is the place for you.  On the other hand, if you're a local...we miss the old place.

Nice location and cozy Italian decor. Very comfortable for a nice little stop off of State street.  I had the Pollo Panini and it was, by far, the best chicken and pesto panini I've ever had. Although much of the credit goes to the quality of the pesto and the thin chicken breasts something must be said for the use of their pizza dough for the sandwich.  The panini came with a salad that had a refreshing lemon vinaigrette.  All around excellent lunch.

It's a quality fast food restaurant, rated 3.5 (4/5/3015). We ordered fish tacos, beef and spicy pork tacos. Don't forget to take jalapeño sauce, chili sauce and salsa sauce. They will make your food even more tasty!!

Epic lunch prices. Phenomenal view, and 5 star service. I should be eating here at least 1x a week. There is outside seating, no inside seating. The place is very small, and there is always a line- but the line moves fast! They take cash, and card (: don't feed the birds! The sushi is fresh, and a great portion of food. You will not feel skimped. I recommend you bring a date here!

I am surprised that this place got some bad reviews, because frankly it's pretty awesome. Everything from the food, the prices, the staff and the wines.   Oh, and yes, this IS a wine bar. Their wine selection is mind boggling for a small place, or Santa Barbara in general. I just didn't expect this kind of selection especially because this I what I am used to in SF or Napa where we are much closer to what I consider traditional wine country.   We showed up more than an hour and a half before our reservation and they managed to seat us anyway. They really do make sure to ask if you're celebrating something because they love to give you dessert!  The food was really delicious, except for that weird warm watermelon salad. Stay away from that. Everything else is great!

SO SAD TO SEE CHANGE FROM PAST: Been a die-hard fan of this gem for years...I've always looked forward to bringing loved ones here  for special occasions and we always spare no expense, ordering cocktails and multiple course meals with lavish desserts.     The service, which they understandably have prided themselves for years, has always been this venues shining star.    However, today I had a very sub-standard experience that was so very unusual that I felt compelled to share with hope that their manager will keep tabs on the staff involved so that this treasure does not lose its fan following over time.    I made telephone reservations 3 days ago for today at 2 pm.    Our small family of 3 was excited to celebrate our Easter Sunday lunch after church here.     I had asked the man I made the reservation with on the phone  several days ago to please write a small note on my reservation about sitting by the window.   He assured me he would.   Well, today we arrived at 2 pm and went to check in with this young, blond college-age host.   She said she had no record of my reservation.    I told her that I had made it several days ago.   She looked again and said she was sorry but there was no record.     By now, I'm feeing befuddled and more than a little confused.    She assured me they would fit us in anyway.    Then she looks down at the reservation book and says that she is sorry because she had us already 'booked in under our name.'   She takes us over to a table further away from the window and I see that a very fresh family of 3 people had just been seated at the table by the window.     We have to believe that that was the table reservation we were supposed to have had by the window and the one she admitted to checking some other people in under.     Well, we are not sure if the people themselves lied about the reservation and took our table, or if the girl just crossed us off the list errantly too early.    Whatever it was, we sat at a table that was less than idyllic.    Then that same blond collegiate host ended up being our server and she could have redeemed herself by being personable and friendly and all would have been great (as usual).  However, she did not offer muffins and she just seemed less than genuine or personable.   She rarely smiled and she seemed just in general lackluster and maybe a bit unhappy about having to work Easter?   The young man working was sweet and helpful, but he was not out main server.    The girl waitress said that gumbo bowl was big enough for us to share a bowl.    Given that there were three of us, and that I was just getting over a cold, I asked her if we could have an extra cup to split the soup.  She ended up bringing the one bowl with a tiny tiny cappuccino size cup that she handed me and it had about 2 ounces of soup that she had in it already.  She said that cup was for me.    I'm not sure why the small cup size....not understanding why she didn't just bring a normal empty cup or bowl that we could have split the soup.  To be honest, not sure why she recommended that the one bowl Of soup was enough for all three of us to begin with.  I would have been more than willing to pay for a cup or bowl for each of us, but when she said it was really big, we went with her suggestion.    The food was delicious as always.   Stellar.  No issues with food quality.... When we went to leave, there was yet another blond collegiate (super young girl) who was stationed at the exit door and when we went to leave she did not say a word.   Every time I've ever been here the staff had always made a huge effort to smile and say goodbye and ask us about how everything was when we left.   That's just part of their charm.   But, man oh man, not sure if it was because they had all their usual staff off for Easter and were using these super young and inexperienced wait staff, but the staff they had on (with exception of the young Hispanic man) was not remotely of the quality I've grown to expect as the best part of the Palace.   Hope the manager will be sure to keep some more seasoned staff on duty on a major holiday day next time.

Major disappointment. This location doesn't even come close to the one that closed on the Mesa. The people are not nearly as friendly. There is a lady who works the cash register and she never even smiled at us and seemed irritated when I was asking questions about the menu. We waited a very long time for our food and the salad had more cheese than lettuce and was missing the tomatoes. They're going to have to step up their customer service game big time for us to visit as often as we did the location on the Mesa.

Good food and good service.  I have never been dissapointed in any dish I've had at this restaurant.  Good place to meet with friends or family.  They will cook from the menu or make authentic for the Chinese palet.  I love the green beans and walnut shrimp.  Every time we've eaten there food is fresh, good and presented well.  You will never go away hungry either.

In a city filled with really excellent places to eat, this one rises to the competition and offers wonderful, innovative and fresh food creatively prepared and presented and a pleasure to eat. The chocolate-covered blackberries in our dessert were just about the best we have ever had, probably organic and locally grown. The wait staff is very personable and helpful. Dining inside or outside makes eating here a pleasure and it will be a pleasure to return here often. It is a fairly new establishment, but I have no doubt that it will enjoy a bright and long future.

It's interesting to me how there are soooo many Mexican food options here in SB! During my recent road trip to Santa Barbara, our friends had recommended us to stop by Los Agaves and try out their food. We figured we had to at least try one of the mexican food stops, and decided to make this our lunch stop.   We opted to dine in, and since it was a relatively late lunch for us, we lucked out for happy hour. The online menu was slightly outdated, as it reflected $5 for a small margarita whereas it actually cost us $7.50 when we ordered. Nonetheless, it's totally worth it since the drinks were pretty strong!   We ordered a few shareable items and sat outside in the patio. As per usual, we were served chips and salsa - ugh, which is always my weakness. We had ordered a mole enchilada, and a lamb and beef taco sampler plate that the hostess had recommended. Whether if you would like to order for take out or dine in, you order through their cashier and then either wait at the front, or take your number and pick a table to sit down in their patio in the back.   I'd have to be honest, I've never had mole sauce before. I didn't really know what flavor to expect, and the dark brownish sauce was very intriguing. It looks like their mole sauce was also a crowd favorite, and I can totally see why! The mole sauce was tangy and savory, it wasn't toooo wet but just moist enough to eat with the enchilada. It wasn't too messy to eat either! I ended up liking it so much that I scooped up all the mole sauce was the chips!  The beef taco was meh, but I really liked the lamb taco. It had so much flavor! Although we only had two items and a few more drinks, we really enjoyed our time at Los Agaves. I can see why it was a crowd favorite during pre-quarantine times!

Very bad experience!! Entrées that were advertised like crispy almond chicken and others not available when I asked about it he got an attitude from the server and then found out they had no ice because the ice machine was not working and they're not smart enough to go grab a few bags of ice from the store right next-door no discount for drinks that are warm no ice tea made got more  attitude when I asked . I would not recommend this place if you were starving. If I could write in with no stars or negative stars I would
everything was really good we got a bunch of things and all tried everything. I loved the arugula/ham/bree/honey sandwhich

Went here on a Monday evening at 10:00pm. There were only two other customers there. This was probably due to the weather being cold for SB. I was too late for food but there is a great selection of beers here which was great. Bar staff were good but since I was the only person buying a drink I would expect them to be. Would like to go back when it's warmer the patio looks like a great place to have a meal and few drinks.

This review is during their trivia night ( happening every Tuesday @ 7 pm.)  Went to Taffy's Pizza for the first time for their trivia night and I must  say it was one of the best experiences I've ever had! Eating pizza and drinking beer while answering trivia questions?! - nothing beats that!  The atmosphere in their outdoor patio was really great. We got a little competitive but all for the sake of fun and good company.  The servers here are nice and friendly and the manager/chef also came to our table after the game and asked us how we were doing, making sure that everything went great and we had a fantastic night.   We got the Taffy's special and it was above average. Their beers on tap are also pretty decent.  I would love to try their wings next time!   Seriously, trivia night is fun and I would recommend you to go and experience it. Don't forget to bring five other friends with you to complete your team!

Okay I can't say enough about this place!! Soooo good! Came here for dessert and the cookies were to die for! Best cookies I think I have ever had... located in a perfect spot in the funk zone- very industrial looking inside which I like. Also located in a spot where they have wine tasting, community style seating- which is fun! All around great atmosphere/ I can't wait to come back and try the breakfast!!

Is Presto Pasta under new ownership? I remember when my kids were young (15ish) years ago we used to frequent the location on Milpas. We moved 6 years ago & when we came back a year ago we ended up moving near the DeLaVina location. We visit there so much it's becoming embarrassing (my son likes pasta). I'm noticing that WOW the quality of EVERYTHING has gone down. First, the customer service is just awful. The employees act miserably. It's so sad! No more smiles & pleasantries. There's only one kid (a young man) that is consistently friendly. The owner used to be in the Milpas location & he was such a quality guy. He obviously cared about the business. Not the vibe you get today walking in a Presto Pasta. Second, the garlic bread is awful! What happened??? Third, the food isn't the same. Those of you who've eaten the old Presto & the current Presto know exactly what I'm talking about. I hope someone will read this & at least fix the garlic bread issue. That was always my favorite part of PP.

I have been here several times.......food is always ok...service ok.....went tonight....its changed a lot...for the better.....is it under new management?    FOOD GREAT AND FAST.....waiters attentive.....manager for sure checking it all out.....  I will be back often....keep up the good work
Goodness, that was amazing. Expensive, but amazing. Get the whole dungeon crab, some local Sav Blanc, and the steamed mussels. Put your name down and go explore the peer for a while.

A room full of kitties looking for a furrever home.  It was fun to hang out with them while we were away from our kitty at home. This is not a  'Cat Cafe' where you are served food and beverages...state laws prohibit that. It's a fun place to chill with cats of all ages, sizes, and personalities.

Adoro il cibo italiano! I particularly LOVE real handmade pasta! Via Maestra is a local favorite hidden in a shopping center off of upper State Street. Outside seating is reminiscent of laneway seating in Italy. I was surprised upon entering the restaurant because it has qualities of an Italian deli with a small selection of imported Italian goods to buy. If you'd rather not dine in, you can order food to pick up. Or you can skip a meal and go straight for dessert with a nice assortment of gelatos (available for testing) and pastries/cakes. The desserts are made by an outside Italian style baker, not in house. Typically this would bother me, but as they import real Italian cake, this only encourages future patronage. Their desserts are delicious. My husband and I shared a pistachio cheesecake and fruit tart. My absolute favorites on their menu include the arugula (rocket) salad with delicious cheese and fresh cut tomatoes, and their pumpkin ravioli with sage butter sauce. Oh. My. Goodness. If i could choose a last meal on earth, the pumpkin ravioli and arugula salad with light lemon vinaigrette, win!

So glad there is a hyper local vegan restaurant in the area. Gorgeous space and patio, courteous service, coffee, wine, smoothies, a transcendent quinoa tahini bowl topped with avocado, greens, and an abundance of herbs and edible flowers.

This place has pretty decent Thai food. It's not the best I've ever had, but for the price and the serving size, it's well worth your money.  The service is quick and the employees are incredibly friendly and attentive.  One thing that I would recommend everybody trying is their mango sticky rice! I love this dish but it's very easy to make it less-than-perfect. Galanga does it right every single time! The rice is perfectly cooked and mangos are always just right/ripe. Their Thai tea is also good!

Yummy pastries! I love to go here to pick up are there an apricot Danish, the long kind you can see several people with, or those delicious custards with fruits toppings ! The people are gracious and the food is delicious! Reminds me of Solvang, a town I used to spend time in as a teenager.

Food is good, service is poor. Skip the beet salad, Banana bread and matcha latte. Try the cauliflower sandwich and truffles or other sweets, especially if you're gluten free! For a quick, fresh, light lunch, this is a good place.
Went for my boyfriends birthday recently and the lobster risotto was to die for!   He thoroughly enjoyed his filet mignon.  Great server and fantastic drinks to boot!

All good here. Great beer and wine selection. Menu is not as big as most, but thanks a great thing. That means they specialize in something. And they do. Grilled fish. Wow. I loved it. Staff was great. You also get a free beer with your Yelp check in. Go here people and eat.

Went for brunch and had a bloody Mary that included a good sized shrimp, which was very good. Also had the Boathouse Signature Breakfast which consisted of Poached eggs over Dungeness crab cakes served over a bed of breakfast potatoes and topped with chipotle hollandaise, avocado and pico de gallo. VERY GOOD. I would order this again and recommend it. Not to mention the perfect view of the beach!

Well. That was probably the strongest drink I've ever had (and I've had my fair share).   I arrived on a Friday around 10:30? I don't remember. Anyway there was a short line and the place was packed with loud, drunk college kids. Apparently 25 is old these days because that's how I felt. It was super echoey and I don't think there was any music. It's casual and I guess a good place to go if you want to get your socks knocked off in an affordable manner.   I probably wouldn't come back if I'm ever in SB again, but I can see why it's a popular late spot for those in college.

Where the locals go for breakfast.  If you are looking for a locals place in Santa Barbara away from the tourist spots, and overpriced food just a few blocks down(toward the ocean) on State Street, than this is the place for you...  They're very friendly here, does not have the pompous air of the other spots.

Healthy food never tasted so good!! I have been several times and have never been disappointment. The Old Town Salad with grilled chicken and the Turkey burger are two of my favorites. Get to their State Street  location early, grab a table on the sidewalk and enjoy beautiful Santa Barbara.

I am a local Santa Barbara residence, my family and I went to Casa Blanca tonight for a very special dinner out for our Children and our grandchildren that are leaving California.  We don't go down town very often we really try to avoid it as much as possible but my daughter-in-law wanted to try this place.      GO TO PLAZA AZUL !  Real Mexica      We ourselves are in the service business for tourists. Walking up to the door I heard two of the bus boys outside dropping the f bombs!  Not the best way to welcome a customer walking into a your restaurant with 3 children under 6 in tow! I've been to Casa Blanca before and had good food and service!  NOT TODAY!  The majority of tourists are gone by Sunday afternoon so they were not busy at all.     Our hostess seated our partying 8, we had made reservations also.  So getting drinks was the first step and that seemed to be a challenge for there 'team' because honestly I don't even know who was our waiter or waitress for the whole dinner, first to get my ice tea, it came with no ice, then to get sugar packets, I tried to ask waiters passing by, but not even a look in our direction! WOW the guy was two feet away from me! So I got up to get the dish of packets myself. We ordered our food, very bland for Mexican food only thing spicy  was the very mild salsa.  My husband ordered cooked jalapeños and they were not hot! Several thinks happened during our meal, our plates were served at different times. My son finished his dinner when I was just starting to eat.  Getting drink refills was a chore!  They wouldn't clear the empty plates. We got up to take a few family photos, when we turned back to table the table was cleared with our to go containers gone, something got my attention to the hostess station, and they were laughing about the note I wore on the back of my receipt describing how bad there service and food was, and one of the girls told me' I'm Mexican but I speak English!  Where in the world did that come from. So then I asked her in Spanish if she wanted to speak in Spanish, and told her I didn't think this was very funny! She walked away from me, the other girl was apologetic but it was lip service! To little to late! They were laughing about her little tip, I told her if it was up to my husband he would have gave no tip! As I said I'm in the customer service Bussiness myself, in fact the day before I had lunch in the valley, I had gone there before when I was working, my bill was $20, I gave $10 tip, the service was great.

I was very eager to get my live uni and oyster fix during my trip here, since I had such a pleasant experience during my last visit.   Came here this past Sunday at 11:45am, and it wasn't busy.  Oh, and they did not have any uni trays (none at all, even in the morning).  ** I was expecting:  -  Freshly Shucked, Creamy, live uni -  Freshly shucked oysters  ** What I received:  -  Shucked Uni, covered in veins, bitter (not fresh) after-taste -  Shucked Oysters, most torn apart, did not taste fresh   As you can tell, I had a very unpleasant experience here compared to last time.  I've had better uni and oysters when I bought them from a local grocery market and shucked them myself.    I'm still very shocked at how the quality went from 100 to zero.. real quick.  The uni tasted and looked like it wasn't even cleaned/washed after being shucked.  I can understand maybe a tiny vein here and there, but this looked like it was sprinkled with veins!  Not only that, even after rinsing it in water ourselves, it still left a bitter aftertaste in my mouth-- no creaminess at all.  On top of that, there were small shards of oyster shell on my uni.  Overall, taste and presentation were terrible-- I wish the guy who prepared our uni put a bed of ice under it... we only ordered one uni last time and the uni was placed on top of a nice bed of ice.  This time, all 3 orders of uni were thrown into a plastic container.  (Just look like my photos and you'll see the huge difference between my 2 visits).  Out of a dozen of the Blue Point Oysters ordered, only 5 were eaten.  Yup, less than half.  We even had to ask for lemon since he didn't include it in our order.  It's odd not to include lemon, especially if they don't even carry tabasco or cocktail sauce.   We brought our tray back and I asked them why the quality of this batch was so bad compared to last time.  They confirmed that they receive fresh shipments every day, but I honestly feel like these were dead for a few days.    They really didn't have an answer for me, and didn't even seem to feel bad (def not a returning customer next time).  The guy there said to me, 'It's like a box of chocolates.'  * EYE ROLL*.  At least a box of chocolates is all edible and fresh.... in that case, I would like to return my box of chocolates. lol. #baii

The reason why we came here is because my boyfriend is in love with their yellow tail sandwich.....which was tasty. I ordered the prime rib chili and my daughter ordered the claim chowder. We had a drink each..... The bill came to approx $90. 2 bowls of soup and a sandwich with three drinks.....The food was good but not $90 good. The view was spectacular and the service prompt and courteous.
Great local place that you can rely on each time for a quality meal.

Love then strawberry white cake.  It's rich but not too sweet and delightful!  I would highly recommend indulging in the strawberry white cake, be it one slice to make your day or getting the whole cake for a party to share :).

So my wife was craving some 'Fries with Meat' with some modifications so I we went and I noticed they have a breakfast burrito special on Monday and Tuesdays get your favorite Breakfast Burritto for $5 rather than 5.95 or more. I like saving a buck when I can so this is great. Aside from that I had an Al Pastor breakfast burritto minues potatoes sub beans. It was awesome. Plus I love that typically Super Cucas doesn't charge extra for substitutions.

We recently had lunch here on a weekend. We ordered the grilled octopus as an appetizer and the chicken-shrimp fajitas as an entree. The appetizer was good, while the entree clearly showed that the kitchen staff had taken shortcuts. The chicken had been cooked separately - probably earlier - and then added to the rest of the sizzling food. This was evident both in the different temperatures of the items (chicken was warm, the rest hot) and in the texture and flavor. When the technique is not there, the whole restaurant suffers. The service was good, but the cooking standards left a bad impression.

This place Shucks the greatest oysters !  Fresh and has a variety low price and friendly Sevice, ask for Torin or Morgin very cool place for fresh shashimi and all fish lobster. Amazing a must try, also take the oysters anyplace near and eat, just bring the oyster ice tray back.  :) bring beer :) enjoy
Another delicious meal here...love visiting Santa Barbara...

Bluewater grill is a quintessential Santa Barbara spot - the location, ambience, food, drinks all scream Santa Barbara! A great place to impress out of town friends, a date, drinks with the girls, etc., etc..   Located on the corner of State and Cabrillo just across from the pier, you have a beautiful ocean view from the second floor patio.   The fish, shrimp, scallops on the fisherman's platter were battered perfectly - not greasy at all. Caesar salad is offered with anchovies but you can ask for it without. The coffee ice cream dessert was so delightful - a must try!  Parking in this pier / funk zone area can be a bit difficult so make sure you plan ahead when heading to this area and expect to either pay for parking or walk from a street parking spot.

I had never heard of milk and honey until a co worker recommended it. I decided to take my girlfriend out to dinner there. When I got there I was pleasantly surprised that the interior design is very luxurious and hip.    The ambiance was really upbeat and fun yet private and intimate due to the dim lighting. The restaurant seemed understaffed but the waitresses did an exceptional job. There were only 2 in the whole restaurant and they were hostesses servers and bartenders all at once. They're house red was great and my girlfriend really liked the cocktail she ordered. The first thing we order was an ahi tartare which was good but I've defiantly had better in Santa Barbara. Then we had a a Brie cheese dish which was amazing. We ordered scallops which were also good but not as good as some other places. Last we had a filet medallion plate which was also amazing. It came out to 107 after the tip but it was worth the money. This is a great place to go for dinner drinks or both

The views, beach vibe and food is amazing !!  Them mushroom garlic noodles - I can eat 3 orders to myself ! And the sushi is just so delicious !!  Everything we ordered in the menu was great, I will def be coming back.

The whole experience was A+.  We sat outside, split 3 apps, had cocktails and a bottle of Rose.  Trevor, our server was a professional.  Super nice, funny, and knows his wine!  We had the steak bites, the crab/artichoke dip, and the Orchard flatbread.  GET THE ORCHARD, it was a flavor explosion in my mouth:)  We will be back for sure!

I was really excited for this spot but then it just ended up being kind of disappointing! All the staff are very polite and helpful, but my partner and I went and ordered four things (and horchata, a quesadilla de Mercado, the chicken doraditos, and one of the ceviches) and it came out to be $40 and there was a very scarce amount of food. Also, the doraditos had no seasoning, I didn't taste any spices or salt in them, and the quesadilla had a piece of either broccolini or asparagus that was so fibrous I had to spit it out. The horchata had an almond milk instead of rice base, and although it could have been very good, the almond base instead just made it very mealy and unappetizing in texture. All in all, there are a lot of better, authentic Mexican restaurants in Santa Barbara that do this Street style type food, and the place just really is not worth the money spent unfortunately.

Went here for Margs with my friends (I'm 3rd wheelin' and was camera boy, but they bought my dinner) and was not disappointed! Got an enormous epic carnitas nachos, healthy salad, and our waitress (Elizabeth G) was really nice and was keeping the Diet Coke flowing for me (I'm designated driver). Doesn't have to be an expensive spot to get 5 stars.

Shellfish Company is a great place for crab and lobster on the wharf. They don't have much of a beverage selection but that's not what we go there for anyway. Their cioppino is awesome. Huge crab claw to top it off. You sign up on the waitlist outside the front door and the wait can be very long sometimes, but once you get seated, it's well worth it. Good times.

I'm happy to say my fiancé and I will be having our wedding reception at Cadiz! After my previous review, Messina, contacted me through Yelp and aplogized profusely for not getting in touch sooner. She called my fiancé and emailed him regarding the event details and menu options. I have not had the pleasure of speaking with her but she was very helpful over the phone and didn't hesitate to offer a tasting. We haven't scheduled a tasting yet but I'm looking forward to it! We'll be having several kiddos at the wedding and I'm so happy Messina also provided a kid-friendly menu. We haven't finalized all the details but I have a good feeling the evening will go off without a hitch!

I love this place for breakfast. So much so, that I've eaten here twice in the last week! Their poached eggs are delish, omelettes cooked and stuffed to perfection, and the huevos rancheros will cure any hangover (ok, not really). I do love the rocket fuel lattes, but they also serve fresh mint tea in a press. It's a lovely way to start the day! Also, the sunflower bread, which is only available Thursday-Saturday, makes for an excellent perch for an order poached eggs. I would give five stars but they don't have avocado for some strange reason (it's in Santa Barbara, geez) and they don't have almond milk as an option. Get with it guys! But that won't stop me from going back...like tomorrow too.

Since first visiting in the first rendition- the parking lot tiny space- this restaurant has always had the best sushi in SB and rivals NYC Nobu. In fact better- and affordable! The Black Cod IS better, try their signature Jalapeño Yellow Tail, salmon Carpaccio, for an elevated sushi experience or their fantastic creative rolls- Vampire Roll a fall spicy treat- not trick!  Their fish is the freshest- ALWAYS. Staff super nice and helpful. The Parklets are elegant and SAFE. I've posted photos- the location is out and above the too busy scene- .
I was pleasantly surprised. The food, service and place was nice on a weekday eve. Like it!

Cool bar on State Street! Great selection of beers and good selection of food too. Went there for their monthly comedy show and a word of advice - get there early to get a decent seat. Long tables to share with others although the seats are a big uncomfortable after a while. But that's okay. Now as for the giant screen TVs, one had the Lakers game and the other was showing Animal Planet! That was a great way to talk to other customers....cuz let's face it, you don't go to a bar and expect to see video of a jaguar killing an alligator!! Good place to interact with others and good for a group of people going together! And if you just want to hang, there are tons of board games!

The Baos solid 5! The pork fried rice 2.5 stars. We started with the pork belly fried rice. Didn't have much flavor but salt! It was too salty... and the pork wasn't tasty. Very disappointing, specially for $15. Then came our Bao trio, YUM! The bai bread was fresh & delicious. Each bao has different sauce which was great. You didn't feel like eating the same thing.  - The KFC was amazing, really good fried chicken & sauce.  - The shrimp was also delicious, really enjoyed that giant shrimp.  - The pork belly: the pork had great flavor, didn't care much for all the cucumbers. The portion of the meat was small in comparison to all the toppings. We loved them so much had to order a 2nd round. This time for KFC & shrimp again and Octopus instead of the pork.  - The octopus was also very delicious. The Baos can be very oily or have sauce. So it can be messy to eat. They need to provide better napkins and more of it.
Finally had the food here a month ago before departing SB. The food was excellent, and they honored the happy hour prices on food even though we sat down right before it ended.

This is a very welcome addition to the neighborhood! Staff is super friendly, albeit a bit nervous because they're still finding their footing. I had the Finney burger on a GF Bun (MUCH appreciated that they offer this!!) and it was absolutely delicious.  Loved the Passion iced tea. I'm not usually an iced tea gal, but my friend ordered it so I thought I'd give it a go. It was SO GOOD! Definitely get some!  Atmosphere is lovely. We sat outside. Nice to have something by Hotel Californian that doesn't charge $800 for lunch.  I love the menu. It's fun and creative and I want to try like 30 different items (including a Pickle appetizer that comes with apricot sauce)  Only thing that wasn't great were the fries. I had the shoestring fries and they came out pretty cold and without much flavor.  Welcome to SB, Finney's!
Nice new place in SB, fresh food, easy choices and lots of variety. Great place to grab a drink and a quick bite.

We have eaten here a couple times and the pizza is fantastic, best in town.  Unfortunately the last time was on a Saturday night and got there at 5PM and was told if we didn't take something precooked from the counter the wait would be one hour so we left.

Ok. I'll tell you what I like about here.  They have a Great Happy Hour! I don't know if most people are aware of the food here. Great Pizza since they have a great big pizza oven outside. $2 tri-tip tacos with super sauce and wings that are really tasty!  Service is good with Oscar tending bar and Christina taking care of tables. Pool tables Are a buck which is high but new felt so it's all good. On Monday they have trivia, pretty tough but it makes my brain move so that's good. We come over 3-4 times a month, fun spot that has live music on the weekends and overall is a fun spot!

Local flavor indeed. Cool little shops, a few eateries, cheap parking, right in the heart of things on the beach. Very enjoyable but beware of those loose boards if you are wearing heels. Yeah they warn you, but I swear there must be some women who don't pay attention.

I've been here several times. The food is great (I usually get the steak and my wife gets ciopino or some other seafood dish), the service is excellent, the views spectacular. The last time I went was on my birthday, and I was carded when I ordered a beer. The wife and I joked about me getting carded on my birthday and the waiter heard and we all had a good laugh. The lobster dinner was excellent. At the end of the meal they brought us a huge slice of mocha ice cream cake. It was unexpected, I hadn't been out to eat for my birthday since we usually cook something special at home. We thanked the waiter and enjoyed our decadent desert. When we got the check we were very surprised to find that they charged us for the cake. Once again, it was really good but totally unexpected because we didn't ask for any desert.  We had assumed that it would be free since when the waiter brought it out, he wished me a happy birthday.   Weird. Nice meal though. Don't assume anything is free, even if it's your birthday and you get something you didn't ask for.

So so good! Already thinking about my next visit here. They gave me a sample before I committed to my order and the barbacoa jackfruit was fire! Such a huge portion, probably can be shared by 2 people with normal appetites! Delicious toppings!
Cool place, nice vibe. Solid bar tenders. Main negative is the young crowd puking in the bathroom.

I have been here on a couple occasions. Overall I would say that my experience here has been positive! The food is delicious, BUT it is a little pricey for what you get. I would suggest one of the wet burritos because it's probably the most bang for your buck. The reason I took away one star is because the service was slow last time I went and the food isn't 'authentic' Mexican food. But it's still great! Would recommend coming here if you're visiting SB!

I give this 2 stars because I felt the service was amazing.   I only gave it 2 stars because I ordered the breakfast burrito #2 with Chili, and it came out steaming, but the inside was cold.  I really have a problem with cold food (when it is supposed to be hot).  I think I was doubly disappointed because it came out steaming and I was very excited to dig in to a nice hot breakfast.  When it was cold, I was quite surprised.  I had to take a few bites to believe it was really that cold when it looked so wonderfully hot.  I did order outside of my comfort zone, so perhaps I am not the best to judge this one.  It was very large, so if you're hungry I would think this would be a great order.  The server was exceptional and my coffee was very hot and full.  The place was packed and the tables turned over quickly.  The atmosphere is quite loud, but that is as expected for a place like this.  They have a very small parking lot which you may get in, but I feel like it is pretty hard to get out.  I parked at Ralph's since I was going to do some grocery shopping.  It is a short 1 block walk.    It is right next to the car wash, so an extra bonus would be to drop the car for a clean and run in for a bite.  I was wishing I had done that.

I've experienced better...at Palazzio's.  The boyfriend and I were cruising through SB around dinner time on our way to LA. He suggested we stop and asked me where would be a good place for dinner (UCSB is my alma mater)...Immediately I requested Palazzios! How long I've yearned for the buttery, garlicky rolls and the tasty, creamy butterflied shrimp in tequila sauce. Alas, the only thing that seemed to remain constant was the portion size (they're huge!).  My pasta, still on the menu, was so oily the noodles had become mushy. The rolls were hard and greasy. My boyfriend's lasagna was overcooked and hard. What a let down.  On the upside, service was nice and receptive. Next time I come to SB I think I'll venture elsewhere.

This store charges an illegal surcharge of 50 cents for purchases below $10 (CA Civil Code 1748.1).  I'm surprised with all the lawyers around that this wasn't brought to the store's attention earlier. The clerks won't necessarily tell you about the charge either.  Aside from this minor, but nagging irritant, the selection is good and the staff is wonderful.
The food is good... Just don't get it to go.  I was told 20-25 and I showed up at that time. Had to wait another 25 minutes. Lame.
What a deceptive place...food looks pretty promising on photos. But somehow they mis-execute the delivery of standard late night food badly. Meat is dry. Noodles bland.

I just tried their pork bahn mi sandwich and a bowl of beef pho.  The portions were generous and everything tasted great.  I look forward to trying their egg dishes and pizza.  Talk about an all encompassing menu!

This place has one of our favorite desserts in town! A warm cookie of your dough choosing in a cast iron skillet topped with your choice of McConnels ice cream. So many delicious combos to try. We love the vibe here.

My husband and have been here quite a few times, and it's only been open a few weeks!  Food is delicious, and great wine selection.  Everyone is so friendly!  It does get noisy with the music and crowd sometimes.  This is not your typical Indian fair.  It all has a delicious twist!  Also not great for families needing high chairs at the moment.  We were told they will be getting some.  Go in with an open mind, and enjoy yourselves!    Update:  we went again last night and it did not disappoint.  We sat at the bar and are always so happy!   Note: If you have a gluten allergy make sure to ask for the rice without the onion strings.  This is a new addition.

First time here for dinner and loved it, everything was amazing and great atmosphere too! Checked in at 8 for my reservation and they say it'll just be a minute, then a minute later one of the girls working the front says hmm, it looks like someone checked in with my reservation already! Some bastard stole my table! Seriously though, WTF whoever you are who did this, that was a dick move. Anyway, it took them a few minutes to get us another table but then were seated right away which was cool since they weren't planning on 2 tables. The food was amazing, started with the stone fruit and burrata, and the stone fruits in it had some great flavor and perfect ripeness. Then had the grilled asparagus with the hot Coppa, again great dish but could have used a little more of the Coppa but that's just because it was so tasty. Last had the crab and gnocchi, this one was seriously heavy but tasted great, just couldn't eat much after this since it was so heavy and rich. Finished it off with the chocolate ganache which had compressed melons and melon ice cream, another great dish. Overall it was so tasty I'll definitely be back, cocktails were good too, I had the California rattlesnake and liked it, could have used a little more booze in it but really when cant you?

This post is strictly for the breakfast I did not enjoy this weekend.  Food - mediocre.  There was nothing special about the items that we ordered.  We could have gotten those items (but delicious) elsewhere for half the price.  Service - lacking.  Nobody came back to ask how our meals were.  The waiter came over in our direction to help someone else and we asked for a refill on coffee but did not get it.  He simply forgot.  We had to wave him down to get another cuppa.  Ugh.  Ambiance - cute.  It's really a cute place.  If I was a tourist, I probably wouldn't mind that the food sucked and the service was horrible.

We came here Wednesday night with our 10 year old niece and had the best time! I don't think she and I really got in good conversations over our 'mmmm!'s the whole time. We started out with the burrata with focaccia and meatballs; the meatballs were good and slightly spicy, the burrata was out of this world. My husband got the pepperoni pizza which he ate the entire thing because it was SO good, my niece got the mushroom and loved it, I got the summer peach and now I want it every night. They surprised us with the orange zest olive oil cake and it was SO good, we attacked it and had it gone in a minute flat. The waitress was really great and kind!

We just went to Sunshine Cafe for first time, and we are so glad we did! The space has been beautifully renovated and is very comfortable. The service is off the charts for a small cafe, and the food is fantastic! We saw so many great things that it was hard to choose. My breakfast sandwich was just perfect and my wife chose one of their many scrambles. Can't wait to go back and enjoy more meals.

I love the vibe of this place. But beyond the vibe, their food is delicious. While I love all of their menu items I've tried so far, it's the ability to make your own creations that really pushes this to the top. All their ingredients are so delicious it's impossible to make a bad combination. See you there soon.

Had dinner at Fresco cafe tonight and it was amazing. Their deserts are absolutely delicious and so fresh. We also had the pleasure of meeting the very friendly and kind owner, Mark who was going out of his way to say hello to everyone in the restaurant. Wonderful place, people, and food!

I haven't come across anything I didn't like here.  Their regular and bonsai burgers are delicious.  The sweet potato fries are crispy and not greasy.  Onion rings are also good.  At $3.50 for a basic cheeseburger, it's not cheap, but I'd take one of these over two McDonalds double cheeseburgers any day.

Let me begin by saying FIND ELSEWHERE TO EAT. That is how upset I am with my experience today.   So frustrated having to write this poor review. I have been ordering shrimp tacos from Shoreline Cafe (restaurant and their shack) since I moved here two years ago. The shrimp are usually big and plump and grilled to perfection upon request.   Today I ordered my regular two shrimp tacos grilled instead of battered. Waiting in excitement to fulfill my craving, I receive my tacos. However, the shrimp in the tacos were small and comparable to fake shrimp you see in a prepared, sealed salad from the market. One of the tacos was battered and the other was 'grilled.' I took them back to the boy who took my order and demanded to know what kind of shrimp I received and to grill them.   Ten minutes later, a man approaches me looking very hostile and explaining that one of the tacos was grilled. I insisted I asked for both grilled and they not only appeared raw, but one was battered, and they were NOT THE USUAL SHRIMP I HAVE ALWAYS HAD. The unknown and nameless man simply wearing a polo shirt explained that the shrimp was different and they were using it 'just for today.'   He brought out two more 'grilled' nasty imitation shrimp tacos and continued to make me feel like I was unnecessarily complaining.   This place is spiraling to disaster. Poor management and lazy, female workers whining and asking 'when can I go home' is not a desirable staff for a restaurant in a perfect location on the beach. I will not be coming back. Still heated from this experience.

Very disappointed in my overall experience. Why on earth would anyone serve pickled ginger with artificial sweetener? Maybe I'm the only person in the world who has an extreme distaste for artificial sweetness, but my taste buds are ruined. I wish I had read the unfavorable reviews before we went. But this sealed the deal that I will never go there again.

Really flavorful and interesting food. I got the vegetable curry. It was delicious and had a great depth of flavor. It was a bit oily, but not over-the-top. My friend ordered the ming bean noodle dish. It was really good as well, the flavor was a bit more tamed. It had a nice garlicky taste and the vegetables in it were delicious, especially the mushrooms which were perfectly sautéed. The pickled vegetables condiment was really nice and reminded me of the super salty pickled lime skin you get with some types of Indian cuisine. Great place - very vegan and vegetarian friendly.
Cajun popcorn, lemon drop martini, and the crawfish pasta BUT u MUST get the bread pudding soufflé!!! This place is a treat!! I LOVE the environment and the friendly staff.
The food here is absolutely incredible! Anything you get will be great, but be sure to try the calamari! By far the best I've had

Arrived there Sunday about 1:30 or 2 with an Older lady friend of mine and her service dog. We were greeted by a very nice young lady and she asked to see the dogs service certificate. We produced it and she led us to where are table was to be. In the aisle we were accosted by What I assume to be the Manager...He was bee lining towards us and asked the Hostess if she had checked the dogs ID, she responded yes, and he demanded to see it once again. While making us stand there in the aisle looking once again for the ID and Iasked if this was neccessary 'Again' he looked up and rudly said 'Yes' nothing else. He then said you will have to sit in another section...I asked if she wanted to just leave but she had her heart set on a Lobster and the view. The Lobster was Good, Service was So So...No place for pur Shells, When we improvised they took it away and replaced it with nothing. We were asked if we wanted more bread but never brought any....little things but I seemed to notice more after 'Mr Charm Free' Management style got us worked up. Wrong career choice.

Their wonderfully tasty Korean food makes this a unique must try spot!!! First please know they are practicing the health guidelines and wear a mask. I was there 7/24/2020 (& multiple times during this shutdown). Ignore Susan J.'s comment--it is NOT credible. They all wear masks, have plexiglass barrier at the counter. I've never seen them 'grab customer's hands' in all of the years I've gone there.  I call total b.s. on her posting!   A very sweet older couple run the restaurant and store.  Hands down, their Korean food is authentic and top-notch quality (fresh) and tasty. Super yummy. It's on par with top restaurants in LA's Koreatown. Bibimbap is a fav dish of mine and I was happily very surprised by how great theirs was.  The grocery store has got nice staple items and snack items are really unique--try the honey butter chips! And the authentic Korean refrigerated beverages complement the food well :)

Its more of a 3.5 but the service was excellent so I gave it a 4 stars. This place is close to my parents so we went there to celebrate my dad's birthday. I remember when they first opened the restaurant and have been there for lunch and dinner which was pretty good. This time we went for breakfast which was a first for me.   I ordered the breakfast burrito and added bacon. It was quite tasty. I liked that it already came with avocado instead of paying extra for it. My husband on the other hand had the pasilla scramble and it had no flavor what so ever. The tri tip was full of fat and the roasted peppers must have been soaked in water because there was no flavor. We were both bummed about it since it sounded really good on the menu. The pancakes were good which my hubby said he will go back for that.   My dad ordered the Lorraine scrambled and mom ordered the turkey burger. The burger looked really good but my mom ate it all before I can get a bite. A good sign that it was good.

I think this place has potential and I thought it was pretty good overall but nothing is calling me to come back anytime soon.  The best parts for me were the ambiance and the service. The decor and overall vibe were great. The service was very friendly and helpful.  What I didn't like was the price/portion ratio. The food was good/fine but not large enough portions or high enough quality for what they were charging. We also ordered a cheese platter which came with brie and honey and yet wasn't served with crackers or anything to put those on. We didn't even have a knife served to us. We were left wondering if we should spoon the brie into our mouths? It just seemed like the plate wasn't completely thought through.  I must say though that we asked for crackers and some were brought to us free of charge, which was nice.  All in all, I think Mosto Crudo is OK and has potential but I'm not planning to go back in the short term.

I'm 109% sure our waitress was hungover (or maybe just super tired?!) because she kept slurring her words, and she accidentally poured more champagne in our non-bottomless mimosas. I'm not complaining (of course) - just thought it was funny :)   Anyways, this is a lovely little place for brunch with friends!  Came here around 11am for brunch on Easter Sunday! Outdoor seating was all taken up (the views are great & the shading is perfect), but there were a lot of open tables inside. We took a seat inside because we didn't want to wait.   What we got:  1) Bello Benedict - The only thing that made this dish unique was the pesto, which made everything incredibly delicious! I NEED this pesto recipe! 2) Pesto Mushroom Omelet - I had a bite of my friend's, and it was pretty solid! Again, the pesto saved the plate. Otherwise it would be pretty mediocre.  3) MIMZ! (Mimosas). Pretty pricy considering ours had more OJ than champagne. But it was still good!  My friend is vegetarian, and she very much appreciates the many options they have for her!   Service was friendly but incredibly slow considering there weren't even a lot of customers. There was a family of about 8-9 next to us that didn't get their food until we were about to leave (and they were seated before we were).

It was our first and last time at this VERY casual (some diners weren't even wearing shoes) again the ambiance was very casual but the prices were high end...way to expensive for the execution of the food. The service was so slow, the food was inedible for me, the tacos had so much cumin I couldn't eat them.  The falafels had too much cumin and were hammered and so was the burger... w/ the strangest ketchup substitute... beet and clove purée, eeek!  what's wrong with straight up ketchup!  The polenta and sautéed veg was a good idea but it definitely needed salt and a cream substitute... oh and some flavor!  This place has a Michelin star??  It's hard to believe that.

Quick summary: strong 4 stars on the food / low 1 star on the people and service and care.  I've been enjoying the food at Goa Taco for some time.
