My favorite is the duck paratha, and my vegetarian friends love the paneer paratha as well.  The concept of using paratha for a taco is fabulous.  Eating at the restaurant is fine enough - it's a simple dive on State Street.  A bit of indoor eating, a few tables outside.  Nothing to rave about but nothing to complain about either.  But the conscientiousness of the people who work there is so lacking that it's a very iff'y experience.  I've used Grub Hub to order their food for dinner parties three times now, and 2 of the 3 times one person at my table goes hungry.  The people at Goa are either so spaced or so lacking in care that one entire order is left out.  If this had happened just once, ok - mistakes happen.  But when it happens 2 out of 3 times - no, that points to problems.  They also are so cheap that they put multiple orders into the same to-go container, even combining vegetarians options with fish entrees (which my veggie purist friends hate). So this makes for not only an unpleasant evening but a hassle the next day to call Grub Hub and get the missing meal refunded.  I'd call them to place the order myself and go pick it up, but they absolutely never answer the phone.  Really.  I've tried this repeatedly.  So if I like their food why don't I just eat in at the restaurant?  Well I do, but I have to be prepared for experiences I've had there like being ignored by the person taking the order for 4-5 minutes before looking up from their display to say hello or offer help (even though no one was ahead of me and the restaurant was hardly busy.  Or the time I waited nearly half an hour (again, restaurant half full), then asking about my order and finding they had just spaced out on it.  Get it together Goa - you have so much to offer.

Love the place. Sometimes I just get a bunch of appetizers because they're quite filling. Not sure I spell it right but my favorite is the Coxinha. My sister loves the cheese balls so you can try that if you're a cheese lover too.

They've got fabulous salsa, tacos al pastor and delicious sopes. I've never had a bad meal here. Burritos are great as well. Lots of Latinos eating in there, which is a great sign for any Mexican joint. We love this place.   Parking is a little tricky, but locals are used to that everywhere.

Twice now I've been to the Hoffman Brat Haus and have been moderately pleased, the service is pretty impersonal, however, since they have an 'order at the front then find a seat of you can' system and then have the nerve to place a tip jar in your face. That aside, the food is good. Some of the options are exotic sounding but lack a flavor to match the name, I ordered the beer brat the first time and never really tasted any malt or hops but it was good, next time I ordered the rattlesnake and rabbit one on the exotic list but still it tasted far from exotic. This place has some good beers and the food is good I just wish it lived up to the flavors they claim to have. The spicy brats aren't really spicy either, gotta pour horseradish Dijon mustard on things to get a bite. Seating can be sparse or too few people taking up large party sized tables.
best Japanese food in town! We ordered a bit of everything and every dish was amazing! the price is a bit on the higher side but it's woth the quality of ingredients

One of my favorite spots when I'm in SB. Amazing food.I usually come here after the farmer market on Tuesday's. My go to is any steak. Tried them all and they are all great. Also the shrimp plate, and drinks are good. Seating takes a while but that's expected since people like myself like to enjoy their food. Plus the food is good and always busy. I recommend you go as soon as they open, I think 5pm. That's the time I'm seated fast. Definitely recommend and I will continue to visit this place.
Backstage is the best place for a good time and a fun staff. Great drink menu and awesome entertainment. Dueling Pianos is the best show on state.

Come for the food - stay for the atmosphere. There is something on the menu for everyone at The Black Sheep. The food is inventive, but not pretentious. The blend of Spanish, Asian and French style foods are fun and create unique dishes and flavors that you won't find anywhere else. Everything I have ever tasted has been delicious. I also appreciate that I regularly see the chef shopping for ingredients at the local Farmer's Markets, so I know the food is sustainable, fresh, and made from quality ingredients. All this set in a cozy bistro-style setting that will let you relax and sip wine for hours.

Came here last week after my friends got married at the SB Courthouse. We had the whole upper room and the service was great. The wine kept flowing and this was lunch time on a Wednesday, so you know it was a good day!  They had a shortened menu for us to choose from and made sure to keep the us filled up with delicious bread and butter.  I went with the trout salad and I was very happy with my decision! It was fresh, flavorful, and plenty.

I have never been able to figure out how this place isn't on everyone's hit list of SB eateries.  The food (Italian in nature - salads, pizza, sandwiches, etc) is reasonably priced, homemade with care, and most importantly - totally f'ing delicious!    They do take-out as well.  I realize it's cliche, but this truly is a hidden gem awaiting discovery. Give it a go!

Good pizza.  My wife and kids thought it was great, but I'm more particular.  It was good, but the sauce was missing something.  I had to salt it and dump a bunch of Parmesan and pepper flakes on it.  It is on the pricey side for pizza.

Great margarita pizza. Fresh sauce and real mozzarella cheese. Salads are good too.   Check in on Yelp to get a 10% discount.  The kids like to watch the food getting made.  They have just about every soda known to man.

I have been a fairly regular Fishouse happy hour customer for years. The happy hour eats are decent  enough & the wine is OK for the price. Today was my first foray into the lunch menu & was I ever disappointed. I ordered the fish sandwich and it was awful, as in take one bite and send it back awful (something I have never done before). For about $13 there was +/- 1 ounce of dry grilled fish on each half with a piece of wilted lettuce in between 2 pieces of dry toast. I am sad a fish gave it's life for this. Fishouse take note: PLEASE take this off your menu stat. I would rather have had a PB&J (and actually ended up eating a Kind bar from my purse). Because of a) the decent happy hour, b) my mom seemed to enjoy her scampi, & c) the waitress was nice (took the sandwich away & deducted it from the bill) I gave it 3 stars.

I'm passionate about breakfast sandwiches. I order them whenever I see them on a menu. With that said, I just had the best breakfast sandwich of MY LIFE. Chipotle aioli, local grilled sourdough, avocado, arugula. Perfect spot for a laid back brunch. With McConnell's ice cream for dessert.  Also, their buttermilk fried chicken sandwich is also fantastic.

Scrupulously fresh and well prepared fish.  Pleasant informal atmosphere. Best place in SB for fish preparations for both lunch and dinner too.  Fish is Knowingly sourced with attention to sustainability.
First visit to Mesa Burger and we were not disappointed! The Mesa Burger with bacon was cooked perfectly. Truffle fries were a treat. A bit pricey, as two burgers and fries with tax and tip was $35!

I've been here a few times and have never been anything but happy with the food and service.  I dine here with the fam and everyone loves it.  I usually order from the breakfast plates.  But I hear the steak is fantastic.  The red beans and rice won't disappoint you either and their pancakes are nice too.

To be honest, I had really high expectations for this place since I heard it talked about quite a bit. I was in general pretty underwhelmed by the food. The service was good. the line was really long but we got our order in quickly and received it quickly with no issues. We ordered the large guac (which was $10 for a small scoop), the Arnold parmando aqua fresca and two tacos (the octopus and steak ones). The guac imo was underseasoned. The avocado itself was ripe and creamy but it really needed some acid and salt, especially since the chips werent seasoned either. The aqua fresca is good but I would not recommend it if you don't like ginger.  My main issue was with the tacos. The tortillas were really thin and flaky, which would have been good if the fillings weren't so runny. My tacos were falling apart as soon as I got them in my hands. It also has the issue of too many things on one taco where all the flavors kind of just get muddled and taste like mush. The shrimp and octopus was completely overwhelmed by the peanut which mostly tasted like oil. My steak taco tasted fairly blah considering it is steak and I love steak. O well, at least they have a good axxess deal.

Great place recommended by a friend Julien. Awesome vegan food experience with a very attentive bar tender. Great cocktails and awesome tap beer selection. Atmosphere is very chill and laid back can't wait to be back..

Everyone loves this place.....Except me. This is a corporate, generic, glorified bar with excellent food and I imagine great drinks with a very boisterous crowd of successful over grown frat bros and fortune hunters. My husband noted that we could be in almost any fancy airport dimly lit airport bar/restaurant in Dallas, Houston or Atlanta. I had to laugh because he was right.  The service is wonderful however and what little on the menu is all good.  I've only had the deviled eggs, the Taboulleh (sp) Salad and the Roast Beef Dip. People love the latter but I was not that impressed and never really loved 'dips' in the first place.  The menu is brief. I wanted to love this place because its open till late (for this town) and just down the street from home.  Too Orange County (Texas?) feeling for my taste but that seems to be the unfortunate direction for everything in Santa Barbara.

Great ambience and menu. Bartenders are fun and can shake up some delicious libations. We always have fun coming here, trying something new, and leaving satisfied and inspired. Great staff and service.
Great burgers cooked as asked! Great service! And plenty of TV's to watch sports! And it's not a la carte.

An update from my previous review... The manager called me and she is fabulous. She felt horrible about all the bad experiences we had at The Project. We talked through the whole thing and she made it clear they are doing their best to correct where they fall short.  We went back from dinner within the last couple weeks. The food was fabulous (as always) and the manager took great care of us.  They do not deny that they still have work to do on their service side - but they are a great local restaurant that we will continue to support.
This place is very delicious. The pork belly sandwich is awesome and so is the SB burger.
YUM!  Wonderful atmosphere, friendly, attentive staff and absolutely delicious food. Alcohol selection and coffee a huge plus. Loved our experience here.

I had dinner here on a recent road trip. We sat out on the front patio warmed by the heaters. We shared the buratta with grilled peaches and prosciutto, a delicious savory and sweet combo. The Shrimp in Vodka sauce and the Chicken Marsala, which was the best ever, hands down!! Not a morsel left, even after a Caesar salad!   Our server Miz was attentive and pleasant.Everything was so good, I  whole heartedly recommend Mizza, if you're ever in Santa Barbara.

OOOH. MY. GOSH!!!!!!!  I love brunch a whole bunch, and all I have to say about Tupelo Junction Cafe is...  BEST. BRUNCH. EVER... UMPH.  I don't mean to hype this place up, but this is definitely one of the best brunch spots I've ever been to in my life... and I wasn't born just yesterday. I EFFING LOVE BRUNCH!  During a little getaway to Santa Barbara, I patiently waited all weekend for the moment when I would be able to bite into some delicious brunch from Tupelo Junction; how I didn't go mad, we'll never know (hint: alcohol). We came here on a Monday morning, so there was absolutely no wait at all. SCORE because Grumpy Morning Jennifer really hates waiting in long lines!  As soon as we sat down, we were served ice water and orange juice in cute little Mason jars, which I thought were really adorable (go ahead and call me a hipster--I don't care). I was really enamored with the whole look and feel of the restaurant: from the wooden tables/chairs to the red brick/white slat walls to the little flowers on the tables. The atmosphere was very inviting and welcoming, and our waitress was very warm and friendly; there is a very homey feel to this place.  We started with some cinnamon apple beignets, which came sitting atop some crème anglaise sauce and were dusted with powdered sugar. The inside was warm and dense (kinda like cake), while the outside was golden brown and crispy (yummmm, fried foods). The apple and cinnamon flavors definitely shined through without making the entire thing too sweet. I was actually expecting the beignets to be lighter and fluffier, but we still enjoyed them, nonetheless... seeing as how we hammered through those fried little balls as if they were about to roll away.  I had a hard time deciding what to order for brunch, but I ended up going with the Dungeness crab cake & potato hash, which comes topped with avocado salsa, a poached egg, and beurre blanc sauce. Toasted baguettes are served alongside this dish. OMG WOW HOLY COW, my mind was adequately blown to bits as soon as I took the first bite; it was something that I have never tasted before, and it was effing delicious. This is how I imagine heaven to taste like.  The crab cake came loaded with nice, big chunks of real crabmeat, and it tasted amazingly fresh. Don't you worry your little head about them skimping on the good stuff--the cakes come with very little fillers here, so every bite comes packed with meat. The well-fried exterior gives the cake a nice little crunch. I thought the potatoes were okay; they're flavorful, but I was expecting them to be more crispy since they're in a hash. The avocado salsa tasted freshly made with juicy chunks of tomatoes sprinkled throughout; it was very refreshing to eat with the crab cake. Some people may complain that there's too much avocado in the dish (WHAT THE EFF IS THIS TOMFOOLERY?), but I freaking love avocado and could probably eat every single one in California if I could. The poached egg was perfectly runny and yummy, and the way the yolk so expertly mixed in with everything just seriously made my day. Ugggh, something about perfectly cooked runny eggs just makes my eyes roll to the back of my head as I let out some incoherent gurgling. Currently drowning in a pool of saliva as I type this up, FYI.  I'm not even embarrassed to admit that I inhaled the entire thing within minutes of it arriving to the table; it was one of the best brunches I've ever had, and I would order it again in a heartbeat. I'm not even kidding when I say that I often daydream about stuffing my face with ten orders of these crab cakes.  Remember to say yes to the Dungeness!

First time here, and I'm from LA, so finding a bomb Lil spot to get a fire breakfast Burrito  in my favorite Lil getaway town of Santa Barbara is essential   thanks for an amazing breakfast and easy location with parking,  friendly staff and quality food!!! Don't have a picture because we ate them, but you will be in my bookmarks for every time I come up!!

Amazing!!! Large portions, great flavor, great variety and food options. I order delivery almost 5 times a week and it's always amazing. Top quality, always fresh. And they are pretty fast. Jose is usually my delivery guy and he's always super nice. Junior is the best, he called to make sure my order was correct when I made a typo on Sb menus. I LOVE this place!!!!! Recommended 10/10

Every step the chef makes I can stand and watch and order. Scarily generous with the ingredients. I ordered something with marinara base, sausage, pepperoni, soppressata, provolone and garlic (an onion?), and added tomato dices and mushroom to it. Allow me to say OMG. And I'm usually a 'Hmmm' or 'Wow' person--far from OMG.   Super friendly service, too. You can see their oven and all the ingredients there while you make pizza, insalata, and more.
Had the swordfish (blackened), tried the salmon and the Parmesan crusted sole. All were delicious!! Sides that come with are bomb too. Must try!

I must say I had bigger and better expectations for this spot. The atmosphere was great!! We were able to sit outside right by the fountain and be mesmerized by the turtles that swam around and jumped off rocks. We were helped pretty fast and the menu items look great! I choose the shrimp fajitas and my boyfriend choose the ahi tacos. We were able to enjoy some chips and salsa while  we waited, to my surprise the salsa was beyond delicious! It was a perfect blend of spice and sweet. While we were waiting we sat there with some empty glasses for a while before we could either flag someone down or before they were refilled along with that we weren't offered a refill on the chips.  But not long into waiting  the waitress dropped off the tacos, and just the tacos. If there's one thing that is awkward is when you go out to eat is when you are sitting there and one person in your party of 2 gets their food prior to you, so they have to sit there and just stare at their food while yours shows up fashionable late. I was really staring at the tacos while waiting and noticed that the portions were very small, the ahi tacos seemed to be the portion of an appetizer not only in the size of the actual taco but the portion of the food. I would understand this method or tiny food if this was an out of body and mind taste of flavors from these ahi tacos, but sadly were not. Again in fashion my fajitas decided to roll up, they came out sizzling and ready to be eaten. So I dig in, I grab a corn tortilla to notice that the corn tortillas were cold, so that's gross, but I didn't dare to complain instead endured my chilly tortilla and continued to make a fajita taco. The side plate that was served with the fajitas were, beans, sour cream, guacamole and red rice. I tried the beans and they tasted as if they were straight out of a can, and the rice matched my cold tortillas. The shrimp fajitas weren't bad, but again nothing to write home about. If you go, maybe eat prior? But you gotta sit by the cool turtles!

If your looking for a great outdoor seating and good sandwich to eat this place has good sandwich and burgers. Have not ventured outside of that but if the rest of the food is anything like the burgers you will not go wrong! Place is right on the corner and fairly priced.

Let's face it. 2020 has been a terrible year. Any amount of normalcy I can find is definitely welcomed. So when I took that first step onto Stearns Wharf I was transported to my childhood in San Diego and all was good again.  Stearns Wharf has been a Santa Barbara staple since 1872 (!!!). It's survived time, weather and even fires! You can drive your car on it and park here if you want. I walked it and I'm 1000% sure it's totally safe but hearing those cars roll out on the wood....I'd have been nervous lol. There are 141 parking spots and the first 90 minutes is free before hitting $2.50/hour with a maximum fee of $20. Extremely reasonable IMO.   On the pier itself you can fish, shop, eat and watch what's happening on the water. There's also the Sea Center (aquarium) which unfortunately you can't go inside during these times of COVID. That said, the cool breeze, smell of the sea, beautiful scenery and the total history where you're standing on are well worth your time. Highly recommended!
This place is terrific! There guacamole is fresh made and the tortillas are made from scratch. All of the chicken here is white meat. The employees here are nice and friendly.

This place is incredible!!! Such great value. I used to frequent their location on Camino real. And then absolutely loved YUME on lower chapala. They have recently moved to a great location close to the public market. The fish is cut beautifully and is by far the best value in town. Bravo Sushi Bar 29! I hope the locals find you before it is too late.

Pretty good breakfast, friendly and helpful staff, and a good location. My over medium fried eggs, potatoes and turkey bacon were all well prepared and of excellent quality. Despite being there during peak breakfast time our food was out in a timely fashion. Nice outdoor seating, good traditional breakfast place with Santa Barbara flair.

I had high hopes for Santa Barbra Public Market.  Having been to many of the major food halls I've seen how great this concept can be.    My initial impressions is that it's a cool place to visit.  There are the things you come to expect from a food hall like: a bar, ice cream place, coffee and other eateries.   Walking around there were only a few places that I would like to try.  There was a ramen place I believe that was really popular, a Mexican food place and ice cream place seem to be the most popular, my wife and daughter tried the ice cream and said it was really good.   My only con was that there were so few options to try.  We only ended up trying the ice cream place and I got a beer at The Garden.  Mind you, we came here AFTER going out to dinner.  I believe this food hall is relatively new, so it may take some time before they get additional unique tenants, so for now I see the potential.  I look forward to seeing this place evolve.

This is where it's at for all things shellfish! The name doesn't lie ;) Get the rock shrimp ceviche with chips and enjoy it on their outdoor patio that overlooks the Channel Islands and sits on the ocean. Super casual atmosphere indoor and out that just can't be beat for seafood.
Came here with a small party and we had a really good time.  The food is really good and so is the atmosphere.  Would definately come back!  The pad thai was amazing as well as the stir fry tofu!

Fundamentals like black beans and homemade tortillas are a substantial cut above at Los Arroyos, in comparison with more mass market Mexican establishments.  However the price point is just too high for me to make frequent revisits--The tacos a mano dish includes two small soft tacos, beans, and rice.  With the chicken option, this will set you back $11.50.  No extra vegetables included, and only a very meager amount of cheese.  A more economical option is to takeaway order a few sides--such as a single taco and a side of beans--and supplement it with some of Santa Barbara's finest produce at home!

Delicious, healthy food you can taste!  I love eating out when it is good food!!  All the ingredients were fresh! Staff is all very friendly and enjoys being there!  I am excited to go back when I am in town next!  I got 2 desserts too, which I normally do not do since most are too sweet, not here!   I had the vegetable stir fry and go the apple pie and banana bread of the day.

Tomato, mozerella, and pesto sandwich on focaccia bread was pretty good.  The ingredients we're fresh but the focaccia bread tasted more like a low cal version.   I didn't see that beautiful indentations on top that is a vehicle to hold pockets of olive oil or sprinkles of dried herbs that forms a thin layer over the bread that it's known for.  Nevertheless​ it's was fresh and  soft which was appreciated. Most disappointing was the tomato soup.  Soon as I saw the vivid orange color of the soup I knew that carrots were used to stretch it.  I wanted that rich red color of tomatoes with it's tangy sweet heartyness with each bite.  Was disappointed I didn't get that.

We come here often with the kids for the simple reasons of convenience and being near the water.  Today, however, I must say I'm more than disappointed with the martini order.  The first drink came out looking like half a Martini, so I mentioned something to the waitress, Haley, and she explained it was not a full drink due to the fact I had asked for no Vermouth. Surprising response and one I haven't heard before.  Against my girlfriends' advice I asked Haley, our waitress,   for another and to please make it a full Martini if possible. She said she would talk to the bar tender.  The picture I posted with this review is what came out afterwards.  Very disappointed and not typical of shoreline, but thought I would warn future martini goers :). 3/4' down from the rim.   Not cool.
Can't beat the price! I had a full breakfast for $5! The food & service were both good. Positive: Price Negative: Small inside & very busy!

The ramen is really good! The soup wasn't too slaty and I loved that the noodles weren't too soft; it was the perfect texture. The service was excellent as well. I went around dinner time so there were other customers in the restaurant, but we were seated immediately! The waiters are constantly refilling water and making sure everything is good so A+ for customer service!

I am posting a second review just to recommend the Pizza Radicchio. Toppings are Mozzarella, stracciatella cheese, Gorgonzola, red radicchio, amaretti cookie crumbles, honey. The interplay of the bitter radicchio and funky blue cheese against the sweetness of the honey and cookie crumbles, all underscored and balanced by the chewy crust and mozzarella, is just heavenly. I don't think I can look at other pizzas the same again. Thank you!

Came here right at 9pm when they opened and there was no line (I'm such a loser I know haha!). Located as part of Velvet Jones (a entertainment/club/bar venue) and you order via the window on the right side of the venue. You order, pay, and pick up right at the window. You either enjoy eating this here where they offer a few tables and chairs (not much) or take it to-go (which they can wrap your order in foil). I can definitely see this place as a hot spot for a late-night snack or comfort food...I really enjoyed my order and would totally come back again!!  SERVICE: Order was served in a timely manner and the guy who took our order was really cool to talk to! Appreciate how they arrived a little before opening time to cook the macaroni, so they are ready to serve orders right away when they open.   HOURS: 9pm-2:45am on Thursday, Friday, and Saturday evenings.  PARKING:  Free parking for 75 mins nearby in the structures. Each hour or part of an hour after the initial 75 minutes is $1.50. Metered street parking also available, which can be free oftentimes later in the evening.   FRITO PIE MAC: This comes with fritos, chili, and mac n cheese. I added HOT CHEETO crumbs for an additional $1. LOVED this so much! Their fritos are larger than the usual ones. The fritos add for a perfect textural crunch and goes perfect with the chili and mac n cheese. I love hot cheetos and loved how the crumbs weren't stale (some places add stale crumbs and it's like #whytho). This would have been good with or without the added hot cheetos. Ordered a small ($8) and it was good enough to share between 2 people.   ---HELPFUL TIP: I recommend putting the small order into a large empty container (offered on the side by the hot sauces) for a cleaner eating experience. The hot cheetos crumbs toppled over the side whenever a bite was scooped out when we first dug into it.
Went by because the Asian bartender from Eos recommended Melissa here. The hype was lived up to. Went for prime rib night. One of the best deals in town. Beers were on point as well.

We got in late and needed a quick bite to eat. This was the spot. This is your standard Mexican fare but done very well. No Tex Mex. No trendy nouevau Mexican food. But very very tasty. It was so good, it almost made me wish I was drunk because I know how good that greasy Mexican food is on a stomach full of beer. Price is not cheap but you won't break the bank either.
Highly recommend. Happy to have found Himalayan Kitchen. The staff were great. The food is greatI! The korma was my favorite. This place really made my birthday special.
Cute place. Coffee is good but I've had better. Employees are always super sweet and easy to talk too! Not the full stars because it's a little pricey.
Certainly packed when we were there. The veggie bagel seemed to be the star for the family

Sat on the patio, listening to their beautiful harpist. Tried the linguini & meatballs as well as the Fettucini Carbonara, both very tasty!! Our waitress was very attentive, refilling our drinks as needed. Gave a good description of a couple dishes when I asked her opinion. We'd definitely come back!
Delicious food, good prices. A lot of healthy and vegetarian options. Their tofu is delicious. They have two large patios for outdoor dining and it's usually not crowded.
great food and service. Old school vibe but really good food.

Excellent service! What a nice man. He greeted with a smile and helped me decide on my dish.   Service was prompt.   Food was excellent and fast. We could hear the chef stir frying the dishes fresh for us.   We had the Crispy Walnut Shrimp and the Seafood Chow Fan. The chow fan had a large mixture of seafood. Both excellent and a large quantity.   Great stop along Coast Village Road!

I usually don't get steak at a pasta place but this place has an amazing steak.  That's all I get.  The panna cotta is so good. I went with 2 friends and we ordered 2 to share among the 3 of us. If we weren't so full, we probably would have ordered one per person. I'm not a huge dessert person and I like the panna cotta because it's not super sweet and it comes with a beautiful balsamic sauce.   I would like to ding it half a star because the tables are REALLY close together. When I got with one other person and we have to sit next to another 2-person table, it almost feels like we are a party of 4.

I'm giving them 5 stars for their turkey burger alone. It was soooo juicy (which is hard for a turkey burger), and it just tasted so good with the fresh veggies and good bread.  I had the fish tacos as well, which left a bit to be desired... the sauce making it super soggy. I also had a tofu bowl which was veggie packed, again with fresh veggies.

The 'Snacks' over priced and meh.... not impressed at all.. We did not have dinner, so maybe we still don't see what all the hype is about.   Specialty drinks were ok. Perhaps , it may warrant another visit, but the price... Mostly hype, not about the traditional Spanish flavors. Really disappointed....  2 stars because the decor was nice.

Loved your truffle fries they were so awesome and had a glass of wine while i waited for  my burgers but was disappointed in them i had the teriyaki burger i had no flavor boo hoo i was really looking forward for a good burger was a long day at wk with no lunch...

3.5 stars.  The clam chowder, ceviche, and oyster shooter were good.  The lobster taco was ok.  Service was sketchy. They forgot my water and then brought my soup before silverware. It took a long time for them to come ask if we were ready for the check.
Can't resist to their pasta!! Squid ink is my fav. Love the Spaghetti Cabarana too. Flatbread was alright. Just don't miss the pasta.

Looking for a later evening place that's open in Santa Barbara AND serves food can be sometimes a bit tough!  After the opera's Friday evening performance, one in our group recommended Opal's up the street, and we were not at all disappointed!   We were quickly welcomed, and enjoyed a relaxing time, with a cocktail each, and a menu item.  My friends ordered desserts, and I ordered the warm seafood salad ... delicious!!  I'll definitely aim to remember Opal's again for another later night (or other time) option!

This is not the old Farmer Boy, and that's a good thing.  Back in the day, I was served sour orange juice and cottage cheese past it's prime.  Now, ingredients are fresh, flavorful and service is snappy.  Worth mentioning: Chilequiles are very good.  I think I could make a case that Farmer Boy's Chilequeles are some of the best in town if you like carnitas (pork) with them.  While I think the Boat House chilequeles win for presentation & visual appeal, their chilequeles are lacking in the pork department.  Farmer Boy is more generous with the pork.  The salsa has a slight tang that I like.  Will definitely have them again.  One minor issue, poached eggs ordered soft, one was medium one was firm.  Not a big deal on a busy day, but a little room for improvement there.  Service was prompt & courteous; the staff truly works well as a team, they all seem to have each other's back.  Our server Nise was on point during our busy Saturday breakfast.  When a large party was taking her time, another server helped deliver drinks & kept things moving.  The family that runs this joint (along with Brophy's) knows service & good food keep people coming back.  I have one suggestion for improvement.  The place is LOUD when full.  The back room has an acoustic panel on the ceiling to help attenuate noise; unfortunately it's not enough.  I suggest changing the artwork to prints on acoustic panels vs. the epoxy alumninum or fiberglass photo prints... although the artwork is pretty, it makes everyone's voice ricochet off the walls and for me, it's unpleasantly noisy for this diner on a busy day.    It's good to see this restaurant back in a much 'new and improved' state.

It's fast casual here.  Order at the counter. Seat yourself. Salsa bar has a healthy sampling of many different types of salsa.  My friend and I shared the following:  -nachos ($9.75): the tortilla chips are freshly baked, black beans, and smothered with melted Monterey Jack cheese. It's served with guacamole, sour cream and pico de gallo. This was ENORMOUS. ENORMOUS. ENORMOUS. This appetizer could literally feed four hungry college students. You can't really mess up on nachos unless you skimp out on the toppings, but it was good here.  -agaves burrito ($13.50): halibut + shrimp, roasted red peppers, onions, Monterey Jack cheese, lettuce + guac topped with chipotle sauce served with a side of rice and beans. And to my recollection, there was rice in the burrito. Eh, the first few bites where good, but theeeeeennn it was too much. That chipotle sauce impeded on everything and everyone on my palette roster. It was simply too much.   I thought it was okay here. I wasn't impressed by anything I ate, but it wasn't bad.

You drive on a private, secluded road up to this Mediterranean castle-like resort. Luxury cars are parked on each side of the circular driveway and you pass a grand fountain that looks so beautiful lit up at night. The lobby is stunning with an enormous arrangment of fresh flowers. We had an escort drive us around in a gold cart to our room and we were able to see the grounds at night. What a gorgeous resort! Our suite had a beautiful view of the pool and the ocean. It had a full bath with a second vanity, a cool fireplace, and an awesome with windows that allowed the bath to be exposed to the rest of the room. I had a great massage in the spa, which was top-notch. The amenities were amazing and so upscale. My favorite part was the eucalyptus sauna. The resort is located on a beautiful beach with small hiking trails that lead up to a pier where you can see a beautiful sunset. This resort was absolutely gorgeous and luxurious and I can't wait to come back for another romantic stay.
You never go wrong at this place. I've been at this restaurant many times. Great food, excellent menu and good service. The fish is fresh, presentation is great and the taste is perfect. Way to go!

So I got my coworkers hooked on Daves Dogs and today one of the girls went and got all of us our lunches from Daves. When she got back she realized one of our items wasn't in the bag. She called to let them know we were missing an item and that she was going to go back to pick it up, well Dave asked where we worked and delivered the item himself plus a little extra. Now that's how you run a business. Thanks Dave!

Someone plz tell me that this is not the same honor bar that Hillstone owns like the one in Beverly Hills.  We used to live 5min walking distance from the beverly hills honor bar and it's still one of my husband's favorite restaurants.  After spending all day in Santa Barbara for our wedding anniversary, we were happy to see that there's honor bar nearby because we knew the quality of food and service can be trusted. We didn't want to go to a fancy restaurant or fast food types so this was a perfect choice.  The wait was brutal. We put our name on the list at 6:25pm and sat down at 7:30 or so. I just know when the food came out and it was a little before 8pm. The host who looked like Julia Stiles was very professional and courteous. And that was the only good thing about this honor bar.  1. When the server came(Connor was his name), I ordered a tuna sandwich. He told me that they were trying to fade that menu hence not to encourage the customer to order it. Huh, then why not just print out a new menu? He recommended an entree dish, seared tuna with ponzu but I didn't want any dish with soy sauce flavor so I told him I'll get trout dip appetizer and think about what else to get. He said they were out of trout also. Ugh! But he kindly offered that 'for your inconvenience, any other choice of appetizer will be free of charge.' How nice. So I ordered smoked-salmon appetizer and told him that I'll keep one menu to keep looking.  2. Food came out and the salmon appetizer was actually a pretty big portion. I didn't think I needed any other dish but wanted to add a side of tabbouleh. I was going to order when the server comes over to check up on the food but guess what, that never happened. Hard to believe but he NEVER came over to ask if everything was ok. After waiting for a while, also with empty water glasses, my husband grabbed another server who were quickly walking by and ordered a side of tabbouleh and asked for water.   3. My husband got a side of seasonal vegetable which was cooked broccoli. They were so salty so was tabouleh but since the server never came to check and we didn't want to wait, we just ate however much we could handle. We could taste that it was definitely ground sea salt but one of every 3 bite tasted like chewing on a big chunk of sea salt rock and could not taste anything else. It was as if nobody did the taste check in the kitchen. Just purely ridiculous.   4. As mentioned above, since the server never bothered to came over to our table since taking our order, we had to flag down other server to get water refills, order a side of tabbouleh, and didn't even bother to order any drinks, etc.   5. Also because the server never came by, he probably didn't even realize we were finished with our meal. That's the only reason I could think of why it took so long to just get the check even after other staff removed our dishes off the table. When our server finally came to drop the check, he didn't bother asking whether everything was ok. Instead he said 'we took care of tabbouleh.' I said thank you and thought, 'huh, two items on the house?' But 3 seconds later when I saw the check, I thought, 'SOMEBODY MADE A CHOICE.' Unlike the server's offer that my choice of appetizer was on the house, I got charged for the salmon appetizer which was obviously more expensive than $6 tabbouleh side.  First I thought maybe he forgot but why else would he take the tabbouleh off the bill unless he remembered the guest experience wasn't at best because of lack of their preparedness of menu? It was just very stinky move and I didn't even want to ask or inform or even look for the manager because of #6 below.  6. We got there at 6:25pm and left at 9pm. The whole time we were waiting and dining, we did not see a manager at all. It's such a small restaurant and where else the manager could be? Doing what?! The staff were slammed and people were still waiting for tables at 9pm. Don't you want to check on tables, guests, etc? This was why I didn't want to ask to correct the bill or talk to a manger. At that point, I realized this honor bar was a minute before crashing if not happened already.   7. While waiting I saw a group of guests who told their server that the food was too salty. Huh, coincident? Somebody really needs to taste check. The table next to us didn't get their food in time, one came out wrong, they ended up getting both dishes free. I'm pretty sure the restaurant was a total mess and poorly managed. Wait, managed at all? Where's the manager?  Besides the bevery hills honor bar, we also love going to Hillstone in Santa Monica and that's how we know Hillstone restaurants can be trusted which is why we waited for an hour to dine at this Montecito honor bar. I warn you though, not all honor bars are run equal. Avoid this location like the plague.
Nice waitress, the food is good for diner food.  Has that 50's diner decor with a modern twist. Pretty reasonable.
I already love this place. It has a nice outdoor space to relax and to chill while you eat. The food was absolutely amazing. Tuscan Dip and  spicy sauce  pizza was delicious!

Everything at La Playa Azul was fantastic!  We enjoyed shrimp, chicken enchiladas and all of the side dishes.  The food quality and portions were excellent, as well as the service.  No wonder this is a locals' favorite.  We will definitely be back on our next visit to SB.

Excellent pizza.  Good location.  Friendly service.  Open air seating.    Started with a tossed green salad.  Very fresh and served cold just the way I like it.    The proprietor was so gracious and gave our party some free bread sticks with marinara sauce.  The bread sticks were so light and  crispy at the same time.  Highly recommend.    The pizza was very good.  The crust was light and crispy.  The pizza sauce had just the right amount of spice to make it extra tasty.  The toppings were very generous.  Love the pastrami on my pizza.     Limited parking so allow extra time.  Great place to eat pizza when in Santa Barbara.  Buon appetito!

Love the ambiance of the outdoor seats. The oak smoked tri-tip was tender but not as flavorful as I imagined (guess my expectation was too high), overshadowed by the tasty bread used in the sandwich, which reminded me of croissant but less oily. I also liked the salad.   After the meal, I bought some tequila lime links from their butcher shop and they turned out to be amazing! So much more flavorful than what I used to buy from supermarkets.

I was very surprised that my omelette was delicious. As far as most breakfasts go, I feel they taste pretty similar but my vegetable omelette was very fresh and beautifully prepared. Their homemade biscuit is more like a scone with a definitive sweet taste. I would go back for breakfast. Our server was friendly too.
The food is tasty I love the fajitas and the agua fresca is great the service is nice I just wish it was a bit more prompt it takes a while to get your food
Love this place. The food is really yummy, the only downside is that when you walk in it feels like a hotel, but once you're inside it has a great vibe and the service is wonderful.

Delicious food. Very nice venue but the seating arrangement is a little funny. At times Ppl are literally waiting to pounce on any seats that open up, making it awkward for the incumbent tenant of those seats
Truly the best place to go in Santa Barbara. Order some clam chowder and sit at the bar. There is nothing better than that harbor view

Another excellent breakfast! There's no place like this in Santa Barbara. We love the European feeling of the courtyard too. This morning we're having French steamed eggs with salad and a classic French omelette.
Ate here on 12/11/18 great food as always plus the excellent service. One of my favorites in SB we will return soon.
I love this place. I live on the the east side of Santa Barbara and have eaten at every restaurant on Milpas. El Buen Gusto has the best Super carnitas Burrito in my opinion.
Food was delicious super flavorful, generous portions my boyfriend & I shared al pastor nachos &  still had left overs. Price is right and most importantly its vegan!!!

Hamburgers are good but it's a place for younger adults than us. I wouldn't recommend it unless you want to sit (if you are lucky enough to get a seat) at a big table with lots of people. There aren't a lot of places to sit. Not a good place to bring company. Very informal. No table service. You have to stand in a slow line to order.

For a Four Seasons, the food at the resort is subpar.  The best food I had was in room dining off the kids menu - the salmon with cous cous and veggies.  And we ordered in rooming dining at least twice.  We ate here for dinner (fish, spaghetti, salad, dessert) and breakfast (frittata and my friend got pancakes).  Breakfast was better, but we ordered off the regular menu, not the $85 buffet.  We also ate at Ty Lounge and Tydes and Bella Vista was the least impressive.  I would eat breakfast here again, but not sure about dinner.  I can't believe the food here is so mediocre.  This was my first stay at Four Seasons and if there was no beach across the street and the pretty grounds/vibe, there would be no reason to stay here.  Across the board at all the dining options, some dishes were good and some were just so boring or underwhelming.  It's odd that it is very hit or miss.  We wanted to go back to Tydes for the view, but it was closed already.  It might be worth dining off property.

Yeah, gonna have to go 5-stars for the Super Deluxe sammie, as well as A+ customer service. Not proud that it took so long to learn about Tino's (heard of them through SB News Press Foodie Awards), but glad we're now hanging with all the cool kids (LoL)! Came in on a Friday at 100pm. Steady flow of folks, but not crowded. They have a couple of small signs hanging above the deli counter, advertising the Deluxe and Super Deluxe. After reviewing Yelp, it was a no-brainer for us: 2 SDs! The lady that took our order (one of the owners) was most pleasant, and didn't blink as I modified the order a bit. She repeated my request, and off she went. Whoever takes your order apparently has it until delivery to the cashier -- I like that. We each got a bag of chips and a 16oz DCoke -- $21 which was more than reasonable. The sammies were just wonderful! Bread was aromatic and soft, yet held up well in spite of the vinegar and oil, etc. The meats and cheeses were plentiful and tasty, and they add just the right amount of lettuce, toms, peppers, etc. not a huge sandwich, but more than enough to make a hearty lunch. We shopped around after to checkout the imported food stuffs. Impressive array of pastas, sauces, olive oils, Italian varietals, etc. interacted with two other family members while browsing and then checking out. Just very pleasant and engaging, kind of what you expect (and want) from a long-time, family run business. A++  Cheers! RS

Recently ordered a tostada salad with steak and it was sooo mouth watering delicious! It came with 3 different salsas. It was so good. It didn't disappoint!!! I happened to have left overs and carried it with me in its plastic bag and everyone else I ran into , was saying how they started to crave their food because it's always good every time. Consistency , great service , and great quality! Thank you !!! It's always a must try in the area!!!

Delicious anniversary dinner outside by the heater. We shared the clams and muscles starter and honestly wished it could have been our main the curry broth was so good. We ordered extra bread to soak it up. Then my gumbo was just as good and spicy. The type of spice that hits you at first but doesn't linger. So good. A perfect pairing with the Malbec, as well. My husband got the classic jerk chicken and said the meat fell off the bone and was also delicious. A good pairing with the Sauvignon Blanc. We'll definitely be back.

Le Cafe Stella has always been a favorite for myself and my family.  My most recent experience was beyond wonderful. I believe my server's name was Rowan.  He was absolutely amazing, so friendly, full of personality, and really made my experience extra enjoyable! Thanks Rowan!

I'm always VERY picky about getting Thai food in the US because growing up in Southeast Asia, I've had really great Thai food and my experiences at restaurants here have never been great.   I was hoping that Galanga would've helped prove me wrong, but the food was fairly mediocre and also pretty overpriced. My friends enjoyed their orders, but my Chicken and Tofu Curry Noodles were too sweet and greasy for my liking. I had to fix it up back at home with my own ingredients to make it more palatable for me. However, the staff was very pleasant and they were pretty quick to get our food out at around the same time despite ordering 4 different dishes!

We were in Santa Barbara for our babymoon so I was excited there was now a fancy, romantic vegan restaurant in the area. It's a lovely atmosphere with a beautiful dog-friendly patio, though we opted to leave our dog at home and sit inside. You won't feel out of place dressed up or more casually.  We ordered a few small plates to share, and it was the perfect amount of food. They don't fry anything, and it felt like a very healthy meal.  The Kung Pao Cauliflower was wonderful, maybe my favorite dish of the night. My husband wanted the Shishito Peppers, which I don't normally like very much but these were great. We both loved the miso aioli the accompanied it, and I found myself dipping other things in it so I wouldn't have to eat too many shishitos, haha.  The Artichoke 'Crab Cakes' didn't live up to my favorite 'crab cakes' made from hearts of palm at Crossroads Kitchen in LA, but they were still very nice and were accompanied by a delicious corn salad. My husband wasn't interested in tasting the crab cakes but he was thrilled with the corn salad. Lastly, we had the Farro Bolognese. The fresh spinach tagliatelle was great but I was not a fan of farro as a meat substitute. It also didn't look very appetizing, with the 'bolognese' plopped on top. I wish I had tried a flatbread instead of the pasta.  We had enough room for dessert so we shared a chocolate tart. This was great, though the crust tasted a little too 'healthy.' I wondered if it's a raw crust, made with dates and nuts. My husband just left it behind and enjoyed the chocolate filling.  I think it's a little weird that their three 'large plates' are two burgers and pad Thai, none of which interested either of us for a fancy dinner. The menu is a little all over the place, it's hard to get a feel for what type of cuisine they are. But the food we ordered was mostly very tasty and we had a lovely date.

Love this place. The view, the ambiance, the service and most importantly the food is excellent.  If your looking for fresh seafood this is the place to go!  I'm obsessed with the Petrale Sole dish, dredged in flour sautéed in olive oil, piled on top of fingerling potatoes and spinach and smothered in a lemon caper beurre blanc... So good

The food was good and fresh. Crowded even on a Monday evening. The  clientele was mostly 20 somethings. The restaurant is really loud-they need some decorations that will reduce the sound. As far as this food goes it was fine, nothing special but very nice.
Best homemade tacos north of the border! Worth the long lines; authentic Mexican food experience with a fraction of the prices!

It is essentially what I expected. The fries taste similar to what McDonald's fries once tasted like, back in the day.  The strawberry shake tasted like dreyer's brand ice cream (not necessarily a bad thing, I enjoyed it since it reminded me of growing up eating those little ice cream cups with the wooden paddle-spoons). The chubby burger tastes like something you can get at a diner or honestly like in-n-out without the sauce and with a different type of patty. The experience was good; fast service and a quick, cheap fast food option. I want to return to try the chicken club next! Down side is the default meal fry size was smaller than I expected.
Solid outdoor shopping center. Was able to pick up essentials at Nordstrom and Sephora.   Parking in the garage is convenient enough.   I *wish*  it was a little more upscale but it has the basics!

This place is amazing, good quality fish love the Faroe Islands Salmon and good food: the Korean Tacos are my favorite, the house salad is also amazing and love the salmon burger. the staff is always friendly, Erica is always going above and beyond and make me feel special.

Time to do yet another scathing (but accurate review). So Farmer ‍ Boy  has this (new) feature where you can use an iPad at their entrance to check in. So I did the whole check-in situation and patiently waited. The wait time was about accurate, I have to say. It's the experience once we were seated that was less than enjoyable. First of all (and I know the staff had little to no control over it). In the corner table, there were about 10 super loudly obnoxious kids talking so loud that I could barely keep my ears from exploding. 2 parents showed up about 30 minutes later to hush them up. The damage was done. Things were too loud and went on for too long. The waiting for the food was what took really long (over 30 minutes to get our meal). I overheard the server blaming on something happening in the kitchen but I didn't buy it . Overall, the experience was nothing to write  home about but it was to write a Yelp review about. I'm giving it 3 stars  overall experience. I would give 2.5 stars if I could. Yelp does not allow 1/2 star ratings. Disclaimer: I am satisfied with the help of the server and the man refilling our coffee  and water. They were very helpful and deserve kudos from the restaurant owners and a raise.

Camille was absolutely great! Her service was some of the best that I have had in SB. The food was delicious as well. My mom ordered shrimp and loved every bite of it. Will definitely be back and recommend to others. Keep it up Camille.

This place is AMAZING!! I truly had the best service of my life from Billie, Gunther, and the manager/owner Bob Murphy. We were able to walk around the resort and to the beach with our wine. The views were stunning and the wine was spectacular. I was especially pleased with the rose and Chardonnay. This place is a must you will love your experience here!!

Gorgeous and DELICIOUS experience. My boyfriend and I went here for Valentine's Day and love it. The servers were so helpful and kind and the food was next level. You must take their recommendations and their vermouth!

Overall our favorite breakfast place.  I tried the salmon bagel and breakfast burrito and both were good.  The lemon scone was excellent!  The coffee is absolute garbage -- two days in a row.  Rather pricey, but the portions are huge so you will be stuffed at the end.  Very kid friendly.   We liked sitting inside by the Window to avoid having our kids out in the sun.

I have heard so much about this place so really wanted to try . First people - very friendly and ready to help you . Ready to answer or serve with a beautiful smile so that is very nice of them because in the morning before breakfast if you run into a mean person , that ruins your day . Luckily not here . Second location - well very hard to beat . Where else you can have your favorite breakfast watching the serene view of the gorgeous pacific oceans and friendly birds who can entertain you for free at outdoor sitting . So these two five stars :). Oh you really want me to review the food . Well no one makes better coffee tha Starbucks because it is individualized . I had 911 omelette which was ok , however they serve you small tasty muffins with butter which is darn good . The potatoes were like any other potatoes you find it any where . So go there for view , service and beautiful smile and if you are still hungry eat the food :). You will not be disappointed, I guarantee you that .

If only the restaurant was larger! Great food, great servers, excellent cocktails but too small and crowded. Love eating outside but it's been so cold lately. You guys need a larger space where everyone can enjoy your food and not wait an hour for a table.

A huuuge THANK YOU to Finney's for bringing on the Impossible Burger! It was my first time trying it and I've gone back for it 3 times since! The place also has a great drink menu and lots of other delicious bites, like crispy brussels sprouts and a balsamic drizzled veggie flatbread that can easily be ordered vegan (just say no cheese ;)  For those of you who don't know, the Impossible Burger is a 100% plant based burger that tastes and looks exactly like the real thing. White Castle recently added it to their menu. Finney's really does it right, it may be the best burger I've ever had in general. Order without cheese or spread to make it legit cruelty-free, with a side of their awesome tater tots.  They've been slammed since they've opened, service is very friendly but can be kind of slow since they are so busy. I seriously can't get enough of that burger.   I know everyone hates plant-eaters, but we know how to support businesses who are looking out for us. Finney's I'll be telling all my herbivore friends about you!

Place was packed but quickly got a seat. We didn't realize you placed your order and paid first. Service was quick and food was delicious.  We got the French Toast and weren't disappointed. It wasn't too sweet or too soggy. Coffee was good and servers were really attentive. The atmosphere was totally adorable and very clean. Highly recommend.

Great cocktails and homemade pasta! Locals secret hideaway and so delicious! The antipasto plate was very ample and full of various Italian meats and cheeses worth every bite as an appetizer!! Recommend!
Great food. Food was full flavored and tasted fresh. Service was also perfect. Server had lots of great suggestions. I could only improve this place with slightly bigger portions.

I've seen the long lines and have always been intrigued. Once Groupon had an offer, I jumped all over it!  We tried to go there about an hour before it closed on a Sunday night and they had already stopped taking reservations by then. It was still packed, so we opted to go on Memorial Day. Once we put our name down, they said it would take about 40 minutes, and their timing was right. It was a beautiful day, so we had no problem just enjoying the view from the pier.  We opted for first available and got some seats in the corner near the bathroom. I was glad we got an indoor seat since the wind started picking up and it got a little chilly.   We decided to splurge and got the 3lb. Rock Crab special for $24.95(comes with onion rings, soup and salad), the lobster roll $18.95(it's a cold roll that comes with slaw, but beware, the slaw has raisins...), and we also had oysters: 2 blue point, 2 chef creek and 2 kumiai. While all very good, our preference was chef creek then blue pint then kumiai. They were all distinct but we tend to like the creamier oysters.   When selecting the soup, I asked our server what he recommended, and he said clam chowder all the way. He didn't steer us wrong; it was creamy, full of goodies and was just delicious. This definitely up in the running for one of my favorite chowder places in SB.  The salad was ok, and didn't stand out too much. The lobster roll was filled with capers and had a good mix of flavors. Since we lived in Boston for a while, the bf's first comment was 'it's good, but the roll is all wrong'. He was right, the lobster was very tasty and they didn't overpower it with mayo. We aren't fans of raisins, so we picked them all out, and after that we enjoyed the slaw.  The rock crab deal was for 3lbs, so in addition to a whole crab, they gave us another 1/2 to get to the weight they promised. The bf felt it was a little overcooked and too flaky. I thought it was ok, and realized this isn't my favorite type of crab to eat in this way. I probably won't order it again. Not because of how it's served, but the texture wasn't what I enjoy as much as other types of crab (like the blue dungeoness crab, that's my fave!)  Also, there are so many other things to try, so when I come back, I'll look forward to tasting other things.   The service is pretty great. This place is always slammed, and our server was very tentative and patient. We never felt rushed even as we methodically worked our way through the crab.  They also have a take out window, and if you don't want any alcohol, it's a great alternative than waiting for a table.   Can't wait to come back!
I come saigon many time always food good and service good too when I eat food there I come home I feel good because they don't use  MSg

This is definitely the best Mexican Food I have experienced in the few months being here.   Service 9/10 - Service is spectacular at this place it really is. They always make sure the place is clean. They make sure the salsa bar is always full. The are very quick with coming to you if you need help.  Food 9.5/10   Food ranks as one of the top Mexican restaurants I have ever tasted. Enchiladas were wow just perfection. Fajitas were so good , and made you just wanting more. Chips and Salsa I just could not stop eating. Portions were very big for the price.  Location/ Decor( minor to my overall rating of the place) 8/10:  This location is pretty good it is right of State Street . Decent amount of parking for the place. Decor is nice for SB not the greatest if you are looking for a lot of ambience.   Overall Rating : 9.4/10  I highly recommend this place and it will be my place for Mexican food. Words can't even describe the greatness of this place. Come you won't be dissatisfied.

The service here has been questionable. The first time I showed up it was 8:30 and they close at 9, they told me the kitchen was closed and there were people being served when I showed up. I think this may just be a SB thing, but that irks me. I would -totally- understand if I showed up at 8:45 expecting a lavish hand-made dish that would take time to curate but that was disappointing.  For my birthday, I came back and granted it was busy, we were told after about 5 min. of waiting and servers passed by us three-ish times (which honestly In theory is no big deal). They would have something for us in 5 minutes. It took time, they had two tables open and finally someone hastily seated us.   They ran out of their bruschetta and asked if we would like to order another app. We didnt have a menu to reference and opted out for another app.   The oil and vinegar provided with their bread was great, I could really taste the quality in the oil especially!  My boyfriend ordered a clam linguini and I did their mushroom half-moon ravioli. It was very rich, a ton of oil on the ravioli which I wasn't a huge fan of. I enjoyed the flavor of the black truffle, it wasn't overly salty but it also was somewhat bland.   My boyfriend seemed to enjoy the clam pasta, but he was turned off when he discovered a bad clam and it didn't sit right with him.  Overall, Via Maestra 42 was recommended by a few people I know. I may give it another shot (because I want to try their butternut squash ravioli). But other than that, I'm going to keep on searching for a better hole-in-the-wall Italian restaurant.
The food is soooo good! Casual atmosphere and fresh delicious ingredients. My favorite is the BAC burger because it's served on pao de quejo (Brazilian cheese bread) and is gluten free.

Great for happy hour, but be prepared for very rushed service. Although our waitress was very nice, it took about an hour to get our full order in simply because our table wasn't being visited. So even if you show up with an hour to spare, your order might not be in by the end of happy hour, and you'll have to pay full price. It's also very loud and crowded, so if you're sitting near the front, be prepared to have other patrons hovering near your table as they wait for seats. As for the food, the burgers are still very good -- go for the bunless option and have your burger served over an arugula salad. The sweet potato fries are wayy too sweet for my taste, they have honey drizzled on them. This wouldn't have bothered me if the beet 'detox' drink I'd ordered wasn't so sugary too. Overall, a fun place for happy hour, but be prepared to wait even if you are already seated.

This is the only remaining Sambo's Restaurant.  It remains a nice casual family joynt, in the middle of an increasingly expensive and overdeveloped Santa Barbara. The patio is nice too. As a young Lad, Sambo's were all over the place, and were a decent Denny's alternative, especially where pancakes were concerned. I might have even been a member of the Sambo's Tiger Tamers Kids Club.   Many folks get caught up in this restaurant's deliberate decorating connection to The Story of Little Black Sambo and the resulting backlash. That turned out to be a key reason the chain folded, despite attempts to rename (The Jolly Tiger) or rebrand (No Place Like Sam's). In 1970, Sambo's had 1117 outlets in 47 States, and now they are one.   And where did the Sambo's name actually come from?  The Chain was started in 1957 by SAM Battistone, Sr. and Newell BOhnett. Get It? And now you know the rest of the story.
Jacob, you are as polite and professional as you are well groomed and hygienic...please go work somewhere else with lower standards.
I am from Germany and these sausages are pretty dang good! Not completely authentic, but they taste great. We loved the sausage sampler and the garlic fries. We also got the schnitzel and a Reuben
F all the haters. El Territo always has been a great place to get good margaritas and and Texas Mexico. They also  have  great chips and salsa!

We have been here several times and have never been disappointed! The restaurant is often crowded and very loud - so if you need more quiet, wear something warmer and sit outside under their heat lamps! I LOVE the clam fettuccini and their clam chowder is delicious! The head cook is quite a chef and watching him prepare the food is very entertaining. He does bang the pots LOUDLY - so be aware of your ears are sensitive. The restaurant has a nice ambiance to it and is cheaper than many in town. The wharf is fun and we love going for lunch as well - they will allow your dog at the outside table and even brought ours a fresh bowl of water - and I didn't even ask them! We will be back! The only reason for four stars is they don't take reservations and the wait can be REALLY long - up to two hours if you really have to go there!
Loved the chicken.  I went multiple times.  Tip.  Breasts are a bit dry.  Thighs were moist and flavorful.

I really like this place, I was skeptical to be honest but I went for the first time on Friday to get lunch with my GF and we really liked it. So much so that we went back the next night with friends and they are hooked as well. I really like that you get to build your own pizza and the ingredients seem to be really good as well. The pizza cooks in just a couple of minutes which is nice because you can get in and out of there for lunch pretty quick and the prices are tough to beat!

I've tried most items on the menu, Santa Barbara dog upgrade to a hotlink is my jam. Pastrami dog also incredible. Really there is no wrong choice here.  The fried chicken sandwich with Mac and cheese is to die for. Just go! It's worth it

Tried out the Globe when I was in SB late last year.  Walking in the space is dark and romantic, with a variety of plush seating options.  That was a little weird given that I was going with my aunt but whatever.  Service was decent if a bit distant.  Cocktail menu was okay but the beer menu was very good.  Had a Sam Smith Imperial Stout that was reasonably priced.  While the uber cool vibe was certainly there and we had a good evening, the food was only decent and more than a little inconsistent.  Think sopping wet tacos, really good steak, and then follow it with thoroughly mediocre taquitos.  It averaged out to be okay overall but there were definitely winners and losers.  I like the small plates concept but the execution in the kitchen needs to improve.  Technically the Globe has rebranded as '18 East' and is now hosting pop up chefs as well as a remodeled space.  May give that a shot next time I'm in town.  Good decision based on what I saw, as Globe seemed to fall a bit short of its ambitions based on what I experienced.

Santa Barbara shellfish company is located at the end of the wharf. The view is simply amazing. We came here on a Wednesday evening for early dinner. I'd say it's pretty packed for a weekday. There was a 30 minutes wait for outside seating so we decided to take inside instead.   Was craving for some oysters so i tried their oyster shooters. Hmm definitely too sour for me. It was really just okay. I got their crab cake. Came with onion rings and salad. My sister got their fried calamari, shrimp, scallops, and oysters. Also came with onion rings and salad. Love their fried calamari and oysters!   Their foods are okay. Service is very attentive and friendly :) But I think this place is overrated. I would come back for the view and fried calamari.

Just wanted to give a shout-out to this bakery for making the same delicious cakes that Wayne produced when he was the owner. A very special thank you to everyone today for accommodating a rush request for my friend's birthday today. Everyone loved the cake and you've definitely gained ten new customers as a result! We had a chocolate cake with peanut butter mousse filling and whipped cream frosting. So yummy and I just loved that the frosting is creamy and not sugary sweet at all. I recommend this bakery to everyone who always finds themselves scraping the frosting off their birthday cake. Those other places are just doing it wrong.

Cielito was recently rebranded as 'Viva Santa Barbara Modern Mexico'...We had a reservation and were seated near the fountain in an area with high traffic  (server & Bus-persons)... Much of the attention we received from the wait-staff was interruptive. Clearing plates too early or literally reaching across our dining plates from which we were actively eating to clear a small plate or glass.  No chips and salsa offered   My wife had Beef Tacos which she reported had meat cooked more than she would have liked...We asked for salsa to moisten and flavor the tacos and liked their green salsa  I had the cauliflower steak on sunflower seed risotto...The risotto tasted like horchata...Interesting and I would not order again.  I doubt that we will return and if we did we would choose the far side of the fountain away from the foot traffic over attention

What happened?! We used to love this place. Man, I really hate writing reviews like this.  I can't continue to recommend this place to anyone who likes to eat things that taste good. The cheese on my salad was not at all fresh, the oranges on it were canned (seriously wtf), lettuce was just straight massacred. And how do you mess up french fries? Some of the worst I've ever had.   Wife said her sandwich tasted 'like freezer' when asked which part 'all of it.' I have had better food in cafeterias... 'pedestrian' was a word that came up on the drive home. My beer was great but not enough redemption value there to feel remotely good about dropping $50 on the tab. Seems things here have gone the way of most SB restaurants -- get a following then cut costs and hope the customers won't notice (they will).   I will say the service was great, so if you're looking for beers with a smile you'll do alright.

Great new edition to the SB restaurant scene. It's not an inexpensive place to eat or drink, but it is at a luxury hotel and the ingredients, service and ambiance are top-notch so the price feels in line. Our server, Tim, was a true pro and he knew about the food and wines in a way that showed his training and professionalism. Oh, and their wines by the glass are insane... Sea Smoke Sea Spray? Sanguis Misfit? Unheard of.   Highly recommend!
solid, flavorful veggie burrito... really cute restaurant... VERY nice waitstaff..... I'd go back for more!

The best brewery on State. Oh, wait. It's the only brewery on State. Anyway, the food is serviceable and the service is friendly. The beer is better than you'd expect from a place in this good of a location. And it is all about the location, proving to be a needed break in the long row of similar bars.

On our second visit to Santa Barbara Shellfish, at the end of Stern's Wharf, it is confirmed. This restaurant serves fresh, well prepared, delicious seafood. Really very very good.   'Twas a beautiful afternoon in Santa Barbara, as we put our name on the list outside the front door. We had a whole page of names before us, but between people not there when called, and the relatively fast turnaround, we waited only about 15 minutes.  Besides, you're out on the ocean in Santa Barbara. There are a lot of worse places in the world you could be waiting for a table.   This restaurant serves some unique local items, like Santa Barbara rock crab, uni, and abalone. I'd say the majority of diners were working on some serious looking rock crab, but I opted for the Dungeness crab cocktail. Nailed it. Best I've had, or at least tied with Musso and Frank Grill. I also had the abalone, which was delicious served over a bed of fantastic rice. Abalone isn't quite the same as it was years ago. This was smaller, but from what I was told, about 4 years old. Still, I hadn't had it in so long, and I really enjoyed it.   I will let my photos do the rest of the talking. All I can say is that I cannot wait to go again.
My egg Benedict's don't have bread . Pretty disappointed read the pic for more information .
I requested for a sandwich which wasn't available but they went out of their way to make it for me! Talk about great customer service. Their tomato bisque is excellent, good sandwiches too!
Bouchon,  Had a perfect  dinner experience at Bouchon... The food was so good. The service was excellent !!! The best part was the desert!!! Can't wait to come back.

Typically speaking, when a location is this perfect the food and service falter. This is not the case. We had an attentive waiter who knew the menu, offered up advice on what's great, and basically went above and beyond. This place is brand new, but the wait staff and the cooks seem to be clicking like a place that's been open for years. My fiancé and I each had drinks, and both my mojito and her pina colda were great. I got the chicken sandwich, and couldn't find anything to complain about. It was spicy and sweet, and just good. Careful it's super hot, temperature wise. My fiancé had the drunken apple salad. It was very good, I would say that it needed more dressing, but that's just my preference. I'm not nit picking, because it was still a great salad! Overall, I'd say the management hit it out of the park so far.
Amazingly delicious yet healthy food. I used to go here all the time and can't remember why I haven't been in so long. Got the baja burrito with tahini sauce for the salad, and left super satisfied.

I'd give this place a solid 3.5 stars. It's worth coming if you're really craving kbbq and can't drive down to the LA area!   First off, this place is TINY (for an ayce kbbq place). There's only 10 tables and each seat about 4 people. My friend and I came on a Monday night around 7 and it wasn't ridiculously busy. At first, the waiter said it would take an hour to get seats since there was a large party ahead of us; however, he ended up seating us after 20 minutes.   Next time I will try the more expensive option, but for now this review is based off of the $16.50 option. What I liked: brisket (my favorite), steamed egg, and the bulgogi was pretty good. What I disliked: japchae (it was edible but definitely not authentic japchae), no rice paper or rice available (I guess they were out), and the sausage.

Was really excited to try Oku as I had seen a few pictures on Insta and the food looked great. The location is very touristy for locals but at least they have valet. I called ahead on a Thursday night at 5:20 and asked for a table for two at 5:30. The hostess said they didn't have anything but that we could sit at the bar. We only had an hour to eat as we needed to pick up our kids from sports at 6:30. This was mentioned but didn't seem to make any difference. When we arrived at 5:30 there were a total of ten people at three tables. Literally 40 free tables, all with irritating little reserved signs on them. We explained that we had just called and that we would be quick and would like to sit down and eat. We were directed to an indoor bar and told we could also sit at the bar upstairs.   Upon walking upstairs we saw an additional 20 tables, EMPTY! But no, we couldn't sit down except at the bar which faces away from the view. A waiter tried to help us out and asked his manager if we could sit down at one of the dozens of empty tables but our dreams of eating (totally joking) at Oku were once again dashed.   We left and won't be back. Too touristy and just plain rude and idiotic service/management.
Ordered the state st sando.  It comes with cheap supermarket bread. What a shame. You would think a speciality market in this cool location would have decent sandwich's. Overpriced too

Excellent choice of savory tapas close to Santa Barbara downtown. Tucked away on a quiet street close to State St. The cocktails are creative. The paper plane and Ginspiration are good choices. We had the bacon wrapped dates which were okay, felt a little burnt. The sweet potato fries were delicious. The Lamborghini sliders and pork sliders are excellent. Our final favorites are the chicken enchiladas and por-que pig. The flavors and sauce are super tasty.

This place misses the marks on flavor, portion, service, and cleanliness.   I came in on a mid-week afternoon, and the outdoor patio seemed perfect. The place was pretty empty. 'Seat Yourself' said the sign, so I did. But even given the obviously slow time and day, my table hadn't been wiped down since the last party.   I ordered the 'Extreme Exotic' Duck and Bacon Brat, and chose the cooked sauerkraut and sweet peppers as my toppings. The brat itself was small, and more like an overfed breakfast sausage from Denny's. The flavor was weak--duck, bacon, or otherwise, nothing told me it was particularly exotic but the menu. And the brioche bun was so big it made the order more like a very dry and bready [sic] kraut and pepper sandwich.   For $8.95 I should have gone to Tino's Italian Market and gotten a super-deluxe deli sub to at least enjoyed some local habit and history.   My server checked one me once or twice, but they didn't show much interest in service until they brought me the check and suddenly it was tipping time. Nice trick, but I've seen it before. The busser/runner/expediter checked on me more often.    The saving grace? The Belgian Fries and the Brunehaut Organic Blonde Ale kept this from being a 1 star review. Even then, that's $14 before any tax and tip. Location and ambience are nice but not worth the price.

I would not have expected quality German food in Santa Barbara, but the other Yelp reviews convinced me to try this place out.  I'm glad I did!  The food here was very good.  To start, the potato salad was warm and nicely spiced.  Our favorite dinner item was the Jager Schnitzel -- the mushroom sauce was delectable, and the pork was not oily like how some breaded meat can be.
