The Black Forest cake was absolutely amazing -- and huge! It was rainy so we ate inside, but I can imagine the patio being a pleasant place to eat.  The staff was very pleasant as well.  It is bit on the pricy side here, but worth it in this case.  I use restaurants in larger cities as my standard, and by that measure this place definitely ranks highly.
Went here with a big group and had a wonderful time! The server was very nice & accomodating, the patio seating was lovely, and the food is really good!

Love the outdoor concept & fun twist on classic brunch items! I had the fried chicken Benedict & was amazed at how much food I got!  The hollandaise was really yummy, but I could have used a touch more as the chicken was a bit too crunchy on the outside for me & needed more of that moisture. But the huge amount of avocado on my plate left nothing to complain about.
Avoid unless you are going to die from hunger.
Standard breakfast, but it is soooo popular in Santa Barbara. I guess because it is cheap, fast and they have three of them.

Very tasty food but completely inept (though sincere) service.  One poor guy who didn't have a clue how to be a waiter. Ambiance was terrible.  No sign when you enter telling you to seat yourself (if we had waited for someone it would have been a very long time). Off to the immediate left are plastic crates, stacked chairs. Inside the restaurant is a very strange counter with odd stuff behind it that looked like someone's office.  Again, food was good but service very slow and confused.
Love this restaurant, one of my favorites, I wish they still served lunch, it was so convenient for work.

Sound and look like greet pizza place  Is just look !  The Waiting time  And order the pizza is nightmare  We try order few pizza slices whit different topping but employees make it so hard on me  He say I CANOT order 3 slice  If you won 4 is need to be full pizza  What???? I don't care just make my pizza !! Either way we got the pizza (3 slice )  after 45 minutes  Because the 'dough is not ready ' Swear this what them say to me  Them offer refund but I really won try it so I whit  And of course Them make mistakes on the toppings  The pizza is not this greet  I don't really think if this pizza or just flat bread whit topping or them just forget my sauce on the pizza? Nvm  I know is new place and of course this mistake   But like this ? I will give this places shot again in the future  Hop till then  something changes  I don't think this only the employees issue  Because why owner make the customers so difficult to order pizza?

Quick synopsis: Hidden' gem? - absolutely Worth the (*uber) drive? - of course (*alcohol involved) Divine menu? - well beyond my expectations  Heavenly atmosphere? - oh yeah Outstanding service? - they must have been sent from above New favorite upscale restaurant in Santa Barbara? - yep  Angel Oak deserves every star. With so many local upscale restaurants, AO is now in my top 5. Beautiful ocean views, knowledge staff, interesting decor, and an incredible menu - does it get any better than this?   The foie gras torchon was very rich. With such a generous portion it's definitely meant for sharing. I opted for the Kobe beef, 3 oz minimum, but that is all I needed, which was also very rich. Let me expound, it was the most flavorful and exciting cut of beef I've ever consumed. It was so tender, literally no chewing was required and dissolved in my mouth. My first bite the clouds parted letting the sun shine through with angles singing hallelujah. In all honestly, it was probably me singing.   Had the chance to enjoy their previous restaurant, Miro, and wholeheartedly believe it is a scale above.  I highly recommend AO.

I rate this place 3.5 stars, but it has good potential.  The employees are very nice, and the service was good during a Saturday lunch hour.  The dim sum is more authentic than any other place in town and is handmade from scratch (according to their menu).  They're still in their soft opening phase, and some things can be improved.  The Glutinous Rice in Lotus Leaf wasn't steamed fully, so there was some rice that was harder and not sticky.  The texture of the rice flour rolls was very good.  However, the rolls needed more soy sauce, and the sauce could be a little sweeter.  We'll definitely be back to try other items, especially after the official opening, when more items on the menu are available.

I'm not generally a fan of chain restaurants taking over what locals used to do so well, but Blaze is simply really good at it. As someone who has medical dietary restrictions, I'm delighted to be able to order a gluten-free crust and dairy-free cheese. And even with these modifications, it still tastes like *real* pizza! Pro tip: When you order online for pick-up or curb-side, especially around dinner time, add another 10 minutes to the stated pick-up time. There is a disconnect between the app and the store's production timing. But no worries, it'll be worth it for such a yummy pizza.

This place is great. Literally the only place you can get authentic Korean food in all of santa Barbara. They have all your staple Korean foods like soondubu, japchae, spicy rice cakes, and more. It is located inside a grocery store that's owned by the same people so you can shop before or after your meal which is super convenient.   I ordered a less known Korean spicy soup dish with beansprouts, roots, and meat. It was perfect for the cold weather. The dish comes with your some of ur typical Korean side dishes; kimchi, potatoes, cucumber, and rice.   If you ever need a Korean food fix, this is the place to come to.

Ok. Just got two out of three cakes. The first was entirely eaten by the birthday girl. The second (just received) was chocolate with peanut butter ganache and chocolate frosting.   I am not a chocolate fan as it's usually too rich for me. But this cake was perfect. The cake melted in my mouth and the peanut butter filling was just the right amount that I could get a hint of it.   I have one more cake to go but I am so excited.

This place is great for breakfast. It's light, airy, bright and has a clean casual ambience. Service is great.  The corn beef hash was SOOOO yummy.  Good prices and has a great bar.  An overall great place to hang out and a great location on state street.

man, I hate giving such a cool looking place a bad rating.  Our server was totally fine and service was not the problem...it was the food and the cleanliness of the place.  There are dust bunnies all over the place here.  Just about everywhere I looked above the tables...ours included, were huge wads of belly button lint.  I was just waiting for a big chunk to float down into my bisque.   I think this was probably the worst food in this price range Ive had in a very long time.   I have no idea why I ordered the 'fried food platter', but I did and I regret it.  Coconut shrimp was terrible, coated in a weird sesame, panko, coconut exterior that was totally burnt, tasted nothing like coconut, and Ive never had shrimp jerky before, way overcooked.  The fried calamari was equally as bad, overcooked and the crispy batter just fell off, plus the old oil flavor really shines here.  The fried scallop was passable.   Best thing our 4 top had was our lobster bisque with puff pastry, Id order that again, only of they cleaned all the dust first.

Literally some of the best Mexican food I've ever had from a hole in the wall spot. The salsa options are all so good and worth trying - even the habanero one. The fish tacos, while not on the menu on the wall was great and the fajitas are great too.

If you don't know, now you know. Bring extra cash and not because it is expensive but because you are gonna want seconds. Everything is great, the speed of service is fast, so do not let the line keep you from this amazing place.   I recommend one of everything, the salsa is great too.

We ordered takeout from here through the yelp app. I was really happy with our order! Chicken kabobs are delicious! The kid's meal with the chicken kabob is one of the healthier kid options I have seen and my son really enjoyed it. The Israeli salad was awesome! So fresh and tasty!

First time here, I met the owner and he gave his reccomendations, Ask what the deli know for and he said moms PLT with a smile. So I sad let me take the full and add the combo, paid 14.07 out the door, while inside it smelt so good i could hardly wait. i have eaten half my sammich and this is awesome. bye off to finish rest. you need to go your belly needs to fill this good comfy food yoru tongue will thank you, im going back sooon! with family! and more bellys to feed

I have now been there 2 times and both times I have found the pizza not to be as good as Lucky Penny or Tre Luna but to make matter worse, the staff can be snotty and think they are doing you a favor seating you......really...lol

Deano's has history.  This place has been on the Mesa for ever.  They make an excellent pepperoni pizza and they have one of the most unique recipes for crust and pizza sauce.  It's the kind of place you either love or hate, but you should definitely try it if you haven't.

First let me say We go here once every couple of years. We HATE Santa Barbaras state street being closed to vehicular traffic. Even that could not spoil eating at Joe's. We started with a simple green salad that had perfect lettuce and tomato with a divine housemade balsamic dressing.  The Charbroiled salmon here is our favorite and the best salmon you will have south of San Francisco. Perfectly charred outside, moist and flavorful inside, excellent almond rice and perfectly grilled vegetables. This was followed by Blackberry pie alamode with slightly tart berry filling, buttery crust and rich vanilla ice cream. In spite of all the covid-isms the staff was friendly prompt engaging and excellent. Mayerling our waitress was a deelight to chat with.

First Time here  We made a reservation for Sunday at 5:15, for our family of 5 (3 kids).  It was the perfect time.  We were seated outside and although the space heaters turned on, any later, the kids would have been cold or too tired.  Upon arriving, there was a line to enter (even with reservations) and were seated quickly.  We started with both refreshing drinks: Horchata and a Costa Blanca (Gin and Tonic).   The kids had the option of a grilled cheese on rustic bread and fries or chicken tenders with fries, the kids were happy with their comfort food but we were excited that they tried other things as well!  Ordered: -juicy manilla buttery clams with crusty bread for sopping up the broth. -vaca which was a delicious strip steak and buttery potatoes cooked on a cast iron. -tortilla espanola an egg dish with chorizo. -verduras paella was the highlight in that it was seasoned perfect, crisp perfectly cooked rice, and juicy eggplant was a great last dish to end our culinary adventure after the dishes preceding were savory, rich, or acid forward.  Of course, to reward the kids after sitting through our adult dinner, churros with three dipping options (de leche, strawberry, and Mexican chocolate).

Originally came in for dinner but Happy Hour was great and we were too full for dinner!  We sat at the bar and bartender was very patient and super sweet! She made a perfect gin martini! Pulled out an iced glass and added  more ice to glass while she poured my husband's beer.  So now the glass is Super chilled and she just rocked it! We ordered shrimp tacos, fish tacos and oysters on the half shell! I don't know what the fish tacos tasted like because husband devoured them! My shrimp tacos were amazing! Just the perfect amount of heat!  Can't wait to go back and have dinner! Thank you Killer Shrimp!

So this place doesn't open until 3 pm for dining but the menu looked pretty spot on. It does however open at 11am for retail purchases and if you go right away you will likely get one on one time with a wine pro. The decor is gorgeous - it feels like such a magical place and then you see the library ladder and walls of wine and it takes your breath away.  Eric is awesome - knows his stuff. Give him your palette pleasing words and he will guide you to the right wine. So wish we had been able to meet Brian when we were there too but there is always next time.
Mother's Day brunch was amazing. A bit pricy, but well worth it. Food and atmosphere was perfect. The staff is absolutely amazing as well. Highly recommend.

Before visiting Santa Barbara this past weekend, the last time I ate at The Palace was in 2002 when I was attending UCSB and my father took me to dinner while he was in town on business.  I was a little worried that my positive memory of The Palace was a bit jaded by being a starving college student.    At my dad's request, we had dinner here this past Friday night.  I had called to make a reservation but they only take reservations for 5:30 on Fridays.  We decided to have an early dinner, worried that we would have a really long wait without a reservation.  As it turns out, they really didn't start getting busy until around 7 so we probably could have dropped in around 6 or 6:30 no problem.    I started with the Cajun martini.  It comes in a pint or quart.  I went with the pint and that was plenty.  It was spicy but not overwhelming. Definitely worth the $10.  We were served a selection of muffins - rosemary, molasses, jalapeño, apricot...and one I can't remember.  My favorite was the rosemary although I didn't try the jalapeno because my honey and father beat me to them.  My mom said that the apricot and molasses ones both tasted like dessert.   I ordered the crawfish crabcakes (entree).  They were amazing.  My mouth is watering just thinking about them.  Crispy outside, tender inside, not greasy at all, beautifully seasoned and topped with a delicious jalapeño sauce.  They were served with fresh veggies (artichoke, zucinni, yellow squash, carrots) and rice.  I honestly didn't even get to the rice because the veggies and crabcakes were plenty for me but I appreciate the gesture.    We spoke with the manager (?) who was very welcoming and personable.  Our waitress was also very nice and we never had to ask for anything.  She had it covered!    I am now sure that my positive memory wasn't the least bit jaded.  The Palace serves up delicious food and beverages in a very fun and festive atmosphere.

Update: I just came in and had the most delicious, un-pretentious, delightful meal. I had the egg and bacon on Parmesan brioche. A Parmesan brioche? Yes! It was so crunchy and perfect, the eggs light and fluffy. I got to meet the owner and his team, and they're all so friendly. It's like having the best of French baking without having to deal with flying to Paris. They're doing some really fun things in the kitchen, I took like a dozen things to take back to LA.  Original post: I haven't eaten here yet, I just give them 5 stars for their food porn, the pictures are beautiful!

Ok this little cafe is phenomenal.  It's located in a kind of random, up and coming area.  All the organic, local, healthy deliciousness one could hope for. The smoothies come in glass jars with a $2 deposit that you get back when you return the jar, or just keep filling up your jar. Reminds of a growler from my local brew pub. The salads are all good. And, my current favorite is the chicken and sauerkraut on sourdough. The sauerkraut appears to be feature of the cafe and im guessing the owners own recipe. I love fresh, raw sauerkraut so this was a huge bonus for me. Nice selection of Kambucha too. Although, a place this cool should have Kambucha on tap. I highly recommend this place.

Santa barbara had a limited amount of places open late for food. We've wanted to try this place for a while and would only recommend it for drinks and late night snacks. Spent $200 on food for wife and me + one pint + 20% tip on togo order and was barely full with no left overs. Wife had Juan ceviche trio, wings, guacamole, and tacos. I had tacos and the Nashville chicken. The tacos were tiny for the price. I thought they should have at least thrown in beans or rice or something to make the tacos feel like it's worth the price. Elsewhere, these tacos would have been less than $6.  The Nashville chicken was two flavorful chicken tenders.  I liked how they gave me lots of pickles, the sauce had a good heat level and it wasn't overly sweet like a lot of other places.  I don't like shoe string fries, so can't comment on them. The flavor of the steak tacos were not bad but just over priced, you can see the size comparison next to the lime. The fish taco was ok, not the best. It could have gotten soft on my car ride home, but flavor was OK. Wife gave me her salmon ceviche because she doesn't like fruits in hers, and some of the salmon pieces tasted fishy. The ceviche trio comes with a taster, but you can't take it to go, so I asked to have them while I waited for my order. They forgot, so I had to remind them at the bar.  The tasters are a pilsner (netflix &pils) and an IPA. Since I couldn't try it together, I would imagine the pilsner going well, but not sure about the IPA.  Sometimes IPA can taste bitter and ruin foods. I wouldn't mind ordering the Nashville chicken again and having a beer or two, but not the over priced tacos

Pretty great pizze. Nice salty crust and good choice of toppings. The beet salad wasn't roasted enough, so it was still a bit crunchy, but the arugula and parm were tasty. The butterscotch budino was different from most i've had, but it was still flavorful.   One should probably arrive early since it was packed when we were leaving. oh, and they were playing a oldie Tiziano Ferro song when we arrived!

When we came to pick up a take out order we ordered 20 min before via phone, we were refused service because they were closing. The food was already packaged and ready and all we wanted to do was pay for our take out order. They refused. We placed the order at 8:40 and came to pick it up at 9:00. Had I known we wouldn't be able to pick it up I never would have placed the order. Both employees were just rude to us when we came in and left no explanation for it. Strange.

I've been here about 5 times now, and i'd have to say it's a solid 5/5. There's a great porch area with toasty heaters and a nice view of the harbor, the service is good, and the food is delicious!  Here's something I suggest; 1 chicken dish or a salad that could suffice as a dinner (like a chicken salad type thing). My husband LOVES this place but every time I go it's mainly because he loves the seafood so much (which he raves about). There's things to order FYI - like I just do a small garden salad and have them make a cream sauce linguini entree, with a little parm - kind of like fettichini (sp)  I guess you could say. So I am really hoping that this place one day there could be a non-shellfish dish and it would cause us to come here even more often :) I know it's silly to insist something like this, but it would just be really nice to have an alternative!  Regardless, my partner always says his food is a 5/5 so this review is from how he reviews his food, and where i'd solidly put this place! My 'alternative' dishes are always good so that 5/5 comes from there too.  That being said I think you will really enjoy this place! It is nice!

Recently ate dinner with a party of six. Food was ok. Waiter was knowledgeable and not overly attentive. We really hoped it would be more casual , less exclusive feeling and less expensive. Not a place for the locals to drop in at and have a quick knosh or a coffee. We live w/i walking distance.

Ordered two chicken cobb salads and a chicken sandwich to be delivered. The description of the salads  says cheddar cheese but the pic in the menu showed blue cheese. We were hoping for the blue, so were super confused when the salads came with swiss cubes of cheese. Never had those in a cobb before, but I guess it is in the restaurant name so .... But then, no pickle!!! I know their pickles are very good and would have made up for the strange cheese choice in the salads, I guess. Menu said dressing would be ranch and  believe we received a dijon type dressing. I used something I had from my frig instead.  Trying to support local restaurants by ordering delivery, but it's hard when you don't get what you expected and there isn't any recourse. Is the kitchen aware of what the online menu and pics even show? I am willing to pay the extra delivery and service fees, but sure would like to receive what was advertised and ordered.

The food is good, no argument there, but not worth the service. I've had my order messed up twice (two separate occasions), and honestly there are better, cheaper tacos in Santa Barbara with excellent customer service.

Let's see... My wife's bagel sandwich was completely cold and my $14 'Montecito' lox bagel was a total rip off. I'm sorry, but for $14, there better be a good amount of lox. This thing had less than an ounce. IV Bagel charges less than $10 for the same dish, only gives you more lox! Definitely not worth the money. Also, anyone who owns a bagel shop should know that you always put the capers ON THE CREAM CHEESE! OTHERWISE THEY JUST FALL OFF!

Got the Santa Barbara Local omelette with hashbrowns and toast and it was damn good. I did ask for extra salsa, which definitely heightened the experience. Portions are massive and I got a huge dollop of butter that I didn't even come close to finishing. Overall a cool spot with a comfy local vibe. These are the kinds of places where you find the best breakfast!

We loved Jonathan  and we loved chad. They always checked in on us and made sure to take care of us. The mimosas are A gradeS just get a bottle of champagne it's cheaper the food is so good. The green salsa is spicy but soooooooooooooooo good I highly recommend it

This is a great place to just sit outside in their lovely patio, take in the beautiful ocean view, and share some really amazing dishes. We ordered the following:  Beef carpaccio: this is a dish I've only tried a couple of times, and am always hesitant, because come on, it's raw, and one must rely on the reputation of the establishment and trust that it's prepared properly. No worries here - the beef was silky, and the capers and horseradish meshed together perfectly. Chicken liver: I can't offer much help here as liver is something I've tried enough to realize it's just not my cup of tea, but those who did eat it thought it was quite good, so there's that. Chioggia beets: this was great, very refreshing and I loved the contrasting textures of the soft cheese, the crunch pistachio, the crisp daikon, and the tender beets. Really good! Carrots: my favorite dish, I wiped up that eggplant spread with each carrot, and think I finished most of the dish myself!  Brussel sprouts: this was right up there with the carrots, the tahini sauce is what made this dish pop, really good!  We also split the pistachio cheesecake and lemon tart, and the former was wonderfully delicious.   Our server was really friendly, and I think got a kick out of the antics of some on our table! The ambiance of this place was so nice that we ended up staying quite long, and never felt rushed by the staff. While a little pricey, I think the dishes and atmosphere warrant it.

Would you like to pay $26 + $4 or tip for a burnt torta, two drinks and terrible presentation? Then check out La Tapatia! $26 dollars shouldn't look like this. I'm this picture, the camarones a la diabla were around $13. Came with gummy tortillas. The flavor was there yes but the presentation was terrible. It seriously looks like you lift the lid up on the throne and this is what you see inside after a long night.   Never coming back again.  No vale la pena comer algo que está quemado y se parece mal.

Excellent food, coffee, and pastries in a charming and super well-located new location. I'm not an espresso elitist but I have to say, theirs is the best in town and I only come here for a reason: decadently rich shots of espresso, perfectly foamed cappuccinos and lattes, and they pair especially with those scones and croissants that send you to another plane of existence... so delicious! Like every one of the Jeannine's locations, you see the benefits of local business with caring staff and management: every member of the staff is attentive, friendly, and proactive with service.  However, what brought me to write the review of the new Jeannine's today was their INCREDIBLE LOCATION - right on the waterfront and with a big, gorgeous (off the street!) dedicated garden patio. They also have this amazing bar setup right above it - wow wow wow I can't wait to come back tonight for dinner! Ever since the downtown location in the plaza closed I was a bit bummed, because I used to love getting a mid afternoon snack and sitting out on their patio and decompressing before finishing my work/errands for the day, and this new location has once again provided another snack sanctuary for me.   Thank you to Lalo, the manager who has the memory and abilities of ten men, for taking a few moments to give me a quick tour of the new spot.
The ambiance was very nice, the waiters were receptive .I would recommend the crabcakes appetizers. The garlic bread was a bit bland though.

Consider this a preliminary review. The deli all of Santa Barbara knows is back and for the most part appears to be as it was. There's plenty of info available about the reason for the move, but ultimately this place was opened by Tino's family after he passed away. The Super Deluxes are back and they are just like you remember them. The only real differences I noticed were of course the new building and renovated interior but there was a slight decrease in diversified products on the shelfs. I did see plenty of high end and high quality Italian deli items, nice wine selection and many of same old deli/convenience store items which seems like a great idea given how they still cater not only to the lunch crowd but also offer some classic Italian deli items. You'll notice many of the same employees here, those same classic hand-painted super-Deluxe signs hanging above the counter and many of the classic italian dishes you expect to see in the case. It may not feel like the old location with all it's history, but this new location with light pouring in, clean shelves, new paint and new equipment is welcome while keeping the old recipes, employees and that sand which everyone loves. It's a good balance of the old and the new.
Big thumbs up for the La Tarasca sandwich. Ham, turkey, Roast Beef, two cheeses, fried egg, bacon, avocado, pickle, all grilled into sandwich. Fantastic deal @ $8.50.

We had reservations here on Monday for 5:30 PM and when we arrived, we were taken to our table. We were seated outside where they were set up along the street. I didn't realize that where we were was where the sun was so it did get really warm even with the sunshade.   I'm so bummed I didn't get the name of our server but he was great! He had good recommendations and didn't overcheck up on us. I love that he told us that everything is made from scratch so once we ordered, our food would take about half an hour.  I ordered the Bouchon Duck which was so good! I'd say that dish is in my top 5 of best foods I've ever eaten. The duck was perfect with the veggies that accompanied it.   My husband got the Grilled Center-Cut American Wagyu Ribeye. He said it was good but not a top contender like the dish I got.   We shared the blackberry lemon cake with ice cream and it was refreshing.   There are other items on their menu I'd like to try so we'll definitely be back. Also, parking isn't that bad on a Monday evening. We easily found a spot by Public Lot 5 and it seemed like we didn't have to pay for parking after a certain time.

Excellent, upscale, authentic.  Octopus was perfectly cooked, and lobster tacos so good. Great service, owners (Carlos Luna and Los Agaves team) on site checking in with patrons. We wish these guys success with their latest endeavor.
To be honest, not the most flavorful tacos I've ever had.  The chicken was bland, but the adobada was delicious.  Thanks for the experience :)

After walking by this restaurant most every day for four years, upon the recommendation of a friend, I finally tried Szechuan last night.  We tried the sesame beef, chicken chow mein, orange peel chicken, mu shu pork, pork fried rice, and the wor wonton soup. The sesame beef was very tender and flavorful, and the mu shu pork was some of the best I have ever had. The orange peel chicken, chow mein, and fried rice were pretty standard, but still pretty tasty. The portion sizes were very generous by anyone's standards. They told me that the order would be ready for pick up in 15 minutes, and it was.   Overall, zero complaints. This will be my go-to chinese place going forward.

This place is absolutely insane. I was skeptical when my old fave La Colmena closed and this spot opened in its stead, but keep your mind open, friends, because Tacos Pipeye is a portal to flavortown.   A+ fish tacos, crispy and flavorful, wrapped in thicccc homemade tortillas. They have a savage salsa bar and the owner/manager is incredibly nice. Prices are cheap -- 6 tacos for 10 bux? Bonkers. I walk here from work once a week and am never disappointed.

I was tempted to give this place only 4 stars due to the price, but after stumbling onto the 8:30 half off fest it surely deserves 5 stars.  It is lovely store, has a great produce selection, and a delicious array of pre-made options.  A really great find for travelers from Berkeley used to the Bowl and Monterey Markets.

Best Indian restaurant in Santa Barbara by far. The food is delicious, the service is pretty attentive, though the decor is a bit outdated and cheesy. The main down size is the small parking lot in the back which can be quite full during their busy nights. The restaurant is also very accommodating for large parties and you can call ahead with orders so you don't have to wait long for the food :)

My first time here, and salmon is my favorite, so that's what I was going to order. But theirs came with black rice and veggies like zucchini that I didn't care for. So I asked if I could substitute a little pasta for the rice. This was the middle of the afternoon, I was one of the only tables there. So the chef should not have been too busy to honor a customer's request. But he just plain said no.   Disappointed, I ordered the carbonara instead. And I thought it was rather plain. Very little flavor, quite dry, and it was strangely piping hot in some places, while cold in others - almost like it had been heated up in a microwave!   I expressed my disappointment to my server, who merely asked me if I wanted it heated up again. Instead I took it home and doctored it up myself, adding some butter, herbs, veggies, & parmesan cheese, and it was much better!   The interesting thing here is had the chef been accommodating to my very simple request, I would have probably loved the salmon entree for my lunch, and left a 5 star review instead of a 2 star. That's called shooting yourself in the foot. And in these tough times it doesn't seem like a smart thing for a restaurant to do.
Authentic and very good bistro on State.  If you have a hankering for French, this is a great choice.

I was excited to try this restaurant, particularly because it is Kimpton restaurant.  I've always loved Kimpton's many concepts over the years.  However, it hurts me to have to post a negative review on this place.  It just doesn't cut it.  I ordered the Shakshuka, which sounded good.  It wasn't.  I was more like a tomato soup with chunks of scrambled egg in it.  I had to ask for  a spoon to eat it.  I was also served extremely hot, burned my mouth and made it hard to eat.  Also asked for gluten free bread option versus pita, and they tried to change me $3 for it.  Also had the G.O.A.T. fries.  It said on the menu it was a 9-spiced blend, but it tasted like normal fries as the spices didn't accent the fries.  Again, disappointing.  Then there was the service, or lack of service would be more like it.  My server was impossible to find throughout.  I also had to flag down the busser to get any water.  This has never been my experience in a Kimpton restaurant before.  I think IHG needs to do some mystery shopping on this operation and then make some corrections.

This is an interesting place. It's located at the end of a road to popular camping spots. They have BBQ and music on the weekends. The store is pretty bare bones. Mostly drinks and stuff that you forget for camping!! They have a grill with hot dogs and fish and chips and the like. Interesting cast of characters. Bikers, mt.. bikers, campers, locals, tourists driving to Solvang,old hippies ;) The guy who was playing guitar, harmonica and keyboards on the patio was cool!! Had a ZZ Top feel to it. I've heard some really good bands play here.was pretty good!!. It's worth stopping  and checking out if you are in the area. Nice views from the patio deck too...

O.M.G!!! This place rocks! I absolutely love everything on their menu! We need more around town so everyone can experience what real food tastes like. The food is amazing and the employees are super friendly. Thank you for existing!!! You will see me daily

Awesome atmosphere with the windows pulled back and totally open-air overlooking the marina.  Great people-watching and fun conversations between tourists and locals at the long bar.  $10 clam chowder and microbrew.  What a deal!
I had been waiting for a birria taco place to open up in Santa Barbara for such a long time! Yona Redz definitely hits the spot!!

A fire pit, live music, delicious beer, and sales that support an eco-friendly, sustainable cause? Fantastic! This was the line on loop in my head a few nights ago at Night Lizard. After several visits to this State Street brewery, I can easily say it's one of my tops. Staff are friendly, beers are tasty, and the vibe--whether sitting indoors or out--is neat. Gotta love all those hanging plants.   Among beers tried are the Gnatcatcher IPA, Tiger Salamander Saison, and Monkeyflower Double White Ale. All are solid brews. I have yet to sample the food menu from Yona Redz, but look forward to it.   There's usually room to sit on the outdoor patio, a Covid-safer option that offers great people-watching. I love holding a tulip glass of a tasty beverage, seeing folks passing by and eying the place. Thinking at them 'yes, you do want to stop here!' I smile a bit when someone does.

Oh my gosh! My boyfriend and I are driving thru town and decided to stop for some coffee and what a good idea that was!!! The food looked absolutely delicious but we were on a mission for something sweet and caffeinated!! The sweetest lady helped us! She had vibrant red hair under her ponytail (im sorry i cannot remember her name ). She suggested the smores latte which was so super yummy and my boyfriend got a vanilla latte (obviously with oat milk!). She was such a big help and answered all of my annoying questions regarding the food and the ingredients in the drinks. I cant wait to drive back through for another s'mores latte!

Really outstanding takeout tonight! Their online ordering system is one of the easiest I've used - very user friendly and allows lots of customizing of the order. It offers a choice between curbside delivery or pickup from in the restaurant. I picked up inside, and my order was ready to go when I got there. They have excellent distancing set up and even have pens for signing marked 'dirty' and 'clean and disinfected'. The two cold items had been kept back in the freezer/fridge and they added them to my bag once I arrived.  When I got home and looked at my order, everything was clearly labeled and really nicely packaged. The food tasted amazing! This was the first Thai food I've had since March and I can't believe I waited so long. Sorry, no photos, we ate it all too fast.

Ordered the Lox and Bagel platter.  They forgot the onion.  Had to ask for more cream cheese which came in a little pre-packed container.  The bagel was toasted, but had sat so long before being brought to the table, it was almost stale.  Excellent service. Good views.  High prices.

Just a heads up- get on the waitlist on yelp before you leave the house.   We waited about 30 minutes when we got to the restaurant and then were seated. It was lovely ambiance   I got a strawberry margarita and a cucumber margarita, and the huaraches nopales (delicious) The bestie got luna de la mora, and a crab enchilada - yum And the fiancé got ceviche and two house margaritas   Delicious and fun on a Saturday night

Don't know how this place is getting four stars (as of this review on yelp)  Let's start with the pros and why they are getting more than 1 star: - Eco conscious  YaY.  Good job founders.  We need more eco conscious businesses!  I ordered: House soup with house salad Berry kale smoothie  Cons: -Bread is meh  -Salad is meh  Meh...like...meeeehhhh...like it doesn't inspire you to finish because it's so meeeeehhhhhh...super bland tasting.  If they had a dressing maybe that could help.  Oh they did have a little dressing.  Little.  Very little.  Soup was good but had a fly in it and I'm all for extra protein and I would still eat the soup tbh but it's just like I'm not out on the farm I'm paying good ol American dollars for what I believe to be a supafly free soup.  Smoothie was super sweet I questioned myself if they added sugar to it.  I thought this place was all natural yo.    Conclusion: Support eco conscious businesses but not at the expense of quality luckily in Santa Barbara we are blessed to have the luxury to choose between so many.  Unfortunately Ah Juice won't be on my list of places to go in the future.  Truly a 1 star spot if your going off taste alone.  Sorry if that sounds harsh.  Hopefully they can take this feedback and improve.

Definitely the best Sams To Go here in SB, and for good reason. Nothing fancy here, just simple and excellent quality sandwiches.   We did some catering for work and the sandwiches were on point and fairly priced. Sandwiches are large and very filling with bacon, avocado, fresh bread, and simples meat cuts like chicken, roast beef.   IMO Roast Beef is the best option. Not much more to say of a quick lunch place other than it's good lol.

This place is great! I went here on a Sunday morning and luckily beat the rush. The line got pretty long after we ordered our food. You order at the counter and find your own table. There are plenty of tables outside on the patio and inside. I had the breakfast sandwhich on brioche. It was so good and filling. I loved that the egg was thick but still soft. We also got a biscuit breakfast sandwich. The biscuit was even better than the brioche! It was tender, flaky and peppery. The pepper was unique and made it extra good. The coffee was pretty good too! I'd love to go back and try some of their other baked goods and lunch items.

Maybe an unusual review however... We were feeding the hungry some pizza at a nearby location and ran out of paper plates.  The manager, I think, donated enough plates to help. We are thankful for his generosity and compassion. Btw, I have enjoyed several meals at this restaurant.  It's simple, no frills, with good food at reasonable prices and great customer service. Color me happy.
We are loyal to Eureka in Santa Barbara. We have the same food every Sunday and sit at the same table. Food is delicious, service is outstanding. Special thanks to David. You are a gem.
Overall very good experience. Diversified, tasty, food portions, nice deco... would give 5 if a bit more space to sit.

This multi tenant concept is the future for Santa Barbara  IMHO. It offers a richer experience for customers like me and it costs less for each of the four businesses. It helps that the Cuban food is delicious and the cocktails are great. Although you pay separately you can sit wherever you like. And the servers will find you.   The beer area wasn't opened yet at ny first visit but that gives me one more choice for my next visit  I had the ropa nueva which was delicious. We split it two ways because it was a generous serving   The question i always asks is 'will you go back?' My answer is 'yes,  I am definitely going back.'

The only place on the pier (wharf) worth eating at. Shrimp tacos are sublime. Bar style seating with a few tables outside. The decor isn't fancy, but the food is great. One of my go-to recommendations for visitors to SB.

The brown butter and sage ravioli are amazing! The rib eye, free range chicken, and lamb are also delicious. It's a bit small, crowded, and loud, but that's how Italian places tend to be, I guess.  Either way, that's the only major downside (well, including that there always busy too!).

WARNING/UPDATE: The bartender at this establishment 'accidentally' increased her tip by $4 on my last visit. The manager assured me that they probably 'misread' my handwriting and that his employees are not crooks. Thankfully, he promptly refunded the $4. Changing the tip amount is fraud and should not be tolerated, whether it's $4 or $40. My advice to other customers is to take a picture of your reciept before you leave. I knew the service was bad, but this is criminal (literally).
What an amazing find! This corner has  been home to a lot of so, so restaurants, but this is the exception. Large varied menu. Everything we tried was a '10' easily. Don't forget dessert!
Friendly owners, cute ambiance and a great wine selection! The food looks amazing too. I'll definitely be returning to satisfy my Crush! :)

After a long day of bike riding, my husband and I decided to stop in for dinner. We were sold on the Mussels with Pork Belly. The creamy white wine broth was heavenly! I wanted to swim in that broth, that's how amazing the flavors of this dish was. If we hadn't ordered other dishes, I would have gotten another serving. Moral of the story - Get the Mussels!  We also ordered the Seared Scallops with Bacon Jam. Bacon Jam has been on my radar lately, so I definitely wanted to try it. The scallops were cooked perfectly. They were tender, succulent, and the flavors were refreshing to my taste buds.  We shared an entree because we figured we were ordering way too much food, and I'm so glad we did. The New York Steak was 14 oz and was more than enough to feed the both of us. This juicy steak was served with creamy scalloped potatoes and sauteed spinach. It was the perfect ending to our meal.   I will definitely come back again - and I really hope they still have the Mussels with Pork Belly!

Amazing!   Ive only been here twice because its more of special occasion place if you don't have the budget, but its worth it!  The atmosphere is sophisticated yet charming, quiet and romantic but also good for groups.   First time around I had the sole and it was to die for! So buttery! It just melted in my mouth! Easily the best fish dish Ive ever had!  The other time I had the sausage stuffed quail, a bit out of my comfort zone, but I am glad I got it! The flavors were delicious!   Judging by all the 'mmmmms' at the table, it sounded like everything we ordered was great!   I wish I come back more often!

I'm a vegetarian (if you already haven't noticed) and get envious when I can't try something because restaurants choose to make it with chicken broth or beef broth.  I'm talking about PHO!  I could never find a veg friendly place to try it. I went to visit my cousin and she suggested to go to Saigon because I haven't tried veitnamese food and I've wanted to try pho.  Is it sad when I say I dream of going to back to Pho it up again.  The place was nice, simple, and fancy enough for a first date, or the 100th date.  Customer service is slow, but let's get real.  I rather have slow customer service and good food then quick customer serice and crappy food.    Oh Saigon. I hope to see you on my next visit.

The deck and views from the dinning rooms at the El Encanto are gorgeous, but the food is sub par for a 5 start resort.  Other than the view, it was a really unforgettable dining experience.  The bar is tiny. There was a live band but the cover songs were simply a terrible choice for the audience and the singer was off key most the time.   In my humble opinion the only thing the El Encanto had going for it was the view.

We got to check La Paloma out shortly after they opened and really loved the food and thought the service was top notch. My partner was a big fan of the hominy cheese casserole when we came that time. I just ordered out for lunch today and wow.. I think the Burnt Ends is my favorite dish I've had there, the beans and slaw are a good combination. Love the rest of the menu and how they focus on California specialties. It's a tough time to be open for restaurants so we will be supporting them the best we can.
Really cool place, best pretzel in town. Kelly is the best !! The service is really up to best even though it's always super busy!
The hostess, a woman, said they had, 'no seats' when they had 4-5 seats open outside. Not too busy inside either by looking at it. Wouldn't recommend if you are looking for walk up service.

Love love love the sandwiches here. My go to is always the Robert Emmett with no cheese and my only complaint is that sometimes the sauce makes the bread too soggy.   If you don't like waiting  in line call ahead or place your order online. You can pay online or at the store and the service is great. One of the best sandwhich olaces in town!

Chile rellenos burrito covered in mole- one of the best things I've ever eaten.  Pupusa: decent, but low filling to masa ratio  Chips with mole sauce and queso fresco: amazing  Horchata: one of the best I've had.  Carne asada burrito covered in mole: Loved everything except for the meat. The meat was very tough.

To say that we were disappointed is an understatement.  I came with a large party (about 15 people), and we were escorted to a more private room off to the side.  It took over 30 minutes for any server to even greet us, let alone take our drink and food orders. It felt like we had been forgotten. One of us had to physically get up and wave somebody down. After we received our drinks, he never came back to refill our water.  Four people from our group arrived about 30 minutes later than the rest of us, and I was both perplexed and annoyed to see them receive their food before we did.  To add insult to injury with the poor service, my food was burned. I didn't send it back because at that point I was so hungry that I didn't want to wait another hour to have it remade.  An hour later, our server came by to take our plates and ask if we wanted boxes. 20 minutes after that, he came back - no boxes. We had to ask for boxes again.  We also had to ask for our check 3 times. I had to physically walk up to the register for them to finally print it.  I have never received such poor service in my life. Honestly, I've received better service at Jack-In-The-Box with stoned teenagers.  I was so excited about trying this place because I heard such wonderful things about it, but our experience was a major letdown.

I love variety, and that is one of the major reasons I'd be hesitant to move back to SB after living in a big city. That said, I'm always happy to see new, different restaurants opening up, especially downtown.   I ate here twice in 1 week... a lot of the dishes are variations of the same thing, but that same thing is quite delicious, so I don't mind. It seemed like the place was still growing into its new space a bit, but I look forward to see how it has progressed when I go back (you know, assuming it is still open when I come back, which, hopefully, it will be).   Highlights were the tempeh tacos (though HUGE garlic breath afterwards!) and the salad. The mixed plate (I forget what it is actually called) is a little bit of everything, so perhaps a good thing to go for if you don't know what to order. I never made it back for dinner, but they have different dinner and lunch menus and several of the dinner things looked amazing (Sama, serve them at lunch too!).   Fresh small plates with a different sort of flavor palette from anything else in SB. Give it a try!

Oh man this place is SO GOOD! I discovered them when they catered a friend's bday party and then I started craving it so I went for lunch! Until 4pm the tacos are served on made to order tortillas that are just amazing! The pastor is so good and I also like my veggie taco! Can't wait to come back

Unique and flavorful food! Had a great long meal with friends here. The plantains, beans, tempeh stir fry and Doro wat are total flavor bombs--just incredible. Worth going for those alone...tons of flavor and a little heat. The gumbo and lentil meatballs were good but not our favorites. Great service and a beautiful patio.

I was there on opening day and returned almost three months later to see if things got better or worse. I was with the same friend from opening day and a friend new to the place.  Unfortunately, since I no longer live in SB, it's hard to recreate the same conditions. We ended up going around 8 PM when it wasn't busy at all. I basically ordered the same thing - Mesa Burger with truffle fries. The food came out in good time. My friend and I noticed that the beef patty seemed to be a lot smaller than before.  My friend ordered onion rings and I believe she got four. Not a lot, in my opinion, especially if you're charged an extra $1 for it. My truffle fries were super oily and cold.  I just had a good burger from a different fast/casual joint (though not in SB) so my feeling is Mesa Burger will survive on location alone.

Heard about the Blue Owl from a friend!  Best lunch downtown I've had in ages. Great service, engaging staff and superb food. Try the Thai cheeseburger or the Fried Rice Salad. Way different and truly excellent! Added bonus...great atmosphere and super music!!

Fantastic beer (love their Leatherback Blonde and Gnatcatcher IPA), comfortable and cool atmosphere, and awesome outdoor/nature ect library! The outside area is great for people watching. And you get to support different environmental causes with each beer!

We went in June so we sat outside and watched the People passing by on State Street. The staff was courteous and friendly. Our waiter forgot a couple of items, our silverware, our straws, our water. Aside from that the service was great. They have these Fresh Crispy Chips and OMG a variety of three tasting flavors salsas. Classic Restaurant style, Green creamy, and a dark red oil Chile that packs a punch! For drinks we had the El Peruano: was like a carbonated grapefruit with a bitter after taste, different and fresh. The Oaxacan Daiquiri: Strong drink served in the classic daiquiri glass. The Margarita Pepino: looks refreshing tasted peppery. The Riviera Hermosa: Colorful Drink. A delicious blend of sour flavors. This drink is bomb!!!! For dinner we had the Filete Atiguo: Chile Relleno was on point. Mole sauce was very tasty, not a huge fan of the tortilla it came in and the Chicken was dry. The Chorizo beans were a bit Salty (good with the complimentary chips), the Nopal was fresh and on point, came with delicious cebollas.  Mar y Tierra: You can taste the grill on this bad boy... But it needed salt.  The Lobster Tail needed the buttery dip and Lemon, and the potatoes it came with were good however they looked like sliced beats or onions so the eye to flavor combination was off. (Update the photo on the menu guys it does not do it justice). All in all it was a good experience.
Great food.  We ordered in from Grub hub and this is far and away the best Thai in SB!!  The curry, mi Larb salad pad Thai and dumplings were gorgeous!!  Wow. Thx for a great meal!!

My husband and I had a nice time at Finch & Fork.  As vegetarians, the mains on the menu are exclusively meat but we did small plates and still ate well. We started with the cheese plate, which is my only negative because it wasn't very varied in flavors (I'm guessing it was white chedder, asiago and manchego maybe?) but it was still delicious and a nice presentation.   We also had: - Deviled eggs minus the trout - Fries - Mushroom flatbread - Macaroni and cheese minus the bacon  They are happy to remove meat items from dishes to make them vegetarian. My husband also had a fig-infused bourbon drink which he liked, and the pickled vegetables at the start of the meal are a nice touch. The service was relatively slow for a Tuesday in my opinion, but maybe this is just a laid-back pace type of place. If you're looking for a nice dinner spot in Santa Barbara I would definitely check out Finch & Fork.
I love this place! Coffee is amazing and the sandwiches are delicious! Staff is super friendly!

Here's something that will perk you up: Peet's '09 anniversary blend. Full of pop. Rich. Dark. Will get you buzzed in the A.M..Otherwise, their coffee from New Guinea always struck the right notes with me. Will it for you? I have no idea.

I have eaten at Denny's several times throughout my travels and never been disappointed until now. The food was medicre at best, the service was extremely slow, the waitress did not bring the order correctly and then took nearly twenty minutes after I had finished eating to bring my check. She never bothered to come back and check on me or she would have realized she had gotten my order wrong....or perhaps she knew she had and didn't want to face me. The whole experience was lackluster at best, the restaurant was extremely noisy and smelled like mildew. Not a place I would ever care to return.
Pretty good food, just had a wrap and it was delicious pretty much on Mediterranean or Greek style food around here. Petra's who had really good Greek dinners closed

This Four Seasons restaurant is in a nice setting, however, the food does not live up to the setting.  Dinner was a huge disappointment.  The pasta was awful.  Salads were simple and could be much improved.  Fish was not prepared very well.    Breakfast is better, the buffet was not very elaborate, though for $85 it is overpriced.  My son who does not drink paid the same price as an adult who could drink unlimited sparkling wine.  The pasta was cold, as was the dumplings.  Sushi was not very good.  One would expect better of the Four Seasons.
Delicious for breakfast or snack. I had a large, but a small probably would have been enough.

It's a long wait, but it's so worth it! If you like shellfish - this is a must in Santa Barbara. Not bad pricing either. I had crab cakes and the gf had the 3lb rock crab special. Hers came with a soup as well as the enormous crab. Mine came with two crab cakes, a small coleslaw, and some onion rings. Even the onion rings were crazy good! Even though it was only two crab cakes, they were really dense and filling. They also came with a side of dipping aioli which was a little spicy, and a lot of awesome. Everything is fresh made right there. You can see the crabs being prepped right in front of you. Service was more than I expected from a place so busy, so I was impressed. I think everyone should check this place out.

My dinner from The Black Sheep instantly put this restaurant on my best-meal-ever radar. It's truly up there with my Mom's Thanksgiving dinner.  Last night I ordered from The Black Sheep for delivery via SB Restaurant Connection. This was my first time ordering from The Black Sheep, and I went big, baby!  See list of items at bottom.  Each bite of my meal was something new and unique. I couldn't believe all the flavors, and how everything was paired together. When I had the beef bone marrow bruschetta, it included the best accompaniment (pear moutarde- don't ask me what it is) that acted as a palate cleanser. The potato gnocchi was soft and pillow-y, and there were decently sized pieces of lobster. Honestly, I don't even know what I was eating at times, but all I know is that I wanted more, more, more of it!   This was my first time trying many of these foods, including bone marrow, duck leg, and more. It was the best introduction EVER. I really, really want to eat here again, but it's on the pricey side for my budget, and it's even more expensive ordering through SB Restaurant Connection, so it's definitely a treat to be savored on special occasions. I cannot wait to work my way through their menu. :)  Appetizers:  -Beef bone marrow bruschetta w/ pear moutarde, ruby stripe mustard greens, orange port sauce,  -Potato gnocchi & lobster (w/ Maine lobster meat and roasted shiitake mushrooms in a tomato saffron cream sauce) Entrée:  -2 Duck leg confit w/ German style red cabbage, dauphine potato, mustard cream sauce Dessert:  -Flourless chocolate torte (Bittersweet Cocoa Berry chocolate torte, creme Anglaise and caramelized cocoa nibs) -Lemon blueberry crumble cheesecake w/ caramel sauce  Note the restaurant does not offer pick-up or delivery on Friday or Saturday nights per Restaurant Connection, so plan accordingly.

Wow. These are such great tacos. I've never had quesotacos before and the birria was great. They also have goat in Saturdays and Sundays so make sure to check that out. Really nice people and a wonderful spot.
This is the only option once you go past security. I thought I saw a deli before security. The tri tip tacos were surprisingly good and the tortillas were great.
When I want more than a taco from Lilly's, or when I'm on the Mesa craving mexican, this is the place to go.

I kind of feel like I never need to eat anywhere else ever again in Santa Barbara. Yoichi and his staff bring the best of Japan and Japanese flavors to the CA Central Coast showcasing the best of local and Japanese sourced seasonal cuisine. Our visit tonight has us longing to return to Japan, but with a satisfaction that a near future reservation will hold us over until our next trip. Kaiseki at its best!

Touristy Shops...go shop somewhere else! Touristy Restaurants...go east somewhere else! Touristy Views...lovely!  Perfect if you're a tourist...like me!  Walking is free, and Parking for the first 90 minutes is free...what a deal!  What more can one ask for?  The one unique extra is the Santa Barbara Sea Center which has a few creatures to visit and lots to learn about the local ecosystem...since we're ocean geeks, we enjoyed it, but average Americans will underwhelmed and bored.

We received a Groupon dinner as a gift from our son and his girlfriend. We were approaching the expiration date so we went on a Tuesday night around 6:00. I expected to be the only people in the restaurant, but about half the tables were occupied and the host said the remaining tables were reserved. He offered seats at the bar, so we happily sat at the bar.  We ordered the hot bread/olives, and artichoke soup. A perfect combination of flavors and warmth. The clear broth was so flavorful, my husband and I dodged each other's spoons to get the spoonful.For our entrees, I ordered the flank steak with mushrooms served on mashed sweet potato, and my husband ordered the scallop special. We switched meals half way through because both entrees were gorgeous and delicious. The Groupon included one glass each of white or red wine. We ordered another glass of wine each and creme brulee for dessert (not included in the Groupon). It was a surprisingly delightful evening. The restuarant has a warm, welcoming atmosphere and we did not feel hurried. We will return!

My family & friends often go here -have  for at least 15 years. The quality and presentation of the main courses, service and atmosphere are elegant, yet comfortably French. In this fast food era (ugh!), it's so nice to feel welcomed and confident in its consistently fine cuisine. Start with the hearty French Onion Soup - topped with meltingly delicious cheese and go on to anything on the menu for a wonderful experience.

Hands down best brewery in town! Great customer service & management, this place really has it figured out. They have so many great beers on tap as well as really good foods such as salads, pizza, and pretzels! Ben, a worker there is an absolute gem and is always so helpful when you are indecisive on what you want to drink! Thanks Ben for your amazing service each & every time!
A smart place to come & see live music. I usually get a tri tip san which on bbq days or I like to eat in the fine dinning section & get something rugged like rattlesnake ravioli etc.

As my husband gobbled up his steak, mashed potatoes, and vegetables, he exclaimed, 'This is really good!  You have to write a review!'  And so I'm writing a review.  ;)  As always, dinner was excellent, and, while it's not as much fun to eat it at home, home works for now.  I ordered the salmon, farro, and vegetables, which is one of my favorite dishes on the menu.  The online menu didn't list any of their desserts, but I was pleasantly surprised to find a case full of baked goods when I arrived to pick up my order.  I selected the dulce de leche cashew bar, and I'm still thinking about its buttery goodness this morning - it was crispy on the outside, almost gooey on the inside, and the cashews added just the right amount of intrigue.  Once I picked out the bar cookie, I returned to my car, and they brought out my dinner a minute later.  All in all, a great experience!

My husband and I were really delighted with the quality and originality of the food. Delicious and beautifully plated. We sat next to a father and his 29 year old son and made new friends. All of that would have been perfect except that the volume of the music was too loud. People were shouting in conversation just to be heard. and the louder the patrons had to speak the louder the music was, or seemed to be, played. One of the owners? Managers? asked how we were doing and I told him that the food is fantastic, but the music was too loud. He replied that 'That is our brand'. Really? This is Santa Barbara, and not New York, we don't need to be numbed by overpowering sound. The other 'person who seemed in management' explained to me that people like the 'energy' of the place. Of course, so do I. Energy does not equal volume. Energy is a great thing, brought on by the enthusiasm of all of the staff, and the quality of the product. They have that wonderfully, noise volume does not increase that. In fact I think that the quality of the food is so good that the too-loud music is a distraction. I have to say though, that the second person who spoke to me did turn it down a bit. That is all that was needed. I am sure that people who appreciate really loud music were not dissatisfied with the result. Maybe we will sit near the from next time, I understand that might help. Amazing food, regardless

This spot doesn't have the same hype as some of the other spots in town, but it's possibly my favorite Mexican food in SB. Really, really good burritos. Tip: I recommend choosing only one of either sour cream or guacamole (or neither)- rather than both - in order to maintain the 'bean density' of the burrito.

Delicious and fun environment! Always leave stuffed to the gills! A friend had recommended this place to my husband and I and it's now a special date night place for us, since we don't go out often. Great service to boot!

Hard to truly review during these covid-19 times. The have nice outdoor seating. The food selection is small but I do think they have a food truck that wasn't there or closed when we were there. It is dog friendly, kids are allowed outside and the service is good. The brews are amazing though across the board.

The Thai food here is good but not great. However, it's probably one of the better asian restaurants in SB. The panang seafood is really good. Skewered beef was okay but the pork skewers were better. The pork was tender and the peanut sauce was good. They also have tasty pickled cucumbers and chilis. Their paht see yew (spelling?) wasn't good and the 'Chinese broccoli' stir fry with pork was made with regular broccoli which was disappointing. The eggplant and chicken stir fry was very tasty. I would come here again, but just stick to what I already know is good.

Well it had been awhile since I've been back to the dining room at El Encanto and it's definitely lost a lot of its standards. We came for lunch and everything started great with our service from Tyler. My in-laws were with us and they loved the view so much they wanted to come back for dinner, and possibly stay the night so I asked to speak with Tyson who must have left or wasn't working because Jessica came to the table instead. She didn't know much about rates or availability for dinner or rooms, so I asked her if she could check on those for us and she left the table to find out (or at least I thought). We waited at our table for 45 minutes for her but she never returned, luckily I knew the bartender Trevor, so I went to ask him to grab Jessica and he said she was off for the day but he could grab Julio the night manager instead. I said no worries and sat down at the bar with my family, and Trevor introduced us to his new drinks on the menu which were the only highlight of the meal besides the service from Tyler for lunch. We decided to forego dinner at the dining room and had a lovely dinner at four seasons and walked along the beach for a nightcap. If you want good service find Trevor, Tyler, or Tyson. Good service starts with a T at the Belmond El Encanto's Dining Room.

Wow, I hadn't expected such a lovely meal at Via Maestra...it's been ten years since we've been here and will definately go back. The salad was fresh, the soup was delicious, and the fish was superb. The ambiance was charming and somehow not too loud despite all the tables being filled. A winner near upper State Street is rare...enjoy!

Was a vendor for Crushcakes. After the Thomas fire and proceeding floods nearly destroyed my business and I went 8 hours out of my way to support this business and then had the business owner try to charge back the transactions after providing free product I vowed never to support this local business again and will suggest others do the same. Cheating small business vendors who enhance your shop is no way to treat others....
Love this hotel but the valet needs a little lesson in manners... rooms are with good light and fun furniture.

We stayed at the Ritz-Carlton Bacara for 2 days celebrating our wedding anniversary. This is NOT your typical Ritz property but its price point is the same. The three stars is based on our experience and it not meeting Ritz-Carlton standards. I know they are trying their best under the COVID circumstances. We truly enjoyed our stay and made the best of it.   Pros--- -Beautiful views of the beach, landscape is romantic -Pinot/Paint day w/hubby was the BEST day ever! Nolan was an excellent host, Highly recommend it! -Angel Oak Restaurant was perfect & enjoyable for our anniversary dinner! Reservations are required  -Easy access & walking distance to the beach -Hotel offers activities but need to reserve (painting, beach walk, hike) -Pool reservation system was easy to do and a great feature but book ahead  -Friendly and attentive staff -Room service fast and friendly -Cart service -the property is very hilly getting to & from pool, villa, restaurants, etc -Heated pool -Not crowded & social distancing was adhered including cleanliness   Cons/FYI's  -Rooms are outdated -Musky room smell - bring room spray -Bed post sticks out and kept hitting it, be careful! -Music on our TV did not work -No fridge in rooms (nor coffee maker, glassware, nada). Wish we brought our own wine glasses, we did bring charcuterie board -Room service comes in to-go paper goods - heck no! -Chips & Quac $20 bucks I am still laughing out loud -Soda delivered to your room comes in a can -$7 plus room charge LOL -Check in line wasn't long but VERY slow -they are attentive to each guest -Pool bar/Room service menu limited and pricey - just drive down the road for fabulous eats

Food is delicious, service is wonderful.  I wish the menu had a few more choices, but everything was beautifully presented and tasty.  Expensive for what it is, but hey, this is Montecito.  The bread on the table is really really good.  Entree salads were substantial.

Someone just left a remark on a review I wrote awhile ago on Yelp. FYI-I am Dyslexic (high form) and Eye Site challenged. I guarantee- I Misspell and Word Burger... all the time...so either get used to it...or just do not read my writing. Do not challenge your sensitive sensibilities on my account. Being critical of a person you do not know...at all...and who has 'life challenges' ...Well it is a pretty ugly practice. I feel sad you felt you needed to insult and shame me in public view... For what many consider a 'disability'...I however don't call my self 'disabled'.It is a challenge...yes...and I deal with it all day ...every day. It makes me stronger and more compassionate. But hey-thanks for reminding me. People who judge ...like you...I think must be suffering from some kind of life issue-loss and/or disappointment ...why else be so cruel? And in full view?  Disappointment hurts. Don't take it out on others. It might come back to you. The Karma Boomerang ...always comes round.  Have a great day.

We had heard good things about Stonehouse and decided to take in Sunday brunch on a blissfully warm winter morning.  Having a light-herated morning, my husband goosed me as we walked up the stairs and amist my giggles, I glanced up to see the whole restaurant watching us.  I feared we were heading into a stuffy environment by the stares of discontent we received and after gently scolding my husband for the scene we just caused we made our way to the beautiful, cozy, romantic patio.  Little did I know when we sat down what a treat we would be in for.  A basket of perfectly baked pasteries was brought to our table along with endless delicious mimosas.  Let me tell you, there was a citrusy-type scone that was so light and delicate, that it sent my tastebuds into a new realm of pleasure.  Both of us delighted in making our way through that basket of delights--you will too.  Next, we received our entrees--I had eggs benedict, hubby had a lobster omelet.  Holy Jesus those entrees were good.  Perfectly cooked, ideal portions. Yum!  For dessert we shared a decadent Grand Manier souffle--PERFECTION!  This place is heaven on Earth.  Looking for beauty, romance and good food?  Come here.  I promise you, you won't be disappointed.  You have my word on it.

My husband and I were staying at the Hyatt Place and the breakfast there sucked so we went across the parking lot to Palapa who was already opened.  The food was excellent, taste is authentic and the salsa was sooo good!   The waiter was friendly and attentive.  If You are in Santa Barbara I highly recommend this place. Excellent food and prices were great!

4.5 Stars Even though I saw a shirtless guy at Abercrombie, someone barged into my dressing room at the Gap, couldn't buy a bracelet at Pandora cuz they were too expensive, had to sit at a dirty table in the Coffee Bean; I guess you could say my Black Friday shopping morning was fun. Thanks Paseo Nuevo!

This establishment is for consuming cocktails and illicit drugs in the bathrooms. Overall a decent bar with average cocktails and mediocre food. The staff can be friendly and warm at times. Checkout the shrine above the bar to one of the past owners that OD'ed in the bar years ago.

I like the place a lot and it's a nice new addition to the downtown beach area. I had heard of it but they have a great Axxess deal which got us in the door. We've been back many times now and the service and food is really good.
A nice cosy place. We went for a salad, we had the Chefs Salad which is very good. We had the Ortega Chile Burger and File Gumbo. The waitress was a bit stuck up but the overall experience was nice.

This place really hit the spot! They serve Chocolate abuelita and it was amazing! I also had a plate of mole and rice topped with queso fresco. The owner came over to ask how everything was and the waiter was super friendly.
Do not order take-out/curbside from here! Ordered last night and received my cooked seafood orders ice cold! un-edible food poisoning temperature !what a horror! Total waste of $50 dollars.

Always excellent, friendly service, and of course, it's just gorgeous.  The spa staff are very caring and it doesn't have that uptight, stuffy feel that many high-end spas can exude.  Lunch or dinner at the casual restaurant is always a treat, and their jitney driver Dylan always provides service with a smile and ready to pick us up after our bellies are full and bodies are relaxed.  Bacara's a great place to get spoiled and pampered for a day!
Excellent pinto beans & rice with tahini sauce. Lots of vegetarian choices. Great patio.

Good food with a cool, chic atmosphere... prices reflect great food, but you definitely pay for the ambiance. It's a go-to Santa Barbara dinner spot, but don't expect to be blow away by what's on the menu.

Living in Santa Barbara, Barbareño is a place that always comes to mind when we want to go out. I've been coming here consistently for years now. Went again during first week open, since lockdown, and were very very excited. Seating is outside only, service was, as always, attentive, warm and friendly. We had a 'first timer' in our group and without hesitation ordered the eggamuffin. A week later, he's still talking about the eggamuffin! The guys ordered cheeseburgers and were perfectly pleased, the gals ordered Acorn Papardelle and the chicken entree, both were especially memorable and of course, very tasty. Here's the highlight of our night, (of our week!): Wine and beer selections at Barbareño, consistently exciting. Whoever chooses, has exceptional taste in both. For our dinner, they had a beer on tap that blew us away for ever and ever. From Central Coast Brewing, in San Luis Obispo, their gold metal award winning 'Monterey Street Pale Ale.' This was special because we were THERE, at the brewery (1422 Monterey St), on the day they won the award, (10/2017). It was SO emotional for everyone. I will never forget people in the tasting room and in the live video of the Denver competition, screaming, hooting, jumping up and down, crying so many tears of joy. Plus the beer itself, exceptional. Since having it at Barbareño, we've spent a week trying to find more. Unbelievably, it's available in 16 oz cans at Trader Joe's ($4/can).  Monterey Street Pale Ale is an American-style pale ale winning gold against 198 other beers in the third most-entered category at the Great American Beer Festival competition in Denver, October 2017.

A recent return to one our my favorites in Santa Barbara did not disappoint.  The waitress made spot on wine and food recommendations based on the preferences of my party. Great pasta, fish and meet dishes.  We will go back.  Reservations are a must!

Get the scone, just get one! I almost got the chocolate chip cookie the other day, they are really good. It was morning aka breakfast time and I shamed myself into thinking I needed to fit in with the other breakfast eaters out there. I'm so glad my co-dependence kicked in at the best time. These scones are light and buttery with a generous amount of nuts and dusted with powdered sugar. If you don't like the scone then don't read any of my other reviews etc. I think the Honor Market won my scone business! Scone on peeps! ‍

Walked from home and had a beer and a quick bite last night and it was GREAT. Nice outdoor seating along both Cliff and Meigs side. Food was outstanding and there is a great selection of local beers on tap, especially pales and IPA's.   Owner is the same for the Mesa Burger restaurants here on the Mesa and in Goleta.   This is going to be a great new spot for the Mesa and something we've been missing ever since Cliffs & Co went downhill and then disappeared and Deannos went bust.    One thing to note is that there is not much parking, so if you're a local, walk or ride your bike.  Otherwise you'll probably have to park across Cliff at the Von's parking lot or across Meigs at the Lazy Acres lot, both of which can get pretty full around 5 pm.   If you're a fan of flavorful foods, you have to try the 'cauliflower' with the shishito peppers, pickled onion and tonnato sauce - amazing.
This place is amazing! Great service and even better food! We frequently come from LA and we found our little gem of a lunch spot! Thank you!

I'm super bummed that I'm not the first to write this review, since I was one of their first customer's the day they opened! But too many drunken nights at Mad Dogs has resulted in my forgetting to actually attempt writing this review. Until I read one of my fellow Yelp buddy, Terri H's, Pink's Hot Dog review and suddenly started thinking hot dogs (and subsequently drooling all over my work laptop).  What do I love about Mad Dogs? The owners ROCK!!!! Eric and Kristen are the kindest ever, running a laidback staff and both work really hard every night to service huge lines of drunk people. My first foray into their establishment was in the middle of the week when my best friend and I decided to play hooky from school and go grab a couple of margaritas on the pier. Except we stopped short when we saw the words 'chili dog' and opted for a beer and ooey goodness instead.   We discovered right off that the owner and his wife had the same name's as my best friend and her older brother = immediate bond. He served us two beers on the house, hand delivered our food and from there spent a good half hour chatting with us about how he got started with the idea to open the place.  The dogs are delicious - I know because the first time I had them, I wasn't drunk! Especially when covered in Hormel chili and spicy nacho cheese and sprinkled with real cheddar cheese! The giant pickles and churros are also the perfect touch - especially in Santa Barbara where good ol theme park/carnival food is hard to come by. The sausage options also proved to be tasty over time.  Everytime we booze in SB, we hit up Mad Dog's. Halloween was the best night, showing up in our Chicago Catherine Zeta Jones and Renee Zelleweger costumes after a night of karaoke to do a small skit for free dogs! My love for this place goes beyond managing to skip the entire line and have fabulous VIP service even in the midst of the biggest 2am rush hour. It's personal and I hate to think of all the great nights I have missed since leaving SB almost two years ago at Mad Dogs.  ...But I'm sure my waistline thanks me for it.

I HATE HATE HATE this KFC.  But it's the only one in town, that I'm aware of.   Maybe they're doing me a favor by making me not wanting to go there.  But as a member of the Overweight Jerks club, i need a little KFC thrown in me once a while or otherwise, they might revoke my membership.  Where to begin...The quality's not there.  They've screwed me on sides. They've charged me for gravy that's supposed to come with the meal.  The service..eh, kinda blegh.  The  weirdest thing that happened was one lady worker was telling another one in spanish that there's an 'emergency in the back and that she needed to go back there immediately.'  I wonder what happened back there.  I'll never find out.    It's always packed in there, despite it being kinda shitty.  I have to wait in lines to get screwed over by these jerks one way or another.  I gotta find another fried chicken source... Oh, who am i kidding, i'll be there next week... Take me now lord...lord sanders,,,
