GOOD. Just GOOD. The ahi tuna tacos are absolutely no doubt my favorite. The triple play is an insane deal, 3 tacos for $9 omg! And you can't beat how close it is to the beach!  Being right by the batting cages is a fun little part too, I have definitely spent more time at the batting cages because of those tacos than any other reason!  Amazing!

Food is very good, for those wondering, it is almost the same food as Los Agaves except for presentation and few items of difference and price (they are significantly more expensive). The drinks are what makes this place very unique, they are very tasty different drinks most made with natural ingredients that make them taste very good. Definitely recommend.

The Jake's Bloody Mary's were the highlight.   We ordered a Santa Barbara omelette and  Breakfast Burrito, neither were picture worthy and I'm usually a big food picture junky.  The burrito was just okay but lacking in eggs, it had plenty of cheddar, avocado and black beans. The omelette was lacking eggs and was served one sided and was watery, very strange, the server said it shouldn't be that way but didn't do anything about it.  The hash browns, blueberry corn muffin and Bloody Mary's were the highlights, overall I was disappointed.

Awesome Mexican food. We have many choices here in Santa Barbara when it comes to Mexican food and this is in the top five in my opinion.  Definitely a local favorite. I love the veggie version of the tostada salad and the rajas con queso tacos as I don't eat meat. All the food looks great.

Celebrated my boyfriend's birthday at The Lark this year and no regrets!  We indulged quite a bit since it was a special occasion =D  Great beers to choose from and creative cocktails were accompanied by a wonderful popcorn snack while we decided on what we'd have to eat.  We got pork belly, crab gnocchi, and pork shank.  Of all these, I'd recommend the crab gnocchi the most, it was out of this world. With as much as we spent, I do think the dessert should have been comped for his birthday, but oh well.

2.5   I absolutely love wood fired pizzas, so when I saw this place open up I was really excited. I have to admit, the wood fired part is the only good part. The dough was really bland. The cheese became watery and the pizza was soggy instantly. It was just blah. The service was really apathetic as well, which didn't make it much better.
New Orleans style atmosphere and food.  Very good.  Everyone was celebrating birthdays the night we were there.  Will definitely return.
Husbands 'spot' to watch the games although I've grown find as well as it's a great place for bringing Kids and the menu is yummy- fried fish and chips is pretty delish there.
Delicious small plates, good prices, lovely service. Can't wait to go back

I had a Greek Scramble, with spinach, tomato, onion, bacon, and feta. It was good, and everything was fresh. Serving sizes won't leave you stuffed but are still filling. Friendly service. Prices are a bit high, but considering the neighborhood it's to be expected.

The food is solid here. Fresh, seasonal, and although the menu may not always change- there always seems to be a farmer's market special that is delicious. The charcuterie seems to be the chef's expertise, so don't skip this dish. There's love on that plate. Service can be a little slow, but we always go in prepared with that knowledge. Don't be in a rush. Patio seating is nice, a little awkward listening to the kitchen noises coming from Arigato. Brunch is also delicious. Shrimp & grits, please!!

So we came here today thinking that we were going to celebrate my best friends birth and guess what...   there is no Velvet Jones on a Thursday. Go here on a Thursday I've never been here anytime but just warning to everyone else who want to come have a great day it's not open. Ok

Wow! Just had brunch. Service was great. Food was so delicious! I had the juevos rancheros & my wife had the pozole Verde. So very good. Their bloody maria is perfect. Definitely will have brunch here again.

We use to be regulars at Petrini's, we really liked the low carb options they had, such as substituting broccoli for the pasta.  But the last time we were there was our last visit.  The quality of the food has gone down quite a bit and the cleanliness of the restaurant was quite lacking.  Regarding the cleanliness of our table the main area was clean, but the back area where the shakers were was not.  It was obvious the shakers had not been moved in quite some time, and the table was just wiped down in the areas they could get to.  The back edge of the table was quite dirty.  So were the shakers with a layer of film of who knows what.  We won't be back, sorry to see this place go down so much.

Ooooooh I just love Tupelo Junction! It is so fun and the food is so fantastic. The last time I was back in SB, we decided that we'd rather do brunch at Tupelo than try one of the many, highly recommended newer places, and I don't regret it at all. The hush puppies were fantastic, and I especially loved the spicy tomato jam with them - so much that I bought a jar to take home. My Bloody Mary came with a claw!! I wish I could remember all the fabulous things I've eaten for other meals at Tupelo, but the point is, it's all great. Southern food at it's SoCal finest.

Be forewarned, this place seems to close early on a whim if the owner, Norbert Schulz, is not there. Once my friend got there at 9:15 on a Saturday night and was told without explanation that they were closed when their website clearly stated 10 pm. I recently had friends and family in town on a Monday night and thought it would be a great place to take them for some excellent casual food and a beer. After checking on their website, Yelp, and Google, all three claimed they closed at 9 pm. Well, we got there and the window was closed, all food and utensils were already stored, and the girl behind the register window told me 'We're closed.' I looked at my watch and it was 8:36. When I mentioned both their own website, Yelp, and Google said they served until 9, all she told me was 'Our website hasn't been updated since we opened and I don't know what Yelp says.' WTF? Great customer service, huh? Needless to say, we left in a huff with yours truly blasting expletives throughout the complex of beer bars.  That irritation aside, the food here is mostly flavorful, creative, and inexpensive for what you get. I wouldn't expect anything else from long-time local culinary master Norbert Schulz.  So enjoy the food and the beers served at Topa Topa and Lama Dog in the same building - just call ahead to make sure you can still order some food when you get there!

Delicious options galore. I stopped in to get a few items to take back to our hotel and could not help myself - I think we bought one of everything.  Every single item was tasty and what we didn't finish that day made for an excellent tasting snack the next day.  We will be back.

Its unfortunate to write this review because I like the food here however this review is from 3 weeks ago. I made an order for take out in which I bought 2 sets of bread along w 2 lbs of shrimp, I like to dip the bread in their garlic sauce because its good. I picked up my take out and when I arrived at home I discovered that I bought 2 sets of moldy bread. I called the Drunken Crab and spoke w I believe is a David told him abt the bread I even told him id provide pics. He didn't want to reimburse me or provide another set instead he said ' that next time I will get my money back for the bread'. Next time? With this moldy bread and lack of customer service to make things right there will be no next time. What I should have done is called the health department because this is unacceptable and makes me wonder how fresh the other food is like the seafood.

Way better than Brophy Boys.  Wayyy better.  Sure, it's a lot smaller.  We waited about 15-20ish minutes to get in, for a 2pm lunch for about 8 people.  we had to be split up because the restaurant is so small.  no problem.  Ordered the clam chowder.  Not bad.  Not overly filled with cheap potato (I'm looking at you Brophy) and large chunks of clam.  I prefer the smaller variety of clam as I find the flavor better... or at least if they had cut up the clam, it woudl've been a bit better.  But a decent bowl of chowder for sure.  I ordered the rock crab to share with my girlfriend.  we were both full at the end.  Fresh and perfectly salty and briny...  I'd go back again given the chance to try their other dishes.

If you're looking for a casual restaurant to have lunch then this is a place you should check out.  There's plenty of tables and outdoor seating. We came here for lunch. You can seat yourself anywhere. The waitress was really nice.   Their menu has salads, sandwiches, burgers, BBQ type dishes, and some southern dishes.  I tried the Chicken & Waffles. You get one piece of boneless fried chicken over a fluffy Belgian waffle with syrup on the side. The chicken was breaded perfectly. It was crunchy on the outside and moist on the inside. The portion was just enough. It wasn't too heavy.  There's plenty of free parking in the lot.  I would love to come back and try the other items on their menu.

The food was ok. I would not make a special trip to sample any of the food again. It was just average tasting.  Spicy Chicken Saute- Mixed Greens, Red Onions, Peppers, Tomatos, Avocado and Garlic Dressing. The chicken was not spicy, slightly overcooked, and dry. The Salad portion was very meager. It was served in a 10' diameter bowl. There was just enough greens to cover the bottom of the bowl. Very pricy for a $14 salad.  Service-Excellent. Very friendly and cordial service.  Parking- 90min street parking available.
Interesting cocktails in a chill atmosphere. Not too loud or crowded or overrun with college kids. Tell the bartender what you like and it made a difference.

This is the best sushi I've ever had in my life.  DEAR HEAVENS.  HAVE to try the box rolls- one of my favorites the the Basil Fluke.  Seriously incredible tasting.  Even the miso soup is top notch.  LOVE

As others have written, this is the BEST Indian food in town! I think it's my favourite Indian restaurant that I've been to (even outside of SB) because the ingredients always taste so fresh. Food that feels good for the soul!  A great spot to eat if you are vegan or vegetarian.

Ok, first of all, I love Santa Barbara and some of its magical culinary finds. Les Marchands is a bit of a mixed bag on the outside looking in. It is owned and run by the same group that manages 'The Lark' in the newly minted Funk zone, 3.5 stars...  The location vibe, energy, and overall feel are a solid 4 stars, the food is an expensive hodgepodge of a generous 3 stars. The food is basic albeit dressed up to appear better than it is which is nondescript. My lady friend and I experienced this first hand which was unfortunate.  We started with the chef's special sauteed mushroom cheese plate with freshly baked french bread which was great but not, wait for it $50 yes you read it correctly $50 fitty bucks... Omg, seriously people it was a $20 plate with a hefty price tag on steroids. sadly it went downhill from there. The Buffalo Cauliflower was ground into a paste, rolled in bread crumbs and deep fried. The beet, hazelnut, arugula salad with Duck confit was a joke. It was advertised as Duck Confit with a small side salad when in actuality it was a small side salad with even a smaller serving of not very good Duck Confit.  We ended the meal with a dessert tart that was simple but good. Sadly we left hungry and disappointed, but now we know. The worst part is the wine list and wine bar portion of the menu. Les Marchands is a wine bar bistro but they do not serve flights and only have a handful of glass options as it's mainly bottle service. The kicker is the wine list is ridiculously expensive for average wine at best with a twist.  As you guys know I'm not a drinker but my lady friend is a wine connoisseur and laughed at the prices on the list which for most Cabs started at around $90 and immediately went north of a $100. We both felt deceived when speaking with the waitress after our meal about the wine bar concept and she said that we could buy the same bottles 20 feet away wait for it at 50% percent less than the menu price and pay a $20 corkage fee and have it served at the table. WTF seriously that's not cool or at the very least let the diners know this when they sit down or better yet just reprice the wine list accordingly.              Aside from average food and a $50 cheese plate this left a very bad taste and feeling about Les Marchands and the manner in which they operate. We will not be back as a result. Which is unfortunate as it has a nice vibe and feel to the space and a great outdoor patio, which is always wonderful in Santa Barbara.
Some of the best semi-americanized mexican food in town! Great salsa bar too

This is a decent, New York style pizza. By Santa Barbara standards, they make an excellent pizza. The thin crust is a bit thicker than I prefer, but in contrast to most SB pizza the crust actually tastes good, as does the sauce. They also serve calzones, sandwiches, and pasta, and I will be back to try them as well.  Nicky's seems oriented towards take-out, although they do have a few tables for eat-in meals; however, they do not serve beer or wine.
The food and service has always been good everytime I came in. I would recommend this place to everyone.  The only thing they might need is to have more indoor seating.

I spent way too much money here during the summer, when I'd stop by and grab a latte on my way to work. Even though I'm not working as often now, I still treat myself on Sundays because the lattes are the best I've found in Santa Barbara. Perfect whether sweetened or left alone, they're a great starter to my day (and end...and in-betweener...). On that note, the vanilla latte is especially good as well. Not sickeningly sweet with just the right amount of vanilla.

Usually love this place and it's my go-to sandwich shop when I'm in the area but today was disappointing. I got a ham sandwich and in between pieces of ham there was some sort of liquid, sticky white stuff. Wasn't mayo.. Idk but it seemed weird. Took a few of those pieces off and went back to eating. Then came across multiple pieces of spoiled lettuce. They were black.   I was so disappointed because I'm so used to the freshest of ingredients when I come here. I hope the situation is rectified because I love the staff and the food! I'll give it another shot soon and we'll see what happens
Luna Grill is a great 'Go To.' Fresh ingredients, everything I've always gotten there has surpassed my expectations. Although a 'chain' it is AMAZING!

Before Santa Barbara had Jamba Juice, it had Blenders. An SB staple since the mid-90s. The Original is always great but my favorites are the Red Pineapple, the Tropical Pineapple, the Red Orange or the Tropical Fuzz.

I wish I had known about this place sooner. The food and drinks were delicious!! I had the spicy shrimp with pasta in vodka sauce. My hubby had the Bolognese pasta. They were so good! Cannolis for dessert! One of the best we've had and I'm Italian. Service was great! We will be back!

We heard good things about Sun Sushi, but unfortunately we had a bad experience. The most disappointing was the sushi. Both such rolls (Box and Rainbow) has no flavor. The fish was too cold and very low grade sushi.   We've been all over the world eating sushi and even lived in Asia (Japan for 2 years) for more than 6 years. This was some of the worst restaurant sushi we've experienced. When we got home we both had to eat something to get the taste out of our mouth.   We're heading back to Arigato Sushi -- they are consistently great with sushi.
We didn't have a reservation and was able to be seated on a busy night. The host and staff are super friendly and the food is good.

I love love love this place. Being a true local, we have to appreciate the non-State Street spots that don't attract tourists. Brewhouse has accomplished that task! Yummy trippels, yummy food, and great atmosphere. I haven't had ANYTHING off the menu that wasn't absolutely tasty. I'm a huge fan of the turkey sandwich with caramelized onions and avocado...melt in your mouth delicious. Then there's the filet mignon enchiladas and the potato crusted salmon. All just perfect. The beers are outstanding and if you haven't tried it yet, go for the habanero shot! Burn your mouth craziness.  I miss home!

I ordered a shrimp quesadilla tonight. Im pretty easy going with food but I was outraged to find most of the quesadilla was veggies (including celery!) It wasn't appetizing and I was craving more shrimp and cheese.

So far been here 3x in the past 2 months. Found it off Groupon and it easily became my favorite restaurant. Breakfast is amazing. Best pancakes in Sb in my opinion. I've done the pancake special 2x. Most recently went in for lunch and got a pesto chicken sandwich. Got a side of tortilla soup too. Both were amazing and perfectly done. The tortilla soup was similar yet a lot better than my other favorite tortilla soup in town from Kyles Kitchen. The staff is wonderful. I'll be coming back a lot and recommending it to people stopping by in town. Highly recommended too for groups of people who cannot agree on what they want. They have a pretty large menu they execute well.
UPDATE!!! The manager is still an arrogant prick! He decided it would be fun to remind me not to come back! Don't worry, Stephen. I had absolutely no intention to

Super cool Atmosphere inside with a great beer selection. And its walking distance from the train station so if your taking the train to SB or leaving on it from SB and you have time to kill this is the spot to do it at... The food looked good but I wasn't hungry, will try it out next time for sure.
Best pizza ever and Dusty is so personable! I came in from outta town and was pleasantly surprised. I can't wait to come by and try their other pizza toppings!!

I LOOOVVEEE SIILLVVEERRGGRREENNSS! love love love it! i just can't get enough. everytime i go...and i go often...i try something new and am amazed!  most recently i took my dad, mom and family friend who were visiting me in santa barbara. i ordered the 'threesome' and got half the turkey sandwich, garden salad and a cup of their tortilla soup. that soup is to DIE FOR! its so freakin' delicious and nutritious.  my parents come up to visit me every once in a while from LA and they love going here and the NUTRITION INFO they print on your receipt! its so fun to see and find out what your eating. i feel like a more insightful customer and really appreciate that they do that.   i love silvergreens and you will to! try it!

Basic divey sports bar. Super cheap drinks, but reeeaallly good bar food! I got the tri tip quesadilla and it was cooked to perfection with lots of tri tip and cheese. It also came with salsa and sour cream. I've also had the french fries which were pretty salty and reminded me of McDonald's french fries. It can get rowdy here with all the different games going on (ping pong, beer pong, flip cup, pool) plus there is usually a younger crowd.

This was one of my favorite places to come before the shutdown.  It's a cute place with sofas and tables, perfect place for a date or just to hang out and grab drinks with friends.  Can I just say, you HAVE TO get the seasoned french fries.  By far, the best fries I've had, and I am a french fry snob.  Service is friendly and all, too. Right now, they're just doing takeout but I can't wait till everything opens again.

This thai food is SO good! I had the pork spare ribs, it was tasty and moist. The cucumber salad was tangy and sweet, a perfect compliment to the ribs. The table also got the drunken noodles, which was also wonderful, not spicy at all though.  The atmosphere is playful and hip.

Good falafel, and it's nice that they have different flavor options! We got the crispy falafel and original, since sadly they had run out of sweet potato flavor when we got there around 1pm on a Sunday. We got the falafel plate and babaghanoush plate. The hummus was good (in the falafel plate), the falafel was crisp and flavorful, and plenty of veggies on the side. The babaghanoush was good, not the best I've had, and the pita was good, but also not the softest that I've had. Overall a good spot, a bit pricey for falafel but not too bad. I would be interested in coming back to try the sweet potato falafel.

I give this a 5 star based on: Location,creativity,flavors and staff Location: East Beach,facing the ocean. Creativity: Happy Hour offerings.. really good prices for what U get. Local Craft brews etc The luscious - gooey dish :Jamon Crocette= divine Fennel sausage pizza was large by wood fired pizzeria standards.. great crust... sorta Napoli' Flavors : see above,add the little desert cup yum Staff:The 2 servers were attentive without overdoing it' Especially professional and competent was Matty..she looked like 'Big Little Lies'Jane Will definitely return.. just to sit by the huge fire pit that faces the Sunsets The American Riviera at its best

Schwarmi bowl is ooooooo Soo good!  I paired it with a local brew ( James Blonde). Service was great and atmosphere amazing.  Meat in the bowl had some tough pieces; nothing's perfect.  Located off State Street .. easy to find location Full Bar Dog Friendly

Overall, this is not a bad restaurant. I am writing this review during the time of the coronavirus COVID-19 so all dining is alfresco.   The  outdoor dining space of this restaurant is nicely designed; it has umbrellas to keep the sun off most but not all of the tables. So, I would suggest that you come at a time when you can have a choice of tables because there are a few tables where you'll be forced to eat your meal out in the sun. This is, of course, in reference to daytime dining. At night, you would be fine.  In terms of food, they serve a broad range of Americana. Overall, the food is well prepared, but it's one of those restaurants where the menu presents nothing exceptional. Certainly not bad, but not noteworthy.   A few things to mention.... First of all, as a fan of coffee, the coffee here is not very good. At five dollars per cup, one would expect something at little bit better. It is very strong, bitter, and it is lukewarm at best. This appraisal is not based upon one single cup of coffee but on the various cups of coffee I've had here. They really should step up their game when it  comes to their coffee.  The other thing which I feel I should comment on is the fact that they are not always fully stocked with what is on their menu. For example, on my most recent visit, I ordered a dark beer. Something, that the waiter guaranteed that they had. A short time later, he came back and said they we're out of dark beer. It's not really a big issue, but I believe if they say they have something they should have it in stock.  All of the staff here is very nice though not all efficient. For example, on my most recent visit the server asked if we wanted more coffee, which we did, he did not return with the pot for fifteen minutes even though there were several other staff members handling the other tables.   Overall, the high points of this restaurant are that you get to sit on a nice patio on State Street. The atmosphere is nice and the food is pretty good.
Very good Indian food, good portions, good service, good value.   Forget about the overpriced tourist infested alternatives downtown; and come here if you have a taste for Indian.  Recommended.

5 minutes into our breakfast, a fat roach emerges from the coffee creamer and dances across the table shortly before my mom orders her coffee. Not really a complaint, but The hot chocolate I ordered is put into a glass container so I can't hold it in my hand and have to drink it with a straw, like a milkshake, so it's a little odd. The place also has a weird smell in the morning, so I'd recommend sitting outside. Other than that, the food is pretty good, I've been here a few times, but since this is my first time inside, and I saw a roach, I feel like I should report about this.

Wow! Wonderful service and best of all, amazing food. This is the perfect place to tickle your taste buds. We had the gado gado salad, Brussels sprouts, chicken bao and the Branzino dish tonight. That branzino dish keeps me coming time after time. It is an incredible Indonesian dish!  The curry at the bottom of the fish is out of this world. To be  honest I have been dreaming about this for the last week and my only mission tonight was to come for this. The fish is cooked to perfection, it will not disappoint ! The spicy curry is like no other. Drinks are delicious and unique too and the dessert is memorable. We had the banana fritters, a sweet center ripe banana fried with sweet flour and topped with chocolate syrup. So fresh! They're like little donuts. So delicious! What a way to end the night.  The only negative thing I have to say is that they automatically add 20% tip to your bill no matter your group size (only two of us). I hate that I don't get to decide how good my service was. It was kind of written into their menu so by the time you read it you're already sitting down and it's too late. When did this become the norm :(

The Hubbster & I were headed to a show at the Santa Barbara Bowl up the street from this joint & needed a quick and easy place to eat prior to the show... and we were pleasantly surprised!  The decor is so-so, but typical of a hole-in-the-wall Asian restaurant.  The food, however, was super yummy.  Now, some of the reviews state that this is not authentic Thai food - honestly, I can't really vouch one way or the other as I have never dined in Phuket.  That said, we ordered the pad thai with shrimp, panang curry & pineapple fried rice - all were yummers and the addition of fresh pineapple in the rice was a huge bonus!!  The pad thai was different from others I've had, but very good.    People have also said the service was poor - now, they're not in tux shirts with ankle-length aprons, but I found the service to be good enough.  Casually friendly.    Go, Eat.  Enjoy & be sure to see a show at the Bowl thereafter!!

Hands down the best Chinese food in Santa Barbara, by far. The food was just spectacular. I always try Chinese places with a classic, like Mongolian beef. Ordered Mongolian lamb, and it was amazing! Tasty, tender, good ratio between green, regular onions and meat. Had a shrimp and a chicken house specialty, and they were special!

My wife and I had come here for the second time and we both agreed it was the last time.  We both spend some time in Spain so the bar was very high to begin with.  Still, the food is only average at the most but the prices are outrages. I know Santa Barbara is an expensive town but, come on, don't charge Michelin Star prices when there is no such experience.  The employees were very nice and observant though. No need to complain here. However, I guess when it comes to SB there are only a few restaurants that can deliver an outstanding customer experience.

Two words: Adobado Torta!  There are various places in Santa Barbara I will say have the best burrito, breakfast burrito, or tacos, but El Bajio, hands down, wins on Tortas.  Specifically their adobado torta (marinated pork).  Tastes even better if you add sour cream.  The torta gets five stars, but there's not much ambiance as the cars hum by on Milpas St.  And they do have a good salsa bar.  El Bajio is great for grab and go as I usually call in my order on the way home or on the way to the beach.  If a torta is not your usual menu choice for Mexican food this will surely reward you for stepping outside your comfort zone.

A cool location on the lower end of State Street. It was on the slow side but busy enough to be entertaining. The music was low which made it conducive to conversation. The place had a vibe that was absolutely funky in a good way. We waited for seating and landed a table almost immediately. After being greeted by our waiter we ordered up a round of drinks. The waiter was a bit of a character in an absolutely good way. The drinks landed with three beers being a hit and the cocktail as a miss. We informed our server who thought we were pulling his leg. We had to impress upon him that there was absolutely something wrong with the cocktail as delivered. He came back and said the bartender insisted it was spot on. After a little more insistence the drink was remade and tasted as expected. The menu had a great variety of options without being over the top with options. Our table ordered up the New Mexico Cobb, Chicken tacos and a couple Nashville Hot Chicken sandwiches. The food name out in short order and it all looked tasty. The chicken sandwiches were very good. The New Mexico Cobb was nice as were the chicken tacos. About five minutes into eating we realized both the Cobb and the Tacos had no avocado. It seemed like a long wait until we were checked in on. When we finally were we asked why there was no avocados on two of the dishes. I'm not sure if the waiter was joking or being serious but we were told they were out of avocados. Hard to imagine that wasn't communicated and was instead left up to us to discover. Would certainly return for the beer, but would likely pass on ordering a cocktail or any food.

Great pizza, I love how everything tastes authentic and homemade, rather than the 'production line' type you get at other places. The wait can be longer than other places, so not the greatest if you're starving or in a hurry, but if you have the time and call ahead, it's well worth it.

Positive part is that food was delicious, I tried beef tacos and they were very nicely prepared. Location is amazing, they have outdoor seating which was very nice.. I thought service was a little bit slow, the person who greeted us at door was not super nice she did not even smile, and the table they prepared for us was cleaned in two seconds only. I will definitely go again for the food.

First time here and I got the steak burrito. The steak is hot and juicy. I didn't get beans in it so I got a lot more meat with some rice. It's served with a half a pepper, cucumbers on the side. You get red and green salsa which both are good.   Im glad I found this spot and will be returning soon.

Dave and his wife made us feel like VIPs every time we  visited the grill to take wedding day hot dogs for our guests. We love the food and the casual fun spin it put towards our wedding. The hot dogs are always great and I recommend Dave for any special occasion.
Best salad bar in town. Great baked goods. Delicious, fresh, home-cooking type food. Cozy, informal, clean eatery.  Many take-out options. One of my favorite places in town.
Ok, place is really cool, great ambiance, bartender was also very nice. The pizza simply wasn't worth $25+ dollars. WAY better options for pizza elsewhere for less.
I enjoyed the matcha latte and iced Americano. Chai latte was a bit too sweet.   Love that there is ample seating and wifi, I remember coming here to study years ago.

I wasn't impressed by Pascucci. The food was okay, but it didn't seem like authentic Italian food. I ordered the Venezia, smoked salmon with a lemon-cream herb sauce over bowties. It was good and filling, but it was a little to lemony and it didn't taste authentic. The bread that comes out at the beginning is a cheesy herb bread which was the highlight of my meal. I couldn't stop eating it before, during, and after my meal.  The service is eh, not very personable and didn't check in as often as I had hoped. The tables are quite close together and you can hear every word of the conversations surrounding you!! I hate when I have to squeeze by a table to get into my seat. Please just take out a table or two and make some extra space!!  It's decent, but if you want something more authentic I would try a different place. For the price you are paying and the amount of food that comes I think it's worth it.

Beautiful place! I came for an art show the other weekend and it was such a great atmosphere. Great wine and beer selection and customer service was excellent. Really looking forward to frequenting here more often!

Everything here is amazing! The layout is open and inviting, including the semi-hidden back patio. The food is sourced from local farmer's markets which makes any of their dishes even better than expected. I would definitely try the white shrimp, it was my favorite!

The first time we came to Lure it wasn't a great experience.  The food was incredible, but our server was really slow.  We asked twice for to go boxes, he forgot, and then just took away our food, including 80% of what was left of my entree.  I emailed the manager and let them know about our experience and they said they would make it right.  We came back tonight and we were really amazed at how much better everything was.  Like the first time, the food was incredible, but this time the service was great and very well-timed.  The manager stopped by to make sure everything was going OK.  We very much appreciated having our concerns taken seriously and they went above and beyond in addressing them.  The restaurant itself is also a wonderful ambiance.  Kudos to whomever did the decor, because it was really perfect.  Great colors, a very relaxing, warm, yet upscale, environment.  I don't care too much for seafood, but this place has definitely gained a spot on our must-dine list.

Probably the best doughnuts in town. Love the breakfast sandwich on a fresh croissant. And I am addicted to the pinacolda Boba. I want to try others but keep ordering the same thing.  The owner is a super nice woman who always greets you with a smile.
Yum!   Great place to grab a burger, watch a couple games and have an amazing beer.  We'll be back!

I never understood the hype behind macarons...until now.  These little cookies certainly pack a big punch.  We tried the chocolate, orange blossom, hazelnut, and the sea salt and caramel.  Nice flavor and good quality- no artificial crap here!  We also had the croissant and the almond croissant.  These were heavenly- tender, buttery, and super flaky.  They had a nice crisp on the outside, but a lovely soft interior.  I could eat them by the dozens.

This is the only location that I've tried, and I've tried it more than once, and I've never been impressed. The flavors have been pretty mild, and it's made me sick a couple times. I can see why other people like them, but I'm not about it...   And my roommate says they need to use more cheese

Well.  Well.  Well.  Popped back into my favorite burrito place in SB for a vegan burrito; papas, frijoles, avocado.  So good.  And they have roasted peppers that are delicious.  The staff here is so friendly and helpful and kind and generous.  The ladies are very sweet and warm.  They always greet me like I'm a friend who they haven't seen for a while.  Wendy rocks that burrito bar like its nobody's business but her's.  She's a gem!  AND the new interior is EXCELLENT.  VERY NICE upgrade.  Thanks Litos.  I'll always be back!

I've been here a few times and decided to bring my mom for her first visit. I usually always get the hash but this time I tried the kale and quinoa salad (although it ended up being spinach and brown rice by the time I got it). It was very good although they were a little heavy handed with the dressing for my liking. My mom tried the hash with the sweet potatoes and veggie patty. We both really enjoyed the combination and she especially liked the toast and jam.  Overall, I'd say that the food is tasty and consistent, a place I definitely think adds some flavor to SB's breakfast/brunch scene.

I went in at 9 am yesterday morning to get fresh salmon and tuna. The guy who works behind the counter taking cash from customers and yet handling all fresh meat without washing and wearing gloves even after I told him that I would use the salmon and tuna for sashimi. He was corky and told me that he know what he was doing. Also, the fish smells not that fresh and I end up throwing half of  the tuna away later. I don't think I would go back again.
Loved the Swedish meatballs, the folks running this place know what they are doing.  Trust me, go here, and you wont be disappointed.

arrived in Santa Barbara today and decided to order delivery ( our location is very close to theirs) Burgers were excellent ( but only warm) and hence the 4 stars...however the fries and waffle fries were stone cold......unacceptable Additionally there were no napkins and no tableware ( knives or forks)....found this to be strange in this day and age with food delivery  generous 4 stars because my wife did not want me to give a more realisitic 3

What price atmosphere? San Ysidro Ranch's two restaurants, Stonehouse Inn and Plow & Angel are literally right on top of each other.  In the beautiful outdoor garden, you used to be able to order from either menu.  The Plow & Angel had lower prices (sometimes they wouldn't give you that menu unless you asked for it). Lower prices didn't mean low prices but it was worth it for the great atmosphere, good food, good service, etc.  Now in the era of Covid, the menus have 'merged.'  That means that the Plow's more moderate prices are gone. The barbecue ribs which were $35 are now $43.  On the new menu, chicken is now $47.  Salmon is $48.  There is no salad below $20.  Many diners, including those paying top dollar for a fantastic cabin, probably don't care.  But it has priced some of us locals out of the market.

A wonderful experience. Incredible food and great service. Robert was an exceptional host. The food is on point and full of flavor. Check out the pictures to view the excellence! We will be back and highly recommend Opal for a great night out.

Wow! This place is awesome. We got beers (I picked a fruitlands sour beer that was delish) and sat outside on the lovely patio. There is a ton of shady and comfortable seating, ok for dogs to join as well. We then ordered food because I love anywhere with a plant based menu. The food is outstanding. The special sauce fries and burger and so good and you wouldn't believe they're vegan. The munchwrap was super unique and delicious. We were stuffed and happy. Everyone on staff was super nice and helpful.
I love this place so awesome great food,  great people and great atmosphere. Santa Barbara is lucky to have this place. Brunch and lunch hours only

Good food and lots to choose from. I always get the Gorgonzola Salad and the portions are quite large. The quiche is delicious as well. Great desserts and nice wines to choose from. Love sitting outside and bringing the dog.
AMAZING wings!! The best of the best! They also have great outdoor patio seating, a nice way to spend a sunny Santa Barbara day.

I really enjoyed Casa Blanca.  I have been to the happy hour a few times but recently had my first 'actual' dinner there.  I organized a dinner for about 10 people with really short notice (granted it was a Tuesday) and they accommodated us no problem.  I was a bit nervous to pick the place, seeing as I usually consider it just a 'happy hour spot' but the food was spot on.  I had two enchiladas with a cheese tamale and it was great.  Of course I love the happy hour drinks and really recommend the beer-rita.  It's a bit funky to have a corona thrown in the margarita but I say, just try it out.  It's not every day you can try that!  I see some reviews saying that the margaritas are not consistent but from my experience (and my friends) we all consider this place to have the best margaritas around.  I might even say each margarita is better than the previous one, but doesn't it always go that way ;) ?  I have had better Mexican food of course, but this has a nice atmosphere and beautiful patio.  I just wish their black bean salsa had some flavor!  I was so excited the first time they brought it out but it's pretty bland.  I say I'm a fan overall.

Tried them tonight for HH. Had to move into the bar to get someone to get service. Everyone was in the backroom eating and drinking. They should put a door on that area so customers can't see them goofing off.  According to one server the manager does not work Mondays and the service shows.
went again yesterday omg loved the poached eggs on salmon their toast was soooo good seasoned just right every bite was heavenly. thanks again guys but try to open at 630 am for us early workers

My favorite breakfast spot in town, but get there early. The yolo and tug boats are delicious and hearty. Good pastries and great local coffee from Dune roasting co. High quality and portions that won't leave you hungry.

I had some time before my massage at Float down the street so I decided to head over to Lilac Patisserie for something to eat.  I ordered a grilled cheese with bacon.  The grilled cheese came with tomato; which I wasn't aware of, but though it tasted ok, it made the sandwich soggy.  I know for next time to hold the tomato.   Overall, the sandwich was tasty.  For the price, it should come with chips or something but the sandwich is quite filling due to the thickness of the bread.

There's one in every crowd. I loathe Savoy, the most over-rated, over-priced, and over- crowded restaurant in town. The salad bar? What's the big deal? You can go to the equally horrific Whole Foods and get a far more wide ranging selection in an ambient seating arrangement. And parking in their super dangerous sardine tin parking lot is better than none which is what you generally get at Savoy as the adjacent lot is mostly mobbed.  You can even choose al fresco, as you can at Ralphs right around the corner from Savoy.  But I personally lean toward Gelson's these days, esp. on Tuesday - when they knock a dollar off the price.  I don't know whether the iceberg lettuce is heavy on gluten.  But again, I'm not sure if what's marked organic at Savoy is not actually hydroponic these days. The only justification I can see for going to Savoy is if your doctor has put you on a kombucha diet.  Savoy boasts the most extensive selection of this epicurean delicacy anywhere on the Central Coast right on its back wall.

This location is nice- highlight is patio fire pit. Everyone knows what coffee bean coffee tastes like right? It's less acidic than Starbucks and their matcha latte was delightful and lacked that powder-y taste they sometimes get when over ice. I recommend getting it with almond milk.  Service at this spot was great!

Ramen!  Thank god Santa Barbara finally has good Ramen!   The broth was rich and flavorful and the noodles were cooked well.  The owner even noticed I was giving my pork belly to my wife and came over to let me know that next time I come in to let them know I like my pork belly a little crispy and they can do that....what attention to detail.

Best hot chocolate in town, no competition. It's made with whole milk, and is creamy and delicious. Not too sweet, but sweet enough! Best pastries, and they will always heat them up for you if you ask. Breakfast is delicious. The hollandaise sauce is excellent on the Benedict.   One of our very favorite breakfast places of all-time, anywhere! It's delicious every time.
Great quality food, friendly service! New twist on tacos it is not the usual traditional taqueria, it's more like gourmet tacos. Love it!

Absolutely the best sushi I've ever had. I'm a travel blogger and have enjoyed sushi around the world at establishments of varying luxury and the creativity and flavors at Sakana simply can't be beat. They offer all the same basic rolls and nigiri as any traditional sushi restaurant, but then go above and beyond with their specialty elements. They craft the most unique menu items with exquisite execution. This is a place everyone needs to enjoy at least once in their lifetime.

I love love love this little gem of a place.  It is my favorite go to breakfast while visiting SB.  You must try one of their giant Yukon Gold Cinnamon rolls, you will not regret it!  I also fancy their Endless Summer Scramble with one of their tasty Bloody Marys.  I am a sucker for a good bloody mary that come with a whole thick cut piece of bacon!
Extremely friendly and attentive staff! Waffles are delicious. Only gripe is the coffee is horrible. LOL. Sorry guys.
Amazing taste, delicious and vegetarian. Great ambiance. Every experience here has been extremely memorable.

Ate here when they were a pop up in goleta and you had to pre order. Now they are in the public market and just as good as they ever were. You cannot pre order or order online at their new pop up. Their hours change so you'll have to check them on Instagram to keep up. But I recommend eating here, their friend chicken is so crispy and the tater salad is my favorite with bacon in it!

came here on jan 12 2020.  food was bland and small portions.  i ordered the smalllll chkn tortilla soup + entree for friend - $30  not worth it = overpriced.  would not recommend and no desire to come back.  there's much better in SB.

Had some friends in town for Alumni Weekend so we all gathered at the beer garden to kick off our night. Since most were from out of town, I was giving on the recommendations. Empty Bowl and Corazon were obviously the go-to's but some wanted italian so I suggested Ca'Darios new pasta spot in the back of the public market.   When they came back with our food, I was appalled at what I was looking at. The ham sandwich my friend had gotten looked like you'd get it sitting economy on united airlines and the Pappardelle looked like the sauce was from a spaghettios can; looked like it to. They also mentioned the service was horrendous and that the there were a lot of angry customers waiting for their food back there.   I just can't imagine Ca'Dario would put food like this out, this had to have been a bad day or some thing.

I asked what type of fish were used in their 'Fish & Chips.'  The waitress said, Basa Fish, from the Northwest.'  Hmmm... Basa Fish are from Vietnam and are a bottom feeder farmed fish typically from the Mae Kong Delta.  They eat toxic crap. Disgusting.  The only Northwest in them is they may have been shipped through the port in Seattle. They are used because they are cheap.  Harry's is one of the oldest Restaurants in Santa Barbara, 1 mile from the Pacific Ocean.  You would think they could do better.

When this opened in SB, it was all the rage.  Everyone told me I had to try it.  And I have.....about 8 times now.  And sorry but...it just isn't that good.  I gave them 2 stars because I couldn't give them 1.5.  3 stars for in restaurant service/food, ONE star for anything take out. Every time I have ordered the take out the order is wrong, very wrong.  This is particularly annoying bc I have a job that doesn't afford me a lot of time to go out to eat, so I have to order from work and pick it up.  Wrong, every single time.  And after the 1st few errors I have taken painstaking effort to ensure that they understand my non-complicated orders.  For example, you ask for a side of ranch, you pay for a side of ranch, it lists a side of ranch on your receipt.....but....guess what!?!?!?!  No ranch in the bag. When you eat on site the food is much better and for ppl who like a variety of beers it has a good selection.  There are MUCH better burger places out there that can get your order right. I tried to give them a chance, actually I gave them 1/2 a dozen...they just don't cut it.

Authentic Italian and so delicious I wish I could marry the chef! Why?! Why didn't I know about this place sooner?!  Usually I only have authentic Italian in Little Italy in NYC and I've searched for authentic in California only to be disappointed by other restaurants but this place is the truth!  I tried one of their specials on my first visit - the Ravioli stuffed with mushrooms in a truffle olive oil.  I can't even find the words to express how delicious this dish was!  The plate was devoured!  It had more than enough truffles and the pasta was so fresh and delicate.  The staff is very friendly and excellent on service!  Warm bread is served at the beginning of the meal and the dishes arrive not too long after ordering.  They also have gelato, sorbet and a wonderful selection of desserts on the menu.  They offer Coke products, San Pellegrino and other Italian drinks, and have a nice wine selection.  They also have items you can buy to take home such an condiments imported from Italy, olive oil, etc.  The restaurant is a little small but the interior and size reminds me of the authentic restaurants in NYC and I love it because it makes it more intimate.  I will most definitely visit this restaurant again!  My second visit will most likely be the same day as my first.

I swear the most perfect number to dine out is five, so we could split everything and it becomes a fun tasting menu. See, this time I went with two friends and we ordered less food and had bigger portions :(  sweet potato dumplings gorgeous pleating and the flavor combo was my favorite of the entire dinner. crispy sage, ah the best topping.   chili udon noods it was a fun treasure hunt looking for the shittake all plump from the sauce. udon retains sauces so well too. a gorgeous fiery red, the color of roast.   cheesy ribs this was exciting, a showstopper for the table. very hearty and flavorful. the best kind of leftovers. the sizzling cheese hardens fast and slides off the braised meat.

We went to this restaurant to celebrate my husband's birthday on Monday night. It was very busy. We waited about 30 minutes for the table inside. The view from the restaurant is great, however one cannot see the sunset, because it actually takes place behind the big mountain. There is a staircase you can climb on the beach to see the sunset apparently.  We waited 45 minutes for calamari and oyster Rockefeller. Manager was nice enough to buy us drinks because of the delay. Our main course came in shortly after.  Seabass and lobster mac n cheese were delicious, however steamed clams were very bad. We asked the waiter to take it off the check and she did. Great service and location. Make reservations to avoid the wait.

We thoroughly enjoyed our meal last night at Spice Avenue.  We met up with some friends for dinner before heading back to bay area.  One of our friends had never tried Indian food, so it was a great first experience.  We ordered Butter Chicken, Rogan Josh, Chicken Biryani, Naan, and raita for the four of us.  All the flavors were really rich and delicious. My two complaints was that the dishes could have included some more meat and the biryani.  I was expecting bone-in chicken that have really taken time to soak in all the spices and flavors in the rice and this version seemed like it just threw in chunks of white meat chicken into the rice.  Besides this we enjoyed our meal and would consider coming back when in town.

Been many times to eat in but I'm reviewing specifically because we picked up a giant order to cater a graduation party and it was a hit with everyone! The cashier was so helpful and nice. Everything in the order was meticulously and beautifully put together. We ordered ten of everything. They didn't miss even any tiny detail. It was delicious and lovely! We'll always remember that event for itself but especially for the FOOD. (The salsa is the best in the state.)
Bomb food and decent beer selection. I dig the small portions because you get to taste more flavors and they have lots of interesting flavors here.

I've visited the Lark several times this year, and the ever revolving seasonal menu is definitely a highlight, but my favorite dishes end up being the appetizers.  We started off with the West Coast Oysters on the Half Shell. You would never expect granny smith apple granita on oysters.. but they work! The acidity from the granny smith really cut through, and the caviar brought back that salty ocean flavor. Really delicious.  Next up was the Southern Pimento Cheese Toast. What. Is. This. Cheese. Toast. The smoked gouda and aged provolone coupled with the sourdough just brought the word savory to another level. Flavor was incredibly rich and voluptuous. The pickled serrano chilies could have brought even more out of this dish, but there wasn't enough, and the flavor got washed over by the savoriness of the rest of the dish. Still great as it came right after the oysters, but I don't think I could have enjoyed this as much if it came after another dish with rich flavors.  Our server hooked us with trying out the Roasted Beet Carpaccio. I had no idea this was even a thing, but clearly it is, and it's delicious. I don't even normally like beets, and this was good. You'd be amazed at what kind of flavors the sweet marinated asian pear brings out of the beets. The buttermilk-thyme panna cotta brought a great creaminess, and the dish was just beautiful to look at. I do wish there was more pear and puffed barley crisp on the dish. The barley crisp was absolutely crucial for texture, and three pieces was just not enough.  Next up was our meat dish, and we got the Slow Roasted Niman Ranch Ribs. Ribs were honestly my least liked dish out of the 5 that we had. Ribs weren't quite dry... but was definitely not as juicy as I wished they were. Flavors were on point, but the doneness of the meat was a bit of a letdown.  We finished off our night with the Caramelized East Coast Diver Scallops. The scallops were cooked perfectly, tender and just melted away in your mouth. The rest of the dish and the garnish, unfortunately didn't bring anything special. I wish there was more pop to the dish, but it seemed like they held back on the flavors a little too much to avoid overshadowing the scallops. I wish there was more of the fresno chile sambal to bring in some more life.  Staff is welcoming and friendly each time, and I love the creativity and the seasonality in the menu, but definitely wish there were improvements in execution. All five dishes and a couple beers came out to around $150 which isn't extravagant, but I think the execution left something to be desired for the price.
I've only gone one time, but it was great! I recommend the Toro Nigiri  cauliflower app was to die for. Rolls were great too.

Fresh and local delicious bread! I've had the Rye and Sourdough loaves, some pastries, almond milk lattes, and l look forward to trying a breakfast sandwich the next time I'm in town. The staff is friendly and some good tunes can be heard depending on who's working;)

We had reservations for Saturday at 7pm. It was busy here. There's outdoor and indoor dining. We sat outdoors. We ordered some rolls, 2 sashimi combination platter, and Miso Glazed Black Cod to share. The cod was to die for. It was melt in your mouth good. Sashimi platters were fresh. The rolls were a bit small though. We also ordered sake to drink.

Finney's.... I've lived in SB for 10 years and have only had one favorite restaurant (which closed) and have been looking for a new favorite. This place has it all; great allergy friendly menu, decent prices, great staffing, fantastic atmosphere, and great location. My husband and I had a wonderful time! I'm still not sure it is my favorite... I think I need to go back and try a few more things! But I will say It was hard to choose an entrée and cocktail because so many of their options sound awesome. The only two negatives: their bathroom was unfortunately gross... it wasn't bad but it makes the difference between 4 and 5 stars in my eyes and there was a 45 minute wait and they don't take reservations. We dealt with this by getting desert first and enjoying a wine tasting nearby before our table was ready. I would very much recommend this place for a casual date or a night out with friends!

Have come to this place for many years and has been in Santa Barbara for many years. Usually come during happy hour but also have eaten lunch and dinner here many times. Lots of choices in this area and in Santa Barbara itself. Located right across from the water with good views from the patio as well as the restaurant. Staff is on point and is there to serve you and help you with any decisions or questions. Come by check it out relax have some food and drink and enjoy

Went here for dinner and the food was on point. We didn't make reservations until the day of, and this place is so busy that the bar is not actually open seating, you have to make a reservation, which is where we ended up sitting. For cocktails I ordered the local, with strawberry, basil leaves, and vodka, yum! We started with the burrata, which was so creamy and some beautiful tomatoes, I think it would have been a nice addition if the tomatoes were a range of colors, like yellow and green, but minor point, we scraped up every bit of it including the balsamic type sauce. Complimentary bread and olive oil, and there was a range of delicious bread, so sadly we went through two baskets. I ordered the scallops which were a bit pricey especially with only four scallops at the $33 price point, but cooked to perfection. The lobster whipped potatoes were kind of a let down, I would recommend a different size or variety of flavored potatoes in the future. The bf got gnocchi which was so melt in your mouth yummy. We were too full for dessert although the bartender recommended Olive Oil cake, which sounded interesting. I would love to come back here again, although it would definitely need to be for a special occasion as this was a pricey dining experience.
The place was a 5 start at some time in the past, now they have removed couple of hot selling items to their other enterprise and now you are stuck with limited menu.

One of my favorite hotels in the world, and one of my favorite places to have a drink. For brunch, sunset, or anytime in between. Excellent service, fantastic drinks, stellar views. It's so cathartic!

Let's talk about the avocado toast at Goleta Coffee @goletacoffeeco MIND BLOWING!!! They make their own Vegan bread in house which is also gluten free!! They top it with their own house made savory seed mix which is insanely delicious. They source their veggies locally when possible!!  If avocado isn't your thing you can get your toast with smashed chick peas purchased from Baba's at the local farmers market. Oh, and did I mention they make their own almond milk in house and don't upcharge for it in your matcha/coffee!!! Goleta Coffee has new owners and if you haven't tried it, we'll just go!!!

Authentic food is served here with fast service. Belly-up to the bar and eat some tacos and wash it down with something cold. Good eats at a good price. Super nice staff to chat with. Great experience.

I hadn't been back to Scarlett Begonia since before their move so I'm glad to be back! We were able to call ahead and put our name on the list which not many places do so I was grateful for that.   We split with the table: the dirty fries, house mimosa (serves 2-4 per the menu), and a cinnamon roll. The dirty fries were straight up ADDICTING. I had to stop myself from eating them all. They're salty and tangy.... greatness. House mimosa came with what we thought was the tiniest OJ ever but in the end we had leftover.. rationing too hard. Cinnamon roll was soft, sweet, and HUMUNGOUS! Probably around the size of my head  I got the eggs benedict (bacon) - they have weekend specials which I assume change. The waiter told us the options were bacon, canadian bacon, and smoked salmon. The eggs benedict was fantastic. The sauce was buttery and creamy and the egg was poached to the beautiful runny yolk we all know and love.   PSA: after eating all this yummy food, you WILL slip into a food coma

This modern Thai food restaurant exceeded my expectations. I give the food three stars and a half stars and another half star for the comfortable and aesthetic location. It is in an industrial-style marketplace. You can get dessert and walk around this marketplace. I ordered three items: special fried rice, Drunken Noodles, and Thai Boat Noodles. The special fried rice and Drunken Noodles is definitely the best items on their menu. The flavor explodes in your mouth and you can't help up say yum! The Thai Boat Noodles has a super strong beef broth, it is kind of too thick for my liking. I have tried Thai Boat at other places in Los Angeles, and their broths are lighter. Also, the service was spectacular. Super friendly and accomodating. Overall, a relaxing place to enjoy food.

This is the first place we hit in Santa Barbara, and almost the last, since lunch was huge. I got the chicken burrito and it was a good healthy brick sized bean, rice, and grilled chicken burrito, and the salsa was la bomba. We couldn't figure out the red/orange seasoning on the chicken though. My guess was chili and tamarind. The chips were good and we ate fat for under $10. I wasn't hungry until dinner at 8. Buen trabajo SBCR.

I really liked Sambo's in the past and it pains me to write this review.   When we were seated I noticed how the table appeared to be falling apart. There was also a mess of garbage on the nearby floor from a previous party that was never cleaned up. Additionally, my knife was dirty and I had to ask for a new one twice.   Later, the side of pancakes I ordered came out cold. Our waitress didn't come by to check on us until we were almost done. I requested new ones and I eventually told her to forget it after it took over fifteen minutes.   Otherwise the food is good, but there is now a consistent lack of attention to detail. Their price point is high for breakfast food- almost twice what Denny's charges. It almost looks like they're making the business decision to trade on the ocean being across the street and letting everything else go. How sad.

Man, the burritos here are fantastic. The salsa bar is not as good as some of the others around town, but the tortilla for the burrito makes up for everything. Also, the burritos are big enough to share or to have for lunch the next day.   Ordering is quick and easy. Food is served quickly.
Sorry, but if you are a Jersey Mike's and run out of white bread, you deserve 2 stars. So sad.

We were delightfully surprised with the quality of the food. Fresh and delicious.  We had an access card, which gives you a buy one entree, get one free. When we pulled up to the address, which was at a hotel in town, I turned to my husband and said, 'I don't want to go to a hotel restaurant'. We decided to peek inside before moving on. There was hardly anyone inside, (but it was a Monday evening) Now I for sure didn't want to go inside. I thought it could be a bad sign that it was mostly empty. My husband coaxed me and said, 'We're here, we have the access card, let's just try it.'  I'm glad we did. I had the most delicious meal out I've had in a long time. Local halibut with fresh vegetables, and some kind of fancy mashed potatoes. (I forget what was in them) My husband had a seafood pot pie. While we were waiting someone came in for a take out of the pot pie. (apparently it has a good reputation)  Our server was lovely and had personality, which we also enjoyed. The prices are reasonable compared to other restaurants in town with similar quality.  We'll go back again. Even without the access card.

I could summarize this review in one sentence:    Come for the beer, not for the food.  They've got a decent selection of interesting beers that you could spend hours trying out.  But good luck hanging out and drinking beer with the buds on a weekend night and you're seated in the large dining room.  The music is so loud, you have to shout to speak to someone sitting across the table from you.  Between the three of us, we ordered a 6 oz filet, a beef stroganoff, and something else that I didn't commit to memory.  They were all mediocre and also part of an insanely LARGE menu.  Do yourself a favor and modernize the menu.  Keep it to two large pages and use your POS system to figure out what people order the least of and 86 it.  I would definitely come back for the beer and the patio and maybe even the music, but I probably won't be ordering any dinner.

Glad to have a takeout dumpling place in town. Pretty good. I imagine it might be better eating in than taking out but I've had the Xlb (soup dumplings), shrimp har gow and pan fried pork dumplings, pork siu mai, as well as the crab cheese wontons, baby bok choi & beef chow fun more than once and if I'm in the mood for dumplings, would definitely go back.

Came here after a wine tasting down the street. My husband and I enjoyed a series of dishes, from brussels sprouts (SO delicious -- citrusy, crispy, rustic), burrata and peaches (if you don't like burrata, you should just stop reading now), and then the tabouleh platter, which was outstanding. Definitely not inexpensive, but that's to be expected for Santa Barbara. Amazing service, enthusiastic recommendations. Would definitely visit again.

You're probably wondering why i ate at a place with only 2 and a half stars, yeah?  Well, i am too.   Just kidding. But really, it wasn't that bad. The 2 places my friends and i were planning on going to were both closed! They must have closed for the whole Christmas weekend. Boo :( ! So, yelpless and pretty hungry, we stumbled across this place and it looked quite nice from the outside. We took a glance at the menu and it seemed pretty promising. Burgers, salads, drinks, etc. Pretty basic stuff.  We were planning on going somewhere with a decent selection of drinks, but as soon as we got the drink menu (being as hungover as we all were) we immediately changed our minds, haha. It was sad. But for once in my life, water sounded so much better than a glass of beer. Weird. :/  Anyway, i got my usual veggie burger with sauteed mushrooms, grilled onions, and swiss. Except they forgot my grilled onions and i didn't notice until half-way through my burger. The burger was alright, definitely not the best veggie burger i've ever had, but also not the worst. My best friend and i also split a Chinese Chicken Salad, and we had to ask our waitress for some extra dressing/an extra plate like 3 times, and then she finally brought over the dressing, but no plate. Clearly she wasn't the sharpest tool in the shed. But service aside, the salad was really good. I was really satisfied and ended up ditching my burger and just eating the salad instead.   I doubt i'll ever be back, but for the record, it's definitely not as bad as people are making it out to be. Pretty solid 3 stars.

So we arrived late Saturday night (Valentine's Day), around 9:00 because we were coming from a party.  I had heard so much about the well appointed rooms, so when we arrived I was a bit disappointed.  It just didn't seem that special (maybe because I was comparing it to our recent trips to the Ty Warner properties, Las Ventanas in Mexico and San Ysidro Ranch in  Montecito).  True, those properties were much pricier, although Bacara can get way up there too once you start getting into the ocean front rooms.    Anyway, everything looked different in the morning.  The sheets whiter, the room bigger, the patio sunnier, the fireplace more ... well, more fiery and once that Santa Barbara sun starting bathing everything in its golden light, just gorgeous.  Our room was above the pool, and I was bummed that it was just so cold.  Because I loves me a pool overlooking the ocean. The buffet this morning was yummy, and good thing we sauntered down there a little before 9 because at 9 a.m., everyone started rolling in.  We had a late check out (4:00 p.m.) which gave us a lot of time to enjoy the property.  Hiking trails are everywhere.  Note to self:  hiking in high heeled boots is not a good look.  But I was not about a little footwear issue keep me from seeing the trails.  In fact, the resort wear here is definitely active So Cal style.  Pack your lulu lemons and cross trainers because there is lots to do.  Lunch at the Bistro was delish.  I had the grilled salmon with artichoke hearts and BF was a little less virtuous with the open faced steak sandwich which was just as good, if not more.  But I am trying to be virtuous here so I will tell myself over and over that I like salmon more than steak until I believe it.  And that I hate fried food (but I don't think I will ever believe that one).  I had a Swedish sports massage with Dawn Erickson and she was Fabulous.  I want to buy a towel warmer and lotion warmer and keep them in the house.   I want her to live next door, and put her number in speed dial.    The place was sold out for Valentine's Day, and I totally understand why (360 rooms or so).  True, there are some things about it that are a little geographically undesirable (you can hear the train, the resort is in a flight path and the beach is very rocky) but the property itself is LOVELY.    Bacara is a beauty!
Rather dull, unremarkable and cold food. Again, are they using a chef or is there food just inspired by one.

Well have you ever heard the customer is always right? I think in this case it applies.  I just spent $15 for chicken and a side salad. This is fast food!  Yard bird!!!  $15 seriously?!?!  Ok so for my $15 I said I can't eat white rice can I substitute couscous? They said nope!  Uhhh no I mean you can up charge me a dollar.  Nope!  So I'm paying $15 in a half empty fast food restaurant in a city that hates chains and you can't tweak your plate slightly. We seriously had a five minute discussion and they said they can't change it. They wanted me to pay another $3.50 for a side of couscous. So almost $20 for chicken?  And it's not even a 1/4 of a chicken.  Do you realize how crazy that is?  Make your menu flexible!  The girl helping me was super cool and desperately wanted to help. The manager not so cool, he didn't even turn to talk to me face to face.  I won't be back.  I've lived in Santa Barbara for my entire life. I work at the largest employer in Santa Barbara  and I work in an area where I'm constantly being asked where to go for specific foods.  If you want chicken?  Go to pollofino don't bother with these idiots.  Learn the first rule of business, unless your restaurant is full your customer is always right!  ESPECIALLY when they're offering you more money!  Idiots!!!  I only gave the second star for the cute, cool girl who took my order and the nice girl am who brought my food

Cafe del Sol suffers from a severe identity crisis. Is it Mexican, American or Italian fare? Few restaurants, if any, can successfully carry off such a varied menu offering and that is certainly the case here. The Mexican and American diners my date and I recently ordered here were both horrid and grossly overpriced as compared local restaurants that specialize in these cuisines. I actually felt sick for 24 hours post meal.  Avoid this establishment for food as much better focused ethnic dining options are available within the area. On the plus side, nice location, decor and attentive wait staff.

This place is amazing!! They have a giant beer list with even bigger beer glasses. The two liter boot is the way to go! The food was great as well. I highly recommend there sourdough pizzas and the Bavarian pretzel!! A go to spot when in Santa Barbara. Cold beer, amazing food right by the beach. What else can you ask for?

Very good sushi, great special rolls, amazing sauce. The restaurant is small but charming and has a SoCal beachy casual vibe, the service is usually great but when they get busy it becomes a little uncomfortable in there. Go early or go late for a good seat, try the yellowtail habanero press box!

Every Cloud Really Does Have a 'Silver Greens' Lining   If the recent rainy season has you running for cover, slip on into Silvergreens for a meal that is simply scrumptious!   Though their prices will have you dishing out a few more dollars for a similar dining experience down the street, I can attest to Silvergreens' much hotter and fresher ingredients than their less costly competitors.   I have now dined at Silvergreens twice and both times I enjoyed the appetizers and the ambiance immensely. I absolutely savored the Chicken Tortilla Soup and their colossal version of the Turkey Club which was thickly stacked with turkey, bean sprouts, tomatoes, lettuce, and bacon and comes complete with mayonnaise and mustard on the side.   And not only that--Silvergreens REALLY DOES satisfy. From those hankering for a hamburger to those simply in search of soup and salad fare, 'Silver's...' menu is chock full of everything from healthy 'greens' to hearty beef and beyond.   So, whether your wish is to stay green and lean or to bulk up by eating everything this eatery's menu mentions, you will doubtless be enchanted upon your exit.

Turn that frown upside down! We too started off iffy with the owner but I can understand his viewpoint of dealing with tipsy people all day - some are going to be FUN and some are going to be NOT. I can't say it was a 5-star experience because my phone had his old Yelp offer and he blamed it on my smartphone not being smart (hey, if I can figure out how to be Yelp Elite for 7 years while using plenty of Yelp offers I don't think it's my phone....just sayin'). But the more we hung out with him we all turned into friends - so much so that we all signed up for the Sevtap Wine Club by the end of our visit AND he showed me that I really do love white wines - I just needed to find the right ones to fall in love with :-)   The wines btw are great, in my opinion. I know everyone has different tastes. We started with 4 red wine flights and then he shared a few whites with us (which were stellar!!) so we went with 4 more white wine flights. And, he lets you pick your own wine for your wine club shipment!!! I've never experienced this luxury and that right there is awesome. I'm used to the winery shipping whatever willy, nilly wine they want. This is a nice touch.  Back to the wine! The reds had great names that he chose for touching personal reasons, which made them mean something to us as well. He even had one named after his dog, Leyla, and proceeds from every bottle purchase go to the local humane society where he adopted Leyla from! C'mon judgers, he's a good guy!  Honestly, the reds just kept tasting better and better - and very rich and unique. The whites, I have to say, were an extremely wonderful surprise! The Chenin Blanc, which I've never heard of before, was fantastic! My hubs fell in love with the Grenache Blanc - also unique and very delicious.   All in all, we spent well over an hour at Sevtap and enjoyed our visit. The owner is entertaining, very knowledgeable and has a great sense of humor. The winery has an amazing location and we've already decided that next time we visit to pick up our wine shipment we'll sit outside on the cushioned couches and enjoy our wine while people watching. Unless we can't wait that long and end up here next weekend!

It's been at least since 2012, and yet another stellar visit and experience here.  The servers never miss a beat here, and are really good with taking pictures.  Hell even the chef ablidged with some selfies with my gf and I.  The whole experience is so above and beyond here, my fav restaurant and the best for special occasions.  The food is delicate, fresh and never tired as they deliver slight changes on the menu per the season and available fresh ingredients.  Two thumbs and two biggy toes up.  You can'tgo wrong here, but make sure you plan ahead for reservations, this is an intimate and small affair.  These guys had my name and number in their files even after it had been 4 years since my last reservation... not to mention the follow up call the day of to see if we were still coming.  Every restaurant strives to be Bouchon.
Fast, energetic and reasonably priced. Go with friends, not for a date.  Try the Impossible Burger and get some sweet potato fries with it. Beer selection seems plentiful.

Cool area right in the heart of the Funk Zone Pizza was thin crust and flavor was fine. The cook on the crust was definitely the highlight for me - a little char on the outside. Fastest pizza ever.  Home made chips were fine.  Overall, I loved the setting and the area. Pizza is never bad, but this was not memorable or a destination for me. Your mileage may vary.

as another reviewer said, by SB standards, this is pizza excellence.  finally an actual WOOD FIRED oven.  finally a CT style pizza.  let's put it this way, when i called up and said 'i saw you have a white clam pizza...' the reply was, 'oh yeah, you know pepe's and sally's? mine's better.'  well i don't like clams so i can't verify the truth of that statement, but anyone that references pepe's and sally's at least knows what good pizza should be like.  my pie is a simple pepperoni.  very tasty, chewy thin crust cooked perfectly (ie till the crust gets a little burned).  yummy sauce.  excellent ingredients.  my partner (worked at Luna Pizza back east) ordered her favorite pie last night and i dont think i've ever had a better manifestation.  sausage, basil, garlic, ricotta.  outrageously delicious.  5 stars for bringing real pizza to santa barbara.

What is a Brat? According to Merriam Webster a brat is '1 a disparaging child; specifically: an ill-mannered annoying child. a spoiled brat.' I do not know why people go on and on about Brats, I avoid them entirely.   During the Old Spanish Days Fiesta celebration my friends brought me to this German pub. It was kind of ironic for a non-meat eater to be hanging out in a sausage bar during Fiestas.   The temperature was cranked up this lovely afternoon so I was happy to get out of the sun into this welcoming pub. It's decor, plush with distressed bricks, high wooden beam ceilings, and studded leather stools was instantly inviting.  Even though Beer and brats are the house specials, I ordered red wine and a pretzel with melted cheese sauce to mix things up. The pretzel was great, the wine was fine and the experience, priceless.

Not sure why the price is higher at this location than the one on Haley Street.    I initially planned on preordering from the other location so was familiar with the price.  This location also doesn't have the Yelp check-in offer (one free empanada with purchase of half dozen).  It does offer a small discount for half dozen and dozen, but the price still ends up being higher than the other location.  I just ended up purchasing from this location because I was already getting donuts from Hook and Press Donuts, which is right next to this place.  Despite the price discrepancy between the two locations, I still think this place deserves five stars for its quality and taste.  I don't have too many other places to compare against, but these are the best ones I ever had.  I never really had a good one, which is why I generally don't order empanadas from any place.  I don't even care for the ones at Porto's.    My family and I got two orders each of chicken, beef, and corn.  They were all excellent so I can't really pick a favorite.  The pastry shell wasn't too thick so you can really taste the filling, and wasn't oily.  I'm guessing these are baked instead of fried.  This place also uses good ingredients, like grass-fed ground beef and free-range Mary's chicken.  You will most likely need to eat two or three these to be full though.  Empanadas came with some dipping sauce but not sure what it was.  Seemed like olive oil with cilantro or something like that.  It was too oily so I didn't even use it.  Empanadas tasted good on their own so didn't think it was even necessary.
