Located inside Mosaic Locale, which is a shared space with two other businesses - Hook and Press Donuts and Draughtsmen Aleworks.

Enjoyed our stay at the Hyatt.  Briana Vargas went above and beyond to make our visit extra special.  The beds were comfortable and the room was very clean.  This was our 2nd stay at this Hyatt and we will definitely be back.

I came here because I wanted to go somewhere else but my bf didn't want to because the portion sizes looked too small in reviews, so we compromised on this place.  I was trying to make a reservation last minute (5pm for 8pm) on memorial day weekend and they were happy to accommodate! When we arrived the restaurant was full but they had a table for us ready to go....so wonderful!  We ordered the bruschetta for an appetizer which was bad, you can skip. It tasted like chlorine and the one I make at home is so easy and so much better. For entrees we got the beef ravioli and lasagna. Both were really good but I don't know how much I can actually vouch for them because I had drank a few generous glasses of wine before dinner, as well as the one I ordered.    The service was ok, nothing amazing but the waiter was nice. I give this place a 3 because I don't really want to go back. I had a nice time but I wouldn't recommend it to a friend and would rather try another Italian place next time. Sorry!

Super bummed about this place. Pre made sandwiches :/ soup wasn't ready at 11:30 to buy. They had a handful of baked items. Donut was really dense. The glaze on top was delicious but took a few bites and threw away the rest. The toffee cookie was excellent as an upside. Wouldn't come unless you happen to be walking by.

Came in for dinner with friends. Place was packed but they found a table for us. Food was really good and nice atmosphere. They sat in a back room which was ok because we had no reservation but they use it regularly. I wouldn't be happy to sit there if I have a reservation.

I so badly want to LOVE this place, but it's always just alright.  They brew their own beer, some are decent and some are stinkers.  The food is average at best.  The burger and a beer lunch deal is a great.  The interior is a bit dingy divey feeling.  They do have a patio which is a huge plus.  I have been a few times and will certainly be back again.  The Brewhouse is sort of like cold pizza, not your first choice but still hits the spot.

On this thank you Thursday I would like to thank Institution Ale Company for opening a modern, spacious, open air micro brewery and restaurant. There is something for everyone here at Institution. Great micro brewery beer, but if beer is not your thing they do offer other alcoholic and non alcoholic drinks. Great menu and family friendly environment. Recently they added a fabulous outdoor beer garden with fire pit. Thank you Institution for bringing this establishment to State street Santa Barbara. To make the experience even better the staff here is always extremely helpful, friendly and efficient. Check it out, have fun.

The food here is my absolute favorite of my Santa Barbara adventure. I had the Ramen and short ribs.  We added a perfect soft boiled egg to our Ramen which tired it all together. The broth was well seasoned opposed to salty as broth can often be.  The noodles were chewy in a good way.  The ribs were tender and simply feel of the bone and came with I think pickled radicchio that cut through the richness. My wife ordered the red bass with chorizo and nice crispy skin. The Ramen was so good we split another bowl. The only knock is that the peons ate kind of what you should eat rather than what you want to eat.

Hands down my favorite/ best restaurant in Santa Barbara. Every time I come here I have the best experience. The wait staff are all very professional which I appreciate and are very attentive to your wants. I love that they let you decide how cooked you want your fish (which I find is a flaw in other seafood restaurants in town). They do allow you to bring your own bottles of wine with a small reasonable corkage fee. Lure is a definite must try place to eat if you haven't already.

my friend and i had made reservations to experience the 'miracle of christmas' at pearl social. unfortunately we left incredibly disheartened and disappointed.  our reservations were at 5 and we arrived at 5, the second group in line. no one was sat in the bar yet. first group had a lovely table in the middle of the space. us? we were sat in the literal corner of the place next to the kitchen and supplies. there were plenty of open tables in the main seating area. hell, we even would have been happy to be sat at the bar.  but no. despite us having reservations, being on time, being dressed up ... it was not enough for us to be treated the same as the other people that came in. we stayed maybe 30 minutes before leaving and going to another bar that always treats us equally.  my first time to pearl social and my last time here. i've had plenty of great experiences at every other place in the funk zone, i would avoid this place at all costs.

Delicious sandwiches for the beach!  Our Airbnb happened to be just down the road from this cafe so our visit was based on convenience.  Instead of having to decide on a place for lunch we ordered a few sandwiches for our beach day.   The deviled egg sandwiches were really good. I'm really picky when it comes to egg sandwiches because I make some great egg salad myself but these didn't disappoint. Each sandwich came with a pickle and chips.   Perfect beach lunch!

I've eaten breakfast many times at scarlet begonia and it's always great! My friend and I decided to try it for dinner and we had an awesome time! The service was excellent and friendly and the food was amazing and healthy and our cocktails were fresh and so tasty! We will definitely be back! I highly recommend it!!!

Plain and simple, this place is awesome!! From drinks to desert this restaurant hits it on the nail every time!! I discovered Tullamore Whiskey here bc my staple is Jameson and Ginger. This Whiskey is a step above, perfect balance and taste. The Caesar Salad is the best ever. Im not a crouton person but these croutons are perfect and fun to eat. The Caesar dressing couldn't be better! The  Pizza is is epic and nothing needs tweaking. Its perfect.  Im very picky for the perfect Meatball and this is speechless. The only thing missing is a touch of Anise seed. I had these Meatballs in a Restaurant in Sonoma years ago and nothing has topped it but these are neck and neck.  Theres to much to say so I will end by saying this place is a gift created by gifted hands! Thanks for creating this special place that I wont stop coming to!!

This place is all about the meat. They crank out tacos fast and it's just super fresh meat and tortillas. You go to the salsa bar and finish off the job to your liking. Parking is kind of a pain in the ass, but not too bad by Santa Barbara standards. Don't eat junk fast food when you can have bomb tacos in the same amount of time and for roughly the same price. Service is friendly and, well...fast.

I love Fala Bar! The food here is sensational! It's fresh, clean and the taste are wonderful! I consume it so quickly, I'm embarrassed to go with anyone else! Although, it's not an experience to have alone, it must be shared! People have thanked me for introducing them the Fala Bar. I have to say I've been lucky and I've not had the wait time others are talking about, I'm sad to hear that actually. I favor the Baba plate and the kale falafel. Hope you enjoy too, I'll look forward to soon reading your post!

This place is mega-hyped!  I would have rounded up because I feel its more 3.5 stars than just 3 but its tooooo hyped.  If I had to rate the place just on chowda I'd give it a 5 because that soup was overflowing with clams! I think they have a great thing going with the location but I wasn't impressed with the food at all.  We had, cioppino, fish sandwich, calamari and coconut shrimp.  Shrimp was pretty good everything else was fine, not bad but not anything to write home about.  I liked their wine list more than their beer list.  The place is super loud.  My review doesn't sound positive BUT I truly have nothing against the place I just don't think it lives up to the hype.  Great place for a beer (or wine) and some decent seafood.  I also think the prices are super reasonable and the atmosphere is fun and lively!!!  If you are in as a tourist its a great location.

Life at the beach couldn't get better than this!  We had a beach front table with a spectacular view. I ordered off the Brunch menu- a Crab and Shrimp  Omelette with home fries and a dense biscuit. The omelette was swimming in seafood!

If you're looking for decent sushi at a decent price, this is one place to consider.  A friend and I went to Edomasa for the first time tonight, and even though I've had various sushi rolls for many, many years, I'd never really tried sashimi until now. We split the sashimi appetizer. Not having had previous sashimi experiences to compare to, I can't say first-hand how it ranks, but it tasted great to me and my friend had nothing to complain about.  We also split a Rainbow roll and Marina roll (crab, avocado, eel and cucumber) which were quite tasty, although I should have known better than to get to rolls that have the crab/mayonnaise mix (as I do not really like mayonnaise). The other rolls that were on the menu all also sounded really good, and I hope I have a chance to come back here and try more.  We were there rather late in the evening, but the staff was quite friendly and helpful -- especially when our server discovered our table was uneven and rocking quite a bit and he immediately helped us move to an adjacent table.

Excellent soups there is a wait is you go in the morning or on cold days as people are looking for comfort foods. Very filling and you could split because the portions sizes are pretty big. They do run out of popular items on weekends.  Parking lot is free but tends to be packed during peak hours.  They do take major credit cards.
Once you eat here, you'll be dreaming about it every night. Everything on the menu is delicious. They cater to gluten free and vegan options.
An updated review from me is overdue! I moved closer to this location & have been back several times over the past few months. Their service, food, drinks, and ambiance have greatly improved!
The burgers nearly gave me a heart attack in a good way.  They have a small selection of quality beers and did I mention their burgers are delicious?  Their onion rings are great too.

I have to update my review. Sakana really grows on you and became a favorite spot for us in Santa Barbara.   The sushi is good - we often get nigiri yellow tail belly, toro, and blue fin as well as salmon. They have a good selection and the fish is fresh and delicious.  Charlie loves the baked scallop roll more than any other place we have been or any other roll he has tried.  I still maintain that the rolls come with too much sauce, I especially don't like the red one that I think is made from beets, which is just my preference.  This is a good place to have in the line up to switch off with Arigato as they are completely different and you can keep things fresh that way.   I only wish they took reservations.

I recently enjoyed two different shows at Soho, The Gene Taylor Trio and G. Love & Special Sauce. I have some disabilities that prohibit me from standing for a long time so I called the club to see if I could make arrangements for seating. I spoke with Heather at Soho and explained my needs and she told me it would be no problem, just let the manager know when when you arrive. On the night of the G. Love show we arrived early and I was introduced to the manager, Kenny, and he told me he had been informed of my request by Heather and he set up a bar stool for me on the side of the stage room so I could watch the show comfortably. I want to thank Soho and especially their two employees, Heather and Kenny, for going the extra distance to help me out. I know setting up a bar was just a small thing to do but it really enhanced my comfort and enjoyment of the show. Cheers!

Lots of options to choose from. Staff was friendly and service was quick. Hotdogs were not bad, but pretty basic in my opinion; nothing really stood out to me. If you're looking for a quick snack, then this may be the place to go. I'd give it a 3.5 stars.
The restaurant is very modern and the food is great! Definitely worth the money and a fun experience!!

I haven't been to Santa Barbara in ... well in more than a year ... but I came up today and joined friends at the Blue Owl.  I remember a smaller version of it before, but they've expanded the indoor and outdoor spaces and punched up the menu as well.  Three of the four of us at the table had the fried rice bowl - mine with chicken and a poached egg on top, plus cilantro, spicy sauce and all sorts of YUM.  The staff was friendly, accommodating - and the food was great.  Thanks for a great lunch today!

I used to think this place was mediocre, but I just had some really good chili verde and carnitas.  Chili verde was generously sized with chunks of tender lean pork in a tangy and flavorful tomatilla based sauce.  Carnitas was confit pork perfection, but I wish I got a little more for $15.  It was tender with some crispy bits, but no dry or over broiled pieces.

First of all, beautiful views of the ocean and sunset. If you're not sitting on the patio, you're doing it wrong. There were heaters on the patio and a small fire pit area as well. Been a handful of times now. The first time I came here was for happy hour and our group was seated right away. Ordered some drinks and a sausage fennel pizza to start while we were waiting for some more friends. Got the croquets and the salami pizza to share as well. Our server at some point just stopped coming to our table which was kind of weird, we had to keep glancing around for someone to come take our orders. Since we were there for happy hour, we wanted to make sure all our orders were taken before it ended.  I've been back several times and love the ambiance and really enjoy bringing friends and family here from out of town!

Ordered 2 tortas... they brought 2 burritos... I told the main guy (same guy whom took the order and yelled back to the kitchen 'two tortas') I ordered 2 tortas... he insisted I ordered 2 burritos... over and over multiple times. Never apologized. Just shook his head at me. This is not what we ordered. One star. Own up to your mistakes and fix the order. We have always ordered only tortas at this place... but I guess no longer. Customers lost.

Love this place! Everything is great, the food, the plating, the unique flavor combinations, and the atmosphere. The restaurant design is very original and well put together. From the front door down to the bathrooms. Definitely will be back!

Been coming here since it opened and always seem to get the long tail boat noodle.  It always satisfies.  The duck buns are very good as are the potstickers.  It seems to be the only place generating a crowd at the Public Market (at least during the afternoon).

I finally checked this new place out last night with a few friends! It's open until midnight on Friday , and we went over around 11, the place was hustling , but not too crowded.  They have lots of craft beers on tap which I love and the ambiance is super cool.  There is outdoor seating with space heaters , and the indoor area is sort of brewhousy. The outdoor eating area , seems a little more upscale restauranty, and it so close to the beach it gives off a really cool vibe.  I will definitely be back!

I came here for the lunch buffet on Saturday.  It was a let down.  The wait staff was standoffish and not very helpful.  The food was very watery and had almost not actual pieces of food in the sauce.  You also have to buy drinks in a can for $2 each. However they do play cool Bolloywood music videos and they also have really cool deco.  I liked the pillow seats.
Good food, great location . Love how they do lunch take out. The price is rite. Orange chicken is really tasteful as well as the dumplings.
Best Acai place in SB county!  I love calling in and then being able to swipe my card and then pick up. Super convenient and super yummy!

The food was absolutely amazing! Definitely authentic Italian food, and that's coming from a full blooded Italian. The service was also super prompt and very helpful, and my water glass was never more than half empty! The wait time for the food wasn't too bad, there were 2 large groups that I'm assuming were slowing things down a little. Either way, the wait time was still reasonable and they gave us free tiramisu for the slow down!
I love this place! The food is delicious and very flavorful. The restaurant is cute and casual with great ambience. The service is fast and attentive and everyone is warm and friendly.

Ive been coming here a while now ... the staff is always attentive , the place is charming , and the food is imo the best sushi around santa barbara. Prices are fair for sushi and this would be my immediate go to for the craving to be satisfied. Parking is a little tricky if you aren't from the area. But it shouldn't deter you from a great meal . Hands down the first place i will suggest for anyone in/around sb goleta or montecito.  Thank you team for making many memorable moments here.

I'm a cupcake lover so I had to go to Crushcake while on vaca in Santa Barbara.  The shop is little but super cute.  Their selection was great (they had at least 8 flavors) and I went late in the day, around 4ish.  I ordered the Coconut Cloud and my husband got the Chocolate Chocolate...at the register I spotted some minis so I got the Lemon Drop and ate it right away...it was great!  We saved the big ones for later...it's later now and just finished them up.  The Chocolate one was good...I think the frosting had either cream cheese or sour cream which made it a little tangy...it was good but I prefer Ganache.  The Coconut was good but could have been more coconut-y.  Both could have been a bit moister.  I'm hoping to go back tomorrow (our last day on vaca) to try a couple more flavors!

Well, the first word that comes to mind regarding the Biergarten is, Perfect! I may be prone to hyperbole but when something or someplace is exactly how you would design it yourself you absolutely need to give it those high accolades. The world, and certainly this town, is chock full of places pouring gallons of IPA's: West coast, East coast, English, Double, Triple, Hazy, Super Hazy! Honestly? Can we just have some solid German and Belgian beers already? Yes you can, at the Biergarten!!  A fantastic selection of beers both direct from Europe, Weihenstephaner, Andechs, Ayinger, Vanderghinst, Westmalle, as well as  amazing local(ish) producers that are killing it in these styles such as Enegren, Stone & Mother Earth. OK there are a few token IPAs for those that just need to be bitter :-)  (Stone again and Offshoot!!) Opened by the owners of the Copenhagen Sausage Garden of Solvang these folks definitely know how to operate. So, of course there is a huge array of sausages available as well as burgers and pizzas and, yes, pretzels. And if you are really daring try the schnitzel, or even the schnitzel poutine. Next time for sure :-) All in all a fantastic addition the SB beer and food scene and that's saying a lot since that scene is epic in this town these days !! Will be going back VERY frequently, hope they have my table ready :-)

Best place ever!!!! You get to hang out with cute cats and cool people all while enjoying a nice cup of coffee! Great atmosphere. It's nice knowing your money helps animals in needs and the animals are all up for adoption!

great food and convenient location if you're on upper state. i like the outdoor seating they offer.  knocked off one star because their kitchen can be hit or miss in terms of modification requests - sometimes they do it / sometimes they don't.

Great experience. Mitch was our server, and he was attentive, but not in the way. He was able to walk that fine line, and I appreciate it. Their octopus dish was the best cooked I've ever had, and I've had a lot. As a local, I was afraid it would be a hyped up new hotel experience. I was wrong! Will be back again, and glad there is another fine dining option in SB.

The first time I had carbonara it was good chunky bacon, ham, green ,olives, peas and a delicious white sauce. I've tried their manacotti I was not a fan of their red sauce there's a cinnamon taste too it but this is a great place to sit down and have dinner.
Before they used Basa fish in their fish and chips and now it is Swai fish. What happened to Cod or Halibut'?

This review is all based on Taco Tuesday: Great prices for Taco Tuesday (under $8), great environment, free chips and salsa, and the kitchen is on their game. Careful with buying alcohol because they make up for their low priced Taco Tuesday deal at the bar. A Corona was somewhere around $5. Taco Tuesday with 2 Coronas can easily turn into a $23-$25 dinner if you include $5-$7 dollar tip. Spend responsibly friends.

A little off the beaten path, but worth the trek.  A variety of tacos and other fInger foods (I recommend the tater tots), it's a nice little spot with great food at a decent price.    Highly recommend the Batter Up (battered fish) and Spicy Crispy Shrimp tacos.  Those two are staples of my Triple Play order (3 tacos).  I'll usually throw in another taco that I haven't tried for good measure.  I got the Gangnam Style (Korean BBQ taco) the last time I went and it was really good!  Come for the tacos, stay for the batting cages.  Just get here early, because parking is limited.

Finney's Craftghouse is my go to spot with my 6 year old from now on, they have large elevated booths and my favorite one has a picture of President Ronald Reagan hold a beer. Pause, take that in, #TeamAmerica.  Finney's Craftghouse also has killer vegan options and Gluten-Free Crust Available for the pizza. Yeah I watched GameChangers and decided to make the switch, the Hand Graded Parmesan Cheese on the Onion  Rings and Fries helped a lot.   Sit at Megan's table and ask for Megan, she is the best and very patient with my 6 year old son.

The fish here is fresh, the special rolls are large, and the pricing is good for Santa Barbara. If those are your priorities, then give Edomasa a try! I give this place 4 stars because 1. The service is pretty slow, especially considering how simple the rolls are (in comparison to higher-end sushi bars like Arigato) 2. I'd say you should absolutely call in your orders ahead of time, but your call may never be answered (one time I called over 6 times in a 2 hour period) 3. All of their crab is imitation crab (minus the fried soft shell option) and 4. Their lobster roll is more mayonnaise than anything (Even my friend who loves mayonnaise couldn't finish his entire roll). I'd keep this all in mind when you're weighing your options!

Very delicious vegan joint. I didn't realize it was vegan until I got here and peeped the menu, which was a delightful surprise!  We got the Belgian fries (amazing aioli sauce!), avocado salad (delicious blend of grill flavor and crisp, fresh textures!), green bowl (solid), mushroom sandwich and mushroom tacos (yum!!)! Great selection of drinks. Service slightly slow on the day we went but not too bad. I think they got a Michelin star in 2019 and I can see why! Will come again :-)
This my favorite steakhouse in Santa Barbara...Filet,Rib Eye are the best.Excellent service and great atmosphere

I've never had a bad experience here except for last night, I ordered online and went in to pay and pick up. The cashier read back my order to me and everything was correct until we got home and opened up our food. Our Baja burrito had no guac or sour cream on top which was the first bummer, then inside of our Baja burrito was just chicken. We asked for no rice but that's all, however they didn't even put beans in the burrito...second bummer. Third bummer is they forgot the bread that went with my soup. We ordered late so it was too late to take the food back and make a complaint. So my suggestion is to check your complete order before you leave.
The place is quite small and somewhat crammed, but the food is great.  I had the rack of lamb - it was delicious.  I would come back here if I'm in the area.

We drive into Santa Barbara from LA and head here for breakfast. It's spectacular. The chorizo and eggs and chilaquiles are superstars! I get the chilaquiles with half red sauce and half green. It's authentic and it's heavenly.

Love that I could pick up live uni, fresh oysters, and salmon poke all in the same shop. My marine biologist bf (I'm not saying this to brag- I'm saying that rating seafood is his only contribution to my life) said that this was all super fresh so I guess I believe him! Since the line was super long for On the Alley next door, we made orders at both to round out the perfect lunch!

Seafood please.  Brophy Bros. is a good spot for fresh, no nonsense seafood dishes.  I had the Cioppino and it was loaded with good, fresh seafood. The broth was rich, not spicy (although I prefer spicy) and the fried calamari appetizer even had my super picky 9 year old asking for more. Brophy Bros., I will be back.

I LOVE SB chicken ranch. This place introduced me to the wonders of Santa Maria style BBQ and never let me go. The chicken/tri-tip quarter plates are perfect in size for a meal. They always have a selection of great fresh salsas. You can tell that this place is honored by the local community. The diversity of the staff and clientele represents the Central Coast accurately.

Finally got the opportunity to try dawn patrol recently and experience was great! The last time we were in Santa Barbara we had a difficult time finding the restaurant because it's unpretentious and tucked next door to Casa Blanca Restaurant. One way street on Gutierrez Street, made visibility difficult. If we hadn't parked across the street on Sevilla Square, we would have missed them again. Free parking up to 75 minutes here.  The restaurant is small, but offers casual friendly space and outdoor patio. Beach surf decor compliments the space well. It's semi-casual dining, you order at the counter and food is brought to your table. The restaurant offers a good selection of breakfast and brunch items. There claim to fame is their 'build-your-own-hash!' which allows you to customize your hash any way you (choice of regular potato or sweet potato hash with pepper amd onions) with choice of proteins, vegetables, sauces (hollandaise, gravy, salsa and aioli) and eggs any style.  We went with the chorizo-hash special and built our own hash (russet potatoes, bacon, spinach, mushroom, tomatoes, cheddar cheese, and scramble eggs topped with hollandaise sauce). It was delicious and hearty breakfast indeed. Hash is actually prepared country style as opposed to shredded. Lightly crisp on outside and tender inside. Everything was so flavorful and fresh. The hash and scramble eggs was cooked perfectly, highlight was hollandaise sauce which made it so luscious. The chorizo-hash was fantastic and best I've had in long time. Good coffee too...wish it was free refills.  Both dishes and coffee ended costing us $37 which was too bad. Our order did take a while to arrive (30 min), but overall fabulous meal.

We used to go here  30 years ago- Went on a Sunday night, ordered a veggie tostada that was supposed to have grilled vegetables and guacamole on top, it had no guacamole and no grilled vegetables just the shredded cheapest kind of lettuce you can imagine it was tasteless and yucky . we asked about the guacamole and they said they were out of it, which it would've been nice if they had told us when we ordered that as we were specifically looking for guacamole ... we will not go back
I called and asked i wanted the whole duck. I got duck breast fick u. You the guy on the phone was an assdss

Patio and Decor: Nice! Hostess: cold and unaccommodating Server: Not particularly friendly-never a thank you or you're welcome. Didn't refill water. Chips and salsa: adequate. Chips oily and stale. Meatball soup: flavorless and over cooked meat ball in salty broth Green/Red Chicken Enchiladas Green was good, rice and beans good, red enchilada tasted terrible. Food was brought out 4 minutes after ordering = everything pre-made (not freshly made to order). Just enough time to melt the cheese in. the oven.

Nope.   Just finished eating some take out from this place and it was incredibly disappointing.   We ordered the dinner special, which includes choice of roll (California or spicy tuna), miso, salad, chicken teriyaki, tempura, and rice. We also ordered a Tokyo roll (yellow tail, avocado, and cucumber).   The miso was good- very solid and standard miso.   The salad was DRENCHED in dressing. It was basically ginger soup with some lettuce in it. Totally blah.   The tempura was okay. The dipping sauce had no flavor. I had to add a bunch of soy sauce to make it work.   The spicy tuna roll was okay. Not spicy at all.   The chicken teriyaki lacked some serious flavor and the chicken didn't look fresh.   The Tokyo roll was absolutely terrible. After one bite, I thew it away. First off, the yellowtail was cooked. Yes, cooked! There was NO indication of this on the menu. I'm a sushi lover and that's I thought I was getting. See photos. The yellowtail looked and tasted like StarKist tuna. Complete rip off.   The food did come out fast, which was nice, but I would have preferred they spend more time making an enjoyable meal. I would have gladly waited.   To top off the experience, they charge a 5% service fee for take out orders. Because putting food in a styrofoam box is deserving of an extra 5%? Sheesh.
Stopped here and between the two of us we had chicken taquitos and a pastor burrito. The fall that helped us was super friendly. The food, although tasty, was okay.

When I first heard about an all vegetarian restaurant near my house, I got my first 5th COVID booster shot. As soon as I went it was a total waste of a shot. There's was not as many hippies as I though. A little too much white people with dreams, but who am I to say, I'm a white person with a dream. Now that's played out, much like the Burgar lifestyle

I really like Dargan's.  You can go here any night or day of the week and just relax and have a good time.  Music is never so loud that you cant hear yourself talk and its usually pretty easy to grab a spot that a group of friends can hold up at.  yeah it can get crowded on the weekends, but so what, there is a reason for it.  It is a great place to go with your friends that is a departure from the monotonous thump thump of the downtown club scene.  good atmosphere, good food, good enough Guinness

we were really surprised we totally walked in expecting to be disappointed most restaurants on the pier are over-hyped and not very good after scanning the menu, we decided we'd just nibble and go somewhere else  so we went for the 6 half shell oysters of the day and a cup of lobster bisque the bisque was surprisingly super tasty and the oysters were delicious! it's a bit pricey and not really worth a long wait but definitely a pleasant surprise

Really great value for the money. My favorite is the chick regular, which gives you a great portion of chicken, rice, vegetables, and a delicious teriyaki sauce on top. The boba is great too. I've tried the mango slushi option but I think my favorite is the milk tea with boba, their most popular. All the staff is friendly. A lot of young guys who are making their way. It's a new restaurant so it's especially popular in the area. I see people from Sonos in line. It's all very fresh food. Careful though! It's highly popular so at a peak time during lunch, you might need to wait. Might not be a good option if you're in a rush. Overall, Great food, great value, I'm a regular.

Made a reservation on the 25th for a special dinner for my mother's 90th birthday, specifically requesting a table inside. Arrived and shown to an outside table. It was really windy and just not going to work. They did not have an inside table and we were left with no reservation for mom's 90th. I was really, really unhappy! The hostess tried to get a table at their sister restaurant with no success. This is BAD customer service and I am not sure I would go back. We have eaten here several times and they have good food, but tonight was a capper.

I've been wanting to try this place so bad and I finally got the chance!! It was so delicious!   Their jamaica was the perfect amount of sweet and tangy. Their Birria Quesotacos were super delicious especially when you dip it in the consome!   You cannot come here and just get the quesotaco, you have to get it with the consome! I recommend getting the Yona Special because it comes with 3 quesotacos, one consome, and a drink! Super delicious!

I first tried The Black Sheep for a work dinner where I experienced the tasting menu. To say that it was life changing is an understatement. I knew I had to go back, so I decided to get a happy hour group to try out the place. My favorite items are the scallops crudo and Ramen, but honestly everything here is amazing. What really takes this place to another level is the friendly and attentive  service. Sometimes restaurants lack the service and have good food or vice versa but this is not one of those places. It is a perfect spot for a casual dinner with top notch food; their happy hour is a great way to try their menu . For larger groups I would say the tasting menu is worth it because you are able to try everything. I am still thinking about their pork belly, mmmm.

Ok, its a Hot Dog.  How can it be so great?  I don't know.  How can it.  But it was.  I had the pulled pork / mac and cheese dog, as did my son.  It was a grand slam.  Everything was perfect.  The growing lad he is, he had seconds and went with the Alfredo dog or something like that.  He liked it too but not as much as the pulled pork dog.  That was out of the world.  And, who would have known that my wife was having a hankering for some wings.  She rated them here as excellent and I have been told I am taking her back.  We shop at the Vons in that lot fairly often so that won't be a problem.  My only complaint is the seating, or lack thereof.  I sure hope they open a second location with better seating.  We will do our best to help them along.

Tacos were competitive to lily's tacos, but I prefer the tongue meat here, the tongue pieces are cubed and chewier.  Tacos were $2ish each so it is pricier than lily's tacos, but the tongue taco is so worth it. I got the green enchilada plate and it was delish, the green salsa is so good paired with the chicken enchiladas. I definitely ordered too much food so I had pack it up, but I would come here again for their tacos, more economic.   Try their watermelon juice! It's the best.

Been here many o times, pretty cool, dark little spot where you can enjoy a nice craft cocktail and some bites. Main gripe is the customer service. The girls working had no tact, we're congregating one the back and acted like they were doing a favor by hardly accommodating us. Generic customer service with a touch of pretentious pride = poor quality experience. Gotta get your FOH together
Ethiopian Doro Wat (chicken) + curried tempeh/vegetable stir fry w coconut rice/beans. Unbelievably tasty! We are locals & will be back w all our friends.
Ordered delivery through a service.  The pizza was Luke warm upon delivery but still delicious.  The crust makes it special.

Yes, it used to be that gay bar. Now it's one of my favorite places to go. The Neighborhood is located next to the Blue Collar Bistro (And owned by the same) and the Brew House. Very cool, open atmosphere. They have a digital jukebox for your listening pleasure (or displeasure when some cowboy puts in $20 and plays every song on the album). There are a lot of booths and several flat-screen TVs for you sports fans. The drinks are reasonably priced and you can order food from the Blue Collar Bistro.   What I like best are the games! They have two ping-pong tables setup in the middle of the bar (in addition to the small pool table). On top of that they have a bunch of random board games for you to play (i.e. Rummicube, Truth/Dare Jenga, Clue, etc.). Usually we'll just cruise down there and get a pitcher or two and play dominoes. But it is a great destination if you aren't trying to go Downtown to the State St. scene. It can get really packed here too on random nights, but I've found that it will usually begin to slow down after 12. All in all it is a great find. Check it.  ***December 07 Update: Rendevouzed with my girlfriend in Santa Barbara. We both went to school there and we wanted to get away for the weekend. Kinda over the old hussle and bussle of the usual State St. haunts, we went over to the Neighborhood for a visit. They had some sweet upgrades since the last time I was there. There was a band playing mainly covers inside, but a good mix. What was awesome is that they finally opened up the back patio... AMAZING! They had basketball hoops on both ends of the patio, foosball, ping-pong tables and a bunch of double overhead space heaters. Had a great time and it felt good to be back in Santa Barbara.

Off of state street..small parking lot... AMAZING CROISSANTS!! I had their chai tea latte (so delicious and wonderful.. So much better than starbucks). For Breakfast I had the ham egg and cheese croissant with fruit and my husband had the fried egg sandwich with potatoes. Everything was so good. I also bought two almond croissants (because that's what they are known for..) to go... I don't even like croissants and this place was truly a gem. They have seating inside and outside, but the weather was beautiful so we sat outside.

Good food. Especially for a location near the beach. Because of COVID-19 we took our food Togo.   I like the chowder fries and lobster roll.   I'm not putting 5 stars because the fish and chips that my wife had wasn't as great as the rest of the food.

Chef Gunnar dazzled us with his swordfish special. Fresh, tasty, authentic. Thank you for the personal service and welcoming atmosphere. From cocktails to dessert one of the best dine out meals we've had in a long time. We will return. Welcome to Santa Barbara Chef Gunnar.

No frills. Great authentic food.   You'll know about the lines before you get there, so once you're prepared for that it's not a problem. The food will not woo fine diners, but it's solid, such good value and it's more about the overall experience.   The busy kitchen is part of the joy of this place and watching the parts of the puzzle come together in this kitchen based choreography is awesome. An absolute joy.
In the name of transparency I only had coffee but in the past I've had many of their pastries and they're all darn good

This place has a great atmosphere! Located in the heart of downtown Santa Barbara, this bar and cafe offers a great menu and drink selection. We enjoyed a table inside with a good jazz music playing in the background. The service is very friendly and they have a good knowledge of wine since they are right next door to the Wine Cask fine dine restaurant and Margerum Winery. The also have a great outside sitting.  I highly recommend this place for a happy hour with friends or a romantic date night.

I never thought I could have a delicious Mexican food from any restaurant inside a food court. They have mind blowing tacos! Don't really care for their horchata. Beans and rice don't really have any flavor but they have a delicious black beans.
Drinks we're good but service was terrible. Our waitress never checked on us. I've been here multiple times but service did not consistent. It depends on who is your waiter.

The pulpo Bao was my favorite had all the wonderful flavors I love and was cooked to perfection.  ! Even tried the carbonara udon, the bao gun softness is so perfect.  . They also have Margerum wine by the bottle which is always a great idea.

What was good: Pepperoni pizza. Serves 2 people as it had 6 slices.   What was bad: leafy green salad for $14 was not worth it.   I ordered to go and was advised that it would be 30-40 minutes to make. I arrived 45 mins from my call and had to wait an additional 10-15 minutes. (Which wasn't the deal breaker for me as I don't mind them making it fresh).   They handed me my order only to see that my salad box was light as a feather, no utensils provided or napkins as a take out order. I asked for utensils and they literally gave me 1 fork and 1 knife, when I had another person with me who would be sharing this food with.   Was not satisfied with their salad price and take out service.   Update: Manager reached out to me and apologized and provided refund. That was very kind and appreciated

We finally got the chance to try Los agaves after the first attempt going horribly wrong at the other location a few months ago (1.5 hour wait, no food, we left starving).   Food: We ordered the Chile Poblano Campestre with steak- delicious! Grilled perfectly with a fragrant smoky grill flavor, topped with cheese. Yummy. Al pastor tacos- i thought it was pretty good but my boyfriend loved it. He said it was the best al pastor he's had. Also got an order of their beef taquitos. This was very good but the tortilla was a bit too thick.   Service: when we walked up it was confusing because we didn't know if we should wait to get seated or walk up and order then seat like the other location. A server finally noticed us and told us to order first. We walked up to the register to order and the person taking the order was not paying attention so she didn't notice us for about 2 minutes. She seemed either still new and learning or distracted because she had a hard time understanding our order, which was nothing special. Just ordered straight from the menu. She was still nice nonetheless.   The reason for the 4 stars is the inside seating area was a bit dirty. Their walls where we sat near the front entrance had tons of food stains and actual caked on food. We looked around and all the walls were dirty. Floors needed to be swept too.   Overall- delicious food! Needs better service and clean your walls lol
Really good...not sure how I never knew this was here?

My absolute favorite Sunday brunch spot!  The Bella Vista sits just across the street from the Pacific Ocean with beautiful views from the patio.  The brunch buffet has so many options: a carving station, sushi of all sorts, crab legs, pastas, omelet station, a variety of pastries, fruit, veggies and even caviar!  A meal fit for a king!  And then of course, there are the unlimited fancy coffee drinks and bottomless mimosas!  A perfect Sunday afternoon there ends with some golf putting and a fun game of croquet in the back yard!

I can't speak for the rest of the food, but the Bucatini all'Amatriciana is the closest thing I have had to Florentine cuisine in years. The pasta is really thick and hard to find. I have to find it at specialty stores that import it, so it is a treat to find it on a menu. The sauce was full of onion, cheese and Italian cured ham and was hot and flavorful. I love pancetta, so this was great for me! Its one of my favorite flavors and I know when it is done right.   I ate here for lunch so the place was not crowded and our service was attentive and pleasant. My mom had the Ravioli al Burro & Salvia, which has spinach and ricotta. It comes with marinara sauce usually but she asked them to hold that, so it was served just in the brown butter and sage sauce. The dish was really good, but not as good as mine. I had to hit away my mom's fork with my own. We ended up eating all of the big noodle dish and taking most of her ravioli home.

Sorely disappointed after reading all the raving reviews. It's a sushi take-out counter kind of place with fine dining prices but not fine dining food quality. It was also really slow -- took us like 30 minutes to get our food. The miso soup was cold when we got it and not a good flavor. I actually threw it away and I never do that with miso. All of the sushi rolls were mediocre at best. Thought about getting a sake but it was like close to $20. We got 3 rolls and a miso and that was over $50. Place is a huge rip and none of the quality you'd expect with these prices. If it wasn't for the high prices, I'd give it a 3 star because it is just whatever blah kind of place but taking into account value, it's definitely a below average place.
Great bar service by Luis today ! 4 pm. Strawberry lemonade is firing. Try the daily Mac, so good
The service is outstanding and they have the best green salsa I've ever had. I put it on everything. The drinks are really good too.
Amazing food & atmosphere. Highly recommend when walking down state street to fuel your shopping.

My first time and I loved it . Patio was just perfect . Not sitting in the street but on a nice patio ! The State Cobb Salad was very good . I will defiantly go back . The server and cashier were really friendly and accommodating .
Conner is the man!!  Great conversationalist, which keeps me coming back and bringing friends!!

I have been thinking about my brunch at Les Marchands for days. I just can't believe what was dumped into the bowl I received. I love the wine shop. I have liked the food at the last few brunches I spent here - I'm not a big fan of brunch in general. Brunch is not a meal that makes sense to me. BUT I did not expect while enjoying delicious wine in my favorite hangout spot to suddenly be transported to Applebee's. Applebee's! I ordered a smoked pork dish that came with French fries. WITH fries. I hadn't had fries in a while so I thought that Les Marchands is a place I can trust to do it right. I figured there would be a little pile of smoked meat, a drizzle of creme fraiche, maybe some garnishy greens, and some fries. Classy to match the restaurant. What I received, however, was a giant bowl of CHILI CHEESE FRIES dotted with flecks of meat. I guess I really don't understand brunch if this is what such a beautiful wine shop will serve to people on purpose. Soggy cheese-soaked potatoes. Yeesh. Please someone explain this to me.  5 STARS for the wine selection and the fantastic service.  0 STARS for the chili cheese fries.

This is my new favorite restaurant in Santa Barbara. The food was amazing and the owner took our food allergies very seriously! Can't say enough good things about this place!  Pro tip: Call them before you go as they aren't always open during scheduled hour due to special events.
New England clam chowder is what we came to try. On 2 visits, we found it to be good but was too thick for us. Needed some extra attention as it was missing zip.

Wow I was really excited for this Bar/ Cafe to open but am always nervous to go to opening day Bc brand new places can let you down....But I was extremely happy!!  Staff was very helpful, knowledgeable and friendly. Prices are great and location/atmosphere is perfect. They have amazing fun and unique Wines and Sherry's can't wait to go back and have lunch there!
The Happy Pig breakfast sandwich was great!  For a little shop that's so focused on cupcakes, the cafe does a great job in providing a diverse menu.

Friendly staff. I had Juanita protein burrito.  The meat was nice and tender. The flavor was yummy. Salsa's were very tasty    My husband enjoyed the fajitas too.  I would definitely recommend the restaurant and look forward to going back.

I had the gluten free pancakes. They were spendy, as everything in this town is. They were light and filling. My wife had breakfast combo and said it was her favorite breakfast so far on our trip. Would recommend to anyone.
my daughters worked here. but ive had enough of their irresponsible plastic packaging, bags, utensils. Natural cafe? BS
Make your reservation and get on in there.  You will not regret it, this just became one of the best restaurants in SB.

I wish I could give this place 1/2 star because it sucks so bad and I don't know why it is so popular. I believe people who come here haven't had authentic Indian food. All their gravies taste the same and are sooooo overpriced. I ordered vegetable biryani tonight and all I got was a mush. If you've had biryani before, you'd know that it is a rice dish that has layers/ beds of vegetables and basmati rice. What I got was a mush of rice with frozen vegetables and food coloring don't believe me? See the attached photo.

I've visited this place a few times and I really like the thai tea when you check in. I always get the pad thai and I was a little disappointed the second time. But it's super filling and enough for leftovers. I like the ambiance, a great place to catch up with a friend.

Fun place to go on the water! We came here after the deep sea tasting room and the vibes were chill with really nice waiters. Food is decent, not great but not bad, but I do have to say the drinks were great! Very very strong and big, totally worth. Definitely recommend coming here after going wine tasting at Deep Sea
One of my favorite restaurants in Santa Barbara. Classy setting and great food. Nice bar area and great service.

This review for Joe's Cafe has no photo's , this is a new cell the old cell was not save and lost....My Bad!  I was in Santa Barbara really early on business one morning, so trying to find a restaurant or cafe around 7am only Joe's Cafe  and Starbucks are open. They had outdoors as well as indoors dining, I was seated right away from a friendly waitress i look over the menu and was ready to order, the three waitress took their lengthy time in conversation with the regular customer over looking me, 'of course i was on my cell' I finally flag someone down who apologize. I ordered the chicken and waffles with eggs which was just OK with a cup of coffee around $22.00  This is the oldest cafe in Santa Barbara EST.1928, the furniture is dark wood with a three arch bar, old black & white photo's  on the wall plus all of the tables has red & white tablecloths. If ever I'm the 805 again that early on a turn around...it's Starbucks.

A must when in Santa Barbara. Beautiful little restaurant. Welcoming feel. All the staff were very friendly. Place was spotless. Food is amazing, simply delicious. You will want a reservation as everyone know how great this place is. If you have to wait I assure you it's worth it.

We were back last night for the second time: as with the first, it was a splendid dinner experience. Style, quality, presentation: everything sets this restaurant apart. It is a welcome addition to the Santa Barbara food scene. I do like the simple, unassuming atmosphere. Finally a restaurant that will change its menu once a month!!

Our Family lives in SB and so wants to support local business. We had lunch today at Flor. Staff is very overwhelmed with limited help. When the waitress asked us would our daughter like chicken in her children's quesadilla we asked if she could have it on the side. Very accommodating as she got what she asked for. When we received the bill the children's quesadilla came to $23 total. I asked to speak with Mngr and female came out and said this is the going rate right now for food on Cabrillo Blvd. I had the filet mignon tacos and my daughters quesadilla was $8 more than my full meal. If you are going to survive in this reopening the owners need to educate the management that this is not acceptable. The only reason why I am giving two stars is the waitress was very accommodating in getting us a table with umbrella. We will not be enjoying Flor anytime soon.

5 stars for this place, hands down! The owner is so friendly and welcoming and I love the vibe here! They were playing Planet Earth on a large projection screen and man, when the scene with the lizard running from the snakes came out, everyone went wild! They also have several board games you can play and lots of seating space. My friends and I came here on the last night of fiesta and this was the only bar we found that didn't have a cover fee! Also, they have a large selection of craft beer and for good prices too! My boyfriend got a toasted marshmallow with his IPA. Bathrooms were very clean as well and I love the open concept of this place. Stop in and see for yourself!

Hesitant to write a review because we don't want this gem to get overcrowded. But they deserve it!  Fun batting cages and delicious tacos.   My wife had trio of ribs, cod and beef and loved it so much she went back for a second plate of trio with cod, Ahi tuna and shrimp.   I had the tuna salad which was delicious.   The ahi tuna was perfectly seared. The cod was blackened just right and the shrimp was fried to perfection. The sauce with the chips was too spicy but I couldn't stop eating it. The guacamole was nicely done.   Will be back.

Driving from wine country back to LA and ended up stopping in for a quick lunch. The place is definitely built for corporate lunches, plain with no frills. I got the fish tacos, which were fine. Their guacamole is fresh and delicious and the service was very friendly.   Wouldn't go out of my way to come back here but if I worked or lived in the area, it would probably be part of the rotation.

The vegan black margherita pizza was really good.  I've never had food made with activated charcoal before, and it was fun to try. The crust was surprisingly light and chewy with a great crisp edge. The sauce was tasty. I'm so thankful that they had vegan cheese. My only wish was that the cheese was a bit better. Some places use a fresh, homemade almond ricotta which is the best.

Service is great! Food is even better! I ordered a spicy chicken omelette with butter croissant and my friend ordered Huevos Rancheros. The croissant is homemade, very buttery, and toasted to a flaky crispy. The omelette was awesome and delicious. The Huevos Rancheros is similar to a tostada...I guess in this case, a morning tostada. It was great nonetheless. One of the breakfast/brunch restaurants in SB.

It hurts my heart that so many people had poor experiences/tastes with Uncle Rocco's, because I really enjoyed it! I had a large NY cheese pizza delivered on a Friday, and I was expecting a long wait but it arrived in under 30 minutes. Good amount of cheese, flavorful sauce, still hot from the oven when I opened the box -- I thought it was delicious. Only reason I don't give 5 stars is it cost over $40, for just a cheese pizza.
Cheap fresh seafood with great location. Always busy. I wish the selection could be a bit wider but this place is still the best bang for your buck.

Nothing is 'bad' about this place, but everything needs work. You might get lucky and come in on a 4-Star night. Probably not, but you might. More likely you'll show up on a 2 or 3 Star night. Like I did.  Service: I was there at the bar, at 6:00 on a Thursday. Bar was full, dining area had one large party. Place was not full by any means. Took them 5 minutes to say hello and another 5 to ask me if I wanted anything. Food order took 20 minutes. People. No.  Food: Cheeseburger with an egg; side of cauliflower fritters. Bun was burned, black on part of it. Burger was cooked well (they didn't ask what temp I wanted, so...ok.) Egg was cooked through. Onions aren't 'fried.' They are deep-fried. Lame. Cauliflower was unseasoned and served with a tzaziki that has no flavor. Again. No. You are a bar! Nail the burger! It's fine. I was hungry so I ate it. What are you gonna do?  Drink: I got a beer. Don't ask the bartender what he thinks. He doesn't know. But the selection is good.  All in all. SB is kind of a drag for places like these, and this place is no exception. They are in the old 'American Ale' space which is a shame, because those guys knew what they were doing. This place doesn't. Pick something else.

The four stars aren't just for the heavy pour on the drinks... We come here too often for the steak bites and the bar food, like jalapeno bottlecaps (spicy), and humungous sweet Maui onion rings. I do, however, realize that there are off-nights but they are accommodating and value their customers. I highly recommend ordering the steak bites medium-rare because they can come chewy or gristly. Their sourdough bread and bruschetta/salsa is tasty! Careful not to fill-up on bread because their sandwiches are hefty. The burnt ends sandwich is delicious! The tri-tip is chopped up (fairly small pieces) but they overload your bread and plate with tons of meat. Bartenders here are a hoot! We always have a great time sitting at the bar and enjoying random conversations... Tip them well - they control the alcohol!  Thanks again Harry's for all the late-night eats and drinks!

My husband and I loved this place!  We sat outside near the fireplace and it was very cozy because it was a bit cool that night.  We loved the porque pork, bacon wrapped dates, lamb sliders and some special called the Fauxtine which is like a poutine without any meat (mushrooms instead).  So delicious! We wanted to go a second night during our stay in SB but didn't get a chance-- during UCSB graduation.

I love Opal so much! Always a classic Santa Barbara staple. LOVE the Jack Daniels Chocolate Cream Brulee (and the original vanilla bean -- must get both! AND the Like water for chocolate surprise, Yes.) Everything from the home made pasta to fresh oven pizza to the warm seafood salad is to die for. Thank you for being you, Opal.
Saigon is okay. Pricey and decent food. Got the spicy seafood pho and just wasn't wow'd. Nevertheless there are many options to choose from on the menu. I would give another try

There is always a wait at this popular restaurant. I've been here twice for brunch and I definitely enjoyed my time. The price is slightly above average for American traditional breakfast/brunch. The price range for my two visits was $16 to $21.

Authentic as it gets!!! Their family owned staff makes the food taste that much better... if it was possible! Only downside is when I am craving one of their dishes on a Sunday :( Perfect meal to leave you satisfied after a craving for exotic and delicious without making you feel heavy and swollen.  Will be back for sure!

The word 'phenomenal' doesn't do this sushi justice. I won't need or want to go anywhere else for sushi in town. It reminds me of the quality of Itsuki when they were still on Calle Real (see my earlier review).  There is a lightness of touch and freshness of ingredients here that makes one realize how clunky most sushi offerings are. Each slice of roll is perfectly round and exactly the right size to be eaten in one bite, not the usually too large and unwieldy slices that are so common. The shrimp in my son's tempura roll was just perfectly cooked--barely done with a feathery crispy crust.   Online ordering was a breeze (and I'm a luddite who hates this interface usually) and it was waiting for me on the 'to go' table in public market exactly as ordered ahead of time.  10 stars!

This is my favorite Mexican restaurant in Santa Barbara. It's a must stop on my trip. When you walk in grab a menu and get on line. It moved fast and there always seem to be a table. Everything is really good. Warming the ceviche is good but a large portion. I had the agaves burrito and it's really good. I got black beans instead of rice but my husband said the rice was good. They have a salsa bar, so try them all. Restaurant is pretty cleans and the staff is very friendly and helpful.
Great coffee with nice presentation. Croissant sandwich was delicious. Nice place to have a leisurely breakfast outdoors and people watch.

Just what we needed on lower state.  Good craft beer and they really did a nice job with this space.  Beer selection is tremendous, just need to add a hazy IPA and see more details on their hops on the menu.  Great bar and indoor and outdoor seating.  Ordering system if not at bar a little different, but I think it works pretty well.

We stopped by again this morning. We had a tray of uni and ahi poke.  Superb!  Simply the best uni and I love uni. Fresh and delicious   I just can't say enough about this place. They treat their customers with respect. Props to Isaac!!  Five enthusiastic stars!
We had the bbq chicken sammie and the tomato soup and it was easy to see why they are popular. Soooooo yum.

Our go to whenever walking the art on the beach in the summer. There is usually not much of a wait. They serve a nice a brunch. Their fish tacos are classic SoCal. With an option of lobster. My out of town friend wasn't impressed with the non traditional lobster roll but it looked yummy to me. I love their grilled artichokes. Hard for me to pass up.

Ordered takeout from here on a Thursday night & it did not disappoint! We called in our order vs ordering for pickup online because for some reason the services they offer don't list everything on the menu.. so we called & it took about 20 mins.   - Tikka Masala: Warm and cozy.. wouldn't call it traditional but it came with pita & rice. Yum! 4/5   - BBQ flatbread: lots of bbq sauce & toppings, really good! Comforting. Ordered with a side salad and that was delicious too. 4/5   - fried chicken meal: AMAZING!!! Soooo good 100% the best thing we ordered. 5/5  - Key Lime Cheesecake: we all LOVEDDD this omg. Great size but we ordered 2 for 4 people and it was so yum.   Overall, we will def be back! We might even eat there because it looked super cozy when we picked out.

My favorite market in Santa Barbara! The produce is always fresh, the meat department is top of the line, and nothing beats the freshness of the bakery. However the absolute best part of this place is the prepared foods section. Everything is always so good, so fresh, and the serving areas are clean!! I love this place!

This is my favorite spot at the Santa Barbara Public Market. I go here almost every time I'm visiting from LA, and haven't had a single thing on the menu that wasn't incredibly flavorful. The shrimp ceviche is wonderful.   The tacos are out of this world delicious -- my favorite is the one with octopus and shrimp.
Tatiana and Madi are great!  Always take great care of us at the bar. One of our favorite happy hours in town largely due to the great staff!

Our poolside server Matt was absolutely wonderful! His customer service was spectacular. His poolside attentiveness was unmatched by the other food and beverage service attendees. As a local I am very proud to have stayed here with my boyfriend tonight! We are having the time of our lives and it's all thanks to our exceptional server Matt.
I love this place. Every time I come to SB I try to eat here. Fresh, delicious, good potions, reasonably priced and creative/innovative offerings.

My friends and I stopped in pre-hike for a pastry and coffee. Long line but moves quickly. Cashiers were terrible and unfriendly but the bussers were friendly and polite! Scones are yum, coffee is forgettable.

Cody's Cafe is our weekly Salmon Salad pickup during Restrictions. Ordinarily, pre-covid, we would lunch there almost every week. As always, the service is still great. The food is made to order and delicious. Christina is always welcoming and knows just how we like our order prepared. The cooks are fantastic too! We look forward to getting back to Cody's and the whole staff soon!

The location is just beautiful. It is secluded, quiet and perfect to getaway for a few days. The grounds are gorgeous and make you slow down, relax and enjoy.   The staff is very friendly and accommodating, making you feel welcome. Since this is a bit of a distance from Santa Barbara I recommend it for a true weekend escape where you enjoy the resort itself and the ocean location.   I was very impressed with the size and quality of the gym. This is by the far the best gym at any hotel or resort in my experience.   The rooms are cozy and nice but in need of some TLC here and there.
I love this place! The cats are so well taken care of and I was really impressed by the staff. I wish I could give it more stars. Go here if you like cats or knead;) to destress.

Got the waffle witch which was wonderful! The waffle is soft and has an orange essence that's surprising. The chicken is tender as stated, fried and with a little spicy kick. Served with a flavorful mayo, arugula and tomato, this sandwich was a winner. They also gave me a complimentary buttery pastry. I'll be back.

I've been to this location many times and the selections are always empty and they need to cook them to fulfill an order.   Tonight we went and ordered Beef and Broccoli and Teriyaki Chicken. Of course we had to wait 10 min for the chicken and once we arrived home we find there is no Teriyaki Sauce and No Beef in the Broccoli.   Poor experience.

'The world is little, people are little, human life is little. There is only one big thing -- desire.'  ― Willa Cather, The Song of the Lark  The area around the Santa Barbara train station has grown exponentially over the last few years.  Now there are a plethora of restaurants, wineries, shops whereas before there was just the original Lilly's Tacos (Still the best taco experience I've ever had).  I discovered both the Spanish restaurant and The Lark last year and decided to try The Lark once again before another SB Bowl concert. While last year I would have given it 5 stars, this year the experience was not quite that magnificent so I will give it 4 stars.    A plus of course is free valet parking.  Given lack of parking in area, that starts the meal off right.  The restaurant includes both indoor and outdoor seating, both times we sat outside. Reservations available through Open Table.   We were seated on recent visit next to Lucky Penny's patio and that creates a weirdness of hearing two outdoor music playlists. These two places really should coordinate.  It definitely detracts from the experience.    Service was solid but not overly friendly.  And some gaps by not offering us the bread course, disappearing for long stretches.  But those are minor irritants.    As for the food, not as wonderful as last year.  The cheese board was not as impressive, crackers not as good as the bread.  Strawberry jam was best part.    The salad my dining companion ordered this year was just okay however the charred octopus was out of this world good. The Santa Barbara Gruner Veltliner we started with was lovely, the Santa Ynez Cabernet was also nice but lacking a bit body. They also gave us a taste of the Lo-Fi Gamay but it was too unsubstantial to consider ordering.   So five star visit last year, 3.5 stars this year.  I will call it a 4 star.    Recommended, but pricey especially with wine.  I think there is better value elsewhere and the competing music ambiance lowers value of outside seating.

I felt like I woke up in the early 1970's when I walked into this place! Very cool! Places like this aren't built... they grow over years as you can tell by all the cool swag adorning the wallpapered walls. Sat at the bar about an hour before they closed. Ordered a drink and a couple appetizers. Drink was stiff and the apps were served fast and hot. Great experience that you should try to catch if you're in the area.

We wish we would have loved this classic Santa Barbara bistro but we found it in desperate need of reinvention and new energy! From the underwhelming staff to the lackluster pastries (I think many of the desserts are even the same, or very similar, as when we visited pre-covid) it was a Renaud's experience far from what we remember. The croissant sandwich I ordered was tired and sloppy, although there was more than enough ham so it was at least filling? We ordered orange which was fresh but small and the patio service was nonexistent.  All in all we left full but unfortunately regretful of our restaurant choice.
Absolutely delicious sushi! Efficient service. Lovely location. I am a total sushi snob and I regret I didn't discover this place sooner. So yum!!!

Dined here Saturday for a last-minute birthday dinner. The staff was friendly and accommodating, the atmosphere was beautiful, yet casual, the wine list is well-curated, and the food was nothing short of amazing.  We got the Eggamuffins, Grilled Avocado, Animal Potatoes, and Especial to share, then everyone ordered entrees. I chose the New York Strip even though I don't usually like to order steak from restaurants (I have a sous vide, which makes cooking steak a reasonably less daunting task), but it was one of the best I've ever had. It was perfectly cooked and the mushrooms and gnocchi were delicious; I wish I knew what I was getting myself into when I ordered it since I will now be craving it. We ended the night with four desserts, including their Baked California, which is always a treat.   My boyfriend and I have been here quite a few times, but this recent experience took the cake (birthday pun). Barbareño is undoubtedly one of the best restaurants in Santa Barbara. We will definitely be back!

I've been here a few times and food is good. We showed up Sunday night for dinner and the service was horrible. The hostess was so rude. Our waiter treated us like we were a burden on him. Anything we needed we had to consistently ask someone else for. $80 later I spoke to the manager who said she would get back to me. Two days later, crickets.  Bummer but I won't be back. I'd rather give my money to other local businesses that treat their patrons much better.

I am not a vegan, but this restaurant is really great. The Thai noodle dish is my favorite. Their hummus platter is truly amazing. I think they do an excellent presentation and food quality is amazing.

I only had the avocado toast and a latte but both were delicious! The place is super cute inside and service was great.  I went by myself and sat at the bar and felt totally cozy. I saw some plates coming out that looked amazing so definitely will be back?

Still one of my favorite stops in SB. The original location on Milpas, or the latest on De La Vina are both fast and delicious. it's a walk-up to order, take your number to your table style restaurant but the food is ALL SLOW WAIT tasting (if that makes any sense). If you want Mexican and you're headed through Santa Barbara with only a little time to spare SKIP Taco Bell for the LOVE of God and your waistline and come to Los Agaves. You'll thank Yelp later.

Came here for happy hour before a concert at the arlington and it was great! The inside of the restaurant alone is really cute, modern and bright. I loved the look upon entrance. We sat at one of the very comfy seatings they have in the bar area. A couple of TV's to watch the game was a nice touch. Not overly sports-ish but just enough!   We started with the Lemoncillo Lemon drop which was way stronger than I had anticipated - but soooo so damn good!   We ordered a bunch of items off their happy hour Calamari - Pretty average as far as the calamari goes but great different dipping sauces Mussels - the sauce seemed really thick and a bit excessive but good.  Charbroiled Oysters - My absolute favorite. The char and flavor was on point! Ahi Ceviche - Felt like a lot more cucumbers and tomatoes than ahi but a great huge portion. Excellent for sharing.  Fresh Oysters - Raw, clean, crisp and fresh. Their horseraddish doesn't play either!!   I also had their watermelon martini which was equally as tasty!   My only ding on this place was that the service felt pretty slow. Our waitress didn't really check on us often, we felt like we always had to search forever to try to find her at all.

LOL!!!  They really did try to pass this mushroom Chow Mein off as Pancit.  I'm still laughing about this.  Plus when they cook the noodles here, they are SO BUSY ALL OF THE TIME that they don't allow the food to really sit and cook in the wok, so you don't get that nice wok burn or wok flavor.  Do yourself a favor here:  take the pan fried noodles OFF the menu as you OBVIOUSLY don't have time to pan fry.  Just serve soups/noodle soups.  Everything else is Meh.  (Including the mango in the spring rolls.  Why?)  The pho didn't have the aromatics.  Man, what a let down (and a stressed out cashier shutting down our questions mid sentence).

A little bummed that there was very little meat in both the chicken tikka combo plate and the curry chicken combo plate. My husbands and I each had maybe 4 pieces on our plate. I wish their rice and chicken were switched in terms of portion size. Flavor was good.

I've been coming here for years, but as of late I can't seem to get enough of it. I always knew this was one of the best places in town to get food, but now I'm thinking it's gotta be in the top 3 (if not number 1!)  One of the best things about it (in my opinion) is the variety you get. You can try amazing new dishes and never get bored. And if you like to get the same thing every time, the quality is very consistent. I love the salsa bar, and if you've never tried it, I highly suggest the chipotle salsa.   All in all, this is just a great local restaurant, and I think it has earned it's spot in the top restaurants of SB. Check it out for sure!

hmmm...  i really torn here.    i WANT to love Julienne...  but after lingering on my experience there Friday night, ive decided this rating is apt and fair  its a lovely, quaint space...  what a neighborhood gem that flies under the proverbial radar should be.  an open kitchen with maybe 4-5 bar seats thats lets guests view the creativeness as it occurs if they wish, interesting artwork lines the walls, and a nice hustle and bustle (though, almost too much bustle) gives birth to an environment of being in a place where you can enjoy a relaxing meal with your friends but still feel like you're where the action is.  we were a party of three ready to enjoy a night of excellent food and wine and Julienne accommodated well...  we brought along our own bottles of wine, and when arrived about 10 min before our reservation time the hostess chilled the bubbly and chardonnay for us while we waited for a table to open up.  bottle service was excellent...  glasses were constantly kept filled as what our water.    the charcuterie was delicious (apologies, i cant remember exactly each part), and accompanied by grilled ciabatta and two different mustards.  the ciabatta was ok...  it would have been better served warm and not grilled with butter already added.    the pasta dish was passable.  sinful and rich with a center filled with ricotta, in the end it tasted like a special at Macaroni Grill.  the salads were a tease...  two of us had an arugula salad w/ beets, feta, walnuts and a vinaigrette...  it was so delicious...  one of the better salads ive ever had...  until the dressing kicked in...  oh man, way too salty.  i ended up passing up my chardonnay for the water (maybe two glasses) during this course.  my main of scallops in an oxtail sauce was earthy and delightful, but for $26 a little underwhelming as far as the portions.  if it had been amazing rather than enjoyable, i wouldn't have minded desiring more...  and while the environment had moments of serenity, it wasn't always so...  when we were first seated there was the obligatory kitchen smoke Sivi F mentioned though that quickly abated...  and the restaurant, while nicely appointed, doesn't absorb sound well...  on a busy night as was Friday, you find yourself having to speak over those around you and the open kitchen...  there was also a ghost (according to our server) who loved Hip Hop and on two occasions the dining area blared some really bad early 00's rap from a kitchen radio.  it was quite comical actually, but did nothing to add to the ambiance.  so in the end... i give Julienne an A-OK.  but its more than that rating.  id happily return because i can honestly see the promise of a restaurant that i would love to include on my frequent rotation to the area.  but for now, and based on this introductory experience, i have to save that promise for another time.

Love it, love it, love it.  Have been now at least ten times to Flor de Maiz, and was always perfect. Love that they are open non-stop. Tacos al pulpo are my favorite, but all the ceviches are wonderful as well.  We sometimes do happy hour, sitting close to the bar and getting the pilsner at a preferred rate and a special happy hour menu (on top of the general one).  A really good deal.  Staff is very nice and accommodating. Highly recommended.

My first time here. I ordered the crab cake benedict. The eggs were poached perfectly, soft and creamy. The crab cakes were excellent. So many times, crab cakes taste like the filler. Either bell pepper or celery etc. That was most certainly not the case here. The crab cake tasted like crab. They used a Chipotle hollandaise sauce which I was dubious about but was very surprised at how tasty it was. They make their own Hollandaise sauce in house and the Chipotle flavor went so well with the crab cake. Their hash browns are crispy on the outside and fluffy on the inside. I'm hard on hash browns as so many of them are greasy, or they taste like onions or some other flavor or worst of all, they are simply dry. These hash browns were very good. They have a full bar so you may indulge in a mimosa, a bellini or in my case, a bloody mary. Their well vodka is good and the bloody Mary was very good although not the best I've ever had but still good. The service here is excellent, the restrooms are very clean. This morning they had the open golf championship on TV. I hate it when bars have something other than sports on the tv. I haven't had anything else on the menu but if the crab cake Benedict is an indicator, I highly recommend this place. They're open for breakfast lunch and dinner.

My mother and I dined here for breakfast, and it was a great experience. My mother is a vegetarian and she completely enjoyed her  meal - the egg white omelette. The customer service was great. My mom said this was the best omelette she's ever had ... and that is very significant to me. The only thing I was disappointed about was the temperature of my entree - the short ribs with sweet potato hash was luke warm. Very luke warm. Almost tepid. Other than that, everything was great.

I don't think there is a restaurant that deserves 5 stars more than this place!  Now first of all, well, I'm dumb because I thought this place was one of the few Bouchons that Thomas Keller owns! Having been a few times to the one in Beverly Hills, I was thinking this one was way better... but now that I know they're not owned by the same person, I guess I can't make that comparison!    Anyway, the wonderful Pumpkie took me to Santa Barbara for my birthday and we went here for my birthday dinner! And I loved it! I highly recommend this place!! All the high praise and accolades are definitely spot on!  Why you ask?!  Well first of all, this one is way more intimate. It is a smaller restaurant and the seating is a bit more tight but the executive chef brings the plates and your food! And basically waited on our table! Can't beat that!  We started with the scallop appetizer and it was absolutely AMAZING! I love scallops and these were absolutely cooked to perfection! They were so fresh (I mean it is Santa Barbara after all - but you never know) and the 4 presentations, preparations and flavors were all so different and just so delicious! I loved this dish and it was definitely one of the best things I've ever eaten! Dare I say these scallops were better than the one at Gordon Ramsey's (psss - they were!).
