If it's on the menu, get this! It's absolutely amazing!!   French Onion soup - well, this was for Pump but this was also one of - if not the - best French onion soups I've ever had the pleasure of eating (and I've had a good amount). The bread and cheese at the top were just so good and that deep onion flavor could only come from many hours spent developing a complex and deep flavor. Another absolutely delicious dish!   The little amuse tart - the chef was kind enough to bring me this tiny tart while the Pump was having his soup! So very kind! My goodness! Even something this small and tiny was absolutely delicious! The fruit was just so sweet and this is the beginning of January mind you! The crust was flaky and buttery and the cream was so good! Delicious!   The venison - I contemplated ordering this because I wanted to try something different but the Pump was nice enough to get this so I could try it and then get something that I knew I would like - the chicken! This is my first taste of venison and it was good - very lean and I could see how some people would call it slightly minerally. Definitely good!   The chicken- obviously a Mary's chicken and cooked to perfection! Juicy, flavorful and delicious! And that's asking a lot of chicken! :) The mashed potatoes were also mascarpone whipped and amazingly delicious! Yum!   Also, the portions are not chintzy at all and because of this and all the food we got, we were both too stuffed to have dessert! This is my one regret as I am sure they're dessert is amazing!! I definitely want to come back and next time, I'll make sure to leave room for dessert!!   Honestly, I can't wait to come back again soon!

I used to go regularly, I hadn't gone in last 2 Covid years. When I opened my container I thought someone ate it and left me the scraps. Huge disappointment I won't return. Birria platter $16.95 to go with a side of 4 flour tortillas. One spoon of beans, birria dripping in oil and two spoons of rice, no chips. Tiny little portion and only got one tortilla. As hungry as I was I tossed it. I really wanted my dinner to be good and it was far from it.

10/10 service. They even text to remind you to bring a jacket if it gets breezy!   Make a reservation. Park across the street or at the public parking structure on Helena. I recommend getting there early as a line starts to form around 5pm.   Came here for a first date and it was the perfect place for it. Ambience is outstanding in their outdoor seating, and you can't beat SB weather. They bring you a fancy glass pitcher of water and won't let your cup get empty. For two people, order one or two tapas and two bocaditos to offset the time it takes for the grand paella to come out (allow 45 min but for us it came out sooner). The Croqueta de Queso and Pan con Tomate were tasty, but the real pregame winner were the Papatas Bravas - addicting brava sauce that paired so well with hot pimentón emulsion and roasted garlic aioli. When it was time, our mariscos paella came out pretty epic! They don't skimp on the seafood and the portion was perfect to share between the two of us. Savory, smoky, just right. I loved the squid and clams. My date said one of the mussels were bad, but everything else was pleasant.   Looking forward to returning to try their drinks and desserts next time!

I took my wife here on our anniversary weekend. I have to start by saying that even with a plethora of wait staff and a relatively empty restaurant, the service was almost non existent. Paired with the fact that the food was mediocre at best, I cannot recommend this restaurant.
My experience atMiddle Child was phenomenal.Ypu have to try their amazing pimento spicy cheese! Such a good welcoming environment. Would feel happy reading a book alone or hanging with friends.

I'm pretty forgiving having eaten at many Pho places all my life (my family is Vietnamese and we like to try out new restaurants).  I almost hate to give only 2 stars because the decor and cleanliness of the restaurant is one of the best I've ever seen and very spacious and I think I even saw a few outdoor seats.  The staff is super nice and attentive.  However, when it comes to the food, this is not where you want to eat to experience Vietnamese.  It's not horrible, but it is probably one of the lowest on my scale of Pho soup...it's like Panda Express of Chinese food...just not very authentic.  The soup isn't very flavorful.  The noodles fall apart.  And the meat is dry.  We ordered out before, and they gave us the meat already pre-cooked.  Normally when you order a Pho Tai to go, they give it to you raw and you boil the soup to pour over and cook the meat (you're expected to eat it when you get home).  This is how it is in the bay area and orange county from my experiences.  I read some of the lesser reviews on Yelp of this place and thought I'd try it out and give it a chance.  It's just as bad as the other not-so-great reviews.  I'd love to see this place get better.  Ie, please run your kitchen better!  Otherwise, if you've never had Vietnamese before, then I wouldn't recommend it, but you can come here cause you wouldn't be able to compare it to real good Vietnamese anyway.  Again, it's not the worse food I've ever eaten...it's just not really Vietnamese goodness.  Also, the price here is way jacked up.  $9 for a smaller soup that tastes worse than you can get for $4 in orange county or bay area delicousness.  However, I admit that most Vietnamese restaurants in Santa Barbara are relatively more expensive (around the $7 mark).  Ridiculous.

Santo Mezcal is an upper scale restaurant by the same people nas Los Agaves, another chain of local restaurants here in Santa Barbara. Having had almost every dish at Los Agaves, I came in subconsciously comparing it to their dishes. The quedo fundido is deeelish! Also had the pulpo (octopus) as the main dish and it was very well spiced and tender. Drinks were on point and their salsa was definitely more flavorful.   Above all though, their service was excellent.

This is more of a locals spot, although you wouldn't necessarily know it by the crowds. Worth the wait though. The people are great, they have a nice bar overlooking the harbor and some greta seafood. If your just there for a quick light bite, go for the chowder and salad combo. The soup is terrific and the salad is even better (great dressing). All of their fresh fish items are very fresh and delicious. A great place all the way around.

Very cute Italian bistro.   Pros:  Service: The service was awesome! Attentive, friendly but not cloying. Wine List: Very good wine list. We ordered a recommended Pinot Grigio, and it was so tasty and affordable at just shy of $40 per  bottle. Great wine selection. Food: Good. Their Caesar salad is a revelation with the whole anchovy. The pastas were just a bit over al dente, but very good.  Table bread was okay; a bit dry. The accompanying olive oil and vinegar was very good.  Cons: Not any.

I came here for dinner tonight and they did not disappoint! Will be my go-to for oysters now. We ordered lobster bisque, a dozen oysters on the half shell, charbroiled oysters, cioppino, seafood skewer, and brussel sprouts. I think the highlight of my dinner were the oysters for me. They were very fresh and meaty and had a very good flavor of the sea. Even though they were $1.50 happy hour oysters, they were very nice quality! The charbroiled oysters on the other hand were just ok. The cheese on it was too salty and did not melt into the oyster and did not have much flavor other than the saltiness. I've had a lot better elsewhere. They were pretty fresh though.  The cioppino was also very good. Flavor was nice and was a hearty portion with a good variety of seafood. Would highly recommend.  The lobster bisque also had a nice flavor. One tip i recommend you to do is remove the puff pastry and put it on the side rather than into the soup because it can get soggy and gross after a while.  Im not going to talk about the brussel sprouts since I'm not a fan of brussel sprouts at all. The seafood skewer I had was alright, nothing too special about this dish, but I've never had swordfish, so that was the appeal and it's not bad. Would not really recommend this dish, it wasn't special, you get 1 skewer, 2 sides and it's $19. Also, the complimentary bread and butter before the meal was excellent and worth the mention. The bread was super crispy on the outside and warm and super soft on the inside. Was so tempted to fill up on it. Overall the food from favorite to least favorite of the night goes like this: oysters on half shell, cioppino, lobster bisque, seafood skewer, and charbroiled oysters.  Service on the other hand was not bad, our server was Angela and she was friendly.  I can't wait to come back and try their lobster specials on mondays and Tuesdays. When I asked how much they were today, was told $60 for lobster. And the lobster special is less than $40!  Also, I recommend getting their Groupon to save on your meal! I saved $30 tonight on my meal and my Groupon was $18 (save $12).

Hands down best french toast I have had in my life.  The hamburger was also amazing.  Yes, my friend and I split both of these dishes. I also tried the fried green tomatos, which were surprisingly good (since I hate tomatos) and the mac & cheese was DELISH!  They serve breakfast all day, which is always a plus for breakfast lovers such as myself.  The bloody mary have a kick and a lot of flavor, but if you don't like seafood beware they stick a crab leg in the drink.  YIKES!  Everything had such a great presentation and was delicious.  We went with a group of 7 girls and sat down right away.
Sadly overrated. The impossible burger was so bland.

Make sure you bring a picnic blanket. Buy your food here and picnic on the beach just a few steps away. Come early so they don't run out of fish, plus it's super fresh. The poke and oysters were fresh. They even shuck them for you. The prices are affordable and would love to try their uni but they didn't have any today.   Free parking for 90 mins. They are strict on the time because the parking enforcers make your tires to make sure you have overstayed your welcome.

10/10 would recommend this place! I saw this on Yelp and it had great reviews so I decided to try it. Wow, that was the best decision I have made. The empanadas exceeded my expectations and more! The best part is it is very reasonably priced and such a hidden little gem in Santa Barbara!

Came to the Mill the last night to grab a beer with some classmates and decided to give Wildwood a shot. I got their brisket sandwich which was on point. The brisket was tender and it had just the right amount of bark on a few pieces :D I lived in Texas for about a year and couldn't find anything else in town that was comparable. Wildwood is the closest that I think I'll get in town.   Down a star due to the side order of fries that I got. They were pretty cold and I was a little bit disappointed here.

So impressed! We rarely eat out because of a very tight budget and when I get good quality food I make it a priority to Yelp about it!   The buns, omg so yummy, soft and buttery! The meat was thick, juicy and cooked to perfection!  The crunchy onions (drool) and the burger was dripping with sauce! There was a brat in there! It was heavenly!   I ate 3/4 of my burger and 1/2 of my Parmesan garlic fries and I was stuffed beyond belief!   We will definitely be back! Environment was relaxing (I used to love Mexican Fresh so I love this location anyway), the staff were friendly and attentive as well!  Thank you!

Still my favorite clean, simple tacos in Santa Barbara. If you want just simple charbroiled meats and housemade tortillas, and basic pico de gallo salsa to hit your tongue, they fulfill that so well. You can taste the quality of their meats, the flavor of their char, it's just so simply good.  Almost every table had the chicken with mushrooms, peppers and onions tacos. Not sure why. This isn't their best thing. The charbroiled beef steak and pork steak are wonderful. The taste of the meats are as good as it gets. It's almost as if they don't marinate them, and just simply add salt and pepper, because the natural taste of the meats is what makes them shine.   I passed by other tables and saw something like a pozole taco, if there's such a thing. I'm going to try those next time.   Great, consistently good place from someone who has been coming here for over 10 years now.

First of all, I must apologize to Carlos and Los Agaves for taking so long to update my review. I initially left a two star rating for a pretty sub par burrito experience, but they were super responsive and nearly immediately reached out for more feedback, and for the opportunity to better. And they did.  Yelp can be a brutal and honestly unfair and unrepresentative experience for business owners and Yelp users. But the greatest possible benefit for Yelp users (and businesses)is not to just savage a business after a single experience and then preaching out forever.  If you're someone who Yelps, do your best to give honest feedback (no matter how brutal), but give but do so in a way that gives BUSINESS's an opportunity to do better next time.  Thanks again to Carlos and Los Agaves, it's pretty much a staple in my diet and I don't see that changing any time soon!

I guess we can say it's pretty good American Mexican food  It's very American here it's not authentic at all but it's still good  Service, eh they are super busy so it can take awhile to get any help -salsas omg the guacamole and the habanero salsa was my fav! It was super good  Parking is kinda hard to find in their tiny lot so I suggest street parking  Burrito was ok but they used good steak  Enchilada was good Hard taco was not so great  Sope was grossssss Chips were amazing

How have I just now discovered this place! Just when you think you have tried all Mexican food in SB, make sure you come here. Tacos were great and tried the rest of the food others ordered and instantly wanted to return to get what they got. Loved it.

I've been here twice in the last two weeks with my husband and this is by far my favorite place to get donuts. The variety is good, the cinnamon rolls are as big as my head and whenever the person ringing us up tells us the price I'm always amazed. You can walk out with a lot and hardly spend any money. Another favorite of ours- the jelly/cream filled donut. Absolutely heavenly!

Unfortunately, my friends and I came here when I had food poisoning so I wasn't able to eat anything yummy.  I would give it another star if I had been able to try one of their cool sounding pastas but alas, I can only rate it on the thing I ate.   The only thing I ordered was a little side of their pasta salad.  It was pretty standard, you can't go too wrong with pasta salad  It was a very good consistency, not too much dressing.  However, I definitely want to go back and try some of their other stuff.  The menu has pretty interesting pastas and whatever my roomies ordered, well it looked and smelled delicious!  I was so sad to have missed out.    The place is really cute though!  It can only accommodate a small group of people so don't try bringing a crap load of people.  You can, of course, dine outside but the day I went there, it was raining pretty badly.

I didn't think there would have ever been a day when I would have to write a one star review, but unfortunately, it's today. I was visiting my girlfriend in Santa Barbara, and we were both craving Korean BBQ. We decided to go to Kimchi since she had been here a little over a year ago and said it was good. I guess in a year, a lot has changed, because she even noticed that it had gotten worse. Let me start by saying their service was the only thing that was decent, and that was not that great itself. All the servers looked confused, and they sort of had a bad attitude towards each other. One of the servers who came to change our grill (I assume he was new), leaned over to grab the grill and another girl server just came with an attitude and grabbed it from his hand and grabbed the grill herself and took it. I felt bad for the guy. But there is a right way of dealing with things, and me as a customer, I would have understood and been okay with the girl nicely teaching him rather than get mad and just snatch it from his hands.  As for the food, it was horrible. I hated every single thing I had. We ordered off the Choice 3 menu, which is their largest menu. I ordered the same things I always order, regardless of the Korean BBQ restaurant I go to. I got the brisket, the galbi (which is the shortrib), and we got beef bulgogi to start with. Of course, we asked for raddish paper and rice paper. So when they brought out the meat, it didn't look bad at all. The brisket had no flavor what so ever, and wasn't fatty, so it would get too dry. I hated it, when usually its my favorite meat at any other Korean BBQ spot. The galbi was decent, but again flavorless. The beef bulgogi was a weird meat, it was chewier than it was supposed to be. Absolutely disgusting!  I forgot to mention, the rice paper and raddish paper were GARBAGE! The rice paper was steamed or something, it came super hot, and it was thin and mushy, it was almost like mashed potato at that point. You would not be able to handle it with your hands without tearing it or mushing it. It was disgusting, didn't even taste right. The raddish paper, again one of my favorites at Korean BBQ restaurants, was trash here. The raddish wasn't cleaned good, the outer layer had black spots, and again, didn't taste right.  I'm starting to wonder maybe I went on a bad day and nothing was fresh? But what bad luck, THIS bad of a day? We then went ahead and ordered a few other favorites of ours, to give those a try because we were still hungry. We got the spicy chicken bulgogi, prime beef ribeye, and vegetables. The chicken was alright, not too bad. The ribeye was a thin piece so it got dry fast even before it was ready to eat. The veggies were the only things that were good, and I would have been surprised if they weren't.  Overall, I HATED this place. I wouldn't say I would never come back because I'd like to give places another try. I'd probably try again a year from now and update my review then!

Stopped by after a night of clubbing and they were about to close. He was nice enough to let us in and buy some donuts. These donuts are nothing special, but they definitely got the job done after a drunken night. There weren't many open donut shops in the area, so this was a good option at that time of night. Overall, good variety of donuts.

Rude, and unfriendly customer service for a flavorless cup of steamed milk that was supposed to be a latte... barista was more interested in talking to a customer than making coffee. To many other places in SB to get a cup of coffee.

Sakana has been my favorite restaurant in Santa Barbara for years. when i found out Teru, a long-time chef from Sakana, was opening his own restaurant, i was so excited. went there today and it exceeded expectations! they have a few tables outside and the service and food were both great. i will definitely be a regular here :).

We came for happy hour - and brought food home! Not joking! We ordered the hamburger and fries for $5.. thinking it was a SLIDER - wrong! Big burger and normal helping of fries. We ordered the flatbread for $5.. thinking it was the size of toast. Nope - huge regular size flatbread serves two people. We ordered the cauliflower with chili sauce - my favorite! Easily could have been the whole dinner. Then we also ordered a pretzel. (Huge German pretzel. When it came I had to put it in my lap). Our table was small and we had to juggle the plates of food around like hungry little thieves. People stared. It was comical. But the food tasted great and we had leftovers for days. Highly recommend it!

After a long hiatus due to COVID,  sandbar sent me an email declaring that 'Happy Hour is back'!  My wife and I were excited since we had loved their happy hour for years before they stopped it.  We always told our friends that they had the best happy hour in town.  So upon arrival we were seated and greeted by our server who was sweet.  We explained that we'd received an email about happy hour returning.  Our server said that happy hour drinks were offered.  I said what about happy hour appetizers?  She said no food items are included.  I told her that happy hour appetizers were what made the old happy hour great.  This review isn't a critique of the food, it was quite good but shame on the Sandbar for declaring that happy hour is back!  We will not be returning!

What a pleasant surprise! While walking around State street around lunch, we stumbled upon Blush not really knowing what to expect. The outdoor seating area was adorable, very comfortable, and the food was amazing. I wish we could have come back for dinner, but it was a busy week. Blush will definitely be on our schedule again next time we're in SB.

My fajitas were amazing and my boyfriend's burrito was just as good! We loved the venue right on State Street. The outdoor decor was nice, relaxing, and pretty. The menu's options were quite varied and comprehensive. I'd be surprised if there weren't something for everyone here!

The Internet never works here. I've come here multiple times and it's always the same response, our Internet isn't working today. If you want to use the Internet, don't come here, otherwise, they have great coffee, tea, and friendly staff.

This is an experience that everyone should have. A unique historical Tavern from 1865 and we are all blessed to be able to enjoy a wonderful barbecue here inside this magical location.  We seat inside and ordered the famous tri tip Sandwich. Because I don't eat much we shared.  Yes it is crowded but is worth the drive. Also we were seated after only 15 minutes. Than we walked around and saw all interesting buildings and joy. Since I became an American citizen one between many others that I feel proud of is how we preserve and maintain the history. That's so important and is our life's treasure.  Visiting a location like this shows us so much of how was life back then and actually is one of the few places in Us that there is no phone or internet signal but believe me, you don't need. Take this as a gifted opportunity to feel all.
Best donuts in town, love the family that owns this place they are so friendly. Ham and cheese croissant when it's warm first thing in the morning is everything!!!

Stop the car !  I believe that you may have a rather good chance of having really good food here.  I did.  It is a valid statement to call it a true bakery with high standards and that they care what they sell to their customers. I passed this place often LATER in the morning, and by that time their shelves are bare. Can't wait have a taste of other offerings from their great selection. By the way, The Lucky Llama Coffee House in Carpinteria stocks some of their baked goods as well but they also sell out quickly.
Great pizza but limited toppings odd hours and long wait time for food.  They really should open at 11am like any other normal pizza place.

This place is awesome, the food is super authentic and delicious and fresh and you can sit outside on State Street it is completely delicious and delightful. The dumplings the bow the spareribs the Ramen so many of the Dim Sum it's all perfect and delicious what a treat to get the stuff for dinner! Normally Dim Sum is only through tea time

Oh my goodness!... This Caribbean and Creole eatery just opened in one of State Street's historic spots is such a nice surprise. They're still in their 'soft opening,' and we happened to peek in before dinner hours, but Harold welcomed us and said, 'Come on in... I'll cook for you!'   The lunch menu at Embermill strikes a great balance. Without being overwhelmingly extensive, there's a range of meat dishes, but also tasty vegan offerings and lots of gluten-free options. There's also a wide range of pricing... all very fair. There are a few simple dishes that I think would please the 'finicky' and also more 'adventuresome' choices. We shared the Jamaican Jerk Chicken with rice and plantains (so tender it seemed it must have cooked for hours), a strawberry, leek and goat cheese quesadilla, and a coconut lassi. Everything was beautiful, balanced in flavor, texture and spices and was out of this world good!... (and wasn't over-priced).   You can tell Embermill is going for a welcoming, non-fussy vibe. Harold and the other folks we met there were warm and fun. The place retains historic charm and offers a refreshing atmosphere with big open windows, tastefully simple decor and a huge farmhouse table and pleasant patio seating (where you can bring your pooch, I believe). They're offering a coffee bar and breakfast in the mornings, too (haven't tried it), kombucha-on-tap (if you're into that), and smoothies. If you're all about the food and a friendly vibe, Chef Harold's got you covered!   With so many new places opening all the time, Embermill is definitely a 'must try' that I'll be recommending!
Outdoor seating includes a parklet! There's free parking and always a line, but it's a great excuse to take a leisurely walk on the beach. And of course yummy food

While I miss the old endless, salty has done a good job of updating the space. Luckily they had the wherewithal to keep the original Mai tai on the menu. Food is good and prices are reasonable. But, WHAT HAPPENED TO THE POPCORN MACHINE?!? I understand the communal machine isn't very COVID safe but at least the could offer free popcorn served up by the bartenders. Please salty, bring back our beloved popcorn!

First, I have to say my wait person was fabulous. She was attentive, extremely helpful, and friendly. She recommended the blackberry tini..perfect choice for an afternoon (3/24/2017) relaxation next to the beach. And, who wouldn't order a half dozen oysters, right? It was a busy afternoon, but a nice couple offered me their table. I stopped by at a perfect time since the parking lot is fairly small. I'm sure it fills quickly. Great ambiance. The menu has plenty of options for you--from food to drinks. I can see myself coming back again.

Walked by this place and decided to give it a shot!  It didn't disappoint any one of us.  My son cannot handle spicy food, so this place was perfect for him as you can order your food with the level of spiciness from 1 to 10.  We ordered mango lassi, lamb and chicken momos, and non vegetarian Thali, which came with a meat curry of your choice(chicken, lamb, or goat), naan, rice, daal, and paneer, and rice pudding at dessert.  Everything was simply delicious.  The naan was particularly memorable, as I've never had such yummy naan before.  It came straight from the oven, crispy on the outside, and soft on the inside.  The price may seem like they r on the higher side, but the portions are just huge!    They do have indoor and outdoor dining.  All tables were spaced out 6 ft apart.  The server was very helpful.  If we ever visit again, we are coming back to this place!

the cruisery is one of the best local spots in santa barbara! they are one of the many establishments that are adamantly adhering to the covid regulations, which makes me feel very safe. the food is as good as it gets and always looks very aesthetic. the drinks are amazing too, my favorite is the 'impeachment punch'. i'm not a huge beer fan but they brew their own beers in house and my friends really enjoyed them. everything is fairly priced and the happy hour menu offers some great deals. the staff is super friendly and welcoming, especially the hostess jenny who made sure to inform us of all the safety regulations they were performing. i'm looking forward to continue supporting this establishment!
Fantastic food. Flavorful, unique, healthy-ish. Brussels sprouts were excellent as were the short ribs. Great service and a nice casual atmosphere.

My wife and I have two favorite restaurants in Santa Barbara and they are both right next to each other! We absolutely love opal. Their food is terrific and service is impeccable. The prices are very reasonable considering the high quality of the food. Also, the owners are very responsive and if you need anything or if something is not to your satisfaction (which rarely happens) they are there to take care of you. You won't regret dining here!

Cute little coffee shop. Open and rustic feeling with great music (love hearing Ella Fitzgerald played!). There aren't any outlets inside so make sure you're charged up before coming. There's seating inside for about 20 so it can get filled up fast around brunch.

I ordered the chicken bbq salad. Overall it was good but I didn't realize till the end of my meal that they forgot to put avocado and tortilla chips . My friend also ordered a salad and she said they forgot to put salami. I wish I had checked mine when she told her waiter. Be sure you have everything that should be included with your meal.  I have to say the waiters were friendly and helpful. I'm sure they would have remedied the mistake.  The three stars are for the mistake not toward the waiters.
Korean food is delicious in general. I can't say because I haven't had a lot of Korean food but this place was great. Also the workers/ owners are so nice that it makes the food taste better.

First Miso Hungry experience and I am obsessed!!! Their NEW LOCATION IS OPEN and so convenient. Far enough away from State Street, but right in the mix of things. Online ordering will be up at some point. This is definitely going to become one of my new lunch spots. Can't wait to try all the rice bowls. Filling and tided me over after lunch for quite some time. I'll bump up my rating if I continue to have good experiences.

During my 4 years at UCSB I had plenty of weekends to explore down town and the plethora of restaurants it had to offer. Pacific Crepes has to be one of my favorite finds! Been a couple times and always left satisfied and wanting to go back.  Intrigued by the 'Crepe' sign outside me and the bf walked inside for a late brunch. Inside is a cozy country-café atmosphere with a semi open kitchen. We were greeted by a French speaking waiter and boy were we shocked. Ummm we only know a little Spanish, no French. After my quick laugh our waiter quickly moved to English (the menu is in English so don't worry). However, it was refreshing to have a new cultural experience in SB because that didn't happen quite often.  There was a French family eating next to us and the little 5 year old girl speaking in French was so cute.   They have some interesting drinks: we tried the Indien and the Diablo. One is with mint and sparking soda and I can't remember what the other was, but both are worth trying.  We both started with the French onion soup: Nice rich onion flavor and so addictive! Always crave this on a cold winter day.  Next: chose a make your own (3-ingredient) savory crepe: goat cheese, spinach, and smoked salmon. Good combo and so delish. Bf ordered tomato, olives and pepperoni crepe b/c he is not a fan of cheese. His was also very good.  (see pics)  Dessert: Crème Brule is great but I was so full by this point I didn't even finish mine. BF got the profiteroles which were the winner. Strawberry crepe with nutella is also very mouth watering.

Always a pleasure coming to Holdren's !  Chloe the server is amazing! always going above and beyond making each experience a special one.  The food is always amazing, Service is always on point! Not to mention James is the best server of all time!  James thank you for everything!  You went above and beyond like always!  you need a raise!!!

My favorite thing about being American is Brunch.   El Paseo's bottomless margs and mimosas help elevate their banal food to Kilimanjaro heights. Who doesn't enjoy munching on bacon with a buzz?! We made a reservation which was wise since the entire place was packed.   I highly recommend this spot if you don't mind buffet-style brunch and enjoy alcohol. Go thirsty, my friends.

I've driven by Uptown many times but went there for the first time last night. Me and my girlfriends had a blast. The bar was clean and we really enjoyed the DJ and dancing. Drinks are reasonably priced too! We will be back :)
Good food.  little bit pricey. Neat location  kinda off beaten path. Has tables with fire pits. Fun place to go for special occasions. Good falafel
Bad service and expired products, its always hit or miss there, your better off going to Von's.

A few months ago my parents were visiting and Arnoldi's is my moms favorite restaurant in town. This was a special occasion because it was my husband and I's first time out to a restaurant with our 4 month old. We had an amazing patio table and the nearby heater was perfect. The food was amazing as usual and the corkage fee still very reasonable. Our server Devon was hospitable and genuinely nice, really good guy. Anyway, this outing with baby in tow was a success and more great memories made at Arnoldo's. Thanks again.

We had an amazing late lunch at this Bluewater Grill location with our Adored Grandson about a month ago. The weather was perfect for sitting outside, and we were lucky enough to snag seats at their indoor- outdoor bar upstairs.   With a killer view of the pier and the beach, it is a premiere spot on the refreshed waterfront area where State meets Cabrillo.  Swaying palm trees frame a vista that made us feel like we were on a tropical get-away, without the hassle of boarding a plane.   Top-notch, professional and friendly service provided the basis for a great dining experience.  As the designated driver, I appreciated the mix of fresh juices they whipped up for me, to add to the vacay vibe.  The kitchen kept us happy with fresh takes on seafood options.  Even 16 year old AG noted how well done everything tasted, and was presented. Dear Hubby and I shared an order of sand dabs and scalloped potatoes, with a crunchy slaw on the side.  Fabulously delicious!  Can't wait to return for the whole experience to be repeated.

I'm going to jump on the bandwagon and suggest the veggie sammy with chipotle dressing on wheat. The hot sandwich is messy so watch out and the cold sandwich is good because you can choose two cheeses with complimentary avo. The philly steak was not the best I've ever had and neither was the italian cold cut. Come to think of it, the veggie wasn't the best ever but it was filling and tasty. Don't order the sides as they are rather bland.I give a whole star to the fact that they have extra large pickles and use them on their sandwiches. This place is an easy stop if you visit the transit center in dowtown sb. If you have thirty minutes in between buses, it is easy to run over across the street to have something made up in fairly good speed and have a few bites before you board again.

This is the BEST Mexican food in town. We've driven by many times and decided to give it a try last week-- I'm SO glad we did! This place needs all the 5 star reviews. THIS is the Mexican place you've been looking for.

Brand new sushi spot in Santa Barbara. This may be my new go-to sushi restaurant here as the fish is super fresh, the rolls are creative and delicious, and the prices are reasonable. I got the baked eggplant dish and my dream toro roll which was insanely good. Highly recommend Teru!

Cuernavaca is among the taco elite of Santa Barbara. The Pastor is insane. Vegetarian options are unusually great, especially the pasilla and potato tacos. Outstanding salsas A+. I only wish this place had beers and chips, and everything would be right in the world. Keep doing you, Cuernavaca.

Amazing! Food, service, ambiance, all perfect and worth every penny! The popcorn they give you to snack on is super yummy, and is free!! Everything on the menu was amazing. Hands down wonderful experience!

When you ignore substitutions based on allergies you put people health at risk. The community trusts you to be reading delivery orders, especially when you ask for specified ingredients to be added/omitted, not being there to watch to make sure an allergy substitute is not accidentally added.
Service was nice it wasn't to busy when me and my friend went I had chicken tikka my friend had chicken tikka masala food was really good would definitely go again

Amazing ambience, amazing food, and amazing service! Everything tasted amazing! The pizzas are literally so crave worthy you'll want them again the next day! Do your tastebuds a favor and go eat here!

Finally they've got it right. Not oily, and the empanadas have f been filled all the way. We are vegetarians , so the mushroom one which used to be very hollow I side, is packed with the filling inside and we also love the vegan option. Please keep it up and we will keep buying. Only suggestion is the make the dip less oily now. Thanks
Had lunch at the new Victoria St location and it was delicious.  The breakfast menu looks very promising and worth a visit.

I had originally came to Convivo with a friend for Happy Hour right after it had opened. I liked the place and we continued to go to happy hour. When my niece got married couple of months later, I recommended that the wedding party go to Convivo for dinner before we painted the town red. Time passed and I went back couple of times, but it had been a long while that I hadn't gone back until this past weekend.  Last Sunday it was a beautiful day in Santa Barbara (gorgeous, I should say). So me and some friends were looking for a place to have mimosas and we couldn't decide where to go. Until one of them, said let's go to Convivo. So we went and got a table in the patio overlooking the palm trees and the ocean. There was a cruise ship that was anchored on the beach. The waiter, Juan, came over with some menus and asked if we were there for brunch, but we already had breakfast. We just wanted mimosas.  Juan suggested the Bottomless Mimosas. So we did!   The price for the Bottomless Mimosas was something that caught our attention, it's only $15 per person!!! AND they are made with freshly pressed orange juice. We were taking pictures and he asked if we were from out of town.  Well, it felt like we were somewhere else. Juan, told us that the Bottomless Mimosas is only offered on the weekends during brunch on Saturdays and Sundays from 11am - 3pm.  We were in HEAVEN enjoying the view and the sun!  He came around, brought us water and asked if we wanted to get the last Granseola plate. And the vibe that me and my friends had for that Sunday was 'go with the flow'. So we said yes and we were not disappointed. The plate consists of two Santa Barbara stone crab cakes, two poached eggs and brussel sprouts. Decorated with micro greens. The crab cakes were so delicious and the brussel sprouts had a zesty taste.   I am definitely going back on a weekend and having those bottomless mimosas!

Muddy Waters is a totally cool, local, unpretentious, somewhat earthy cafe showcasing local artistic talent, and hosts bands during the week and on weekends.  Counterpeople are totally interesting and nice, and usually great indie music can be heard across the speakers.  Scones are delicious, and, surprisingly, the sandwiches are extremely good here.  Cute little outdoor patio with various vintage signage in back.  There's also a small bar area in front, along the counter, and a few types of beers are served. Cool, versatile, unique space... located several blocks off of State Street, so has its own DIY character and underground artsy atmosphere.
Very good and always a line at the door but it makes it kind of fun for the anticipation for some amazing carne asada. The salsa is very fresh and I've never had a bad meal here.

I discovered this for taco Tuesday. But now it's Wednesday and I find myself you're having yet another crunchy chicken taco. I don't even like eating meat. But these tacos are so good. Salsa bar fresh and delightful, staff are nice people.  Service is very fast! It's a walk up counter and then they bring food out to you.  High quality food that I will come back for again!!!

Wife and i came for a b day dinner and Rich and Gabe were fabulous. Food is a explosion of taste. The drinks are crafted to perfection. In door and outdoor seating is comfortable and spacious. Definitely worth a trip!

Great place. Couple minor comments...the fruit salad option is a little weak.  Also, would be great to turn down the music a bit.  Can be hard to hear when you're in a booth...it's not a night club. Thanks.

Because we were already getting full from some of our other stops, and had more pastry eating to do, we didn't order much from here. My friend loves the little olive rolls. But for me, I chose a medium sized hunk of ciabatta. It was soft and squishy, just like I like it! My friend's kids split the chocolate brioche torpedo. For me, it was really dry and flavorless. It was the chocolate that made it palatable.

This is a really good fish place in the SB area. If you go here you must have the Grilled Oysters. They definitely are the best thing on the menu. They are cooked well and are very tasty. We also had the mussels which were good but not great. I had the Sand dabs for dinner which were cooked perfectly and tasted great. It is located in a mall so the parking is very easy. The prices are fair for the food you get as well. I would guess it gets very crowded on the weekends. We went there during the week around 5 pm and were seated right away.

Lab social has amazing drinks that are well crafted and only 7 dollars during happy hour!! It's a steal. The espresso martini they have is so smooth, spirit forward, yet has a well balanced finish with the mint that they incorporate. I love the decor inside it is very hip and they have a lot of plants which makes you feel like you are in a garden when sitting at the bar. I will definitely be back to try another cocktail.

Stopped while driving through town down to LA. Really quick and easy. Breakfast all day. The donuts were awesome and the breakfast sandwiches we had were really good too. Would definitely go back the next time we are in town.
Best noodle bar in Santa Barbara. Their drunken noodles is so good! The Monday special fried rice though is my favorite.

Such good quality of ingredients first of all. Delicious flavor combinations on the sandwiches, and cute little shop as well! Every sandwich I've had there is delicious, fresh, and filling. Susan  at the counter is awesome. She just recommended the 'OkeyDokey Artichokey' on wholegrain sourdough, I think it's my new favorite!

Good food, use to be better. Seems they are in attempt more to please the white folks rather then their own people. Yo soi Mexicana and I know good food. I can say la super rica is one of the few  semi authentic places you'll find here in town. The hot sauce  is no where near spicy and that's well becuse u know ..  most don't do hot I guess. U give it four starts because all the workers are awesome, welcoming and u can tell they work hard. I love that restaurant formation allows you to see as your food is being made . Over all great spot just a few adjustments would be appreciated:)

This was my first time eating birria so I don't have anything to compare it to, but it was bland. I really wanted to like this place! The pics made it look so good! The consumè just tasted like grease and strongly of cloves. I was expecting it to be more like a spicy au jus where you can taste the beef simmering and chilis and spices. The taco on its own with the green salsa was really good and flavorful. I'll go again but it just wasn't what I thought. Great staff and I love the simplicity of the menu.

Paradise Cafe is a toss up between two and three stars. As a rule I like to give the benefit of the doubt though, so three it is.  I find myself at Paradise Cafe once every 18 months, and each time I return I always wonder why I don't go there more often? Its location is convenient, downtown but slightly off the beaten path. It looks great indoors and out. Clean pleasant indoor decor, and one of the best patios downtown, period.  The menu's nice too. Simple Americana. Burgers, fish, chicken, steaks. Wine list seems reasonable. Well stocked, nothing crazy. If you're looking for a safe nothing-fancy place to eat -- and there's nothing wrong with that at all -- then Paradise Cafe may be just the thing.  Unfortunately, each time I do eat there, I remember again why there are 18 month gaps between visits. The service is well meaning, but poor. Well intentioned high school or college kids may be friendly, but good waiters they make not. The ritual of greeting, menus, drinks, orders, food, checking, dessert, coffee, check seems to be lost on them.   The waiters are unsure, seemingly hoping you'll fill in the blanks for them. Food comes before drinks, drinks come out one at a time, and apps are moved aside to make room for entrees. Too bad really, cause it seems the waiters simply haven't been told what to do. They look bright enough, just never trained. Corners are being cut.  So, service is subpar. The food was a mixed bag. Sad veggies, messy plating, and indifferent starches. The guys on the line are cooking by numbers. No love. One bright spot was provided by whomever was on grill. The burgers, fish, and steaks were all spot on, thereby saving the day.  Still when all is said and done, mediocre is not what you go out to dinner for. Especially nowadays, credit crunch and all. When you eat out, it should be more than just meh.  And so, the Paradise Cafe is certainly not paradise itself, it's just located there. A fact it takes full advantage of with its superb location and patio. If you have plenty of time, and could care less about veggies, then Paradise Cafe may be the place for you.  Me? I think I'll wait another eighteen months.

Had breakfast last Sunday, and I have to say this place has stepped it up.  Service was so on point my wife and I were 'Wow'd' by the changes since the last time.  The breakfast burrito for only $10 was delicious!.  We live close by and have come here feeling 'meh' on many occasion, this was the complete opposite.  They worked together as a team leading to a great customer service experience and we walked away content and satisfied.  That's all you need to know.

Wow, how have I not reviewed this place yet?  This is the best place for quality Mexican food in Santa Barbara.  I think the website and yelp reviews are a little misleading - I was thinking this place was a little bit more classier/formal.  But it's an order at the counter/take your food to the table/make sure not to step on the tortilla chips on the floor-kind of place.  Everyone gets tortilla chips when they sit down, and you get to go to town with the salsa bar.  They have an avocado one which is soooo good!  I've had a fajita dish and a carne asada-type dish.  Both were great!  You actually get pretty fair ethnic diversity in the customers when you go this restaurant, which is always a good sign - this is a spot that everyone, locals and visitors, want to make sure they go to Los Agaves!  I have to say, I think Julia Child had it wrong on the Mexican food battle in SB.... this beats La Super Rica!

So excited there's a wing place in Santa Barbara! They open in the late afternoon so save your cravings for a late lunch, it's worth it! I tried the pineapple bourbon, strawberry árbol, and classic buffalo(my favorite) the wings are large and well priced for what you get. The bags they came in were also super cute - they feature chickens, fires, and French fries(hopefully foreshadowing a new menu item!!). Excited for new flavors, I would like a spicier option as I like quite a bit of heat. But everything was great! Excited to go there with friends once we are able!

Amazing staff, super friendly and helpful. They have an entire vegan menu along with the normal menu which was wonderful! The server was funny and friendly and will for sure have me coming back to this hidden gem! Truly a great find

Finally made it over to New Sichuan Garden, and very happy I did. This place is excellent!  I'm a huge fan of Sichuan cuisine. Nothing compares to a night in Chengdu, but we are lucky to have two restaurants in SB that can satisfy those cravings. I like Stone Age, but I gotta say, this new place edges it out.   We had the lamb on the sticks with massive chili, which was excellent. Then the Beef in Dry Pot, which was absolutely delightful. Also some lotus root slices, love it! Spice level was just right, and not too salty. I can't wait to come back and try out like five more dishes.  One thing, and I'm sorry to bring this up:  This place is WAY too bright inside. I mean, it's brighter than a hospital! Florescent light overload. Please, laoban, dim the lights! We are having dinner, not performing open heart surgery.

Came here for lunch with two others - I was really excited to try a ramen place in Santa Barbara. I think because this place is so new, the waiters were a bit flustered - our table was not properly wiped down and they seemed confused as to which table ordered what.  We all ordered different types of ramen. I had asked for the Chasu ramen but I'm pretty sure I ended up receiving the Tonkatsu instead, which I only realized several bites in.  As for the ramen? All 3 of us agreed that it was okay. Not great and not bad, just okay. The broth was fine, the noodles were kind of stiff. The toppings in the ramen were fine but missing seaweed, which is a staple for me in ramen. The one thing I really didn't like was the chopped up pieces of ginger in the broth which meant I was eating ginger chunks ever other bite.  Maybe I'm biased? I live in LA and I guess for me there's just such better ramen there, so perhaps my standards are higher. Nevertheless, I can't really recommend or strongly not recommend this place, but I probably won't be going back.

Loved my salad, very fresh, assortment of greens and herbs mixed in. My big issue is the size. For $16 this was way overpriced. It is about the size of a large side salad and I weigh less then 110lbs and I'm hungry 2 hours later. Could they add in more greens, avocado and olives to make this more substantial and like a meal??? It's not a meal in itself but very tasty.
Ambient was great but not food. Didn't know vegetarians ate only hummus and gunoush (sp?). And smoking is allowed outside, so try for indoors.   But we liked what we ordered but no return plans.
Wahoo's is a great fast-foody type place. I love the burritos and the staff and Jeff the owner are top-notch. Cool place.

Came here on our last day of vacation after reading all the reviews on Yelp.  I have to agree with all the Yelpers!  Super good almond croissants!!! We bought four more for a drive home.  Good way to end our SB trip!!  We got the omelete as well, but I would recommend the Croissants 200% over all the other things in the store.

Perfect location in the Funk Zone; this place is super small so be prepared to not find a table to sit at. Traditional street tacos with a variety of salsa to choose from! The staff is friendly & helpful. For being a highly recommended restaurant, I haven't been too impressed with their food.

I ordered from Rudy's a while ago with my girlfriend at her recommendation, and I have to say, I really enjoyed it! We had take out so I didn't get to enjoy the cool atmosphere inside the restaurant itself for long, but what I immediately noticed is their large selection of sauces, so of course I got one of each. Rudy's definitely doesn't skimp on the sauce, we got a total of 5 containers of sour cream--something we didn't complain about.   The only mark against the restaurant that I personally have is the price. I expected it to be a decent amount cheaper for the one $ sign, but we ended up spending over $40, granted for a decent amount of food, but it was just more expensive than we expected.
1980s banquet food. Smothered steak in mushroom sauce, fishy shrimp on pizza, dry chicken.

The ordering and service only deserve a one star today. Waited 1/2 just to order our food. 2-3 people in front of us had no idea what they wanted and the second cashier didn't step in until we asked. People behind us got tired of waiting and left.  No ice tea today? Are you kidding me!   Waited 15-20 and no chips and salsa. We had to go get it ourselves.  It's just a total failure at Los Agaves today!

We decided to try this place and order take out, we ordered popcorn crawfish, jambalaya, gumbo, and steak.   I did not like the popcorn crawfish at all, I was expecting something crispier but instead it was very doughy and chewy. The rest of my group liked their food, the jambalaya pasta was good. Overall, maybe the food would have been tastier if it was fresh and we ate there? But it was not bad.

I expected more from Big Eye considering all the hype and reviews, but this place is a more limited and expensive version of just a common poke spot. Don't come here hungry or you'll just feel like they took your money for the ambience. I'a fish m. used to have more things to eat and a better poke bowl in my opinion. The menu here is too limited. Not to mention that the poke bowls here are just drenched with sauce, I also expect my nori to be crispy when served, I expected gourmet flavors but not a lot else happening for me at Big Eye. PokeMee is still my go to for a DIY style poke bowl, I like variety and substance. I expected more quality from Big Eye, but it's just disappointing when expectations are not met, and the reality is this place can't compete with other poke spots in the area for such a limited proposition. I wish i'a fish market hadn't closed, i'a used to be a good spot for oysters too, something big eye is definitely not. This place is totally overrated.

This place has a unique menu with a variety of different tacos, including some Asian fusion tacos. I highly recommend the seafood tacos. The Battered Up and the Crispy Spicy Shrimp tacos were delicious and fresh. We also tried the Gangnam taco which was made with beef bulgogi and radishes carrots, also a must try. I would not recommend the carne asada taco as the meat was dry. The orange sauce on the side was bland, but the tacos have enough flavor you don't need it.
Just had the Korean BBQ Burrito.  It was excellent.  Service was great.  I'll be back again.  Thanks guys.

Just had a fabulous quiet lunch in the back courtyard facing the hotel pool during happy hour. Lovely energy. Great decor and stain glass artsy windows.  Happy indoor plants and succulents.   Great local craft beer selection.  My gf had a great ceasar salad with avacado. Quality live oak lemonade (alcohol) drinks. Loved the chicken sandwich special with pineapple and bacon! Big fan

This is my husband and my favorite restaurant in town. We love pretty much everything on the menu. Some of our absolute Favorites are the grilled romain salad and the tuna cones. The pasta, meat, and fish has also never been a disappointment. What stands out most is the staff. We love seeing them and they remember us and come over and say hi even if they aren't assigned to our table. We take our close friends here for special occasions, and we come up with something we can call a special occasion to come just the two of us. Also- their bread and salted butter is insane.
Soooo good. If you ever go, make sure to set your hipster timer. Its takes forever to get your bowl of goodness.

We went to CPK for lunch. It was not very busy and we got a table right away. Our waiter (Pedro) was adequate but lacked energy. Hopefully it was just an off day for him.  We both ate off the lunch special menu. I had 7' pepperoni pizza which was good but not fantastic. It came with a very odd Sedona tortilla soup. The flavor was mediocre and soup consistency was very thick. Not at all like a normal tortilla soup.   My wife had a very good half Piadina sandwich and baked potato soup. Her soup and sandwich were both fantastic
Food was amazing, staff was friendly and helpful. Loved there Halibut. Sit outside in the patio on Saturday or Sunday and enjoy the scenery.

I didn't want to order a crappy beach burger so I walked here instead to get lunch. Ordering was convenient. There wasn't much people even though it was around lunch time.   I ordered the Santa Barbara Style Char with only 1 patty (instead of the 2). All the patties are 100% fresh lean beef served with crisp lettuce, tomato, caramelized onions, mayo, pickles. I loved the regular bread that came with the Santa Barb Style burger as it was slightly buttered and toasted. Yum! Tried it two days later with the whole wheat bun and it was a bit disappointing. Just keep the same bread it comes with! Beef was cooked perfectly.   Prices were fine and I would definitely consider coming back again.
Add some kind of 'democratic' element (cooperative) to this company and as far as I know it would be perfect.   The food here is wonderful!

This was my go to happy hour spot before covid ...Had lunch there yesterday and a few drinks , amazing food and incredible service ! Can't beat the view  five stars all around !!! Love the Spritz so refreshing and peach pizza was delicious
I love this place... it's one of the best Japanese restaurants in town.  Great sushi and Japanese fusion type cuisine.

We enjoyed strolling around LaArcada for the Christmas lights and carollers and festivities for the Christmas Season. We decided to grab a bite at 1114. Tables were open around the turtle pond but we were told that would be a 25 minute wait and were ushered into an inside table in a corner. We were completely ignored by all waitstaff. We flagged down a bartender who took our order (we shared a pizza and had wine) and it was another 20 minutes for the wine and we had been there an hour before our pizza came out. Will never darken their door again. The bartender was a bit quirky and entertaining and were it not for him, I'd have nothing nice to say. Get it together. Adding an arcade and pool tables won't save your restaurant, trust me.
Great Burger and Macho Salad, though a little in the pricey side.   Great service!!!   The waitress was extremely personable and efficient.

Come here and order their Phyllo Wrapped Tiger Prawns, their Spanish Paella, any of their delicious steaks, and the entire dessert menu! Their drinks are amazingly yummy and they offer a lot of variety. This place is a little pricey, but the food is satisfying and their service is incredible. We once had to wait even though we had a reservation, and they made up for it by giving us a couple of free appetizers. Make sure you make a reservation, and enjoy their intimate and inviting atmosphere.

This is as close as you're going to get to go to Ottolanghi outside of London.  The food is absolutely brilliant and service is spot on.  The wait staff is as enthusiastic about the food as the kitchen.    Wedge salad is scrumptious, broccoli is perfection.  The duck breast is the most tender I've had in the US and the duck mouse is paired with the most delicate citrus and ginger, it's a dream.   Plating is organic and elegant. There is so much intension put into the dining experience at Lark, I can't wait to return.

Stopped in today for lunch and ordered the turkey club. Interesting they chose to include an egg instead of avocado... the sandwich I thought was OK a little on the dry side but the bun, turkey and bacon were all good.. I was missing that so cal avocado we all love though.   The space was nice the staff was pleasant and the cost was all in line with typical SB spots.   All in I would go back to try other items on the menu but I was not impressed with the turkey club.
The best in SB county- Fresh and made to order. A total treat.

I am a breakfast sandwich addict, and I have to say that the breakfast sandwich here is incredible. Toasted English muffin or bagel with scrambled egg, tomato, avocado, melted cheese, Mayo and lemon pepper. Shoutout to Herman for making them perfectly every time. Coffee is delish, toasts are good, and they have a great selection of baked goods. My hubby and I make the 45 minute walk to enjoy Lighthouse almost every Saturday. So good.

The brats are tasty, the beers are good, and the Ruben is killer. However, the service can be spotty and occasionally terrible. I went there twice last week and sat for a full 15 minutes, inquiring several times if I could get some service. There was literally not a single patron in the restaurant. I don't know if the staff hadn't yet come in (it was just after opening) or whether they simply couldn't be bothered. After waiting and waiting, I simply left. I liked the food enough to come back and try again but got the same terrible service in one week - leaving before getting served. Too bad! I liked this place but I won't return until I hear that the service has improved.

Thank you yelp for this find!   Our order... We liked it all!! Hot Tea ~$3.75 Hot Combo Platter ~$15.45 Half Dozen Oysters ~$9.95 Shrimp Cocktail ~$12.25 Cup of Clam Chowder ~$4.75 Bloody Mary ~$8.20  Get here when they open to get a seat on the patio or at the bar to enjoy a lovely view. We definitely enjoyed our dining experience and hope to be back soon!

I have been a solid customer of the chicken ranch for at least 10 years. I feel like every time I eat here my experience gets worse and worse. I come here biweekly  and order the same thing every time (BRC, light rice +guacamole). Today they made me a chicken burrito with guacamole- I am a vegetarian so obviously I cannot eat it. I took it back to get my order fixed. I was not offered a price adjustment but they remade my order and got it incorrect again this time I got a bean, rice and sour cream burrito ( I do not like sour cream) made up of about 90% rice. I was not going to go back a third time but already felt uncomfortable by the woman working behind the counter the first time I took my order back so I just gave my husband the burrito. This will probably be my last time at the chicken ranch for a while.

Today we came in with 9 adults and 2 kids and we were seated promptly. The staffs were very attentive. Most of us ordered fish from the grill and they all came out delicious.   It's a good place to come hangout during happy hours, or to celebrate special occasion on a Saturday when it's less crowded (no happy hours on Saturday).

Absolutely the best breakfast/brunch/buffet of our lives!! Phenomenal food with just about anything you could possibly think of, and everything was absolutely stellar... The king crab legs were sweet and juicy, the pasta was mouth watering, the crepes were scrumptious, the desserts were great, and the cappuccinos hit the spot. Along with that the service from everyone -from the hostess to the waiter to the entire brunch service staff- was awesome, with down to earth personalities and incredible attentiveness without hovering. Yes it is more than the average breakfast but you get way more than the average breakfast both in selection and quality, along with service you won't find anywhere else. I hadn't been here in over 10 years and was thrilled to see that they still take care of their customers - rich or poor, dressed up or dressed down- like one of the family.
Super nice staff, and good old Taco Bell food.  Quickly prepared.    All good here.

Ok, so we came to this place late in the evening looking for desert. So my review is just that, we did not have a full meal here. I was not at all impressed, the desert lacked flavor and it was expensive. We were quit surprised at what our shared desert and espresso's came to. Looking at all the other reviews, I MIGHT give this place another try, but not likely.
Amazing view of the beach and surf good fast service, great food,  nice place to go for lunch

Honestly I don't get what all the hype is about this place I think it's over priced for the portions you get. Yes the chicken is good but everything is super plain but I guess that's why they have so many sauce options which by the way if you think this is a healthier choice the sauce completely ruins that factor. I personally come here for the soft serve ice cream is sooooooo delish mmmmm BUT the drive thru line is seriously ridiculous I've had to NOT get ice cream before because I couldn't get into the lot :( and it's very dangerous to have the line come out to on coming traffic hmmmm there's gotta be a better way.

This is now our third visit to Santa Barbara in three months. Today we stopped in the Sandbar for breakfast. They have a great deal for $5.50. I posted a picture of what they offer. We were greeted and seated right away. Our waitress was wonderful she informed us of our options for breakfast and took out order right away. The food came out fast and hot. The Sandbar looks to be a fun place. The football game is on but not overwhelming, we can still have conversation without yelling. I give this place 5 stars because it's clean, great service and wow good food. Stop by on your next visit to SB and tell them Mike sent you. They may look with crossed eyes but tell them anyway.

Can you say finger lickin' goooooood?!?  Instead of joining the madness for Halloween, my boyfriend and I decided we wanted to take it easy (and by easy I mean fill ourselves with food until we popped) so we made dinner reservations at Palace Grill and ordered a movie on pay-per-view for when we came home.  We were seated right away and our lovely waitress (who was dressed as the dude from V for Vendetta) came by to take our drink orders. After a long and grueling process of having to choose from the huge, delicious-sounding, jaw dropping, mouth drooling menu I finally ordered the Creole Lamb Chops (medium-rare) and my boy ordered the Jumbalaya Sauce Piquante.  SOOOOOO GOOD! So savory, juicy and tender, easy to go down with the mashed potatoes and delicious steamed veggies. If you don't like mushrooms, don't get it. But! if you do, then I highly recommend this dish. As for the jumbalaya it was pretty darn good.  We topped it off with the bread pudding (which was the point at which I exploded and had to drink it down with a rootbeer, my favorite kind!), which was good but definitely not the best I've ever had.  All in all, this place made me super happy :) didn't get any of the singing though, I have to admit, I was kinda disappointed!
So great! Absolutely love their brunch! Can't wait for Finch and Fork to be reopened! Beautiful atmosphere! Best chef in town!

This was the worst meal we ate over the entire three days were were in Santa Barbara.  IT STUNK, we hated it.  It had all the potential to be a great place.....cute courtyard, big open area with a lot of tables, cool little bar area, good menu.  The food however, was the pits.    I hated my meal, I only had a few bites.  I got the vegetarian lasagna...now, I am not a gourmet cook by any means but I can even whip up a tasty vegetarian lasagna.  It was the oddest creation I have ever seen...it was swimming in something that resembled tomato sauce but actually tasted like watery tomato soup.  Plus, the cheese to veggie ratio was WAY off......actually, they skimped on both the veggies and the cheese.  Paul hated his paella, he kept using the words gross and scary.  We both left so much of our food uneaten!    Plus, it was way overpriced and the service wasn't that great.  The place was empty with the exception of a couple people at the bar and the waitress still wasn't very attentive.  (We should have known by the fact that the place was empty!!!!!!)  We ended up leaving there and going for pizza.....what a total waste of money.  Paul wanted me to make sure I added his comment 'This place must makes its money off of unsuspecting tourists!!!!!!!'
I love the food here! The prices are so reasonable on top of that. The owner is very attentive and personable. I recommend this place to all my friends.

Not sure why the tap beer on the chalk board listed as all 16oz came in a 12oz tulip. Server forgot toppings we ordered: Avocado, Ketchup.  Server never re filled our water bottle after we drank the first bottle.  Ok food, Ok service.

Strike 1. Nasty, rubbery-topped, soggy-bottomed 'torta' fresh from the microwave. (Yes, microwave, only nukers do what was done to the lettuce and tomatoes) wet, watery meat. the only savin grace was the 1.79 beers. i had 5 while watching my buddy gulp down his own pile of fishy filth.  dude, that's strike 2 and 3 as well.   tastes like poop and spray paint.

There are many factors that help me decide how I would rate a restaurant.  It doesn't have to be a fine dining spot to achieve a 5 star review.  What it does need is to hit top marks in all categories and that is what the Wildwood Kitchen did for me last night.  From the call to the restaurant to make a reservation for our large party to receiving the check at the end, the service was impeccable. Our server, Allie was helpful and attentive. The bus staff was totally on top of things and might have refilled our waters no less than 10 times!  We ordered a variety of dishes to share family style and the food was terrific.  For starters we had the artichoke toast, mac n cheese and kale salad.  They were all great and Allie assisted in deciding the perfect quantity. Nothing worse than running short for a large party, but the amount was spot on!  We chose the smoked meat sampler and each of the varieties were tender, moist and flavorful.  I think the ribs and brisket won top billing for me, but they were all done perfectly.  Loved trying all the sauces and found that the jalapeño was my fav. The corn bread was wonderful - moist and buttery and delicious!  Probably my two lesser favorite items were the cole slaw and shepherds pie, but it didn't take away from the meal at all as they were still good.  Yep, you don't have to spoil me with frills and luxury.  Just a nice setting, good food and great service wins the bill.  I would definitely go back again!

This restaurant is bigger than it looks on the outside. It is a nice place for a large group of 4+ people. There is usually a long line on the weekend for lunch or dinner.  Their menu is pretty average. as their prices. I think most of the locals come here, because on the menu, Pascucci claims they buy their ingredients from local farmers or stores. I have no qualms about that.  Their menu is pretty standard for an Italian-American cuisine. I like the fact that you can request low-carb pasta. All of the stuff I ordered here are alright, nothing memorable, negatively or positively. They do have an extensive wine list, as expected in Santa Barbara.  I would come here again, because it is centrally located on State Street and there is no Olive Garden nearby.

We've made this place a regular spot! Our go-to is the fried rice with an egg and beef and the Pork Bahn Mi. Whether we are dining in or taking it to go, the staff is always welcoming. The food is consistently delicious, the decor is fun, and the vibe is comfortable. Good for groups, a casual date, take out... or even dining solo!

This chain restaurant (I think it might be the only one left?) has childhood memories so despite the potentially offensive decor we went there for breakfast.   It was packed with tourists and the food was bland at best. My husband's toast didn't come for a bit which was problematic as he is an egg-dipper so couldn't get started right away which was irritating.   Mostly we went there as they were the only restaurant serving breakfast.

I love the history most of all at this place. It has a warm family vibe. The backyard opens up to a large patio with seating under the bistro lights, and bocce ball courts for all to enjoy. The food was good, but not amazing. I would go back just to experience the nostalgia and hang at the bar to listen to their great music they play!

This place is amazing! Not only is the food delicious but the staff is super friendly and ordering online to pick up is easy peasy! They always have our order ready when promised and I have never ordered something I didn't like. It's a little hidden gem on Milpas Street, and now they have another small location on State Street. In addition, they have family meals and you can order things frozen/cold to re-heat for later! Highly recommend!
Updated review for Cullen M at Toma, he is an awesome server and extremely efficient.  Go there for great service, outstanding food and great drinks!

The prettiest restaurant I've ever seen in my life!!! Make sure you make reservations to sit in the patio area! Bonfires at every table, lights hanging from trees, beautiful flowers, and charming music. What a dream :')   Cheese Sambosik (5) - Fried puff pastry rolls stuffed with mozzarella and feta (2 pcs)  It's as good as it sounds! One bite of that delicious cheese pastry and I was sold!   Tzatziki (6) - Fresh yogurt, cucumber, garlic, mint  Definitely on the lighter side than most! They don't give any sides, so we used our complimentary pita bread with this!   Chicken Shawarma (18) - Strips of free range chicken breast slowly broiled on a vertical rotating grill  My favorite!! The chicken was absolutely scrumptious and juicy and that garlic sauce on the side totally elevated the dish! I also appreciated their salad because the lettuce was so crisp and refreshing.   Lamb Shawarma (19) - Strips of marinated lamb slowly broiled on a vertical rotating grill  My bf actually preferred this dish because he loves the intense flavor of the lamb. The sauce on the side was similar to a tzatziki sauce.   We didn't get to sit in the patio area, but one glimpse of it was enough for me to fall in love with the place.
Eaten here 4 times on 3 trips to SB. Really enjoy drinks, food, vibe. Going to be a regular stop on my future trips.

This place is a gem! We have our holiday luncheon here with about 15 people every year and every meal is always delicious. I always get the grain bowl with Kahlua pork and it's super flavorful. Most of us order the burger and fries and everyone raves about it. The staff are very accommodating to our large party. We look forward to returning again next year!

It's been a while since I've dined at the Chase so my friend and I decided to stop by for an early dinner. We also wanted to check out the Christmas decor as it is pretty wild! It was beautiful inside with many twinkly lights and the Sinatra style music playing in the background definitely sets the mood. I ordered the chicken picatta and my friend had the shrimp picatta. We shared a bottle of the Chateau St. Jean Pinot Noir. It was delicious, we were stuffed and happy. The only reason I would not give 5 stars is because the food is a bit pricey. The price point is just a bit higher than I would normally see for this kind of menu but at least you won't leave hungry, the food in consistent, and our server Giovanni seemed very nice and attentive to our table.

First time at this restaurant and I won't be back   From the very beginning the woman out front was unfriendly and gave my friends and I  (as well as our children) dirty looks. We make it upstairs and decide to eat on the patio   We had no clue we had tO order and pick up our food at a window outside. No one told us because no one  acknowledged us. When I finally asked, two men laughed at me and was condescending. We finally get our food and it was... to be desired. The Brussel sprouts were the worst  ones I've ever had. The nachos were fine. Skip this place.

We came for appetizers and the waterfront patio. Nice place to sit and talk with a great view. I was genuinely surprised at the poke nachos. They give a good portion of poke, I still had poke left after the chips were gone.
Love this place, whether it's breakfast, happy hour or whatever, you get great service, food, and amazing views every time! Lobster mac and cheese for the win.

Hubby and I came here to celebrate a new job opportunity. It was a Thursday and (thankfully!) we made reservations at 7pm same day. When we got In, there was a line waiting for the hostess. She seated us at the perfect little two top in the corner. We were immediately greeted with water, bread and butter, and fried Olives. We put in an order of the ahi Tuna cones and a couple martinis, and all came Out super fast, and was super delicious. We ended up splitting a bottle of Chianti while we enjoyed our main course of lamb meatballs and shrimp gnocchi. Service, ambiance, food and drink were phenomenal! We ended the night with a couple after Dinner drinks and a flaky strawberry pastry and the manager/owner Tom even came over to check in on us, and even gave us a better amaro than we had previously picked!! We will be back!!!! Thanks for Making our night so special!

Each visit to La Paloma has been as exceptional as the first. Staff are very hospitable, friendly and our server Bobby was perfectly attentive. We had a variety of entrees and sides including the ranchero salad with pulled chicken, crispy tacos, Santa Maria tri tip, ranch fries, brussel sprouts and glazed carrots. All were delicious. Patio is inviting and comfortable as well. We shall return, often!

This definitely lived up to the hype! The flavors were delicious and really gave us a new appreciation for Mexican food! However, the wait was loooong. Understandable, I guess... And the portions were a bit small, so we had to go to Mony's after to get some snack tacos to fill us up. But all in all, we would visit again if we come back to Santa Barbara!

Hands down the best Italian place in Santa Barbara. I'm getting hungry just thinking about it.    The food is authentic and I have yet to try a dish I didn't like. The gelatto is imported from Italy, which had me question its freshness but I assure it tastes great. (What do I know about shipping gelatto anyway?!) If you enjoy wine, you'll likely find one you like on their list.    Some people have issues with the atmosphere here because the place is small and a combo between a deli, gelatto parlor, and sit down restaurant. I say it's charming. However, calling ahead to put your name on the list isn't a bad idea as it can get crowded.
Food was pretty good. Our party had a negative experience with one member's order not being passed to the kitchen correctly. Great to have a meal and then walk by the marina, though.

I ate at Oku for the first time yesterday and loved it! I loved their vegetarian options. The food was delicious! Sarah our waitress was very attentive and gave us amazing service. Highly recommend Oku

My husband and I dined here for our 1 year wedding anniversary. I had made reservations a couple weeks beforehand, and had read that dining on the patio was the way to go. Good thing I did, because it was romantic, warm, intimate and there were only 2 tables with a 2 seater and then around 4 other tables that sit 4-5 diners. Make sure to book on the patio - so pretty!!!  We arrived early for our 715p reservation, and we were told it would be a couple mins until our table was cleaned and ready. This gave us an opportunity to take photos in front on the restaurant. The host came out and since we were celebrating our anniversary and that we were waiting, he offered us a couple glasses of bubbly while we wait. HOW AWESOME! Then he came back out to take our photo.  A few mins later we were seating in a cute table next to the exposed brick and pretty lighting. There were heat lamps so it was perfect. We brought our own bottle of wine and the corkage fee was $25 for weeknights and $35 for weekends and holiday. You are only allowed 1 bottle per 2 persons, so take note! We brought in a 9 year old J.
