Lohr Cab reserved, and the manager came over to open our bottle, brought over a decanter and came by with an anniversary card and rose petals for our table. How romantical!  We ordered the Goat Cheese and Pecan Tart as a starter. I was a little weary about this because I really wanted the Savory Foie Gras, and the hubby didn't care for it. I was pleasantly surprised that the tart was amazing. Very light and the warmness of the tart really made the flavors pop. A must as an appetizer if you're into smooth and creamy cheeses.  My husband went with shallot glazed rack of lamb (med rare) and I ordered the staple maple-glazed duck breast and confit of thigh (med). My husband lamb was delicious and meaty; however, my duck was to die for. The breast was so much better than the thigh and I'm more of a dark meat eater. The sauce and crispiness of the duck was so good, and the knife just simply sliced the meat tenderly. Super filling that I had to take half of the entree home.  For dessert, my husband ordered the blueberry lemon upside down cake and I ordered the warm Santa Barbara chocolate molten lava cake. My husband really enjoyed the cake as he is into fruity and tart desserts. My dessert on the other hand was awesome. If you're a chocolate fan, you MUST get this dessert. This is how a molten lava should be, just pure warm fudge coming out and all in the center. The crust was crisp and crunchy and definitely not overcooked. Coffee paired well with desserts, and my husband ordered a cup while I finished the rest of my Cab (red).   The service was impeccable and we couldn't have had a better experience on our 1st year wedding anniversary. Such a special and memorable place. We will definitely come back on our next trip.
Had lunch here it was ok nothing specail but very reasonably priced

GUYS, THIS PLACE IS AMAZING. It is one of the most interesting and unique places I've ever been to and I cannot recommend it enough. Although it doesn't have a Michelin star, it IS mentioned in the Michelin guide for California which is cool.   Inside, there is a bar on one side and several tables on the other. They also have an outdoor section (this is during covid, so I can't speak to it's permanence). Additionally, they provide clean blankets for guests in case you get cold. It is nicely decorated and I really loved the laid back ambiance. Our waitress, Christine, was also great and helped with some food recommendations!  Now let's talk about the star of the show: THE FOOD. This restaurant encourages 'family style' eating so that's exactly what we did. We ordered the mie goreng, hot chicken bao, crispy duck salad, spare ribs, and eggplant and pork balado.   Now, as a disclaimer, I have a pretty low spice tolerance, but I'm working to improve it. Everything was a little bit spicy, but with a bottle of water to myself, I pulled through and it was totally worth it.   First, the mie gorang was a yummylicious, slightly spicy noodle dish. It was packed full of flavors that I just cannot describe. The hot chicken bao was a little smaller than I expected, but it was still one of the best chicken sandwiches I've ever had. The crispy duck salad was one of my favorites. It had a good balance of  sweet and savory. The spare ribs dish was my favorite of the night! It was fall-off-the-bone amazing with a slightly sweet sauce. Lastly, the eggplant and pork balado did not disappoint. It was a little spicy, a little savory, and very tasty.  The only downside of this place was that it was a bit pricey. Each dish costs about $12-$30 and they recommend you order 1-2 per person. It came out to be $40/person with no drinks. However, for the mind blowing flavors that are hard to find in the rest of Santa Barbara, I would gladly pay it again.

We stopped in for a snack to enjoy the view. Beautiful setting and tons of space on a weekday morning.  The iced tea was quite good.  We shared an omelette and it was also quite good with generous portions.  A nice place to relax and look at the beach.
Doh! Thought I was planning ahead for a busy day by ordering a large order online BUT online ordering does NOT work.

Loved that salad bar.  I wasn't able to find much in the deli that didn't have cheese or meat in it, and all I wanted was a quick to-go lunch.  So jumped to the salad bar, and was able to find a nice assortment of fresh veggies and dressing.  I was happy.

This place worked really well for our family. Super casual and kid friendly, but still really yummy food. The fish tacos were super good! Really crispy fish and the pickled onions were delish. We also tried the burger and a breakfast sandwich. Location fit perfectly into our beach day!!

Good deal for good Mexican food.  The meat on the tacos are flavorful. I like the beef and pork.  Have not tried the tongue. Reminds me of the tacos I had when I lived in Mexico for a year.  I come back when the cravings hit.

Our last night in town and the Weatherman was in the mood for Italian and a glass of wine. We were seated immediately but had to wait a little bit to order drinks and dinner. We sat outside and the space was little tight. We were closer to others than we would've liked to have been. I decided on the Eggplant Parmesan and a Caesar Salad, the Weatherman ordered Risotto. I will say that my Eggplant Parm was 5/5, it was seasoned to perfection and came out piping hot!! The parm came with a side of fettuccine but that needed some more loving, it tasted like an afterthought...I left it on my plate. Hubby enjoyed his Risotto, I tasted but it wasn't for me. He didn't finish it though, we may have overdone it on the bread basket and wine before the entrees arrived.

Service was good.  We sat in the private 'booth', which was cozy and cool.  Salmon salad awful.    Cheese weird.  Samosa okay.  Curry not that great.  Spice avenue a little more pricy, but much better!!!
This is an amazing vegan option that never disappoints. Fun spot at the public market that currently has a lot of outdoor seating. Super friendly, waste conscious, and Covid compliant.

We came here for breakfast this morning and the entire experience was perfection from start to finish.  Moses was our waiter and he was personable, attentive, funny, and all around excellent.  The food was incredible, as well!  We had the pork belly Benedict, the boathouse special, and our daughter had the French toast with blueberries.  The food was fresh, full of flavor, and delicious.  Also- the view and ambiance are amazing.  We will definitely return!

Every dish in this carefully crafted seven course Japanese meal is well thought out and executed. The flavors are well balanced and allow the high quality local and Japanese fish, meat, and produce to shine. Pair with sake recommended by the expert servers and enjoy a beautiful meal.

This is where Lama Dog, Topa Topa. and the Fox Winery Tasting room are, so how can it not be great.  It's also been nice to see the little shop area come into its own.  Somehow all these places together just work.  I just wished the City of Santa Barbara could get their liquor license regulations changed so you can actually walk around, place to place, without having to leave your drink behind.  If it's all one building, it should have one global license, right!?  Oh well.  Still a great addition to the Funk Zone.

Best smoothie in town!! Love the selection  and prices are so much more reasonable than other smoothie places. I wish we had one where I live. When we come to visit SB we always make it a stop on our itinerary.

Bottom line: excellent food, good portion sizes, reasonable prices, and first-class service in a great setting.  Went here Monday night for my 4th time in about a year.  Each time my wife and I have tried a new dish and we have never been disappointed.  Have not yet had their pizza's but one in our group got the filet mignon pizza and said it was fantastic.    In addition to the food always being good, the service is always first class.  For example: It was busy the night we went and we didn't have a reservation, so we waited at the bar for about 15 minutes.  While there, the bartender pretty much gave us a full-on wine tasting at no cost until we found the glass we each wanted.  Meanwhile the manager kept checking in on us to make sure all was well and continued to check in on us throughout dinner (and not in an obtrusive way).
Best damn pizza in Santa Barbara. We just love this place and recommended to all our friends.

I was here three times and I always have a good time. The food is tasty. The craft cocktails are good and refreshing. The service is good. Staff are friendly and helpful. Highly recommended if you have a big group.

Amazingly uninspired and tasteless cuisine.  Basic and flavorless for the most part but it doesn't prevent them from indulging in financial rape.... Must be the SB Pier setting and tourist opportunities. We tried the calamari sandwich (ok), garlic oysters (say what? And why?).... The accompanying onion rings were devoid of taste and drilling with grease. Seagulls seem to like the place though....

Had a good time here with a group of about 30 of us for dinner. The restaurant was very accommodating and able to get us situated with dinners for everyone plus some margaritas going around. The food was good! Nothing incredibly memorable (in either a good or a bad way). I'm just happy the service was good, and I found parking in their tiny parking lot! Having read these reviews prior, I came in with low expectations.   Is there probably better tasting Mexican food in the area? Without a doubt.   Should you be scared away from coming here if you have a big group? Nope, come! Great for big groups. They also seem to have a pretty extensive bar if you just want to come here for drinks.

Remarkable food - who knew a Kale salad could be this good!  little pops of cilantro and the peanuts made this a REALLY GOOD salad.   We all shared the Veggie Club - I thought it may be a bit much with the feta but the French Feta is creamy and mild, perfect for this sandwich.  We had everything to go so I can't speak of the ambience but this is my new go to for a healthy, delicious meal in Santa Barbara.

Prime location and great food. I absolutely love their carne asada street tacos. Make sure you pay extra for guac - it makes all the difference. Parking can be annoying to find but that's expected of state street. Will definitely be back soon

Super romantic spot with amazing cocktails. Came here once on a really memorable date and once for a birthday - they were soooo sweet to us for birthday girl. Complimentary champagne and bday desserts. thank you for such amazing service!! Everyone who works here is amazing. Julia was amazing!! Super classy. Def a great spot to impress a date. Tapas were all stellar.

I hate to say anything negative about a place trying to get homeless cats adopted BUT their new policy of pretty much only allowing the 1 year and younger cats to go as 'bonded pairs' is a misplaced understanding of cats in general. Most if not all adapt to being a single cat once adopted. In addition it was pretty clear when I went to adopt one cat that the 'bonded' cat I would also need to take home was nothing more than a cat my desired cat had taken a liking to a few days prior. There was another family there at the same time and they left without a cat for the same reason, they only wanted to adopt one cat. This mentality will preclude a lot of cats from being adopted at a younger age when they are more likely to find homes. Whether it is the owner of Cat Therapy or the organization they are affiliated with making this policy, they are precluding lots of adoptions.

Our waiter ruined our experience dining here. He had ADD or was high; we weren't sure which. He couldn't remember anything we asked for, rarely came back to see how we were doing, and looked spaced out in general. The food was fair...nothing spectacular, unfortunately. I'm bummed because I had my wedding here two years ago and the restaurant, food, staff, and overall service were amazing.   They DEFINITELY need more vegetarian options. Hello! This is California! And Santa Barbara at that!!!
Great tacos ,we drive from LA and we never leave Santa bArbara without stoping at Lily's tacos i love the al pastor !!!
Nice and friendly staff with a quaint cozy Italian cafe feel. Really can't go wrong. The toast and jam is Phenomenal.

Opal continues to be a favorite. Its consistency, quality of food and service never disappoints. The menu has changed some, but still most of the favorites are there and liked the new addition of salmon tacos. They are a standout. Cocktails and wine list excellent, well priced. Know we can take guests and never be disappointed or have to make excuses for a bad night or chefs night off.

HI BOARD GAMES. This will be my new place to go before work. Sesame bagel with cream cheese and tomato is so good, why I never thought to put two and two together is stupid bc GOD ZAMN ZADDY it's addicting. You know when wale is addicting? Avocado toast. You know makes bomb avocado toast? Java station, son. Wanna be basic on a Wednesday like your girl! Get the avo toast with Yerba latte. DELISH.  Staff is extremely friendly.

Hands down, the worst service I've ever had in Santa Barbara!!  This whole experience was a giant disappointment. I had been so excited to finally go to Hungry Cat since it had been recommended to me by multiple people on different occasions.. what a letdown!   It started on a good note when my mom and I were sat by the very nice and accommodating hostess. From there it was all downhill. We sat in the dead center of the restaurant where we were ignored for the first 15-20 minutes! After trying to make eye contact with ANYONE, our waitress finally decided to ask what kind of water we would like. I'm sorry, but it is never, ever acceptable to sit at a table without water or some small form of acknowledgment for almost 20 whole minutes!!! We would have stood up and walked straight out of the restaurant if it hadn't been a busy night downtown and almost 8pm at this point. (Though we did have plenty of time to sit there and discuss our other options)  After we were finally recognized, not much improved. We continuously were left feeling abandoned with complete and utter lack of interest from anyone in the restaurant. This despicable serviced continued on until the check was finally dropped.   The only reason for 2 stars instead of 1 is for the good food. However, no amount of incredible food could make up for our horribly disappointing experience.. it was all tainted.   I will never return.

We waited more than four years for this restaurant to open after Peabody's closed. As a local and an aspiring vegan, I am delighted Oliver's opened on Coast Village Road. Beautiful setting and decor. The dishes are hit or miss. Some have more flavor or substance than others. I've eaten here 3 times. I would eat here every week if not so pricey. I hope they start having lunch service soon.
Embarrassed to admit I've been here twice in two days buts it's that yummy. Also fresh and staff is great. Just bring some cream cheese please.
Lemon grass salmon. Delicious. Very comfortable with great service. We stop here the 3-4 times we come to Santa Barbara. We look forward to it each time.

This place has a fantastic menu and the service was outstanding. They have so much space that you don't even feel the effects of COVID from a distancing perspective.  However, the food did not live up to the great descriptions. My husband had the Pacific rim fried rice which was salty and greasy.  I know that should be expected to some extent based on the contents but it seems it could be slightly less. I had a breakfast grain bowl which was incredibly greasy.  The Pacific rim bacon I ordered on the side was very good.
Seriously delicious. A little over priced but otherwise pretty good. Their chicken karrage is bomb! But for that and 2 rolls $50 is pretty pricey.

We called in an hour before coming in for brunch, and when we got here, we were seated right away. I really wanted to try the pumpkin waffles or the french toast, but I didn't think I could finish a plate of that, so I opted for something savory instead.  I went with the wild mushroom, asparagus, black truffle cheese scramble which came with a bread of your choice and salad. Oh man, when the plate came, I was debating what to do because I didn't realize that 'wild mushrooms' are the mushrooms that I don't eat! I've only seen it in Asian dishes, and when my dad tries to give me some, I always refuse. But I decided to take a bite, and whadaya know? The mushrooms weren't super chewy like how they've always tasted to me, so it wasn't too bad! In fact, they were quite tender, so the dish ended up being okay. The salad and baguette were good. Oh and they have delicious homemade jam that we tried - yum! Banana bread was a little too cakey but dense at the same time, but it wasn't bad!   The restaurant is cute, service is friendly, and the food is good! I'm sure if it weren't for the mushrooms, I would've enjoyed it more, so a solid 4 stars!

The cake on the cupcakes was incredibly dry.  The frosting was sickeningly sweet and overly heavy.  Drinks were good (tea for me, coffee for hubs), but does not make up for how disappointing the sweets were.  Our family came here a week ago Thurs during our SB vacation after some play for the kiddo at Kids World.

This place lives up to every ounce of hype! I just had the Frog legs, Tuna Crudo, cauliflower nachos and oh lord the Pickled Mushrooms! I could take a bucket of those things home. Come check this place out before it gets too busy to get a reservation

Great service and ambience. Their plate portions are very filling but their gyros are thin, oily, and not the best. I'd recommend getting their chicken instead. Also, their pita bread is dry so make sure you get hummus with it.

RUDE and HORRIBLE service. I came here for the first time with my kids for lunch today. I ordered the lunch combo enchiladas and the mole enchiladas. The food was great. Really good Mexican food, but nothing that's that hard to find in Goleta. There's good food all around this town. But when it came time to pay, the RUDE owner started to get a little hostile about the bill. I used my axxess card, which seemed to make him mad. Initially, he wouldn't discount my buy one get one free meal because the waitress had split my combo meal for my kids. I didn't ask her to, on purpose, and he got all mad about it. Then he said he would give me the discount. But when he went to use his phone of access the discount, it said mine had already been used. I have not eaten at this restaurant before! So he rudely again said that this was such an annoyance for him. Because he was so mean and rude to us, we paid quickly and ran out of there. What's sad is that even though the food is good, if you have a mean owner/manager, you'll lose good local business. Spend your money elsewhere. There's better food at Los Arroyos, Los Agaves, or Altamirano's in Goleta. This guy doesn't deserve it if he's going to be such a jerk to his customers. Too bad...we would have come back, too...

other indian places may be nicer in decor and variety, but i come here for their great just-made crispy 'n warm naan bread. priceless (and free!). lunch buffet is good ($7.95). nice people working here.

Our new favorite vegan spot! Vegan, gluten free food is hard to come by in Santa Barbara so we were excited to find Fala Bar! Delicious tahini, hummus, fries and 'burgers'. I highly recommend this gem!
Not outstanding but not bad. The food is good but the huge amounts of oil, butter and other additives may result in a bigger belly.

Let me start with the drinks! absolutely fab, love the food selection. I had the special which was steak bites and also the Rockefeller oysters. The oysters were phenomenal, had a monkey 47 martini with fresh stuffed olives, so good! The pizzas are to die for, had the cup and char and mushroom truffle, followed by the mariniayed skirt steak with chimichurri sauce. To die for! They had more options on the menu but this was absolutely incredible, I highly recommend anyone in Santa Barbara to try this place. Make reservations because they get packed! Anyways, hope you guys had a good time here if you went and if you haven't you're missing out!!

I took my mom here for Mother's Day and had an excellent experience. Prepare to dress nice and maybe even formal since this is considered a high class sit down restaraunt. They have an extensive wine list with lots of local wines from Santa Barbara Wine Country. We split a salad. The balsamic dressing was shockingly sweet, but complimented with goat cheese and grilled squash. As an entree I enjoyed the duck ravioli with porcinni sauce. I highly recommend this dish as it was very well balanced. Every bite was so memorable.  My mom as a pescatarian ordered the squid ink risotto with clams, calamari, and shrimp. Everything about it was risky yet so delicious. For dessert we had the canolli. It was crispy with a custard filling.  The food is why I rated this place so high.  The ambiance was very crowded and tight. They really squeeze you in the front of the restaurant.  The back may be more spacious.  The service wasn't as satisfactory as I expected.   I had to ask for a water refill and cracked pepper which is basic service 101. It also took an unusual amount of time for the server to take our dessert order. Maybe our server was trying too hard not to be overly attentive, but he came to the table the absolute minimal amount of times. Everything else was great and all the servers are authentic Italian which really adds to the Italian restaurant experience. I look forward to coming here again and showing others.

I've recently adjusted my diet to include more plant based foods. For health? For glowy skin? For the environment?  All of those.   My dear friend Julia brought me here to try the Vegan Cheese platter (there were two vegan cheeses, anise crackers, and the rest of the platter was various 'tapas' type fruit pastes and nuts).  Funny how the cheese platter was not my favorite part of the meal.... it was the street tacos that did it for me.   We finished the meal with baklava and vegan vanilla ice cream (couldn't tell the difference between this ice cream and the kind that's made with animal milk), sprinkled with a delicious lemon dust.   Excellent service to boot.   Orgasmic.

Let me start by saying this restaurant is blessed with an amazing, fun and friendly staff. Ask for Andrew who will make your experience great. Andrew is blessed with a verve and zest for life. He is infectious and will guide you through the menu.  The food: Superb. Fresh, delicious, piping hot with the pasta fresh and made in house.  Salads are great. Just simple beautiful great. Get the Mista and share it with some bruschetta and a nice Brunello.  Apps: solid. Try the Gnocchi as an app to share. Marvelous.  Mains: pasta amazing. The ravioli is to die for. Great salmon with a beautiful lemon risotto. Fish great, Steak great. Lamb delish.  Ask for my pasta: Penne with Putanesca sauce with a slab of perfect salmon.    Save room for dessert! Great selections! Lemon, fruit tart, Misu off the chart.  The restaurant is small so perhaps ask for an outside table and wait till Andrew is there. He makes that place perfect as does the rest of the staff!

My family and I had dinner here earlier this month for a birthday and I truly wish I picked elsewhere. I understand we are all going through tough times, but come on. We made a reservation 2 days in advance for 6:30pm. We arrived about 15mins. early and weren't acknowledged until about 6:35. The worker took us to our table and I asked if we could have a different one (they only had 4 other tables occupied) the worker said no its been reserved. Well that table never got occupied. After us there were only 3 other tables that arrived and since our table was next to the hostess stand I heard they did not have a reservation. Anyways thats no biggie. It took about 30 minutes for someone to come offer us drinks! Again we were understanding because of Covid. After we got drinks we waited another 25mins. to order food. When they were taking our order they then informed us some items weren't available so we had to quickly think of something else we wanted to order before the server left for another 20 mins. Once the food arrived we never saw our server again. Our drinks were empty, no napkins or condiments were offered, etc. I was so disappointed by everything we didn't order dessert for the birthday girl and just left. We won't be back.  I also saw another review about them adding extra tip that happened to us too luckily it was quickly resolved. I'm all for supporting local businesses and being extra understanding, but this was not acceptable.

Oh, no!! If you've been here before but haven't visited in awhile, then I'm sorry to tell you but it seems like they changed vendors that provided the smoked tri-tip meats. Quality has decreased drastically and you can definitely taste it.  If it's your first time, then it'll be a toss-up. I can confidently state that it was so much better before. Their Au Jus was richer and the meats used to soak it up better. Different sauces. *shakes head* Does anyone know the name of the previous vendor that they used to use? I'm more interested in finding their storefront (if available) over returning to the Tavern.  Sorry, but I wouldn't recommend the Tavern anymore. Not after switching to a less flavorful vendor on a food item that brought out most of the crowd in the first place.  By the way, came on a weekend last. And there were very few people there. Usually, it would always be packed with a line to the tri-tip (for the tickets at the bar & sandwiches outside). Hasn't management realized this change?!

we had ali as our server and she only came to our table once. i have never been ignored this much. i usually am so chill about it and i understand it's the weekend but she was so inattentive and absent the whole time we were there. this lovely girl asia helped us out at the bar at wine therapy/unbearable afterwards and she was 10x more attentive which speaks volumes since she's at a bar. thank you asia for the great service!

overhyped and overpriced. Zaytoon has taken a page out of every Santa Barbara Chinese restaurant's playbook and made their food seem so exotic, that residents won't realize they're overpaying for grilled meat.  they will claim the real estate made them charge what they charge but for a restaurant off state st. and away from tourists, i really dont think there is justification to charge what they do. its kebab. its nothing special.  there is a mall in Sherman Oaks that sells the same stuff. best middle eastern food in Santa Barbara but also happens to be the only real middle eastern restaraunt in santa barbara. can you now guess why pricepoints are so high?  out door dining on an elevated patio is a plus.  it can be a hard to find at night.  the food is enjoyable but nothing to write a positive yelp review about.

I used to love Arigato years ago. Sushi has historically been phenomenal. Went here recently for my daughter's 11 birthday. We made the reservation weeks in advance.  Upon arrival, we got an unfriendly greeting and were informed of the 90 minute policy...meaning we needed to be out of our seats in 90 minutes. What? Such a turn off. We came to relax and enjoy our $400 meal, not be watching a clock.   In addition, the quality is suffering. We had a miso glazed fish that was fishy is smell and taste. Not what I expect from Arigato!   So many other new places in town that are as good or better. The high prices, sub par service, and good but not superb food will have us choosing someplace else in the future. We want to be wanted!

You always know when a place is good when the average age in the restrained is over 70 (no offence) . Great quality amazing service and good prices! over the years I have taken many friends here and they have all returned.

Yes, it's pretty awesome. Easily one of the best for what it is, and everyone knows it. I've been coming here since I was a child (disgusting, I know) and so has everybody else, it seems. It's a famous place and the line is an absolute bitch. Over an hour at lunch on a Saturday, consistently. But, yes - incredible tacos, what I think is a pitch-perfect hot sauce, great vegetarian tamales and it's still cheap despite the legend. I seem to recall that even the fascists at the New York Times stepped out of their limos for a brief moment to say a few kind words. Always worth the time to stand in line, and the wise will order extra tamales to take home for later.

I used to go to Harry's in the 70's and it was a lot of fun. Not so much now. The martinis are strong but pricey if you order top shelf vodka. The Tee Off and Joe's have the same drinks for a reasonable price and the bartenders are much more personable. I went to Harry's last week and ordered wings and a martini and my bill was $28 before the tip. There are way better options in town with much better food.

Love the ambiance and great patio at Bossie's! Lots of healthy options. I had the fries and and avocado toast. The toast was enormous! I couldn't finish all of the (v tasty) bread. The avocado on the toast was lacking and not seasoned well. The arugula looked as though they had just picked it and put it on the toast, in a good way!! I like big fries so these were perfect and the chimichurri aioli was inventive and tasty.   I'm excited to come back!

I really enjoyed the pool and the hot tub. The room was undesirable. We were told that we were going to be placed in a room with a pool view which ended up being a view of tall bushes and a lemon tree. The room had no ventilation and the bathroom had a drain in the middle of the floor where the shower water drained. The fan was ok at keeping the rooms temperature at a comfortable setting but the room was stuffy and smelled weird. There was no window to open beyond the sliding glass door which had no screen. I won't be back..it was not worth the price of over 200 bucks for 1 night.

Over the past few years these guys have really turned it around and once again become one of my favorite places. It's still not a place you come if your in a hurry but that's just fine with me. The service has become friendly and good, and the food is great.
So good, great people working there and everything is delicious. I haven't had anything bad there, and the new goat special is excellent.

We are locals and came to the Bistro to celebrate a special occasion.  Our server was Melissa and she was amazing.  Our wine and food plus the dessert that Melissa selected for us was delicious.  We enjoyed the mussels to start and half of us dined on the short rib special and two on the tenderloin.  The champagne and the Fiddlehead pinot noir were outstanding.
My favorite Sushi/Japanese restaurant in Santa Barbara.  Food was much better than I expected and service was so friendly and pleasant. I'll probably frequent here :)

This place is cozy, and cute atmosphere.  Here's the review:  Mabo Ramen - beef , tofu in spicy broth with ramen. First time trying this type of creation and my oh my, it was DELICIOUS. i have to say that I'm not a huge fan of ramen, but i do like this little creation. It gave a good kick with its spicyness. there was alot of beef. The portion was just right . (ratio of ramen to beef and tofu). There's not alot of veggies (or none at all) in the soup. It's about 13 bucks for a bowl. hmm, pretty expensive but if you live in sb, and with the lack of good asian food in the area--this place does help satisfy your cravings.   We came when it was extremely crowded on a saturday evening. We enjoyed our time here and the waitress was somewhat attentive.   Sukiyaki - Chicken, tofu and veggies in sukiyaki broth. I thought it was a little too sweet for my taste. BUt, i do like the dish with brown rice. yum. I don't know, its also quite expensive . about 18 dollars for a pot. I wouldn't get it againbecause compared to their other ramen/udon soup selections--the sukiyaki was subpar. I would stick with the ramen selection.   would i come back again? yes , its a friendly environment with great food. lots of food selection and affordable in sb.

Love, love, love this new restaurant! Amazing views, amazing drinks and amazing service. I only tried an appetizer but it was amazing too! Definitely worth a visit  I can't wait to go again and try some food. The menu looked.... Dare I say amazing!
Unbelievable that everything in here is gluten free. Went here for my daughter who is GF and was surprised how great everything was. Staff is so friendly and professional. We'll be back!!

Finally, an Irish pub that can actually pour a proper pint of Guinness in southern California.  It is nice to see they actually have a legit nitro system, comparable to the ones from Ireland.    This is a good place to grab a bite away from the hustle and bustle of state street.  There were plenty of available tables and no wait for dinner.  This fish and chips were solid along with the steak pie.  Also had chicken tenders off the kids menu and my daughter had no complaints.   4 stars.

Their tacos are definitely modern and has a fun twist to each one! Definitely get the 3 taco set which is cheaper than buying 2 tacos. I would not get their Banh mi or their korean style tacos, it was not good at all. Their batter up taco is the best! Their guac and chips is definitely worth the money, you get a cup full of guac that's great to share!

Very sad to write this review because I used to love this place. I ordered a Cajun pasta and it tasted sour like vinegar, as if they had poured a whole bottle of Tabasco. I double checked and asked to see if it was Cajun sauce and was reassured it was. I asked if I could get something else because it didn't taste like Cajun sauce. The lady went to ask the manager who said I could buy a new meal with a 10% discount off. Not sure who taught them how to fix a meal.... I had barely eaten it. I asked for a side of sauce because they wouldn't take it back. They brought me a side of Alfredo sauce and it tasted worse. It tasted putrid and awful. Will not be returning...

Simple Chinese food. Quick and easy dinner on a week night. Very friendly employee helped and I felt very covid safe.  We had pork chow mein and the orange peel shrimp. My weakness is egg drop soup with wonton strips so of course that was a must. Everything was great, as always.

Love the name, Pickles and Swiss! Came here for lunch because of the reviews from yelp, asked the cashier for recommendations and she said she couldn't chose and would recommend like 12 sandwiches!  Since I can't eat cold deli meats because I'm pregnant, I decided upon their Roasted Chicken and walnut pesto panini and their Crispy Chicken West.  Both were equally disappointing!  The roasted chicken was dry and chewy, and the pesto was unmemorable.   I'm not sure if the breaded chicken in the crispy chicken west was baked or fried, but it was dry, dry, dry! And the baguette it was served on was not soft. That was dry as well.   We also ordered a side of their tomato bisque which I thought was pretty good, but my husband didn't care for it.  Overall, we both walked out of there very disappointed with our meal.  The highlight was definitely the pickle! Now that was a well seasoned pickle. Not too sour, with perfect amount of spice.  *They offer samples of their dill pickle popcorn which I thought would have tasted very good, if it weren't for the fact that the popcorn was stale and soft.

Amazing, authentic Caribbean food. Jerk chicken and plantains were on point. So glad to have stumbled upon this place during a SB weekend trip, and will be back. Gorgeous outdoor patio, live music, awesome food, good service.
Wait time matters. The food is ok. There's some great pizza in SB. This one is adequate. But the wait makes it unbearable. Go somewhere else.
Classic old-time diner look and feel. Great service every time! The food is so tasty- breakfast or lunch!  We love coming here

This place looked promising as it offers dim sum all day. I happened to walked by last month when I was in town and noticed it. Fast forward to last night. Happened to Be in town again and made a pit stop on state street for dinner. Sunday night and long weekend, the area was pretty busy but not overly run. Getting a table was easy. We sat ourselves and waited fir the waiter. Just like all the restaurants now, scanners are the new norm. They still brought out a menu, smaller one. Think it was more of the happy hour.   Speaking of happy hour, there was offered for drinks and ramen. Drinks was like buy one get one fifty percent off and ramen was buy one get one thirty percent off.   I rarely leave a low star rating but some just doesn't seem up to par. First off, location wise is great. Right in the heart of Santa Barbara on state street. Which means, it's a big tourist trap. Also caters to a good amount of college kids. Like I mentioned earlier, they served dim sum all day so I had to give it a try. Food, was just ok. Seemed basically frozen and then warmed up. The ramen was a another issue. I understand some people like their noodles al dente but even sitting in the broth, it did not seem to soften up at all. The other dishes was just ok. Food wise, nothing really to rave about . Service was good though.   Good atmosphere with the outdoor dining. Heat lamps are offered. There's plenty of people watching while dining as well as live music. Also, when the bill came, happy hour price for ramen wasn't  taken off. Had to Let them know.

I've eaten here a handfdul of times when we sail to Santa Barbara and go to shore for some food and checking out the town. I like that it's a very casual place with lots of outside seating. The menu isn't as fancy or unique as some of the places around it, and I'm totally good with that. I like that it's quiet, tucked away and not super loud or chaotic like many other places to eat (especially along State Street). I especially like this bc we usually have our dog with us and she can chill on the patio with us at our table with no fears of getting trampled on. AND hello they have a menu for dogs!!!! On our most recent visit we ordered eggs and rice for Twinkle, and it was served in a paper bowl type thing. I loved that she got to have her own yummy food, and we enjoyed traditional breakfast things. They even made a customized vegan breakfast for me and it was SOOOO good!   I definitely recommend Breakwater for casual, quiet dining when in SB Harbor.

I don't really mind coming here for happy hour because the prices aren't bad and you can sit outside.  But otherwise, the food is overpriced and it's not even that great.  I'm a fan of the grape margarita and the free chips and salsa.
The most comforting and uniquely delicious Mexican food around. The drinks are also highly recommended. Must try it if you haven't already.

Such a cute little spot on Canon Perdido! First time there and it was a wonderful experience. The staff was extremely kind and attentive. I tried the caramel latte and it was probably the best one I've had in town! I also had the seasonal soup and grilled cheese sandwich. The seasonal soup was french onion and boy oh BOY was is tasty!!!! The broth was so flavorful and the cheese was so melty and delicious in every bite! The grilled cheese had such a perfect crunch, the cheese and onion jam were insanely good! I will definitely be going back and taking my parents to try those wonderful looking pastries!

In hindsight, I would have gone to Renaud's earlier during the day. I went for lunch around 1pm, and the pastry case was practically empty. Kind of disappointing.  The macarons are a little pricey at $1.74 each, and sadly, I was  disappointed. I got a pistachio, vanilla, coffee, and salted caramel. They tasted pretty good (not amazing), but the textures were inconsistent. The vanilla and pistachio were pillowy and soft as a macaron should be, while the coffee and caramel were chewy and difficult to bite into, as if they had been sitting in the pastry case for days.   For my meal, I got the croque madame sandwich, and it was very good. The light organic mixed greens salad that it was served next to complimented it well. The sandwich probably would've been better with more bechamel, but it was still great. I also tried the raclette cheese tartine (melted raclette cheese over potatoes, caramelized onions, and bacon on multigrain toast), and that was really tasty as well. I would recommend both dishes, and despite the slightly disappointing macarons, my lunch was good enough to raise the rating to 4 stars.

My advice, close to 6PM... sit outside towards the ocean....not for the view of the sea, but to watch the fishing boats come in to off load their catch.  When I was there a boat came in with a load of huge crabs... just amazing.

Just experienced their new layout and menu! The 4 course pre-set menu was over the top amazing! Chef was able to make modifications so we all got exactly what we wanted. The wine pairings were excellent and I'm very excited that the wines could be purchased by the bottle to take home. The ambiance for the pre-set menu feels upscale and almost spa-like. The new side for the Black Sheep Express has high energy and attentive service plus our favorite small plates.  Ruben is a gracious owner that knows people by name and always makes time to check in to ensure the food, wine & service is extraordinary. We also love it when Chef comes out to say hello and ensure we're enjoying his master pieces.   Hands down, the most consistently executed dining experience in Santa Barbara.

5 STARS- I would give more if I could. This place is everything and more, with fantastic small batch beers, fun ambiance, and friendly bartenders. They also have good FOOD, which is hard to find at great breweries. I have liked every beer I've tried there, and I'm a serious beer & wine snob. The brew masters (brew dudes?) are around to talk and answer questions, and get you hyped up about their experimental flavors. Chip is super Cool and knowledgeable, and inspiring. Can't wait to go back!

I recommend coming here in the afternoons for day drinks! They have a great patio, plus they just renovated their entire menu. The food is much more fresh now. Ask for Elliott or Ethan for the best drinks!

This place is a hidden gem. Probably one of the best and most authentic mexican restaurants in Santa Barbara. Anything from tacos, alambres, tortas, everything is amazing. Their flavors are spot on with anything you get.  The place itself is not too big, but there is usually an open table.  And I forgot to mention, they have a self serve salsa bar...

This Subway location offers a perfectly satisfactory experience. It is clean and the employees are quite friendly and very fast. I pop into this Subway whenever I need a reasonably healthy, fast, affordable lunch on State street.
The Hang-Over Noodles were really great! Still really good food, and very clean! My favorite food stand inside the Public Market.

Sevtap is great! They're in an awesome location with a convenient parking garage right nearby. They have options for indoor or outdoor seating. The staff are super helpful and gave us the option to try some wines before committing to a bottle to order. We opted for the Rhône Blend and it was so good! We also ordered the turnips to share and omg, so delicious! They brought out a bread basket that paired so well with the wine. Such a fun spot to meet up with people. I definitely recommend!
Nadia the owner was very friendly. We split a large bowl of fried rice. There are a few beers on tap. Another time I had an eggplant sandwich.

well located gem of Santa Barbara . Great margaritas !! Irish breakfast, good lunch options, small bites, delicious diner. Changing menus and specials. You will always find something tasty. Friendly service. and do not miss the happy hour also on weekends!

FIRST OF ALL, The Owner is a Racist FUCKEN PRICK!!!. Cannot treat people with color correctly, I watched him treat a customer like complete garbage who does he think he is? Being a LOCAL from Santa BARBARA the way he treats his customers is unacceptable.. TRUST me the FOOD is overly priced and TASTELESS I would come here when 'CHADS'  was SAMBOS and they actually cared for their customers and can handle feed back unlike MR. CHAD haha sorry asshole you're miserable in your own skin so you have to treat others like shit.

I was excited to take my dad for lunch on the harbor. The line wasn't  long, and we got a table in about 5min. The staff was friendly. The menu  is very outdated. A little pricey (paying for the view?). And the food  was just OK.

I have been obsessed with Shang hai since high school of course however I hadn't been here in years since I graduated. I finally went and ordered the orange chicken, friend rice and some noodles. Everything was still good but the lady at the counter insisted it was too much food LOL I was like girllllll, no you got the wrong one. I'm still laughing about it I didn't take it offensive because I know cultural differences are real but it was still a little uncomfortable just so you know don't say it's for 2 ahaha.

Mac N Cheese was pretty bomb, cookie sundae was good but hard to determine where the cookie even was in the melted ice cream. Wine selection is good. But I will agree with others the staff is so hard to flag down. Their service is terrible and this isn't my first time here.

I'm going to be honest - based on reviews and what I'd seen online, I expected something... more? But the couple that runs and owns the place is so adorable and they serve our favorite SB coffee (Handlebar.)   Their pastries are really good, as were our breakfast sandwiches. Was it bad? Not at all! But would I go back again? Probably not.

We decided to go for dinner, this place is so yummy! And we had such a delightful waiter, his name is Cristian. He was very helpful with the menu and was just pleasant. This would be our 2 dinner and we will return.

This review is for croissants only. They used to be the best in town, now Costco makes a better croissant. Ever since the shape went from crescent to straight, they haven't been the same. What a shame!

One of my go-to spots in Santa Barbara. I've been half a dozen times over several years and the food and service is always spot on.   Posole is good, you can add a meat as well.  My favorite is the baby back ribs, fall off the bone tender and a deep and complex flavor.  The service is old school, always fussing over you.

The service was superb. My friend and I were seated right away, even though the restaurant was quite busy. The staff was SO friendly, one of the friendliest experiences I've had in a restaurant. The food was very good, with a lot of flavor. You get complimentary chips and three varieties of salsa when getting seated. The complimentary salsas ranged from mild to spicy, but all had really good flavor. I would definitely recommend this place if you're craving some quality Mexican food, and some top notch craft cocktails. Prime location too! Right on lower State street.

I agonized over this review. Bottom line it is unlikely we would return. The small plate  food was good. The overall dining experience was not.  We started out waiting in a line to order, ala McDonalds. When ordering, the order taker did not rush us but you felt subtle pressure not to delay people waiting behind you. Again, like McDonalds you pay when you order (including leaving a tip under the watchful eyes of the order taker).   We ordered a bottle of wine and received two wine glasses and two water cups. I carried them to the table where my partner was sitting in two trips. At the prices this restaurant charges this lack of service is inexcusable.  The wine was brought out an plopped on our table, no tasting offered. The wine was not good. I did not realize how important a drinkable wine was to a meal. We are absolutely not wine snobs, all we look for is a drinkable red. This was not drinkable (no, it was not a 'big' high tannin wine). We discussed how we ended up with this bottle; I thought the order taker *might* have been warning us off, partner thought he was saying it was good (maybe to get rid of it?). In retrospect we should have returned the bottle, but alas we did not.  Well situated patio dining with great people watching. All menu options are vegetarian and vegan options are clearly marked. The food was good, ordered 4 small plates and enjoyed each of them, at least until we sipped the wine. The real killer was the pricing, it is expensive. At this price point we have higher expectations for service.  It is impossible for us to review this restaurant without comparing it to Olivers. Two of the dishes we ordered were the same at both Olivers and Satellite. We preferred Olivers food by a slight margin, but both were good. Pricing was essentially the same, food options were very similar, the dining experiences were night and day different.

FAKE PRICES listed in Online Menu!!  Mesa Cafe is not what it used to be. They list specific entrees at $10.95 and then the exact same item is $17.00 at the restaurant!!!!! This is true for ALL the prices. The wait staff went from being a bunch of cool and attentive local favorites to a completely different group. Our waiter and busser couldn't even speak basic English. And they were rude.  I used to love this place. Now it sucks!!

Clams galore:  I actually came in for something simple. Fish and chips. Did not see them on menue. So opted on the Clams Linguine.   #1.The very best. The finest. The most PERFECT Clams Linguine I have ever had. Seasoned to perfection. $18.99 and a TOTAL of 42 clams. That's fourty-two littleneck clams of descent size. That was amazing. The clams did not stop coming. #2. The house chardonnay wine was very good. It was more then half a glass in a VERY HUGE glass. #3 Wedge Salad. EXCELLENT!  #4.   GREAT service from Chelsea. Thank you for being so accommodating.  R

Aaaaamazing!! The lengua is cooked to perfection, soft, melts in your mouth like its supposed to. Al Pastor is phenomenal! Includes the staple garnish of pineapple. Alambres are full of incredible flavors! Service is great! Heading there in a few actually.  Seeing the bad reviews though is a bit comical and unfair. They are by people who obviously know next to nothing about authentic Mexican food. One bashing the sesame seed salsa and saying it's unnecessary.... Compadre, let me enlighten you, it's a Mexican salsa, deserving of special occasion. Not everyone can make it because of what it takes. Another complaining of the tortilla size..... It's street food!!! That's what they are supposed to be. Another bitching asada being spicy?? Go to McConnell's if your wimpy tongue can't take it.   Bottom line, these guys cook amazing food!!

Get the salmon six step and box tuna rolls. This unassuming place has the best sushi in town by far. The staff and chefs are super friendly and place is very clean. You can't go wrong with anything on the menu. They offer curbside during the Covid 19 pandemic. I've been to most of the local sushi spots and this place is hands down the best in town.

Can't believe I waited til now to try this place. I got the BBQ Chicken Bacon Ranch pizza for lunch and it was soooo good. They're pretty big for 'personal' pizzas too, so half was more than enough to fill me up and I had the other half for dinner later. Excited try some of their other pizzas!
Quant little restaurant with a country decor and feel. Neat location alongside the water and the meal was deelish, I enjoyed the Scallops and a glass of sauvignon blanc!
Stopped by on our way from LA to SLO, and seriously the best service in a fast food restaurant ever. Sparkling clean, cheerful speedy service, and solid (as you'd expect) food.   So impressed.

I stopped by this place for lunch with a couple of first responders and I have to say I was not happy with my order. Firstly the meat served was not freshly cooked and was dry. I ordered the chicken and ribs. It was advertised as fresh off the grill, it was more like fresh out of the microwave. The chicken was vert dry and the ribs were just as dry even with bbq sauce on it.

Came here for dinner with friends at 8 pm on a Friday.  There is nothing like Barbareño in Santa Barbara. The food is fresh, daring and tasty. The ambience of the restaurant is hip (east coast feel) and romantic.   The restaurant offers homemade sourdough and butter. Both the bread and butter alone are worth the visit. As a starter we ordered the eggmuffins for the table. Each small bite taste like an actual egg mcmuffin. They're small bites but tasty. Would we order it again, not for $10.  The entrees were unique, tasty and worth every dollar. We tried the burger, olive raviolo, and Santa Maria bbq. The burger meat was wonderful, perfectly seasoned and cooked medium. It came with a tangy and salty spread, avocado and bacon on brioche bread. The side of potatoes was not impressive at all. The Santa Maria bbq was good. The meat could have used more seasoning but the beans were well seasoned and cooked. By far, the best dish in the table was the olive raviolo-fresh raviolo with an egg that you cut through to get egg yolk as sauce. Make sure to ask for more house sourdough to absorb the egg yolk. Dishes are NOT big enough to share.   For dessert we had the date and bacon tart. Wow! Please order it. It's sweet and savory,   With dinner we also ordered 2 bottles of wine. The manager poured us a taste of Refugio wine called Barbareño. We wished we had ordered a bottle of this one instead of the other two.   Overall, Barbareño clearly knows good food. Wish the meat was better seasoned, the wine list bigger, more starters and kinder waitresses that didn't rush you.   Stop by and try their food before they get too popular.

If you hate gluten for whatever reason this is your place, for the rest of us it is ok, but nothing memorable.   I had a Turkey sandwich and some nondescript fluffy cake, the bill came in at around $35 plus tip ( which you pay while ordering ), How do you screw a Turkey sub ? Well you serve it on a Brioche, the sweet and salty flavors just don't mix well. My mistake (they also offer one other type of regular bread), so it's on me.  The cake tasted good, but left a greasy feeling aftertaste which I am going to attribute to whatever they bake it with.   The service was nonexistent and the place is open and clean with a notorious lack of decoration of any kind.  It felt a little expensive and bland for the price, but I guess for gluten sensitive folks it is worth it.

Been going here since they opened, and I've sat back and watched sandwich prices almost double in that time.  I can't do it anymore, I'm done.  Yesterday I spent $11.50 for my #8, and $3.25 for a 20oz iced tea.  I can get a pound of deli meat and make sandwiches at home for a week with that money, which is what I'll be doing from now on.  I will really miss you Panino, but $11.50 is absolutely nuts.  I've always said we were so lucky in SB to have such great, unique sandwich places like here, 3 Pickles, and So Coast Deli, my friends in other cities are always so jealous of my lunch options here.  Well, I just lost an old favorite.  I sincerely hope you guys take a good hard look at your pricing, because from what I can tell by talking with other people in town, I'm not the only one who feels this way.

Second time trying to buy a gift certificate . Went in and man with ponytail tells us he is busy and can't go to office to get one. Maybe  you should keep those where they are accessible. So lazy, $200 will be spent somewhere else. Really?!

The service was outstanding Reuben the owner was amazing. OMG the food was bonkers good.  The duck carrots, the ramen noodles and the risotto was some of the best food I ever have eaten.   Thank you so much for looking after me! Wow oh wow

I wish I could give it half stars. This is my second time here and food is super tasty. I can't understand anyone complaining about the meat being bland. The only thing I haven't liked is the side of hummus. The price is appropriate for the food. The service has been a little slow both times - young workers who were fairly new at what they were doing. For that and the hummus, I knocked a star, but this is a yummy, healthier meal (I like it as a bowl) for me any day of the week.

This is a nice local burger joint located in a great spot in the heart of downtown.  The food wasn't better or worst then any other local burger joint I've been to; but i was glad to see they had sweet potato fries, milkshakes & malts on the menu.   The location is probably the best/most unique thing about this place. The seating is outdoor, primarily facing state street, so it's a good place to people watch while you eat. The only perceivable draw back is that a lot of the local homeless liked to gather in front and around the place, which can cause uneasiness and may bother some tourist who aren't familiar with that class of people.
Good ice cream, crowds are big as well as the ice chunk I got in one of my scoops. get more than enough cuz u definitely will need more then u think!

BUSY eatery with many cuisines from which to choose! Stopped by on a Thursday night on a little tour of SB eats with my sister. Walked into what I thought would be a deli, and I was sort of overwhelmed by the quality and quantity of food available here!   From drunken noodles and sushi to tacos, wine, and cheese--this place is a wild mix of high quality eats. We contemplated getting the tacos at first (my sister said they were good) but then opted for a quieter restaurant because it would have been too loud to try and catch up over the clinking of pint glasses and laughter of other diners at the pub section of the Marketplace.  Really too bad. I was looking forward to trying their tacos! Probably will stop by again in the future when conversation isn't the main goal of my meal
Dustin and Jeremy (the bartenders) and Hakan (the owner) were amazing and super fast. Great service and good beer. Enjoyed the Hoppy as F**k. Good nachos

My favorite diner in Santa Barbara. This is the best coffee of any diner-style establishment in town. They serve French Press/Dune coffee.   Get the chilequiles if you are there for breakfast. You won't regret it. I always order chilequiles when they are on the menu and these are exceptional. Thick crispy chips (not soggy), zesty slightly spicy green sauce, Cajun sausage and plenty of avocado and sour cream. I have probably been to Cajun kitchen 20+ times purely due to craving the chilequiles.

Had my birthday dinner here last night.  I booked the private room for a party of 18 people. I was told we would be charged a minimum of $500 for the room/food.   This restaurant has a lovely decor and the food is consistently good.  Our waiter was friendly.   That being said - I would not recommend having a large party there.  Our food came out over a period of 35-40 minutes from the time it was ordered.  One dish was brought out approx. every 6-7 minutes. The guest to my right had her dinner 30 minutes before her husband's dinner arrived.  We had folks who were sitting next to people with food who were really hungry and it was embarrassing and really disappointing to see people eating and finishing their meal before others had been even served.  I was the hostess for this event and I was served almost last...  No explanation.  No apology.  I finally went up to the hostess and complained.  I was told that they had been busy...  They were not... And now the food would start coming faster.  It came a bit faster.  If you can't accommodate serving a larger party at approx. the same time, don't take the booking..  When I went to pay the bill I remarked that the bill having come in at $518 had made it to the $500.  The hostess sourly told me that they had said $550.00 and that I had 'heard' $500.00.  No 'thank you for your business'.  No 'How did you enjoy your food', No  apology for the fact that  so many of my guests sat with empty plates while others ate their full meals to the finish. And no 'Please come again'.  Waiter never asked how we were enjoying the meal, did we need anything else, nor did he refill the glasses of water (which was tap water and tasted bad) This was a big birthday for me and the service and hostesses rudeness put a big dent in what would have been an otherwise perfect evening.

From amazing customer service to melt-in-your-mouth sushi, I just found my #1 sushi spot. Luckily, I came in before the dinner rush so I was seated very quickly with my party of five.   Our service was exceptionally amazing, as he catered to our needs of being indecisive on what to answering any questions we had. When service is good, customers are happy because it makes the experience there so much more enjoyable.   As for the food itself, every single piece was incredible delicious and had a wow factor that I still crave to this day. Their price is pretty expensive compared to the average prices, but the food makes up for the price. It's worth it. I highly recommend the sweet shrimp and the wagyu roll. The sweet shrimp comes with fried shrimp head, so the chefs aren't wasting anything. As for the wagyu roll, the were more than eight pieces and the meat is mouth-watering. It just melts in your mouth and makes you wanna order more.

I pick up dinner to go from Finney's on occasion. It is located 1 block from my office. The food is pretty good and the employees are friendly. Since we cannot eat in the restaurant due to COVID-19 it is inconvenient when the order is not quite right. Very hard to send it back or ask for something that had been forgotten. I ordered the pastrami reuben with extra cheese and extra sauce on the side. No sauce. No extra cheese. I also ordered the barbecue chicken salad which was fine except the barbecue sauce was not closed completely and had spilled out. We were truly surprised when we tried the truffle fries we ordered. They were cold and uncooked. They were actually raw inside. We couldn't eat them.  Because business is slow currently, it seems paying attention to details might be particularly important. We will return to Finney's but l will check the order before I leave the restaurant.

First off, this sandwich shop has some really fun sandwich names!  It has to be delicious!  I used to visit the one by UCSB back in the day.  This visit, I enjoyed the 'ménage-a-trois' it did not disappoint!!  I also added some banana peppers for good measure.  Staff here is a friendly and very efficient!  Easy parking in their lot so you can get in and get out.  COVID procedures are still in effect as of this writing, so it's take out (or delivery only).  Be sure to add this to your lunch list, either on a work day or weekend!!  Enjoy!!
Great service. Family owned. Donuts are baked with love, just try a cronut. The juices are cheap and healthy. Good bagels too!

Awful experience and poor quality food. Panera used to be a favorite of ours. I placed the order online for my husband to pick up on his way home to Carp. He had a horrible experience with he staff, not telling him they were out of baguettes or chips that we ordered and instead put a single apple in our bag as a replacement side as if we weren't going to notice. He asked for another apple since we ordered two meals. Also when the staff member handed the bag over it ripped and a new bag was not offered.  When my husband arrived home with our meal I saw my modern caprese sandwich was nothing but two pieces of bread with barely nothing in between them. My salad which I choose from the online menu because of it not having any meat, had chicken on it.    His salad was perhaps a half of a half order, which we did the pick two but I have never seen them give such a small serving.  I appreciate the manager attempting to make it right by offering us a refund but the slap in the face is that we have to go to the store to get it processed.  Thewent in the trash where it belongs
Gourmet Döner Kebaps with fresh veggies. Service was quick. Food was delicious. We met Marco the owner while we were there. He seemed very friendly, and eager to provide a good quality meal.

Went here two days in a row (it's walking distance from friends' place).  First I had a red velvet crushcake and second I had a peanut butter cup. Both I liked, and the frosting was pretty good. But they need to protect their cakes or something - both times the cake was so dry! It was crying out for more oil or *something*.  I mean, it's cupcakes... I can't give it less than 4 stars... but of all the pricey cupcakes I've had, I wasn't that impressed. They have the 'style' down, but the product needs work.

Stopped in to try this new spot with a friend last week. YUM!  I confess I am a carnivore but welcome all types of food and this was great!  The taste and textures were like real meat.  Why not be more healthy! Love all the info about helping the environment too!  Great Mexican fare, tasty food, nice atmosphere.  I'll definitely be back again for a nice meal and will tell my friends!
Patio is gorgeous. A great place to pop in for a quick drink and appetizer.

I'd give more stars if I could. The interior and decor was just as delicious as the food. We were greeted immediately and seated in the back outside area. The place is stunning. The menu is unique and creative without being intimidating.   Service was absolutely on point. Cocktail menu was super enticing but I'm pregnant so they made me something fancy and without alcohol. Decisions were hard.   We had the Burrata Salad, cheese board & polenta dumplings followed by coffee and warm chocolate cake. Every single thing was beyond satisfying. The place filled up quick.   We left wanting to try several other menu items. We will definitely be back!

I've been here twice now (I like to go at least twice) and both times I've received outstanding service and excellent food. The staff has been super welcoming and helpful. The food is tasty and very unique. They have pickle popcorn here too! Mark, specifically, was incredibly kind. Thanks Pickles & Swiss!

I went there over the 4th of july weekend and thought nothing more of this little shop when we first walked in. But wow i was really surprised when the food came out! It was rly delicious actually. We got dragon roll, spicy tuna roll, and this roll with the rice crispies which i forgot but a hidden gem. Theres also a patio, might bring my dog next time!

Pretty good Indian: I asked my coworker what his favorite Indian places were, and didn't get much of a response beyond 'there are 3 places.' Not a good sign. Still the food was decent, they had the usual buffet, but I wasn't wowed by anything they did.  The atmosphere was just all right... it was very open for an indoor place, but I wasn't too pleased at all the traffic from the kitchen: it's a very loooonnng room, so that's part of their problem. No notable music, nor any notable decorations on the walls (the usual paintings and such, with rather harsh white paint).

I like this franchise usually predictable with acceptable food. This location never got to putting in an order. Hey, I'm old and really do not hear as well as I'd like.  They have an order window with outside seating. At the window, attempting to communicate with the girl at the register, thru z window. Screen. And I could not understand her. On the 3rd try. I figured out that when i covered my mouth she would take the order.

Nice spot to hangout and have a beer with some cheese (from Cest Cheese!) down near the ocean front.  Relaxed atmosphere, the bartenders are very nice and easily accessible now from the waterfront parking lot (just walk out the back of the parking lot into the back of the Brewing Co.

I eat salads all the time and I know that it's not the most economical thing to buy at a restaurant but they do such a wonderful job with the ingredients and the amount of dressing that I am always pleased that I indulge every once in a while.
Best doughnuts in Santa Barbara. The service is always great! The maple bars are delicious
Pretty sure I got food poisoning from expired cabbage in the chow mein 2 days ago. Should have trusted my nose.

Probably my favorite breakfast in Santa Barbara!! Great location with panorama views of the ocean, well priced, and remarkable breakfast food! I was blown away by the portion sizes and the Eggs Benedict was one to remember!! I highly recommend!! You can come casual or you can come to celebrate. Either way it's a place everyone should check off their bucket list!

Beer wise, it's the best Happy Hour in Santa Barbara. $3 for craft beer and I believe it's from 3-8 or 9pm. They have a couple of TV's so you're able to watch sports.   Food wise, the kitchen does close around 5 or 6pm but you could always get some hummus.
Super delicious, beautiful atmosphere, I was able to make a reservation for a groups of 8 for brunch, great service... 5 stars for sure.

I only tried a burrito at test pilot.  and believe me I've done my fair share of burrito trying in Santa Barbara and this is by far the closest I've gotten to a taqueria tasting burrito can't wait to try the rest of the menu in the future!
I really like the service, and the atmosphere  here great and fresh food drinks are so good i got the  whiskey  smash sooo good

Fresh fish at its finest. I would get my Ahi Tuna fresh here for making Poke at an annual Luau a friend holds in Visalia. They would always make sure I was getting a prime piece of Sushi Grade fish when they knew it was for Poke. I have bought all sorts of fish here over the years. Scallops, the in house made Poke. the cooked crab, and all sorts of yummy stuff. Never have I been disappointed with anything I have bought here. But recently I was sad to see my favorite person there was no longer employed. Julie always had a smile and was just the most pleasant person to work with. She will be missed. You could tell she was able to keep the place running like a clock. I will still go and get my fish from them, but it will be a different experience without Julie there.
Very romantic and classy. Went here for Valentine's Day and it was great. The service was good and the food was good. It was a pre set menu but I will definitely go back again to try other stuff.

Compared to the local average, this restaurant serves up a more consistent set of delicious dishes than can often be found. With heavy Latin influences and a thankful poke at several fanciful ingredients. As a boy who grew up eating rabbit and thinks frog legs are device, I was very pleased to see both on the menu. The rabbit was excellent with a very mild and not at all gamey flavor.   We also ordered a few tacos, the pickles and one or two other tapas that were all hits throughout the meal that evening. The seasoning on all dishes was spot on and not heavy handed.   I definitely enjoy a restaurant where one can focus on sharing the dishes, which can make for a lighter (relatively speaking!) and more enjoyable meal!

What an awesome group of people that work here ! So friendly and helpful. We love to come and sit with our pup outside and enjoy fresh seafood. Favorites are the Margarita's, calamari, grilled cheese & lobster and of course the chips. Amazing Happy Hour! Don't miss this little gem of a restaurant!!
Literally the best FOOD in Santa Barbara. This place never ever disappoints. Family owned, tortillas de mano. Does not get better.

Oh my goodness, this place is good!  My boyfriend and I stopped in here on a whim on our way down to Ventura a couple weekends ago and it was fabulous.  I am NOT a fish eater, but when I saw that their salmon with Thai curry was paired with torrontes, my absolute favorite wine varietal, I just had to order it.  I am soooo glad that I did.  It was the first time I have ever enjoyed salmon and it paired so beautifully with the wine that I almost died.  My mouth is watering just thinking about it.  My boyfriend got the pasta with chicken and a chipotle cream sauce, which was delicious as well.  We will surely go back on our next trip to Santa Barbara.

Came here for an afternoon snack.  Walk-in and seat yourself if you find space.  Casual seating.  All bar seats inside and some tables outside.    We just had a 1/2 dozen oysters and a cup of clam chowder.  Oysters were fresh, but the chowder was not as good as Brophy Bros.  It wasn't as flavorful.    Food came out in a reasonable time, but our server was not very attentive.

After 13 years of this being my 'neighborhood' sushi place (and always busy), I finally ordered out some sushi. Delicious, fresh, great selection, reasonably priced. I can see why it is always packed. So far the best sushi I've had in Santa Barbara.
Amazing service, friendly owners!  We get poke every Friday, they remember our order and are happy to see us!  The new location is clean, and we get the same amazing service as the Goleta location.

Still five stars in my book! Yesterday I had the new Winter salad and it was terrific. Every time I go here, I am totally impressed that they are so consistent with the quality and variety of flavors.

If we're not eating a big lunch on a road trip up or down the 101 we stop here for a bowl.  With the congested (but free!) parking, healthy ingredients, chalkboards and girls in earth tones and unbrushed hair this is like the Trader Joe's of acai bowls.  Good assortment of bowls. Always busy, so don't expect a seat, and always loud, so expect peace, and never a bathroom so don't expect pees.  There is NO backyard, btw, only a dark garage.  Bonus: heavy, real bowls if you eat it here, ice cold acai, big portions, a varied menu, and well-staffed, including the girl who looks like Jessa Duggar. (Her mission is to deliver you a bowl). Minus: no ginger shots, and the high noise level (thumping radio, whirring machine, self-important monologues about sustainable living).
Really like this place.  Great healthy, organic, tasty options. Good selection of coffee and smoothies!

20 min wait with only two people in line. Guy kept taking his time with other 'priorities' in the kitchen and then just when we were hoping he'd take someone's order in line, he took a phone order in the slowest fashion possible. Of course icing on cake, no thought of an 'I'm sorry.' Really frustrating and unacceptable. We been treated poorly here on multiple occasions.   Plenty of other great good options in the area with better food and better service. Try those and leave this spot alone.

An excellent place we stumbled upon. Good organic smoothies and desserts. Good for soups and light lunch. I go mostly for smoothies but they have some good veggie and gluten free soups they switch up. I enjoyed the chicken soup.

Love that my favorite Brewery and Taqueria in town, paired up and opened The Project- Cocina &  Taproom. The location is prime, right across from the train station, in the Funk Zone. I really enjoy the vibe that this place has going on. And, nothing beats tacos and beer!!

Reunion Kitchen is a new addition after the forced upgrade to the building.  I was happy to see it open after such a long time!  Went with two friends and we were surprised at the extensive menu.  It all looked good!  Pretty expensive, but looking good.  One of us had a hamburger, which was lovely and juicy.  The sourdough bread was tasty, but had a hard time holding up to the juicy burger!!  Unfortunately, the fries were a real disappointment.  They were cold and sort of tasteless.  Such a shame.  The next meal was the clam chowder, served in a sourdough bowl.  It was very tasty, but the chowder was cold!!  One of the managers came over to ask how our food was, and we told him it was cold.  He immediately offered a new bowl that would definitely be hot.  My friend declined his offer... but he was back in about 2 minutes with a piping hot bowl of chowder!! we were all impressed!  And finally, I had the BLT.  The bacon was fantastic!  Lots of lettuce (maybe too much?) and the balance of B to L to T was good!  Again though, the fries were cold and tasteless.  So, overall, it looks like a lovely place.  Lots of choice on the menu.  They need to pay a little more attention to serving the food whilst it is still hot!

This review is for breakfast, and I would not recommend coming here. We stayed at the adjoining Indigo Hotel (which I would recommend) so this was a convenient option on our way out of town. I would put a pretty heavy wager that someone accidentally ran water through already used grinds in the coffee machine (hopefully by accident). It was the WORST tasting coffee I have ever had and I can't believe that it could really taste that bad without some explanation. I also heard the people behind us complaining to the server about how bad the coffee tasted. I couldn't even finish half a cup it was so gross. Anyway that is not a good way to start. We then tried their espresso, which wasn't as horrible as the coffee, but it was just okay. After placing our simple order of standard eggs and bacon and an omelet, our food arrived 40 minutes later. The restaurant only had about five people in it, so it was not because they were swamped. The same person who ran water through the old coffee grinds probably forgot to put in our order. After asking our server what happened to our food he nervously said they were just about to plate it. Never apologized for the ridiculously wait either. The food was fine, nothing special, kind of bland.

Loved this place. Great service -waiter was very knowledgable about the wine and the food. Food was excellent and ambiance was gorgeous. What more could you ask for? I recommend getting there around sunset so you could walk around and see the property before it gets dark. Our favorite dish was the brussel sprouts. They had a touch of maple syrup and bacon and we couldn't stop eating them.

They are good at trying to keep people safe. That's the four stars rather than three.  They used to have larger bagels that were perfectly toasted. Today I got given a variety I didn't ask for, then after waiting a while longer, I got one that seems smaller than they usually are. Barely toasted. The bigger than average bagels are worth 4$, these are not. I won't get bagels there again.  The breakfast burrito is still big enough to warrant the high cost though.

Really good seafood.  Not sure of the standards of those giving this place less than 4.  Ordered seafood kabob, clam chowder and calamari.  All seafood very tender.  Drinks are very stiff so be careful.  Request to sit outside as most seats have view of marina.  You will wait 35-45 minutes most nights but worth the wait.

Thanks to Lin (KTYD/Sunday Tribe), I rediscovered this somewhat hidden gem of a deli! My order was prepared with promptly with love and I had plenty of choices when it came time to select a soda and chips. My sandwich was satisfying and I almost saved half for later...almost. So tasty, I ate the whole thing. It was that good! I will be back to try their hot sandwiches.

We were staying in a hotel close by and in walking State Street found the Himalayan Kitchen.  While we have had Indian food before, we hadn't had Himalayan or Nepalese.  The seating is at tables with beautiful placemats on a white table cloth, covered by glass, with red cloth napkins beautifully folded (Bishop's Hat?).
