Be sure to read the section at the beginning of the menu - about the chef (born at the foot of Mt. Everest, if I recall correctly).  I sooo wish they'd had a little page to take w/us about th chef and the food.  The menu is extensive - offereing soups, appetizers, salads, main dishes from vegetables, chicken, etc - soooo many things to read and from which to choose.  Each staff person who came to our table was SOOO nice and so pleased when we expressed appreciation for the meal/food.  We asked lots of questions before ordering - it was soooo hard to choose.  They were attentive.  They brought a VERY flat piece of bread with a surface that made me think of a sea anemone, along with a tomato based dipping sauce.  While we are vegetarian, I was intrigued by the offering of yak  - and the explanation that it was an authentic dish.  It was SOOOO good - (No, I do not remember the name of the dish I got -it's the first one under Yak on the menu!)  There was a broth, potatoes, etc.    My husband got the first of the chef's specialties - yogurt marinated vegetable kebobs over sizzling onions.  It was very good. He also got lentil soup - VERY different than anything we've had (we eat lots of lentils) but so very good. We shared our meals, so got to experience several new flavors.  I cannot eat spicy foods, so asked for mild seasoning.  I really didn't taste any flavoring, so . . would maybe ask from 'some' spice next time.    If we lived in the area, we'd visit here often to try different menu items  Prices vary by what you get.  You can get soup very reasonably.

I generally assume that most restaurants here in Santa Barbara are overrated, so I was slightly skeptical at first. However, this is a place that truly deserves every star it has and I'm so glad I came here for lunch.  This is a really cute storefront, managed by super friendly owners who serve delicious food (quickly) at a very fair price. What else could you ask for?  The bulgogi is delicious, as is the spicy pork. Out of the two, I think I preferred the bulgogi because the spice on the spicy pork was a little underwhelming. If you're looking for spicy, get the spicy rice cakes, as those were satisfyingly hot.  I'm definitely looking forward to going back and trying some of their other options. What a great restaurant to have just a few minutes from home.

I order from this Domino's all the time! It's always hot, and the employees are so helpful. I'm obsessed with the new Spinach & Feta Artisan Pizza, and the cheesy bread stuffed with jalepenos and bacon! They also have gluten-free pizza which is great for when my in-laws come to visit :)

This place should be called Golden Greens Cus they earned a gold medal in my book. Fuck Silver.  This place is awesome.  I went under protest and I had to eat some humble pie once the meal was done.  The food is delicious.  The portions are nice.  The ingredients are quasi-healthy and the price is right.  I will be back over and over again when I'm in town.   The two dinner meal deal is the way to go too!

Best hot dogs in the county. Basically it's hot dogs on steroids. Dave used to be a mobile hot dog stand and finally opened up across from San Marcos high school. Fast service friendly staff and close to my office.  Try the hot cheetos corn on a stick and the home made corn dogs. So bomb

Very good gluten-free loaves. My whole family tried three varieties and we loved them all. Toasted or pan fried with a little butter or dipped in olive oil is good since they are just slightly dry but good for a gf bread. I just wish they didn't put rice flour as a base. We don't eat a lot of carbs and try to stay away from grains so this was a splurge. I'd like to see some interesting, ancient flours that are Lowe in carbs and more nutritious than rice or whatever other base they use (I can't remember but I asked).  They were so friendly and answered all our questions and patient as we took a while to decide what to get.

We were not familiar with Gladden until the stay at home order due to covid-19. Since then, we've used Gladden several times for their food delivery service, which I believe is a partnership with Zoomers for Boomers. The produce is good and some of it is organic. The delivery persons wear masks. Some of the items seem to be priced on the high end but overall we're very pleased with the quality and with the delivery service.
Pretty good pizza for the price. Especially in Santa Barbara  $9.70 also the portion wasn't skimpy so I'd definitely come here again. Good deal !

Delicious sandwiches made with quality ingredients. Be prepared for a bit of a wait, although it is definitely worth it. Amazing staff who work really hard  I love the #12, pesto & chicken is the best combo. Nice selection of chips and drinks as well.

I think the low reviews on this place are crazy.   Everything I get here is delicious. The pad see ew is one of my favorites. It's slightly smoky from the char of the wok. That char mixed with the oyster sauce that's in the dish makes it so darned good. Someone complained about it being too eggy. But this dish has egg in it. So I'm not sure what the issue is.   Their larb is the best in town. To me it is perfect. Spicy,  sour, umami.   I highly recommend Galanga!

This place is delicious. At first I wasn't sure if it was just a fancy $20 burrito place but we weren't disappointed. The shrimp/fish enchiladas were amazing. The sauce is so yummy. My husband had the shrimp which were big and juicy. My son had the fried beef tacos which were also good. We also had a cocktail which was fresh and delightful... I could've had 3 but didn't want to get too crazy. Our friends also enjoyed the food and said they were so glad we chose this place. Extra bonus for getting to use Yelp to go on the waitlist!!

The appeal of this restaurant is LOCATION, LOCATION, LOCATION. A wonderful view of the beach especially if you are seated at the patio. So, one could say that the long line is worth the wait.  Talking about the food and drinks, they are also great! I agree with other reviewers when they say, this place is beautiful as it is delicious. Despite of the busyness, the waiters are attentive.  The beachfront view plus the great food and service, no wonder this is one of the best or highly- rated restaurants in Santa Barbara! Go visit this place, you won't regret it!

Found this little French breakfast nook and was very happy with the CROQUE MADAME (Grilled Ham & Cheese & Egg brioche)! Came with a side salad. Absolutely divine!  Perfect spot to pop in for a quick morning treat! Coffee was great too!   Outdoor seating was comfortable.

I really don't get the lower-than expected ratings. Dined here for my 34th anniversary on April 20th. I admit it was off to a slower start than I'd expect but after that, our server was super attentive and the dishes we ordered we're one-of-a-kind spectacular.  We had an anniversary dinner that was truly memorable.

This was the first place I ate at when I moved to Santa Barbara for college about 7 years ago..(time flies!!). Such a great find, thanks to Yelp and a few locals.   First, the atmosphere is amazing. It's casual and fun. There is lively music and a magician going around inside doing some magic tricks for customers. There were beads and masquerade masks on the walls. Really awesome setting.   Second, the food was delicious. Every table gets a basket of cornbread that were different flavors with different dipping sauces. The cornbread comes out hot and fresh. I think my favorite was the jalapeno cornbread. I forget the exact entrees we ordered but I remember they were all flavorful with the perfect amount of Cajun seasonings and perfectly made. A lot of Cajun places skimp on the seafood, such as crawfish, but not here!! Every time I ate here, the food was awesome.   Lastly, the service was great. The servers were super nice and also knowledgable. Very impressive.   I can't wait to come back when I visit SB again!

Ordered a cake and cupcakes for my son's birthday. The cake and cupcakes were cute, but the flavor was absolutely sub-par. I was very disappointed. I ordered them from a bakery because I wanted something that was delicious. Had I known they made grocery store level cakes I would have gone elsewhere. Lesson learned.

Absolutely delicious food.  My traditional eggs benedict was perfect and the cold brew coffee was nice and smooth.  All the bakery items in the display case looked wonderful and I'd definitely come back here the next time I'm in Santa Barbara.

My favorite Indian food place ever, however they have given me cheese instead of tofu 6 times. I'm not kidding when I say 6. The last time my sister ordered she didn't even want to bother and just threw her food away. If you have a vegan menu you need to be careful because people have allergies. We know the difference between cheese and tofu also. Just wanna make it clear that you can really taste the difference
Disappointing food and disappointing service. Sadly I won't be coming back. I recommend Blaze Pizza instead for a similar style of restaurant.
Very overrated, and exceptionally mediocre.  Absolutely nothing original.  The only thing above average is your wait time to be seated.
Cool spot. Came here after the beach for a quick beer and snack. Toni did a great job helping us on the patio. Enjoyed the beer and the jalapeno poppers.

This is one of my favorite places in Santa Barbara! I've come here a few times and each time has been consistently delicious!  my favorite dishes have been:  -salmon -king scallops!! -lamb -yellowtail crudo  I'll be back for sure!
Love the different kind of salsa! Avacado, pistachio, and mango salsas are my favorite. Not to mention the food in general is worth every penny
Drove by on my way to LA, great menu selection, food was great and service was super friendly and fast, highly recommend.

I would skip this restaurant. The food was unappetizing and very undervalued. My taquitos were the saddest taquitos that I have ever eaten. Dine somewhere else if you are craving taquitos. You will pay a heavy price for unsatisfactory service. My $13 taquitos were the worst taquitos that I've ever had. Pictures speak louder than words.

The good  Oyster Rockefeller Clam chowder Amazing atmosphere  Sunset and drinks Probably the best spot by the water.  The bad.... everybody knows the good, so they are all there and it can be challenging to get a good seat.
Great food, great service, and great price. The menus is huge and it's hard to decide. I got the Spaghetti Carbonara and the wife got the Ravioli Sampler. It was good! Highly recommend this place!

After a long steep hill hike with Monkey, we were looking forward to grabbing some fish tacos.  Monkey was thinking lets go to this place as we always see a very long line so maybe it must be good but Hell No it was not.   Monkey don't even know what he ordered but it looks like a steak taco and he got a nice big portion. I ordered the #4 pork taco and two freaking order at $2.50 an order and it looks like a slap on the plates for dogs.  It didn't feel like it was done with love or care. The meat was lightly cooked, I can hear it oink.    I experience better than this even at Carl's Jr. Mexican selection was better than this. San Diego tacos are better than this and I even ate on the streets of Tijuana and it was not only cheap but tasted better than this.  I don't mind the price but I love food and I love food that taste good.  Maybe for a blind senior citizen, my plate would have been good for them.    Oh don't forget to take cash as they don't take card so I end up buying lunch with the last bit of cash I got.  And don't forget to tip as you might get looked down on if you don't.   After this I'm afraid to even try Mexican around town.

Decided to check out Islands and wasn't disappointed the food was delish and the service was outstanding we did have to wait a bit but it wasn't bad they texted us to tell us our table was ready. La Cumbre needs more people friendly places, while we waited we walked around and it was a ghost town...sad compared to how it used to be

OK, so I could NOT wait to give this review! I wanted something different for my birthday, which was yesterday, so I searched 'vegetarian restaurant in santa barbara' and Mesa Verde came up. I saw it had 96 reviews and 4 stars so I read further. Time after time I read how the kale burger was amazing, the empanadas were incredible, Market Salad fantastic and I thought OK, I'll check it out. I kept remembering, 'never try something new/unfamiliar on a special occasion.' I work very hard, I have 4 children, I'm going to school full-time and I REALLY didn't want to waste my money, my time, and have a bad night because of the typical bland food I usually have to eat as a vegetarian.  My first impression when I drove up and saw the outdoor seating area with these gorgeous streaks of fire was, 'Hm, well that's impressive. Wonder what their menu looks like.' I am SO used to trying to eat an appetizer or put side dishes together to create a meal out of potatoes, rice and a few bland veggies, that I suppose I've learned to deal with mediocrity (with four kids I am on a budget you know). So when I picked up the menu and started reading these SERIOUSLY INCREDIBLE INGREDIENTS, I realized I was saying, 'Mmmmm' and 'Oh my God no way' out loud and my kids found that too funny, of course. I could not wait to eat!  We ordered the following dishes and I seriously had to stop ordering:  *ROASTED BRUSSELS SPROUTS W/ JACKFRUIT 'CHORIZO'   *WILD MUSHROOM TACOS. LEMON CREME FRAICHE. HARISSA. PEPITAS. BABY SPRING ONION   *GRILLED PEACH SALAD. AVOCADO. SHISHITO PEPPERS. CURED MEYER LEMON. ROMESCO. BLACK LENTIL. PEA TENDRIL. MARKET RADISH. BLUEBERRY CHIPOTLE SALSA. WATERCRESS *RAS EL HANOUT LINGUINI W/ PUMPKIN. TOMATO. TARRAGON. ONION. GARLIC. THYME. WHITE WINE.  *SWEET POTATO TRIO. MASH, ROASTED & FRIED W/ SAGE AND BROWN BUTTER SHAVED BRUSSELS. HAZELNUT. MANCHEGO. PUMPKIN SEEDS. MALDON SEA SALT. LEMON JUICE. ZA 'ATAR. *HUMMUS. FIG. CARAMELIZED ONION. HONEY. MALDON SALT.  I copied these from the menu because I started to write Brussels sprouts, three potato something or other, and wanted to do their menu justice. Hope it's OK that I copied/pasted, but these are the items we ordered. There were SIX of us including myself, my starving and very buff husband and three teenage boys who don't know the meaning of the words, 'I'm full' plus my 8-year old daughter who, despite her slender appearance, can really pack it in! MAN we were stuffed! I topped it off with the 'Artemis' Brew: raspberry leaf, hibiscus, rose petal, rose hips, pau d'arco, St. John's Wort. This floral brew is immune-boosting, mood-boosting, and great for women's health. Served with raw honey.' May as well put it on the dessert menu 'cause I'm comin' back for more o' dat!  So, as you can see, I'm in LOVE with this place! My new fav for sure! I'm completely obsessed with their food and canNOT wait to go back...for dinner...again tonight!  If you're a vegetarian you'll be in Heaven, guaranteed. There is NO MEAT in the building so you're safe to order at will! My hubby even (who swears there's nothing better than a steak) ate well, got full, nuff said.  You're nuts if you don't go to this place!

This place has somewhat of a split personality.  Or at least it did when I was here on a Saturday night for dinner.  After winding through the mountains and surviving the setting sun glaring into our eyes, all the while wondering how the many motorcycles that make their way here ever actually GET here safely, we scored a parking spot in their lot.  The creek that trickled below and the thicket of trees and rocks that I could see just beyond the lot made me want to climb down and up and all around.  The fact that the bullfrogs were just starting to really ribbet as the sun was setting made me itch to climb down those rocks even more, so I could make a grab for a chubby one!  We were early for our dinner reservations, so we followed the sound of the band into the bar and sat and listened and watched people dance for a bit.  The vibe was laid back and warm and casual and fun.  About 10 minutes before our reservation time, we checked in at the restaurant next door.  The girl who greeted us was polite but not overly friendly when she told us they were running a little behind.  Since we had the time, we wandered around the side of the restaurant and back, where little rustic buidings had signs that explained they were old jail cells and even living spaces.  Neato to look at, and clearly original in their structure.  (Bring a flashlight if you want to be able to see the inside of these structures in the evening)  The sun had set by now, but the sky was bright enough with moonlight to see bats out hunting for bugs.  Neato!!    We ended up being seated about 25 minutes late for our reservation.  Honestly, for how slow this was, the attitude didn't match the level of apology that I would think is appropriate.  While the bar next door is casual and warm, the vibe in the restaurant is definitely different.  The wood plank floors and rustic walls and decor make it feel very casual, because nobody dresses up to come here.  The menu items include exotics like venison, and also traditional things like prime rib, chicken, and lamb, but aren't super pricey.  The service here however is on the cold side.  Cold, like we think we're a high end restaurant so we're going to be un-warm, in a fashionable way.  or at least that's how our server was.  And it's all wrong in my opinion.  This is a rustic place!  The bar next door is loud and warm!  But you walk in to the restaurant, and it's hushed tones, slow service, and just okay food?  Hm.   All dinners come with either soup or chili or salad.  The salad was a nice mix of dark leafy greens with grated carrots on top and a couple of cucumbers.  The chili with beans was tasty, but a little salty for my salt-sensitive palate.  The bread basket had thick slices of warm white and wheat bread that was squishy and good with butter.   Prime Rib - one of the daily specials.  I think that it was sliced and then cooked again, which when it comes to prime rib is odd.  It was chewier than any prime rib that I've ever had and medium rare when I asked for rare.  My veggies were far too crunchy for cooked cauliflower and carrots.  Boneless Half Chicken - this was really good.  Moist and flavorful, with a crispy skin.  Apple Cobbler - served warm but not with vanilla ice cream as I think cobbler should always be served, but with whipped cream - clearly from a can.  The crumb topping was good, and the tart apple filling had a nice amount of cinnamon and lemon zest.  Our not so friendly server never checked on us throughout the meal.  The busser was the one person who we did see a lot of, who WAS warm, who delivered our food, checked on us, refilled our waters, took our empty plates, and then even wished us a safe drive home and a good night.  Why isn't HE a server, I ask?  Oh, I should clarify - our server did crack a warm smile - when he dropped off our check.  Which is honestly the worst time to break out your smile, dude, because everyone knows why you've timed this to come with the check.  I would definitely come back here, just because the rustic scenery around here is so gorgeous and the bar vibe was so warm.  I've heard a lot about the tri-tip sammich that Cat Cora called out on The Best Thing I Ever Ate, and we even saw (and smelled) the outdoor grills that cook said tri-tip.  So I'd come back for that, and hope for a warmer lunch-time vibe.

Brewhouse is popular on Yelp!   It has a bit of a mix of divey bar with music club. On the night we went, there was live music inside, but you could hear it from both the inside and outside seats. Bathrooms were pretty gross, always a negative point for me.  They make all their own beers, and had a good Apricot Wheat, but I wish they had more of a girly light beer since the other beers are all just too filling.  Huge menu- with lots of late night appetizers up for grabs. We went for the steak bites, which were okay, but lacked some flavor.  Definitely okay.. But I think there are other places in SB I'd recommend before Brewhouse.

The Enterprise Fish Co is always one of our favorite places to eat.  Monday night we had their 2 pound lobster special.  It was excellent!  The service is always good, however on this visit it was exceptional.
Great food, fun place to hang, service was outstanding, and I have had the vegi paella twice this week!!  I'll be back...

Beautiful grounds. Good service overall. Bellhops and valet guys work hard. The villas are spacious with nice balconies. Villas are clean. Nice separate shower and bath. Nice big flatscreen TV. The beach is horrible. The Spa Restaurant has below average food and unorganized when it comes to reservations. Room service is good. I'd like to see more activities offered from the resort. The price is decent for what you get. Didn't get a chance to see the gym or visit the spa itself. A quick 15-20 minute drive down the 101 from State Street in Santa Barbara.

Breakfasts are good so I tried the lunch menu. Big mistake. The lunches we had were below average in food quality and flavor. The s andwiches are dry, the tacos were plain, the salsa tasted like it came out of a can and the salad tasted like it came from a bag.  I also question the cleanliness off this place as I witnessed a dog puke on the patio and the dog owner (maskless) clean it up. The staff did not clean the floor and other guests were seated.  . I will not be back.

Went to Lure today for lunch.  Started out with Fanny Bay oysters out of British Columbia, then Deb had the Sand Dabs and I the Fish and Chips.  Deb said she's had better Sand Dabs...  Lure is nicely decorated with a full bar and extensive menu with reasonable prices.  If we lived close to La Cumbre Plaza it would probably be on a monthly rotation.

Went here for a wonderful birthday dinner! Stylish and fancy restaurant but very welcoming. Enjoyed such a delicious meal including a crab cake appetizer, duck with a squash risotto, and paella. I love how they decorate for holidays.

We went on a Saturday night. Very busy restaurant. Made a reservation by phone for the upper patio for the view.  They sat us in the back of a covered tent type thing that was by the sidewalk.  When I told her I made the reservation for the upper patio they said that it wasn't written down. They did move us to the other open patio which is by the sidewalk which is much better than the original place they put us in the back. The fish was fresh that we had and good. The prices are high.  We ordered an expensive bottle of sake and the waiter didn't even pour it into our glasses.  he just put the bottle down on the table. . I thought for the price point that the service should've been better. There's a ton of great sushi restaurants to go to and I don't think I would go back here.
Food is super delicious! I had the Anacapa burger and it was sooo tasty. Run by a wonderful family, very warm and inviting.

After passing huge crowds at 3 other places on Monday morning last week... we passed here and saw none. Parked easily right out front. Our very tall, super smiley server delivered! Had to have the crunchy french toast combo.AS GOOD AS IT GETS. Husbands toast came with some wonderful homemade boysenberry jam. WOW! Nice,strong, aromatic coffee.

It's a little pricey but your paying for excellent food service and atmosphere. The salad and soup bar are both so yummy and the hot and cold items look delicious. I've never gotten one of their sandwiches because I just buy a ciabatta roll and stuff it with items from the salad bar! Delicious. Did I mention the hot tea is amazing.
Good food, but for $8+tax+tip, even I would like to see bigger slices of cake.  Makes heading a few doors down to Andersen's Danish Bakery for layer cake an easy decision.

Restaurant? Cafe? Takeout? Cafe and Blackbird both have this Happy Hour confusion. Don't have it. Not sure. Not good! Terrible Pinot and Rose. Champagne was only saving grace. 16$ during unhappy hour. Blackbird: Sink broken in ladies' room. Both toilets broken in Ladies' room. Chick pea fries... no!  Spaghetti, not aldente and no apparent bread crumbs. Bartender listens to conversations and interjects.  Does the hotel know that the cafe and restaurant are SUBpar?  Do people pay 300 a night for this?

Humm were to start, a steakhouse that doesn't serve steak during lunch ok that was a bit odd but forgivable as they had some Tritip sandwiches, however the waitress looked as if she was dressed to have a lazy day at home. That aside, the food wasent bad and the service accommodated my family and i even when my son spilled his apple juice everywhere. I have been told good things about this restaurant so i will give them another shot, but for dinning service. TBC

This place is amazing, the service was excellent we grabbed a mixed box. The inside of the box has a description about Turkish delights. I had a hard time making my mind the box was a perfect choice and the selection was great all of them were delicious. Will definitely be back to try the rest.

My Husband and I decided to stop into Hook and Press while we were on a Mini-Moon in Santa Barbara.  We arrived around 10:15 on a Tuesday.  John, the owner, was there and greeted us with a smile. There were plenty of each flavor of donuts left, which I was surprised as it was later in the morning and definitely after breakfast.  Score! (Donut shop tip: if you want the full variety of donuts, from any donut shop, you should get there in the morning).   I am normally plain Jane when it comes to donuts.  I decided to go outside of my shell and ordered the Butterscotch Walnut Brittle.  My husband ordered the Maple Coconut Bacon Donut (no pigs were harmed in the making of this delicious donut!).  We loved every single bite.   The cold brew and Cappuccino also hit the spot.   My sister, a few weeks prior, had brought back some donuts from her trip to SB;  the Horchata and POG, both equally as delicious as those we tried on this trip, even when a day old (pop those bad boys in the microwave for 25 seconds and they are just like freshly made donuts).  We decided to sit on the State Street patio so that we could people watch and enjoy our sugary delights.  The ambience of the shop and patio are definitely SB local and it is worth sticking around to enjoy your treat.  Thank you Hook & Press, we will be back on our next trip!

They have by far the best tres leches cake  not only that but they will hear you out if you have a problem with an order. Recently I had a problem with a cake and the owner himself took the time to help me out. It's amazing to know there still people that care for it's costumers and it's business. Definitely a place to visit.

I am only writing this based on the lunch buffet. There is a good chance that the food from the menu is quite good... but I had the buffet I used to come here years ago. They had lots of fresh vegetable dishes on the buffet. They had a garlic string bean that was amazing. Unfortunately almost everything now seemed to be cheap processed food that was deep fried, or reheated. Nothing fresh or actually made in the restaurant.   Not sure if I would ever go back... but if I do, it won't be for the lunch buffet!

Amazing location on the Stearns Wharf.  We ordered a whole local Box Crab and local King Crab. Though the prices are very high, so was the quality. Plus, the ambiance of eating on the pier is quite the memorable experience.  Both whole crabs we ordered were divine. It takes much more time cracking them open than eating them, but that's the whole point. It was a great experience and the food quality is excellent.
Dangelo's is the best breakfast spot in Santa Barbara.  It doesn't matter what you order, it's all good.  Service and outdoor dining.

I LOVE this place. The tacos are ermygerd! The ambiance is nothing fancy but the food is good, especially the fish tacos. I love the self serve chips and salsa- whatever the seasoning is that they put on those chips, I can't get enough of it. I have been here with small and large groups of friends, and attended several parties with groups of friends here, and even gone to eat here by myself. The service is always good and the food is always delicious! The margaritas are also really good and very well priced.

I'm one of those proud Jewish ladies who claims to know enough Spanish just to get by. When in fact, the majority of the Spanish I know comes from translating the street names since moving here to Santa Barbara from my native Brooklyn. De la Vina Street was an obvious one. Salsipuedes Street still confuses me. But all along I just assumed La Cumbre meant 'The Ghost.'  And you want to know why? Because the interior shopping area of La Cumbre Plaza is not unlike an empty ghost town.  Have you been? It's an outdoor mall that is both charming and utterly depressing all at the same time. From the outside you think 'Well, that Macy's must have been quite impressive in 1967.' But now it's just sad. And don't even get me started on the Sears. I have to take two Prozacs just to get within 25 feet of that place.  But then you walk into the interior of the mall and you go 'Hmmm, this is actually quite nice.' It's just a shame nobody shops there. We have a Pottery Barn, a slew of higher end clothing stores and even a Williams Sonoma. Who knew? And you'd be lucky to see maybe one or two shoppers in each. I've been there half a dozen times now and each time I find it eerie to be the only person walking along the corridor with its lovely benches and neatly pruned trees. Occasionally you'll catch a glimpse of another shopper and perk up, only to realize it's just your reflection in the shop window.  Well, one day, my antidepressants were really kicking into high gear (or maybe it was the shot of bourbon I'd had in my coffee), but I gained the courage and strength to walk the entire length of the mall from Macy's down to Sears. As I got closer to Sears, I began hearing voices. Not in my head this time, but coming from somewhere in the distance beyond. I cautiously poked my head around the corner and discovered the Plaza Deli. Bustling with happy diners out on the patio eating what appeared to be the most wonderful sandwiches and salads. I had just stumbled upon the beating heart of an otherwise dead and soulless shopping mall.  My first reaction was anger. Because why on earth had nobody told me about the Plaza Deli before?! Shame on you Santa Barbarans for keeping such a lovely little secret from me. Shame on you!  My second reaction was bliss. Oh, it's such a kitschy little wonderland of antique soda bottles on every shelf and old timey bottle caps glued to every surface. I felt like I was transported back to the 1950s (not that I'm old enough to remember). But the best part was the menu. After paying Santa Barbara prices for so long I was shocked to see sandwiches and salads all for around $6 or $7. I'm used to paying double that for lunch at some places. There are other so-called delis whose sandwiches comprise mainly of three inches of lettuce and just a thin slice or two of meat. Well, the Plaza Deli is quite the opposite. I kid you not there was at least 4 inches of meat on my sandwich. I couldn't even get my mouth around it...and everyone says I have a very big mouth.  I'm also a fan of salads, but when the salad is mostly lettuce with just a few sprinkles of other veggies, you can rest assured I won't be back...you hear me Panera Bread? Well, last week I ordered the chef salad from Plaza Deli and I thought I'd died and gone to heaven. The thing was mostly meat, cheeses, eggs, and an assortment of vegetables. The only way I can describe it is if you imagine going to the Saturday farmer's market, then shove everything there in your mouth at one time. That's the Plaza Deli's chef salad. In fact, I had to dig way down just to find lettuce. Now that's a REAL salad!  If you haven't discovered the Plaza Deli yet, get yourself to La Cumbre Plaza immediately. You won't be disappointed. And try the steak potato chips while you're at it. They taste just like a sizzling steak in your mouth. And BTW, La Cumbre doesn't mean 'The Ghost' after all. I just Googled it. It means the peak or summit. You're welcome.

We skipped this place the first few times Yelp recommended it based on that profile picture. I kept thinking those colors plus 'Nectar' meant it must be a juicery. Nope! Those are some fantastic and colorful drinks. I don't normally go down our orders one by one but that's how we ordered here and everything deserves some commentary.  Drinks:  Orange Blossom: Mildly citrusy vodka drink but the rosemary syrup added a great extra complexity. Geisha: Sweet and tangy, I barely got a sip away from my companion. Passion del Diablo: Legitimately spicy, and with a dried pepper as a garnish if you really want to burn your mouth out. I love a good savory/spicy drink and the mango was a great complement. Ginger Lemonade: We ordered this after seeing it on the happy hour list not realizing it was an alcoholic drink. Oops! It barely tastes like one and has exactly the flavors you'd want (ginger, lemon, sweet). I'm not doing the bar justice here, though the crowds we saw every night congregating spoke louder than words. I'm far more of an enthusiast than a connoisseur of drinks, but I felt like there was a skilled mixologist at work.  Food (we ordered our food one bit at a time. None of the portions are overwhelming, but I didn't feel like they were skimpy either):  Filet Steak Bites: Alright, the bites were tasty, no question, but they're really just a vessel for the Kashmiri Almond Sauce. Nectar knows they've got a winner with this sauce, since it's served with a few different items. Seriously, I could dip anything in it.  Roasted Asparagus with Almond sauce: Thin and cooked a little past al dente, but with such a good char and the sauce that the flavor carried it. No question I'd order this again without hesitation.  Nectar Fries: Apparently these are very popular. They're light and crunchy and a decent add-on. We weren't disappointed, which considering the rest of the food should be taken as a compliment. There's a house spiced ketchup to go with them but we ended up just using them to scoop up more of the almond sauce.   The 'special' consisted of chicken skewers, Hummus and flatbread, and a small portion of the Kale Salad. We wanted to try all this so were really happy to get to order it all together.  Chicken Skewers - tasty kebabs. No complaints here, and a perfect accompaniment to the rest. I wouldn't usually say that about the meat...  Hummus and Flatbread: Wow! I'm not the right person to judge hummus, I normally steer clear, but I was gobbling it up with the chicken and flatbread. So tasty as a condiment, and my companion was fighting me for it. The only comment we had was that there wasn't really enough bread to go with the hummus, we'd have been happy with either twice as much bread or half as much hummus (it was a huge dollop).   Kale salad: So simple, just some charred tomatoes (yum!), some parmesan cheese, some curried almonds to add a kick, and an incredibly light oil and lemon dressing. Wouldn't have ordered it over the beet salad except that it came with the special, but we were raving about it (especially the almonds, which we had to fight over).  Desserts: We'd overeaten but couldn't resist.  Chocolate Citrus Torte: Good to scratch that chocolate itch but the guava coulis and dollop of whipped cream were divine.  Tiramisu (special): Nothing unexpected but we were so happy we'd ordered it.  Special mention to the Pistachio Gelato with inverted waffle cone. We conversed with a neighboring table about this; didn't try it ourselves but it looked stunning and elicited lots of happy noises.  All in all we felt like we'd found a real hidden gem coming in here.

I came in around 5 on a Saturday and was given a menu. In the 5-10 minutes after that, I didn't see my server but I did see 2 other tables that walked in after me get immediate service and drinks while I was still waiting to get a hello. I left without anyone coming to my take other than dropping of the menu. Guess they don't want local business and would rather rely on tourists who will put up with crappy service.

The salsas here are amazing. A diverse selection, all must be tried. The tacos are large, about the size of two traditional tacos. The Norteno is my favorite. All of the quesadillas that I've tried are wonderful. I love how they have blue corn tortillas. The only place in town that I know of who does. And they are excellent. They have quite a unique menu and combine creative and lively flavors in every dish.  Attention is also paid to the detailed presentation which goes above and beyond most 'order at the counter' Mexican joints. Service has always been good and very friendly.  Although they are the size of two, the tacos are still on the pricier side, which is why it's good that they have a BOGO Axxess deal. It's good to be a local;)  Gracias, Corazon Cocina!

Happy hour is the shit.   I came here with my boyfriend around 3 on a Saturday. So the bar was the only option for happy hour until 5, which is when the outside is also available for happy hour. Anyway, we were seated right away and immediately ordered drinks and appetizers. The two for one deal was great and the portions were reasonable for the pricing. Definitely coming back!

From Sunday through Thursday day night most rolls and sushi is 50% off. Hibachi is also discounted on Sundays and I believe through Thursday as well. The service is great and they make you feel at home. The hibachi chef is amazing and makes you enjoy his artistic skills when preparing your food. The sushi rolls are fresh and delicious. You can sit at the sushi bar or grab a table. The host and the people that work that do a great job. Enjoy an excellent restaurant.

First impression: There is a cage full of finches outside.  Who can resist a cage of finches???  Our Axxess card (buy 1 entree get the second free deal) brought us here tonight, and I am glad that I was able to try it, but I most likely won't be returning.   We ordered their wings as an appetizer, chicken piccata, and a chicken marsala with a side of fettuccine alfredo.  They have happy hour with select dishes at lower price, so the wings came with 6 for $6.  The wings taste like the wolfgang puck wings - spicy with a bit of asian infusion. Best described as spicy plum sauce wings served with a blue cheese ranch.   The chicken piccata came with veggies and mashed potatoes, which were pretty good.  The texture of the chicken itself wasn't too good. The chicken marsala... man... I wanted it so badly to taste good, but the salt was just overwhelming.  The chicken marsala was served with some mushrooms, and every bite of the mushrooms filled my mouth with salt.  I ate the fettuccine alfredo last.. If it weren't so salty, it would have been good.  But alas, I was left with a thirst that not even the largest oasis could satiate.   I do have to give props to the waiters though - they made our experience great, even if the food was sub par.

Very impressed with the deli sandwiches. I ordered 8 sandwiches and they had them ready in 10 minutes. Friendly staff and will do business with them again. We might try their sushi next time around. Thank you!

THE BEST TACOS I HAVE EVER HAD IN MY LIFE!!!!!!!  Let me explain that strong statement. First off.. let's be real, tacos are pretty much always delicious as long as you're buying from mom&pop shops (aka NOT Taco Bell, Chipotle, Guadalajara Grill, etc). But these ones only have 2 things: tortilla and meat.  The tortillas are made to order! There is a worker making tortillas constantly throughout the day, rolling, pressing, and grilling tortillas to perfection.  Next, the meat is marinated delightfully. Had the chicken, steak, pork, and marinated pork. All divine and delicious in their own right.  As I was eating, I had the epiphany that tacos are not about toppings (e.g. slaw, salsa, sour cream, guacamole, etc.). The foundation is a great homemade tortilla and delicious meat. This taco accentuated this ideal and for that reason is truly THE BEST taco I've ever had.

Simply the best donuts in town. Amazing and unique flavors (Creaminal Thai Tea Oreo, churro, Passion Fruit, matcha cheesecake) and I really nice tender but chewy dough. Expertly made, beautifully presented, incredible taste. Everyone needs to try this. Really like that you get one of each flavor.

Super cute little Danish place in the heart if downtown Santa Barbara! The decor inside reminds me of the nearby town: Solvang. We just stopped in for a bakery item, but we were really intrigued by the menu and would have loved to stay for a meal! The bakery items are very traditional. We ordered a Danish layer cake. It was moist, rich, and sweet!

If you go to milk & honey be sure to ask for Emma as your server!! She goes above and beyond to make you feel welcome and ensures you try all the best things on the menu..hard to say what is the best because it's all truly delicious. Thank you Emma for making our experience wonderful!

Mission impossible: find a pastry that does NOT contain any dairy, nuts, or chocolate! I tried Lilac, Savoy, AND Green Table thinking that their health conscious ways would provide the best variety in this and alas, struck out all 3 times. Luckily, the last stop was Savoy, because on the walk back to my car, I decided since Andersen's was right there, it wouldn't hurt to ask, AND JACKPOT!!!! The gentleman behind the counter was so helpful and sweet and boxed me up a lemon something (bottom right corner glass case). I've been here many times, always to pick up pastries to go and even at their busiest, I have always gotten such great service and their sweet treats NEVER disappoint. Their fruit tart and apple pie are my favorite. You also can't go wrong with their cheesecake bites.  Customer service: check! Knowledgeable on dietary restrictions: check! Sweet tooth fix: check!
So many organic options... yum acai bowls! Great friendly staff. Owner cares about hiring right people. One of few high quality food on this street. Rest seem more commercial.
The food and staff are great. Got takeout, had the Swedish Meatballs with perfect mashed potatoes and gravy. I'd take a shot of that gravy if no one was watching.

Cocktails anyone? Joe's is known for stiff drinks. Yet, this was my first time actually dining and eating food here. I ordered the Meatball marinara sandwich and switched out the fries for mashed potatoes. Yum!  Savory Comfort Food after a night out on the town with friends. Surprised the service was so attentive and enjoyed the outdoor patio area on State Street under twinkling lights.  Delicious meal. Do plan for $25-$28 per entree and drinks are about $12-$15.  Meal portions were enough for leftovers to take home or Come hungry.

My girlfriend's and I date spot. Reservations make it easy to get a table even on the busier nights so you know you will be able to have a spot on the busier nights.  The waiters and waitresses, in my experience (on about 10 occasions that I've visited so far), have always been helpful and patient. Have a question about the special? Don't know which wine to order? Don't know what an ingredient is on the list? They will answer your question.  There is bread, olive oil and balsamic vinegar on the table for while you wait for the food.  I can only speak for about three dishes on the menu cause they are so delicious, it is hard to break away and try something else in the fear of it not being just as good:  - A mixed baby lettuce salad with shaved parmesan cheese in a light balsamic vinaigrette: Perfect little salad to go along with the main dish  - Homemade veal tortellini in a cream sauce with sliced ham: my absolute favorite dish here. It's delicious and filling.  - Ravioli stuffed with prime rib meat in a pink cream and tomato sauce: the meat is so tender in this it practically melts in your mouth. Another favorite of mine.  If you want to go Italian in Santa Barbara, this is my recommendation!

Yelp never fails. Absolutely fell in love with this place. Hands down some of the best Mexican food I've ever had. My boyfriend and I both ordered the huevos rancheros they were fantastic. The eggs were cooked for to perfection and love the ranchero sauce. We also got chips and they have a salsa bar. The salsas were all amazing in fact I think they should bottle them and sell them like hot sauce. I would definitely buy them. Anyhow we sat on the patio and enjoyed the perfect weather with our pup Hey look. This restaurant was everything we were looking for good food, fast polite service and an outdoor patio that was dog friendly. When we're in the area will definitely be back. If I could make a suggestion the only thing would be to have umbrellas on the patio because it did start to get a little bit warm in the Sun.

Hate to do this to this beautiful golf course but I wouldn't feel right giving any kind of positive review here.  Just played here on Saturday, Feb. 15th and the condition was horrendous!  I was informed that the course hadn't been watered in years with the exception of the greens.  Fairways were basically all just dirt with very few signs of any grass remaining.  The severe drought in California has basically ruined this gem of a golf course.  Place is a beautiful setting for golf and it is really sad to see this happen here.  My prediction is that this course will have to close soon as I don't see how they can continue operating with no water.  Even the water features were about 2/3 empty and the course was almost unplayable with the severe dirt/tight lies and roll out on all the shots.  I was still charged the ridiculous price of 75.00 to play this course.  I felt that I really got ripped off here because there was no consideration for the extremely poor playing conditions and nowhere on the website was there any indication that this course was in the shape that it was.  By the time you arrive there are no other courses within 1/2 hr of here so they know that if you want to play golf that you will have to just pay the price and play here.  Shame on Rancho San Marcos for trying to dupe their customers and make them pay full price here!  This used to be one of my favorite tracks but I will not be returning here unless they somehow miraculously find a water source!

I recently dined at Norton's after watching the episode on Diner and Drive-in's.  Upon entering, I noticed the wonderful aromas of food. The staff was friendly and I was able to find seating quickly. I tried the 'Mom's PLT' upon the staff's recommendation as most popular item.  It was excellent and exactly as described. It has crispy pastrami, lettuce, tomatoes and chipotle mayo on sourdough bread. The sandwich was prepared fresh on the grill and it as served with fresh pickles. Other patrons had the pastrami hotdog and they looked excellent. I will be stopping by again in the near future. There can also be a long line, as a large crowd gathered a few minutes after I arrived.

Very large local roasting company which I love, right off the main strip, good prices, great coffee, and facility. Cool how they actually are roasting coffee inside, they also have a coffee bar, get a coffee select your own off the bar. Employees where nice, like i said the choice of coffee is awesome and all the ones I had that week where on point. Nice indoor and outdoor sitting area, also you can purchase a numerous amount of there merchandise. They actually have 1 free refill if your drinking there within the hour, pretty much awesome. Support local stay away from those chains!

The service from Brad was great, drinks were perfect. The food was not the quality we were expecting. The steaks was seasoned and cooked properly, the mashed potatoes we dry and lumpy. The pork was over cooked, they asked how I wanted it, asked for med, came out well. Got a side of the bourbon sauce to try as well and it almost tasted like a teriyaki sauce. For what we paid for dinner expected a little more on food quality side.  Service 5 Food 2

A finally tuned well-oiled machine. This restaurant should be a model for restaurants. From the staff to the food to the ambience, the Lure never disappoints to deliver a sensational experience. Even my worst experience after 15+ visits has been a 7.5 out of 10. Average is at least a 9. It's remarkably good and well managed.

My family came to the Lark for Mother's Day Breakfast and all were looking forward to having a delicious meal. Unfortunately, the experience left much to be desired...  Specifically, our waitress did everything she could to avoid eye contact with anybody that needed something in our area. For example, if you wanted to get her attention that you were ready to order, she would see that you needed something from her and immediately avert her eyes.  I watched this happen to several other diners too and eventually saw that folks were getting up to walk over to her to ask for their check and to place their orders.   What a disappointing way to spend a holiday breakfast, following the waitress around on foot in order to get a refill, to place an order, and to get the bill. The food was good, but honestly not worth all the walking and the experience was overshadowed by their staff's buffoonery.  What a disappointment.

There's much I like about Bettina, namely the food and drinks, but sadly, it's the service that leaves a bad taste. I have experienced two friendly and helpful servers (and pretty sure one of them unfortunately no longer works there), but there are others here with real attitudes. I get it... we all have bad days here and there, which is why I've reserved judgement until now. Hey girls, it's the SERVICE industry. If you don't like people or have things to complain about with one another in front of customers, find a different line of work.

Lil bit of everything here. Wine, beer, food. Wine: enormous list for bottles, but not too many options for glasses. A small selection but appreciated variety of beers. Food list is pretty fancy, no bar food here, rather hoity-toity actually. I had my eye on the brownie instead.  Love the atmosphere, casual but sexy swanky at night. Made for an awesomely intimate date night. Service was outstanding.  Bartender was very attentive, giving us lots of details on their offerings, and providing a taste of several that caught our interest. We moved away from the bar into a corner but she and her counter part both made time to walk over and check on us. Definitely will be a regular spot.

Lilac Patisserie is located on State Street and is conveniently open early! Great for breakfasts.  Their Quiche Loraine is fantastic! The eggs are cooked perfectly; it is fluffy and not soggy. The pastry is flaky and buttery. This came with a salad with a very good vinaigrette.  The Cherry Scone is so very good too. Not dry.
best vegan restaurant in town  their grilled 'cheese' is to DIE for and goes great with the California salad. Highly recommend trying everything on their menu at least once!

Pizza Rev opened today with free pizza coupons for everyone. Although the line was long and it took about half an hour to order, the pizza was worth the wait.  The staff was friendly and helpful, the free choice of fresh ingredients and the large meat selection are only a few good things to name. It got personable as the staff asked for your name and addressed you with it during the process of making your pizza. The pizza itself was a delicious thin crust with all the ingredients you like. All in all a great experience. I had PizzaRev down in LA before, but this one was definitely the best so far. Keep up the great work!

We will not be returning. Unoriginal selection of beers paired with mediocre, small portioned but pricey food options? No thanks. We got the sweet potato fries which tasted like the freezer variety, and a bland white pizza.   It wasn't an awful experience, but it definitely wasn't worth the price. Having to find the menus, wait in line and order at the front was a big and confusing downside.   I wish we had gone back to Santa Barbara Brewing Company instead. We'll go there or try out Hollister Brew Company next time instead.

Best place ever!! I love these shelters which are figuring out new ways to get enough revenue in order to keep the doors open! The cats are so friendly and there's always at least one that wants pet, play, or food. And if you fall in love with one you can adopt it right away!
Best bakery in town huge servings. Small coffee is a large coffee anywhere else. Olalaberry scone, WOW! Danish was huge!

3.5 stars  This is a fairly new food joint in Santa Barbara. The set up is clean and modern. The food is a bit expensive. The Udon Carbonara was pretty good. I'd pass on the curry yakisoba. The shrimp chips has potential. My bag had some chips not fully cooked/expanded. The secret bao that day was ok. I actually liked the pb&j oddly enough! I wanted to love the place but to me, it did fall a little short

We had an amazing time at this restaurant. The wait time was long, but that doesn't really matter. You are able to go walk on the beach and enjoy the beautiful California coast.  Once we got seated, the service was good, drinks great and food amazing. We went for breakfast and enjoyed our anniversary. We will be back soon for dinner and watch the sunset.

We got orange chicken and beef chow mein delivered and it was DELICIOUS!! And reasonably priced with generous portions! $33 total for 3 items (including the rice that came with the food) which isn't bad at all for a entire full dinner for 2 people. Me and my boyfriend even had leftovers, there was easily enough food for 3-4 people. This was our first time trying this place and we will definitely order and get delivery again! (And again and again and again)

This place came recommended to me from a fellow Los Angelean, and she was right.  This place is better than Jamba Juice.  We tried the peanut butter chocolate smoothie and I am beyond hooked.  It was so creamy and peanut buttery.  We added a boost to it.  I am very eager to try more of their flavors.    The size is good, and the price point was very reasonable.  My biggest complaint about Jamba Juice is that they keep increasing their prices.  I'm not sure that the value is really there anymore for JJ.  I see why Blenders is all over SB.  I'll be visiting again very soon, Blenders!!

We tried Oku last night and it was delicious! The sake mule (sake instead of vodka based Moscow Mule) was refreshing and tasty. We shared a variety of dishes and everything was spot on, especially the spicy calamari, sashimi tacos, short ribs, and mushroom salad.   It's also a beautiful space. We were downstairs but the upstairs ocean view is even better. We'll be back.
Good place, a little overpriced, but what do you expect from a burger joint near state st.
Delicious taco spot in SB. The avocado salsa is really special. It is so delicious. I enjoyed the carne asada tacos but the shrimp and fish tacos were way to bland. The staff is nice.
What a sweet crew and nice space.  I will become a regular here :).  Nice food, wonderful space, yummy beer choices!
The BEST Swedish meatballs I've had outside of Sweden!!! Very nice, clean fun food...EXCELLANT!!
It's not that I didn't love my meal here, it's just... the service was not what I had in mind.   customers remember the little things, right?

We just went here for the first time today and it was great! We were walking by and decided to stop for lunch. I got the lunch special salad which had roasted beets, goat cheese, candied pecans and lemon vinaigrette. It was $10 and so delicious. My boyfriend got the burger with bacon on it and he really enjoyed his too. Our lunch total was around $23. The decor is cool and we enjoyed spending time here. We would recommend this place.

This was my go to spot for the longest time for a peanut butter smoothie that'll basically replace my lunch. And whenever I ordered this, I'd specifically ask for soy blend not because I'm better than people it's literally because I'm lactose intolerant and don't want to spend hours of my life on the toilet. For some reason or another that didn't get through any of y'all today cause now I'm writing this as my butthole is burning due to how many times I've had to frequent the toilet. Please get your lives together.

Do yourself a favor , and avoid this location at all costs. My wife and I have been coming to this location for years, and up until today, it has been very good . There have been changes in the food quality for the worse. First there were bugs on the fruit of my wife's waffle when served, which was sent back. Second, most of the food served , is obviously from a different vendor than in the past, the potatoes , sausage, and biscuit were dry and flavorless. Third, the ' Flat Whites' we ordered, were bitter, and not good. Fourth, I did speak with the on duty manager, Aaron, who apologized, did not offer a refund, which would have been appropriate, however; he did give me a free cup of coffee, which I threw out, because it tasted bitter. There you have it. Sad, we were beyond disappointed .
Fun, spacious, bright and airy inside.  Cappuccino was terrific and I'll be back to try the 'goodies' and lunch when I'm back in town.  Recommended.

Alito's atmosphere is lovely and airy. Moorish details, a soaring ceiling and gold leaf paint details romanced the eye. We went there with friends and had a memorable night. We all loved the food and gave each other bites.  I've had a lot of shoe leather, thin beef in my day at Mexican restaurants. Their thin beef was succulent and flavorful. The chicken enchilada was so packed with different flavors and textures I didn't share. The nopales and peppers veg on my plate were cooked just right and in some yummy herbs and spices. The corn tortillas were clearly made by hand and wonderfully fresh. Eduardo, the chef, came by our table and answered questions about their cuisine and was very charming. This is the upscale Mexican place to go for special occasions and to impress without breaking the bank. I certainly will not be waiting for a special occasion to go back, because I'd like to taste my way through the menu. Delish deserts too.

We ordered through Uber eats. The Mr B salad ($20) and the beef shawarma.  The salad was as if it was a side salad. There was no option to add meat. Very disappointed. The beef shawarma was good, but seemed light on the beef. We may try again as a dine in, but will find another place for that type of food.

This is a fun place for cheese and wine. They have Wine samples and a fan static cheese plate appetizer that includes blue cheese, Brie, and boscottis. Sit outside of you can to enjoy the nightlife as it happens. Sitting outside watching people walk by is so entertaining

BEST HAPPY HOUR IN SANTA BARBARA!!! the spicy ceviche is worth having because they give you such a good amount fir it being an appetizer! And I highly recommend having all of their oysters!!! A place I would highly recommend checking out for happy hour!
Ordered pepperoni pizza. Mozzarella sticks, garden salad and drinks.   Pizza 7/10 tooooo greasy Mozzarella sticks 4/10 breading is blahhh Garden salad 10/10 great balsamic dressing OVERALL 3.75 stars
Amazing service.  Great Mongolian beef, and orange peel chicken.  Cannot beat the service.

This place is too dangerous to be this close to my home. Come on people... Delicious crust and wonderfully full flavored pizza 5 MIN from home? No mercy. Anyway, I judge a pizza place by their simplest version which is to say a margarita pizza. If you can impart enough flavor to something that simple through sauce and crust then any other topping is just bonus. Not that all the pizzas have sauce but you know what I mean. Arancini and salads are regular orders too. Cocktails are great, as well, and the bartender is helpful and talented. We try to pace ourselves  because I could eat here every day. The interior is cheerful and a breath of fresh air for Santa Barbara.

UGH ! ordered take out from the mesa location- you know when you put leftover pizza in the fridge and you can smell it?- no smells from here tasted like- uninspired garbage- at best-  ick! nothing special!

Mmm I gave into my cravings once again and visited IHOP tonight. I had been thinking about the Harvest Grain Pancakes and they did not disappoint. The service was also wonderful (I was nervous reading all of the negative reviews about the service). The plate was delivered in a timely manner and the pancakes were nice and hot. Thank you for the nostalgic experience!

This place is healthy, fresh & tasty and great for pick up! They were really friendly and speedy with my order. My husband got the kale burger and I got the Falla me to Heaven salad, plus sweet potato fries. So good!

The $5.00 off $25.00 coupon is amazing. I love to shop Gelson's for that reason and for many other reasons: *Parking is easy.  *Covid (writing this 12/19/20) is taken very seriously, with carts sanitized and line etiquette strictly observed, inside and out. *Always fun to try new things. I love the organic tortilla section and the terrific selection of kombucha. Great place to buy chips, too------SO much variety. *The produce is unfailingly good. Some things are out of my price range (only organic will do for me), but whenever I get bananas, apples, other fruit, lettuce, kale, broccolini------it's always super fresh and tasty, high quality produce. *Occasionally there's a free tasting of local wines and such. I'm not a drinker, but it's still fun every great once in a while to try fine wines/tequila. *The cashiers are THE BEST. Five Star Staff all the way.  In my many years of shopping at Gelson's I only ever came across one semi-surly cashier. 'She won't be here long,' I thought to myself. Never saw her there again. Gelson's appears to pride itself on happy, friendly employees.  Truly great people. *You can sometimes find things at Gelson's that you can't find anywhere else.  I love to browse the ice cream freezer, just to see what's new. *Best Part Is: The store got some new plastic carts-----I always hated bumping along on those tiles, with my metal cart banging away.  Too noisy. The new carts are much quieter and glide like limo-quality shopping carts.

I've been coming here with my family for the last 25 years, and it is great to know this place hasn't changed. They have the best service and friendly staff. The food is delicious as ever, large portions and a huge variety. Prices are excellent, and their lunch specials are the best. You can't go wrong with the cashew nut chicken, pra yam, and pad thai. The best Thai Iced Teas in town. This place holds so many memories for me, and I'm happy to call this the best Thai restaurant in SB :)

The hubby loved the concept and I loved the pricing. It's subway for pizza. You choose your pizza then the toppings and it's all ready for you freshly baked in a wood oven in 5 minutes for under $10.   I ordered the pesto pizza with tomatoes and artichokes (next time I will probably add some sort of meat). My hubby ordered the margherita with sausage.   The pizzas were ready in about 5 minutes time and there were plenty of places to sit down. We loved that this place was family friendly but also great for couples, friends, etc.   They serve beer, wine, and a pretty nifty soda machine that has a gazillion options.   Great option for college students or young families, everyone gets exactly what they want. If you are expecting gourmet pizza this is not, but for the price, it's well worth a visit.

great burgers, great kale salad, great everything really.  love this place! Attentive service, fast and efficient from local Santa Barbara kids, consistent in every way, we go often and its always fun. Its a little bit of a wait, because they don't take reservations and the tables aren't large so its better to be on the smaller side

Only been once. Main courses wagu beef and roasted chicken were excellent.  Salad and side dishes okay - not 5 star.  Bummer our intimate dinner was marred by next-door table of 6 that included a loud buffoon.
Wow! Food was covered in hot oil, nothing was delicate.  If you like (fat), (hot oil), this is an incredible place.

I LOVE Thai food! And my recent order of Green Curry chicken was delightful. BUT, and it's a big but and I cannot lie, the three others in my party all had screw-ups with their order to the point that they would have been returned if we weren't all starving and lacked the desire to spend gas and time to return to the restaurant. COVID SUCKS!! If it wasn't for COVID, this expensive order would have been returned.    Given this experience, I probably won't order again from TAP until indoor dining returns and I can return food.  I would comment that it's over priced, but I recently moved back from 20 years in LA, so I was spoiled (the competition for Thai in LA wouldn't support these prices). But really, the price is based on what the market will bear, and I haven't had any better or any worse Thai food in Santa Barbara.  TO TAP: I would suggest having a 'heat' scale based on 1-10. A '1' you could serve a baby. A '5' is average Thai spicy (not American spicy), and 10 is the hottest you can serve without violating laws or being required to acquire additional insurance. Add a sentence 'we really don't suggest going above a 4 unless you're familiar with really authentic Thai' or something similar  I had a 7 one time based on a similar scale (in Vegas). I ended up having to see a Urologist the next day because what goes in must come out, but I felt blissfully enlightened (if also on fire) while I ate it.

Anyone who knows me knows that this is one of my favorite restaurants of all time.  I ALWAYS have to eat here (or their sister restaurant Santo Mezcal) whenever I am in Santa Barbara.  I always get the Agaves burritio which comes with shrimp and halibut with the most delicious chipotle sauce.  Really, everything on their menu is delicious and fresh.  The rice and beans are also delicious.  Los Agaves has the most epic salsa bar.  I always get one of each.  Literally every single one is good.  I love that they give you a heaping basket of tortilla chips to enjoy while you wait for your food.  They have a parking lot behind the restaurant which is very convenient!

Best service!!!! Love this place, the food is great and the service amazing. All the staff are always so on point, always asking if you need anything, great salsa bar and the don't charge you for chips, unlike los arroyos in Montecito which has horrible service, is overpriced and has the worst staff and service possible in the food industry. I'm so grateful for this restaurant!!! We appreciate you and your staff!! Thank you!!!

When my friend who is a local Santa Barbaran said this place had the best Mexican food, I was skeptical.  I mean, just in Santa Barbara alone, you have iconic hole in the wall's like Lilly's and la super rica.  And I get pissed if I pay more than $10.  But I paid $13.25 for the tres combination, and it was the best Mexican food I've ever had, and with huge portions.  Must-go for a Gaucho who visits often.

My husband brought me home a calzone at lunch.  The cheese inside was chewy, and the crust didnt seem like it was cooked all the way.  I only ate about 1/4 of it and gave up.  With so many pizza options, I wont be back.

My original plan was to make the short drive over to the Biltmore for Super Bowl Sunday evening; enjoy a soy matcha latte, and the ammmaze Super Bowl commercials: ie, I forever adore Doritos, have ever since the Goat spot.  And Shakira+JLo, te adoro.  I stayed for a full dinner which was a tasty vege burger and Cesar salad.  Ohhhh and finger foods. The TY bar has a limited menu for vege plates, but my server was wonderfully kind and brought the full dinner menu+room service menu as well.  Three menus to choose from was fun! I appreciated the consideration for my specific diet choices, ya gotta live sustainably.  Additionally, there was an evening breeze sweeping in from the out-door patio and the bar staff lent me a cozy pashmina scarf.  Sooo sweett, really.  It made Super Bowl Sunday memorable and elegant.
These cupcakes are a treat! Give 'em a try if you're into that sort of thing.

Red Lobster for tourists! We're on vacation with money to spend to create a great memory tonight! We were seated at a very small table in the back near the kitchen, after passing several other open tables. We were looking forward to fresh seafood since we were at a spot next to the ocean - but they were out of SEVERAL seafood entrees - and ended up ordering 'breakfast' burgers. Instead of the 'over easy' egg on the burger, we asked that it be cooked over hard. Whoops, NO modifications, said the waitress at our table in the back of the restaurant next to the kitchen. ('No soup for you!' - Soup Nazi, Jerry Seinfeld.) Thankfully, there are many other better-rated restaurants in the area with clean menus, better seating and who let you modify your order. Start at those first.
Delicious and service is on point. Also love the ambiance, great place for fresh seafood. I will definitely be back to try happy hour menu!

3rd strike... I arrived at 8:20pm, after their rush hour, but the waiter was still very much in a rush. To be fair, he was the only one serving the six occupied tables, but hectic and the noise coming with it kill this place's atmosphere. He brought the wine list too late, forgot (again) to ask whether I wanted bread, and seemed to drop the dishes on my table while flying by. His courtesy was cold and formal - what a contrast to the previous crew! The Andalucian soup lacked taste, the Florentine pasta too (and lukewarm pasta and sauce are not my favorites), but the duck leg was very tender and juicy. After another mediocre chocolate cake desert (this time not dry, but bland, and the 'strawberries' were about 1/4 of one exemplar), I sadly give up on my favorite restaurant in town. Luckily, The Little Door has finally reopened and appears to be doing well.

I have dined at this location several times and love the atmosphere! My favorite burger is the Fresno Fig Burger and you must go there for 'Hoppy Hour'! They have $2 off the beers and great deals on appetizers. I have had the same servers several times, I think it's Danielle and Kelly? They are great! The staff is energetic and welcoming. Definitely check out Eureka Santa Barbara!
Delicious food. Awesome service. We had the fried asparagus spears, brussel sprouts, and prime rib. The prime rib was lean with very minimal fat. We'll be back for sure!

Food: Probably not the best mediterranean food in the world, but pretty good for SB. However, I do like their falafels.  Ambience: I would never sit at one of the indoor tables, the outside is too pretty! Especially at night with all the lights on. I love getting a table with the fire pit in the center too.   Additional: If you're into hookah, this is one of the few spots in town that has it!

We stopped by on Mother's Day with no reservation and got seated at one of the last tables they had available! Mothers got a free mimosa glass , which my mom enjoyed! The service was awesome! We got greeted by a friendly man who gave my dog a treat and told us he'd be right with us. They were busy and still managed to be friendly and accommodating.   My mom ordered their salmon lunch plate (4oz) and I ordered their vegetable pizza with no cheese to make it vegan. They offer vegan cheese for an extra $2 if you want it. The salmon plate was a great portion. The rice and veggies added not only to their presentation, but it was a perfect pairing to the grilled salmon.   One of the employees came around and gave out roses to all the mothers. It was an extra sweet gesture that made the afternoon that much better. Everything was delicious and the overall experience was one if the best we've had, we will definitely be back!
It's not right to sell such overcooked Flan like this for $10  It tasted like sweet scrambled eggs. Still giving 3 stars because everything else I've eaten here over the years has been quite good.

Looking for that elegant lunch location? Bella Vista fits the bill. In keeping with the Four Seasons sense of style, and high level of service and attention, and pair this with excellent food and atmosphere, and you have an amazing meal waiting for you. We had to start the cold pea-gazpacho and an appetizer of the truffle fries and sweet potato fries, then had an amazing salad and a small pizza. All the while we had very attentive service and the VIEW! The Bella Vista really has an amazing view of the ocean and Butterfly beach. A special shout-out to the valet parking staff, they were amazing and accommodating in every way.  We will definitely go back.

I'm from Southeast Asia, and this type of food is my roots.  I've been curious to try Empty Bowl in the past, but the thing that held me back each time was the price of the items on the menu.  However, I finally decided to give in and give it a try, and this was my experience:  I ordered the Bangkok Street Noodles.  The ingredients were fresh enough and the spice rack was fresh as well.  The flavors shined with citrus and garlic and the noodles tender.  The drawback, though, is that these are all things that are the norm at any place. So, not sure if this is a huge plus...which brings me to my overall synopsis of this joint:  The food here is good in flavor, but lacks what Asian food is all about, which is CHEAP, flavorful and large portions.  The Empty Bowl has the flavor down, but lacks the two other main points.  Therefore, I'm giving it 2 stars.  I realize SB is not cheap to run a business, but come on peoples!
Good food. Good location. HORRENDOUS service.

Love this place! My family has been there 3 times in the last week. Always the best service and the food is delicious! I really like the Coke Freestyle so I can choose whatever soda I'm in the mood for. I usually get the Pasta Combo with a Caesar Salad. Last night while we were eating we had a great conversation with the owner Albert. He's a really great guy and the place has that classic Italian Family Restaurant feel. Would recommend it 100%!

Wow oh wow!!!  I am in love with this place.  What a fantastic dining experience from the get go.  This was our first time to SYR and found the grounds absolutely beautiful and tranquil.  Stonehouse is a lovely setting, though our table wasn't out on the terrace.  Nonetheless, inside was very comfy and elegant.  Service was impeccable.  The food was divine - we had the black cod and duck, along with the signature mashed potatoes and broccolini.  Dessert was freshly made ice cream (mint chip, espresso and vanilla bean), which was so flavorful, and the grand marnier soufflé - so so amazing.    Treat yourself and dine here, but be prepared for the $$$$ prices.  So worth it...bon appetit!

We came here for the first time on a Saturday Morning, it was busy but no wait time. Menu has all the American breakfast items as well as some Mexican choices such as Huevos Rancheros and Chilaquiles. We ordered and played mortal Kombat! Great for kids or adults to spend time waiting for their food. Who doesn't love Video games! Each game is .50 cents and the food came out within about 15 mins.     Service and food were both great and had good portions. They also have a loyalty program where after 4 visits you get a free coffee and after 8 you get a free entrée. When we went to pay I expressed how great the food and service were and how we would definitely be back. The person at the cash register gave me a coupon for a free peanut butter and jelly French toast so of course we had to go back the next day, Sunday when we went it wasn't as busy service and food were still great. No complaints at all.

Sharkeez is one of my favorite bars in Santa Barbara! The bartenders are fun, their bottomless mimosas and Bloody Mary bar is awesome...and they always have good music playing. Also one of the best places in SB to watch any sporting event...lots of tv's and the crowd is awesome. Always a fun time!!! Ask frank to flare!!!
Enjoyed chile rellenos, cheese enchiladas and margaritas all outdoors on their lovely patio.

Came here for brunch with my friend this morning and we each ordered the Eggs Norwegian and the Eggs Florentine respectively.  The Hollandaise sauce was incredible!  Absolutely delicious with a great tang, consistency and low on salt.  We both just ate in silence because we were enjoying it so much and later joked about the silence of good food.  My Eggs Norwegian were perfectly done with the Hollandaise sauce pairing perfectly with the slight saltiness of the smoked salmon.  The English Muffin was even perfectly toasted so that it was still easy to cut through while providing a perfect counterbalance to the softness of the poached egg and the smoked salmon along with the creaminess of that Hollandaise sauce.  The fruit salad on the side was also fresh and perfectly ripe.  Would definitely come again as they made one of my favorite meals perfectly yummy!

Delicious authentic tasting Mexican food. I tried the Asada burrito and it is one of the best I've ever had. The beans and rice are cooked perfectly and the flavor is exactly what I'm always looking for.

One of the best meals I've had! The restaurant was very simple and had a cozy feel to it. I imagined it to be a lot bigger, but it was just perfect. It was good we had reservations, since there was a wait after we were seated. We were seated inside near the patio doors (which were open), and it was SOOOO hot! It appears that they do not turn on the air conditioner in there, so I was eating my delicious meal in the humidity and heat of the Santa Barbara weather on what felt like a 90 degree night!  The waiter was wonderful, and he made great recommendations and gave us a little history about the place. I was not aware that this was the very first of the Bouchon restaurants, and not the one in Yountville. Surprisingly, the owner of the restaurants had served us our salads and our main entrees! I felt so honored after knowing that; I seriously wanted to take a picture with him!  All of their food is made from locally grown farms, and all of their wine is from Santa Barbara; It's just a perfect restaurant to get all the flavors of S.B. in one place! We ordered their Special salad of the night which included walnuts, figs, blue cheese for an appetizer, and it was definitely good enough to share ($12). SOOOO GOOD! One of the best gourmet salads I've had yet! As for my main dish, I heard that their game meats were superb, so I ordered their famous Duck (Bourbon & Maple-Glazed California Duck Breast: duck confit, thyme-infused jus, succotash of corn, fava beans, applewood-smoked bacon and Windrose Farms butternut squash)! OOOOOOHHH! It was VERY VERY VERY GOOD! The first few bites of the sliced, succulent duck just made me want to say 'YUMMM!' every time! I had also ordered a red wine, which was suggested by our waiter but I had forgotten the name of it, but it was very good with the duck. My boyfriend ordered the Pan Seared Pacific Scallops (saffron & fine herb risotto, market ratatouille, rustic sweet basil pesto and organic baby watercress). It was also VERY DELICIOUS!
