Again, I had forgotten the name of the SB white wine that was served with his dish, but it was also well paired.  For dessert, we ordered their blueberry upside down cake with lemon zest ice cream! Again, it was DELICIOUS!   The food and service was just impeccable! Definitely worth every penny (To our surprise, everything cost about $105 including tax!)! Highly recommended!

This is a relatively new place on the Santa Barbara scene and it is a great addition. Started with the imaginatively prepared with the smoked beef tart mandarin, pistachio, coffee cured duck ( great flavors) Moved to the Acorn tagliatelle with wild mushroom, sage, carrizo . Then a perfectly done burger with pickled avocado, bacon, olive oil brioche. Finally capped it with a divine eucalyptus icecream with lavender. This is a beautiful restaurant with impeccable service. Trendy and fun and a must for foodies.

i dont even know where to start here. we went there tonight for dinner and it was absolutely horrible. i ordered chicken and i assumed that since i work at a restaurant that serves real chicken this restaurant would do the same, nope! what they brought out looked like a frozen chicken patty and tasted like it had just been microwaved. the worst thing was it was covered in a sauce that tasted just like crazy sauce at little ceasers pizza. my girlfriend had some shrimp palermo that was worse. i dont know what happened to thosde shrimp between the ocean and the restaurant but it was as chewy as bubble gum and wrapped in ham i think, but we still dont know. anyways there is a reason that they dont make shrimp bubble gum. i would have thought that a rsetaraunt that was ten blocks from the beach would have decent shrimp, nope!  this place was a huge dissapointment and i shall not return
One of our frequent haunts. My wife's favorite place in town. Good food, great service, fun location, all in all, you won't go wrong here.

4.5-4.75 on Yelp's average, have to agree and say that's pretty god damn accurate. I've been here MULTIPLE times and they more than likely always deliver. Appetizers that are mustes; deviled eggs (best in Santa Barbara in my opinion) and the Blistered Shishito Peppers, better be lucky to get the right blist ;). Entrees are always a definite treat. Everything stands out and should be tried with no hesitation. Sundays are the best for specials, slow roasted prime rib for an unbeatable price of just f*in pay it. Drinks are made adequately strong and tasty. Dessert ends the night with a lil mystical adventure within your tastebuds wanting more. Finch and Fork is definitely a five star in my books! Never had any issues whatsoever all times I've been here. Always a memorable fantastic flavorful experience! Shoutout to one of my favorite servers as well; Curtis! Hell of a guy and know's his shenans, will treat you with the best customs! Ask for him!

Obsessed with Acai bowls. There are tons of options and they are delicious. Unpretentious, chill vibe. Only feedback: it takes what feels like forever to get your order given the amount of staff they have on. Seriously... please add another blender and examine the efficiency of operations... it could be even more fantastic! That said, worth every penny, just don't go if you are in a rush.

The three stars are for the vibe and live music when they have it.  otherwise...2 stars.  First time, service good,  food could use some work.  Second time, service horrible, took over an hour to get drinks.  manager kept coming to offer us more excuses and apologize, By the time we finally got some service they told us, sorry the kitchen was closed...even though we ordered food over an hour prior.
best gluten-free pastries and baked goods i've ever had!

I love the small-plate concept of this place because it allows me to try many different things and not have to choose one giant-sized entree. I like the laid-back vibe of this place and it was great to have somewhere new and fun to eat that is kid-friendly. Service was great - we were surprised at how quickly our dishes came out after we ordered! The price is definitely reasonable depending on how much you want to eat!  Things we tried:  Tater tots! These were actually mashed potatoes fried in a crunchy coating. Not your traditional tots. The mix of creamy and crunchy textures as well as the slight onion-y flavor were spot on.   Clam chowder: Fresh and incredibly flavorful. Not super creamy like I usually prefer, but the rich, smoky, bacon-y flavor made up for it.   Truffle mac: A bit too rich for me, but my husband and daughter devoured it! It definitely has a noticeable truffle flavor, so if you like truffle, you will not be disappointed.   Turkey burger: The texture was moist and juicy like a burger, but the flavor reminded me of meatloaf. Not my favorite.   Cheeseburger: My husband loved it.   Apple Crisp: Took about 10 minutes after we ordered it (our server warned us that it would be a few minutes since they make it fresh). It was worth the wait.  Overall, I look forward to returning to The Pan to try  more things! It's a new favorite.
Great food, service and atmosphere. The food was amazing! Can't go wrong with any of the dishes. My favorite was the flat bread, grilled octopus and The seared scallops, gnocchi & soft boiled egg.

You don't want to end up at the ER eating uncooked chicken. If I could give a Zero star I would. My family and I were lovers of this place. We order often and even my boyfriend ordered constantly food from Kyle's for himself and his employees. Recently I got extremely sick eating a chicken sandwich and ending up going to the Doctor for extreme stomach pains due to uncooked meat. I kindly addressed this with the managers, though it took multiple attempts to reach anyone in charge. After expressing my experience, I was told to expect a call back and compensation for the bad experience, however I never received a call back from the manager I spoke to, or by anyone else. Since we really like this place, I decided to give them another try. I requested the meat to be well cooked to avoid another horrible experience, the manager made clear he knew who I was and the experience I had been through, however, he did not extend any courtesy only a smug and insincere assurance 'oh yeah i know you you are....yeah, we fixed the problem'. I am very disappointed of this place.   This will be my last visit with Kyle's kitchen.
Fresh seafood, great service, awesome location. A hidden gem.   Must try their poke and seaweed salad! Also, indulge yourself in their assortment of oyster choices..  I will be back!
Best donut shop in Santa Barbara hands down. Can't wait to visit tomorrow morning!! P.s. the drinks are amazing too. Oh and did I mention the location is just amazing?

Loved the atmosphere of this place and the coffee was amazing ! The music playing in the background was great. The staff were friendly. Had an overall good experience and it would probably be a good study place. There was parking right in front of the place so it was great !

I agree with the Yelpers Joaquin, D and Annebel G.  Cute place but the food didn't cut it with me, lack of flavor and that was a general consensus with everyone at out table.  It needed a pinch of salt or something. Oh well, I can say I've been here but that's about it.

Ate @ Miso Hungry last night w/ the GF.  4.5 stars for them! I'll give you the lowdown.  The good:  Service is excellent.  Staff is friendly and knowledgable.  Menu is simple w/ options for customizing. Prices are reasonable. Fair for SB  Quality of ingredients are fresh. (A must for raw fish right? Lol)  The Bad: They may be more than fair, although I'd like to see slightly larger portions for the proteins.  The Ugly: Nothing really ugly to speak of...other than finding parking when it's busy...lol  Miso Hungry is an awesome place to satisfy a sushi craving. They are fast, fresh, and friendly(3 F's are good in this case)!  My gf has already been coming here for her 'sushi burrito' for a while now. I will also be making my appearances on a more usual basis now that I've tasted their bites!
Love all the food here but the chicken curry noodle soup is one I crave. You can have any meat you want. But I just love this soup. Highly recommend this joint for dining!

Awesome location in the heart of Santa Barbara and perfect for a lunch that isn't to heavy.  I had the Hamperini which is ham and some pepperoncinis and their very own jalapeño spread.  So delicious.  I love the light spiciness of the sandwich and I love the grated Parmesan cheese on the exterior of the sandwich.   The staff was very friendly and my sandwich was so fresh.

Falafel was bomb! I loved the kale burger today with my gf and the ingredients were so fresh, bread was super soft. Really exciting menu and some amazing items that I am ready to try on my next visit!

We stopped by for lunch around 1pm on Saturday. It was raining so it was probably less busy than usual, but it wasn't overly busy and we were seated immediately. Our server was attentive and helpful with the menu. We both ordered the Cobb Salad, which was delicious. It usually comes with a light French dressing (which tastes lighter than typical French dressing), but I also tried it with Ranch dressing instead. Both versions are delicious and the portions are perfect for lunch. Water refill service was timely throughout our meal. Overall, a good experience. The food was good, the service was attentive, and the ambiance was comfortable. Also, the prices were reasonable, but a little on the high side for lunch ($13 for the Cobb Salad). We also used our Axxess card to get buy one, get one free, which helped.

Stopped here during happy hour for a bite to eat. Liked the atmosphere, and the fact that it was very cool (some of the tasting rooms get pretty warm in the afternoon). Tried the grapefruit jalapeno martini ($7) - tasty, the grilled asparagus (pretty good) and Sicilian oil w/ dipping bread (pretty good; was excepting something a little different). Met some nice folks there, too.   Came back again later at night, and it was not so good. Ordered the daily drink special, and the guy behind the bar first said he'd never heard of it, and then, when I told him it was their daily drink special, he threw 2 ingredients into a glass and passed it over (was not good; he winked at my friend, as if to say, 'let's see if she'll drink it; tasted it; was pretty bad; he comped it; we left. Overall, had a really good time the first time, not good the second. Based on the average of the 2 experiences, I'll rate it a 3.

I went to Arnoldi's last night (Sunday) with a group of people for a birthday celebration starting at 4:30. The place was not crowded. I stood at the bar and waited for the bartender to make eye contact. Finally, I asked him for a Moscow Mule when he got a chance. Ten minutes later I asked the waiter who approached if he could get the bartender to make me a drink. He said that there was only one bartender and he was busy. After about 15 minutes, I looked at the bartender again and he finally remembered that I had ordered a drink. He messed up on the drink orders of various other people in my group. He was also very rude. The waiter informed us that he could not give separate checks. The food took a long time to arrive. My $17 pasta was nothing special. After waiting a long time for someone to ask if I needed a box for leftovers, I finally brought my plate to the hostess, who boxed it up. One of my friends accurately summed it up, 'The best thing about Arnoldi's is its consistency. Consistently bad service and consistently mediocre food.' I do not plan on returning.
Thank you for being here with fresh, healthy, delicious choices in a sweet space. Can never pass on the kale wheat-berry or beet salad.

The fish here was really warm lol, definitely didn't taste very fresh. I think I was really craving sushi, so I decided to try it out, but the rolls made me want to puke.   +1 because conveniently located.
Fun atmosphere, great tapas and paella menu. amazing culinary artisry from the folks who brought us The Lark.  The meatballs were amazing, spice notes so complex they literally made a little tune.

LOVE! The fact that it's on the Mesa makes me automatically love it even more. Casual beachy vibe, sooo many fresh delicious burger topping options, and friendly staff! It's hard to find a spot where you can get a bomb vegan burger for a good price. The shoreline burger is my fav! I love the red pepper hummus, salsa verde, pickled red onions, and the sprouts. So different than anything I have ever tried, I get it every time now because I'm so obsessed with it! Their veggie patty has the same look and texture as 'real meat' and it is so full of flavor! Make sure to get sourdough bread if you're going for a vegan burger bc the brioche bun has egg.

This place was a little bit of a letdown. When I read that the name is a Public Market, I am expecting a large place with tons of vendors similar do Anaheim Packing District or Grand Central Market. However, the vendors at this Public Market was pretty limited and also a few closed down - probably due to COVID :(. I did try the Corazon Cocina spot and it was good!
Never disappointed in this restaurant. Good food. Fast and friendly service. Had 3 eggs scrambled with wheat toast and coffee. Reasonably priced. We come here each time we visit SB.

Super friendly, neighborhood feeling, cozy place to grab a beer and some pub food. The burgers and wings keep me coming back. The fries are crispy and not greasy! And they're not stingy with the ranch dressing. Plus the staff and kitchen aren't fussy about accommodating my special requests -- in fact the staff now even remembers my specific needs. I love the relaxed feeling here and the food is really solid for a pub menu.   I'm not a big beer fan but their selection is fantastic.  Kid friendly :-)  Give it a try!

What's not to love about being at the harbor viewing boats, paddle boarders, kayakers, the mountains and the ocean.  Add to it the variety of people watching and you've got a winning combo.  Of course, the food and drinks are delicious as well.  An oyster shooter w/vodka always gets things started off right, followed by a tasty Michelada and one of my favorite dishes Cioppino or the Salmon Salad.  You'll find me hear at least once a week, it's a vacation in my own backyard.

I felt like Rip Van Winkle as my daughter and I opened the door and walked into to this marvelous space. I have not been down to this part of State Street for some time and was astonished to see the completed Californian Hotel, event space, and this nice restaurant. Lower State Street has bloomed!  It wasn't busy and we had the luxury of studying the glass cases and menu. Soon we were getting helpful tips from Edwin. It's really all about Edwin. He was polite, helpful, and treated us as if we were the Hotel's favorite guests. After much deliberation, we were sitting at a high-top marble table sharing a generous slide of goat cheese cake. I had an expertly crafted machiato and Edwin brought us a chilled kraffe of water and two glasses. Yes the prices are a bit more than what we are used to paying at the local donut shop, but this is an environment worth the premium. I will be back with a newspaper for a lingering breakfast.   I hope I keep waking up to find more places like this one -- a place as delightful as a good dream.

I had their tortas al pastor today via curbside pickup. I thought it was great!!  A sandwich on a good soft bun, with marinated pork, refried beans, tomato, and avocado. Nice big size, and it was very tasty!  The pork was crispy in all the right places, nicely moist, and above all: flavorful! And there's a yellow-curb turn-out right in front of their shop, for the easiest curbside pickup ever (during the Covid situation). Ordered & paid on their website, so it was just an easy matter of pulling up in my car and having a very nice lady (in her mask) bringing the food right out to me thru the car window. Opening a restaurant at a time like this is probably tough, so I wanted to support them because of that, and was very pleased at how good it was.  I shall return for more!!!
Fuck the LA vs SD Mexican food debate. THIS is my favorite taco spot in all of California. God it's way too good. This place really just hits different.

My husband and I were enjoying a child-free afternoon wine tasting in the Funk Zone and after a couple of tastings we wanted to grab a bite to eat. We love downtown Santa Barbara because of all of the dining options - they're all great! We saw that Santo Mezcal was open and already bustling with guests seated both inside and outside on their patio.  We were seated immediately at their communal bar and the service was top notch - even during a busy lunchtime service. I love that their drink menu was just as large as their lunch menu and I could see behind the bar they were stocked with fine tequilas and other spirits. I ordered their version of a Bloody Mary but with Casa Migos, which was absolutely delicious! Spicy and flavorful! My husband ordered their Michelada which was also very good. We received our drinks quickly and more than one staff member asked us if we were okay or needed anything.  For food we opted to share plates and order a few appetizers. We picked their shrimp empanadas, ceviche, and taquitos and I have to say I don't think I've had that delicious of a meal - just being appetizers! The empanadas were made from corn, not flour, which was fluffy but crunchy on the outside. The shrimp filling was flavorful and everything you would want in a bite. Their ceviche was bright, flavorful, and enough to share with 2-3 people. I couldn't stop eating it! And lastly, their chicken taquitos were crunchy and paired well with the rest of our meal.  But, I do have to say how impressed I was with their selection of salsas, which was delivered to our table before our meal. I think one can tell a lot by a restaurant, especially one that focuses on Mexican cuisine, based on the flavor and freshness of the ingredients and these salsas were divine. There were three to choose from and each one stood on it's own and didn't fill us up - something most other chunky salsas risk doing.  Overall, we 100% enjoyed our dining experience and can't wait to return and bring friends. It's a lovely environment, their staff were professional and welcoming, service was efficient, and their restaurant was clean and a trendy Mexican vibe. Exactly what Santa Barbara needed for this area!  --I do have to say, in reference to the other Yelpers who gave this restaurant less than4-5 stars, I encourage everyone to look past the pretentiousness of some diners and consider Santo Mezcal if you're looking for an enjoyable experience, an assorted menu, and drinks that will impress. As a local, who arguably has a multitude of Mexican restaurants to choose from, the Santo Mezcal staff works hard to provide an unmatched experience and I am happy to say that it was noticed and we look forward to coming back time and time again.

The food at this amazing restaurant is just unbelievable. I loved every dish. Everything was perfectly done with lots of flavor. The steak is cooked to medium rare and the paella is fresh and flavorful. We ended the meal with churros that came with chocolate, raspberry and caramel sauce. The outdoor seating area is very nice and there was upbeat music playing.

I rarely give 5 stars for restaurants.  I've visited this location twice.  Go here if you have time to enjoy your food.  They do their service the French way, allowing you to enjoy your food rather than have one serving served on top of each other.  (In other words when you get your appetizer you can actually sit and enjoy it before the main meal is served).  I brought my parents here for their 46th year anniversary. The wait staff was courteous, the food was good and reasonably priced.  Don't go here if you are in a rush though, definitely to sit and enjoy company and relax.

This hole in the wall is better than it has any right to be.   It's got tacos. I think it has fish and chips as well. So not a huge variety of types of food. But it's got a lot of tacos. It's got a wall full of tacos. And they're not all traditional. Within the category of taco, they've got some artistic masterpieces.  The Gagnum style is a Korean short rib style taco... if there is such a thing. It's like crack. It's different. It's amazing. It's cravable. I will go just to have three of them.

Tried going to Carlitos across the street but they stopped serving at 830 even though it says they are open till nine. Must've been divine intervention. I had the best calamari at Opal that I've ever had in my life! The service was great the food was amazing and the ambience was just what I needed. I would definitely be going back to Opal soon

My experience here was great. I ordered the Tonkatsu, Salmon Sashimi (2 orders), and the Crunch roll, they were all on point.  Tonkatsu: like most reviewers had, tasted really authentic and was a good portion amount. It comes with rice, salad, and miso soup which was very enjoyable. Price was very fair  Salmon Sashimi: Sashimi is like the steak compared to fish. It is hard to make and prepare so they usually cost more than you would want to pay for. I had 4 piece which were two orders and it was exactly what I was hoping it to be.   Crunch Roll: I really liked how they fried this roll, the chef definitely knows how to provide authentic material. However I didn't like that the roll had carrots, I should have known earlier and told them to take it out.  Wasabi was also on point, which most japanese restaurants can't provide.  Overall Food quality:5/5 Overall Food price: 4/5 Customer Service: 5/5

Fantastic food and drinks at this spot! Also a pretty big plus that it's right by the water where you can see the beach and the wharf.  My favorite thing to get here is their grilled octopus plate. It's the perfect portion, the octopus is always perfectly cooked, and their rice here is something else. No clue what makes it taste so good but definitely worth ordering separately if you get something that doesn't come with the entree.   Only slight downside is that it's a pretty popular spot and can get busy. Sometimes the service can slow down, but nothing an order of chips and guacamole can't fix!

JUMPN JUMPN! This is a busy spot! We got lucky and caught a table as ppl were leaving not knowing we were supposed to get on the waiting list. After the waiter asked and we said no he didn't even trip he politely helped us and got us started with some cider and beer! They consistently check up on the tables to make sure you are being attended to! The cider i had tasted like apple juice! It was delicious! We also happened to grab a pretzel and enjoy that before heading out. It came with a white cheese sauce, dijon mustard and a sweet mustard (i think) anyway it was all so good! To the french handsome dude named matthew (mattu) thanks for your service!!

Mouth watering food and very resonable price in Santa Barbara. I love everything about this place. Very kind and friendly hospitality from the owners. They always treat you like a family member. Simply the best Indian food in the town. I give them all the stars.

There are only a few Indian restaurants that I frequent in Santa Barbara, and Flavor of India is definitely one of them. While the spice profile of their dishes isn't as complex as other restaurants, I find that Flavor of India is delicious in its simplicity. The restaurant is located on upper state and has it's own private parking lot behind the building.   The restaurant itself is very cozy - some of the booths are a little awkward to sit in, but the friendly service and food more than make up for it.   Service here has always been very friendly and when I visited with a friend that had a dairy allergy, the wait staff was incredibly attentive and conscientious of his allergy.   While I've been here many times and tried many things on their menu, my ALL-TIME favorite is the Chicken Korma. They just do Chicken Korma like nobody's business and I LOVE it! I would also recommend any of their lamb dishes - those always turn out well with a side of garlic naan. I'm getting hungry just thinking about that Chicken Korma - seriously, order it.

My wife loves loves this place. I don't disagree.   Pretty tasty options, a lot of pastries options.  The hostess/owner is super friendly and they draw a lot of regulars.   I've had the huevos, which are decent. I aslo have had the veggie omelet, which is nice. Neither really jump out at you, so if you're into lobster or crab, you might want to try those, but I haven't tried them myself yet.    I really like the outdoor seating and the pastries though.  Also, if you're just looking for coffee, you can join a different shorter line.

Pushy waiter that kinda got in the way. DIDN'T HAVE MY FREAKING BOBA... AGAIN!! Expensive meals (like the chicken) but don't come with rice. Dark kinda dirty. Seats are close together. Nice smelling though and a welcoming environment.
Drinks are great! Service is good but the food? lets just say it is not authentic. Torta was almost meatless the rice had a strange flavor to it.  Not the best. Needs improvement.

Definitely the best Thai/Asian food in Santa Barbara.  Friendly, fast service, but pricey given it's just countertop dining inside the Santa Barbara Public Market (appetizers start around $9 and entrees are $14 and up depending on protein choice). Let's hope the owners do convert this into an actual restaurant.  We sampled the summer rolls, roasted duck buns, Northern Thai Curry Noodle 'Khoa Soi,' and the Thai iced tea. I wouldn't hesitate to order any of these again in the future -- they were all very, very good. That said, I think each could be tweaked slightly for improvement. The flavor of the chicken in the soup was off, not necessarily in a bad way but in a free-range organic sort of way. It just didn't match/blend into the soup flavors all the way. Candied pork would work better than duck for the buns. And, finally, the sweetness and strength of the tea could be tamped down just slightly (the tea should still be quite strong and sweet).  Be aware that street parking in the area is extremely limited (lots of permit-only blocks and loading zones even on weekends) and that nearby parking lots (mostly connected to churches) charge $5 fees. If the SB Public Market wants to succeed and draw more traffic this is an issue.  The correct web site address for Empty Bowl is http://www.empty-bowl-noodle.com/

I want to give this place more stars, but I just cant. The setup of ordering at the counter then finding a table just DOESNT work when you dont have enough tables! We went last weekend, ordered for 4 people, then stood around waiting for an open table for at least 10 minutes. The kitchen let us know our food was ready, but we were still standing there with no place to sit! After another 5 or so minutes, they finally convinced us to sit outside even though it was FREEZING that night, and also sprinkling. The heaters helped, but the patio isnt covered so we got a little wet. The servers were very nice and tried to help as best they could, but it was not very pleasant just standing there waiting for a table, then sitting outside in the cold. The pulled chicken salad and the brussel sprouts were fantastic! The larb and the albacore on crispy rice were just ok. I liked the menu and the food, but they really need to work out a better system for seating people. It was really awkward and not a great overall experience. Or at least compensate your customers in some way when they are completely inconvenienced!

I love empanadas.  And, these don't disappoint.    From the fresh ingredients to the flavor punch, to the chimichurri sauce, these little dough homies are on point.  Authentic empanadas that taste just like rural San Rafael I had in Argentina.    Just a small operation out of a shared commercial kitchen, these guys put love and authenticity into their product.  The fact that they are not fried, makes a huge difference.    They are like mini scolding hot pot pies, with love.

The front room... 3 stars... the BACK ROOM.... 6 stars.  clean, level, spaced out billiards at brewery!?!?!?!?  great service, at what looks like a sports bar/pool hall?!?!?!?  Televisions placed high up but not so you crane your neck and encircling the entire room?!?!?!?  Yeah, this place has everything.  (oh YEAH, and the staff is not just really good, but kinda hot too. if you plan to play pool competitively, good LUCK keeping your eye on the cue ball)  ;-)

Food is good most of the time, but they are a total ripoff. The prices change, depending on who is working the register. (Sometimes a gallon of water is $2.49, sometimes it's $3.99). It's like they make it up as they go. The last guy working there charged me $1.00 a piece for the tiny little cups of salsa, and .50 cents for a paper cup. I live two blocks away and used to shop there several times a week, until now. Beware of price-gouging.

Best empanadas I've ever had! We ordered two for the table. I wished we had more! The lobster tacos were good but that Mexican rice was fantastic. Was a long wait for a Thursday evening. Limited and tight outdoor seating. The side salad was amazing, sweet & crisp.

This is our favorite sushi place. Not just in Santa Barbara - anywhere. Bummer they don't take reservations, but you can always put your name on the list and get a cocktail at good lion while you wait. Everything is fresh and delicious, especially the specialty sashimi's and anything with bluefin tuna.
I just want to give a shout out to Joe - he is always smiling and does a great job taking care of us.  Thanks, Joe!

Empty Bowl was delicious, and just like the name says, I emptied my bowl! I had the Northern Thai Curry and the Long Boat. The noodles in both were wonderful, and both bases had fantastic flavor. I love spice, and thought the curry was perfectly spiced at a medium heat level. But for my 'white-tongued' friends, it might be a little too much. I also found that the service was fantastic and friendly!
Finally tried this spot and no complaints here. Not something I would eat everyday but was tasty and filling. Plenty of toppings to choose from.

We had a quite disappointing experience at Bluewater Grill. The view and cocktails are great, that's why I gave 3 stars instead of 1, but a dining experience was absolutely not satisfying. We ordered a steamed mahi-mahi (it was important to get the fish steamed since I'm on a strict diet and can't eat anything grilled/sautéed) and a grilled swordfish with herb sauce and vegetables. Each entry costs about $25-30, so we expected a good quality dish. The mahi-mahi came grilled instead of steamed and waitress said that they steamed it first and then grilled (which doesn't make any sense so I'm pretty sure they just screwed up our order and lied to us, because I can distinguish between steamed and grilled fish, and that was grilled). The swordfish came without sauce and tasted over cooked. Overall impression that we went to a cheap fish fast food, not a restaurant. Definitely not worth spent money. If you want a good fish go to SB fish company, if you are up for the view and cocktails, then Bluewater Grill might be all right.

100% satisfied with Fernando's from start to finish - you know it's good when all your guests make multiple trips to snag a churro till they're gone!! We have done two orders now for 25 people that runs $125, was able to get a last minute order placed and they were more than accommodating even though they already had a busy day of orders! Will be back again and again - I love being the 'guy who has the churro hookup' :)

Apero has one of the best cocktails lists around in downtown SB and the bartenders are incredibly friendly! The bar itself isn't too big but it makes a cozy spot to interact with strangers and to make new friends.   I recommend the cantaloupe cocktail - they make it with their housemade cantaloupe puree and it's freaking amazing.

Meh.  Was excited about this place cuz I love sushi, and I love burritos... What better than a sushirrito?!?! I ordered the eel roll, all the ingredients on it were unique (seaweed salad, lemon aoli, etc) and the guy recommended adding crab. The roll is originally $10, but with the crab addition alone it bumped it up to $14, which I find very pricy. The seaweed was extremely tough and chewy. Every time I would bite I would be pulling at seaweed instead of having it be a clean bite. Almost like how cheese is super melted on a pizza and when you take a bite you have the string of cheese dangling.... Like that. Also, the roll was so loosely wrapped and everything fell out anyway.  Great concept, but poor execution. But honestly, it's like a Sushi Subway, so who knows how fresh the ingredients are anyway. They're also located in a weird spot so we'll see how long this place will last. I will not be coming back here again.

Charming bakery off the beaten path with delicious breads and pastries. Our family of four stopped here for breakfast and were pleasantly surprised. While seemingly a bit overpriced, the food does stand out so it may be worth it. Service was a little slow on a Monday morning. The kids loved their pastries and my eggs 'rose' (poached eggs on kalamata olive sourdough with artichoke spread) were extraordinary. My husband gobbled up his special cauliflower dish too. We visit SB at least once a year and we'll definitely return.

So we decided to go to the Cruisery because no other restaurants were open around 9:45 PM. My wife who got off work did not want to drive far and find something on the way home ended up here. We were both starving and ordered a lot of food considering they looked like tiny appetizers. Looking on yelp the tacos were bite-size that came in threes for one per order which cost about $14 for the fried fish taco and $16 for the steak tacos. Which is highly overpriced for tacos that did not come with anything else. We also got the wings which was $15 for 8 pieces that had nothing particularly special about them. The Juan's ceviche trio, comes with salmon, tuna, and shrimp. The salmon ceviche for me was too sweet for me because of the pineapples, the tuna ceviche was the best out of the three at least for me, the shrimp was so tiny and not enough(either put bigger shrimps or more small shrimps). When my wife brought the food home it came in so many bags, they literally wasted big containers for the tiny 2 bite tacos (one bite if you have a big mouth). One order of tacos came in a huge container, they could have put two orders of tacos in one container vs one container each per taco order. In my photo I combined the 2 taco orders of fried fish and steak into one container each. These tacos were one bite tacos for me...so $5 a bite. The fried fish taco was very chewy, hard and dry(I thought it was fried chicken). It was one of the worst fried fish tacos I've ever had. The steak taco was a little bit better but it had a lot of rosemary that kind of killed any other flavors. The guacamole was just smashed avocados with a lot of salt. I will say the fresh tortilla chips was actually the best. I most likely won't be coming here ever again for the food. I also told my wife to use the Axxess deal but apparently she told me that they don't take it but on the Axxess app it clearly says buy two entrées and get a pint of beer for free. Maybe if I just want to drink and have overpriced tacos as a snack I may come back. My wife sent me the photo of the receipt and was surprised by the entertainment district fee, they basically charge you 4.7% that's not part of your tip so just want to throw that out.

Luna Grill serves delicious Mediterranean style food, and the prices are reasonable, as you get a good sized portion of food for your meal. The hummus is great and the gyros are tasty. The salmon was surprisingly really good!  I would definitely come again!

Service is great and the food is delicious!! Portions are small but overall a great place to hang out with friends and have some  drinks.  Great atmosphere and the decoration of the place is beautiful!  We had the corn ravioli and the Ora king salmon.  My favorite part was the dessert!
Always the best breakfast, amazing bread, pastries and breakfast options. The pain au chocolat is to die for. My kids love it.

We went back for the 2nd time in a few days, that's how good they are! We went at 11:30 and was seated right away, before the crowds came at noon. We ordered the shrimp scallops pink sauce linguini which my son LOVED! Crab louie salad was a good way to eat crab without peeling them, their clam chowder was so GOOD!! The baked giant clam was okay (wanted to try the oyster rocker fellar next time and crab cake). Fried stuff doesn't look that good. Hubs loved tge garlic wine small clams.

Came for lunch. Food was good as always with a hillstone restaurant. They did put Mayo when my GF asked for no Mayo but it wasn't the end of the world.  Strong drinks.  Bit pricey but you know that when you walk in
Used to be good when they first opened and now they have become just another tourist trap. Expensive and mediocre food. Dont be fooled by the decor and price thinking this will be some great seafood.

Appreciated the free calamari, but it was not crispy and dipping sauce was a little heavy on the lemon.  After ordering salmon, was told they didn't have any more.  Settled on the cod instead and it was pretty good.  At these prices, I'm surprised that you have to pay for simple (and cheap to produce) side items like mashed  potatoes - seriously?? - 5.00 for a side of mashed potatoes?  Bread brought to table was ok - not great - not fresh tasting -not spectacular.  Kids meals - at least the pasta - was a large enough portion to satiate a gerbil.  We ended up getting another portion and the kids were still complaining they were hungry.  Husband had the seared ahi tuna and it wad also good.  He was disappointed we didnt eat at the shellfish place (I don't like shellfish) so he also ordered shucked oysters - they were awful.  He's only hoping his stomach won't be hurting later.  Was the food okay? (perhaps even good) - yes.  Was the food worth the money you pay? - hands down - no.    Update:  After returning home, both dh and I had stomach problems.  Originally gave 2 stars - now they get 1 only because I have to.
Pricey but absolutely worth it. My favorite is the fancy lattes. Great food and wonderful atmosphere. They have a menu that has something for everyone!

Lovely birthday dinner delivered. Chicken cordon blu was perfect as was meatballs and fettuccine They included a complimentary bottle of their Stella Merlo, which was very good. Perfect evening in during quarantine. Thank you

Fantastic service from Angelina! Attentive and friendly.   We dined on the patio for our 4th of July dinner. We were immediately made to feel comfortable. Angelina offered us blankets and a space heater - although it wasn't that cool out, but the attention to detail was appreciated.  Dinner was delicious: short ribs, steak tenderloin (cooked perfectly), brussel sprout appetizer, and syrah wine. Lovely view. Nice mix of families and couples. Overall a very comfortable and delicious dining experience. Didn't need the pomp and circumstances of Angel Oak as this was just our speed for the evening.
I really enjoyed the seafood tacos I ordered. The outdoor area was scenic and perfect for bringing my pups. Our waiter was very nice and fast. We had a nice experience.

The fried chickpeas that were  given at the table were delicious! My kids couldn't get enough of them. Based on reviews and this first morsel we were so excited for what was to come. The server was nice and attentive too.  The sopes toppings we ordered were delicious but the actual sopes were tough and too dense.   The steak bites were inedible bc they were so tough, but the haysteak fries were enjoyed by the kids.   The wings were good but was mostly Wing and very little drumstick. The bathroom was  gross.  The toilet and the sink clearly needed a good disinfecting.  (Appears they get the  wipe the basin type cleaning and not the under the edge and rims kind).   Was so hopeful for what could be a true gem.

Love love love love this place!! The food will not disappoint you and the view is gorgeous! Definitely do not come when you're hungry because there is always a one to one and a half hour wait but it is very worth it!! Best seating is outdoors I tried the lobster sandwich and it was phenomenal. While you put your name and you can go for a walk on the beach and watch the dogs play the sunset is one of the best in town. I would know I grew up on the beach!!

Santa Barbara has so many wonderful annual events.   Solstice, Fiesta, Festivals, etc, etc....  But this one event is more significant than any other.   Bierbara!!  A celebration of Saint Barbara who are town is named after.    Third Window Brewing is named Third Window because of Saint Barbara.  She was a martyr around 500 AD.  She was a devout Christian with a Pagan father.  He built a room in a tower to keep her from the rest of the world.  While it was being built she had them add a third window to represent the holy Trinity which upset her father to the point he had her executed.   Or so the story goes.   Every year Third Window has a Feast for Saint Barbara.... My favorite event of the year!!!  Always on or around December 4th.    It is elegant with amazing gourmet food and beer and wine pairings.  It always sells out so get your reservations in early.

A really nice sleek and laid back restaurant. Food is really pricey and not worth how much I spent. The ahi mini tacos alone were 9 dollars and literally are the size on my thumb. The wings weren't the best but weren't the worst. I think the best thing out of all of our meals was the pizza but I mean how do you go wrong with pizza. And during happy hour it was only 6 dollars! The coconut shrimp also smacks. Idk if I'll come back, but not bad.

Been here two times...  One time before studying abroad for 5 months in Berlin, DE. And then again since returning.   The fries are awesome! Must have! I love the aioli sauces and curry ketchup most of all! The chicken and apple brat is really great.   In no way is this place anything like a true German restaurant but it does well! I loved this place so much more before getting back to school.

Came here on a Sunday during their happy hour and ordered tacos for $2 each. I was pleasantly surprised by the quality of their carne Assad's tacos! The meat was extremely moist and I think they taste better than Lily's tacos! I wished they had more Salsas available but this place is extremely small.  The cashier was very nice and  gave me more onions and cilantro for free. This place is nice and convenient if you're trying grab a cheap quick bite. I'm sure this places gets crazy at night when the clubs/bars close. I definitely want to come back and get more tacos!
Bomb sandwiches, great service, and nice atmosphere. The grilled cheese and tomato soup is the best!
Didn't enjoy it. The crepe was burnt and the iced tea was stale. And, it was $20 for one person.

I am a local, grew up here in Santa Barbara, and I have a high standard for dining and eating nutritious, healthy and delicious food (as I cook a lot at home and have worked in some of the best fine dining restaurants in the world). Ah Juice is the best organic, clean food establishments in town while also so hearty, homey and delicious! I cannot say enough about how grateful I am to have a place like this in town. For the quality of the food and juices, the prices are reasonable. You can really tell that a lot of thought and care is put into the menu and dishes/juices.  I particularly love the veggie burger and the soups (every day there is a soup of the day). The soups are always filled with an abundant assortment of vegetables and spices and everything needed to make it wholesome, nutritious and delicious!  I also love the ginger lemonade! However for breakfast or lunch, everything is delicious!    It is a small, quaint, inviting place and there is usually parking out front or a half a block up on the the street.  My favorite part is that I always feel like I am eating home cooked, wholesome, organic, nutritious and delicious food and I don't have to worry about what I am putting in my body when I consume anything at AH Juice! A hidden jem, more people should know about this place and enjoy its benefits!  Bom Apetite!

Really a 3.5  Went here during non-peak hours so we got to choose our seat. Our server, Sean, was EXCELLENT! He took great care of us and even noticed and took care of things that were out of his and the restaurant's control. If this review was about him he'd get 5 stars. The reason I'm not giving a 5 is because all the food was bland, which was quite disappointing. As far as pricing goes, you pay for the view, so one can't really complain about that...
This place rocks the hampernini is on point also grab a bag of their pickle popcorn super good. Staff is friendly and nice too!!

There should be a sin at the front that read, 'Please Seat Yourself', so that hapless wonders don't stand there waiting for Hell to freeze over just to be seated. Waitress are a bunch of chatty Kathys who flirt for tips and make ya wait for drinks.

This the best Italian food in Santa Barbara. It's a very local place, small and no hoopla, but the food is exquisite.  I didn't take pictures because we ate everything so quickly, but it's house made and delicious. Secrets: breakfast everyday is incredible: fresh, fresh, fresh, and really italian, and there is a case full of the best gelato in town.

One of the best quesadillas ever. Grilled over the fire, which definitely helps.  I order the cheese quesadilla, the spicy salsa and guacamole. I am never disappointed.   Husband swears by the chicken. I don't like chicken but tasted it and it wasn't too bad. For chicken.   Son loved his tri-tip quesadilla and daughter liked her 'cheese taco' because she says she hates quesadillas. (She actually loves them, eye roll)  Anyway, if you are in the area, it's worth coming here. We made a trip to Santa Barbara just to come here, so there's that.
My friends and I loved everything about this restaurant. Huge tasty portions, tasty cocktails, nice views and great service. We will definitely be coming back to try more of the menu.

Maybe I just bought the wrong meal, but I was really disappointed in this place.  First of all, SB really lacks Vegan restaurants, so when I heard of this place I thought I must try it. I ordered the turkey sandwich. Not to say it wasn't delicious, but there was nothing special about it. I could have made it at home for a lot less then 10 dollars.  Also, we ordered chips and guac. The guac was nearly brown from sitting out, and the chips were some store bought brand and kind of stale.   I was really disappointed and probably won't go there again. The service was bad, took forever to get our check, or for anybody to notice our plates were empty. It's too pricey for what you get, and our meal wasn't fresh at all.
Great drinks and best Mac and Cheese in the world. It's a very nice place, valet only, great food and great service.

My partner and I went to Belmond el Encanto for dinner Friday evening and had a fabulous time. The food and service were delicious. Our server Alex was especially attentive and 'went the extra mile' to make sure we had a great time, which we did. The only negative I would mention is that there were quite a few tables close together, and because if the overhead tent it did get pretty loud. It was absolutely lovely but it felt like being in a crowded space as opposed to an intimate one.

You see, I really like chirashi bowls. Really like them. They do a good chirashi (sashimi) bowl.   Also, the dinner set is precisely the right combination of things (tempura, maki, edamame, spicy beef, gyoza, rice, salad), and tons of food, so you can take some home. Unless you are high capacity.   They are a decent quick, no-fuss option for sushi in SB, with reasonable prices, but the fish isn't scary as a result. We can take our baby in there, and they don't look nervous. The waitstaff used to be kinda lousy, and then suddenly one week (we live a couple of blocks away and go there ALL the time), they all became overly perky and kept asking how we were doing. They've now reached a happy medium, which works well for us.
a good place for unique hawaiian decor and island styled burgers. their veggie burger is pretty good too!

The downtown location is a great spot, the original Pastrami sandwich is superb. The downtown location is super small and gets crowded easily, which means that you might not be able to find a seat. The Milpas location has more seating, but somehow I find the Downtown location better tasting. This is the best sandwich spot in SB
Love there wood fire pizza especially the blonde bunny, margarita, and pepperoni! Great delivery service too.

If you want healthy options this is the right place. Soups, salads, vegetarian, vegan and meat dishes too! Their food is delish, similar to home cooked. All the menu options taste fresh! They are very fast and consistent and the staff are helpful and friendly.

Had the crab cake Benedict, Mediterranean omelet with sausage and the cinnamon roll.   The crab cake Benedict comes with house made crab cakes on top of the English muffin and egg and hollandaise sauce. The crab cakes are  tasty with peppers inside. Could be a bit more crispy but was very tasty.   The Mediterranean omelet was really good with lots of peppers.   The cinnamon roll is huge and tasty. It comes out warmed. Nice cream cheese frosting.   The best thing are the buttery hash browns. You can taste the butter with every bite.   The downside of this place is the service. They don't refill coffee or water. The wait staff always needs to be flagged down. If they fix this, Farmer Boy would be perfect.

Stopped in on a recent shopping trip with he wife (help). What a wonderful well hidden place. I don't know how we missed it before. They have a wonderful outdoor seating area catering to happy hour customers. The drinks are inexpensive and the bar service is friendly and prompt. The place is large with dinner seating both inside and outdoors. The dinner service was just as good as the happy hour service. I didn't get to try the sea bass this time but I will definitely try it on my next visit. The meals we had were delicious and the music in the background was perfect. I'm not picky I just know what good is. Check this place out and you will agree.  Flash

The food is very good, we will be sure to come back when we are in Santa Barbara.  I had the combination golden noodles with the rice noodles.  MJ had the spicy drunken noodles. Both were very good. I am going to bookmark this place so I can remember to come back again.

The food is imaginative, well executed and delicious. The service and glaring fluorescent lights from the unattractive, commercial refriderator in the dining area are serous negatives.  Our first visit, when the owner/chef was there was just fine (except the refrig);the second visit was an embarrassment as we invited friends after extolling Bibi Ji. Service was beyond slow--a drink order of tap beer was forgotten, courses dribbled out and most disappointingly the tandoori chicken arrived 30-40 minutes after several prompts and the other dishes were all consumed.  We really want to  patronize Bibi Ji but they need to get it together and forget the refrig thing

Came back two weeks later because the ham and cheese croissant we got the last time were SOOOOO GOOOD.  Came in, ordered the same thing this time 'ham and cheese croissant'.  This time when it was delivered, it was the ham and cheese off of the counter, not the same.  Still good, but not magical like we had the last time we were in.  When I asked why it was different, I was told the last time 'I must have ordered the ham and cheese 'sandwich' '.  Well, I don't think so, but OK.  This was still good, but the other was great. We drove 175 miles and were looking forward to the magic again.  :-(

China palace is expensive, which is why I gave it 3 stars and not 4. I suggest making your own rice so you don't have to spend money on it. The chow fun is 很好吃, as well as the mapo doufu and Hong Kong style noodle. You pretty much can't go wrong here. The portions are pretty generous (sometimes it seems like you get more in the restaurant than in takeout): 2 dishes plus rice are usually enough to feed 3 people who have average to large appetites.

Had pretty high hopes for this place. Being French myself I always like restaurants that are authentic. This review is for the service and the setting, not the food.   I went there with my mom on a Sunday around lunch time, it was full. We walk in, no acknowledgement we were there for at least a few minutes. It's awkward, I don't like that. Next a waiter comes over after serving another table and puts us in a table in the corner. No menus, we just sit there, in very old very uncomfortable chairs I might add, until a waitress comes by to tell us the table was already reserved for other people, in a not so pleasant way. Great communication guys... The waitress doesn't even apologize and we end up sitting outside, again no menus, we had to get them ourselves. (It was one of those situations where you feel like you're bothering them.) By that point my mom and I were done and the menu wasn't appealing so we left. Too bad. There's a lot of potential. We won't go there again though.

Freaking delicious!!!!!! Found this place just on a whim and so glad we did!!!!!!   Service wasn't great, but it wasn't awful either. Our server just seemed a little overwhelmed - maybe someone called in sick?   They had live music which was really nice - we sat on the patio though because inside was a little loud (personal taste).   Will defiantly be back!!!!

The food here is pretty good.  We tried the Crab Cake burger which had a decent sized crab cake tasting very good.  The other was the fish and chips which was moist and soft.  Also gave the fish taco's a whirl which was essentially the fish from fish and chips in taco form.  So that automatically got a positive nod.  While not the healthiest of foods, it was pleasantly delightful.

The only reason I'm knocking this iconic restaurant by a star is that we didn't receive the raved-about muffins.   We had a marvelous outdoor seat that afforded us a view of the beautiful SB coastline.  We were greeted by our server quickly, and our drinks were provided just as fast.  The orange juice is good!!!!  Very good.  Tasted like Valencia oranges.   I ordered the corned beef hash eggs Benedict, hold the spinach, and hash browns.  My husband had the 911 omelette (his avocados were given to me).  Water was refilled promptly, and our food came out about 12 minutes after we ordered.   The food was perfect!  I asked for soft poached eggs - done!  The corned beef was tender and only a little tart from its preparation. The hollandaise sauce was tart with mustard, and bread t from a mix. The hash browns needed salt - no big deal - easier to add than take away.   His omelette was spicy with chorizo and chiles, and it had a snappy pico de gallo.  He had the hash browns and sourdough toast.  He finished his dish, but at least half of mine remained (I still wanted a mini muffin, but I was left wanting). If you would like a great meal with a wonderful view, but at a price ($50 with tax and tip), then go to Sambo's.  You should not leave needing another meal for at least 6 - 8 hours. Yum!!!

My husband and I regularly dined at the restaurant that occupied this space back in 1990 called 'Acapulco.'  They had awesome margaritas and we always loved sitting on the gorgeous patio.  While Acapulco is long gone, on a recent visit to Santa Barbara, we were happy to see a restaurant still occupies the space and the patio is still as gorgeous as it was back then!    For nostalgia's sake, we dined at the new-to-us restaurant Viva.  We sat on the patio, right next to the fountain, which was great... the turtles living in the fountain are adorable and super entertaining! :)  Viva's menu is modern Mexican with some twists.  They have a lot of choices for Vegetarians.  I had Farmer's Market Enchiladas-- butternut squash, kale, onions, poblano rajas, jack cheese, rice, adobo sauce, and cream.  The enchiladas were good, although quite salty.  My husband had the shrimp and crab enchilada.  He said there wasn't much crab, though.  I liked their  blood orange margarita.  Would like to try the Happy Hour sometime.

Excellent food!  Great find!!  Perfect comfort food!  Especially loved the fig flatbread!  My husband had the chicken tiki Marsala.  Excellent! Our son had the Swedish meatballs.  He enjoyed every last bite.    We will definitely be back!
Goddamn! This food is perfectly cooked! I guess I no longer need to go to Lily's. The meat here is better quality and flavored way better! I want this food to never leave my plate!

Oh Java, how do I love thee? Let me count the ways...First and foremost, the coffee is of the utmost quality, and is lovingly prepared by possibly the finest (and by fine I mean talented and sexy) barista this side of the Mississippi! The looseleaf teas are stunning, and just as delightful as they are aesthetically pleasing. Ms. Etta James can be heard overhead, singing 'At last...Java Jones has come along....' Starwhosit's?

GOD I missed this place when I was living out of state! My east coast friends have probably heard enough about the chicken ranch! I was having dreams about it, seriously! The chicken rocks but I personally go for the tri tip burrito. Omg I'm hungry now haha

The bacon wrapped dated in honey are AMAZING. Such a cute name too 'date with a pig'. The slider with Swiss cheese and aioli are one of the best burgers I've ever had! Definitely my new favorite spot on the Mesa!
Perfect place for a date night. My husband and I love this place. The shrimp ravioli and paella are so flavorful.  The drinks are strong, interesting and delicious.  Great service.

We've ordered a few times during the pandemic as well as dined in. Food consistently amazing. It's great that this location has been able to manager a high level of service when others can't find solid employees.   Only bummer on our visit yesterday was. It being able to have the lobster roll. The lobster dip wasn't on the menu but they were able to make it happen.   Our table was well attended to and we all left full and happy.

This is a little over due....but ! Never to late. I had my wedding rehearsal brunch with The Shop cafe on October 10th. Everything was amazing ! Food was so good ! Service was great ! Drinks were always full - literally couldn't have asked for a better experience. Mind you we were a group of 23 ranging from ages 2 years old - 80 years old, everyone left satisfied.   The owner I wish I knew his name was so helpful. We contacted him literally a week before the event and he made sure he would accommodate us on such last minute. We had so much going on with our wedding that this was last one our list out of our 1000000 things to do. But to be honest this might have been our favorite event. Being at a ONE huge table with 23 of your closet friends and family with amazing food and drinks in beautiful Santa Barbara , what more could we have asked for ?   Me writing this review says a lot , my husband and I are in the hospitality industry so we critique ever last thing and really I have nothing bad to say about this establishment except I wish you guys were in Los Angeles ;)   HIGHLY RECOMMENDED!!! We will be back :)

Loved this place! They have a great selection of burgers. I enjoyed the traditional hamburger and the fries. Yes, it isn't the cheapest burger but it is one of the better quality burgers in town. I will be back!

This place is for old people not kid friendly at all and has very uncomfortable furniture inside all of the rooms it looks nice but it's not usable the location is way up in the hills and nowhere near the ocean with nothing much to do around the hotel itself the hotel has very little amenities a few eating spots very poor fitness facility and the spot is terrible only good thing about the hotel was the view from the pool and that the floors are heated in the bathrooms. Room service was terrible and it's very overpriced compared to other five star hotels although the staff was friendly I would never stay here again

We had dinner here before a show at SB Bowl a few weeks ago, and we had been here before a couple of times over the years. We like it because you can almost always get a table for two, the menu offerings are great and so are the prices. Unfortunately, it does seem that each time, our orders come out not quite right. This time, I ordered the Cobb, no egg. Our server seemed distracted, so I asked 'Sorry, did you hear the no egg part?', to which she said yes. Salad came out with egg and it was all tossed together so I couldn't pick it out, and I had to wait a frustrating amount of time before I could find her and send it back. Meanwhile, hubby had to eat his (hot) meal. Eventually, I got the correct salad and had to gobble it up in order to make our show. Not the end of the world, and the salad was quite good. Service just always seems a smidge off when we come here. Not so bad that we don't come back, though.

I lived in Santa Barbara for 8 years and always found Enterprise the perfect spot for an evening of drinks and food.  The ambiance is exactly what I look for when eating at a seafood restaurant near the beach.  The staff is friendly and helpful when I come by and they always do what they can to help accommodate my request.  I will forever be a fan of this restaurant and its service as it will be a must visit stop whenever I visit Santa Barbara.

Very cool venue, but mediocre food and consistency.  If you've got a large party or want to enjoy your finger food in a hip environment, this is a pretty cool place to be. With high ceilings, warm lighting and a wood-themed interior, this place makes a great first impression. Everything is clean, and there's a handy sink that's close to the tables so you can wash your hands, should they get too saucy.   The food itself was a bit disappointing, starting with the shrimp. My friends and I ordered a couple of pounds of shrimp to share (we partnered up in 2's) and each duo decided on their own sauce. Two of our orders were the same: a pound of shrimp with a combination of their sauces, cajun, lemon pepper, and garlic pepper in the 'call me stupid' level of spicy. I've been to other peel-your-own shrimp style restaurants before, and the texture of their shrimp are way better. The shrimp here are either overcooked, not fresh, or a combination of both, because the meat was just too soggy and lacked the firm, bouncy texture that makes a good shrimp.   Onto the sauce: two of our orders were both a combination of three sauces in the same spicy level, but tasted completely different. I noticed my friends were struggling with the heat while I wasn't, even though we all handled spicy very well. I tried their bag of shrimp and their sauce was way more flavorful, way spicier. Pretty much how I wanted mine to taste like. In addition, another friend ordered the Drunken special sauce, thinking it was essentially what we ordered. It tasted a bit watered down and was mostly tomato sauce to them, so they asked if they could have a bit of our sauce on the side to make up for their dissatisfaction. Our waitress wasn't too friendly about it, stating that extra sauce would be a hefty additional charge. Even though my friends weren't trying to get more sauce; they tried one shrimp and said it honestly wasn't what they expected.   Before that problem came up, our waitress was actually pretty friendly, but after that, she was never quite the same. It's like our (in my opinion) reasonable request had offended her and that was it for us. On top of all this, the food isn't exactly cheap, either. For the same price, I can probably get better quality, bigger portions, and a comparable, if not better, environment. Just down the street.

Delicious and great prices. Every time you buy something, on your receipt you might get a free cookie or a discount off your food if you purchase something at a certain time. When I was a student in Santa Barbara, I ate here at least once a week (in reality I probably ate here at least three times a week). Only once in the whole time I went to Silvergreen's, I've dealt with an unfriendly staff member. Thankfully I never encountered them again but all the other staff I've encountered there are friendly and helpful. The fries are delicious and their sandwiches are superb. I've tried various sandwich shops in SB and Silvergreen's is still my favorite place to go for fresh (non-toasted) sandwiches.

Santa Barbara landmark serving wonderful street food. There's always a long line, but it moves relatively quickly. Some good veggie options. It's hard to find seating on the patio, so its best to grab your order to go. The food is good and never disappoints. Besides the lines, the other downside is that it's cash only.

Went for brunch, and eggs Benedict was just ok, a bit overcooked.   Very nice outdoor patio overlooking Cabrillo Blvd and dog friendly.  I just with they had umbrellas as the sun was wicked hot. Good service, casual atmosphere with lots of woodwork and beautiful outdoor plants and trees.

This is a place I was expecting to be over-hyped, but it totally lived up to the rave reviews for me. It's a casual, almost dive-y, sort of place that only accepts cash, but it's all fresh handmade food (you can see them cooking) and the prices are so low! They have my favorite guacamole hands down, and every special I've tried has been drool-worthy. I recommend this place to everyone.

As a pizza connoisseur I was disappointed. Nicky, the owner, has a big personality and was entertaining when we picked up the pizza. But, unfortunately, he was the best part of the entire experience. The pizza was good not great.

Been here many times and never been disappointed. They staff is very knowledgeable and pleasant. The food is great the ambiance is great - what more can u ask for ???  I love the artichoke appetizer, the calamari and any of the ceviches. The steamed clams are amazing! The strawberry spring salad is the bomb! Can't forgot the charbroiled oysters. I love all the food here and always look forward to going to lure. Great happy hour all day on Sunday !
Gino's is great for pizza by the slice.  One of my favorite places, convenient location.

We live right around the corner from Cody's. We eat at Cody's very frequently. We are sad to hear that it is temporarily closed because of the coronavirus. Thank you Cody's for your friendly service to us always! The staff is always very friendly and accommodating. My husband and I often get the same thing that we always order. He gets the turkey burger with roasted jalapeños, no bun and extra salad to replace his french fries. I get the chicken tostada salad. But I love the fried chicken, have often gotten the steak, my grandson used to love coming when he could get pancakes anytime of the day or night. Occasion we have had the chocolate cake which is delicious. We look forward to when our neighborhood restaurant is open again.

My experience started off terrible and ended terrible. To begin I waited in line while the cashier was on the phone with a customer only to pick up the phone yet again and take another order for 5 minutes before taking mine. While another staff member just stood there doing nothing then proceeds to take someone else's order that had just walked in. I spent over $100 there and to be honest the croissants especially chocolate were no better than Albertsons and the ones I was served on airfrance put renauds to shame. An airline... this place has gone down hill since they expanded. The hazelnut roll I asked for was grabbed from the back not from the front case which I found weird they tasted stale or at least a day old. The lemon tart was overly sweet it made our tongues pucker with the need to down a glass of water. The $15 stollen was stale and lacking dried fruit and nuts. Not traditional stollen.  This is the type of place that I will forever think of as a stop on the way out of town for a road trip as in fast food. There are better more traditional bakeries in town that actually care about quality and consumer experience and are not just as dark un-updated hole in the wall. Would be nice if the tarts didn't all come in the same mold. Where is the creativity for the price you charge. Service and quality are below sub par. Get it together or stop expanding.

5 stars for the whole experience! It was great from the valet, host, environment, server, drinks and food! I had the Gnocchi wich was really flavorful and my favorite, and the Jidori chicken and all the elements that came with it were delicious. The hamachi was ok, I felt the fish was sliced too thick. I definitely will be back and looking forward to it!

Bueno   A month ago, got some leftover tacos from this place that were really good, so wanted to come.    10 minute wait on Saturday evening.  Ordered the corn appetizer and duck taquitos.  The corn was advertised to have Serranos and a bunch more stuff.  Just tasted corn.  The mole for the taquitos was good, but not great. Duck was dry and didn't have anything with it to accentuate the flavor.  I wouldn't order again.   Tons of wait staff, all running around doing stuff.  Our waiter evaporated after taking our order.  Had to ask for the manager to give us a bill.   Overall, I'll be back, for the tacos, probably skip the rest of the menu.

Guys!  This is the pizza joint you've been looking for.  Best movie/game night pizza place in Santa Barbara.  This is my new go-to.  So so so good.  We got the pepperoni and the white pizza with roasted veggies but it's that sourdough crust that is super bubbly, crackery and with the perfect amount of chew. There's a few quirks to ordering but nothing crazy.   Such a Santa Barbara gem.

I've dined here several times, with girlfriends, with my husband, and with groups of friends. Staff is always super polite and happy. I love the new menu items. The wine list has reasonably priced choices that are pretty luscious (reds) and crisp (whites). Went this past Saturday and we were seated right away. Usually, we try to sit at the open table, but it's always packed. We had a great meal, great service, great wine, and positive vibes. Thank you!
We would eat here about once every two weeks.  This is not a fancy place, not very large, but the staff is super nice, parking is easy and the food is good.

I came here for the first time just the other day and I got their Thai Basil Beef lunch special. I felt like the aroma of the basil was missing and how can you have Thai Basil Beef without the taste of the basil?!??! Although the food was quite bland, I like their deal though. An entree comes with fried rice, salad, soup, an eggroll & wonton, and fruits. The portions are small but I do like variety!   Food deserves 2 stars but +1 for the super friendly service! :)

It's been a long time since we have been here due to Covid. I asked for the fishouse Bloody Mary and the waiter said it was off the menu. It was my favorite Bloody Mary in town. Please bring it back! This is a great local restaurant with really great drinks and great seafood, appetizers & salads. The clam chowder is definitely one of the best in town. It has always been consistent but when we first sat down and looked at the menu it was obvious to me that they have a new chef. The menu was well written with great selections and the quality was top notch.  It's a great place to go watch a game and have a drink or two and some great appetizers too. I for one am very grateful they survived this past year.

This little gem is tucked away at the Lark plaza. Everything was amazing. From the $7 fries starter, to the chicken and waffles, everything was flavored perfectly.   Chicken & Waffles $19 - together is to die for with the maple syrup. Its a huge portion so I would share.  Egg scramble with asparagus, bacon, onion(?)- flavorful and a nice balance of veggies. It comes in a small skillet with potatoes.  Shashuka - Im normally not a fan because it's too tomato-y for my taste but this one has a burst of flavors and comes with THE. BEST. Piece of toast on the side.   Id get all 3 again to share with the table! The sweet options like pancakes were tempting~ maybe next time because Ill be coming back!!

Hands down the best Mexican restaurant of the area. I have been to The Mex Authentic multiple times and they have never failed to please me.  Prices are affordable, about $10 for an enchilada, $11 for a burrito/bowl at most on the menu. Portions are pretty big, very filling and SO good! I always go for the chicken enchilada it tastes amazing with sour cream and guacamole.  Service is always on point. They often check on you, refill your soft drinks, waiters are always very nice and polite. They split checks for large parties.   If you like margaritas, try the Mexarita. Wanna add a little spice? Get the Jalapeño Cucumber Mexarita, it's not too spicy, you won't regret it.

Wow wow wow! I LOVED this place.  From the drinks, to the service, to the ambiance, to the decor- this place is awesome!   This place sells both delicious and instagram worthy drinks (check out my pic to see what I'm talking about). I ordered the Bela rose- a matcha infused with of pomegranate and strawberry elder flower. It was delicious! I'm super excited to try their other specialty drinks. It should be noted that they do serve regular coffee as well for those who aren't as adventurous.  The space itself is super cute- it's filled with plants and is tastefully decorated. It isn't too big, but it wasn't very crowded either (they've only been open for about 3 weeks though).   If you're looking to step out of your comfort zone when it comes to coffee and tea- this is the place! I'm excited to bring my out-of-town friends here when they come for a visit!

Came here while on vacation.  I found it after looking all over yelp and everyone seemed to enjoy it. The Food, the wine and the atmosphere were wonderful.  We started with some Pinot and Burrata starter. The cheese was so fresh and fabulous!!! And the bread was also very tasty.  I ordered the piccata which was cooked perfectly. I especially enjoyed the garlic in the dish... soo tasty. I would definitely recommend this place to anyone.

Oh so cute. This restaurant has a European vibe when sat  outside. The birds flying around, the brick patio... so relaxing. The food was delightful! Our server was kind and the loCation is outstanding. We will be back!!

I have driven by Stone Age a few times and it's always been full of Asian diners. To me this screams authenticity! The food has to be amazing and authentic, right? Wrong.   I visited on Wednesday and it was fairly quiet. I was happy to see that they were offering the axxess deal of buy one entree get one free. I ordered the combination fried rice and the chicken chow mein. I feel that these are super basic dishes but that's what I was in the mood for and I feel like you can't really get that wrong. Right? Wrong.   The combination fried rice was so bland and had hardly any meat in it. It was more like a pilaf side dish with chunks of what looked and tasted like spam. Gross. The chow mein looked and smelled amazing but it was so salty that I couldn't finish it. Very disappointing!   After finishing my dinner I was curious to see what the yelp reviews said about this place. I was surprised to see that it had 4 stars and such great reviews! I decided to give Stone Age 2 stars. 1 for service and 1 for offering the axxess deal. I might return to give them another try but I think I will stick to Mandarin Palace for great Chinese food.

The food ended up being pretty good here for the most part but there were a few issues. I ordered a mild green curry with shrimp, which actually ended up being pretty spicy. They also left the tails on the shrimp. We ordered delivery through Restaurant Connection which was the only delivery option, and it took 1.5 hours and a dish was forgotten (not sure whether the fault or the restaurant or the delivery service) but we did get a refund after calling. Our friends did really enjoy the pork belly fried rice but the drunken noodles had a very high ratio of vegetables to noodles.

Very nice outdoors & indoors space. The 4 of us (SB locals) had good burgers( fries were hot & crispy)!salad & a pasta dish. All was good food @  very reasonable price. Had nice red wine & margaritas. All good. Recommend giving this place a try.

Meh....pretty thoroughly average.  The milkshake was a little disappointing.  The fries and burgers are perfectly decent.  No indoor seating.  Staff was fine.  I probably won't be back though....I'd rather have In 'n Out.

Be prepared for a wait. The first time we went we decided not to and just went elsewhere, and when we came back for lunch at about 2pm, we still waited for about 30minutes. We ended up getting one of the coveted outdoor tables (only 3) but be prepared it's cold out there by the ocean. Bring layers!  We got the King Crab special which was by the pound. Lots of good fresh seafood options.

Awesome local seafood restaurant! They recently opened and I've already been there twice. On top of their seafood market they also serve a small menu. I've tried the seafood taco sampler, which was amazing! The salmon burger was also really good but I'd lean more towards the tacos. Everything was fresh and tasty. Quality fish.

I have been coming here since they opened and have never had an an unpleasant experience until today. It was later in the afternoon and I ordered a quiche to go. I waited 20 minutes until I had to ask about my order, as did the women sitting next to me. The manager informed me I had only waited 17 minutes. This was unprofessional and rude, just apologize for waiting for a piece of quiche at 2 o'clock in the afternoon.

Thrilled to have another (GOOD!) sushi restaurant in Santa Barbara. Reasonable pricing/on par with other high end sushi joints. The tower roll was delish. My only tip to the manager was that it seemed like there was something missing...perhaps music given how sparse the decor was. Service was good but we really didn't need 4 people on standby to clear our plates the minute we finished the last piece of a roll or to take the wrapping from the chop-stix. Will definitely be back & know this is a new establishment so am confident they'll work out the kinks.

I WOULD give 4 stars for the food and ambiance, but I really had to knock it down because of poor service.   My friend and I came on Fiesta Friday. The first impression of a restaurant's service typically starts (at least for me and my friend) with the hostess. The door was open and we checked in and the hostess let us know they opened at 5pm. Fair enough, and seeing that it was 4:54pm, we decided to hang by the staircase and check the menu.   We returned and stepped through the door of the restaurant on the dot. Who I assume was the manager, who was speaking with the hostess, was the only one who greeted us nicely when we walked through the door.
