The hostess then walked over and rudely told us again that they do not open until 5pm. She didn't even bother to check her watch to confirm. At this point, my friend and I were going to turn around and walk out because it was apparent to both of us that it would be difficult to get seated, much less order food from this restaurant we've been wanting to try out since it opened.   We were taken to our table, hostess drops off our menus and promptly leaves (how welcoming). We then get addressed by a (kind enough) server who gives us pleasant service (when she was actually around to give us service). The food runners were prompt and kind, and they ended up being *more* attentive than our server was--we had to flag a food runner down after sitting in our seats waiting for the check for about **20 minutes**. Our server kept talking with other employees; we were seated outdoors and could not get her attention. We finally got our checks and give tips that were proportionate to the service that we experienced.  My friend and I would return here to eat, but the service was so frustrating that we will definitely find quality food with a better experience elsewhere.

Old school bar meets modern today! The staff and service are excellent! Interior is interesting with maroon etched ceiling, dark wood and neon clock in the back....  I recommend the beet endive salad, salmon, fresh catch of the day and the ginger Pimms specialty drink...  Will definitely go back to try another drink specialty!

Line at kimchi was too long so my friends and I came here LOL. Thai food is always good though.  You get a free thai iced tea that's HUGE when you check in on Yelp! And you get complimentary shrimp chips to munch on as you wait for your food.  TOM YUM SOUP: Nice and spicy! And flavorful with these really cool mushrooms.  PAPAYA SALAD: Probably the best papaya salad I've had. It was refreshing and seasoned well.  PAD THAI WITH TOFU: Disappointing. The noodles were way too soft with barely any seasoning. Barely any peanuts too. I don't recommend this dish (which is sad because pad Thai is such a popular dish to order).  PCU: Noodles were better than the pad Thai noodles for sure. Good amount of broccoli and overall tasty.  YELLOW CURRY: More on the liquidy side but good flavor! It has a variety of veggies inside the curry. I didn't find this spicy but I'm sure you could make it spicy if you asked.  Basically the appetizers (soup and salad) were better than the entrees in my opinion. I wasn't particularly impressed by any of the entrees. Servers were great though and the interior is cute if you're looking for a hipster place to eat. But if you want good thai food, I think you can find some better places.

Such a cool, unique location!  We love driving the motorcycle up for tri-tip on Sundays or they have excellent event staff who can help with large group events.  The atmosphere is totally unique and my team has always enjoyed having dinners together for business or holidays.

I would like for someone to please explain to me as to why it took over an hour to make 3 sandwiches when we called for a pick up. Please explain to me why it took you over an hour to make three sandwiches all the while other customers are getting their orders before us, after coming in minutes later.  This is the worst service we've ever gotten from any establishment in Santa Barbara, the area We work in everyday. This is beyond subpar professional.  Oh and your constant excuses of 'it'll be ready in 10 minutes' for fucking hour. Our time wasted because employees here don't understand how orders work.  Fucking ridiculous.

My husband and I were anxious to try this new restaurant, based on the good reviews posted.  We were completely disappointed.  We would have been better off burning the $50 we spent and eating the ashes. The staff seemed untrained and couldn't answer questions.  Service was  slow and the food arrived cold and poorly prepared.  Eggs, bacon, croissant, pancakes -- you would think those basics could be prepared properly.  And $10 for orange juice!  I could understand that price if we were in Juno in February.  But oranges in Santa Barbara are hardly a rare commodity.  The location might be great for tourists.  But as locals, we won't be back.

This was a fun place to go with a large group.  They were able to seat six of us in a large booth where no one felt 'squished' in.  I would recommend to make reservations, as it does get really crowded.  All the food we ordered was delicious and with some folks having enough to either share or take some home with them.  Our waitress was very attentive and helped us may selections that we were really happy with.  The bar is very well stocked and the cocktails we ordered were very good.    After reading the other reviews I'm really looking forward to returning to try the Lobster Bisque covered in puff pastry!

The ambiance is lovely and the service was great, however the food didn't wow me. I've been to the Olio Pizzeria and loved it so it thought this was a slam dunk. My risotto was good, simple with porcini mushrooms but my husbands pasta with sausage, the sauce tasted like simple purée. I would try it again.

Estilo Jalisco is one of the best spots to open in Santa Barbara in a long time. It's a little shop near the beach that makes delicious tacos and aguas frescas. The shop also makes mulitas, quesadillas, etc., but I've only had the tacos. They're one of the only spots in town that grills over charcoal, which creates a distinctive smoky flavor. From the grilled items, I love their chorizo and chicken. They also make a chicharron in green salsa that you can't miss. On Sundays they're especially worth a visit, because they make an incredible beef stew with a tangy, savory tomatillo-cilantro broth. Considering that they make a food that's delicious and distinctive from the other restaurants in Santa Barbara at a budget-friendly price, I expect Estilo Jalisco to do quite well in town.   Beyond this, they're open till 10 pm Tuesday-Thursday and 1 am Friday and Saturday... which is much appreciated in a town where places don't stay open very late.
As a vegan, I was really excited to discover this restaurant in Santa Barbara. Vegan version of the well-known chipotle. Even make your own kombucha!

I love this place! Santa Barbara is full of so many boring pasta places that serve the same crap. This place is more adventurous in food options, has great wine and beer, is off the main d-bag drag, and has great service! Check it out!

I called the other evening to order some pizzas and the person on the phone was unfortunately very rude. She told me that they were no longer taking any orders and practically hung up on me. I understand that they are very popular, but would have been nice to have some sort of an apology or better service turning us down if they ran out of food.
Terrible hamburger.  Dry and flavorless.  Same goes for the salad.

Have eaten here a few times and been impressed. Normally such a large menu is a warning sign but so far everything has been good and the service spot on. You can't beat the view and now they are also open for lunch (and breakfast on the weekends). If you opt for one of their inventive sandwiches (the halibut was amazing), get the warm potato salad as your free side which is almost a meal in itself. Portions are huge so come hungry; reservations are advisable if you want an outdoor table since this place is already hopping.
Yuuuuuum. Everything about this little shop inside the market was amazing. Fish was fresh and delicious! Staff was knowledgeable and extremely friendly
Amazing atmosphere, and service. Definitely a special occasion restaurant. Make sure to visit the bar- loved the old school lounge.
So glad I finally tried this place. The food is fresh and so good. Ice Thai tea is on point as well. This will become a regular spot for lunch.

I've lived in Santa Barbara for two and a half years and only began to try this when I come for visit the city after I have moved away.  Perhaps it's a good thing, because eating here on a regular basis would definitely take a toll on the somewhat shape I am trying to stay in.  MEAT.  GRILL.  DELICIOUS.  There's a crispy pastrami sandwich that I LOVE here.  They crisp the pastrami so that it almost tastes like bacon.  There is definitely a meat overload, but with the crispy texture it doesn't make it become bland after the first 10 bites.  The staff here is always great and friendly - I try to come here every time I visit SB!

Sadly...if this is best pizza in Santa Barbara...too bad. Pizza had good taste and they didn't skimp on the toppings. But crust and some toppings burnt. And the crust was so so soggy, it became disgusting to eat.  In the trash.

A great place to hang out, drink and chill. Good food, fun bartenders, smart waitstaff. Husband and I will really miss this authentic Irish pub. Go on a night where there's an Irish band & dancing, during fleet weekend or a lazy Sunday afternoon, you'll thoroughly enjoy yourself. They have great lunch specials during the week too! They have my Magner's in a bottle and Strongbow (Magner's British brand) on tap, and plenty of beers. Good place to also catch televised games.   I spent the better part of my 21st birthday here years ago and watched Obama accept his presidential election here, this place will always be special - for various reasons.

If this is the best Mexican food in town, I will never eat Mexican food in Santa Barbara again.  This place is only good for an American palate, meaning pretty bland.   The chips were poor and hard. The salsa was full of spice but made with stewed tomato, not the best I've tasted.  The beans were different, more ranchero style, meaning not mashed, but not too bad a flavor. Just missing that distinctive refried taste.    The Mexican rice was seasoned nicely.   Mr. B had the tongue entree.  He liked it, but to me it had a bad flavor, kind of old or gamey.   The verde sauce was delicious and came on both on the tongue and my cheese enchilada, which was pretty good.    My crispy beef taco I would not get again. Not very flavorful and I tried to step it up with salsa to no avail.

My coworkers and I decided to leave work early and go down the street for happy hour. I had been here before for lunch, which was delicious, so I was sure our time would be great. Wrong - the service is the worst customer service that I've had in a while. We walked in for happy hour (with a reservation) and of the five servers present zero servers acknowledged us. On top of the terrible service, they made us go to the bar to pick up the beverages that we ordered. The mixologist had zero sense of urgency. Mind you, it is 4pm on a Wednesday; the place was empty. This place is delicious for food; however, if you're planning to go for happy hour BEWARE.
Michael Brand a restaurant manager here, I believe, delivers excellent service and care. Thank you Mr. Brand!

Very authentic Italian pizza, as well as pastas and secondis. The sauce with the quail and chicken were delicious! And the pea pesto risotto special was unique and unlike any risotto I've ever had before-I hope to try it again. My only complaint would be that they didn't give us a complimentary dessert for my birthday-which has never happened to me, especially at such a fancy establishment.
Nice reception.  Fast service.  Beautifully decorated.  Great food. Our waitress was super friendly and polite. Nice spot. A little pricey but worth it.
Hidden jewel! The empanadas were tasty and fresh from the oven. Beef was my favorite, delicious.  The cheesecake is also one of the best I ever had!

REALLY appreciate that every employee had a mask on, and even most customers too. Rare to see lately and so necessary. So many people do not understand the severity of COVID. Nice to see Goat Tree is being a good citizen and also keeping their workers safe.  Hadn't been back for a while due to pandemic, but finally came back and outdoor setup is super nice, under those huge palm trees shading the tables. Food is always good, and service is good too.  A bit more upscale than other places, but prices are very reasonable.

Good food but over priced. Ate here twice. Limited menu options on account of covid. Liked the burger and the Buddha bowl was good but still too much for white rice and veggies.  Breakfast was probably the best meal. Delicious French toast! They have you mask unless seated at your outdoor table.

I went to Cafe Fresco for the first time in 7 years. It sort of dropped off my radar as I'm not in Five Points much.    They have lots of good sandwiches. The pizza still above average. The pasta is good.   But I ordered the Chinese chicken salad and it was inedible: romaine lettuce with sesame vinegar and oil, plus dry, tasteless chicken and a few Mandarin oranges. Just horrible and for 14 bucks, totally worthless.   I switched it out for the veggie burger---which was equally horrible (and which I'd never ordered before). A mushy bizarre patty on a brioche bun. The cheese and tomato were edible, but the rest of it went down the garbage disposal.   I remember the chicken tortilla soup being good, but I could be thinking of another place in town I used to go years ago.   Great bakery (big cupcakes ,cake slices and brownies).   But avoid that horrible salad, veggie burger, and tortilla soup. It was PURE oil and it, too, went down the sink.

Fried chicken! Prime Rib! Cavatelli! Anything off the food and beverage menus! The staff is friendly and accommodating. George in the bar really knows how to make a drink and is a great host when dining at the bar. I feel weird about liking a hotel restaurant so much but it really is a stand alone restaurant. Oh and the annual Chamily dinner is not to be missed!

BEST FISH TACOS IN SB. I always get the batter up, 2 crispy fish and 1 shrimp. FIREEE. The sauce on the side is spicy though but I LOVE IT!!!!!! I make everyone go here. Nothing but good vibes. A classic locals spot. Hit the batting cages right after. Cheers
Great vegan meats. Vegan orange chicken was fantastic. Lots of excellent vegan and vegetarian options.

Came here a couple months ago for a family dinner. They were well accommodating for our big group and the service was prompt and polite. The waitress made some great recommendations that were delicious.  We will definitely be back again!

WOW..what a great romantic restaurant! We were seated outside and had a great view of the courthouse. The service was great. Everyone working there was very attentive. We shared the brussels spouts and that was great! The skirt steak really was flavorful with the sauce and the sides. Highly recommend you try it!

Montecito Coffee Shop delivers quality food and service every time.   Breakfast  is lovely and they have an excellent selection to truly enjoy.  Lunch during the week except Sunday is traditionally delicious, fresh and well prepared.   No problem bring the pets they will receive excellent service with fresh water and a nice place to sit down when experiencing the front patio dining area.   Always good food with friendly local staff that make you feel welcome.  Don't hesitate, meet family and friends, they will enjoy it too!

Outstanding meal!  If you want to skip the line order online and walk around until ready!  Had basically every taco there and they were all delicious. Fresh tortillas send it over the top and all meat were well marinated and cooked without being overdone and dry like most places. Expect meals prepared as you order. Will definitely stop in again!  Only down side is kids quesadilla is half a quesadilla and comes with rice. Would be nice to have a straight up quesadilla.

This place is a gem! Great food, big portions, friendly service, vegetarian and vegan AND gluten free options! Pretty patio seating in the heart of Santa Barbara. I haven't tried anything that I haven't loved.
Consistent with delicious sushi and friendly staff. Sushi happy hour (5-6pm Monday-Thursday) is a great deal. I also recommend their Negi Maki. One of our favorite neighborhood places.

One of my favorite stores. Just a small step under Whole Foods. Great selection and if you just want to grab lunch or dinner there are so many options. Sushi and sandwiches are always a good choice. I'm here at least twice a week..

This place is much like Santa Barbara itself: overpriced, way overrated, and generally mediocre. As others have said the seating system is confusing and nothing about the experience is worth tipping for. I've had my order served warmer and experienced better service at the Denny's across the street. If I wanted cold eggs and sandwich meat for breakfast I would have gone to 7-11 and it would've cost me 1/5 as much.

Crushcakes is such a cute shop in downtown SB and is always busy all day everyday! Surprisingly, my favorite flavor of cupcake here is the lemon...my heart is smiling just thinking about it. Their red velvet cupcake is mediocre compared to Sprinkles or Doughboys in LA simply because they use sour cream rather than cream cheese as their frosting. I know you're trying to be different with the frosting, but cream cheese is the only way to go for red velvet!!! The staff is extremely friendly and I would definitely recommend this place over WhoDidily cupcakes on the other side of State St., simply because you get your money's worth at Crushcakes.

We weren't actually looking for this place in particular. Just driving up and down the coast looking to see what was available for breakfast. Came upon a cute like breakfast/brunch place rich next to the beach. It was a nice day so we decided to sit outside. My favorite thing about this restaurant is that they give you chocolate chip mini muffins before your meal. Super great food! The service was okay.
Great thai food locally. I love their curry and their noodles. Awesome flavor and great experience every time I have eaten there.

Best tapas Santa Barbara has to offer. Obsessed. Not obsessed with their prices though so if you're feeling like dropping a few hundred you're in the right place...... if not stay farrrr away. Food makes it worth it, outdoor patio is cute
Possibly the best selection of and tastiest muffins in SB.  They are huge and packed with fruit, nuts, and flavor.  Could use some better to-go cups for hot beverages.

I just loved this place!  First off, the place is really cute starting with the outside patio that makes you think you're in a garden somewhere. The next item I have to compliment is the bloody mary.  It was really fresh tasting and just the right amount of zing.  For brunch, I had the brioche french toast with mascarpone which was bsolutely delicious.  All in all everyone's dishes look amazing.  They also have these nice little touches like homemade granola samplers topped with a dollop of yogurt and your check comes with homeade toffee.  If I am back in SB it is definitely on my list of places to go back to.
Always a nice place to go. Aesthetically it's really beautiful. I really enjoyable happy hour also. The seafood stew was a little confusing to me and not that tasty.

Oh, how the mighty have fallen. This used to be one of our favorite Chinese spots in SB. Unfortunately, the food now taste of poor quality (though the flavor is still good), the service terrible, and the cost definitely can no longer be justified. Very sad.

Met friends to have dinner somewhere else that was not open for dining in and we decided to go here instead.  Staff was amazing, super friendly and the drinks are pieces of art.  Almost too pretty to drink....almost!  Great small plates.  Great staff!

I did some light math, and I'm pretty sure I've spent over $1000 here. Granted this was over the span of 10 years since high school so not that bad.  Food: 5/5. The. Torta. I've gone to dozens of places in Southern California looking for something similar, but to no avail. A perfectly toasted bread, simple delicious carne asada, grilled veggies, cheese (seems like mozzarella), lettuce, tomato, and mayo. To reiterate, I've gone to dozens, and dozens, and DOZENS of other places and nowhere comes close (for this torta).  Other items I've eaten (that are also good): California burrito Fries and meat  Breakfast burritos  Chile Relleno  Service: 5/5. You order at the counter and they call your number for you grab your food. With that said, every person that has ever taken my order at this location has always been super sweet. Food comes pretty quick too.  Venue: 4/5. I love the hole-in-the-wall feel and the outdoor seating is great for that Santa Barbara weather. Not the cleanest place sometimes, but again, hole-in-the-wall.  I'm sad my parents don't live in SB anymore, so my visits there are becoming less and less frequent. My search for a torta equivalent in Southern California will continue and until then, this spot will always be a must-go-to every time I visit SB.
Another great restaurant here in Santa Barbara !!! food and service is quite on point . Mizza is delicious establishment

I've been here a few times now and have tried a number of different menu items. I've never been disappointed! The made to order items are just as delicious as the pastries. I highly recommend trying the croque-madame (a French dish, it's a grilled ham and cheese sandwich with a fried egg on top). They make it on their homemade bread, which is nice and thick.

I love the interior space, it's super cute. I love the idea & the menu layout BUT ...NO GLUTEN FREE?!?!?!??? So confused.... you don't need to have 100% gluten contamination free offerings to cater to many many people who have less severe allergies. I am a celiac but I can eat something made in the same space as gluten.. I can eat fries from a frier that has had gluten in it with 0 problems. Many MANY people are the same. I cannot however of course eat bread or a biscuit etc made from wheat flour. I wish they catered to help us gluten intolerant out a bit more... just more GF options on the menu would help

I love this place. The food is absolutely delicious. You really see the difference when everything is freshly prepared. They're still tinkering with the menu, so some of my favorites have disappeared, which is just as well because otherwise I would never have tried anything else on the menu. (Still, I'd love to see the Questlove salad back again, guys, and the full-size yogurt parfait!) Nowadays I am always getting the Fried Chicken Tugboat (biscuit, fried chicken, poached egg, avocado, and sauce, so good) or the french toast with fruit. They're usually just open for breakfast and lunch, but they're also open in the evening when there's a concert at the Bowl.

I've been here twice - the food is always pretty good. I had this polenta brunch item a while back and this weekend I had their Fig burger which was tasty and huge! I upgraded to one of their roasted beet sides for $1.5 or something more.   But as others have stated - service is mediocre. It's not terrible, but it just feels...somewhat insincere and a little scattered. Doesn't ever feel that joyous.  I would come back because I like the food and the area is super cute but the ambience isn't wow.

Mesa Cafe is a cute, homey neighborhood diner in the Mesa. I went for the Saturday of Thanksgiving weekend with my husband, toddler, and in-laws, and it was a great spot for a casual lunch, where we could park freely and walk in with three generations of diners.  The place was busy but not overrun, and we got a corner table right away with booth seating and room for a high chair. The baby was immediately supplied with crayons and a kids' menu--his first time getting crayons at a restaurant, and he definitely made use of them. He also enjoyed looking around at all the other tables full of families, as well as the colorful surfboards on the walls. Both the atmosphere and the service were cheerful and welcoming.  I was happy with my food, too. I ordered the chilaquiles rojos and added tri-tip, and this was a good, hearty meal. The chilaquiles were tortilla chips smothered with red sauce, topped with two eggs (I got mine over-easy) and served with guacamole, sour cream, feta cheese, and green onions. Solid, and while the tri-tip was nothing special, it did add some nice heft to my lunch (I do tend to want meat when it isn't there).  I wouldn't go out of my way to go to Mesa Cafe, but it's the kind of place I'll probably go back to because it's pleasant and convenient. I liked my chilaquiles, my son liked his crayons and grilled cheese. We all left pretty happy with the experience.

Hands down the best restaurant i've ever dined at in Santa Barbara. I can't wait until my next trip down to SB as this place is a must!  Friendly staff, well stocked bar and pizzas are hand tossed, wood-fired and topped with fresh ingredients. I'm a craft beer addict and being able to try the Italian Re'Ale pale ale sent my taste buzz into a craze that I had to purchase bottles to go!  Very clean place to dine and the atmosphere is superb!
All places need a greeter like Maggie who makes guests feel so welcomed and looked after.  'Invest in joy' she said, and I am writing it down so I will remember.

Burger was okay.  Sadly due to a gluten allergy I couldn't try the fries as their fryer is contaminated.  It seemed a bit overpriced for what it was... gluten free bun fell apart leaving me to believe it would have been better off lettuce wrapped.

Great food but worst OJ in town... Menu says 'fresh OJ ' wtf that's false advertising... I ended up paying $9 after tip and tax for a 99 cent OJ of fake canned/Tropicana/Minute Maid airline or Denny/MCD type OJ. Definitely not fresh nor pressed. C'est se foutre du monde ça. This is the garbage mass produced low cost loaded with artificial sugars OJ you buy in supermarkets. Definitely not Naked or Odwalla where you have pulp. If you want real OJ go to Jeannine's, The Shop or Tre Lune/D'Angelos. Now for good the Mediterranean & Baja scramble & omelette are phenomenal and the diner atmosphere & decoration is great as is service very nice/professional. This feels like a real classic American diner that you get on east coast or in Midwest and always has. Only one in SB now that Essau's is gone. Worker Bee in Carp is phenomenal old school diner too highly recommend. Momma bear would bring me here as a kid after school with my nerd folder organizer box in 80's for chocolate milkshakes at the counter, they had best in town. They still have real milkshakes made with those special classic blenders here so that's great. This is under new ownership from group that owns Brophy. I love all their restaurants but this OJ here has to go. Also my toast was served cold. The food is very good just disgusting OJ, not fresh at all and way overpriced. This was always a true locals spot and still has that vibe so that's a good thing. The new owners did a great job respecting the history of restaurant while also modernizing it. This is one of oldest eateries in town with Joe's. Nostalgia 101.
I had the Pad Thai, Pineapple curry, Tom yum! It was all amazing and the service was excellent! I will definitely be back soon!!
There were so many good reviews, but it was just okay. We had Pud Thai, Pork soup and beef soup bowls.  Also, the price was too high for an open market setting...

I took a little trip to Santa Barbara last week.....Sunday.  Hadn't been up that way since the virus hit us, so it had been a while.  For the past few months, it seems things have eased up a bit and many restaurants have moved tables out to the side walks and even onto parts of the street.  State Street had 3 or 4 blocks completely blocked off to traffic and the tables were numerous for the restaurants.   One of my favorite restaurants, Holdren's, had tables set up all the way out almost to the center of State Street, like many of the other places.  But!  They also allowed for their bar to be open!!....... as long as the seats were properly distanced from others.  So, I chose the bar and decided to have a look at their menu.  Nope!  My favorite meal, turkey and dressing, wasn't there.  Too bad, but not a complete surprise.......... it was a specialty dish they served on Thanksgiving, which I've experienced several times (and will probably do the same this coming November) and always a favorable time with ample portions of everything and of course, the world famous (maybe) butternut squash soup. So I decided to try the Seafood Linguini.  EXCELLENT.  An ample array of seafood items, clams, mussels, shrimp, huge scallops, all served over the pasta and with a terrific sauce. The barkeep, I believe he said his name was Andrew (but I might be wrong) asked me at the proper moment if everything was okay, would I like some grated cheese, more bread, etc.  Just the right amount of attention. I've never had less than an excellent experience at this restaurant.

I haven't actually been to many Lazy Acres Markets, but if the service is like this all over then good for them! The people are always very helpful and willing to go the extra mile for you. Great job!

Really good mexican food. I recommend getting the chile rellenos here.  Just getting one with beans and rice is sufficient. I also like the fact they have three different salsas they bring out. Not to mention it is in the funk zone of santa barbara which is a really happening spot!

While I found the facility and grounds breathtaking , the business end of this place is lacking . And unfortunately when you have a family wedding there , and are at the mercy of their business/accounting it's too bad that they send you away mad and disappointed .  1) Wedding coordinator actually sent the bride to the 'altar' before the groom was told to go there, and bride spent a full minute waiting for him while he was waiting for coordinator to tell him to go! He was literally around the corner waiting! 2) It took forever to check in even though we were the only ones waiting at the desk because the agents spend 10 min helping other guests who should be with a concierge. Don't keep me waiting forever while you show the guy in front of me where the walking trails are in Santa Barbara! Hire a concierge and let these agents check in guests !! This happened all weekend !  3) Morning after the wedding our fire alarm starts chirping relentlessly every 15 seconds while we are trying to sleep!! After calling the desk 3 times before they finally picked up, they sent an engineer 30 min later, he looked like he fixed it, but it continues to chirp after he left!!! We left at 11, it was still going!! 4) We were asked at checkin if we wanted a paper on Sunday morning, but it never showed up, and again I called but was told 'we are all out '. Annoying . 5) And finally , I come home to find that they charged my credit card for 2rooms not 1, and have not heard back from mgmt regarding my refund !! $760 Go there for a cocktail on terrace, enjoy the view then leave!
Always good food but the burrito they created for Santa Barbara Buritto week was the best. I hope they keep it on their menu.

The food was good but the wait to order was ridiculous.  The menu ranges from salads to burgers and everything seemed fresh.  My burger was delicious and the staff were good.  Would love to give this place more stars but the waiting really bummed me out.

After finally getting over the fact that this rusty's is what replaced the legendary bee-bop burgers. I decided to finally visit this location, months later this hands down is my favorite rustys location in town. For the most part it's very mellow due to the fact that it's the most unknown of all the locations in town. Very spacious which is awesome for my 2 year old that loves to run around. They have 3 party rooms. We used two of the 3 for my son's party. Don't expect to be blown away by the food. It's rusty's and will taste the same no matter which location you go to. But you will get a greater salad bar selection vs the locations on Milpas, Carrillo, and Upper State. All in all despite that fact that it replaced bee- bop. I'm putting my emotions aside and saying I highly reccomend this location.
The service is excellent and the fish is fresh and prepared to perfection!  A favorite of ours in in Santa Barbara!

I've recommended this place to hotel guests all summer since it's right across the street (Hyatt Santa Barbara), and have only just tried it for the first time yesterday. Unfortunately, we missed breakfast so I only got to try their lunch food.  My friend and I split the fish fajita burrito and the chicken sandwich (choice of french fries or salad with both). The french fries are decent! A little soggy, in my opinion. The fish fajita burrito was the biggest disappointment.. Literally just fish and bell peppers inside :( Such a sad soggy burrito! It could definitely do with some additional ingredients inside, for almost $11 too! The chicken sandwich was alright. It came with sprouts and some sort of sauce I can't remember what. The chicken was fairly dry, but I liked the sandwich more than the burrito.   Their fried calamari is apparently a 'must try!!' and two of my friends got it and the plate looked pretty sad. It didn't look fulfilling at all. The fish and chips and the tri-tip sandwich looked like the best bang for your buck that you could get out of everything I saw my friends order.  You can order pitchers of beer here! You can also get fruit smoothies, ice cream, and milkshakes. I may come again for breakfast but not so sure about lunch. (I wanted to order their blueberry wheat germ pancakes! They also have banana wheat germ and one other kind).   The location is great, super convenient for those who are at the beach so obviously business will likely stay consistently well.

I went with 3 friends and I ordered what the server recommended: 'seafood soup.' and I ordered a 'bloody Jerry,' their version of a bloody Mary but made with Clamato juice. I gave it a whirl... yuck! I could barely taste the vodka and doubted there was any even in it and as for calmato juice, the drink was a pale pink served in a martini glass with a HUGE trimmed stalk of celery. and the glass rim covered in dry pepper! HUH???  And the poor soup: there were exactly two shrimp and 3 calamari and it was loaded with raggedly chopped white onion... so much onion that it was used as a 'vegetable' to hide the fact the cook had NO IDEA on preparing a broth... and the 'garlic toast' had not 1 bit of garlic on it. I refused to drink the broth, but I managed to nibble down the 'fish'. It was a friend's birthday so I did not want to make a stink in front of her. I just calmly said, 'uh uh,' and shook my head in dismay. The broth was a peppery mop water. HORRIBLE!!!  I at first asked the server, 'May I taste the soup?' 'No,' she said, 'because they make it from scratch.' BEWARE... that soup was wicked bad. That 'cook' knew nothing about soup and it cost $15 for that bowl of poop water.

I visit this place today , the place is gross and I slow, one of the employees was yelling in Spanish to another employee using bad worlds , the cashier forgot about my coffee, I ordered ranchero eggs and the place was half beans and cheese , the tortilla was soggy and the guacamole starting to get black , I regret visiting this place . I wish they did turn down the annoying music in the background. The cook has a very messy hair and no gloves or apron .

Had the Brussels sprouts and chicken wings (lemon pepper). They were both a tad bit vinegary, but they were still very tasty. The Brussels sprouts has bacon pieces which complemented the sprouts very well. The portions were good. The service was a less than what I expected. I had to ask at least two or three different people for water and coffee refill. But since the food (both in taste and portions) was actually pretty good so I would go back here again.

Great Irish Pub! I love getting the potato wedges and a Guinness or the Guinness tasting. The onion soup is tasty ,good burgers and traditional Irish pub food. I like all the options for seats either outside,the bar or indoor seating by the little fireplace. Fun place to go before or after a movie or going out .

Yes yes and yes. Honor Bar gets it. Simple but super tasty menu. Always high quality food. Ding's Chicken Sandwich is amazing. Service is always a 10. They must have a serious training program. From hosts to servers to bartenders, all so friendly. This is how you earn repeat guest visits. I'm a big fan.

This place is great for a food coma. The portions are huge at a relatively inexpensive price. Plus even though it's not super fancy it's always clean and the staff are nice. I always get the enchiladas when I'm here and can barely finish my plate. Whenever I've gone it's been pretty empty (which can be nice). But that might be because it's a bit out of the way and kinda hidden if you're not already familiar with the place (or haven't seen the sign).

Service was a little slower than other Peet's but the staff was friendly. No outdoor seating, which was disappointing. The indoor seating is decent with privacy somewhat to each table for working &/or talking. The place is somewhat clean & stocked.
Great little restaurant in SB. Nice atmosphere. Good food, simple yet refined and well prepared. Very nice staff and service. Well priced.

My gf and I went there as a stroll down memory lane...well, a stroll down her memory lane.  She used to go there 20 years ago as a kid when her parents took her and her sister camping in Carpinteria.  The muffins they give you to chomp on before your meal comes out were excellent.  The eggs and hashbrowns/potatoes were good and what you'd expect.  The pancakes were fluffy and delicious.  If ownership from Sambo's is reading this: bring back the old indoor paintjob of Little Black Sambo...my gf was so sad to see it was gone!

I was contacted quickly by their head office.  I was offered a free pizza at my next visit.  I accepted their offer and a month later I returned for the same pizza I always order.  It was cooked great and the cheese and toppings were as they should be.  I have returned another time since then.  I guess everyone has a bad day once in a while.  I will continue to make my regular trips.

Came today for 2 'quick' to-go fish tacos at the take out window. The cooks in the back refused to do the orders and only focused on the restaurant orders. After 45 minutes we, along with about 5 other customers, got our money back and gave up. They hadn't even started our orders by the time we brought the manager in. It seems like someone told the cooks to focus on restaurant orders only. Sad to say but you lost our business forever. Poor management.
They sent me a pizza with nothing on it. Didn't even have the audacity to call and confirm/tell me they didn't have certain ingredients  .

Back in Santa Barbara is another sushi place, at Sushi Bar 29. They had tempura ice cream, and if you know me, this is one of my favorite desserts because of how it defies logic by frying ice cream in hot oil. Here, the tempura batter was fried nice and crisp which added great texture to the cold smooth green tea ice cream in the interior. This version is an adequate serving of the dish and nothing more.  Beef Sukiyaki is an excellent dish that I rarely find in the Santa Barbara area. The broth here was excellent -- it had a strong soy and mirin flavor as expected and really nailed the taste. But don't be fooled by the large pot because it only contains a few pieces of low quality beef and a few pieces of vegetable and a small bunch of yam noodles. The amount of content in the bowl was very minimal, which is really a shame.  Fresh, sweet, juicy uni at Sushi Bar 29! While smaller and more pricey ($16) than Sun Sushi, their uni still hits the quality mark as they are fresh, vibrant, and delicious. Highly recommend everyone to try some fresh sea urchin in their lifetime.  My biggest problem with this restaurant is that place has both the unpleasant attributes of penny pinching and expensive food. I think it's pretty ridiculous for an upscale restaurant to charge for something as basic as tea, sauce, and condiments if you ask me.

Yellow Belly is one of my new favorite places. It always seems to be bustling but it has a cozy feel to it. There are two outdoor patios and a small indoor area. The ambiance makes YB seem more like a house than a restaurant. The beer is great and the servers are friendly. I had the brussels sprouts and Mac n cheese. Topped that off with sharing their delicious chocolate chip cookie with my friend. Overall, YB is a great place to hang out and grab a bite. I will definitely be back.

Delicious spicy broth. When people say, 'spicy', it's usually mild, but this broth was pretty spot on with description and flavor. The noddles taste authentic and are textured as opposed to tasting like generic plain attempted ramen noodles. Service was average. We called ahead for a group of 6 and they added 20% gratuity.
The burger and beer for $6 is a great deal. our burgers tasted like it was off the backyard grill.
I can't wait until they open back up, I'm going through withdrawal!! This is the best sushi in Santa Barbara!

Eh, Had the steak and shrimp. Shrimp was really good. Steak was something like i got in the galley when I was in the Navy. Kinda tough and not much flavor. I feel this place is old school for a more older clientele and has a dinner club feel. The service was adequate but it seems iike the staff just didn't give a $hit. First time there and I just feel it's not for me.  Would have like more of a selection at the salad bar.
I love this place. Cute decor, bomb tacos and a great vibe! Plus, you can hit the batting cages afterwards. Then even have beer! Beach Tacos and beer...Heaven. plus they have these

BEST hidden gem in Santa Barbara! I've been coming here for 30 plus years for birthdays, lunches... the beer. They have a wonderful array of delicious on-tap European beer varieties that you can't get anywhere else in town. The German potato salad is AMAZING! Ken and Laurie have been foodie mainstays in SB for years. Laurie's pies are phenomenal! The setting is unique and so refreshing, after seeing shi-shi red tiled expensive places come and go. I love the outdoor patio, the low roofs, the decor, the kitsch (in a really good way). Delicious soups. I'm going to be heartbroken to see it go... R.I.P.

Has continued to be one of my go to spots for Mexican foods for burritos . Always excellent and consistent and waaaay better than some of the trendy Mexican spots on Santa Barbara. Def better than Super Cucas across the street. If you go, def get some of the pink habernero  salsa. OMG is it HOT but all tasty at the same time. Great local hole in the wall. Every thing is good. Burritos, tortas or even the dishes on the side that change daily. Try it all and you will not be disappointed. Wish I still lived in SB so i could go there right now.

Great customer service, coffee, food, and of course cupcakes too! Often have CrushCakes for lunch meetings and have used for catering breakfast meetings. Affordable and the presentation was great too.

Absolutely love Shalhoobs, what a gem.  Not only are their tri tip sandwiches (which come with amazing fries) delectable, they also have a wide variety of other food. Anything with tri tip at this place is going is going to make you happy as it melts in your mouth and gives you a flavor explosion.   My other favorite is their avocado salad. It comes with lots of chopped avocado, crunchy broccoli and it is just really well put together and flavorful. To add meat it does get a little pricy to around $17, but if you want a healthier alternative and still get the tri tip, it's well worth it.   Other things I've seen that are so exciting but I haven't tried on the menu are their street corn apps, their falafels, and a crispy chicken sandwich.  The atmosphere is so great and inviting, perfect for larger groups, and the food comes out quick!  Definitely drop by whenever you're around, you'll be happy you did.  - J

Thank you so much for the delicious lasagna and salad dinner to go tonight.  What a perfect meal for a Coronavirus Monday night. Kudos to Michael Amador for keeping our tummies happy and full during this difficult time.  We and appreciate all you do for your community

I had high hopes for this place, but it fell flat big time. I ordered the BLAT with grilled chicken and a side salad. I specifically asked for no tomato and they put tomato on both the sandwich and the salad. Not a huge deal, just annoying to pick it all off. And if you have an allergy, I would not trust this place to be mindful of that. The grilled chicken was not seasoned at all and the food overall was just extremely bland. My sandwich was stacked so high that the avocado all fell out as I was eating it. The side salad is lettuce, cucumber, red onion, and tomato. My bf got a burger with onion rings and he did not enjoy either part of the meal. The onion rings are beer battered and basically all batter. The burger was also unseasoned and not enjoyable. The service is ok. The staff is nice enough but my bf did have to get up and ask for his soda to be refilled and we were never offered a refill on our waters. We both left the restaurant with a stomach ache, I personally felt ill. I had stomach pains for a few hours afterwards and I can only assume it's because the food is not fresh because my meal didn't have any allergens that would've given me that reaction. Lastly, the cleaning spray they used on the tables just smelled funky. Idk how to explain it but we actually moved to an outside table because the inside one smelled so musty and mildewy.  The patio is nice, but not worth coming back for. Additionally, the menu is too scattered and overwhelming. I think they should stick to one type of food and work on increasing the quality rather than having too many different menu options that aren't cohesive.

We've been back a few more times and the food has been consistently good!  I've taken out of town family there and they liked it, too.  Even my son says it's good and he's a chef! Go, you will be happy.

The food here is delicious! We have received poor service a few times unfortunately but I guess they don't have to be that helpful when the food speaks for itself. We keep going back despite it because I can't get enough of their ahi ceviche!

What a little gem this place is. Not only is the entire staff outstanding but they are the only outlet I can get quality licorice tea and raw valerian root! If I had to put a face on Santa Barbara it would be Vices & Spices!  For valerian extract: The longer it sits, the more potent it gets!

If you want crepes in Santa Barbara, this is the place to go! Mix and match a huge array of fillings to make your perfect crepe or have them pick one for you. They've got everything, from sweet to savory to hearty.   It's a mom n' pop business and the owner Ivan is very friendly. The place is a bit rustic and laid back -I wouldn't stop in if you're in a rush. A great lunch, date or brunch spot, with outdoor seating for smaller parties. Try their French onion soup!
Lived in Santa Barbara for 11 years and Holdren's was always consistent. Any of the steaks are a great choice but my all time favorite is the sesame seared ahi tuna with garlic mashed potatoes.

When I first moved to SB, the one Indian spot I happened to try out was let's say....lackluster. I thought, well I guess that's it for me and my love of Indian food here. Come to find this gem was opening right on State Street and I couldn't be more thrilled. I've pretty much tried the whole menu (not all at once calm down) and I cant stop singing their praises. Give it a try you won't be disappointed! Chefs menu is amazing and let's you try a little of everything. Only wish they had more than beer and wine but hey, no biggie when the foods that good.   Service: 5 Food: 5 Presentation: 5 Ambience: 4

I got the spaghetti bolognese.   The sauce was really bad...too heavy on the basil or oregano or both....It was inedible.  I told the manager that I didn't like it and wanted something else.  He pointed at the bowl of spaghetti and asked 'What am I supposed to do with this then?'   I have some ideas for what he can do with it.    They all involve shoving it somewhere private.
Gotta love this place! Portions big enough to feed an army and lots of good cocktails!!!!

Love this place. Imagine Vietnamese style tacos, or Japanese fusion tacos or poke fusion tacos, this place has it all. We loved everything we tried and will definately be back. If you love tacos then you will definately love this place.   I mean I had a poke taco with a crunchy crust that was amazing. The bahn mi taco was delicious, the pickled vegetables always do it for me. And let's not leave out the spicy shrimp taco and a rice ball filled with spicy tuna. It was all amazing! We will definately be back!

I honestly don't know what has been done to this place but this is not the Pascucci I have been coming to for years and it USED to be my favorite italian spot. Right before I came in I told my partner this is one of the best italian spots in town, you'll have to understand my embarrasment when the food was TERRIBLE. Let me first start off by saying the waiter and waitress were good. First thing, what the hell happened to the cheesy bread that always was served with every meal? I ordered the shalhoob bacon wrapped 8 ounce steak medium well done with with mushroom sauce on top with Italian broccoli and a side ceaser, my girl ordered the spicy garlic shrimp and some crispy triangles. The only thing that was decent was the shrimp, the steak came out SO raw it had to be sent back to be recooked, the mushroom sauce was cold, it came back overcooked and burnt on the outside, and tasted gummy, the italian broccoli was disgusting and mushy, like they steamed it first and then baked it, those triangle crispy things were not good at all, it was mushy and you could only taste the cheese, no pine nuts or sundried tomato taste, had a mushy pasteyness to it. Its literally like i looked at the menu saw some great things and got served crap. So the meal came out to like $60, they took off $5 for this horrid meal. I dont know if the chef or the owner is different but this food used to be good, it is a horrible shell of its former self and I will not waste money on this place again when there are other italian spots that take pride in their food. This doesn't deserve 2 stars, the only reason you dont get a 1 star is because I remember the good days. Presto pasta is now better eats.

Tried Mesa Pizza for the 1st time the other night and our pizza was super yummy! We got the White Chicken pizza, pizzas with white sauce are always my favorite, and it was one of the best I've ever had! White sauces on pizza can have a tendency to be overly rich, but this one was just right. Will definitely be coming back soon!

Amazing causal taco place located near Downtown Santa Barbara! I needed some tacos after wine tasting so decided to come here! The line was out the door but it was definitely worth the wait! I ordered carne asada, chicken fajitas and al pastor! Delicious!!! The salsa bar was fully stacked with all the options you need for tacos!
This is the original location for this great teriyaki house (the other two are in Goleta and Isla Vista...oh my!) Love the salmon teriyaki and it's cheap!  Can't really say about their sushi though.

This place is great. I get their tacos. They put a creative spin on them. Set in the public market makes it easy to get a beer right there and then get Ice cream or a cookie to top off the experience.
always get the chile relleno burrito. its delicious. they always use fresh ingredients which you can taste. its no wonder they've already expanded to a second location.
Expect ok food, sometimes bad. The location makes up for the uninspired food. Order dishes that are hard to screw up and enjoy the view and location.

My table got so many weird looks from the employees that we couldn't enjoy the food. One employee kept walking by with a disgusted look on her face. They were looking at our food most of the time, but it made us question if there was something wrong with our food. That made us lose our appetite.   We were also getting looks from the kitchen. Maybe they were checking us out? Either way that is not the experience we wanted, especially since their tapas are not cheap.   We had a reservation, but after being seated it took a while until a waiter came. It was probably about 15-20 minutes before we got water.   We ordered a couple drinks, a few of the bites and tapas, and the seafood paella. My favorite was what ever tomato sauce was in the prawn tapa. My favorite was tomato sauce and bread. TOMATO SAUCE AND FREAKING BREAD. Everything else was ehh.   Our waitress was nice, but our overall experience was a night to not remember. We were uncomfortable.

So goooood, I can still taste the warm moist Gluten Free Coffee Cake!  Located on upper State Street in Santa Barbara, Lilac Pâtisserie is a new addition to our local epicurean culinary artisans.  The ambiance is simple and elegant on a soft white backdrop allowing the pastries to have center stage showcasing their beautiful colors.  A friendly staff greets you as you walk in while your eyes wander across the many choices of Gluten Free delights. Skeptical at first, I tired a few of their samples and I instantly became a loyal patron. The young gal recommend their Coffee Cake freshly baked and still warm from the oven.  Paired with a classic cappuccino and an Astrology magazine I could feel the stars align as I took my first bite of heavenly delight.

The remodel is awesome, it looks like a picturesque restaurant with the sun setting in the background...maybe even somewhere you could propose to your high school sweet hear.  If it wasn't for the HAIR deep-fried INTO my french fry it would have gotten a little higher score.  OH..and not did they put their special topping on my fries, but my mom's salad as well (yes..I went there with my mom..get over it).  I mentioned something to the waitress who was really apologetic and nice. She said the manager would come out soon, but no manager showed up..(probably getting a haircut in the back). They offered a free dessert, but wasn't really in the mood to see that dish. We still paid full price, and got no apology from 'the manager'.  I think 2 stars is for the beach.

In my opinion pickles and Swiss is one of the best sandwich shops in town. All the ingredients are fresh, the staff is friendly. Will and Lovens are always so helpful and welcoming. I'm also addicted to their popcorn!!!

I used to buy my cheese and meats from them my restaurant. They always treated me awesome and let me know the stage the cheese was in (if it needed to sit around etc). I was always addressed by name and they took a sincere interested in how my business was going. They also support local businesses with deals etc. often grabbed the truffle grilled cheese while picking up my orders! If we did not move out of SB I would still be there several times a week!  They do know what they are talking about. I liked it better when it was just the shop but hope the restaurant area works out well for them. Great management also(I forget his name)   Much better than any other cheese/meat shop in town!

I am here 5 days a week with my Labrador dog Leo & must be best restaurant in Santa Barbara. It's a complete meal with all food groups Bread, Vegetables, Meat, and Cheese with rotisserie Chicken or Lamb/Beef 5 stars!!

Perfect spot for date night, a birthday, or celebrating an occasion! Because Zaytoon is a couple blocks off State Street, I think you have a better chance of not having to wait if you walk in, which is what I did on a Saturday evening. The ambience is so charming and romantic with all the string lights, fire pits, and fountain. There are currently no belly dancers, hookah, or indoor seating, but the outdoor seating has plenty of heaters.  Our cocktails, the Farmer's Passion and Mezza Mule, had the perfect balance of alcohol and sweetness, and you bet I ate the passion fruit garnish! The beef sambosik and mixed shawarma were incredibly flavorful, but the kebab combo plate was the star. Each kebab, including the chicken, was juicy and tender. Despite all the meat, the meal wasn't too heavy because of the refreshing fattoush salad. Tip: ask for the free pita to make little pita pockets! I couldn't have asked for a better meal!

Very good margaritas!!! I had the mole chicken enchiladas,my favorite. Food was good.The prices seem better than downtown Santa Barbara. I would give it a 5 star if the atmosphere was a little better. Maybe just a nice tablecloth with a candle would be a little more classy.

Amazing hotel with such such comfy rooms. We had a iv and there was an area right outside the room where he could go in the morning..so so convenient..there was a small lawn with a gorgeous fountain right outside the room which was awesome. The GYM WAS BEYOND FANTASTIC!!! the views were great. Service was great at the coffee shop and the bar. Room service we got it in the lobby was great. THE YUCCA FRIES ARE A MUST Have!!! The chips and guacamole were totally yum! We got takeout in the lobby and hung out which was so fun! Having a fridge and microwave in the room was so helpful. We will definitely come back. Highly recommend!
come grab an empanada and a beer from draughtsmen. A+ bites to go with great beer! love this little spot

Yum! I can't think of a reason not to go to Pizza Guru. It has a warm, local feel to it from the atmosphere to the food. Organic meat in SB can be hard to come by but no need to worry when dining in the guru, the have your back. This place got it right.

Still great after all these years.  We stopped by on a Thursday night to see if they had an outdoor table for us and our dog. After a quick check to make sure it didn't interfere with any reservations, we got seated right away at a table on the sidewalk and our dog laid on the ground and got lots of attention from the employees.  Because of Covid, they also built a little outdoor parklet, which I hope they are able to continue using because it is a really great addition to their set up. It was also really well constructed and you could tell they put a lot of effort into building it, with the paint job, the cute lighting and shade sails, etc.  Additionally, their website is updated everyday with not only their specials, but updates about the restaurant itself and how they're running it. Today's update was to mention that their staff will continue to wear masks for another month just in case there's any unexpected consequences of re-opening. It's nice to see how much effort they're putting in to keeping everyone safe and informed.  Onto the food and drinks. I got a passion fruit martini and my husband got an old fashioned. Both were delicious.  They still offer a bread basket which was great and the butter was nice and soft.  We got the salmon tacos for an appetizer - it was really good.  My husband got the pasilla crusted steak and loved it. I've had it before and it is really delicious.  I was really in the mood for seafood, so I got the lemongrass crusted salmon and it was phenomenal. The salmon was HUGE and the curry sauce it came with was really, really good. It also came with rice and julienned vegetables which were light and refreshing.  Forgot to take photos of the food this time, but next time we are in Santa Barbara, we will come back like we always do and enjoy a delicious meal.

Another neighborhood gem, where families with kids, big family groups, friends and couples are comfortable, and enjoy great food. You order at the counter, and they bring the food to you, along with an appetizer of very good chips. The salsa is at the salsa bar, and there is a really good selection. This isn't your run-of-the-mill Mexican restaurant. It's upscale, and the creativity is off the hook. Be sure to check the list of specials. I got the shrimp in a tamarind sauce, which was amazing. Six big shrimp, cooked perfectly, in a sweet and slightly spicy sauce, served with a killer salad, and perfect rice. My wife had the Pescado Veracruzano, which was tender, not overdone, and in an inventive twist covered with capers, along with the usual stuff for a good Veracruzano. Yeah, I understand why our friend's friend ate there every day that he was in town. An it was impressive watching what else came out of the kitchen. Beautiful! Service was top-notch.

I am appalled at the rude treatment received by myself and fellow coworker. We walked into Eureka around lunch time and no one knowledge us and they were not that busy. We decided to sit at the bar. No one was there and 2 servers passed by without a glance. We then decided after 10mins to get up again and go to the hostess table since we finally saw someone there and ask for a table. He sat us right by the bar but next to a family. Again many servers walked by and didn't offer us waters or tell us they would be with us in a moment. Another point to bring up is we were by the register in the middle of the area and 2 serves had been ringing up other customers. After another 10-15 minutes we were finally asked if we have been helped. We ordered our food and had to leave as soon as it came. I can understand if they were slammed but to be completely ignored for that amount of time while other customers are being taken care of is rude. Worst customer services experience ever.

I'm so jealous that this is all the out in Santa Barbara. This is the kind of food that we need in the Valley. And to have these quality of tacos inside of a batting cage park, I think we are spoiling children. Or they're really lucky to have this amazing food instead of high sodium hot dogs and chips.  What an amazing breath of fresh air East Beach's tacos are. So clever with their ingredients and flavors. Makes you not look at a taco the same ever again. And after having East Street, why would you?  Honestly, all their tacos are delicious - you cannot go wrong - but the best and most unique taco here is the Ahi Poke Taco. All the flavors from the Hawaiian ahi poke bowl but substitute the rice with a wonton crispy taco shell instead! Me and 11 girls bombarded this joint and literally ordered everything on the menu so we could try and bite different things.   Winner: Ahi Poke Taco Runner Up: Beach Bomb - rice ball stuffed spicy tuna.  WATCH OUT! Don't buy 1 taco for $4, but 3 tacos for $9. I know, i know. Trust me, you will want 3 tacos. Oh and drink refills are free.  I posted food pics on my instagram and I had my followers drooling & begging me to bring some home.

We loved our stay. The hotel is beautiful, the room we had - free upgrade to a suite for Marriott members, was wonderful - It's the Ritz so we expected high prices at the hotel but these are nuts. The only reason I did not give it five stars. The restaurant are more then over priced they are laughable.  We got two drinks at the pool bar. Pina colada's was 50$ plus tax and tip  Passed on the $30 hamburgers  Quick easy drive to great food.  We just brought in water and sodas rather then pay the crazy prices. I was taught  to respect money and the Ritz is worth the money, but the food prices are laughable.

Pretty disappointing. I've been wanting to eat here for several years based on what I thought was a great reputation in town.  I don't get over to the Mesa much but when I saw a coupon in a mailed flyer a couple months ago, I clipped it.  It offered a free entree with purchase of one entree and two drinks. Even with a discount of $12, the bill was $53 for lunch (before tip) -- I can eat a great dinner out for less than this -- so I was expecting a very delicious meal.  Both my companion and I felt the food was vastly overpriced for the quality, flavor and even presentation. The quantity was generous enough but the items we ordered (empanadas and the mushroom burger) were both rather bland.  The 'burger' was served on a doughy, barely cooked pita-like bread rather than on an appropriate item to hold a messy burger in place like a brioche bun.  It was completely falling apart after a few bites... which would have been okay to eat with a fork except it was just not flavorful or complex given the list of ingredients. The most interesting item was the ketchup made from beets.  Also the side fries weren't thoroughly cooked except for the smallest ones.  Definitely not a $24 item! The empanadas were somewhat better but again, not a 'wow' and not even a 'hey, really tasty.' Just bland and again, not worth $24.  The half-off discount didn't even cover the $13 cost of a chai tea and lemonade (that was mostly ice of course).  All in all, very disappointing food and therefore completely overpriced.  We took the food to go so I cannot really comment on the service. When I read another lengthy review (12/5/21), I now see that the prices have been jacked and the quality has been wavering. I totally understand the precarious state of the restaurant business right now during covid but I cannot recommend this restaurant at this time.

Picked up some food from here last night for dinner. Ashley answered the phone and was incredibly helpful. She guided us in the right direction and made sure we got all the utensils and sides we needed to make the take home experience almost like dining in (without the amazing view obviously). The food was amazing as always. Thanks Ashley and the team for making it a great night for us!

I had breakfast here last weekend. The place was very busy. They have expanded into the street and made a 'Parklet' And we're very careful about social distancing. They open at 9 AM and had an amazing menu. I had the lemon ricotta pancakes which were outstanding. The eggs Benedict also, a winner! Everybody ordered something different, and everybody was pleased with their choices. Our waitress was fun and entertaining, And made some excellent recommendations.We will definitely be back.

This place reminds me of the kind of cafes that are in France, Germany, and Switzerland. It's small, fresh, and great coffee and food. You are lucky to have a place like this, Montecito. I hope this business thrives and stays open for the next time I have car trouble and have to stay in Montecito because it's really late and rainy and I needed sleep.

I met the owner of this Brewery back when it opened and complimented him on the beer and design buildout of this location. I go back regularly for the beer and people watching on State Street. Unfortunately the staff and in particular one of the managers were very underwhelming in their service but more importantly the Manager appeared to be a leftover Skater dude with stringy hair and trucker hat that would rather argue with people than work on ways to improve the experience. I stopped going here after 2-3 similar types of experiences. Too many options to get treated like a visiting LA weekend  vs dedicated local beer enthusiasts. The state of service in SB is problematic at best but this kid needs to curb his attitude when confronted with simple expectations from guests. Feel free to PM me for more information. Truly embarrassing experiences for this Location
This was delicious. Every component  and ingredient was thought out and it came across in the flavor. Good portion size too. We were stuffed. The pulled pork was so delicious and tender. Bravo!
Cute place - sandwich shop in an old house.  Very good quality sandwiches and very fresh.  Bread was excellent.

I have lived here for 10 years and had never eaten here. I always thought it was a cafe that didn't really have a lot of food offerings. A friend suggested we eat dinner here before a play and it was so good! It was a Saturday night and we sat right down. There was almost no one else there. The prices were very reasonable and the food was great! I got the Churraso Na Chapa and the combination of everything in one bite was soooooo good. The chimmichurri was a little oily but good. The salmon was delicious and the x burger was very good too. This is a little hidden gem that I will definitely be back to.

Loved the boa - especially mushroom tempura. Kung pao cauliflower was very good - not overly sweet, and the use of cashews complimented the cauliflower. The Szechuan dumplings were good but not exciting, and a little one-note in terms of flavor.

PROS:  - Beautiful interior, very comfortable French bistro feel, brick wall, pretty chandeliers...  - Overall food is very good - Steak Frites is usually very nice (perhaps sometimes too much cinnamon) my friends love the mussels, french onion soup is very nice  - Patio on State Street  CONS:  - Menu is limited. They could use one or 2 more items to pull in a few more people. I would like a few more options for smaller plates to eat.  - Twice I have been there for dinner and it was so warm we were all sweating. That was probably a one-off thing  Looking at the pros and cons, it seems I should have a higher review. It's a nice place, especially if you are with people you like - it's a nice night out....

Gorgeous outdoor patio with super tasty tapas, wine and homemade breads all by the Art Sevtap, the wine maker himself. We were blown away by the quality and freshness, perfect pairings with each of the white wines on the tasting menu. I'm a genuine foodie and usually difficult to please. Can't wait to go back!

One of the few recipients of Michelin's Bib Gourmand in Santa Barbara, Sama Sama serves up high quality reasonably priced Southeast Asian food.  Food - Unique menu features a variety of Asian influenced dishes including their Best of Santa Barbara Signature Wings. I was satisfied with everything we ordered but the Nuri Ribs and the Seared Octopus were phenomenal. The pork fell right off the bones and the house made bbq sauce was addictingly good. The octopus was tender, tasty and arguably the best I've ever had.  Ambience - Lively dinner crowd on Upper State Street that feeds off the energy from The Good Lion located next door. Interior is simple and the walls are bare. The focal point is a large wood bar that has an impressive selection of aromatic herbs. Sama Sama's good value appears to attract to younger crowd.   Service - Walked in last night without a reservation and was seated relatively quickly. Staff to table ratio seemed a bit low causing our waitress to be a bit elusive. That being said our food was prepared surprisingly fast and exceeded our expectations. As an fyi gratuity is automatically applied so no need to pull out your calculator.

Sadly, I didn't get the chance to eat here because I came with my parents and my dog and my mom's dog and when we arrived there was a sign that said NO DOGS. This includes their outdoor seating. We had made a reservation in advance to celebrate Father's Day and drove up from an hour away to go here. They said we could get tri tip sandwiches instead and eat in the parking lot. Hard pass. We had checked Yelp and it currently says that dogs are allowed. If you had time to make a sign outside, you had time to update Yelp and your website. I just rechecked their website and it says nothing about dogs being banned. It was almost 100 degrees so leaving dogs in the car was absolutely out of the question. Please update your website and Yelp to reflect your new policy. I don't think I'll be going out of my way to come back; this experience left a bad taste in my mouth.

We came back here a few times during our vacation.  The acai bowls are just so delicious and refreshing. We grab some on our way to the beach. It made the perfect breakfast on a hot sunny day.  He place does get quite busy in the morning.  It takes them a while to make each one so be prepared to wait.

Nectar hosted a tea and small bites tasting with Far West Tea Traders. What a wonderful unique epicurean experience... Pairing tea with food! I thoroughly enjoyed the boutique tea, especially the cold brew high mountain sourced tea. The Indian inspired vegetarian dishes were a perfect compliment. I hope this becomes a reoccurring event!

The Santa Barbara Fishhouse never disappoints. I had one fish taco one shrimp taco and a beer. The service was quick the food taste great and it was a beautiful afternoon to sit outside on the patio, which has an excellent Oceanview.

After a fun tour of the SB Funk Zone, and a delicious meal at The Lark, we all ventured next door to Pearl Social and were happily seated in their lounge are. Such an enchanting space, and perfect for socializing amongst the beautiful people of Santa Barbara.  Thank you Shannon and Alberto for taking such good care of us all. Shannon's drink recommendations were spot-on for all of us.   Definitely our new favorite chill and cocktail place in the Funk Zone. And thoroughly enjoyed the fun music selections.

Came here while staying in town for a week and had the best bar service I've ever experienced at Eureka . The bartenders recommended one of the best cocktails I've ever had the wild child and they were extremely friendly. The Eureka burger was amazing!!! Napoleon/ Napy was extremely helpful and gave us great service! Thank you and will be back because of this!

Food, service, salsa are all amazing. The prices are very reasonable for what you get. I've been going to their De La Vina location ever since they opened and I will keep on going back. Very accommodating staff

Go here... Like today... Like now... Moms PLT... game changer... the Guy at the counter likes to be a little short sometimes... he's there every time I go... i think he owns the place and might be a bit bitter because he works so much... they still get 5 stars. YES... Nortons...

I ordered the Korean spare ribs. I was disappointed by the quantity of meat on the ribs, there was only five or six bites of meat in the whole meal and it was about $20. I would say that the prices are expensive based on the quantity of food. I thought the sauces in the dish had very good flavor and taste. However, the ribs were burnt to a crisp and tasted ashy. I really like the atmosphere in the restaurant and the fact that they had patio seating. I might go there again but I wouldn't order the same dish. I wouldn't consider this restaurant to be a authentic Vietnamese/Asian food.

I've been in here more times than I can count, and for the most part I always had a good time. The beer garden is great and it's always a good friendly vibe. Last night, I went in for a beer and I hit I in my vape in the beer garden. I always hit my vape back there and no one's ever told me it was against policy. Last night I was kicked out for hitting my vape, and the bar tender couldn't have been more rude and inconsiderate about it. I mean come on man, did you wake up on the wrong side of bed. I've seen people drunkenly stumble in and get noticeably belligerent. I had to awkwardly walk out, after been declined my beer and while people sitting at tables stared at me. Good Job policing the likely evils of my vape considering the pain, trauma, and hell it brought on the innocent customers of Modern Times.

Husband and I walked by Petit Valentien this afternoon and the Ethiopian lunch dishes we saw on the patio looked great, so we stopped in for dinner about 30 minutes ago. We weren't in a huge rush, but we didn't have hours to dine, because I had dough proofing at home and needed to attend to it. Hubby walked in and checked in with waitress and came out saying we should take any seat we wanted and she'd be right out with menus. So, we sat next to the Xmas tree on the patio and waited... and waited... and waited.  We figured she couldn't see us so we moved right next to the front door, where she couldn't miss us. After waiting several minutes, I decided we would wait 5 more minutes and then find somewhere else for dinner. After 3 minutes, a 30-something brunette waitress came and dropped off menus. I let her know the table was wobbly and she said she'd bring a block for it (which she never did). Oddly, I detected a twinge of attitude from the waitress with that exchange... not sure why, but there was an element of nastiness in her tone. She also turned on the table-side heater, which was very effective, if somewhat atmosphere-killing, because of its very bright coils.  I pulled up Yelp to look at the pics of the entrees and we settled on what we wanted to order. After about 5 more minutes, the waitress returned and informed us that dinner would take longer here than other restaurants. I let her know I was proofing dough at home and if we ordered immediately, how long would it take. She replied that if we ordered faster, the food would come faster. (Duh! Crazy how that works!) Again, a twinge of nastiness.  Hubby and I heard the specials and I asked her to repeat the sides on one of the specials. Attitude was ramping up from her. I asked her what her favorite item on the menu was and she replied, 'I can't say. You're the one who's going to eat it, not me! The chef wouldn't even tell you.'   I didn't dare ask another question, because she was in full beeeyatch mode. We both ordered off the regular menu. Then, she said it would be 20 minutes and we ordered wine. Before she left, I asked the waitress if she could please ask the chef not to hold the entree until we finished the appetizer... just to put it all in at once. Her response? 'The chef doesn't need to be micromanaged like that.' And that, my Yelp friends, was my limit to the abuse I was going to take from this witch of a waitress.  I smiled and said thank you, but we aren't going to stay. She said okay, opened the door, walked into the restaurant, and I could hear her behind me exclaiming with a groan as she entered, 'Oh my Gaaaaawwwdd.'  Good thing they're in a tourist zone, because Petit Valentien can have a fresh supply of customers the waitress can abuse. As for this local, I'd recommend one of our other many fine dining establishments and give this one a wide berth, if service is important to you.  Two last comments: First, we noticed the restaurant had a Help Wanted sign posted, looking for servers, which could hint that maybe the attitude starts at the top and they have a hard time keeping servers. Second, I noticed another one of the local Yelp Elite crew (and other reviewers) also commented on the rude and inattentive service here. Seems to be a trend and unfortunately, it was our unlucky turn to experience it tonight.
Fills in the gap for us always Take out for social get togethers, business and family gatherings Always consistent  Great menu

On the way to Crush Cafe (just down the street), I passed Renaud's and noticed a small crowd of people outside. That's a sure sign that this place is good. Anyway, after breakfast, I stopped by Renaud's to see what all the fuss was about. The baked goods behind the glass looked awesome. Unfortunately, I was full at the time and opted to pick up some macarons for the wifey and myself.  The macarons are pretty darned awesome. If I remember correctly, I ordered the vanilla, chocolate, pistachio, raspberry, and mocha. They were all good. And, according to the woman who helped me, they're made by Renaud himself. Impressive.  Next time I'm up in Santa Barbara, I'm stopping by here again for sure. So should you.

Our most favorite breakfast place. We go often, they made us feel like family from the first time we went. The food is fresh and cooked to perfection. The plating is lovely and the coffee is great. These people are outstanding restauranteurs...like from very high end training! Go...you will love it!
