They share the same open air dining space as Shaker Mill bar, so it was really crowded with dance-club level of noise and ambiance. The good food makes up for it.   I went for the plato mixto with marinated chicken while my boyfriend got the Sandwich Number Two. He said it was great and the pork was tender. But I barely heard him over the inhaling of the food I was doing. My plate was sparkling clean before he even finished his sandwich. It was that good. The marinated chicken was flavorful and the rice and black beans well seasoned. The sweet plantains were the perfect ending to the meal. I did feel that their portions weren't that big though. Would still 10/10 come back.

This place has an awesome location and patio space, but they don't look to evolve to the interests of locals. Their draft beer selection is probably one of the worst in town, a solely German beer selection is not the way to get more people in the door when there are amazing local craft breweries and tap rooms in walking distance with far better and more popular beer selection. Also they were out of cheese dip for their pretzels, that's not very German...so you can't feature good craft beer because it's not German but you run out of cheese dip. Amanda was an amazing server though!

A fantastic first experience here at Uncorked! I have wanted to try this place since I saw the wine machines (GENIUS idea and so fun!!) We came with the Axxess deal, one of the few Buy 1 get 1 deals in town. 5 stars for food, we tried the Pulled Pork Nachos, Lobster Sliders, and Pepperoni + Arugula flatbread. Nachos were delicious and the lobster smelled+ tasted fresh. The flatbread was amazing and a great size too. That wine card system is dangerous, we went back 4+ times each for new pours. A man who I presume might be one of the owners? Came by and asked if we were having a good time which gave that personal touch and we loved it. We also got a complimentary tasting which was so unexpected and really solidified how much we ended up loving this place.   They do seem understaffed here, but it only made us appreciate their hard work that much more. We watched the two in the kitchen work hard to push food out which was quite a wait but we totally understand why so a +1 to kitchen staff for excellent food and presentation. I also wish they can expand someday and move to a bigger location. More seating and outdoor space would just be awesome for a place like this.   We will 100% be back.

Charles and Jennifer Fredericks both classically trained culinary chefs decided to open up this cute butcher shop.  Jennifer was the former manager of Santa Barbara winery and Charles was the former chef of Bouchon.  This is the place to go to make a special meal.
Came for brunch and was blown away! Amazing selection of normal brunch/breakfast choices, as well as delish Mexican choices. The service was okay, but it was busy.

I love this place. I always enjoy my meal and the staff is super friendly. I'm vegan, so they always help me find the tastiest dishes for the way I eat. My family isn't vegan, and there are options for them as well, making this our family-favorite place to eat dinner. We never leave hungry and always leave happy.

OK. Other friends dragged me back and it was even worse.  My friend got seriously burnt onion rings.  Horrible.  She even asked the waitress if they were supposed to be that dark and she had the gall to say yes.   I got a pesto quinoa something.  All of us at the table tasted it.  Not any part of it tasted anything close to pesto.  pale green and bland bland bland.   And the other friend order veggie parmesan and only scarfed most of it down because he hadn't eaten all day.  He said it was way too spicy.    All three of us promised to never ever ever go back.   On this night the waitress was very sweet but the staff in the kitchen should be fired.  I'd be embarrassed to serve what we were served.  It makes me crazy when a restaurant with a great location does such a terrible job when it is so easy to do a good job.  This isn't even 'average' food.  There's no excuse.  The owners need to wake up.
Cool location on the water.  Was a bit loud and crowded so we just a couple beers at the bar.  We tried some calamari, it was not too great.  But the clam chowder was the best I've ever had!

Stopped here for breakfast a couple of times while in SB because of the great Yelp reviews. Originally planned to just get some pastries to go, but ended up staying for a fully breakfast, after seeing the menu.  My omelette was pretty great, even though the waitress incorrectly filled my order.  Returned a second time to follow through on the pastries, and wasn't disappointed.

This place never disappoints. Whenever me and my partner are in Santa Barbara, we have to stop here. And when we have gotten disappointed by seafood restaurants in SB, we always make up for it by going here. Prices are very reasonable. The oysters and the fresh uni are also very fresh and yummy! One of my favorite SB spots.

If you can't tell from my reviews, I love food that tastes amazing but doesn't feel super heavy. Panino does this so well. I really love how fresh their ingredients are, especially given that they're dealing with heavier components like bread.   Panino does a great job supplying a wide range of options that you will undeniably be satisfied with. Top three recommendations:   1) Roast Turkey sandwich - the honey mustard is great but the surprising star of the show here is the cucumber  2) Prosciutto w/ Fresh Mozzarella - I mean how can you go wrong with this order? Get this (and any other sandwich) on their Focaccia. It's amazing  3) Greek Salad w/ Chicken - Only critique is the chicken slices are real big. But the flavors are even bigger

I can easily give this place five stars. While it's not fancy, with the food tasting like it does it doesn't heed to be. My gosh the flautas were delicious. Crispy and still soft on the inside. So good. Nachos were a huge hit as well. Jamaica Agra Fresca was one if the best we've had. Absolutely delicious!
The best place in Santa Barbara to get a healthy meal. Highly recommend the smoothies and juices!

First discovered this place when I had planned to take my gf to Brophy Bros. but they were too packed to accommodate us within a time acceptable to our stomachs (no fault on Brophy Bros, I know how good it is and can't blame people for lining up to eat there). On the walk back towards the car, we noticed 'On the Alley' and decided to take a look inside. This place is literally a hole in the wall, and not in a bad way.  The inside is simply a chilled display case for beverages next to a counter for the register and another counter for condiments. Seating is 'on the alley' and they bring your food to you. I've been here so many times since then. I've tried the fish tacos, shrimp tacos, ultimate grilled cheese, crab cake burger, fish and chips, garlic baked clams, and the clam chowder with bread. I love them all.  Even though I've moved away from Santa Barbara, On the Alley is one of the places I will definitely stop by to stuff myself with good comforting seafood.  Be warned, this location is literally 'on the alley' in a relatively high traffic part of the harbor (not wharf). It is somewhat loud, particularly on the weekends. There are locals, human and avian, and tourists walking and biking about. There are boats being lowered into the water about 50 meters away and cars and trucks do sometimes drive by right next to where you will be eating.

The place is certainly fun, prices low and the service awesome. I understand some people take issue with the owner and I have never encountered him. The people who have helped me, however, have always been courteous and efficient.   That said, the food is not my cup of tea. The bread is ok, the white cheese tastes super processed, veggies are subpar, and their red meat is not so good. I got the Philly cheese 'steak' most recently and had to take the meat out. It was rubbery and frankly, off putting. I always leave thinking, ok that's why a 6 inch is under $5.  I will say that I feel guilty for these negative comments, but I definitely cannot recommend the steak. I do really want to love this place that so many people adore, is so quirky and with friendly service. So with that, I say, the food is not my cup of tea... Yet! But I will be back once more to try the bacon avocado or a veggie sandwich.

We had been anticipating the opening of Revolver Pizza for weeks.  Finally had a chance to try it last night.  A couple of observations:  -the pizza is 5 stars for sure - great crust, great ingredients, best pizza in town! -garden salad - 4 1/2 stars.  The salad itself gets 5 starts - very fresh ingredients and pretty to boot. I'm not a big fan of ranch dressing so it would be nice to have another option for dressing, hence the 1/2 star ding. - business model - interesting....we tried to order a pizza the two previous Fridays and they were 'sold out' at 6 pm.  We got smart last night and called at 4:00 p.m. to reserve our pizza for a 6:30 p.m. pick up.  We arrived at 6:30 but still had to wait another 15 minutes before the pizza was ready.  True, it was a Friday night and good for them.  The tables out side were filled with millenials enjoying the awesome pizza.  Perhaps a mid-week order doesn't require the pre-order and wait time. The vibe is pretty cool (although the music inside was great but a bit loud - yes, we are 'old' people) - love what they've done to the patio.  I wish them all the best and welcome them to the Westside!

Love this place, it is a good healthy option for dinner. They have great enchiladas and specials. The servers are always friendly and attentive. The finch aviary out front is always fun to look at on the way in or out.

Came here last week.  I ordered the eggs benedict and it was yummy.  A little pricier compared to other 'breakfast' places.  But they charged an extra $2.50 for two sausages for my friend and another $2.50 to substitute turkey for pork sausage for me... which I didn't even mean to order, but I misunderstood the server's question.  I didn't know they'd charge me extra!  Food was good, I just think $15 is a little much to spend on breakfast...
Rushed me out, delivered the wrong sandwich, charged me for ice tea then tells me that they put it away 30 prior to closing with now offer for a refund. Kiss that $20 goodbye

Ask to sit on the outdoor patio just off the bar on the second floor -- or at the bar for that matter. The views are the best! We arrived at that odd time after lunch and before happy hour, but we enjoyed the experience so much, we stayed for happy hour. The service was great (across 2 shifts), and we enjoyed everything we ordered, although I will say that the lettuce in the Caesar salad was a bit brown around the edges -- my only complaint.

Your music is too damn loud!  Turn it down, no one can talk!!!!  This is not the Neighborhood, it's a family friendly environment.  Where I want to come eat, drink and talk, not get blasted out of your doors by the sound of late 90s reggae.

This place is pretty good for being a beachside restaurant/bar.  This review is a little random since my order was very strange-- I was here at Leadbetter Beach for an early morning race, and we finished a little before 9 am, at which point we all wanted to have a nice drink.  Ha!  Sounds weird, unless you wake up before 5 am and paddle 5 miles in the open ocean, and are hungry and tired and your body is worn out, in which case, a nice drink sounds awesome.  Anyway, I had a mojito and a bowl of yogurt, granola, and fresh fruit.  I know, totally weird.  The mojito was delicious, but it was way too small, and it was $10!  My breakfast was cheaper than that, and the perfect size-- nice cuts of fresh fruit, but not enough yogurt to mix in with the granola.  My friends really enjoyed the fish tacos.  Service was good, but the server had a lot of trouble figuring out how to split our check in the end.  Luckily we caught the errors before signing and had her redo it.  Twice.  If you're hanging out at Leadbetter Beach, it's convenient to eat here, but not worth coming to this place just on its own.

One of my parents new favorite spots in town. The restaurant is beautifully decorated with seating both inside and outside on a private patio. Plates are small so order a variety of dishes for the table like tapas and taste a little of everything. The food was delectable and each bite as tasteful as the next. Kitchen is good at respecting allergies and special requests. Friendly staff.

This was by far our favorite place on our trip to SB!  For our appetizer we had the antipasto which was out of this world! The Goat cheese with Honey was so freakin delicious! I was never a fan of goat cheese but mixed with honey put that cheese on the favorite list for me! We even came home and bought the cheese and honey! PERFECTION!  We ordered the Shrimp Spaghetti and Bolognese for dinner. The flavors were so amazing and delicious! The Shrimp had a truffle sauce that had excellent flavor! Overall we were very happy with our meal! It was our last dinner in SB and had we went before we would have def gone back!   Can't wait to visit again to eat at this delicious restaurant!

I have been there twice. The desserts are super good. Not too sweet. But full of flavors. The brunch is also good. The chai latte is tasty. The only problem is that the place is crowded with not enough seats...

This place is definitely unique in Santa Barbara. The jerk chicken spice was excellent. I had the blackened catfish, which was served with coconut grits, and the combination was perfect.  I can't wait to go back and try the rest of the menu!

I visited Arch Rock on Sunday evening and it was relatively empty. This is great for me, because waiting sucks. I sat outside and our servers were very attentive.   My friend had a two for one Axxess deal, which was awesome. I ordered grilled cheese and our sever graciously allowed us to sub fries for tator tots. They were AMAZING.   I enjoyed my meal very much, I drank a delicious lemonade, and the service was swell. I would come back here again.

My husband and I sat at the bar and had a wonderful cocktail made with care by the barman who personalized the drink. We tried an appetizer and wow, that was so tasty and creative : Squash Blossoms with goat cheese - exquisite!! Then we came back for dessert and we chose their berry one with pistachio pastry - that was amazing!! And the barman was friendly and attentive. Can't ask for better than this! Will be back soon!

Good food, diverse, yet pricey wine list, and some of the worst service I've ever encountered sitting at the bar in a restaurant for an early seating. Watching service around my party I saw those dropping 100-400 dollars on wine alone getting watched over attentively, while I wasn't even shown the bottle on an obscure btg pour. Come for the well executed food and great wine list, but don't hold your breath on service if you aren't willing to drop a large chunk of change on wine.

I think the price here is fair and the food is good. I ordered seasonal fruit and my friend ordered breakfast plate. My toast is soft and the goat cheese is amazing. The fruit on the toast is persimmon. Loved it. Like the texture of the mixture of toast, persimmon and the cheese. The mushroom from the breakfast plate taste really good. The ham is fair and the egg is half cooked and taste good. Enjoy the food here and also the vibe. The location of this restaurant is great I can see the Santa Barbara court from my seat.

A great go-to lunch spot. Always consistent. Amazing grilled chicken. Love the old town salad (mmm that tahini dressing), soups, chicken pita and now obsessed with the portabella sandwich. You can't go wrong here.
Great service, good food, reasonably priced and a clean, cozy setting. They offer gluten free pancakes which are quite yummy; cooked perfectly. Their turkey sausage is a little spicy which I liked.
Went here for my birthday a while back, best lobster and steak i've ever had. Its pricy but worth it for the food you get, also great service.

Yes. All of it. I order asada and al pastor and they're delicious. Great pricing too. Line moves fast. The salsas are amazing. I love their hottest one. It hella burns but so good. If you order to go, make sure you ask for lime and salsa because they won't put it if you don't ask. I was so bummed when that happened. Parking is all on the street and they're a bit narrow so it can be tricky but then again that's SB
Had a really creative and flavorful sando from this spot. They're really thoughtful in the art that they're creating. Soft bread, quality ingredients, and huge variety of chips. Get in here!

I like the Hungry Cat a lot, and it's always a treat when I get to come here.  The menu certainly isn't extensive (so maybe not the place to bring picky eaters), but I'm always pleased with what I order.  The drinks are pretty strong so you definitely get what you pay for, especially during happy hour.  Protip: the beignets are awesome but aren't always listed on the menu, so be sure to ask for them - you won't regret it.

I'll let you know right away that I've always enjoyed the food here, but then tell you that one of Ahi's hands doesn't seem to know what the other's doing, service-wise. Example: one server seated my friends and I right in the worst of a chilly draft. These things happen, but when we asked another server to close the door letting all that air in, she seemed confused and said, 'I don't think they'll let us.' ('They'?) So I pulled over a different server and asked her to close the door, which she simply walked over and did. All's well that ends well.  Example two: I asked the server too scared to close the door (mistake) if they kept Asahi on draft. 'No, we don't,' she hesitantly replied while I stared at the bar's tap labeled 'Asahi'. But hey, maybe the thing blew a mainspring, so I ordered a bottle instead. A few minutes later, my friend asked another server for a glass of Asahi, and -- lo and behold -- straight out of the tap it came. WHAT THE. But the glass they served his Asahi in smelled funny, so things evened out.  Still, I feel like a dick complaining about these problems, because damn, does Ahi serve a fine shiitake mushroom sushi. I'd always instinctively avoided mushrooms, especially on sushi, but if anything can convert me, this stuff can. My bowl of unusually veggie-intensive miso soup went well with it, though I couldn't quite forget about the hardened crusty stuff I felt on its side. Be apprised that the appetizer that translates to something like 'coffee shop potato steak' has novelty value, but you wouldn't want to make it a staple. Just get a few more rounds of that mushroom sushi instead.  (NOTE: If you order a glass of sake, you will pay like sixteen bucks for it. But it comes in a little wooden box! So hey.)

We came in this Sunday morning and it was insanely crowded on state street because the finish line of a marathon was directly in front of the restaurant. It was a bad start because the line was out the door. It took a bit to get to the register to order. After we ordered I had to go to the bathroom but the place was a mess because of all the foot traffic due to the marathon. So I sat donn and waited for my food a little grumpy. The staff was very friendly despite being slammed with a crowd. The food came out pretty quickly and piping hot. I ordered a veganized build your own hash which was very tasty. I sat down in a bad mood but they had me impressed by the end of the meal.

The deli sandwiches are really delicious and fresh. They are, however, very pricey for what they are. Go to South Coast Deli around the corner for a better bang for your buck.   Everything else is really expensive too and some of their refrigerated desserts are gross. The tiramisu tasted like it was made with cool whip.   I go here when I need a quick sandwich or SCD is closed.

Absolutely fabulous!   Must search my phone for photos and add after but always delicious the best almond croissants I've ever had...  it's truly another pastry animal altogether. You've likely never fully lived until you've had one... even if it's soooooo not on your eating plan like mine (whoops, thx enzymes) it was worth the consequences!   I do wish they had some gluten free and dairy free selections.  I hope they will have them soon.   Nice location with dog friendly patio tucked in then end corner of the parking lot in Loreto Plaza by Gelson's for years has been a go to for locals...  A lot less dreamy in description without worthiness but I will aim to post some better photos for y'all who haven't been there!   I went to high school just up the street so I've had my post office box there about 25 years!  Love to fequent many other businesses in the Plaza like Federal drug and other awesome iconic spots like Chaucer's bookstore and some changing businesses over the years. So this is absolutely somewhere I am near often!!!    The food is well loved and the service is definitely adequate or better in my experience lately.  Depends on the day the person etc.   Renauds is a slice of French pastry heaven in a foodie town...Tarts, croissants, macarons etc. all gorgeous! One of our well loved places that anyone visiting or living here should frequent for a bite even if you just grab a single macaron it's worth it. Tarts etc. gorgeous single or quadrupled in a box. Delightful.o Great as gifts for others if it's not on their food plan also...and if it's they will beyond thank you for it. ;)  Cheers!

Great food. I just went for the first time and had the chile verde burrito. It was delicious! It had that great spiciness that creeps up on your throughout the meal. Great variety of salsas at the salsa bar, and the host keep his eye on the dining room and always seemed to come over whenever I needed a napkin, drink refill, etc.

If I could give this place no stars I would. I went here years ago and this place was good. My husband and I tried it out recently and I don't know what happened but literally it wasn't edible. We sent a few things back but I wish i could have sent everything back. I'm not sure if there's a new chef or what is going on but I wish i didn't have to pay for a dinner like that. The only decent thing was the wine!

Had a fun time here! Was out for a night in Santa Barbara over the weekend and this place was great. 3 floors? Huge bar! Really nice inside. The staff was great and so were the drinks. We didn't eat, but only because we just had dinner down the street, but the drinks were great! Would return for sure.
The first and last Sambo's location. Full of history great coffee. Price of breakfast is very high  but quality is good.  Not sure I will be back as there are better place to eat Santa Barbara

They have a great happy hour menu but this is the go to place for brunch. The shrimp and grits is so delicious. The grits are just as well made and creamy as scarlet begonia's but it's also served with a gumbo sauce that makes it even better. The crab cakes were just ok, a little too starchy for my taste. Great service and will definitely be back!

I use them for catering up to 3x a week! They offer an amazing and unique selection for lunch and breakfast, including salmon, tri-tip. Chicken salad, grilled veggies and garlic bread. My clients rave about the food. I have never set foot in the actual restaurant but they are my favorite caterer in SB.

I am really disappointed with this location. The hot food and deli is always a sad sight with barely any options usually just empty tins. I bought their split pea soup that they make there and it's labeled vegetarian, however there is bacon or ham in it (see pic). The people on the smoothie bar are terrible and extremely slow. Literally took 15 minutes to get my smoothie when I was the only person there. I also had to ask someone to help me because they are not attentive at all in the smoothie/coffee bar area. Everyone is usually really nice though. However, with how expensive this store is I expect a lot more and they are defintely lacking.

Obviously the line and being cash only are the biggest draw backs, but it is popular for a reason. Everything is hand made and very delicious, even for vegetarians. I recommend the vegetarian tamal and the spicy gordita de frijol. Also, the Mexican coffee is amazing and vegan friendly!

Went to this Santa Barbara Institution for a friend's birthday and first and foremost, the food was fantastic. Fresh food, large portions, incredibly tasty, and you could tell it was handmade with love. You could pay double elsewhere in town and not get the same level of quality. If I was giving it a rating on the food alone, it'd be five stars no question. Everyone enjoyed their meal.  I also will say the alcohol is more reasonably priced than you'll find in SB. I got a cocktail that typically starts at $12 elsewhere and it was only $8! Wine also came with a heavy pour. Definitely a good place to start your night if you're heading to State Street next.  However, I'm going to echo a lot of reviews here when I say that the service is where it falls a bit flat. While friendly, it was definitely not very attentive and missed out on what surely would have been at least another two rounds of drinks had they come to the table. Tried to do a drink order with the person who delivered the food, since we hadn't seen our server, and the message was not relayed as I was almost done with my meal before I waved over the server to see where my drink was. We also may have considered dessert, but by the time we saw the server again we were ready to go. The place didn't look especially busy, but perhaps they were understaffed and this person was in the weeds.  I don't want to give many details since anyone can have an off night and by no means do I want to get this person in trouble. We definitely enjoyed our food enough for me to give this place another try. Here's hoping next time I can add back those two stars :)

It was very tough deciding on either 3, or 4 stars. All the employees were very nice. They dont have duck which was the only reason why we came in...wanting duck...Thai place doesnt have duck SMFH. Since we were there we were like well lets try something new today. Ordered many plates, all were freakin delicious, the chicken wings had next to no meat on them though. Like the chickens must have been starved before they were killed because it was just skin over bone which was super weird. Yet what little meat was on there including the skin tasted awesome. Overall I'd give it 3.5 stars. And IMO 95% of Thai spots have, and very well should include duck on the menu. Its been a long ass time since I've come across one that doesn't.

Try to support local business but wow , I waited 25 mins for an omelette to be served after them telling me they didn't see the ticket !  Throw some meat in a plate and cover it with an egg mix . Not what I had expected from this place , I have been a customer since they opened and always had great food .....I guess we blame Covid !
Decided to give this place a try while visiting. Food was good but nothing memorable. Got the cheesesteak.

We had a group so ordered the large chicken platter and added some falafel and baba gannoujgh...it was plenty of food and flavored well. Loved the garlic sauce and the pita tasted like home made tortilla. Very happy with this place

Yeah, I'd totally be back here A LOT if I lived in the area.  After a 3 mile morning run, I wasn't going to waste that on fattening food (saving that for later). Grabbed the Pesto Caprese and Great Greenie smoothie. Both very good!  Give it a try for a healthy breakfast!
Really good food, with lots of specials that change frequently. Small parking lot, and there is usually a line out the door at peak times.
It's americanized Mexican food. Non the less it's pretty good food. Definitely not abuelitas cooking but in a pinch it'll do.

Beers are just too pricey. $8-9 for a 10oz with a $1 tip just isn't worth it. The beers are sub par. However, they allow dogs, that's cool. The owner seems cool. It's just really hard to spend $30 bux for 3 beers and no dinner.

Had a great experience here on a previous visit, so decided to have our special occasion dinner with the family at this restaurant. Food is absolutely awesome. One of the top one or two steak that I have ever ate . All of my family agreed. Great service and atmosphere.

butter fish great. eggplant in garlic sauce - good.. lamp skewers .. dipping sauce divine ...............they forgot the rice ...........after 8 times in a call back to them they claimed not to understand the name of my street to enable deliveriy .,..I am an English teacher .. dtr finally went to get rice.Rip off of time .. terrible manners .. food was good.

This place has all the makings of a great new hot spot! Killer views, unique menu, and friendly staff!!  However I was underwhelmed at the taste of the food, everything was fried, and over battered!  Also pretty expensive.  My group paid over $40 each just for lunch.  They are definitely catering to tourist! But come one, us Locals want to be catered to a bit also!

Arrived at Killer B's in time for happy hour on a Sunday afternoon. Nobody told us that we only had 30 minutes until our 'bottomless' mimosas ended- so I pretty much paid $15 for 1 1/2 mimosas ... STUPID! At least warn people when they order it that once happy hour is over you're not going to refill their cups anymore. It would have been cheaper just to buy them individually.  Ruined my day- won't be returning.

Just finished a visit for happy hour.  What a disappointment !! The lobster roll was soaked full of some dressing that overpowered any taste of lobster.  The roll was dry and stale.  The fish and chips were weird, the fries soggy and limp.  If you like overpriced raw seafood, this is the place for you.  Otherwise, skip it.

This place is great! Love the vibe! Got the rosemary Thai tea and hummus, and loved both. Delicious all around! The hummus had a nice spiciness to it. Loved the bread that came with the hummus. Nice crunchy crust, soft squishy insides and perfectly seasoned with garlic and salt.  I'll be back to try more of their food and the very interesting drink concoctions!

The wine and beer menus are extensive and above and beyond the average selections. I had the Syrah. So sumptuous I had two. My friends all enjoyed the pretzels, but I didn't like the cheese sauce. Several of us ordered the beet and arugula salad. I loved mine, but everyone else thought theirs were overdressed. We had a wonderful time here and can't wait to come back.

I just cannot profess my love for Rudy's enough. I work a block away and come here often. I've NEVER been let down. My go to is the chicken fajita bowl and it easily lasts me two meals. I've also gotten burritos, salads, and tacos and have loved it all. They also have a great assortment of Mexican soda, horchata, and a Coke soda machine for those days when you just need a fountain Diet Coke (none of that Pepsi nonsense). They have delicious chips and a great salsa bar (that includes spicy onions and carrots). Their staff is amazing and they're quick with great service. I'm here at least once a week and would visit more if my budget allowed!

Arigato has gotten consistently worse the last decade as its popularity/busy-ness has exploded. Our sushi plates were unimpressive and the fish low quality. Our small plates came out randomly and were often too cold or piping hot (microwave?). The server was sweet but trying desperately to sell us on ordering more more more, while not knowing anything about the food except for item names. Weird experience as well as being reminded multiple times about a 90 minute dining limit. The dining experience would be just if the bill was 1/2 the price.
A new take on hotdogs. Interesting menu of different Hot Dog varieties. Pretty good for a fast food place.

My husband and I go to Enterprise Fish about once a week for lunch. Are usually order my favorite the crab Louie which is always delicious. However yesterday when the waiter brought my dish it was so small so I asked him what happened to the lettuce that used to be in the salad he said we cut it down because people tend to eat just a good portion of the salad and leave the lettuce. Well I love the lettuce it's a crab Louie salad I maybe had a little over a cup of lettuce I was very disappointed so I guess next time I will have to ask for extra lettuce in my salad. It's the same with the salad dressing you don't get very much salad dressing anymore because I guess people didn't eat all of the salad dressing on the crab Louie.I hope that they will change this and will truly have lettuce and a salad.

Great brunch place on a sunday.  I had the eggs and bacon, was tasty.  The biscuit and gravy was also good.  Had a bite of the cheese burger, was also good.  This place can have a wait, so I would recommend smaller groups.

By far the best Thai food in Santa Barbara! The panang curry, curry noodles, garlic chicken, chicken satay and sizzling platter are all delicious. Before I found this place, I had given up on Thai food here, but this rivals any place I have eaten at in LA. Great price for what you get as well. This is definitely one of my favorite restaurants in SB.

Excellent food and service and ambience and drinks. Lots of tasty vegetarian options (they could offer the croqueta de queso without gelatin or just not use gelatin - my only quibble). Inventive and delicious. Dog friendly. No attitude. Will be back!
Well I felt like I was in Ga! The hospitality was great and they even bring your food to your table for you. Loved it!

I thought from the reviews, and menu, couldn't miss--organic eggs, fair trade coffee etc. Ordered at the counter, mediocre staff, take number to table. They found our table--1 meal was hot (scramble w/lox & cream cheese), the other lukewarm. When I told them that, they took it back and brought it back later hot, but didn't seem to care at all.

Yummy and flavorful loose leaf teas. I love the queen victoria tea which is a blend of black, oolong, and green teas. I appreciate the jasmine rose green tea as well along with the mao zen tea. Place is super cute inside with reasonably priced tea pots at around $20. Staff are warm and friendly. There are tables inside where you can enjoy a cup of tea with friends. Adorable and quiant place to be!

Was surprised to find Beach Break Cafe gone but pleasantly surprised by this new place. Cool looking decor and very welcoming. Super friendly people. A little pricey but not surprising for SB. Food is good and fresh. A bit slow on service but seems like they are still getting in their groove having just opened. Definitely coming back! And bonus, it's still dog friendly.

Amazing thin crust pizza... Freshest ingredients and for lunch personal pizza with either Caesar or arugula salad with delightful long slender croutons---pizza comes with two toppings for $10.  Drink water bc drinks are $2.95.  The lunch menu was not given to us with the option of personal pizzas but provided when I showed her the image on Yelp.

Atmosphere was nice, beverages great, service excellent. My duck breast was sadly over cooked. It was dry and the leg was barely edible. The scallop trio was the best thing we ate last night. The server highlighted that the wine list was very local, but how about highlighting some local spot prawns and uni?? The menu has a lot of what people expect, but could use some more creativity.

Everyone wants to go to Brophy's when the weather is great, but the real inside scoop is a cold, rainy day.  The bar is half empty, the fog is clinging to the mountains, the boats are bobbing in the moorings and the chowder warms the belly just so...
Love love love this place. Drew knows his sh*t if you're able to get him. Can't go wrong with anything on the menu.

Amazing Mexican food in an unsuspecting residential neighborhood! The service here was so warm, they acted like we were friends. Here you walk up to the counter to order.   They brought chips to the table and had a help yourself salsa bar.  When food was ready, they brought it to our table.   Food was incredible! My husband is a taco connoisseur and he said they were the best restaurant tacos he's ever had. My chili verde burrito was so tasty and just the right spice and tons of melted Jack cheese. It was so big I could barely eat 1/3 of it.  I so recommend stopping her if you are craving Mexican food - dare I say almost better than my mama's?

Our friends recommended this place and I'm glad they did. After a short 15-minute wait we found ourselves at a lovely table on the sand. Our server was fun and attentive, serving up some might fine fish and shrimp tacos. More than just tasty, the portion size was excellent. I also appreciated the NA beer available. Eager to return!

I visit here quite often, always coming back for the delicious food! This is my go to to-go spot in all of Santa Barbara. I can never get enough of the Spicy Red Craft Ramen with chicken and, of course, the almighty soft boiled egg *chef's kiss*  The portions (of ramen) are large and quite filling. I've tried all of the ramens on the menu and, honestly, all are very tasty. The Garlic Ramen is a little too strong for my preference but that's just me.  The service is generally quick, food comes out shortly after putting your order in, I've never had to wait more than 10 minutes. The servers are friendly and attentive as well. 11/10 would recommend.

I was in for a lovely surprise when my boyfriend brought me here for my birthday. There were many other tables celebrating birthdays. It seems like this restaurant is a popular destination for those celebrating a special occasion.  Ambiance: It's a beautiful setting overlooking the ocean inside the four seasons resort hotel. There is an outside patio with outdoor fireplace as well as some seats inside. Valet parking is also complimentary which is a plus.  Service: Very polite waiters and friendly service. I felt very comfortable and waiters were very accomodating in terms of food requests.  Food: I thought the food was decent, but definitely on the pricier side. I tried the seafood sampler which was pretty good. Also ordered a shrimp spaghetti with meat sauce , which actually was not on the menu but the waiter was creative and offered us something to accomodate our food preferences. The pasta was cooked al dente and tasted fresh (I believe it was made from scratch as the noodles had an elastic texture and it didn't look like it came out of a box). The dish wasn't greasy or stuffing。  Seafood sampler was ok. I don't eat mussels or uni and the rest of the seafood were served chilled. I'd probably try something else next time !  Overall, I would recommend this restaurant for superior service and the ambiance!

I am a frequent guest at this Denny's as its one of the only late night places to get a decent meal. Also, Miranda, the manger makes this place. She's always there to greet us and make us feel special. She knows our drink order, knows where we work and seems to always care about how our day went ! This is something you'd expect in a nicer restaurant and not a Denny's so it's very nice to have. Thank you Miranda!!! We will always come back as long as she's there

Happy hour review only: My go to happy hour location in town because of unbeatable deals that last all the way until 8pm! $1 oyster Wednesdays are always excellent, but even on regular days they offer blue point oysters for only $1.50. Margaritas, moscow mule, and house wine are very standard- no complaints.   As for happy hour food, I recommend the calamari. It's not too bready but just right.  Oysters: are a must.  Lobster roll: came cold and was dry...but made for a good picture so it was worth it.  Brussel sprouts: are mediocre in quality. Tangy, chewy and not crispy which is how I like it.   Sometimes seating in the bar area might seem crowded but with a little patience, I've never had an issue with getting a table. We've even been able to find a full table together for our group of 6 on a busy Wednesday evening. Also, parking is stupidly easy with a huge parking lot right in the back of the restaurant.

Really loved their craft beers.  The stars were their Pumpkin Ale and their Double IPA.  We also ordered their Margharita Pizza (great special for $10: pizza and a beer!).  Loved the cheese on that and it paired well with the IPA.  Very cool staff as well.  Loved the location and openness to State Street.

This was good and tasty but nothing special. I got the avocado chicken tostada. The tortilla bowl had a great crunch to it, but the chicken was over cooked which is disappointing since they are supposed to be known for their chicken. The avocado sauce was good but I wish they also had slices of avocado on top to make it come together. I had a side of Mac and cheese which was good (no picture because I was too hungry and ate it on my drive home).

At first I thought our pizza was going to taste strange but the combination of prosciutto, arugula, and cheeses was pretty tasty! I like how the arugula was added after it was cooked.  I would have loved to have had a sample platter of pizza to try because so many sounded good.

I'm only giving this place 3 stars because other than the pescado sarandeado, nothing else looks really appetizing. HOWEVER, I really like to go to this restaurant for the pescado sarandeado because the sauce in the fish tastes amazing. It's a little spicy but sweet at the same time. Its crunchy on the outside and it tastes great if you add a little lemon to it. It comes with rice and beans but I am not too fond of that. If you are a fish person and are into mojarras, you should definitely try this dish.

If you needed a sign to eat here, take this as your sign. The food is impeccable and so worth the price!! I would highly recommend making a reservation as it does get extremely busy here.   I got the enchilada xaoxaca or however you spell it and it was phenomenal. Also got a tamarind margarita which was also super delicious.

Came here with a group of 8 for father's day dinner (4 adults, 4 kids) and the food did not disappoint.  Had mussels (amazing and huge!) and oysters (delish!) for appetizers. I had the seafood cobb (shrimp, scallops and crab) for my entree which was awesome.  The salad was refreshing and it really hit the spot on a warm day.  Cocktails were great. The kids loved how their meals came in these mini galvanized buckets. Hostess, bartender, staff were all very friendly.  Our server was pretty slow and we had to ask for the same things multiple times. I wouldn't say that this place is that great for large groups. Although we had a group of 8, we were split into two tables that shared a half wall that divided our party. I probably wouldn't have more than 6 to a group when dining here.

Came here for lunch with my friend. We sat upstairs on the patio, it was absolutely perfect. The outdoor seating with the umbrellas was nice blocked the sun but also provided some sun protection too. My friend really enjoyed her clam chowder and the service was on point. Christian oue server was very attentive, friendly and overall a pleasure to deal with.  The indoor seating and ambience was also really beautiful too. Loved that it's right across from the beach and you have a nice view of the ocean. Would definitely come back!

Brunch was wonderful. Menu had options for any appetite. Coffee was good, pastries were out of this world. The Kringle made me want to get on a plane, fly to Denmark right away, and become a Danish citizen. Great outdoor dining and people watching. Kind, attentive staff. Will be back. Only negative was busy at brunch and dirty table cloth wasn't switched out before we sat down...just had new paper placemats added. BUT not a big deal food makes up for it, and would come back over and over.

As a local, I've been here three times, and last night was the best overall.  My 3-stars is an average, however, because bad marks go for inattentive service twice of three visits - though our guy server last night was GREAT.  My other occasions were frustrating because we were summarily ignored during a lunch service (May 2018) on the patio, to the point i had to get up from the table to ask someone to get us water and check on the order - and this was when there were only 25% of the tables occupied inside and out. My first visit was also under attended by wait staff.  The happy hour menu is creative, as is the meal menu, and the drinks are top notch.  The ambience is average indoors, but beautiful on the patio. The noise level indoors is terrible, have to yell to have a conversation, even if not fully occupied. During busy happy hour last night, i left with a headache from the noise.  Too bad, because i might return, but would think twice.

Pretty good place for dinner to watch the sunset. This was the few options available at Bacara for dinner and it was well worth it. I got the steak and the gnocchi. Both were delicious. Since it was for a work dinner I didn't pay for the bill but I would highly recommend eating at this place because the view and food were amazing.

Absolutely the best Shrimp Ceviche I've ever had! I really love the Salsas too my favorite is to mix the Salsa Fresca and the Roasted Tomatillo they also make a great Pico de Gallo and Tomatillo Salsa. Ever since the pandemic I've been taking advantage of  Taco Tuesdays they have 1.50 Soft Tacos and they're delicious!  I suggest a side of Black Beans and or some Guacamole to go along with their Tasty Tacos! I have had the Al Pastor, Carnitas, Chile Verde, Chorizo, and Carne Asada and they are all Muy Sabroso (Very Tasty!)  You Will Not Go Hungry Here!!! The Portions are always Very Generous and the Staff is Very Friendly :)

First the good stuff--the food has always been consistently excellent and was last night.  The service, on the other hand, has never been worse.  This was the first time that we didn't need to wait for a table which would have been great if the rest of the experience was good but it went downhill from there.  It took way too long to have our order taken, mid-way between getting our drinks and the order being taken, our server was switched, and then it took forever to get our food.  Once we got the food, we had to flag down the server for condiments.  Our new server never checked up on us and I saw our old server make several passes by our table without checking to see if all was okay.  When I made my way to the restroom later, I had to literally squeeze past a group of servers chit-chatting in the middle of the walkway.  We had to ask for our check and then the server put it down without clearing away our dishes leaving no room on our small table to deal with paying the bill--we had to ask him to clear the plates!  Very disappointing--you guys can do better than this!

the prime days of the bacara are over.  work brought me here 9 yrs ago and the memories of this place was still fresh.  9 yrs later i returned and though everything still appears the same, but with close looks at the details, everything has deteriorated and aged ungracefully.  our shower handle was broken off but was never replaced, the in-house tubs were jet tubs but the buttons were all broken...  the flooring showed serious aging, and some lights in the room didn't work...  and the most alarming was we found blood spatters in the bathroom wall that wasn't cleaned.  we were assigned to a 2 bd ocean view room with a massive living space, a bit gratuitous for a happily married couple, but we still appreciated the thoughts.  the pools are still as magical as it was 9 years ago, the spa however, had cracking tiles and poor grout job.    apparently this resort has gone through a series of different owners and management companies, and it shows :(  it's still as picturesque as ever, but just wished it was better maintained to uphold the ritz standards.  there really isn't too much to do around the resort besides lounge around the pool or go to the spa.  we opted not to visit the beach because there was a tar warning that you may get tar on your feet / shoes...  thanks but no thanks.  oh, and don't even get us started on the food & beverage pricing.  $42 for a breakfast buffet that lacked sustenance...  no wonder everyone opted for the cafe located at the entrance atrium, even that, was $12 for a starbucks quality breakfast sandwich.

Nice idea but poor execution.  They make toast on the grill here which at other places is yummy, but if the grill isn't clean it tastes of old oil and will come with bits of egg or meat or grease that you weren't expecting, making a fresh breakfast dish taste... stale.  The chilaquiles look beautiful in pictures online but are essentially just a couple of ingredients, plus the fried eggs on mine had uncooked egg whites which is just gross.  I'd like to think the kitchen was having an off-day when we were there but life is short and I'd take a lot of convincing to return.

Beautiful location in sunny, SB! Sit outside on the patio if you can.  If you have to wait, take a stroll and enjoy the scenery!   So, I wish I would have tried the breakfast, 'cause the burgers weren't all that.  Got the Santa Barbara burger...since when is guacamole spicy?  Sambo's - you could have warned me (i.e. write it on the menu description). Their burgers get an 'ehhh'.  You should probably stick to breakfast here.  They do give you chocolate chip & blueberry muffins - great way to keep one from scarfing down their food (i.e. me).  Not too shabby!

Great spot for an intimate get together. We popped in and were delighted by the honey lavender martini, Brussels sprouts, and the tacos. The outside patio has a cozy fireplace- it's divine. Such a fabulous restaurant!

Cute little restaurant right near state street. We came here for dinner & enjoyed everything we ordered. Ned time J would like to try the Salmon Square role because that looked very wonderful. The staff was friendly & the prices weren't too crazy. I would definitely come back!

I have been here a couple of times.  We have always had great service. We love the blood orange margaritas. The food was ok.  Romantic...I guess because its dark I agree with other reviews this is a great place to meet and have a drink and then move on
It's okay. The food doesn't seem extremely fresh, but the service is kind. Because it is so close to the beach it can be very cold so bring a sweater. It is a very beautiful spot!

Two of us came here for lunch yesterday and both dishes were excellent.  The wild shrimp tacos are so fresh and full of flavor.  They come topped with a non mayo slaw that is delicious, pickled onions on homemade tortillas.  The rajas tacos were equally as good.  Best rajas I've had in town (better than La Super Rica and that's saying something).

This place is simply amazing. I was in SB again recently to attend my brother's (Preston L.) graduation. My boyfriend and I wanted to grab some sandwiches to take to the graduation. So, I called my brother and he told me South Coast Deli has good sandwiches and it is near the UCSB campus. SO glad he made this recommendation!!   I was initially overwhelmed by the many different options of cold & hot sandwiches as well as paninis. My eyes were drawn to the Chicken Pesto Panini (seriously, everything I love in one!) and the super cute server at the cash register literally hoorayed as I settled on my choice as this was her favorite. She even threw in some avocado for free as she said it was a make it or break it decision. SO glad she convinced me. That was one of the best sandwiches I ever had.  My boyfriend went with the Roast Beef, which the server was not as excited about, but it was pretty good! I don't usually like spicy, but the spicy mayo in this sandwich was the perfect kick.    South Coast Deli is your neighborhood deli - no frills and just chill. There is a huge blackboard like wall with tons of writing on it. They have some magazines for reading while waiting or dining. And there are plenty of community flyers posted on the walls. What a wonderful gem!

Came here after a haircut at Montecito barbers. Haley Jo took make at the bar and made it a soul-refreshing dinner. Goat cheese salad had crisp fresh ingredients. Lady next to me smiled like 'oh look at the piggy eat, so cute'. Then my pistachio pizza arrived and I thought, shit... I might have over-ordered. but the taste truly made one believe in a higher power. After crushing that and a hand wash and cold water on my face. Why not the well-placed chocolate cake in the line of sight of all food being cooked. It was so rich I had to order coffee to unglue my mouth. All and all, DELICIOUS dinner after a very productive day.
Great fresh food , pie , and amazing service and coffee. Love that they remember your birthday and send you a coupon for a free slice of pie. Free internet also

Came here early in the morning for our Mothers Day weekend getaway. This place I chose over Jeannie's... the interior decar is sooo sweet I just HAD to show my mommy, how this cute little place exist!  David was our server and was very sweet on recommending the mimosa for us.  We ordered the following:  - 2 mimosa, Strawberry and blackberry (I prefer the strawberry, a little more sweet) - 2 crab cake benedict...AWESOME! & Perfect! - Dave's crossant sandwich, was okay.  Upon arrival,  you get comp Danish dish!   Thank You David for just everything! ^^ (hope you remember us!)
Location is super busy but the croissants and the croque monsiers are great!

The food was amazing! I asked for the yellow to be extra spicy, and not only was spice added to the dish while cooking, I was given extra dried chilis and chili sauce! The tom kha was so delicious. Portions were huge for only $10! Thai tea was just as good as it was from the previous restaurant!
Great coffe sandwitches, a fire going on a rainy day.  And the owner is awesome.  Wish it was open later!

This is such a stunningly beautiful property. It is much larger than it seems and you need a golf cart to take you around. I like how it is almost like a park in there and you can walk around and explore or take a golf cart if you like. The grounds are very lush and I love the proximity to the beach. We had a wonderful stay here for my 40th birthday. The room service food was quite good. My 2 year old daughter loved her chicken and veggies.   The room was very comfortable and had great ocean views. I love how you can see through to the room from the tub. The balcony was an awesome place to relax, eat, and enjoy the views.   The lobby is very comfortable and I worked from there one morning when I was up before the family.   All in all, it was a wonderful stay and a great property.

I was meeting friends here for dinner and called to ask if they allow dogs on the patio. The person who answered the phone said no, which is understandable. I thanked them, drove my dog home, and came back only to discover a dog. Inside the restaurant. At the table next to me. I brought this, and the fact that I was told they did not allow dogs even on the patio, to the waitresses attention and her response was 'oh there's a dog? I didn't know.' She then walked away and ignored the dog that she and the other wait staff were obviously aware of since it had been wandering around the restaurant.

Good food hall with a Santa Barbara flair.   It has been interesting seeing this market evolve into its current form. It offers great curb appeal and the single-story interior is surprisingly large. The entire block is a gentrified section of downtown Santa Barbara and I enjoy how well-kept it is. I also enjoy how the mid century tile mural of Santa Barbara's history (from the Vons that used to be in the location) was preserved and added to the western facade. If you look at it from left to right, it depicts a whimsical timeline of how our city came to be.   This venue was originally more of a market with the actual grocery section in the front. I assume that when it was removed, the focus was to shift to food only. Either way, I feel that the place has evolved nicely although there have been noticeable speed bumps along the way.   While this place always seems to have a few empty storefronts, those that do occupy the space are of quality. I'm a big fan of Empty Bowl and Fala Bar. Both offer exceptional food. There's also a great sushi spot though I've never tried for myself. A kitchen area in the rear was built to be a rentable commercial space. There is ample seating throughout and the market as a whole is very clean. There always seem to be janitorial staff working while I'm here.   I look forward to seeing the SBPM further evolve.

The owner and chef here (Justin) used to work at Intermezzo, a place we'd frequent when we lived in downtown SB.  Justin and his wife opened Julienne shortly after we moved back to LA, and we've been dying to try it ever since.  There was a swing dance at the Carrillo ballroom last weekend, so we thought we'd finally give the Julienne a shot.  And I'm sure glad we did.  Julienne is located two blocks off State Street, around the corner from the Wine Cask/Intermezzo and right next to another of our favorites, the Sojourner Cafe.  We parked in the lot across from the courthouse and it was an easy two block walk.  Tip:  The guard leaves that lot at 11pm, so if you can hang out until then your parking will be free (I don't know about you, but free parking always puts a smile on my face).  The dining room here is small and cozy, decorated in earth tones with leather seating and rich wood trim, and sports about twenty or so tables.  The kitchen is petite and open to the room, with a small bar in front of it.  Local art adorns the walls and modern Jazz reverberates throughout the room.  I'm liking this.  The menu is creative, featuring about 15 dishes with choices like Duck Dumplings, Braised Oxtail, Braised Quail, and Crispy Pork Belly.  There's also a few vegetarian plates like the Cauliflower Forets and Market Lettuces with Roasted Beets. They also have a fresh soup of the day as well as a number of Charcuterie selections.  The mantra here is fresh, local ingredients and seasonal flavors (Gordon Ramsay would be ecstatic) and the goal is to offer regional cuisine that is uniquely Santa Barbara.  IMHO, they've succeeded at the goal, and brilliantly so.  The wine list here has some interesting selections, both by the glass (~$12) and the bottle ($40 and up).  It's not an encyclopedia ala the Wine Cask, but then I wasn't expecting that from an intimate SB bistro.  We opted for a Mourvèdre (Chateau La Roque), which complemented our choices perfectly (especially the quail) and was very nice for the price.  We started with the Charcuterie platter, which included chicken liver toast, duck pate with pistachios and dried cherries, Mortadella, pork rillette, Sopressata, and pickled vegetables.  Every item was top notch, and the rillete and pate were notable.  For main courses, my GF opted for the Cauliflower Florets and I went with the Braised Quail. Both were delish and the quail was definitely a standout.   Very flavorful and well made.  You could taste the freshness and quality of the ingredients, much of which is sourced at the local Farmer's market.  Yum.  Dessert unfortunately wasn't quite as stellar;  They offer a small number of choices as well as various cheeses, ports and dessert wines. Nothing was really calling to us, but we ordered the sorbet and the churros, both of which were adequate, but not really what we were hoping for (if you're in the mood for something a bit more decadent, I'd suggest the Andersen's bakery a few blocks up on State St).    Note that if you're a size queen (portion size, that is) then Julienne might not float your boat.  The dishes here are larger than Tapas, but smaller than a traditional plates.  But then this is a place you come here to indulge your senses rather than wallow in depraved gluttony.  That said, we left completely satiated, no complaints in that department.  Quick tip: I'd suggest reservations, especially on the weekends and if you're dining after 6pm.  We arrived on a Friday night right when they opened at 5pm and were able to get a table without phoning ahead.  But by the time we left the place was pretty crowded and their phone was ringing constantly.  Walk in at your own risk.  All in all, an enjoyable, relaxing place to dine in the California Riviera.   Restaurants like Julienne are one of the reasons I chose to live in SB and the same reason I miss it so much now.  I'll definitely be back.

This place is a gem! They have a beautiful outside patio and spacious seating inside as well. Love the industrial look that they worked with.  It seems to be a popular spot, when we arrived, there was a line to order. The wait time after, however, was pretty fast.   We ordered the warm brown butter cinnamon roll and the breakfast sandwich on brioche. Each one was plentiful for us to share.   The cinnamon roll was so good! It was soft and well balanced in its sweetness. We wished it would've been warmer, when we got it it wasn't as hot as we would've liked.  The breakfast sandwich on brioche is pretty big! I really like the flavors, my one complaint is the egg patty is pretty thick and sometimes that's all I would taste.  We got drip coffee to wash it all down. Unfortunately, the coffee wouldn't be the reason why I would go back. I'd skip the coffee and get another drink.

Good spot to grab a hot dog after drinking!  I had the hot link and a final glass of beer to end my one night in Santa Barbara.  Apparently they have shaved ice too, which I'll have to try some other time.  It is what it is though... a joint to grab a quick drunken bite.

Best crispy tacos in Santa Barbara! Support local business and pick up your favorite breakfast burrito, nachos or crispy tacos to enjoy safely at home or at a park. All staff wearing masks and hand sanitizer on the ordering counter. Waiting area outside for pick up!

The Mesa has needed a burger joint for a long time and Im glad we have one now.  The food is good but a little pricey in my opinion.    You will most likely enjoy this meal but I was expecting a little more for a $12-$14 burger.  I would frequent this spot a lot more if it was a Hamburger Habit.

Located downtown towards the upped end on State Street.  However, welcome to Turkey!  An absolutely stunning place, Lokum offers all kinds of goodies and lovely things.  They are very generous with samples of all -- everything from baklava, Turkish delight, halvah, kunefe, katmer, rice pudding, Turkish coffee, and tea.  So, go already.

Trendy little spot Downtown.  We had pesto zucchini noodles with shrimp & goat cheese - this was surprisingly awesome, didn't think that I would like the zucchini noodle part but it came out fresh and light. Also has the sesame crusted salmon on soba noodles. Fish was excellent and hot with good Asian style flavors on it. The sobs noodles were good and went well with it but they were really cold, like right out of the refrigerator. I'd think it would go better if the soba noodz were hot. But overall we thought it was great. Staff could be a bit more attentive seeing that we were 1 of 2 tables seated in the restaurant.
Went in for Brunch had the Huevos Rancheros the best I have ever had service was spot on the blonde manager Kate was very friendly we will be back . Thanks JC

You can rest assured that if there is a cat cafe in any city I visit, I will find it. And from the several that I have visited, I can say that this place has the friendliest, most sociable cats in their shop. We went when they opened at 11am and So many of them were active! Several were napping the entire time, but the ones that were awake were all so curious about all their visitors, and would run around sniffing everything and climb into your lap to demand pets. They actively played with all the toys and some of the newer ones that were initially keep in a cage kept escaping to come out to play that the staff had to just give in and let them roam around as they please. They also have free coffee and tea here, and I also saw someone buy lunch to bring here to eat and I guess that was allowed too? Overall it's a very relaxed atmosphere here and the cats' personalities make it super fun and cheerful. It was much more of a 'let's all play together' vibe versus a 'aww how cute all the kitties are' that most other cat cafes have. It made it so all the humans visiting the cafe were a lot more friendlier with each other too, which was nice. I really enjoyed my visit!

Hope you like tacos, because all this here Lilly sells is tacos.  $1.6 apiece and sourcing meat from every nook and cranny of a cow, these are legit street tacos for a good price in Santa Barbara.    'How are the eye tacos?' I asked a neighbor in line.  'That's the one I can't get up the courage to try...'   Spoiler alert, it tastes like all of the other parts of cow head, but no one has to know but you.  And it is delicious.

Wow! I was in the mood for Thai and thought I'd give this  place a try. The yelp reviews don't lie! This place is awesome! The atmosphere is quaint, maybe a little dark. Tons of awesome Thai trinkets and detailing adoring the walls. As far as the food goes - I had the roast duck curry, which, frankly, was the best darn curry I've ever had. First of all, even for $18, there was so much duck in the curry and the portion was huge. The curry had such  a delicious and subtle flavor that i ended up eating all the excess liquid up with a spoon! Inside the curry was tender, juicy duck, delicious sweet basil, tomatoes, yum! I often order duck curry, as it is my favorite curry, and this is my new favorite place to get it! Can't wait to go back again and try something else!

Dave's Dogs is incredibly good and there's nothing else like it in the area. Dave and his staff exemplify what customer service should be like. I found out about him a year ago when he had a mobile spot in old Town Goleta and was blown away then as well. Since, I've been to the milpas location, and now was so excited to find out he has a store front, close to my house. Dave makes the experience personal. He comes over and talks to you, repeats how grateful he is to you as a customer, talks about what's new and good. Last night he threw in a large bag of chips just because we were asking some questions about them. Told us even for being older customers he still does the 3 for $10 combo which wasn't on the menu, which is something he doesn't have to do. All the hot dogs are amazing. Super creative styles. I tried the new meat lovers grilled cheese, and it is really good, glad I gave it a shot. All the other staff is as friendly as Dave is, and theres just this atmosphere there that they enjoy being there. Anyways, can't say enough good things about these guys. Try them out.

This is the most unique restaurant in SB!  Tapas style eats with a super creative menu.  The drinks are some of the absolute best in town and the bartender is just as tasty and super friendly!  Great staff, cool ambiance, tasty food and beverages.  What more would you want?

I worked across from Intermezzo for 3+ years and never actually went, so when a friend was visiting from out of town and asked for a nicer central restaurant to go to, I decided to give this place a shot. We had a larger group, party of 7, and made a reservation for 8:00, but they were very backed up and we ended up waiting for probably about an hour. That being said, they were incredibly apologetic and gave us two rounds of free champagne while we were waiting, which definitely helped. Once we finally sat down, we had our own little area to ourselves, which was awesome, and thus began probably one of the best restaurant experiences I've ever had. Our server was so charismatic and knowledgeable, and the owner John even came by to check on us and chatted with us for quite a while. We ordered a bunch of dishes to share, including oysters on the half shell, pâté de campagne, steak frites (which was a special), gambas al ajillo, panko herb chicken breast, and sides of pan roasted mushrooms and potato gratin. One of my friends ordered the limoncello and ended up talking to our server about it, which we found out was made on site! He ended up taking us down to the cellar to show us his limoncello and mandarincello batches, as well as a chili-infused vodka, and all of this included free samples! Not only that, but there was a slideshow on the tv in our room, and while we were talking to John, he mentioned that he was in some of the slides, and said that if we were able to guess which ones, he'd give us a free round of shots. I scrutinized the photos and ended up guessing the correct ones, so we got free shots of tequila. We ended up closing out the restaurant and had such a fantastic time with the friendliest people. On top of that, all of the food was AMAZING. The steak frites was so perfectly cooked (and I'm not usually a big steak person), the chicken was delicious, and I absolutely loved the pâté. Oh, we also got the meyer lemon crème brûlée and espresso chocolate mousse for dessert, both incredible as well. Who would have known that a place I literally sat across the street from for years would end up giving me one of the most memorable restaurant experiences I've ever had?!

The food is actually really great. I've been here a couple times and I've narrowed it down to ordering just the chicken curry with noodles and baguette, and if I'm really feelin' it, I'll get pho. However, I like the place down the street for pho and regular vermicelli. They are understaffed so prepare to wait a long time for your food or for.. anything. Service: 2/5. Food: 5/5.

Was very lucky to grab a box as they sell out in under a minute! I drove up from Ventura and it was well worth it! Absolutely fantastic flavors and the owner, Tommy, could not have been kinder or more appreciative! The whole experience was so easy and pleasant. The mochi donuts are such a unique treat and I must say I am craving them and cannot wait for Mor Doughnuts to return! Would love to see them setup a storefront where scoring a box wasn't so difficult. I will say though I was slightly disappointed though not to receive one of the beautiful shiny boxes like what I had seen in the photos.

I have been coming to Bucatini for years and this visit I tried their new vegan burger.  I do not eat hamburger but if they tasted like this, I would start:).   I have never liked the vegetarian patties offered at restaurants but this one is really good.  If you are vegetarian or just trying to eat less murdered sentient beings ( ok, I admit I am an animal rights human), you should try this new plant based burger.  I will be back for another one soon.  I also really like the ambience and service at Bucatini.

Amazing vegan fried chicken sandwich on telera bread with a spicy buffalo sauce. The vegan molcajete in the lava stone was impressive and delicious. The vegan carne asada looked and tasted like the real thing

I work in the area and try to give every restaurant a go. I've walked past Sachi Ramen numerous times and always see the delicious looking items on their menu outside. The service was excellent and the staff really treat their customers well. They were very attentive and polite, which is the main reason I may return and give this place another shot.   I'm by no means a ramen expert, but I do frequent other ramen places such as Nikka Ramen in Goleta, SB Craft Ramen on State St and a few of the more popular places in Ventura and Los Angeles. As other reviewers have mentioned, the presentation is definitely lacking on a $14+ dish. I consider that a moot point since it's all going to be mixed up in my belly anyway. Moving on, though- I'm not sure if it was the entree, cleanliness of the restaurant, or the dishware they were using. There was a fragrance of wet mildew-like laundry or damp socks that I could not stomach. Best I could describe it, if you've every repeatedly rinsed your water glass to the point you can smell the lip and know it needs some soap to make the smell go away. The broth tasted excellent but was quite watered down and I wasn't crazy about the thin noodles they used.   I regularly eat spicy food (especially ramen), but I had a bit of a stomach ache after eating here to be honest, and on that note will probably not return.

I love this place because of its colorful, healthy, delicious vegan options!   Kimchi bowl: my favorite dish because of the brussel sprouts, kimchi and coconut rice--subtly sweet and creamy!   Curry bowl: also very good, not spicy--i like spicy so i wish it had a little more kick to it but my friends who don't like spice love this dish!   Nomsters: the peanut sauce is YUM! And the rice paper raps are so fresh.   Dillies: i had a bite off my friends plate and they were very good and i want to try them again.

They have the best albondigas soup in town. Their chicken soup is full of fresh vegetables- both great low carb options if you order them without rice.  This is a family owned business. All of the staff are friendly. If you have any concerns the owner always makes things right!

So my 10 year old son and I went to hit up some donuts before school starts and... yes! Obviously these donuts aren't your average donut that you grew up with. Yes, gourmet donuts like these are trending these days and yes, Hook And Press is doing it proper.   The coffee... meh. Not my style. A little too bitter but I gave mine to a homeless guy on state street who was super thankful, so that felt good.   I'll return I'm sure.

Review based on breakfast service: Had the potential for four or five stars. The food is generally excellent, if smaller portioned. However they started cutting corners, like providing fake creamer for the coffee. Surprising at this price point.  When I asked for real cream or milk the waiter wanted to bring me more of the plastic creamer packets, which I hadn't touched, as if he had no idea the difference. The servers are friendly enough but inattentive. Had to wait a long time to get anyone's attention for what we needed during the meal and also had to track somebody down to pay the check after waiting a loooonnng time. I've been twice now and I won't be back due to these issues, although I rather liked the breakfast. Also a tip: they stop serving breakfast around 10:30.

I didn't even know there were any Sambo's left. I went as a little, boy and probably haven't been to one in 30 or 40 years.   Food was very good, service excellent and the location can't be beat. We sat eating our breakfast overlooking the ocean.   Santa Barbara is beautiful and this restaurant has a great location.  For those of you that are old enough little nostalgic also.

Stay away. Stay waaaay away.  Apparently, having a working TV is too much to ask. And you can't call for assistance because the phone doesn't work either. Hana and Devin at the front desk told me they would fix it in the morning.   Hmmmm. I'm leaving in the morning. Sooo. That doesn't help so much..   This is a shoddy operation. Stay away at all costs.  I guess the mythical Manager is named Gabe. No one knows his last name or how to get a hold of him. Shocking.  All for the low, low price of 240 a night.  Consider setting your wallet on fire and sleeping in the street first.
Very good customer service. Servers are the most polite of anywhere I know like they have actually been trained.   Food is very good for fast food. Chicken is not overcooked. Always tender.

Great place, price and unique food.  Very cool, but not 5 stars.  If your looking for a light bite, give it a try and you'll be surprised on their unique take on Mexican food.  I really like it, but not a five star place.

We called our order in before we went.   I don't think that they read any of my special notes as my sandwich did not have  any of the modifications requested.   Egg sandwich was trying to do too much. The bread was too thick and the mushrooms were a bit much.   Coffee seemed very watery.   Croissant sandwich was tasty. Eggs had a tad too much pepper but the eggs were prepared nicely and were nice and fluffy. I generally don't like ham and had asked for turkey or bacon nested. But the ham away very good. You could taste the quality in the ingredients used.   We also got the infamous almond croissant which we both felt was overhyped. It had a strange slimy custard-like filling inside which was off putting.   Im not sure if we would return considering all of the other dining options in SB.

This spot is great! There was a wait so we were next door at their pizza joint. (Honor Pizza) We were able to get cocktails and a pizza while we waited for a table. Michael our server was great and very attentive and Sophia the manager was professional and nice and always checked up on us! Definitely a must when in Montecito! We will be back!!!

Had a lovely experience at Noemi today! We ordered the mushroom pizza and it was absolutely fantastic! Love the fermented dough and it's light airyness!! One of my new fave places in Santa Barbara and will definitely be going back to their patio for more of their yummy slices!!

I love supporting local businesses but SBRC is sadly a disappointment. Overpriced and over-roasted beans. All their beans are roasted medium to medium dark! Honestly what's the point of paying $42 for 12 oz of Kona beans to have it practically burnt. They really should offer a LIGHT roast for their beans, so that you can actually taste the flavor of the beans and not to mention that a light roast packs the highest amount of caffeine.

Delicious food, but wish they had a better drink menu and a bar.   We went on a Friday and glad we made a reservation, it was a 1.5+ hour wait without it. We started with the Kung Pao cauliflower, wontons (which we ordered an additional order since they were sooooo good - as legit as a dim sum restaurant) and tuna tartare. I'd order them all again, but if I had to choose just one, I'd go with the the wontons (shrimp and scallop with a yummy sauce).  For dinner we shared a few KFC, Shrimp and Mushroom boas. My wife LOVED the KFC - made with breast meat and were crunchy but juicy. I loved the shrimp - huge shrimps that were also crispy and tasty. Our friend loved the mushroom - which were breaded too. Next time I hope they have the octopus.  The boa buns were amazing - light and fluffy but not too dense or 'carby'. They complimented the fillings very well.   Only thing I wish they had was a better drink selection. They had a limited selection of wine and beer (some local but majority were Asian brands), but a cocktail really sounded great.   The place is really nice inside, where it was lively but not to loud. Good for a small group or dinner date (tapas style).   Overall food was a 5, ambience a 5, service a 5 and drinks selection a 2 - hence my 4 rating (high 4 though!) We will definitely go again.

I have heard mixed reviews about the establishment. Upon sitting two gents rave about the brussels sprouts and offer up a taste, and they were good. However, everything my party ordered was lacking. The yellowtail had a flavorful description. The yellowtail's plate was filled with flavors of waterlogged over sized bell pepper,    un-marinated 'marinated' cucumber, aioli that tasted like mayo with no hint of citrus, flakeless chili flakes, something red not listed on menu, and unsavory fish.
