The deviled eggs had little flavor of the listed items, mediocre, not great. Such simple palette teasing items left much dissatisfaction.   Service was slow, extremely slow. Even the gents across from us whom are regulars complained the service was unusually slow for slow night. After asking for our check, 20 minutes pass and my husband finally approached the hostess.   We left and went to 7 for a burger. They are consistent and always have a wonderful egg's florentine during brunch.   Overpriced for little flavor.
We love this place some of the best food in our area. We don't normally eat fish but the fish from lure is outstanding in my opinion
Awesome service. Food that is creative, authentic, unique, filled with international spices and peppers and lobster, fishes, beef. Divine. The owner is fantastic!
Horrible Pizza I'd say 2/10 would've been better off at Dominos.  Was unflavorful and just not really a good experience eating here.

One of Santa Barbara chill spots with amazing flavors .  Walked in on New Years day and had the phyllo crusted Seabass  that  with a passion fruit sauce . Along with the mango salsa this was a well put together delight to start the new year! Jason was great at the bar and so love the atmosphere every chance I get to visit .. see you more around Film Fest time .

This is the place to go for tacos in Santa Barbara if you're indecisive because you truly can't go wrong with anything.   We got the flautitas tapatias with beef. I don't know how they cook their rice but it was so delicious. The guac tasted super fresh and the beans were very well cooked too.   For myself I got the costar al pastor, taco azteca (steak and rajas), and taco gobernador (melted cheese with shrimp). Everything was super tasty, and I would definitely be back for other tacos next time.   Street parking available.

underwhelmed. the lasagna was dry and old. only tiny bits of dry meat sauce between the thick dry  pasta, and none on top.    fococcia bread dry and stale.   service was lacking. finally had to go leave our table and stand to wait at register, after waiting for check.  I'd give it 1 star, but at least they were open. my bad. should have are elsewhere.  won't return here.

Excellent seafood. The service was fair but the food made up for it. The mixed drinks are ok. I will stick with the white wine. Nice view of the marina. We will definitely go back when visiting Santa Barbara.

This place is Amazing. My first time eating inside the restaurant. As a mom and having kids I always appreciate a lot when people treat my kids like theirs and make them feel at home. Cara Wilkins was especially accommodating, kind and very patient taking my little girl order. It was amazing to see how kind she was. We will definitely be back again.

Such a disappointment! I think all of the crowds they see from tourism have gone to their heads. ... as a local who made a reservation 2 weeks in advance and reserved a very nice spot for a special occasion, table 233. Upon arriving we were seated at a crowded table in between two tables and two dogs. - definitely not adhering  to social distancing what-so-ever, there was maybe 2 feet.   Upon asking to move to a quieter more distanced table- the server said no problem and also informed us the table we had reserved was converted to a 6 top- understandable but you know what we were aiming for.   After being reseated by the server the host then came over and asked us to move. Un-believable and just tacky. Saying we could wait for another table. So we left. I'll miss Loquita a little, however,  it's not worth all that with so many amazing places to eat in my hometown.
I love this place in the mall. The teriyaki bowls are delicious. Miso soup is so good & it's a great place to pick up for take out. I have had the rolls all the time!

My family and I asked for a table for 6 people. They told us it would be a 20 minute wait. There were 13 empty tables on the patio. They were waiting to clear one table that happened to be in the direct sunlight. We had a newborn with us. After we explained that we really needed to have the baby in the shade, they put two of the more than dozen empty tables together to accommodate us. When you have more empty tables then guests, you would think it would be easy to seat a medium sized group.

I've finally found my sushi spot in town! & it's close to the newer Handlebar coffee. My husband only eats rice dishes and I love a little bit of both sushi and Japanese homey dishes. In most places in town, they are only good at one or the other. This place is great at both. The family feel of this restaurant is great. They were all so friendly and we felt really welcomed.  We can't wait to go back!  I've also had their food delivered and it travels really well.
Good sushi, very fresh fish, nice creations. Deserves definitely more stars than on yelp so far!

Happy hour: 3-6pm everyday  Happy hour special: $2 off starters, $1 off all beers, $5 house Chardonnay and Cabernet  We were on a happy hour mission so we tried not to eat too much at each place. We came in for drinks and got some clam chowder. I personally was not impressed with the clam chowder but the wine was good! This place isn't so much for those who like liquor or beers.   All in all, good place to gather some friends and wine about life. Haha, get it?

Love love this place. Not only is the food amazing, and the atmosphere and ambiance feels traditional Lebanese.  favorite drink is the watermelon crush Vegetarian combo appetizer gives you a bit of all the yummies. If you like watermelon like I do must try the halloumi cheese with watermelon.  They used to have belly dancers before Covid which made it even more fun.  Every SB trip we have to come here so highly recommend.
Most authentic tri tip sangich that you can get everyday during the work week. Service is excellent, food is delicious. 5 stars.

As stated in many reviews the triple play is the best way to go. I really like the spicy crispy shrimp tacos but the ahi ones are great too. Fast and friendly service. The sauce they give you is super spicy.

Kyle's Kitchen is an unexpected gem in Santa Barbara. At first they look like some sort of chain restaurant like Eureka but no, they actually have really great quality fast food.   We came during a Sunday and their daily deal was chicken sandwiches and I must say, I was pleasantly surprised at how delicious it was. I guess I wasn't expecting to have received such a quality crispy chicken sandwich given the sort of sub-par marketing and location. Needless to say, I have definitely come back and I am excited to try more things from their menu.

For the price, Arigato isn't worth it. With that said, the sushi restaurant itself isn't all that bad. It's the best one downtown without a doubt. The menu lacks creativity, but the fish is fresh and the view of State Street is enjoyable.   My favorite roll is the Dracula roll. It's a glorified Spicy Tuna roll presented with Sriracha drawn on a plate to resemble blood. My absolute favorite item on the menu is the Godzilla ice cream. It's deep fried green tea ice cream. Again, both items are delicious but lack creativity and are awfully small considering the price!  Would I come back? Probably. Will it ever be my favorite sushi joint in Santa Barbara? No way. Shintori has already claimed that spot in my heart.

I'm not quite sure how I managed to live in Santa Barbara all those years, yet never stumble upon the Brewhouse.  Hidden back behind the train station, and away from the busy bustle of State Street, the brewhouse is the perfect place to grab a pitcher and a bite. The bf and I both agreed that the beers were good, as was our corn beef sando. On a nice day we could probably post up here for a few hours to eat and have a pitcher... or two! Next time I come I am going to try the turkey dip, it looked delicious!  I highly recommend this place for a lazy afternoon to be spent with friends, pitchers, and food.

I love this bakery!!! Their conchas are so soft fit for a king!!! By far the best panaderia that I have ever been to!!! Their closing time is funny: it is when they run out of bread!!! Yeah they are that awesome!!! Only thing is cash only. You will have to go to the gas station accross the street for atm services. However the bread is totally worth it!!!!

Loved the food and the prices, very efficient.  We had the Saag gosht, everyday curry with chicken, naan, and potato curry appetizer ... all first quietly, tasted so authentic.  Be careful about ordering 'spicy' .. I think medium is best, still a very nice kick.  Really good, will be back!

My Groom and I love to walk on the weekends; it's been regarded as some of the best conversations and decisions time for us.  We headed over to Dunkin from the Mesa.  Having not been out to restaurants for 15 months due to Covid and our own concerns and fears we thought this would be a great start to acclimate in to society.  Only upon arrival Dunkin was gone and so went our memories...:(. So sad how many things, people, places Covid has taken away.  My Groom was devastated.  I hadn't realized how difficult this could be on him until I suggested we go to our favorite empanadas place bc next door is the Hook and Press where they sell donuts.  We set about our walk and by the time we arrived there my Grooms anxiety was somewhat talked out.  He ordered the donut holes and an americano I ordered the fudge cake crumble donut with an Americano.  It was early on a Sunday therefore not a lot of people were out so we were comfortable sitting outside.  We had our first sip and bites, the smile on my Grooms face said it all.  The donut I had was fresh thick combination glaze cake, was rich with flavor and essence of lavender, ' oh my '  I said. 'Let new memories begin.' :)

So I'm only giving 4 stars because I ordered their food for takeout, but their beef is top notch!! The burger with baby lettuce wrap is one of the freshest and juiciest I've tasted. And I'm a burger whore. Their chili is just spicy enough and melts in your mouth. Delish.

I use to go to Tap very often before the pandemic and everything was fine. A friend sent me an order from there this week , and asked for it to be MILD , the pad Thai dish was so spicy it  literally burnt the skin  off inside my mouth . I couldn't eat it and the chicken was so dried out you could use it for ice hockey pucks . I called the restaurant to discuss the awful situation with them . The owner was rude -  took the defensive saying impossible we don't use chiles in that  dish. Instead of saying I'm so sorry let me send you a corrected  dish She actually hung up on me . I was shocked at het outrageous , unprofessional demeanor . The order was sent from Grub Hub a present  for my birthday . She told me to tell my friend to get a refund from them I explained to her it was  a gift  And not Grub Hubs fault  It was her chefs fault  She wouldn't take responsibility for the disaster .Naturally I would never deal  with her again or recommend Tap to people like I did in the past . I wouldn't tell my friend what Happened because it was so kind of him to Get it fit me as a birthday gift,  And I didn't want to hurt his feelings . I explained that to the owner  several times ,and she said no way would she make good on the dish.  Poor customer service on her part . Shame on her !

on a whim we decided to try this place. and better than in n out?? i dont think so. the burger was basically of cafeteria quality in terms of flavor, and texture, the presentation was a step above. but i think i would rather eat carls jr which i havent eaten in years than this place, maybe even mcdonalds, i was THAT underwhelmed. fries were ok.

When I think of Santa Barbara I think of one thing when it comes to food: Santa Barbara sea urchin. I had to have it out there... otherwise it would be like going to Italy and not having pasta. We yelped and found fish market. Located on the pier with limited parking. They had ONE sea urchin left, which only had 5 pieces :(. There was also a $7 shucking fee.I hear they offer trays of it for $8.95 but they ran out. I was kind of disappointed because I've had uni for about the same price at nice restaurants in LA with the same (or better) quality. Oh and the guys that work there probably need to learn a thing or two about customer service.

I remember when Chubbies first opened(!) A Santa Barbara mainstay for many, many years, it's a small, old-school no-nonsense burger joint with great quality food and a small dine-in area.  Menu options are simple and straight forward, single and double cheeseburgers, fries, shakes and sodas. Ordering online is fast and easy. Food is made to order and has always been hot and fresh at pickup. I'll take our local Habit or Chubbies any day over the absurd lines at In-N-Out. I also think both have more flavor than In-N-Out. Chubbies is a fried patty vs. Habit's char-broil, but I find they are comparable in terms of overall enjoyment and freshness. Chubbies fries are skinny but taste much fresher and crispier than McDonalds. Shakes are certified 'best since Fosters Freeze' by my son. Prices are great too - you can't lose with Chubbies!

Their Torta Ahogada is amazing! Now this is Mexican food. The sauce they use is spicy spicy but that good heat you get mad at your mouth for not being able to handle it because you want to keep on eating.
Used to be wonderful but the past 2 dinners were all wrong, cold and not tasty. Won't be back.
Love this place (5 stars)  Been here a couple of times Great nan. Love the chicken dishes. One was in a red sauce. Another a red sauce. Delicious.  I definitely plan to be back.

5-alive cocktail = 4 stars. Alma Rosa Pinot Noir = 5 stars.  Brussels sprouts = 4.5 stars. Artichoke cakes = 4 stars.  Pad Thai = 5 stars Lotus bowl = 4 stars (Lotus bowl pros: amazing, high-quality ingredients, including the finest forbidden rice I've encountered, all served at roastingly wonderful temperature) (Lotus bowl cons: was hoping for a more distinct or richer flavor) Mushroom tostada = 4 stars.  Apple crumble dessert al a mode  = 5 stars.  Service = 5 stars.
We are trying to eat healthier to reduce blood sugar. Eating vegan is not my first choice, but if you have to, this is a good spot to get your vegetables in.

You do not need to go to the South of France.  This place is right next to the pier.  The view from the terrace at dinner is straight out of the South of France.  The Riviera.  My favorite place in Europe! And the food is better than the  South of France!  It is wonderful Italian food!  It is the most wonderful, fresh, delicious food ever. The chef is a woman.....and she is brilliant!   This restaurant is an enormous gift to Santa Barbara!  Yowzers!  I wish I had taken a picture!  Next time!!!

Housed in the former Santa Barbara Fish Market, this Santa Barbara hot spot exudes local charm, historical significance and modern style. The space is warm and inviting with vestiges of the Lark's industrial past seen all around. The staff are personable, fun and attentive. Entrees range from $17-$42 but many can be shared family-style.  Enjoy an alfresco garden party on the lovely patio trimmed with delicate lights or cozy up under a heat lamp with a fleece blanket on one of those chilly Santa Barbara evenings.   My friend and I shared several appetizers. The Rosemary & Brown Butter Cornbread served with a child honey butter was the perfect opener. You get a very nice herbal punch from the rosemary but it's very subtle and refreshing. My friend and I loved the wonderful marriage of rosemary and sweet brown butter.   The Crispy Brussel Sprouts come garnished with medjool dates and sesame seeds and drizzled lime. They are crispy and crunchy with a touch of sweet from the dates. Our server warned us that the brussel sprouts are a good sized --they can almost double as an entree--appetizer so we swapped one of our entrees we were going to order for a salad.   The Yellowtail Hamachi Crudo highlights some of the fresh summer seasonal ingredients bringing together compressed watermelon, shishito peppers, summer gazpacho, candied coriander along with some fresh lemon and basil. The enticing flavors are very well balanced.     We shared the Little 'Gems' Wedge salad featuring house smoked blue cheese, warm glazed bacon, spicy pecans all tossed in a refreshing herb vinaigrette. This beautifully oversized salad is dressed with so much fresh Santa Barbara bounty and is just so beautiful to behold. We really enjoyed this salad especially on a warm Santa Barbara summer evening paired with some wine. My friend picked up a nice Tempranillo from one of his wine tasting stops so we shared a bottle over dinner. There is a $25 corkage fee.   The Hand Cut Pappardelle was excellent. So rich, robust and infinitely creamy. It's accentuated with piedmont white truffles, shaved parmesan, English snap peas and pine nuts. It looks small but it is so filling (2 of us didn't even finish it) especially when paired with one of their salads and an app or two. I would definitely order this one again.   We shared a wonderful dinner at the Lark. This place is very popular so be sure to book your reservation. Valet parking is available as the parking fills up quick. It's a short jaunt to the beach so you can enjoy a nice post-meal evening beach stroll.

Service at the bar was great!! Drinks were very good. We tried both the ginger lemonade and coconut ginger mojito. The raja peanuts and onion pakoras were good but not great, the onion strips needed salt.

Delightful, delightful! I had the caprese, lentil soup, and osso bucco, accompanied by some nice wine. They claim to be authentic Italian and I couldn't agree more. From the classic preparation of the caprese (without the misguided addition of balsamic vinegar) to the fall-of-the-bone perfectly tender and aromatic osso bucco with risotto...this was executed to perfection! Mollie's is what a fake like Bouchon aspires to be.

If I could give more stars I would ! My honey I have have came here numerous time. Delicious food and amazing  service. Tonight Andrew tooK care of us and he was amazing. Didn't bother  us too much like some restaurants servers can tend to do sometimes. He was polite and went above and beyond. Even took care of our sodas for being return customers. Such a cool guy ! Will continue to come back and hope he is here :)

Seriously great food. Intense flavors and not greasy at all. If Santa Barbarans can get over their pretentiousness of the fact that it's not a fancy restaurant it will do really well. Everything I tried was amazing... the sesame beef was great. The price point is a little much for the establishment but a good choice nonetheless.

Loves this place!  I just wanted a classic American breakfast on vacation and a clean comfortable place to sit and they delivered!  The food was awesome. The line looked long but it moved fast. They clean all the tables and follow COVID guidelines.

Another gem in Santa Barbara! Love the location, walking distance to the beach. My first time here it took me two walk arounds to find the place. Outside seating is perfect way to get your day started. I order the Breakfast Sandwich on Brioche and the Ricotta Toast with Strawberry Jam. Both were delish!! I was blown away with the fluffy egg on the brioche. The egg had a fluffy light texture which was contrasted by the crunchy bacon. I really enjoyed the ricotta toast which had the perfect amount of sweetness, crunch and creaminess. Can't wait to visit this gem again.

Well I wish I had gone to this place when it first opened because I've been missing out. I looked at the menu online and it didn't look like it had very much for vegans so I took a pass. But we took a chance tonight and it turns out they have a separate plant-based menu especially for people like me and it was delicious! The sunchoke bravas were amazingly delicious, the right combination of tender and crispy and flavorful with the accompanying aïoli. The brussels sprouts were in the Santa Barbara tradition but as well done as they are ever done here. And the under named vegetable plate was a delicious combination of farmers market fresh vegetables and orzo with whipped butter nut squash. My non-vegan friend had a spiny lobster pasta dish and there were lots of other options for people less restricted than I am.  The service was also perfect. Our waitress was friendly and attentive without being annoying. The other servers who brought us our plates were also helpful and articulate, and the manager was charming when I asked to meet her.  I suspect it gets a bit noisy when crowded; we were there early when it was very easy to talk. Otherwise, the ambience was delightful and creative in decor.  I'm glad I took a chance on the place!
A good taco bell, they got my order right and even added the right sauce :o)

Used a Groupon to go to one of our favorite spots in SB.  Not only did the AMAZING December sunset set the stage, but the waiter Jesse we had was exceptional.  Very attentive to our needs and the fact that we had to send two items back as they were cold.  He was attentive from the moment we sat.  Waters filled, plates cleared, smile on his face and not too pushy.  Had great recommendations that we were pleased with.  After complimenting him we asked him how long he had been a waiter and we were surprised he said he was a new waiter.   Good job Boathouse.  Nice hire.    Food was good, but came out cold.  We split the calamari, the blackened sea bass and a bowl of clam chowder.  Bread came out cold, as did our meal, but we kindly asked them to re-heat, in which they brought out all new.  They then took half our bill off due to the inconvenience.  Not necessary in our eyes, but very great service.  We had 2 lemon drops and wine.  Great tasting libations for sure.  I love this restaurant.  Food and location great!
Great beer selection and fabulous burgers. The brioche bun is so yummy. Try with an egg on top!

I should love this place but I don't.  The food is ok, some things even rate  a good.  It's the service, or lack of, that I can't get past. The place w cute, sort of like a diner. Customers mostly look like regulars. Either they don't mind the curt attitude or I haven't  been accepted into the group yet. I won't be sticking around to find out. Too many other options.

Love this little place and everything they stand for. Healthy, delicious food at affordable prices and allowing their employees medical freedom. If you're afraid of covid and love masks and being an obedient little sheep, stay home, this place isn't your vibe.

Went for lunch at 11:30 today with the girls from the office.  It wasn't busy but we waited a long, long time for our food to arrive.  One of the gals ordered the melted mozzarella panini and it wasn't hot when it arrived.  The potatoes gratin were super delicious the rest of the food was just OK.  I would go back to try the dessert crepes, the dessert menu looked scrumptious.

This is a restaurant that needs to be avoided. The service was horrible and the management is completely indifferent to any issues. I have contacted the restaurant repeatedly and they have not responded that is why i am writing a negative review. This restaurant knows how to get negative reviews removed through Yelp so be weary that there should be many more negative reviews. One of the people who worked here was very rude however i was not able to get their name.

Yuck. Everything I got was gross. Tofu salad with the slimiest tofu EVER. The dressing was all vinegar and there was a piece of half cooked broccoli? Wth the gyoza was soft with some kind of unidentifiable veggie smoosh inside. The tempura vegetables weren't bad but not great either. My daughter loves the veggie roll from here but not worth a $55 dinner when you need a bowl of cereal after

As a CrepeAHolic, read this review carefully:  I've sampled Crepes from all over California, I'm here to tell you:  these are the best Crepes in California. Add a white Mocha or Hot Chocolate for a superb breakfast experience.  Go out of your way for the Crepes here and thank me when you're done.

I love South Coast Deli! Any location in town is fantastic. They can get busy at times but they always have great service and great food! Today I ordered a Mexican Chicken Caesar Salad and it did not disappoint! SBMenus.com delivered for a reasonable fee - great turn around time from when I placed the order to delivery to my house.

Santa Barbara needed a place like this so bad - and I'm happy to report its wonderful!  The drinks are outstanding, the food is amazing (we came here 2 nights in a row just to keep trying new items from the menu) and the waitstaff & bartenders are friendly & helpful.  It's a little off the beaten path, which I love because its not overrun by the State Street crowd - lots of locas here.  I love it - it feels like a great little place you would find in Portand.

2 of us came here for happy hour post exploring the area with the 2nd oldest building in California! We had a glass of Syrah from Napa as well as a Voignier from BallardLane-both were good at $5 each and came with complimentary crostini topped with mashed potatoes+lots of bacon bits. Sat outside which was perfect for people watching while sipping our wines. Also took-away 2 slices of cakes w marzipan in it and even 2 days later, they both tasted divine! Extra star is for the fact that I was given a taste of the cakes before deciding which one to order! Explored the huge space upstairs as well and even played the piano for a bit.
Delicious food. Quick service. Great price.  I get the Bacon B-egg-el, and instead of a bagel sub whole wheat bread. Add avocado and lemon pepper. It is my favorite breakfast in SB!!
I can't believe I've lived here 35 years and never eaten here. This is my new favorite restaurant.

My husband and I just went to lunch (I made a reservation). Most of the seats had direct sun on them, so if you don't like sun, don't go during the day or sit inside. The food was ok, to be honest, I didn't personally  think it was amazing but it wasn't bad. I wasn't a fan of the Brussels but the crispy asparagus was my jam. The other food was fine. Service was fine. There were a lot of people working, not sure what they were all doing. Our server was nice. The restaurant was nice and clean and you can't beat the location. I'll definitely go back and try it again especially for dinner, since dinner has been open longer. It isn't cheap but again, it's SB and you can't beat the location.

Excellent! 5 stars on service, 5 stars on food. Came for breakfast on this misty  Saturday morning, and this place was hopping. We were seated quickly. Angel was our server, and he was on it, very polite and hard working.  I ordered an omelette, they were happy to add spinach, mushrooms, onions, green peppers, jack cheese and zucchini. Vegetables weren't cooked to oblivion the way some places do, these vegetables were nice and crisp tender. Also had the hash browns and English muffin. My husband had the huevos rancheros with chile verde, and a bloody Mary. The service was prompt, food came out quickly and was served piping hot, everything was done to perfection. Best breakfast in the Santa Barbara / Goleta area. We've been here multiple times, and the food and service is always on par. Nice to support a local business. Great service, great food!! Yum!

I have eaten here a couple times now.  The food is excellent and I have always experienced excellent customer service.  For lunch one time I tried the tuna melt (awesome) and had a really good mint green tea.  Today I had a breakfast burrito.  The breakfast burrito was delicious.  It wasn't greasy and came in a wheat tortilla.  I loved it.

Hendrys has an ideal location right on the beach if you're looking for views, but the wait was 1.5 hours on a weekend. You can do takeout and it is ready in about 20-30 and you can take it to the beach or benches overlooking the beach. I ordered the kale salad to go and added chicken. The salad was very small, if I didn't add chicken it would have been pretty disappointing. The salad itself was not very good, the kale was super tough and there were minimal toppings. Definitely wouldn't go back for food, but maybe for a drink with a scenic view.
Acai bowls are very tasty and options are plenty. Service is professional and friendly.

Visiting Dutch Garden is like a being transported by time warp to the old country with it's thoughtful, old-world decor. The owners and staff immediately put you at ease and make you feel like you are part of the family -- which is a mark of a restaurant that knows who they are and what they are doing.  The food is fantastic -- whether you are having a ham hock, schnitzel or fresh catch of the day.  My family and I were impressed and will certainly visit again soon!

Stopped in today with my fiancé and parents.   Immediately we were impressed with the service they set up a table for us outside. Their location is great, right on Haley street in the heart of Santa Barbara.   They let us know they were having free wine tastings and they even brought us out a nigiri sushi appetizer as a gift from the chef and it was very good. It had three pieces of ahi and three pieces of salmon on top of some farmers market tomatoes and avocado.   They had a great selection of local wines and a very interesting food menu to match.   What we ordered: - Ahi and Salmon Nigiri (one of my favorite dishes and just as good as any top level sushi restaurant with interesting presentation) - Wild Mushroom & Truffle Sacchetti with sage cream (this was probably my favorite dish along with the sushi) - Pulled pork nachos (very good, had a slight Asian theme and the pulled pork was very tender) - Filet Mignon Sliders with Bernaise sauce (very interesting but needed a little more salt and pepper also comes with fries) - Lobster Sliders ( I liked this dish but also needed a tad bit more seasoning)   Overall this is a great restaurant and definitely worth a try. This is their first week in business and I was very impressed. I would be happy to go back.

Underwhelmed by Thai food in Santa Barbara? I'm going to help you out:   Order the Larb Gai. It's light and refreshing and crazy addictive. I fell in love with this dish and was sadly disappointed when I tried to order it elsewhere. Tap Thai does a lively version that is heaven, especially on a blazing hot summer night when you don't want to cook.   Spicy. basil. chicken. That is all.  Such an easy dish to overlook on the menu, but not one for which there will be any leftovers the next day.  The peppers are sweet and a great contrast to the sauce, which isn't cloying like so many standard dishes. The egg is a nice touch and it all sops up well with the rice.   I never leave disappointed when I order these two. And yes, I order both :)

Amazing new find! RK & D is located directly in front of a nice clean part of East beach with beautiful ocean views.  The staff is phenomenal! Literally... every staff member. You can really feel they work as a team trying to make each guest experience great.  The food was EXACTLY what we were looking for during lunch. I was craving a nice salad and some yummy sides and Boom! Found it.  I had the Caesar salad with grilled chicken which was perfectly balanced- it wasn't swimming in dressing it was light but still flavorful with delicious croutons.  We also had the avocado won tons ( which were excellent), baby bleu salad ( also delicious), and my toddler had the kids steak and tots plate ( I must admit if I was a kid I would be spoiled with how good that steak was).  Will definitely be coming here again for a nice relaxed and enjoyable lunch by the beach ..away from the crowds. For parents- there's also a cute playground right by the restaurant on the beach if your little ones went to play!

Everything is fantastic on the menu!   There's typically a line outside the door when I drive by.  The homemade corn tortillas are awesome!  :)  A local favorite and a great spot to try if you are visiting Santa Barbara.  One if my fav restaurants in SB!

Covid 19 update For April 27, 2020 . I called on Sunday, they were answering the phone. He said chiliquiles would be on the special Monday. So I made the trip . Traffic was light from LA to Santa Barbara but heavier than I expected. At the taco place there was a line, but only 4 people and we were social distancing. Inside the cashier was taking to-go orders over the phone in addition to taking our orders. As far as parking went, there were 2 open spaces in their tiny 3 car lot, but I parked on Milpas.     After ordering I went to what use to be their dining area to wait, there were  6 or 7 chairs placed in a semi circle and since there was only 4 of us it was easy to keep distance.      The tiny neutral gender restroom was unlocked and I washed my hands 3 times while I was there.      The take out came beautifully packed in clean crisp containers. There is no self serve salsa bar at this time but the fellow at the window let me pick from pre- packed. He let me take as many as I wanted and yes, it tasted just like their usual pico de gallo. I love that stuff.       So the message from the cashier was 'we are all healthy '. When I told the fellow at the pick up window that I hoped they make it through this challenge the customer behind me said 'they will, he owns the building.

Nice date night spot, but entree was lacking.  Ambiance is very nice. Servers were very helpful as well. We started with an artichoke salad special which was very good. For my entree I had the carbonara. Right off the bat I was disappointed with the portion size. As I started eating I noticed there was hardly any sauce. Tasted like a tad of butter with maybe some white wine added, very thin. Not like carbonara I've had before. There was also only two tiny specs of bacon/prosciutto. After dinner we went to get food elsewhere because we were still hungry. Looked at other Yelp pictures of what I ordered. Mine didn't look as good as those pictures. Maybe they had an off night, but I don't want to stress about how my meal will come out.

I was looking for good places to eat while in SB this weekend and ran across Dave's Dogs on Yelp...  Holy Cow.. the dogs here are so good!  I got a corn dog and Dave Dog with Chicken Sausage and a side of Onion Rings.  I'm embarrassed to say that I finished both on my own... because they provide very generous portions.  If you are looking for a quick bite that you will remember, check this place out.  I ordered ahead and I'm glad that I did, because the line got pretty long after I got there.
Delicious dinners in the outdoor parklet. Lamb chops were prepared perfectly with red wine sauce. Roast chicken was moist and savory. Excellent wine pairings by the glass. Very good value.

Phenomenal happy hour and best waiters and bartenders around. However I would never go when it's not happy hour as everything else is extremely overpriced but what do you expect when you are paying for the view with a bunch of tourists.

A breakfast joint and serves pizza as the owner says, they have a variety of different breakfasts ranging from Shakasuka to Pho along with pizza and burgers. I came looking for a good bowl of pho as Noodle City Is closed on Sundays and the other reviews intrigued me so I gave this place a shot.    Small mom and pop shop on Victoria St and nothing that stands out but I will say it is a great bowl of pho in SB. I've had a lot of pho and their beef pho was really good! For a breakfast pizza joint, you wouldn't think so, but it's a good surprise. Small is pretty small compared to other joints, but upon finishing up everything alongside the broth, I was full and satisfied. The soup was well seasoned and flavorful. The owner let us know the meat cuts were prime rib and roast beef for their own take, and it comes with a side of bean sprouts, jalapeños, basil, and lime.   Compared to noodle city too, I can taste the quality in comparison, particularly since the beef was so fresh.   I would come here again for pho, or to try their fried spring rolls and banh mi!

This is the only truly beachside dining location that we found in Santa Barbara. Their Moscow mule was the best I've ever had. Service was good with a nice patio. Seems like they might struggle a bit with parties of 10 or more, especially inside where the dining room is small, so highly recommend you call ahead in these cases. The dining room has the strong smell of a seafood restaurant, which didn't bother me but may bother others who don't care for seafood. But that problem can be solved by sitting outside where you can enjoy sunset views and rolling waves.

When you want seafood that is prepared well and delicious, this is a great spot!  Service was quick and attentive, nobody at any table appeared to be forgotten.  When you can't decide which type of Oyster, always opt for a mix. Getting a flavor of each is a nice way to take to edge off your hunger. When you want pasta, buy maybe not an entire bowl, opt for the lobster Mac n cheese. With huge chunks of lobster, you can't go wrong. One of the best I've ever had! Entrees - so hard to pick! I ended up with the macadamia crusted halibut (outstanding) and my companion tried the spicy pasta seafood. Both were good sized portions that could easily allow for leftovers. I cant wait to go back and try more things on the menu. Unfortunately my stomach couldn't take any more.
1000/10! Cheap and high quality food!!! The sauces they have are amazing! The sauce on tacos are just perfect! Sweet, savory, sour, spicy, tangy! Omg so yummy

We had such a wonderful time on our getaway to the Four Seasons Santa Barbara. A beautifully refinished old building that has such charm and character and nowhere feels forced. Subtle details compliment outstanding services!   For someone who has been biased to the Ritz Carlton group, this Four Seasons stood out....it looks like selecting lodging on future trips just got a bit more difficult!

I ended up here after I cancelled my reservation at loquita because my party of two multiplied from two to four and we didn't want to wait until late night to eat. As a good yelper does I searched for other options and found this highly rated spot. At first I was hesitant because the menu was so limited, but every single thing we had was phenomenal. Small town Santa Barbara struggles with our restaurant options and I was happy to discover this place!  Every dish is beautifully presented. The flavors are fresh. I highly recommend the pasta dish, but for reals come here with a friend and get a little bit of everything. I'm pretty sure we ordered over half the menu and were not disappointed with anything.  The inside of the restaurant had a clean minimal vibe. And it's the place that could be well suited for a casual after work hangout or a fun date night. Be aware this place is 21 and over so do not bring the bebes. The service was on point for the vibe and our servers made great drink suggestions. I'll definitely be back when I'm feeling fine and fancy.

We ordered coffee here almost every morning! I ordered a vanilla latte with oat milk and my husband got a drip coffee. For breakfast, I tried both the yogurt bowl and the breakfast sandwich. The sandwich was exactly what I was hoping for it to be. I recommend substituting the bread for an everything bagel. Lots of nice open seating and dogs are welcome!!

Our first time trying Luna Grill. Did takeout through GrubHub. It took over an hour for our order to be ready. We waited at the restaurant for an extra 20-30 minutes after our food was scheduled to be ready until it actually came out. During that time the staff asked what we ordered and then said that it would be 'right out.' No one apologized for the delay, offered a complimentary anything, etc.  The food was just so-so. The salad we paid extra for (family size) came with a whopping 4 olives and a few crumbles of feta. It looked like it was thrown together from across the room.  Definitely not satisfied or impressed. Wouldn't recommend this Luna Grill. Many other customers were in and out super quickly as we waited. Maybe it was just our order that was slow?

Been craving some Birria tacos for the past year since the dish has received a ton of attention on social media food blogs. Finally a local spot and the dish deserves all the attention. It's perfect. Juicy and flavorful. Yona crushes it. Excellent addition on state st. Keep slinging the Birria and I'll keep coming.

Love the habit. Yuuum. Their veggie burgers aren't that great but their fries and chicken sandwiches are so good. I normally don't splurge on them but their milkshakes are incredible. For some reason their mayo is exceptionally good too?

Some of the most enjoyable Indian food I have ever had.  Eat the Uni fried rice appetizer first- delicate and delicious.  Cucumber salad was generous and tasty.  Loved the papdum naan plate with six or seven sauces.  The chicken tikka is enough to share and the tikka quail was tender.  Delicious!

Delicious pasta and food, with big portions! The garlic rolls are amazing, careful not to fill up on them before your meal! Nice casual atmosphere, good service.  Good prices for the portions you get! Nice casual place

I am so sad. This restaurant had such high marks 6 months ago. Even 4 months ago when we last enjoyed a terrific meal. In the last 4 weeks we have been back twice. The last time (and probably the last time) was a Sunday evening a few weeks ago. Our appetizers were overcooked, and took forever. The wines we ordered off the list. - the first 5- were not available and the rest of the list looked like every other list in santa barbara: short-sighted and disappointing. The music was so incredibly loud -out of bounds loud- and the kitchen staff were in a foul, aggressive mood that made our table and many of the tables around us feel very uncomfortable. We paid our bill, and bowed out leaving most of our entrees untouched. What had started out as something potentially awesome has dwindled exponentially.
Totally worth the line any day of the week. Their GF crackers and vegan truffle cream cheese are must grabs for any picnics

We had such a great time here! Loved Paradise Cafe so much and was so bummed when they closed. But La Paloma Cafe is a welcome replacement. There's a lower and upper patio and both are beautiful. String lights, plenty of heaters, palm trees, and a beautiful ambiance. The prices can't be beat (an entire half chicken- enough for 2 people for $20?) and our server, Amanda was so sweet, attentive, and knowledgable. Some highlights  - Hibiscus margaritas (definitely opt for the pitcher) - Santa Barbara Mission Chicken  - Santa Maria Tri - Tip - La Paloma Salad  This spot is definitely a new favorite and we can't wait to return.

We really, really wanted to like this place.  Like, really.  It's so cute, the ambiance and service are great and the coffee was really good.  BUT, the food was WAY too expensive and not all that great.  I can understand paying a ton for food, IF it's good.  This place was just disappointing.  However, I would highly suggest you order the yogurt and granola if you come here.  They gave me a small sample at the end of my meal, and it was delicious, and I wished that I had ordered that instead.

If I could give them zero stars, I would. Came here for Julie's 21st birthday. Out of all the places we could have gone in Santa Barbara, we unfortunately chose this place, which we've been to many times. We will no longer go here.  First off, it took them 35 minutes to seat us (group of 4) for sushi. They wouldn't let us sit at one of the 10 empty hibachi tables, because they were hibachi only. This should have been the first warning sign. Eventually, since it was getting late and every other restaurant was practically closed, we pleaded them to let us sit at a hibachi table. They did.  Anyway, we ordered drinks, food, and spent a lot of money. We didn't get a roll we had ordered, but they still tried to charge us for it. Despite it being clear that it was Julie's birthday, they did not bring out a cake like they did for other people, which is fine but annoying. Overall, the service was very bad, and the waiter made it clear she didn't care.  They then put a mandatory 18% gratuity charge on our bill! Wtf?!   Steer clear of this place.

Happy we can still enjoy togo food these days. Salmon salad was good, I was happy with the portion size. Only downside was that I requested one passion fruit and one balsamic because the passion fruit is pretty sweet, unfortunately both dressings were passion fruit. Overall, happy with the experience.

I loved Bibi Ji! We got the tasting menu which is $50 a person. Each dish was great, the cauliflower appetizer was a definite stand out. The entree consisted of naan bread and about 8 different curries and sauces - this was so filling and yummy. The one thing I was unhappy about was that the uni and the oysters weren't apart of the tasting menu. Would have loved to try them! But will come back in order to do so.

We love Blue Owl and heard ownership may change so pray the menu doesn't. We just ordered our favorite Thai basil burger with egg and tried on Blue Owl website but that item wasn't available ( except for late-night item which must be a much smaller version ). Had to go with Yelp to order instead as we wanted this delicious burger. Hope the menu adjusts on the website as we've raved about it to friends. Wishing Blue Owl continued success.

Terrible customer service. I tried to use a coupon posted on their Instagram page and the cashier was going to honor it but another employee (she wears very heavy/cakey makeup) told the cashier not to honor my coupon because it wasn't a physical coupon. I was dumbfounded because here I was spending almost $40 on dinner for me and a friend and you really can't honor a $4 coupon?? So many questions... Why would you post a coupon on social media that won't be honored? Why be so rude about not honoring it?   I posted about this experience on their Instagram and my comment got deleted and I received several messages about my comment being deleted because my comment was 'hateful'. Ummm okay. Apparently saying 'cakey makeup' is hateful but making a bad customer service experience worse by sending unsolicited rants is okay.   Food is overpriced and not great.

Yoichi ' s is the first 'destination' restaurant to appear in Santa Barbara in many years. It's worth driving from LA or SLO to dine in this small spare space with its wonderful kaiseki menu. It's not clear that a place this sophisticated and refined can survive in a town like SB, but it deserves to do so. The décor and the wine list still need some work, but the food is  exquisite: the four of us shared the various entrées  (there are only a few choices to be made with this set menu) and found them uniformly excellent,  with the Hokkaido king crab particularly memorable. Although it isn't cheap by local standards, it is a lot of food (consisting of small,  light, carefully composed dishes) and well worth the money. We'll be back!  01/28/18 Have returned to Yoichi's several times since my first review and it has remained  absolutely consistent and absolutely outstanding. The menu on this most recent visit included a pickled cherry blossom from Japan with a marvelous cherry-fruit taste, and a whole thumbnail-sized abalone liver, among many other wonders.

I just about died and went to Heaven.  This was our first sit-down meal in almost a year (thanks Covid!).  But it was well worth the wait.  The service was impeccable (Thank Louie) and Chefs Jeremy and Louie are killing it!  We were there for Valentine'S Day and got the Prix Fixe menu.  The ceviche melted in our mouth, and the squash blossom masa chicken tamales and trI-tip were to die for.  Literally every part of the meal was Incredible.   Get the Brussels Sprouts, that is all I have to say.  They are AMAZING.
Perfect to go quarantine food. Easy to call in ahead of time and pick up...  sweetheart roll & saki....sooo good

Best tacos we encountered in SB and maybe top 3 tacos ever!  Location: Easy to find off a main street. Don't mind the batting cages, or better yet, embrace them and get some practice in!   Ambiance: This place has plenty of outdoor seating and their service is quick. Very casual.  Food: My bf and I both did the triple play. I ordered the battered cod, carne asada, and grilled shrimp tacos. All of them were amazing. My bf ordered the battered cod, the grilled shrimp and crispy shrimp tacos. I will admit, the crispy shrimp tacos are really good.  Price: Such a good deal for pretty good sized tacos! Less than $10 for 3!  Would I return? Most def!

Yikes. I used to love it here. The Brussel sprouts were soggy and tasted like Chinese food. I love Chinese food but these were not good. The fish was bland and overpriced. Service was good but did not even ask why our food was barely touched. Just cleared the plates. Tried dessert and the spice in the olive oil cake was too overpowering. We didn't eat that either. Manhattans were very good but very small. Very expensive for this level of refinement. Second try post covid and not going back for a while after this.

Delicious tacos! There are plenty of good Mexican restaurants in the area but La Super-Rica's strengths are there taco specials which are often piled high with a combination of ingredients born of their genius.   My husband and I shared the #7 Tri-tip with grilled peppers and onions with bacon on top of 3 tortillas, the #16 Super-Rica Especial, and lastly, our feeble attempt at a healthy veggie option, the Tamal de Verduras, a delicious vegetable tamale.  The portions turned out to be so generous that we were glad that we didn't order 2 items per person. I think if you opt for the tacos without all the fixings which are the ones typically ranging under $3, you may need a second item. But these special dishes were heaped high with toppings and we struggled to finish them.   The #7 was a solid option, the excellent trip-tip cooked with the peppers and onions was smoky and delicious although a tad tough, and the Tamal de Verduras was the perfect pouch of homey goodness sitting on top of the husk it cooked in, drenched in a cream sauce which I need the recipe for right now. But the star was the #16. True to its name, it is really their specialty. The pasilla chile is charred at the edges and oozing all over with cheese with tender and nicely marinated pork pieces, all sitting on top of their housemade corn tortillas. Oh, and how could I forget, we ordered one horchata to share and I immediately regretted not getting my own. This was the real thing, generous in cinnamon and not watery in the slightest with a little bit of that graininess that comes from the rice.   They have a nice salsa bar next to the pick-up window where you may ladle as much salsa as you please, but if you want limes, I believe you would have to ask and when picking up your food at the window once your number is called would be a good time to do that.   Now for the details, parking is difficult with their teeny tiny lot only capable of holding 3 cars and not comfortably. You would also need the parking skills of a valet driver because once you park head-in, you'll have to reverse your way out of the lot, there's just no way around it. I also noticed some people sneakily double-parked in an attempt to do take-out orders but stuck around quite a while so if you're parked in the lot and want to leave, you may end up having to go hunting around for the owner of the car blocking your only way out. Aside from this miniature lot, your only option is street-parking on busy Milpas St. or in the residential areas which, much like LA, has street cleaning days during which at certain hours of the day, no car is permitted to park under threat of being towed. So do yourself a favor and watch for those signs.   As for the atmosphere, it's really not bad at all. It's no Los Agaves, but their teal color scheme is cheerful, and the odd setup of a tarp serving instead of a roof in the dining area lets soft sunlight filter into the room casting natural but gentler sunlight into the area. It gives the feel of being out on a patio without all the discomforts. A really nice touch they added was placing machines that blow strong gusts of wind at the ceiling of each door threshold so as to blow away flying insects from coming in. This is so thoughtful because the last thing you want to do is eat while simultaneously shooing away flies trying to land on your food and do whatever disgusting thing they do. To me, they always look like they're plotting some evil rubbing their front legs together like some gross minuscule many-eyed villain. Glad to have fewer of those around while eating.  When I went, the line wasn't bad but true to many previous reviewers of this restaurant, there was definitely a line so prepare to do some amount of waiting. They do keep the line moving fairly quickly though but maybe try to go during off-hours.   Lastly, many have mentioned this already but they are definitely CASH ONLY. So stop by an ATM on the way.

You wouldn't think a restaurant called '4 eggs and pizza' would have great pho, but  they most certainly do. I had the beef pho and spring rolls, both of which were fantastic. And as the other reviewers mentioned, they use quality beef. The spring rolls were crispy yet fluffy. Excited to try the bahn mi next time
We love to come here for the Happy hour and, of course the phenomenal view of the harbor. Get the salmon tacos, they are quite delicious and are ample enough to share.
My favorite Mexican restaurant in Santa Barbara! The fish tacos are incredible, I always get one of each!

my boss took me here for the first time a while back and it was really yummy and their salsa was on pointt. get the spicier one on the side if you like spicy salsa. The enchiladas are really good and ugh the rice is super good as well. I love the staff because they are really friendly and my boss is a frequent customer there. The tacos are good and it really has a nice mexican vibe on the inside which i appreciate. Come hereeeee if you cannn

Natural Cafe is a chain of restaurants around the Central Coast of CA. Each one I went to was consistently great.  They have a full menu with sandwiches, soups, desserts, large salads with fresh ingredients and well prepared protein choices (fish, chicken, etc.).  To Go or Eat In options are available too.  You order at the counter and are given a number. Then get your own drink from the beverage counter, pick a seat and in short time your order will be delivered to your table.  The photos here are from the Santa Barbara location on Hitchcock Way.

Quaint, cozy little sushi spot hidden in a corner of Montecito. I would say it's the best sushi in the Santa Barbara area for sure. I usually get sashimi so just raw fish with no rice or seaweed, that's the best to really taste the quality and freshness of their fish and their fish is truly fresh. I feel like I am tasting the ocean in my mouth, in the best way possible. And their house sake is great! Goes well with the sashimi I usually get.
Barbacoa and asada tacos were good Steak and shrimp fajitas okay Chicken strips and cheese quesadilla was a hit with kids Chips could be fresher, but salsa bar was nice and spicy

We got to Santa Bárbara way too early on a Sunday morning. We craved mimosas and we walked over to the restaurant and we were so sad because it wasn't open yet. We were at a crossroads, should we wait for the place to open or should we leave? (We were 45 minutes early) We decided to go for a long walk. We came back and it was finally open we were tired , thirsty and hungry. The mimosas were very refreshing and perfect for a warm sunny day. My husband ordered his favorite Chile relleno, I thought it was weird because it was morning but he said it's already 11am lol. I ordered the chilaquiles. They were honestly the best chilaquiles I've ever had. This is saying a lot since my mom's are amazing. We have been here many times and this time was memorable and delicious.

I debated about this review for quite awhile, to be honest. I don't necessarily want to speak negatively because I do like their food...but when the experience is repeated and repeated and you finally have had enough...well, that's the point of Yelp right? So here we go!  THE SERVICE HERE IS TERRIBLE! I'm sorry but EVERYTIME I come to this restaurant, our servers are somewhat rude. They seem to always be out of SOMETHING whether it be an entree item or just basic ingredients (like granola or quinoa). They never (and I mean never) have coffee available - its always an 'Americano' or they would have to brew a fresh pot....which she makes seem to be such a pain in the ass. I don't understand it...everywhere else seems to brew their coffee beforehand just fine.   The kicker was the other night where the restaurant was pretty empty, they asked if we had a reservation (?), we said no - the made us wait 10 minutes (keep in mind, there was 2 of us) and then sat us. We waited for 15 more minutes, no water, no server! We finally just said 'f-it' and left and went to Sojouner.  Heres the thing...their food is good. Its not GREAT! But because its the only Vegan restaurant in town they can get away with being expensive and having shitty service. I just don't think that's right. Marys Secret Garden in Ventura is a little pricey but the service is AMAZING and the food is absolutely incredible.   So ya...Adama. I've given you so many chances...I'm done. We're breaking up.  Sorry.
Just ate lunch there today.  The steak with the sauce is amazing.  They didn't rush us out either.

Exceptional dining experience from the ambiance to service to food. Full 5 stars from the whole family.  Food: 5 Atmosphere: 5 Service: 5  Cost to worth it rating: 3 (on the spendier side but fantastic quality) Opentableable: not for dinner, only lunch, but can email them   Return worthiness: yes !  Eve award: best outdoor dining   Highlights: - Ambiance: beautiful white lights on trees with a backdrop of the old house and Spanish guitar playing pop songs. I feared since it was a fancy hotel it might be a bit formal and stuffy, but it was pleasant and fun. Everyone was having a wonderful time. - Chicken tortilla soup: the menu noted it as 'famous' so I had to try it. I'm so glad I did as it was one of the best I've had. Deep red color with bits of chicken, cheddar cheese, and avocado hidden underneath with visible colorful festive red, green, and yellow crispy tortilla strips. 5 stars - Chilean sea bass: beautiful piece of fish with tender pieces which easily fell off with a slight push of my fork. Fresh vegetables including white turnips, cauliflower, and beets in a miso sauce elevated the dish even further. 5 stars - Steak Diane: very old school with a tableside flambé fire show my grandfather would have loved. 2 decent sized pieces of perfectly medium rare cooked filet was a great deal for the price. Deep brown sauce and just eat with a spoon Robuchon silky masked potatoes were worthy supporting co-stars. 5 stars  - Chocolate soufflé: it's been forever since I've had one of these and it was spot on. Tall, fluffy, and light but with just enough chocolate and sugar. Chocolate sauce and espresso gelato paired well. 5 stars  - Service: impeccable. Very professional and the water guy was our MVP always keeping us topped off after a warm day. 5 stars - Wines by the glass: I was super excited to see some of my fave French champagnes offered by the glass. Also got another still varietal I don't usually see which was a nice pairing with my entree.  - Kids menu: we didn't expect one as I know it's not a family restaurant per se, but my 10 year old is used to nicer restaurants. He didn't end up ordering anything off of it but it's nice to have the option.   Lowlights: - Finding it: map apps led us astray in the middle of nowhere when putting in both Stonehouse and San Ysidro Ranch. Had to call to get the exact address to use.  Exceeded my expectations, been recommending it to friends ever since, and would love to come back.

Cute little Mexican place in a great setting. Right by the fountain where there are little turtles and coy fish swimming around. Very modernized, somewhat health conscious take on Mexican plates. I got the crab and shrimp enchiladas and they were amazing! The guacamole is good, as well as their margaritas which you can choose with a flavor to accompany them. Loved our waiter Sparky, he was very friendly, upbeat and helpful. Would definitely come back!

An upscale version of Los Agaves, this is a great date night restaurant with amazing ceviche and great drinks. It is a smaller restaurant and can get crowded but it is worth the wait. You can sit outdoors and people watch or enjoy the ambience and beautiful decor indoors.

Been here twice and both times I ordered the halibut ceviche!  The ceviche was great on a hot day and the fish was very fresh. In a nutshell, fresh and tasty food at reasonably prices! Good service too!  You start off by waiting in line (both times the lines were out the door), placing your order, getting a number and then find the line of people waiting to be seated.  Shortly after being seated they bring you a basket of warm homemade tortilla chips (complimentary)!  You can go over to their salsa station to get a variety of salsas to dip the chips in!  Their steak and the Anaheim peppers are tasty too!  There is a small parking lot behind the restaurant or you can try street parking in the residential area a block behind the parking lot.

Great stay.  Very nice location, across from the beach, with breathtaking ocean view from the room.  The service was satisfactory and the manager was accommodating.  The hotel is about 1 mile ( a good 20 minutes) walk to downtown Santa Barbara, Stearns Wharf.  I truly loved the walk (or two, or three) to downtown each day during my stay since I got to walk along side the calming ocean so I did not mind staying 1 mile from downtown.    My stay was very satisfying, so thumb up for Hyatt Santa Barbara.

This place is pretty freaking good.  I got the burger and it must have weighed 5 pounds.  So good.  I give it 4 stars because the service was really slow and took forever and a glass of iced tea was $4!!   I mean come on.  I'll be back for sure.

Probably one of favorite restaurants in SB. Everything on the menu is delicious, so order what you like! I love the kabobs atm but the halibut and allll the steaks are also high up there! The salad bar is hands down my favorite, WAP the marinated cabbage and fresh baked bread. Droooool The drinks are poured to purrrfection and the lights are nice and  dim,  ya know, so as the heighten the senses and enhance the flavors. I go alone sometimes.. but it's special spot for a date or family or even business meetings!   10/10 would go again and again!

Chef Ramon blends unique and traditional flavors to create truly one-of-a-kind delicious dishes! I have been a fan since the pop-up sunday days two years ago and I am so happy that corazon now has a permanent home in the public market!

Check out that massive burger. But for brunch, try those shrimp and grits! Don't forget the Sunday bottomless mimosas!  The Burger looked phenomenal with those tempura fried onion rings....so big that it barely fit in my friends mouth!  I had shrimp 'n' grits, with a wonderfully poached egg on top. Let me repeat....a GREAT poached egg (i'm picky about them).  Although we went on Saturday, the staff was happy to offer us the usually Sunday bottomless mimosas! YAY for saturday drinking!  I also ended up purchasing a whole loaf of Cranberry Almond Ginger Bread. No not gingerbread, but a loaf of superb fresh baked bread! I brought it back to the family, and they loved every slice during the California Christmas weekend (SOOOO not that COLD)  It isn't cheap, but definitely worth the indulgence once in a while.

Another great meal at Roy! Had the pork tenderloin with cranberry sauce, vegetables, and his super yummy potato cakes ... soup & salad included ... for $20!!! Everything was excellent!! Love this place!

This place never disappoints, and we've added it to our usually dining out rotation. Such a good variety of tapas, even for vegetarians. We always receive great service and the ambiance is so cozy. We love the Some Brie Sauce, Brussels sprouts, shrimp tacos, and sweet potato fries.   We also love that you usually don't need a reservation for this place, unlike their sister restaurant, Milk and Honey (which is also amazing!).

Not the best Chinese food I've had but not the worst either, would give 3/5 on the food. Gave 4 stars though because food was fresh and made to order, delivery time was very reasonable and food was packaged with care for the delivery. Will definitely try other dishes in the future.
Good fresh baked bread friendly service That location isn't the greatest because of parking though

A hidden sushi gem located in the corner of the public market. I came here yesterday to order a blue fin tuna roll and a chicken katsu bowl for my kids. My kids are really picky eaters and have high expectations, and this place completely met it all for them.   The blue fin tuna roll was awesome. The piece of fish in each roll was big!! Sometimes restaurants will put a small piece of fish overcome with a lot of rice, but not this place. My son was very close to licking the bottom of the tray. Super fresh fish!  The chicken katsu bowl was huge and delicious!! It came with miso soup which complimented the meal perfectly. The katsu was fried to perfection. The tonkatsu sauce was the perfect dip for it. It was so big that my son couldn't finish it, so I ended up finishing it. I can confirm how truly delicious it was.  Service was great, and all of the people working there were so sweet. We will definitely be back!!

Best turkish delight shop ever in California. The Lokum are freshly made and have distinctive flavors, unlike the boxed ones you buy at the supermarket. These are very high quality. Loved the rose petal and walnut Lokums. The baklavas are also super delicious: layers of honey, pistachio, chocolate and thin filo dough. Extremely fresh! Its all very expensive but worth treating yourself at least once (boxes go for 30$ minimum).

This place automatically receives 5 stars for being so close to the beach.  It's nice to come here for breakfast after a jog. Coffee in hand, just looking at the beautiful ocean, and the various people along the beach.  The staff here are friendly, and the coffee is good, as well as the food. I've only ordered the pancakes, and the French toast. Both were pretty tasty

This is the place bland goes when it decides to give up and go for mediocre bland.   Let me tell you about my Greek Salad. It had a lot of lettuce (good!) and about three medium chunks of feta cheese. I think it was feta. I might have been styrofoam.   There were six tiny tomatoes (well make that three since they were sliced in half.)   And TWO OLIVES.   Yes. Two olives.  The only reason this place gets two stars is that the manager seemed friendly and suitably embarrassed when explaining that there is a formula they have to follow when making their salads.   That said, I can't honestly believe that Panera at the corporate level is like, 'ONLY GIVE THEM TWO OLIVES.'  So, yeah. Your guess is as good as mine.

Man do I miss Sharkeez.  The best is having a race to see who can finish a fish bowl first.  Just the typical UCSB downtown spot.  If you go a lot and have big groups get in touch with the hostess and they can totally set up happy hour parties where for every 6 people you bring you get a free appetizer sampler platter and drink specials.

I love this spot. It's classic SB and the roastery makes for such a good experience on the nose. They have so many coffees on tap that it's like a full blown wine tasting and free cup comes with every half pound. Not a bad gig. Free wifi too to doesn't hurt. One of the best spots in town to read a book.

I wasn't looking forward to another upscale grocer in this area, but this one blows the others out of the water. Place is immaculate and selection is amazing. Was approached by someone in the beer area, requested if they had a certain beer, he said no but he knows it is available to order and he said he will order it next time...will see if that happens.   Yes it is expensive, but they do have some good specials and the service is amazing.  Please make sure to tell your staff to wear their masks properly though, especially in the meat department handling fresh food where I saw a worker with a mask not covering his nose. Also, a lot of personal conversations between coworkers that probably shouldn't be had in front of customers...particularly by the entrance manning the line.
Every thing was surprisingly good here. If you have to get only one thing, my vote would be the short rib, it was amazing!

The warm sun shining down on my skin. The wind blowing through my hair.  When I first moved here, I wasn't accustomed to the beauty and fresh air that an outdoor mall..ehm..shopping center allows.   I have come to appreciate how pleasant it is to walk around in the open spaces in and out of stores. Shopping is a much better experience here than in indoor malls, which many of us are probably very familiar with.  Walk up and down State St.  Paseo Nuevo is in a great location somewhere in the middle-ish of it all. When you get tired of brand names, Macy's, Nordstrom, etc., there are other independent stores along the street and in cross streets. Or you can just go and eat something.

Oldest restaurant in Santa Barbara, historic place. Dutch gardens is my favorite restaurant in Santa Barbara, everything is amazing. Food is so good and the beer is great. The service is always great and the restaurant is extravagant.

I've had breakfast here once before and I loved it they give very large portion and great for sharing a plate most definitely! But let me tell you if you love a good steak Jills has you covered! This was my first time having dinner here and I was a little worried we might not have got the best service because it was a birthday dinner of 20+ people so sometimes the kitchen and staff may struggle but I was wrong they cooked the steaks to perfection! All of are food was hot and came fairly quickly with no mistakes I was really mind blown how well they took care of such a large party! Jills has the best steak I've had in Santa Barbara! I can't wait to come back and try the sirloin. The ribeye was amazing not to much fat on it just enough to give some juicy flavor to it as to be expected on a ribeye. Hats off the the servers and the chefs all the staff was exceptional! I'm going to recommend everyone of my streak loving friends to Jills!

So paying thirty bucks to get into see a musician doesn't mean you're going to be able to, you know, see the musician, because the layout virtually ensures you can't see the stage.   The spots where you can actually see the musician you've already paid to see? The spots with an actual view of the stage? Those are 'VIP spots.' You have to pay more for those. If they forget to rope them off before the show and you just walk up and stand in them because you don't know their internal rules because, you know, you're (a) not psychic, and (b) you don't fucking work here, you can expect that they'll let you stand there long enough that you absolutely won't get a decent spot elsewhere, then run you off. Even if, say, they haven't sold any of the overpriced VIP tickets, they'll just let the one corner where you could actually see the band you've already paid to see stand empty in the hopes of upselling you by charging you extra for something you thought you already paid for.    Why? Because fuck you, that's why. If you don't start out by paying as much as possible for your ticket, you're a piece of shit and deserve whatever you get.
Great menu, good for kids, the outdoor seating is nice. Full bar & several TV's. BBQ was great, the salmon sandwich was delicious.
A hidden gem in Santa Barbara. The chorizo scramble is to die for and their fresh fruit is on point! Finally a breakfast spot both my fiancé and I can agree on!

This place is great for happy hour!   They have many tasty dishes reasonability priced, however, one thing that needs improvement would be their service... I understand they can get busy and overwhelmed, but that is no excuse.

I come here way too often! I love their salsa bar, it's always so fresh with a great large variety. Hard for me to try new things here since I crave what I had the time before, I'm happy to say I am a regular here! I live the mole enchiladas, pozole, and tostada salad! I highly recommend this place! My favorite Mexican restaurant in santa barbara.

This place is great!  Two for one pizzas?!  Are you kidding?  All I wanted was a simple pepperoni pizza just for me so for my second pizza I got a simple cheese and a side salad. They were hot and delicious. I ate two slices of the pepperoni on the drive home in the car!  I highly recommended this place. The guy working was friendly!  I will be back to try something else the next time!

Came here for a light dinner after we finished at The Painted Cabernet. There is a nice outdoor seating with heaters so it was a very nice ambience. The menu was good and there were good vegetarian options for my daughter. Loved their roasted chickpeas that tied us over until our orders were served.  We ordered the steam mussels, fried calamari and the vegetarian burger. All in all a good choice to end a great day.

CRUNCHY PEANUT BUTTER BURGER!!!  That's why I came, but how can you tell American Ale has AWESOME BURGERS & BREWSKIS?!?  It's practically hidden from the Santa Barbara's State St. where all the foot traffic is.  There's no neon saying 'Eat Bumpin' Burgers HERE!' or gaudy awnings to attract attention, just a simple, inconspicuous front.  The vibe inside is unpretentiously laid back.  It's like where superheroes would hang out & unwind to avoid the masses, after a day of battling despicable villains.  No teles within the sanctum to numb your mind, rather, it's a throwback to simpler times with just brews, burgers, and your buddies.  Fortune shines on me though, cuz I'm backed by yelp ;)  So, FOUND IT!  Counter/Bar service and the chaps runnin' thangs were cool blokes.  There's also tables inside to enjoy the grub and they said 'No Prob!' with bringing kids in at the time we came (5pm).  However, just in case Captain America was actually here (lol!), didn't want my young squires to expose his secret hangout, so we acquired a few items to take on the fly...  *PB & J BURGER:  Hello CRUNCHY PEANUT BUTTER!!!!  Yeah, I got a CRUNCHY peanut butter thang, but to find it on a burger?  That's a rare bird and I search for it like I'm Indiana Jones.  Only the second eatery I've discovered that offers this concoction.  This is much closer to home and SOOO DEEE-LISH!  I asked for extra PB and it came out a drippy, crunchy mess.  Eat your heart out KFC, cuz THIS was TRULY, finger lickin' GOOOOOD!  Oh, and the beef... moist, juicy, AND flavorful. Great burger and combined with garlic fries, nothing but a BIG satisfied grin here! *AMERICAN BURGER:  Yelp check-in deal for this bad boy!  Their signature blend of FRESH ground chuck beef AND pork, TIMES 2!  Add to that, grilled onions and American cheese, what results is one hearty burger!!!  Throw in some garlic fries and my little man SILENTLY devoured this thing. *LENTIL BURGER:  Wifey was pretty impressed with the quality of this veggie burger.  Not as firm as beef, but firm enough to create the sensation of biting into a patty, and a tasty patty at that.   *MAKE'N BACON BURGER:  Whole buncha bacon between the buns!  Ya got bacon, Canadian bacon, Italian bacon, maple bacon jam, with American cheese.  If you love bacon, this is calling your name!  Combined with the same delicious signature hamburger patty, it makes for pretty good eats. *ROASTED TOMATO SOUP & GRILLED CHEESE:  Tomato soup was really quite flavorful, but the grilled cheese was for wimps!  You'd think with the kind of burgers they're crankin' out here, the grilled cheese would also impress, but, that ain't so.  Thin and all bread.  Automatic 5 star vote cuz of the LOVE I got for their PB & J burger, but their other burgers are also noteworthy because of the juicy signature meat mix and quality veggie option.  Friendly service and short wait times just seal the deal!  Just so you know, there is a parking structure across the street in close walking distance.  Couldn't even finish my review, before I picked up a second helping!  Oh YEAH, I'm a confirmed fan! ;)

Hibachi or Sushi?   Do I have to choose only one?  I absolutely love this place!  The food has always been delicious each time I've ventured into this hot spot.  The presentation put on by the chef is an absolute treat...bouncing egg yolks, volcanic onions and juggling spatulas---I mean, really---this does not happen everyday for me!  :0)   Hibachi chicken and shrimp is my favorite with a California roll to share!  Yum!  ;0)  Prices are reasonable and the staff is awesome!  Try it out!  You'll love it!   Reviewed by Yvette L. Brownstone

'Not good' pretty much nails it. Last night I ordered a vegetarian slice and the cashier said 'do you like olives and mushrooms?' The slice came out with cheese, a few mushrooms, and a whole can of olive. I said, 'this isn't vegetable - this is just olives!' And he replied with 'you said you liked olives!' And literally shoo'd me away with his hand. SO rude! But this review probably won't make a difference because people go here late at night when nothing else is open for the rudest, greasiest, most expensive slice in SB!

The food was not great.... even a little too salty and way overprices and there was too much help and not enough yummy  flavor.  They spent too much on decor of what was an authentic spot.   Trendy wasn't needed.  Always a great place for a sunset drink...

This is still one of my favorite happy hours in town. I absolutely love the seared ahi poke with caramelized onions and fish tacos. Only bummer is that everybody else loves this place too so you have to get there pretty early before it gets filled up.

Took a chance on trying something new. The decor is fun, young, bright with reclaimed wood and eclectic artwork. Only about 10 tables and since all were reserved we sat at the bar (only about 12 stools). Porchetta Arusso (hmmm not sure about the spelling) looked good to me. A pork dish wrapped in pork belly and served with sauté spinach. I have to admit, when it was delivered it seemed to be the smallest portion I'd ever seen for $20 but every single bite was worth every penny spent. It was amazing. Tried my friends roasted Vegas...again small portion but the flavor was incredible. Personally I'm tired of restaurants serving up huge portions of fatty meals that make you feel like you got your money's worth. Every bite of what I ate was 'knocked out of the park' in flavor. I highly recommend.

I have tried fajitas in many places but this one was different, the meat was very well marinated, you could taste the true beef. the chicken was also good but not as good as the beef. At first walking in here it felt like it self service, no one directed us to a table, so we just found a table took the order number with us. food did not take long to prepare even tough the place was pretty busy. good place will go again.
