Delicious food; we tried the beet salad, mushroom crostini, bacon wrapped pork tenderloin and the Sea Bass. Impressed with all four dishes. Great flavors and cooked at just the right temp. The ambiance was perfect and the server was fantastic!!

Himalayan Kitchen is one of my go-to restaurants in Santa Barbara.  Some of the other reviews mention how the food doesn't seem fresh or the environment of the restaurant isn't great, but I completely disagree. I have yet to have any instance where the food did not feel freshly prepared to me and the scent of the restaurant is the scent of the kitchen! What do you expect when you're putting in all these amazing spices into your dishes? My favorites are the tofu saag, chicken korma, veggie samosas, and the garlic naan. I would love to hear other suggestions on things I need to try!  The staff has always been really friendly and accommodating when I've dined in, but they are also super flexible with their catering. I've ordered large catering orders from them twice this year and both times they were knowledgable about how much needed to be ordered to feed our group. Further, they were really accommodating to how I wanted the food to be presented (certain number of trays, naan and samosas cut in half, etc.).  Additionally, they didn't require more than a two-day notice, so if you're looking for a restaurant to cater your event with limited time HK is your place!

Such nice people work here, and I love that the matcha lattes are made with real matcha powder with no sugar added! I get it with almond milk, and it is delicious  They have a great selection if loose leaf teas that are great as well!

I accidentally found this place trying to get to Hook & Press next door & I am so glad I did! We were on our way out of SB and I took a dozen empanadas ($45) to go. I chose half chicken and half Gaucho (shredded beef).   That same day I ate one hours later cold and it was delicious with the Chimichurri sauce they give you. But the next day I heated them up per their instructions in the oven and they tasted like they were just made. It was flaky, buttery, and they don't skimp out on the filling of delicious shredded beef with seasoning and potatoes.   I've had Argentinean empanadas in Spain and these were better. I actually liked them more than the ones from Portos in LA. They are pricey but my family was super happy to have this treat from SB so it makes a great gift!

I had been wanting to try this place for months (even since I saw the gorgeous photos of the interior on Yelp!).  The location is great and the decor is fantastic, but the charm ends there, unfortunately.  I waited at the bar for drinks while the bartender continued to serve everyone around me except me.  We made eye contact several times and then he looked over my head and just took orders from other people.  I ended up not getting a drink.  I tried my friend's drink (the vodka one) and it was entirely too sweet.  Given my inability to get served and the quality of her drink, we left after that.    Also strange: there are only 4 or 5 drinks on their menu, even though they have a massive bar.  I saw a photo of the menu on Yelp from a year ago, but the current menu has far fewer drinks.  Test Pilot (across the street) is MUCH better and worth the wait.
Last night I had the birria and it was so good! The drinks were also good. The service was great, they're polite and very attentive . I'll definitely be back.
Pretty good food and good selection of beer. Laid back service, especially once the food service stops.

I've never eaten Cajun food before, so my opinion is likely to carry little truck with people from Cajun country.   However, I liked the place!  We arrived around 11:30 a.m. on Sunday.  It was packed out and we had to wait for about 15 minutes to get a table.  My Jambalaya was full of flavor!  Spicy hot that made my brow quite warm.  Everyone else finished their tame breakfasts and watched me savor the last bites of my Jambalaya.  My only complaint with the Jambalaya is that they put MSG in it.  I know this from the 'dry mouth' that later comes after the meal.  Shame! Shame! Shame! Cajun Kitchen Cafe!  While a small sin, why do that sort of thing when your food is so good?  I'll certainly come back for that Jambalaya and say, 'No MSG' to see if that works.  Most cafes like this one have crappy coffee.  I took a taste of Sofie's coffee.  Solid!  Very solid.  I ordered a cup of my own.  You should to.  They have great coffee.  These people work very hard and do an excellent job of making good food.  I love going to a restaurant and seeing everyone hustling like a team to make everyone happy.  Want to see what a well oiled machine looks like going at full throttle?  Visit Cajun at rush hour.
Great place!!! Gets kinda packed but go on a mellow night and it's great! Especially the cozy upstairs!! Love it!

Renato and his culinary team are phenomenal.  Our business group of 20 devoured the ethereal, flourless gnocchi, eggplant parm., beef meatballs, plus delicious salmon on puff pastry, melon and proscuitto appetizers.  All scrumptious, beautifully presented and graciously catered on time for our holiday 2019 party.  The dessert array was as stunning as tasty, each of the 29 little gems, e.g., mini cannolis, were whisked away, supplemented with vanilla and dark chocolate gelato.  Applause, appreciation and recognition from all of us in Santa Barbara!   Grazie, 42, for the third annual event! Sharon, The Cat House Hotel(R)

I'm going to admit it, I devoured my dog before taking pics but that should tell you just how good these babies are!  These aren't your average Oscar Myer weiners my friends, these are gourmet dogs done to the max.  I opted for the SB style hot dog that had fresh avocado slices, OMG that sucker was awesome, and my buddy Jim had the Western Dog that had oodles of bacon garnished on top with a BBQ sauce, it looked amazing and for someone who said he wasn't hungry it was gone pretty fast.  The guys running this stand are just as awesome, super friendly and fun to converse with, don't be shy start a convo with them, they like feedback.  Grab a beer at the market, head on over and get a gourmet dog and have a few laughs, heck you might even find yourself posing for pics with the owners.  On a side note I'm pretty sure I'm still a bit drunk after all the festivities last night. Lol

Had a great meal here.  Ordered the Tonkatsu, not spicy, with chicken and it was awesome.  Our server was super friendly, helpful with the expansive menu, the meal came out fast. They get super busy dinner time on the weekends so either go or order out early to avoid the 1.5 hour wait.

Disappointment, much? :(  1) They don't even have a cooking license, so they only 'heat' pre-made items. No cooking allowed, period. Everything's just refrigerated   2) Wake n bacon : not worth the price. Yummy, but not worth it.    3) Crabmelt : Super fishy tasting, def not quality ingredients.     Especially after learning that they don't cook any of their items in-house, it was a huge turn off.  Tasted very un-fresh and wasn't pleasant.

Given the current situation, a little notice: I have not actually been to the brick and mortar location, and have only done delivery, but this is a review based on their food. Every time I have ordered from them everything has been well packed and delicious and their prices are reasonable. I usually order a breakfast sandwich with their seasoned potatoes while my partner has tried a variety of their dishes and every time, it's excellent. They also seem to be very quick and prompt with service. Highly recommend for a simple and straightforward breakfast bite.

While I thought that this place was not bad, I did not find anything extremely noteworthy. I ordered the egg scramble with chorizo. It tasted over salted, but it could have just been the chorizo.  Pricing wise, I thought it was towards the more expensive side for what you get. For example, I was told that upgrading from regular pancakes to blueberry pancakes would be roughly +$2.  However, I must say that service is amazing. I think that management should just reconsider some of their menu and pricing options.

So two gift cards and apologies later and I am updating my review. It's really not fair to ding a place based on one person. It's always great to see a business that cares about their customers and goes out of their way to correct the mistake.

I came here with my family for my graduation dinner and we absolutely LOVED it! We were a bit early for our reservation but they were able to seat us immediately which was awesome. Literally everything we tried we loved. We started with 2 rounds of crab cakes, fried calamari, and pork belly & scallops which were very good. For the main course we ordered different things but we adored them as well. We ordered the salmon, filet mignon, mahi mahi among others things. We then topped off the night with chocolate martinis and key lime pie!   If you want a taste of quality seafood in a true Santa Barbara atmosphere, go here! You won't regret it.

Best Sushi in town!  All the specials are 'to die for'. The halibut carpaccio is the bomb and one of my favorites, and the Red Dragon... Tiger Roll... Mexican roll..Dang.. I guess they are all my favorites!  My son is a 'sushi snob' and found this place while he was attending UCSB. He and his housemates would try to eat here once a week.  While we were visiting..my wife and Tried this place for the first time... and now we are hooked!  Reminds of of a small sushi bar in Tokyo that we visited in 2019.  We love to sit at the sushi bar, enjoy the  bottles of ORIÓN BEER and talk to the chefs... it's a blast... and if you toast a cold Orión or Sake... to the chefs...  the whole place lights up with smiles!  If you want the best sushi..go to Sun Sushi! Buy some Orión Beer and bring on the laughter and smiles and great sushi.   We love this place! 5 stars!!! Every time! Arigato chefs!

We were well attended to by the excellent serving staff - they were spot on! The restaurant was beautiful and cozy even on this chilly day where we sat outside. The bread, wine, crab cakes, onion soup, and dessert were very nice.  However, the main courses were unremarkable, especially the pork dish which was bland. The gratin potatoes on the side were so crunchy-undercooked that, even with a knife, it was difficult to shave off a piece of the tower for a taste.  We both felt that we, with our average cooking skills, could have made this better at home.  We left feeling disappointed and won't likely return. [Date of visit 1.19.2018]
Great sandwiches and really cool genuine Italian goodies everywhere. I do all my Christmas party shopping here.

I have been to Derfs a couple of times. Each time we come, it is very mediocre. The service is pretty slow and the food is average-nothing special. The workers are usually friendly and nice so that's a plus.  They do have a spacious patio that allows dogs for all dog lovers out there.

Really enjoy the food but in a couple visits have been served our food 'cool' not  even warm. Think Convivo needs to make changes so hot dishes get to the table warm. On one visit a hot dish arrived cool twice in a row. Just not acceptable for this caliber of dining.

I loved this place. Easy to get a table in the shade, nice counter guy with good recommendations. All vegetarian. The focaccia with garlic infused balsamic had the best flavor and was warm...yum. Yoga pants salad had a light but very flavorful dressing with pickled cauliflower and lots of goodies inside. Sea salt chocolate chip cookies were huge and a good deal for 4 for $10. Peach burrata with pest and corn were more subtle but I really liked this too. Overall can't wait to try other menu items. Will definitely be back
Hot damn amazing quality for a place you might imagine is a tourist trap. Fresh, local, friendly, good.

I am a little confused by bridgettes 'review' below- Brewhouse has no bouncers, no outside bar, and no pizza on the menu!  maybe she's thinking about the Neighborhood next door- or some place in Redding- wherever that is!

Wow Wow!!  What a beautiful place to eat on the beach in beautiful Santa Barbara!! The food and drinks were amazing and the ambiance as well . There were 7 of us for dinner and Nicole was our server and she did a fantastic job ! We closed the place down as we were the last to leave cause we were having so much fun! The decor is beautiful inside and you can sit outside in patio with heaters too. Ryan the Manager chatted with us a bit and he was nice as well!  Cant wait to visit again soon for lunch & dinner !!
Cancelled my DoorDash order without reason after waiting 45 minutes! All their postings say they are open and their phone just kept ringing and ringing. ZERO stars if I could.
I have eaten here a couple of times now. I am not impressed with the burger, onion rings, or the chicken sandwich. I will avoid coming here again.

Arrived a little early for dinner. Seated at a tiny table less than one foot from tables on either side. Asked to move to a corner table fa4 apart from other tables. Was told that was saved for larger parties yet they had seated one woman there earlier and seated 2 people at same size table next to the one we wanted.  This was not a great start for a restaurant that has sister restaurants all over so cal that we love.  Asked waiter Tristen if we could move;  he had to ask hostess; were finally told we were welcome to move ourselves - no assistance.  Waiter was doubling as bartender.   Told him we were in no hurry. After 45 min told bus boy George ready to order. Made mistake of ordering soup salad entrees at same time. Husbands soup delivered but no salad for me.  Informed George.  Few minutes later entrees delivered.  George said salad on way and mixup cause we moved. Not true ordered from table moved to. Told him to cancel salad as already eating entree.  What a shame.  Awesome view of harbor and boats.

I love Don Paco the best, but when they're not there, for convenience I go to Super Cuca's. The people are very friendly, they're efficient, and they have lots of seating, even though I always get it to-go. The salsa has gotten weaker over the years, but it's still good. The portions are big, so I can make 2 meals out of a burrito and save money!

This place is fantastic! The location is perfect for walking from state street and it's a little hidden which makes it perfect for not being too touristy. The food is bomb, and the prices are super reasonable for a Japanese restaurant. The garlic edamame was sooooo delicious!

Coolest place ever! Hard to find because its a window in a wall, quite literally. Look for velvet jones and you'll see it.  Super friendly customer service, really sociable. ULTIMATE comfort/ drunchies food. One large is enough for two people. I highly recommend all the toppings. SO good.

This was a first--I've never been to a shop dedicated to Turkish delight! They have loads of options and they're all really beautiful. I'd rather look at them than eat them, though...they're not my jam. It was fun to sample a variety, though, and try something different.
The pasta dishes that I've had here weren't very good but their kale salad with salmon is great and have been back several times just for that.
Super easy to order online and pick a time to pick up your order. Also we got $5 off cause it was our first time ordering online from them. Food was amazing!!

Excellent Sicilian pizza & cocktails in a casual environment.  My favorite pizza is the Diavola with salami & olives and the olio martini which comes with a splash of grapefruit, basil, and drizzle of olive oil.  They have great appetizers and salads, cheese and charcuterie too.  Tables are a bit close together and the restaurant gets warm in the summer so sitting outside on the patio is key.  This is one of my favorite quick, casual date night spots.
Food just eh, but the view of the harbor is entertaining. Tourist place, and not crowded. Not a local fav.

Came here to hang out with a friend before dinner on a Saturday night.  Usually, I would order an Iced Chai Latte but that night I wanted a hot one. Bad idea. The drink was a bit watered down for my taste. Somehow I wasn't able to taste the Chai tea and was a bit disappointed but didn't ask them to re-do my order =/.  The staff were friendly. The place was cleaned and neat. The restrooms are located outside, behind the store so I got a bit lost because my friend gave me the wrong directions.....failed.  Other than that, this place has a nice atmosphere. It's a good place to hang out with friends and catch up over a cup of coffee/tea. =]

Really fun little spot right by the ocean. The service was great and the wagyu gyoza was delicious! My sushi was a little fishy, perhaps I got a bad batch, but the fire cracker shrimp was delicious. I would totally come back and try something else!

I've always loved this spot, but it would've been nice if they had called out my name over the microphone as I stood there for 20 minutes beyond the pick up time waiting for my order. I had ordered online, and I figured my name would be called when they finished it as names were called for people who ordered in person. By the time I walked in to check on it, it was cold and they were 'sorry'. Strange system.

We chose Finch & Fork for Christmas dinner and we're so incredibly impressed with EVERYTHING, especially the Director of Food and Beverage, Patrick Bryant and our server, Curtis. In fact, I'm so impressed, I've stopped eating my PHENOMENAL dinner to write this review. We decided to arrive early and have a drink at the bar. We were graciously greeted by Patrick and he immediately went into Service mode. We had delicious drinks in our hands within 5!minutes of our arrival (and they were busy!) We felt like we were the only guests in the restaurant and felt incredibly special. Our server, Curtis was perfect. He gave us great recommendations and you felt so at home with him. Hands down great meal and service made this Christmas so special. Thank you!!!

We love this place for many reasons.  Great staff, salad bar, full bar, cozy atmosphere.  And, we will come back.  We have been sampling lobster around town.  To date, the Tee-Off has the best in this area, though I have yet to try it at Reunion (the new East Beach grill).  Every restaurant has its charms and distractions.   I knew I was taking a chance with Chuck's char broiled lobster, but at $48 I figured they would be careful.  It did not come out well (dry & blackened around edges...overcooked it seemed) , but the waitress, whom was very nice, disappeared after serving it.  I kept trying to get her attention but she was gone.  She did not even stop to chat when dropping off the check.  I honestly felt she knew there were questions about the lobster that she was not interested in entertaining...visually it was overcooked...did she even look at it?  We did not want to return the lobster--paid in full and left a 12% cash tip, normally we are 25% at this place.     As foodies, we wanted to learn more.  Food is one of our great interests, and even a bad meal can turn into something special if you learn something new.  I really wanted to ask 'Was this a good example of the best lobster you can get with char broil?'  Regardless of the answer (yes or no), I would have learned something interesting.  In my case, I learnt nothing, except to avoid that waitress.

Went here for my anniversary this weekend. Ordered the calamari, boyfriend had the paella and I had the steak. Everything was good but my complaint was the steak was not hot and you could tell the polenta cakes were made in advance. Service was good too but despite writing in my reservation notes that we were celebrating, no mention of it was made. I used to work for a romantic restaurant and any acknowledgement of celebrations is so important to guests.
This little place is modest and simple, but the food is delicious! The breakfasts are classic and the service was attentive, friendly, and nice.

Hana is must stop for me whenever I'm back home in Santa Barbara. I became addicted to the vegan bowls during my pre parent 'clubbing days' . My go to is the  Vegan chicken bowl with all the condiments! Remember, add spicy chipotle Mayo to ALL things HANA!  -805-

Always great! The tacos de camarón are the best, especially when served with a Mexican Coca-Cola and a side of tortilla chips and salsa.   If you're in the neighborhood, Cesar's Place is a great place to stop by and enjoy!
Delicious food, paired with excellent service and classy decor, how can you go wrong? I'm so glad this Santa Barbara favorite continues to thrive.

I've only stayed here using points, but I would say the nightly rate is pretty steep at $250.. but SB isn't a cheap town.  The location is pretty convenient, right along the water and walkable to state street.  It's also nice to be able to take advantage of the ocean trail that runs long Cabrillo for 4.5 miles round trip.  I've only stayed in the Santa Cruz building where the gym is located which from the sounds of things are where the updated rooms are. No major complaints.  The gym is clean and while small has all the equipment I needed.   I appreciate that they are dog friendly since SB is such a great town to visit with your pup.  RE: Parking - just find parking on the street. There is a park close by and plenty of parking even at night.. save yourself the money if the valet cost bugs you.  The front desk staff is hit or miss, when I arrived this time the guy was yelling over the phone and did not even acknowledge any of the guests waiting to check in. Strange.

another friend and I came up to Santa Barbara for my birthday and we split the salmon and arugula salad it was just enough for a late lunch and Noah was our waiter, he did an excellent job and made our experience truly enjoyable. thank you Chas for another great meal.

Awesome burgers. Calamari steak sandwich is on point as is the friendly service and comfortable atmosphere.  A lifelong resident of SB and this was my first visit here.  Will definitely come back again!

Really delicious stuff and a nice break from the doughy, proteiny stuff we 'Mericans usually eat for breakfast.  I can eat a ton of just about anything but a child's portion here is plenty.  Trust me on this.  A bit pricey but super fresh and yummy.

Location is good, and atmosphere is nice - looks like it would be a lot of fun when it's busy. Service was great, the food was decent but did not blow us away. Cocktail specials are good but are $15 - $16 each. Would return for an event or a fun night with a crowd.

This restaurant bar almost feels like a local's secret. The ambiance is luxurious and romantic with Mediterranean interior design. The service was very pleasant and knowledgeable of the menu.  I had the scallops - they were great. The wine list is exceptional and I loved the atmosphere overall.  There is a patio that is more private, but still located right on state street in the heart of downtown.

Really reasonable prices and great sandwiches, soups, and salads. We grabbed lunch here while working remotely nearby and looking for something healthy we could eat (everything else that turned up in my search with greater than 4 stars was Mexican - not a surprise!). I had a half turkey sandwich with soup, and the half was a generous size. While they have some trendy drink options, the rest of the place is kind of old school vs trendy (in a good way). Great lunch spot!
Not friendly and overly expensive for half cooked vegetables. Super excited about the Ravioli , but the tomato sauce tasted canned. Nice atmosphere. That's what you are paying for.
Very decent Pad Thai dish. They gave a lot of noodles and were very filling. The flavor itself was also well-done. I wish they could've had fewer noodles and more chicken/egg balance though.

We love the boathouse! We came here for my birthday breakfast and to relax on the waters edge. The service was great even when we sat on the exterior or the outdoor wall. They gave us plenty of attention. We had pork Chilaquiles, Huevo's rancheros (vegetarian friendly), and a custom omelette for our teenager! Food came out very quickly and was warm! We will be back soon to try dinner!

Great food, especially if you like oysters. And a good bar. Beautiful redesign! Can get very noisy due to high volume of people, that's my only qualm. Service has always been nice, never less than acceptable!  Wonderful happy hour (but with comes the crowds and noise, as expected at a good happy hour).

The food was very good, especially the special salad of duck breast, lettuces, nectarine,and strawberry, with a blueberry vinaigrette. The pork chops and the burrata with prosciutto were wonderful. The green beans that came with the pork chop were divine.  The service was great at the start, but then our waitress's shift ended. My husband and our friend were thinking of getting another glass of wine, as a fourth person was to join us. It was 8:45 p.m. I asked the waiter what time they closed, and he said that they usually closed at 9:00, but that just that day the owner had said that they were changing to summer hours and closing at 9:30 or 10:00. The waiter left us a dessert menu. It had a variety of dessert liqueurs, cognacs, and ports, but no dessert wine like a Riesling. My husband asked whether they had anything like that and the waiter said he could ask the bartender. He never did. We were planning to order multiple drinks and a dessert, but nearly 15 minutes had passed by the time he returned to our table. By then each of the remaining tables except ours had a check folder on it. The message was clear: our presence was not desired.  When the waiter had left the dessert menus, we'd given him our plates, asking that the leftovers from two of the plates be boxed. Mine contained half a pork chop and a bunch of green beans and potatoes. My husband's contained 2/3 of a plate of pasta. The waiter brought us two bags containing the boxes, giving one to me and one to my husband. On the drive home, my husband said he was looking forward to frying up the pasta tomorrow.  That was not to be. When we got home, we discovered that the pasta had, apparently, been disposed of, and one of the boxes contained nothing but the bone, a few potatoes, and a couple green beans our friend had left. I remembered, too, that when the waiter picked up our plates, he'd set mine on top of our friend's. Not only were we given the scraps of someone else's meal, those scraps had been smashed under my plate.  Disappointing.
Chef Juan did an absolutely amazing job Recreating the menu with his toe curling good food !

Only a 3 because the service isnt the best. After you are seated it seems to take forever to order drinks, food, etc...the food and drinks however are wonderful!! A favorite spot food wise and located near the funkzone. Close to a parking lot as well.
HEAVEN ROLL!! If you are a carnivore like me order the beef plate!! so good and comes in a fajita type cow plate! lol

Great place for Mexican food in Santa Barbara. You can't really go wrong with what you order. My husband got the lamb and barbacoa tacos which were incredibly savory albeit a little oily. I got the fish tacos and shrimp tacos which had a great charred taste from being grilled. The best thing we got though was the ceviche which was fresh-tasting, tons of seafood, and tortilla chips to eat alongside.

Came here like two years ago, got some sort of burrito and spit it out it was so gross. Just came back for the first time since and it hasn't improved at all. All of the deli salads and especially the fruit salads are not at all fresh. Ended up trying chicken breast and cheddar on a wheat pita from the deli.... The chicken was inedible.  I'm not usually a complainer but I showed it to a worker who agreed that it was not good but didn't offer me a refund. I'm not one to ask, just felt like it should have been offered.
Best in town for clean food choices and prices!  Hope they never leave!  We eat here every time we visit.

It's tough satiating your Asian food cravings when you are living in Santa Barbara.   But guess what...Saigon not only welcomed me with open arms, but made me a part of their family. Not only did I go on a weekly basis and feel completely comfortable going alone, but they also always accommodated my large reservations whenever friends and family came into town!  I will always be in the mood for their pho, but I highly recommend their:  1) Duck curry soup (with noodle, you can opt rice) 2) Vermicelli  3) Spring Rolls  Love you Saigon, miss you everyday.

Great service and very good food priced reasonably. Spent around $100 for two persons which included two appetizers, two main dishes, and a shared vanilla crime brûlée.  Loved the Lemongrass encrusted Salman in Thai sauce as well as the phyllo wrapped shrimp... Yummy! The grilled eggplant, portobello mushroom and goat cheese appetizer was nice too, however the eggplant could have been less fried and more grilled. My husband ordered the SeaBass special which was super packed with flavor, definitely a recommendation.  The ambiance seemed a little bit dated but was overcome by the excellent service by the waiters and cleanliness of the restaurant.  We would come again, there were more things on the menu we still wanted to try.  Overall, it's apparent the chefs are very talented because the flavor of the meals were perfectly balanced and every ingredient on the plate seemed to have a purpose. Good presentation as well. This is a top notch restaurant.

This place is awesome I love there pastrami sandwich and they make them pretty big. The prices aren't bad either. They also have a really friendly staff, I would definitely recommend for anyone looking for a good sandwich spot.

The view is really great here. I normally hit Brophy's when I'm in town but couldn't as I had my dog with me and they're not dog friendly.   The menu is somewhat limited due to Covid restrictions so I opted with the lobster tacos. If not for the creamy salsa, the dish would have been incredibly bland. I was hoping for more flavor in the rice as well but no go. I will say, at least as it relates to my experience, that the view was far better than the food.

Amazing food, outstanding service. Our server Xan served us with professional, complete, friendly attention.   The Beet Salad, crab cake, and smoked salmon complimented the sole. Could not recommend this establishment more highly.

This spot is the tops, for real! We came in on for an early Friday night dinner (we went to see a film at the local theater after), and I am so glad we found this hidden gem! Found it on Yelp, then cross referenced it on Axxess (if you don't know about this service, do yourself a favor and check it out- http://www.sbaxxess.com/), the reviews looked amazing, and we were not disappointed. I had the pan-roasted salmon and the bf had the lamb couscous tajine- both such different flavor combos, and both so delicious in their own right.  Then we split the chocolate tart with homemade mint ice cream- SUCH a great way to finish- SO SO GOOD!!!! The only thing I would say, and this is just a small thing- the server, while well meaning, was a bit awkward and said a few odd things (remarking about our eating habits- we laughed it off, however others would be offended).   Also we never got any of what looked to be delicious bread, I mean I was probably okay without- but it looked amazing.  Funny all other tables got it so quickly, and there was never even a nod about it.   We had an issue with using the buy one, get one entree through Axxess- they figured it out eventually, it was just odd how it was handled, the server said she applied it, however upon close examination the discount was not applied.  They righted it at the end, just took some time.

My wife and I came here because they serve the best tempura in town. The batter is light and crispy. They were very generous with the shrimp and there was a wide variety of vegetables. The presentation was impressive. We also ordered their Futomaki and it was very tasty! My wife ordered the Cold Soba & Tempura. She said it was delicious. This is my choice when it comes to eating Japanese food.

The food was decent, but it seemed to be pretty pricey for what I was getting. I can only imagine what the rent on ocean front property is like for a restaurant, so I imagine a lot of the rent distributes its way to the final bill.  That being said, the ambiance is quite comfortable and the atmosphere on a nice day is wonderful. Also, the service is exactly what you'd expect for the prices -- excellent.

I'm giving it 4 since, I have not eaten there yet (I plan to very soon... so I'll make sure to update). I can not get enough of this place. There flaky pastries and marzipan. I love how there whip cream in the pastries is always so fresh. Must stop place, its charm almost gives me that Disneyland type of feel.
Tacos now $1.40, but still fantastic!  Lilly's has expanded to triple it's original size, but still offer the same great food and friendly service.

The food is fine, the service is the problem. Most of the employees are great, some have worked there for a very long time and those people are amazing folks. Obviously with Covid going on, there are many changes occurring to restaurants. I work for an office that orders weekly from SCD totaling roughly $100 each week. Recently we went to pick up our order and had an awful encounter that has led us to seek a new weekly eat out spot.  We walked up and stood in the outside line. It is not apparent to customers (clearly) that you must walk inside to alert them of your pick up arrival. We walked up to the front entrance and waited behind a couple. Either my friend or I suggested to them that they go inside and check in with the staff. They were hesitant until and small young man came out and instructed them that coming inside to check in is correct. As they proceeded to walk in and we walked in behind them, another group started yelling about a line. Obviously we had been standing  in said line for a bit. The young man inside acknowledged there was a line, which we again acknowledged that we were aware of due to standing in it for 5mins. This group started to shout rude things to us as we were just attempting to check in. The small man had come back outside and went up to them and said 'Sorry, I tried.' You tried what? What does that mean? We were not in the wrong by any means but this employee in a sense sided with those who were shouting? Extremely unprofessional. We relayed the experience to our manager who agreed that we should not continue to patronize a restaurant where the some of the staff is so poorly trained. I would suggest making the line more apparent so there is no confusion. Additionally some kind of sign outside alerting people to go in and check in first would have helped. The food is ok but nowhere near tasty enough to put up with this disrespect.

Goodness sakes!!!  I used to come here often. Prior to pandemic. I went one evening to buy a chicken shawarma. The kitchen had raw meat piled on the counter bleeding all on the floor. Cook was piling it high with no gloves and no face mask. However, when he saw me so he put on my his mask quickly with his bloody hands.  After I placed my order he went digging in the dirty dishes for a pan. Rinsed it only with water and didn't not wash his hands from the bloody meat.  I told the front what I saw and ran out of there.

Wandered in while on vacay in Santa Barbara for an afternoon snack. We were promptly greeted and seated at a great spot on the patio.  We had the bruschetta & caprese salad, and both were amazing & full of fresh flavors. Unfortunately we had dinner reservations elsewhere, otherwise I would have totally stayed for dinner.   To top it off our server was s super friendly & attentive and even offered to take our picture before I could even ask. Great experience!

Whoever made the macaroons that I bought on Christmas Eve: You deserve a raise! I've had macaroons from Renaud's before but these were even better than usual. Everyone else who tried them agreed. So amazing! Everything about them was PERFECT. I'm ordering more today for my baby shower :)
Amazing tacos, go there all the time. Had a catering event at our office. Made sure to go with them. Go with the Carne Asade tacos.
Very good quality way overpriced. Selection baked goods and menu very limited options. Very popular with SB yuppie crowd

I've been wanting to try secret bao for a while and finally got around to it, it did not disappoint!   I have a gluten allergy and the staff were super accommodating. They let me know which dishes would be possible to make gluten free and which would not and were thorough in asking additional questions about ingredients I was sensitive to.   I ended up ordering the pork belly fried rice (no soy sauce) and they subbed in a different GF sauce. It was delicious! Super flavorful and packed with yummy ingredients. It was a huge portion enough for me, a little for my husband, and a little to take home as leftovers!   My husband ordered two baos -- the KFC and the pork belly. He said both were super flavorful and delicious. His favorite was the KFC, he is a sucker for a friend chicken sandwich so this spin on one of his favorite meals was a definite winner. He said the KFC had a little bit of a kick which he enjoyed.  All in all we had a great experience and will be back again!

Really small portions  Soggy fries  2 onion rings on a $17. Platter?  Terrible Cole slaw  Gluey chowder  Hands down the absolute worst seafood I've ever had anywhere  Didn't expect something great but this was awful
The food here is amazing! Perfect little 'hole in the wall' place to pick up some delicious fresh made food or regular old snacks!

I popped by on my way home to pick up some empanadas because I wanted to try the new vegan one.   The Vegana, which I've had before, is very good with quinoa, peppers, onions, cheese, and spinach. The Hakuna Batata is also yummy, with sweet potatoes, spinach, and shallots. Of course I ordered extra sauce and had lots left over to use on other things. It's really delicious.  One day, I'll make the time to dine on their cute outdoor patio.

We have enjoyed Renaud's many times, for breakfast, lunch, special desserts or just a snack. But I was really blown away this year by their Galette Des Rois (King's Cake) to celebrate Epiphany (January 6).   I've had the traditional Mardi Gras style cake many times, either homemade or store bought. However, Renaud's has really WOWED me with the french style King's Cake!! It's everything I love about french pastries, buttery, light, delicious almond filling! It is just fantastic and so fun.   The cake comes with two adorable little figurines to hide in the cake and a gold crown! It's such a fun cake to share and delicious with coffee. I really enjoyed this seasonal item and I can't wait to order another next year.
This lil place has been here forever. They recently did a real nice job giving it a fresh look. Unfortunetly i heard last week that they have closed for good. Hope that's not true!!
Love the hole in the wall mexican decadence of their enormous carnitas tacos! No fuss, no muss, just great authentic food with killer salsa! Definitely a great place to splurge!

This place is good - maybe too good.  I would come here more often if it weren't so popular (meaning busy).  My friends and I have said many times 'Oooh, I want La Super-Rica for lunch but lets just go to ____ and get tacos cause I know the lines gonna be super-long at Super-Rica.'  I usually just get tacos here.  Meat and tortillas - mmmmmm - bistec, costilla, adobado or chorizo.  They have good tamales, chile relleno, enchiladas, quesadillas & chilaquiles.  I love their guacamole.  I've probably been eating at La Super-Rica since 1993.  I always enjoy it.  I don't remember any problems or bad food or service over the years.  Much love to La Super-Rica and thanks for all of the tasty meals!

Nice staff and good service but the food has failed to impress, twice now. I'm a chef and have had legitimate Tom Kha. Their's was so laden with coconut milk it lacked the balance of the lime. I kept pulling pieces of lemongrass out of my mouth.   First visit I had an udon bowl so bloody hot with chili it was impossible to eat. I have a very high tolerance.   It's always packed so I guess it's good enough for SB, which says everything.

Overpriced, bad service, menu items not served as described.  Waiter could care less.    I asked specifically about the items in the burrito and the waiter gave completely incorrect information.  It is difficult to get anyone's attention here to get service as well.

Used to be great. Been here a few times since new ownership and service has gone way down. Don't accommodate for substitutions anymore and not as friendly as they used to be. Hate to see places no longer value top level customer service

The vegan chicken bowl is incredible!! I hadn't tried this place before the world closed due to Covid, so I had it delivered, and it is really well suited for delivery. That's a big plus these days! Easy to reheat if your driver takes the long way, and super flavorful. Got it with the guava boba which was excellent as well.

First impression: beautiful lobby, the grounds are beautiful. We were two couples and we went for each of our anniversaries. We went there looking for a nice quiet getaway. Much to our surprise, when we got to the pool it looked like raging waters. There were SO many little kids and strollers all over. For the size of the hotel, the pool size was TERRIBLE. There was no room to move anywhere, very claustrophobic. Although there is an adult pool, it's a piss pond. It's so small and did not look sanitary. On Saturday when we went to the pool, there was not one chair to sit on. We complained to the manager, who was very nice and understanding, and she offered us a free cabana the next day with free drinks and such. We took her offer and stayed in the cabana. You're in a luxurious cabana with a tv and everything but the kids were SO noisy and loud that you could not enjoy. What kid belongs in a $600+ per night hotel?!   The rooms: We were on the first floor. The rooms are nice and cute and a good size. I guess since we were on the first floor there was an odd smell in the bathroom and the water pressure was horrible. Ladies, if you have long hair, prepare to be in the shower for hours to get the shampoo out. It felt like drizzle. The water doesn't get hot fast, you have to wait. And there is only one sink. The bathroom floors were very dirty as well, not acceptable at all!   After years of wanting to go, we left highly disappointed. The only reason I'm giving two stars instead of one was because the staff was amazing and the manager, Alyssa, was so great, and went out of her way to help us.   Do not even attempt to go down to the beach, you will get oil on your feet for sure. There was a massive oil spill.   Bacara should take a drive down to the Four Seasons with a pen and paper and take notes on how a high end resort should be.   PS: Don't even get me started about the Bistro Restaurant. I could go on for days about how bad it was. $45 for breakfast for one person?! HORRIBLE.
Really pretty pier but not much to do.  I would probably just come here to eat .  There's a museum and a seashell store.  Water looks kinda dirty

We have become quite regulars at the Drunken Crab.  Tonight was fun as we finally were able to sit outside.  The staff was extra on point.  The waiter Joseph (who can write backwards) and Gabby the new manager were engaging and fun. During the week the place is a bit quiet due to the block of State Street but the staff made up for it tonight.    We had the perfect amount of seafood and it didn't break the bank.  Great place.  Great food and great staff.

Amazing meal!! Pop corn 5 stars (I never thought that could be possible) Brussel sprouts Amazing!!! 5 stars,  I would go there again just for Brussel sprouts.  Fried chicken 5 stars pretty dam good.  I will totally eat here again.  This is a very hip and trendy restaurant but the food is very good. Bill was very fair for all the food we eat.  Definitely will return.
One of my favorite Mexican joints.  The guacamole appetizer was like 50 avocados.  The chicken mole' was beautiful.  And they serve tons of food.

Ordered the vegan scramble with potatoes and toast. Presentation good, nice fresh veggies. NO seasoning on any of the food.. no salt, no pepper nothing. I had to put in a ton of salt and even that didn't help. I appreciate the vegan offerings, but if did your research, a tofu scramble should be seasoned. The tofu was just thrown in completely white, not seasoned. A good tofu scramble would be yellow from the cumin, nutritional yeast and turmeric. My boyfriend's eggs were not seasoned either. I thought seasoning was like the number one thing for a chef... I guess if this is there 'healthy' style, sure but not worth $14. Also no vegan butter or jam offered for my toast.

Finally got to stop by here to try and get some brunch! I was so sad that breakfast ended so early. I got there at 11am with my sis-in-law and couldn't order my croissant sandwich with smoked salmon so I had to resort to the mushroom pasta. The pasta was good, however, I usually like my pastas with some type of meat. My sister in law ordered the crab crepe, which came with a salad and grilled cherry tomatoes. Delicious! The vanilla latte I ordered was good too. The only reason why I didn't give 5 stars is because I thought it was an all day breakfast cafe.

We went to Jane in Santa Barbara Monday for dinner. We made reservations which was helpful. We ordered the Kale  salad with Salmon and the Grilled chicken with chilies. The salmon was moist and the salad dressing had some lemon in it which gave the salad a great flavor. The grilled chicken was moist and flavorful. We have been to the Jane restaurant in Goleta and in Santa Barbara many times and the food and service are always great.

Quick recap:  -          Coffee: Good -          Portion size: Average -          Service: Order at the counter -          Environment: Hipstery modern -          $: Expensive -          Perks: Backyard Patio  More details:  We went there for coffee. This review does not apply to food. Cappuccinos ($4) and Espressos ($3) were really good, but the real deal are their Signature coffees. The burnout ($8.75) is super beautiful and really good. Wish I lived nearby and could come try the other ones.  Something super cool: They do their own almond milk and it is quite tasty.  Overall? Really nice presentation. Good coffee. Cool environment. Super cool homemade almond milk. A bit pricey but worth the premium

Great little sandwich place off State St. the guys were really helpful (& patient with our toddler) with us on our first visit. Better for take out or delivery, but there's a small communal table inside to dine in.  Thanks for the great sandwich & all the pickles, Joe!

Their burgers are so flavorful, juicy and delicious! They have homemade sliced pickles too! The chicken sliders are delicious also. I ask for a side of their delicious truffle sauce to dip my scrumptious French fries into. I've been pleased with everything on the menu and great service. We go there about 4 times a month.

Today i was very exited to come to one of my favorite restaurant!! Which i always recommend sence im a local and also a lyft and uber driver so i always recommend last sunday i was here and ask for the same  drink with the cricket salt rimm!! And my expirience has always been amazing!! Sadly today i brought my son who i hardly see to my favorite restaurant!! And when we order our drinks i was deny the cricket salt!! For my hibiscus margarita!! Then when i said i been here several times!! My server responded me very offensive and said 'well that is gonna be a lil extra!!!' Like making me feeling i cant afford it!!! Then after it took him a great long time to come and ask for our order!! Very disappointing!!! I am a local and love all the sister restaurants!! Los agaves,flor d mezcal and my fav santo mescal!!(jeremias) step up ur serving manners!!!
The best pizza in SB!  The BLT and Sicilian are amazing, love that delivery is included.
I love the Lure, but their removal of the cheesecake from their menu constitutes blasphemy!

This place was excellent, both food and service-wise. The only caveat: if you have a hefty appetite, be prepared to either leave hungry or rack up a sizable bill. Their happy hour deals are quite fair, though. Highlights: grilled hearts of romaine salad, patatas bravas, lemon garlic prawns (what is in the sauce that makes it SO magical?!? I mean other than butter and garlic...) and habanero pulled pork sliders. I left more than satisfied; my partner, strapping fellow that he is, did not.

Vibe - awesome! Very fun place. Good wine and beer selection. My only complaint is food portion sizes for *some of the items. I had a skirt steak and broccoli dish - it was good but the amount of steak served was barely a toddlers portion, so that was disappointing! I've also had a pasta dish though (with pepperdel?)  and that was plenty of food - and yummy. My dad ordered the half chicken - it was a lot of food and seriously delicious! So I would steer away from the small plates (because they are too small for the price) and stick with the full entrees. I've been here a few times and enjoy it.

OHHHMYGOOOD, look what and amazing place this is!!! I can't believe the transformation of this spot, this has become a super classy bar downtown and who knew that they are also open late, so thankful to have a great spot for late night drinks and food.  Yea I did say food.  Eight girlfriends and I were celebrating two of their birthdays, we were right away seated at our tables when waitress came and gave us our menu, and since it was bunch of us we just started ordering drinks as we were arriving, and I did say they have food, right?  Well we got tempura green beans and potatoes chip, and fries (few orders) to nibble and share between us all they were all so good, the tempura definitely a must.  Service was great all along, and waitress super friendly.  We left after 10 and on a weekday that's late for a place to be open.  Will definitely be back soon, also couple of the salads with sardines were amazing! !!
First time going in awhile. The vibe is still really great. Love Reggae wedensdays! and first time having food there, delicious!

Two Words: ya changed!  I remember going here when I was a UCSB student and there were huge lines. There still is.   It's not the same. Quality has decreased tremendously. Ordered mojado burrito and first bite was cold cheese. The whole burrito was warm while the other burrito was piping hot. Even the guacamole wasn't fresh. I have never had guacamole that tasted as bad as theirs.   I honestly am writing this while my stomach is churning. May have food poisoning.  The people are very kind tho.

Uberly disappointed! First off, don't come in here wearing casual wear, like shorts and a baseball hat, because you will be snubbed on service for those all dressed up. Our server kept talking with others making our wait time go up, we easily waited 20min to place an order. The soup was warm, not hot at all, then I felt something on the tip of my spoon, turns out I had a dirty spoon with dried food all over it. I didn't make a big stink about that, I just grabbed another spoon from a adjacent table. I ordered the Capellini Organici, it was the saddest looking pasta ever, it was nothing like I remembered. I even looked it up on Yelp, huge difference compared to what's posted online and what I got last time. There was no basil, I had to ask for a side of basil, it was still super dry, lacking sauce. I only ate five bites and pushed it aside, and the server never cared to ask if any of our food was good... Luckily, the '$14' club sandwich, which was surprisingly small, however was very good and I was able to eat something to get full on. Getting a refill was nonexistent with our server, another server overheard our complaint and he began making sure we were topped off, which was kind of him. This was the biggest waste of $62 ever. We had a craving for something fine, came here and it was just a huge let down, one thing after another into an amazing domino effect...

Called coming into SB.  Ordered 4 beef tacos for pickup delivery.  Tacos were so crispy and stuffed with cheese and lettuce -- I love that Colby shredded cheese.  This is still my FAVORITE TACO in the USA.  Someone even backed into my new car. The nervous lady was relieved when I told her 'Thanks for being honest -- no prob -- I just drove from Arroyo Grande to Pt Loma SD and back to SB on my way home...'  Just rubbed a little paint off bumper on my VW Atlas and headed home with my dorado tacos el mejor.

Wish the food was as good as the first time we ate here! I always order the Salmon Lover because of the  Panang Curry Sauce. Each time I have eaten here (about 7 times), the amount of sauce goes down. It used to be very saucy like a curry. Tonight it only had about two tablespoons as decoration on the plate under the Salmon. Not what you expect when you order a curry! If i wanted more sauce they charge $5!?! The thai iced tea used to be very strong, but that too has become weaker and weaker as the years go on. Think this will be the last time I eat here...

Ordered something to go, and realized they were all wrong after I went back home. I asked for fajitas with shrimp and steak, and a wet burrito. They gave me  fajitas with shrimp and chicken, and a burrito none wet. How's this possible? I wondered if it's because I used Axxess card? Still not a good thing to change my order sneakily. Food itself is about average. But I'm not coming back again. Why? Who wants to be disappointed arriving home with wrong orders?

I enjoyed this patisserie. The cafe is tiny in space with limited seating inside/out. They're known for their fluffy croissants which are huge in size, buttery, and moist inside; they aren't flaky and don't break apart easily.   I had their ham and cheese croissant which was delicious and hardy. It's what you expected it to be like; good taste but nothing surprising. It came with a legit fruit bowl that isn't your typical melon/honeydew mix. It had these tiny green grapes that popped in your mouth. They looked like grapes off the vines used for wine. They also serve freshly squeezed OJ which was is a plus.   If you're looking for a light pastry bite, come here and enjoy the calm setting. It's intimate and they serve freshly baked goods on the daily. They're also well known in SB so you can't go wrong.

I first went here on a Friday night out looking for some food after already having a few cocktails. Santa Barbara, for as much good eating as there is, can be a tough place to find a bite to eat after 10. Roy, however is open til midnight (I believe) every night, which is great.  The cocktails are nice pours, and they have this great $10 entree deal going for a seemingly unlimited time. I've only had the chicken marsala, which is delicious. It comes with potato pancakes and vegetables.  The service is touch and go, sometimes it's fine, sometimes it's painfully slow, even if you're the only people there!  Anyway, I've almost made a trip there a weekly occurrence.

I'm a local and swear i will never be returning to this spot. I ordered take-out for breakfast only to find they gave me the wrong order when i got to work. I Walked all the way back over to return it and get the right order, walk all the way back to work, and one of their to-go salsa's looked like it had been partially used and then put back. THERE WAS SOMEONE ELSES LEFTOVER FOOD IN MY SALSA. Really didn't want to bother walking over for a third time so i threw it away. Beyond standards in so many ways. DO NOT GO TO THIS LOCATION.

This place is epic. They have their food, drink, service and decor game on point. The only real homemade pasta in down. Best drinks of any restaurant around. Service is knowledgeable without being pretentious. Ambiance is beautiful. I can't remember the last time I had a 10/10 experience at a restaurant in Santa Barbara, but they nailed it. Kudos! I will mos def be back for more very soon.

yeah this place sucks. the food is not good or very Thai at all. we only accidentally came here cause i mistaken your choice for your place. there is a huge difference. i'm just directionally challenged. go to your place on milpas.

I have to bump this rating up due to my second visit.  This time I arrived at Something's Fishy I was towards the end of a day-long State Street drinking binge.  As soon as I entered, the sake bombs were calling my name.  There's no telling exactly how many I consumed, but let's suffice it to say that I had enough to not remember anything after they brought me my soup and salad.  Not only was our group extraordinarily loud, messy and drunk but we were buying sake bombs for a group of minors across the restaurant.  So thank you Something's Fishy for putting up with our drunken antics and not kicking us out.  Thank you also for not carding those sweet newlyweds who couldn't have been much past 19.  P.S. I hope I didn't throw up in your bathroom.

It's not a five-star restaurant. It's a sandwich place that has the tastiest pastrami in SB. I've tried other sandwiches here. They've been fine. But Mom's PLT is the staple here. Order it. Enjoy it. Don't ask how many calories are in it.  Seriously though. It tastes too good not to have.

The tacos are insane here!!! Nice size portions and flavor is 10/10. Even the cauliflower non-meat tacos are sooo good! I usually get the Sal de Mar ceviche with watermelon and sashimi fish- it's spicy and tangy, and a huge plate of food for lunch! Everything is good here, it's my go-to lunch spot around the corner from my work, and inside the Public Market is a fun place to people watch and grab an adult beverage any time of day!

I stumbled in here the day of the World Cup finals. We were trying to go to Sharkeez but it was at capacity, and thank goodness it was because instead of a room full of fried food and yelling we got to sit in the lime green oasis of this restaurant and eat delicious and healthy food! I tried the fish and the vegetables, both with non-spicy (sweet) sauce. SO SO GOOD!! You get three choices of sauce--spicy, non-spicy or curry. You also get to choose noodles or rice with your entree. I preferred the plain white rice to the egg noodles which were a bit garlic-y. The sandwiches floating by our table on the way to customers looked good, and so did the big coconuts--I think they were a coconut water drink. They also serve some coffee drinks and beers and smoothies--which I want to try when I go back. This is counter order service and the waitress brings your order to your number at your table. They had lots of big screen TVs and we enjoyed watching the game too! So if you want to eat well and peep the tube, consider this place over a standard bar. Or just go in for the amazing food.

This place is solid! What an amazing breakfast joint. Had the fried chicken Benedict which was perfect, with a nicely crisp fried chicken cutlet. Fried potatoes were good, could have used more seasoning and crisper. Had an amazing latte with Handlebar coffee. Fantastic and a must!

The food is a bit pricey for the amounts but it is 'Tapas'... Definitely not a traditional Spanish Tapas bar but the food is all very good. The drinks are delicious however, are not very strong. One negative was the Sangria, it tasted like I was sucking on a cinnamon stick.... but I loved my whiskey sour. Very fun place to grab a drink with friends.

Definitely must visit in SB for lovely dishes that you can't find at most other restaurants. I love seeing fewer items on the menu, because that typically means fewer things to go wrong, and more time to craft each item.  Reservations - Make at least a couple days in advance. No walk-ins on Thursday May 6, 2021, only a wait list if the current reservations fall through. We put our name on anyways, and they called us 1 hour later to tell us there was an open slot. We were already eating by then but they said tomorrow Friday 8PM was available so we took it. We showed up almost an hour earlier and they seated us anyway.   Service - Very friendly, cheery, happy, and made us feel excited to be there.  Complimentary lemon poppyseed popcorn - Extremely dangerous, avoid at all costs. How is that no table around us is eating this and we're inhaling this like butter?  Crispy brussel sproputs (sesame, medjool dates, garum lime) - Very crispy, soft, and hollow. Salty and soggy.  Caramel Cauliflower (harissa, lemon yogurt, marcona almonds, pickled kumquats, mint) - As incredibly saucy as this was, the flavors weren't that strong. Difficult to finish because it was so rich.  Cast Iron Mediterranean Branzino (muhamara, english peas, walnuts, charred lemon, pea tendrils) - Soft, gooey, falls apart in your mouth, with the perfectly crust skin.  Buttermilk Fried Chicken (smoked date caramel, amba aioli, pickled cucumbers, scallion) - Thick, chunky, deeply fried chicken that was dry on the outside, slightly juicy on the inside.   Next time: Baharat Spiced Chickpea Tagine, California Delta Asparagus, Lamb Shawarma Rillettes, Crispy Pork Belly.

This is the go to spot for great tacos!  Limited parking but it doesn't slow people down. My favorite is the fish taco. Everything I have tried is good. You can also hit some balls at the baseball batting cages next door if you up for it.

Great spot to grab tacos and even hit some balls in the batting cages. The 3 taco deal is great. The best taco I had was the spicy battered fish. Wish the batting cages stayed open later. The special hot sauce is good and they have a good amount of outdoor seating. Can get busy in the evening for dinner but the line moves fast. Small kitchen that packs a punch!

This is my favorite place and staple in Santa Barbara. Also if you like brunch this is my home away from home. Me and my roomate have made it a point to get brunch here as much as possible and have never had a bad experience.

Best mole in town, best meat in town, best burrito in town. Love coming here and the volume of traffic will keep you waiting longer because everyone loves this place.  I recommend the Pollo En Mole Burrito. Devine.

What is there to say besides WOW!  Rising from the ashes and taking a chance by opening in the thick of COVID, Venus in Furs is an incredible addition and unexpected surprise.  If a tasting room for natural wines ONLY had a baby with a super sexy NYC cocktail lounge, it would be Venus in Furs.  Not only is it absolutely stunning on the inside, with one of the most breathtaking bar facades in town - the menu offers unique natural wines but best of all, cocktails with wine.  Truth be told, I wasn't expecting much from a wine cocktail but with descriptions that actually made me laugh out loud (shout out to the 'lingering notes of summer '08), I had to try at least one. I went with The Gentle Whip and it shot to the top of my ultimate favorite cocktails list FAST. Not only is it delicious - it is fresh and vibrant with notes of vanilla that will make you beg for more.   If you're still reading this just stop what you're doing and GO already!

Drink selection here is probably a 4/5 but so/so quality bar food with an exceptionally long wait every time I'm here lately. The turnaround times for entrees is also insanely long and on more than one occasion I've had subpar attitudes from waiters here.   The beer variety is their truly redeeming quality and they do a good job sourcing from local brewers!
Great place with great food. Kitchen staff is wonderful and the owner is a delight. Thank you for all your many great dishes and flavorful salsas.
Baos are fantastic. I've had the KFC, shrimp, mushroom & pork belly. The KFC is my favorite. I've also gotten their japache noodles & they're super good too

Red-velvet pancakes.  Yes, you read that right!  What an amazingly, well-put, and delicious dish this was!!!  They give you a spoonful of frosting and fresh strawberries and bananas. They also added little chocolate cut-outs that were shaped as hearts.   I also had the mixed berry Arnold Palmer which was okay. I thought it was going to be something spectacular but it was just mixed berry juice and lemonade.   Overall, the pancakes were pretty heavy but that's what you should expect from red velvet.   YUM!  Two red-velvetish thumbs up!

'I don't think I ordered this'!  It was my quesadilla and yes I did order it. I've never had an open faced quesadilla before today. It was amazing. Super delicious. I don't want to go home. Lol. The guac was delicious too. Some of the best Mexican food I've ever had. Santa Barbara surely DID NOT disappoint in the Mexican food category. Just what we were hoping for on this trip.
I thought the pizza was really good.  I got the Summer Peach Pizza my husband got the Pepperoni.  Both were great.  Thin crust and crispy outside.  Good flavor.  Few outside bench seats to eat at.
Three medium sized pizzas for over $60.00. And there was nothing particularly compelling about the pizzas in fact I wouldn't order another if they were half the price. Wow I don't get it...
One of the best pizzas I've ever had. The Parma and 4 cheese are great. Fried artichokes are delicious. Raw artichoke salad is delectable. Great craft beer selection. A must in Santa Barbara.

Cafe Ana is a nice addition to the Santa Barbara foodie scene. I've enjoyed ordering from their breakfast menu - Ingredients are fresh, often made from scratch, and thoughtfully prepared (getting that perfectly cooked egg is no easy task!) The portions are pretty generous and the presentation is beautiful.  Update: I really wanted to give them 4-5 stars however a few days after writing the above review, they completely changed their breakfast menu. Theyve now downgraded their breakie sandwiches in terms of portion and no longer offer any of the original sandwiches. They've basically stripped out ingredients AND increased the price by a whopping $5!!!  The Avocado is now not included (which was why I loved this sandwich so much) So I'm paying more for less? Ordering the same sandwich I was used to getting costs nearly $15 vs $10! That's just bacon and eggs, folks! Feels really off. Not sure if you think paying $15 for an egg sandwich with some bacon or ham is worth it - But this experience left a bad taste in my mouth. Pretty disappointing!!

The fresh and tasty sweet potato taco and tres leches are reason enough to try this delightful eatery. The ceviche and other offerings looked excellent. Ample indoor and outdoor seating with friendly staff and reasonable pricing.

With a solid bar program, interactive chefs and some of the best real estate in Santa Barbara...this is a win (If you are up to this kind of fare).   This is a unique review because it is during Restaurant Week. Normally, the check would be around $100 per person absent spirits. I'm not saying I didn't succumb to that amount but with a prefix menu of $40 this is easily the best choice for any option that restaurant week offers. Ever.   View the menu. I chose the beet salad. Ever so subtle by leaving the fontina at the bottom (side story. Pm me for details) lovely olive oil dressing the frisée and the radicchio. This was a very generous salad for a 3 course tasting. I couldn't finish it.   Next, the Faroe Island Salmon (between Scotland and Ireland). Perfectly seared on both sides skin off. A gin yogurt helps to elevate flavors through natural evaporating of the alcohol. Combined with a cous cous that absorbs the yogurt and assists the heirloom cauliflower, this dish is a winner.   Dessert finished with 'Milk & Cookies' which delivered. 'Cookie dough' was made with cream cheese in place of egg yoke. A panna cotta, surrounded by a lake of sweetened condensed milk and cream, was layered with chocolate chip cookie crumbles.   The most notable tidbit is that the restaurant currently has no head chef...leaving the very capable sous chefs (the one tonight having staged at the three Michelin starred Per Se in NY) to curate the menu. It's your best bet for a great experience. Restaurant week goes until Sunday, March 3rd.

Always depressing when a place you love goes south.  I gave it Covid credit three or four times.  But when you tell the waiter: the salad is drowning in dressing  (after heroics to flag him down) and he shrugs.  And when you say the lasagne has cold spots and he wonders what you want him to do about it.  Even the coffee was poor.  It was so, so shitty. I had to face it. There's no service to speak of.  The food was beyond substandard. It's another heartbreak. We have to face the future without another favorite.  They lost their way.

I just can't go to California without walking down a wharf. They are so unique to the area.   I especially love this one because the city is so amazing and fun, and the views from the wharf are breathtaking. Not unlike all the piers, this one has a variety of restaurants featuring primarily seafood, and also has a few knick knack shops.   Funny story: my catholic friend would not let me go to the palm reader saying that it was demonic. Later I went by myself (of course) and found the price for a reading to be $40, which truly is demonic! I'll never pay for that shit!

The Quick: The most incredible indulgent (guilt free) food and awesome beer. Lots of different options and variety. The much wrap supreme is what dreams are made of. So much goodness...and it's plant based!! A must visit for meat eaters and vegans alike

This is by far, on the very top of my list, of one of my most favorite sandwich I have ever eaten. I ordered Mom's PLT. It contained crispy pastrami with chipotle mayo, lettuce and tomatoes--- a taste of heaven. The only downfall with this sandwich was I split it with my wife. I wish I had ordered the whole thing for myself. The flavor was amazing sort of reminded me of crispy bacon at some point. The owner said they were looking at opening in San Diego. Oh God, I'm never going to be able to get rid of my food baby.

I had pretty low expectations of this place as the last pizza spot occupying this space was mediocre when we ordered there during the pandemic, but Fatte's was SO good! We ordered a large Hawaiian and a large Chorizo pizza and they were both delicious. The pickled jalapenos had the perfect spice level, the bacon and chorizo were baked to a crisp, and everything was loaded with flavor. Perfect cheese-to-crust ratio, and the sauce zesty. After a less-than-satisfying Hawaiian pizza from Rusty's a few months back, this is definitely our new go-to neighborhood pizza joint. Two large pizzas comfortably fed four hungry folks + plenty of leftovers, and the total came out to just above $30 before tip with their BOGO special. We'll be back!
Cool exposed brick space, but you can still hear your friends! Music is not too loud. Beer is delicious!!!

This place is great. Lots of times there are coupons but I go with or without a coupon.  The food is always yummy tasting, salsa bar is always clean and stocked with tasty salsas, love the charred chicken or steak taco salad. Normally a tostada salad but here it's a taco salad.  & all the employees I've met are really great, friendly, helpful, & make sure to get your order right, with a genuine smile- even with custom orders- ()-  Great place!!

Classy, intimate , great location because it's not too touristy and the food is delicious.   They have a very small menu. But the selections are not bad. Tonight was full house. Service is good. Parking is street parking. Definitely recommend this place for when you're on a date.

So nice to sit outside and have a view of the mountains behind Santa Barbara while eating dinner. This place has great ambiance for a walk up to order restauranta. The food is great. The meat a little to fatty for my taste otherwise would be a 5. Huge portions plus chips and numerous salsa choices make for a full, happy belly.

My husband and I tried all three of the vegetarian tacos last night and they were outstanding! It is refreshing to have a Mexican restaurant in town that can distinguish itself from the others by offering new and different flavor combinations. We appreciate they put some effort and thought into their vegetarian menu. It was so yummy we're considering dining there tonight, too!
Um dos lugares mais descolados que já conheci.  Vale o visual, bebidas, comidas e serviço.  Os shots de ostra com vodka e pimenta são imperdíveis!

This is one of my favorite Santa Barbara restaurants. Originally it was not on my radar because it didn't look as vegetarian-friendly as other spots I frequent in SB, but a few years ago we planned a large group dinner here for work and the whole thing was stellar.  So we have done a few more work dinners here since then, and I've also stopped in to eat dinner at the bar and had lunch here a few times with friends.  What do I like? For large group dinners, they have been extremely accommodating and worked well with the staff at my workplace to create a menu that is appropriate for dietary restrictions and also well-paced for a large group. The staff is terrific, the food is great, the wines are unusual for SB and excellent, and the atmosphere gives our out-of-town colleagues a nice intimate experience of Santa Barbara. They feel like they are seeing a special little corner of downtown SB that most tourists don't see.  But I also come back here on my own, and that's not so common for me - usually I have 'work restaurants' and then totally different restaurants for socializing. I come back here on my own time (and my own money) because the food is so thoughtfully prepared and delicious, the staff is terrific and helps me to select French wines that I have never tasted - a fun break from my usual California wine patterns, and the atmosphere really is intimate and unusual for SB. It feels like a grown-up restaurant without feeling stodgy.    And for the occasional midweek day off from work... long lunch with a couple glasses of wine and good conversation in a quiet corner, breezes through the windows that open onto La Arcada... This is my SB pick for the long conversation over lunch with wine, no contest.

Because I was basically a Thai food virgin when I first tried this place, I felt it was only fair to come back and edit my review now that I've tried other places.  This place is still pretty good, but nowhere near as good as this other place I love in Long Beach.  Their Green Curry isn't quite right, and also you should note that their service really really sucks.  The last two times I've come here, the servers disappear for 20 minutes at a time, never refilling your water and taking forever with the check.  Last time I came I tried one of the soups and I really enjoyed it.  One of the other girls wanted Pad Thai, and while I thought it was really good  the time before this, it was just kind of strange this time.  It was just kind of off.. can't really describe how.  And the Green Curry.. ugh what a disappointment after trying Green Curry elsewhere.  The sauce isn't quite right and I don't really like the green beans in it.  What you DO have to try is their Thai coconut ice cream.  The server claimed they are the only place that serves it and they get it straight from Thailand, but who knows if that's really true.  Either way, it was really good- kind of icy like home made ice cream with bits of coconut in it... and the flavor was just fantastic.

Really great 'white collar' tacos. This quickly became a weekly tradition, dragging myself out of bed in time to get to Corazon right when it opens (they run out!). I usually get 3 tacos --haven't had a ceviche tostada yet...they're gorgeous but pretty expensive for the size-- favorites have included the octopus, braised short rib, fried cauliflower... it's hard to choose, they're always amazing. with great homemade tortillas! The ever-changing menu is never disappointing. I really hope they open a stand-alone restaurant eventually.

I had all but forgotten about 'the Italian market' until we parked in front of it at its 'new' location a few weeks ago.  I would frequent it at its old location on the east side as a young single person when I worked for the City many years ago. We would wait in line at lunch for their amazing sandwiches.  My husband and I  went last week and ordered a couple of sandwiches to take to the beach for dinner. Unfortunately, the sandwiches were not as good as I remembered. Maybe it was the sandwich roll that was the difference, smaller and more commercial tasting, I am not sure. Maybe it needed more dressing as it was on the dry side. Whatever the difference, it was just ok. What a bummer.

JACKPOT! Excellent concept and execution! I feel as though I've stumbled upon a goldmine (of poke).  Excellent deal for high-quality poke and there's so many tasty options for creating your own poke bowl. I tried a combo of both ahi & salmon on a white rice base with lotsa different toppings incl spicy mayo and I was stoked! The bowl was HUGE; the price was not. The poke tasted fresh and did not have any fishy smell. Hallelujah!  I used to go to Lazy Acres to get poke bowls and I still love that place overall but they can't compete with Poke Ceviche when it comes to creating a badass bowl of your choice. I'll definitely be back but wanted to leave a review as a thank you!  P.S. store felt very clean and sanitary, and I had a friendly server (or poke-maker?)

As I stated previously the fish is super fresh & crisp here. It's a great spot for sushi to-go or at ANY time of day because they are open ALL DAY as opposed to all the other sushi restaurants in SB who close after 12:00pm.  They are reliable and consistent. The sushi chef is super friendly and I always feel cared about coming here.  --They got rid of the awful chick from CO (unfortunately I knew too much about her from overhearing her conversations about her personal life.)   She was evil and for no reason at all! (I tipped 20%) She gave me dirty looks the entire time I was eating) SHE'S GONE THOUGH! Thank God. Bc this place ROCKS.

I liked this place, they do their own beers which is cool and the outdoor seating is nice.  I got the buffalo wings, very good.  My cousin and friend both enjoyed their meals as well.  Not sure if I'm in a rush to go back but I certainly wouldn't mind it.

An entirely Americanized 'Middle Eastern' joint, Zaytoon is a pretty great place for friends to gather for some hookah and (what I call) Lebanese tapas.  My favorite combination: +The Vegetarian Feast (which includes hummus, baba ghannouj, falafel, cheese sambosik, labneh, and a tabbouli salad) +Mango Tango hookah +And a group of hilarious friends  Other pluses: +Their website's great +They cater +They're open to large parties +Reservations can be made online  Now, the food isn't MIND-BLOWING or anything, and the hookah is just... well, hookah, but for a night out with friends, it's a great option.

Eureka is a safe choice when looking to find a delicious burger. I got the cowboy burger and the shoestring onions that come on it make it unmatched. I also ordered the Mac and Cheese balls. They are a cool novelty item, but have nothing else going for them. Nevertheless, I will be coming back since the bone marrow burger sounds good!
