I owe my visit to the Garden because of my buddy dAnimal. He is passionate in about great locations and went enjoyed two good pints. Fun location and a lot of great location. One of my tells for a great place is to walk up the the waiter or bartender and ask what they would have this evening. The Garden gets 4 out of 5 because the bartender just could not make a recommendation for me.   I am fine selecting my own drinks, but it's always nice to be surprised by random selections.

I visit South Coast multiple times a week for lunch, and Richie and Eddie take such good care of our whole office! They have 5-Star Customer Service at all times and are FAST even during the busy lunch hour. I would highly recommend to anyone looking for a salad/sandwich. Definitely great size meals for the price too. Can't say enough good things about the food and the staff!

Service was really good which is why i gave it three stars , but this restaurant is not allergy friendly at all. Barely anything on this menu states garlic and we picked this restaurant based on that info, yet everything i chose including the lobster mac n cheese had a ton of garlic in it . Never ever i had lobster mac n cheese with garlic in it . The only item in the menu that i could eat  was the lobster roll which I ordered connecticut style (drawn butter) was tasty but it was disappointing . They also serve raw garlic fries and the table behind us ordered them so the horrible stench of raw garlic made it feel  like i was at a dodgers game . Probably won't return to this restaurant as the menu was deceiving and as hard as the server tried the kitchen just didn't cooperate and left me no choice but the lobster roll . I wanted the steamed clams. this is the only restaurant that said they would not make me an order minus the garlic. It wasn't busy either so not like i asked for a lot. just don't throw the garlic in the sauce and wasn't willing to accommodate  . Therefore I can't recommend this restaurant for persons with allergies no matter what it might be.

My friend and went to Java Station to study and get some work done in a good atmosphere and we were not disappointed. Lots of space both indoor and out with nice fresh air flow. Fast wifi and accessible outlets were also a plus. I ordered a matcha latte with oat milk and the cashier recommended I add vanilla. I am so glad I took their advice because it was a great new way to enjoy the matcha flavor. The cashiers were also very patient and gracious in helping my friend decide on a drink. They ended up with a pretty solid caramel latte with whole milk. My friend also ordered the carnivore melt with tri-tip. The portion sizes a pretty big, so we definitely could have shared. Unfortunately, I'm not a carnivore, so I didn't have a taste, though it looked and smelled delicious (my friend recommends for all the carnivores out there). 5 stars for customer service!

This was my first visit to Bacara...  I was in Santa Barbara on business and didn't have much of a chance to enjoy the resort. What little time I did have there, I maxmized however! Quick trips are made that much easier when you have a nice place to come home and rest your head.  My visit didn't start off on the best foot as I was left to wander the massive grounds in search of my room and you can imagine after a long day how frustrating that cold be. But the story gets better. A glass of wine on the rotunda, Kobe beef dinner and a Jameson and all was forgiven.  My room was beautifully appointed, the bed extremely welcoming and the staff meet all my expectations of a resort that caters to those with disposable income.  I will certainly be back and plan on enjoying more of the resort, time permitting...
Sunday HH all day!  Good wine list.  Food was delicious. Split a burger and a flatbread.  Jodi was a great server!  We will be back.

The aesthetics, food and service were nice; however the portions that were served were not commiserate with a fair price point in my opinion. There are way better places to eat in Santa Barbara, so in that regard, this place earns it's 2 star review by being cheapskates with its customers.

Awesome spot for lunch or dinner. Great selection of vegetarian and vegan dishes. Dishes have a good combination of flavors. Sweet savory spicy yes. Will return to try everything on menu. Be sure to order dessert because the baklava is bomb. You won't be disappointed eating here.

Hmm.  I have never had cupcakes from this company.  I have seen people at the outside tables (they do have a cafe).  And, I have been curious.  So, I bought a dozen assorted cupcakes to share at an art class.  My wife said that people had told her that the cupcakes were 'dry.'  To sum it up, everyone at the art class raved that we had brought crushcakes. I ate one cupcake.  It was dry.  Sure, the frosting is great, but the cake was dry and undesirable.    I may try the cafe, but I have no further interest in the cupcakes.

The food was only fair my lobster tail was burnt on the outside and not cooked allege way inside very disappointing considering the cost the tails I cook at home are far better   Don't think I'll be back

Unfortunately this place is a prime example of high-concept, high-shine hipster  haute cuisine that is more show than substance.  I've now tried several of their flavors.  They have a lot of 'innovative' flavors --- passion fruit and berry and whatnot --- and they're artfully designed with consummate swirls and just-so garnishes.  They look very fancy.  The problem is they aren't actually that good.  The dough has a strange pillowy texture and the donut is so tall that it can be (although this sounds weird) kind of annoying to chew.  It doesn't have any particularly great flavor either; it just tastes like slightly sugary dough.  All the flavor is in the icing.  And those flavors are pretty good, with a decent amount of punch.  But the icing layer is very thin so what you get is a huge mouthful of plain dough with a little dash of some exotic flavor.  The donuts aren't bad by any means.  They're okay.  The problem is they cost about $4 each and that is unreasonably high given their fair-to-middling taste.  About three blocks away is Spudnuts, which serves good old donuts.  Not farm-to-table or anything fancy, just, you know, extremely unhealthy fried dough, with a sizable layer of traditional icing like chocolate, vanilla, or maple, and some traditional toppings like peanuts, coconut, sprinkles, and so on.  Those donuts cost around $1.60 or so and are, at least for my tastes, actually better than Hook and Press.  Hook and Press would need to be at least twice as good as Spudnuts to justify their prices, and they are not really even equal.  I'm open to the idea of gourmet donuts.  I've been to the much-ballyhooed Voodoo Donuts.  Once in a while I might be willing to pay $4 for a donut if it's really gourmet.  These donuts, I'm afraid, are actually just a ripoff.  Your donut budget would be better used elsewhere.

We ate here last night and it was outstanding.  It's been 5+ years since I'd been here so I wanted to try it again.  Totally worth the trip.  Great wine list. We had the scallop carpaccio, the risotto and the filet.  All cooked perfectly and served with care. Well trained friendly staff.  Highly recommended.  Will definitely return.

I Used to order from this spot very regularly. Now they raise their prices and don't even bother to update their menus online. Which for a business that works with delivery is an issue. Bad business practice in my opinion. Won't be frequenting this spot anymore. It's clear this restaurants best days are clearly in the past. Such a shame!
Egg rolls are good- chicken chow mien tasted microwaved and burnt :( so sad. Ordered to go and to food was ready in less than 5 min- probably was microwaved.

One of the best sushi place in town! Has lots of varieties you can choose from and lots of different type of Japanese cuisines.  There ramen is something to look forward too and some of there unique rolls! There oysters are amazing as well! Staffs are always nice and fast. They are very responsive and try to get what you want! Nice cute interiors and cute place for a date. ;)

I love the ambiance of this place! I enjoy the fact that they offer indoor and patio seating. I'm obsessed with the tacos toro! It's two delicious peppers stuffed with melted cheese, shrimp, octopus and this amazing chipotle sauce! YUM! I was feeling indecisive and my friend suggested it. I'm glad I listened! I literally went the next day to have it again! Of course it pairs well with a lovely margarita! One thing to keep in mind is that parking is difficult to find because they do not have a designated parking lot. There is a parking structure down the way that you can use. Enjoy!

I've only been here once, but my wife and I were talking about coming back before we even got our drinks!  Service: Plenty of staff on a busy Wednesday night, our server Matthew was VERY knowledgeable on all aspects (former kitchen staff) from the seafood to the extensive wine list. A minor issue was quickly handled by Chase, the shift manager. All of the hostesses were courteous(and none of them on their phones!).  Food: Piping HOT bread and softened whipped butter set the bar for the rest of the impressive meal. The salad was fresh, and not overdressed. The fish cooked to order (M.rare) and dessert was simple, rich and delicious.  Ethics: Lure's 'thing' is all the food is sustainable and also a lot of it sourced locally. Really incredible.  Price: Amazing value for the quality and ethics. It's as reasonable as I could expect for all that we got. Two people can have a drink and ample food for under $100. You might swing happy hour for $50? I'm down to try!

Love coming here when we are in SB. Been coming here for 10+  years and it never disappoints. Great food, large rolls/portions. I've had the salmon and avocado roll, tuna  and avocado roll, CA roll, spicy tuna roll, yellowtail roll, and Tai-dye roll. The salads are good as is the veggie udon.   Prices are very responsible. The place is pretty small- 4 tables seating 4, and about 10 bar seating, so it can get busy there.   Also there are only a few pages of the menu available online for to-go orders.

Husband and I had a charming dinner when we stayed at the hotel. I decided to go for breakfast the next morning while my husband had surgery around the corner. Half of everything went wrong. My pancake was burnt. Where was the fresh fruit from the menu? So I asked for a new pancake and the fruit. I then got a strawberry pancake. Um no. So that one was replaced as well. The fresh fruit was another disappointment because the blueberries were rotten. I ate around them. An hour into this mess and the staff said they would offer half off. Then the manager came along and comped it and then offered to give me breakfast or lunch another time. So I took her up on it and came back for lunch. I asked for the steak sandwich and requested the steak medium rare. I got medium well. I had to ask for Italian dressing and salt and pepper which were nowhere near. And then when I mentioned that the steak was not how I requested, and even said I don't need you to replace it, she got snarky with me about how I ordered the most expensive thing on the menu and it was free so I didn't have any right to complain. Needless to say, I did not not leave her a tip because I was really offended at this point. I had thought I might stick around and order dessert and leave a large tip, but not when you talk to me like that. If someone comes in for a comped lunch, maybe it would be safe to assume that there was a problem and you should go out of your way so you can be rewarded for making it up to the customer. Lost opportunity.

I went here for the first time to try it out, and it was good except for the fact I ordered chia seeds, and boy it was it too much chia seeds. I had to scoop about 2 tea spoons of it. I ordered the large which was about $18, so be prepared to spend. I like the fact you have unlimited toppings included. The service was great! I was little lost at first and the cashier showed me how I go about ordering. I would def like to go back to try something else since my choices were simplify okay.

Great place for a quick breakfast.   Dog friendly outdoor seating. Food came out pretty quick after we ordered.  The Gaucho Bacon Sandwich Combo - $4.50 + $3 - Very good bacon egg breakfast sandwich. The combo was a waste, I needed the coffee but the hash brown was meh.   PJ Steak Burrito - $7.95 - Steak was very tender. You also get the normal breakfast burrito fillings. I never understand why they give you a small scoop of salsa with your big burrito. I had to go in and get more.   Overall, good quick meal.
Some of the best Tacos in Santa Barbara right now! They have lots of other great food too, but I'm on a taco kick

This review is for the brunch.  Now it's Sunday morning, and I want breakfast food and a bloody Mary, and the hockey game before we hit the art fair and the zoo.  So, short on time, we walk up State street and say we are hitting the first place that offers all of it.    I think we did well here. They had the hockey game already on on the big plasma and we scored a little table right in front of it.  I was surprised they had breakfast and didn't expect too much from a bar.    But what DID surprise me was more brekkie options than I expected and I found my Rio Grande Omelet with roasted corn and poblano peppers, jack cheese, and applewood bacon hitting the spot.  The cheddar jalapeno potatoes w/roasted peppers were good. Could have used more jalapeno.  You get a choice of corn tortillas or Hawaiian bread with your meal. My friend chose Hawaiian bread and it was grilled and buttered. Yum.  He had steak and eggs and said it was good. I always say you can't screw up breakfast, so what I liked is the choices, while not extensive, had a bit of a flair.   But what I really really liked, was the fact that they made champagne brunch.  For one hour, you get free Mimosas.  I know, only one hour??  Yea, I can drink a LOT in one hour. Especially if you are timing me.  But we didn't realize this little perk and had already ordered a Bloody Mary.  And What???A do-it-yourself bar Bloody Mary bar?  Oh, I was in heaven. Cuz I like my Bloodys my way. With a lot of olive juice and some garlic.  So, like Burger King, I got it my way, and had fun doing it.    If I had more room, I would have tried the coconut macadamia pancakes.  I enjoyed my 'brunch', my Bloodys, my champagn, and my hockey at this kitchy, Hawaiian themed bar.
Love this place! Just found this week and have gone twice. Their burritos are delish, shredded chicken is fresh and amazing! Well priced and well worth the trip!!!
Saw this place on Triple D and had to try... Really enjoyed the Cali Tuna Melt and Mom's PLT (as seen on TV). Good stuff!

Thanks for the food poisoning. Had an incredible stomach ache and nausea, I enjoyed my afternoon in the bathroom. Other than that, waited for a long while for the guy to finish flirting with 3 girls and then when he took another customer who just cut in when I was the only person waiting in line... Ordered the Paneer taco and been dip with chips. Taco was interesting but good. Bean dip just tasted like refried beans nothing special. Scared to go back since I know 100% that this is what got me so sick.

Just came here for lunch. Service is super slow. The server was not engaged and took forever to take our order, and it was not even busy. We had the NY steak sandwich which was pretty delicious, soft bread and sweet carmalized onions. Also had the chicken BLTA which was fairly bland actually. Great patio for lunch or drinks.

The restaurant facility itself may not look all that elegant and appealing but the service and the food really blows that away. They serve food for each meal of the day but I personally would recommend going for breakfast and trying one of their acai fruit bowls. Slightly on the pricier side but so worth it and you will be healthily full for hours to come! The culture here is also something that is unique and interesting. Great place for family brunch!

Average Italian restaurant with wayyy above average prices. The waitstaff is in attentive and the food is just ok. We had the duck ravioli and the chicken with pancetta. Neither dish stood out. Wish we would have just gone back to Bouchon next door.

I've been wanting to try this place for awhile, what time better than when I'm helping my sis move.   I love how though they are a restaurant with vegan and vegetarian options they don't try to shove it down your throat by only offering those options. They also have meat options for you carnivores out there! But given that I was at a vegan restaurant, I was down to try the vegan and the carnivorous options.   Bread. With Olive Oil and Balsamic Vinegar Dip. The bread was nommy. I liked how they had two different choices of bread, one white and one with poppy seeds and sesame seeds. The bread was fluffy, light and nommy. I liked how green the olive oil was too, what I learned in Italy, green EVOO is legit and bomb!  Raw Spinach Mushroom Lasagna. Chilled Zucchini Noodles with Marinated Mushrooms, Red Bell Peppers, Spinach, Raw Nut Ricotta, served with Raw Marinara, topped with Fresh Sprouts. This was soooo nommy!!!! THE best vegan dish I've ever had! The veggies were really fresh and it was delish! This is coming from a major carnivore too. It was so good! I loved the taste of all the ingredients and the textures of everything definitely made it a lasagna.   Herb-Crusted Rack of Lamb. With Grenache-Pomegranate Sauce, Wild Mushroom Melange and Potato-Spinach Croquette. This was well grilled and pretty damn good. And if ya know me, ya know I can make decent lamb chops. If it's good enough to impress me, it's pretty damn good! The flavors of the herbs on the lamb were delicious and it was grilled to medium-well perfection. I do normally prefer my lamb chops more on the rare or medium-rare side but they tasted so nommy I forgive the chef.   Tuscan Fusilli Brown Rice Pasta. With Zucchini, Squash, Leeks, Mushrooms, Cherry Tomatoes, Chicken, Parmesan Cheese served with Marinara Sauce. This was bomb! I don't usually eat rice, but I think this brown rice fusili might just be changing how I feel about rice. This was oh so very nommy! The marinara was delicious and all the other ingredients were fresh and yummy. You can order it with tofu instead of chicken and vegan mozzarella instead of parmesan to make it vegan.   French Lentil Walnut and Wild Mushroom Pate. Sautéed Mushrooms in Herbs and White Wine, puréed with Lentils and Walnuts, topped with Sprouts, drizzled with Balsamic Reduction, and served with Rice Crackers. This was so good! We actually got this on the house because all of our entrees came except for my sister's fusili pasta so our server let us choose any appetizer on the house and we got this one. This was a lot better than what I was expecting, it was really flavorful and delicious. You could definitely taste the flavor of the herbs and wine in the pate. I've never had such a yummy vegan dip before!   The service was surprisingly formal for a more casual establishment as this, but I enjoyed their service a lot. Our server was really nice and made sure we were taken care of. It was extremely nice of him to offer us the appetizer on the house because shortly after we put in the order for the appetizer the fusili came out.   Note: We came on a Sunday night and it might be a good idea to make reservations because we saw people get turned away. We only made them because we were making a long drive from the Bay and I'm so glad we did.   This was THE best vegan restaurant I've eaten at to date and I'd gladly come back next time I'm in town. Can't wait to try more of their menu!
The service has greatly improved recently. They have a different closing staff at night now, and they are very pleasant and efficient. And the sandwiches are delicious!

The regulars weren't there tonight as they didn't get my order right I thought, I would celebrate being able to drive again with my favorite burrito. I said a beef fajita burrito, no rice or cheese to go. She didn't hear me because they were in I! I ended up tossing it. I should've checked before I left.  I love Rudy's anyway just not tonight
We have only been there for the bar bites menu from 4-6 (I believe those are the hours). Great prices and very good service.

Revolver is a rare gem of a place. They make some of the best pizza I've ever had on the East coast or West coast. I'd say it's probably the best pizza in California, period. Yes it's artisanal but not detached.   Chef Nick clearly knows what he's doing. I recommend everything they make here. Even the Rossa even though I've never had it(I need cheese)but they're doing everything else right so I'd imagine it's amazing too!  Everything is a star here. The gold stars are definitely for the margarita pizza with roasted vegetables, it will change your life in the best way possible. I'll never look at vegetables on pizza the same way again.   Gold stars also for the Sicilian slices they they're amazing as well! What are you waiting for? Go get some amazing pizza right now!

Better than Renaud's or Helena Avenue Bakery.  In fact, Bree'osh is better than the bakeries I've just recently tried in the San Fran South Bay Area. Every time I get a kouign amann I think 'Welp, Bree'osh did it better'.   Quality food with quality technique.

What an awesome addition to State St! The pretzel and cheese are to die for! So so perfect! Margarita pizza was also really good! Pizza, pretzel, cold beer, awesome service! What more could I ask for!
I love this place !!! Great sushi !!! The gyoza chicken perfect !!! And the salomon fresh. !! The service good but the restaurant full all the time maybe because that...

Had a great time here! Loved the wait staff.  Ordered the exotic mushroom platter & the scallop trio for appetizers. They were both great!  I ordered the halibut, too dry for me. My mother ordered the duck, simply amazing! My uncle ordered the venison, not my thing, but was tasty!  Had a great time. The people that worked there were so nice, including the chef.
Mesa Burger's veggie patty is one of the best I've ever had. You can sub it onto any burger! Does anyone know what it's made of? I'd really like to know because it kind of seems like magic.

I probably give out too many five star reviews, I'll admit, but Los Arroyos has the most incredible pozole I've had in the states.  It comes with all the right garnishes: radishes, cilantro, really fresh avo, etc.  Anyone who knows Mexican cooking knows that pozole is all about the broth, and this is rich, deep, and complex.  The hominy is cooked to slightly crunchy perfection.  When I told the server how much I enjoyed it she gave me a smile that said 'I know'.

I can't quite get into this place. The space is nice and big, with a dining area separate from the bar, high ceiling, nice decor, service was great (possibly the best aspect) and everything tasted okay. Maybe it was the fact that although the food was good I know I can get better for a fraction of the price somewhere else that is equal or better in quality. Maybe it was the prices (in fact I'm almost sure of it). It's hard to leave a mexican food place after dropping $100 on two people and feel okay about it. We did get drinks that were $10 a piece so that didn't help. The good thing about that is that the ladies said the margaritas were strong so at least its worth your dollar. I guess. Apparently the happy hour is late here, starting at 9 pm I think, and that would seem to make more sense to go. Probably explains why the place was filling up with people starting their night out. Bottom line: try it but go for happy hour if you can, order a la carte to save a couple bucks, or at least just get tacos. FYI I got the chicken mole (one of my favorites) and it fell short - sauce was plentiful but it was spooned over the chicken instead of mixed together and it felt like I was dipping relatively bland chicken breast in a mole puddle. Chips were thin and dry too... might as well chew on broken glass. You know the type of chip I mean. I know you know.

Nice breakfast. Nice coffee. Overpriced but good cupcakes. For some reason, their black beans (for breakfast) are not hot. They must keep them warm in a slow cooker or something and so they are lukewarm, which is a bummer, because they'd be much better if they were hot!

You sit with your feet in the sand and cold beer in your hand. You're steps from the water. Sure their don't have gourmet food, but for a beach cafe this place can't be beat. One of the best I can say I've ever been too.   If you're in SB and want to enjoy something the locals do, pass on Sterns Wharf or the Marina and keep going down the road to Ledbetter Beach and Shoreline Cafe. It's totally worth it. Trust me!

I was so disappointed with my experience at Lucky Penny. The last time I had their pizza, I enjoyed it delivered over to Figeroa Mountain tasting room, so I wanted to give this place another try sitting at their space.  It all started when we placed our order. We were not greeted by the lady at the register.  I had to look her in the face and say 'hi,' we'd like to order so and so.   After you place your order, you'll have to get your own silverware and napkins. We couldn't find any seats so I walked in at The Lark and asked if we could sit inside since the restaurant was closed and we were told only the patio. All the way in the back, I found a table to sit at.   We get our food delivered by one lady. Two pizzas and a salad. Only one fork was given to us since we didn't know that we had to get our own silverware.  I had to dig in the under the sink to find a fork. The margarhita pizza was basically a wood-fired pizza with tomato sauce on it with blobs of mozzarella cheese and shredded basil on top. The 'Ode to Dan Russo' is their most popular pizza and it contained pork and fennel sausage, calabrian chile, smoked mozzarella and arugula. We both thought both pizzas were too salty.  You have get up and get your own water with their tiny little cups. With us sitting all the way on the end, it was such a hassle to get a water refill. When we were done, I wanted to get a cardboard pizza box for our left over pizza and I was asked if I wanted one of those paper to-go boxes you can find at the prepared foods section at Whole Foods. Lame. I had to cram my pizza in that tiny box. I'm not sure why we couldn't have a pizza box!  My dining companion was livid because on her way to the restroom, she saw a group of people seated inside The Lark!  She thought felt like we were discriminated against since we were the only Asians at the restaurant. I just don't understand why we were not allowed to sit at the Lark when they were closed and owned by the same people as Lucky Penny when others were allowed to!  There's no service at this place, so don't bother tipping! I'm glad we didn't!  I was even considering putting a tip in the jar after we ate if the service was great.    I'm crossing my visit to The Lark off my list since I don't want to deal with the non-existent service. For a restaurant considered one of the best in Santa Barbara, they need to better train their staff! Their food is not cheap (close to $40 for 2 pizzas and a salad), so if they want to charge the prices they are charging, it better come with a more welcoming staff and better service!

This was our last stop of my birthday evening and I even got a free icecream! The waitress noticed my birthday when she checked IDs and we were both shocked to see it arrive!  Before the icecream we got Baja sur fish tacos to split along with lots of fresh chips and salsa. To drink we got a huge frozen margarita and a raspberry mojito. Both food and drinks were good accompanied by timely and thoughtful service.

When I was going to college in SB, everyone was all about the Freebirds burritos. But anyone who anything about Mexican food knew that Cucas was the real burrito king.  Came by for lunch on a recent trip to SB and was surprised to find a new location. A few blocks away from DP, Cucas is now located where the old Korean joint Little Asia used to be. Sure the location is new, but the food is just as good. The breakfast burritos and the esteemed California burrito are the stuff of legends - many late drunken nights and early hungover mornings were graced with these fine species of burrito. Tried the shrimp burrito for the first time this trip and was impressed too!  The service, unfortunately, was mediocre. They ran out of horchata and pico de gallo when I was there, and the server was pretty slow. He was pretty young though, not like the older dudes who used to run the older location. I'm thinking it was an off day but, if not, then get it together, Cucas! Your Gauchos are counting on ya!
Amazing vegan selection.  Really fresh high quality food.  So glad I found this place.  I definitely will be back a ton more times!!!

You know, I could easily see myself becoming a permanent resident of this resort if given the opportunity. Such a beautiful property, relaxing atmosphere with several infinity pools on site. Body turned into mush from how relaxed I felt during our stay here.   Love their spa & the Eucalyptus steam room in specific. That whole section of the property with an adults-only pool also contains a relaxing Jacuzzi within the spa, redwood sauna, fireside lounge areas outside, wellness classes and a rooftop terrace.  I'm also big on hotel soaps & toiletries and was pleased to see they supplied us with Fresh brand products and Gilch​rist ​& Soam​es soaps which were made in England. The shampoo was so thick and luxurious, you know I had to ask for extras. ;)   If visiting the property be sure to checkout the new incredible Angel Oak Steakhouse with scenic ocean views. Executive Chef Vincent Lesage comes from a background of having worked at a 3 Michelin starred restaurant in France, food is therefore showcased according to high standard training. Additionally, they offer an exclusive wine collection with a cellar that houses the restaurant's 12,000-bottle collection.  Do yourselves a favor and take advantage of staying and dining on this exceptional property.

Back to square one!....... Let me start by saying ...we are still, fairly regular customers. Purely due to the cordial and polite staff in the front of the house. They do everything possible to ensure that the patrons/ guests have a good dining experience.  As for the back of the house!!!! A total disaster on all fronts.  Cold greasy fries ...potato and or sweet potato fries! Cold soggy fried wings....even though we have repeatedly over the past 3 or more months, ordered/request extra crispy wings tossed in 'heat hot' spicey sauce.  With a side of 'heated' extra sauce.....which everytime is forgotten and ends up coming straight from the fridge!  But that's not the worst...... the meat is no longer all white, but more often, grey to purplish and rubbery, in place of the moist fall of the bone high quality wings they once served!!  Such a pity it's gone to pot....or is that what the smoke in the kitchen is...lol.  We shall be contacting head office again!

This is always our go-to for a nice dinner and sitting right on the beach...nothing better! Last night I got the fish of the day grilled tacos with extra salsa and husband got the ahi grilled tacos. Both dishes were so good! The wine isn't great but my husband enjoyed his beer (no beers on tap) FYI They close a little earlier now,7:30pm. It might be just for Sunday? Make sure to call and ask

Let me first say that I probably go to Starbucks 3-4 times a week.  I usually order one if the refreshers and when they shake it and let it sit there is room at the top...I ask for them to top it off...I have gotten more ice and more water in the past, so now I say top it off with concentrate please. Some barristas are good with that while others it appears to be an inconvenience. Today at this Starbucks the gal put it a load of ice when my order stayed lite ice...I asked her for the lite ice and she reluctant took out the ice then added water. These drinks are over $4.... She started to walk away and I said oh and can I get a pupicinno...I caught her rolling her eyes!!!! Really!   She started with the pup and her canister ran out ... she looked around and said we are out of whipped cream right now. I walked away.   Not how I expect to be treated at Starbucks. Gotta rethink my coffee stop.

If your looking for a bagel in downtown this is the place to go. They have a wide variety of bagels to choose from and all the fixings from cream cheese to all the toppings to create you own sandwich or melt. The staff was extremely friendly and accommodating.

Left at Albuquerque moved about 2 blocks on State Street and somehow lost about two stars of its greatness. How did that even happen?! I used to love the old Left At Alburquerque. It had a fun outdoor area and usually didn't get too packed inside. The food was alright but the drinks were the main reason to come!  The newer location would get packed, the drinks didn't taste quite as good, and really, it was just claustrophobic! Oh well... why did they move?!

Taffy's is back!  I used to love coming here as a kid though most recently it seemed the quality had gone downhill in the past few years.  Not anymore!  My son and I ate here the other night and everything was perfect.  The pepperoni pizza was delicious, flawlessly baked with the cheese melted and pepperoni crispy yet not sliding off the dough.  The large chef's salad was packed with tons of fresh ham and turkey and complimented with the best ranch dressing this side of the Mississippi.  And the breadsticks...I don't think I've ever had breadsticks like this in my life, both soft and steamy which crispy and crunchy at the same time, served up with warm marinara and more of that delicious ranch dressing I was raving about.  We'll be back very soon!

Revolver is no doubt the best pizza place in Santa Barbara. Thin, perfect crust, all homemade sauces, the best ranch dressing ever to dip your crust. I love the pepperoni pizza the best. Also music is great and the vibe is very cool. Thanks Chef Ron!!!
Enjoying a celebration with La Presidenta Stephanie Petlow Old Spanish Days  Great Music food and drink at uptown.

I was struggling with whether or not to write this, but they're just not ready for prime time yet. So many things wrong:  1. terrible guest flow: do we enter through the dining room or the bar? no host/hostess stand so we don't know where to wait/stand for a table.   2. no real ambiance whatsoever. music was way too low to provide any background=everyone's talking in hushed tones so as not to be eavesdropped-on.  3. server was good. did his best despite the odds stacked against him.  4. two glasses of wine took 12 minutes to reach our table. bartender was watching baseball and not paying any attention to orders. also, their wine list, while affordable, sucks.   5. wife and i ordered a salad to share and two entrees. 35 minutes post-order the entrees arrived. no salad. we were not the only table this was happening to. chaos and confusion in the kitchen. the food was fine. not great, but fine.  basically, this place is nowhere near ready to host locals. it's not on state street so tourists won't be their regular market, so not being ready for locals is a death sentence. some advice:  1. provide a clear path of entry for guests and take that 'we're open' sign off the window--it's unsightly and looking at it from the inside is ugly.  2. get a host or hostess to greet guests as they arrive  3. get a real bartender who's interested in doing his/her job or get a second bartender.  4. when you're in the weeds in the kitchen, send FOH staff out to communicate and offer an app or drink or something as make-good. that will go a long way to engender goodwill among hungry guests.  5. pare-down the menu. there's simply no reason to have so many dishes and different preparations. you can't be good at all of them--focus on what you do well and offer guests choices but 200 menu items is way too much. a pared-down menu will also help you get food out of the kitchen faster, so you won' thave as many excuses to make.  6. a crappy wine list may fly in tarzana, but you're in wine country. do a better job with your list. nobody in SB wants to drink crappy napa cabs in an italian restaurant.  7. staff-up on weekends. our experience was not good largely due to poor staffing and long wait times for food.   we may give it another try in a couple of months because the wife enjoyed the flavor of the food, but if it's up to me, no-go.

Sorry to say that their social distancing and sanitation efforts are falling short. The food has never been good but the location brings us back. We have been twice during the COVID outbreak for to-go orders, hoping to support our local businesses. But our safety has to come first and they are not controlling the order/pick-up area. And they are using a public counter for all to-go orders with lots of people going in/out with no thought to spreading the virus. When we asked about their cleanliness, we got blasted. We know they must be struggling but getting rude when we asked if the pen they gave us to sign the credit card receipt had been sanitized was uncalled for. Hope they survive all of this.

Top notch sushi in Santa Barbara!! The three musketeers salad is one of the best sashimi salads I've had, kinda like yuko kitchen in LA. Their double white roll, don't cry roll and seared salmon belly will keep us coming back.

I love the food so much! The veggie kebab ate is the most affordable priced meal in town!!! I am taking off one start because the music in the patio is so loud you can't hear you partner speak! We had to move inside, which is crowded since people seem to not want to sit outside (maybe because of the club level blearing, horrible music??)

I was mistreated here by a older kind of crusty street type Mexican woman who worked there who definitely seemed like a racist. I ordered a quick lunch by phone and when I got there she hassled me about giving me a can drink instead of a fountain drink and then she wouldn't give me the three little plastic containers I asked for to put my salad dressing in. 'Two is all you can have' she said. Ugly racists. I'll never go back there
Our fantasy lunch, if only we'd had time is the CLASSIC Canjun fare, Red Beans and Rice. The label says nothing. Just it is oft THE BEST.  Next time we are there, Fo Sho!

These are some pretty darn good tacos! We got ours for take out. I was really impressed as we were kinda disappointed by the other Mexican joints we had visited while in Santa Barbara. I had the chicken tacos and I have to say 2 was WAY more than enough. It was a ton of meat and very filling. I will be back and definitely recommend this place over the other places we tried.

The food was great! The service was not very good though. Make sure you ask for the bread while your waiting for your food it is so good!. Definitely a place where you sit down and take your time. Can be a bit crowded.

I was going back through my old Yelp reviews and this was one I'm happy to say I was totally wrong about the first time.   I can't tell whether my palate has refined as I've gotten older or I was just trippin' the day I wrote this review, but either way... Backyard Bowls is the gold standard for acai bowls. In fact, every time I come to Santa Barbara it's a must do (in addition to McConnell's, of course). They've since opened a couple locations in LA but for some reason it just isn't the same as back in SB. Fruit's always fresh, portions are generous, and the acai is high quality.   Won't foresake you again, BB!

This is my second visit. First was for a quick wine and nibble, and now back for brunch. Easily our new favorite restaurant. Clean white modern space - not typical Santa Barbara which is fun and refreshing - would be a great place for creatives to sit and be inspired. Food is insane. Amazing flavors, lots of love in the details and home made touches. Lots to choose from on the menu and daily specials sprinkled in. Brunch prices $12-$18, need to go back because there's 4 other things I want to try.

Food ranges from inexpensive and traditional dishes like tacos, burgers, omelets, to seafood entrees which can be a bit more extensive. For the price of some of the dishes, I'm not blow away like I'd expect, but ignoring that very tasty.  Location is the definition of picturesque, right in the middle of the Hope Ranch area on one of the most scenic beaches in Santa Barbara. A favorite of mine to take visitors or impress a lady. Especially on that last one because it also doubles as a pretty well-stocked and lively, albeit pricey, bar.

Great spot for craft cocktails! Good ambiance and quietly tucked away on a side street at the end of the busy state street in downtown santa barbara. A group of us were spending the weekend in SB and this was our first drinks stop for the night and we couldn't have been happier. Every drink was uniquely crafted, refreshing and smooth. Ginspiration point is a must try - you won't be disappointed. We also got midnight in the garden, spicy cucumber margarita and the gimlet. There were quite a few other interesting drinks on the menu, will be back next time for more.

We held our company holiday party here in December 2019, and the event went so smoothly! Everyone had a great time, the food was amazing, and the staff had everything on lock, including arranging the buffet, clearing the tables for dancing, and keeping everyone with a drink in their hand. If you are considering a venue for a special event, I would highly recommend Eleven14! The new decor is fresh and vibrant, and the vibe is energizing.

Oh man. This was probably one of our biggest disappointments while staying in Santa Barbara. We asked our hotel for a brunch recommendation. This wasn't even as good as Denny's. It was that bad.   Great service though. Close to beach. Right by State Street.
It would be a crime to give them anything lower than 5 stars.  A staple of every trip to Santa Barbara.  Matt Cain, Matt Cain, bang bang.

Had been a while since I'd been here and was as good as the first time.  Got couple Victorias on tap, I got the seabass veracruz as a special which was amazing and I ate off the tables Molcajetes because everyone at my table ordered waaay too much food because we were all so excited to eat here. If you are in SB and gonna get mexican (and not just a burrito) this is the spot and easily one of best places in CA

I don't want to write bad reviews, but few cashiers were not happy, not friendly every morning. I know his/her name, because I have receipts with cashier's name on it. But I won't write it down. They don't even say thank you! Or have a nice day.  I usually go there Monday and Tuesday some Friday early morning. Other workers were always greeting and very helpful, knowledgeable. Food selection is always good and fresh flowers and vegetable, good quality of meat and fish.  Please do not give them shift, if they don't want to work. Otherwise I need go somewhere else.

The food was okay but everywhere you looked the tables were covered in dirty dishes  we had to buss our own table and from the time we ordered to the time we left we never saw someone buss a table of the FOH is dirty I don't even wanna see the kitchen

I am a vegetarian and twice I have ordered the veggie beet patty and received a real cow patty instead . I'm not sure why think it's okay to serve someone animal meat if they don't eat it  if they are out of a veggie patty they should make the customer aware and ask them want. I have since spoke to the manger who offered to make it right and I really appreciate that and look forward to enjoying this restaurant again after this mishap.

I'm definitely the wrong demographic for this Zone.  When I came of age, 'funk' was a 1960's African American musical style that still moves me.  This zone doesn't (least ways, in the same way).  This is millennial territory, with a series of hipster heavy wine bars and cutsie graffiti-splattered store fronts that wants to be Soho and Napa and Berkeley mashed together.    Interesting.  The wines were good and the small plates to go with a nice way to while away the afternoon.

It did not take long for us to be seated because not a lot of people were there. Our server Jamie did not come and check up on us like they do at other restaurants even though no one was really there. Just like in another review we had to keep waiting and waiting for anyone to check in on us and we had to ask for things while servers were passing by. The food was not that good and have eaten better Mexican food at other places. Since there was out door dining we had to sit outside under trees and the whole time there was bugs from the trees falling on to the table, ourselves and the food. I think business should be more aware and when placing tables outside or trying to do there best to help prevent stuff like this.

I was dying to go here (We'd been to the Petros in Solvang a while back and being an optimist, imagined it would be the same or better). On a recent visit to SB, we went in for dinner. First of all, the service was outstanding--warm and welcoming, eager and friendly, very caring and kind people, from the hostess to our server to the manager--everyone was so very sweet, it made up for our letdown with the food. To be fair, we live in the Bay Area with Kokkari in S.F. and Evvia in Palo Alto, and we'd had an unbelievable time at Petros Solvang--so we went in with expectations off the chart. Maybe this was the problem--it wasn't that their food was intrinsically bad; it was good. It just didn't sparkle, didn't blow our minds . . . the watermelon and feta salad was good, albeit not super alive with flavor. The grilled octopus was a good dose of protein, though with slightly muddled flavor (we were expecting it to be charred and tender), which is a tough call for octopus. While we were looking over the menu, we didn't see lamb listed, and were expressing disappointment to each other. Our server who had not yet come to our table, and was doing something else at the adjacent table, overheard us, and came over to reassure. He was just so sweet: going over not one or two, but three different lamb options. I mean, seriously, what kind of Greek restaurant doesn't have lamb, right? Petros had terrific lamb options: we had the lamb loin or was it the chops? They were good; again, not so memorable that I'd track down a postcard and write home about them. But they were good enough that we scarfed down everything :-) At some point during our dinner, someone spilled water everywhere near our table on the floor, and the manager was on top of it, asking someone to mop it up (before we tripped). We didn't stay for dessert, but I'd give anything to live near Petros, and I could go in for lunch anytime, we'd have a ball. Btw, the next day we were having dinner across the street at sunset, and Petros was the site for an outdoor wedding party/reception. We watched a gorgous and touching show--traffic stopped on the street while the bride and groom took photos, her white train trailing the street, veil blowing in the breeze . . . I damn near cried.

Great wood fire pizza. The location is small and intimate. Seating is all out door and is very limited. It's mostly bench table sitting. If you bring baby stroller make sure it's a small scroller. Good local beer !

Wow, over-rated much?  The service was good, the view was good, but the food was so-so.  I had the crab and avocado omelet which was tasty while I ate it but the taste stayed with me throughout the rest of the day no matter what else I ate.  It put me off crab for a long time and I LOVE crab.  Blech.

Very very good restaurant. The staff is soooo nice I love them!! This isn't a five star restaurant though, the food is really good but not amazing. Good vibes in the upstairs section but the downstairs lighting needs improvement. Great location and experience overall worth going!

Love the food!! Service questionable when it comes to a specific person he's extremely rude and has no hospitality skills what so ever. Hope someone finally says something to this guy because he seems to never improve. Server's name is Pedro and after working in other restaurants its shocking that he is still in the restaurant business. Probably the rudest server I've ever dealt with ever.

Other than serving one of the best burgers in town, the ahi tuna salad is as good as it gets for me. Fresh, hearty and healthy. The salad, was a nice alternative to the more decadent choices o. The menu.  For casual American style dining, a little something for all tastes.

Sucks here. Called an order in at 11:11am for a 'quick lunch' and was waiting patiently until about 11:45am to ask if they forgot about me. The cashier said that I have to understand that there was a big order before me in which I replied 'you have to understand I'm on my lunch and called my order in ahead of time'. I work at the hotel across the street and will definitely not be recommending this place to our guests. So at 12:04pm I took it upon myself to take the next pizza that came out so that's how that went. Also they have the worst customer service and pizza quality is garbage. I eat here mostly because it's cheap and easy. Always expect terrible service.
This place is great. Always great service and the food is pretty good too. We don't come here much but when we do, they always remember our dog, Bacon! Thank you.

Pleasantly surprised with the quality food and professional staff.  Need quick, quality food to go.  This restaurant is the place.  Salads, falafel wrap and chicken Santorini Bowl were enjoyable.  Get the carrot cake cookie for the kids.  They will love it.  Good job Luna Grill!

Bueno hoy como hoy aquí el lugar es súper bueno, el Omelet está bueno para pasar una resaca con los amigos, el unico inconveniente es lo que pasó después con el señor. Esperemos que todo esté bien con el.

A local favorite! Lure is my go-to spot for quality seafood. They have expanded their outdoor dining area to comply with health guidelines and it looks great!  We ordered various appetizers and were satisfied with pretty much all of them. As always, the ahi ceviche was excellent!

Love the atmosphere of this place!  The live outdoor music is cool and there is plenty of outdoor seating to enjoy it. The complimentary chips and salsa are good.  My friends and I gobbled up the corn and black bean salsa.  GET the HALIBUT CEVICHE if you want something healthy, light, but crazy ginormous portion that will make your eyeballs bulge out of your face when it arrives!!  It's literally a mountain of sweet, delicious halibut chunks mixed with onions, cilantro, and tomatoes on top of a crunchy torta.  Drizzle some Cholula hot sauce and it's a party!  Everyone in my party enjoyed their meals as well.  Service could be slightly better.  Our served rarely checked up on us and it took awhile to get the check.  Otherwise, I will be back in the future when I'm craving some Mexican food!

Great night gone rogue...  Let me start by saying food, brews, and ambiance are usually on point at this joint. Come here at least once a month after work with co-workers to let off some steam and chill over beers and brats.  Tonight- was a different night though.  We had Niko as our server and while it was a while before we saw him for our initial order, his lack of presence and attention only diminished through out the time we were there. Out of the six of us we found ourselves flagging anyone else we could (bussers, runners, a hostess) to get some refills as Niko was far too busy entertaining his friends behind the bar to check on us. When we asked to split the bill he told us '3 ways max' - which was fine- but then he told us: 'I don't keep track of what you order...' even though he used an iPad to place all our orders. We soon found ourselves dividing it up on our own to split the 3 ways and pay each other back. For all this we left an appropriate tip for the service (low) and as we walked out Niko took it to himself to sarcastically yell at us from the second floor patio that 'he really appreciated the tip' and 'would be sure to remember us next time...'  Maybe if you remembered us from past visits Niko or, in the least, gave us the same attention as your friends- you would've earned the gratuity.

First official meal in Santa Barbara and Esau's satisfied my every brunch craving. The place has a funky, laid-back style with California and Mexico inspired dishes. I tend to always go with an omelette no matter where I brunch and usually end up longingly eyeing whatever my date is eating. Not this time! I loved how flavorful and different my dish was - an omelette special with corn tortillas and delicious home fries. I couldn't get enough.  Added bonus?  Despite Esau's diner status, the coffee didn't suck!!  WIN.

YUM! This place serves up a wonderful lunch! I was so impressed with everything. The service was fantastic, the atmosphere was great and the food was delightful. We had the gyoza, which was crispy and good, the tempura, once again a great crunch. The sushi was fresh. I loved the mesa roll!!! All the flavors were on point!
Tasty ramen, but inconsistent preparation. Last order I placed was missing the egg from both bowls of ramen.

Totally unexpected goodness on the actual night of my birthday ... we had a long day out on the town, hiking, the mission, walking to the beach the length of state street, then seeing Malcolm Gladwell at the Arlington (wow) .... and decided we wanted somewhere quiet with a good bar - we were very late on Friday when most places were already closed.  We wound up going into this place because  a very nice lady at the end of State Street (divine inspiration gallery) gave us this business card as she pointed the newbie tourists down the right way onto State (at the very early part of our day) ....  The bartender was like an old friend ... very cool and unpretentious ... the waitress was equally comfortable and never made us feel rushed even though we closed the place down ..... the guys in the back who do dishes and clean the floors - everything behind the curtain, came out at the end to take care of all that needs attending to and they just struck me as salt of the earth kind of guys ... I made a point to give them each a personal tip .... THE PEOPLE of this place made my birthday really special.  I just felt the love and care and humanity - the main reason people go out to eat (besides not having to cook themselves).  The drinks were generous - exceptional - the flatbread with procuitto and arugula was amazing ... the arugula was the sweetest peppery-est, freshest I've ever had!  They brought it out with a red chile oil that we drizzled on it ... wow, great unexpected depth.  We also enjoyed a great warm spinach salad with crispy chicken on it, which could have been horrible, but it was really really good! and that was in addition to high end thick bacon.  We couldn't eat it all .... yet we stayed for a great multi-layer rich dark chocolate cake dessert complete with birthday candle ... and they made a fresh pot of decaf for me with a smile .... the whole experience just warmed my heart.    I can understand why people don't go in ... the decor is very guy-ish, kind of old school tacky even with christmas lights up year around and faux plant/ivy garland hanging from the ceiling .... this isn't an obvious stand out, but I urge you to go in and chat with the bartender .... let hipness go and just enjoy a genuine meal with genuine people who aren't jaded by years of serving tourists .....   Chase, we salut you!  Thank you again for a special birthday evening.
The absolute best apple fritter ever! Crispy-crunchy on the outside while light and fluffy on the inside. I don't know how you do it, but I am now an official Fritter Friday fan!
Classic Santa Barbara staple. Proper cocktails, Classic portions. Not fancy, just a great place!

When we were in Nepal several years ago, my husband, son and I fell in love with vegetable Momo's, little steamed, veggie filled dumplings served with a spicy lentil sauce. We live in Southern California, and have had a hard time finding these little darlings. Yet here they are on State St in Santa Barbara! Every time we've come up in the past few years, we head straight for the Momo's. Very common Nepalese food, but delicacies to us!!! Everything else we've tried at Himalayan Kitchen has been good too
Fun atmosphere and great food. Really enjoy there wings an excellent choice. Order the plum Collins and it was a fun twist on the Tom Collins

This restaurant has been sitting in my bookmarks for so long, and I'm so happy I finally drove the 86 miles to come here!   Chin and Jenn have been part of the community of Santa Barbara for a long time, and it's awesome that not only is there an amazing space for capoeira and other classes, the quality of food and service is great as well.   Their pao de queijo is big, hot and tasty (twss) I also love the yucca fries and the frango appetizers. If I lived in Santa Barbara, I would come here all the time.

The chicken tikka masala was delicious, especially the sauce.  I regret having not taken pictures to inspire Yelp readers to go check it out.  I will take pics next time, I promise.  The chai was great too.

We had our wedding reception at El Paseo on November 9, 2013 after getting married at the SB Courthouse. Wow, we could not have hoped for a better experience! Paul and Tyr were both great with planning everything- it's true, if you have their help you really don't need a wedding planner at all. We got a great deal on a lunchtime reception for 60 guests; we started planning our wedding in August and El Paseo worked wonderfully with our fast timeline! Our wedding was on the smaller side but even with only 60 people it didn't seem like the place was empty; this place can accommodate a LOT of people but also works well if your party isn't huge.   Our guests loved the outdoor space, which looked beautiful with our flowers and the architecture of this old building. We had a lot of family who had never been to California before and this was a really unique experience for them. My husband and I had the semi-dramatic entrance to music down the cute stairs and our photographer got some beautiful shots at the party. The dance floor was perfect and our friends were easily able to DJ our party with an ipod, Spotify, youtube, etc; they had everything set up for us and we brought our own devices. Everything we did was pretty DIY and I have to say I experienced zero stress on our wedding day. The cake, cupcakes, and flowers were all easily dropped off. Our guests were comfortable and happy even before we arrived. Food was absolutely delicious- Chef Alex is amazing. Our vegan guests were the most vocal about the food; a lot of friends said they had never had a vegan option at a wedding before that they absolutely loved. Appetizers were so good and they put each kind of appetizer on a special plate so my husband and I could also try them after taking pictures with our family. Mic worked great for speeches and announcements. Cake was cut perfectly after we got cut the first slice. Everything went so smoothly.  If you have your wedding reception here (especially off season.. November was great!) you will get an amazing deal. We had an open bar the entire time and every guest left happy. Even though we had the party from 11 am - 4 pm our guests danced a bunch and partied pretty hard. We didn't have to worry about dealing with cruddy rental companies or cleaning up everything afterwards. Tyr checked in on us the entire day.. I can't say enough good things about El Paseo so I will just stop now. :) You get the idea!

Dare I say I've found a new regular spot??  So I kind of tricked my sweetie into going here...he's not the most adventurous eater, and I've been wanting to try this place out for a while, so I did what I had to do. I actually expected him to be disappointed with the meal, but he ended up liking it even more than I did (and that's saying a lot, considering I was very happy).   It takes really good food and service for me to return to a restaurant, considering how many choices are out there--having said that, I will absolutely be returning!

Prosciutto and Brie sandwich with apricot jam... What?? So yummy!! Fast delivery through sbmenus.com to my office. Just loss of a star because the bread was a little greasy, and my coworkers turkey sandwiches they said could use a little more of the chipotle sauce. But other than those little things.. Awesome!

Lovely outdoor seating with ample umbrellas to block direst sun. Delicious Hollandaise sauce, good service, and mimosas.  On the harbor so obviously a great location.  Service was friendly and speedy, too.

This place is exceptional! Amazing food, vibes and service (Luis/Louis?).  When making a reservation ahead of time it is $25 per person. If you want a fire table top you need minimum of 5 people in your party I think.  Food here is just so good. Words cannot explain. Halloumi cheese, Baba ghanouj, cheese rolls, falafel, hummus, and tzatziki were all amazing! All amazing appetizers and mouth drooling.  Entrees we're so amazing I died. Lemon chicken is always a crowd favorite if you don't know what to order! The first time I had it a couple years back I literally licked the sauce off the plate (no joke).   Lamb kabobs are amazing and so are chicken kabobs. Honestly you won't be disappointed by anything.  With the crazy amount of appetizers we got, 4 entrees,  4 drinks and 5 gals was about $300 total so it wasn't that bad!

Hands down the most authentic Italian restaurant in town. When you walk in you feel like you are in a Roman Cafe. No where else in town will you walk in to a restaurant and hear the Italian language all around you. Via is the place where all the Italian implants in town come to feel at home.   The only reason they lost a star is b/c they are so authentic that they offer 'true' Italian service. Those of you who have had the pleasure of visiting Italy will understand what I mean. Italian's are very non-chalet.. so don't expect speedy service in this Italian Gem. Do expect incredible food and the best Espresso in town( Lavazzo, of course).  Absoutely the best Italian Restaurant in SB. How Pascucci get's the vote every year, is beyond me... and most other italians who have taste buds.  Grazie Mille!! Alla Prossima, Via!
Love it here...modern diner setting, simple menu, affordable, friendly service! Not crowded...ordered the clam chowder, on point!!! Perfect spot for a casual breakfast, lunch, and dinner.

Good quality, fast and friendly service. You can tell their ingredients are fresh and well prepared. If you're looking clean, healthy and tasty food on the go or for a fun meal with family and friends at the Public Market this is a very good choice.

We are eating here now, on a busy Sunday morning. It was packed, but we were seated within 10 minutes at a great window table. Had coffee in our mugs within seconds, ordered and had food on the table in less than 10 minutes, all the special requests done perfectly. They really know how to run a great restaurant efficiently. Will be coming back here for sure!   Update: 3/12/16 Came in today (Saturday) and the line was down the street it seemed. I planned to order to-go and was prepared to wait while I sipped coffee. I was greeted immediately, and able to order right away from the front. The young man was super knowledgeable about the endless sides and alternatives as he rattled them off by memory and experience. I said I just wanted to drink some coffee while I waited and he filled a cup for me down the bar while I rang up. Very thoughtful and I was well taken care of. I added to my order with no issue and only waited 10 minutes. Excellent service, again!

This was the best breakfast I've had in Santa Barbara!  The view was so lovely, the outside seating was comfortable, and the heat lamps were cozy.  Everything was delicious! The ice tea was fresh and unique tasting (sage lemon), and the latte was also yummy.  I had the eggs Benedict and loved it - my husband had the pancakes and they came with a cute bottle of warmed syrup.  We were super impressed and will definitely go back : )!

A couple of very good sandwiches the other day.  The bread was noticeably fresh and probably the best ciabatta I ever had.  I usually prefer french bread, which also looked good and fresh on my friends sandwich but I would get this again (Chipotle Turkey).  Flavors were great.  There was a very short wait for food and what seemed to be a manager came by a few times to see if everything was good. service was great too.

We went here for brunch. The location and outdoor seating area are great; that would be the reason to go here. However, the service was abysmal. We waited an hour for our egg dishes to arrive. That would be a long time for any meal, but eggs?!?! To be honest, the eggs were good, but I can't get over the rest of the experience. The entire time we were waiting, the server never acknowledged us, the long wait, or even offered us fresh drinks.   The iced coffees, by the way, were served in plastic cups with plastic straws, even though we were dining in...very wasteful/environmentally inconsiderate.   The hostess/manager? asked how our meal was and when we shared our wait time, they said 'I'll check and see what happened', but never returned or did anything about it.   Overall a very disappointing experience and we won't be going back. Plenty of better places for brunch in Santa Barbara!

This was not a restaurant this was an experience. Not only was our waiter Nicholas  warm, inviting, attentive, knowledgeable and accomodating... but the food snd drinks were delicious! The shrimp fried rice the salmon roll and the crab and shrimp stuffed Gyozas were flavorful fresh and came out immediately. To top it all of  f the owner, Tina, came out and talked to us. She was the most sweetest down to earth kind person and we were shocked to know she was the owner! if you are in Santa Barbara, this is the place to be!!!!

Very cute outdoor middle eastern restaurant with an awesome outdoor atmosphere with fire pit tables and greenery. The food is good. The taste is good but the meat wasn't as tender as some of the other places that you have been at. Nice place. Will def go again if in the area. Prices for the entrees are from $22-28.

Normally not a fan of 'fast food pizza' but this place tastes amazing and has great staff. My only complaint is that the onions seem to be a mixture of almost every vegetable by the end of the day - so a few random ingredients ending up in the pizza is really the only downfall - will be back soon!

A proper taco bar on state st. Food isn't the best price in town but the shrimp tacos are amaze balls. If you're looking for authentic mexican for a quick bite, this is your best bet in this part of town.

If you go to that other smoothie place, you are a chump.    My only complaint is that the standard size is kinda too big.  I get the strawberry colada with the soy junk instead of milk, because sometimes dairy gives me the creeps.  I also get the calcium supplement because both of my parents have osteoporosis and I don't need to be falling down and breaking my hips by the time I'm 30.

This place is horrible!!! I finish my food way too fast!!! And I have to come back again in the same week, to get something else! it blows away my wallet! Just kidding!!! Empty Bowl is AMAZE-BALLS!!!! Totally recommend!!! The BEST noodles, curry, duck buns. Pot stickers, oh no, I'm drooling again and I just had the khao soi MY FAVE  #nomnomnom A MUST VISIT! DOES NOT DISAPPOINT!!!!!! EVA!!  Must try their daily specials during the week.

First of all we showed up with no reservation. It is Mother's Day.  Who knew vegan was so popular.  They made us wait at the bar, one seat and the other standing until a couple left. Told us all tables were reserved but as we waited, we watched  the empty tables stay free.  The food was amazing but as we laughed and enjoyed our night, part of our laughter was at how none of the tables were filled. That is why you get 4 stars NOT 5. We can't wait to come back!!! No reservation really needed. Hahaha
Refer to other review   They stopped messing up my order. Thank god because this place is the BOMB!!

Excellent place! The owner is so kind and super attentive. I had the Santa Barbra scramble and my friend had the Veggie Scramble. Both were delicious. The home potatoes were great, not too oily like some place. The coffee was hot and SO GOOD! And my cup was constantly  being refilled which I appreciated. Good location too. Will come again if I'm ever in the area!

Okay guys, I know this is a chain, I know things slip through the cracks, there are off days, servers can have bad nights or they are so busy service isn't what you desire. But seriously? I love Eureka burger, sue me.   This is probably the only restaurant that I actually will order outside of my comfort zone, and you know what? I've never NOT liked anything. I was hooked on the cowboy burger with those delicious onion strings for the longest time. Then one day I decided I'm going to try something different and had the Fresno Fig Burger which may have been the best decision of my life. Who would put fig marmalade on a burger? Wtf is a fig? I honestly don't know the 100% answer to that, but Dayuuuuum it is tasty. Seriously. Give it a chance. Also, Brussel Sprouts. I was never a fan, my boyfriend thinks they are absolutely disgusting, but the Brussel sprouts here at Eureka will blow you away. We can't go to Eureka Burger without ordering the Brussel Sprouts, we just can't.   While those are my favorites, there's more. My bf alternates burgers, wether it's a bacon burger, burger with egg, burger with jalapeños, etc, and they are all good. Also, corn dog pops, bomb.com. Their riblets appetizer is killer. Margaritas, strong and tasty, usually limit myself to two or three and the boyfriend has to drive home. They also have HAPPY HOUR with cheaper drinks and appetizers. I can go on and on but I'll start to wrap this up.   Now for service, I've had amazing service and sub par service at this location. The sub par nights are usually when things are very busy or I can tell the server is new and still learning, which, you can't blame someone for that.   Overall, if you want a burger, a beer, some good mixed drinks, head over to Eureka.

The lamborghini sliders are by far the best sliders I have ever had. Even better than the Kobe need sliders I had at cut in Beverly Hills.   The  date with a pig I have been craving another bite of since I left last night. And as always, the sweet potato fries were perfect.

The first time I came, I only got a slice of cheesecake and it was soooo good. I was impressed enough I knew I had to come to try more things. My next time, I got a sandwich with some lemonade and it was really tasty. I finished the meal off with a slice of cheesecake and knew that this is somewhere I'd be more than happy to suggest to friends and to come back to myself.

I have to admit, I came in looking for an Italian deli with fresh Italian cookies and although I didn't see any cookies, I did see a couple of cannoli in the refrigerated section as well as some premade foods.  This is a small neighborhood market with assorted sandwiches and cold cuts.  There's an area with tables and chairs if one chooses to eat in rather than take your food to go.  And a counter where sandwiches are ordered. I wish they had had fresh cookies!  Good news is, free street parking is available.
Great restaurant, very slow service: 1:15 since we ordered, but no food yet.

I wanted to like this place, my in-laws had just grabbed lunch here about a week ago and they talked up the food and service so we were excited to come with them to celebrate an early birthday for my father-in-law. I can say that the service was amazing. Our waitress, Kathy, was attentive and friendly the whole time. That's who the star goes to. My husband got the chicken Parmesan dinner special, I got the filet mignon dinner, and my in-laws both got the 8 oz. top sirloin dinner. Both sirloin steaks were TOUGH and barely edible, we let the waitress know that the steaks were not good and she said she would let the manager know that we were unhappy with the steaks. The manager never came to the table to see what the problem was, or what could be done to make the situation better. Our waitress did come back and say they were going to take the steak (one) off the bill. When the bill came, they had done just that. There was just one steak removed from the bill, they left the baked potato and dinner salad on the bill (even though this was part of a meal), mind you, we didn't order things a la carte...it was supposed to be part of the meal. When my father-in-law went and spoke with the manager, the manager brushed him off and said that they 'took care of things' by taking off the one steak. In the end, it wasn't even about not charging us for the steak, it was about checking with customers to see what went wrong and seeing how it could be fixed. Maybe my review won't make a difference in the long run and maybe our experience was a one off, but it was enough to put us off our evening out. Never going there again.

This market is great -- so great in fact that we got lunch from here two days in a row.  Think Whole Foods but with honestly better prepared foods.  The kung pao tofu from the deli counter was delicious, as were the other items we tried - miso salmon, rice salads, vegan potato salad, sandwiches.  The market also has a great bulk food selection, olive bar, and lots of cheese samples.  Only thing I wasn't crazy about was the mango black iced tea from the coffee bar.
Friendly and welcoming service from Ally (spelling?!), delicious food and innovative and tasty drinks. Back patio looks like something out of a novel, warm and cozy. Highly recommended!

Wasn't my best experience here with the cashier. She may have been having an off day! I grew up eating Rusty's..so after visiting the nearby museum I took the kids in for some pizza. From what I remember growing up it hasn't changed. We got a pan cheese pizza, and salad bar. Pizza was delicious-salad and toppings were fresh.  There was plenty of parking on the lot-and there is a few arcade games for the children to play.
This place is so good!!! I got the steamed dumpling and the basil chicken and it is amazing. Good price too! Def recommended

Very good food.  We had both the seafood pancake and one grilled Bulgogi beef, and it was way too much food for 2 people.  They served lots of starters (like Kimchi), it looked pretty good. The seafood pancake was not really authentic, it didn't look or taste exactly like the pancakes I had in Korea (or even in good Korean restaurants in the US), but they were still delicious, and I will definitely have them again.  The grill was also great, they have on-the-table grilling, which is a lot of fun.  The meat was perfectly tender and well seasoned, very tasty.  The value is also very good, considering how large the portions are.  The only reason I don't give it 5 stars is because it's not as faithful to Korean flavors as other restaurants I have been to (in other cities, there is nothing even remotely as good in Santa Barbara).  The service was mixed, but positive. One of the waitresses was super friendly, nice and helpful, explaining what each item was and how things worked - really excellent.  The other 2 didn't seem to care much for the costumers, but they were still polite and quick.

This place is great. So this restaurant to me is located in a great downtown area. Their food is over the top. If you are ever in Santa Barbara I highly recommend this place. They have great omelets and very good French toast. They also serve many other things but it would take me forever to write down their menu because of all of the food to choose from!
Fun local vibrant and games too! This is a new place that's catching on with the locals and UCSB students. Go when theatres live music - great place for the game too

I have to try all types of cupcakes from everywhere... this place was alright. It was featured on Cupcake Wars - which I watch religiously. I love cupcakes!!!  I tried their signature red velvet...  Red Velvet: Good cake but the frosting was a bit too much. It had a lot of frosting.   Strawberry Blush: I liked the frosting and cake for Strawberry blush. I am big on strawberry. So it was really yummy!  I wish I had the Pancake, Bacon, Maple one... but it was a Saturday. :Sigh:  $3 a cupcake. I like the fact they use whipped cream and no fondant. I was shocked about the size of the cupcake. It seemed like the cream took a large portion of the cupcake.   SERVICE: Fine. Standard.  AMBIANCE: Cute little shop. Cafe is cool too.  PARKING: Parking lots - 75 min free or street (hard)  Cute Shop. Decent cupcakes.

Los Altos is always a go to for me because it never disappoints.  There are many restaurants in Santa Barbara that call themselves a 'Mexican' restaurant but the food is not authentic. Fortunately Los Altos is authentic so I recommend everyone to try it once and you won't be disappointed.  Los Altos quality of food is great, in good portions and tasty. They also have daily specials for example on Wednesday's they have $5 burritos, Thursdays they have $5 Tortas, Friday they have 3 sopes for $6.99!! Check out their Menu for the rest of the days.   I would recommend trying all their food although I would not recommend their seafood because that is not their specialty.  The staff is also awesome and very polite even if it gets busy.  Overall I would highly recommend you to give it a try

I went to Pickles & Swiss for the first time last week and it exceeded my expectations! I ordered the spicy crunch baguette, and the combination of the crispy chicken with the coleslaw and the bread was amazing! I would probably get extra sauce on it next time but overall a great sandwich!

I'll be honest, I'm not the biggest fan of Mediterranean food, so this review is biased and for anyone that feels the same way that I do.   I ordered their pistachio spread and it was alright. It tasted like straight-up sour cream to me with some added herbs and spices, but the pita bread that came with it was pretty fire. I also ordered their Malawi combo (chicken, beef, lamb kabob hummus, salad, and baba ganoush) or somethin like that and it had its pros and cons. Their beef kabob was excellent. Perfectly cooked, and squishy. Their chicken was pretty whatever, it was dry and tough. Their lamb was just odd, but I never really liked lamb, it wasn't enough to change my opinion at all. I'm also not a very big fan of hummus, but I'd definitely give a slightly positive rating for their hummus. It was unlike any other hummus I tried before. It was pretty soft, slightly citrus-y and kind of pleasant. Their salad was weird to me. It was straight up tomato and parsley. A very new and aggressive taste that I couldn't handle. Lastly, their baba ganoush (think eggplant hummus) was surprisingly pretty good. I never had baba ganoush ever so I didn't know what to expect, but I do know that I absolutely do not like eggplants.
