But the baba ganoush definitely surprised me because I was able to actually enjoy it.   So this review might make me sound like an absolute hater of foods, but this type of food is really not my thing so I am sorry. I had a wonderful experience here trying new things and going out of my comfort zone of foods, but the 3 stars is purely based off the food, not everything else the restaurant has to offer.

Big flavors. Lots of noodles bursting with flavors.  We had one of each dish and everything was fantastic. The curry noodles were creamy, spicy, delightful.  The lettuce cups with chicken larb was layered with spice, cilantro, and other tasty zingy things.  The hangover noodles were comforting. The papaya salad could have been brighter with more lime and spice, but well shredded with healthy amounts.  The pancit noodles were super fresh, a nice chew to them, with great flavor on the beef.  The scene is bustling downtown and they are busy busy busy.  We took our huge order to go and it all packed well.  Watch out for the wait time, when we ordered they said it would take 40 min and it was 40!  So plan in advance for the wait.  It's good, so worth it.

Good sandwiches, but I recommend checking on Yelp for the wait time if you're going to go to the store to order. This is particularly relevant if you go during lunch hour and you gotta get back to work quick.

Love this place. Brings back memories of when I was a child. This location in particular has really improved since I've been going. The last time I went the service was really good and the food was on point. They also are open a little later for us working professionals looking for a quick substantial meal Side note: they gave everyone a free bowl

Came and tried this place because they had an axxess deal. I loved their tacos. We tried the al pastor tacos and their Jamaica.   From all the Mexican dipping chips i've tried they make the best ones. It is probably because they seem to make them fresh.  Looking forward to my next visit so I can try other items on their menu.   Oh, if there's a large line and you already started eating they can help you out by waving them down.

Always delicious! The noodle soup is my go-to was when I feel a cold coming on: amazing broth with garlic, ginger & plus protein, bean sprouts, cilantro, jalapeño & lime topping options- not too spicy, just right!

It was okay. The ambiance is the winner here nothing like lounging on a big outdoor couch with a fire pit going.   Their wines were tasty but it tasted the same. There was no stand out. The owner was really REALLY nice and very knowledgable with aging and cellar stocking.  I wish they had a small cheese plate so groups can enjoy the whole experience.

We came here for dinner and had a phenomenal culinary experience! We reserved a table outside on their spacious patio and once we sat down our sever brought over the most delicious tasting popcorn. It had a wonderful spice to it and was very hard not to devour the entire thing in two seconds. We did have to ask to be moved to a different table outside as the one we were originally seated at was surrounded by these little birds that kept flying by our head and trying to get close to our popcorn. It was just a little annoying and I wanted to make sure we enjoyed our dinner, which we ended up doing. They like to serve everything family style so you order all items at once and they come out all together. We ended up ordering three items for the two of us to share...we went with the Whipped Sheep's Feta & Pistachio Dip, Crispy Halloumi & Butternut Squash, and the Grilled Prime Hanger Steak. Everything had phenomenal flavor and the hanger steak came with these blistered shishitos peppers which were prepared to perfection! The three courses were a perfect amount of food for the two of us. The service started out really good as the restaurant wasn't too crowded but once the place started to fill in, the attentiveness faded off a little. Wasn't too big of a deal but was just something we noticed. Overall we thoroughly enjoyed our dining experience and cannot wait to come back again!!

This place is right in downtown Santa Barbara and easy to walk across the shopping Street and come here for lunch. Food was delicious and there was no waiting. They also have buffett times which we missed. Sometimes they will give you the food from buffet station (like yogurt, etc). So be careful about that not happening with you.

Consistently friendly & helpful customer service and GREAT breads and pastries. I'm a longtime customer and my favorites are the hygge buns, shiitake shallot loaf,farmer market special, and the sourdough focaccia. But honestly everything is amazing!!

We loved this place the first time 3 years ago and we have been coming back every time we come to SB. Calamari are super fresh, tacos are delicious. Service is good and friendly and the location... you literally on the beach. To make the wait easier, we set up our towels On the beach, go for a swim while we wait for our table.
Do not order or eat at this location food is always inconsistent and not what I ordered ???????no disrespect to Los Agaves owner but this location is not up to par whatsoever

We are SB locals, and we had the best date night here. Starting with our server Andrew, this young man knows what he is doing and has a great attitude (not to mention a former Don). His manager Kevin, came by a few times and checked in on us, made us feel welcomed. We had appetizers all night and everything was A++, tasted great and fresh. Drinks +++. We will be back.

The service was fabulous. Drea was the best. We had cocktails with blue cheese olives and scallops with caviar and the best view in Santa Barbara! This is a must. Seafood and drinks .... this is the place to go.

OMG OMG OMG I'm going back here this weekend! I DREAM about the Venison Sausage Burger at this place! It really doesn't get any better than Cold Spring Tavern. You have a beautiful scenic drive that leads up to the place, and it feels like you've just came across a hidden little gem of a woodsy, comforting restaurant in the forest. Which, by the way, you have. The service is great, and the waitresses are extremely friendly and helpful. I love the warm ambiance, which is often in stark contrast to the foggy, often cold surroundings. It's a wonderful place to go with friends, feel warm, and enjoy amazing chili or burgers with wild game.   I highly recommend the venison burger or the chili sampler. Great place to take a date if you're looking to impress in Santa Barbara!

I don't get it. BBQ was invented as a poor mans food. So why is it so expensive everywhere. Well this is not the exception. I came here and for me and my girl to get two dinners (a sandwich and a half rack of ribs) it costs us $35. This could have been excused, because as i mentioned it seems like the BBQ standard. BUT!!! the food was not that great. It wasn't $20 for the two of us great, but it defiantly wasn't worth $35.  Then the place it's self was kinda dirty. They have it so you pick up your tray and get your drink and extra BBQ sauce, napkins, and so on. All of the stations were dirty. The BBQ sauce was in huge buckets that you ladle into your little ramekin. The BBQ sauce was crusty and not even that good. The Ketchup was in one of those that you pump into a ramekin and it was old (tasted like vinegar)  If you can't guess by now. I say no. I will not go back here and if someone asked me about BBQ i would recommend going to McDonalds for a McRibb sandwich before going here. (and yes, i do think the McRibb is disgustiing)

Was here 2 weeks ago and the Crudo Bar is still a gem.  The mixologist knows his trade and about wine as well.  He was the same mixologist that served us for my first review of this place.  We tried a variety of different small plates (about 10 total) and all were fantastic.  We will be back again.  Two thumbs up!

We were in town and had heard great things about this place. As soon as we entered we were greeted with friendly smiles and immediately shown to a table. The menu has a variety of options, and even a few things for those who don't particularly like fish. I ordered the beet salad to start and it was wonderful. The dressing was bright and flavorful, and the addition of mint was totally unexpected. My main course was the Shrimp Stir Fry. The ship were delicate, juicy, and cooked perfectly. The veggies were normal stir-fry vegetables. My husband had the Fisherman's Stew and loved it. The description said a 'Ciopinno' sauce but this was much thicker. Nonetheless, he loved it.

Holy Batman alert the Sodium Police!  My blood pressure is going up even thinking about their food.  First of all, I had low expectations since this was clearly a 'white' Chinese restaurant.  So I'm not expecting XLB or congee here...  So we ordered some safe white foods: Sizzling Rice Soup and Crispy Prawns.  When the soup came out I immediately gagged on the spoonful of what tasted like Morton Salt Soup.  I tried another...then asked the waiter for a glass of hot water.  When he turned around I dumped almost all 12 ounces into the soup and stirred.  Even with that alteration, I'm pretty sure I've had Cup O' Noodles that were less salty.  I'm no health nut, but eat here at your own peril.

Love love love...  I've been to all of their locations.  The Scotty's is my fave at the moment.. tastes like mint chip ice cream.  They have awesome avo toast and cashew cheese toast, on local gluten free bread, that I used to order all of the time.  I've been on some pretty strict elimination diets in the last several months, so I haven't been ordering the toast or the beloved nitro coffee that I'm head over hills for.  Also can't have citrus, so that rules out most of their fresh organic juice.  As far as the smoothies go, they have been super accommodating when I've switched a few ingredients. I totally know that can be annoying when customers modify the well thought out ingredients in an item, but I'm grateful they haven't taken issue with it.  They have other goodies and supplements for purchase as well.  Thanks for being in this area, and keeping it healthy and organic.

I've been going to Super Rica since I was a little girl. Definitely a locals favorite. The line down the block is 100% WORTH THE WAIT.  Order a few things off the menu, get guacamole, a side of rice, queso de cazuela, and some tacos and you'll be happy. Sometimes the tamales are on special, make sure you get one of those.  This is not Sonora, Jalisco or Oxhacan style, No massive burritos, no crispy taco shells or yellow cheese, no baskets of chips and no huge plates of rice and beans. This is Mexico City style food, as good as you can get it.
Great food, great service! :)  We had the  Fish and chips, chowder, chicken and mushroom pie, haggis and deep fried twinkies and mars bars...all FANTASTIC!

Food was good but not warm. My French toast was cold for some reason. Cute court yard setting but you can hear 3 different musics going on from the restaurant itself and from two other restaurants next to them. It's quite pricey when breakfast items don't service with any side but maybe because they use quality ingredients.

Service can be a little slow but that's okay because you're sitting outside, on the beach. Great place to people-watch. The thing we always get here are the banana pancakes (wheat germ) which are pretty good, portions are large but then again, pancakes are pretty heavy.   Order inside, find a seat and food is brought to you.   Nice relaxing place to go with decent food.

DON'T LET THIS REVIEW SCARE YOU AWAY.  I love this coffee shop! Before I start with the bad, I will focus on the good. The service is always great, the people who work there are awesome and super friendly.  So, you are probably wondering why I'm giving it a two star rating. It is because of their somewhat recent change in pastries. The coffee is still good and I always pick up an iced coffee when I'm getting a fruit bowl next door, but their new pastries are awful. The fan favorite (or my favorite) was the cornbread raspberry muffins, but ever since they switched suppliers they have gone downhill. I probably sound like a pretentious prude, but I really want to help. I hope the owner or manager reads this and I hope I'm not the only one that feels this way. Keep in mind, of the new pastries, I have only tried the scones, muffins, and croissant a few times. Also, their vegan donuts are not too bad, but the muffins and scones are extremely bland and by bland I mean they taste like flour. I hope they consider changing back to the old supplier of pastries or look elsewhere for tastier options.  If you are looking for a good iced coffee to help you study or get going this is your place, but steer clear of the pastries until they improve them.

The best meal that we have had in Santa Barbara in a while. We started with the sardines, and then the roasted beet salad, which was excellent. Then the prawns which my wife devoured most of, and then the entrees, I had the beef cheeks which were as tender as they could be. My wife had the divers scallops, which she said were great, I am allergic to scallops so I didn't get to taste them. We finished it off with the blueberry lemon torte.  After the meal we talked about all the different tastes that we were experiencing. Highly recommend this place  Here we are during the Pandemic, anniversary day, and we were supposed to be in Portugal. Dine in not available at least we can get to go, we called an ordered from here and picked up at our designated time. Chefs salad, roasted beets, baby back ribs and duck confit. Took it to the beach along with a bottle of wine and enjoyed every bite. We love this restaurant and there meals are prepared so well. Five stars!!!
Great mahi mahi sandwich, prime rib sandwich, salads, drinks, and the free bread and salsa combo is fantastic!

I went here for my birthday because I had heard that it was an amazing Italian restaurant! I was pleasantly surprised by the food because it was AMAZING but the customer service was absolutely terrible and it took 45 minutes for the food to come out. Also, the restaurant is fairly small and the tables are very close together, so it's really hard to talk to people and hear what they're saying. Overall, food was amazing but the rest...not so much.
I have yet to have something I don't love. Their salmon salad and eggplant salad are amazing and their drunken noodles with tofu are my favorite. If there's a line it's worth the wait.

Update to my last review. We returned to Max's last time we were in Santa Barbara. Everything was great but not as delicious as last 2 times. I don't know if it was a different chef that day, or the chef had changed, but I didn't love it. I feel bad because I really wanted to love it. But it was still good, just not AMAZING as before.

They reduce bacon infused chicken broth for hours for the super savory gravy that is the star of the YOLO... A delicious biscuit topped with fried chicken that floats atop a generous bowlful of gravy.  Oh, and don't forget to add a poached egg or two!  Other honorable mentions:  Just the Tip - tri tip steak sandwich with caramelized onions and a horseradish sauce Jean Claude Van Hamme - ham, Brie and honey goodness! Oatmeal - not comparable to the amazing chef creations above, but very well made for just being oatmeal.  On top of all that, this place takes Axxess (buy one get one free)!

Came in for a late lunch after the buffet was over, ordered chicken tikka masala and vegetable momos. The momos were freshly made and tasty, however the chicken tikka masala was too blend and creamy, not so impressive. The naan served was good, but the curry was somewhat disappointing.

Paloma is now serving Brunch! We've loved the dinner menu and were very excited to see what the chef did with his modern take on 'Californio' breakfast.   Takes on classic Mexican dishes, with creative and flavorful surprises. We started with the baked brie garnished w mezcal pickled kumquats. Inspired by Julia Child, who loved this location, we thought it was playful and delicious. The farmers market fruit plate was clearly the finest and freshest from the day and we loved the savory 'chicken and waffle' as an app to share.   Huevos, burnt ends bowl and pasole with a mezcal sidecar were all resounding successes, very impressive and beautifully presented.   Our server, Bobby was professional, gracious and a pleasure to speak to. Clearly invested and proud of the food. All the brunch cocktails were fresh and packed a punch.   There isn't a space in SB that I can think of with as much charm and 'neighborhoody' appeal. Tucked off state and surrounded by beautiful Spanish buildings, we will absolutely be back again and again.

This was our first time to try the Goat Tree.  The name was cute so thought we'd try it.  A friend and I went for breakfast and used our AXXESS card which gave us a buy one get one free.  I had the chicken and waffles dish which was ok - but the waffle wasn't even hot enough to melt the butter.  My friend ordered the egg  frittata.  AgaIn just ok.  We both ordered Americana coffees and were being surprised when we got our bill to find a 'refill' cost the same as the first cup ($4.50).  Can get much better coffee at Starbucks for less.  Not likely to go back.

My first review in a long, long time ... and my first meal out in a long time as well, inspired by my hubby's need to get out after 5 months hunkering down ...  Spent the day in beautiful Santa Barbara. Came across OKU as we walked along the beach front. With seating outside on the patio and safety protocols in place, we decided to give it a try.  I won't bore you with pandemic details (as we've all had much of that), but I will let you know about the food. FANTASTIC. FRESH. MOUTH-WATERING.   Hamachi. Amaebi. Uni. Scallops. These were my choices. Rockshrimp. Softshell crab. Those were his.  Notice a pattern?  Cocktails were scrumptious. Traditional cocktails with a Japanese or Asian twist. YUM.   Service was pleasant. Felt as safe as possible, given these challenging times ... highly recommend for delicious food that represents the best of Santa Barbara and that you cannot create at home ... a TREAT.

Luckily the food was really good, otherwise it would have been a truly crap experience. Service was pretty bad. Considering how many 'awards' this place has 'won' or how many recognitions it has achieved, from a service standpoint...I didn't experience any of it. Resting on ones laurels, is truly dangerous. 1. We were late being sat for our reservation time (I get it, it happens). 2. Hostess, walked us to our table and barely even made eye contact...threw our menus down on the table and say 'enjoy' as she walked away with her back to us. 3. Waiter took some time to get to us and was truly unimpressive....no charm, no character, completely boring. 3. First round of drinks took 20 minutes (one beer and one prosecco) with no apology upon delivery (in all fairness, 2nd round was quite quick, but delivered by the manager). 4. Food was delivered on time and it was very good. 5. I booked through Yelp and stated in comments that it was a birthday dinner and I requested tiramisu with a candle....this did not happen. I wanted to request it, but I could not get a private moment with the waiter to remind him. Bill was full amount, paid for all drinks, and the dessert (not that I mind paying for items I ordered, it's just proof that they didn't care or acknowledge our slow drinks or the birthday request.  So, all in all, the food was really good, but the service would make me not return or recommend to anyone. This was not an Italian experience, nor was it an experience worth repeating.

Definitely a dive and barely and tables for you to dine on but once you get in line and order your food you shall have a table when your food is ready. i had the carnitas burrito with cabbage and it was great. as i heard the carnitas was great here i had to try it i must say if u get the middle parts of the carnitas its nice and tender which i loved but the tips were burnt. but burrito was great. salsa and the salsas i had were perfectly made i believe in house. and the staff super friendly anything u need u got.

THE BEST FOOD. Haven't been here since pre-pandemic. But the  blackened fish on the menu is still incredible. The best fish I've had, ever. It's somehow even better every single time. I wish I had more space to try the specials (like the ox tail soup!). The chef accommodated my partner's onion intolerance, and we really appreciated it. The waiter--he was the only one as it was a nice quiet evening--was wonderful. They also gave us some free wine, which was really nice! I really like the chef--thank you, Chef Harold for your amazing dishes and kindness. Embermill is a must try if you're visiting or just live in SB. The outdoor patio and the indoor space (with open windows) are really beautiful.

This place is new since I was last in SB, and Good Lord, what a find.  My 2 girlfriends and I kept saying, 'Oh my, try mine?'  Best new food I've had in a long time!!  I had the chilaquiles, and don't remember what my friends ordered, but all our dishes were fabulous; tasty and creative.  AND THEN, there was this tequila infused strawberry cocktail...which is when I fainted...jk!!  Servers were super nice too.  Well Done Shop Kitchen!!

I've never been a huge fan of New Jersey, but if it smelled half as good as this place, I would have reconsidered taking Chris Christie out of my Myspace Top 8. While I find the name a bit hyperbolic, the Super Sub is the 'hero' I'd call to defeat the villainy of Subway. It's the kind of sandwich that makes you wish you could thank John Montagu himself for craving his 18th century midnight snack.

Reasonable prices, delicious food that is also healthy, right on lower State.  I've been a happy Natural Cafe customer for over a decade.  One of the nicest meals for the money in Santa Barbara.  This location is a bit more 'trendy' and loud than the Hitchcock and Goleta locations, but the people are nice and the food is delicious.

Ugh, I love Brasil Arts Cafe. I've been coming here semi-regularly for the past three years because I have dreams about their coxinhas. My regular is the P.F. with medium rare steak, always cooked to perfection. Mix the beans with rice and the yucca root thing, soooo good. My friends and I also always get a pao de quiejo and coxinha. Ask for the chili sauce - it's vinegary and delicious!! I've tried a few of their juices as well, all of which are good. The staff is always friendly and the location is cute.

Kyles kitchen is probably the least guilty burger I love to eat! You can taste fresh ingredients, and there has never been a time where customer service lacked its standards. All of the employees treat you like their care about your order and the quality of their work! You can't go wrong with their food. If you not in the mood for a burger their salads are also filling and delicious.

A little annoyed at their hours. They are so inconsistent it's like they are open whenever they feel like it. They have a business hours sign clearly displayed on their door but you will arrive during normal business hours and they will be closed with no other sign giving explanation. I usually take orders for lunch at the office or my husband who works down the street, so it's irritating that they don't stick to their schedule. At this point it's easier to assume they are closed. Lost a customer.
Best brunch in the area! Friendly service (Tim is the best), delicious and high-quality food, and a beautiful courtyard seating area.

Great breakfast in the correct price range. The huevos rancheros was fantastic, and the over easy eggs were done perfectly which is really hard to find. Usually over easy eggs are undercooked in the middle of the whites but they nailed it here! Also, the espresso drinks were impressively good and the service was friendly and attentive.  Will be back for sure.

Mesa Burger is definitely one of my favorite burger places in Santa Barbara! I found this place through Yelp previously and was encouraged to go by our local Yelp Elite community ambassador, so shout outs to her!  The parking lot can be fairly tight and difficult to get out of when crowded. When you walk in to Mesa Burger, I thought the interior design was great. I like the big open view outside, how you can see the burgers being prepared (similar to In-N-Out when going through drive through window), and the employees were very helpful with suggestions.  I've gotten the Mesa burger several times, and it's very yummy. I love how Mesa Burger use brioche buns, which is one of my favorite type of burger bun. Also the patty is very yummy and cooked perfectly in my opinion. I always upgrade my fries to truffle fries, since I'm a big fan of truffle fries. My gf normally gets the Hope Ranch burger and thinks it's very yummy. She normally gets onion rings as a side and says they're good. Mesa Burger also has sweet potato waffle fries that are very good, and one of my friends thought they were better than the normal fries! So I'd recommend trying Mesa Burger's sweet potato waffle fries, if you're into that type of fry. The portion size of the burger is fairly good, not very big that it's difficult to put in your mouth, but not small. The presentation of the burgers are very nice as well, so it's great for a photo! The only downside is some of the other burgers other than the Mesa Burger can get fairly pricey. If you have a Santa Barbara Axxess card, there are great buy one, get one free deals. So that's always a plus for Axxess card users!   Overall, I think Mesa Burger is a great place to try if you're ever craving a nice gourmet-ish burger! The ambiance is nice, the food presentation is good, and the food is definitely yummy!
I love your pizza!   Is there a chance you guys will ever be available on DoorDash???
Okay who is the fat pile of cow dung that complained and made my large fries into small fries? I am a really skinny guy with a very fast metabolism I need those extra calories jerks

The best food in Santa Barbara.  This place had a unique and interesting menu.  Everything on there looks good and so far it has not disappointed when I do order!  The only thing is, I wish they were opened more.
Loved this place. Salads, sandwiches and all were great. Spicy margarita opened up my arteries, may live longer. Great views. Fred Astaire danced on our table. That never happens in Texas.

Friendly staff, and great location. I ordered the sea bass and it was alright. The asparagus was undercooked and the potatoes were not good. My friends ordered the fish and chips and the fisherman's platter and they said it was good and a nice portion.
Ultimate Chirashi came with only 6 pieces of fish for $34 complete rip off. Had the lobster roll and wasn't impressed. Very disappointed and mad that I tried this place vs my usual spot.

Only came here for the buffet.  The service was nice, however it didn't make up too much for the food.  The Naan was a bit stale, and the meat selection and flavor wasn't the best.  I was surprised, by the vegetables, as they were cooked well and had good flavor as well as spice.

I promise the aesthetics/location do not reflect on the quality and food at all. Yes, I understand this place is situated in batting cages, but let me tell you.... this place is IT. We waited in line for 30-45 minutes and it was SO worth it. I ordered two Ahi Tunas and Shrimp and my partner ordered the Batter Up, Gangnam Style & Ahi Tuna. We both agreed that the Ahi Tuna was the one. The Ahi Tuna is raw ahi tuna, Napa cabbage, marinated red onion, red pepper, sesame seeds, wasabi aioli, wonton skin - SO GOOD. We also got a side of guacamole and chips, which they make in house.
Alright I don't have a lot of energy today so I'm gonna keep it real simple. This place is good. Nice cool & friendly atmosphere on a blazin hot day.
overpriced. zero customer service. place needs a renovation of the building and overhaul of mgmt. 1 star for view.

Kinda pricey for brunch (~$17/dish) but the food and service was great. Went on a Wednesday so it was pretty empty. Split the Endless Summer Scramble with my husband. And split an order of biscuit sampler and cinnamon roll between 4 people. Perfect amount of food. Loved the maple bacon busquit (no need for butter!) and the sinful cinnamon roll.

This is my new favorite restaurant in sb. Incredible food (my boyfriend and I ordered salmon and halibut and both were fabulous). They have a simple menu with options for meat-eaters, seafood-lovers, and vegetarians. Good wine and beer selection. Very friendly service. Amazing environment! Reminds me of Portland.
The very best. The food is super fresh, authentic and inventive. Also everyone who works there is friendly. I'd eat there every day......
We go here everyday when in SB. Lots of ingredients, awesome bowls. Fun atmosphere. A healthy way to start your day or get refreshed.

Satellite is an extraordinary reprieve from the everyday humdrum. It never fails to offer the perfect blend of stimulation for all senses. The tunes are typically unfamiliar and fun which maybe says more about my own musical knowledge than anything but it lends itself to a solid foundation for the perfect ambiance. Dim lighting, amazing food and cozy corners make Satellite a must for anyone looking for someplace to enjoy the company of old friends and possibly make new acquaintances. The menu is creative, inspired, and promises bold flavors and whimsy while the wine selection and knowledgable staff are always on hand to offer the perfect pairings. Satellite is a must for you tonight!

Simply amazing! From the drinks... to the food... to the location, it couldn't be a greater restaurant. We had a variety of margaritas and everyone loved them. Nice and strong which is always a plus! Everything we had for lunch was amazing and great flavor. I loved my ceviche with pineapple. Such a unique taste that melted in my mouth! Loved the outdoor setting as it couldn't be more perfect. Enjoyed it so much that we stopped back for dinner!

We ordered for delivery  Shawarma rice bowl Shawarma wrap French Fries Hummus   The shawarma of the day was chicken and it was very flavorful. We both thought that the wrap was better than the rice bowl. There was too much rice.  The wrap was delicious! The veggies were a great touch and the cucumber sauce was a nice addition. We dipped the French fries in the cucumber sauce and the hummus and it was great. The pita that came with the hummus was a little stale, we were glad we ordered the fries.

I love this place. Clean store, friendly staff, affordable sandos. Loads of Italian imports and domestic items. Frozen handmade ravioli, lobster, beef or cheese, also has McConnell's ice cream. Indoor seating. A/C. Cold refreshments. Cards accepted.

I loved the Santa Barbara Roasting company - the ambiance, the coffee, the bagels, the vibes everything was so refreshing! I also used the yelp checkin offer to try out their house made coffee. Absolutely love this place. Would come back and recommend to everyone visiting SB.!!

Great ambiance with the outdoor seating. The food is spectacular with amazing taste. The steak and eggs was prepared perfectly. High quality ingredients and full of flavor. Also the Endless Summer Scramble was great. The House-made bagel with cream cheese was a good addition to our meal. One more thing is the great service. Enjoy.

I have never been humiliated like this in my entire life. The owner is rude, lies, and nasty with his staff. The food is mediocre, it is mostly a drunken bar for locals who don't know how unsanitary this place is. Do not waste your money there. Very disappointing. Go to Tee Off on States Street instead - their prices are reasonable, food is great and plentiful and the entire staff is very friendly and professional.
We have been coming here for 30 years now. Enterprise is one place that hasn't changed in SB as far as quality and service. Excellent seafood!

Altogether wonderful.  Cool State St. little hideaway parklet.  Excellent food.  The 'share plates' are joyful and tasty.  Eggplant dish that is swoon-worlthy.  And service that's above & beyond.   Urge you to give Mizza try -- I'm certain you'll be pleased.

Hubby and I ate here a couple weeks ago when we were in SB visiting the in-laws.  I had made a list of potential spots for lunch/dinner based on Yelpers' comments for the time we'd be in town and Saigon In and Out sounded good for lunch one day.  Hubby and I shared a starter of the Green Papaya Salad.  It was very good.  It wasn't as spicy as some I've had other places, but it was tasty nonetheless.  For an entree I had the Bun Tom - Bo Nuong Xa (Shrimp & Lemongrass Beef w/ Rice Vermicelli)  and hubby had the Tom Xao Dau Bokchoy (Shrimp stir-fried with snow peas and bokchoy).  Yum!  Yum!  Yum!  I love bun.  I love it so much, in fact, that I almost always order it when I go to a Vietnamese restaurant.  The restaurant is cute with lots of tall plants between the tables (gives a feeling of privacy).  It was also very clean (even the bathroom was spotless, although we were there early for lunch (11:30-ish).  The service was also friendly and attentive.  This place is on my list for a return visit the next time we're in town.

This place defines 'beyond expectations'!! Me and my husband decided to give it a try but not expecting a lot bc, you know, it's Santa Barbara (we came to SB from the Bay Area and have struggled a little bit adjusting to the 'meh' food scene here). Walking in it was already a pleasant surprise: very beautifully decorated place: tradicional Japanese bamboo and curtains and super cool cement floors. From the moment we sat down we had AMAZING service: attentive, knowledgeable, sweet and polite. Not only was our server outstanding, but also everyone in the restaurant checked on us throughout the meal to see how we were doing and every single one of them bid us farewell on our way out. Honestly one of the best services I've ever had in SB. And then last but not least, the food!! OMG! So fresh and delicious. So delicate and with such well balanced flavors and the presentation was just out of this world. Honestly we were blown away by our experience. Thank you very much Sun Sushi team. We have found our new favorite restaurant in SB!!!
Music was waaay too loud techno, which really spoiled what could have been quite a nice place.

Pretty standard bar. Seemed to clear out randomly at times though, not sure if big groups just left or what. Indoor/outdoor seating and plenty of it. Lots of outdoor heaters also. It's a pretty big place actually, it goes far back. Stuck to cranberry vodkas but had a few of the jello shots also which were pretty good. Cool because they came in a tube instead of a cup, much easier to drink/eat.

I am giving this 2 stars only because it used to be my favorite sandwich but that has changed.  I went in recently and got my usual sandwich which has gone up a lot in price and the quality has gone far down.  The meat is far thinner sliced and there is much less of it.  I counted 3 slices of meat on my sandwich, 2 slices of cheese one piece of lettuce a few tomatoes and some onion.  The bread made up 90% of the sandwich.  For 12 bucks I expect far better.

FINALLY tried Mizza and wasn't disappointed. Loved (almost) everything we ordered, great atmosphere, excellent service, and the perfect setting for a lovely bday Sunday. The burrata & heirloom tomatoes app was scrumptious, the salmon pasta, very flavorful and the salmon perfectly cooked, I liked the toppings of the veggie pizza but wasn't a super fan of the crust (doesn't mean I won't come back and try another pizza on their menu ) and the cannoli was good although that dessert in general isn't my fave (it was my bf's bday so had to get what he wanted ). The flourless chocolate cake and limoncello cake are what I'll try next time. Had a great time and will be back. Gave it 4 stars just cuz I wasn't that excited about the pizza and that's something they're supposed to so well. Would recommend the restaurant though!

Just got takeout here. Fantastic homemade Mexican flavor and the interior has some very cool art to check out while you're waiting. The owner/server was super friendly and helpful. We had the chile relleno chicken burrito and the chicken mole rojo enchiladas. Will definitely go back!

DAMN! The carnitas are no joke here at Lito's.  So while in SB a friend recommended that we try the Pozole and the carnitas at a joint called Lito's.  So we pulled up the Yelp app on our phone and found the quickest way to get there.  It was a Saturday and we arrived just after the lunch crowd had left.  This is a good thing because Lito's is a smaller restaurant.  You walk up to the counter, place your order and then have a seat.    All the food looked so damn good but since I am a pork pervert, i had to go with the carnitas.  Best decision of the weekend.  My cousin got the pozole and her two friends split another carnitas plate.  So we were seated and the food was brought out a few minutes later.   LORD HAVE MERCY!! those carnitas were so damn good.  Crispy, juicy, lots of flavor.  The carnitas plate came with a side of rice and beans and tortillas, dollop of guacamole and some jalapenos.  It was perfect.  I couldn't have asked for a better lunch.    I tried some of my cousins pozole and it was also great.  Lots of flavor and a lot of meat and hominy.  The only problem, well its not really a problem, but the food coma sets in after eating here.  I managed to finish my entire plate and then proceeded to take a nap.    I guess there is a reason that this place was featured on Food Network.  Its just that good.  Next time I am in SB I WILL be coming back!
Over-priced, average quality, plus deceptive business practices. They literally take Simply Grapefruit juice from the container and fill up plastic bottles to make it seem fresh squeezed. LOL!

Some of the worse service ever!!! I have been dinning at Playa Azul off and on for the past 20 years.  I have always had good experiences with both the food and the service but last Saturday was unforgivable.  When we walked up to the host stand, 3 of us, right from the start they seemed annoyed we were there. The waiter was so indifferent and the service was just bad.  Food was average but I will not be returning.  So sad, I have had good memories...
This is the best smelling restaurant I've been to outside of India
Great place.  Good food and drinks, nice staff and a lovely outside patio area.  Good location at top of State Street.  Prices are very reasonable and portions are fairly generous.

We had to try Sambo's for breakfast on our last day in Santa Barbara.  This place is so cool.  The older I get the more I like things that have been around a long time.  That is if they are good.  Being a little kid growing up in the 70's I remember my Mom and Grandparents taking my brother and I to our local Sambo's.  Pancakes!  They do them right here with Tiger butter.  I always remembered the Tiger butter thing.    We came in on a Sunday morning about 11:00 a.m. and there was a short wait to sit inside.  Outside wait was longer.  We got a nice booth.  Service was good.  Our waitress brought us some freshly baked muffins.  So good!  Most restaurants mush their corned beef hash but Sambo's doesn't.  They leave the corned beef in bigger pieces and it's much better that way.  The Eggs Benedict over Corned beef sounded interesting.  It was delivered hot and good!  Great hash browns too.  We will be back someday for sure!

I think this might be my second review. This time the food was great but service was not so good.   Come on Mr. Waiter you can't seriously be upset if one of your patrons doesn't like meat or spice. She doesn't like it. Accommodate her. Figure it out. You make the whole table uncomfortable.   Plus if we sit down at 7:30p I'd like to be out by 9p or 9:30p. When it's close to 10p (2.5 hours for three people?) and you haven't come back to pickup my credit card...I'm doing the quick math for a new tip amount.

Under New Management and it shows. But is it enough?  We stayed here for my birthday and it's changed a lot since 2014.   Pros:  - great resort like feel, the grounds are well kept - The staff this time were very friendly and professional  - they brought me a small dessert free of charge for my birthday  - room was updated with new electronics, some new pattern bed spreads and art - Beautiful view - Get a ride on a golf cart if you have bags, if you don't it sucks having to walk from your room. We got golf cart, so worth it.   Cons: - Main restaurant is still closed, have to stick to only room service type menu and bistro - room service menu is weak, need more  - complimentary wine in room was horrible (tasted like it went bad)  - Beds are rock hard, woke up soar on the side I sleep on. Def not a good night sleep.  - iPad kept crashing (their custom app sucks, whoever developed it should be fired). When it worked it was a good experience but the confirmation purchase page needs adjusting - far from everything Santa Barbara  - Spa pool and service haven't changed (do not expect Terranea, more like a small average spa)  Definitely better than 2014 but still in need of improvements. With a few tweaks this place could rival any 5 star ocean front location. Keep improving guys, you're almost there.

Very creative.  Relatively limited menu, but uniformly good and large portions.  The wine is the most unique part.  You get a card that keeps track of your charges, insert the card into the wine machine (16 different wines, rotating frequently) and choose whether you want a taste, a half glass or a full glass.  The wines range from 'meh' (to me) to 'wow!' (again, YMMV).  A good way to find a wine that you like without breaking your budget, or to try a new wine that sounds interesting, or both!

We enjoyed a beautiful brunch here on a gorgeous Sunday morning during our Santa Barbara trip. This beautiful patio made me feel like I was in Italy. The breakfast flatbread we split to start was so good, and all of our entrees were amazing. Great Bloody Mary and amazing ocean views and breezes will bring us back!

Stopped in in our last night in SB and we're pleased enough with our experience here, but can't say it knocked out socks off. Service was very good and genuine. The space is small and we felt a bit cramped but not too bad. Black Sheep (presumably named after the owner and based on his familial role) has a good (if short) wine list favoring local wines. The beer list is even better, introducing me to a couple of local SB brews, one of which I took home.   The food is creative and well prepared. I had a nice serving of Wagyu beef that was a touch beyond the medium rare that I requested, but not so bad to send it back and destroy the timing of our meal. The Missus was a little less satisfied with her fish selection. Good but not sensational. In fact, that about summarizes our experience, good but not sensational. Regardless, I would give it another try if we were local. The Black Sheep is an honest effort from some young restauranteurs and should evolve over time

I ordered a breakfast burrito with egg cheese and avocado. It was tasty, the portion was big, they were generous on the fillings. The take out process was quick and easy. I ordered and paid online, just walked in and grabbed the food togo. Their online system is great.  They have a limit of 3 customers inside the store. The parking could be an issue sometimes since it's a busy one way street.

Came here first stop in Santa Barbara, it was great food and service.   Tasty, fresh food and great vibes.   I don't think anything here can taste bad!! Recommend to my out of towners (I'm from New York- nyc area)  Great staff and grub, just do it like Nike says!

WOW.  Where to begin.  First, I guess, the food.  It's fine.  It's nothing you'll remember but it's not terrible.  After that, it's all downhill.  We arrived when it was fairly busy to discover quite possibly the stupidest seating/ordering system ever conceived (and I've seen a lot).  First off, as you approach the front entrance, there are basically two-and-a-half lines.  One on the left, one on the right, and then a mass of confused people in the middle trying to understand the difference.  When you finally make your way up to earshot of the woman who is rudely yelling out that you are in the wrong line (as if you should know the system your first time there), you learn that the right hand line is for getting a table.  But not a normal table like you would have with a waiter.  A table 'reservation'.  You get a ticket with a number on it.  Or, if there's no tables ready at the moment (which was the case for us), you don't get a ticket.  You give her your name and then awkwardly stand around and wait to be called to be given a ticket.  (Yes there are 3 elements to be tracking now).  Then, once you are called to get a ticket, you are told to get in the left-hand line and that when you finally get to the front, you can order.    (**Did I mention you're not allowed to order unless you have first waited on the list, and then waited in the right-hand line?  So some people accidentally waited in the left line only to reach the front and then be told they have to go get on the list to wait in line 1 to get a ticket to wait on line 2**)   Once you order at the end of the left-hand line, you are told to 'go find your table'.  So you search around trying to match your number with the stand on one of the very many tables there.  Then, you sit down and wait for your food.    If you want water, you can get it yourself from a 5 ounce dixie cup.  If you ask for a large plastic cup (which they have), you are told 'no, those are only for ice tea.'  If, perhaps, you decide you want to add a food item, order another latte, whatever --- wait for for --- you have to START THE WHOLE PROCESS OVER AGAIN.  Absurd.    Add to all of this that the restaurant (which probably seats 150) has ONE COED BATHROOM.  Yes, you read that right.  This place needs to pump the brakes on the ordering logistics and just hire some waiters/servers and do it the old fashioned way that has worked for several centuries.  They tried to re-invent the wheel and ended up with a triangle.  Also, build another bathroom for the love of god.  Hard pass.

Knowing better than trying to show off my poor skills in the adjacent batter's cage, I sat down at East Beach Tacos for a triple: Batter Up, Ahi Poke, and Grilled Fish.  The Grilled Fish was the weakest of the three so I hit a proverbial double.  Not bad.
This place is SO delicious!! We went back the very next day we liked it so much! The Brussels sprouts and spring rolls were two of our faves!

Came here for dinner in late March. We arrived about 5:30 and were seated by 6:00. The restaurant is at the end of the pier, small and limited seating, but we found the food to be excellent and worth the walk and wait. We were seated outside and had an amazing view of the sunset setting over the mountains.  The dungeness crab was about 2.5 pounds and my wife thoroughly enjoyed it with the spicy cocktail sauce and the butter. i had the Ciapponi which had an amazing broth and huge rock crab leg on top. it was served in a sourdough bread bowl.  For starter, we had the clam chowder which was really awesome. It was light, not too 'fishy' flavor, and not too salty. It had plenty of seafood and was chunky. It just had an excellent flavor and I could easily go back again for some of that alone.  Overall, great place to come. Friendly staff and attentive when we came, but I can see that this place may get crazy in the summer months. It was a little cool sitting outside, but the heaters were great so don't be shy to ask them to turn them on if you run cool.

Love this place when we get a craving for hot dogs! We have tried a few different ones, but my favorite is the Hawaiian dog. The owner is so kind and you can tell he really cares about his business. It's one of our favorite lunch spots!
Fun location in the Funk Zone. Great authentic Mexican food and friendly service. Awesome housemade chips too. A five-star hole-in-the-wall.

I liked the chicken patay with peanut sauce.  It was delicious.  I thought the chicken was well seasoned and moist. There were a lot of portions, enough for a meal.  I wish I had ordered the beef to try out also. I would go back anytime.

So, here's the thing with Sojourner that is pretty darn cool: there's no way you can eat something unhealthy here! With exception to dessert, everything is pretty guilt free. When I was visiting Santa Barbara, a friend of mine took me here and I liked it for the most part. My only two complaints are about the service and the inventiveness of the food. It took an hour and a half to have our meal. That's wayyy too long for a little restaurant like this. It's not like I'm talking about Spago here where you have a two, maybe three course meal. Now on to the food. It was ok, but it was so uncreative and boring that I wanted to go in the kitchen and help the chef myself. I thought that Sojourner was fine for a small, vegetarian meal, but would I come here again? Probably not.

Pizza Guru is now my daughter's high watermark for pizza. She LOVED this place.  My husband and I enjoyed our pizza, too, but I can't say it blew me away to quite the same degree. Still, a solid choice -- can't go wrong!

We came upon this beautiful spot yesterday as our friend's band was performing songs there by Frank Sinatra, Billie Holliday, Nina Simone and more. Every spot inside was reserved but the hostess graciously found us seats on a comfy leather couch outside. The menu has a lot of variety but we decided to share the lemon brined fried chicken, peas, carrots, potatoes and sausage gravy. Oh my! Couldn't have been happier with our choice. The chicken was flavorful, the veggies a delight and the gravy was icing on the cake. I had a glass of the Happy Canyon Sauvignon Blanc and it paired very well. The service was great and the atmosphere was mellow and fun. We'll be back!

This place was great! I was on the pier with my parents around sunset and we decided to see if we could grab a quick, light dinner. We noticed some of the other restaurants on the pier were empty but didn't have the greatest reviews here on Yelp. Although the Santa Barbara Shellfish Company had a wait, I had a feeling it would be worth it and I was right. We added our name to the list and grabbed a seat on the bench right outside. There are pretty views here of the water and sunset. The inside of this place is super tiny so we were waiting for an outdoor table (which there also are not a lot of). We had a nice waiter and the food was great! We are New Englanders so we are a tough judge when it comes to seafood and New England Clam Chowder. The chowder was great, just like back home, and my parents really liked their fried seafood plates as well. I wish they had fried clams on the menu (they did have clam strips as a special that day, but the strips just aren't as good!). If you want to grab food on the pier, this is the place to go!

Great pizza if you can get them on the phone and often they are very rude. Customer service is terrible in general. 5 stars for the pizza if it arrives. Stopped ordering from them for almost a year now because of the service. I recently tried to call them again but had the same experience.
Sweet Jane!  Good food in a fun, festive dining room.  I really enjoyed my dinner here but nothing was truly exceptional.  I'll def. be back though.

Great service from a family-run spot.  We arrived at 1030AM and they weren't quite open, so we chatted with the gentleman running the wine tasting joint across the street.  I didn't love their salsas as much as the other reviewers and I ordered the enchiladas and was somewhat disappointed.  The mole itself is very thin and there isn't much depth in the flavor profile but that did not stop me from inhaling the whole plate.  Husband had three assorted tacos, including the fish, the barbacoa and the pastor.  All were gone with a quickness.  The young gent that opened the establishment and was setting up the outdoor tables was so warm and congenial.

This shop has some really delicious breads and pastries.  I stopped here on a weekend during brunch time and there were many people already seated and eating at the outside tables but they still had a great selection left too.  I bought a box of attractive, delicious pastries and enjoyed every one of them.  The staff behind the counter were polite and friendly.  The shop is small, but clean and with a comfortable atmosphere.  Parking is a little hairy right there along the main drag, but I got in and out safely and did get a parking space right in front of the building.  I will definitely come back here and bring my friends!

Went to Eureka last Sunday for lunch. The wait wasn't bad and our server Nicole got to us pretty quickly. The food came out fast and it was really good. However, once we got our food it seemed like we were abandoned. We tried calling for some drink refills but we were ignored. The worst part was it took 30 minutes for us to pay out and leave. We were honestly shocked that no one took initiative to help us out. All of us would have been okay had one staff member updated us on the situation or problem.It seemed like Nicole was struggling. I was hoping one of the many staff members or a manager could help her out, but I guess not. Overall good food, but service was not so great.

A must try place for matcha mavens and foodies alike! I personally enjoy everything from the matcha lattes to the vegan entree items (mac n cheese, tabouli salad, raw veggie wrap)! I enjoy cheese as much as the next person so having fresh chev in the roasted beet salad is a HUGE PLUS! You should add avocado to make it even more creamy! :)   Other favorites include: The dirty brownie (and it's VEGAN), blueberry muffins, rawesome applie pie (vegan), raw fudge (vegan), mamma maca smoothie (for peanut butter x chocolate lover enthusiasts), sb greens smoothie, mac n cheese (I love regular and the vegan), avocado toast (pesto is the best-o), and the green table burger (add a slice of goat Gouda!).   Personally, can't pick one item whenever I go so it can be slightly taxing on the wallet but...... EVERYTHING IS GLUTEN FREE, LOCALLY SOURCED (even the bread is from a local bakery!), freshest juices made to order and a laid back environment where you can eat in peace and stay as long as you like! Be sure to stop by and try a minimum of two items, you won't be disappointed ;)
This place is amazing and prepare good cold sandwiches, very clean place and nice people. We get food from here when we want something good and fast.

You just have to try this place for your Fish and Chips 'fix' - the best we have ever had - PERIOD!  There are many other great looking food items on the menu - a definite return visit is on our Bucket List.  They open at 6:30AM so you can get your seafood 'fix' early.  The staff at the counter are very professional and the line moves quickly due to their efficiency.  Try it, you'll love it!  You're right there near the ocean!

Very happy this place has opened.  If your going to the Public Market in Santa Barbara you must make it to Empty Bowl.  They serve really amazing and fun dishes filled with homemade love.  They are in the center of the Market with a large bar that does counter service for those who want to wine and dine on some gourmet noodles and great beer and wine.  Chili plates and chopsticks on the counter. Nice china and flatware.  Colorful dishes with flavor to match.    A must try is the Pot Stickers.  Homemade by mom, yes Jerry's mom makes them all by hand.  Also the Papaya salad is amazing with great flavor.  I love all the noodle dishes here, but some of my favorites are the Long Tail Boat Noodle, and the Bangkok street noodle,  that is widely know in South East Asia on many little street corners.  You know those little places you find in Bangkok that have the little plastic chairs and lots of scooters out front and you wonder why its so busy.  Then you get this bowl of hot steaming broth and rice noodles with some pork, cilantro, pepers...oh my!   I am getting hungry just writing this.    The owners all come from some serious restarutant backgrounds and they take the wine and beer list as serious as the food.  You will find a nice pairing of whites and reds on the wine list, champagne, and some really nice local wines as well.  The beer selection rocks with a couple of local beers on tap and Thai beer in the bottle. They also have Anchor Steam IPA one of my favorites.    Last thing, you gotta get here early.  They are open all day from 11 am to 8 pm on the weekdays and 9 pm on Sat and Sun.  They are really busy at lunch and dinner durring the peak hours and you may have to wait in line.  But the line moves nicely and if you wait inside the railing they will serve you beer and wine while you wait.

I love this place. They serve really close to Asian food though it was a kinda fusion style.   Always get their Korean glass noodles. I love it a lot.  Bf loves the pork belly bao as it is so fatty. I like the shrimp bao a little more.  They had seasonal items all the time.  Price was reasonable.   I'm looking forward to their new location now!
Only when one moves away does one finally realize what one had...  That 'one' was me.  And that 'had' was you.  Sigh...

Bless their hearts. Pizzas were good. The $7 salads, though, consisted of exactly 7 simple romaine leaves (a buck a leaf!) with a scattering of pine nuts, SDT and cheese. The Caesar was just as meager. Gotta love the brashness to charge those prices.

Great bar with strong drinks (but not too strong!) and tasty snacks. Had whiskey sours and fried artichokes -- both . Love the vintage chairs and the friendly staff -- felt like a classic neighborhood bar. High ceilings, good people - what's not to like? I hope this becomes a Santa Barbara staple!

A uninspired spot that claims to be serving quality BBQ. All I can say to that is: bleh.  Clearly there is no local competition, or this place would have been run into the ground. And clearly the locals who support this place, don't know better. Brisket is on the outside menu...though they stopped doing brisket long ago. Tri-tip, which is a RELIGION on the Central Coast, was embarrassing, and blase. Last, the BBQ sauce: is TERRIBLE. They tried to get inventive by adding clove or some other off-putting spice, but it failed miserably. I didn't try their ribs or other fair, since if you can't get basic BBQ sauce down, and can't do a simple thing like tri-tip correctly - WHY EVEN BOTHER? This place survives on older clientele, and ignorant consumers who sadly don't know any better. The interior design is kitschy and horrid, and my suggestion to you is: just keep on moving...nothing to see here.
I liked it...it's not that I didn't like it. We started with the filet mignon and sausage bites. They were good. I had the seared ahi salad, I probably should have ordered a steak.

Okay this place is actually in LaCumbre Plaza next to a starbucks. Anyway its a small storefront, and its pretty tasty. The restaurant itself is booths and tables with a white and mirror decor. Not a lot of silly faux-chinese decor here. I got food for takeout. It was pretty good. The menu was pretty standard white people chinese. No jellyfish etc. Anyway it was very good and clean, light tasting even, not heavy-handed. Which is nice for a change. Very good. I'd eat there again.

Service was great! We were welcomed within a minute of arriving, we had water at the table immediately, and our drink + food order were taken quickly. Food arrived in about 5-minutes. Keep in mind that it was close to closing on a Tuesday.   Aside from the staff, the food and quality were just okay. Yet, the prices are great (two breakfasts, a juice, and a side of fries for $35 - this would be closer to $50 at most restaurants in SB).   This is a diner, so maybe this is an unfair review? The syrup for our pancakes and waffles was coagulating, oil was leaking out of our fries and building a puddle on the bowl, the waffles were alright and although the pancakes tasted better they also tasted slightly raw (doughy).  We ate here because it was one of the only restaurants still serving breakfast at 2pm.

You really don't leave hungry. I believe I took a good amount of leftovers home. The pasta I got (rigatoni w/ chicken) lacked in flavor, I think it should have been a little more saltier. I gave for an early dinner so service was fast. After 2 years, I have not returned, I like going to Bucatini (lower State) more often.

This place isn't always consistent, but the food is good when they are, albeit a wee bit pricey for the portions. The employees never seem to smile or look happy, which is concerning, but perhaps they're just trying to be French?    Almond croissants are good, as well as the mocha.
Great pizza!  We tried the original crust and thin crust.  I liked both.  The restaurant was kid-friendly.  Service was friendly.  If I'm in town, I would definitely return.
Wonderful service, the owner is brining the food out personally.  The flatbread is not effective, the cheeseburger eggrolls are Devine.

I love this almost hidden little gem... Collabed with ParadICE this coffee shop has great coffee and amazing and unique flavors. My favorite is the Iced Maple Sea Salt Latte... If you're in or around Paseo Nuevo. I highly recommend this locally owned and delicious shop!
Such a cute cafe! I stopped by here to get some coffee and it was delicious! Next time I would like to come by to try out the food.

I've gone here twice.  The service is great but every time I come out of there - I feel like I have the worst taste in my mouth.  There is some sort of seasoning that they use which is weird because it is pretty bland food.  I don't think I'm going back.

I enjoy going to Union Ale.  The beer is good, the food is very tasty;  I normally prefer healthful foods, but at Union Ale, my favorites are the artery clogging types of foods such as potato skins, sweet potato fries, sliders etc.  Mmmm.    If you are looking for full service, it's not the place for you.   I notice many of the negative reviews have to do with the lack of sit down service and the reviewers are correct; you usually order at the bar and pick up your food at the bar.   However, if you go knowing that it is not full service, you shouldn't be disappointed.  It's a great place for groups and to watch sporting events, as there are several rooms and they will play different sporting events in different rooms.

I went through and tried almost every hot dog on the list. Most, I thought worked out, but there might be a few that is questionable. Nevertheless solid 4 stars for the variety an especially for the combination that works.

Without a doubt the best sushi spot in Santa Barbara. Their fish is consistently super fresh every time I go making for delicious rolls. Location is a little random in a shopping center off state street but the quality of food makes up for outdoor dining in the parking lot.  My go to rolls are the salmon or yellow tail delight and the volcano.   I usually just get takeout but last time i went I ate inside and the staff were incredible. Absolutely phenomenal sushi
Delicious food made with care and skill. Good staff good prices good food. Terrific fish tacos! And their soups are tremendous.
Great location, downtown close to the beach. Fun ambiance, good service. The food ok, not bad, not the best.

Was dragged here by friends, why they think this place rocks, I have no idea. Its filled with old people getting their drank on and humping each other in corners and the dance floor. Not kidding, it happened.  Got the Cajun Ahi appetizer, thought the fish would be fresher, it was just meh. Tried the calamari, the batter was not tasty. The endless summer pale ale was not bad. Bartender made us a nasty, gross shot that made our nights go south - the Bonsai.   Don't try it, you'll regret it.

Vegan muffin, hot coco, oatmeal, coffee, fruit cup and a breakfast burrito cost $35. The oatmeal was nasty, and I love oatmeal. The fruit wasn't ripe and they forgot to give us half our order. Coffee and muffin were tasty but I won't be back. There are to many more deserving places that will provide better quality food and service. Ok for a coffee and muffin stop, but I wouldn't recommend this place for anything else.

This place is awesome!  I came here on Sunday with the other half.  We really enjoyed our food!  This pace is packed, so be prepared to wait, especially if you are with a big party.  The food was awesome- we split the tri-tip sami and the avocado and broccoli salad.  SO good!  This is an awesome place to spend the afternoon in the sun in the funk zone.

My Husband and i decided to go to have dinner at Moby Dicks. Well again and this will be the last time i go there. As we arrived the host was not a bit welcoming ..He just stared at us but to others he would welcome in a different tone. My husband ordered fish n chips and a salad. The salad with ranch dressing from a bottle. It was not good. The fish n chips were bitter and it was fish paddies. We told the waiter who returned them and said cooks admitted It was bad. I ordered clam chowder. The bowl was small n the taste was also bad. This place is terrible. I have to say they need to learn to cook.

My husband and I were devastated to see the Figueroa Street Jeannine's location close. Being just a few blocks from our cottage, it was our favorite brunch spot, and we'd built a rapport with the staff there. So, we were excited to hear another location was opening by the beach, even though it's a little bit more of a hike for us. We've visited a couple times, now, and have been thrilled to see what Lalo (manager) and staff have done with the space! The service they provide has always been top-notch, in our experience, and this location is no different.  The interior of the restaurant is light, bright and airy, with beautifully tempting displays of fresh pastries and other baked goods by the registers. We stopped by this afternoon for lunch, about an hour before closing and were *extra* tempted by the 2-for-1 deal on pastries, but we opted for a heartier brunch and ordered a couple plates of their classic eggs benedict. The eggs were cooked perfectly, nice and runny inside, and the fruit and potatoes were just right. The hollandaise, as always, was light and lemony. I'm really picky about restaurant brewed coffee, finding most of it to be weak and bland. But, Jeannine's brews theirs right, and today's cup was as expected: dark and rich but not bitter. So yummy!  On weekends, I particularly love their biscuits and sausage gravy as a side to their classic American breakfast of eggs, meat, and potatoes. But, you can't go wrong with their waffles, breakfast burrito, or burger, if you're in the mood for lunch.   The succulent-laden patio is where this particular location really shines, with plenty of shade provided by bright yellow and white umbrellas, hovering over a large collection of tables, sharing views of Cabrillo Blvd, West Beach, and Stearns Wharf. With counter ordering, plenty of tables, and attentive servers, you can expect pretty quick turnover, so that intimidating line that winds out the door goes faster than you think. Don't miss it!
I went in there for lunch because I had heard good things about this place. I was disappointed when the pineapple curry I ordered was as runny and tasteless as water.
This place is extremely pricy. The freezes are $6 and they are very plain tasting. The vanilla flavor is okay but definitely needs almond butter and chocolate chips to make it a little more eatable.

If I was a local, I'd be here all the time!  Love me some Cajun food.  I'll definitely hit this place up whenever I'm in town.  Outdoor seating during COVID times at this location.  Sit-down, full-service restaurant.  Dog friendly.  Breakfast and lunch only.  I wish they were open for dinner!  We came for breakfast on a sunday.  There was a special that included a bottle of champagne, little carafe of OJ and a plate of beignets.  Highly recommend and perfect for a party of 4 to start.  Hopefully that deal sticks after COVID times.  For my entree, I went with the fried chicken benedict.  I hate hollandaise sauce, and this creation subbed that out for gravy!  The gravy topped poached eggs, fried chicken and bacon on an open faced biscuit.  There are several options for an included side.  I went with grits and mixed in some gravy and meat with it.  Bomb!

So, we went down to SB last weekend to spend some time with the girls and decided we wanted family style Italian one night.  Peterini's says they are the oldest restaurant in SB, and that means 'old school' in this case.  Dishes served in gratin pans straight out of the oven, smothered in red meat sauce and cheese...really good garlic bread, and no wine list...just burgundy and chablis....oh well.  You don't walk away hungry, and the gingham plastic table cloths seal the memory. Next time I think I'll try the linguine and clams, just to see what they can do without the red sauce.
My husband and I love coming here! The fish is always fresh and the sauce choice is awesome. Service is always quick and courteous. Would definitely recommend.

The food here is pretty good. Although the last time we ordered the beet salad and the beets came out with the imprint of a can. Obviously not fresh.  We have actually been here multiple times and one thing that stands out is the place is freezing. No attempt to keep it warm. Your plates come out of the kitchen warm but soon are very cold which makes for cold food. Disappointing in that respect

2 years later made my way back here again. Still love the ambiance. It has changed slightly but definitely recognized the spot.  Ordered the flight of beer which comes with 5 choices, so I picked 1. 3 wheats to the wind - my fave 2. Beach please 3. Hazy ex-girlfriend 4. Hoppy ass fuck 5. Redhead redemption I was impressed by the amount of beer that came with the flight for the price. I couldn't finish it but to be honest, this wasn't my first drink of the night. Very much enjoy this spot

The prawns are farm-raised Indonesia.  The pizza's great. The Pinot Noir's by the glass are great, but there are a lot of items on the menu with prawns. Where are the prawns from? Wild? farm raised? No one knows. I find out they get them from Kanaloa seafood, and since that place has a great reputation, it must be o.k. So by chance I'm at Kanaloa, and they get their prawns farm-raised from Indonesia. Is the water clean? Is the operation sanitary? Who knows. Who knows what the farm is even called. It's a mystery.  Also, what a lot of patrons don't know is this place used to be called Brigitte's and it had great pizzas also. Food is good here, but the food was great here in the mid 1980's also. Their little pizzas are a carry-over from Brigitte's.

Came here for a quiet dinner--I ordered the filet mignon and tiramisu. The waitress was friendly and helpful, and brought complimentary bread while I was waiting. It was good, but was cold--would have been nice warmed up.  The filet mignon was delicious and perfectly cooked. It came with a side of bok choy and scalloped potatoes. The bok choy was good, but I wish there was more--there was only 2 of them. The scalloped potatoes were underseasoned but okay after I added salt/pepper.  Tiramisu was meh--wish I had ordered a side or something else instead.

Came here for a little weekend getaway. I thought it was a really small but nicely kept hotel. Our room was really small, but homey. Pretty much the bed took up a good chunk of the room.   The location was nice, it's a bit further from state street and all the restaurants. but its right across the street from the beach, and it's nice and secluded. Especially if you just like those strolls along the beach.   The only thing is, I think it's a bit expensive for the teeny tiny room.

Maybe their other dishes are better, but I ordered one milk tea with boba and it was like drinking a little bucket of sugar. The boba were soft which, is good, but still very, very sweet.   The other dish that we ordered was a vegan bowl and this was the more horrible thing ever tasted and an embarrassment to what good vegan food could be. It like they boiled the vegan meat which is a shame bc you can treat it as any other meat - stir-fry it, broil it or anything. Not just boil it and slather on cheap teriyaki sauce!  Such a shame. Again, maybe I ordered wrong and their other dishes are better..... I hope so for their sake!

Based upon previous yelpers we tried this place finally. What a great find! It's definitely a local favorite and that always speaks volumes to me.  Our server Jasmine was a delight as we're the other servers all helping each other. My wife has allergies and Jasmine was patient and very knowledgeable of what was in the food. Pad Woon Sen was fresh and good. Coconut soup was probably the best we ever had. Chicken Satay was awesome - I appreciated that it was thin and lean rather than thicker pieces at some of the other places. And don't forget the ice cream! They make their own. I asked Jasmine her favorite and she suggested the Basil Mint Chip which is not in the menu. So GOOD! We found the portions just right and the prices fair. It was my service dog's Birthday and Jasmine even gave us a free ice cream on her behalf!  We went on a Wednesday approx 5:30 and they were busy non-stop. Again all locals. We'll be back!

This has become our new favorite restaurant in town.  The food is unique and really good.  The fig burger is excellent with a touch of sweet and savory at the same time.  And clam chowder is better than most that you would find back east!  Add to that the owners often come around to the tables and check on how you are enjoying the food, it is really a nice experience.
This ones definitely better than the one in Goleta. The staff is friendly and the food is good.

I love madam Lu. Whoever the front ladies are that work there are super kind and welcoming. I usually order take out and enjoy seeing my family sharing madam Lu's food. I personally really love the sizzling rice soup and imperial shrimp.

The staff is extremely unprofessional. I understand it's a sports bar type restaurant so the overall vibe is going to be more 'relaxed' but my experience was unbelievable.   I went to their happy hour with a friend and noticed we weren't getting the best service, i.e. it took over 10 minutes for our server to greet our table. We looked around and realized why - one server to eight tables. Clearly poor management and not the server's fault. Seeing that my friend and I both work in restaurants and have dealt with similar situations, we disregarded the poor service and proceeded to enjoy our time.   Then at one point our server came up to us, holding back tears, telling us someone else was taking over our table because another employee told her we commented on her terrible service (which we didn't, we commented on the restaurant's poor management). No doubt it was the hostess who had been giving us dirty looks throughout our time there.   Absolutely unprofessional! We couldn't stand being there for even another second so we left and ended up giving a generous (25%) tip to assure that server she wasn't so bad and honestly, to keep her from crying more.  Solution? 1) Hire better hostesses who, instead of listening to guests's conversations creating drama, actually do their jobs. There were always people waiting to be seated when the place had only 8/25 tables sat. 2) Train servers to conduct themselves professionally. Servers shouldn't be so easily offended. I've had some people say very rude things to me and NEVER has it occurred to me to approach the table in tears telling them how 'hurt' I was. That was absolutely absurd and I've steered clear of Sandbar since then.

They have two restaurants in sb and one in goleta. I've been to all three and I must say the food is so damn good at all three places ! This is the OG restaurant though, so it's always nice to come back!! They have an extremely small parking lot, and street parking is difficult so that's why I don't come to this one very often.

Amazing food. Amazing staff. Amazing ambiance.    I was here on a Saturday night for late dinner, as part of a bachelorette party. We were a little rowdy (sorry about all the penis straws), but the staff here was so absolutely wonderful and attentive, we immediately felt welcomed. It made the diner just that much more memorable for our group.  The food is beyond reviewable, that's how great every item we had, was. Photos attached. The scallop crudo was easily the best scallop dish I've ever had in my life.   Please support this business! It deserves to stick around for a long time.

THE ABSOLUTE BEST BREAKFAST BURRITO IN THE WORLD!! We always get the chorizo, egg and cheese with a side of sour cream and salsa and it's HUGE. My husband and I often share it. And it's super Affordable!

This place was outstanding and will definitely visit again when I'm SB.   FOOD: We ordered their carne asada with crab flauta and it was amazing. The steak had that chargrilled flavor which made it even more mouthwatering. The crab flauta was also really good. The kids had a breakfast burrito and pancakes and again, everything was delicious. The pancakes were like fluffy clouds and the burrito was packed with eggs, ham, and bacon (kids didn't want the tomatoes nor peppers). The roasted home potatoes that came with the breakfast burrito were also amazing. Even their michelada was superb. The only thing that was so so was the wife's chilaquiles. They were not bad, but nothing special.   SERVICE: Waiters were very nice and patient. It took a few inquiries to get my michelada but it was worth the wait!  LOCATION: Located on the famous State Street and about a block from the beach, does not get much better than this. We sat outside (small patio on sidewalk) and really enjoyed the weather and the crowd.   NOTEWORTHY: A truly savory and delicious Mexican restaurant in SB. Definitely worth a try!

Very friendly, accommodating staff. I have had Tacos, burritos, Alambres, and quesadillas. I have never been dissapointed. Very delicious always with prompt service. One of few places that actually carves pastor off of spit. It is awesome!
Delicious! Try the Tri-Tip quesadilla! Lots of cheese, generous meat... Mmmmmmm Great burritos or combo plates :)

I love the concept of this place! The ingredients were all very fresh and the pizza took only 90 seconds to make. I wasn't sure what to order but the staff were realy friendly and made a few suggestions. I got the ensalada de procuitto, which was super yummy but after seeing the carne pizza I wish I had gotten that one. I'll have to go back :)

This place is OUTSTANDING!! We visited the location at Hollister and Turnpike and loved it. The dogs are made to order--but in record time. Everything on the menu is super creative, looks like an edible work of art, and tastes fantastic! From the traditional Chicago style to the out-of-this world El Nino, all are off the charts and highly recommended. Our fave is the Santa Barbara, veggie style!
