(...Oh, and the staff are cool as hell too!) You definitely need to try Dave's Dogs today--they will blow you away!

Cute place, large outside patio. Stopped in for dinner, we both had salads, I had seafood louie, she had a cobb. They were both huge, mine had crab and shrimp, lots of seafood, hers was packed with yummy stuff  Service was good too  A good basic place, no complaints, I would go back, breakfast is probably pretty good
The food and service were very good. I am anxious to return to try more things. The Chicken Piccatta and coconut cake are every bit as good as everyone claims!

We have ordered take out from Uncorked many times over the last year - probably more than anywhere else. Every time we are thrilled with what we order. This most recent time we ordered the salmon wellington and beef wellington and were impressed by how tasty the puff pastry was, especially after traveling from the restaurant to our house. We'll be back often!

We stopped in with the kids  (12 and 13) for an afternoon bite to hold us over until dinner. The restaurant is located at the back of a courtyard walk with a lovely fountain out front.  It was a beautiful, sunny afternoon and we sat at a table close to the fountain.  The antics of the turtles in the fountain provided for great entertainment and conversation for us!  They were offering a small bites menu with only a few entree size dishes available at that time. I went for the beet salad, the family went for tacos de pastors and tacos Ensenada.  The salad was a nice size portion with a lot of fresh greens and a great flavor profile, but the number of beets and other toppings was disappointing.  There were two small tacos on each taco dish served with rice and beans and a lovely green salad with a citrus dressing.  Our kids were pretty hungry still so we asked for a side of rice and beans for each of them and they were happy to accommodate.  On the next day we found ourselves in a similar place being hungry mid-afternoon.  The kids wanted to go back for more of these tacos so we did!  The hostess remembered us from the day before, including what we ordered to drink.  This time I tried the chopped salad.  It was nice, but not as flavorful as I expected from the description of roasted corn (which it wasn't) and marinated chic peas (if they were, I couldn't taste it).  The tacos made a repeat performance with the family with each ordering an extra this time for a total of 3 each.  Three hit the spot.  I will give them a 5 as it is tough to find a nicer restaurant that appeals to everyone in our family and they did just that.

This restaurant has the worst service ever. The food (decent) is not worth the awful service. By the time I received my food it was cold. When I asked to speak to a manager they told me there was no one available to  speak with me. If you want good food and good service go somewhere else. FYI - this is not the first time this has happened to me at this restaurant. They declined to speak with me last time, too. They just don't seem to care?
This place is so good I can't get over it. My first here kebap was hands down the best meal I've had all week. The fries are great too. I'll definitely be coming back.

Had lunch there today with my best friend. We have been here many times and have loved it. However, today that was not the case. The food was mediocre at best. I didn't even finish my food. The service was ridiculous. We showed up at 1:30pm, it wasn't even 2pm yet when the waitress came with the check, and said that her manager wanted her out of there by 4pm, so whenever we were ready she could ring us up......she said this several times....we weren't finished with our food and they were ready for us to pay and leave. What's going on Harry's? I'm very disappointed with your customer service. I wont be dining here again that's for sure. Please train your staff properly.
Breakfast here on our last morning in Santa Barbara, got the French toast and an omelette.  Food was good but service was slow. I would come again for breakfast next time I'm in town!

The pizza deserves 5 stars but the overall experience (at least for pick up) only 3 stars. We ordered Pizzas and they weren't ready yet when we arrived. Most of their tables outside were unoccupied so we asked if we can eat the pizzas outside with a drink instead. They said all tables had a reservation within the hour. We said we needed less than 30min. They still didn't seat us even though it was 4:30pm and most tables were open. It's OK but the message could have been delivered in a more accommodating way. Maybe offer a water / drink while waiting in the heat and/or a 'sorry for the wait'.

PREFACE: This review is for the customer service only. The food itself is amazing and 5 stars!!!!  The man that took our order as awful. I've not had this bad customer service in a loooong time. I don't know if he was having a bad post-fourth of July day or if he is like that all time. I'm really hoping the former rather than the later, for his sake. In a nutshell he was rude, has a total lack of affect and understanding and completely unhelpful. It was the weirdest experience! When we tried to ask understand how the Triple Play order works he just looked at my friend with a blank stare and said something like 'read it on the board. There's no token. Mumble mumble.' And then, when our food was taking an extremely long time and we had seen 5 orders come out who had actually ordered after us, I approached the window to ask him if they had forgotten our order and also if we could at least have our chips and salsa while we waited. More blank stares. More rudeness. He didn't even reply but walked back to the line, looked at something, and said to me 'I'll call your name.' I asked again, if could just have my chips while I waited. He's rudely repeated, 'I'll call your name'. I turned around and walked back to my seat, confused and stunned. Two seconds later, he called my name for chips and salsa. Why not have just handed it to me? That would've been the normal thing to do. Not to mention he did not offer an explanation as to why our 4 tacos were taking 30 minutes while everyone else's food keeps going out .... Another 5-6 minutes goes by and we're just so confused as to where are food is.... Still. My friend then goes up to ask and comes back also without explanation. It was so weird!!! That guy sucked. Hard. And we're both having an internal dilemma because that carne asada taco was the best we've ever had.   I give 2 stars because the busser was amazing. He was so incredibly helpful, anticipating our needs before we even knew it.  you are awesome!!  Food, YESSS!!!! The man who works there, NOOOO!!!!

I've lived right by here for many months and I can't believe I never went until today. We got the grilled cheese, chicken & Mac + cheese, biscuit with jam, and some desserts to go and everything was phenomenal. The biscuit has to be one of the best I've ever had, and the grilled cheese was delicious. The service here was also so quick and upbeat. Will definitely be going back regularly!!

The drinks at this Starbucks is so watered down and tasteless, compared to the other locations..  We live about 4 blocks from the Mesa Location, but either drive downtown or go to the La Cumbre one by Macys to get my Chai lattes. The Frappuccinos there are no different. It sucks because it should all taste the same .. I suggest you guys make your drinks and go Compare with other locations to see what I mean. This has been the case every time we give this place 'one last try'

I came here with my parents who were visiting. We got squeeezed in without a reservation due to someone canceling, so that was great. I had high hopes about this place but they were mostly not met.  I asked the waiter for all of his recommendations and ordered those.   We started with the grilled veggie salad...it was okay. It seemed pretty standard salad fare and not worth the price. Maybe it's the foodie snob in me, but there were not unusual touches or spins that elevated it to a level higher than green salad with veggies.  The bread wasn't hot and was standard sliced ciabatta. Pretty good but pretty boring. The house olive oil and vinegar were fine, and the olives they brought out were quite nice. We asked for butter but theirs was sub-par.  I had the tenderloin with potato tart and truffle oil. I wasn't expecting this, but it came topped with an absolutely towering mountain of argula...ew. I just ate a salad and didn't need another one. I removed most of it. The tart was thin and amazing...rich without being too greasy. The truffle flavor could have been stronger, although I adore truffles and wouldn't mind eating them as black lollipops. The beef was well cooked (but not as rare as requested--I like it still moo-ing) and very tender but extremely underseasoned. I could taste almost no spice or even salt on the meat.  My dad got the pappardelle with quail and mushrooms. It was uninteresting and tasted exactly like plain pasta with meat sauce. They were stingy with the quail. Worse, the flavors of the quail and mushrooms were overwhelmed by the heavy-handed sauce and the result was very standard 'noodles n sauce.' That's what you go to Olive Garden or the cafeteria for and they should be more mindful of the flavors in their sauces.  What my mom ordered, however, was exceptional. She had the duck ravioli and WOW. The sauce was amazing and tasted of browned butter and mushrooms, perfectly balanced. The filling was silky but still substantial and the pasta was tender and beautifully formed. We all loved it.  For dessert we tried the chocolate and banana bread pudding with vanilla ice cream, topped with caramelized bananas. It didn't seem/look/taste like a bread pudding in the least, but it was delicious The banana flavor was similar to that of homemade banana bread and the chocolate was not overpowering. The only gripe I had was that they brought out the plate with the scoop of ice cream pretty melted and running over the plate, which just looked ugly.  I thought our server was brusque and aloof but my dad liked how he stayed out of our face and let us eat our food in peace.   I don't think I would come here again on my own volition. The Olio pizzeria next door is a lot less stuffy with better prices and better food. I had a lot more fun eating there and spent a lot less.
Came in AGAIN because bossies is soooooo good. Sam is the best. Great customer service and so accommodating. Love this place again and again and again.
Offensively expensive.  So-so cuisine.  Would you like to speak with a knowledgable sommelier and maybe get good value for your wine dollar?  Then don't go here.

How could you not give this place 5 stars???? Location, location, location!!!!  It is right on the beach.  Have a beer and stare at the beautiful Pacific  If your not in a hurry you can wait for a table on the beach... didn't have time for that on this trip but we were seated right away on the window.... almost as good.  Just had a couple beers and the shrimp tacos that my hubs and I split.  Just a quick snack during our bike ride.  Read other reviews that stated they paid for parking..... not the case if your ride in.  Hope to go back next time we visit Santa Barbara.

Went here for a friend's birthday. This is the latest incarnation of a great space that can never keep a business for some reason. Cafe Luck was okay, The Pan had small plates and good drinks, but never seemed to be open (weird licensing problems, I recall?), and now this latest from the Pascucci people.   Cocktails are pricey ($12) but not that special, watered down with too much ice. The bartender, though nice, got absolutely lost with all our orders and either overcharged (booo) or completely forgot to charge (free drinks!). Plates are small and took ages to get out of the kitchen, which should take no time for something like a hummus/baba ganoush plate.   And the cheapest thing: they are using plates from Cafe Luck! Are you serious guys? Buy your own plates.  Growing pains, or a harbinger of things to come?
This place is super cute. The food is very tasty and the prices make for a great deal! The staff is super friendly too. Definitely recommend.

My husband and I celebrated his birthday with a weekend in our favorite city, and a Saturday night dinner at Opal. We arrived around 7:50 for our 8:00 reservation, and were seated almost immediately. I think the atmosphere at Opal is romantic, yet still fun and upbeat. It's definitely appropriate for a date, or a night out with friends. My husband and I started with a salad to share. He loves caesar salad, but I'm pregnant and can't have the raw egg in the dressing, so we ordered the Mixed Gourmet Greens salad instead. He deemed it 'too healthy' (that's my husband for you...), but I really enjoyed it. For our main course we both ordered Filet Mignon. He had the Chili Crusted and I ordered the Herb Crusted, and neither of us were disappointed. The steaks had a lot of flavor. The Whipped Truffle Butter mashed potatoes that came alongside were rich and creamy. I was hoping my husband wouldn't finish his, but no such luck. For dessert we ordered a Dark Chocolate Ganache for Two Cake... Oh lord, can Opal do dessert! This cake is big enough to share, but you should just order two because you won't want to. Then again, I'm pregnant and my husband prefers unhealthy salads, so perhaps you're better at sharing cake than we are.  On top of everything else- the ambiance, the food, the reasonable prices- what sets Opal apart is the obvious care and attention that they show their patrons. The service is impeccable, and the owner goes above and beyond his duties to ensure that everyone who dines here leaves happy.
Their veggies always seem to be old and rotting. Perhaps convenient for processed foods.
Just got back from lunch at Del Pueblo and I have to say the bowl of Albondigas I got was outstanding. Super flavorful and fresh. Definitely a 5-star soup!

No.  Admittedly, I am very picky about my Cajun/Creole cooking. My family hails from Louisiana, where they know how it is done. And my mother still makes the best gumbo and jambalaya I have ever had...lucky me! Still, I have been to several restaurants of this theme that have satisfied me...this simply was not one of them.  First of all, what was with the abundance of pasta on the menu? There was so much of it, I thought it must be their special take on things and I should try it, so I did. How this pasta, called 'Jambalaya Pasta' or something like that, was remotely like jambalaya and not unlike any regular ol' pasta dish at a place like The Spaghetti Factory, I have no idea. Second of all, one of our maple muffins (complimentary before the meal) was hard as a hockey puck. No, really, that's what we did with it...it was a fun game at the table. Third of all, our waitress was terrible...a grumpy older lady who seemed annoyed from the minute our group of women sat down...and could not get things quite right, from my botched drink order to her hasty removal or setting down of dishes. Imperfection is fine, but there was never any apology...just little, unnecessary side comments implying things were our fault, not hers.  The best parts of the meal were the drinks (large, pretty strong pours) and the dessert (bread pudding souffle had to be ordered at the start of our meal because it is a 40-minute preparation).  Downer.

Don't even know what to say other than what the F#*K was that.....how dare you even call that food.... a toddler with a microwave and play dough could make more appetizing food than that...... just don't.... seriously grab a microwave sandwich form 7-11 you'll be a lot less disgusted which is terrifying
Pastries to die for! Hands down, best in Santa Barbara. Char and mom are super sweet and add that special charm that makes this town so great.

Highlight of this hotel is its location. Literally across the street from the beach front. The Spanish Renaissance decor and flare is appealing. Valet parking attendants are super kind, welcoming, and set positive vibe. Rooms can be very small (King no balcony).  Cafe Lido very good service food was fresh.  Major Management Issues:  Arrived at checkin time per reservation. Told we had to wait room not ready. Had already unloaded all bags to take to room and had to retrieve vehicle from Valet and put everything back into the car. Had about 1 hour delayed checkin. Front desk non apologetic and not informative of amenities. Needed to use bathroom during wait and was told to use my room key-guess what?! I had not been given one remember my room was not ready on time. Front desk gave me a temporary bathroom key reluctantly.  Room was cleaned quickly previous guests trash left in drawers, shower not cleaned (see pic), cups dusty and dirty. Had to make several calls and wait for housekeeping to clean it up AFTER waiting late checkin. Front desk offered complimentary bottle of wine (we never got one) later offered to apply $50 credit for food/bill.  Whole checkin ordeal set us back 2.5 hours wasted vacation time!  Hallways are never vacuumed (not during my four day stay). Not a dedicated janitorial for the hallways. Housekeeping in the room improved after first day.  Management needs to HIRE MORE STAFF. You stretch your housekeeping way too thin that is VERY OBVIOUS. I feel bad for them and it is reflected in your outcome.  As a guest I don't want to hear from your front desk how short staffed you are. Not my problem!!!
Always yummy. Food was fresh and service was great. Love this place. Def need reservations.

First of all, I initially never understood the name of this bar. But really, it's quite clever! They have 29 signature cocktails (more on that in a second). BUT it gets better. 29 is the number of the periodic table for copper. 'So what?' you might ask... Well, I was really impressed that that's the reason for the GORGEOUS and class copper bar. Also, who doesn't love a good Moscow mule in a copper mug?  Okay, so now that I've told you about clever I think all this is, let's talk about what a fantastic and unassuming place this is!  For starters, every single cocktail I've had here has been FANTASTIC.  My favorite is the 'Chai as I might' (also clearly I love punny cocktail names), but every single one I've had is delicious.  However, what keeps us coming back here is what they call 'Phil's cup'. This is a wonderful way to try a little bit of high-quality liquor that you otherwise would never get a chance. Basically, Phil's cup is a preselected and rotating 1 oz pour of a high-class spirit. Every single pour I've had from the Phil's cup has been out of this world, and I remember each and every one. For example, once it was a 21 year old barrel aged rum that was so smooth and silky as it lingers on your tongue.   My husband and I came here to celebrate the completion of my graduate degree, and it's really the perfect place.  (It also makes for a great first date location: classy, impressive, not pretentious, and relatively quiet).

One of the first times I went here, they had Mario Kart 64 going in the back, which should tell you how cool this place is...This is also one of our after-work happy hour spots (until 6pm) in the Funk Zone. Not the biggest on-tap beer selection but decent. The inside is aesthetically pleasing and filled with local art pieces for sale. There's usually live music whenever i'm there at night. Bartenders are very nice and engaging (Hey Brad!). There's a back patio area with it's own bar and tables for groups. Check this place out if you get a chance! 10/10 would recommend.  P.S - Look for the piano!

Cool new place in Santa Barbara. Jus tried it for the first time the other day and I'd say overall it was pretty good but they may still have some kinks to work out. The service was a little slow but that's understandable from such a new place. I got the burger which was good but a little on the cold side. Very well could have been an off night. I'll be back again to give a second chance because it is a cool and fun location.

Lunch here satisfied a craving after a recent New Orleans trip.  I enjoyed the red beans and rice, and thought the gumbo was pretty good.    They also have Cafe Du Monde coffee and beignets.  The coffee is comparable, and the beignets are good, but a little more airy than at Cafe Du Monde.

The Mrs. and I had a late lunch yesterday at Brophy Bros. to round out our day trip to the Santa Barbara wine country. We arrived shortly after 2 and lucked out on finding a parking spot being that this place is located in the Santa Barbara harbor. There are signs posted stating that there is a 90 minute time limit, but I don't think it is enforced.  We checked in with the hostess who said the wait would be an hour for two people, which we expected. Reservations are not accepted. She handed us a buzzer thingy and said it worked all over the harbor. FYI-You have to give the hostess your ID or credit card in exchange for the buzzer. There were a few locals that had shown up that seemed taken aback at the fact that THEY needed to put their name on a list and wait for a table. I'm guessing they didn't have to do that before COVID.  We were able to order some drinks from the downstairs bar and walk around the harbor while we waited. Brophy Bros. sit. next to the Santa Barbara Maritime Museum and shares the same building with some other small shops. It is a short walk from the restaurant which ends at the very ahem.....diverse Santa Barbara Yacht Club. If you are into awkward experiences, grab a drink and go sit on the seawall that runs next to the yacht club.   After less than an hour....our buzzer summoned us to the restaurant. The hostess said she had two seats at the bar upstairs. We were escorted up the narrow stairway to what is actually a small space that consists of a bar that runs the length of the room, a few tables for larger parties, and a narrow outside deck with more tables for twosomes and threesomes. I would say if you get more than 4 people at a table in the bar area, it is going to be tight.   The seats at the bar were tight. There is no separation between you and the person(s) next to you so if you are not vaccinated and COVID paranoid then Brophy Bros. isn't your spot. The Mrs. and I loved sitting at the bar. It had a great view and the bartender not only bartended, but he also cooked. Right in front of us he cooked up Cioppino, steamed clams, and shucked oysters. The smells were incredible.   The Mrs. ordered a margarita and I had a glass of Sauvignon Blanc. To start things off, we ordered a half dozen oysters and watched as they were literally shucked in front of us, arranged on a bed of ice, and in front of us in a matter of minutes. We aren't a fan of large oysters and the ones served to us were small/medium. Also, they weren't overly fishy. I had a cup of clam chowder that I have to say was one of the best I've had and it was literally served in a cup.   I ordered the fish and chips and so did my better half. I was shocked that she did because usually that isn't her thing, but she said it looked so good she had to try it. The fish was battered perfectly. It was flavorful and not greasy at all. The chips, nay fries, were a perfect golden brown and crispy. I've had fish and chips a thousand times and this was the first time that the chips were actually crisp. Usually, they are soggy.   There service we received at the bar was incredible and overall the staff was friendly and there were some pretty demanding customers, but they maintained their professionalism.   By the time we left around 4, there was still a long wait. Just be prepared and BE PATIENT.

We came here on a Sunday morning and it was fairly busy but they were able to seat us outside quickly and they were gracious about us having a puppy.  I ordered my standard 2 eggs with bacon and it was a nice serving. I didn't eat the toast but heard it was good.  My brother ordered the challah french toast and it looked like a small serving but after tasting it, it was incredibly rich and ended up being a perfect portion.  This is an order at the counter type of place but all of the staff was pleasant and offered to help get things like coffee and silverware.

Good food, nice variety, friendly service, good atmosphere & kid friendly. We dine here often and always leave happy. I think at busier times, they could use a couple more servers, but overall they're pretty efficient.

Don't understand what all the hype is about. After moving from the Bay Area and eating great Thai food, a friend suggested Zen Yai. Very disappointed. Normally I finish my plate, but couldn't at this place. Everything was either way too salty or bland. Stick to Tap Thai or Your Place Thai instead.

We had been trying to come here precovid but it was always so busy and reservations were needed to get a table. Now that they are finally almost fully opened my fiance and I were able to squeeze in!  First of all their tandoori Naan was so fluffy and you can tell them fresh. The crispy cauliflower does have a bit of a kick to it but it should be expected since it is Indian food. So if you're a pansie and think mild salsa is too hot then you should stay away from the Indian foods for the most part. The Barra kebab was so tasty I wish there was more of it! The halibut was cooked to perfection and the sauce was just so good. It all paired well. As for the butter chicken I definitely expected it to come with more chicken that what it had and some rice to go with it. The dish was very small so we ended up ordering some more rice on the side. Over all it was a good experience and will come back again to try something new.

This was a great addition to Milpas Street in Santa Barbara several years back you get to sit on the patio an order at the window and the food that they create is so beautiful and tasty they can provide breakfast or lunch they're not open all day so that something to be aware of they also have selections of beer and wine along with items for the kids if you're looking for something fresh and farm to table you definitely want to check out the shop kitchen I absolutely love their tugboats really there's so many things on the menu I love I can't even list them all regardless of what you pick I guarantee the taste will amaze you and he will be back if you're looking for a discount to try this place check out Groupon

Tucked away but not to miss!  If you're hanging in the funk zone and need a bite, Brass Bear should be your destination.  We ordered and they delivered next door to DV8, but I was so impressed with everything going on inside. Cute swinging chairs (for folks waiting for a seat), a bar with engaging bartenders, and a bustling restaurant scene on a Sunday afternoon.  This place is popular with families because they have a room next door where parents can drop their kids off while they dine! So smart. A bunch of toddlers were happily playing with a few toys while being monitored by someone hired by Brass Bear while their parents and friends could enjoy a meal without interruption (or additional cost of a baby sitter!). It's not offered all the time, but on weekends for sure.  While my SB friends talk a big game about their grilled cheese with ham, I went with their chicken salad sandwich and I know why their sandwiches are stellar. The bread was thick enough without being overwhelming or chalky like ciabatta, perfectly toasted, and with a hint of some earthy flavor. It complemented the chicken salad beautifully. I don't know what their recipe is, but I dig it. I also ordered the zucchini sticks with a Sriracha  tzatziki. They were grilled zucchini sticks vs fried, so a healthier side option! The tzatziki wasn't fire-y, but had a kick.

Great staff. Kiersten and Kathy do it again with a smile behind that mask  As you can see from the mushroom croissant that my friend had they are very generous with the mushrooms. Even though sometimes there a fair amount of people when you when we regularly find an outdoor seat  I bring my own sea salt.  It's particularly good on the mushroom cheese croissant

Wow.  This place has the best tacos in town.  Yeah, the line can sometimes get long, but so what.  It's worth it.  Located in the SB Public Market, so there's always a place to find a table or just sit at their counter and watch the chefs at work.  The Norteno (skirt steak) taco is my favorite.  So many flavors.  It just overwhelms you.  Try to eat slowly!  I also like the Ensenada taco (beer battered cod).  Great place.  Rumor is that they're opening a second location on lower State Street across from the Amtrak station where Union Ale was.

Aside from the outrageous room rate ($800), exorbitant overnight parking charge ($50), on top of resort fee ($50), in addition to just stupid food prices, this is a magical and peaceful ocean front resort to get away from the rigors of life.   And in light of covid,  they are taking great care and precautions to ensure the safety of their guests.  Getting a complimentary room upgrade to an amazing suite by a super friendly front desk person most definitely impacted my rating.  :)
I think they got a few new guys working the grill..not as good in preperation and the servings, as compared with the size of my usual Santa Barbara Salad, is much smaller.

I do enjoy this place. I used to like it a bit more a few years ago when I use to go to school here.   I do think the service is very good. They have someone who plays music outside, which is very soothing.   I have had several dishes, one being the pumpkin and shrimp pasta, which was a bit rich, but had great flavors! I also enjoyed the parmesan fries with bacon aioli, another rich dish,  but still just as good! The portions are definitely ones to share.   I can't say this is my favorite place anymore but I will always have a soft spot for this place, so many memories.

Always the freshest fish. Our favorite stop in Santa Barbara. Park near by walk in . Ask for a bag of ice to keep your buy fresh. Come early for best selection.  If you join the seafood club you will get 10% discounts on all your purchases.   Store is very busy and kind of rushed but everyone there is always polite. They seem to have down the customer service process so even if it seems hectic there is a method to their madness.
Love this place.  Good selection of wine and spirits.  The take-out food is some of the best Mexican food in SB.  If you haven't tried, you're missing out!!

Really 4.5 stars but bumped to 5 stars because they saw us standing outside, hungrily reading the menu, and they opened up a few minutes early :)   Three words: New Potato Pizza.  Remember these words when you order, and you will be doing the happy dance with every bite. This was one of the most well-balanced, flavorful pizzas I've ever eaten (and we're talking decades of pizza-eating experience here). The crust could have been a tiny bit more crisp, but that's a very minor criticism. I'd order this pizza again (and again and again).   My friend and I split the arugula farro salad with green goddess dressing. The greens were marvelously fresh, the farro perfectly cooked, the cherry tomatoes lusciously sweet and ripe. The peppery bitterness of the arugula was the perfect complement to the pizza.   I'm so impressed with my first visit here. Both items had such well-balanced flavor profiles with high-quality, perfectly prepared ingredients.  Deceptively simple, yet so difficult to master.   Service was warm and friendly, our food arrived quickly, and COVID-19 precautions were all in effect -- masks and cleaning protocols. Thanks, staff of Bettina, for a great lunch. We'll be back.

I know my noodles and Kai's has the best buckwheat noodles outside of Asia - believe it or not! I use to frequent this place regularly and still pay a visit whenever I'm in the area. The owner obviously takes pride and is always quick to say hello. The waitresses, likewise, are uber friendly.   It's nice to find a Japanese place frequented by Japanese. That should say it all! Spicy Tuna rolls, yakisoba, udon, and buckwheat dishes are highly recommended.   REAL JAPANESE!!

Still one of my favorite spots to grab a coffee and post up for some work in Santa Barbara. Very spacious facility with nice clean restrooms and a couple parking options. The coffee is delicious and unlike a lot of coffee shops in the area, this one actually offers filling breakfast options - double check on social before visiting but this has been the case every time i've gone. Great spot near downtown! Will be back.

The turn around on my cake order was so quick! I walked in and placed the order and came back the next day to pick it up. They have unique flavors, and I ordered the Tiramisu cake. It was a hit! It was also pretty reasonably priced. I will say the icing to cake ratio was very high, but that was likely because we ordered a small (4') cake. If you like icing, it's a great place!
Simple, fast, inexpensive, and delicious. Not to be missed. Don't worry if there is a line out the door...it moves quickly. Closed on Tuesdays and Good Friday.

Disappointing.  Since when did mexican food use cheddar cheese? My chili relleno was overpowered by the taste of cheddar cheese. Service could have been better as I ordered a second margarita and it never came.  On the plus side the margaritas we ordered were  tasty as were the chips and salsa. Not a place to go for authentic mexican food and you could tell by the clientele who were all white
Honestly the best hotdogs I've ever had, didn't know hotdogs could be that delicious lol

Hendry's is everything you want in a beach cafe: An open space with a big patio and well-stocked bar right on the beach. Great seafood too!   The bar is fantastic. It's the little things like ordering a glass of rosé by the glass and being asked if you prefer sweet or dry.   Parking can be a hassle with the popularity of the Boathouse and Arroyo Burro beach it's always worth it.

The Pina Colada is a SIX star drink. The rest I will have to try and update you on. Mixology is a strength here and I recommend you come and try.   Service is definitely good. I wish them all the best and will becoming back soon. When I can try the whole menu.
Awesome pizza. Great service. Quick and easy. Well worth the money. Large pizza is GIANT

Blush is good.  Really good appetizers - I love almost anything with jalapenos.  My burger was great and my wife loved the veggie burger.  Really good cocktails too.  Awesome service from our waitress.  Gets a little loud later in the evening, so get your talking done early.

It's too bad that this lovely looking space has the worst food and service in town.  Again, it is gorgeous inside and out in the back patio but the food was inedible.  We were a group of six arriving for happy hour.  We each ordered 2 drinks and we each ordered multiple items from the happy hour tapas menu.  First, we were told that we could not sit at a table if we wanted to order from the happy hour menu.  We looked around and noticed that at 5:00pm the entire dining room was empty.  Not one single party was seated in the dining room leaving tons of open tables.  Instead, we were told to sit on the reception area couches but we were not given any table settings to eat the food.  We were expected to hold our plates or eat off our lap.  They made an error on the drink order and we had to send one back.   The chips, salsa, and guacamole arrived and we all agreed the chips were stale probably from yesterday's batch while the guac and salsa were flavorless.  We ordered the fried olives and they were okay at best.  We ordered 3 other tapas which made it a large order in my opinion but not large enough to give us a table!  Then we each ordered the chicken, steak, and fish tacos.  The chicken was GREEN.  Then, at first when I took a bite of the fish taco, I could have sworn it was chicken!  The waiter assured me it was fish and as I took another bite I discovered it was rotten, hard, and rubbery- you couldn't even chew it was so hard.  Our group loves Mexican food and we all agreed we had never been served such flavorless, stale food.  The hospitality was completely absent and the wait staff did not look happy.  Then, I tried to order a second drink and was told that happy hour had ended.  I told the server, 'No problem I will pay more, just charge me the regular bar rate for that same margarita.'  He said he had to ask the manager for permission???   Truly a disappointing experience seeing as how lovely the interior decor is.  I'm not sure why they insist on cramming every one in the bar and reception area when the ENTIRE DINING ROOM WAS EMPTY ...but also, now I understand why it was empty- the food is disgusting.

Came here for dinner on a quiet weeknight, but the line was deep and did not let up. Staff is super friendly and the food is outrageously good.  The price can add up, but the ingredients are so fresh and every thing was top notch, down to the salsa and chips!  This is definitely a gourmet taco stand, so know that coming in. There is a lot of thought, heart and soul going into the food and the stand in general.   The La Lola Ceviche was one of the best I have ever had with Sea Bass, Tomatillo, Serrano, Avocado, Tangerines, Cucumber, Pickled Onions, and Habanero. The bowl is a really good sized portion and enough for two to share as an app.  The Butternut Squash Taco called to me and was so delicious. Butternut Squash, Queso Fresco, Salsa Macha and Pomegranates on a corn tortilla.  Mole Con Pollo is Mary's Organic Chicken, Black Almond Mole, Rice, Onion Mayo Crema and Fresh Cheese on a corn tortilla. Crazy good!  The Noreteno is Grilled Skirt Steak Asada, Oaxaca Cheese, Guacamole, Salsa Mugrosa and Cilantro on a Homemade Flour Tortilla. Fantastic.  Fried Plantains and Black Beans  The Peanut/Arbol salsa had great spice and so did the Salsa Cruda, which I could drink or bathe in!
Really not pleased with the visit tonight. I've had so much better from this place in the past. We had a hair in out fries.Nuff said.

You people that give this less than 5 stars are spoiled lil brats. Like the reviewer that gave Harry's 2 stars... because (ready for this?) because of the pandemic. What a tool.   This is a throwback. Not only from the booths, the decor, the pictures on the walls but the waiters and bartenders. It's fun. It's cool. It's iconic and it's something that many places in the US just don't have, character.   Love Harry's and thank you. Thank you for having something that is so nostalgic you can't help but love it.

Horrible customer service, especially the cashiers. Very high-priced items that you would think the customer service would be at the highest level. The cashiers are rude, unfriendly and unprofessional . As locals, we promise ourselves not to ever be back but unfortunately this store is on our way on a Sunday jog. There doesn't seem much hope as there are reviews from a year ago regarding this issue.

Great hangover food for sure! Had brunch here after a rough  Saturday night and the tonkotsu ramen totally hit the spot! Rich, thick, delicious broth, pork belly and the added soft boiled egg was fantastic. Spam musubi was ok- I thought the rice was especially thick and the spam was especially thin lol. The other highlight of our brunch was the tempura! Service was friendly and efficient. Highly recommended!

A new favorite in Santa Barbara! Delicious mussels, baked clams and salads. For a lighter lunch try the goat cheese and spinach salad! Stay away from the pizzas as no one was very happy with them. Fish and chips were delicious and so were the carnitas tacos!

Soooo good! Soooo fresh! We were impressed with the empanadas here in this quaint little spot. We had 6 different empanadas to share with the potatoes and chimichuri sauce and it was delicious. Highly recommend if you are in the mood for something different. Enjoy!

Secret Bao is one of the experiences that I was hoping would wow me. in a town with exhaustive options of seafood, California cuisine and Mexican restaurants, Secret Bao was a stand out.   a modern twist on Korean and Asian dishes like a Spanish octopus bao or Korean fried chicken bao. The shrimp tempura one was my favorite with shrimp you could really sink your teeth into.  and although there are many takes on a classic dish such as fried rice, Secret Bao's refresh with their pork belly fried rice, kim chi and furikake was a fresh welcomed rendition. great flavors and even a subtle texture with that dry fried rice.  my kids also couldn't get enough of their well-seasoned and crisp KFC. and we finished it off with some donut baos complete with strawberry ice cream and condensed milk.  there were so many other entrees we wanted to try but unfortunately our stomachs were too full from grabbing a snack beforehand.  though fortunately, that means there will be a next time! so next trip to Santa Barbara, we'll be coming for that udon carbonara and kalbi bossam!   a lovely restaurant overall and customer service also A+. thanks to Kathleen and their gracious hostess who topped off the already great experience! --

Always love Brophy Bros.  The online ordering and pick up were seamless.  We ordered clam chowders, salads & plenty of bread.  All were packed up securely.  Worked perfect for us since I picked up early and was planning on reheating later.

Drinks and food came out really staggered so one of us would have drink or food and the other did not. Everyone we asked to help is would ask what we ordered. Plates were really sloppy. Food did taste good and the manager did comp a drink when she realized the service was sub-par.

Ahi sushi is great! Very fresh and reasonably priced. Their uni shot is to die for. Mama Mia roll is delicious.  The friends I went with loved their rockin rainbow roll , seaweed salad, and teriyaki chicken. Sashimi is at a great price point. They have happy hour from 8:30 -close which has some options of sashimi. Normally there are daily specials with either sushi, ramen, entrees etc. The interior definitely needs some work. Every cup looks dirty, they probably so should buy new cups ASAP. It was very off putting to have these. Seem similar to a cafeteria style cup. Gross. But don't let that take away from the sushi. They need to remodel the inside. Ultimately, It's a little hole in the wall but could be nicer.
Love love love.   Been here 5 times since they opened.  A new favorite with a great set of servers (Katie and Peyton to name a few).   Grrrreeeaaattt food !

Been here 3 times - twice for lunch and once for dinner. Very good food each time. Can be crowded so be patient. Sit at the sushi bar. For lunch their fried chicken sandwich is really good but a bit much for one person.

My husband watches this golf blogger who recommended this place so we stopped by last Sunday!  Amaaaaazzzingly flavorful! Love love love You get in line to order and they seat you after which I appreciate as people tend to save seats I. Other establishments  We ordered the tugboat and the Chilaquiles. My husbands didn't add the pulled pork but I recommend you should as it comes with the egg and sauce. Never the less it was delicious! Very flavorful and portions were large  My tugboat was perfect! Fried chicken with a poached egg and this yummy sauce! Can't wait to come back and highly highly recommend. If we had more time we would have got some bubbles and sat longer

While playing in the light and shadows of burgerdome hoopla do your happy dance for Islands. Lots of competition in this market and consistency is key and that's what is happening here.  At this level it's a staff situation to make or break. Fun time burger names. VeRy good onion rings and good tasting burgers bookended by bottomless soft drinks. Sign me up Nancy.

Love this place! Their salads, pizza, lasagna and tacos are sooo good! Don't forget to try their french pressed coffee drinks. They make a darn good latte. Don't forget to ask for Marla as your server, she is the best!

Shang Hai has re-ignited my Chinese food addiction in the wake of the loss of Stone Age on Milpas Street. Nestled in an unassuming shopping center, one would not think much of this eatery from its humble exterior. Luckily, this is one book that should not be judged by its cover. Once inside, it feels clean, sanitized and precautious in terms of COVID19 safety measures, I am told by husband (who usually picks it up the order after I call it in.)   Shout out to Helen for her fantastic pictures of the Salt & Pepper Chicken. Over the years of Yelping in Santa Barbara, I have come to rely on the reviews of Lily Z and Helen D, especially in finding great Chinese food.   The Salt & Pepper Chicken is crispy and full of flavor with the addition of flash fried jalapeños and minced garlic.   Another favorite of mine is the Sesame Beef (or Sesame Chicken). It is slightly sweet with nutty notes from the sesame seeds.   Shrimp Fried Rice is another stable in our take out order. We like to substitute white rice for brown rice. Don't forget to ask for a side of Sweet & Sour Sauce! It is a family recipe made in-house from scratch with fresh ingredients opposed to the pre-packaged sweet and sour sauce used at many restaurants.
Tasteless  Cold Over priced  $70?? For this food ??  With all the other premium choices at public for sure won't be eating here again

We got one of the best tables for our breakfast Sunday brunch!  Service was great and they were very helpful if we needed anything.  I can't wait to go back and eat there again.  I would love to make this our go to place in SB.  Another plus it's at the dog beach so I can't wait to bring our fur baby!

Came in for breakfast on St. Patty's Day.  They had the requisite green beer I was looking for!!  Food was delicious.  The waitress was friendly but the service was a bit slow.  Overall 5 stars and I'll be back for sure!!

Updated review - I came here a few weeks ago for a quick drink and the service was great.  Chips and salsa were solid.  You can't beat the location.  I will give it another try.  Who knows - maybe next time it'll be 4 stars.  Cheers!!

Tuna cones. I saw them on Yelp and I needed to have them. Ahi sashimi, sesame, ginger, soy, chile chive in a sesame seed cone. The restaurant is on the waterfront and was a great option for a dinner when visiting downtown Santa Barbara. The restaurant was super busy (this was pre-covid) but we had reservations so we were seated pretty quickly.   For appetizer, we ordered the tuna cones, of course, which come in their own special holder which sort of looks like a wrought iron candle holder. Except instead of a place for candles it's a metal circle to hold the cone. 4 come to an order. And they were all I had hoped for. There was a good amount of time in that cone and the sesame  code was so tasty.   For dinner I had the Rigatoni with sausage. It was creamy and a little spicy from the sausage. Very satisfying. My BF got the cioppino. Cioppino is one of his favorite dishes and he really liked it. It looked like there was a good variety of seafood in there as well.   Overall it was a great meal and next time I am in Santa Barbara post the pandemic I would love to come back.

Hey Guys sometimes our best Experiences are Right under our Nose  The Breakwater Restaurant an Iconic Santa Barbara Eatery. My dear Friends Ed and Tanya Bailey Love the place, so when it was suggested I was Excited and that Excitement kept Building. Breakfast served until 3pm,Oh My! Basted Eggs  can be a challenge for some Restaurants but not the Breakwater! Basted Eggs  and Chicken Fried  Steak prepared to Perfection. Ed and Tanya both Enjoyed their Food as Well.  As we Celebrate the Christmas  Holiday season and Break Bread  with Family and Friends the Breakwater is a Great option or just about Anytime. Thanks To the Breakwater staff for the Great service and Oh Yes an Old Fashioned Ice Cream cone for dessert   Merry Christmas    and Happy New Year to my Yelp Family  in  2022

I am by no means an expert on Thai food, but when I eat here it feels more like a fusion. The dishes are not all that traditional, however there is nothing wrong with this. The food tastes great and mostly fresh but if you're in the search for traditional Thai food, this will not feed your craving. I do love the atmosphere that the lighting inside the restaurant sets, great for a date. One thing that I thought they excelled in was customer service. The place was bustled with guests and servers but we were greeted and seated almost immediately. After quite some time no one had approached us about drinks nor with menus. Finally a nice guy approached us and he apologized what seemed like a thousand times, he even threw in some free Thai teas! Besides that small kink, the experience was great and I have gone back a couple of times. It is not my first choice when I crave Thai food but also not my last and I would definitely recommend it to anyone in search of a chill date setting.

Tried 8 of their different wings. I'd say the bourbon one was my favorite. I wasn't a huge fan of the Thai peanut or the Parmesan wings, but the rest that I tried were decent like the chipotle lime, lemon pepper, and cali bbq. I ordered a raspberry coco mead but i guess that didn't have it in stock so the guy behind the counter brought out two different raspberry flavored beers for me to choose from. They also didn't have the chili watermelon boochcraft so we ended up choosing a different flavor. It was pretty cold out, and we asked if the overhead heater could be turned on and the response was that the heaters weren't ready, not sure what that meant, but we just ate our meal without the heater turned on.

All of the negative reviews kept me from coming here for a while, but I am so glad I finally gave it a try! My husband had the tri tip sandwich which was soooo delicious. The side of macaroni and cheese tasted like canned cheese instead of fresh which was the only drawback for me. I had the brisket and coleslaw which were both prepared perfectly! The best part though was the spicey BBQ sauce. Yum yum yum! We will definitely come back when were in the mood for BBQ.

Totally rad vegan options, whether you are a full blooded vegan or secret meat eater, I promise that the kind folks will not judge. Even our meat eating friends will agree that the 'cheese' board is amazing and you will not miss the taste of inflammatory dairy if you order this option.
Went to get another beer and in the one minute it took an employee took my bottle of water and threw it away! 'Sorry I threw away your water' and bring me a cup of tap. How about a free beer instead?

Their albacore tuna and veggie burger are on point. Parking is limited and can really be frustrating when you want to eat here. Better to walk if you're close by. You may have to wait a bit during peak hours.   The staff is friendly. There have been times our order isn't correct, but the food is still good. Since it's in the neighborhood is a regular spot.

Accommodating a large group of 12 on busy night in December is always appreciated. The service started off pretty sluggish considering it was a busy night and they apparently had just one waitress waiting tables. I totally get that but would expect a restaurant of such high caliber to be prepared for these situations.  Happened to meet the founder chef Cadario who seemed to be a pretty good at what he does. I must admit the food that we ordered was real good except a few misses like bread used for brucshetta was very crusty and hard to chew.  Honestly I am a nit picker when it comes to a real good brucshetta and the only pizzeria that impressed me is pizza express in England, UK.  Moving on to the salads with interesting cheese combinations was a hit. The puttanesca was just the way I cook pasta at home. Tiramisu was delicious but pannacotta not so much. Everything was fine but somehow I just felt it wasn't a five star experience hence the 4 is still very generous in my view.

Cool dive bar with decent drink prices. The place is super loud, which I don't mind, it's a bar... they have karaoke... football on the TVs, etc.  one of the cool things I noticed at the bar was their tap system, which fills beer glasses from the bottom of the glass, thus minimizing the foam... fun to watch.

Walked in for the first time and thought the restaurant looked great. Order a few dishes togo, and the results was not the best. Felt like pretty generic Chinese food with mediocre quality. Not sure if I would try it again, maybe if I'm in the area.

When you're sitting in the salon getting your hair done a couple of doors down, its lunch time, and you want a yummy  wrap that encompasses all of the ingredients of a reuben sandwich, you come to Montecito Deli and order the Reuben Piadina! The warm piadini fits perfectly into your hand. Once you bite into it, you get all the layers of the reuben sandwich bursting into your mouth! The pastrami perfectly seasoned,  the sauerkraut with just the right amount of sour and tart, and the pickle, tomatoes, and thousand island dressing. And we can't forget that yummy Swiss cheese that's perfectly melted.

I'd been craving good California rolls and my brother told me about this new place. Unfortunately the main sushi spot in town is Arigato Sushi and I feel that lately it hasn't been good at all (I ask you, how can you make a California roll salty?? But I digress...) So me needing and craving a new good place tried here. I got the imitation crab California rolls and MAN, they were AWESOME. I satisfied my sushi craving and then some, let me tell ya. I also tried the Crazy Rich Asian roll which had lobster in it instead of crab, truffle, and thinly sliced wagyu beef on top and those were also bomb (expensive but worth a try!) Their yaki soba was also great! The only thing I don't recommend is the gyoza. I found those gross tasting; especially the soy truffle sauce which I thought I'd love bc I love truffle but ended up hating lol. My brother enjoyed the cauliflower dish a lot too.   Honestly this place has great food and awesome atmosphere right on the beach and staff was nice and attentive too. I'm a fan!

I don't see this as a bakery, I have come in a few times and there never seems to be much there, might be a good eatery, we travel 2-1/2 hrs round trip and are disappointing each time so we no longer go here. And the staff seems not happy, don't greet you like they are being bothered that you walked in...

I had a such a lovely time. Manny did a great job taking care of us at the bar. He is incredibly friendly and welcoming AND quick. He was able to answer all my questions and promptly responded and delivered everything. The food and drinks were so delicious- the artichoke was great and the aioli it came with just took it to a whole new level. The lamb sliders - yum. The fish and chips were PHENOMENAL! Unfortunately, the duck confit fell short because the the fish and chips were so great. The staff was so pleasant. And the atmosphere is amazing. I can't wait to go back!

An average place for Thai food having lived in Los Angeles and East bay I know my expectation is high. This restaurant is in downtown prime location wiling dishes priced average $11. The portion could have been bit more. Ordered chicken pad see ew and green curry. The pad see ew was good but the green curry was bland and too watery. Bottom line won't be visiting this place again for thai food as there is too many other cuisine options in Santa Barbara. I will stay with my east bay restaurants for Thai food

Went to brunch with a friend recently, we sat on their outside patio. It was a beautiful day & the wall of greenery made us feel like we were somewhere else.  I got a coffee & my friend got an orange juice. The mug I got was big!  I ordered the lemon ricotta pancakes with a side of bacon. My friend got the eggs Benedict with potatoes.   The pancakes were so fluffy & the lemon flavors really came out with the berry compote. I didn't even touch the syrup they gave me!   My friend let me know she always got the eggs Benedict & they never disappoint. I will be returning to try their dirty fries, we saw them come out as we we're leaving. Wow, a large bowl of fries topped with  garlic and other stuff that smelled amazing.
Best kale peanut chicken salad this side of the Rockies!  And my husband loves the roast beef dip with creamy horseradish. Delicious
Fun place to catch a game, good mix of their own beers, cool use of decor, decent bar menu, but why does the men's room look like I'm peeing in a jail cell?  I guess the world will never know.

This is our second time here and it's only been 1 day. We can't get enough of this place!! From beginning to end, everything was amazing.  The flavors are so rich and gratifying. We've yet to order anything that we didn't love.  If you stop by their flat bread and hummus is an absolute must. Trust me, you won't regret it!! My husband and I are not even close to being vegan but if this is what it tastes like we are sold!! Additionally the atmosphere is comfortable and welcoming and the customer service is impecable.

Fresh and flavorful vegan lunch! We ordered two lemonades, one sparkling and one with rose, both were refreshing and light, not over sweetened.  Our lunches were the empanadas and the pulled mushroom. Both were spicy, the harissa sauce was no joke hot, but flavorful and worth the heat. The ingredients were all so modern and fresh. I ordered baklava to share and I was glad I did, the sauce that accompanied it was tart and a surprising flavor to pair with baklava but it worked! Normally it's a super sweet syrup but they went the other way and I loved it. Wish we lived closer, I'd go every week.  Hard to find a good vegan place that doesn't make all its food taste bland and the same. Every dish seemed unique here and I can't wait to taste them all!

This is perfection in Mexican food! The salad with mango's and oranges was incredible. Homemade tortillas with shrimp & that fantastic salad with rice was my choice & my Beloved had flautas.  There's no table service nor actual liquor but for this creative, healthy quality food, I can let that go!

We picked this place for their good Happy Hour deal and longer hours to celebrate a friend's recent engagement. We were all in very high spirits from the event of the day that we wanted to continue our celebration. Yes, it was a group of 8 and we may have been a bit loud. But it's not like you get engaged every day.   The thing that irked me the most, was the way Dan (the manager) handled the situation. We had already been there for some time, as we were winding down the drinks, food, and leading into dessert. When I guess we had a huge laughter outbreak, and he came over to us and yelled in our faces 'You guys need to be quiet!' while waving his arms up and down. Then he just walked away from us. Please, you're the manager. Show better respect to your customers and show you know how to handle situations.  It just put a damper on our celebration and a terrible putrid taste in my mouth.  Besides, the food here is just mediocre. Nothing special really. And at that price, you can definitely do better elsewhere. I will not be coming back here nor recommending this place.

I think I have tried all the ramen spots in Santa Barbara by now and Sachi was by far the best! Also, super underhyped on Yelp. I can't speak for their sushi or robata, but their ramen is the best in town! If you are only going to try one then I HIGHLY recommend the spicy baby short rib ramen. The flavors from the broth, egg, protein, were spot on. Perfectly cooked and the quality was there. Just thinking about it makes me want to go back asap. So glad I found my go to ramen spot!

This is the best Mexican restaurant in the area. I've lived here for 20 years. There are more Mexican restaurants that have extremely famous reputations and are very good, but for me this place blows them away. The food is authentic Mexican food delicious flavor and a great salsa bar. We love coming here it's so much easier to just sit down and eat some food then dealing with the other places that are such a scene and honestly a nightmare. If you want some great authentic Mexican food that's really easy and you can just sit down and eat, this is the place. The nachos are bomb

This is a tasty healthy place to eat, with Vegan options. Have had the Portobello Burger which was tasty. Have also had the Tostado which is very tasty and filling.   The servers have always been friendly and I have never had an issue getting my glass refilled which shows they are attentive to their tables.   As far as the restaurant itself; there is lots of indoor seating and even a counter you can eat at. They also have a few outdoor tables which are nice on a warm day. Finally the bathroom is always clean which is a good sign.

I love sushi and i love sushi even more here on the west coast because the yellowtail is OFF THE HOOK. So much more flavorful than i'm used to! So Sun Sushi is great because they really do focus on sushi- there are a few other dishes they offer like tempura but i mostly saw sushi on the menu. I love love love spicy tuna on crispy rice- consider trying it because it is yummy. We mostly got regular rolls and only tried 2 of their special rolls.   Parking is tough but you can park on the side lot in between sun sushi and the hotel next door!

Love The Middle Child!  Tonight we had the farmers market pizza and the White Shrimp with lemongrass, butter, garlic, wine, sweet peppers and toasted levain bread. The pizza crust is the best I've ever tasted. Everything was so delicious including the beer and Stolo vineyards Pinot Noir. The service was excellent as usual!  Next time we'll have to try some dessert.

This review concerns the bakery.   Even following them from their move to this location our family has been buying bread and pastry from Our Daily Bread for many years.   Not any more!    Over the past few weeks, the quality of their products has declined dramatically.  The cases are clearly filled with at least day-old products - choose carefully and look for volume so you can be assured that the breads/pastries were baked recently.  We have found that there are only a few types of bread/pastries in any quantity on the shelves - and if there are only a few left, they are clearly older than a day or two.   Their bread stales quickly so beware.  Shouldn't bakeries clearly label and at least discount day+ old products?  Last week we purchased a few croissant, pastries, breads for a party. The croissants were deformed, oily (where's the butter?), dense, tough and heavy - the baker clearly has no concept of what a real one even looks like, let alone tastes.   Ditto the pastries - Costco does it better!  After a bite or two, we tossed all of them.   The counter staff is still hard working and charming, but we will be taking our business elsewhere.     Stand warned.

3 words: I'LL BE BACK.  Cute little corner bakery/restaurant that really delivered on food. Fresh and tasty - perfect for a beautiful sunny Santa Barbara morning. I kind of want to go on and on about it, but sometimes the simplest reviews are the best.  That being said:  - California Omlette: delicious! who knew having just fresh tomatoes inside would make such a wonderful meal. Fruit and toast on the side.  - Mango Chicken Quesadilla: also wonderful! I'm going to try this at home myself! the salsa they have is so fresh and really counters the sweetness of the mango. Cesar salad for me on the side.  - Lemonade: fresh, squeezed. all good  I have to say I had some food envy of other tables - I'll be back!!

I've been addicted to CFA for years and thought it was a shame when I moved here a year ago that the closest one was in Oxnard. Naturally, I was thrilled to learn a store was opening in town.  It's embarrassing to admit how many times I've come here since it opened, and each time, my experience has been about what I expect from Chick-Fil-A: tasty food made fresh (that hasn't been sitting under a heat lamp for hours), clean inside, and friendly service. Their speed of service was slow at first, as is typical of a newly opened establishment, but has been picking up noticeably.  Where this location still lacks, however, is consistency of flavor. I'm not sure exactly what they're doing differently, but my chicken and fries rarely taste the same twice. It's still good, but tends to be slightly off the mark from that classic CFA taste. For that reason, I'm withholding a star -- but hopefully, this will be resolved with time.  Welcome to Santa Barbara, Chick-Fil-A!

Opal gets it's fifth star for the wonderful server that we had. My wife had the salmon and it was awesome. I had a fish special which was cooked to perfection. Our server gave great suggestions and even better service. Give Opal a try!

Really tasty food. I had a craving for pad Thai and this place did a fantastic job. We also ordered the pork belly fried rice and although it was a bit spicy for me, the flavor was amazing. In the end we had left overs which will be great tomorrow.

Good service and good drinks. Dog friendly with water bowls & menu items for your canine friends. Tried thr outside seating on street street which had nice chairs and tables. Server was excellent and made sure we had our drinks in a fast fashion as well as correct food. Spacious but cozy with a nice view of the foot traffic & outside activity occurring in the city.

Hidden off state street, in the Victoria court yard, you will find this cute lil restaurant with indoor and outdoor seating.  Came for a quick lunch with my oldest, and she wanted pho. After wandering state st for a second, we found the restaurant and decided to sit in the lovely patio, with shade.  I ordered the banh mi with pork, and a small pho for my daughter. The lady asked if I wanted the banh mi spicy with jalepenos and I said yes!   After a few minutes our food came out. The banh mi was tasty, bread had a crunchy crust, but soft interior. Pork was tender and flavorful. Fresh cilantro and pickled veggies. The jalepenos added little heat too.   The pho was another level. I've had quite a few in my time and around town, but was blown away by flavor of the broth, tender cuts of meat, and side toppings, and that was all from just a slurp bite from the lil ones bowl! She also agreed that this was a better tasting pho around town, and happy its close to home. Will definitely be back!

I am writing this review and giving 5 stars for the only dish I get when I come to Sambo's; their california eggs benedict. I had never had an eggs benedict before this experience because I wasn't eating eggs but my friend insisted we try this place one day after work (great breakfast spot after working night shift). Absolutely delicious. The eggs are poached to perfection and the hollandaise- THE HOLLANDAISE - is worthy of being eaten along, with a spoon, out of a jar. Its the right amount of savory with a bold flavor that other egg's benedicts haven't been able to compare to. The home potatoes are sauteed with chive or green onion or something that makes them so tasty. I'm addicted. Just as a contrast, I thought I could have this same dish during my stay at the four seasons. No comparison! I feel fortunate to have been able to have Sambo's be my first eggs benedict experience (lol), yes it's that serious. The service is always prompt and the complimentary yummy mini choco chip muffins you get while waiting for your meal are a nice touch. This place is for gluteny, and nothing less.   The only thing that's slightly weird is these little girls, that are waitresses, wear such short shorts! It makes me slightly uncomfortable. Also, there's outdoor seating with beautiful views of the ocean.

Nice place! The architecture of the place is very classic Santa Barbara, and the servers were very cordial and accommodating. I ordered their pot roast (a special for Tuesdays), and it was HUGE. Really flavorful and tender, too! After eating my fill, the majority of it was still on my plate.  Bread and salsa as a starter was a bit bizarre, but it works! We also ordered the calamari as an appetizer, and were a bit saddened how small the rings were. It was still yummy, though.  I would definitely go here again.

My husband and I have been fine dining for decades. While we enjoyed the service in the wonderful lighted patio and the food was tasty, we found the menu overpriced. The moderately sized halibut entree would've been on the high side at $46, but we paid a whopping $56, which wasn't worth it (and the menu listed $54 anyway). At these prices we should've at least been served an amuse bouche. Also the lemon tart was very runny. And we didn't get all our leftovers as requested. We won't return.

So happy with this new addition to State Street!  We dropped in Wednesday night which happened to be their opening day.  I never expect much the first few weeks that a restaurant is open but I was surprised that everything was fantastic. It may have helped that there were only 4 other tables when we were there, so service was very attentive. We shared the fried dumplings, topokki (rice cake appetizer) and the seafood soup.  Everything was flavorful and served super hot.   I am looking forward to trying more things on their menu.  If I had to mention one critical thing it would be that there are no descriptions listed on the pictures in the menu, so you are left having to ask the server what dish it is should you want to order it.  Otherwise, everything was delightful, can't wait to go back and I hope they are able to keep up the good service and tasty korean food.

Found this place while I was looking for a ham and cheese croissant, and D'Angelo's Bakery did not disappoint--the croissants are excellent. They sell out, so calling in advance to get one is recommended. Along with ham and cheese, they offer plain and chocolate crossants and they also have an amazing selection of freshly baked bread loaves; sourdoughs, ryes, ciabattas, country wheat, rosemary and more. Delicious!

Probably one of my favorite coffee shops I've been to. Cool vibe, nice and open, and killer coffee. They do a good job of rotating all the roasts and flavored coffee daily at the serve yourself bar so you can go a while without trying the same roast twice. Love this local favorite.

I came here for dinner with some family friends. The food was great (I had the shrimp gnocchi-delicious!) but what really stood out was the service. Everyone was incredibly friendly and helpful, though not in an intrusive way. Also, the fried olives were surprisingly amazing. I recommend for a classy evening.
Food was decent. Beer was good. Atmosphere was as expected in a bar. Overall, didn't impress me, but I didn't hate. It either.
the best sandwich shop. Really great service, so faste and pleasurable since the sandwich come out hot and quick. The prices are inexpensive to. Friendly owners and servers Try it.

Soooo gooood! I've been a fan of South Coast Deli for a few years but, this was my first time at this location. Friendly staff, clean, quick service, and great location. The food is always fresh and delicious and made with quality ingredients. If you get a drink, try the lemon berry açaí stubborn brand soda, it's the perfect refresher!

We were ridiculously surprised and happy with how amazing this place is! We came by back in December to see the place closed for the family being on vacation so we were kinda bummed but hey we made it back now and were extremely happy with our whole experience!  The place sits on a corner with a small parking lot in the back, don't expect to find parking there and opt for the street parking which allows 90 minute parking, plenty of time to eat.  entering the restaurant we were hit with the crazy heat from the kitchen which is just behind the ordering counter, but luckily there is another dining room to sit which is much much cooler. at first we were looking up at the board thinking that was the menu, but then a cashier hands us menus to read in hand so we thought those were the same. that is, until i looked up again while ordering to see the items on the board were the 'specials'. ah well, next time we will take a better look at the menu board.  we ordered three plates that you don't normally find at a mexican restaurant, seems here they do more 'hard to come by' menu items that don't necessarily succumb to the gringo pallet.   we also ordered horchatta which was cool, sweet, and smooth, as well as a pacifico beer.   each of the salsa was also surprisingly flavorful! usually these spots have a bunch of salsas that either all taste the same or all you taste is spicy concoctions of chiles. BUT the salsas here each had their own unique and tasty flavor. we are so used to adding salsas to each of our meals we felt bad when all we used the salsa for were the chips and barely used any for the food itself.   the first dish we ordered was the Tacos Plaza de Toros, which is three tacos each topped with steak, mexican chorizo, and chicharron, and a side of guacamole and salsa. initially i thought each taco would be a separate meat, i.e. one steak, one chorizo, one chicharron taco. BUT come to find out, each taco contains all three meats, which isn't bad at all! the steak is tender and flavorful, slightly dry. the chorizo was also a little dry but had a great kick to the taco, and the chorizo was more like bread crumbs but piled on nicely atop each taco. the tortillas were soft and fresh, and the guacamole was absolutely delicious! i thought about adding salsa to the meats since they were kind of dry but then the flavor was still so good i didn't want to ruin or change the flavor of the taco!  the next dish we had was the Chile Nortenos which were two giant poblano chiles stuffed with monterey jack cheese and shrimp with a chipotle sauce. the chiles were huge and open face, tons of melty ooey gooey cheese, perfectly cooked shrimp all in a delicious chipotle sauce with a tortilla underneath it all! this was absolutely delicious, filling, comforting, and so so good! it came with a side salad and rice too which was hardly touched as the salad was kind of an odd side dish and the rice would just be wasted space in the stomach. but i did have  abite and the rice was good.   our last dish was the Quesadilla de la Esquina. three puffy quesadillas stuffed with oaxacan cheese, poblano chiles, huitlacoche, sour cream and other herbs and spices. this also came with a big side of guacamole and salsa. The puffy quesadillas were warm, crispy, golden brown, the cheese was stuffed and loaded into each quesadilla, the veggies and other fillings were all delicious and covered in the delicious flavors and ooey gooey cheese! oh so good!  at first you think this place is kind of expensive, but then when you actually see the plates, with how much food and how much guacamole, then all the salsas available, and then the quality and taste of the food, it is definitely worth it!! our meal was about $50 but not a single penny was wasted or thought to be overpriced. we will happily return and enjoy another meal!

Have always ordered from here and never had a problem, have always tipped good as well because I'm always satisfied but today was no bueno. Had ordered a stuffed cheesy bread with bacon and jalapeño and they didn't put any bacon or jalapeño in it and it came as flat as the pizza. And the pizza wasn't even cooked all the way, looks like they were in a rush and just threw everything on at once. I called them back and this girl answered I think her name was Marian , but she was rude and unprofessional when I had told her what was wrong, she told me 'well did you ask for the pizza well done' should I have to ask for my pizza well done or are you guys just sending out raw pizzas? She had the worst attitude I asked to speak with someone else. Wont be going here again, rustys has better pizza and customer service! Give them a call (805)564-1111

I went there again and the food did not taste good. It is not even close to authentic as they claim it to be. Mexican food is so delicious and can be made vegan easily. I don't know how anyone could insult Mexican quinine like this and get away with it. The prices are way too high too. I am vegan, but I much prefer just going to a real taco shop and ordering their vegan option to eating at green go.

It has some work to do... The grounds are beautiful but for the price you pay, it would be nice to see one really get spoiled here. Valet seemed great. Friendly. So were the bellhops. After a long drive, we were pretty bombarded with concierge at the front desk. Just needed to breathe for a second and not plan every moment of our stay quite yet. The hotel is quite secluded and could use with more food options. Even casual fare, snacks. So, bring your own perhaps. Do NOT eat at the Bistro.  If you want to eat at Angel Oak, make resos well in advance as the even the bar area fills up. The rooms are dated. The floors need retouching and the mats need to go. They look very old. The hotel provides slippers but bring good ones or socks from home as the floors are very cold. Bathtub and shower were great as was the comfy bed and neat balcony. The partial ocean view was gorgeous. We noted a trail in back of the hotel that you can walk or jog on. So peaceful. Ocean for miles. Also liked complimentary water bottles, coffee and the red wine in the room. There is a safe in the closet. The room was clean and quiet. Very important. The winery on site - 3 stars. I saw some small, cute dogs with their owners so pet friendly here! Just something to be aware if if you have bad allergies or are sensitive to their barking. The lobby lounge was alright, dated again. Costly but great service. The flowers in the lobby were so beautiful. They are said to have an amazing florist. No expense spared here. Did not use the pool or hot tub but they looked ok. A more quiet pool would be outside the spa. 18 plus (adults only) and no cell phones. The spa shop was amazing with the best brand names and even had some clearance items. We made purchases but did not experience services to comment. The gift shop had some great items for purchase too and the staff member closing up that day was super nice. In short, we would not stay here again.  There are too many amazing choices in Santa Barbara for the same or less money.

First time here. Guy that took my order seemed super overwhelmed. Maybe he was new? My 9 yo twins wanted spaghetti so I asked his opinion on portion size. He said they'd pry be better off with their own dishes, so I went with it. Their plates were HUGE (smalls). I didn't appreciate the dishonest suggestion or maybe lack of knowledge. We also ordered a medium pepperoni pizza that my 12 yo shared with me. Took almost an hour. Plus there was a lack of communication bc some of their staff didn't speak English so when I went to grab 'my food' he shook his head 'no' bc they called two orders at the same time....they did however have quite a bit of people come in and out while we were there and it was between 245-345...so maybe that's good? No idea. Won't be back, though! Also, they Are no affiliated with Giovanni's in Orcutt!

as we drive by:  her: 'oh holy sh*t, it's dark as f*ck' me: 'where? I don't see it' her: 'It's GOT to be a nice place. it's so dark in there'  A nice place it was indeed.  Took the lovely lady here for valentines dinner after digging around yelp.  What actually attracted me to this place was the picture of the pork belly that's on the yelp page.  SOLD.
