I love my meat with slabs of fat still attached.  Went for the 3 course sampler meal.  Portions were SMALL.  I felt like the amount they gave us couldn't even feed the baby.  but 3 courses + down time waiting in between courses + bread, i was surprisingly full.  Waiter knew his wines and helped us wine n00bs out with pairing something delicious to have with our meal.  But alas, I have more complaints.  I got the pork belly, and the fried rice that it came with it was extremely salty.  Looks like the chef just kept dumping the soy sauce in it.  It was also a bit stuffy, and the noise levels were a lot louder than i expected (its still not loud though, just that from the outside, a dark place makes me think its a real quiet place), so be warned.     meow.

Has anyone noticed the slight smell of possible plumbing issues?  And, the overuse of bleach? Cover-up?  Anyway, the bakery products are fine but on occasion my bagels and bread were hard as a rock.  Make sure you ask how long the baked goods have been sitting out.   The other menu items are over priced and obviously not fresh.  Are all the chain establishments going towards pre-prepped microwaveable food? No, thanks!

Thank you Thursday! I want to say a big thank you to Meet Up for all the amazing food over COVID. They adapted to the COVID life by adding online ordering to lots of platforms and then established safe measures such as digital menus and online ordering when dining inside. Instead of glassware they switched to plastic that looks like glass and water bottles. When I had the Chinese food craving, it has been my go-to order for spicy fish fillet in chili oil, general tsao chicken, orange chicken, sautéed green beans, and hot and sour soup. Tons of easy parking and friendly staff make this place a win-win! I can't wait to place my next order.

To be honest, I was expecting a wider variety of entrees here. Nevertheless, for was delicious. The real novelty is the muffins they bring out with wacky flavors (all very tasty). Their gumbo was great, and their fish items are also great. I just wish we went when the performers were there, it would have added to the experience. All and all, this place was delicious, but pricey.

Wow! We've been in Santa Barbara for years and how are we just now finding out about this place?! Stephanie and all the staff are just phenomenal, so sweet and kind! Stephanie even cut our tuna for us so we didn't have to do it at home! The fish is so fresh and so reasonably price, why would you go anywhere else? Definitely a local hotspot! The ahi sushimi grade tuna is only $25 per pound and if you sign up for their loyalty program you get an on going 10% off! What a deal! So excited!

Love hitting this spot for a quick and fun lunch. Find myself frequenting almost once a week now. They have great, clean inside seating as well as a nice patio area. You can also grab to go and eat at courthouse garden across the street. My favourite is KFC bao.

Went on my lunch break. (12:30-1:30) got there right at 12:30. It was fairly busy. Three of us got seated by 12:35. Brought our drinks we ordered and an hour later still no food. Asked our waiter how much more time and he cut us off mid sentence and said he would check. He went about what he was doing for the next 5 minutes, never checking. People behind us got seated around 1, and got their food before us. We never got our food and we left at 1:30. Won't be coming back.
Beautiful setting!  Great location! Good service!  Small plates! A little pricey! We were there the 1st night.

I really like this place, thank you Yelpers for your very awesome referral.   The space is intimate, the food is simple, the service is great, and the prices are wallet friendly.   6 of us brought my mom here for her birthday and it was perfect, the menu has a handful of small plates and appetizers and a couple salad options as well. It is French so lots of butter every where, but that's expected. I liked that the menu was simple, no over complicated puffy menu items but the usual things like Veal, Lamb, Flank Steak, and Black Cod. All of which we ordered, and all of which we loved. The black cod especially was divine. I loved the brie cheese with the veal, adds a nice creaminess to the milanese style.  For small plates I would suggest the escargot, it actually comes out in an egg roll, the snail was tender not chewy and seasoned well. Mushroom crostini wasn't bad either, again, beware, lots of butter.   Our server was very attentive, and it works out in our favor that the space is so small, she couldn't really go anywhere without walking by us. The restaurant also has really cute outdoor seating, and since it's located in a quiet alleyway, you won't have to scream at your date due to cars driving by.   And the cherry on top? 3 small plates, 6 entrees, 2 bottles of wine, 6 post dinner coffees and 1 dessert later, the tab was an awesome $280 + tip. Everyone, and I mean everyone, loved their food and and experience. I'm so glad that I came here, I'm definitely going to make sure that I'm a regular when in town.

This spot is to die for! And I'm not being dramatic. It's that good. I literally have dreams about their veggie burrito. It's the perfect amount of spice and if you want to add a little heat the side salsa always does the trick.   As I'm sure you can tell my go to order is the veggie burrito. It's full of beans, cheese, onions, mushrooms and zucchini. I know what you're thinking. Zucchini? In a burrito? I know, it's not usually my jam either but Yona Redz does it right. All I have to say is give them a chance.   I have not been since they have gotten their liquor license so I have yet to try any of their drinks. But if they put as much passion into the drinks as they do the food I know they won't miss.   Looking forward to my next visit with much excitement

Literally been waiting in line for over 20 minutes. I don't know what the issues is with their staff but they seem to be majorly understaffed to handle the customers that come in. I literally saw over ten people walk out from just the wait alone.  Update: I finally was seen and the bartender was majorly friendly...except... he manhandled the fruit that he pressed with his bare hands...sans gloves! If you want a bartender to handle your beverages with his bare hands, please come here!

Good, Americanized Thai cuisine. Fantastic staff--attentive and friendly. It's really tough to find decent Thai in Santa Barbara, and this was my go-to place while I was there. Try the Pad Woonsen.  Dress warmly if you want to sit by the front solarium area as it gets chilly in the evenings.

This is where you go when you are a tourist or a new student at UCSB and think that the Pier is the only place that exists in the city.   It is lovely, yes, but it is overpriced and there are many better restaurants in the area.  The same can be said for Longboard's upstairs.  The food is decent, but again, overpriced.  I happen to be a bigger fan of Beachside in Goleta, which I believe is owned by the same guy and if memory serves, is a bit cheaper.  For finer dining, go to Sage & Onion or Ca'Dario and leave the pier for your afternoon walk.

I LIKE IT A LOT. I've never been thrilled about tapas just because in my experience they're bland... and too vegetabley... aint nobody got time for that. THE PAN, however, is awesome because the serve little burgers and meaty delicious things. This place is excellent because I can never finish an enormous dinner entree by myself and each little nomnom is only $4-$6. Between 6 of us we only spent about $10 each (+ tax and whatever) and got a million things: 2 burgers (which I didn't try but looked great) chili-cheese dog (which was AWESOME. tasted like good quality meat.) chicken pot pie (something i've never really liked but it was so buttery and great) truffle fries!  onion rings! (pretty impressive proportions on both of those, fed at least three of us) tater tots! (fried mashed potatoes! truly a miracle) two of my friends got the fried chicken sandwich which was suuuuper tasty. we also ordered a bunch of desserts:  the frozen bananas were great, the apple pie was served in an adorable cast-iron thing, and the pudding came in, like, a condiment cup (possibly the only thing that seemed proportionally ridiculous? but still tasty!) I had a Firestone 805 Blonde Ale, $5. great. I don't think anyone knows how great this place is, it was pretty quiet for a Saturday night. I want to go here every weekend forever, because I hate crowds and often dream of truffle fries.

We came here after a long evening in the funk zone. It always catches my eye when we are in SB but I always forget to make reservations.   We were able to get a table on a busy Saturday night but we did have to wait an hour. It might have been a shorter wait for the bar or communal table. The waitress must have said five times that it was family style restaurant and that her job was to make sure that we didn't order too much or too little. I've lived in So-Cal long enough to know that this just means they make dishes that are a just slightly too small to be a full entree but definitely the price of an entree. That way you are tempted to buying three plates for two people on average. I wasn't too hungry so I ended up getting the scallop dish which came with this amazing corn polenta. Note that they call it a 'platter' and charge $34. It's not really a platter, more like three large scallops spread out artfully on a plate. Unfortunately they were out of the pretzel which seems super popular as the people at the two tables next to us also tried to order it.   Anyways, the flavors, ambiance, and drinks were excellent. Definitely met my expectations and all the hype.

I suffer from serious nausea from longtime workplace harassment. This place is a Godsend as it was at one point, all that I could keep down because of my stress. I am so happy that Santa Barbara finally has an excellent place to get fresh pressed juices. Thank for being around and I wish you many years of continued success;

As a girl who's had kitties around her entire life, but who's current landlord does not permit pets, this place is basically heaven. The cat room is a very clean, plush and comfortable space for both kitties and humans. Relaxing jazz music plays in the background, an aromatherapy mister gives off a light and pleasant scent and I sit on a fluffy pillow sipping a latte and petting a purring fuzzball. Bliss. Why go to a coffee shop when you could come here? All the kitties are available for adoption and I wish I could take one home, but since I can't I'll make a habit of coming here to support this unique business!

Definitely a must have if you are a fan of Thai food. I can safely say that this is one of the best Thai places I have ever been to... Had my fair share of heavy, oily dishes that made me feel disgusting afterwards. But the pineapple fried rice was perfect- the meat was perfectly cooked, the rice was perfectly cooked, the veggies were perfectly cooked as well. All seasoned well. Also. Can I mention that the pineapple fried rice and pad see yew were deliciously moist? Unlike most Thai restaurants! Left feeling happy, full, yet light.   I heard about the grandpa that owns? works? at this restaurant. I CAN TESTIFY THAT HE IS THE NICEST MAN EVER. My friend requested limes for her pad thai.  He came back shortly after with lime juice that he squeezed into a little dish. He also courteously gave me an extra dish so that I could scoop the fried rice onto it.    Sidenote: my friend ordered the rad nah, but it did fail both of our taste buds :( Otherwise, love this place, wish they could come to LA!
Personalen var super trevlig, mest lokalbefolkning. Rejäl amerikansk frukost.  Vi åt majsgröt, cornbread, bacon, hash brown m.m
S.c.d. Grilled cheese is my go to here. Good place if you need to grab a quick lunch and there's also some great outdoor seating if you want to people watch. The staff is also friendly.
Great service.  French fries are stupendous and veggie burger was  very good.

A definite must! Loved the breakfast here! Best cafe latte I've ever tasted! So yummy! My only complaint about this place is that there are too many excellent choices to choose from and just choosing one left me wanting to go back. Loved the outside seating and overall experience. Didn't feel like a slop house at all.

SAMA SAMA is where its at, ladies and gentlemen! We came into town early on a Sunday morning and were excited to have lunch. We found parking nearby and took a stroll on State Street. I loved seeing all the expanded patios for the restaurants and that they finally closed down the street to pedestrians only! Due to COVID, you have to walk up to order and then choose a table outside and take your number with you. I ordered an iced coffee that came in the prettiest glass and reusable straw! Onto the food!  We started with crispy rice salad which was a really fun dish to eat. The lightly fried rice kernels with the meat and veggies added an interesting texture and a fried egg was on top. My husband got the friend chicken bun, which you can get steamed or fried, and he chose steamed. Pickled onions, greens, and an aioli made this sandwich and perfect for one person to chomp on. Then came the green papaya salad and the signature wings. HOLY WOW. The crunch from the wings were unexpected and perfect. The sauce hit your taste buds like fireworks with the marriage of sweet and spicy at the same time. No wonder these are the most ordered item! Its a great dish to share between two people. Pairing that with the fresh salad was a great idea, and both of us were satisfied at the end. I can't wait to come back to order an entire table full of those wings!
Excellent selection of cocktails, middle eastern fare, and really good service. Best place for drinks in a beautiful atmosphere!

A very interesting experience.  A small Italian restaurant tucked away into a quiet parking lot on upper State Street, Via Maestra 42 gives off a very homely and cozy atmosphere. With very limited seating inside (their 'patio' is deflating...just a handful of tables in front of the parking lot), you may have to wait for the chance to grab a table, but it'll be worth the wait.  When we first arrived, my mother made the [ironic] comment, 'These are really small tables. There must be really small plates.'Lo and behold, the portions here were modest at best, but the taste was exquisite. The portobello ravioli, one of the specials that evening, was rich with flavor, finished beautifully with a delicious espresso and accompanying gelato.  I'll insist that the meal you have here is for the quality of the flavor, over all else. Wonderful service, wonderful meal, and a must for anyone on the hunt for a great experience! Cheers.
Old fashioned + apps and entrees ... Friendly staff and cheerful atmosphere with friends make this Fourth of July happy ...

Food was amazing! My friend and I ordered the calamari to start. Then I ordered the pineapple fried rice, and my friend ordered the pad thai. The food tasted just as incredible as I imagined. It's places like these that make me a huge fan of Thai food. Our server, who I wish I could give a shout out to, was extremely nice and accommodating to all our needs. It was a lovely experience :)

My experience at The Black Sheep restaurant was great. The location was nice, the food was flavorful and tasted delicious(chef's kiss). Our waitress was super friendly and offered great suggestions as to what food to try. It was really nice of the chef to stop by our table to ask us how the food tasted, i really appreciated the gesture. Definitely recomend others to come try the flavorful food!

I've driven by this place about a half dozen times in the last year and always thought that I should try it. It's got a welcoming, clean look, is only one block from State, and has its own parking lot. Today wasn't going to be the day either, but when we found that the sushi spot we wanted to hit was closed, we pivoted to Kanaloa. I'm here to tell you that it was a great choice.  I had the grilled fish sandwich and fresh chips. My wife and daughter got poke bowls. It was all extremely fresh and delicious. I'd do it again in a heartbeat. In addition, the service was very friendly and helpful, ordering was a breeze, there was plenty of seating, and the food came out quickly and perfectly done. I'm not sure it's always this awesome, but I simply don't care. I will be back.

The Cruisery has easily become one of my favorite spots in SB. The front gives you somewhaf Finneys vibes: restaurant, good food, alcohol. But the back area is truly where it's at. They have fun cocktails, great beer, games to play, and an awesome vibe. The bartenders are super attentive and quick! I come here basically Thursday through Saturday and Tyler has become my favorite bartender. As soon as you walk in he greets you, takes your order, and gets it right every time. He's a great conversationalist and makes you want to leave your tab open all night! I would definitely recommend the champagne Papi beer or the white claw cocktail they have (forget the name). Also, plenty of TVs to watch sports from either the bar, couches, or table areas. Now that they're serving brunch on weekends, I'll be here all day every day!!! Highly recommend.

Casual and fun place with a cozy patio and a simple yet delicious menu. We were seated right away and sat outside. We enjoyed the company of locals and friendly dogs. With a couple of draft beers (from a nice selection of draft beers and local wines), we nommed on the roasted pepper pizza and sourdough grilled cheese and tomato soup. This is a great spot for a casual bite!

Bummer. Love Test Pilot and The Good Lion so had high hope for this.  But Shaker Mill couldn't be more corporate and soulless if it tried.  Our bartender (the one that had been there for a while but was quitting his job soon) was barely human. Maybe he was over it here and that's why he was quitting.   He made us two drinks, one of them being the miniature Pina Colada. Do not order this if you want to feel anything on your brain, BTW.  I'm not someone who needs their drink to be large but this is one drink that needs to be served in a larger glass. This Pina Colada is laughably small and just no fun.  I even spiked it with a float of Hamilton and it was still light on booze. A loser of a drink.   We decided to cut our losses and go to dinner.

Just had breakfast here this morning. Wife had breakfast enchiladas and I had the chilaquiles with a small refried bean bowl tostada. They were both delicious. This was sadly our last meal in Santa Barbara as we will be traveling down the coast but it was a nice way to end our cuisine tour here in town and easily the best breakfast we had during our stay, which also included stops at Chad's and Renaud's Pattiseree.
Great food, nice staff, reasonable prices... one of our local go-to places!
Gluten-free pizza!!  And tasty, too.  Also... their calamari is TO DIE FOR.

The food is mediocre, but the view is beautiful.  So fun to sit on the deck on a warm summer night with friends.  Just be clear when you speak with the hostess.  Otherwise, she'll seat you inside. And if that happens, you might as well go elsewhere.  The food could be so much better.
I always have great food and receive great service at this restaurant. Planning a brunch there tomorrow for my birthday because of easy outdoor seating, which is Covid friendly. Looking forward

Probably the most mediocre food I've ever had in Santa Barbara. The concept sounded so good. A Mexican inspired grill and bbq. Their website was enticing but the food we got was nothing like the photos. The chicken was tough, bland, under spiced. Real barbecue is dry rubbed and smoked all day until it falls off the bone. The accompanying potatoes were an afterthought. The grilled salmon was over cooked after the server said it came medium rare. The wine by the glass was hellishly overpriced even by Santa Barbara standards and the so-called cobbler was simply a bowl of cooked berries and apples with ice cream on top. No crust. This place needs to step up their game or risk closing. Weirdly, The Paradise Cafe that occupied this space for decades was also mediocre at best. Maybe there's a curse on the building.

Overpriced, but decent food. Nothing has really wowed my taste buds here.. then again, I haven't tried everything on the menu. I've had 4 entrees total from here, never want to go back.. I only go here when I have my axxess card. Lol. Still, the food is decent and it's a classy looking joint. You could do a lot worse in Santa Barbara.

Really disappointed with this food. I ordered online to go and it said 40 minutes. That's a long time for to go food. And it took the full time. I get home so excited about the chicken tikka masala, and they were generous portions, to find the chicken seriously dry even in all of that sauce and the sauce had a boatload of sugar in it. When you order savory, you expect savory. This was so sweet it could have been on the dessert menu. The lamb saag was a little bland with big chunks of what I think was the shell from cumin that I had to pull out of my mouth. The naan was delicious, but one cannot live on bread alone! I guess I have been spoiled throughout my life with great Indian food, which is my favorite. In 16 years here, I haven't found any place I thought was great.
Adoro o local , dentro do shopping a céu aberto na state street! O atendimento é exemplar e a pizza maravilhosa!

Being one of SBs only veggie places, I am sad to give it poor marks (since I'm a veggie eater).  Been going here for years (since it's one of the only places I can eat) and I've discovered the two things worth paying for: Golden Dal and Cobb Salad.  So, this is my main complaint...they charge you an arm and a leg for plain steamed veggies with no flavor...c'mon, I could do better than this in less than 10 minutes at home! Why pay $13 for microwaved veggies over plain brown rice? Yea, it's one of those places.  Tomato sauce tastes like it's Hunts from a can...bleh, can't be bothered to throw a few herbs in there?  I tried a few vegan desserts and they were terrible.  It was little more than vegan margarine with a dash sugar thrown in for the icing and a flavorless oddly chewy brick for the brownie.  They do have some great desserts  though, like the carrot cake (not vegan) but it's totally hit or miss.  I do love the atmosphere there, however, very homey feeling with lots of wood, plants, and warm lighting.  Le sigh....if only they actually COOKED decent food...for my money I'd get thai or indian.
Try the blackberry margarita! It is to die for with it's sweet agave syrup and powdered sugar.

We enjoyed our experience at Sama Sama!  We had the refined wine that was very rich in flavor.  For our food, we had the brussel sprouts, noodles, Vietnamese wings, and signature wings and we were very very pleased!  The flavor was beyond our expectations. This place is a must try in Santa Barbara.

My parents took me here when I was visiting for delicious, fresh seafood tacos. The tacos were awesome, the fish delicious, and the selection of drinks interesting and tasty. The counter person was a bit on the snarky side, but everything else was fab. I enjoyed the patio seating as well.

Excellent food, friendly service, reasonably priced.  Been there about 10 times since they opened and enjoyed everything I ordered, every time.  It's great to have a local vegan restaurant that is clean, tasty and run by a nice owner.  Thank you Far!

This is the best breakfast in SB and by far the best croissant I've had outside of France! When I first discovered this little gem my partner and I went 4 days in a row. It's consistent, the staff is friendly, the owner is very kind! I feel good supporting this local business and hope to see them grow!     UPDATE: I love y'all. I can't imagine the struggle you have faced staying open through covid. We've been supporting throughout but no longer. The new girls at this location constantly forget to put in my order. Your online system isn't set up optimally so I can't place my order on there, but I can't pay ahead of time if I call... which results in my food getting cold me waiting in line forever watching my bag on the counter ... etc. and now your back to normal hours but I can't order food after 13O ?? That has not been the case before it's a real bummer but I don't see us coming back anymore ~

We called Roys to place a Togo order today 4/8/2020, we were politely greeted by the woman working and answering the phone. Our food was as always, COOKED TO PERFECTION.  There is a deal going on today where each entree was $20.00! What a steal considering this chef knows what he's doing in the kitchen!  I ordered the New York Steak it came with soup, salad, potato cakes, veggies and bread.  My girlfriend ordered the Seafood Pasta which also came with soup and a side salad.  And of course... we got their famous bread!  During a time like this, us locals are very thankful and grateful that Roy's is still open and feeding the community. Thank you Roy for always working hard and putting your love and soul into your entrees.  We will be back!  -Breana and Carissa

Such a fan! I stumbled on this little treasure about a month ago. A friend and I stopped by on a slow night to check it out. We ended up sharing a few drinks because their menu is so unique! I decided to ask if they do parties there as my birthday was around the corner. They allow you to make a reservation online (for parties of 6 or more).  The staff is very kind and fun, and they do an amazing job on their cocktails. If you're looking for a great cocktail place with a good atmosphere this is your place.  Drinks are reasonably priced, and their selection of drinks are some of the best in town.

I am so glad I finally got the chance to try out Hook & Press! First off, let me just say WOW their flavors are so interesting and delightful. I highly recommend pre-ordering (even the night before) to ensure that you'll get your choices. I ordered the Coastal Fog for a friend's birthday, and the Blood Orange Creamsicle + Browned Butter to share.   They are certainly gourmet donuts (or 'yeast-raised rings' to be more precise) and are definitely worth the extra price when you take into consideration all of the thought and expertise that goes into crafting each ring. I believe the flavors are often rotated to ensure that there is constant creativity and newness to the variety of flavors - and I really adore that. But I think that there are a few flavors that are consistent and do not switch out - I believe the browned butter is one of them?   Speaking of the browned butter.... OH MY GOODNESS is the browned butter a delicious donut!! I'm serious - the moment I took a bite into it I melted in love. I know that's sort of dramatic because I'm talking about a donut here, but I'm serious, try one yourself and you'll understand my hype here. Dramatic metaphors aside, the folks at Hook & Press have nailed it - they have done an impeccable job at maintaining a professional, approachable, and super aesthetically pleasing business, and they truly deserve the awards that best represent SB's dining scene. For my specific experience, the Staff was suuper accommodating and friendly, and I could tell how busy they were -  I can only imagine how crazy it can get on a historically busy day (e.g., Valentine's Day), but I can definitely tell that the staff is so friendly and genuine.   I only wish that I could have the super power of eating all the browned butter donuts I want and still have a healthy blood sugar :-) Alas, I am only a human.

The BBQ'd oysters are TO DIE FOR!! The Blackened Ono was done to a T. Not over cooked, but just right! Dreaming of going back for more! Great wine selection, decent draft/bottle beer selection, outdoor seating is comfortable and staff is topnotch. They don't take reservations so you might want to go early, especially on busy weekends.

Came here on a whim after searching for a place on Yelp... lo and behold we arrived at 4 Eggs and Pizza. It took a little while to find it bc it was within the complex through the hallways right behind Arigato sushi.  Saw the menu and it looked pretty intriguing and the pictures made me crave for some pizza. We were greeted by Ron, the owner, and he was kind enough to explain and describe the menu/pictures. We told him we were kinda bummed that pizza wasn't served until 11am, but he said it wasn't a problem, he just get the oven ready with no problem at all.   We ordered:  the English breakfast (no black pudding- at least for this visit)- the eggs were perfect with yolky goodness, sausage was good and mildly spicy, the ham was seasoned well (don't let the pale color throw you off), the beans reminded me of pork and beans, and had mushrooms and tomatoes. Brought the baby, and he loved eating every morsel of that breakfast.  Half 5 meat pizza- Mmmm. So good! Just the right amount of crunch for the crust with tasty toppings!  Half BCR pizza- bacon chicken ranch. Turned out to be my favorite of the two halves. Tomatoes were juicy, bacon was crispy, and the chicken wasn't dried out- perfect!   Was thinking about having the pizza to go for later in the day, but Jade, Ron's wife recommended to eat it while it was hot. All it took was one bite... we only had 1 slice left LOL. Guess we'll have to come back again for another pizza. Although we were one of the first people this morning, Ron and Jade really took care of us. Brought out every condiment we asked for, took the time to get to know us, shared the story behind the name (4 Eggs... 4 continental areas and their foods- Americas, Europe, Asia, Africa). Ron love food and cooking and it shows in the quality and taste of our entrees. Jade cooks the Vietnamese food, but we were too stuffed to try any other food... next time.   If you're looking for a casual, friendly, tasty, and welcoming spot with a nice outdoor patio... well, you can find it here at Ron and Jade's 4 Eggs & Pizza. Thank you for your great food and hospitality!

Chipotle Club my favorite n the best in my opinion. This monster is so big that I can't even finish it. With half of it you'll be satisfied. Excellent costumer service specially from Ricardo. Great man!!
If you've not been here you're missing out! Always such fresh fish, creative rolls & excellent service. I'm a big fan of the 'trio' nigiri the 'yum' roll & the eel bowl. Whatever they make is delish!

One of my all time fave bars for 3 reasons:  1. Awesome whiskey selection 2. Pool 3. Jukebox.  I spent many a dollar and many a night here, and i doubt i will find many bars i would feel more at home at
Umbra pizza was so delicious, tastes different with great aroma and interesting flavor due to the black Italian truffles
super cute little cafe with lots of healthy and delish options. they make housemade almond milk and don't charge extra for alternative milk!

This place is small gem in downtown SB. It's a great place to relax and have some drinks, cheese and a light meal. The restaurant is rather small, and the kitchen is out in the open in the corner. But don't let that fool you, bc the food is great. I ordered a glass of wine, bruschetta, and gnocchi with pesto. The food looks small but was a good portion for on. Everything was very simple, flavorful, and fresh. All the pasta and gnocchi are homemade! I would definitely come back again.

I don't do Thanksgiving so got food to go for the holiday that I enjoy in a quiet solitary peace. The smoked fish platter, the bahn mei sandwich and salad are so good that I had to yelp. Can't wait to go back and sit in their parklet and dine with friends.

Reasonably priced, this Italian spot has a rare dish on the menu, a meatless pasta whose sauce was so delicious, it needed nothing else. You can add chicken, but it was great as is. Staff is nice, and I will be on my third visit soon. Very good! In fact, the second time I was in the mood for a hamburger, and it was excellent, too! Go with your mood.

Best. Tacos. EVER!!!!!!  I rate these the 2nd best tacos I've ever had (and I've had plenty throughout LA and SD). I came for the ahi tuna tacos as seen in the Yelp photos, but every single taco here is amazing.   You can get a trio of tacos for $9 (I highly recommend!) which is a great deal because you can mix and match any tacos you want, and each taco ranges from $4-7. 3 tacos may not sound like much but the portions were ginormous. My bf and I can both eat a good amount, and we were struggling to finish the last taco.   Now onto the actual tacos, here's my list starting from my fave:  1) CARNE ASADA STEAK- fantastic fantastic fantastic. Steak seasoned to perfection and perfect amount of tenderness/chewiness, with the perfect ratio of meat and condiments. Every bite was a piece of heaven and time...moved...slow.   2) AHI TUNA POKE- The ahi is fresh and insanely tasty, and comes in a crispy, fried shell. This one is SPICY! If you're someone who can't handle spice, I would request for them to not add wasabi. It's also generously loaded with fish. Really hits the spot for that sashimi craving.   3) BATTERED FISH- The fish they use I think is cod. It's buttery and flakey. I just love the word 'buttery fish'. The batter is thin and crispy, like fish and chips, and in taco form, it's just divine.   4) CARNITAS- I only had a bite of my bf's taco but it's very tender and tasty. Solid taco.   5) BANH MI- Also only had a bite of this because I was stuffed to the MAX. The meat is seasoned sweet and it's also very deicious. The cilantro adds a nice flavor.   Can we also talk about their flour tortillas?! So good, I'm obsessed.  The only downside is they mess up your order sometimes. My bf ordered the Gangnam taco but he got the banh mi one instead. It seems like mix-ups happen pretty frequently here. The plus side is that you can't go wrong with anything here.   I'm so in love with these tacos I came here again for breakfast the following day.

I spend all my money at this restaurant, as a minimum wage earning human, in order to appease the addiction I have with the fresh quality of the fish.  Also their drinks do their job and are delicious.

Got the tbone steak as the special and thought it was ok. Not too much flavor in the steak for some reasons. I also got the scalloped potatoes as an upgrade and it was just ok as well. This place gets such rave reviews but everything was just ok and I wasn't astounded. I'll pro baky try another steak place next time.

Stunned we are by all the five star reviews! Relatives? Friends? Who knows. The pasta is routine beyond belief, the inconsistency startling (sometimes it's al dent, sometimes it's Olive Garden mush). In a city with so many competitive dining options including the amazing nearby Pascucci and Persona wood fired pizza, it is indeed a wonder that this place survives. If you're hankering for Italian food you have many superior options.

I like the atmosphere here, energetic and cozy. I used to get the pizza here often and I eventually stopped, it's just not impressive to me, looks better than it tastes. I eventually switched to the sandwiches which I find are more 'special'. Recently came back for pizza and wasn't disappointed or impressed.

Bad service! Came here at 2pm mid week and they told us it would be a 45 minute wait. After waiting about 30 minutes they rang our buzzer and upon entering the inside of the restaurant it was nearly empty!! The deck outside was full but probably 1/4 tables inside were being occupied...I was baffled they made us wait. Next, when they sat us, they gave us the table by the bathroom in the corner!! By far the worst table in the entire place all the while all the tables with the gorgeous widow view were empty. There was literally no reason to treat us like this. Management needs to look into this.   2 stars because the Spritz was good.

Thank you so much for all that you are doing to stay open through all of the changing and Covid virus that we all are enduring. I am truly looking forward to enjoying State street as it once was and finding you open after 1 am so to get fed really good pizza and salads. I am glad they allowed you to have outdoor seating too. I'll be coming back up to have more pizza this week. An hour and 10-minute drive but it's worth every mile. You and your crew are always polite, and the quality of everything down to your salad dressing is amazing. Please stay open as quality family recipes such as yours are really hard to find.

After having such a disappointing breakfast at their cafe next door, I was hoping their cupcakes would make up for it...which they kind of did.  We ordered the Crushcake (red velvet cupcake), Strawberry Blush, and Chocolate Chocolate cupcakes.  Overall, their cupcakes were good and moist and frosting was not overly sweet.  We liked the Strawberry Blush the best.  If you like cupcakes, Crushcakes is worth a visit for a little sweet treat.

Omg I crave this place every day! You can't go wrong. My favorite is the Tri-tip sliders! Seriously if you need a place to go, go to Shalhoobs. It has been 3 weeks since I have been there and I keep trying to carve out time in my schedule to go to Shalhoobs. EVERYTHING IS PHENOMENAL!

I would more so recommend to come to sandbar, when it is just a restaurant rather than when it is a full blown bar.  It's located right in lower state which is convenient.    It's a small, cozy place with fun decorations and overall vibe.  The employers are lively and provide pretty good service.  The food most definitely will put in a coma.  It is delicious don't get me wrong.  The portions are quite large and they provide you with unless chips before (which if you have limited self control, could be a problem).    Last time I went I got the supreme burrito with carnitas. You could definitely share it with another person. The burrito is huge on its own and it comes with rice and beans too. I wouldn't say that it was the most authentic meal I have had but it definiely hit the spot and fulfilled the craving. I recommend splitting a plate with a friend.Their drinks are pretty good as well and are decently sized.  At night, on the other hand, Sandbar becomes a completely different place.  I like to go out on the weekend for sure but Sandbar is not the place I choose to go.  Since it is pretty small you just end up in a cramped space that becomes really hard to navigate. It also gets pretty awkward cause there always ends up being at least one old couple trying eat some food that didn't know it turns into a bar and then proceeds to stare at every one in anger.

Jane fed my fiance and I real well during out short day trip up to SB. Not sure where to eat lunch, we consulted a tourist mag and saw an ad for Jane and were interested. We found it right away and were excited to try it.  It's like Jane took diner food and gave it a major upgrade. We started w/a large plate of tempura-fried red onion rings drizzled in balsamic glaze. We finished them w/out any remorse - well, a little remorse since entrees followed.  Ben F. had the El Macho burger - bc he's pretty macho. It was big and messy and tasty-looking. I had the Grilled Chicken Sandwich - usually not my style but HELLO! Fried brie?! Are you kidding me? It was rich, buttery and greasy. YUM. The skinny fries that came w/our meals were OK...nothing in comparison to everything else. The side salads were excellent as well. We couldn't finish our food of course [damn you and bless you, onion rings!] so we took our half meals home.  We had a great day in SB and this lovely, Spanish-style [what ISN'T Spanish-style in SB?] restaurant was the cherry on top. It should be a fried cherry if anything.

I'm a really big fan of the Old Town Salad and Gobbler Burger. Both delicious items from the restaurant... just BE CAREFUL & double check when you order something TO GO because they are not always on top of getting the whole order correct. I've had TWO instances (in the past few months) where I've been really excited to eat my favorite salad with the best dressing ever (Tahini) and so I ordered the Old Town Salad To Go (because I work downtown and only have a quick 30 minutes to pick up my food) and came back to work only to find out that I have a box with chicken on the side, and a box with my salad (with rice, carrots, feta cheese, tomatoes, etc) but NO DRESSING which is just really disappointing because I swear I order the salad mainly for their delicious dressing and I can't even go back to get the dressing because of my time crunch. And now the salad tastes super dry and I'm not sure if I'll be back to this specific location because I'm not happy with them forgetting to put dressing on my to go salad multiple times...  On a positive note, I love that they put the chicken in a separate box so that it doesn't make my salad soggy. In addition to this, I don't think that some of the employees have enough training here..  An example of why I think they may need more training is because every time I order something on the phone (more often than not), I tell them that I would like no onions and they usually always tell me that 'they aren't sure if there are onions and that they'll just write that in the order notes'... In my eyes, I feel like they should know what each menu item contains rather than just guessing and writing it in the notes.  Anyway, food is good, service isn't bad, just make sure you double check any to go orders before you leave their location.

We went to Oku Sushi one night, and WOW this place was jamming, however we had made reservations for 2 people (yes make sure you make reservations) they are very accommodating.    Our server was very helpful with suggestions, being that my mum cannot eat raw fish, so we started off with the Krispy Korean Cauliflower, and I did not really know what to expect, as I don't really like cauliflower, well OMG!  This was the best cauliflower that I have had.  We also got the shrimp and crab with ponzu, both were so good.  My mum got the Snow Crab California Roll and I got the Way of the Dragon.  I loved my Way of the Dragon, and mum thoroughly enjoyed her crab rolls.    Will definitely be back here next year, that's for sure.

Nachos are the best in town, with quality tortilla chips :) $5burrito wednesdays is an amazing and tasty treat! Ive gotten the veggie burrito many times and I love it! Also, the salsa bar is fire! So many good salsas ranging from mild to really freakin hot (habanero). Customer service is always friendlu and the inside of the place has a nice warm vibe. They also have a bogo axxess deal which is great too! I love Los Altos!!
So much fun!! If you want to gain a pound get the eclair swan for a desert splurge! Decadent to say the least!!!
Cafe latte was great and coffee had great pickup. There a huge roasting machine in here so you can see the process from start to finish .

When I saw this on DDD, I bookmarked it right away. Ever since I moved down to Santa Barbara about a year ago, I've been dying to go to pastrami heaven, but with my work schedule and my boyfriend's school schedule, we never quite made it during their opening hours - until two weeks ago, and sandwiches just aren't the same anymore.   We ordered the Pastrami Reuben. My favorite part had to be the kraut because it was the perfect balance to the salty pastrami. I loved the sour kick, the toasted bread, the melted cheese, and the Russian dressing. It's everything you look for in a sandwich. When I first saw the prices, I thought it was a little steep, but once I received the sandwich, I understood why - it is packed, PACKED with meat!!   This is definitely a must if you're ever visiting Santa Barbara. I can't wait to come back and try their other sandwiches!

All the hooha about this place because its French and wahwah.  It was good food, snotty service, and snottier clients. I am admittedly one of those snobbier clients  :)  Thats because my croque monsieur standard is crispy but light, not burnt and cracking.  And because they charge a robbing $1.85 per macaroon. SERIOUSLY? Only in Santa Barbara do  people who've never had a real macaroon pay for this.
Love this place. Great margaritas! Real ingredients and good options. Also, really nice service. Will definitely come back and recommend this place!

I went there with my kids last two weeks in a row. Everything from the spicy tuna to the chicken bowl the fried calamari legs and even Musubi was great. Great staff great service fivestars all the way

Located inside Lama Dog, the Nook provides patrons of Lama Dog a terrific option for food. Serving mainly burgers and fries, you can't go wrong with any to pair with your beer.   I got the lama burger with truffle fries ($1 upgrade) and I was pleasantly surprised with how juicy the burger was. The patty was so juicy and the bacon was terrific. I love how the brioche bun held its integrity too. I was highly recommend getting the chipotle aioli to put inside your burger. The fries were great but a little on the salty side. I guess that's why you have your beer to drown out the saltiness.   All in all, the Nook serves up great burger options while you enjoy your beer at Lama Dog.

We love Chase!  The food was wonderful and the service even more so. The atmosphere is warm, fun, inviting, cool! We came while in SB holiday shopping and were wowed by the amazing decorations; everywhere, hanging from the ceiling, atop the bar, and every other place one could be squeezed in. A complete delight. I had the grilled salmon which was excellent, and my husband loved his chicken piccata.  Terrific experience!

Decent but bit pricey, but isnt all food in SB/ Goleta area? haha  The thing i like about this place is that they give have real ramen to offer and its pretty decent ! Costing around $10 bucks a bowl! This is probably the only legit ramen place is SB. Yummmm... ive also tried their oyako don and it was really good but i like edomosa oyako don bowl better because it is more generous and they gave you more chicken and egg ratio rather than alot of rice at edomosa i mean. But the rice here is very moist and it has alot of sauce to its rice js not a good egg rice chicken ratio for the price.   ive also tried their tempora calarmie its really good but you only get 4 pieces for $7  The rolls here are also decent, but a bit on the pricey side! i'll probably be back cus its the only place to get ramen around goleta/sb area also,  The decor is very japenese like very cute:)
Worst poke bowl place that I have been.  The rice was so warm that it made the whole dish warm.  The regular size is 2 small scoops of protein while most other places will give way more.

When we first arrived, we were told the wait would be 45 minutes and with nowhere to sit, we quickly thought maybe we should go somewhere else. The cool part was that they took our phone number and called us when our table was ready allowing us to enjoy some drinks across the street. I had a a great experience here and will definitely come back when I'm in town

WOW! So disclaimer, I walk by Santa Barbara Craft Ramen quite frequently and most times, it's packed. But this was another spot that for whatever reason I hadn't adventured to... ROOKIE MOVE! They have what is in my opinion the BEST ramen I've had in Santa Barbara. It was flavorful, you get a GREAT serving size of meat, and the noodle to broth ratio is perfect.  I additionally tried both the chicken karaage and the Haley street roll. The chicken karaage is seasoned to perfection and you get such a great portion for the 7 dollars. The Haley street roll was fresh and I didn't even really use my go to soy sauce wasabi mixture, which says a lot about the sushi. I'm pressed it took me a solid 3 years to actually go to this restaurant. But when you know better, you do better... period.
Yum! Dawn Patrol has been one of our favorites for breakfast since their doors opened. Love their Build a Hash and gluten free pancakes. Customer service is always friendly. Highly recommend DP!

I cannot tell you how disappointing Bella Vista was for New Year's eve. Oh wait...yes I can.  If you read my review for the Christmas Day buffet from last year, you'll see how joyful I was. So, given the fact that New Year's eve was 60% higher priced, I expected the same joy. There was little joy. Yes, they had the blinis and caviar station, and the sushi station, and oysters on the half shell and alaskan king crab legs....they also had a couple of cooked seafood items (seabass and lobster corn dogs) and soup and a couple of salads and a couple of meat dishes to take away. But there were no carving stations, no cured meats, no cheese display, and on and on (just read my list from last year's review). The worst part? They couldn't keep things stocked. The caviar station was always out of either blinis or caviar or creme fraiche...always. The sushi station couldn't keep up. And so on. The most fun item was the freshly made cotton candy. After that, the selection of sweets was fairly small.  Personally, I won't go back. Your glory has faded, Bella Vista.

Absolutely beautiful beachside resort with classic Spanish Architecture, charming rooms and beautiful views.  Even if you are not staying here, I would advise you to enjoy a meal here at Angel Oak or the Bistro and soak in the amazing views of the ocean and Goleta coast!  Great service from the staff even during these covid times.  Staff do not enter your room unless cleaning is requested.  With the exception of my last stay, rooms were impeccable.  (Last room had some issues including a stain on the bedding and a smell of smoke in the room and the front desk promptly moved us to a different room).  I've been helped by Madison at the front and she has always provided exceptionally friendly and hospitable service, even when it's 4pm and everyone wants to check in. The grounds are hilly and with the adjoining hiking trail, you'll definitely want to bring some walking shoes to get around.  Enjoy the jacuzzi and pools and make sure to use the online RSVP system for the pools.  My only suggestion to the hotel would be to stagger check in times since there seems to be so many people In the lobby at check in, I felt rather uncomfortable and waited until 5pm to check it to avoid the bulk of the crowds indoors.   Overall, this is one of my favorite weekend getaways from LA, highly recommended!

Shang Hai has the BEST Chicken Chow Mein I have had since moving to Santa Barbara. The noodles are a great texture and not too greasy. The amount of chicken in the order is a healthy portion as well! I loved the fragrant green tea that was brought to the table when we sat down. I will be returning to Shang Hai for sure.

Went to Red's last night. The band and atmosphere rocked. Even the photography hanging. But not so much the bartenders:( They took there sweet time. One charged me $12 for two cocktails (I tipped $2.) Then on the same next round the other tall bartender charged me $15 And the drink was WAY weaker. Of course no tip for you! It was maybe a shot of vodka.  T.I.P.S = To insure proper service

You could NEVER go wrong with delicious Mexican food! I enjoy getting takeout from los agaves on Milpas, the agave enchiladas are to die for if your into see food . The staff is already friends and the managers are always on top of things. Definitely recommend trying them out. You will not be disappointed!   Thank you los agaves !! :)
Stopped for a quick breakfast after a run on the SB waterfront.   Popular spot.  Food was good and service attentive.   Egg options were plentiful and we enjoyed all meals.   I recommend this place!

We had a great time here. I was worried based on the reviews. Our waitress was a doll and the food was very good. We had fish and chips- freshly battered and fried!! YAY. A very tasty cheeseburger and surf and turf. The lobster and steak were done just perfect. Plus they have Summerland Sauvignon blanc.  :)

Just stopped by for a quick taco.  Great little arrangement for outside seating. Heaters to keep you warm.  Fast and attentive service.  Tacos, beans, and rice were perfect texture but need some salt. The regular salsa was flavorless and like water so I asked for the hot salsa which was 1000 times better. Have to try more items before rating higher so I plan to go back.

Thank you so much for my amazing birthday day.  Everything was insane and I enjoyed cooking it.  I'm not sure of the persons name who worked yesterday but he was super accommodating even when I asked for stuff not in the cases.  Made sure to hand pick me the best products.  I made a seafood linguini and shared with my family

The view at Oku can't be beat, but I wondered what getting to-go would be like. The fish quality is superb! The rolls and nigiri were so fresh and so delicious that I was blown away!! This was the decadent to-go order I had been wanting after cooking every meal for myself for some time now. The XO shrimp were delicious. The garlic butter noodles reminded me of childhood. The bok choy was the perfect accompaniment. I was over-the-moon about every bite. Bravo!

I went for lunch today.  I hadn't been there for awhile even though I live nearby on the Mesa.    I was seated by a woman wearing a mask but the server had no mask on and the runner had his down under his nose.    I did not even wait to order and left.  I tried to call them w these comments but no one ever answered the phone.  Never going to try this place again.  If the blow off Public Health guidelines so evidently, what goes on in the back kitchen?

Update:  We went back recently with the family and had a wonderful dinner, the service and food was back up to par and the family dining atmosphere is always a plus! Coloring books for the kids for the win!  Previous review  Bummer the service is so poor at this establishment, we love the food and it's great for family dining! We had a recent experience where our server was more interested in hanging out chatting with the regulars then actually being a server. We watched refill after refill head to their tables and never once were we asked if we would like refills on our drinks. We had to ask for everything from napkins to cutlery to ketchup and even then it took multiple trips to bring us everything. When we were finally able to flag our server down for refills the meal was basically over. These days it's not worth it to diner out if the service is going to b so bad.
Ordered spicy beef tendon with yang chow fried rice via Grubhub. Great packaging, yummy entrees. I'm glad this is an option for Santa Barbara which doesn't have that many great Chinese food places.

Santa Barbara, to my wife and I, has always been about the fish and chips at Brewhouse. It was just one of those givens. But I think we have a new go-to.  Based partially on convenient location, and partly on some great Yelp reviews, we decided to give this place a try. It seemed pretty relaxed, and with some good food. Can you ask for more? (considering this IS Santa Barbara, isn't it appropriate?)  As mentioned elsewhere: the tri-tip is superb. If there's two of you, feel free to get a large order and share, as my wife and I each fell short of the smallest portions. Next time we're in the city, we'll have to try the other items off the menu.   The sides are good (if you have much to say about beans and rice, there's probably something wrong) and the tortillas are hot and delicious. I opted for the corn, and they're a tad flimsy. Best to double-up, or tear off pieces and scoop, rather than try to make a taco out of them. The salsas are all great, but there is one in specific that stands a bit taller than the others. Rancheros, perhaps? (that's what I get for waiting a few months to write this)   CONS: Can't think of any. Seriously. This place is so good, it makes me want to try a local Mexican chicken bbq place within walking distance of my house.  The price is good. The food is great. The location/parking isn't wonderful, but vibe here is so legit that sitting on the small porch, sitting inside, or even taking this food to go is a good solution to your casual lunchtime needs. If this style of grub is something you're interested in, don't cheat yourself! Stop on by!

Unfortunately I had a very bad experience at this establishment. I came here with a group of my friends. We went to the host stand and we're quoted 30 minutes. It was a Saturday evening so the wait was very understandable. We waited around 30 minutes and then were approached by a manager to get seated. We were so happy to be seated in such a timely manner. After we seated we order waters, some food and cocktails. Then, a group of girls began crowding our table and one of them set her dirty glass on our table. We felt uncomfortable as we had a private table and didn't want their dirty glass on our table. I handed the glass back to the girl which she found VERY offensive. The entire group of girls (about 6 of them - including a staff member) then surrounded our table and began threatening us telling us that we STOLE their table and that we didn't want a 'problem' them. We explained to them that we had been seated therea by the manger and were not aware it was their table. They left to the small table next to us and the employee who threatened us grabbed a table from outside and placed it directly next to us so all six of them could be accommodated. Our drinks arrived and we were hoping the issue had been solved since they got seated next to us. The manager who witnessed the altercation began sending us free items and apologizing. This REALLY bothered the girls who continued to side eye us and make threatening comment. The free fries sent them over the edge and they felt the need to approach our table AGAIN and threaten us. At this point the staff member who was originally threatening us began apologizing. She kept approaching our table even though we just wanted to be left alone at that point. She then moved the girls to another able again NEXT to us. They still were not content and felt the need to direct negative comments toward us. Management attempted several times to get them to calm down and move to a table away from us outside. They refused. They moved in to threaten us AGAIN! This entire time my party and I had remained seated. NEVER approaching their table. We definitely responded to their comments but we did not physically approach them. We ended up leaving with everything comped by management and the girls eventually moved outside which was a HUGE relief. Ultimately I'm disappointed that these girls were not told to leave the establishment. Simply because they were friends of a staff they were allowed to stay. I can genuinely say we did not feel safe. It was truly unfortunate that we couldn't just enjoy a drink without being stressed for our wellbeing. I understand allowing them to stay after the first incident but after they approached our table MULTIPLE times threatening us when we NEVER left our table they should've been kicked out. The drinks were good, the bar is very pretty but be aware they do not protect their customers.

The food has great flavor and excellent ingredients.  The atmosphere is super casual and clean.  Wish they had a bigger selection of savory items and salads instead of drinks and treats but it is what it is.  Definitely try the burgers and the pizza!  Drinks are overpriced imho but then again...it's Santa Babs and its Matcha.  Do give them a try.

This is one of my favorite restaurants in California. The food is beyond wonderful. I can't say enough about how good the food is. They don't try to be terribly fancy, they just execute flawlessly. I had the seafood stew and an appetizer that had grilled prawns with polenta. Both were outstanding.  The service is also wonderful. You feel very taken care of, but they're not intrusive.  If you can only eat in one place in Santa Barbara, make it here. If you're going to try for dinner, you'll probably need a reservation. Their reputation is well known and they are busy.

Mauricio amd Claudia were great as usual. Delicious food, love the chips with mole, great service, patio seating for the summer and the cutest indoor dining room on in the side. We couldn't have been more satisfied with our dinner, the mole burrito is my absolute favorite. We cannot wait to see their new food truck up and running around town!

No hesitation here with the 5 star review! Excellent food, stellar service,  and guaranteed high quality product every time.  When I want fresh and delicious pasta, Aperativo is my go-to.  I appreciate their dedication and consistency. Highly recommend for date night since its a cozy intimate venue with an open kitchen. I'm also a big fan of Tues pasta club for take out,  excellent wine pairings every time.

Yum yum yum. Every time I come to this place, everything is just great. Food is simply delish and fresh! Authentic Mexican food flavor. The only thing is over price for the amount of food you get. Other than that I would come everyday if I could. Service is a thumbs up aswell. Hightly recommend this place.

Super fast service even on a Sunday during lunch rush! The menu is just tacos and when you get them they'll just have the tortilla and meats, but they have sauce stations in each room to dress your with the fixings to your liking!  The taste wasn't anything spectacular, just a standard delicious taco. I found that the tripas taco was a bit dry, but Carne asada was my favorite!! The prices are great though! $6.53 for three tacos!
Only no.6 on list of Mex rest in SB? It is a true gem.  Good mole is hard to find, but not if you go here.
The most amazing homemade chai. Always a pleasure, and lots of indoor and outdoor seating.

Food is awesome, better make a reservation before you come. Too many people are waiting.  If you like raw bar, better order the Grand seafood platter directly, you could get variety seafoods in one dish.   Outside tables are a little chilly around the beach at night, but the sunset is great.   Definitely will recommend friends to visit here

I've eaten here many, many times and I always get the same thing: lobster Mac n cheese. It's my absolute favorite and so worth the money! My partner and I usually share it when we would have a beer at lama dog.

How did I not know about this place when I was in college? Los Agaves completely shits on every single restaurant in Santa Barbara (let alone Mexican restaurants). The salsa bar is amazing. I highly suggest anything on their special menu posted behind the front desk.

I really love sachi's ramen. The chashu is always very tender, the broth is tasty, good sized portion, and delicious veggies inside. The skewers are scrumptious too. Great for a rainy day or anytime really. Happy this place opened up in Santa Barbara, was very disappointed with the choices. Oh and affordable! compared to other ramen places where a comparative bowl would cost $25, sachi's cost $12. Hope their reviews get better, probably just some initial issues that have been worked out.

Came here this past weekend for lunch with some friends. Line was fairly long but it moved quickly and we got our food quickly as well. I ordered the chicken fajitas and they were excellent. Good size portion of seasoned chicken along with three tortillas and a ton of veggies. Everything tasted fresh and was very good. Also tried a sample of a friend's nachos and they were very good as well. Prices were reasonable given the portion size. Service was a bit of a letdown (not overly friendly, forgot to bring out another friend's entree). That's the only thing keeping it from being 5*. The place certainly met the hype. We will be back!

We wanted to come on a Sunday evening, and we checked the hours before coming. The site said they'd be open, so we headed over. We arrived, found the correct entrance in the back per all the hints in the area, and tried to get in to no avail. Nothing on social media or their site indicated they would be closed. We reached out via Instagram and didn't hear back either. Really disappointing, because as a UCSB alum, I'm a big fan of Cajé and love trying inventive cocktails. Perhaps another spot is better suited for us.

Their vegan mac and cheese is damn good!!  Some of the other vegan menu items are hit or miss (salads and soups = good, veggie parm = kinda soggy and not great, mushroom burger = ok).  But that mac and cheese y'all!  Been hunting for a restaurant in SB that makes it vegan and finally, it exists!

We had a very enjoyable dinner at the Bluewater Grill. Our server Sean was on point and the food was delicious. We started with the crab cakes and had the Alaskan Crab legs. 5 stars for this place, good atmosphere, service, and food.

Awesome breakfast/brunch spot! Fast casual, order at counter, sit outside. Croissant breakfast sandwich was delicious...egg cooked perfectly, buttery croissant, side of tasty potatoes. Omelette great too. Coffee refills for dining in. Awesome pastry selection. On State Street. 75 minute free parking in the garage. Would recommend!
This place has an extensive menu and food always consistently good. Especially enjoy the B.A.Burrito. But the Brekkle Tacos are good too. From coffee, food to cupcakes, this place has it dialed.

As a local and a lover of tacos, I SO WISH I could give 5 stars. I ordered veggie tacos because I'm a vegetarian, and was very disappointed to find that they accidentally put some meat in there.   However, in terms of service they are EXCELLENT! By the time you finish paying, your order is ready. Although I don't recommend this place for vegetarians, if you don't mind a little meat - this is the place for you! A Santa Barbara classic.

Dropped in for coffee a few weeks ago. The place looks great and someday I would like to eat here.   The lattes were very nice and the staff given the peculiarities of the customer in front of us in line were very accommodating regarding the delay. They should be commended for their service.

Before, I was dying to try this restaurant out!  So many good reviews, so many hangovers, and so little time to be bold go beyond Isla Vista and Goleta.  I finally made it in the summer after graduation.  My friends and I wanted a nice lunch so why not try Renauds! Normally, I'm not a big fan of sandwiches since I work in a cafe also.  But after trying a croque madame, that was a quick slap to my face! LOL! I ordered the croque madame and my friends had the croque monsieur.  I wanted the madame mainly because of the sunny side up egg.  Ok another point, I'm not a big fan of ham either.  LOL! Once again, blew my mind.  Digging into my sandwich, the taste was so fresh and simple.  I could tasted how the ham, cheese, and the egg combined so well together.  The moment I reached the yolk, the yolk just spread its rich protein towards the sandwich.  I didn't mind it.  Gave a boost more flavor and I added some black peppers to it.  The salad was extremely fresh.  I tasted a bit of vinegar.  The vibe of the restaurant was extremely calm.  By the time I finished my sandwich, I was perfectly content.  The tea I had with it was perfect.  My friend ordered a cake for his own bday.  It was extremely chocolate.  This was all in the same weekend.  We went back the next day to pick it up.  Later that night, the cake was just AMAZING.  Perfect chocolate macaroons and the perfect amount of chocolate inside the cake.  I wasn't overpowered at all by the cake! It was so good that I want to order a cake for myself! Dang, give me that Hello Kitty cake I see on the website.  :)  I sometimes crave the sandwiches here at this restaurant still....thats how much of a lasting impression this place gave on me.  Smiles, good friends, good atmosphere, and good service.  I wish I can come back again just to experience it all at Renaud's.  :)

Love this cute small shop on State St. The artistry for each donut is perfect. From the lemon thyme to the blood orange slice on top... they really take pride in details. Highly recommend coming early because when I left at 10am the line was out the door.

We went to The Cruisery on Sunday and had such an amazing time! We loved sitting outside on State St. under the umbrellas. There was a band playing and it was just a blast. The Pineapple Express cocktail is a must! We highly recommend!

Came here last saturday night, took about ten minutes to get seated with no reservation, awesome! Our waiter took atleast 15 minutes to greet us and take our drink order. He was extremely unattentive, I had to ask for additional butter and a water 3 different occasions. Despite the mediocre BBQ salmon and shitty service I will try this place again and order something different for sure.

I was super bummed about our experience at Convivo. Unfortunately, I also chose it as my birthday lunch and we had subpar service. We sat at a great location outside so everything started well. But, our service was running around with his head cut off trying to serve all his tables out on the grass, on the other side of the patio and then us. He was not on top of anything and we had to ask bussers quite a bit of times for water, turn on heaters, our food, bread, etc. We didn't get our drinks until 45 minutes after we sat down.   The food was good. I would come back but not on a weekend. They're packed due to their location and they don't have enough staff to sustain it.   Bread is nothing like you've ever tasted! I would just come for that and some wine. Bucatini is good. I would get it again. Until next time. Hopefully times are better.

Gone are the times when a brand alone drew the crowds. Our generation expects substance, content and an experience. This hotel is famed but also extremely aged. The rooms, especially bathrooms, are in desperate need of renovations.   Our spa experience was good but the pool area/jacuzzi had no staff, towels weren't removed, and definitely not a Four Seasons level of service. The restaurant had beautiful views from the outdoor seating and probably the highlight of our visit. The food was just okay, including the Sunday brunch.

I get it people in business want to save money but jesus this place skimps and cuts every corner i ordered a kids meal that should be called a dollhouse meal little kids dont eat a 3 inch pizza? Its a joke. My 3 year old was still hungry. Second my pizza was thinner than cardboard with sausage sliced razor thin and seven pieces of sausage at most. My side salad i ate in three bites the only thing good here it their selection of soda on the machine. Other than that bland pizza sauce flavorless assembly line pizza. For the 23$ i dropped here i could of gone to california pizza kitchen down the street or persona up the street ill never be back here again. I always wondered why its always empty and now i know. The staff here is not in a good mood ever. I dont blame them for working here

Returned to Santa Barbara today after a year away and had to visit Corazon Cocina for my fix of tacos. I had two: Butternut squash and Al Pastor, both served on blue corn tortillas. Excellent, as usual!

This Coffee Bean location is not good at making cold or hot drinks. The interior is really cramped, so not really somewhere that you hang out. Back to the drinks. The coffees here are made piping hot, making it impossible to drink them, and weirdly, lack coffee flavor, because they are so hot!! The blended drinks are slightly better, but lack flavor also. Not really sure what was going on at this store, but not a good experience.
I am so happy that I recently discovered this wonderful little tasting room. The tapas are delicious and the service is second to none. The homemade bread is fantastic.

From a visit in late September '19:  Buena Onda is located away from the busy State Street, which allowed for a more ambient dining experience outside. These empanadas were delicious, and the salads that came with our combo was much-needed-- considering how cheesy those empanada were! The chimichurri was also a yummy addition and we wish we had more. The combo (2 empanadas + one side) left us full and ready for the Incubus concert.  Can't wait to come back here again next time I'm in the area!

The pizza was delicious!  Unfortunately, our evening was almost ruined by the rude hostess. We were waiting for ten minutes in front after a waiter told us he would seat us in a minute. We asked the hostess if there was a wait since since we had been there waiting for so long (with tables visibly open) and she began to berate us for some reason. We dealt with her mistreatment and allowed her to seat us. She sought to seat us away from the heaters on a winter evening but we quickly moved. Our actual waiter was very nice, fortunately. Again, the food was great but service needs to improve to meet basic standards!

I typically don't write a review based on one visit to a restaurant but my grandson and I had a FANTASTIC lunch at this place yesterday.  Our server, Stanzi was one of the happiest, nicest and best servers that I have met in a very long time.  Being the crawfish connoisseurs that we are, we ordered a pound of Cajun-style crawfish with a mild heat level and Dungeness Crab Legs with the Drunken Special sauce, also with a mild heat level.  We placed both orders Louisiana Style meaning including corn cobbettes, red potatoes and sausage.  Even though they were temporarily out of live crawfish, we had the frozen and they were just great.  AJ and I have eaten enough crawfish to know the difference between live and frozen and we would have been hard pressed to tell which was which in this case.  The Dungeness was really good but we both preferred the Cajun sauce which is LOADED with garlic.  Anyway, based on this one visit I give this place five out of five stars.  I will be back, possibly today but I am pretty sure I won't be disappointed when I try other menu items, particularly if Stanzi is our wait person.

This place is amazingly delicious! So creative and beautiful in their menu offerings and plating of the foods. I recently had lunch here. Shared their hummus, which is so tasty and then had the jackfruit tacos. They were stunning and delicious! I highly recommend this place and can't wait to go back. Quick note: took my 75 year old mother, whom thinks the vegetarian/ vegan thing is a bit much... she LOVED her tacos too! She had the potato/ walnut tacos. TASTY! GO!!!! You will not be disappointed!

Hi guys!   This place is very good place for lunch. They have delicious sandwiches and salads.  I usually go there and get salad. Today I had a Mexican Chicken Salad, and it was yummy. It similar to Caesar Salad,but with more of onion flavor. Before I had chipotle salad, it was very different and it has different flavor. You should try them, and you won't be regret .
Trust this local when I say Sambo's is a taste of #oldschool Santa Barbara! The way it used to be! Plus - bloody's & muffins are #onfleek!

We had a cinnamon roll and chocolate croissant here. The service at the counter was harried and sloppy although polite. The two people in front of me were visibly frustrated for different reasons (one ordered takeout and got their order on a plate and one had been waiting a while for their coffee and the wrong drink was made). Our pastries were good but not outstanding. The cinnamon roll had a ton of sweet icing without a taste of creamcheese (I think it was a sugar icing). And not a lot of cinnamon taste either. Croissant was good but standard.

Cool place to hang out at and watch a game.  My friends and I stopped by here before we were going to head back to L.A. after a great weekend in SB.  The Lakers were playing so we wanted to watch the game first.  The service was good and also the food. They also have a good selection of beers on tab.  We also ordered a bottle of their house cab and it was nice. It was only $18 for the bottle. We munched on a some fries and a pizza.  It would have been a better experience if the Lakers would have won the game.  But no stars were removed because of that. The kamakaze shots were pretty good too!  Good way to enjoy a Sunday afternoon in Downtown SB.  Peace out Yelpers!

Was really excited to try, but a major letdown. And after seeing rude owner response to bad reviews I lowered my rating even more. Ordered the eggs Benedict with the salmon and my wife got it with the fried chicken. Both were cold. Not even warm. The salmon was hard to put down, gag worthy - And I love salmon. Pretty expensive for such low standards. Sadly the star of the meal was the side of potatoes, which were really good and hot. I don't know how you could mess up serving cold fried chicken - cold eggs that were over cooked (no oozing yolk) and cold hollandaise sauce. Very disappointed based on reviews, and the food looked like it would taste amazing. But when its cold, it's just not good.

Im so happy Alessia came to town last summer, every single thing on the menu is good. The little cafe is so well designed and inviting, there is room to stand and lovely booths to sit. Small splits of champagne wink from the sparkling clean shelves and next to minto colored pastry boxes that remind me of LaDuree in Paris. Delicious, elegant, and so creative I think the savory e lair withthree kinds of caviar is my personal favorite.

The noodle bar is located in the center of Santa Barbara Public Market. Just like all the other places inside the market, the noodle bar gave a very modern, clean, and hipster vibe. We ordered the 'Bangkok Street Noodle' and 'Long Tail Boat Noodle'. The street noodle was pho noodles in spicy and sour soup with bbq pork, minced pork, and fish balls on top. My favorite part of this dish was their bbq pork, because it was very tender. The boat noodle was pho noodles in dark colored soup (couldn't tell what the soup was made of...) with beef and beef meatballs on top. I was not impressed by the boat noodle, and I would recommend that you try other things on their menu.
Really good tacos! And it's fast too, only waited a few minutes.

Thanks to the recommendation from Eater Santa Barbara, I found this gem. Road trip dining doesn't get better than this.   The best homemade sourdough to start with a special smoked sea salt butter and a nice glass of wine while perusing the menu.  Went with the Acorn Flour Tagliatelle with Maitake Mushrooms and charred oak broth which was light and healthful and hit all the right notes. Then followed with the unique Eucalyptus dessert complete with kumquats, sourgrass granita, hazelnut and bee pollen. And Handlebar Coffee (my favorite).  Every dish a visual and delectable pleasure. Can't wait to return!

Old town salad with tahini dressing and their veggie chili soup was just okay. I thought the decor looked tired and there was a bleachy smell inside the restaurant. I thought the service was slow and forgetful. I ordered tea and asked the cashier about hot water and no one brought it over and I had to point it out. Will not be coming back.

I was in town a couple of weeks for business in Santa Barbara, and my boss ordered lunch for the whole department on a Friday. I completely looked over the email she sent out asking for our lunch order choices from Metropulos, so she ordered me a gyro hoping I would enjoy it.  Okay, I'm from Alabama. I now live in Texas. Do you think I actually know what a gyro is? I mean, I can't even pronounce the word for goodness sake. But I politely took the warm foil-wrapped concoction and put it on my desk thinking that I probably wouldn't touch it. Out of curiosity, I opened the package and peeked inside.
