It looked simple enough. It smelled wonderful. Then here's what happened next.  I took a bite of the gyro. O-M-G. I had never eaten a gyro before that moment, but I am pretty positive Metropulos makes the best gyro you can buy in the United States of America. Ten minutes later, I honestly didn't know what happened. The entire gyro was devoured. Every last little crumb was gone... I think I even licked the foil. I'm going to get weird now and let you know that I still dream about that gyro from time to time. In other words, it is AH-MAZING. A food-gasm in your mouth. A delicacy of the highest order. If you live in Santa Barbara, just do yourself a favor and order one now. I'm pretty sure that it will change your life.

First of the atmosphere is awesome! It's hard to stand out in the sea of restaurants in Santa Barbara But this one does it. The cuisine is delicious and I Love the outdoor area to dine. This place is so quaint but so beautiful!

Stateside was the new 'it' restaurant in SB, and within a short walk.  It also represented a potentially livening facet of the Arcadia court, which I had previously dismissed as a haven for upper middle class tourists willing to spend too much on living room art.  On first approach: a terraced rock fountain onto which 'Stateside' was light-projected to great effort.  Ok, a bit much.  Some more steps inside revealed an open, almost cavernous modern interior, complete with NY-style underlighted bar and stage in the corner.   Actually, though, not so bad.  I like it for what it's trying to bring in terms of real city feel, even if Stateside is consciously aware of that fact.  The food was inventive, but not extraordinary.  Specifically, the ceviche was oddly and petitely served in small shot glasses, and my  tuna steak came cooked ala dessicant (read: well done).  But the jazz trio in the corner was a mediating factor.  I'm willing to stick with the game for a while, to see what they end up making of it, even if 'Stateside' is streaked in light in pretentious small-caps font.

Wonderful!!  I ordered an al pastor burrito. Meat was cooked perfectly. Tortilla was unlike you find in most burritos, was a little crispy probably warmed on an open flame. The pinapple salsa was the perfect compliment to the pork. Nicely melted cheese and warm beans. I added avo and sour cream. Was fairly priced at $12.   My wife ordered the alambra Santa Rosa. Shrimp was fresh and it came with 3 delicious home made corn tortillas. Thick and warm - truly homemade tortillas. She said it was 'bomb.'   Kids had chicken tacos. All I can tell you about that is they ate all the meat out, so must have been good!   High recommend!!

This place is a hidden gem for Korean food in SB! They have pretty much all the traditional dishes (bulgolgi, tofu soup, ddokboki) and for reasonable prices too. The lady serving us was super sweet, and there are also tables to eat at even though this place looks like a market only on the outside.

This is a great go to for tacos and also serve up other things like burritos, gorditas and tortas. Not only do they have a food truck, but they have a hidden gem off Milpas and Jennings Ave. serving up fresh homemade tortillas. When you walk in, you're immediately welcomed and greeted with a smile and leave with a full belly. One thing to make mention is they don't add the toppings or salsa on top of the tacos, they let you do that with their fresh salsa to choose from; they also have the cilantro and onions for you to add too.

Quaint Italian restaurant in Santa Barbara. Not ideal for large groups. Service was wonderful. The servers were warm and inviting, offering recommendation for new guests.   Out of the 3 dishes we ordered, I only liked 1. Mushroom ravioli with black truffle sauce.  Burrata and heirloom tomato salad -- The salad was arugula based. I told my friend that I liked burrata cheese but hated arugula. We ordered it anyways. So much regret. First couple bites and the arugula left a bitter taste in my mouth. The cheese tasted very fresh and the tomatoes made up for it but definitely not worth ordering if you don't like arugula. We ended up not finishing the salad. What a waste.  Tagliatelle with scallops were okay. It was olive oil based and I felt like it was too oily for me. Like oil overload. Again, this dish had some arugula as well. No thanks. The scallops were cooked well but the dish overall I just felt like it was missing something..   The star of the show... Mushroom ravioli with black truffle sauce. OKAY folks, this dish was the highlight night. TASTED INCREDIBLE. I loved every single bite. Something about the black truffle sauce made me feel some type of way.. Ugh so addicting.   Place is kinda pricey. Perhaps we ordered the wrong items? I may have to revisit to give them another try...

I'm ALWAYS craving Zaytoon now, ever since the first time I had it. The ambience of the place is great too and I love the firepit tables! My go-to is the chicken kebabs since i'm not a huge fan of lamb. Only disappointment is that they don't have plain beef kebabs, only beef kefta but it's a mixture of beef and lamb, not just beef by itself. The chicken kebabs are always super moist and flavorful so I'm not really complaining. The garlic sauce and rice are to die for as are all the appetizers. I love coming here and can't wait to be back the next time I'm in the area!

This was my second time here, and I had their famous Lemon Ricotta Pancakes the first time, so I thought I'd try something new this time and ordered the Quinoa Oatmeal. Unfortunately, the Quinoa Oatmeal was not my style, so I didn't end up liking it that much, but definitely willing to come back and try something else, as I had enjoyed the Lemon Ricotta pancakes so much previously. Our waitress was very friendly and attentive as well. Once again Scarlet Begonia did not disappoint in terms of service.

This used to be our go-to spot, and it pains me to write this, but the quality has declined significantly. They consistently forget little things in orders, which is no big deal every once in awhile, but when it becomes an every time thing, its a waste of our money and effort to order here. The final nail in the coffin was the order we just received. Half the condiments were open and spilling over in the bag (not the delivery persons fault, they didn't close the containers - Luna did). The grape leaves weren't rolled right, and half of them were opened and spilled out everywhere. They completely forgot my husband's side of fries, and stiffed me on my entire meal (again, not the delivery person's fault, Luna bag's the orders). Big waste of money and time. Real bummer of a way for this stressed mom to end a long day.
really good and the mole is great.  We like the one on Milpas best.

If I were just rating the pizza, I would give it 5 stars.  It is moderately priced and delicious, and works well for groups of people with different tastes.  Most recently, however, I had a less than stellar experience with the staff.  I've seen other people describe this place as a subway or chipotle for pizza, which I agree with.  And in these type places, I never know if it's customary to tip or not.  Sometimes I do, sometimes I don't, and I'm never sure if I'm doing the right thing or how much one should tip (which, when in doubt, perhaps you should always just tip).    This last visit, however, I didn't leave a tip.  So, yes, I recognize that I could be labeled a bad customer in this respect.  While I was waiting for my pizza to go, the employee who checked me out complained loudly to another employee about my lack of tipping (and I was sitting about 10 feet away) and remarked to the other employee, 'I mean you tip a delivery person, right?  Why not us?'. Which, yes, you have the right to complain about my lack of tipping, but perhaps it's best to wait until after I leave, and also- I feel like you tip the delivery pizza guy for the act of delivering the pizza (not when you go to pick it up).    On top of this, they forgot to add one of the pizza toppings that they usually add post-bake.    I will probably go back because I do like their pizza, I'm just a little disenchanted by the staff and this recent experience.

We enjoyed Flor De Maíz Happy Hour. The menu is incredible and authentic food. The green salsa is by far my favorite of all time. I'm a local and have to say Flor De Maíz and Los Agaves are my favorite Mexican Restaurants in town. A must go if you are visiting! The variety of margaritas are delicious. I tried the dry rosé as well. You will enjoy all the food here!

I'm not sure how one can rate this place anything less. Easily in the top 3 Mexican Restaurants in The 805. The ambiance, service and flavors were phenomenal, the drinks were precise and playful. The entire experience was closer to a hidden gem off a backstreet in Mexico City than an impression of a 'Mexican Restaurant' in downtown Santa Barbara.  New favorite go to when I'm craving authentic Mexican creative cuisine. Portions are made for sharing!

Up Side there cheap but you get what you pay for . As for the meats absolutly no flavor what so ever! But now that I think about it prob. Not that great of a deal since there so tiny about 2 1/2, to 3 inch max.  The last time I went there it was pretty gross they made me the wrong taco so I took it back to the counter and they asked for my recipet which I could some what understand but three people came over to see what was going on then they took the wrong taco off my plate and put the meat back in to the pan that held the meat for serving the new orders instead of throwing it away or just letting me keep it. I mean I could of sneezed or coughed on it not very sanitary at all it was the Goleta location. Eat at your own risk!
Pretty good Ramen. They were out of pork belly, but the pork shoulder was decent. Broth was very good. Service was good. Thanks!
Delicious, great variety & super friendly and welcoming. This little gem of an Italian restaurant is incredible! It make me feel like I'm in Italy. A must try if you're visiting.

Wanted to try this place after seeing it on a lot of lists for must try in the Santa Barbara area. It's in the plaza where a lot of other restaurants are in Montecito including ice cream Starbucks and cute boutique clothing shops. The meat lovers pizza was really good I think their sausages homemade based off the other menu items and there were a ton of pepperoni probably a bit too much actually but overall the pizza was good and I was able to call ahead of time for it to be ready in about 10 minutes. The nice thing is that you can also do your payment over the phone to keep it contactless.There is plenty of parking in the plaza and there are a few picnic tables in front if you want to eat there but we chose to pick it up on the way back to LA which was a good way to check out another Santa Barbara restauarant

This is the hot bar in town right now, so expect a wait. Again, it's a bar. I have read other reviews where people seem to miss that point.   The food is good bar food. They have a good beer selection. The place is a lot of fun. No place better to watch a big game or hang with your friends.

You can't beat the views and the grounds at the Four Season Santa Barbara. It's located right across from the ocean and you're close enough to hear the waves crashing. The hotel exudes money and it's a lot less stuffy than I was expecting.   This property is definitely a great spot to grab lunch/dinner but I wouldn't necessarily stay here unless I didn't plan on leaving the resort.

Once again my gf introduced me to another hidden gem in SB.  After a nice winding drive up the mountain that had a spectacular view of SB below, we turned off the road and headed down the old stagecoach road to get here. I can ramble on about what you see upon arriving, but just look at the pix peeps have posted.  Anyways, we were starving and so we went inside the tavern part and purchased our food tickets. For you noobs, it's hot dogs on Saturdays and tri-tip on Sundays. We walked to the grill area next to the restaurant and waited a short while and then we were rewarded. HUGE amount of freshly grilled tri-tip served on a toasted french roll that had garlic butter spread! Wow! This was the shiznat! The sandwich was delicious and I spooged in my pants numerous times while enjoying my food.  Needless to say I will venture back here again in the future with my gf to enjoy the yumminess of Cold Spring Tavern, but in the restaurant the next time.

I've had way better Mexican food in Cleveland if that paints any sort of picture. I was invited with a group last minute so figured worth a check in. But after 25 years in Santa Barbara I find it really hard to believe a place like this serves food-service canned beans and rice (no flavor and undercooked), mediocre salsa, ill-prepared mass produced tortillas. The drink were meh as well as was the service. I love it when I ask the waiter if they have some hotter salsa and he tells me to flag down some other guy for it. Took till end of night to get a glass of water.  With the variety of really great Mexican food in this town, this place is going to just ride location and tourists into yet another oblivion. The days of past when this place was Acapulco scored way better and the place sucked back then too.
Great Customer service, great food and good vibes. Will definitely be coming back here again. Nice to see young entrepreneurship in the city.

Pretty dang tasty for late night drunk mac-n-cheese! Definitely get the tots, they are buried underneath your mountain of mac and toppings. If i'm ever awake again between 11-3 and happen to be downtown I'd stop by :)
Good food, good service, very small portion of good potato's. Cheapest thing on the plate and they always serve a tiny portion.

Definitely a 'meh' rating. But people LOVE this place!  I cringe from locals who adore this joint, because the food barely exceeds the boring mark. I just don't understand it. I've eaten here maybe 6-8 times, and every visit confirmed my suspicion that this place makes a killing off of its Montecito patronage, but at the expense of maintaining any kind of culinary viability. I never hated my meal, but I never remembered it either. Do yourself a favor: drive the extra 2.8 minutes and go to Milpas.

Only popped in here quickly after a stroll along the beach.   Choice of beer is average but.....  ...they have Caipirinha - made with real cachaça.   We were there early so no live music but the tunes they were playing were great. Nice vibe place.

Took awhile to be seated and acknowledged.  Took awhile to get water and bread.  Waitress hardly checked on us and food was just on.   Son got sick that night due to undercooked lamb.  Place was busy, but service just sucked.   And waitress takes bill, but another guy brings it back.  Like waitress was just done with us?  We like Holdren's, but our experience just wasn't good.

Had the grilled Salmon but substituted the spinach cream sauce that it usually comes with for the mango salsa, probably should have gone with the sauce bcuz it turned out to be a pretty dry dish. The salsa was uninspired and more like a pico de gallo topping.  The place was really packed, seating outside felt extremely cramped.  We had a pretty cool view, however!  Got to watch a commercial boat load up a truck with a couple tons of live sea urchin...which turned out to be the most memorable thing about this meal.

Found this place by walking up Haley and was absolutely amazed by the atmosphere, quality of the drink and incredible service. I love the presentation and that they're very environmentally conscious. A great place to study, meet for a drink or quiet self-reflection.

I've loved SCD's salads and sandwiches for years - the flavor combos are amazing and I have yet to try something I didn't fully enjoy. Best of all though is the staff. I've been stopping into the Chapala location for years now and appreciate how the team is so friendly and greets many of their customers by name as you walk in. This is my go-to spot when I have friends visiting that want a sandwich or salad for lunch and they always tell me they wish they had a location where they live. Can't recommend them enough!

Taffy's Pizza is my favorite Pizza place in town. It's in a great location on 2026 De La Vina Street near Cottage Hospital. There is plenty of room for parking. There are plenty of tables and even a large patio outside with a large seating area. It's a perfect place to have a birthday party or group celebration. The pizza is fabulous. It has a nice crispy crust with plenty of delicious sauce and very fresh toppings. The restaurant is clean and cheery.  There are several TVs to watch and also a small game room.  The nice gentleman who takes our order is super friendly and helpful. The breadsticks come with a delicious marinara sauce and ranch dressing.  Everything we ordered was totally delicious. We will definitely go back and we highly recommend Taffy's to our friends.

Cmon Santa Barbara habits!! Get with the program and offer the spicy fried chicken sandwich like all of the other habits in the country. It's soooo good! I don't understand why it's not offered in the Santa Barbara locations.. please? It would do so well!!!
This Place has the best damn '5 cheese truffle oil pizza' I have ever had.. Sooooo Gooooood, it's a must have pizza pie !

The restaurant is good. We've eaten there a couple times and had great food. The open kitchen is cool and the seafood is very fresh. I really dig the bar there. They have great microbrews from all over the west and have a huge selection of oysters.

First off, I have heard great things about the food here so I was looking forward to coming in. I have tried most of the places here in town and haven't found a good place that is not extremely over priced. I was so stoked to find they were open on New Years Day! Lucky us, we thought.   We called ahead to see if they were taking reservations. A flustered man answered and gave us a wordy, stressed out, strange explanation as to why they weren't taking reservations. A simple 'No we don't take reservations, but the wait isn't very long' would have sufficed.   We arrived with no one to greet us at the door and stood for about 10 minutes, wondering if the 'Please Seat Yourself' sign that was facing away and tucked in a corner was actually for us. Eventually a woman came forwards and told us it would be about 10 minutes- fab!   For lack of a better word, it just got weird after getting seated. We were told on the phone that they stopped serving at 2pm, and we were sat around 1:40. It took about 15 minutes for someone to even come over and take our order even though we were clearly ready a while before that. Our server offered the 2/4 of us that ordered relevant dishes, 'home fries or hash browns'. We both ordered hash browns. About 5 minutes later she came out and apologized, they were out of hash browns. We both agreed to have home fries. When our food began to arrive, hash browns came out on only one of the plates. Weird. No one said anything.   I say 'began to arrive' because one plate took about 10 minutes longer than the rest to come out.. It was a two egg and toast plate. No one said anything. Again. When the two egg plate arrived, the poached eggs were pretty much hard boiled eggs- rock hard. No wonder it took 10 minutes longer, they destroyed the eggs. Again, no one said anything.   Once we had finished eating, it took about 30 minutes (no exaggeration, I swear) for anyone to come over to our table for us to get our check. Finally one of us went inside to ask for help and our server came back out again. I kindly pointed out that one of our dishes took way too long to come out, and then came out with over-cooked eggs. She was understanding, apologized, and offered to take the dish off our bill.    When she returned with the check, the said she took $4.00 off to cover the cost of eggs on the plate instead of the whole plate. WEIRD.   They took off $4.00. It was a $7.00 plate. They actually took off an arbitrary, stingy, amount off of our bill. I don't care about the three dollars, but its the principle of taking half of a dish off. I believe the manager's name on the comp was Alex. I hope I never meet Alex.   A lesson in customer service: If a dish takes so long that everyone else eats cold food, the food comes out wrong, and even the dish that comes out late is not up to par, you can spare the $3.00. By mentioning something to our server I was hoping to reconcile what were unhappy about so that we could leave on a good enough note to return again, however they handled it so unfortunately AND unprofessionally.   A positive: eggs benedict was delicious and I wish I could return to enjoy it again. The quality of a restaurant is not only measured by the food, but also by the management and service team which this place is obviously lacking in.   Also, a member of the kitchen staff whistled at me as I walked by to go to the dirty, trash covered restroom. Harassing guests is also a bad idea.   For service and food quality combined try Jeannine's on Figueroa St., Tupelo Junction on State St., or Finch and Fork on Chapala St.

The service was amazing.   No doubt the best breading on fish and chips I've had in Santa Barbara.   Renee was our server and was on her game.  The Brussel Sprouts were perfect.   Loved the whole experience.   Welcome to SB, see you soon.

Enjoyed this simple eatery, right on the Montecito strip.. do they have a strip. The services was mediocre, and the food was okay. I  had the burger with the avocado, and french fires. The fires were those thin sliced ones and were very good.My mom had the pancake with the 2 poached eggs and salmon, which by the end of the brunch, it was almost gone. The desserts, coconut cake looked delicious. If you are looking for a simple dish this would be a good place to stop. not my first choice but all around descent dinning. thanks :)

Came for the fishbowl drinks. Unfortunately they were like out of our top choice drinks but we still liked the ones we got! Sweet and juicey if that's what you like.  Outdoor seating was nice though too. Felt very comfortable.
My favorite spot for my morning latte. Alison and her crew are awesome. Highly recommend.

While strolling down State Street on a short visit, the decor of Lokum drew me in.  Who can resist chandeliers and tiled floors and colorful edibles?  I wish I had taken a photo of the display cases because everything looked so beautiful and so yummy!    Turkish delight is a great candy choice because it's not so sweet.  This can be good and bad because it meant I felt I could get away with eating more, and I wanted to eat more and more.   There are several types of baklava available.  I tried the 'juicy pistachio'.  Soooo good.  Then I took three different types of Turkish delight with me.  I didn't care for the red pomegranate flavored one because it was too close to a bubble gum taste.  But I fell hard for the pistachio nougat.  I went back the next day and bought more to take to my sister's (well, let's be honest, and for me).  The store sells commercially made delights and ones they make themselves.  The local ones are so much better.  Lokum also offers different types of coffee.  This place is definitely on my go-to list when I return to Santa Barbara.

Absolutely recommend! Have been twice and loved both meals. Mussels/clams are in an amazing, the broth is SO good. The beef cheek or short rib corn pudding also excellent! A welcome to the SB food scene. If it was cheaper my wife and I would be there all the time!

Reliably one of the best lunch deals in Santa Barbara... with Ethiopian food on the weekends too!  Really like their fish, and you get great soup and bread for a bit more. Cozy place with good service.

Mulligan's for breakfast/brunch is a nice venue with friendly and efficient staff, but they have one policy that I find off-putting:  They will not make a substitution without charging $1.50.  Not a lot of money, but charging extra for leaving the cheese OFF and adding spinach to my omelette is not customer-friendly.  I didn't ask for an addition - I asked for spinach instead of peppers, and held the cheese.  It would seem that this is a win-win.  Whatever their reason, they're shooting themselves in the foot when other, busier establishments are willing to accommodate.  We eat breakfast out often, and enjoy Farmer Boy, Cody's, Breakwater, and Chad's regularly.  ALL of them are happy to substitute without nickel-and-diming customers.

Grabbed a sandwich combo again today.  Decent food as always and they are quick to have things out of the kitchen.  It does seem like their daily board specials do not really change often though...  Don't be mistaken, this is a solid three stars and I come here all the time because it is good food.  It is not super special or unique, it is just solid food for people that work in the area and it excels at that.

We dined here for the second time last night and we had another great experience. I love the ambiance inside and I love the casual vibe. The service is great and the staff is friendly. All of the dishes are fantastic and they always have daily specials! Also, on Wednesday, there is NO corkage fee so be sure to bring in a bottle of wine! The cocktails are amazing. My favorite is the Mission Bell. We have ordered the risotto of the day both times now and it was fantastic both times. Overall, we will keep coming back and supporting this amazing restaurant.

Laquita is a nice addition to the culinary scene. Good Spanish food is always nice.  They make it pretty clear they do things 'tapas style' which is mostly small, shareable plates (so don't expect a sizable entree all to yourself). They say the paella serves 2-4, which is a bit of stretch for a party of 4.  We had a little tiff with the hostess. It was raining and cold, and despite having reservations, she tried to send us to the patio. We insisted on inside. You should specify inside (if that's what you want) when making your reservation.

We ordered to go, but the staff was very unfriendly over the phone when we had called to place the order. When we got there both people working seemed disinterested and annoyed.  The place is run down and needs to be cleaned.
So authentically good!  What a whole in the wall.  Love the pistachio salsa.!  Wow, my new favorite mexican restaurant in town.  I am glad to have discovered this little gem of a restaurant.
Don't order the meatless burger. They haven't figured out how to cook one. And the kicker is, that when I emailed this picture to the manager received only silence in return.

Since Yume has opened in SB I haven't gone anywhere else. I'm a HUGE sushi fan too, and have gone to pretty much every sushi restaurant in SB. This place has it all if you're looking for a nice, not too expensive and delicious spot. The prices are so reasonable, the food is amazing, and they have amazing portions. The lunch specials are so awesome too!! I highly recommend this place.

awww what a fun/funny place to bring out-of-towners... the red booths and throwback menu provide enough conversation until your awesome servers comes over and plies you with the strongest drinks in sb.  whenever you need a late night bite, fight the urge to give in at chilis or some equally horrible place and head straight for harry's.

Its upsetting to not give this better stars because the service is good and the ambience is lovely but the food just isn't good.... this was my fourth visit.... so clearly I've given them many tries.  The food is so heavy and way over seasoned and over done. They do have a nice wine list though, and really lovely people working there.  Easy valet parking and dog friendly.  They need to update their menu though - they rave about their Brussels sprouts and honestly - they aren't good and we always order them.....

We love Jeannine's and stop in here every time we are in SB.   We like the lunches here but the pastries are delightful also. The sandwiches are great - the smoked turkey, the egg salad, the veggie, all tasty.  I would suggest substituting a salad for the chips that come with the sandwiches.  The BBQ salad is really good.  The Figueroa location has a lot of seating & feels roomy.  The upper State Street is small but has several outdoor tables.  Either has good service.

The dry rub wings are the best wings in town. Trivia outside on the patio is fun too and it's convenient since you can eat dinner simultaneously. They have a strong selection of beers on tap. It's a great local pizza spot and they even have a locals discount. All the guys we've ever interacted with there are very relaxed and friendly.   The pizza is not my favorite but it's not bad. The dough is thinner and all the toppings, including cheese and sauce, are on the light side.

Really dig this place.  Their coffee is good.  Both places have real wobbly tables though.  but I love their muffins and tried a few of their smoothies. All excellent. One of these days, I'll go and try some of their food -- but in the mean time, it's a great place to just get some coffee and get a little bit of work done.

We were looking for a place to eat that was away from the crowds on State Street. Located in a small shopping center we discovered a place that had plenty of seating that had a causal and relaxed feel; Mesa Burger.  The interior has a nice clean and industrial ambiance and with good weather, we decided to eat on the patio. You order at the counter and the food is brought out to you from the friendly and helpful staff. Surprisingly enough I didn't even have a burger! I ended up having 'The Pit' which is a delicious deconstructed burger over a bed of fries. We added a side of sweet potato crisscuts because why not, and I was in hog-carb heaven.   It's clear that Mesa uses nothing but high quality ingredients in their meals. I was impressed with the great flavors, freshness, and creative spins on the highly competitive burger joint market. I'm already salivating on my next visit to Mesa Burger.

Started with the squid ink calamari. It was cooked nicely and perfectly seasoned, served with lemon and aioli. The squid ink is a bit of a gimmick though, it just makes it look burnt in my opinion.  I got the margherita d.o.c pizza. The pizza tasted good, but was a bit soggy towards the center. The cheese also ran off the slices too easily.  Service was perfect. I think they even refilled soft drinks from the can without charging extra, which is a nice surprise. Our vegetarian friend found a vegetarian appetizer and pizza, both of which he liked.
Really solid Mexican food in downtown Santa Barbara. I recommend the salsa bar. Parking is convenient with 2 public parking lots nearby.
Brophys is a decent place to have a decent bite. over the years its gone up and down in service. its kinda bar food but its good.   Oysters, crab salad and the shrimp is good.   view is the best.

On a rare occasion I get cravings for charbroiled oysters and I've been meaning to try Lure. If this review were just for the oysters it would be 5 stars, they were so good. The only thing some people may be picky about is they weren't all the same size and they were charbroiled unevenly (some more dry, etc), but honestly I don't care. The tequila pasta was ehhhh. Huge portion, but the worst shrimp I've had. The shrimp were frozen-y tasting and very dry/rubbery, did not taste the most fresh (weird for a seafood restaurant). The pasta wasn't bland, but overall not worth the $23 by any means.  I would come back for a cocktail and some oysters or appetizers.   Pros: Menu has many options Dairy free options available (limited)  Attentive service  High tops and bar available for full menu ordering  Charbroiled oysters were so good  Large wine and cocktail menu  Nice ambiance  Outdoor seating  A couple high tops are near a fireplace   Cons: Pricy Shrimp were not cooked well  Entree didn't feel worth the price

Fernando was an excellent waiter and the magic guy on Fridays is down right memorizing.  Great food and atmosphere -- except the new part, weird vibe over there, ask to be seated in the better section.  It's worth the wait.  Food is lovely -- limit yourself on the deep fried and save room for the traditional dishes.  Yes, order the martini.

I came with a co-worker who grew up in Mexico and she raves about the shrimp cocktail so I had to try it and now you should go try it. It's not the kind of shrimp cocktail that I'm used to with shrimp coming out of a martini glass over ice and cocktail dipping sauce. No no, this looked like ceviche and was a goblet full of a gazpacho-esque tomato based sauce and maybe 10 shrimps. I really enjoyed it and thought it was worth the price.  The chips are fresh and the salsas are yummy. I'll be back for the fried fish.
New girl working was very nice tonight for our takeout order! Answered phone politely,  gave us appropriate amount of napkins, utensils and condiments. We felt like valued customers! Thanks

Pretty good buffalo chicken pizza, however there have been multiple instances where I wanted to place an order and nobody ever answers the phone. Unreliable delivery service. Their online ordering also has never worked, it just reroutes to an error page.

I have made many a pilgrimage from Los Angeles and waited in many lines for mouthwatering tacos at this venerable taqueria over the last 30 years. Day before yesterday, I took my girlfriends mother for her first meal there. She's English and a very picky eater. She told me she wasn't hungry so I told her not to worry and just go sit down at a table while I ordered. I ordered 2 of the tacos adobada,  2 of the tacos de rajas, 1 pork chop taco, one guacamole and Atole. I figured that I could take all leftovers home. When I brought it to the table she timidly took a bite of the Rajas and it was all over. About 3 to 4 tacos and it was over. No leftovers. The Atole was amazing by the way. Not to thick and starchy. Well cooked and flavored. Delicious. La vid

I had an enormous craving for sushi today, so I did what always do... yelp for sushi places in SB. From a couple of stores I found, I chose Ahi and I'm so happy I did.  I'm not a big eater - so I went for the individual nigiri sushi. They might seem a bit pricy for one piece of sushi (1.50~3.50ish) but honestly, it is worth it. It's been a while since I had that blast of freshness and flavor when eating sushi. I might go back in a couple of days.  Recommendations: Uni, Shiitake, Kanpachi

YELP   Ordered: I've been for breakfast and min h! I like breakfast more. I got their French toast and my husband ordered the chili omelet. For lunch I had the veggie quiche with salad and my husband had the venison burger. Irish coffee.   2/2 everything aesthetics and vibe: this is the most cozy and authentic rustic place you could go to! Everything has history to it! It feels like your stepping back in time.   1/1 product, my French toast was delicious and the perfect amount! I believe they make their syrup. The quiche had more cheese than egg in it. Irish Coffee was bad too much alcohol in it for our taste.   1/1 staff, mostly friendly! Our first experience was fantastic! Donna and the staff were welcoming. But the second time we had Bryan as our server. I highly suggest he gets trained in customer service. He made us feel like we were annoying and a burden to him.   .5/.5 price, on the more expensive side, especially dinner! But it's doable for breakfast.   .5/.5 exterior matters, the best mountain gem! It feels surreal especially in a misty day.  Total: 5

Atmosphere-4 Food-3 Service-3 ------------ I had hoped this place was going to have one of those amazing breakfast but the food was very average. It isn't a bad place but the food was stuff I could have made at home. Staff was nice! I just expected more based on the other reviews.

Food is always very good but our server, Nico was absolutely terrible. Was too interested in his friends at the bar, and was too troubled to simply split a check stating, 'I don't know what you had to eat'. We left an appropriate tip for the poor service... it's just that, a tip. Nico then pops his head out from the second story balcony and yells at us on State Street in a very unprofessional and sarcastic tone, 'thanks a lot, I will remember you guys', threatening us! Such a shame, this place has great views/location, really good food but Nico was a terrible. Because of him I will not be returning. Unless you want to be threatened by a server, I recommend you don't go either.   *update*  The manager contacted me and completely apologize and offered a gift certificate. Very thoughtful. I was informed the problem server we had is no longer working there. I'll be back, The food is just too good.

This place has something for EVERYONE! We started with the spinach artichoke dip which is served with their homemade pizza crust for dipping and it was yummerzzz. My son enjoyed the chicken tenders and fries but wants to try their pizza next time. I got the Bahn Mi which was authentic and delicious. My husband had the BBQ burger which I had to steal a bite of and it was our favorite thing on the menu so far. I can't wait to go back for the Pho and to try some of their breakfast items. We are excited to have this new place, with so much variety, in SB! We plan to eat our way through the entire menu.

Great place that has super high quality pizza at surprisingly reasonable prices. Honor Market delivers a unique twist on typical pizza flavors with a crunchy medium thin crust that's somewhere between Neapolitan cracker and San Francisco sourdough (ala Cheese Board and Arizmendi). I imagine every combination is good; we had the Wild Mushroom, Sweet Pepper and Spicy Kale. On our visit margherita pizza was the special (to make this a local family takeout it will have to be a regular item). The secret dipping sauce is herbaceous and slightly spicy from garlic and peppers -- a great twist on the ubiquitous side of ranch dressing so many take out places offer. At $15 for a 16' pie ($18 if you add sausage) there isn't a better deal in town by price. The quality ingredients are equal or better to anything in the 805 and while selection is limited the taste/flavor/preparation rivals the best pizzas anywhere.
The food was great. Our server's name was Christian. He was even greater. Thus, the five stars.

This place was disgusting. The service was poor and seemed poorly run. They jam in as many tables as they possibly can, this creating zero ambience. Was really disappointed we gave this place another try. Much better places near by.
Best clam chowder I've ever had!!  The service was amazing...they should teach other resturants how to be in the hospitality business.

This review is for the croissants! We have tried a few different flavors including plain, almond, peppermint, bacon/jalapeño/cheese and they are all fantastic. Today I tried the seasonal peppermint chocolate croissant which has milk chocolate whipped ganache in the center- i was hesitant about peppermint in a croissant but this was so good! They will have the peppermint chocolate croissant through December -go get it now! They also have a full breakfast menu and I can't wait to go for brunch!

My fiancé and I recently had dinner here on Sunday night. We made online reservations ($25 refundable to reserve) and came right when they opened. We were seated immediately and were in awe of how beautiful the outdoor courtyard is. The lights and fire pits make this a super cute and romantic place for date nights. We started off with some hummus and pita bread that was savory and delicious. The pita bread was warm and super soft. For entrees, I ordered the lamb kebab and my fiancé ordered the mixed shawarma. We are both new to mediterranean food but we honestly really enjoyed the food. Service was great and attentive. The overall atmosphere, food and service make this a great dinner spot, we can't wait to come back here again!

Second time in a month. Place is amazing. Olivier, Lauren, Anchor on Draft. The food is simple, elegant and delicious. Get the Chips rendered in beef tallow, Zucchini Fritters and artichokes. Highly recommend this place.
Hotel was great however I had a bad experience with their parking valet. Needless to say, I would not recommend using their valet.
Solid burger and fries. Side salad was great too!

Just got back from Silvergreens and I can hardly contain myself!  I was majorly impressed with this place.  The menu is amazing... I'm on a healthy kick and hate being limited to tofu and quinoa.  There were so many spectacular choices... I decided on a Chopped BBQ salad.  My husband, who isn't on a healthy kick, opted for the Crispy Bacon Western Sandwich.  Upon getting our receipt, we found that the nutrition facts for our meals were printed out on our receipt (thanks to a new gizmo called Nutricate, which Silvergreens is first to use.)  When the food arrived, it was spectacular.  My husbands sandwich wasn't greasy, and looked amazing!  He enjoyed every bite of that thing... the side fries were also amazing (though I wish he would have tried the equally awesome looking green bean fries!)  I got the full salad, thinking the half would be too small and was blown away - it was truly the size of a small child.  I ended up needing a to go box, which I got from the amazingly friendly staff.  As we left, I said thanks to the gal who helped us... all three of the people working also said bye to us as well (which I normally may not have liked, however, they were all amazingly genuine!)  I simply can't wait to go back, which will most likely happen tomorrow, and the next day and the next.  They use all local ingredients when possible and do their part to be eco-friendly.  I am in LOOOOOOOOVE. 5 stars for this place.  I'd give them 10 if I could!

For a no fuss, straightforward, fresh delicious meal, On The Alley is a perfect stop. They're located at the harbor and are located near Brophy Bros.  It's first come first served and there's only a couple communal outdoor tables, so takeout and going elsewhere seems like good idea.  We stopped in for a quick bite and had a good experience here. The line wasn't too long for a weekend afternoon when we stopped in. Food came out nicely prepared and served in a timely manner.   Fish & Chips - These were great! Well cooked, golden brown, perfectly crispy with a good batter and flakey fish. Fries were crisp and soft on the inside. Served with tartar, ketchup and a lemon slice.   Friend had some fish tacos which were enjoyed and we also had the calamari which tasted fresh and was cooked well.   Parking is at the harbor with a per hour price, about $2+ or so.

I've been to Dave's a couple of times, and while I mostly agree with all of the plaudits this place receives, I can't give it '5 stars' in good conscience: they sell hot dogs - presumably to drunk people and teenagers from the high school across the street, and it's not like they are doing to hot dogs what Carlos Salgado is doing to tacos at Taco María. They ARE outstanding hot dogs though.  The 'Pizza Dog' is my personal favorite, consisting of a hot dog covered with pizza sauce and parmesan cheese, held together by an outside seal of melted and nicely-browned mozzarella and pepperoni. It somehow actually tastes more like a pizza slice than a hot dog. The 'Santa Barbara Dog' features a wiener wrapped in crispy bacon and then garnished with grilled onions, tomatoes, mayo, ketchup, avocado, cilantro, mustard, and crema - which makes it suspiciously close to the 'TJ Dogs' ubiquitous in my current city of residence, San Diego. Call it whatever you want, but this combination of stuff on a bacon-wrapped dog works unexpectedly well, harmoniously combining elements of fresh, creamy, crispy, and savory.  The much-hyped 'Hot Cheetos & Cream Cheese Dog' is food for adolescents. A younger or intoxicated version of me may have appreciated this much more. I also wasn't crazy about the 'Chili Dog' when applied to their hot dog-sized kielbasa link. The marriage of kielbasa and cheesy chili was a better idea in my head than in execution here. You really want to order a regular dog if you go the chili route.  The 'Western Dog' is perhaps the most straightforward hot dog I had here. Beware that a bacon-wrapped hot dog smothered in tangy BBQ sauce, grilled onions, spicy mayo, and jalapeños could result in substantial heartburn or worse but, if it was like the one I had at Dave's, it will probably be worth it.  Food/Décor/Service: 4.0/3.9/NA  What To Order: Pizza Dog, Santa Barbara Dog, Western Dog
great option for brunch. cool space w creative cocktails. had a burger and some oysters. probably would do the same thing next time.
The food and service is great. This place is clean and there are several rooms you can choose to dine in. I'd recommend the tiger roll or tuna carpaccio.
A great location for a burger.  Great seating and large selection of local be taken.  Fries and really flavorful.  You won't leave hungry.   Outdoor seating is perfect.
I send 20 customers a day here.  Why I like is the two-sided menu.  One side kids menu and pub fare... opposite side great $25 a plate options...

Opal has a great location between Santa Barbara's two big theatre venues; as we had opera tickets for late July, we decided to try it for an early supper. We were given lovely table by the window and seated promptly with our reservation. The venue is quite noisy (low ceiling, crowds of the young-and-prosperous). My meal began propitiously with a delicious Mojito cocktail; I ordered their special house gazpacho starter, and a smoked chicken quesadilla with baby greens. Sadly, the cold soup was a strange mixture of a very thin and hotly chile-spiced broth and a large amount of tiny, tiny cubes of various unidentifiable vegetables (I'm assuming cucumber and carrot, onion, and maybe jicama, but it was hard to tell). The quesadilla was OK, but not exciting. Looking at other reviews I'm thinking maybe it was just me ... but I've eaten in great restaurants all over the world in my many long years, and Opal just struck me as the Santa Barbara restaurants impressed our great former resident, Julia Child, who when asked what she thought about those establishments in her new home town, replied that they 'seemed perfectly nice.' You go, girl!

Amazing is the word to describe this place,  Super cool people, nice , cozy and super creative and delicious coffees. Heard from a friend of mine, definitely was a wow when I saw the coffees. Breakfast was good but the drinks and the setup was incredibly amazing.

There's a lot of competition as far as Mexican food goes in Santa Barbara. Rose cafe is hands down the best Mexican food in Santa Barbara really. For a first timer I would suggest any variety of their combination plates. The enchiladas are pretty damn good and kinda goes well with the beans and rice. I always get an extra side of cream and pour my complimentary salsa all over it.  That's one thing about Mexican food, Chinese food, and Indian food. If you mix it all together on the plate it just seems to work.  Some notable highlights are the steak ranchero, which is very flavorful and filling, and the tacos Guadalajara which are also amazing. Any taco there is pretty good to be honest.  On weekends there's a little bit of a wait sometimes but it's well worth it.
Love this restaurant and they seemed to have really improved their DoorDash game too.  Amazing food and great staff.

We love Jane, both locations. Excellent starters, salads, burgers, mains, drinks and dessert. Besides the delicious menu, we also enjoy the great service. From greetings from the front to exceptional service, we are always pleased. Never ever had a bad meal or experience there. They don't always have it, but when they do try a soft serve Ice Cream Sunday. That's my daughter's favorite. Jane's coconut cake is mine!

Was really excited  to try the new Caribbean / Creole place in town. Went yesterday during lunch to meet a girlfriend while on lunch break. We both were looking forward to trying the food.   There are two different menus.. dinner menu seemed more appealing and offered shrimp and grits which is not on the lunch menu.   We went around five after eleven (opening) and sat right away. Service was a bit slow during the meal as only one or two girls were working. We asked for waters with lemon but they brought them with cucumber. We were told they didn't have lemon. Odd but ok.   We ordered the pumpkin soup, Greek salad, catfish sandwich, and the jerk chicken. Pictures below.  Pumpkin soup was a HUGE disappointment... it tasted like chicken broth with a splash of pumpkin flavor. It was very watery. Not thick, hearty, or even a bit creamy like one expects of a pumpkin soup. Most important, it lacked pumpkin flavor. It wasn't accompanied by toasted pumpkin seeds, bread, or croutons. Fail.   It took a while between the pumpkin soup and the salad. Greek salad was very underwhelming. It was tossed romaine lettuce with feta, green olives, and a few black olives. I questioned the server if this was standard as most Greek salads have cucumber, red onion, tomato, and only black pitted olives.. she said it was 'their play' on a Greek salad. Again, this is a Caribbean/Creole place so a play in a salad could have included jicama or peppers or a unique dressing with flavors authentic to that cuisine. Another fail in my opinion.  The catfish sandwich was very tasty. It was well seasoned with nice color. It was served on ciabatta and had grainy mustard, tomato, butter pickles, and onions (raw). Onions were a bit much and they were not cut consistently. Would have been better to pickle them. The sandwich was also served with a side of spinach tossed with the same dressing as the Greek salad. A better side would have been Caribbean slaw, curried vegetable salad, or warm bean salad. Just suggestions. The sandwich was good but it definitely needs some tweaking.   Last dish was the jerk chicken which is probably one of the most popular Caribbean dishes out there. THANKFULLY, the spice rub was done right! The rice was cooked and flavored well. A little too mushy for my taste but my friend enjoyed it. The plantain was tasty. I feel they should offer the option to do a traditional fried plantain or steamed or grilled.   I would review this place again after trying the dinner menu options. Hopefully, some of the kinks are worked out. They do not serve beer or liquor at this time as they are still in the process of getting licensed.
So freaking amazing. Worth the hype and worth the wait. #16 special and the veggie tamal. Homemade tortillas are amazing too.

The best noodle soup I've ever had.  Tender chicken, perfectly balanced thai curry sauce, freshly made noodles. Service is fast, friendly, and professional. An easy five stars and a must stop when in SB

Such a rad little spot.   Walking in and it's immediately noticeable that the owners and staff here take immense pride in this little cafe. Parking might be a little tricky but I'm sure there are plenty of spots around the block, I walked here while getting my smog done.   The shop is clean, open, and super well decorated overall with a great modern vibe while being super comfortable.   I'm a pretty simple coffee drinker but I had to try something new in a place like this. I got the burnout which is one of their signature drinks. At $7 it's not exactly super cheap but most certainly worth it. Along with that I got the avocado toast which you would think is over done in Santa Barbara but was perfect here with just the right combination of flavors    Overall this cafe is a great addition to the area and I would definitely go here again for a treat.   Plus they are super environmentally conscious and center around sustainability. Always a bonus.
If you love sweet succulent lobster with buttery roll .. Delicious. Chris is as sweet as his lobster

Best spot on Milpas for breakfast! While I have yet to try something I haven't liked, I have a few suggestions.  If you are in the mood to indulge yourself, order the Eggs-in-Yo Face with bacon and avocado. Oh. My. Delicious.  A thick cut piece of house made bread toasted to perfection with poached eggs and a pile of chopped bacon.  If you prefer a lighter fare, try out the Good Day Sunshine; A delicious salad served in a bowl lined with potato chips. It's the perfect balance of trying to be healthy, but with a small cheat.   They also have great lunch selections! Yet again, there's not one that I've tried and didn't like, but the Sweet Maria and Just the Tip are my go to choices.  All sandwiches are served on house made bread that can hold up to the meat and condiments inside.   The staff is always friendly and helpful, but it does seem difficult for them to hear your order thru the small window, so speak up! They bring your food to the table and with a recent change, also bus the tables.  To recap, go to The Shop Café and try anything!

Noticed this cute little sandwich/coffee shop while staying at the Spanish Garden Inn- as it is walking distance.  They seem to draw quite a lunch crowd and rightly so.  This charming shop has a number of delicious fresh sandwiches.  I had a difficult time narrowing it down and finally settled on Prosciutto w/Fresh Mozzarella and Roasted Red Peppers (It contained Imported Italian Prosciutto, Fresh Mozzarella, Roasted Red Peppers, Fresh Basil & Greens with Olive Oil and Balsamic Vineger).  YUM YUM.   I like that they have a robust vegetarian menu separate out from the main menu for our veggie friends.  I also enjoyed the outside seating.

i love brophy's! i get the craving for their chowder and a Firestone DBA often. i am getting excited just thinking about it. the atmosphere is upbeat, not quiet, and very relaxed. it sits on the harbor so you get the fresh fish smell. ;) bartenders are always fun. good luck getting a table-they are always busy. i feel like it's a local's spot and is appreciated in that sense. i'm a fan.

aesthetic: 3.5/5 food: 5/5 service: 3.5/5   i came to dawn patrol w a friend for a post-hike brunch, and we really enjoyed our meal here!! we both got the build-your-own-hash -- my friend's scrambled egg topping was made perfectly, and my veganized version (veggie sausage) was so crispy and had a really delish, not-overly-meaty-salty flavour to it. it was the right amount of food too, and we left feeling a good, not overly-stuffed kinda full :)   ambiance-wise, there's lots of seating and a cute, tropical vibe corner with giant plant decor (of course, we had to pick that area to sit in lol). there was a line out the door when we came around 11, but it moved quickly and we got our food within 10 minutes of ordering.   i would have loved to see more vegan options on the menu, like vegan pancakes, but for this one vegan item that dawn patrol has, it'll definitely please your tastebuds!! with the super fast service and affordable + yummy brunch options for every diet (gluten free pancakes, anyone?), dawn patrol is so worth checking out!!

Their staff was pretty rude on Valentine's Day when I was picking up my online order. I ordered $75 worth of food only to be left with street corn without cheese or the sauce it comes with. Left an email including my order #  to see if they would resolve only to not receive any reply at all. Other food was great so can't knock that but not a great experience.

Best smoothie joint in town, no scratch that, ever. Triumphs in quality, quantity and service!  I'm from Northern California so I only ever get blenders every once in a blue moon. I wish so badly that they could open some stores up here but oh well. I always make sure to get my fix when I'm in the area.   The flavors are so simple and they list out all the ingredients as well. I usually get the strawberry, and it's only $5.25 for the medium! For that size at Jamba Juice, it's probably $6.   Service is always friendly and they never take too long on blending, unless it's crowded but I've never had to wait more than 5 minutes or so for my order.  Definitely come here if you're around and craving a smoothie, you won't regret it!

I love this restaurant it has a nice patio, good ice cream, good food. We have never had a bad meal here and the staff is great. It's pretty kid friendly too which is a plus. The view of the harbor is really cool and gives the patio a very unique atmosphere. We bring friends and family from out of town here and it is always well received. I like that they will bring out crackers and also a quick order of fries to keep the little ones happy.
Espectacular! Muy buen lugar con muchas opciones para ir a comer. El ambiente es muy lindo y la verdad que tiene muchas opciones impresionantes. Vale la pena ir!

The food is good no doubt about that! However the service is lacking. Multiple occasions my boyfriend and I have gone either just the two of us or with a group and some how they always seem to forget at least one persons food in the kitchen while the others have arrived to the table. Once my dish came almost 5 minutes after my boyfriends. Overall the food is unique and tasty but they should really improve on there order at the counter service system. By the way.. They do have a great salsa bar!!

Amazing! While I missed the little plaza location they used to have, the move has proven to be for the better. They were able to expand sitting and the inside is just beautiful. We went for Sunday Brunch, expected a bit of a long wait and still got seated within a reasonable time.   We ordered a variety of plates for the table and every single one was incredible; the quinoa oatmeal, the dirty fries, the smoked salmon and a couple more I forgot the names of because we ate them so fast. Chef Jonathan Ramirez has truly brought it a sense of freshness and deliciousness that can't be beat. COMPLIMENTS TO THE CHEF! Our server Jonah was also superb, he was super busy as Sunday Brunch typically goes, but every time he approached the table he was friendly and apologetic for any wait (which wasn't bad at all) and delivered perfect recommendations. The manager Hilary also stopped by to check how the food was and to introduce herself. Just excellent service.   The great service combined with the savory food is the main reason I will return!

Cute swirly soft ice  cream with no dairy!  A rich, pure combination of dates and  chocolate.  Special edition seasonal  flavors.  Healthy and delicious.  Phenomenal fresh juices.   Yes, a bit pricy but we'll worth it.

Always a winner.  Offering specialties from Portugal his month. Had the marinated anchovies as an app. Delicious. Also finished with a 22 year old port, a perfect end to a meal which also included the farmers market board and short ribs with a robust earthy cinsault from Languedoc.  Beautiful dinner.

(Pre- COVID-19 Review) For a seriously amazing PEPPERONI PIZZA, check out Bettina's take at the Montecito Country Mart. Not only do they use the same pepperoni served at New York's Prince Street Pizza, they also don't skimp on it. Literally every bite I had, had pepperoni. And all that savory goodness is balanced with the sweetness of Ojai wildflower honey. I also really liked their charred, yet chewy crust.  Great place to stop at if you're travelling between Santa Barbara and Los Angeles.

Wow. I'm a nurse and ordered for my other friend who is a nurse and is working. The person on the phone was so rude the entire time, he even MOCKED ME when I said 'thank you so much have a great day!' And he replied in a squeaky mocking voice 'you too ma'am oh my god' and hung up!!! I will be calling first thing in the morning to speak to the manager. My place of work calls Rusty's multiple times a week for orders and I will make sure we NEVER ORDER FROM THEM AGAIN. Period. Do not order from this establishment, who employs absolutely rude and inconsiderate people who berate customers that pay for their time, training, services, and food.

This restaurant was really cute, both inside and outside seating. The vibe seemed Mediterranean inside and then outside had flowers and succulents throughout. The service was timely. It was pretty crowded, but nice you could hear the party you were with. We tried a few appetizers and cheeses. We tried two cheeses, but they don't double the sides that come with the cheeses, so I'd recommend getting only one cheese at a time. The shrimp was really delicious and cooked to perfection, but the patatas bravas was a little odd and did not taste authentic.

Promising. Pork bao and fried cinn/sugar donut with ice cream will bring me back. Entrees seem overly ambitious in their effort to be fusion.  Sometimes simple is best. Carbonara was creamy but too salty.  Clean, bright interior, attentive staff. Reservations usually needed which can be frustrating when you just want a quick bite.

AMAZING SERVICE! After eating out for almost every meal this last week on a staycation in Santa Barbara and finding almost every other location severely lacking in the service department, intermezzo was a shining star of excellent service and has been the highlight dining experience of our week!!  Our server Paul was very attentive, friendly and had great drink recommendations! The other waitstaff was equally attentive, refills on water, clearing plates, etc.   Beyond the stellar staff, the food was delicious and filling, we had the meatballs (delicious) and two pizzas which were way more then enough food! So tasty!  I honestly can't recommend this place enough!! We will be back soon!   Also we arrived and our table wasn't ready yet so we were handed free glasses of champagne as we waited for our table. Class act!
This is probably our best go-to place in SB. House beers happy hour. Great vibe. Great service. Does on TV. Secret parking lot. Fish and chips. Free Wi-Fi.

The food was good. This review is not about the food. The experience was TERRIBLE. We were Pretty Womaned in the worst way.   My husband and I came to Santa Barbara this weekend to celebrate our anniversary - we were married here last year. We made a reservation for our celebration dinner at Finch and Fork after reading all the great reviews. Our reservation was for 8:30. Way past the bedtime of the two screaming fighting children at the table next to us. (Admittedly this part is not the restaurant's fault, but it sure did compound our terrible experience.*)  Shortly after we were seated, our waiter, Brett, came by and asked ME if I wanted anything from the bar. I said I wanted to start with water and he then walked away without asking my husband if he wanted anything. When he came back to see if we wanted to order, he literally said 'Do you want me to explain the menu or are you good?' in a tone that said 'don't make me explain the menu'. Any time he came to our table, he was clearly rushing us to move on.   To make matters worse, throughout our dinner we both noticed our waiter checking in with other tables and being way friendlier. He also told all tables within range about some charity raffle the restaurant was holding. Did he mention the raffle to us? Nope. Apparently we didn't look fancy enough to warrant a raffle offer or even decent service.   Not that it matters, but my husband and I are both attorneys. We have no children and thus have a decent amount of disposable income. I probably would have entered the raffle! In other words, like Julia Roberts in that scene in Pretty Woman, we could have paid if we were given the chance.   * Not the restaurant's fault, but the kids did call the manager 'Uncle Marc.' He could have seen the awful seating situation and offered to move us. Granted, he did stop by to check in with us but at that point it was already too late to salvage the experience.

Latley the sauce has been really light to where I wanna order extra sauce but I'm not an extra sauce kind of guy the pizzas have been very different from one another and consistency as of late and I've been going to different pizza places for now guess next time I come over I will order extra sauce see what happens i am a long time customer here.

Finally decided to eat in. Updating review to 5* based on my kids (and my experience). 'wow dad you gotta rate this place 5 stars on Yelp!' She was right. From the helpful staff constantly helping me out as a rookie chef, to the tasty ingredients and plentiful quantities. We really had a good time out as a family. It is definitely a good place to take the kids when one parent has to work. Kids are not too pricey (age+$3), so with just one adult it wasn't too pricey. Plus the kids had a blast choosing and then cooking their own food. My favorite was the brisket and pork belly, both cooked just to be slightly crispy. So good!  I do have to figure out how to be a better cook, I think I was just impatient and was asking them to turn the fire too high to cook faster but then it would burn the leftover after I was done cooking and smoked. The staff came over a few times and swap out the grill and turn the down. Anyway it was great food and good fun, and as my daughter said definitely 5*... We'll be back the next time mom has to work late :)

Hadn't been here in years. Had 2 crunchy chicken tacos and from the secret menu 1 chicken enchrito. Everything came out hot and tasty. All the people were friendy, the cashier, food preparer person, and the guy cleaning the tables. The place was clean, with a parking lot in back. A lil under $9.

An absolutely beautiful,elegant and historic property nestled into the American Riviera. I can't wait to come back one day. Also they have great customer service. I highly recommend staying at El Encanto

Piizza is really good but the service was unpleasant. The cashier/waitress was not attentive or friendly. She let our pizzas sit out for a few minutes and wouldn't bother to bring it over to us when she brought the people next to us their food. Also napkins weren't provided and when I asked, her demeanor was a bit rude. It's unfortunate because that's the first person you see and represents the restaurant but can ruin your whole experience. I recommend better training or hiring more personable employees.

My previous review neglected to mention the addition of Dim Sum to their weekend offerings. Where was I for that announcement?!  They have about 30 items which you order off a card in Sushi-checklist style. Service was good as ever, and the food came out quickly. I haven't had Dim Sum in over a year, but it compared favorably to a number of places around San Jose I've visited. The Stuffed Eggplant and Shrimp items were very good.  I'll have to give their dinner menu another try, but providing good Dim Sum in this town is enough for an extra star until I find out more.
Loved the Hazy IPA, the croquettes and the beyond burger. Great atmosphere and a huge patio. I'll definitely be back.
Want to meet a friend or coworkers for a drink, sit outside, and enjoy a great happy hour menu?  Come here.  You'll enjoy everything brought out to you.  There is something for everyone.

Went for lunch and what a disappointment! After reading the reviews I was expecting to eat good food. Pesto pasta was so covered with sauce that it was unpleasant to eat. No balance in flavor! I questioned whether the meatballs and the lasagna were homemade and not just a processed frozen dish. Service was so so!   So overall, I will not go back.

Great place for breakfast. Fresh bread and pastries great coffee and great breakfast. I get the huevoos rancheros. Customer service is always good. When u walk in the place smells amazing. Nice seating inside and outside to take advantage of that beautiful Santa Barbara weather. What more can u ask for definitely a most for any breakfast person.

Two days ago I contacted cafe Ana via the website to reserve tables for our company who had a hearing across the street.  We wanted to provide coffee, snacks and lunch to several of our speakers and guests.   This was my first experience at cafe Ana and I selected it due to the proximity of our hearing.  WOW, was I blown away (as were our guests)!  The table was reserved upon our arrival, the owners were VERY understanding and accommodating to our evolving plans and even worked to find room during a busy lunch rush!  The ambiance was light, bright, modern, clean and upbeat.  The staff was beyond sweet, constantly had our waters filled and the food was on a whole new level. We ordered the meatballs, chicken salad sandwiches, ham sandwiches, pastries, lattes, and the FRESHEST OJ!  The homemade chips w/seasoning are delicious!  One of our guests is a local restaurant owner who as so impressed by the food. I am very happy to have discovered such a lovely spot.   The cashier that added our tab at the end of the afternoon was just as sweet, outgoing and friendly as could be.   This will be a regular lunch spot for us.

One of my favorite sushi places in SB! I have eaten at the restaurant and ordered to go and both times I was very impressed with them. The fish was always fresh and the rice is seasoned well. This is my go to sushi place in Santa Barbara for sushi and they never seem to disappoint. I really liked their rolls especially the yellow tail delight and the spider roll. Their special sushi and sashimi is also great and you get 10 pieces chosen by the chef that day.

The food here is consistently great - favorites include the wings and fries. There's something about those fries that keep me coming back for more. Sandwiches are also a good bet, especially the fried chicken on brioche. And best of all, you can wash it all down with their cocktails. The mojito is an excellent choice, but really, you won't go wrong.   And on Wednesdays nights, there's line dancing so it can get loud and busy, but it'll be a good time regardless!
A friend recommended we come here.  Said the food was exceptional.  When we got here, there was a long line, which is a good sign, but I found the food just okay.

Starbucks has nothing to fear because this Coffee Bean is mediocre.  If you need a quick coffee fix and you don't like the roast at Starbucks, you could come to this Coffee Bean in Paseo Nuevo. There is a bakery case and refrigerator for snacks. The interior is small, and there's very limited counter seating facing outside. It offers outdoor seating to people-watch.  The drinks themselves aren't that good. Their ice blendeds have too much powder and taste like a protein shake. There's a 50-50 chance the baristas will ask you if you'd like your tea sweetened. And they'll always be out of something. But it makes up for that by being a reliable place to get a plain cup of coffee fast.

This is a lovely restaurant & cafe  that makes an incredibly delicious cappuccino. (Vegan) It was refreshing to be greeted with warmth and kindness by the staff and my waitress, Merari, was so polite and attentive she made my meal even more enjoyable. I recommend coming here if you are looking for good food and a casual ambiance.

If you love tequila/mezcal and Mexican food - this is a great spot to go! Their cocktails were excellent - definitely recommend the mezcal ones.  I also got the octopus tartare, crab enchiladas, and queso with tortillas. It was all very tasty and well priced! A very fun spot

We passed by around lunchtime and the smell of the food made us backtrack and try it out.   They were serving lunch buffet but we ordered ala carte.   We ordered chicken samosas (2 pieces/order) and it was the best I've ever tasted. I highly recommend it with the red sauce.    Next was lamb tikka masala, lamb vindaloo, basmati rice and garlic naan. The tikka masala was very delicious with the rice. The garlic naan was made fresh so it was piping hot when they served it. It was thin, crispy and so good. It's like very thin crust pizza with garlic and cilantro. It can be eaten by itself but it is also good dipped on the tikka masala sauce. The lamb vindaloo was so-so. It came with suspiciously red sauce with some zing in it but it wasn't great-tasting.   The service was superb. The servers were very attentive and nice.   The place was clean, bright and airy.   We will definitely come back to Tamira. And we will order extra samosas for take out.

Great food, TERRIBLE SERVICE!!! Really disappointed about the service and had much higher expectations. Was told you needed 6 or more to make a reservation so attempted to walk in and was told we couldn't be seated without a reservation (there were only 3 of us). Then, went outside and called for a table, came back in and was finally seated only to be ignored for around 40 minutes.

It was a ridiculously hot day today in Encino! 118 degrees. There was no relief in sight other than Blasting all of our air conditioners at full throttle, but The DWP and our Mayor asked us not to do this. So, What to do? Head for the beach and just drive. That being done, my wife and I end up in Santa Barbara at 3:30 p.m. It was a comfortable 79 degrees and I wanted an ice cold beer and lunch / dinner. We happened upon The Bluewater Grill just across the street from the S.B. Pier and Marina.  These nice people really had their 'Covad Protocol' together. Safety was of paramount concern and everybody acted responsibly and hassle free.  Seeing this was our first big time, grown up, serious sit down meal at a restaurant since March 9th, I was not only comfortable but excited to 'Be Back' in restaurant mode.  My fried seafood platter with cole slaw and home made potato chips was really very good. My wife's branzino and French fries was equally as good.  Eating this lovely meal 40 degrees cooler than my backyard, while looking at the deep blue Pacific and with a staff so well trained while giving their patrons 'It's Nice To Have You Back' treatment, made our first outing in months a very nice experience.   Thank you Bluewater Grill - we'll be back.
This is hands down one of my favorite restaurants in Santa Barbara. The view is amazing, the food is delicious and the staff is always on point. It is a MUST stop when visiting Santa Barbara.
stopped going; place is getting ore and more indifferent to its patrons.  Feels like they are doing us a favor when we dine there, and not v.v.

I love Santa Barbara dining, and FisHouse does not disappoint! I came here for dinner with a friend and ordered the special lobster dish, which was so flavorful (yummy, buttery goodness!). My friend ordered the prime rib, which was also full of flavor. Our waiter was very attentive and the ambiance is definitely more intimate/romantic.  They have a great happy hour but I recommend that you arrive early. It gets very crowded!

We spent a month in SB dining out at all the recommended places. This one was a disappointment, from the time I asked about the special, to the check. Without provocation we were told there were no substitutions-perhaps it would have been better if there were...My fish was so-so, the vegetables few and limp, my husband's pasta dish good but ordinary. For the price, there are way too many other choices. We will not go back.  Perhaps dinner is taken more seriously, but we will never know.
You don't go here for sushi, because there are way better places in the city.  It's basically SB's mom-n-pop version of Benihana...

I feel a small warning should be given for the cocktails. THEY WERE ALL STRONG AND VERY FLAVORFUL! If you are planning to bar hop, this should be your first place first. We made the mistake making this our last stop. The bouncer and our server were very friendly. We had to purchase food as a requirement, so he offered chips and salsa, which was $2. Not bad for the serving- chips were good enough to share, salsa was small and was lacking liquid (i like the liquid in pico de gallo!), but can't complain due to price. We ordered the Rock'n Tea ('good choice' per our server), Flaming Lips, Cadillac Margarita, and I think another Margarita (I don't remember). I got the Flaming Lips- I like jalapeno margaritas. I thought I could handle it. OMG IT WAS SO STRONG! And it was so spicy my throat swelled up. I drank it so fast and chased with a chip. My friends were like why!! I drank it fast to get it over with, but i wasn't gonna waste it! The best tasting drink was the Rock'n Tea, and since it tasted the best, it didnt' taste as strong, but all our drinks had a heavy pour haha. I think I would order that instead of being a brave soul. I gotta come back! Looks like a lot of people come to enjoy here since many shots are ordered and sports games, etc. were playing on the large screens inside (no seating inside, but you can easily see the huge screens from outside).

The burgers here are amazing! It has a great atmosphere and great service! I would recommend anyone come here for burgers and beer. It's like Santa Barbara's own little Eureka Burger but so much better! Great food, great drinks and the outdoor patio is perfect for a group!

Aldo's is great italian food.  The location is excellent....a bit of a walk from Paseo Nuevo, the decor and building is adorable...you feel like you are in a little house in Tuscany.  The service is great....the bread is fresh and the food is delish.  I had the chicken parmigiana and it was delicious.  In some ways I favor this restaurant more than Palazzio across the street.  The reason is because this is your typical italian restaurant....it's not 'family style' like Palazzio and it's more than just pasta.  The layout of the restaurant makes it like a private dining experience for everyone....no one is sitting on other people's laps because it is so crowded.  I would probably choose Zaffiro (in Goleta) - which is basically Pascucci over Aldo for convenience.  Aldo is a great restaurant if you are downtown and craving true italian.

I used to like this place when I was an omnivore, but it is impossible to avoid animal products there.  Even their beans and rice are made with chicken stock!!   Whether it's traditional or not, there are a large number of people who don't eat food made from animals, and beans and rice can be plenty tasty without chicken stock.  Vegetarians/vegans will need to go elsewhere.

Only great things to say here.   Staff as friendly, prompt, and very knowledgeable when it came to the food.   Food was sooo good. Ordered the garlic Parmesan fries. A bit much on the rosemary they included in but besides that, quite tasty. And they give you plenty so you don't have to be greedy.   For dinner my husband and I shared the porkchop with brussel sprouts and perhaps the best bacon Mac n cheese I've ever had. Quite a generous portion too! Walked away completely full and with a very happy belly! We I'll be back!

Average. The fresh tortilla's were nice, but the meat was bland. I also think that because I grew up eating more Americanized versions of tacos with gauc and a bunch of other toppings, I thought that these needed more of everything. Overall, just okay to me.
Was pretty apprehensive about a vegan restaurant but this place is the real deal. Even my 'meataterian' husband enjoyed his food. We will be back!
Amazing! Don't miss tasting these brisket sandwiches.

Do not hesitate to visit this beautiful restaurant on the beach! We went for breakfast and were so impressed by the service and food. We wish we had more time in SB to return. We had the Chilaquiles and the Benedict with ham and gluten free bread instead of an English muffin. Both were out of this world! Can't wait to return!

I came because I had my Axxess card offer still and I'm glad I did! I ordered the lasagna and garlic cheese bread.
