I got my lasagna and we never got our cheese bread so we told the waitress and asked her to please cancel our order and if and asked for a refund. Another gal came out and told us the truth, that our order was given to the wrong table. She said that we were going to get a refund and still get the cheese bread. Taffy's could have given us a refund and be done with us but instead they were honest and corrected the error. I really appreciated that and will definitely be coming back. Good food and good service.

We ordered two extra large pizzas from Giovanni's for game night and they were a hit! We came here because they have a great axxess deal where your first pizza is 50% off, but we will definitely be back for more. The guy who rung us up was very friendly, the pizza was delicious, and it was overall a great experience! The only downside is that they don't deliver.
Highly recommend get yourself the Yona special or try the quesa-pizza to share. They're the best tacos in town! Don't Forget to get the consulme to dip your taco in

I went in for lunch and was happy to see they offered 1/2 sandwiches and small fries, etc.  I ordered 1/2 the PLT (Pastrami Lettuce and Tomato) with a small order of fries.  It was good, but.... the crispy-fried pastrami was way too salty.  After a few bites, the salt simply overpowered all the other flavors of what was otherwise a good, creative sandwich.  Might go back to try the Reuben or Cheesesteak sandwich.  The fries were nothing special...

Amazing vegan/veg restaurant in SB. Surprisingly, Sb doesn't have a lot of veg only dining options.  The atmosphere is causal, the food is plated beautifully, and the food is great!  I highly recommend the empanadas, macro bowl, and DEFINITELY the baklava/ice cream dessert!

Much nicer inside than the Gray, geriatric pictures here look. Very top San Francisco feel. Chairs amazing comfortable thick leather..most comfortable restaurant chair ever....$2000 chairs?...how did they do that?  Drinks....amazing....really....some of the best in town especially lemoncello martinis and orangecello....wonderful inexpensive lovely white wine at happy hour. Top Rating.  Service....just great...several of the waiters from Ventura branch to get them going. Smart move.  Food. A little mixed.....but great on certain things. Quite frankly we found the calamari a bit medium in quality...the red sauce tasting of a little cheap garlic....the Thousand Island sauce much better and nice.... Crab cakes an honest disappointment...they looked so great in the pictures...but tasted mostly of filler...just the truth. But here is the AMAZING stuff...Baja Tacos scary delicious....just succulent...we fought over them.....they are NOT the Grilled Fish one...we didn't try that.(UPDATE...WE HAVE NOW TRIED THE GRILLED FISH, AND IT IS 'THE WINNER'..BEST OF THEM ALL TO US.) We tried the shrimp taco also...it was not the amazing quality of the Baja one. Clam chowder...wonderful ....as good as Brophy's but different style...a bit short on clams....but yummy ..mixed it 50/50 red/white....will get again for dipping the bread in!...Bread ok...pretty good...quality of Brophy's only not sourdough...more homemade roll style....not outstanding. So glad they came to town. Now we will have to fight you to get a place at happy hour...drat.This is really upscale feeling, all around, really like a top San Francisco place. For fair prices...hope they keep it up, that is hard to do, but the owners are making smart decisions. ***Update....Horribly bright lighting when we went back for lunch...some of the brightest worst lighting ever in a restaurant. Uncomfortable Sterile like an operating room. Please tell the management also if it bothers you. Fortunately, they had turned it way down to Romantic/comfortable during Happy hour...but not till 5. Management...you seem sharp....Please, Please turn down the lighting a LOT right at Happy Hour at 4.(lunch too if you want us back then.) (LAST UPDATE 6-21-16..WOW, THEY HAVE TURNED DOWN THE LIGHTING AND NOW IT IS COMFORTABLE...THAT IS IMPRESSIVE RESPONDING.PLEASE KEEP THIS UP) Thank you....everything else tasted great again...as above.
Nice sushi spot I got my signature dragon roll.  It was great.  Love the shrimp tempura.  Quick food.  High quality.  Tasty  Nice service. A+

My date and I decided to come here after an unpleasant encounter at another seafood restaurant down the street. The Drunken Crab. We walked in to two hosts and a server in conversation for a few minutes before they greeted us. We were poorly directed to the happy hour section and sat in the back corner of the restaurant. We were helped by a emotionless waitress with a nonchalant attitude. After we order and received our food, we didn't see her again until we had to flag her down for our bill. Although we enjoyed our food, the brussel sprouts were flavorful and delicious, we will not be recommending this spot to anyone looking for exceptional service during the happy hour rush at least.

This is such a gem of bakery and service is always very friendly.  Such nice people working here providing fresh bread with fresh ingredients from the local farmer's market.  My husband loves the sourdough, and i love their gluten free breads (sage & garlic and date & almond are my favs).  Visited them once a week during the pandemic and it was always such a warm, welcoming environment.  I'll gladly wait in line for fresh baked gluten free bread any day.  Also their Instagram page is awesome and they post mouthwatering pics of their creations and all of the smiling faces of the obviously happy people who work there.  Love supporting local businesses like this!

Decided to have takeout on the way back to Lompoc and I always see this place when I enter SB. I definitely liked the atmosphere and can tell this is a nice to-go spot for family pre-COVID.  I ordered the bbq personal pizza and it hit the spot. The slices were perfectly cut and the pie was made to full satisfaction. I definitely plan to visit again. No pictures because I finished my pizza too fast.   Service is quick!
Ordered drinks, with brussel sprouts as a snack.  The drinks were okay but the brussel sprouts were the best I've ever had!!

This review is for dinner - they tell me breakfast and brunch are their busy times.  As for dinner, they need a better chef.  Tupelo Junction Cafe is pleasant but not luxurious, and the service is friendly and efficient.  But the food, oh the food!  Take the fried green tomatoes (please) -- soggy.  The fried chicken?  Tough and a weird off-sweet taste.  The ribs?  They said they were bloody mary ribs, but to me the flavor was just yech.  And their Blackberry Mojito was a total misfire, neither fruity, minty, sweet, sour, or anything else enjoyable.  Not for me.

Wow.  How the mighty have  fallen.   Not like it use to be.  The rolls were drowned in that  brown (plum?) sauce.  A lot of that mayo sauce on our. Asked deluxe roll.  Couldn't taste the scallops. Bad after taste. Pretty bad.  So disappointed.  We were looking forward to good meal.  We work hard for our money.  Hate wasting out money on bad food.   What happened?

This sandwich shop is the soul of our community!  Thank you all for stepping up and taking care of EVERYONE during and after the devastating fires and debris flow. Your lovingly prepared food nourished the entire town.  The sandwiches are THE BEST!!!  XOXO
You get decent pizza hot and ready for $5. There's not much to complain about- you pay only FIVE dollars and get a whole pizza that tastes good. What do you expect? I think it's awesome.

I feel like I just licked a salt pillar and I like salt, so not terrible. As former Juilenne fans we were excited to try the smokehouse but it came a bit underpar. We got the smoked oysters and a selection of meats: ribs, pork belly, tri trip and brisket (beans and cornbread). It wasn't terrible but everything was over salted, except the beans which were mostly undercooked and tasteless. We don't regret going but probably won't be back. Also we're downing a ton of water now.
Delicious Italian food. Affordable and fun atmosphere. Lots and lots and lots of great choices.

Be careful if you order take out - the orders are often (I'd say 50% of the time) wrong. Do you like salad dressing with your salad? Sorry, missing. Those two entrees for the kids? Hope they like Top Ramen tonight because they didn't include them in the order. Oh, and do you like clams you ordered on your pasta? Hope so.  So, if you order take out or delivery, beware.

4 stars-could have been higher. We chose this on a whim from a menu view strolling around downtown Santa Barbara. Wifey loves Italian. Will say up front service was exemplary, friendly, not too obtrusive, and responsive. Outdoor dining to comply with Covid restrictions. Meal began with delicious fresh crusty bread with olive oil and balsamic. Should have asked if they baked on site. Starter to split of Caprese salad.Three tomato slices topped with Buffalo mozzarella, atop large fresh basil leaves and drizzled with olive oil. The cheese was delicious but the tomatoes were quite hard and low on flavor. Unfortunate. My wife chose a dish with tubular pasta with bolognese sauce, her favorite. The dish looked and smelled great but the pasta, to our taste, was too al dente. Quite hard IMO. Now I will say that I think the kitchen would have replaced it if we had asked but time did not allow. Again, unfortunate. I base my comment about the kitchen on the way they accommodated my menu choice. The menu listed a warm seafood salad of mussels, clams, calamari, shrimp, and scallops with a lemon white wine sauce. Sounded great. I asked the waiter about the size and he explained it was not entree size. He suggested they could make a double size. As I thought about it, he then told me the kitchen could convert this dish into a pasta dish, say with fettuccine. Perfect. And that's what it was, perfect. Delicious plentiful seafood, pasta cooked just right, and a very complimentary sauce held the whole dish together. I did not leave a speck in the bowl(bread sopped up that sauce nicely). Too full for dessert we left happy but wishing for just a bit better results. Recommended.
Great quality food for takeout! They seal the soup so it doesn't spill. Same great quality as always ! Keep up the great work Fresco Cafe !

I moved away from Santa Barbara eight years ago, but still have dreams about the tri-tip sandwich from SCD. I developed an addiction to them such that I had to discipline myself to only have them when I achieved some kind of goal or milestone I set for myself. The rest of the sandwiches are very good as well. I always try and stop by whenever I'm driving south to LA or visiting friends in SB.

We lived in Denmark when I was a child and I miss the marzipan pastries. It used to be I had to go to Solvang to get the really good ones. But the Solvang bakeries have declined in quality and Andersen's has gotten to be excellent!  Just one concern: When the server puts the pastries in the bag, could you please tell them not to throw the dirty food serving paper in with it?

The food is fresh, creative and authentic. It's one of SB's excellent restaurants. But, I'm posting to praise our server at lunch today, Adrian. He was everything you could ask for in a good service professional with the accent on professional. He knew the menu, the specials and beverages expertly. He was patient, attentive and he has a terrific sense of humor. I'd give him 6 stars if I could.

There are not many food options in the Funk Zone so it was nice to have this area that's right next-door to The Lark which is closed for lunch. I got the pizza that had chorizo and an egg and it was good. The person I came with ordered a salad that you can also get in a sandwich form. You basically order at the counter and then there's outdoor seating for you where you get your own silverware.

Great pub food in prime Santa Barbara! We ordered a ton of appetizers but particularly enjoyed the Chicken Sandwich, Cobb Salad, and German Pretzel. The quality of the chicken is super fresh and tender. Also ate the Buffalo Chicken Pizza, & Bratwurst Minis which were delicious, although my fiancé said the minis could have used a little crunch.   Service was on point too. The waitress was friendly and our dishes came out fairly quick.   Admired that they did not serve straws with our soda (they make an effort to be eco-friendly)!  The menu is large, so I did not order everything I wanted, but will definitely be back to try what I missed out on during my first visit.

we've been here a few times - a fun local spot, great patio. Nice burgers, fried chicken sandwiches, fries to die for. BUT here's why I'll be going back MANY times: clean clean clean. I've been to some State Street restaurants, and seen very little sanitizing. These folks at Creekside are downright impressive. Tablecloths changed with every customer (stack of fresh cloths on hand. PLUS they spray and wipe down the tables after each customer before they lay out the new cloth. AND they spray and wipe down the chairs. Maybe I was just there when a particularly attentive staffer was on hand, but if they do this all the time, they're my new favorite spot.

I ordered the Biscuits and Gravy. They were amazing! Such good flavor and they didn't jip me on gravy. No one likes when you have too much biscuit and not enough gravy! My girlfriend got French toast with fruit and she said it was amazing. We both got coffee which is self serve and free refills.   When you order you order at the counter. Someone doesn't come and take your order which is fine with me. Then, a server will bring your food to you. Really nice staff and we both highly recommend this restaurant to anyone. You won't be disappointed.

Ah man.. It pains me to do this. Ive been looking forward to coming here since they first opened. I live out of town and always working but I had an opportunity this past weekend and I took it!  The staff we're super friendly, fast service, and SB needs a good wing place I really want this place to succeed. The actual chicken wings were cooked perfect and you can tell they are fresh and good size. Wings are my all time favorite food but wingman rodeo I know you can be better than this.  Here is what happened. I tried to order a 20pc wings since its difficult for me to travel over here and asked the kid behind the counter how much it would be. He said 'About $50'. There's signs saying there's a national chicken wing shortage so ugh okay but I'm determined. Ordered a 10pc half atomic buffalo half Strawberry aloe? Arbol? Strawberry something. Came out to $27 after taxes but its okay, whatever, Im here lets do this! Wingman Rodeo! I made it! I get an awesome to go bag. And pull over to sit and eat because I dont want my expensive wings getting cold and soggy.   My receipt said Atmoic buffalo, the box said atomic buffalo, the kid repeated atmoic buffalo, and I bite into my first amazing looking wing and.... Its barely hot. Not spicy at all. I'm not bragging or anything I'm not a spicy food expert but man I was confused and heartbroken. I wanted to feel the burn man!   The strawberry flavor was a other big disappointment. The wings we're definitely coated in a sauce but it didnt taste like stawberry at all. Not even close to strawberry. I was expecting maybe a strawberry jam mixed with habanero peppers or something it said it was hot on the menu.   I'll be back once the chicken wing shortage is over and try their other flavors but $27 for 10 wings with flavors that completely miss the mark... Its gonna be a while.

My friend and I had a really great dinner here. We order the salmon six step (which is my favorite), the shrimp and vegi udon, and the salmon skin salad. Everything is fresh and the flavor is great. The most important was all the staff were very friendly. We really enjoy it. Definitely will recommend my friend to have Japanese food here.
Was disappointed In the order, all items were small compared to other restaurants. I got one of the favorites, chicken marsala, with 'naan' But instead received small portion of rice and pita bread.

I love institution ale their beers are great and so is their pizza and pretzels. I wouldn't recommend their garlic cheese bread though because I've gotten it a few times and every time their are GIANT chunks of garlic riddled Inside. I love garlic but it definitely needs to be chopped smaller so your not eating chunks the size of m&ms.

Good vibe not as classy as I would like total dude spot but have to say for being a dud spot major props on the ahi salad with mixed greens it also comes in a wrap pretty killer for all those lady and men out there watching the waist but still want to be in a sports bar major props to this place for creating a menus that is even inviting to the skinny bitch sport fan in all of us! Pool in the back which is totally killer and did I mention the cute boys oh yes ladies if you are looking for cute men this is the place to hang especially in the back throw on your best pair of tight blue jeans and white t shirt and play some pool and your sure to meet a few cuties enjoy - the blonde

My daughter made reservations for 10 of us at Arnoldi's Cafe for a birthday celebration. When we arrived we were seated in a very bland room that looked like it is used for storage most of the time (in fact the large wine refrigerator was in the corner and we were contently interrupted when the waiters were retrieving wine. There was one long table set up for about 30 persons and it looked like our table was an afterthought. Service was SLOW despite it being early evening. It took 45 minutes to be served our food after we ordered. The staff was clearly more interested in setting Hors d'oeuvres out on the large table than fixing our food, despite that fact that nobody had arrived yet. My lasagna was overcooked and rubbery. My wife did like her Spaghetti. Others around the table said their food was fair to good. Expensive for the food quality and quantity. In four days of eating out in Santa Barbara, this was our only disappointing experience. Too many other choices in Santa Barbara to go back.

Although I was glad to be seated without having to wait for nearly 2 hours like most other restaurants nearby, we were not impressed with the food and felt that all menu items were excessively over priced.  The salad as you can see in the picture came out with the dressing on the side and was basically mostly a small bowl of arugula with a few dried fruit and goat cheese.  The got cheese was interesting in texture and flavor so that made this dish somewhat unique.  The French fries were overly salty unfortunately.  The fresh juice I ordered was probably the only menu item I enjoyed but at $14 it was a hefty price tag for the amount served.
Love everything. Great sandwiches, super friendly service. Great to go as well to take to the beach.

I've always loved Garrett's for their convenience and value for a quick breakfast, and especially for the service, but damn if they don't have Fox News playing on the TV whenever I go in there. It's plain out offensive, and while many will object, it's time to take a stand against those so responsible for the divisiveness of our country so that I will be boycotting Garrrett's until they choose to stop spreading propaganda rubbish to their clientele.
For the price I expected Mexican food with flavor. It tasted like nothing, really, nothing Service was short and unhelpful We wont ever come back

Still here waiting for a manager to come over..........  We went here for breakfast, my husband came here we ordered our meal and the waitress brought out something I didn't order.  I asked her what it was and she couldn't tell me.  I told what I ordered again so she took the food.  No apology.  After my husband is finished with his breakfast my food comes, I ask for a to go box and she brings one with the check!  No apology.....  When she came back around, I asked her to come over and asked that my meal be removed from the bill since I only got to watch my husband eat breakfast. I asked her what happened . She then proceeded to tell me how I ordered the country eggs Benedict.  I said I clearly ordered another dish.  She argued with me.  Then said she would talk to the manager.    She comes back and says because the order was eventually given to me, they will not remove or discount my food.  I asked to speak to the manager.  I'm still waiting.....I overheard the waitress telling the manager that I clearly ordered the other item blah blah....  Still waiting.........  The manager never showed.  Another person dropped off our check with the item removed.  Unbelievable!    We definitely won't be back, they couldn't have handled this situation any worse. Btw-  I did ask the witness what I ordered before talking with the waitress.    What I did get to taste, the potatoes were mushy, the eggs bland but the country fried steak was pretty good.

My family came here the day after Thanksgiving so of course we took the fact that no one probably wanted to work into consideration however, such bad customer service! We got to the host stand where another party of 3 were waiting before us and the hostess/waitress kept going in and out from the restaurant to the patio section without acknowledging any of us. We even asked if we needed to write down our names but the waitress said no and that she'd eventually get to us. The waitress said it was our time to be seated but we had to tell her the other party had arrived before us and the waitress seemed flustered. Finally, it was our turn and we were seated and waited a very long time to even get water. It started to get very cold and windy and the outdoor heaters weren't much of help so I asked if we could be seated inside. The hostess said they would just clean up and move us but we were completely forgotten about until we were given our check and asked if we wanted to still be seated inside, obviously useless now.   The workers seemed to be overwhelmed and just a hot mess when we asked certain things or if one of our drinks had yet to arrived. When I went inside to pay, the waitress was just standing there on the iPad and didn't even look up until she was does doing whatever she was doing. We didn't enjoy our visit and I won't be coming back to this place.   For any 1 star reviews I always like to leave some positivity so the patio seating area is extremely cute and has a great European atmosphere vibe to it. Seeing how this place has overall great reviews does suggest that the food was indeed delicious but the customer service was a complete flop today and I rather give my money to other local organic food spots.

I asked for a loaf of olive sourdough bread and what they sold me was a loaf of burnt olive sourdough bread.  Yes, burned on top and bottom.  Thankfully there are other very good bakeries in town because I won't be returning to this establishment.  When they were learning to bake bread,  they should have been learning some integrity along with it.

If I had to pick a restaurant that would define California for me, this would be it. Avocados, fresh ingredients, healthy and healthy-ish food that you don't have to make yourself!  Definitely decent food. But a little overpriced for what it is.  I go to the location in Goleta frequently. Their baja burrito is pretty good; their sandwiches and soups are too. Their lemon herb dressing, now that's something to talk about, I could eat gallons.

Locals love it. Tourists love it. A win win for anyone visiting Santa Barbara, or craving some of the best clam chowder in town. Make sure to sit upstairs at the bar and order a Bloody Mary (pairs great with their steamed clams and the ocean view).
The calzone has gotten a little more expensive but it's worth it. It's huge and very tasty. People are always nice and service is always quick unless you catch them mid Friday night rush
This is our favorite sushi restaurant. The food is so fresh & the staff is so attentive. The delight rolls are my favorite. Ignore the strip mall location- it's so good.

I like brophys for the atmosphere. They got cool staff and a cool vibe. Everyone is pretty chill and it's always a place where you see people from town. Saturdays are my fav cuz it's always an event. Since selling our home in santa barbara i really do miss nights down here.

Sad to write this after many years of going there but they just don't seem to care anymore.  The plate, beer and silverware were literally tossed at me by the woman who had already turned away before the fork hit the counter.  The food was passable for prison fare and when I asked for guacamole she looked at me as if I had requested open heart surgery.  And, it wasn't cheap.  I recommend the one on the Mesa still, not sure what the ownership relationship is anymore but the Mesa crew should stage a coup over this Haley St. location!
Great food, great service, and great COVID protocols. Sufficient social distancing and temperature checks every time we show up to get seated.
Absolutely delicious. They are fast, friendly and serve amazing pho and their pork bang mi sandwich is amazing, amazing enough to order twice in a week. We will be back!

Came here after UCSB graduation. My son has eaten here and loved it. There were 6 of us so we ordered many dishes to share. Potstickers, wontons and fried shrimp appetizers. The shrimp were pounded flat, dry and tasteless. We had some dishes that were pretty good and others not so much. Nothing extraordinary. Both pork dishes were too salty. Our service was very friendly but sporadic. We literally had to get someone's attention each time we needed something like chopsticks, tea, more rice. Our server forgot one of the dishes we ordered then said it takes longer to make. This was after all our dishes came out in shifts. When it finally arrived, we were basically finished eating. It was pork belly with preserved vegetables. Over cooked and salty. There weren't many people in the restaurant so it was hard to understand the service.

I love this place! Great lunch specials and such delicious food. I love their chicken picatta. The breadsticks are fresh and warm. Their gorgonzola dressing is amazing. I would definitely recommend this place. However, I haven't experienced great service yet. I know that it can get really busy during lunch but refills are a part of someones job no matter how annoying they are.

Astoundingly good. This isn't just dinner. It's a full experience. You feel like you're at a theatre or museum waiting for the next dish/piece of art to come out of the kitchen, all perfectly choreographed and coordinated so you're never bored or hungry, but also never rushed to finish whatever delicious dish you're on.   Of course you pay for quality this high, but for a special occasion or celebration, it is worth it and then some. Not many restaurants this unique in the country, let alone Santa Barbara.

This place is consistently amazing.  The fish is incredibly fresh and the service is perfect.  They are present enough to keep you well served but not overbearing.   It is usually very busy here but I always seem to get a table within 15-20 minutes.   I always make a point to stop here while in town.  Keep it up.    Thanks!
Favorite sushi spot in town! Order the habanero yellow tail melt! You won't be disappointed!

Maybe if I still lived in Santa Barbara I would  like this place or I would go back. Just in comparison to the French bistros here in SF I unfortunately have to say that Cafe Luck fails  to stand out.  Besides the fact that I  dislike empty restaurants I didn't think the food was that great. The French onion soup was just ok being a little bland and for my taste not thick enough. The steak and fries were also just mediocre. I missed the herb butter that seems to be the usual garnish for steak frites at a French restaurant.   I would like to say though that my husband loooved the fish special of the day which was a salt crusted sea bass.  So maybe I just choose the wrong food but then again.. steak frites is a staple at a French restaurant so in my book you have to get that right!  Fun fact: Cafe Luck is part-owned by the co-owner of Lucky Brand Clothing. That guy is all over the place in SB: D'Angelos, Luckys etc etc....

Im an odd ball, it's a hotdog joint, but the usually I go for the burger with no tomato when it's not busy. BEST BURGER imo definitely the price is out of the water especially packing it with fresh veggies and baconss... 5/5  but... Definitely cant wait for some sort of prototype  SPICY Fried chicken coming in the near future though.  Cheers to 2021
It was definitely  on the sweet  side, which  worked for our  children.     Decent  but the place seemed a little  old and tired.

I've been dining at Pascucci for over 20 years.  It was where I first started dining without my parents (in high school) when I would go shopping at Paseo Neuvo.  So much has changed but I love Pascucci just the same.  Their prices are good and so is the food.  Service was fast and friendly!
Such an amazing experience! Cute little spot, their tomato soup is amazing! Maleny took great care of us! Will definitely return!!

Since I had not eaten at Mulligan's for over a year, thought I might update my review. Ate there tonight with a party of seven. Food was good; everyone happy, from the Mexican combo plates with enchiladas, tacos to battered shrimp and salmon, fish and chips, it was all good, well served and reasonably priced.  A portion of the restaurant is still closed off, perhaps for lack of staff, so you might want to make a reservation if you have a seating preference (terrace or inside).  Parking is ample, atmosphere relaxed and family-friendly. Notably, most entrees are available with Senior options at a savings...but, honestly, I still took home half the only-slightly smaller meal.

Salads, sandwiches/wraps, pizza, soups, & dessert make Fresco Cafe the perfect lunch spot, whether catered, to-go, or dining-in. Breakfast & Dinner served too apparently but I will stick with lunch! Plus there are other food options making sure every coworker is satisfied.   Fresco is one of those order at the counter, receive a number (on a wooden spoon), choose a seat, and someone will deliver your meal. A complimentary bread & butter basket is available by the utensil station inside the main dining area.   My favorites...    * Gorgonzola Walnut Salad (+ salmon)   * Warm Goat Cheese Salad (+ protein)  * Cobb Salad  * & any of the desserts, yum! Yes, I have a major sweet tooth.   I will add that while most of the ingredients taste fresh, I will not order a side of chicken to my salads anymore as the last time I did it tasted awful & rubbery, almost inedible :/   Be warned, the Service can be slow at times, taking a while to receive food... which may not be good if on a time restriction (as most of us are during work hours).

We visited Arnoldi's with a large, local group (which had a reservation) last Saturday night.  We waited an hour and a half to get our food.  The bar was staffed with only one bartender, on a Saturday night with multiple large groups booked.  We were so hungry by the time our food arrived that I can't even give you an accurate review of it.    Not impressed.

Wanted brunch for my last day in SB. Finch & Fork was closed so this cute cafe was our next bet! Parking was free for us(?) not sure about other people. There's a big lot so that's convenient.   First impression was how home-y it felt, but also how confusing it looked. You got a winery, a coffee bar, a little market, and a brunch place all in one which is super convenient! But the system to order food was very funky. The cashier who took our order seemed very forgetful and almost hungover. He asked me many times what I wanted and couldn't finish getting my boyfriend's order in without forgetting to ask him something about his order. That part met me oddly, so we sat and waited for our food. They forgot to give me my side of fruit as pictured so I had to go and ask for it. Thankfully they didn't charge me or question me because that would've been a hassle.  The sauce on the eggs benny was very sour and vinegar-y to the point where I had to scrape it off. I personally didn't enjoy my food, but my boyfriend ordered more after. Their fresh OJ is really good too. Highly recommend that despite the price for a small glass! I give it a 4 stars for the half and half review!

Definitely the best Chinese restaurant in Santa Barbara, including its' dim sum. It's also very clean, which is a big bonus for me. The staff is also always friendly. Here's a tip- grab a drink here...it's strong and not too pricey. The food isn't greasy and the dim sum has many options. Definitely my go to Chinese restaurant here.

The broth is everything to me and this broth is so good! I am definitely looking forward to coming back again. We needed a place like this in SB! I definitely recommend the black garlic ramen and the spicy ramen

I have gotten pizza to go here several times. The service is very friendly and you get your pizza, made your way, pretty quickly. The pizza is alright, not great, but decent in a pinch and priced fairly for what you get. What separates this from really good pizza is the crust, it is like Wonder bread compared to artisan bakery bread. There is no complexity, structure, or chew to the crust. They have all the crusts already rolled out and ready to go throughout the day and night. The other issue is that they use the same couple of roller cutters to cut all the pizzas into slices so sometimes I have tasted BBQ sauce on my pizza because the pizza they cut prior to mine had BBQ sauce on it and it transfers to my pizza.

Loved this place, we were at the Funk Zone in Santa Barbara checking out a few breweries and wineries when we decided to find a spot with some food. Their menu we saw on Yelp looked pretty good so we decided to check it out and it was much better than expected. All the appetizers we got were amazing, and the drinks where just as good.  They have a small outdoor seating area in the front, bar and table seating on the inside, and a patio out back.

Woohoo! It only took me a year to go here! Man, why'd I wait so long! I could've eaten here multiple times!   This sushi place took over the former Ahi Sushi, on upper State, I believe (which I used to frequent a lot.. for ramen.) they don't have ramen here and I remember not liking Ahi's sushi very much, but wow Sun blew my mind! Definitely the best sushi I've had in the last 7 years of being in SB. (Ok, I've been to Yoichi's, but that's not a spot you'll drop in last minute to spend less than your arm's worth.)  I trusted the reviews and went for the Box Roll and a salmon roll on the side. It was dinner and unfortunately I couldn't try their uni (my fav!) because they ran out. I usually don't go for super 'saucy' rolls, but I really enjoyed the Box Roll! It's a bit sweet but it tastes really fresh. The salmon tasted really fresh too.  Service was amazing too! I came here with my dad and they were so nice to him! (He tends to avoid any new place on State St. because of bad service lol) That was such a plus.   Definitely a spot to come back to! Good job!

Lito's is the best! The food is amazing. They make us vegan breakfast burritos. The food is high quality. They've remodeled the restaurant and it's soo much better! The ladies who were there are so kind, so service oriented and so fun. I love to get there early in the morning to be greeted by one or more of these beautiful, friendly ladies. Good parking. Clean bathroom. Reasonable prices. I'm not Duke for nothing.

I love this place for fresh, delicious Italian dishes.  It is a great place for lunch or dinner.  They have a professional, passionate, knowledgable, and friendly staff that is happy to provide recommendations.  Their wine list is varied in varietals, vintages and price points.  Located on State Street in Santa Barbara it is a wonderful place to wind down after walking all over downtown for the day.  Cheers!

This place is awesome! A place with a large assortment of drinks plus quality Mediterranean food!? Thank the gods for combining two wonderful things in one place finally in DTSB!  Stellar service and patience as some girls and I sampled many beers and wines - there's 22 selections of beer including alcoholic kombutchas and ciders on top of some specialty mixed drinks. An indecisive persons best nightmare. Averagely priced around $8-$15  Food is bomb as well, naturally we tried 50% of the menu at once. The shawarma meat sounds like it switches daily and you order food at the bar. Self serve water at a corner by the bar.  Grape leaves - (5) per order -$8. Good to share and it was my first time ever trying them. Decent and worth a try but not my favorite   Kofta lamb burger - burger week deal and this was delicious! Lamb didn't taste overly game-Y as some people fear. Would definitely recommend if you want something different and the fresh cucumbers & pickled veggies were a nice touch. The patty is not thick though so you will be content but not super full after.   Falafel burger - burger week deal also and it was solid and extra saucy. Also delicious but personally would skip this unless you're planning to share/split it with other food items  Green falafel salad - bomb, super fresh greens and the ingredients worked well together. The green apple added a nice touch. You get about 4 falafels in this. Great for sharing as well. I'd highly recommend this and can't wait to try the red version also  Chicken shawarma bowl - 9.5/10. One of the very few places you can get a great shawarma bowl. It did take longer to come out but that's a good sign of fresh meal prep. Great protein portions. Super dank sauce. As I mentioned the shawarma meat does rotate daily but the chicken was great!   Baklava - first time trying this and it was pretty good and potentially worth sharing but I wouldn't die for it. It had a harder texture than I thought.  Overall it was a pretty good experience and had a cute ambience. Great hipster interior design. The darkness levels changed semi often which is a good or bad thing depending on what type of vibe you want. There's one projecting TV screen on the wall so it may not be the best place if you want to catch an epic game.   Nevertheless service and bartenders were super awesome and friendly!

Came here after the Microburst hit Santa Barbara and left half the town without power so perhaps my experience is out of the norm.   We had to wait 1.5 hours to get a table outside. We tried to get a drink at the bar and were treated very rudely because apparently you must order drinks in the giant line for to-go orders that took 20 mins to get through. However, we were able to procure drinks due to a very friendly woman from Houston (who also bought us a bottle of rose later for my birthday) which made the wait tolerable.   Service was 'meh', but the food was pretty good, albeit a bit overpriced. I think Brophy Bros is worth the money and wait more than this place as the food is better and the service is fantastic.

In a town with terrible food and marginal service, this place stands out. The food is really solid (always get the corn as a side) and the service is friendly. The owner is really cool and friendly and almost always there. Good place to watch sports. I love the pulled chicken sandwich. Really good and the perfect size to not be totally stuffed. Everything I've had has been good. I did order the sweet potato fries as a side recently and it only included like six fries. Not sure if that's typical. My go to place for a causal meal and/or happy hour.

Our server last night was horrible! I didn't get her name but she was a (white?) female with short dark red hair... She never came to check on us, I had to keep calling her over when I wanted something. She never refilled my drink even though the empty glass was at the edge of the table. Even if she was serving the table next to us, she never stopped at our table to see if we needed anything.   Because she never checked on us, we were there for a long time as there would be long periods of time where I wouldn't see her. At one point I even asked another server, Virginia, to refill my drink. Virginia was so friendly and when she brought my drink she asked if there was anything else we needed! That's all we expect from a good server- attentiveness. Thank you Virginia!

Still the best Sushi I have ever had and I eat a lot of sushi from a lot of different places. Order the jalapeno yellow tail ngiri and the spicy broiled mussels. If you love oysters like me their hot shot is the greatest. Just writing this review makes me really want to go there right now...
I want to love this place but seems like for a 13$  Burrito it was kinda small....  Wish it was more consistent

Rock lobster! Someone call the B-52's cause someone dropped a massive lobster bomb on me.   My goodness, what an otherwise shiny beacon in a very problematic venture in finding a place to eat. The Arch Rock Fish joint delivered on all fronts.   It's like a cross between a young and hip gastropub and a classy fish joint. And while I generally think the whole novelty of eating fish near the sea is 70% of the time nothing more than cliche and a novelty that they're over charging you for trucked in fish, this place felt really cool to eat at.   They have a huge board with the revolving daily specials. They have the upscale dining set up so you can feel all classy.. you know, when you're wearing your lobster bib and getting all those claw juices on your shirt and jacket.   The first thing you'll notice when you walk in here is the wall of wine in the glass behind you. Now I do have to wonder how well those wines are going to taste considering they're right in the path of potential light rays.  They have a burger and beer special, which if I was in time for, I would totally go for as it sounded pretty damn tasty. But no, this night, after many other places to dine fell, I saw the lobster and man was my aggression needing an outlet.   So I ordered up that sucker and sure enough they gave me a bucket, the utility tools for cracking apart a whole tourist lobster, who was visiting from Maine and boy did I go to town on it.   Shell pieces flying everywhere. You know, I didn't even put much butter on it. The sucker was well cooked and you know what, I thought for the price of the other items like the lobster po'boy or such other items, that the whole lobster was well worth the price tag.. and sort of a bargain.   I mean, yeah, you have to do the work in cracking it apart, but again.. I needed to get some aggression out.   Also had some calamari, which is always a go to appetizer for me as I love me some breaded many legged deep fried freaks. They were tasty!   The service was pretty much on top of their game that night. So I had a swell time. Really helped in pick up my mood for the evening.   Oh yeah, I should mention that they do have a good selection of beers. A local one in Telegraph and many other craft beers. So that's always a plus in my book.
We went here for Valentine's Day dinner before a show and it was great. The lobster bisque is so good! It is must. Good service and good seafood.

FINALLY a great margarita paired with great, authentic Mexican food in SB!! I can't figure out why this place is empty on a Friday night.. Makes me sad. YOU HAVE TO TRY THIS PLACE!!! Great food, awesome service. Our new favorite, go to place!

Great option when in Santa Barbara with children! Ordering over the phone was a breeze, they have several locations throughout Santa Barbara so no matter where you are staying there is a close pick up point . Price is fair, the pizzas were huge and the kids love them. They are already on time, fresh and hot.

Did I walk into a day-time underground Rock N Roll night club transformed into a wine tasting room in a city I've never even heard of? Why yes, yes I did!  Our fabulous foodie friend and road trip organizer, Lorrie M. took us to this awesome winery to meet the owner and taste his awesome wine selection.  The tasting fee was very reasonable and we got to a taste a few others that were 'off menu'. I love when wineries share more than what's standard. :)  The owner here is quite eclectic, chillax and looks like an all-around bad ass to be honest. He has good rock music blasting throughout the place and it seems like they possibly even have bands playing here since there was a killer stage inside.   My favorite wine we tasted was called Autumn which was a 2012 Cabernet Franc AND the name of his first daughter he and his wife are about to have. Amazing!!  I definitely recommend my wine-o friends to swing by this winery if you ever end up in Solvang, CA and you're wanting to step out-of-the-norm when it comes to wine tasting.

You HAVE to go here if you are in Santa Barbara. Simple as that. It is on this perfect little dog beach all by itself with AMAZING views, great food, lovely atmosphere, and friendly staff. I always bring friends from out of town here because it is so essential for a Santa Barbara meal. Brunch here is amazing as well as dinner. Could be a cool date spot too!

Ate on their patio and had the fish tacos and raspberry lemonade. Food and service was great but their orange couches on the patio are in dire need of replacing. They were super dirty, ripped everywhere and taped in many spots, they were also wayyy too low for the tables also you're eating like a toddler. Other than the patio couches, everything was great. New couches.

We came in for the new lunch service and had the best customer service from Zack. He very patiently explained how the lunch menu worked, and had the most enthusiastic attitude. Plus, our was delicious, as always.
We had a wonderful meal.  The outdoor patio was perfect and I loved my taquitos salad ... I could have licked that plate.

Still love it! Friendly and attentive customer service. We were the first ones in at 5:30 pm with my elderly parent. It has an East Coast Italian vibe that's California-ized so it's more laid back.   Their menu is large and it takes some time to read through. But we weren't rushed and we had bread and olive oil on the table just as we opened our menus.   Started off with the lattughine salad and eggplant parmigiana appetizers. The salad was enough to split and I prefer more dressing, but the eggplant was hot and gooey and we devoured it quickly. The sauce is a classic marinara with some sweetness. There was enough extra sauce to mop up with our bread.   My favorite dish here is now the pollo nel coccio, better known as the terra-cotta baked chicken. It's served in a terra-cotta bowl with grilled polenta and porcini mushrooms in a wine sauce. Ridiculously savory and delicious. It's like the hidden gem of the restaurant.   Others with me ordered the branzini and the sole. Each raved about their fish. We finished with a chocolate mousse, a tiramisu, and the dessert special - a warm chocolate cake with ice cream and berry sauce.    Great restaurant to enjoy spending time with loved ones.

The outside of the hotel is nice. The bar looks ok and bar tender is attentive. Pool area and fire pit and jacuzzi look nice on pictures, but in person it doesn't look great. The restaurant, bar, pool and Lobby are all far away from the rooms. The configuration of the hotel is very very odd. I kept being told it is a very old hotel. The rooms are not spectacular at all, you can get the same room at the  Best Western down the street and their rooms look better and have balconies facing the ocean. The air conditioner didn't work in our room so we had to open the window. On our first night the tv didn't work at all, operator kept saying the staff was sending someone to fix it. Not until the last day did we get multiple channels that worked on the tv, the rest of the time only ESPN showed. Also the tv wouldn't turn off when you hit the power button, it would just turn off by itself during the night like it was on a timer. Breakfast is horrible. Although there is a buffet, the food on the buffet is nasty. They can make you omelettes but they taste like they are made out of a machine and not hand made. Most of the staff is exceptionally nice, of course there were a couple of rude staff, but the nice staff made up for the rude ones. I felt the the staff was exceptionally nice because they know the hotel is crappy. We called about fixing the tv and air conditioner but there wasn't much they could do. I would NOT suggest staying at this hotel there are plenty if surrounding hotels that look nice on the  outside as well as inside.

I had stayed there perhaps 30 times during a life transition I made in 2017, and I loved the place. I took my wife there last night, and it obvious that a new corporate owner had taken over and wrecked the place.    Our room was so small that anyone seated at the desk had to get up, push the chair in, and get our of the way if the other needed to use the bathroom.  Dozens of other weird short cuts. NO glasses of any sort for water or wine.  One piece of soap for the shower, none for the sink which is in another part of the room.    This truly WAS a four-star hotel.  Now I'd be happier at a Best Western.  How disappointing.

Ordered the burger and the wrap and didn't finish either as the taste fell flat. Didn't add the cheese to it since I try to cut out dairy whenever possible and it also really skews the entire flavor of the meal so the ingredients itself on freshness and flavor is not accurate. The gluten free (non-vegan) bun was not tasty, pushing me away from yet another failed gluten free alternative. This feels like one of those vegetarian places that vegetarians or health nuts resolve to love simply because there's a lack of choices. Then again, it's all a matter of personal taste so I would still support this place and recommend everyone to check it out. The staff is super nice, prices are a bit high for portions but it is local and organic.   Another con - I never really get it when a 'green' restaurants uses one time use disposable plastic cups so wish that this would something they change.

Another minority bad review here...first I will say I've been here a few times and hate to give a bad review but I ordered the 'best' ham and swiss today and it was all bad. I ordered the combo. $4 added to my meal. Must be some pretty good fries right? Nope, they were soggy and flavorless and the quantity was lacking. It was about the size of a small-medium mcdonalds fries. The sandwiched tasted okay, but it was drenched. I mean I put the sandwich down on my counter to unwrap it and juices were leaking out the bottom. Once unwrapped the sandwich was literally soaking wet. I could barley hold the thing it was so slippery. It took 5 paper towels to clean up after I had eaten half of it. For that price, the sandwich better be very good.
Overpriced for the setting. Staff is nice. Not enough options for vegetarians. Food is fine, but nothing remarkable.

I lived in Santa Barbara for several years and visited Stella Mares regularly and always had a 5 star experience! It was my go to in Santa Barbara for special occasions as they always had great service, great food and provided overall a fantastic experience.  I visited last night for the first time in about a year to celebrate my 30th birthday and was pretty disappointed in my meal.  We were greeted by a friendly hostess who showed us to our table.  Then our server came up and said hello and ran us through the specials. Then, another server came up to our table and gave us the same spiel. Then server 1 came back and took our drink orders, then server 2 came back to take our drink orders.  I politely had to tell server 2 we were already being taken care of.  We were never served water and had to ask halfway through our meal.   The biggest bummer is they've changed their menu. They've taken off the nicer cuts of meat, changed their cocktail menu to be more millennial friendly (mason jar cocktails at a French restaurant?) they don't have foreign wines by the glass, and no more mashed potatoes!   I understand places need to change and adapt, but it was apparent they were using cheaper, lesser quality ingredients. The artichoke we had (which has always been on the menu) was salty and over seasoned. The New York steak I ordered was over cooked and tough.   In the past, the servers always have asked if we were celebrating anything and provided a complimentary dessert for birthdays or anniversaries.  Our server never asked, and I paid $11 for a starchy piece of lemon cake. This used to be one of my favorite desserts and again, I could tell it was lesser quality than before.   Since this place is special to me, I'll be back to give them another chance. I hope this was just a fluke!

Great beer, great outdoor atmosphere, kind staff. Food okay. Pizza on the salty side..but still worth trying. I heard from a friend their burger was just 'meh' Will go back for beer and try out other menu items.
Fabulous lunch - delicious, colorful, and healthy! Their salads are SO yummy! The service was great, too.

Doesn't look like much when you pass, but this place is incredibly delicious. True authentic Mexican food in the heart of Santa Barbara! I love this place and will continue to comeback and bring my friends!

Friends recommended Zaytoon. Initially, we thought, what could be so great about this place? But they kept reminding us, check out Zaytoon! Make a reservation at Zaytoon! Boy, are we glad they did.   Walking into Zaytoon feels like walking into another world. The outdoor seating area is gorgeous, walls of trees and string lights all around. I forgot we were still outdoors at one point!   The staff is really on it - bringing water / menus, taking your order, even clearing the table to turn it over for the next guest. They operate so efficiently, it's impressive!  - pistachio spread: didn't really taste any pistachios... it was good though. cold yogurt with pomegranate seeds and spices with pita bread.  - falafel: it's hard to do falafel well, and they do falafel sooo well. To get a little taste of everything I was interested in trying, I ordered a side of falafel. Next time, I need to get falafel as a main! - Salmon with basil cream: this was good. the rice pilaf pairs so well. The fattoush salad it comes with is pretty large and tasty - i liked their version of 'croutons' - crispy, flat and thin.  - Mashawi combo: comes with a lot of food! T finished almost all of it. He  liked the chicken the most, which says a lot. The baba ghannouj was delicious, and the hummus was a close second.   We'll be back on our next trip to SB!

Pretty good burgers! We weren't able to decide so we got the 3 sliders and fries.   The sliders were Mesa burger, montecito and mission burger. Mesa burger was the regular burger, Montecito had the mushrooms and grilled onions and the mission burger had the pico and tortilla strip- kind of like a southwest burger. They are all very tasty and just the right amount. My friend got their regular burger and it looked humongous! The sliders were perfect size.   Plenty of indoor-outdoor seating. Order first then take a seat.

Nice pier--good view of the ocean and the harbor. Restaurants, small touristy shops,  and parking. Clean and nice enough to just walk around, and there's a nice dolphin fountain in front. State Street is basically just across the street, and the SB Zoo's just down the road.

I've been eating at SCD for a few years and I have never once been disappointed.  It doesn't matter what I order, I'm always glad I decided to eat here. My personal favorite is 'Mark's Panini' with the tabasco aioli. The ciabatta is always toasted perfectly, the chicken never dry, and the cheese always gooey and satisfying.  If I have a monster of an appetite, I go with any of the 'three-way' sandwiches which I often can't even finish. And don't even get me started on the breakfast three way. HEAVEN!  When I think of sandwiches, I always think of South Coast Deli.

How can this restaurant list 'vegan options' in their description! We asked for those and the only option we were given was a sandwich without the bread (baked w butter) and we had nothing but a decorative avocado to eat. We asked about the salsa but that was apparently bot an option either. Wed do better at a steak house.
Blood orange Margie !!!  The steak nachos were delicious with the meat all over nachos .  Salsa red was good and chips fresh.  An oasis if you are walking in the area in the sweltering heat of SB

The food was extraordinary.  For a food with such great food, they seemed to be unprepared for food allergies.  When I discussed my allergies with the server, she said I could have steak or chicken and left it at that. I had to ask if I could have the scallops and she said yes.  When one of my friends asked for devilled eggs, they brought me one.  I asked about the source of the mustard in the eggs.  They were unwilling/unable to look at the jar.  With the food we did have, it was great.  My dining companions will probably give it five-stars, but I have to warn people with allergies to be vocal about having the food adapted to suit their needs and be careful, because they don't seem to know about all their ingredients.

I came here by word of mouth. I'm not a phan of Pho but that is what I should have ordered. My buddy was raving about it. I had a 8' combo pizza for $11.99 and I didn't feel like it was a deal. Hot and fresh toppings but nothing out of the ordinary. So four stars for the pho and the fact that a friendly local and his lovely wife are running a good business.

This market has been here since I was a little kid going to the neighborhood catholic school. I'm 36 now, and went to the neighborhood catholic school from kindergarten to 2nd grade. Yesterday I was feeling like a gyro so I went to Yanni's because I heard good thing, and I was definitely satisfied and not disappointed. This was the first time I stepped foot in this market since I was a little kid, so it was pretty awesome to learn that the same family had been running the place. As I mentioned, I ordered the gyro and it was super delicious. All the ingredients tasted fresh and it was made to order. It was your traditional gyro with lamb and beef, diced onions, tomatoes, on flatbread with tzatziki sauce. The bread was toasted and the tzatziki had a nice freshness and tang to it. It was a little pricey, close to $10, but overall I was very pleased. I will definitely return and maybe try a different sandwich. The tri-tip and roasted lamb was also on the menu. I might have to one next time.

If I were to write this review when they first opened I would have given them a 5.  Since then I feel like they have gone down in quality.  I made reservations earlier this week for father's day and when we got there we were seated in the front right by the bathroom and service area.  My father in law wasn't too happy abt it since they had done this to him before.  He asked if we could sit at another table but they told us it was reserved but no one was sitting there even when we were getting ready to leave.  I get that this weekend was busy but seriously the table was empty the whole time we were there.    That was the beginning of it all.  Our waiter was just horrible.  He pretty much ignored us.  We brought a couple bottles of wine.  Instead of asking if we wanted to taste or let the wine breath he just poured.  He never came back to pour more and we ended up pouring ourselves more wine.  Needless to say we didn't stay long enough to open the second bottle.  The food was brought out by other waiters and he didn't come back to ask us how the food was until we were pretty much done eating.  We had to flag him down if we wanted anything.  We even had to ask for bread when he brought out our appetizers since he hadn't brought it out yet.  I just hope that he was a new waiter and just didn't know what he was doing.  He kept dropping things in the dinning area as well as back in the kitchen (we could hear it since we were right there)  Like I said I like their food but the service just ruined our whole dinning experience.  I don't think we will be going back anytime soon.  My husband's bday i next month and we were going to go back then but after today I think we will be going to bouchon instead.

I've come here a total of 3 times and here is what I've gathered from my experiences. DO come here for the drinks, don't order the wings (serving and quality is a little lackluster compared to their other options), DO get their burgers and onion rings! Enjoy!

Delicious coffee made fresh with coffee beans that were selected with great care. The coffee is something to really sit and savor; this is not something to just chug or drink. this is a coffee to sit and appreciate which is not difficult to do because the coffee is truly in a class of its own! The space itself, the Golden Line Coffee bar is stunning. I stumbled upon the pop up coffee bar (Sat & Sundays 7:30-2pm) which is in proximity to the Saturday farmers market, one street behind lower state street near where World Market is; and am so glad I saw it. The space has gorgeous tile and absolutely beautiful fountain, and an overall ambiance of earthy calm coupled with a lustrous vibrancy. Just a gorgeous space to sit and really savor an even more gorgeous tasting cup of coffee. I think I am in love!  the coffee and the space is that inspiring!! Go, drink, enjoy ps the menu shows that GoldenLine Coffee Bar does coffee flights; so technically on a sunny day such as today, you could hang out with a friend or whomever and try 3 varieties of coffee. YUM!

This review is for lunch. I got the clam chowder in a bread bowl with salad. Overall, it was good.. it isn't the best clam chowder in town, though. I loved the fruit exlir drink. I will update my review once I try dinner.
Great food. Good prices. Attentive staff. Fast on a rainy night. Thanks.

We brought my mother-in-law for lunch.  She is English and we were relieved that she enjoyed the food.  The food was hearty and we were very full.  It's a very spacious and clean pub.  They also had outdoor dining.  We had very good service.

Quickly got a decaf coffee and glass of wine this evening. Very chill and nice vibe out on the patio. Overlooks all of Santa Barbara and very mellow. Enjoyed every second of it. Waitress a little slow and not too perceptive on that we were very tired. Need staff with better social cues LOL... Loved the views and the couch setting. They have heaters too! I didn't like that, when I was taking photos of my evening coffee on my 5D Mark III, a staff member SCOLDED me for taking pictures because of some wedding event. I am paying to stay at this resort and I am allowed to take photos of my own food... Thanks very much. Extremely nosy and unprofessional of them! Belmond is usually amazing, maybe just some of their staff wasn't trained properly.

It was the first visit of Rusty's Pizza! My friend recommended Rusty's Special, which was so good. Meat, pepper, onion (maybe), and cheese were put on it, I remember. (This time, I forgot taking pictures) Usually, every pizza can be chosen from three or four sizes. We shared the biggest one and shared it, but we could eat all! lol If you order only one, you should have the biggest one! It's tasty, so you'll be satisfied with it ;)
Pizza was so so, for the costs of 25.00 for a 10' pizza was way over priced and order a bbq but was smothered with tomato sauce on top of the bbq and didn't get much taste of the BBQ chicken

Man, this place...what can I say!? I must have eaten nearly a thousand sandos and salads from here in the last decade and I've never had a bad one. In fact, they're all GREAT. You literally cannot go wrong. Super fast & kind service, superb selection, and big portion sizes for the price (which seems harder and harder to find these days). Susan and Kirsten are so nice!! I go here multiple times a week and never leave disappointed--neither will you! Best Deli in Santa Barbara.

There is no place better to get fresh seafood in Santa Barbara. The staff is friendly, helpful and knowledgable. Its a tiny little place that smells richly of the ocean. The seafood is visually stunning. I've found that customers are equally as friendly, chatting it up in line while we wait to place our orders. Prices are reasonable and you cannot beat the quality. I've purchased halibut, spot prawns, seafood salad and my absolute favorite, local mussels and little neck clams. The staff is great about not picking up open ones and really make the pound worth the money.  I've yet to have a bad experience at the Santa Barbara Fish Market. I will continue to be a very happy patron for years to come.

This review is for their beer.   The brewers are way behind the times and the beers are very sloppily done. Very flat flavors with no character or bite. No balance whatsoever. Worst brewing company I've ever been to. Craft beer fans stay away.

I used to work at Nordstrom's and passed this place on my way to work everyday. The Nordies employees used to rave about it. Finally I got my butt over there and boy was I impressed.  I asked the man at the register for his advice on a vegetarian sandwich and ultimately decided on the Caprese. It was DIVINE! They grilled it, so the bread was slightly toasted with the mozzarella melting on to it, and the juicy tomatoes were soo soo good. I sat outside for about an hour after eating my sandwich on their beautiful patio doing some reading for an upcoming midterm and no one bothered me to leave.   I am looking forward to going back again and trying another one of their sandwiches (or possibly the salads they have in their deli counter that look fantastic!).

The hotel itself is beautiful and a very nice place to stay. Service was also up to par. They remembered us throughout our stay, which was a nice touch. I would highly recommend spending a night here (I loved the style of the rooms and the fireplace!). The views outside are gorgeous and they have a state of the art fitness center.   We also came for afternoon tea, which I would not recommend. The tea was great but the food was either very dry or sweet. We have had better experiences at Mandarin Oriental and Four Seasons. Not worth the $$$ in my opinion.

I wish everyone would shut up about how good this place is. Honestly its better than 2 stars but because of all the hype and unwarranted 5 star reviews it gets 2. There are so many great 'authentic' places around town that are just as good or better but you don't have to wait in a line to eat at. Don't waste your time in line just to sit at a dirty table with all the other tourists that are heading to SF or LA
Everything here was delicious. The matcha and chai lattes were very light. The Elvis banana bread with peanut butter and bananas was heavenly. The fried chicken sandwich was so good too.

Had the pleasure to have coffee with some friends! We had an amazing time. The coffee and pastries where amazing out of this world! We were in awe by the awesomeness of JP, our amazing Brazilian server and Alex the manager that were constantly making sure we were having a great time! Can't wait to go back! Thank you D'Angelo for hiring amazing people that can make having coffee a wonderful experience

Nice casual breakfast place with lots of outdoor seating.  Good selection of coffee as well as an assortment of muffins, sandwiches and other goodies.  But as I was hungry, I went with the bacon breakfast burrito (ham being the other option) for around $6.  It's reputation is well deserved as it is huge, comes filled with almost too much bacon (if that's possible) and served with sauce that has some real heat to it.  They said WiFi is available although I couldn't log-in.  Cash only.

Stone Age has become this place. If you're looking for great northern style Chinese food, there's no better location in Santa Barbara.  Some of our guests from China said this is the best Chinese food they've ever had outside of China.
Really really great pizza! They only have a few kinds of pizza on the menu, but why have more when they're all great!

Quick Summary Had a wonderful return to the Belmond. Excellent service, food and ambiance throughout the night. The El Encanto Dining Room is back!  Food- 5 stars They also had their Free-range chicken and tomahawk displayed to the table before bringing it back to being cut (we didn't have that experience when they restaurant first reopened earlier in the pandemic, but it's returned!)  Roasted beet salad- very good. plated well and they even separated and plated two individual dishes for my girlfriend and I  Maple Farms Duck Breast- this was pretty good, but the star of the show was the California Surf and Turf  California Surf and Turf- everything about this dish was phenomenal. I usually prefer ribeyes over filets, but this was one of the best filets I've ever had. Perfectly seasoned and so buttery. The oysters along with the spinach were mouth-watering as well. This was ridiculously good  Goleta Lemon- I liked this dessert more than the Floating Island and the tacos. It's good but not memorable  Tres Leches Chocolate Tacos- I would pass on these tacos in the future. It's a good idea and good ingredients. But it's very sloppy to eat and your hands get all sticky because the chocolate shell melts on your fingers   Service- 5 stars Phenomenal service from our waiters. Really appreciated that they split our salad into individual plates and even nicely plated each of them. Very helpful without being overbearing. Had a wonderful birthday dinner  Ambiance/Value- 5 stars Excellent views from the terrace. Lovely band in the background. It doesn't get much better  Bottom Line One of the best cooked filets I've had. Excellent food, service and ambiance. Will be returning!

Great local Italian eats. Service is excellent. Food is a consistently very good. Ambience is ok inside. Sit outside when it's warm. That's the best way to go -- as long as the bocce court out back isn't too rowdy.   Frutti di Mare - excellent! Yum. I think I've ordered it at least 10 times.
Every Friday for lunch... burritos, tacos, stuffed chiles. It's always busy, but the wait isn't too long. I order via sbmenus.com on Fridays for my helpers and everyone loves it.

Just had dinner last night and what a gem we found.  Lovely, romantic atmosphere with service levels consistent to a two star Michelin rated restaurant.  Our server, Eric 'the bartender,' provided one of the best services we've had in years.  Unbelievably attentive, Eric suggested to start with their signature escargot, along with a MacMurray Pinot Noir, and a perfectly paired Chardonnay.  Our main entrees showcased a perfectly prepared filet mignon, a sole fish rounded off with their decadent chocolate tart.  Eric provided us a very memorable evening with his contagious personality and upbeat spirit.  We will be back.  Thank you,

Apparently, there are some 'Deluxe King & Balcony' rooms that have a partial ocean view. When booking this room, I was told that we could have one of them unless an emergency arose. However, they never made a note of that in my record. We had a view of the parking lot. The room smelled strongly of something...cleaning solution?  It was all fine, but not what I would call a 'Deluxe King and Balcony'. It's more like what I would call a standard Holiday Inn room.  Disappointing.

I'm a foodie, so I like quality rather than quantity and this place is amazing! So far, I've had the cacao black bean tacos, divine, the flavor combinations are perfect! Grilled Eggplant, OMG! So delicious! Market Salad is wonderful! Hummus, fig pizza is very tasty! Empanadas are yummy! The desserts are a must try as well! The drinks are amazing too! For anyone that desires great tasting food, great service this is well worth checking out! I highly recommend this little Mesa Gem!

The restaurant is cute and cozy. Unfortunately, the open kitchen is located in the main room so when they're cooking, it can get very warm and uncomfortable. I suggest sitting in the other room or out on the patio during summertime. Food is typical Italian food. It's really nothing special. For appetizers, we had smoked salmon that tasted just like the ones you get any grocery store. I would avoid ordering that because it really is no different. We also ordered seafood cioppino, which actually had good flavors in the broth but seafood didn't taste fresh. For entrees, I ordered the lobster and crab pasta. It was flavorful and cooked al dente but was quite greasy and heavy. Chicken entree has decent flavors but was ordinary and dry. We got chocolate lava cake for dessert and it was yummy.   Service was nice. Waiters were attentive and constantly walking around to check up on patrons. Price is also reasonable for a $$ restaurant.

Easily the best sushi rolls i have had in town.  would have gotten 5 stars if didn't need to wait 45 minutes for our food while listening to club music.  Next time I'll just call in an order and pick it up.

This place has gone very downhill.  Went with a friend for dinner tonight.  We made a reservation using open table and arrived about 20 minutes before our reservation time.  Our fault, however I counted 17 empty tables and we couldn't be seated early.  I get staffing issues in restaurants, but really, what was going to happen in a 20 minute period?  We stood there for at least 15 minutes waiting, the the hostess said 'I am happy to seat you early now.'  Oh thanks, except now it isn't exactly early anymore.  The service was very slow and our waiter seemed to care more about every other table. I ordered a Grey Goose martini with a twist.  The martini came dirty, with two olives.  I didn't touch it, but it took about fifteen minutes for the waiter to appear with my friend's wine, at which point I mentioned that my martini was incorrect.  He looked at it and said 'oh, that is for a different table.'  He took the martini that had been sitting on our table and actually delivered it to another table!  I was shocked.  Someone paid $16 for a martini that sat on our table for a quarter of an hour.  Unfortunately, this isn't the first less than excellent experience I have had here, this is just the first one that made me change my review and decide not to go back again.

I am a Huuuuge fan of bagel shops and especially gourmet bagel shops.  Jacks is in this category. Was the bagel I ordered good?  Yes, it was extremely good to be 100% accurate.  The reason why I gotta give only 3 stars and not 4 or 5 is the prices. We got 2 bagels to go and it was right at $15 bucks... no drinks and no lox .... I buy gourmet bagels in Beverly Hills all the time and the bill is quite a bit lower than this was. I do want to say what we ordered was phenomenal!  Just not 15 bucks phenomenal

Went back to this wonderful Italian place after a long time. Tried 4 dishes one after each other, ravioli, seafood, chicken and veal. All Tammy and to perfection. I asked for Caesar salad on the side. It was welted in dressing an not appetizing to eat.

BEST NY style pizza.    ....Gino's is a tiny cubbyhole on Figueroa st and serves excellent NY style pizza by the pie or slice the combination of sauce , cheese,crust and toppings are a delight to the senses and friendly fast service is the time.The whole pies have names of Monsters ...my favorite is the Gotti.Treat yourself and visit this outstanding pizzaria !!!!
I love Barbareño so much! Their food is very local and very delicious. The tri tip and fried chicken are definitely winners!

The place is fantastic. Great ambiance, warm staff and delicious food. The lamb meatballs are delicious as well as the smoked shrimp. The prices are very reasonable too. Another great choice if you're seeing a show at the Granada or Arlington. Delicious. They have good drinks there as well and it's a great place to people watch from the patio as well as being dog friendly. They always bring our pooch a bowl of water.

Perfect place to grab a glass, taste of some lesser known but good local wines.  Good variety of wine-friendly foods - flatbreads very tasty (Especially fig, bacon, and blue cheese flatbread) and place offers a super fun way to taste wine!  Hidden little patio is perfect too!!

We dined at Bettina for the second time last night and my first impression was confirmed, Bettina is a keeper. After the disasters of last December and January Coast Village Road has made an amazing comeback. Five new restaurants have opened while only one closed and Bettina is the latest addition. Great food, great vibe and a great staff!!  We had the chanterelle pizza on both visits and it is one of the best pizzas i have ever had. On the first visit we started with the chicken liver mousse and had the salami, chamomile, pecorino & gardinieria on our second. Next time i might have both. We had the leafy greens on both visits and one is more than enough for two.  Our server, Alejandra, was charming and gave us great advise on menu choices. Co-owner Rachel visited us frequently, giving menu tips and making us feel like friends.  Thank you Alejandra & Rachel, we will be returning soon and often,
Great food and great service everyone is friendly the food is actually very authentic Mexican food

So far I've only had a turkey sandwich here. It's the best goddamn gobbler I've had. Excuse my English. Here's what's phenomenal about this place. Their fast as hell. Hell's a four-letter word. When I say fast this is what I mean. I paid for it sign the receipt started my timer. 34 seconds later you put the sandwich in my hand. Not three or four minutes later not 34 minutes later but 34 seconds. It's going to be gone in 47 seconds. JK, my wife doesn't quite eat that fast!

I'm here at least a couple times per week. Super chill. Unless Ed catches you with something other than a Java Station item on your table then he'll be an asshole. Rebecca is amazing. I hope she never leaves. Definitely a weird place but I love it. I bring friends here often.

ATMOSPHERE: Parisian vibes, order at the counter and food is brought to your table.   SERVICE: Excellent. Knowledgeable about all pastries, orders, and ingredients. Food comes out in a timely manner.   FOOD: I've had the privilege of visiting France, from little towns in the country to Paris, and this place can hold its own anywhere. Pastries or breakfast, you will not be disappointed. And don't forget to try their mixed berry jam, made in-house of course! My favorite thing to do is order any sandwich (I love breakfast sandwiches), eat it open face, and use the second piece of toast (or croissant) as a vehicle for jam. Do please try a croissant, they are layers of buttery goodness.   OVERALL: A must in Santa Barbara for locals and visitors.

Unless you're going there to eat don't waste your time. There's only about 4 stores, a wine tasting shop, and a few restaurants. The stores just sell trinkets made in China that you can find cheaper and get a better selection right down State Street. If you want to eat and enjoy a better view I would suggest going up the road just a bit to the marina. There you get to look at the water, and get the added view of a hundred interesting boats.

Review on the meatball sandwich: it was disappointing to say the least. Meatballs had no flavor, portion was not huge. Folks at my table agreed even Subway's meatball sandwich was better. Guess I just expected more since it was from an Italian place. Otherwise, service was quick and painless.
