Eaten at Ca'Dario (different locations) before but first time at Pizzeria Veloce inside the Public Market.

Atmosphere is very quaint. Gives off a 'Day of the Dead' vibe with all the brightly colored plastic skeletons hanging from the ceiling. Service can be a bit slow at times, but you can tell the waiters are trying their best. I got the tamales and they were okay, but very heavy on the breading. Wish there was more filling in each. Seems like they don't have a soda fountain, so drinks are served in bottles - a bit disappointing. The Spanish rice has great flavor and complements the food nicely.   The glad here is SO good. There is nice consistency and has just the right amount of sweetness with whipped cream and caramel added. Would definitely come here for flan again.

Really cool cafe, great service, happy people and amazing food! I felt like I was back in the south when I ate there! I can't wait to go back! A special place where you can sit outside and take all that Santa Barbara has to give :) I will be back with bells on!
As a local I have trusted The Palace for taking family and friends. Always consistent and great service. And, I love the Cajun martini!

If you have seen any of my other reviews you know that I love tacos. When it comes to tacos I'm brutally honest. While I was in SB this weekend I was in the mood for some taco so I decided to try Lilly's Tacos. They were ok not the best but definitely not the worst. Price wise they are a little price per taco for being ok. If you can't find another spot near by these taco will do. I give them a 5 out of 10 on the taco scale.

This is a hidden, authentic gem.  Parking is tough to get close, but worth finding your way.  I prefer it over Super Rica and there's no waiting in line. The tacos are excellent along with some of their specials throughout the week.  Homemades tortillas grilled to perfection with melted cheese.  Be sure to try their mini Quesadilla type offering with cheese and meat sandwiched and melted between two tortillas.
This place is bomb AF (is that appropriate for yelp? Whatever, just effin eat here) *insert fire emoji*

Came here for my Mom's birthday and was floored.  The place is small, gorgeous and family-run, with Chef John in the back and his lovely wife Liz treating us like we were dining in their home.  And the food was spectacular.  The following is what we had (descriptions are from their menu, because there's no way I'd remember everything).  Appetizers:  - Santa Rosa Island Crab Salad with Papaya & Fresh Ginger-Lime Dressing - Lobster & Angelhair Salad with Asparagus, Sundried Tomatoes, Olives & Basil - Artichoke Heart Salad with Fresh Herb Vinaigrette  My favorite appetizer was probably the lobster and angel hair salad.  But the flavors in every appetizer were so distinct and deliberate and never overpowering.  Chef John is just an incredible talent.  Entrees:  - Colorado Lamb Loin with Roasted Garlic, Tom Shepherd's Green Beans & Rosemary - Grilled Duck with Fresh Peach Chutney, Exotic Grains & Ginger Sauce  Liz informed us these are the two dishes the restaurant is known for, and it's easy to see why.  I liked the lamb slightly more, but you can't go wrong with either.  The one hiccup of the night came when we were informed that they only had one more serving of the lamb and it was slightly overcooked.  We tried it and sadly had to send it back.  But to make up for it, Chef John had about 2/3 of an order of the duck (it didn't have the leg) and sent it out on the house.  Extremely gracious.  Desserts:  I wish I could remember what all the desserts were, but the unanimous favorite for us was the homemade peach ice cream with almond cookies.  It was stunningly good.  If you have a special night coming up and want to make a first class go of it, look no further.  This was flat-out one of the best meals I've had so far this year, if not the best.

California Pasta is super simple, and quite amazing. Located at this outdoor mall area, and can be missed if you don't deviate from the main street. While it may not look like they get busy, they do. People were coming and going while I was eating, and all the tables just outside of the door were filled. It may not look like a fancy place, but the food is great. This is where you go when you want great quality food for a good price, and as quickly as possible. They're quite efficient and we had our food in front of us in no time.  I had the Chipotle Chicken Panini, which comes with a side of pasta salad. While the panini was delicious, I advise against the Pasta Salad. There's little to no flavor at all. I don't remember if there is an alternative to the pasta salad.
Received an email with a promo code (YUMMY) to get 15% off and the website won't accept the discount. Bait & Switch! Ugh!

Best darn coffee shop west of the Mississippi. Rare coffee and teas from all over the world. Blue Hal Booth owns a one acre coffee on the kona coast. sells only in Santa Barbara between Thanksgiving and Christmas. life is too short to pass up a visit to his coffee shop.

I love Jack's Bistro. Great breakfast and lunch spot that is dog friendly with outdoor tables. Large variety of bagels, made fresh daily and they taste the way a bagel should.  Tea and coffee options as well as fresh juice. I went here at least once a week when I lived in Santa Barbara with my dog and can tell you it was either packed or we got lucky and went before the crowd got in. A bit slow but for me it was worth the wait. No ac though but the outdoor tables have umbrellas.

The food is SO great here - I absolutely love the bowls. All the ingredients are fresh and fast. However it sounds like you are entering a construction zone when you are here. The blenders are so loud. Would recommend earplugs or a really cool hat. Or a friend who will cover your ears for you while saying 'earmuffs'  Pro tip: kids island bowl is cheap and definitely enough food to satisfy an adult.

Meh. I ordered the fried fish with chili and got some thick-battered half-fried fish of some sort with bits and pieces of bell pepper, canned button mushrooms, and canned bamboo shoots. Nothing hits the spot like canned vegetables, right? It also came with an egg roll and some fried wontons which I thought was kinda weird. The fried wontons were alright but the egg roll I could not finish. Ugh... I've never had thai like this before and I hope I never do again.

The Korean Fried Chicken Sandwich is absolutely delicious and even though I'm not typically a fan of thick cut fries, theirs are superb! Add to that a surprisingly yummy sparking Rosé and you have yourself a crazy good meal.   I'd give the place 5 stars if it wasn't for the following: 1) They have an ant problem. As harmless as sugar ants are, an infestation of them is not appetizing and they need to do something about it. 2) The outside tables and chairs are VERY uncomfortable. The tables don't allow you to easily put your feet under them and the metal chairs dig into your back. We moved inside after about 10 mins and it was much better. If they fix these two annoyances, it's definitely a 5 star place.

It was our first time dining at this ocean view restaurant. Let me start with the green salsa they provide with your chips. Probably one of the best I have had. The ceviche in a coconut was delicious . We also tried the strawberry salad with goat cheese. Super fresh . They have a lot of fun drinks. The service was very efficient . The decor amazing we are definitely coming back

Just returned from Lazy Acres, without fresh baked bread. Very poor customer service, once again. The place is teeming with workers, but none are helpful.  Bakery had no fresh bread this morning (not the first time) and could not tell me when they would have it available.  With their prices, I expect better service than I get at the local Von's (horrible!).  Go to Gelson's--much better service (the manager's station is up front and he/she is always available), better deli food, better baked goods (and fresh bread is always available).  Or Whole Food's.  I won't return to Lazy Acres.

Great beer selection. Great atmosphere. Great for a party, a family or a quick stop after work. Their take on classic dishes is innovative and delicious without being so different that you don't get the classic pizza fix if you need it. Our server, Julian, was friendly, attentive and excellent at suggesting great options without being pushy.  We ordered the Burratta Bruschetta and Smoked Pancetta. I'd order both again Every time if I wasn't so eager to explore the menu!
The CORRECT spelling of this restaurant is Ca'Dario.  (There are other reviews of Ca'Dario listed as 'Cadario'.) Try the Veal Chop was outstanding.

Omg. What a great place. We visited our friends in SB and they took us here. It's a nice little trendy spot. Trendy is not at all important to me. I don't care what a restaurant looks like as Long as the food is good. This place had a great vibe AND the good was great.  Brussels sprouts  Pimento cheese  Charcuterie board  Beet and burrata salad  Pork  Pavlova   All. So. Good.   Oh! And a really nice and attentive staff.   And great drinks!!! I had a Moscow mule. It was a little different then how they are usually made but still great.   I can't wait to go back when we visit SB again.

My husband and I returned for a Valentine's weekend stay and every little thing was impeccable! The last time we were here was 5 months ago for our wedding - the wedding was fabulous, but service was spotty. What a difference 5 months has made, as the team here completes the transition into the Marriot family. This stay we opted for Club access, which is an incredible value, and staff here is above and beyond.   This is a unique, gorgeous property. It also holds a special place in our hearts, being the place where we got engaged and got married. This stay truly solidified it as a mainstay in our lives, and a place we will be marking many a special occasion. Thank you for the continued m

We once loved this place and I even yelped about it. While the atmosphere is still beautiful the food and experience has changed.  For example, The mac & cheese which was once legendary is now better suited for a food cart.    Another example, the sliders are so small I had to put on my reading glasses to see them.   Furthermore, they were really pushing the bottle waters on us in our most recent visit. It would have been nice to know in advanced that they would end up as part of our bill. We weren't even interested in water and some how they sold us four bottles.   Finally, the portions are about half the size they were 5 years ago. In addition, our server was delightful when at our table but the poor guy was spread so thin. The wait period between table visits was uncomfortable to put it nicely.  A classic case of remembering how great this place once was and dealing with letdown of what it has now become.
My favorite restaurant in all of SB. Amazing food and they are very knowledgable about their dishes, how to make them vegan, and how to accommodate various restrictions. The G&Ts are amaaaaazing

Oliver's is great! I've been finding myself in a couple of vegan spots lately and I'm not vegan by any stretch of the imagination. I'm one of those who will not dare consume an animal product in my coffee drinks, like could not pay me any amount of money in the world to drink cows milk - but will devour an entire block of cheese a couple hours later.  I eat what I want, when I want but my sister is Vegan and I'm totally not opposed to others living that life. I bake a bunch of vegan items and I really do love a lot of vegan options but the commitment to an entire lifestyle isn't right for me.  I'd asked my bestie if we could grab dinner at Oliver's on our way home the other night. We usually eat brekke at Jeannine's or grab coffee and pastries in Montecito on our way out to wine tasting in Los Olivos but we are never quite in need for dinner on the return because we've either already eaten a bit sooner than we hit Coast Village Rd. or I've way overdid it on McConnell's froyo stop a couple exits before here.  Either way, I knew I wanted to grab dinner so I intentionally asked the gal at McConnell's to only give me half of my froyo order because I needed to fit in dinner tonight.  My bestie is definitely not vegan so I didn't mention to him this restaurant was and as it got closer to dinner, I simply suggested this spot might be 'vegetarian' I didn't want to totally scare him off of the idea.  We'd eaten a late lunch and ordered a few plates to share which was plenty!  We shared the Apple Salad, Kung Pao Cauliflower and Spinach & Artichoke Flatbread.  All three dishes were very yummy. We both really enjoyed the salad, but agreed that the Spinach & Artichoke flatbread was our fav. It was super filling and would be a great share for a larger dining party.  As much as I wanted to try dessert and under normal circumstances would have totally tried to do dessert (my froyo) before and dessert after dinner, my dessert stomach was already full and we had to table that one for our next visit.  Service was great, the salad came out quickly and the other dishes seemed to take a bit longer but it was a Sunday evening and we had no place else in the world to be so it didn't matter much.  We'll definitely return in the future. This is a great option if you are staying in the area or passing through and want a really scrumptious meal in a nice calm atmosphere.  It was a chilly night and as lovely as the patio was I wanted to cozy up in a corner booth and keep warm indoors. The patio seems perfect for warmer evenings.  Plenty of street parking out front.
Best tacos ever!!! Great owners. You're gonna be full. Don't miss out. This place is fantastic.

I love this place! The lounge and outside dining area are my 'go-to' place for lunch, tea, and sophisticated date night. I look forward to the jazz on Friday and Saturday evenings and can't say enough wonderful things about the fantastic service from the managers Tyson, Jessica and the very courteous server, Alfredo. I recommend the avocado Gazpacho soup, the Charmer cocktail, the gourmet doughnuts, and the kale salad. The ambiance and decor are sexy and sophisticated. This place is a special gem!

Food = OUTSTANDING Service = Not-so-outstanding  I dined here last night while Green Bay was making their way to the Super Bowl -- it is on the corner of State & Haley, prime spot for people-watching.  We were seated by a hostess/waitress? who was awful, unfriendly, unattentive. She took my bf's friend order, then I asked for a cocktail menu, which she brought, then left for like 15 minutes, when ANOTHER server, a gentleman, very attentive, polite, obviously has experience, took over where she failed. He ended up being serving us through the rest of dinner.  My man & I started with drinks, him, their Italian beer (they have both light & dark, he chose the light), which was yummy, myself, their Buca Iced Tea, which was surprisingly strong, Tequila-based. We started with their garlic bread which was buttery, soft, freshly baked and scrumptious! We ordered a pizza, I forgot the name, Cirque something, with kalamata olives, mushrooms, proscuitto & artichoke hearts, in addition to a pasta dish that was equally as yummy. It had bits of proscuitto, red onions and other stuff. Red sauce. Served piping hot, which I LOVE. I saved some of the garlic bread to accompany with the pasta -- it was perfection.   It took a while to get the check, as they were transferring from lunch service to dinner service, so they were busting out the heat lamps, table lights and turning tables. My overall impression was that this place focuses on FOOD over SERVICE, which was slightly disappointing as this place is in a PRIME location. The dining experience was excellent, service hit or miss, I suppose depending on your waiter.   I would dine here again.

We stayed here to celebrate our 4th wedding anniversary. We had a beautiful suite with a private patio. They had a bottle of champagne with chocolates in our room and also dishes and water for our pooches. It was a wonderful experience and the service was excellent! Our favorite beach is also across the street of the resort. Love this place!

Calls itself a 'sushi bar' but there is no bar to sit at even though the pictures show people seated at a bar.  Pick up your order and find a table in the common area of the market. Sushi is ok but no better than one would expect in a plastic box at a supermarket.  The prices are equivalent to sitting at a good sushi bar ordering from a chef which are far too high given the above.

I live in Santa Barbara about 8 years, and  I love it here. I Love our local people and the good food we are able to find here.  Santa Barbara have very much of good choices for eating out.  Today I walked into Lurefish House in my welcome mood to a new neighbor who just become a part of Santa Barbara member. As much as I was excited to check in for a good food and just hope for an acceptable service since they are still newly, I ended up leaving in hungry with my big disappointment.  I am considering if I ever give Lure another shot or I just stick with my local places where I mostly find an welcome honest and attentive service. My experience today at Lure:  I was holding back my entree order and decided to go home in hungry still because I had been waited in almost 3 hours just for few appetizer dishes which one came out  in a plain flavor while the others was too salty. Clam chowder was salty Ahi ceviche had a bitter taste and the cracker was soft... Crab cake was burned and had strong smell of bell pepper  Chairs were dirty with food on it and no one even bother to help us clean, so I just made myself become useful.  Serve is very slow and lack of warm touch and attention.  As I noticed every other table has been served some breads even the lady next to us who just simple had a side Caesar salad dish, but still be treated nicer than our table which is directly to servers' vision. I waited for an hour later but no one offered till I asked because my food took so long.

Was at Costco and they were offering $100.00 in gift cards for $74.99. Wanted to take my daughter and her family out for her birthday, we had never eaten nor heard of this restaurant and they live in Santa Barbara so I thought we would give it a try.  It was a fantastic deal.  Boy, what a GREAT experience.  There were 5 in our party 3 adults and two toddlers.  The receptionist/hosts were fantastic and made sure we were sat right away (I did make a reservation, knowing this time of year is busy in Santa Barbara).  The service was impeccable, the waitress (Maegan) was fantastic, she was there at every instance making sure we had everything we needed.  The bus boys/assistants to servers were right on top of everything, constantly replenishing the wonderful warm sourdough bread and filling out glasses of water constantly.  Removing our plates to make room for the main course, etc.  The food was excellent, I had the Sea Bass which I love.  I had asked Maegan how they cook it and she had said they normally grill it, but she suggested it be sauteed with lemon and capers and that is my favorite way to have Sea Bass.  The portions were generous in size and we were very full to say the least.  My daughter had just an appetizer for dinner, but it looked wonderful and she really enjoyed it.  My son-in-law enjoyed his scallops and even the kids ate all of their meals.  Overall, I would say it was a very successful meal, including the delicious desserts.  A hot fudge sundae to celebrate the birthday girl, with candle intact and a piece of delicious mud pie for anyone who wanted something different.  Yummy!!!  I would definitely suggest this restaurant if you are looking for a fair priced, excellent service and fantastic quality of food type of place to have a meal.  The total bill was reasonable and the view was also another big plus.  Will definitely visit again in the near future.

Such a perfect place to visit in Santa Barbara for some to-go specialty desserts! The service was amazing, and we were able to try various samples of the Turkish Delights which was nice since there are so many different options for flavors. My 2 personal favorites are the Marshmallow/Almond/Almond Cream & Wildberries/Chocolate/Almond Cream. Any of the Turkish Delights with cream in them are sooo good! The Delights are chewy and not too sweet, and are perfect for sharing. The baklava is a must-try as well. Super flaky and the layering is perfect. The honey pistachio ones are delicious!! The interior of the store is beautiful as well and the next time I come back, I definitely will stay for a cup of Turkish coffee.

Just the name will make you smile.   Then they have brioche. OMG brioche.   Grab a loaf of it to go and have it every day with brie and jam. Or as a sandwich. Or just by itself. There is literally no wrong way to eat brioche, and you will want to do nothing but eat brioche once you've had theirs.  They make breakfast sandwiches and they are amazing. An Eggs & Salmon Brioche is just perfect, and a simple Eggs & Cheese Brioche is fully of goopy gruyere.  Grab a Sourdough Baguette if they have one but don't bother to get a bag because you'll eat the entire thing right away. We also tried both types of Cinnamon Roll and can't decide which is better.   That's all I could justifiably try in a single trip, but I'm not above swinging back to Santa Barbara just for more of this because every mile would be worth my while.
The best part about this place is a waitress Morgan she's super hot and pretty much the only reason I eat breakfast

me and my girl got takeout from here and the food was great! Not to mention they were quick in prepping the food too. Place look packed and definitely seemed like a great brunch spot. would definitely want to come back here to dine on their outdoor patio next time I'm in town.

Loved this little place. We were out wine tasting and stumbled upon it. Ended up buying a bunch of cheeses, cookies, salami etc, had a picnic at our hotel. So fun. They have these chocolate caramels.....soooo good.

So. Let me start with backstory. I've always loved Chucks. I get a medium rare steak and it's fairly rare, side of horseradish. Its awesome, epic, I get a huge salad and take half of the meal home for lunch the next day.    Now onto what happened tonight. Ill try to be kind and unbiased. I paid off my college loan today and wanted to celebrate being debt-free with my parents and kiddo. So I take them there. Both my parents and I got the exact same thing. 18oz T-bone steak medium rare.   They both come. Theirs is perfect as expected. Mine is considerable thinner (less bone more meat) and not medium rare, its pretty much medium except for right against the bone. They probably cooked them together and didnt account for the difference in thickness. Keep in mind I'm a chef, I cook $200 per pound Japanese wagyu monthly, I know wellness.   So, I think to myself, this is not an occasion where Im going to pay for their best steak and be  disappointed. It's why I go to Chuckes (to eat a fairly rare, medium-rare steak that never makes me feel bad after). So I show the waiter. He apologizes and asks if It's ok. Confused I say, well, I did ask for medium rare. So he sighs loudly and takes it back.   He returns informing me that the staff says its got the full range and is medium rare deeper into the steak. A sort of fair potential assessment. So I point out that my parents steak is perfect. He looks at it and says 'That's rare. Do you want rare? Or is this ok, they are just going to throw it away.' At this point I'm being patronized about the rareness, I feel guilty about wasting food,  and I just say, Ill just eat it. He brings it back from the kitchen. I proceed to exchange pieces with my parents. Its chewy and medium until right against the bone, where it is indeed medium rare. The waiter is very polite at this point and do my best to be gracious and give a proper 20% tip. And I'm not going back ever. Fair enough, they are an establishment in this town and don't need my business.   Make your own judgement. I think the odds are you will have a decent experience here. But I'm going to cook my own Hearst ranch steak and drink my own bourbon, I don't feel good about this experience.

This place smacks of arrogant pretension. It is an obvious hang out for the Montecito set to go slumming. The food was a clumsy presentation, a sauce drenched mess of small portion. It was lacking in soul almost as much as it was lacking in generosity. I spent well over $100 for my wife and I and we walked away hungry and disappointed.

I told my husband that Santa Barbara (and California in general) Mexican food is way way better than the stuff we get in the midwest. He sort of half-heartedly believed me until trying these tacos. He immediately said 'this is the best Mexican food I've ever had.' He had carnitas, asada, and chorizo. I had rajas, pescado, and camaron. All were amazing. He particularly loved the chorizo. I was really into the pescado. I was hesitant to order tilapia, because I never order tilapia, but it was so good! Seasoned so well and crispy on the side it was grilled on. Pretty good salsa bar. I loved the avocado and peanut salsas. Yum yum yum!!

From the sever to management just indifferent to their poor service. I was blown away when the server could not get drinks, appetizer or entrees right we asked for the manager twice. Instead of probably coming over and dealing with the situation the manager turned his tail and coward in the back texting on his phone and bussing tables. And when confronted with his shortcomings he threw a tantrum seeming drunk and incoherent.

We live only an hour away from SB, so with as many times as we take a casual drive up there, my husband and I haven't ever ventured onto Stearns Wharf.  In fact, the last (and only) time I was on Stearns Wharf was in 1977 on my honeymoon with my first husband.  Anyway, this was a fun, cool (literally) thing to do, and for some reason, there was a lot of wind, lots of clouds, and it actually grew quite cold out on the pier.  Plus, it started to rain.  I put on my hoodie on this August day.  Whaaaaaat?  We were contemplating eating our lunch at Santa Barbara Shellfish Co. right on the pier until the weather was too cold to do so.  We still enjoyed walking around and gazing out over the water.  I thought our car was going to fall through the wooden planks; it sounded so unstable and creeky as we were driving over it, but we made it back to land without a hitch, lol.

Came here this pass weekend and we had a blast .  The Bartender was really friendly and she had really nice recommendations., we ended up taking the  beer flights. this place is a great addition to the funk zone .
This place still rocks. My favorite restaurant in SB. Get the Bangkok street noodles!!!
For the love of god, hire new bartenders.  I mean, you're a better restaurant than that.

After a poor lunch at the Santa Barbara Shellfish Co. I needed a seafood fix before catching Amtrak. I got the Seafood Mac and Cheese, and a cup of clam chowder. I was pleased with the large chunks of clams in my chowder. It was very flavorful. The Mac and cheese was even better.  It was loaded with lobster, clams and shrimp. They did not skimp on the cheese either. I'm glad I came here. Service was very good. I will definitely come back.

I love to visit the Plaza deli for lunch.  The sandwiches are tasty and fresh. All of the food is reasonably priced.  This is one of the only non-chain restaurants left that you can find lunch for $6... and its good food too!
Well prepared food but with COVID their street-side dining is nuts.  I'm sure that the insects and the exhaust fumes from the constant traffic of Cabrillo Blvd is not enhancing the dining experience!

Finally - I get the chance to head back to brophy's.  The reason i decided to give brophy's another try was that i was contacted my management there and they asked me to head over and try them out again. To begin with this was an great gesture- their stars went up in my book right away.  When you take the time to make sure your clients are all satisfied it shows you care even tho there is always a line out the door and they could have just brushed me aside. Soo needless to say i was impressed.  unfortunately I wasn't living in the SB area anymore and it took me a little bit of time to get over there. But i am happy i finally made it!  I ordered their seafood platter and their clam chowder (which was something i was disappointed in last time) an entree and some wine.  I got outdoor seating and the food was great. a lot better than my last experience. They have really stepped up their game.   Thanks Brophy's for allowing me to see a change i will definitely be back there again

The Boathouse is absolutely breathtaking. The location is deep into Santa Barbara and right on the beach, making for a perfect ambiance. The trees, cliffs, and ocean are beautiful sights to enjoy outside. The outside is such a fun place to eat dinner and they have an interesting fire pit to keep you warm. The inside is just as amazing as well. The windows are large and give inside patrons a great view of the cliff and the ocean as well. I can't describe the view other than breathtaking.  The bar area is a bit crowded and right in front of the entrance. Perhaps a better location of the bar for patrons to sit and chat would make for a better flow of people moving in and out of the restaurant.  The food was good. I had the clam linguini and it was delicious. My friend had the sea bass, but for some reason she wasn't too impressed by it. The coffee came out pretty warm, which was not as bad, but a hot coffee would've been better for the ocean breeze. The waiter also seemed to be totally adrift during our dinner and wasn't very attentive, but he might have had a rough day so I can't get mad at him too much. The place was very solid. If ever I'm back in Santa Barbara after graduation, you bet you can find me here.
My new favorite place too! Great food and a great vibe! Love the concept for some quick delicious bites and awesome cocktails. Highly recommended.

we were looking for a dine in option on state st. came upon this place..... they were very nice. happy to see people. our food was really good. I had the chicken fried steak. all was served hot and with a smile !!
Tacos were delicious.  Corn tortilla didn't last too long before it fell apart, so start with those first before the flour tortillas. But everything was delicious.

This place is about authentic as it comes. I have traveled all over Asia and when I walked in, I forgot I was in Santa Barbara. It was truly a Chinese culinary experience. The hot pot flavors were absolutely delicious. We got the combo pot and we are very happy we did. It really allows you to get two totally different flavors. The broth is chicken based and full of flavor. I suggest you don't go too spicy as I can handle heat, and '2 chilies' was almost too much. The staff is friendly and helpful. They helped to explain the process. Our server even brought us her favorite combo of dipping sauces. They offer free appetizers and dessert with your hot pot which is so generous It was really delightful. Try this place out! Be ready for a uniquely Chinese experience! It's really incredible!
Still the best...pizza is so good with that wood-fired taste. I wish the ratings were higher because 3 1/2 stars is NOT representative of this pizza. Give it a try!

Top burger place in Santa Barbara. Also to our surprise, the staff (who are college aged) were super nice and accommodating even though it's more of a casual grab and go type of place. Great customer service always goes a long way especially when the food is amazing!
Cute spot for tapas! nice, clean and modern! Great ambiance! We ordered drinks and appetizers on happy hour I recommend he pork belly quesadilla! Quite delicious, drinks were great! Cute Latin vibe.

Go here for the wheat germ pancakes served only at breakfast time and enjoy the view at the beginning of East Beach. They serve Peet's coffee as an added bonus. Compact little play area near by just right for 2-4 year olds.  Otherwise its just your average greasy spoon serving up hamburgers and hot dogs at lunch time.
Food had zero flavor. We got the tri tip salsa and Mexican Caesar. No flavor and ven asked for extra dressing and it was pretty disappointing.

I really don't get why people are giving them such a hard time...This was our first stay back since Ritz took over.  We needed a break from all of the Covid-19 quarantine and decided to take advantage of their Healthcare discount (Thank you so much Marriott).  For the price we got it for, we cannot complained.  We knew going into it that the restaurants, pool, and spa were not going to be open.  So we planned accordingly.   We packed a cooler with a lot of white claws and beer and brought in a bunch of wine.  A great time was still had... Hotel:  Looks pretty much the same as I remembered it.  The grounds were nicely kept.  The lobby looked the same.  The hotel is located a tad away from the town of Goleta.  Maybe a 10 min drive to nearest restaurant and shopping.   Room:  We had standard king rooms.  A lot roomier than most.  nice sitting area with patio.  Wifi was strong throughout.  Just wished they had coffee.  IT cost $7 at the coffee shop.  The water pressure was a tad weak.  And they only give you 2 towels.   Service:  Overall, I thought it was decent.  Check in went really smooth.  We were able to request rooms next to each other (2 families stayed).  They gave us comp roll away beds.  Luggage and roll away came in a timely fashion.  I requested extra towels via the app...that was useless.  But when I called, it was brought (along with dental kit) to our room.  Sometimes no one picked up the phone, other times, we got service fairly quickly.  So there is still work that could be done.  You can call for shuttle service and bell service.  People were friendly throughout but were pretty insistant upon you wearing a mask.  No mask, no service.  There was not beach set up due to the coronavirus.  So you pretty much had to take care of your self.   Food/Bar:  We didn't buy food or drinks at the hotel.  WE just brought in our own food and ate at the tables and benches like everyone else.  Very chill atmosphere.  Everyone brought in their own wine and were drinking it out of a plastic cup.    All in all, a nice getaway for the price (under $180 a night).  I wouldn't come now if I had to pay the standard rate,  Just be aware of whats open and closed.

If you are particular, and stay in 5 star hotels pretty regularly DO NOT COME HERE! The service is not good & they have a terrible attitude. They are also completely full of themselves and charge an exuberant amount of money for sub par service.  I planned a brunch here for my wedding attendants after the restaurant at my first choice (San Ysidro Ranch) suddenly burned down. I dealt with one of their restaurant managers who was very nice but could not keep his details straight. It was one screw up after another. When we arrived to do the walk through, there was a new manager and she had no idea we had even booked it.  After going back & forth they finally got there sh*t together!  It's a very long walk from the lobby area to the restaurants. They arranged to have golf carts shuttle the guests down but the carts never showed up. Anytime we asked a staff member for help or information they were clueless. The rooms are nice but very bare. For that amount of money I would expect some atmosphere.  I'm giving it 2 stars because the food was actually pretty good. Do yourself a favor...save your money and spend it at The Ranch or The Biltmore. You'll be much happier.

Every time I visit Santa Barbara I visit Carlito's. Even though there is usually a wait, they let you make reservations. I have always enjoyed the food in general that I have tried and so has my husband, children, and other family members who have eaten with us.  The fajitas and home made corn tortillas are delicious. My kids didn't like the tamale trio but everything else was great!
Love this place; love the sweet potato fries and tuna tartare. The drinks are to die for! The service is kind of eh, but the food is sooo good, you almost don't mind.
This is one of my favorite taco spots in SB! Quality and authentic. I love the chicken tacos and beef cheek. Plus you can't beat the prices.

A client had a cake from here delivered, and it was the best cake I have ever eaten in my entire life! It was chocolate with strawberries, pure heaven. You should get a cake from here to forget these uncertain times and to help this wonderful place stay in business!

Bouchon it's time for a reality check. I've been a regular customer for years and have always enjoyed the quality of your food and the professional wait service you offer. I used to dine there once ever two months or so as a regular customer, but understood that you are a special occasion only place for many. As such, I chose to dine with you tonight for a post-pandemic birthday celebration. I was disappointed that there was virtually nothing new on your menu, but thought perhaps you were catering to regulars who have preferences for old favorites. To be fair, this was your exact model pre-pandemic. It is truly time for some fresh offerings. But, the clincher for me tonight, was our no-name server (he never introduced himself) who provided barely average service. If I am going to spend $300 for a meal for three people, I expect at least basic attention. We ordered three first courses, three entrees, three coffees, and two glasses of wine. Once our order was taken, we had virtually no additional service from our waiter. The restaurant was not overfull, and the experience was in a word disappointing. As much as a regular as I have been, I do not plan to return in the near future. I will patronize SB restaurants that seem more invested in my patronage.

I can't say enough about The Creekside.  The food is amazing, the waitstaff and bartenders are great, and their family-friendly location makes it a fun time for everyone.  The owner, Dave is always giving back to the community, the prices are reasonable, and it's a perfect location for private parties and fundraising events.   I highly recommend the candied bacon, Chimichurri Tri-tip Sandwich and their Sangria is sooo good.  They have TVs everywhere so it's a great spot to watch sports and have a bite while the kids play with the fun games they have out.  Also, Country night on Wednesday is great and they teach dance lessons.  Enjoy!

Best sushi I've ever had !!!!  It's a cute little hole in the wall whimsically decorated sushi spot! You MUST try the 'Super Shintori ' roll, it's to die for!  The staff are also very friendly and the quality of food is just on point!

If I could base my review solely on the food I would give it a 4.5 but that's not all you go to a restaurant for. I've been to this restaurant several times and it continues to have the same struggles. A staff that's not trained correctly, meaning they don't know how to greet customers, know the menu (should be top priority), and a basic understanding of good customer service!  It seems as if the owner is trying to manage everything and is unable to do so. A couple other things that I noticed is that the menus are reused and are getting dirty. It would be so easy to use a QR code the way most restaurants are now, as a result of COVID-19. It's easy, clean, and saves on paper! The other thing is they have an appetizer that is goat cheese with tomatoes that's simply amazing, but it's served with tortilla chips of all things!  This is a Caribbean restaurant so it seems kind of odd to serve tortilla chips. A baguette or toasted baguettes would be perfect with it! Anyhow my main beef is the fact that the owner doesn't seem to be training his staff on how to be great servers and ultimately the restaurant is suffering as a result.

This really is a gem tucked in the woods! One of our favorite places to go, especially after a day of hiking or mountain biking. Breakfast on the weekend is delicious, reasonably priced and huge portions! Finally tried their tri tip sandwich last Sunday and it was SO good!! Definitely gets busy in the weekends, but it's always a fun atmosphere!
Very good value. Surprising how good it is compared to the prices. Loved the gnocchi and the duck confit. Bravo!

I love this place. I've been several times. It's perfect, very casual caffe. I recommend: tuna melt sandwich, seafood breakfast burrito, salad with tuna, clam chowder and the burger. All great. My favorite part of the restaurant are the tables set on the sand. You can't ask for better views and ambiance.

Love this place.  Best fish 'n chips and great owners.  They're very likable and the food is just great.    If looking for fish n chips or other UK food, it's a great stop.    You will have to find parking on the side streets as it's in state street.  It has some seating for sure but I always get an order to go.    It's definitely a craving and I end up last minute idea to rub over there.

My boyfriend took me here for my birthday, and I was pleasantly surprised at how unique and interesting the dishes are. They say that each dish has a story rooted in SB's regional culture. I think it's great to eat food that has a little narrative heart behind it.  We ordered the Bao Buns which were flavorful and satisfied my Asian food craving (haha). The next dish that came out was the Egg McMuffin spin off that is a tribute to the McDonald's nearby. In theory, great and kind of comedic. Taste and texture was lackluster for me. The Uni Carbonara was pretty to look at, thick in texture, and different in terms of flavor. I probably would not order again.   The Lamb Al Pastor, on the other hand, was crazy delicious. The lamb. The sauce. Had me smiling like a dummy because it's wonderfully done.   By the end, the Burger and Brussel Sprouts came out. I was a balloon of fullness, but I ain't no b*tch and kept eating. Burger was bomb. Potatoes cooked in duck fat with that aioli, bomb. Brussels were just alright for me.  Got my birthday dessert and chose the Eucalyptus. Like a strange coconut Panna Cotta type thing with kumquats. Sounds weird. Tasted pretty good.  Overall, service was kind and generous to us all night. Felt cared for. Some great dishes here and will be back to try next month's menu. Thank you for letting us bring our dog and for making my 25th a sweet and extremely full experience. 5 for experience. 4 for food.  Now, I've written my narrative about you, Barbareno. I'll see you soon.

Atmosphere was beautiful, but as a huge foodie, food was mediocre for the insane price tag.  The surrounding garden was nice but to my surprise, very small.  We browsed for a total of 5 minutes before our table was ready and that was plenty of time to see everything.  The complimentary starters were lacking compared to similar places.  We received a few pieces of bread, mainly crackers, and olives.  Nothing was outstanding.  We shared the 'kobe beef' flatbread and the flavor was pretty one dimensional.  I mock their kobe beef as I've had Kobe beef in Japan and theirs lacked a certain fatty & umami flavor.  I ordered the 'award winning' ribs and they were very average at best.  Husband did enjoy the night's special venison.  A pet peeve of mine is when they tell you the night's special without telling you the price, the same went with the water when we first sat down.  For such a restaurant, you would think distilled water is included (distilled and sparkling water were $8 each).  I never mind dropping a pretty penny for outstanding food, but I didn't get that here.  Very overpriced in my opinion since you're purely paying for the ambiance which was beautiful to say the least in their upstairs patio overlooking the ocean.  However, indoor dining area looked dark and outdated.  Sadly, I was looking forward to Stonehouse the most on our baby moon, but unfortunately, it was the biggest disappointment.

The name throws you off a bit.  This place is really a nice Sports Bar too with tons of TVs everywhere.  On a warm day the doors and windows are open letting in the breeze which makes it nice to sit and watch  game.  The food was good and the bar maid was Hot. wink wink

Go to the Boathouse @ happy hour for a delicious, well proportioned (and portioned) Kobe /cheese/burger (hold the cheese, in my case - beef is too good to fuck up w/ a layer of cow's oil), complemented by any of several excellent red wines. Enjoy.   PS: I, too, have witnessed, twice and on separate occasions, female wait staff sitting next to and hugging/kissing/fondling a male patron. Better tips perhaps? In any case, WTFC?

Been here many times before, and honestly liked the food... but having my food ready 40 mins beforehand so it's cold, then not even caring, or trying to make it right is absolutely absurd. Then our friends had to wait so long for their food. It made no sense. While I understand times are tough right now, it honestly seems like they don't even care.

Surprised I hadn't written a review on this place yet. Solid great food and one of my favorite places in town. I go with the sopes or enchiladas for good times. Would recommend eating in, to-go can sometimes take awhile, and why wait in that silly line when you can sit down and eat chips?!!

The whole roasted chicken we ordered for takeout was excellent, and was plenty for dinner and lunch the next day for two people. The chicken was freshly prepared and included wonderfully seasoned cauliflower and two types of sauce. Bring a sturdy bag if you are walking to pick up, the lightweight plastic bag barely covered the container.

My mom and her friend ate lunch here today and just raved about it. They loved everything they had and said the margaritas were all fresh and really good. They said the servers took excellent care of them and made sure they had everything they need. My mom is the most picky person on the planet and if she likes it that means something. The loved sitting outside.

I got off work late and wanted to celebrate a little victory, and decided on the Biltmore for its lovely ambiance (especially their decorations in winters)!  We got the seat in front of the fireplace (yay) and checked out their menu. I was grateful that there was a legend for what was vegan, vegetarian, and gluten-free (although the labeling was hard to read). There seemed to be only one vegan option (the olives), but upon asking the nice server, he was able to verify that other things could be made vegan.  I got the veggie burger (made vegan) with the truffle fries (veganized by removing the parmesan). Delicious! I would probably get regular fries next time, because I found these were really oily (yes, there's truffle oil on them, but there was a lot and I've had truffle fries that weren't this oily).  My partner had the tuna empanadas and thought they were okay. He loved his cocktail (I think the one with cacao bitters?), which I found really sweet.   There were A LOT OF OLIVES. They were tasty, but we brought them home and are still eating them a couple of days after!  All in all a delightful time. Service was excelent, the food was tasty, the ambiance was romantic, the decor was festive.
This is the worst McDonald's you could ever visit. Quality is terrible, customer service is bad, ice cream machine broken, and they only take cash.

Attempt to try this place for three times and it had shut its door closed all three times even it is during the business hour according to its website. I am not going to try again and make a fool of myself.

Very refreshing after dining on State Street the last few nights. Lots of people picking up to-go family packs for the evening on Monday night. The chicken and tri-tip were tasty, but not as good as Uncle Frank's in Mountain View.
Pieology wishes they were this good. Great selection, fast service and a great location. You definitely want to get a pizza here.

I had a great weekend at the Belmond El Encanto. From the moment I arrived, I felt especially taken care of by the staff. They are a member of Fine Hotels & Resorts so be sure to book through them to get the upgrade, complimentary breakfast and resort amenity.   We were able to check into our room at 1pm, which was great. There was a fireplace and a view of the ocean. It was an upper bungalow and felt very private. I loved that they give a complimentary bottle of local wine in the mini bar.   The gym was nice with new equipment and the grounds were very well manicured.   The pool was on the smaller side and packed while we are there. I think they could have used a larger pool and pool area, or perhaps an adult pool as there were quite a few children in the pool. The hotel movie selection was nothing to write home about.   Room service for breakfast was better than expected with custom organic green juice, chicken apple sausage and a fruit platter. It took longer than expected to arrive, but I didn't mind as we had a gorgeous patio to enjoy.   They are very dog friendly and brought a dog bed and bowls to the room for my dogs.   The highlight of the stay was using the Faraday electric bikes and heading into Santa Barbara. These bicycles are incredibly high-end and so easy and fun to use.  I was able to explore State Street, the Warff and more all by bike.   I would stay here again and definitely recommend this property.

My friend and I were staying at the Hotel Indigo for 1 night. When we were headed out for dinner I noticed this hotel restaurant was packed. The food looked amazing. Seemed like a very popular place. Next morning we were headed out but wanted a quick early breakfast and this place was open before 10:00 a.m.   We ordered coffee, the Breakfast Burrito and Chilaquiles with 2 Over Easy Eggs. The Green Sauce on the Chilaquiles was fantastic and my friend like his Burrito. Next time in Santa Barbra Santo Mezcal will be my dinner destination to try.

My favorite pizza ever is the Big Cheese from Pizza Guru.. And I would laugh at someone who said their favorite pizza was cheese. That's like saying your favorite sushi roll is a California.. That said, I know cheese pizzas normally suck, but seriously The Big Cheese is life changing

We've been eating at this restaurant off and on for a few years now. The food is always excellent. My problem is this: I picked up food to go two nights ago and they assured me everything was good with the order. When I got home I discovered that they forgot to pack the salad. I called back immediately and they promised they would refund it shortly. Two days now, nothing back in my account. I'm pretty offended by this. It's not like there's a bunch of teenagers behind the counter. Are they just too busy? Maybe a one star review will wake them up? (It did. Read on...)  UPDATE: After nearly 10 days of waiting there was still no refund in my bank account. I had used my ATM card so it should have been much faster.      I called in again and reached the manager. He was aware of, and concerned about, my one star review here.       After some insistence that he had refunded me, he offered me a $25 gift card as compensation for the forgotten salad and my Frustration. This was a much appreciated and healing gesture. I excepted and picked it up 15 minutes later.      They did the right thing. The manager said that he had entered the application for the refund but for some reason it did not go through! Something in that system is broken.     Rest assured that if it does eventually show up in my bank account I will refund it to them.      In the meanwhile, thank you Ca Dario for your generous compensation. This goes along way toward repairing my impression of your brand.

I'm sorry but how can you not love Chucks! The food is always fantastic and the staff is very nice. I admit though my favorites are the salad bar and the mushroom side dish. We also always get a steak and either fish or shrimp dish. I highly recommend a surf & turf combo! I only wish they did more desserts.
Food is no good, beer is ok, the lounge area smells like armpits. That is all.

I had dinner and drinks with a few friends tonight and had the perfect Tuesday night! Shannon was amazing. She was prompt, professional and friendly. She always made sure our glasses were full and our plates were clear! My favorite dish was the calamari! She also had some great wine recommendations!

Wow!!   This place is amazing, we will be back, I visited back when they first opened a couple years back and what a difference.   Our server was fantastic, make sure to sit in her section, Hannah was our server. She was the star of the night. Very attentive and laid back, didn't rush our meal, my girlfriend and I visit SB from time to time. It's where we met and now live in San Diego. If you get to read this review we thank you and hopefully the tip reflected that.   We started off with the Tuscan dip (make sure to order extra bread), albondigas (meatballs) and carpaccio. Good portions and good flavor. My girlfriend and I were there for about 2 hours almost 3 hours. I had 5 Manhattan on the rocks and my wife had 3 cosmos.   To finish our dinner we had the rib eye between the two of us (it was good) and the delicious flourless chocolate dessert cake thing that I loved. The food was a 4/5 but the service was outstanding 11/10! Thank you for the great night out Hannah, Cheers!!

3.5 stars. This place is kinda too overhyped for me. I honestly was expecting a more upscale type of restaurant considering there was a downstairs. Since there were two parts to this restaurant, I was under the impression there was a casual and fancier versions of the restaurant.   The wait was extremely long, so I figured two things: (1) This place must be that bomb (2) it's so fancy we should've made reservations....hmph. It is busy because the size of the actual restaurant is small!   While waiting my cousins and I each got a bowl of the clam chowder downstairs. Then after the long wait, we went upstairs to be seated. I shared the Calamari and fries with my cousin. YES the calamari was super duper fresh....but there wasn't anything WOW about it. I also had an oyster shooter which was pretty fresh! But I think I've had better...  The other seafood dishes my other cousins and friends got seemed plentiful. I just felt like yes the seafood is fresh, but it looked so bland!   Ehh I am sure there are better seafood joints?

Live music was great, which is what lured us in when we were walking by. The service was also very good but, the food sucked. The food presentation was very nice, but still the overall taste of it sucked. I ordered a the grilled filet mignon medium and it was well done, a poor cut and had a hint of propane as an aftertaste. She ordered the duck and it tasted only like oily propane meat. No real flavor outside of the propane which I guess is cool if you are into propane as much as Hank Hill.

I was visiting in town and my friends took me here. I was impressed by the modern, simple, yet elegant ambience. The chicken and beef kabobs were both good and the blonde beer was excellent. Overall, this is a nice casual dining place for fun, healthy eats that won't break the bank.  BTW, I am originally from the Middle-East and I had no complaints about the kabobs; they were fine. I would only suggest heating pita bread slices, instead of adding cold slices to plates.

We waited a long time to go here and always thought it would be so over-rated. Then the time was just right and it totally blew us away! We had the corned beef hash eggs Benedict and the steak and eggs. Both were the best we had even had! The corned beef hash had huge chunks of meat and together with the spinach and avocado made this legendary. The steak was melt I'm your mouth cooked to perfection. Not only that, we got our own carafe of SB Roasting Co coffee and incredible service. Nick took care of our every need and provided some of the best table service in town. Looks like we have a new regular breakfast spot! Locals Tip: go at 6:30! No crowds and great service. Thanks Sambos!
We went here for breakfast and we so enjoyed the food and prompt service! The coffee was great too. We will definitely be back again soon. Thank you for the great service!
Crushcakes Wednesday special cupcake, ding dong imitation is life changing! I legit crave it! You must try this if you're a chocolate lover!!!
Best mexican restaurant in Santa Barbara. The food is really good and the service is nice. Great value!
Empty bowl never disappoints me. My food is always so delicious. The drunken noodles with beef are my favorite. Service is great. Very efficient.

Today I saw a flag at the entrance of the restaurant, and the waiter explained that today is China's National Day.  Bless my Chinese friends.  We arrived at the restaurant about 6:00pm on Friday. There was one table left. Friendly recommend it's better time at 5pm or 8pm if you don't want to wait.

We are locals and were big fans of this place but it has definitely taken a turn for the worst. Our food came out cold and our server was not very receptive to us. We really used to enjoy this place but the whole experience was just horrible. It took 1 hour to get seated, 30 mins for drinks and another 30 for food that was cold. I'm ashamed to say it's not worth our time and we won't be coming back

Came this past Sunday with my family to brunch and had an amazing service. The food is amazing great prices, plus bottomless mimosas couldn't go wrong. I don't remember our waitress name but she was sweet and gave us excellent service

What an amazing experience I just had at this place! Our server Sean deserved a solid 6 stars if possible. Great interaction - he understood my taste and was able to adjust our orders accordingly. Then food was superb. I missed having an espresso instead of regular coffee, but everything else was so perfect that I will let it slide this time.

This review of the Bistro 1111 is only for breakfast.  The buffet is amazing (visited 2 mornings in a row) for only $22 you can get all you can eat of everything imaginable...cereal, fruits, eggs, oatmeal, potatoes, ahi tuna, etc.  The pancakes were very good , they had a slight crunch to them and are dusted it powdered sugar.  Omelette was very meaty and cooked just right not over bearing or anything.  The bacon that they serve has no fat on it what so ever so every bite is pure meat which is a +. The waffle was a little to thick and it came topped with fruit which i really didn't care for.

Great place. Part of the 'House' chain. BoatHouse at Hendry's beach, Casa Blanca on State, etc. They are good people and run their restaurants well. Great food. Great atmosphere. Great service. It's a winner

I love Italian food and have tried many restaurants in the area and this is definitely one of the best.   First off, service is great. The ambience is perfect for date night, lighting is quite dim. The restaurant is a little crowded but cozy (not really suitable for big crowds).   The food is spectacular. It was so great that I ordered two entrees!  I had the Ravioli Al Burro E Salvia and the Tagliatelle Verdi Alla Bolognese. I love love love pasta and I would say I'm pretty picky about how it's cooked and prepped properly but both dishes were amazing. The ravioli were fluffy and each bite was heavenly. The bolognese was creamy and rich. For dessert, I had an assortment of gelato: hazelnut, chocolate, pistachio. You couldn't really taste the hazelnut, very faint. My favorite was the pistachio but it did taste a little artificial.   Overall, I would definitely come back here. Prices are on the higher side but that's typical in SB.

Yelp doesn't allow half stars, or I'd add .5 to my 3-star rating.  First of all, Jan & Hollis are nice people who own this poorly named good Thai place.  We live within walking distance & mild dyslexia kicked in as I thought it was called 'MENU' Fan (for years) before my wife corrected me. It's written in cursive, & also spelled incorrectly ('Meun Fun') with their email list gathering website.  'Menu Fan' seemed a dumb name for a decent Thai place. Unfortunately, 'Meun' is not much better.  I've been to Thailand 4-5 times and have no idea what that anagram for 'menu' means.  It always fascinates me when local businesses succeed in spite of being poorly named. Someone slept through their Marketing 101 class.  The food (the main point, of course!) is good.    There are a couple of original dishes we get every time. The Fresh Rolls are delicious.  Fresh salad wrapped in rice skins w/a wonderful salad dressing dip. The quality overall is decent.  It's not cheap & portions are not large, but it's good.  What has disappointed me is their lack of interest in substitution or cooking anything other than exactly what's on the menu.   I like almost every vegetable on the planet but can't stand bell peppers. They will omit them from an order, but refuse to add Bok Choi, or broccoli instead. Seems a small gesture to cook what the customer requests. Perhaps the chefs are on autopilot and can't customize orders? I don't believe I should have to pay more for a simple substitution, but that wasn't even an option.  After 8-10 years of patronage (including catering), we've easily spent thousands there.  It's diminishing not to be able to order exactly what we want.  I've had the same problem trying to get a batch of noodles spicy enough. It's comedic. No matter how many 'very, very, very spicy' requests, the drunken noodles taste the same.  This is probably fine w/folks who like blander, less spicy food, but it's kind of funny how I have to doctor everything once we get it home. When you eat inside the restaurant, they'll bring a hot sauce/fish sauce tray that's great. They've never done this with takeout.  While they offer white fish on other dishes, they refuse to add it noodle dishes. The chicken used in noodle dishes tends to be dried-out white chicken, so I wanted fish.  [Oddly, the chicken used in satay along w/a delicious peanut sauce is much more moist & flavorful.]  It's disappointing Meun is unwilling to make these simple substitutions to make their food more tailored to folks patronizing the place.  They refuse even for takeout where the cost of labor is extremely low & most customers (like us) tip without any service at all.  We believe in supporting our local businesses and restaurants on the Mesa. There is much to like about Meun.  It's just starting to feel like they've lost the desire to create more interesting dishes.  Or to co-create based on the orders they're getting over the phone today with customers asking for some alterations.    'Your Choice' on State near Gelson's has been around 30 years. They offer more authentic Thai food with duck, a broader range of spices, fish, & young coconuts to drink. It's more similar to food I've eaten in Thailand. I'd order from them more often, but my wife eats a rather bland diet these days.   Perhaps Meun is doing a 'Natural Cafe' version of Thai food?  Less exotic, less spicy, no organics, but easily scalable?  Sadly, 'The Sojourner,' one of the last true natural food restaurants in Santa Barbara, had to close its doors, while Nat Caf is franchising all over the place.  Meun has two locations now & it remains to be seen if that will make their food less interesting. It's hard to be creative once you have more than one restaurant to manage. Meun has some of the early markings of a franchise where you simply can't 'Have it Your Way,' to quote the old commercial.  I hope Jan & Hollis will read this & rethink things. I know Jan & her chefs have a lot of creative ideas that could go into the food, if they're not thinking to standardize the model for franchising & expansion.  The Fresh Rolls are a wonderful example of that.  I'd like to see more exotic food on the menu like duck, crab, and fresh fish & meats that are not dry or bland.  The pandemic has made things challenging for most businesses, less so for those that always had a takeout model.  Meun has had takeout from the beginning. They may miss revenue from the small tables inside, but I suspect they're doing as well as Chinese takeout, Pizza delivery, Chicken Ranch & other locations doing takeout before Covid.  I wish them well & hope they'll consider expanding menu options -vs- more locations in the future. We're not giving up on them, but after years of ordering, just felt like we needed to keep it real with some real feedback that could make them a truly FIVE-star restaurant.
First time trying a mulita and I LOVED it! Decent pricing for Santa Barbara.  Wish the salsas were a little spicier.  Will definitely be back!

Drinks review only.  This place is a straight up dive bar.  We came on a Saturday afternoon around 5PM and there were some sketchy bros about.     The place is pretty cool 'cause they have all free games like beer pong and a ping pong table.  Drinks are cheap too.  PROS: -Strong pours -Cheap drinks,  2 whisky shots for $5, large beers for $5  -Lots of games inside.  Beer pong, ping pong, pool table.  All these games are free to play/use. -Lots of TV's around   CONS: -Straight up dive bar. -Annoying/sketchy crowd and we arrived around 5PM.  Not sure what the crowd is like at 9PM. -Bathrooms are disgusting

Came here and ordered the classic Reuben sandwich and onion rings. The onion rings were fine but were pretty greasy. The Reuben, though was really not that good. The bread was soggy from a huge amount of thousand island dressing on it and there were only a few pieces of corned beef on the bread.  The whole thing just tasted like soggy bread with thousand island dressing on it. The service is exceptional, though. Jennifer is amazing! And they have McConnells ice cream! I may come back to try something else because they have a huge menu and I could have just not ordered the right thing.

Reasonably priced? Nah, very pricy for what you get. 7.99 for a half of a New Yorker, pickles that taste like a**. The Jewish delis here on the East Coast know how to make pickles. I am giving this place 4 stars because their pastrami is very tasty, not your Carnegie Deli stack, but tasty. It hit the spot for sure and I will make another trip back when on the West Coast again, hopefully soon.

So yummy and So fresh and you get a ton of food for the price. The chicken salad sandwich is good, their soups are always amazing. I love the salads too - the waldorf salad is to die for.  A friend of mine just ordered a calzone here the other day and it smelled amazing and she loved it as well. Everything is so fresh and tasty. hIghly recommend this place.  I've never tried any of the baked goods bc I am too full to even think about it, but they have some amazing looking cupcakesm, lemonbars and other itmes that I hope to get around to one day as I'm sure they are great based on what I have experienced from the place.
Yesterday, we returned and I enjoyed my meal. Mildest dishes were Saag Paneer & Mango Chicken. Rohan Josh was so tender!!! Plain, Sweet & Mango Lassis are all available. Excellent Raita.

I've been wanting to check out The Lark for a while and finally had a chance to with a great group of friends! We did a walk in on Sunday, Halloween night, and luckily even though there were no private tables available, we were given the option to sit at the large communal table which was perfect. The weather was perfect and it felt like we were eating both indoors and outdoors simultaneously.   We ordered all of our waiter's recommendations- the crispy Brussels sprouts, fried halloumi with squash and pomegranate, fried chicken and the branzino. We also ordered the prawns and a couple of drinks. Everything was delicious and the service was incredibly attentive and friendly.  They also serve a house popcorn which was easily the best popcorn I've ever had. I already found a recipe online on how I can recreate it. My favorite dish of the night was the branzino - phenomenal. A must try. The fried chicken was a generous portion but I wouldn't order it again. I'd order everything else again though! And I'll definitely be back for that popcorn. They have a great dessert menu as well but unfortunately all of us were too full to try anything. The final bill wasn't also terribly expensive considering how much food was served. It came out to about $50 per person for appetizer/entrees/drinks.  SB usually lacks in their food quality but The Lark is a gem. My new favorite. I'll be back to try more things off the menu as well as bring my parents and more friends!

My wife and I stayed at the harbor inn and picked up a quick bite to go. My wife got the salmon with mashed potatoes and asparagus that was delicious and I got a cup of minestrone that was good. The food is very pricey but it is expected anywhere in this area. Giving 3 stars because we didn't try much food from here.

Absolute best breakfast/ brunch in Santa Barbara. Can't go wrong with anything on the menu and you have to order a cinnamon roll. They're famous for those. Their mimosas, cocktails, lattes, are all amazing. Ambiance is really nice even at their new location. I loved the old spot so much but they've done a nice job with the new one as well. If you're looking for a spot for breakfast or brunch, look no further!

Stumbled upon this place today while looking for a new place for a bite to eat. Cool little place at first glance, lots of outdoor seating, cute wine bar, 8 kinds of frozen yogurt with a decent menu, pastries in a case, and small upstairs area for live music. Seems like they might have tried to take on too much...  I got a tuna sandwich and an orange juice, while my boyfriend got a BLTA and a coffee, which was Peet's brand. Our options for sides were chips, salad or potato salad (so&so's world famous), we picked the famous potato salad.  Our total was $28 which seems a bit pricey, we asked the guy at the register about it after the fact and he said that the orange juice was about $5 (not on the menu) and that the sides of potato salad were an additional $3 charge (ALSO not on the menus).  We got our food and were thoroughly disappointed. Potato salad was chunks of potatoes with olives in a mustard looking sauce, looked great but had NO flavor. Tuna salad sandwich was smothered in a yellow mayonnaise and the tuna might as well have been straight out of the can (also NO flavor). As for the BLTA it was not toasted and my boyfriend was quite unhappy with this. The coffee was burned and the orange juice was delicious and fresh.

The Monte Cristo is one of the best things I have ever eaten, and I crave them constantly... I haven't had one since moving out of the area over 4 years ago, and I haven't stopped missing this place. It's always a wait, and I never once minded the crowd. Treat yourself and try it yourself.

If you are craving tacos but don't want the same boring meat I totally recommend Lilly's . I got the lengua, asada and adobada all of which were super delicious. I think my favorite was the lengua though, super tender and moist at the same time. The tacos may look small but after 4 tacos I was pretty full. I will have to come back and try the tripas!

2/13/20 was my 6 th visit with cousins visiting from the East Coast. The food and service were flawless as always and our server Dani gave us the time we needed to chat -LOVE BLUEWATER GRILL !!!   Wow my 5th visit and the best food I have had in Santa Barbara in 18 years! REALLY! Blackened ahi and blackened swordfish- our waiter Chris was great and I just learned that the chef is a woman - Chanel and she is so talented and as always the Manager, Colin, makes everyone feel so welcomed and appreciated THANK YOU EVERYONE!!!

Great for breakfast, always had a good meal here. 3 stars for not having fresh brewed coffee, they have serve yourself coffee dispensers with flavored coffee and fake 'kona' - which is a let down for a coffee snob such as myself.

a place with so much history...   my first visit to this place was when my friends bad got signed to roadrunner records... (their choice spot at the time) harrys has an awesome liquor selection and i have only 2 words... HARRYS MARY... the most epic bloody mary in the SB area... well ok the mesa cafe has good BM's too... but this gets my supreme vote  you cant really knock a place like this... it offers a lot of variety on the menu and each one is prepared very well!!! nothing to fancy, just a touch  of some SB history... if you live there or visit there, this place is a must...  dont be fooled by the old plaza that its located in... also yeah its shoved in a corner and some what hard to find...
My go -to restaurant. Especially if you're vegan / vegetarian. Almost anything on the menu can be altered and they use coconut milk for basically everything. I highly recommend the yellow curry.

Delicious tacos that won't fill you up. Almost feels like a scam.  Each taco is $11+. Any first time visitor would assume that one order at Goa Taco is sufficient. No, that is not the case. You can eat one taco in about 4 bites. There are no sides included in the price. You'll definitely be left wanting more. But then you would have to pay for another taco. They need to really fix their prices or business model.  Are you willing to pay $22 for a meal? There are better places in Santa Barbara. Are you financially stable? Their tacos are delicious, you should try them out at least once in your life.

Saigon is one my wife's favorite places in Santa Barbara,  her tofu vermicelli is perfect every time.  My duck curry had some great flavors but was just a little short for me. Some of the duck and rice were slightly under and asking for extra spicy didn't seem to add anything to the dish.

Ordered through Door Dash. Surprised how inexpensive, delicious and quick it came.  Portions are HUGE and you'll have seconds and maybe thirds.  Love that with your delivery option you can get groceries. Ie I got garlic chili sauce that I kept meaning to pick up from the market.  I'll be ordering again soon.

LOVE this place! A little on the pricer side, but it's quaint decor and cozy ambiance will suck you right in. I love how it's kind of tucked away too, in fact, my husband and I actually found this by accident when we were trying to find a side entrance into The Lark haha! Highly recommend getting a glass of wine or beer (or two...or three) here, they have quite the selection. Brownie points to Les Marchands for letting us bring our pizza in from The Lucky Penny too :)

This is an update on my previous review. I should've done awhile ago but was very busy.   I finally ordered a whole pie which is an entirely different experience to slices. I ordered a margherita. It was good except there wasn't much basil on it. I didn't realize that until I left. I noticed an inconsistency in the amount of basil they put on different pizzas and slices.   So again, not NY pizza but more like pizza from Naples area. The whole pizza is quite tasty.
This restaurant is top notch! Superb food, attentive wait staff, nice ambience. Great drinks and wine list too!! This is our anniversary restaurant.
Tried Dave's dogs on two different occasions .food is always cold! maybe if they'd serve hot food would get a better review?
Good location and a good friendly staff.  As a alternate to a burger they are good option.

This place hits the spot like no other. There's a reason Black Sheep has stood the rest of time in Santa Barbara...a place where I'm almost certain the restaurant turnover has set a world record! The chef is VERY creative with the meals...certain things you would think were obtuse...nahhhh, tell your brain to shut tf up and enjoy the creativity this place has to offer!  I'm not much of a meat eater these days, but I do splurge every now and then and they definitely hit my meat craving spot! (Get outta your dirty heads, people!) Enjoy the delightful cocktails with your meal, the AMAZINGLY friendly and efficient staff, and the reasonable (by Santa Barbara standards) prices, and you'll have one of the best meals in town!
I am in heaven.  All the Santa Barbara wines we love, fresh fish and excellent service.  Food is great, beautiful views of the ocean.

Meh. We came here on date night on Saturday and it was pretty packed. The complimentary vinegar popcorn (too small a portion), was also too vinegary. We had been to a few other places for drinks before we ended up here, and so we weren't too hungry...so just got the main course.  I got the wild salmon, and H got the pork belly. It was underwhelming. The wild salmon was actually sushi, and was nothing spectacular. The pork belly was good, but overpriced.   Overall, no bang for the buck.

My family visited Mizza this past Saturday evening and there were 7 of us. I had ordered a delicious pizza to go in the past so figured it would be a great place for my mom's 65th birthday. I made a reservation the day prior and they were happy to seat us all in semi-private covered outdoor area (due to covid-19 orders). There were several outdoor heaters and shades that could be dropped to keep the heat in. Although we never got cold. They were very busy when we showed up at 6pm, but happily sat us down immediately. They have delicious pizzas, pasta's, and of course meat for the steak and potato boys. We started out with Grilled Octopus, Bruschetta and the Beef Carpaccio. While I love calamari and am not a fan of octopus, they nailed the texture and flavor and we all wanted more! The carpaccio was also amazing and so full of flavor. It was the best I have ever had! For main courses we had the Spicy Sausage Pizza, Pepperoni Pizza, Salmon Piccata, Fettucine Bolognese, (2) Butternut Squash Ravioli. Everyone had their own dish and because I am a major foody, I had to try them all. Each one of them was amazing! The sauces that they use for their dishes are all so full of flavor and different. We had a 4 & 10 year old and both were also very pleased. We also ordered wine and mixed drinks and they were all very satisfying and not watered down like many restaurants, pretty stiff. All of the staff was very pleasant and attentive to our needs. I was a little hesitant to bring my family here after seeing some negative reviews on service, but I can say that definitely was not the case for us! We had a great time, the food was affordable, service was stellar and we will be back! Thanks Mizza and we hope you are able to make it here in Santa Barbara!

This place keeps amazing me with its simply-delicious bowls and sandwiches.  Their prices are the best too! You get generous portions of organic, plant-based decadence, for street-corner buckaroonies.  Do yourself a favor and take the elevator up to the third floor, and order something at The Honey B.  Anything!  It's all so yummy.

I came here recently for dinner on a weeknight with some co-workers. It was my first Eureka experience and it was a pleasant one. I ordered the veggie beet burger and thought it was fairly tasty, though to be honest I can cook a yummier beet burger myself. But I was just thankful there was something vegan-friendly to order here! I would recommend making the patty firmer as it fell apart too easily. Perhaps the addition of flax or breadcrumbs would do the trick. It could have used more flavoring where vegenaise or maybe a hummus or tahini dip would have worked well.
