The service was good, nothing to complain about! We had a friendly and accommodating server who was nice enough to give us all separate checks. He was efficient at taking our orders and prompt in bringing them out. It was a busy night so kudos to him!  I probably wouldn't go out of my way to return but if my co-workers invite me again I'll go!

The most atrocious service comes from a dining establishment where the servers refuse to serve Blacks. The one who served me did so with a side of contempt with a dollop of attitude. She gave off the most horrible vibes and talked to me in rudest most condescending tone. Even the high school age busboy  showed his nastiness. The entire waitstaff crowded around the hostess stand with their backs turned to me. Afterwards, they turned around and glared at me for even showing up. These prejudices still exist and it's very open in Santa Barbara.    Furthermore the food was barely edible. How can anyone screw up enchiladas with beans and rice? The cook sure did because the enchiladas were not thoroughly cooked and the beans with rice lacked seasoning.   When presenting my card for payment the sever acted like she did not want to take it. She took her sweet time ringing up the bill while she gossiped to her co workers about the fall of some uppity N****r coming into the restaurant.   Needless to say, I will never step foot inside that pig sty again. I don't recommend anyone else doing so either.

Love the history of this restaurant and all the memorabilia (no I am not racist, but it is a part of our past). Huge selection of egg dishes, amazing mimosa's. Good place to come with a small group. Service is great and mini muffins....mmm.....they are like little bites of heaven!

Ok so we came at the beginning of June 2020 when Covid was rearing its ugly head. Normally this type of hotel is too pricey to justify paying but they had some really good specials for certain professionals which we could not pass up. We booked for 2 nights and were super excited to come and see the Ritz. It is in a secluded location along the coast in the city if Goleta, just outside Santa Barbara.  Upon first glance, the grounds are very pretty, lots of trees, quiet, if not plain. There was a calming breeze that blew through the entire grounds. We walked from the front desk to our room. The hotel seemed to not be at capacity, which made sense due to Covid. We got to our room, opened the door and were a bit disappointed. The room was so basic, I was expecting more. The decor was old. Some people might've called it 'classic.' It was old fashioned to me, and not in a good way, like the whiskey drink. The only thing that seemed new was the shower tiles. There were old wooden sliding doors on the closet and all throughout the room, spanish tiles on the floor. Whatever. Plus I noticed the phone did not work when trying to call the front desk. I tried to call via an outside line on my cell phone and nobody answered after letting it ring for a while. *Sigh. We were determined to make the best of it. Covid, among other things, had gotten us down and we needed a family getaway.  We went out to dinner, at one of the few restaurants that were open for outdoor dining at the time. Afterwards we walked down to the beach, which has direct access from the resort grounds. This was beautiful. Calm, amazing skylines when the sun sets. Lots of families spaced apart enjoying family time. THIS is what we came for.   The next morning we got up with a plan to head to a beach. The phone front desk button was still not working, so I called on my cell phone and requested it be fixed while we were gone. I was assured it would be. So we left to a nearby beach and spent the amazing day there. Upon returning, and getting In N Out for dinner, the phone was still not working to front desk. And again, no answer on outside line. Ugh. Then I wanted to order up some sodas through room service for the family and found that 8 cans of soda was gonna be $72. No that's not a typo. $72. A sandwich at the cafe was $20. *eye roll. While eating our dinner on the balcony, I noticed bird crap ALL OVER the roof of the building next to us. Not pretty. After dinner, I tried to relax with some TV. It wasnt working. I couldn't turn it on. I called the front desk (no, still from my cell phone) to report it and was told they could send an engineer at 1045pm. Hey lady, my kids are sleeping here. Are you serious? Forget it. I'll just check out in the morning and forever regret staying at the 'Ritz Carlton.' It was an extreme disappointment. And I didn't even pay the normal, full price.

The food at the Black Sheep is always tasty, fresh, and creative. The reason I'm specifically leaving such a good review today is for the service we received Tues night (7/24). We sat at 6:00 pm for dinner and had a show at the Lobero at 7:30. We did NOT mention this to our waiter, and I acknowledge that mistake on my part. When our second round of plates were coming out very slowly, we spoke to the waiter about our deadline. The owner came over just a couple of minutes later to apologize (not needed!) and to let us know they were expediting for us. We took our last bites at 7:20 and left - they even expedited the check for us. We thoroughly enjoyed the meal and made the show comfortably. This was completely our fault for not giving the deadline up front, but the staff went above for us and we are thankful. The owner even gave us coupons to come back! Completely unnecessary but you can be sure we'll be back again.

Nestled into the end of a strip mall in Montecito, Sakana is a true hidden gem of the locals scene in Santa Barbara. Pulling up to this unassuming and intimate restaurant right next to a Vons, you'll find a dim-lit and charming interior boasting incredibly creative sushi with picture-perfect presentations.   Like Nicolette Sheridan, this is what we call a sure thing.  First, the menu is huge. If you're a strict traditionalist then you'll be disappointed since their spin on sushi is quite novel; combining fish, textures and flavors in ways I had never before seen. We ordered the Surfer's roll...twice...maybe three times because every time it hit the table everyone began using their chopsticks as weapons of war. We went on to devour the Red Dragon and Montecito rolls with impatient precision. The ahi tuna appetizer is also worth noting. The sushi is served in press box form so you won't find any seaweed holding things together or impacting the overall taste. Every dish is positioned like artwork with splashes of colorful sauces striped across and as you eat and dip the image on the plate transforms.   It's not cheap but it's also completely worth the price tag. Plus, they are cool with BYOB so our feast for four ended at under $40 each. For a bonus, see if you can convince your dining companion that the HD fish screen is actually a live feed of the tank in the back kitchen. The fish is fresh enough for it to be convincing...

I was visiting family for Christmas and my grandfather choose this place. I should have second guessed his judgement at 86, but I went with it haha.  We arrived for a 5:30 reservation and were seated right away. However, I was a little turned off that they sat us directly next to a table that looked like it had previously had a group of 2 year olds at it. The table and the floor were covered in food and trash. I bring this up because the table remained that way the entire time we dinned, even though they were not busy.   They have a decent menu, with something to pretty much meet most people's taste. Pasta's, seafood, steaks, etc. Below is what we tried...  -Mushroom & Parmasean Fries- These were probably the best thing we ordered. They are actually thick slices of simi fried portabello mushrooms. They are very good and a healthier option for an appetizer -Guacamole, Salsa, & Chips- Sadley this was not good. The guacamole was flavorless and the salsa was watery. -Tomato Bisque- Decent, cream based soup. However, it came out luke warm. -Shrimp Linguene- My mom's choice. She said it was very good -Spaghetti and Meatballs- My grandpa's choice, which he liked. -Salmon, Brocalli, and Mashed Potatos- My choice. This was decent.They give you a large portion. -Chicken Marsala- My dad's choice. He said it was ok. Not the best, but not the worst.   Now..to my complaints.  -The silverware had dried food on it. Not once, but twice.  Ive worked at low end restaurants and five star places, and there is really no excuse for this. Its gross, check your silver wear before putting it out. -The restaurant itself was dirty, Not just the silverware, but the tables, floor, booths. etc. Our table was sticky, there was remnants of food on the chairs, and the floor was covered in past diner's meals.  -Nothing was complete. Although seated at a set table, we were missing place settings. Also, some of the setting were linen, while others were paper napkins. It looks like they were having an inner struggle with being a Denny's style restaurant or an Olive Garden. Pick a theme and go with it, it looks bad to have different things.  Our waitress was nice and attentive. Overall though I just couldn't like this place. A dirty restaurant is a big no for me. I will not return.

Soul Cal Smokehouse is on point with their bbq meats. Had their baby back ribs and chopped meats sandwich. It would make feel soulful afterwards. Just to add, the potato salad is better than my moms. Sorry mom.

Came in for a late lunch on a Friday and it was surprisingly crowded. The location is excellent, so I assumed the food would be bad.  Turns out this was the exception to the rule. Food was fantastic. While the menu doesn't have any unique options, what we did have tasted great.  Prices were pretty reasonable too. Highly recommended.

Hopped off the train excIted to try this place!  What caught my eye was their Cheeseburger Egggrolls. When I arrived, I was surprised to find that it is a fast-casual restaurant - you order at the counter and wait to have the food delivered. Worked well for me as I wanted to get lunch over with so I could get my day started.  I ordered the Cheeseburger Eggrolls and the Buttermilk Fried Chicken Sandwich.  The egg rolls are as good or better than you would imagine. I was pleasantly surprised! They stuffed an In-N-Out cheeseburger in an egg roll. Crispy on the outside, cheesy, meaty, and onion-y on the inside. Definitely a must try.  The chicken sandwhich was alright. I wish I would have taken the employee's suggestions and ordered the fig burger or the other chicken sandwich. The chicken, by itself, is super crispy, but heavily breaded and skinny. Better in the sandwich than by itself. The coleslaw makes the sandwich. The fries I opted for were crispy but a bit dry and lacking salt/flavor. Their ranch made up for it (it was super good!)  Overall, I would try this again. Reasonably priced for the food you get. I know I will crave those egg rolls again.

This is usually our go to for seafood in Santa Barbara. Everything always tastes great, and the portions are usually large enough to be able to share a few things amongst a group. The only bummer is the noise. Sometimes it can get a little loud and echoey, but with the food being so tasty, it's still worth coming back.
Great all around. Atmosphere is clean and welcoming. Service is friendly. Food is fantastic. Especially recommend the sausage pizza with added quail eggs on top.

mochi doughnuts in SB? yes Asian food representation! True story: my friend texted me about MŌR Doughnuts while I was out camping in the desert, I ran around and finally found enough signal to place a preorder to pick up on Christmas. Best decision ever.  Spending this Christmas alone due to covid, I wanted some cheer! These mochi doughnuts brought cheer and so much more. Tommy, the owner, is the sweetest person ever and after chatting for a bit, he really did brighten up an otherwise sad day. You can tell he is genuinely just a caring person, who deeply cares about his customers! He even offered to deliver the doughnuts to me because I was running late on pick up!  I tried the mango, ferrero rocher, matcha, churro, and strawberry sprinkle doughnuts. Tommy was giving away free strawberry doughnuts to kids that day, but dropped in one for my kid at heart :)   All doughnuts were incredible. My favorite was mango and matcha! I absolutely love the chewy texture of the mochi and can't wait to try other flavors (and past ones I missed) as they drop. Is eating four mochi doughnuts a week bad for me? *shrugs*  Some logistics to know: - weekly preorders announced on @mordoughnuts on Instagram (typically 12pm on Thursdays) - $12/box of four doughnuts - sometimes rotating flavors as Tommy comes up with more delicious ones! - pick up downtown on Fridays and Saturdays between 10am-1pm - order online, pay by card, choose pick up day  Thanks Tommy for bringing such a joy and treat to our community!

If you don't get the bourbon barrel cake while here, you're missing out. One of the few places whose kitchen stays open late, this place is a revelation. The brussel sprouts are life-changing, the kimchi burger is divine, and I have tried most of the menu and haven't been disappointed. Their vast whiskey selection will help ease your troubles too. A must try in Santa Barbara.

No attention to detail and bad employee attitude sabotaged what might have been a positive experience.  No need to review the food at Pickles & Swiss when the service was this poor. Strike that, it's not that the service was terrible, it was downright arrogant.  We were looking for a good sandwich place I saw the great pictures on Yelp. After trying to negotiate the one way streets and terrible parking around State Street we walked into Pickles & Swiss famished after a long drive. It wasn't that busy, but seeing this was our first visit we obviously needed some extra time to look through their extensive sandwich menu. Usually when I have questions I ask the cashier for explanations or even suggestions. The ginger manning the register acted put upon when asked question about some of the sandwiches or suggestions. It was the whole 'Uhh, yeah' 'That's that is says' 'Uh huh' in a condescending and smart-ass tone.The last time I experienced this type of apathy/snobbery was when I shopped at 'The Android's Dungeon & Baseball Card Shop' back in 2009. And I thought THAT was the worst service ever. Anyway, just because one person hates their job that doesn't mean this experience couldn't be salvaged.  We chose to eat outside because there isn't a lot of room in this small shop.. We had two separate orders, which were placed at the same time. When the first order was ready the guy that gave us our food was similar to the ginger at the counter. No smile, no can I get you anything else, no personality. He was some guy schlepping food orders outside waiting for his shift to end. So we get our food and there were some mistakes with the order. Note that we made no customizations, we ordered the sandwiches as they were written on the menu board. One of our sandwiches was missing an ingredient. So I went back to make them aware of this. One of the kids behind the glass said my sandwich doesn't come with lettuce in it. When I told him it did and he gave me attitude. but after I pointed to and read allowed the sandwich ingredients from the menu BEHIND him he said the menu was wrong. Anyway, I got my lettuce after another kid apologized and said it was their mistake..  Another error was that we ordered several kids meals but only two of the kids meals came with animal crackers.. After the lettuce hassle I wasn't going to go back in over small cookies.   But, the most embarrassing gaff was that none of sandwiches we ordered came with pickle spears. I mean, c'mon you're called Pickles and Swiss but you forgot the pickle? Pretty much every picture on Yelp shows their nice sandwiches with a pickle spear.. There is another Sandwich shop in Santa Barbara called Three Pickles Deli...Hell, bet I'd have a better shot at getting a pickle spear there.   Yes, I know this is a college town and it has to be frustrating working with Millennial employees but there needs to be senior management on site.  Until then, I don't recommend this for tourists.  Let the locals enjoy this place and the attitude of their staff.  - Good salads - Grilled Cheese had Parmesan cheese on the outside. Very cool

I went to the downtown location. I asked to see the difference between the Regular and Super sizes. The first employee said no they can't show me, the cashier didn't know so he called over the same first employee... I ended up just getting a regular , foods okay just the employees are very lazy.

This place is amazing. Every single time I go in I am get the same welcoming atmosphere and delicious smells from the kitchen. I'm addicted to the fried rice which isn't going to surprise you once you order it. AJ always gives me the best recommendations I could eat. I haven't left here disappointed or hungry ever and it'll be the same for you. This is a regular lunch stop during the work week for sure!

I needed to grab a quick bite to eat and Yelp steered me to this location. I was surprised to find a long line out of the door at 4pm (late lunch crowd,) but I now understand the $1.60-per-taco-madness. The restaurant itself is a self-serve no-frills kind of place. There are about five taco selections to choose from and than you can sit down and load up on toppings and some delicious sauces. As far as quick food goes...this was satisfying.  My vegetarian tacos did not blow me away but my 'meat eating' husband seemed very satisfied by his food. Would come again if I was in a hurry and on a budget.

This place is such a cool tavern with a nice piece of history! Looks kind of like stepping into a different world, very charming and small inside. Great stop to enjoy the the quite woodsy nook and get a couple of drinks. I believe they do barbecue in the summers, would love to come back for that!

I recently called to make reservations for a birthday dinner and I asked if they did anything for birthdays and the employee said that they do a free dessert, but if we brought in our own cake, we'd get charged a slicing fee. So I told him that we'd love a free dessert and he told me that he would put a note for our server.  We get to Sama Sama and order drinks and 6 dishes. The food was pretty good, but could've greatly increased the amount of heat in dishes. And a lot of flavors become monotonous. There were only 2 waiters and when our waiter was busy I had asked a female server for more water. She takes the empty bottle and never returns with water.  We order a dessert and tell the female waitress it was someone's birthday (and I assumed she knew that it was comped because that was what I was told). The most super tiny dessert comes out with a candle (and in my head I'm thinking it's comped..maybe that's why it's so small, the 4 of us was able to get a tiny scoop to taste). The bill comes and they charge us for the dessert. I tell the waitress and she says 'we only put a candle on top for birthdays.' This is one of my pet peeves..a restaurant employee assuring me that they do something special for a birthday and then something else happening. I didn't want to cause a scene at the restaurant so I bit my tongue but it just irked me..especially since it was more of a taste than actually being able to enjoy dessert.   Thinking about it now gets me irritated.

Haven't been here in two years but have to rate now that I am on Yelp.com. I like some of the authentic food here but what I saw blew my mind away here. I ordered a chili relleno and pork taco thing about 2 years ago and as I was waiting the cook at the stove dropped a spatula on the totally dirty greasy floor and just picked it up looked around and continued flipping meat. I asked for my money back. Some of the ladies making fresh tortillas just laughed at him. Zero stars even though Julia Childs liked this place.
This is second time we have almost got in accident because of customers blocking main road to get in They need to fix their parking issue before someone gets hurt. I will never go back.

My friend and I needed to eat before heading to church, and after reading some reviews, we decided to eat here.   Unfortunately my experience wasn't as great as other reviewers. My chilaquiles absolutely had no flavor. The beans didn't have much flavor either. I even put hot sauce on it, and it didn't help. But I was hungry, so I ate most of the chips. I left the beans.   When we were seated, the server asked if we wanted any drinks. I hadn't looked at the menu because he came right after we seated, but I knew they had to have orange juice on the menu. So he came back with a large glass of it. I realized there were different sizes, that I wish he specified before taking down my order. And when I ordered the chilaquiles, he started listing meats, starting with the shrimp.. I told him no meat, and he still continued to list the options.   The positives are that the portions are large, so in that sense you're getting your money's worth. And it's more affordable than other brunch places around it. The staff is very nice too, always coming by to check on us. But if I were to come back here, I wouldn't order the chilaquiles again.

Overpriced and just okay...if you are trying to spend a leg and an arm for a cup of black coffee THIS IS YOUR SPOT...  Okay in all seriousness, the reason this place has draw is because of the atmosphere, they create a safe space for people to work with fancy craft coffees. I ordered a black coffee and it cost about $3.50 (give or take with tip).  I also ordered the Harvest bagel for $7.  The bagel was pretty good, I couldn't taste the balsamic reduction, but the produce was fresh and I love Jack's bagels so overall it was a good food option.  The coffee is standard, I know some people are really picky about their coffee, I am not one of those people.  All coffee tastes relatively the same to me.    The interior of this coffee shop is a lot smaller than their location on upper state, but they do have an outdoor space that has some tables.  I don't like the outdoor space here very much because it is right next to a busy street and you are separated from the cars by a very thin wood wall.    I prefer the upper state location, but their aren't many coffee shops on the east side so this is a good option if you live on the east side and don't want to drive too far.

Had the cioppino.  It was very good but far from some of the best I have ever had.  The fish was fresh and the soup base was good.  Great service and very kid friendly (have a kids menu too).  Great place.

Sourdough crust heaven! I've been waiting for a crust like this for years. Which means I have not eaten pizza for 10 years. Topped beautifully with choices of meat, cheese veggies, sauces. Check out the menu- they offer complimentary sparkling water too. Best seats in the house are at the bar with a view of the pizza oven and the chefs producing such.

I first encountered this restaurant as a tern and it's still here, withstanding time and a devastating flood down the road. Today, we had the egg drop soup, almond chicken, cashew chicken and chicken with broccoli ( with chili's added). My favorite sauce was in the chicken with broccoli and I like the slices of chicken, Long or wide and flattened. My son's was the salty cashew chicken sauce with broken up chicken pieces. You can count on the traditional taste of all the dishes. Consistent. Take the egg rolls over the pot stickers. The tea was delicious too.

This is my favorite coffee shop in Santa Barbara. I've since moved away unfortunately but every time I return for a visit, I make a stop in here.  The coffee and espresso is great and I love the atmosphere. Especially the outside deck when it's sunny (which is all the time in SB). They've got a nice selection of regular black coffee including some organics and all the espresso regulars, latte, cappuccino, mocha, macchiato, americano, and iced coffees. They also sell pastries and muffins from local bakeries, as well as make sandwiches and smoothies.  There is free wi-fi internet of course. And private lot parking, which is somewhat limited to about 15 spaces. 2 bathrooms located outside, just grab the keys from the front counter.

Nice relaxing atmosphere, great service & very good food. Unfortunately they did not have a few items that I really wanted to try. Ended up getting the pulled pork sandwich which was quite good other than the bun was cold and the pork could have been hotter.  My sidekick Got the egg plant Parmesan. For her the egg plant was a bit under cooked but the flavors were very good. The fettuccine Alfredo on the side was probably the best I've ever had.

If you are at all a connoisseur of Japanese food, and even more the cuisine of the Kansai region, this is worth the trip to Santa Barbara from just about anywhere on the west coast. This is NOT a 'sushi place' so don't come here if that's what you're looking for - kaiseki (懐石) is a very traditional Japanese multi-course meal, which incorporates sushi but also other dishes like soups and grilled or braised fish.   Have been to Yoichi's several times at different times of the year, and each time at least one (sometimes all) of the courses caused us to stop, stare wide-eyed at each other, and ask how we were tasting something so impossibly unique and delicious. Yoichi has more than once prepared meals that were better than what I had during years traveling in Japan. The fish is unbelievably fresh, the taste and texture is absolutely perfect, and each meal is seasonal, inventive yet in keeping with traditional kaiseki, and flows perfectly together. The sake pairings are always spot on as well. How they don't yet have a Michelin star is beyond me.
The Best Chinese Cuisine in Santa Barbara! The food is fresh and delicious. The service is always quick and excellent. The owner is always there and he is always accommodating and very friendly.

I know the current trend is ramen ramen ramen, but this is ramen AND robata, and the skewers are what I'm all about here.  First visit I sat at the robata bar and picked out a selection of items. My favorites were the shiitake mushrooms and the chicken. They are thinly threaded on, so they cook up fast and are served just a couple minutes after my ticket reached them. Great option for a quick meal. The first chicken skewer I ordered was done *too* fast and was a little pink in the middle - I was sitting right at the bar so I pointed it out and bam the guy put a new one on the grill and it was perfect. The chicken was tender and sweet, with the right amount of smoke from the grill. I'm not aware of there being another robata bar in this town, and I got my order so promptly, so I'm a fan of this option.  Second visit I also got a couple skewers, again awesome with the shiitake. This time I got the ramen. I think the seasoning isn't as robust, so that people with more delicate taste buds can enjoy the ramen and those who need it kicked up can dive into the condiment basket, which has some chile sauces and togarashi. Maybe that's a compromise on their part, but I've found that people who usually have a hankering for ramen prefer it to be more strongly flavored.  There were three of us, and we got the tonkotsu, plain pork, and vegetarian ramen. The person who ordered vegetarian seemed the happiest of all, and he's the one who usually needs things the most spicy. The pork slices were tender, and I'm in the group that likes my pork slices like charshu pork, so I found them plain and didn't feel they needed to be there.   Ambiance was plain, music was loud. It gets kinda busy at lunch.   I'll be back for the robata.

Had the opportunity to sit in the private room with some friends for a rehearsal dinner a couple weeks ago.  I had only heard how great this place was and it definitely lived up to its reputation.  My meal started with one of the best crab cakes I have eaten.  According to the waiter the crab cake consists of at least 90% crab meat and it was evident in the taste.  This was nothing short of amazing.  The freshness was apparent and the cake was cooked to perfection.  Next up was the main course which I opted to go with the Wagyu Beef Filet which was served with local veggies bought from the farmers market.  The server told us the chef hits the farmers market three times a week to ensure the food is fresh and I can appreciate businesses supporting other local businesses.  The taste of the meal was off the charts.  The beef was cooked to a perfect medium rare and the flavor of the veggies was fabulous. Desert came in the form of a molten chocolate cake with a scoop of Mcconnell's (a local handmade ice cream shop) vanilla ice cream.  I don't think I can put words to how wonderful the desert was without doing it an injustice.  Needless to say I will be back to Bouchon next time I visit SB.

The best restaurant in Santa Barbara- hands. down. My partner and I got a waitlisted spot tonight, and we could not be more impressed. First, the quality of the food here was the only even remotely comparison to that of Japan. We felt like we were back in Shinjuku and Kyoto experiencing local Japanese cuisine. Second, you should go for the small-space, romantic vibe. The place is very small, which makes the experience all that more intimate. Finally, you must go for the service. As if we were in a high end restaurant in Japan, the staff were so knowledgeable about the food, friendly to us, and made the entire experience just feel good. A must-do for SB locals for a special occasion.

They kitchen was able to make the salmon pesto dairy free. It was so delicious!! My friend for the rosemary chicken and mashed potatoes and loved it. This was my first time getting food from the crocodile and I was pleased. I will definitely be going back!

I am always intrigued by hole in the wall joints...I know they can be hit or miss...but when you strike gold its so exciting. Loa Agaves unfortunately wasn't gold. The place was a bit dirty and pretty empty. The biggest downfall was that the man I ordered from at the counter was quite cold and rude.  On the plus side, they made my tacos to order so I knew they were fresh. And they did satisfy a street taco craving. Still, there is too much good Mexican food in LA to waste it on a place with bad service.

I've been to the Boathouse on a few occasions and it's wonderful that they are at Hendry's beach for anyone with a dog. The food is good. I tried the mushroom and spinach omelette, and the pork belly Benedict. I definitely recommend the pork belly Benedict. On another occasion, I've eaten a salad and a burger and they were solid. You probably can't go wrong with any of the food choices here.   The boathouse is an instant winner when you consider the location, sitting right at the oceanfront. It has a great vibe for one to hang out, have something good to eat for all types of eaters, and order as many drinks as one can handle. There is plenty of parking, an outdoor public restroom in addition to the restaurant's restroom, and plenty of shade in the outdoor seating area for a good time on any occasion. The outdoor seating area has a glass wall fixture built around the perimeter with slight openings throughout to allow guests to enjoy a gentle ocean breeze.

Came for the Ethopian but unfortunately they didn't have it on Fridays :(   I got the soup du jour as a side with the lunch special - forgot the name but it was tomato based with olive oil. Was surprised that it was a cold soup... I know it's meant to be cold but personally enjoy soups warm/hot so it was just eh. Flavor was also just okay, nothing special.  The lunch special was shrimp and tortellini. Portion was small so I wasn't full after both the soup and this main dish, but the flavor was really distinct from other tortellini I've had! Writing this review like 2 months late so I can't put my finger on it, but the dish was light and very comforting.  The area this restaurant is in is super cute, great vibes for outdoor dining.

Eating here is mostly for the nice view of the beach and set up outside. I wouldn't say there's anything very special about this place from what I ordered.   I ordered the crab cakes since it's their Signature Breakfast. But I felt like they lacked seasoning and fell a bit flat for me. But they were crispy and decent size. My friend got an omelete and thought it was decent.  There weren't that many menu items to choose from other than typical breakfast items so I don't know if there's anything I'd come back to try. Nothing bad here but pretty mediocre experience.

What an awesome experience m! I was looking for a place I can recommend to other people heading to Santa Barbara.  The service is top notch and the food... For starters we took the grilled romaine which was not so good, hot salad with beans some weird combination the waitress recommended.  The lasagne and gnocchi with shrimp were good and the highlight was the ravioli   For dessert we got served hot chocolate cake and some kind of pavlova with strawberry parfait both were very special and good.  You do need to make reservation few days in advance!

I'll keep this short and sweet: a really great restaurant in a historic hotel. From beginning to end this was an exceptional experience.   We like great food. We like great service. We like interesting locations. We like having a great time together, especially while celebrating special occasions. We like   Can't wait to eat here again!

Husband wanted Indian food, and I was craving  chilli momos. We had been to another Indian restaurant last time we in SB, which he liked and I didn't. Previously I had been to that restaurant myself before it had changed hands, which was good then. We read a couple other reviews of other Indian/Nepalse restaurants, on that other review site, and settle here. The place is very clean, and nice menus.  This is in the tourist, University catered to State street so it's a bit pricer. I waiter was very nice. I asked if they had chilli momos on the off chance it was an off the menu item. He said no, but I heard him go ask the chef anyway. So points for trying.  My husband and I order usually order the same item when we go to these types of restaurants, Thali. One veg, one non. Its a great way to for you try a few different dishes for a lower price.   Food came as it does in Nepal. brass plate with little metal dishes filled with the dishes. I got the curried chicken Thali, which came with rice, yellow dal, salad (cucumber, red onions, and tomatoes) the best I have so far as a thali, a vegetable korma, naan, dessert and tea. Ooooh it was good. the dal was just right, not too soupy and not to thick. The curry was also not to spicy or greasy.   When I go to these place, I can usually tell within the next 4 hours, how good  it really is. If I've become bloated that means too much salt, and then hard to sleep, if I've gone to dinner)  There were plenty of Indian folks eating there so you know that a good sign. Will return when I am back next year.

We ordered the mixed shawarma platter and it was amazing! The meat was flavorful and delicious without being too greasy and heavy, and the pitas and rice were great as well. The portion was perfect for our family of 5, and we had leftover hummus and tahini which we used for sandwiches the next day. The baklavas were a nice addition as well. Would definitely recommend, and will be trying this place in person once the pandemic is over!
Oyster shooters and drinks. Nuff said.
Always an ambrosia. Just had the bacon wrapped filet and if you have not looked closely, every vegetable has its own distinct flavor, the fresh baked bread, the filet superb.

I do like this place, but beware.. do not come here on a hungry stomach. You'll be spending a lot of money and leave still feeling hungry.  They price gouge for sure. ~$14 for two pieces of crostini with steak tartare on it? A few pieces of Hamachi was ~$17... it costs half the price, taste a lot better, and more fresh at a sushi restaurant.  A board with 3 little bits of cheese and 3 small helpings of meats were $34.  I was not impressed at all. Food was average, portions were too small. We spent about $50/pp and we all left feeling unsatisfied.  If you want to grab a drink and literally- a bite to eat... then this is the place for you. Otherwise I would not recommend it.

Persona is a really great place for make your own pizzas! I haven't been there in some time because I usually always end up going to pizza rev now for convenience but persona is delicious. I would switch it up sometimes and either make my own or go with the preselected margarita pizza. Both are super good and their ingredients are always super fresh. They definitely don't disappoint and the sizes of their pizzas are definitely good for one person.

Went back here again and all I can say is Pork Chop, Pork Chop, Pork Chop....get it. That's all there is to it. My wife got the salmon and loved it also! We asked why didn't come back more often, it's that good. Get the Pork Chop!
I stopped by for an order to go.  Veggie egg rolls were delicious.  Pork dumpling was very good.  I will go again.  Staff very nice!  About 20 minute wait but worth it.

I had $15.99 all you can eat ( 3 meats:  beef brisket, marinated chicken and pork. 4 side dishes.) The beef brisket is very thin and  tender. The marinated chicken and pork have nice flavor.  There are 3 different kinds of dipping sauces.  All three sauces were excellent. They pair really well with the brisket. Very ambient atmosphere. I will come back again!   PS: LA's all you can eat places are definitely better but you're in SB people, not Koreatown! Lower your expectations a little bit

I haven't tried their non-pastry food, but I can say Renaud's is by far the best place to get desserts. If you are ever stumped on what to give someone as a thank you or present, or to bring to a small gathering, Renaud's pastries will definitely impress.  They taste out of this world, are so intricately and flawlessly decorated from what I can see. These bakers are the real deal!  Such an extraordinary blessing and privilege to enjoy and I'll definitely be returning soon. Pictured is my most recent purchase.

Tried a drink and it was really good! It's called the citrus 2 & tastes amazing. Daisy was really helpful to me during my first time visiting a pressed juicery. She gave me samples of the juices I was interested in before I made a purchase. The juices are really healthy and delicious. I gave 4 stars because the prices are really high. 6$ for one bottle of juice although they have promotions where you can buy 6 for a smaller amount!   Overall I was satisfied & will be returning!

There's a great selection of hard to find Korean groceries here and the food is amazing. It's seriously like being let into your Korean friend's house for dinner. There's homemade quality but amazing taste. Come here and try it!

We stopped in for breakfast on the last day we were in town. I ordered the chorizo breakfast which came with chorizo and egg, a side of hash brown and your choice of corn or flour tortilla. I chose corn.  I requested my hash brown to be extra crispy which came out great but thought that the actual chorizo was a little more saltier than I expected. It was edible but I could only eat enough to fill two small corn tortillas so half of my breakfast was still on my plate. Which is just as well, I didn't need to polish off the whole plate anyway.   A strong 3 star spot for breakfast.

For the club- It is so difficult to rate clubs because they're clubs- and that means that they're full of weird individuals. This place was NO execution, however, we had a lot of fun. There was an extremely weird fashion show that was both terrible and tragic, that we could have done without, but other than that, it was a great club experience.  As far as clubs go, it's exactly what you think. If you're in your late 20s (like me) then this place reeks of spent youth and meat-market-esq quality. I loved the boys trying to 'freak' with me while in my 'bride to be' sash. Way to bring it back! Overall, its a club. It wasn't terrible. So BAM. I feel like that's a glowing review.

very close to state st.. easy to find. marina parking $6 (they dont validate) bathrooms are outside & so they arent squeaky clean. quoted an hr & half wait but we were seated in about 50 mins-65 mins. only about 10 tbls inside & maybe 10 on balcony. long bar. annoying pirate bartender in the downstairs bar/ waiting area. bloody mary is delicious . fried shrimp was delicious. big meaty & not greasy. crisp & mealy. cioppino was out of this world. mussels also were perfect. seafood kebob was also delish. 2 drinks & 2 entrees & 1 app= $66 before tip. service was attentive & food was quick to come.
Awesome tri tip! I requested rare and they delivered. Great value and very friendly staff. 10/10 recommend when looking for a quick bite.
Great breakfasts! Best corned beef hash in town. Really good coffee. Hope they make it

This little market is really convenient when my roommate and I want Korean food. Since Korean food is sort of hard to find in Santa Barbara, this place does the trick. It's a super cute grocery store that's run by a Korean man and his wife. Super good food and at a decent price. They also carry some Korean grocery items as well as some other ethnic food ingredients.

I had the tonkotsu ramen with pork shoulder, which was good but felt smaller than when I had it a couple years ago. Overall it was enough to fill me up! The pork shoulder was very soft, and the broth had a good amount of salt. I would want to come back to try the chicken karaage!

Stopped in with family at 1:30pm today. We were asked to sit down anywhere we wanted to. We wanted to sit outside as it was a beautiful day. There was only one table that was open outside so we sat down. Within 5 minutes a busser came to the table and with an angry voice said 'you sat down at a dirty table before it was cleaned, next time just tell me.' We just wanted to get out of peoples way by sitting and were a bit irritated of her attitude. As we were looking over the menu, we noticed that we had 30min before high tea would begin and had thought it ended by 2pm. We asked the manager if we could start early but were told we could not be served high tea until 2pm. A few minutes later our server came to our table and we informed him of our situation. He left to bring us waters and when he returned, he informed us the chef would serve high tea earlier for us. It was a very nice gesture, although we didn't end up being served our food until a few minutes after 2pm.
Authentic Mexican cuisine. Would love if they had better wine. But food makes up for it!

I put an order in for the berry cake for a birthday and it was amazing. Everyone was raving about the cake and not one person noticed that it was gluten free! As I picked up the cake, we got their lunch sandwiches and salads to go. They were just as amazing! Their food was delicious and their rosemary bread is to die for!   I'd recommend ordering in advance. We got there on a Saturday around 11:30am and the line was out the door! Luckily we called our orders in and we let a staff member know that we had a prepaid order so that we didn't have to wait in line. The staff was so kind and checked to make sure the cake looked good to me before boxing it up. We will absolutely be back!

Alessia came from a restauranteur family and brings decades of experience including time at Bouchon. The pastry counter is open at 7 but breakfast isn't served until 8. It's right across from the El Presido with a clean, classy interior.  Breakfast: We got the crab crepe, french toast, and granola yougurt for the kids. Everything was well-cooked and beautifully plated, especially the yogurt dish. Only feedback is slightly too much hollandaise on the crepes and the french toast was a little soggy on bottom. Otherwise high quality and well portioned.   We wanted to give the kids some solid food and ordered a croissant, it was so good we got up while waiting for our breakfast and got more pastries for later.  Pastries: 5 stars, the croissant is better than the one at Bouchon and Alexanders. It has an incredible outside crunchy layer and soft cloud-like inside. Cheddar bacon scone and kouign-amann also amazing, loved having these for a quality breakfast the next day.

Favorite Mexican food in town. Their quesadilla with carne asada is great! Tacos and salsas are good. Fun little spot on the Westside. I just moved down the street and tried it out. Definitely recommend. I wish they opened earlier than 6pm, but it works!

So far I've only had the eggplant parmesan, but I'm in love.  The portions are big, the prices low, and the staff nice.  We ordered for take-out and, upon entering the door for pick-up orders, were hit with the strong smell of delicious foods, mainly tomato, garlic, and onion.  Our food was hot, packed to the top of the containers, and fantastic.  The dining area is pleasant and small.  It's a casual establishment, which  makes you feel comfortable enough to loosen a belt buckle and settle in for good eats.

Well, about 2 and a half weeks ago I posted a review that was not very flattering.  The take out food we ordered did not meet expectations.  I was contacted by the owner via yelp and I was called by the manager (Nicole).  Nicole was extremely courteous and extended me a gift card for a future purchase.  Having been in the foodservice industry I know that on any given day, consistently is not always guaranteed.  We really like Joe's and we will go back.  I sincerely appreciate the professionalism and follow up.  We'll be back.

Went here a couple times many years ago and then several more times during my most recent trip to SB.  What can I say about this place? First off, this place has amazing seafood. Though SB is known for many places with good seafood, this is one of the best. And the fact that it is way off State Street means that it is mixed with a more local crowd. Though there is not HH at this location, the pricing of most of their food is pretty reasonable.  During my last trip, my favorite dishes were their calamari (as it just melts in your mouth) and their house salad with the famous garlic caesar.  Some of the fish dishes can be on the pricy end, but sticking to apps and some of the cheaper entrees, we were very satisfied.  One note, I remember their clam chowder being delicious, but didn't have that same experience this past time around. Don't know if it changes day to day, year to year, or if my tastebuds have changed, but I wasn't as impressed.  Overall, this place has a great ambiance and overlooks the harbor and shouldn't be missed if you are in SB.

Don't let looks deceive you!  Looks like an average Mexican restaurant.   In reality it's the best 'average Mexican restaurant' in Santa Barbara.   Here's what I mean.   There's a salsa bar (no big deal) -- the salsa chipotle, salsa quemada, and salsa tomatillo are all standouts. The marinated onions and peppers are five stars as well.   They have a happy hour (no big deal) -- happy hour lasts until 7p, which is super clutch for working adults like me! (Beer and taco combo happy hour are good deals)  They have daily specials (BIG deal)! -- These are weekly recurring specials. So you can reliably get your favorite torta for half price every Thursday! Torta Tuesday's and taco Tuesday are great!  Now to the meat of the review   They have Asada, Pastor, Carnitas, the list goes on (sounding familiar? No big deal amirite?) -- But they're all above average in flavor and dish composition.   My point is...don't come to Los Altos for amazing Mexican food. Pay four times as much at other popular Mexican restaurants in town for more noteworthy dishes.   BUT DO come to Los Altos for above average food, no waits, great service, and price points that won't break the bank!
Horrible customer service and no accountability.! No way will I ever eat there again!

Excuse me,  a bit hangry here. This is a disaster and a half!! The customer service isn't great, not attentive (early morning, not many customers/ tables). I asked for no butter on my food and they couldn't do the hashbrowns this way. So they brought me fruit that didn't look pleasant (not fresh) but appreciate them trying and over easy eggs. They messed up the egg order and gave me way over easy instead of over hard. They actually gave my dad the over hard when he asked for over easy. I'm not saying my plate shouldn't have been charged but it was a complete inedible disaster and that would have been a nice way to apologize. Here's a pic of my dads food. This is not a breakfast turkey patty, LOL, turkey hamburger. This is laughable at best to dine here unless you're adventurous and can (will) eat anything. :)

On the website it says the are open from  Monday thru Thursday 11:30-9:00pm. I tried to order food at 2pm and I was told that they were closed and not serving any food with out being able to tell me when they were going to open up for service.

This place has a good location.  Has plenty of parking and an indoor and outdoor seating option.  The food choices were delicious and the customer service was good.  Came here for dinner on Thursday evening.  It was not busy and had plenty of tables available.  This place is dog friendly, which is a plus.  Had our Golden Retriever with us and we ate outside.  Outside had heat lamps.  Recommend checking out this place if you are in Santa Barbara

Have heard about this place for years but have never been able to make it by. Paul, the manager, was the most respectful and kind guy I've seen in the industry in a while. Thank you to Frank from the Santa Barbara hotel group for recommending this to me. I will be back every other week when I come up for business. The hospitality is as top notch as you could give. Cheers Paul and Frank!

Stopped by for dinner on a Friday night of a holiday weekend with reservations.  Tried the pork chop, seabass and Brussels sprouts.  Pork chop was cooked perfectly with a juicy interior and nicely grilled exterior.  The flavor of the sauce was ok, with some sort of clove/herbal component.  The seabass was excellent.  It was served as a crudo, with interesting accompaniments.  My first time trying raw seabass...I'm a fan. My favorite dish were the Brussels sprouts which were nicely crisp with both a sweetness and hint of vinegar.  The desert we had was the praline ice cream, however the ice cream wasn't smooth and tasted as if it was over churned.
The hollandaise on the Eggs Benedict was pathetic. That would have been ok if it wasn't so expensive. It didn't help that the OJ was bitter. On the plus side my niece's breakfast burrito was good!

Food is down right delicious. Chips and salsa to start. I like how there's a bean and corn relish and a tomato-based salsa.  Ordered: chili-relleno and a chicken taco with rice and black beans (maybe you can get other kinds of beans, I dunno), steak torta, and our friend had a roasted vegetable salad topped with guacamole. Yum.  Superior service.

My husband and I decided to give this place a try, the street tacos are delicious! At $1.60 each, it's a great price for what you get, especially in Santa Barbara! I got the Taquito plate, the chicken was delicious and well seasoned, but the beans and rice had no flavor :( Salsa was delicious, but hot!
So delicious and such a great value! Not really a place you want to eat at, but can be picked up to go and on certain days they deliver to local tasting rooms. Highly recommend!

Jason - great bartender!  Made a great mock cosmo for me and he was right it's psychosomatic, all in my head that I need a drink, so it was perfect.  Loved the oysters, clam chowder soup and especially the Shrimp & Crab Louie salad.    We went out to celebrate a great art show opening:  Black and White at Jewish Center Gallery in Santa Barbara, very excited to show my work in Santa Barbara.  Great service and amazing views of the yachts and we caught a rainbow as well!
Short lines and good food here. Bloody Mary's with the crab claws were a huge hit and the crab cake Benes were equally amazing. I will come back!

After spending the afternoon on the urban wine trail thought I'd check out the Santa Barbara pier fishing scene.   This pier is cool bc you are allowed to physically park on the pier, cost: first 90min free, then $2.50/hr thereafter.    The area where fishing is allowed is the very end of the pier where anglers are packed.  Nearly everyone was bait fishing and crabbing.   I found an open spot and since I already had a 3/4 oz spoon rigged thought I'd give it a try before switching to bait.  After my first cast a kid to my right offered me some of his bait.  I accepted at first but then on my second cast I hooked a nice sized 12' mackerel.  Everyone was reeling in mackerel, often 3 at a time and filling their buckets.  I ended up getting 5 decent sized mackerel over about 45min.    The guy to my left was surprised to see me land so many fish without any bait.  Other crabbers were pulling in occasional rock and spider crab nothing too large though.    Overall this was one of the best pier fishing outings I've had.

Ginge Eats - favorite dish: tempura cauliflower   Pros -Uniquely delicious curry options (seafood yellow curry is killer!) -Seasonal dishes  -Fun tasting menu with pairings   Cons -Removed gol gappas from the menu   Great place for groups or having a fun date night dinner!
Simply delicious! Loved the food and atmosphere and enjoyed the great service. Was so happy to see this place doing so well after only being in the Public Market two weeks!
Great design, lighting, ambiance and more billionaires per capita than most places in town.

They're salads and sandwiches are amazing. Dark Sky's is amazing because the roast beef and horseradish are just put together so well. I would say just don't get the BLT because it is not worth the only. They're salads are great especially the Asian green salad. This place is great even though it is slightly pricey.

A combination of the margaritas, food and ambience make this place great. The only issue, which you see in all of the other reviews, is the service. It wasn't terrible, but it seems like they're under staffed. Do yourself the favor and wave your waiter/waitress down when you need them.  Will be back for sure.

For me, this is the best happy hour in town...but I'm not talking about your traditional happy your. Besides the M-F standard time slot, Arch offers late night happy hour from 9-12pm as well as all day Sunday. I would highly recommend a late night dinner at the bar. The happy hour menu items are very affordable and very high quality...no nachos, wings or fried stuff here. Our favorites are the stilton, honey and truffle oil grilled cheese (for only $4) and the homemade guacamole. House wine and well drinks are $3, margaritas are $4, and house martinis are $5. The bar area is very small, so go early on a weekday or try the late night happy hour for the best seating options.
Great sandwich selections.  Would recommend more poultry or variations from time to time.  The potato chip supplier should be changed.

Great menu. Loved all the apps we ordered. Waiter was very formal and astute. The outside patio is very elegant and perfect for wine in the evening by the seaside. They have complimentary valet service as well.

I love this place! I always take friends here for brunch when they come visit me. This is probably my most favorite cafe/restaurant in all Santa Barbara.   My favorites are: ***Almond croissant*** everyone is raving about it. They weren't kidding, Order It! It is absolutely amazing. I cannot come here without ordering one. No where else makes it better than Renauds.  Madame croque - super good, this one of my favorite orders. I usually get it with ham. There's an option to request to have it with mushrooms instead.  Fried Egg sandwich - this is my fave! They only serve it for breakfast (until 11 am). They use the same thick slice of bread as the madame croque   Chicken w/grilled onion sandwich - this is tasty, you can't go wrong with ordering this.  You can request the kind of bread you want.  There's a good selection of macaroons. I think the salted caramel was my favorite

I absolutely LOVE this place. It is my all time favorite to get a casamigos Paloma !  I have such a hard time getting a perfect Paloma anywhere and Lure has the best one. First off, they use CASAMIGOS which is the best tequila in my book, and they know how to perfectly mix the cocktail to be the best in town.   WE came here last night and had a scrumptious dinner. I got a lobster tail, which was so satisfying in flavor and in presentation. I got the rice pilaf and spinach as my sides and I was extremely pleased with my selections. My brother got the baseball steak, fries and Brussel sprouts. I tired his steak and it was delicious, his Brussel sprouts did lack a bit of taste and they were dry but he still enjoyed his dinner. My husband enjoyed an alaskan halibut, with a Caesar salad and asparagus.  And to end the night we all shred the hot (not so hot) fudge Sunday, even though it was delicious, I do wish that they would have put more fudge in our dessert.   regardless, Lure is my all time favorite spot hands down

To this day I'm still confused about the Wine Cask. There's a couple of different and separate elements to the place - I came to eat in the restaurant, but despite it being a Saturday night, it appeared as if the bar was the only part that was open. As the menu differs from room to room, it was disappointing, but somehow I managed to find reason to live.  So it came down to the Cask Burger, which was admittedly fairly standard in ingredients but very well prepared otherwise.   As nice as it was, the thing that sticks out to me most is the service, which wasn't particularly attentive. As someone who rarely brings up the subject in reviews, I would be remiss without mentioning the amount of trouble it took to get the bartender's attention even when it wasn't that busy. And by the time he started focusing on the small group who was ordering bottles, I could understand why he might start playing for a better tip from the big spenders, though I wasn't happy that I had to call an audible with the check, swapping out my card for cash when I realized it would get me out of there sooner.

Protip #1 avoid on summer weekends at all costs  Protip#2 perfect for early morning walk, watch the fishing folks set up, enjoy the ocean view without hoards crushing you  Protip #3 Sea Center opens really early for an attraction and looked very cool (The Santa Barbara Museum of Natural History Sea Center)  Protip #4 keep a sharp eye out over the side as you walk out for fabulous sand sculptures  Protip #5 ran out of time, but there was a pretty sweet looking wine tasting place on the second floor right next to the sea center. I'm sure the views would be fab. (Conway Family)

I love this place. I work in the area and come in from time to time for a soy vanilla latte. The staff is so friendly and started remembering my name shortly after my first few visits. I've been in during peak hours and not so busy ones, and never have to wait long for my drink. I attribute this to the efficient and effective way the staff works with each other, as well as their ability to form relationships with their customers. I've seen staff recognize a customer as soon as they walk in the door, or park in the lot, and start making their drink before they even reach the counter.  I've always had a pleasant experience at this location and highly recommend it.

If you're in Santa Barbara you have to enjoy a meal here. It's one of my favorite restaurants of all time. We returned this past weekend and it was amazing as always. I wish we had something like this restaurant in LA. It's probably a good thing they don't because I'd be broke and obese.

We had dinner here before heading home for the day. The staff were some of the friendliest bunch of people I have encountered at a restaurant in a long time! They were all so happy and genuinely seemed to care about their customers.  As soon as we sat down they brought us mini jalapeno corn and banana walnut muffins with butter. They were so good! Kicking myself now that we didn't order some to bring home!  We started with the Cajun Popcorn, crawfish tails in a buttermilk batter with cream sherry dipping sauce. These were really good! I did expect them to be crispier, they were more pillowy. The smaller pieces were better, not as much dough. We asked for lemon to squeeze on them cause they definitely needed some acid. Portion was massive, we didn't even finish half of it.  For dinner he had the Chicken Tchoupitoulas, a juicy chicken breast with a creamy tarragon sauce on top. The sauce was very unique, tasted one way when you first ate it, and then finished with a strong sherry flavor. We both really liked his chicken, it was cooked perfectly and not dried out at all.  I thought their pastas looked amazing, but decided to get the Redfish since they said they happened to have it that day and they don't always. They fly it in from Louisiana. My fish did not disappoint, it was some of the best fish I have had at a restaurant. Very mild flavor, super moist and flaky, blackened to perfection with crispy edges, served with a garlic butter sauce. I loved my fish!   The sides left something to be desired though. Potatoes were just plain boiled red potatoes, not much seasoning. Veggies were criss cut carrots and squash, slightly undercooked and covered in lots of dill. If I were to dine here again, I would see if I could get a different side.   This was a good dinner and we left full, neither of us finished our meals. I would definitely eat here again and try a pasta, the table next to us got a few different ones and I was jealous!
Dont be fooled by half off like we were.  They are doubling the price on the simple rolls that are normally $4-5 elsewhere.  Plus the portion is small!

Big anniversary dinner w/friends in a party of 5. Bar service was excellent. Dining room service not so much. Waitress was nice enough but preoccupied...forgot our bread, forget plates, forgot to open the champagne, forgot the drawn butter (never did get that), she disappeared. As others have said, it's a team concept so you pay a couple hundred dollars for 3 different waiters to run up to your table calling out the name of the dishes since they have no idea who ordered what. We may have to give Lure a break a bit.  But believe me, they won't miss us. The place was hopping. Once we got our food its still a hard 7.5/10; grilled oysters 10/10.

I went to the Bistro at Bacara Resort tonight and I cannot recommend it enough. I came with my family who was visiting out of town for my college graduation from UCSB and it could not have gone better. Our server Alex was attentive, friendly, and extremely professional. He anticipated our every need and made my family and myself have a wonderful night. The food was excellent and the service was impeccable. The manager Shawn also frequented our table and made sure everything was going smoothly and really went out of his way to make sure our time there was the best it could be. I felt extremely taken care of and my family couldn't stop raving about it. Will be back and recommend to everyone. So glad I chose to celebrate my graduation here, great experience. :)

We were looking for the best eggs Benedict in SB. Yelp listed this one as number one. We were very disappointed. Have to say ingredients were fresh eggs cooked perfectly. Guess I am just used to the original with English muffin and creamy White sauce.  Price was very high. People nice and friendly.

My new favorite place on State! For a table of three, we shared the Crispy Duck Salad, Signature Wings, and Balinese Grilled Short Ribs. The salad and wings were out of this world!! I have been raving about this place since I tried it, and my friends agree that Sama Sama Kitchen is worth the price!   Dining in was an okay experience, although the tables felt a little too close together for comfort. I would for sure order takeout for a nice night in or before a night out (post covid of course!!)

This is an updated review on our 4th visit. This was on February 11 at 7:00 pm. I made reservations two weeks before. Because of the rainy weather I specifically asked for indoor seating in the main dining room which has a spectacular view. Now a little more background, This was the last weekend of the Santa Barbara film festival so I imagine a lot of VIPs were in town. As we were driving up it started to sprinkle. When I went to the front desk they told me that I would have to wait as the main dining room was filled. I told them I made a reservation for indoor seating. They looked ip my reservation and offered seating in the fireside room. It has very little view. It was so obvious what happened the A listers noticed the rain and changed their outdoor seating to indoor. So rather then shuttling them to the fireside room they chose to move me. No apologies were made. After we were served our food I heard another waiter mentioned the specials for the evening, one of them was Osso buco. Unfortunately our server neglected to mention any of the specials. Not sure if I will be returning
This place is close to my house so I order to go a lot and everything is reasonably priced and delicious! Don't skip on the bucket of fries!

My friend and I decided to come eat here for the second time the other weekend and we were really excited since this is definitely the perfect place to go to on a Saturday night.  I ordered the Kimchi fried rice and my friend ordered the grilled cheese sandwich.  The food was SO GOOD, kimchi fried rice, perfect amount of spiciness.  The people working there were also super nice to my friend and I: they made conversation, made us laugh, and answered any questions that came up.  The awesome thing about this place is its hours, especially on the weekend since it closes at 2:30am.  Definitely recommend coming to this place for a nice dinner or after a long night at the bars.  Everyone gets along with each other inside and it's just a very fun atmosphere to be surrounded in!!
Sooooo much better than the previous operation!!! Go check out the new operation, well worth it for the ayce menu! Love this place, a lot!!

First I have to say that I come from Hawaii and I have been missing some good Japanese food not just Sushi/sashimi.  Let me get my criticism out of the way the sashimi/sushi was cut way to thick BUT frickin yum it was (we eat poke so no worries we like chunks of raw fish with shoyu, yeah baby)  Other then that the service was really good the young woman taking care of us was patient and went with the flow of our table where we were ordering one thing at a time and laughing our butts off (we work together), The Unagai was delish and the fish was really fresh, we had one of the sushi/sashimi platters and some rolls, really good quality.  And they have Asahi beer on tap, wow, really tasty.  And the Miso soup was spot on.  This place ranks up there for unpretentious Japanese food.  I loved the setting reminds me of a place called Irifune's in Honolulu, but that's another review.  Oh and they are open till MN.

I couldn't walk by Sandbar today without thinking about my amazing weekend! We got dinner and champagne bottle service for my friends 21st. A huge thanks to Cassidy, our bottle service queen, and our server, because we had a great time. The only thing better than the customer service was the look on our birthday girls face when the sparklers came out. Will be back soon! :)
Great and convenient store located in my neighborhood!

Awesome surprise. We hadn't planned to eat here but it turned out to be our best experience in SB second only to Buchon. Our server Jazz was very helpful and knowledgable in helping us make menu choices. The cocktails were outstanding, and the food was very impressive. Will come back.

Updating my review from the last time I was here. I believe I had mentioned working here many years ago. I went tonight not expecting much and I had a waaay better experience. Food was amazing, service was not amazing in the sense that we waited a while for our waitress a few times but she was very nice. The new outdoor patio was completely gorgeous and to top it off the owner came to chat with me at the end of my meal. What a nice extra touch. I will be coming more often now.

We've eaten here several times now and they've always been great.  You won't find any exotic dishes here (ie, sea urchin & mussel shooters) but they pull off the classics so well!  It's a wonderful combination of excellent food and user-friendly menu.  So, a great place to bring your grandparents or your kids.  Or co-workers.  Very crowd-pleasing.
I really like this place. It's a good place to go for reasonably priced and consistently good quality sushi. There are lots of options. I love the Mexican Roll and their yellowtail dishes.

I have only one thing to say: Lobster Bisque covered in puff pastry!!! Need I say more? Well, ok yes! As if their Bisque couldn't be any more delicious, fresh, creamy and top notch delicious?! No, they have to bake it with a puff pastry shell on top to just to take it to the next level! Only thing that I would add to this piece of masterpiece is a dash of sherry at the end. (But as a foodie/chef I have to put my 2 cents in- corporate take note)  So if for no other reason, their Lobster Bisque is a must, must, must try. Oh and the rest of their menu is good too. Cioppino ? Sea bass? Grilled corn? Served w a  fresh and warm crusty sourdough?Yes, smiley faces all around. Plus they have a decent HH too. Pretty cool industrial ambiance as well.   Now go get your Bisque fix and thank me later! Sea u there :)))
Great location, good staff, decent food.  Good for families.  Indoor and outdoor seating.  Recommended.

I sat in the bar downstairs and had a nice cold (NA) beer and the fried shrimp platter and oh my goodness was it good! The batter on the shrimp was just absolutely delicious and the fries and the coleslaw were perfecto (and I almost never like anyone's coleslaw but mine). I wish I had room for the clam chowder too but everything that came out of the kitchen looked great. The server/bartender was rocking it as the place was packing out. I can't wait to go back and get more delicious seafood. Go! Enjoy!

It's supposed to be a 'cool and hip' place, with the name to match but the food, taste and service lacks substance for me. I was underwhelmed. Meso-meh. Chipotle meets trendy sushi/noodle/asian joint. I see the appeal, just wasn't for me.

If you're reading this, you probably already know this is one of the best restaurants in SB so you can expect great food, lovely ambiance, fantastic wine selections, and excellent service.   What you may not know is that the owner of the restaurant, Mitchell owns 2 other top-notched fine dining restaurants in town - Seagrass and the soon-to-be-reopened Wine Cask. You may know that the restaurant business is finicky and difficult to survive but he managed to keep growing in this competitive little town. This is very telling of Mitchell's business acumen and his craft in keeping fine dining alive during these harsh economical times.  When you dine at Bouchon, you should take advantage of what fine dining restaurants have the luxury to offer - excellent service. Ask your waiter/waitress what is the most popular dish, what is the chef eating, what is Mitchell's pick, where they buy their food. Ask away, they were trained to be knowledgeable about their food and wine selections. When the food arrives, don't just eat to taste but smell and look at how fresh the ingredients are.  You will notice the composition of the plate is thoughtful and proud. Prepare to spend at least 2 hours there. Take your time to enjoy your food and the conversation with your company. Zip a little wine in between. Try not to finish everything so you will have room for desserts and leftovers for the next day which are all oh sooo good.

I'm seriously, you guys. I don't understand all the 5-star reviews for this place. Yes, the service here is very friendly, but the sandwiches are only okay. Honestly, the sandwiches are made of cheap deli meat (and not that much), and AMERICAN CHEESE! On top of that, it was only slightly cheap. Frankly, I would rather eat at Chipotle or buy a grocery store sandwich.

3.5 stars.   My last visit here was over 15 years ago and thankfully not much has changed at this long-time restaurant on the mesa. The cozy and hip vibe here has always been a terrific alternative to the crowded touristy scene downtown and while the tapas are hit-and-miss (not to mention pricey), the service is solid and the ambience can't be beat. Alcazar is small and intimate, with low lighting and music along with classic films playing silently on the back wall; Fellini's La Dolce Vita was on during our meal.  My family and I were in the mood for a light dinner and this was the perfect spot. We started with the Date with a Pig (bacon wrapped dates, wildflower honey, blue cheese), which were delicious, if a bit sweet (I prefer this dish to have more smokiness from the bacon). The Manchego y Membrillo (manchego, pressed guava, olive oil) was an interesting combination of light cheese and fruit; my wife didn't care for it, but our daughter and I liked it. The Carne Con Champiñones (slow cooked sirloin tip, Spanish sherry, butter, sautéed mushrooms, onion, jack cheese, toasted bread served pintxo style) was rich and enjoyable and the Sweet Potato Fries with spicy aioli were perfectly cooked and delicious.   Unfortunately, the Gambas Alcazar (sautéed tiger prawns, arugula, lemon garlic butter) was bland and entirely forgettable. Our daughter also had the Albondigas (served med-rare sustainably sourced meatballs, Roma tomato purée, manchego, parsley), which she thought was merely okay.  Overall, the food was good, not great, but the service, ambience, and location were top-notch. I wouldn't come here when I was starving or on a budget, but as a place for light bites, a cocktail or two, or a reason to get away from downtown, Alcazar is a fine choice in Santa Barbara.   Notes: I didn't learn this until after dinner, but Alcazar is home to the 'Official Drink of Santa Barbara,' the Ginspiration Point (Cutler's gin and Nostrum pineapple turmeric ginger shrub, along with Bénédictine, lime juice, Chartreuse, and an egg white). They also have a sister tapas restaurant, Milk & Honey, which opened downtown in 2007.
This place is pretty good! Fresh ingredients! The space is cozy, it looks like it was a house before it was turned into a cafe.

Small deli within the open mall....with really great sandwiches....had one with apricot preserves that was simply delicious.  Big portions (shared mine).  The guys behind the counter were very friendly and helpful with the menu.  You can sit outside and people watch...which is one of my favorite past time activities.

This place was awesome! Dinner was absolutely amazing and we tried a little bit of everything. The staff was very friendly and had good insight on each beer, wine and food choice. The owner has rotating artist displaying  their pieces on the wall throughout the restaurant. We highly recommend this place

Save the jalapenos, guys !!  The 'Kickin' Cowboy' salad is fantastic and spicy and full of flavor (DO add the chicken ...) but really requires only about HALF the jalapenos you usually put on it. Every time I get it, I LOVE it, but half those peppers go in the trash (and I generally like spicy food !) Save a few cents and reduce your weekly jalapeno order ... keep everything else the same.   Love your ENTIRE restaurant and the burgers are also amazing. Can't go wrong at Kyle's.

Finney's Crafthouse has it going on.   My group of friends descended upon Finney's Crafthouse during an impromptu State Street crawl that ended at the beach in Santa Barbara. This is an interesting 'corporate' looking Gastro that turns out to be a small local chain and from what I was told is beloved by locals and tourists alike. I actually went to college at UCSB and had not heard of Finney's Crafthouse before so I assume they are rather new.   There is a full kitchen but we came for the libations and Finney's has a notable list of Craft brew both on drought and bottles/canned. The prices are a bit outrageous but welcome to DTSB, right? Even with the inflated prices everyone in my party thoroughly enjoyed themselves so I guess you get what you pay for and the servers were extremely jovial. I can't complain about my visit to Finney's Crafthouse and would advise anyone on a similar crawl to make the stop.

I've been to Santa Barbara countless times. Seriously, literally, countless (not even including the two months that I lived there). And I've never really felt inspired to try Something's Fishy. I walk by it on State Street day after day and nothing really calls me in.   Well this time, with a rumbly tummy and little motivation to walk any farther than the door in front of me, I was seated at the bar before I even knew what happened. The waitstaff was a little difficult to flag down and we were seated at the farthest end of the sushi bar where it sort of tapers off. Not a lot of chef contact happening.  frown face.  We ordered three rolls, salmon sushi and unagi. Everything was made super fast and put all onto one big plate. I know this is a weird issue but I don't like all my rolls on one big plate. It makes it hard for me to distinguish what I'm eating. Just a weird pet peeve.  Not only did the big plate keep me from separating the rolls but the rolls themselves were just plain. Everything was sort of the same. And extremely mediocre. The only thing that really stuck out to me as notably delicious was the unagi. I was tempted to order another one it was so good.  Disregarding the nomnom unagi this entire experience was shockingly blase. Next time, I'll kick myself in the pants and walk a few doors down to any other restaurant on State St.
Great variety of fish.  Addictive grilled oysters. Love the crab cake salad.

We came here for the first time yesterday. There seems to have been a lot of hype, and quite a few people waiting for this place to open.  Let me save you some agony.  For a joint with 'Bread' in the name, the bread I got on my panini was horrible!  Spongy, tasteless, not fresh tasting.    I got a roast turkey and artichoke panini, ....bland, un-inspiring, plated with absolutely no care whatsoever.    There were so many workers there buzzing around the sitting area, at any given moment, you get the feeling you're in a busy cafeteria, the interior is designed much like a typical fast food chain, just not inviting, a place you can't wait to get out of!  Fresco is just a block away, don't bother with this place, I'll save you the agony
Flavourful and perfectly cooked vegan taco. Very creative ingredients put together so well. There was an over abundance of staff but it's a new place.
Can't believe I've missed out on this place for years. Had it for our first time 10 days ago and haven't missed a day since. The food is excellent and the healthiest food we've had here in SB.

Review for Cotton Candy Martini:  Mission: Cotton Candy Martini and it was accomplished We were able to drink our drink and get out of there before it got super busy.
