Definitely a swanky place on a Saturday night, everyone was dressed up clubbing style. The inside is more for dinner, but outside is like a lounge with its own bartender. There were good amount of standing room, but sitting wise you might have to come here early and grab a seat.   Overall defintiely a cool place to hang out and grab a drink.

All of the sushi and beef are extremely fresh and delectable. The Korean cauliflower is to die for and drinks are well made. I would recommend the Santa Barbara Sling or the Scorpion Shell  (To Share). Views are incredible and they even have a fireplace on the second floor deck! Excellent spot for lunch with a wide variety on the menu! Complimentary Valet Parking in the back of the restaurant as well! Great spot for work lunches, private groups, or a romantic date out. Finally SB has sushi on the water!

The food here is fantastic and there is plenty of documentation in other reviews. My only gripe is that the restaurant clusters too many things in one area, causing a crowd and frankly, an annoyance, to do the following: 1. Get drinks 2. Get salsa 3. Use the bathrooms 4. Get utensils  All four of these things are in the same small space to the right of the cashier/order area, which results in a large cluster of people in a narrow area.  Los Agaves  - please either have table service or move some of these areas around. It's also surprising that you have single use bathrooms for so large a restaurant.

Best breakfast burrito in SB, by far. Everything about it is delicious. I especially like the seasoned potatoes and the salsa you get with it. Not to mention, it has a good ratio of everything in it. Nothing over powers. I get one of these babies at lease once a week, and I have never had a bad experience, it is consistently delicious, not to mention a great deal compared to other breakfast burritos in the area.
Always my favorite spot in Santa Barbara for prime rib and clam chowder.  GreT spot great staff and great food
Love it.  Awesome Beer & Brats.   No confusion over what this place is or is not. They do Beer and pub food, and they do an awesome job*
Amazing food and drinks the service is off the charts. Keep the amazing job, we need a bar like yours.
Just ok.  Food was average, at best and the service was unusually slow.  Location and outside patio were great.  A bit disappointing as our group expected more from a Montesano restaurant

Came here once we arrived in Santa Barbara! Love the area this restaurant is around because it is next to the waters and was just so pleasant and relaxing! We ordered to-go and sat on the picnic tables nearby instead of waiting over an hour for seats inside. We had:  - lobster bisque - clam chowder - oysters - lobster sandwich - ceviche  My favorite was the lobster bisque in bread bowl - though the bread wasn't the best, the soup definitely made up for it. The oysters were decent and everything else didn't stand out to me.

Love this place. My husband had been a few times with coworkers and mentioned that every time he's gone with people everyone loved their food.   To start we ordered hummus and pita. The hummus was delicious, super garlicky and lemony.  I would literally put that hummus on everything if I could.   I ordered the green falafel wrap. I loved the flavor explosion the green apple slaw provided. The falafel is flavorful and crispy    The husband ordered the peanut butter and jelly burger. It's good but I personally think the peanut butter dominated the whole burger and you couldn't really taste any of the flavors of the meat or jelly. He loved it though.    I liked that you ordered and paid at the bar, it made the atmosphere super laid back and chill. If you are looking for good Mediterranean you'll be happy eating here.
delicious and creative menu! nice hidden gem for sure. i thoroughly enjoyed the french toast and fried chicken dish! they serve handlebar coffee as well which is great.

The food was okay...... but for the price you would expect table service and for the food to be phenomenal. Not the case. The staff were nice but seems like the owner is attempting to run the place super short staff as there were only 2 servers for the whole restaurant. Doubt we will be back.

I didnt realize to get good Mexican food in SB you have to go to the restaurants not the stands. I'm really impressed with the authentic flavor and style of cooking here. I HAVE GOT TO come back and I HAVE GOT TO bring more people.

I came with my mom to Mesa Verde. The dishes were good, the atmosphere is lovely. However, the prices are a bit high for the portion sizes. Overall worth a try if you are looking for a vegan option in SB.

Used to be a big fan of this place, but the service has gone downhill with the change of ownership and the prices have gone up. Definitely better places to go for breakfast in town. Shame though, as the bussers are excellent but the servers can be be better.
Burgers and fries better than In/Out fast food places.  Cobb salads too!  Nice and clean interior and patio dinning with potted native plants.  Fast food CEOs could learn something here.
Love this food! You can get the basic mexican food options with some fun, adventurous options too. Definitely recommend for groups and picky eaters.

Have been meaning to eat here for years and finally did. Have to say, it was a huge disappointment. Why? The food (chiles rellenos plate and shrimp caldo) was just average, and yet each dish cost $14.99! The shrimp were small and lackluster, the chiles greasy and bland. The beans and rice were afterthoughts. The atmosphere? Outdoor tables next to sidewalk and Milpas traffic. I mean, all of that is fine and acceptable for casual order at counter food, but the prices don't reflect that. For the cost, I'd expect really delicious and carefully prepared food and better setting and service. Service was just, whatever, without a smile or warmth from both the cashier and the cook who brought out food. I would never return. Instead, I'd go to Lito's, Los Agaves, Cuernavaca, even La Super Rica for goodness sake.
Lovely local sandwich shop that's the heartbeat of the upper village ! They also served all the first responders during the tragic 1/9 debris flow 10/10

I have been back to this place and I have been treated beautifully. Their pizzas are very good, be sure to order them thin and lite on sauce if thats your preference, otherwise they just come out standard. Great place

Have been here a couple times for brunch buffet and it is always delicious!  They usually have some good cauliflower and veggie options which is a nice substitute for rice.  Came for dinner and everything was delicious!   I'd request the vindaloo be a bit spicier next time-I think it was toned down for a more western palate.  But overall food was awesome! Service was great too.  They are very welcoming and accommodating.  And get the begain bartha (eggplant)!  So yummy.

Wow the tacos!! Unmatched! And the margaritas were amazing! They brought out chips and salsa and the space is huge. There is a heated patio and also a nice date-night-esque interior seating area. It is upscale in my opinion but still down home Mexican. It is usually packed around prime dinner time.

So happy we ended up here on a cold, rainy December day where you either stay in hotel or see if you can find Oprah's Montecito compound. The hostess was super sweet and took my number to call once somebody, anybody left their seat. It was a perfect cozy place to linger on such a day. My husband and I dined here with our 5 year old son. The duo of dips was a fantastic warm up - a pimento style cheese dip coupled with guacamole and WARM chips. Nice touch on the kitchen drawer warmers. The real star of the show is Dings Chicken sandwich.  We decided we'd be happy to have it a few times a week for... forever.  The cocktails are good, pricey like everywhere you want to hang out and be served. Bees Knees and Marg are winners. Only negative comment: The marg with campari and mint should be replaced.  It was like drinking grapefruit juice after brushing your teeth. Luckily our server happily replaced it. I look forward to going here again.
Good food Great Service.  Our server Chelse was so good. Friendly,  positive and attentive.

I don't eat Cajun food often and I was blown away at the Palace Grill. First time experience:  Friends and I ordered popcorn crawfish as the appetizer. It was crispy and pretty yummy. The muffins they gave had interesting flavors- not all were my cup of tea though.   For the entree, I got the jambalaya pasta. It was huge and FULL of shrimp, chicken, etc. the sauce was amazing!! It wasn't as spicy as I wanted but that was fine. I couldn't stop eating and wanted more, actually.   The pricing is a tiny bit expensive but I think it's worth it for the seafood. Service was great. I look forward to coming here again the next time I'm craving Cajun seafood. :)

I like to write at coffee shops. I often spend all day there. Thus, Goleta Coffee Company, with WiFi, comfy booths, and food, SHOULD work for me.  But there are 3 key problems: 1. The WiFi is TERRIBLE. I've tried 3 different times. It just doesn't work well. 2. There must be a school nearby because there are dozens of very loud high school kids there often. (I guess that the school has open campus because at lunch and during particular hours (study hall?) there are so many kids there. After school is worse though. In groups of 5-8, they take over tables and booths and talk loudly. You can tell that the staff is annoyed (I overheard a barista telling one kid that last time that they were there, they left the water running in the bathroom.) Yet, the staff does nothing about it. 3. There is no air flow.  The food is fine - not great, but fine. The coffee is fine.   As others have mentioned, the atmosphere is sort of indie, but the bathroom is a little gross.  I wouldn't consider this for a regular work spot. It is too bad because the booths are nice and there are decent power strips.  They close at 4, so beware!

Love, love, love this new spot which is a very welcome addition to SB's eateries! The design and space are lovely- modern and clean design. The food is fantastic. I have had their breakfast sandwiches, egg polenta dish and their vegetable green soup and butter lettuce salad - all excellent. Their baked goods are the best. This cafe would be a popular one in any big city and we are lucky to have it in SB!
Had a spectacular first night out at this charming location. I very much enjoyed a great meal, my favorite wine, and all by a perfectly placed fireplace. I know I will be returning on my next trip

The sushi and sashimi are surprisingly fresh and delicious. My recommendations are the sashimi entrée or the chirashi don entrée. Go with chirashi don if you want the vinegar-infused sushi rice (really good); otherwise, go with the sashimi if you want a little more variety (raw scallops are on point). Can't go wrong with their rolls either; we shared two amongst the table and were pleased by both. Go for the special rolls for a more unique take.    My family's only complaint was that the pork tonkatsu had too much batter so it was over crispy and broke off while getting with chopstick. The batter can definitely can be lighter, true to more authentic fried Japanese meats.   Overall, my family and I were pleasantly surprised by the fresh, clean restaurant, and understated ambiance. And, just a block away from State Street. Would definitely come again.

Brophy's New England Clam Chowder ($7.74): cute seafood spot by the harbor! The clam chowder cup was yummy and made a perfect midday snack. I got an outside seat with great views and a lot of people watching. I need to come back to experience a full meal!

I see baskets of muffins and bowls of gumbo Paintings on the wall, artists nicely draw,  And I think to myself what a wonderful world!  The moment I walk into the Palace Grill, I'm instantly transported to the city of New Orleans. I had the opportunity to visit New Orleans a few years ago and must say the environment is quite similar to New Orleans. The decorations, especially the lamp pole with beaded necklaces hanging off of it, are exceptional and give off the New Orleans vibes. The dim lighting and red neon lights give off a nice glow to set the mood for the hourly sing-alongs.   The only thing they're missing are beignets as a starter (which is listed under the desserts section), and are substituted for complimentary cornbread muffins. I'm not complaining because those muffins are delicious! I have a bit of a sweet tooth so my favorites are the apricot and the banana nut muffins.   I started off with a bowl of SEAFOOD GUMBO, which is a classic in Lousiana. They offer two different kinds of gumbos, including the gumbo ya ya and seafood gumbo. The difference is that the gumbo ya ya has chicken and andouille sausage, and the seafood gumbo contains seafood. I was craving seafood that day so I ordered the seafood gumbo. The soup was exactly what I expected: rich, thick, and creamy. Yum!   For the main dish, I ordered one of their most popular pasta dishes, the JAMBALAYA PASTA. The pasta dish included bits of chicken, shrimp, and andouille sausage all mixed in with a tomato creole sauce and topped with a sprinkle of spice and green onions. I enjoyed the flavors and spices of this dish since it contained penne pasta and not rice, which is traditional. I also ordered the blackened FILET MIGNON. There's an option to choose if you want the steak blackened or grilled. The steak was tender, but it was a bit overcooked since I enjoy my steak medium rare. Other than that, the food was quite tasty!   Furthermore, the service was great! The server was patient and took the time to explain and point out what was inside each muffin. The most unique concept of the restaurant are the hourly sing-alongs! The servers pass out a songbook to each table, walk around with a glass, clink glasses with each other and other customers, and we all have a jolly good time!
Found this hidden gem from the axxess card! So delicious and wonderful ambiance!! Will definitely come back again!!!! Loved my golden beet salad and the green bowls are a must have!!
Not impressed with the ahi poke. Didn't taste like quality fish and way too much mustard. Chicken tacos were just ok. And $6 for a fish taco?? Seriously?

Where else in Santa Barbara can you get a decent healthy meal for only $5? If you watch for the coupons that occasionally show up in the mail box you can get some especially good deals. This is not exactly the nicest El Pollo Loco I've ever been too, but they get the job done and the food is pretty much your standard El Pollo Loco. Their chicken is really quite good and they have a nice variety of side dishes. If your not in the mood for eating regular chicken, they also have various other menu items, such as burritos, tostados, nachos, etc. The seating is about average and they also have a fair sized parking lot.

I can't say that I agree with many reviewers that this is the best Mexican ever, but it was defintiely good. The food here is very made from scratch, including the home made tortillas. The dishes were all recipes you'd find closer to central Mexico, do don't expect any of that border crap here like chimichangas and whatnot.   Their guacamole is great, as are the enchiladas de plaza, adobado tacos, and it looked to be cash only.

Tonight we enjoyed Dave's Dogs! Bratwurst with sauerkraut is my favorite! Chilie fries, tator tots with blue cheese, corn with Flaming Hot Cheetos, buffalo chicken wings, and 'the Dave', 'the California' and 'the Jalepeno'. Ohhh yummy! Reminds me of Top Dog in Berkeley in the good old days!
Delicious Ramon, pleasant atmosphere, lovely service.  A bit of a different menu than the Goleta site but just as satisfying.

THIS PLACE SHOULD BE ASHAMED OF THEMSELVES   I originally came in because I am a huge fan of Chinese food so I decided I was going to give this place a try because I pass by it all the time. To my pleasant surprise , I have learned that they just recently opened up a sushi bar. I AM A HUGE enthusiast for sushi and I had to try it. As I was looking through the menu, I couldn't believe my eyes. There is a sushi restaurant in the Paseo Nuevo mall called Sushi Tyme, and a good 90-95% of the sushi on this menu is straight up plagiarized from their menu. The menu is literally copy and pasted. Yes I completely understand that sushi restaurants are all going to have some similarities in one way or another and have parallels, but the similarities are so glaring, it just can't be ignored. In case any of you think that I am assuming this without any evidence, think again. I decided to do my  own research and go on the sbmenus page for both of these websites to further reinforce this belief. The left column is sushi tyme's and the right is Empress Palace, and trust me even though it may not look like it,  I am one two different sbmenus pages. Everything including the menu description is identical, and this place should be ashamed of themselves.   How convenient for a Chinese Establishment to open up a sushi bar out of nowhere and rip off the menu of another establishment. I understand that this is a business, but where is the sense of morality and ethics?!?

Nice little spot with outdoor seating in La Arcada, but I wouldn't call it French. More French inspired.  We ordered several of the apps. The mushroom Cristina was like mushroom gravy over toast. Smoked salmon and capers was fine, but how can you screw that up?  Steak au poivre was so-so, and the duck confit with blood plums was nice. Both came 'with Yukon potato wedges.' Two small wedges.  Dessert was...fine. Again, what can you do with crime brûlée to mess it up?  Overall, wouldn't eat here again. Far too many great restaurants in Santa Barbara.

I have been to there other locations and have never had a bad meal. Great chips and salsa bar. There special pork tacos were tender and full of flavor. No matter what you order you won't be disappointed. From fish tacos to chicken mole they are all fantastic. There Molcajetes is some of the best you tasted, what makes it so good is there sauce. A true winner. Give it a try.

I was nervous to try this place after reading the bad reviews, but I decided to give it a try since I couldn't find the Starbucks that was supposedly there also. I had a warm chocolate croissant that was dripping of melty chocolate goodness and a mocha that wasn't so bad either.

We absolutely loved this adorable cafe.   The food was outstanding, service was timely and the ambiance is quaint.  Avocado toast was fresh and eggs poached perfectly.  They use red potatoes for the the home fries which keeps them Moist but crispy. Overall very happy with our experience and will definitely be back!

We picked up Sun Sushi tonight to have an end of the week treat. Currently, it's take out only and you can order online conveniently. Ordering was easy, and they texted when the order was ready, which was actually on the lower end of the 35-45 minute estimate. When I walked inside, my food was ready and I paid and left. The food was well packaged, fish was fresh, and everything was delicious.  One thing to keep in mind is their ordering process is awkward and is setup in a way that can trick people into double tipping. When you place your order, it asks for a tip (typical) but the default payment method is to pay in person. When you get there and they run your card, 20% of the receipt is a section for gratuity. So if you are not sure that you previously added a tip line from home, or your spouse picks up the order, there's a good chance Sun is getting a percentage tip on top of your total + tip from earlier. Not a big deal but something to keep in mind.

Went here with the girls on our trip for tea and snacks. They obviously had the best teas, Earl Grey, Darjeeling, Organics.. We didn't do the full after noon tea. We had tea with appetizers. I think you're paying for the view and the ambiance more than anything. Great decor, feels like your in Italy when you walk in. I would suggest this for a romantic spot to watch the sunset with your hubby with some tea and desserts. But a little too pricey for what you get.
Great selection of food and wine. So you can be naughty here and order one of their burgers or a little nice and order one of their great salad like the papaya and crab.

Backyard bowls is a cool, modern place serving tasty food for the health conscious! I got to try the Deigo bowl.  The Diego bowl is absolutely amazing!! The slight peanut butter taste just elevates the bowl for me as I love nutty flavors. Also the toppings provide the right flavors and texture contrasts. Just a good bowl overall.   I also liked the fact it is within walking distance of many attractions, like Stearns Wharf, the beach, and State street.

Incredibly creative coffee concoctions! Not only are they beautiful to the eye but they are delicious to the palette! Baristas were super friendly and helpful as well. My only negative was it's pretty much a place to work on your tablet so there's not much  social connection going on..... like people saying hello and such. As long as you know that, it's cool. Every drink is beautiful and delicious I doubt there's a thing that would even be not tasty. We had the burnout and the Rose matcha.... the rose matcha was  outstanding!! It's the kind of place that would be a shame to get your drink to go...... you would miss the beauty of how they serve and display it.

I decided to give Cadiz a second chance since I liked the food the last time but was not fond of the service.   This time,  I made sure that I talked to Messina about my reservation and to let her know I was coming back.  Our service was superb this second time.  I know that it was done knowing that they needed to redeem themselves,  but they definitely did.  I hope that they keep their service up to par every time.  The restaurant is very fancy looking and a little on the pricey side.  They definitely should make sure the service is good every time.    We had Gabby as our server instead of a male server last time; I do not remember his name.   Gabby was very tentative and made lots of conversation with us. Our waters remained refilled without much question and we even got free dessert (due to a hiccup with our steaks).  Drinks were also promptly made.   Four of my group ordered the 16oz ribeye steak, medium rare. Now,  I understand that medium rare is pretty tricky to get,  but they should make sure they've got it down for the $32 price.  Two of our steaks were more on the medium side, one was medium rare,  and the  fourth was practically medium  well.   Being on the medium side isn't too bad so we didn't want to bother returning them.  However the medium well steak a no go. Therefore,  we had Gabby send back the medium well steak.  I don't know anyone who would enjoy a medium well ribeye.  It came back on the rare side so that was good.  As for drinks,  we ordered our favorites:  Madrid and espresso martini.  Both drinks were delicious.  The ambiance of this place is very nice for dinner.  You can in the patio that is nicely decorated.  You can also sit inside where there are tables that include comfortable booth-like, patio chairs and regular comfortable chairs.  The lighting is dim and the decorations are minimal yet perfect.   I will dock one star for the mistake with the steaks and the idea that the service may hiccup of they don't purposely know you're coming back after a bad experience.

Sama Sama is one of my favorite date night asian fusion dinner spots in Santa Barbara (the only one)?  The type of place that you wish you had 10 stomachs due to the delicious variety of their menu.  It is very conveniently located downtown Santa Barbara on state st.  One of my go-to dishes is the hot wing appetizer- super fresh crispy, juicy mouth watering wings. Literally the best wings I have ever had, all my staff dinners are now here, not solely because of the wings but partially :)    We were on an appetizer hype tonight so we also got the papaya salad and paan seared octopus.  The papaya was incredibly spicy, the flavors were muddled together to perfection- it was so refreshing. The octopus was cooked incredibly well- crispy on the outside and juicy on the inside.    Lastly, I ordered the Chinese Sausage Fried Rice which I probably should've saved for another visit.  We were already stuffed after the appetizers!  It was piping hot, the Sausage was perfectly cooked, I was eating it as if I hadn't eaten in days. The flavor was rich, creamy, flavorful and crunchy in the best ways.  Serive is warm, ultra-attentive and prompt with bringing out our meals.  (Everything comes out the PERFECT temperature) I'm sure at some point.  Thank you Sama Sama for a wonderful visit, as usual. This review was long overdue and am so happy my fellow yelpers see my yummy perspective of this place! See you tonight!!!

We came for 24 hours in SB, and we stopped here twice! That tells you how good it is. We did some bar hopping in the day and had some tacos.......um the most delicious tacos, and margaritas for a quick stop. I don't even like margaritas, and I was drinking up the spicy concoction like it was no ones business. Came back later that evening for dinner, since the food was so good earlier in the day. Shared a delicious tomahawk steak with these amazing cheesy potatoes. Followed it up with some flan and churros and we went home happy. Very nice and sweet wait staff! Can't wait to come back because I just love the indoor décor and vibe. Right across from the beach and worth the wait, get in line, you won't be disappointed!

Well pretty bummed this place no longer seems to be the place I fell in love with. Brought some friends and vouched for how good I felt it would be. It was a Saturday around 8pm and it was pretty empty. Lots of employees walking around but no one came to seat us for what felt like 10 minutes. Finally we get seated and the table is filthy. The bench seats are filthy with pizza crusts and stains. A server came to us and said she be right with us but at this point my friends weren't stoked and wanted to leave so we did, employees watched us leave and didn't say anything. Pretty embarrassing for me. Seems like it's morphing into a new barbarians or something since that has closed. Buuuut the new Ramen place across the street was Awesome!!!

Does not disappoint on the second visit!!!!  We drove from Ventura just because we had a craving for some pickles and Swiss sandwiches...  They even remembered the family from our last visit ( and what we ordered) how cool is that!!!  Will keep coming back....  And they have later hours in the summer which is awesome!!!  Come and try it, you will not be disappointed....

Good Mexican food, definitely more delicious and authentic than a lot of places in this price range. Would I say this is the best Mexican I've had in a while... yeh, not really. Still, it's a pretty great place to grab lunch. My biggest complaint? No taco chips?? Pretty unbelievable... had to deduct a star for that.

I stopped in after searching for a Gluten Free pizza within the Santa Barbara area as I was on an elimination diet to help narrow down my food allergies.  I like my personal pizza with a little pesto, mozzarella, artichoke hearts, sautéed spinach & sun dried tomatoes. They typically just add the spinach but without a blink the lady taking my order turned towards the pizza chef (possibly the Guru himself) and asked if he could sautée mine for me, he kindly obliged with a smile.  Being made scratch to order does take a few minutes then there is a little pizza oven action too so I found a good spot to sit and played with my phone. The pizza was brought out steaming hot and tasted wonderful! I was quite impressed and gf dough tastes awesome when prepared by the Guru! Thank you all for your kindness and in making me an amazing personal pizza... and exactly the way I like it. I will be sure to come back when visiting SB!

Been here twice and it does not disappoint! The line and parking is always a mess, but be patient! Parkings frequently open up so there's no need to freak out or get mad! The lines moves very quick so don't turn away if you see a line.  Triple play is the way to go! 3 tacos for  $9.75. Order an additional taco if you need to!  First time here I got the guac (waste of money). Tacos I've tried : Batter up - Cod (Order this!) Carne Asada- marinated steak (skip this one) Spicy Crispy Shrimp (tempura shrimp. The chipotle aioli is what makes it spicy so you can def ask them to hold off on it. GET THIS) Gangnam Style - Korean style marinated steak (honestly not bad. Better than carne asada I'll tell you that.)  Ahi Poke (amazing! hold on the wasabi aioli because again, I'm a weenie for spice) The banh mi - Viet style pulled pork (Not my fav. Banh mi should be viet bbq pork in my opinion, not pulled pork) Carnitas (again, like the banh mi, not my fav. They do not do a good job at carnitas here)  Solid taco spot here in Santa Barbara. You will see lots of random people of all shapes color and whatever you can think of! This place is quick quick go. There is outdoor seating and a bathroom, but most people will eat and go within 20 minutes so the tables are constantly being wiped down.

MEH. They do have a decent beer selection and a very generously sized portion of their basic house salad which was not only huge but delicious. That's the end of the good for me. The crowd is mostly loud college or surfer bartender types that have likely all gotten wasted at the neighborhood next door. It's a brewhouse I guess. If you're a dude you'll probably love it. If you're a lady you might not. If a guy wants to take you here on your first date I definitely wouldn't shy from making an alternative suggestion. Especially if you want to avoid ditsy blonde server that very may well hit on your potential love interest because she really shouldn't be in the customer service industry but is pretty enough that she ended up there. If your in laws are in town and you don't actually want to be able to have a conversation, then it's a perfect place. I guess it's all subjective.

Of the three restaurants I tried for dinner, Bouchon was the best.   They get a small demerit because, when I made reservation on line, I indicated outdoor seating. When I arrived they said they had not noted that, had no outdoor seating, and originally tried to seat me smack in the middle. The waiter said I'd have a view of the restaurant. What I wanted was a corner and air circulation; he found me a table right by the open door. So it turned out OK.  I tried foie gras, with apologies to the animal. Decided it was more cachet than anything else; I've had both duck and chicken liver I liked more. But at least now I can say I tried it. The sauce was fabulous, used bread to get to all of it. Got the duck. When I see duck on a menu I rarely order anything else. The breast was roasted and leg/thigh confit. Came with a fabulous sauce and a succotash of corn, beans, squash with bacon. It was excellent, every component. Very substantial portion. I was thinking I should take some away as I was getting full but figured that would not work since I was leaving town next morning. Don't know where my brain was as I had a cooler in my car and the place I was staying would have given me ice! Whatever, my bad, I ate it all. Everything was excellent. Had a glass of Pinot Noir at waiter's suggestion, but honestly, not my favorite wine. In retrospect, I think I'm better off ordering a wine I really like and forget 'what goes with what'!  Wanted a light dessert. The creme brulee looked tempting but I was having some lactose issues and that is pure dairy so I went with the lime curd meringue tart. Again, excellent, lovely, the blackberry coulis made the dish, added just the right contrasting note, good lime curd, crust, meringue.  Attentive staff, lovely presentation, pleasant setting added to quality of food.  The gentleman sitting next to me had ordered venison and, as tables are pretty close together I overheard him tell waiter he did not have venison often but this was the best he'd ever had. Reminisced that when I lived in the Pacific Northwest a co-worker of mine who hunted brought me venison chops but I could not get past the thought that I was eating Bambi and ended up giving it to my cats, who had no such scruples. He replied that if he was eating Bambi I was eating Donald Duck. I think a difference is Donald Duck never had a 'duck' personality, he was a cartoon human in duck form. But let's not try to be logical about Disney characters!  Whenever I return to SB this will be at the top of my list of place to go.  I have now completed all Yelp reviews from my vacation and feeling depressed, not knowing when my next vacation will happen.

This review is based on Monday afternoons alone. The drinks are decent, considering the cheap price. The food leaves something to be desired, but I'll happily overlook that considering the inexpensive bill. They also let my kiddo hang out in his stroller en-circle with his fellow colleagues in the cheap menu section, which is frowned upon by many a happy hour establishments (seriously, when am I supposed to relax with my fellow mamas??). I treated for three of us having drinks and snacks and the bill was around $15...that's crazy cheap. Added bonus: the walk home from this place is fantastic...I almost forget that I am sans child...almost. I would give five stars for atmosphere alone. So much potential for the space.

Have been wanting to try Bettina for awhile, but just haven't. Fast forward to COVID with limited options for takeout and we finally got to try it. Delivery wasn't very quick with Restaurant Connection, but that's not the restaurant's fault. I ordered the baby gem Caesar salad and a pepperoni pizza. Both were fantastic. The Caesar dressing was homemade and so so good. The pizza was also great, but I think the crust is what made it. The pizza was more of a 'gourmet' style pizza, but I definitely don't think it was worth $30. Overall, Bettina was very good and we will order again for our pizza fix.

Ordered the steak salad and my daughter had the shrimp tacos. Salad was plentiful and filing but the tacos were tiny and more for an appetizer portion. Single layer tortillas fell apart but the taste was delicious. Salad was good but definitely order the dressing on the side. My lettuce was swimming. Overall good. Inside has some cute character.

My girlfriend and I had a great time at Edomasa's sushi bar just recently.  This Santa Barbara staple is one of the town's only late night options. The prices aren't bad either. We first tried the Godzilla roll. The vinegary, garlicky marinated tuna was unlike any kind of roll I've ever tried.  Between good laughs an hot miso, we joined one of the servers for a few drinks. She taught us all about Shochu; a Japanese vodka distilled from grain or potatoes. Helps promote longevity. I guess it's like Europeans and their daily glass of red wine. Perhaps just another reason to get drunk.. I was lucky enough to get the last Yellowtail Kama in the joint. That and some hot rice put a nice finish to the night.

Horrible service.  There is an Axxess deal and it gets you a free entree for the price of one.  They made me pay for salsa because he claimed the deal is only for a free burrito so even for extra salsa you have to pay.  Ridiculous; I've never paid for extra salsa at any restaurant in my life. Never coming back!  And he charged $1.90 for salsa.  Oh and the best part is when I got home the two orders came with a combined seven chips.

Feels like the closest thing to an authentic experience outside of Mexico and maybe there are better taco spots in San Diego but La Super-Rica is pretty darn good. Their homemade tortillas are delicious. My eyes are always bigger than my stomach and I want to try everything on the menu. I usually order a couple tacos and a quesadilla. It's cash only and really no seating with COVID but this is the kind of place where it's socially acceptable to eat standing while you stuff your face because the food looks so inviting.   I crave the tacos at La Super-Rica and is a must stop when I'm in Santa Barbara.

This is an update for Pascucci. My first review here noted the slow service as the only negative. I just dined here a few days ago and have an update to report. I want to first mention they have relocated from Paseo Nuevo to a couple of blocks down State Street. The new location is nowhere near as nice as the former. I regretfully report that the service, food, and atmosphere have fallen to subpar levels to where I can no longer endorse this place. Sadly this was the poorest service I have had anywhere in years. I had to ask for water a few times before anyone even came to greet my party. When the water had arrived, there was no ice...the water was lukewarm. My son complained his water was hot, and upon inspecting it, the water was indeed very warm. The food took a very long time, it was cold. Cold food that should be warm is a sign of the food dying on the server counter. Food 'dying' in the kitchen means the food was placed by the kitchen and was sitting on the counter before being ran to the table. Also, the presentation and quality is not as nice as it used to be. The service was utterly disappointing, and I do not plan on returning, and will not endorse this place.  On a side note, just in case you are considering this place, the new location feels unclean. The seating in the patio area was dirty. The restrooms were not clean. The men's room did not have toilet paper or toilet seat covers. I did mention this to the staff when I first noticed this, and it did not appear they addressed it. I am not sure if this place is under new ownership or management, but this restaurant was just disappointing.

We've enjoyed several lunches and dinners here.  The location is great and the ambience too.  It's a very welcoming restaurant.  Granted, it might take a while to get your food or water, but it isn't because people are rude; they just seem busy.  They have a couple younger employees working who didn't always have answers to our questions, but if you're patient, they eventually figured out the answer and came back to let us know.  It does have a family feel, both because it is family run, but also because families of all ages come here.  The menu is limited but what they make is really good.

This is a solid little lunch or dinner.  Everything is super fresh and though the food is simple it is delicious and sometimes simple is just what I want to eat!  The salsas are always fresh and I love the marinade they put on the chicken.  It seems like a half chicken meal is always enough to feed two people reasonable and the rice and beans make for serious comfort food.

You have a choice of valet parking or a trolley that goes down State Street to the wharf. If you want to go down farther on the beach or zoo ask for a transfer    Our wharf has a beautiful Dolphin fountain up front, benches including one with shiny tiles for the memory of 'The Popcorn Man.' For locals many of us remember his delicious popcorn, pinwheels, and multiple choices of candy. If you aren't from here, the bench will make you feel you did know him. There are two classic restaurants as you start walking down. The Moby Dick serves local seafood and above it is a more casual restaurant.   Make no mistake about it, this is a tourist trap. Accepting it for what it is, I find Sterns Wharf a fun diversion. If you buy a smallish purchase will allow you 90 minutes free, $2.50 an hour after that. The Moby Dick offers valet parking. If you park anywhere from State & Victoria there is an electric shuttle that goes from down State to Bud Bottoms fountain at the base of the wharf for 50 cents. Beware, you might find a store like Rocket Fizz on your way and choose to get off. Make sure to get back on the trolley to get to the beautiful.  Off the side of the wharf you will find cute games and beautiful sand sculptures by 'panhandlers.' Personally I will throw a couple quarters to these men and women for their creativity.   Although fishing isn't allowed its not enforced. It's enjoyable to relax a few minutes and some of those fishing enjoy company. You will also find a few people offering entertainment. The man who performs card tricks halfway down is a personal favorite.  The candy shop is overpriced and my friends enjoy the different types of fudge that melts in your mouth. Although it costs more than I would like to pay every visit I always give in and buy big several pieces of English Toffee   Next door is the shop with all the tourist magnets etc. Great place to buy things to send to your friends far away even if you're a local.  Don't forget near the far end of the wharf is our Sea Center from the Museum of Natural History. I still stop by once a year and the kiddos will adore all the touch tanks.  Go back to the Popcorn man's bench to wait for the trolley that will take you back down State Street.
Above avg steakhouse and bar. I do think it is a tad overpriced but everything was good. Nothing to complain and nothing to rave about. You won't be dissapointed and you won't be overwhelmed.
Love brophy's bar atmosphere.... It's the view that gets ya! Great salads and chowder... Another place that's great when it's not tourist season and a weekend.

My Friend and I were looking for a spot to have a bit to eat and a drink. We were in the funk zone and saw the outdoor fire place and patio light and both turn to each other and said 'what's that place?'   Great service, food and beer! We order our sampler beer and food took a seat by the fire but I was still a little cold to this nice man turned on the heater for us--yup we got spoiled.  Definitely will be back with my hubby cause I know he would love this place too

The entrees here can be a bit too rich, but the warm country apple tart with caramel gelato is absolutely delicious--worth a trip to Stella Mare's alone and I don't usually care for apple desserts. I also LOVE that you can just order assorted chocolates for dessert...which are obviously best enjoyed at the table next to the fireplace.

Tried this for the first time yesterday and it didn't disappoint!   Had the butter chicken, and it was super flavorful.   Had the garlic naan and that was good.   We also had some potatoes and a lentil dish (can't remember the name) but they were both good!   Would highly recommend!

Patxi's pizza is always a hit. Service is always good & im so glad I was able to come back a year later. This time I came back to have the BBQ chicken deep dish. It was tasty and made fresh.  Pizza takes 30 minutes to make & they tell you that once your ordering your pizza.  Lots of seating, the place is rather large. With a bar   Both times I've been here I've only tried the deep dish & next time I'm going to try the thin crust.  Server was nice didn't get her name. Boo.   I'll see you soon Patxi !

Everything you may have liked about the other American Ale (good, thoughtful beer selection) and the old Union Ale (decent, fairly varied menu)... has gone somewhere else. This AA serves mostly beers you can buy at a supermarket, and it's one of pretty few restaurants in this town that don't have a single meat-free option on the menu. Seriously; no substitutions, no salad, nothing. The vegetarian in your group (and this is SoCal; you've got at least one) may choose from fries or coleslaw. Really?  The service is lackadaisical at best. The music is terrible. Oh, and there's no parking as far as I can tell (the lot is valet for the foofier place next door). We found the place with about 3 customers in it at prime dinnertime, and it's easy to see why.

We decided to try their happy hour deal the other night. I wasn't expecting it to be anything to great but was pleasantly surprised! I got the chicken sandwich and my husband got the cheeseburger. The onion rings were super crunchy and delicious. My sandwich had a lot of flavor and was very good. I've been here in the past for breakfast and have always had a good food too and prompt/friendly service! Highly recommend coming here!
Came here on Easter Sunday. We were sat pretty swiftly. We enjoyed our feet in the sand, a beautiful view of the ocean and some tasty eats!!

This has been are go to place for several years ......last night was not good at all. We ordered our oysters on the half shell like always....joke!!! They were so small I couldn't even believe it. Also the bread was hard as a rock....both orders!! My husband and I are hoping it was just an off night for our favorite place in SB!!!!
Hidden gem in Santa Barbara, you could walk right by it and not even see it but don't let that throw you off, best Gyros in town, tri tip sandwich is amazing as well, cool little Greek market also

Surprisingly, Cold Spring Tavern has one of the best veggie burgers I've ever had. It tastes and looks like they make the veggie pattie in-house, which is a plus. I can't for sure say what's in it but it seems to have a black or white bean base as opposed to an alternative-meat product. The burger is huge, very filling, and delicious. You won't be fooled into thinking it's a meat burger, but that's just fine because it wholly stands on its own in ticking off all the boxes of taste-bud excellence. You have to order it from inside, but otherwise it's a great option for vegetarians who still want to enjoy this idyllic little restaurant up in the mountains, especially if coming with friends to enjoy the tri-tip cookout and music on weekends. This place has an eccentric, eclectic mix of patrons which makes visits a blast. You'll see everyone from bikers to SB townies to cruise ship tourists. Everyone is always friendly, despite parking being a bit tough to nab. If you're going on the weekend, I advise arriving early to grab a good parking spot and picnic table to enjoy the music, drinks, and food. While this place is known for their meat cookouts, the veggie burger is well worth a try!

Delivery for a future time has always worked out wonderfully. Delivery for 'now' was absolute crap. Put in the order online at 1:10 pm. The tracker said the pizza wasn't even in the oven until 2:10. Delivered to our door at 2:50. That's an hour and forty minute wait for pizza. Come on...

Lilac Patisserie is a breath of fresh air! I am someone with an allergy that requires a gluten free diet and it's so nice to go somewhere that is a dedicated gluten free restaurant. They have so many options and you absolutely do not need to be gluten free! We got the waffle and breakfast sandwich and both me and my partner were so happy. We also got the blueberry muffin that was delicious!
We do the Pasta pickup on Wednesday and it does not disappoint.  Seriously good food. Fresh, homemade and paired with great wine. Love it!!!

Very nice Kaiseki fixed menu dinner. Very authentic and traditional. very high quality ingredients Excellent service. My favorite is the sashimi dish, the Madai and Shima-Aji were out of this world. Nice reasonable Sake.  It's fairly expensive and would like to have more high quality Nigiri.

Confusing dining experience. I'll start by saying that the food was excellent, I would go back again and again to try everything on the menu- BUT given the poor service, I'm unsure if I would give it another go. The hostess was very friendly and gave us a table on the patio without waiting. But then we waited literally 15 mins before we were even given water or were greeted by anyone. Plenty of bussers, servers, etc. walked by us and nothing. I hate complaining so I just told myself I'd leave if no one came over for another 5 mins. Two minutes before my cutoff time, a manager came over and offered us water and cocktails. We ordered cocktails with him and he said our server would be right with us. When she came over (she was super friendly and informative) we ordered all of our food because we were starving at this point. Our first tapas came out about 5 mins before we got our drinks. Other courses were then served before prior courses were cleared (expediters juggling plates to put down our next course), and I waited ten minutes with an empty drink before anyone asked me if I'd like another. I ordered a glass of wine to go with my paella and it arrived when we were almost finished with it (paella was outstanding by the way). I REALLY wanted to love this place and come back often being an SB local, but for now, I think I'd rather spend my money elsewhere and not deal with chaotic and disorganized service. Also, I'm not a complainer, check my other reviews, they're mostly five stars. I'm honest. And for $125 for 2 people I expect better service than this.

I got my free birthday Grand Slam breakfast and really enjoyed it! What a surprise! Nice staff but the atmosphere is drab and no muzak/music--only clanging from the kitchen so they can work on that. The scammish vending machine game in the entry must go-- I hated to see a little girl lose a few dollars to it as it's probably rigged.  But other than that, this Denny's isn't bad for breakfast.

Yay! I finally went back here after they moved from their Mission location to Hollister. It was awesome and I'd definitely go back. My friends were also happy :)   Whatever you do, try their Al Pastor! You can't go wrong.

Ok to start things off  before we walked in to the restaurant I asked my wife do you want to give CPK another chance do to prior visits , unfortunately we did I was hoping the chicken tikka pizza was back again let down. So we ordered bread while we waited for our food the bread was hard and cold When my wife received her plate  I noticed a hair on her bowl. I forgot to mention that their was only one waiter working we asked for a ice tea he brings us a coke theirs 2 mistakes so far the third  mistake I ordered a sandwich He asked turkey or chicken I ordered turkey  Omg sandwich came out guess what it was chicken . The manager came to our table and asked I heard their was a hair in your food we said yes their was but the problem had been taking cate of She replied okay and walked away. No sorry no nothing

While walking along the main road, we were hungry and looking for something close so we stopped here. It was nice to sit outside and people watch while taking in the nice cool air.   Unfortunately we tried their Mule and neither of us liked it. He wouldn't drink it and I had to jump sugar in it. I realize this is not the fault of the server but after my husband mentioned not liking it and asking for a coke, the server seemed to avoid us the rest of the experience. It was just obvious and weird. We were still expecting to pay for the product.   The burger we ordered was good and so was the coconut shrimp appetizer.

Thanksgiving Brunch with one dog, two men and myself was really good. FANTASTIC SERVICE. Polite and quick. We sat outdoors and pleased the service was very attentive and the price was reasonable: $22.95 per person.  We have been there several times and each time the service is super.

The only reason this review is getting 3 stars and not less is because the quality of food and a select few waitresses.   The wait time is always listed but it doesn't help that it's extremely inaccurate. 80 minutes has turned into 2+ hours on multiple occasions. The prepping for pre-orders also takes a verrry long time.   There is one lady there that is great. And it's always a pleasure when she is taking care of the table. There is a gentleman that helps take food out, but he is careless and always in a rush. He places our trays of food on the opposite side of where we sit and it makes it so I have to literally stand up to reach all the way across to get it; it's just not convenient.   All in all, decent service (+ the one exceptional waitress) and great food.   Possibility of changing the rating throughout better experiences

I love the food here and the atmosphere but sometimes the service isn't great. My friend was here for a birthday a while back, and our waiter brought our food. My friend was on her phone about to take a group selfie at the same time as our food was being brought over. The waiter says 'Get off of your phone and enjoy the dinner' it was very uncalled for and kind of embarrassing but we brushed it off. We had a great meal and then the check came. My friend pulled out her phone for the second time to tell her mom how much the bill was, and our waiter once again comments 'I know it's your birthday but get off of your phone' it was just so awkward and inappropriate I don't know why he felt the need to comment. Like just give us the check and leave. Waiters like this confuse me because rude comments like that don't get you good tips so I have no idea what he was thinking. Just overall really awkward experience. We should have went to Eureka Burger next door.

Polvorones!!!! The best I've had in a long time! And huge!!!!  I'm just talking about the pink sugar cookies, which are my favorite. It's not overly sweet or lardy. Lots of places are just blah and leave that film in the mouth from all the lard.  I enjoyed it so much I had to come back before going back home. I also bought their bread, which was freshly baked. Omg so good!!! I was bummed they were out of conchas. I bet it's delicious as well.   Definitely coming back here when I'm in town.

If you plan properly (our wait time is 20 min at 5 pm on a weekday) and are lucky with a nice waiter/waitress (ours is good), this place cannot go wrong. Generous portion  and classic flavors, chowder is really good even I am not a big fan for chowder. Mai tai is so so.

Worst meal I've ever had out in SB. I should have sent it back.  I've had sanddabs many times - at Lure... the whole meal was extremely bland.  It was like the whole fish, potato, spinach was frozen from a pouch and reheated.  The fish was very soggy, flavorless laying on the top of the flavorless potatoes, which made them even more soggy.  The spinach was like out of a can - absolutely no flavor.  We all used to meet up at Bluewater when they first opened - and my friends and I never go anymore.  Thought I would give it another shot - it's too bad as the location is awesome.  Gave 2 stars as the waitress was very nice and attentive (she asked about the oysters but not about my meal).  The oysters were good.

Ok...here's the deal. I couldn't even wait until the end of the meal to write this review.   We ordered a lot. We ordered seconds of most. The wine recommendation was amazing.   I usually write a lengthy review. Tonight I am making the following recommendation: eat here tonight; eat here often.   Huge praise from a local SB self proclaimed foodie.
Love this place! It has unusual food like bone marrow on toast. Must go again soon!

Lively energetic atmosphere and the customer service was good.  It took a minute or so to get drinks and food but it was worth the wait.   We sat at the bar because the place was super busy.  They have both inside  and outdoor dinning  and they bring you chips and salsas while you decide your menu options.  I ordered the Enchiladas Criollo: Two chicken enchiladas with melted cheese, green sauce, coloradito red mole, onions, Mexican sour cream and cotija cheese, served with green rice.  Everything was very tasty and it looked beautiful of my plate.

I think they put something in their baked mac & cheese because it is sooo good and I always crave it... Also, their tater tots are amazing. Wash all that down with a couple beers or margaritas and I know I'm doing my part in contributing to the US obesity epidemic. Just kidding.  I've been here many times and I've never had a bad experience, so I felt compelled to write a review. It does get kinda loud sometimes but it's nice during happy hour. Actually, I really recommend coming here for happy hour. The servers are usually quite attentive and friendly.  It's cool that they have gluten free pizza.   Overall I like this place especially compared to many other establishments around the area.

I have been a royalties reward member for a little while now.. so I have frequented this place enough.. they have pool tables over looking the best view in Santa Barbara.. great happy hour prices with 22 oz beers :) and great staff!! the staff who work here are the most genuine people I've met in santa barbara!! a quick shout out to marcelo who always makes me feel comfortable he is such a hard worker!! and to Mike who always makes me feel welcome the staff here are phenomenal!!

Who knew this was here. Wonderful! Best pastry dough and multiple filling choices. Can't wait to try them all! Had the Caprese and the the prosciutto. My friend had a pollo and a Carne...both excellent. We'll be back....soon
Santa Barbara trips never complete without a meal at Farmer Boy ... They never fall to please!!

Some of the best tacos I've ever had. Corazon Cocina is a modern looking taco booth inside a food market called the Public Market. Though there can be a line, service is fairly quick and friendly. The owner personally comes out to help you with recommendations when ordering. We were given samples of their unique, tasty juices when ordering.  Now to the delicious food: We had the Norteno, La Corriente, and the Sal de Mar. All were so good. The Norteno had a flaky flour tortilla that was just the right amount of gooey inside matched with the hearty steak. The Corriente was also fantastic. The Sal de Mar came with large amounts of tasty sashimi grade fish. There are many salsas to pair with your food. Prices are very fair. I love the no-frills, focus on food atmosphere they have here. Great taco!
Good location...great food....had the pork belly hash....wine list..large menu....bring friends...indoor outdoor seating

Came here for lunch on a rainy Saturday afternoon and basically I must echo Matthew S - too expensive! $50 for a lunch that is not even filling and is served on paper plates is pretty darn ridiculous. The cold and wet day made me want some warm chowder, theirs is thick and chunky, but needs a bit of salt. My husband and I each had our own bowl. I had the fishwich with grilled yellowtail. It's served on an overly grilled buttered sesame seed bun. Because the sandwich is so saucy, they need the bread to be pretty hard. The sauce - tabasco vinaigrette and tartar sauce is pretty tasty and the fish was tender. My husband enjoyed the 3 tacos sampler and savored each one as much as he could.  Overall friendly service (walk up order and they bring the food to you) and food was tasty, but damn for $50 you could throw in some fries?!!  I wasn't able to get a photo of the meal because I was trying to take care of my baby while eating...please note the bathrooms while new, clean, and spacious, do not have a baby changing table :(

Wow....if you'd like to see this place succeed maybe offer up some constructive advice rather than simply slamming it!  Ok....this is my 2nd review of this place & I'm sticking w 3 stars for now. 4 of us went for lunch last Sunday. Service was ok and very friendly, but they'd only been open a week & are understandably working out the kinks. I'd suggest a few things....fewer plates/clutter on the table.....maybe consolidate meats i/o 4 plates of meats (we all got the all-u-can-eat), larger & fewer sides (maybe ask what we'd like more of?), owner should 'make the rounds, work the crowd' a bit, & servers should check in more frequently. Kimchi was also a little too salty. but maybe that was just our batch. I also think it would be nice to have a spicier meat option....really make us SWEAT!! All in all a good 1st visit and we will be back again. GOOD LUCK!!!!

****Disclaimer, I haven't been here in 2 years.   That being said, I just called them and they still make everything I loved there.   Everytime I go to a Sandwich shop, whether it be a nice deli or Quiznos, I know I'm always going to be disappointed because nothing will ever compare to the 'Santa Ynez' sandwich at Cantwell's. Admittedly my expertise is in burritos being from San Diego, not NYC.   It's about the bread, just like in Carne Asada chips how it's about the chips, it's about the bread. Roast beef and cheddar cheese with grilled onions and horseradish sauce on a grilled DUTCH CRUNCH ROLL. Just amazing.   Also, their breakfast burritos are really good. Being from San Diego, mornings in the 805 always sucked because I knew I wasn't going to get a good breakfast burrito, until I found this place. It definitely was my BB spot for the 2 years I knew of it while up there.   It's also has a nice little market area where you can get olives, wine, champagne, health and beauty profucts and flowers.   Just think of this place as a 7-11 sized version of Lazy Acres or Whole Foods.

Man that was a damn good burger! I haven't had a burger that good in a long time. Not skimpy and very flavorful. And I love the waffle fries also. I will definitely be back.I had a cheeseburger with the works. Yum!
I have three words for you. SWEET POTATO FRIES. If you do nothing else, get these. They're phenomenal. My burger was very good, but the fries were the star of the show.

Went for the $25, 3 course tasting menu during SB restaurant week. Fantastic. Parmesan roasted cauliflower starter, burger (hard to chose all the main options looked great), finished with bread pudding yum.   Great wine list at reasonable prices, especially for downtown SB. Liked the long list of half bottles.   Service was spot on too.   And we had a cozy spot right next to the fireplace.   Zero complaints.

Our family LOVES the Shop. The food, outdoor ambiance, staff. All foody fantastic. BUT...  I am disheartened that their payment system has a default 15% added tip. The only apparent service they provide, above making the food you order, is to walk the food to your table. You still have to stand in line to order. Then stand in line again if you want to add on, say, a hot chocolate.   The staff says you can opt out of the automatic 15% tip, but then you are left with 10% or no tip.   I just have never believed in tipping unless someone provides a special service. If you order and pay for a coffee, you should get a coffee. Why tip them to make the food you already paid for?
Ah, only the best street tacos in sb. + don't forget your sauces. Carnitas are always juicy and grand, only with onion and cilantro topped with your choice of sauce/salsa. Call ahead!!

This place is good but I consider it your done in high end pizza. Place is very nice and quite hipster if that is what you are looking for. Pizzas are one per normal appetite for about $22 each so a bit on the pricey side. If u have a couple of drinks the bill could go up to a $100 pretty quickly but shouldn't be a problem in this neighborhood. Worth trying but not a regular stop.
What an awesome place. The food was fantastic, the service top notch and the owner 'Jane' was so sweet and unassuming. I will definitely be back for lunch very soon.

Great place up on the mesa near lazy acres.  They have really good japanese food and give you a ton for the money.  I used to get takeout here a lot, but it's a good place to eat in as well.  I'm not a sushi guy, but I've been told that theirs is very good!
What would a week be without Los Agaves for dinner or lunch?

So delicious, we are here twice today! We had the East Beach roasted veggie salad, and turkey sandwich. Although simple fair, the turkey was delicious, and the veggies had just the right amount of char.   The environment is loud, so it was a great place to dine with an infant. When she got fussy, no one noticed. Open atmosphere with serve yourself water and utensil station.   A great, casual, and health lunch spot in the middle of State Street.

My family loves this beautiful place, the service is excellent and very friendly. The omelette maker lady super attentive to service. Buffet is incredible, desserts so Yummy strawberry  with chocolate my favorite:)

I've known about Zaytoon since I was practically a kid, but I never had the appreciation for it until now (my palate has since improved lol). Recently I went there for my birthday dinner with my boyfriend and mom, and I wouldn't have wanted to spend the evening anywhere else.   For drinks, I ordered a Sunrise cocktail. I love spicy anything, so this blend of fruit with a spicy twist was absolutely right up my alley. My mom LOVED their wine selection (definitely a wine mom), and her favorite wine was even on their list (Opolo). They have an unequaled and high quality cocktail menu, with drinks I've never seen or heard of before. It was nice to be able to branch out of my usual safe-zone (bloody marys, vodka sodas, and margaritas) and try something new. Plus, their presentation is exceptional.   The foooooood though! I have no words. Their vegetarian options are mouthwatering. I ordered the vegetarian feast, which I split with my mom. Initially I was a little worried it wasn't going to be enough food for me (I'm a ravenous beast), but as we finished up everything I realized I was perfectly satisfied! Anything more and I would have been pushed over the edge.   If you're not willing to commit to a whole feast of food, their cheese sambosik is a MUST TRY. I swear I could have just eaten those and been happy.   100/100 experience. I'm going to have to go here more often! Or at least order take out 3-4 times a week.

Love the pizza and service BUT Is it me or are the pizza sizes changing? The Mega hasn't been a mega the last few times we have ordered  pizza and we've always ordered a mega and know what it should look like.   even though we order a mega it looks more like a regular large.
Great meal for a very fair price. Excellent service with friendly staff. Not fancy but lots of good choices that are well prepared. Can't go wrong here.

AMAZING FOOD! I make an amazing pesto pasta, but Cadiz makes a pesto dish that even rivals mine. My fiancé got the paella which was really lovely and flavorful. I wish it was a little spicier, but overall I really enjoyed everything we ordered. I plan on returning.
just had a western burger with grilled jalapenos.... it was delicious... foods great, but the service was just okay. forgot salad dressing, forgot silverware, and 5 dollars for chips and salsa???????

Benchmark accepted and then cancelled my UberEats order. When I called to ask why, I was told, 'the app won't load on our end'. Okay, can I place an order? Sure, visit our website to order using Grubhub. I tried, but Benchmark wasn't accepting orders. I recognize COVID is challenging for small businesses, but help us help you...

Our first experience at Primo was great. The servers were nice, and good at their job. Beautifully decorated, a real fun vibe going on in there. The food was all delicious... They did everything right. The short rib pasta was so good! And the prices were very reasonable, better than what you would think. Will definitely be back.

As told by local friends, this is a Santa Barbara come up! It's about time they opened up a seafood restaurant/bar like this one! There are several seafood like places like this in the LA/OC area & we so happened to be nearby.  * Tip: - Come here hungry - Do not wear your favorite clothing or anything white - If you have cuts on your hands, cover them  * Ordered: - Cajun fries (3/5 stars): Big potato like wedges.  - Calamari (4/5 stars): I was not expecting their sauce in it. This was a pleasant surprise to the calamari because it's usually given dry & salty elsewhere, but they add some type of sauce that tastes similar to BBQ. - 1lb. of jumbo white shrimp with their drunken special sauce, & mild (4/5 stars): Compared to another order that was medium, we think mild is a lot better to be able to enjoy the food.  * Extras: - Salt & pepper - lemon  Use both to squeeze & dip your fries & shrimp in. Don't forget to have fun with the ketchup!  It's a fun seafood concept. Be prepared for a mess & thankfully their sinks are nearby & not just in the bathrooms. Clean ambience & no lines for a Saturday evening.
We celebrated my birthday here this year. Our first post-covid restaurant experience. The macadamia crusted seabass was delicious!

Delicious! The tapas was so good and the restaurant was very accommodating in allowing me to get my food to go as my gal was not feeling well. We got the caprese, arancini, chickpea hummus and grilled steak skewers to start with. This was plenty to share for 2 but we had to get the seafood paella which was amazing! Next time we will have to dine inside as I was really impressed with the interior atmosphere.

Vegetarian friendly comfort food. We've been frequenting Dave's Dogs ever since we discovered their food truck over on Milpas. Whenever we'd have a softball game, we'd grab a dog afterwards. As a vegetarian, I'm not a big dog eater, but these guys have converted me big time! When they opened up a location on Turnpike, only a few blocks from our house, life got dangerous.   My favs are the veggie Hawaiian, the veggie Mac 'n cheese, the veggie corn dog and you can't go wrong with the cheese curds or deep fried Oreos. Now mind you...you don't want this EVERY night. Well, you might WANT it, but I don't advise it, you'll hurt yourself. But, it is happiness incarnate, so only go if you're prepared to start a new addiction!!

We went on seafood buffet night and I thought it was great. My wife didn't but she was tired and not really that hungry.  They had a full range of everything (sushi, tataki, shellfish. pasta, dessert).   The service was nice despite a language barrier.  We had a nice setting by the fireplace. The place had great ambience.  It is in the Four Seasons (to which I would not return).  I did not have much in the way of every day fare so I don't know if I would like it on non-seafood buffet night but as for this trip I give it a smiley face.

I can tell you, I've seen the progress of the building of this restaurant and have had some insider information. I'm so looking forward to the grand opening, I assure you all this place is going to be Great!

I got an 8-piece order of fried chicken at the deli, and I asked for fewer breasts as I mistook the thighs he was putting in for breasts. Well, the guy snapped angrily at me 'you get 2 thighs, 2 legs, 2 wings, and 2 breasts' and proceeded to literally throw each piece of chicken in the bag. The other guy standing there chimed in 'that's it - that's what you get.' Well, if you can't be polite and reasonable, what YOU don't get is my business. Why should I spend more at Gelson's for that kind of treatment? Lazy Acres is far better. Renaud's bakery is the only thing Gelson's has going for them.

Taco Tuesday and I receive such a pretty platter of 3 petite tacos. And then I tasted them. Veggie, Fish and Asada. Poor quality, tough, sinewy meat and a pile of bland unseasoned veggies. Even the fish taco was chewy! And not a bit of seasoning on anything. They offer two bland sauces. The red bottle is just bizarre and the green bottle is sour. I don't know what went wrong. How do you mess up tacos?! How do you mess up salsa?

An absolute treat! Everything here tastes amazing! My birthday had a wonderful start to it by dining here with mimosas, shrimp & grits, and their cinnamon roll. So rich & heavenly. Everyone is so kind & efficient here. Cute ambience with the bar area and back patio. If you're looking for a yummy brunch spot, look no further!

We have walked by this place a million times and finally tried it out.  The bartender was very friendly and gave us a generous pour on the wine.  It's a great space which is run by the same people from Crush Cakes.  Check it out.
Not sure why they have such low score --- still excellent food. Update continues to show great foodstuff

My wife and I ate hear for dinner (so many of the yelp reviews made it seem like it was for breakfast only).  Dinner was great.  My wife had the fried chicken salad and I had the white cheddar burger with caramelized onions and fried pickles - bread and butter, not dill :-(.  Service was excellent and desert was good too.  Highly recommend.

We went on a beautiful blue day in October and it was glorious sitting outside and enjoying such sweet fresh seafood. We ordered the lobster roll and uní and I highly recommend both. The order line was long for late afternoon but went fairly quickly. Anytime is seafood time! There is plenty of outside seating too.

My husband and I have a long list of places that we love to visit whenever we're in Santa Barbara (these days, to visit my brother). Deviating from the list can be a bit trial-and-error because if we don't love a place, we feel like we missed an opportunity to eat at one of our favorites.   Fear not, though, as Bree'osh exceeded everyone's expectations!  It's in a nice spot in Montecito with some outdoor seating and a little parklet area. They were very organized with spaced markers on the ground for distancing. Inside there's just enough room for one party to order at a time. They have a screen up and the payment dongle is on the customer side so it was an easy transaction.  I got a green tea - I could tell from the boxes that it would be high quality and it was. A nice pyramid teabag with just the right amount of leaves and space for them to expand. It sounded like the coffee was pretty good too.  My brother and I both ordered the Croque Madame - which was a good size with a nice side salad. It was delicious - just the right amounts of flaky brioche, creamy bechamel and the egg on top was nice and jammy. My brother felt like they could have used a slightly saltier cheese, which is a fair note.   My husband got a bacon and egg brioche roll and said it was like a love letter to the egg. He said the scrambled egg a great custardy texture.  Really delicious food. We'll definitely be back and I think I'll try a tartine next time.

Omakase Oh My God-se! By far the best in SB to date.  10 stars for the artistic presentation and 9.9 stars for the flavor (I was hoping for yellowtail belly :(  ).  Highly and enthusiastically recommended.

I've driven past here dozens of times but today was the first time I ever turned off the main road and wound my way down to the Cold Spring Tavern. What an amazing place! It's an old stagecoach stop, and retains all of the charm (and rickety wood construction) of its historic past.  Outside, a stream cascades down the rocks at the bottom of the canyon, and the building is surrounded by lush growth.  The restaurant is divided up into intimate rooms, with great mood lighting, and views of the woods.  Lunch offerings include salads and sandwiches and burgers. Both the chiliburger and venison steak sandwich were excellent, as were the accompanying fries and salad. Apparently the quiche is amazing, with a wonderful pastry crust, but the person before me got the last order!  Service is friendly, and the staff are definitely having a good time working there.

Good spot for our Sunday brunch after staying at the Hyatt.  The location was great.  I highly recommend the giant peach bellinis if they are on the specials menu.  My friends enjoyed their mimosas as well.  Our server was patient and friendly.

We decided to try another shot at some Baja Mexican food while on vacay and quickly decided to try this place out.   I'm happy we came early otherwise we might not have found s seat. The dining room is tiny with a handful of tables and only 2-3 booths.   Their speciality seems to be their burritos. They're giant and messy. The price point for a grab your own salsa, drink and table seemed high. Flavors we're ok. Nothing spectacular. Salsa was tasty. Guacamole was good. Service ok. But sorry to say this place won't be on our list of repertoires.
Never been unhappy with a meal from Los Agaves! Need to try them for take out. Great salsa

This is my go-to coffee place in Santa Barbara. I last visited here six years ago and liked it. I even thought about it when passing on the freeway prior to doubling back and visiting the same day.  When I last visited years ago, there was no covid and you could have free coffee up to an hour. Not sure this is still the case.  We stopped in Santa Barbara solely to get coffee. When we arrived, there are two kiosks outside to submit and pay for an order. You can also order inside from an employee. The set-up is order on one side, pick up on the other. It's convenient.  During covid, there are clear barriers up and people wearing masks. All customers had masks.  I was tired and had to drive, so ordered a double shot vanilla iced latte and my wife had iced tea. We liked everything. Prices are a bit higher because it's really good coffee. We didn't have any food.  It's really relaxing to sit at an outside table and enjoy coffee. Nice we can do it again.  People were passing by with tacos from the nearby place and saw a few women carrying yoga mats to a yoga class.  I can't imagine visiting Santa Barbara again without at least once coming here. You can take your green logo cups and straws and your star loyalty points somewhere else because I'll be here instead.
Support our local business. Go to Rudy's in downtown Santa Barbara. Their burritos are to die for

Food was ok. My potatoes were really burnt though. I asked for a iced mocha but the lady said the espresso machine was off already and she couldn't  turn it back on?? Ok anyway food was ok but the customer service was horrible. The lady acted like she was in a rush and there was only another couple there in the entire cafe! And when I went to pay she just threw the money at me maybe she got mad I used my axxess card bc she threw that also. Couple more I would like to try on the menu but I don't think I will be back due to bad hospitality
A reliably excellent cup of coffee, with some nice food items available.  Glad they are in business, it's been a favorite meeting place for at least 10 years.

First time trying this place, but I was very unimpressed. I ordered a veggie Burrito. The tortilla wasn't soft and fluffy and it was very average to below average compared to some other veggie burritos I've had in town. Guacamole didn't taste freshly made and it was $9.50! I realize this is Santa Barbara but in my experience, it was very overpriced for a very average meal. Decor could also use a little sprucing up.

The other restaurants nearby were very crowded so we decided to try out this place. The food is good but nothing fantastic. The one thing I did not like was they added to much rice to their burritos. The salsa bar is good but they don't offer really hot salsa.

Simply the best. Pretty blown away by both the presentation and taste of their signature drinks. I think it's worth the price given how much care is put into crafting the drinks and especially given how much I typically pay for an alcoholic cocktail - and these taste even better! Note that their signature drinks can't be take away because of the presentation, so plan on drinking them there.   I've been here twice in one week and have gotten the Jupiter's Forest, Bella Rose, and Burnout. My favorite was probably the Jupiter's Forest because I'm a sucker for cold brew based drinks and the flavors were so well-balanced and unique, but the other two were amazing as well. The Bella Rose was my 2nd favorite - it's floral and matcha flavors were so light and fresh. The Burnout was a bit less unique than the other two in my opinion, but it tasted like some of the best hot coffee I've ever had.   Pro tip: make sure to check-in on Yelp to get a BOGO coupon! And it includes the signature drinks!
