A pretty kick-ass little food truck. Thanks y'all for coming to Goleta on Wednesdays. This last visit we got pulled pork sandwiches; this time they had a bunch of crispy fried onions on top, don't remember getting those last time. Anyhow, good addition. The bacon-cheese hushpuppies were fabulous & dangerous. Light, fluffy & mouthwatering. The ribs were pretty tasty but definitely on the scrawny side. Last time we ordered 2 ribs, they made up for the scrawny size by throwing another little guy in. This is a good policy! After all, 2 ribs for $5 is the same price as a pulled pork sandwich so if this had been someone's meal, wah-wah! Wish you guys did dessert!
Second time at Jane, it was just as good as the first if not better! Very nice atmosphere, excellent friendly service and delicious food and drink. What more can you ask for. Thanks Jane!

I selected the Mar Monte hotel form the reviews I found on yelp and trip advisor, which suggested that this hotel was a great mix of location, value and vibe. We stayed in a deluxe king room with a mountain view (a.k.a Motel 6) which was pretty descent.  The room was pretty small and there was nothing really special about it.  Parking was cramped and there was no mini bar.  However, the bed and pillows were extremely comfortable, the TV was fantastic, and the robes were comfy.  A special note: the black out drapes, which allowed u to sleep in super late, very nice.  The location was indeed good, directly across from the beach walking distance to the Zoo and minutes to State Street.  We enjoyed the hot tub and the pool, although getting there is a bit awkward.  Reasonable experience, however next time we visit Santa Barbara we will probably try somewhere else.

Such a fun place!! Get's very busy and is pet friendly.  Seating is very limited inside as place is small.  Worth the wait!  Everything is amazing!  Lots of seating outside, we had a group of four so we had yo steal a chair from another table!  Food comes pretty quickly and the orange juice is fresh squeezed!!!

Beer http://www.yelp.com/biz_photos/SZU9c8V2GuREDN5KgyHFJw?select=gkm6pFW982dQMFsA3hUjsw#hIw22R-lt-ejZ7keOg6xxQ  Crab out http://www.yelp.com/biz_photos/SZU9c8V2GuREDN5KgyHFJw?select=gkm6pFW982dQMFsA3hUjsw#Te3wy_G0PeSmeaMYNOkCVA  Chowder http://www.yelp.com/biz_photos/SZU9c8V2GuREDN5KgyHFJw?select=gkm6pFW982dQMFsA3hUjsw#DATPzeYehB6wwQeObqN35A  Massive Crab http://www.yelp.com/biz_photos/SZU9c8V2GuREDN5KgyHFJw?select=gkm6pFW982dQMFsA3hUjsw#STsGEdvWWICHi1HDOHu89g  Ciopono crab leg shrimp scallops clam mussel bread bowl Parmesan cheese  Rock crab special 3lb

Fantastic pizza and salad. The salad was going to be for my girlfriend but it was so huge we had to share it. Then we got the salty surfers pizza, however I added anchovies and made it even saltier one. Looking forward to coming back here
Good burgers, fries, onion rings, and milkshakes. Meatier burgers with that charbroiled taste different than in n out. Clean and well managed.

The perfect place to bring a date or a group of friends for a fantastic breakfast/lunch experience.  They also make their own pastries, and their scones are phenomenal, all of them.  The banana kalua french toast and the breakfast quesadilla are the best items on their menu, in my opinion.  Plus, the staff and management are friendly.  Give this place a try!

Have always seen this place and finally tried it. The service was good, our waiter was prompted and helpful. The food was ok. Way to overpriced for what it is. I had the grilled muscle plate, it had my boyfriend and I cracking up with how small the portions are.  Luckily I filled up on complementary bread and salad and had some of my boyfriends steak.  It's a one and done place for me.
This place is lovely to sit and people watch on state street with friends or at the community tables.  All the food is so tasty and presented beautifully as well! It's a fun new place to try!

This place has everything you can ask for.  Careless staff, horrendous custom brewed beer, and bad food.  Compounded by the fact that this is really the only true 'sports bar' on state street its a real shame.  Id take a movie theater hot dog and a warm keystone over what they serve here.  Like the movie theater hot dog that hasnt been eaten in 3 months and is wrinkled.  0.7/5 stars my brother says try some water and playing shuffle board.

I was recommended this restaurant by our hotel and was pretty excited to try something new. I saw that they were doing outdoor seating and I thought, ' perfect! '  We had done out for seating at FisHouse and Brophy Bros, and felt safe with the measures that were in place for COVID. However, the table seating at flor de maiz left me worried.   There are two lines of tables outside on the sidewalk. There's space between the tables, but probably JUUUUUST 6 ft, if that. The problem is all the people walking in the street are riiiiiight next to you, and some of them aren't wearing masks.   Like as soon as we sat down, this group walking on the sidewalk with no masks split between us, half on one side of us, half on the other. And they were shouting at each other from across the row of tables, a lady practically talking INTO my face.   Personally, I was counting down the time until we were leaving from then on. It's just not safe, especially sitting on that outer row of tables on the sidewalk. To top it off, people are walking up to the restaurant without masks on so you get clumps of people just waiting to be seated dans masks. I just would pick somewhere else if you're worried about COVID.   Food wise, it was okay. We had the chicken mole, and it was good. Drinks are pretty light. Service was good though, no complaints there.

Pretty good for a fast Mexican meal. It was extremely crowded so get there early if you can. Also, this is not a sit down restaurant. You order at the window and find a table. Mole sauce was some of the best I've had. Food was fresh and salsa bar was nice.

Best Italian in town. Every item on the menu is delicious. Small, homey dining room. A mouth-watering desert case. The owner, Renato, is the greatest. He catered our wedding and absolutely nailed it. Guests were raving about the food. Especially the pumpkin, sage, browned butter ravioli. The cheese table was amazing too. Just go.
Good food, friendly service, and the stiffest drinks in town. There's a good reason it's lasted so long.

This seems to be a popular hotel for corporate people to stay in. The site does have an ocean view, but you are on low floors so the views aren't  worth photographing. Just like most beach hotels,this is solid 3 if you rate it by actual room details and lack of perks. Staff and service is about a 4. The hotel is a nice area to be on close to the each and away from too much chaos. It sitll close to a lot of shops and restaurants. I would stay here again for work but that's it

Food isn't great. Service is ok. Huge portions. There's a salsa bar with 3-4 sauces and a few pickles. There are much better Mexican food options in SB. I think their sister restaurant close to Carrillo is better.
Good casual spot for Indian food. Got a free mango lassi with check in. I also liked the veggie curry. Get the curry waffle fries!

I cannot believe I took so long to come here. It truly exceeded my expectations. We sat outside at the expansive outdoor section that engulfs the building. It was happy hour, as a bonus.  We tried the $5 HH Cabernet and Turkey sandwich and fries. We also got a small side of macaroni and cheese. The sandwich and fries were enough for two people and quite tasty. It included a generous portion of bacon on top and avocado. The Mac 'n Cheese was exceptional, not too cheesy but an ample amount was there. The food was piping hot and the service was superb.   As we continue to be safer outdoors, this is a great option for a day or night out! Hell, it's a great option even if you're not seeking an outdoor dining experience. It's great for large parties or an intimate night out with a friend or partner.   There is on street parking in the area. See website for hours, location and menu.
Came in with great expectations.  Very disappointed. The baklava was stale. Service poor. Very overpriced.

Take my picture Jeanine.......because you won't be seeing me again.   Total rip off.   Pretty disappointed with our visit here. I've been to the other location and really enjoyed it but our meal here was pretty weak.   The service was slow and really unorganized. Everybody was just standing around in the parking lot and nobody explained how the ordering system worked. Once we got our food, we waited for silverware and then again to find anybody to bring us hot sauce.   Worse than the service was the absolutely absurd prices. I had the farmers's omelette. It's a veggie and cheese omelette....NOT WORTH ALMOST $18......what a joke. And.....to be honest it wasn't even that good. It was fine at best.
Had a great salad with Ahi  tuna. Very tasty. Had key lime pie for dessert. Perfect!

I love this place. It's a great restaurant for a date as it's small and intimate but I also go on my own a fair amount and sit at the bar while I eat. The staff are friendly but unobtrusive and being able to watch the magic going on in the kitchen (which is open from the bar) adds to the experience. They have an interesting list of beers from Europe and are knowledgable about what their products. The wine list is great too but the real jewel is the food. I have never been disappointed in a meal there and I've been there a fair few times this year. The foie gras torchon is one of my favourites as is their pork belly. They change the menu regularly to reflect the local growing seasons so ask your server what they suggest. And be adventurous! They always have a fantastic homemade ice cream for dessert as well. Their 'Captain Crunch cereal milk ice-cream' was great.

The short story: Don't ever have afternoon tea here. Waste of your money. If you want a nice view come here and order the overpriced drinks but ordered at the bar. The service is seriously lacking for a 5 star hotel.   Long story: Came here for my bday celebration and has the afternoon tea service. It was a joke. Tea was served in a French press (yes, the stuff u make coffee in), no teaspoon (we had to ask for a spoon...what is tea without a iteapot and teaspoon?), sugar was packets (yes like the ones at a diner and no sugar cubes) sandwiches were stale and were tasteless but pastries were good and the tea selection was limited (the didn't have 2 of the 8 or so teas in stock). Is it worth the $60/p.p, No? Go elsewhere for your afternoon tea party. The view is great but soured by the sub-par to average wait service. This is a 5-star hotel (supposedly) but the service was for a 2-star or less hotel. Super disappointing. This place has lots of potential but somehow the details and execution of service and food is just completely off. Which is sad.

JUST NO.  Haven't been here in YEARS. Decided to give it a try.  First off, FYI- you can call and make a reservation, but not on the actual day you need them. You have to do it ahead of time by at least a day.  They are dog friendly on the patio, which I appreciate.  They are open at full capacity inside now, but still have outdoor dining.  Being a group of 6, with no reservation, we were sat pretty quickly outside.   Problem started with the bathrooms. The sewer smell was so incredibly horrible, we could barely breathe, and we had masks on! It wasn't the cleanest either. I was informed by a server that they are having issues with the city about it, but it put a bad 'taste' in my mouth, quite literally, upon arrival.   Walking back to my table, I started to see what a dump the place had become. It's filthy, I got grossed out  walking back to our table. The bread and salsa station is filthy and is actually falling backwards as both the legs up front are off the ground. Food and trash all over the ground, everywhere, and it was only 6pm on a Saturday.   The front entrance of the restaurant is where the kitchen is, but there is a table at the front for their to-go orders right when you walk in the front door, and it was just complete chaos and looked horrible, dirty, and unorganized.   Now the food. It was gross. If you came for the stiff drink, go for it, but pass on the food. Trust me.  Steak was over cooked, I even asked the waitress how their chef was on his temps so I could plan accordingly. I was assured that if anything they are a lower temp than normal, and I could always cook it up more if need be. The baked potatoes are old, precooked and then reheated so much so that the entire first half of the potato is hard, dark, and inedible.   My friend ordered the burnt ends sandwich and was stoked because she had never heard of that before and loves the burnt ends. But they don't serve a burnt ends sandwich. We were informed by the manager that they don't have enough to do that for every sandwich and they just chop up meat from the prime rib and tri-tip to mix in with whatever burnt ends they have. Here's a thought, how about you just only serve the burnt ends, and when you are out, you are out. Elevate your menu like you care about what goes out to your patrons. OR explain that when someone orders so they are not disappointed. When she discussed this with the manager, there wasn't an apology, he just took the plate and they went and fried up the pieces in a pan and brought it back on the same bread it was served on. It was disgusting.  Not once did anyone ask how our food was for me to correct it quickly, and we had to be somewhere, so I just doused the steak in salt and ate it. $25 for a steak, I can do better at home.   When we told the waitress about our experience and the frustration with the food, she apologized but nothing else. You'd think they would have comp'd something. Anything. Or had the manager come back over himself.  Sliders and fries were good.   Clam chowder has always been good.   But nothing else was.   It's a shame, the history and nostalgia of this place is what made me want to bring our friends and family to experience. Hell, I even was one of the very first staff to open Harry's, under the new management of John Scott.   We won't be returning, not unless it's for a stiff drink at the bar.
Happy hour is pretty good.  Service is attentive.  The burgers were really good.  The beer flight was ok.  I would definitely come back - the portions are decent.

We had breakfast at Chad's this morning and it did not disappoint. We were appreciative and impressed by their attentive service and delicious food. Their complimentary mini muffins to start are so good! We love Chad and everything he brings to our community. Thank you!

We came on a Saturday at around 1pm. In a group of 4, we wanted to sit and enjoy some margaritas. We were quickly seated, but once seated, we must have been forgotten about because it took over 20 minutes to just get water. I went to the host stand to ask for our waitress so we could get waters. When the waitress came she said 'you were looking for me?' We finally got some cocktails 40 minutes later. They were not very good. The margarita felt like it was made with the way too sweet premix you get from the grocery store. The staff seemed to have helped everyone but us. They do give you free chips and salsa. I would not come back though.

Stopped in at 6am on my way out of town, the staff was friendly and welcoming. They made me a fresh,delicious, and huge Ham & Cheese Croissant and a great cup of Tea for my road trip and sent me off with a smile =)

I was walking around state street and saw Jersey Mike's subs. I was interested to see what they had to offer walked in was immediately greeted by the friendly staff. I ordered the original Italian (#13) but got turkey instead of ham. They loaded my sandwich with meat. I got a free cookie because it was my first time. My sandwich was great and priced so reasonable! I'll be telling all my friends about it.

Another Santa Barbara restaurant week choice that did not disappoint!! We ended up ordering off the regular menu because the choices were too enticing to pass up. Also the corkage is free for your first bottle - that's a bonus

My wife and I came here to celebrate our one year anniversary a few months back and we had a really nice time.  We took the train into SB and walked around town and then took a Lyft to the hotel.  I wish I had known sooner that we could have come here and just hung out and enjoyed the day here at the pool or the beach or the spa.  I guess I didn't realize how much of a resort this place was laid out to be.  Next year we're coming back for a few days and we'll probably skip the SB visit and just spend our days here relaxing.  The staff was amazing and so welcoming.  We had some champagne upon arrival, wine left for us in the room, and chocolates delivered to us as a gift for our anniversary!  Loved the handwritten note, and personal touches all throughout our time here.  The restaurant and cafe are fantastic, the pools look awesome, the cabanas are private, and they have two jacuzzis.  They have an adults-only pool which is much more low key so you can avoid the kiddos splashing and such.  There were a lot of kids, families, and parties going on while we were there.  It made it feel much more like a resort than a retreat, but not a huge issue for us.  The spa is enormous and though we didn't get massages last time we will next time.  We checked-in early and were given an ADA accessible room, which was supposed to be a bit more private but didn't have the opulence and features I was really looking forward to.  Next time we'll hold out for the more luxurious suite.  The place feels rich and clean. They have nice tennis courts, easy walking paths to the beach and a small nature area that's worth walking around.  The customer service really was great and despite being less than 100% stoked with our room the experience and special-ness of this place made it feel like a great getaway.  Again, we're already planning our trip for next year, and that should tell you enough about our overall feelings toward this.  Do experience the restaurants on-site, and plan to just hang out around the grounds and enjoy.

I came here last night, first time since they originally opened. I am aware that the restaurant changed hands a couple of times since my last review.  I was pleasantly surprised by the attentiveness of the waitstaff despite how crowded the place was. We showed up with a party of 9 people and on a crowded night it only took about 40 minutes to get a table. Honestly, not bad. There was a table of 4 waiting and they were getting frustrated as they waited with an open 4 top in the corner.  The atmosphere hasn't changed, still has the sliding dividers that allows an 8 top to adjust to 2x 4 tops. They simplified the menu greatly, offering standard Korean BBQ fare. The quality and quantity is OK and generous, respectively. We got Bulgogi, Brisket, Miso Marinated Pork Belly, pot stickers, and Veal Intestine. Everything was OK to really good. The Bulgogi was a little on the salty side (not enough sweetness in the marinade) and I would skip the miso marnated pork belly as the marinade doesn't really improve the flavor and messes with the texture in a bad way. The Brisket was OK. The Veal Intestine was really good. It had a clean finish and a good juiciness to it. If you aren't a fan of innards, you should skip this, but if you like intestine, you will LOVE this.  The banchan was vastly improved as well, with copious helpings and quick refills (although we did have to ask once at the beginning of the meal, but after that though they were on top of it).  However, at the end of the day, I can't give this place more than 3 stars. The food is fine, but it isn't special. Same with the atmosphere, it feels hallow and cold despite the use of warm colors and literal hot plates in the tables. Maybe I am just spoiled by the great places in LA.  A few suggestions:   iGrill, you are a Korean Restaurant right? Maybe you should carry some Korean beers instead of only Japanese beers? I know a lager is a lager, but there is something special about sitting down with a Hite or OB and enjoying some BBQ.  The pot-stickers... Korean Mandu is not hard to make. There is no excuse for ANY Asian place that serves frozen pot-stickers except for maybe Panda. These are just frozen wholesale pot stickers that have been deep fried.  When you replace the hot grills with the new grills, I would recommend waiting until the meat is cleared off of the hot dirty grill by the customer first. Sliding the cooking meat onto a cold grill is just cruel! Just talk to the table and let them know that you will be replacing the grill top after the current round is cooked. This gives us time to cook the pile of meat and then eat it while the new grill is getting up to temp.  Pricing: Look, iGrill is priced right for your K-Town, popular Korean BBQ joint. Unfortunately, they aren't as good, they aren't as quick, and the atmosphere is as electric as the places down in LA. $17 per person minimum is pretty steep for what is just OK BBQ. I know SB is expensive, but you can step up the atmosphere to make the price tag worth it. K-pop isn't an acceptable replacement for that Korean BBQ feel iGrill is lacking.  If you are interested in Korean BBQ, this place will do you right for about $3-$4 more than you should be paying. If you like Korean BBQ, this place will tide you over between trips to K-town. If you LOVE Korean BBQ, maybe you should just go down to LA instead.

Love this place!!! The ambience is very much like French brasserie and I love the zinc bar. Plus they have an outdoor seating area which is nice for Santa Barbara.  The staff is very friendly and service is good!  The menu is just right with the amount of items offered. My favorite is the entrecote with fries. The salad and the sauce that come with this dish are exactly the same they serve at Le Relais de l'Entrecote restaurants in Paris. So good!!!    And, btw, their champagne by the glass is reasonably priced.  My friends and I really enjoy spending a nice long lunch here (just like Paris).

OMG the first bite of the pork belly was one of the best most amazing bites I've ever had! I am so happy we got to try this! This is my experience with Bao and I am now a fan! The carbonara is like Heaven in your mouth so rich and creamy and satisfying I highly recommend! Fresh delicious and super bomb food I can't even put it into words! The fried KFC one literally made me say Holy shit! Had to put a dollar in the sweat jar! This food is fire!!! So FN good! Pure bliss! Flavor bombs!

I don't like to leave negative reviews like this....  but then again, I guess I also don't like to be grossed out by my breakfast!  sorry!  I have been wanting to try this place for awhile as we live pretty close, and it was such a disappointment.  I think my freezer makes a better breakfast.  I will say that we were completely unaware of any specials that they had until we were walking out and saw the sign with them listed.  Would have been nice if they could have made mention of it, as nothing on the menu was very appealing at all.  My husband got a combo meal that was right out of the freezer, and I got an awful breakfast burrito.  Was a killer looking across the street at the Daily Grind while muscling down the burrito as DG makes an amazing bacon breakfast burrito.

The pastrami on rye -- in the same league as Langer's in L.A.  and a little cheaper, slightly greasier, so friggin' good.  The place only seats about 15 people comfortably, so when it gets busy just sit on a stranger's lap and maybe get elbowed in the head a few times.  You didn't order that but it comes with the meal.

This past weekend we booked a stay at the Hyatt Santa Barbara through hotels.com.  Customer service with hotels.com was excellent but once we arrived at the hotel there seemed to be some confusion in what we booked and what we received upon arrival.   First, WHO KNEW HYATT SANTA BARBARA IS PET FRIENDLY. Yep we were able to bring our little pooch and he enjoy the weekend with us.   At check in we were greeted by Amanda at the front desk. Any issue that were not clear or incorrect through the hotels.com, Amanda cleared them up quickly while providing excellent and very friendly customer service.  Fyi.  This hotel is hugh and many rooms faces the ocean.  But the portion that is dog friendly, is not the portion that faces the ocean.  Its the portion of the hotel that on the backside of the hotel NOT facing the ocean.  The room was very nice, clean and roomy.   We had an issue with our first room that was on the back side of the hotel facing the freeway, wayyyyy to noisy.  We phoned front desk in the morning and they quickly switched us to a much nicer and quieter room.  And i have to confess, we all sleep way better the following night.  Even the pooch. This Hyatt is very beautiful, very well kept and in most rooms comfortable and relaxing.   info you might find helpful. parking here cost $26.95 a night.  Having said that, there is plenty of parking all around the hotel on the side streets for free. Just sayen...also if you have a pet, you cannot bring your pet to the pool/jacuzzi area. Only major downside, i was so looking forward to the jacuzzi.

Great place for locals, not gonna lie. Best drinks in the Mesa area with an awesome bartender crew. Also try their breakfast menu. However ***** beware of the young blonde hostess called Carlee (sic?). She acts like she wants to be anywhere but there. Dont be offended, just dont tip.

Lovely lunch at this lovely spot!  Busy with walk in traffic, there are 20 or so seats out front in a nicely decorated semi enclosed cabana. We enjoyed our server,  who explained all the dishes we asked about- we took her recommendations for sharing a salad & main course and topped lunch off with a stellar slab of chocolate torte and Italian coffees.  Can't wait to return and try items from the deli counter and gelato bar

Came for a dinner and we were lucky to have arrived a bit earlier, because the line never seemed to go away during the dinner service. They have indoor and outdoor patio services and they offer Asian fusion cuisine with a dominant fusion with American and Japanese.   Our server Amanda was very professional and despite running several tables at the same time, followed up with us adequately and handled the busy night very well. Kudos to her professionalism and good work that night.   Ambiance-wise, I liked the outdoor seating more than the indoor as the indoor area was very loud. Despite the cold, there were several heat lamps outside so it wasn't too bad until it reached later in the night.   Food-wise, we ordered a mix of items from their menu. Sushi bar Nigiri - Hon Maguro, Kanpachi, Hotate, Ebi, Tamago, Ankimo, Unagi. Hot items - Saigon wings, XO grilled shrimp, Miso Yuzu fresh wild salmon, and handcrafted gyoza: shrimp and crab w/ ponzu. To me, I thought the food was pretty average. While visually appealing, the food's quality did not amaze me that much.   To start, the gyoza wrapper barely had a textural crunch to it. It was just a soggy wrapper wrapped around a piece of shrimp with crab meat filling. While a nice piece of shrimp and crab meat, the soggy wrapper made the appetizer a bit unappealing. The Saigon wings were nicely sauced and moist, but the exterior crunch was also not as crunchy as one would have wanted. A bit too soggy from the sauce or not deep fried enough. For the XO grilled shrimp, this was quite nice. The noodles used were ramen noodles made in the style of garlic noodles. The seasoning of the noodles and flavors were nicely developed and the shrimps provided were pretty adequate. Not the XO sauce I was expecting, but at least the flavors were interesting to have. For the Miso Yuzu fresh wild salmon, it wasn't bad. The yuzu gravy sauce was critical for the item. The salmon itself was on the plainer side and without the yuzu gravy sauce, it was pretty boring to eat and not as delectable. The salmon was moist and a decent size was provided. For the accompanying mashed potatoes and vegetable sides, they were nicely done and can hold themselves up in the entrée. For the sushi bar items, I think I can avoid most of them in the future, because the quality of the raw items was more average than I would have expected. The kanpachi and hon maguro were brushed with their special sauce on top and hotate had Himalayan sea salt on top which kind of accentuates the food, but due to the lack of freshness/quality, there wasn't as much to accentuate. The Ebi was plain as is, unagi was made as expected, and the ankimo had green onions on top which didn't really cover the strong fishy taste. I didn't mind the ankimo and it was interesting to have, but the wife couldn't handle it. Not a negative on the restaurant part, but the ankimo also wasn't as fresh hence the strong fishy taste. The tamago was a miss for me, because it tasted really bland. There wasn't a strong sweet taste to it and all it had to offer was a thick slab of egg on top of rice.  Desserts-wise, we had their yuzu cheesecake and their Lemon Meyer Tartlet. The cheesecake wasn't bad, but it wasn't absolutely amazing. I totally thought the yuzu was going to be incorporated into the cheesecake, but it was a glaze on the plate instead. As such, it wasn't as spectacular, because it was just an average cheesecake slice. Visually, it was a bit more on the unappealing, because  the edges had the appearance of a yellow cake and not a smooth cheesecake. For the Meyer Lemon Tartlet, the lemon sabayon mousse had a nice texture, but overbearing tartness to it that the wife couldn't stomach to finish it all of. To me, it was nice, but I do agree that too much isn't preferred. For the lemon curd bottom, it was surprisingly lacking in lemon flavors. Despite palette rinsing, the lemon curd lacked in lemony flavors compared to the mousse.   All in all, average food all around. Nothing absolutely spectacular based on what we ordered except for the XO shrimp and salmon. The rest I could forgo and probably won't suggest a try unless you really want to. Worth a try for a date night out, but for serious eats or foodie adventure, I wouldn't suggest this site as it has a stronger emphasis on drinks and alcohol than food it appears.

I have never experienced such blatant ableism and ignorance in my life. The waitstaff here has never gone out of their way to be even somewhat pleasant as far as I'm concerned, but they were beyond rude today. My mother and I came in and ordered some food which was pretty good, being a vegetarian and finding tasty Mexican dishes is hard because it's the spices in the meat that really make the meal usually, but I trust Los Arroyos would never serve a bad meal. My mother, who is DISABLED, got up to use the restroom and I was sitting at the table enjoying my food a waitress rudely came up to me and demanded that I go get my mother out of the restroom because she'd been in there too long. She added that she and other coworkers had been knocking on the door constantly and she wouldn't come out. What?! Obviously if she was in there for a concerning amount of time I would go check on her, but y'all interrupt my meal to tell me to go knock on the bathroom door? So my mother leaves the restroom as soon as I say it's me and the whole front of the house is staring at us like it's crazy to have a disability and need more than 10 minutes on the restroom. The waitstaff here is rude, inattentive, ableist, and I will never come back to this location or suggest it to any of my friends. Absolutely appalling and unprofessional behavior.

Great pizza.  When it comes to call ahead-- absolutely Terrible service. Don't bother calling ahead to save time either. The team is utterly incompetent and desperately needs better training and oversight.
Cajun is not my favorite but, all the same the food is good. Fun place for an occasion. If you lucky, a magician will stop by your table.
Quaint little place for breakfast.  Service was prompt and friendly.  Coffee and water refills constant and food was right on spot.  Really like this place.

The pros: literally feet in sand facing beach, staff were efficient & friendly. Clam chowder was good & hot.  The cons...well I think it's fair to say this is a great place to enjoy some cocktails, beers, milkshakes & smoothies.  Fish tacos & calamari were ok. Not bad, but a bit disappointing.  Definitely next time we'll know this is a nice chillax near beach spot but good somewhere else in SB for main meal

I love this place! The eggplant special, the pad thai, the creative ice cream flavors. Contrary to the other reviews of people who are never satisfied unless a ruby red carpet is laid out for them upon arrival, I have never had bad service here ever. One of my fave places in SB.

Absolutely wonderful food, drinks and service! The staff here assisted my boyfriend in his proposal and after popping the question we celebrated at Angel Oak! We enjoyed wonderful food and everyone from the hostess to servers provided excellent service, friendliness and accommodations. Thank you again to everyone who made our night so wonderful!

I was sad to hear that they do not offer organic beef or turkey burgers... Alas, I opted for veg. as I normally do while dining out and got the veggie burger.  It was really excellent!  Got it with a super fresh salad with ranch- the ranch was perfect as well (which is pretty important in my world) :)  Maybe they can offer an organic meat option for a couple extra $$??   Service was very friendly and helpful.

Delicious! This place has everything you need for breakfast and lunch.   We had the Kahlua French toast which is made with challah bread and bananas, and the Breakfast Club Sandwich. It's a club sandwich with eggs, cheese, spinach, and onions with a side of delicious fingerling potatoes. We ordered a side of ham (which was big, like 3 pieces!), a side bowl of fruit, and a glass of grapefruit juice. Next time we will have to try their breakfast pastries. It's located in the plaza and it has both indoor and outdoor seating. We are definitely coming back!

We were reluctant to try Los Agaves because we are so fund of Los Arroyos. However, our friends made us give it a try. Boy are we glad we did. Amazing amazing amazing. The absolute best Mexican in town. Their dishes are out of this world. The owner is extremely charming and handsome. The place is very popular and for obvious reasons. Every dish we have tried, we have loved. The only complaint is that they are not closer to the Mesa.

My go-to place to eat, grab a beer and have a great time with friends. It's affordable and delicious and I have probably tried everything on their menu 3x and still love it. Brussels sprouts are the best in town (besides The Lark), nachos, truffle fries, veggie burger, and tacos are some of my favorites. Their happy hour is also incredible, 2-6p and 9p- close. Who can beat that? Their service is also always great.  Enjoy!

Ambiance/Service: The setting was nice.  It was dimly lit but not so dark that it seemed like they were trying to conserve electricity like so many other places.  From the beginning the hostess was very accommodating and rearranged two tables for my party of four to sit.  I enjoyed the mood set by light chatter and music in the background that was not overbearing.  Although there was a lady with a laugh that could wake the mummies of Egypt that made me cringe, I give this place an extra star on service for putting up with her.  As far as our service is concerned, it was good.  Our waitress was attentive and made sure we were doing ok throughout our stay.  Food/Drink: We went later in the evening for the drinks.  Unfortunately, the drinks are very expensive at $10-12 a pop.  On the plus side, they have a wide variety  of cocktails to choose from.  I went with the blood orange, habanero cocktail.  It was good but no bite to it, which is why I ordered it.  I followed that with an extra dirty vodka martini that was made well and tasted good.  My gf had the 'geisha' which was very sweet and very weak.  Wouldn't recommend that drink.  She followed that up with the 'prickly pear' cocktail which was significantly stronger and was good.  My other two guests had good ole whiskey and sodas which were way overpriced.  We had chips and salsa as an appetizer.  Nothing out of the ordinary, the salsa was good and had decent flavor.  One plus is that they brought us 4 complimentary appetizers (bruschetta with tomato and basil), very tasty and a nice touch.    Wrap-up: The place was decent and the atmosphere was nice but it wasn't anything extraordinary.  the prices were very high and my bill certainly reflected it.  Service was good but not good enough for me to return.  I'd probably consider stopping by again if nothing else was open.  Overpriced.

I love this place. It's perfect to hang out and meet people. There was karaoke going on in the front with a fireplace towards the back if you're cold. In the other room were darts and billiards. The beer selection was good. Overall, a very comfortable place where you're going to have a blast!

I had my wedding here in 2015. Although the actual hotel is gorgeous and the amenities in the room are wonderful, I wouldn't consider the price of the rooms worth it. More in the 600-700 range seems appropriate given the location and competitive pricing in the area. However, the three stars are actually for our wedding coordinator at the hotel who was a nightmare to work with. I was 21 when I got married and was treated like it through the whole process. Devin, the coordinator, treated my fiance and I like children because of our age and that he was still a student at UCSB. It was clear that she assumed we couldn't afford much by the way she interacted with us every step of the way and then just days before the wedding tried to undermine my decisions by changing a lot of last minute layout details that were contradictory to what we agreed upon or were paying for. She attempted to switch the chairs from formal wooden and embroidered chairs we agreed on to cheap plastic white chairs which caused another headache for me, an already stressed bride. This is one of many examples of undermining predetermined decisions. Overall, the hotel is wonderful and amazing but I wouldn't host an event here again because the lack of professionalism on the part of the event coordinating staff.  I do want to retroactively add to my review that they responded by offering us compensation with a free two night stay and discounted the overall cost of our wedding. Although these were thoughtful and agreed upon compensations, they do not override the memories from my wedding day that I can never get back. We are grateful they worked with us on these issues but I do want to make other brides aware that they may also encounter issues although they can be worked out with staff if they do arise. We did enjoy a wonderful return visit for our first wedding anniversary and would consider visiting other Belmond locations in the future. All good businesses should see to it that a guest is compensated for a difficult or disappointing experience so they were right in seeing to that.

was one of those days in santa barbara where a warm bowl of ramen just hits the spot. windy and 65 degrees. i never really go out of my way for ramen but i'll honestly be back, my ramen was super good and just enough to fill me up for lunch. i can't compare it to any other asian places around the area cause i typically don't eat this sort of cuisine but sb craft ramen has made a future customer that's for sure. definitely worth stopping in on a chilly day downtown.

Americanized Japanese.  I used to come here a lot before I expanded my sushi palate. i.e. sashimi or nigiri. Now, I know better.  If all you've ever tasted is the Cali Roll, the sushi is alright. The hot items never tasted quite fresh. Hot food may or may not be contained in tin pans heated by a hot water bath.   If you like japanese cafeteria food you should come here.  One plus to this review, the downtown location is probably the best out of all your sushi teri options.    Sushi teri to real japanese cuisine is like panda express to real chinese cuisine. It doesn't compare to the real deal.

What a great find! We were staying at the Upham hotel and I looked up restaurants that were within walking distance and Milk & Honey popped up. We went at around 6pm on a Wednesday night and there was no wait for an outdoor table. I underestimated the portion sizes (past experiences with tapas left us hungry usually) and ordered 6 items for 2 adults and a toddler. This was way too much food! Haha   Date with a pig: bacon wrapped dates with honey. This was really good but on the sweet side. This one was my husband's favorite.   Burrata: this one was so refreshing! I loved the crispy basil. This one was my favorite.   Organic street corn: yummy corn slathered with the toppings, has a bit of a spicy kick to it. My toddler liked this even though it was spicy.   Chicken tacos: fairly standard tacos  Some brie sauce: this one was my second favorite! I didn't expect such a large portion. It came as our last dish and I was already so stuffed but we still enjoyed this dish a lot. This one was also pretty sweet.   Mole sliders: good but nothing too special, had a good spicy kick to it  The Bachelorette: drink was yummy and sweet but pretty small size  Overall, great place for dinner! Would definitely recommend as a place to catch up with friends or to go for a date night.

Came here for breakfast a couple days in a row, and I have to say that the 2-3 ladies who run the show do a great job.  I thought it would be a disaster with the morning rush and people jostling for tables, but it all actually works out quite well.  We found the bread and pastries to be very authentic, and were reminded of breakfasts at our B&B in the Loire Valley!  It's great that they have free refills on the coffee, so you can just sit and relax before heading out for the day.

Great outdoor patio, good service and our breakfast was good. I got the SB omelette that was filled with a lot of sautéed veggies, which I enjoyed. He got the salmon bagel plate but the cream cheese looked more like a cream sauce but he enjoyed it.

One of my all time favorites been coming here since I was young its like a second home!  Family style cafe with great food!  Amazing salsa love the breakfast burritos insider tip ask for a little green onion!  The staff is the icing on the cake!  Great people!

I was excited to try Olio Pizzeria because I've heard of many  rave reviews about it. I'm a lover of pasta and pizza, anything carb involved.   The restaurant is located in the Victoria Court with plenty of parking. It's tucked behind Olio E Lemone.   We had the Black Truffle Pizza, Fennel Sausage, and Tropea Pizza. It was just okay. I've had better pizza. The Black Truffle Pizza was overwhelming and greasy. The other two were unremarkable and plain as vanilla. I also order pasta, and that was the best item on the menu.  Our server was attentive, hence the 3 stars. But I sadly won't be returning. I'd rather eat my calories with tastier bites.
Newly opened and moved right to the top of my list! Fantastic food and drinks!! Great service.

This place is decent. I prefer it over super cucas down the street but it's still not my first choice. The menu isn't very extensive and the chicken is cut in too large of chunks for my liking but the food as a whole is pretty good. Service is a little slow, I usually have plenty of time to meander over to Blenders and finish 1/3 of it before my food is ready but I do love the patio area. So over all, Mexican Fresh is pretty good for the Mesa area.

I've eaten a countless number of pastries and lunches from this establishment and a few breakfasts.   The food is always great but I can only take so many rejections from this location because they decided to close the kitchen early.  Really annoys the crap out of me to be told 'we stop serving lunch at 2:20pm' when it's 2:21pm and I know they're supposed to serve until 2:30pm.  The kicker on this day was having the two green employees laugh as my wife and I walked out.  That's why I'm here, writing this review.  This business needs to raise the standards of service to match the food.  I'm now currently eating a few blocks away at a more Savvy establishment that provides a reliable experience.

local favorite! the best pad thai I have ever had in my entire life. also enjoy the chicken curry noodle dish. everything on the menu is a home run! also to-go dishes come with the best napkins ever (the small details count haha!)

Delicious gyro pita with chopped tomatoes, fresh red onion and a handful or French fries inside. I went with a friend who lived on Greece and she said its the best she has found since coming home.  service may seem a little sketchy but give the guys behind the counter enough time to notice you exist and the food (and a free coke in my case!) are worth the wait!

I've eaten here twice now, and I can say hands down this is one of my favorite places in SB.  The food is executed with precision and grace that the pallate rarely finds in a place offering such simple, delicious raw and cooked cuisine. The chef insisted it is a 'tapas bar.' I recommend you make a meal of the delicious offerings. The tiramisu is to die for.

Very good authentic Mexican food. Cute place With a nice atmosphere and good of outside seating. Come early and always be prepared to wait in a little bit of a line to order food. But the wait is definitely worth it! Friendly staff and reasonable prices. Definitely a nice stop if you're in Santa Barbara

I love this place! Reasonably priced and a great menu. The food is always hot and arrives quickly, no matter how busy, and the staff is attentive and very friendly. The decor is amazing, and the restaurant is always clean. Breakfast served until 3 pm... How can you go wrong?!

Los Agaves but an order of magnitude more hip. Big plates, big energy, big service. Vibrant date night atmosphere paired with the classic Milpas Mexican menu. More pricey than their sister restaurant but I find the added swank to be worth the extra dollar signs. A great addition to the funk zone!
This used to be one of the best places in town. Left tonight wondering if Dario just went on vacation and phoned it in.     Nothing was great anymore. So sad.

Mr. B is a must try here in Santa Barbara. The food and service was excellent. The food is authentic (I've traveled extensively in the Middle East, including to Amman, Jordan) and it is something you can't find anywhere else in town. The falafel and Babaganoush are great and I would highly recommend. Also, the Zataar was great, almost like a Jordanian pizza. Give it a try!
Definitely a must try in SB Amazing view  Great food-(unlike their competitor in the adjacent pier) Superb service  Fair prices Highly recommended

I really wanted to like this place, but the portions are much too small and the food is far too salty to justify the steep prices. $15 for a very small bowl of pho with bland broth and two pieces of beef. $4 for a tiny bowl of sliced pickles. $16 for pork khao soi which had good flavor but the meat was almost entirely fat and inedible. The food is also incredibly over-salted. I appreciate what they're going for, but it really misses the mark for me. I'll be staying loyal to Empty Bowl to fulfill my Thai fusion cravings.

We thoroughly enjoyed our meal here. It was a Friday night; we got there at about 5:10 and only waited fifteen minutes for an outside table for two. By the time we were seated, people were waiting half an hour or more. So if you want to sit outside (and you do!), keep that in mind. Service was friendly and prompt. We thoroughly enjoyed our food (chowder, crab cakes, fish and chips). Mmm. I'm not as big a fan of seafood as my husband, but the fish and chips are perfectly battered and fried, not greasy, and the fish is mild and moist. Just right! The view and ambiance can't be beat. After dinner, you can walk on the beach or the sea wall, or walk over to the wharf.
Awesome place! Amazing food, friendly staff, a diverse selection of dishes, and a super inviting atmosphere overall. Especially enjoyed the short ribs and tiramisu. Would highly recommend!
I went into this Starbucks recently and was impressed by the friendly staff and quick service! Definitely my favorite Starbucks location in town!

Really yummy cheap eats.  Everything we had here was very good, especially the guacamole and the New Mexico Enchiladas, which is pretty much cheese enchiladas in a skillet, smothered with red sauce, with eggs baked on top so the yoke is runny.    I will definitely make a point to eat here any time I'm in SB.

The location and feel of this place is great!  The food was just ok. We were really excited for some great seafood on the wharf, but it was not that incredible. We shared the seafood pasta and the seafood skewers. My first impression is that it was a tourist trap, but it seems as though many locals come here. Maybe we were missing something?

Really wanted to like this place because I loved the atmosphere and it had a really good vibe. Unfortunately the waitress ruined that for us  When we where first seated our waitress asked for our drink order but didn't come back to give us drinks until we tracked her down about 30 minutes later, this is how the whole meal was: us tracking down are waitress. But when the check was ready she was more then attentive.   Also the dish I ordered: Mushroom ravioli was lacking in flavor and was cold when It finally reached our table  I'm sure there is better Italian food on state street, just need to find it

Like jamba juice without a blender for the trophy wife that's hard pressed on time and doesnt want to miss her private yoga appointment.  the bowls are good if you're rich,  cant be bothered, and allergic of gluten or  whatever the next trendy thing to avoid is.       she probably didnt vaccinate her children either. ugh.

I'll admit I'm not all that experienced with bowls, but epic bowls definitely smokes whole foods market bowls. But uh lets be real here, who spends 7 bucks on something that could only really serve as a glorified breakfast. I made the mistake by hitting this place for lunch, and it just felt super inappropriate. But I mean, if you have that kind of cash to burn on bfast, it's pretty great. Personally, I prefer the Jamba Juice version, which is cheaper (they use a normal smoothie base without that fancy acai shit) and just generally better overall. Bring Jamba Juice to state street.

Epic. The white pie is awesome. My pic shows it just after I got home and the basil leaves already turned black. That's because it's real food!!!  Someone did me a favor and got generous with the pepper flakes. Heaven. And the crust is worth eating on its own;  good chew, salt on point.  I kinda cringed at the price ($19) but when you put dinner into perspective it's worth it.  I only heard about them because the local news mentioned them at the early 5:30pm broadcast and said that when they are out of dough they close the door. So I ran over there.

Perfect breakfast spot. There aren't too many tables and it fills up fast. I had the huervo rancheros which were awesome. The tortilla was crispy and the refried beans were really good. The staff was really friendly as well. Not the cheapest breakfast you'll find but worth it.

This USED to be one of my favorite restaurants in Santa Barbara when I lived there a little over a year ago. I am not sure if they have new owners but now the food is now thing but subpar. My favorite dish was the eggs Benedict with their famous pulled pork. I went a couple months ago to learn that they no longer carried the pulled pork. I was a little upset so I order the chicken and waffles thinking it was still going to be good because I've had such good experiences whenever I would go.  When I received my 'chicken and waffles', it was simply a waffle with two dry chicken strips. When we used to go my boyfriend would get the chicken and waffles it was a real piece of fried chicken.  I am so sad that their menu has changed because it used to be a little gem in Goleta.  I recommend Boathouse breakfast because it's always up to par with the consistency of their food.

The pizza was good, but it wasn't 45-minute-wait good. We tried to call ahead, but no one picked up and the voicemail message seemed to start in the middle of a sentence. The music inside was so loud that we couldn't hear the staff and the staff couldn't hear us when we were ordering, and it's not exactly COVID-safe to have to lean into someone's face in order to make yourself heard. Was it a good pizza? Sure. I can tell they really care about their ingredients.
Huge thanks to Olivia and team at Corazon for making our dinner (with a 3 year old) such a success. Perfect recommendations, quick service, fun music, and yummy drinks.
Love this place. Feels like a Sinatra palm springs haunt. Great steaks done proper with all the courses. Great staff, cozy, dark, little treasure
After a little detective work we discovered this is a Mecca for brunch; they serve some of the most delicious huevos rancheros I've ever had. Their bloody Mary's were 100% and service on point!

I want to give them a higher rating, I really do, but I feel like I did not get the experience that I was expecting. I went with a group of friends and the atmosphere is great for big groups. The place is huge with lots of patio dining and big tables. Also, they have complimentary valet parking which is really nice especially on a weekend in downtown SB.   Now for the food and drinks! We wanted to order a pizza, but they ran out. We wanted to order schnitzel poutine, but they ran out. We wanted schnitzel, but they ran out. We wanted ranch, but they ran out... Honestly, at this point I was ready to leave. Their menu was heavily limited. Literally, everything we wanted was not in supply; it just felt like the restaurant was unprepared for the weekend night. Especially with COVID-19 restrictions being lifted, they should have been prepared for the influx of individuals coming by on a Saturday night. We ended up with 3 sausage platters, a pretzel, and a burger. The sausages were good, they had that nice snap to them and were packed with flavor. However, they did not come with buns which is a little odd to me. So we ordered a pretzel, which was alright, the cheese sauce was the show of that order. The burger was mediocre, I did not find it special and honestly, could have gotten a similar one from in-n-out. Now for the beers, we ordered two beers that the waiter had recommended, and maybe it is because I am not a beer connoisseur but these beers were so watery. I did not really enjoy them.

We asked for a cheese burger and they put no cheese on it... they call it cheese burger for a reason. Also I said I want applesauce 5 times and they gave me none. I really dislike hamburger habit the food tastes good but NOT there service... I don't recommend it...

Owner (Crista) and hostesses are happy to accommodate their friends, but heaven forbid you're here in SB to visit (we live here!) or not part of their social circle.  Two snotty hostesses had no shortage of unnecessary attitude while they sat their acquaintances at open tables while we (having gotten there early for our reservation) stood and watched in frustration.    On a positive note our server Vivian did a wonderful job on our Mother's Day 2-course visit, but she couldn't quite make up for:  1) the botched CONFIRMED-WITH-A-CREDIT-CARD reservation - we ended up waiting 15-20 minutes;  2) the crappy corner table inside (Crista was seating her friends outside on the patio);  3) the coffee drinks made incorrectly - we had to send them back;   4) the main course made incorrectly - we had to send them back;  5) someone else's credit card written on the back of our kid's menu - we turned it in and hopefully no one else used it;  6) the fact that we were reprimanded for having our 3 year old color (the crayons were provided by the restaurant!) on what was apparently a limited supply of Mom's day menus (how would we know that?!);  The rude hostess originally offered to take 1/2 off our bill if we wanted to sit at the 'bar' outside, which looked like a table cloth thrown over a portable drink stand.  Are you kidding me?!  While the owner was happy to shoot the breeze with her friends, never once came by our table to offer an apology for screwing up our mother's day brunch, and made every attempt to avoid us on the two trips we made to the restroom.  I'm disgusted by her attitude.  And we got the check with not even a token thrown in to make up for the disaster of an experience (even to comp the coffee would have been a kind gesture).  Fortunately it was SUCH a fiasco that we couldn't help but laugh as it was unfolding, and of course our daughter didn't understand so we turned it into a game for her!  From reading other reviews it certainly sounds like service isn't a priority at Scarlett Begonia, and that certainly played out for us today.  Deserving of zero stars, but giving 1 star because the food was good and Vivian held it together for us!  Will never return but will gladly walk on by in the future on our way from the parking lot to some restaurants around the corner that actually care about their customers' experience.

(I'm only reviewing them as a coffee shop here.) Probably the 2nd best espresso in SB, but light years ahead of everybody else. Excellent cappuccinos. Rather average mochas though, must be the chocolate? Anyway, the excellent baked goods, muffins and cookies are absolutely fresh (I have seen the bakers walking them over from next door). Wish they had indoor seating for cold weather but there are well-functioning heaters outside (which of course can't reach all seats perfectly). The granola and the one other prepared breakfast item both look excellent, haven't tried them.

Corazon Cocina delights the senses. From the incredible smells at the counter to the eye popping food.  Not to mention every delicious bite  (see pictures). We ordered 6 tacos, way too much but couldn't help ourselves. My favorite was the Birria taco - so flavorful and juicy.  Jeff loved the fish taco the best.  The cauliflower taco was really interesting.  I will need to convince my husband to try something similar at home.  I love the location within the Public Market.  Wish I had saved room for a Rori's ice cream.

Wow! I live in SB, and this is one if the best taqueria experiences ever! Serious. Had a couple if street tacos and a Corona - for only $10. The outdoor seating is super cool and entertaining. Total 5 stars

If you don't love brunch at The Biltmore there may be something wrong with you. Seriously though. As you walk on property, you're greeted by staff who is as friendly as they are attentive before being ushered onto a sun drenched patio adorned with Spanish style architectural accents, golden patio umbrellas, and a sweeping view of the ocean. From there, you're free to indulge in as much bottomless caviar, mimosas, bloody marys, oysters, crab legs, sushi, prime rib, breakfast favorites, crudites, pots of creme, lemon tarts, and mini cakes as your heart desires. The over-the-top indulgence of the brunch is decadent and glamorous the way you imagine your life in Santa Barbara to be and lives up to The Biltmore's tried and true flair for the finer things in life.

Ok, so, I've been here twice and both times my meals had chicken that tasted horrible. Today I ordered the tequila chicken pasta. I thought, they can't mess that up, so I gave it a whirl. I got my order pretty quick, the service was so so and somewhat robotic. I get in the car and set my food down on my lap, only to find that the dude didn't shut my pasta all the way so all of the sauce leaked out and into the bag!!!!! It didn't leak from the bag, so I guess they have amazing bags there, whatever. It burned the flip out of my leg though!!! I finally get back to work, and try the chicken and BOOM! all I can taste is refrigerator with my dry, sauce-less  noodles. The 'garlic bread' was a joke. It looked, smelled, felt and tasted like something grandma uses to scrape her heels or scrub dishes with. By all means, give it a go. However, I will not encourage you to.

I think i found my new favorite brunch spot while in Santa Barbara.  The family walked up State street hoping to find a baby friendly brunch spot. My wife recalled seeing this on yelp so we decided to give it a try. Score!   Skirt chicken fried steak. Tender, savory gravy that was spicy. HUGE portion with a side of homestyle fries, a flaky biscuit with an amazing side of jelly.  Mimosa in a mason jar filled. So much win.  We were sat at a Private corner table where my daughter can get loud. Service is top notch. Can't wait to go back.
Must. Eat. Tacos.  Lilly's has been holding it down for years. The best in town.

We came here for the first time in 2017 & our minds were blown by the gluten free biscuits. Next SB visit in 2018 they were no longer on the menu so we gave up all hope. Then recent visit in 2021 I decided to check out the menu again & GF biscuits are back!  So obviously that was our first breakfast of the trip. They are still the best GF biscuits we've ever had but they are not as good we remembered from 2017, were a little dry. Still definitely on our SB must eat food rotation. For the gluten-y menu, I love that they use locally milled organic flour for their breads & they have sourdough bread.  Excellent: drip coffee, house made chai latte with almond milk, sourdough bread, GF brownie - one of the best GF brownies ever, farmers market bowl Great: avocado toast, everything bagel   Expect a long line on the weekends but it moves pretty well, the staff is efficient, & it's worth the wait.

We went there for dinner. I really wanted to have a seat outside but ended up sitting indoors. I'm glad we sat inside because it was pretty cold that evening.   The view of the ocean is spectacular.   I wasn't too impressed with the restaurant decor. The service was good. The atmosphere was pleasant. The food was good. For appetizers we had the ahi poki. The portion was small but you could tell that it was pretty good quality. The rest of the meal was decent. I guess I expected more from a Four Season's restaurant.
The Chashu Pork is my favorite but the ramen is always so good. Always a tough decision!

Fantastic tacos. Really perhaps top tree tacos in quality and taste. The price is very reasonable as well. The only thing is I have kids and the place gets cold and windy for dinner because it is only outdoor seating and if we take it to go it gets cold by the time it is consumed. Otherwise they deserve a 6th star

The service here is almost always really slow. People usually like to come for the Sharkeez bowls, but I personally don't think it is worth the cost. What I like coming here for is the vibe. I usually have a good time here, but I would def recommend pre-gaming elsewhere before coming here because you will spend quite some time waiting to get served a drink.

Wrestling with a 4 or 5 here. Love the restaurant and the bar has some awesome options! We came twice. The first time the drinks were superb! The bar service excellent!  The second was for dinner and we sat at the bar as we had not made reservations and there was a different shift. I felt like they were a bit grumpy. Granted, this is after wine tasting so I may not have been perceiving things properly. I was starving! The trout was excellent - crispy skin and tender inside and the dish was just divine.   Definitely worth a visit, and next time I may say 5 because it's right there.
One person and ten people in line = 15 minutes plus to get rung up on the cash register.  This is the quality service you can expect from this Panera.

Fun spot with multiple pool tables in the back.   If you're local this is one of the spots where you will probably know most of the people inside on a busy Friday or Saturday night.   Patty in the front is awesome as always.
Another fabulous experience at Ca'Dario made even more special by Judy. Ambiance, cuisine and hospitality outstanding. I just left and can't wait to return.
Enjoying a outstanding Santa Barbara Style dog, highly recommend! Many tasty styles on the menu, try them all.
The food is good, but service and ambiance is (for the lack of a better word) PAINFUL! Would NOT go back for sure. My recommendation: take out...

Love the fire pit  and the service our server was Mariay she was amazing. We made sand bar apart of our post wedding crawl.  It was our wedding night and we got the most amazing service. Thanks for contributing to it. The margaritas are good just stick to fresh lime juice and agave...skinny anything else is going to taste like house mix sweet and sour.

Bowl was delicious, however, the service is incredibly slow. I came in with a friend on a Sunday morning and asked how long the wait would be for a bowl. They said 15 minutes. We ended up waiting 30+ minutes for a smoothie and a bowl. The girls seem flustered over cutting fruit and taking simple orders at the register in a timely manner. The other customers seemed a little confused too. A 30 minute wait is usual for a dine in restaurant, not a smoothie bowl place. The most popular açaí place in my hometown has it prepared for you within 5 minutes. They also use the little multi slicing tools for bananas and strawberries, so the fruit is still cut fresh without the time needed to individually slice each item like the girl behind the counter. The 4 employees could have been less nonchalant about their work, especially when they know customers are waiting. Please give accurate wait times! We had some place to be and thought we could grab a bite in 15 minutes. We wouldn't have minded so much if they had just told us the truth, we would have just came back after our event.

So I order damn near $70 worth of food for take out.  1 of the orders had 24 chicken wings.  In the notes section I put please make well done.  That's fine.  However I ask for 3 LARGE containers or a bunch of small containers for all the wings and I'll pay for it.  They send me with 1 and a ton of ranch!  I call in and the worker there says well you have to pay for it.  I tell the guy I already said I'd pay for it and proceeds to nothing!  I ask for the manager which is obviously not on duty and give my name and number for a call back !  I used to support this business when it was Nicky D's but I guess not with this new cheap business.  Sweating me for some damn hot wing sauce after spending some good money on their food.  Lesson learned.

This is a beautiful spot at tha Bacara resort and spa. The bistro is a pleasant walk through the grounds of a 5- star hotel. It is a lovely place to sit. The server was pleasant and charming but we were a bit disappointed with what we were served. Bloody Mary's were spicy and gorgeous. The waiter asked us did we want the bacon on them...Yes! Please!, however bacon was not available after all, apparently neither was vodka. I don't think there was any in my drink. We both ordered the Lobster Roll tastefully presented with a mixed baby green salad with a light balsamic-vinaigrette. I enjoyed it but it wasn't the best I have had. My friend found 4 (four!) pieces of shell in hers. The server did check back to see if everything was satisfactory and when she told him about the shells he didn't seem concerned at all and made no offer to correct the problem. 'I'll pass it along'.  Coffee was fresh and delicious, the butterscotch Creme brûlée was as good as it sounds.  This was a special occasion, with a generous tip the bill was just over 100 dollars. You are paying for the location here which is an ocean and pool view.
Another happy night in a beautiful patio. Nice decoration and nice weather to hang out. Like it! The only one thing is parking... Have to find the street parking

Love this place! They have great drink specials and delicious bar food at an affordable price. The TRI TIP NACHOS are heaven and the FISH & CHIPS and WINGS are also fantastic! The nachos include BOTH liquid cheese and real, grated cheese. Awesome!! Definitely try the nachos. You won't be disappointed! And, FYI, the small sized nachos are BIG!
Javier had excellent service! We felt very taken care of and the food was amazing. Habinero salsa is actually spicy, which I love! So if you're into spice go for it.

Grateful to have great vegan options & amazing fresh juices. Cute interior and outdoor seating. I'm a repeat customer & I can't say enough about how nice it is to just order without having to remove animal products like cheese, mayo, etc. as is the case at so many eateries. One can just order, relax, & have a healthy meal here.

Update: returned 2 days later, the place was packed, much different than our first experience. The bartenders were hopping, the stress was high, but the drinks were still on point. The owner/manager (not sure), Jorge was friendly and introduced himself and bought us a drink. We ordered the Ceasar again, and we also tried the deep fried olives. The salad was just as amazing as the first time, but the olives just didn't hit the mark for me. I do hope they add more Tapas to their menu. It is fun to try new items!!

Simple and welcoming, this place lives up to it's mission statement of nourishing body and soul. Serving unique dishes created with wholesome natural ingredients in a tidy little restaurant with art lining the walls, the staff is friendly and nice and the food is yummy.  I highly recommend the linguine,  another in our party had the Curry Chicken salad and raved about it.  It is possible to order small or larger orders of most items on the menu.   The Sojourner offers a decent kids menu. Organic ingredients are used whenever possible.  Stop in if you're in the neighborhood!
I hope this place opens soon. I just discovered it and really loved it.
Croissant french toast. That blew my socks off.

There is something very warm about this place We love coming here. The wine selection is great and they offer wines at reasonable price points. Everything we have eaten here tastes good. We love oyster Mondays. Check the website for other specials.

Louie's is a romantic spot with food that takes no risks, but is perfectly enjoyable.   The preparation was spot-on and while the service could use some polish, everyone was friendly.   I went with my dining club. The menu is not tailored to people looking for inventive dishes though, so foodies may be left wanting more.  I would definitely return though, with a date or with my parents. High marks for the rack of lamb special and the Banana a la Louie dessert. Recommended.

This is a wonderful placeI ordered the Bruschetta not expecting much since it is a bar setup - but honestly the bruschetta and all the foods here blew my mind. Everything is DELICIOUS. The staff is wonderful and fun  . The Bruschetta are served with goat cheese and the crispiest baguette slices

Such an average overpriced turkey sandwich of the likes I've never had. Two slices on plain bread and a crappy side salad or romaine.m for $15.  Service was barely there as well, even without a crowd. Save your time and dollars and hit Via Vai across the street.

Doesn't look like much when you walk past.  But man this place is great,  Fresh chips and open salsa bar to start  For food we got Red pozole with pork was amazing ! And came with some handmade corn tortillas that were delicious.  We also got the combo plate 3 tacos - crispy steak, grilled chicken, birria , Spanish red rice and refried pinto beans also very good. To drink - Aguas frescas - fresa and jamaica    YASIRA was our server and was great ! Excellent service !  Will be back soon  Thank you   -to late to post pictures, we finished everything before the review

What a delight to finally have a sit down Mexican restaurant in Santa Barbara that has the full package; amazing food, atmosphere and great customer service.  As you all know I love and know my Mexican food and I'm happy to say that this is one of my favorites.  My 'Slam Dunks' are the Cancun Enchilada diablo style (Spicy!!),  Nachos al Pastor, Burrito al Pastor and the Albondigas soup.  The service is always on point and the food is always consistent and delicious.  There's always great beer on tap, from local IPAs to the traditional Mexican beers.  My wife loves the cucumber margarita made with Agave Wine.  The atmosphere is comfortable, welcoming with a delightful Mexican feel.  I would recommend sitting at the bar to enjoy the full experience.  Don't forgot to try the chips because they are delicious.   The Mex Authentic is 'Sa Da Tay'

Great location and excellent service here at enterprise. Had Lucas and Zack as our bartenders and they both did a phenomenal job.  The drinks were up to par, had a Moscow mule and beer from happy hour menu (both great) paired with a shrimp quesadilla and Baja fish tacos. 10/10 recommend this place if you're looking for a good happy hour spot, with great food and drinks and even better service!

Our Sunday brunch was fabulous. The variety of seafood appetizers was quite memorable, especially the scallop ceviche. Our waiter Mauricio was attentive & offered great suggestions. He even offered me a blanket as there was some fog before the sun broke through illuminating a gorgeous view. The champagne paired nicely with the appetizers and our main courses. The desserts were incredible and they even brought out an extra one since it was our anniversary weekend. Tasteful live background music could be heard from the patio. A great experience overall!

Overall, I LOVE Jersey Mikes. I've been to some of the other locations and enjoy the fresh cut deli sandwiches.   However, this location needs a little bit of work. The first time I went in, the workers seemed stressed out despite it being a short line. Understandable, since it was the first week they've been open. The second time I went in, both gentlemen were pretty unenthusiastic and short with me. One of them forgot half of the ingredients that came on the sand which and I had to gently remind him. I went to sit down in the empty dining room and every single table had crumbs and napkins strewn about.   Hopefully they can work out the kinks. Will definitely be returning still!
We came here to get some coffee and macarons. Social distancing did apply and only a few people inside! We ordered the sprinkle, peach, and chocolate macarons and it was delicious!

Lilac is my hands-down everyday favorite. Check their website for the special soup and salad of the day. My favorite cake is the berry patch, my partner's is the chocolate decadence. I love their breakfasts, sandwiches, chocolate chip cookies, and coffee drinks. Simply put, everything is so good.
Lively atmosphere for a new bar on lower State Street. The El Presidents  cocktail was made to perfection. Highly recommend sitting at the bar and enjoying a nice evening
Good for for a decent price.  Wait staff is friendly.  Lots of booths.  Old people like to hang out here.

Ordered two dogs and an order of boneless wings. Everything was great!   Just an FYI that this location doesn't serve alcohol. So no yummy micheladas or fun drinks at this spot. Go to Milpas location for those

Hit or miss! First time I went, it was with a new person, and I was impressed. I think we were both confused if it was a date or just a hang out... Either way, the first time around I liked the food enough to want to revisit!  Several months later I went with my current boyfriend (different man) as a treat for his promotion and unfortunately discovered that my first experience had over-exaggerated the flavors of the food in my mind. He loves prosciutto so we ordered the Prosciutto di Parma and found it very salty! We make this dish at home sometimes, but I had to eat twice as much melon to counterbalance the salt and we quickly ran out of melon.  He also loves broccolini so we ordered the Broccolini with capers. Same story! The broccolini was cooked to an almost burnt crisp and covered in salt... I found it hard to eat and could only really get through it with the complimentary foccacia. I don't send food back, but perhaps I should have. I was embarrassed, but my boyfriend was being a good sport about it all!  We ordered the Maitake Mushroom (with extra arugula) and Pepperoni Pizzas. Other than the fact that the pepperoni pizza was dripping in grease, it was tasty, but not the best.   I don't know if it's because we were there on a Wednesday night and the A-team wasn't in the kitchen, but I was not impressed by the food, only the service and the atmosphere.  The bar is great and the staff is great, but I will not be returning for the food.

Very happy to report that we enjoyed it all. We went for lunch on Friday afternoon. Let's start at the beautiful view on the patio. Santa Barbara's iconic waterfront need a view like this, we now have it! We started with the Spritz, a Prosecco drink. We brought a $90 bottle of Syrah to enjoy, corkage is $20. Later we both had a gin cocktail with Suze and grapefruit juice.   We split the dishes:  Octopus | pork belly, frisée, soft cooked egg  Wood oven za'atar flatbread | accompaniments  Cavolo nero | little gem, radicchio, mahon, rusks, garlic citronette   Fig Pizza| fontina, rosemary, caramelized onion, radicchio, pancetta   And a Pasta with Squash  The bill was around $140. We went all out as we were celebrating a birthday. The food, presentation, and drinks were all excellent.  The only minus points would be for the lunchtime waitstaff, not what you'd expect for fine dinning.

Amazing food! I love that you can order by the amount of food, by the portion, especially because I can eat a lot of meat and don't eat all the breads or other stuff. I generally only eat some type of protein (meat, fish, or chicken) and a salad, and this place offers the best brisket and chicken evah! GREAT salads too. I do wish they wouldn't smother the chicken in brown sugar, it doesn't need it :) Yummy regardless.

So excited we again have true brioche now that Xanadu is gone. The young French couple were suffering through a second day of 100+ degree heatwave when I went. They were out of the miches (loaves), but they made one for me to pick up at 2pm. It is beautiful. From the aroma, it's also delicious.  It's true sourdough brioche, and I hope santa barbarians learn to love it.

IHOP was soll man sonst noch dazu sagen, lecker Frühstück und sonstiges Essen.   Ebenfalls zum mittagessen kann ich es empfehlen.  Sowohl die Panecakes als auch die Crepes sind zum Frühstück lecker...  Die allgemein eher kleinere Auswahl zum Mittagessen ist lecker und kann empfohlen werden..

We are so happy that Lure opened it's doors in Santa Barbara.  The food is delicious, the service is prompt and friendly.  The décor is attractive with an updated ocean theme with white-washed woods, nautical touches and handsome tilework.  We especially enjoy the patio happy hour with drinks and snacks served out of doors while we sit in big comfortable chairs.  At peak dining time it can be busy and a bit loud, so if you want a quieter table, be sure to ask the hostess desk to help you if possible.  A great addition to the uptown dining scene.

Bad 'policy' for groups over 8 people so we won't be eating here anytime soon.
