According to the person on the phone, if you have over 8 people in your party each person must order an entree - even if there are kids in the group.  We planned to take our family out for an early evening birthday party today with my in-laws and their families - a total of 13 people, 3 kids.  Sorry but there's no way I'm ordering a full entree for a child who won't eat more than 3 bites.  I also had the intention of making my meal a variety of 4-5 appetizers (which would have added up to more than the price of an entree) since they sounded appealing.  I'll go elsewhere to get my Mediterraean fix...Zaytoon there are more and more options every day in Santa Barbara for this type of food so be careful about turning away future customers - no wonder so many restaurants are short-lived in downtown.

I bought 3 hot dogs to share with my GF.  We had the Pastrami Dog, Santa Barbara Dog, and Dave's Dog. They all tasted pretty good.  I have a couple of complaints.  I split the dogs in half to share.  Depending on which half i got I may or may not have gotten any bacon meaning the bacon slice was quite small. Also, the dogs themselves are the smaller variety.  We both ended up with heartburn which is very uncommon.  The cost also is a bit high. I do understand they need to make a profit. Given they get very high reviews overall, take my review with a grain of salt.  For myself, I probably won't go back.

Visited this place on a recommendation, was skeptical as I'm not a really a hot dog lover, but now all I can say is- I'm hooked.   The service, attention to the customer, and quality and freshness of the food is top notch.   With different friendly faces to walk you through the menu this place is beyond welcoming for newbies checking it out for the first time  and regulars attempting to try one of every hot dog on the menu at least once.  Parking can be a little challenging @ times and lines can get a little long but there's always someone on Daves team willing to chat you up while you wait.

This place is great. Chef owned and operated with a Japanese family run staff. The fish is extremely consistent and is of a slightly higher grade than anyplace else in town. The cold Sake list is affordable but good and the special board is local and or seasonal changing weekly or so. Best sushi in town. Ambiance is nice but its geared towards foodies and all the $ seems to be on the plates.

I'm not vegan or vegetarian by any means. I passed this spot up for a long time in the public market. I finally tried it and am sad all this time went by without me eating here, it is delicious!!!! My new favorite spot in the public market and maybe even SB. The food is so good, filling and doesn't leave me feeling weighed down. As a meat eater, this food is incredible flavor wise and really is satisfying, don't miss meat at all. Could eat this spot everyday! I don't know much about vegan food or falafel but doesn't matter, I highly recommend this spot to everyone!!!
Location is awesome, and so is the restaurant itself. But the best part of El Torito is its Taco Tuesday. $0.99 tasty tacos....can't beat that!
Really good fish and chips. Love it. Only to go. Seat outside. No long lines. Have the wait for awhile for your order but worth the wait. Crispy fish and great nachos with salsa. Trust.

Jamal is the best! I seriously love this place.  The attention to detail is nuts and everything is off the hook delicious.  They are dog friendly outside and just attentive and kind.  Fairly priced for the quantity and quality of the food.  Best eats on the Mesa and one of the best vegetarian we have had in the area.  The desserts are also amazing.  Come one, come all and leave happy.

Yup, totally deserves the 5 stars. Met up with a couple friends, sat at the bar. Great service even though they were busy. Had the Helles beer, perfect summer beer, nice and light. Got the sausage sampler platter - 3 sausages - all delicious - Andouille, Three Pepper, and Bacon Cheddar. Checked out their website later on and found out they make their own sausage!

Nice atmosphere, service was decent, food was very good quality. We went on a friday night and it was jam packed, waited almost an hour for a table. ordered the sliders medium, they came out super well done, but somehow were still kinda juicy... that means the meat was ground there and great quality, so I will for sure be back to try a burger and hope it is cooked right. the burgers are the table next to us looked delicious. The flatbread was good, the fried chicken was good, the fried pickles were meh... bland and the batter is too much, but over all the experience was good and we will be back!

One of the nicest restaurants in Santa Barbara. I recently celebrated a birthday here and was blown away by the food and the service. The atmosphere is very cozy and aesthetic, which made me feel welcome from the start. We started off with their freshly baked baguettes and the Foie Gras which melted in our mouths. We ordered the roasted duck confit and venison meatballs which were both large and delicious portions. For dessert, we got the upside down blackberry lemon cake and creme brûlée. Great restaurant and will definitely be returning.

If you want to find the best vegan gluten free carrot cake cupcake, look no further. Seriously by far the best thing I have eaten in a long time. The frosting is to die for and the cupcake itself is so moist and delicious.  Also during this Covid times they were super efficient on the phone, got our order right away, and I just picked it up. Super easy. Also don't sleep on the vegan banana nut muffins!! ;)

Off of the main strip, State Street. The Funk Zone used to be less of a touristy feel. Had a locals vibe for a while back when rent was affordable.  The property has changed hands and investor money has come in. It's become slightly more tourist oriented.  There's more wine bars here than most of Santa Barbara.  It makes for an incredible location to shoot couple's engagement photos

My wife and I joined our close friends for a pre-Valentine dinner. I have always loved Bouchon but only dine there 1-2 times per year if I'm lucky. As always, I was impressed by the whole dining experience.  I am always startled by just how good the service and food actually is. I then wonder why I am not here more often. Starting with the gracious hostess who seemed genuinely welcoming in her demeanor, to the waiter who instantly recognized the bottle of Pinot and Cab that we had brought in and was there with the appropriate stemware almost immediately, to the very well described menu items, to the near perfect pacing of the courses, and, of course, to the delicious food.  The lobster pot pie was phenomenal along with the old standby crab cakes and trio of scallops.  My entree was excellent; the wine country version of steak and potatoes which was a perfectly cooked cut of flat iron steak, a potato cake, and deliciously seasoned broccoli with bits of bacon.  My wife had the lamb which was delicious as always.  Dessert consisted of the killer chocolate lava cake and the creme brûlée.  This last experience confirms what I always tell people;  Bouchon is really the best restaurant in SantaBarbara.
Awesome Mexican food. Everything we ordered was great. Hard to go wrong here.
Wanted to add a quick update that on a five day trip to Jack's, we ate here four times!  By the last day, they remembered us and our orders.  A definite recommend.
Are you kidding me?! What is the wait time all about?   Save your time people. There are tons of better places steps away.

Had the ribeye medium rare, not bad. Had the lobster Mac and cheese, a B at best. But had the steamed clams appetizer and they were delicious! Cocktails were great and staff was pretty nice. Couldn't remember my server's name but he was a bearded, very handsome young man, and he was very polite. The place gets crowded so go early.

Fantastic pizza. Have been coming to taffy's for 20 years since I was a teenager and now bringing my kids here. Love it. Good food at reasonable price. Classic arcade in the back with some of the same games for the last 20 years.

DO NOT DO DELIVERY WITH THEM!  Today's highlights - pizza arrived 2 hours after ordered.  Opened lid to find that all the topping slid to one side of box - driver clearly did not lay it flat. Stone cold, pile of pizza topping slop.  Wings?  Stone cold, dry as a bone.  Caesar Salad?  Somehow they served that up hot...except you dont serve hot salad do you!    This is not the first time Patxi's has failed on delivery, it is so common I strongly urge you not to do it.  It's a decent pizza if you eat at restaurant.  They do, however, always seem to have staff issues.   All in all, if you are going to pay highest price in town for a Pizza, this is not the place
Overpriced place and I did not like that much their food. I loved the drinks we tried!
Great Mexican food!.. Definitely hand in hand great along with Los Arroyos .. They offer different options with different salsas. They have expanded to different locations throughout Santa Barbara!

The Monarch fell short on food and service.  We came in for lunch today.  Sad to say that I am writing this review from another restaurant because we had to go get lunch after having lunch at Monarch.   We came in for the Pasta and there were excuses as to why there was not any pasta on the menu today.  (The table across from us came in for the pasta as well and were denied).   Our food took at least an hour from order to delivery and the restaurant only had 8-10 customers in addition to our table.     We gave honest feedback to our server about the service that fell on deaf ears.   The entire time we waited for our food I told my wife, I hope its at least decent to make up for the long wait and lack of service.    But the food was bland, and disappointing as well.   No apologies only excuses from the server.   We live nearby, maybe we will check it out in a few months.    Do NOT BRING KIDS HERE even though its in a family friendly hotel.  They were very proud to tell us that they dont make accommodations for children.

Absolutely my favorite spot for breakfast in Santa Barbara! Casual outdoor dining area on Stearns wharf with absolutely gorgeous view of the ocean and the hills of Santa Barbara. Francisco, Blanca and the rest of the staff are always friendly and offer excellent service. They serve a range of espresso drinks, and a nice breakfast (and lunch) menu. My personal favorite is the huevos rancheros.  A great place to eat and appreciate the beauty of Santa Barbara.
Always can depend on Valentino's for delicious food! Have been coming here since I was a kid and the food has consistently been tasty over all these years! Great sandwiches, pizzas and salads!
Max's has great food and a calming atmosphere for midday meal. Take a nreak and go see what it is all about. I have eaten there several times and have enjoyed every meal. Barbara

Great addition to SB! The atmosphere alone is worth going for. The interior is cool and has a rustic feel but the patio is the best part, completely surrounded by plants it really make the place feel cozy.  We fortunately had a reservation, which I strongly suggest on Friday, Sat, and Sunday!  This place books out completely so plan ahead.  The food is served family style which most the time I believe is just another term for charging higher prices for a regular sized dish but this place actually served the dishes a bit more generous than most places.  We started with the crab salad on local organic greens which was delicious. It was practically a head of butter lettuce with a nice amounting crap on it with a light citrusy dressing.  Next was the broccolini with a yogurt sauce and walnuts which was probably my favorite. The broccolini was nice and charred but not wilted and soggy. So good!  For our entree we shared the scallops which were prepared perfectly but a tad bit too salty so for future reference, I would order them unsalted. We also got a side of the Brussels sprouts which were prepared differently than most places where the big trend is deep frying with bacon:-/ These were fried but in a fish sauce with a citrus lime flavor. So good!  They have a great cocktail menu but their wine selection could of been better especially being in SB and next door to wine country.  Service was 5 stars. Even as busy as they were our waiter circled around more than enough times.   Only reason I docked a star is because I don't eat meat and the selection was Very slim? I had to modify the scallops but it would if been nice to have more to chose from.

A divine French restaurant! It's not France, but it sure is close. I've dined here twice now, once with an Axxess card deal where it was buy one entree, get another entree free! That was worth. The second time I dined was for a friend's dinner. The restaurant was kind enough to accommodate for the 10 of us and our waiter was so fast and efficient with taking all of our orders and bringing out our order in a timely manner.  We had to try the escargot, frog legs, and I personally got the NY steak for our entree. Delish! We topped off our night with their creme brulee which was super creamy.

Most people agree, this city does not do Vietnamese cuisine well. And getting noodles for takeout is the worst. But like a perfect storm, I found myself ordering pho to-go during the December 2020 COVID surge. Initially I thought it was going to be just 'edible', and nothing more than that. Surprisingly, it was better than expected!  My order was packaged neatly, with the sauces in their respective containers and the broth kept separately. All the lids were snug and the bowl was wrapped in a separate plastic bag to ensure the contents did not spill out during travel. Everything was still super hot upon arrival, the rare beef slices cooked in the broth and I didn't need to reheat anything.  To the surprise of no one, the noodles did clump up a bit. It took some effort to separate the strands once they were submerged in the broth. But the consistency was great and it still had a chewy texture. The cuts of beef were tasty. I found the broth flavorful, but on the saltier side. Toppings are also minimal compared to dine in.  Can't compare to pho restaurants in San Jose or Orange County, but it'll save you a trip up the 101 and you don't have to go through LA traffic. And for Santa Barbara standards, at least it's not Pho Bistro.

Gluten free and dairy light eater.   Best Mexican food.   I have reviewed a few Mexican restaurants over the years. This one ticked many of my boxes: ambiance, food and drink options, and wait staff.  FYI. Tell your wait staff your gluten free. They will let you know items that are available for you.   Ambiance: sits right off lower state street with indoor and outdoor seating. Outdoor seating www comfortable. Nice lighting inside the building. We are at dusk and it was pleasant.   Food and drink: I spent so much time pouring over the menu both at my hotel and at the restaurant. There was veg, fish and grill options. I ordered the mole negro. Husband had the enchilada cabo azul. Both were excellent in taste. Mole is an art and this one was the best I've ever had.   We had an appetizer, ceviche with halibut. Flavorful and filling. Enjoyed the cold appetizer. I like to base my reviews on the side items such as salsa. Their chips and salsa was exceptional. Roasted, verde and regular salsa all had great flavor. It was hard to pick a favorite. Our rice and beans added to the meal and wasn't just a way to fill the plate. Corn tortillas were house made and so excellent.   Our waiter Ricky was extremely helpful and always willing to check with the kitchen with any questions, although he knew the menu pretty well.   Ordered a margarita pepino with mezcal and it was excellent. Not too sweet or spicy. Just right.

Happened to end up at CrushCafe for the first time last weekend for lunch with a friend.  I'd been to the cupcakery many times so figured we'd give the cafe a shot for lunch in route to the art museum.   The staff was cheery, we sat outside on the sidewalk and the wait wasn't long at all.   The menu was short, sweet and to the point (in a good way) as everything looked like it deserved to be there and of course that made the decision of what to get a challenge.  All the breakfast items looked (and smelled) wonderful but as we were there around 1:30 my friend and I both opted for lunch-ish panini's.  I had the grilled veggie panini with green chilis, eggplant, tomato and grilled zucchini and my friend had a brie-onion-pear panini - both were great.   Each came with the small side salad, soup or fries.    I'll definitely go back again to try some of their other lunch offerings and I'm eager to hit 'em up for a breakfast burrito some time soon.

Love this place! It's my go to spot now, they have indoor and outdoor seating, you can also sit at the bar. When you first walk in you'll notice a valet, don't let this fool you, it's the entrance. Once you walk in, you'll place you'll order and if food if you wish, they'll direct you to stand by the bar and pick up your beer there, if you order food they'll give you a number. I recommend finding a seat before ordering so you're not walking around with your beer. If you want some water go up to the bar and help yourself to the pitchers.  I recommend their fries and margarita pizza, their fries are classics and delicious. We like to bring a board game and enjoy some beer. Also did I mention this place carries the Boot? Just like in the movie beerfest.

A cute brunch place hidden from the busy shops and traffic of State Street. And this place literally is hidden in a courtyard in the middle of the building. They set up plenty of umbrellas on the outdoor patio to block the sun, so you can enjoy sitting outside without getting sunburned.   The brunch menu offered a variety of items that would please anyone's palette! If you are a fan of cinnamon rolls, definitely order it! This thing is huge, so it's meant to be shared with the table. It comes out soft and warm, with lots of ooey gooey frosting, but doesn't taste overly sweet.   I also ordered the open faced BLT topped with an egg, and served with a side of potatoes. I liked the thick cut bacon, which was cooked to a nice crunch without being overly greasy or burnt. The sweetness of the heirloom tomatoes helped balance out the smokiness of the bacon and richness of the egg yolk.   Parking: Free street parking.

This restaurant, hands down, has the best complimentary chips and salsa I've ever had. This alone would make me want to come back! The green salsa is surprisingly creamy yet still hot. I'm still thinking about the chips as I write this post.   The tacos de camaron were amazing as well. The shrimp included in the tacos were almost the size of my palm!! They were very generous with the amount of shrimp per taco too. The rice was alright--definitely not the highlight of the show but did it's job.  The taquitos de pato en mole coloradito was (duck taquitos with mole) was definitely interesting. The duck was shredded and fried into taquitos, and the mole was slightly bitter. I'm glad we were able to try this but I'm not sure if we'll reorder again.  The esquites Mexicanos was delicious. The corn was perfectly seasoned with cojita cheese and sour cream. Definitely the fanciest esquites I've had.   Overall a great experience! The food was satisfying and the service was friendly. I'd recommend coming while the sun is about to set so you can enjoy your meal with a beautiful view.
This pizza place is fantastic! Friendly staff, quality food and the pizza was cooked perfectly. We got the veggie pizza and it was one of the best I've ever had, I will definitely be coming back!

I love love love their fried chicken. And the fries are like English chips (of fish and chips fame), so that made me very happy:  chunky and well fried. Mother had the Saturday night special, which was pork belly, coconut rice and bok choy which she happily devoured. No complaints there.  The vibe is casual, the parking is doable  but on the street, and the service is quick. Also, the tea service is so beautiful, which was unexpected but much appreciated.  I cannot wait to go back!!

This was the most amazing dining experience in many years. Ever dish was infused with delightful flavors and textures without anything overpowering. I highly recommend the butternut squash soup and risotto. The Pad Thai is beyond delicious.

Five out of five!  This place is truly a good fast food place.  We have tried the following food:  spicy chicken deluxe sandwich Grilled chicken sandwich Chicken tortilla soup Chicken nuggets   All of these were really tasty. However the soup and the deluxe sandwich were amazing. The helpers are nice. We also got our food pretty quickly. There are lot of parking spots.  Please try it soon if you have not tried it already!

The decor is really nice but it gets crowded and the staff can be so rude! I got the ahi carpaccio, which was nothing like what I'd consider carpaccio. It was badly cut and also had vile wasabi squirts on it that, if it weren't for the color, I would swear was something else. There should've been a warning on the menu. My friends liked their burger and the mole enchiladas. We all decided not to go back though because of the not-very-nice people.

We came back here almost a year after our first visit and it did not disappoint. My girlfriend and I got a flight of wine and mushroom shortbread. Everything was exactly what we wanted and the food was delicious!

Hidden gem vegan beer and eatery. Always super easygoing and friendly atmosphere. Beautiful outdoor seating, super dog friendly. Wish they had a bigger menu and dessert options! Thank you Modern Times for making your space so enjoyable!

This was my first time dining here at this location as I've been to the Camarillo location several times.    I loved the decor and how some of the tables resembled a dining room, as if you were a guest in someone's home.   The service was great, Ricardo was awesome and he checked up on us every time he was walking by to make sure we were doing Ok.  Highlights: Get the ceviche. The portion is incredibly generous and it's spicy! The calamari was good. crispy and not so oily. It does come with a sweet Thai chili sauce instead of marinara and an aioli, so if you're into marinara, you'll have to ask for that on the side.  I ordered the spicy seafood pasta (can you tell I like spicy food?) and it was good.  The noodles weren't overcooked which was awesome, because I legitimately hate it when noodles are super soft. I found out halfway through eating that there was a lime tucked away under the bowl (it's served in a rectangular bowl on a plate) and it tasted sooooo good with the lime so search!! This also applied for the tequila lime shrimp pasta my dad ordered. When eating it, we could taste the tequila but when we added lime the taste was gone and it was so delicious.  My brother ordered the baseball steak (??) and liked it.  He expected the steak to be seasoned, but it's not. It's literally steak that's grilled to perfection so if you like flavor, they have steak sauce for you!   All in all, this was an amazing experience. We will definitely be coming back again.   Thank you, Ricardo!!!

This location is always fantastic. The guys and gals working there (Shoutout to Ricky) are endlessly helpful and friendly. In the two years I've been going here, they always remember me, have never gotten my order wrong, and are always willing to do my weird or challenging orders.  They are right near my work so we go there often, often using them for catering events too. Everyone working there is very fast, even during the busiest parts of the day, and like I said- never an order wrong. The portions are fantastic, the food luxurious. I have NEVER seen anything skimped on or restricted to where the food is less than desirable. Not to say they waste- its just perfect.  The attitude here is uplifting, welcoming, thankful, and its really a joy to come here and be remembered by name, treated like a friend, and catered to with such positive feelings. Never change!  I think my only complaint is the inability to leave tips via credit card transaction- c'mon upper management! These people deserve tips! I try my best to remember cash, but alas, its all about my cards.  You guys/gals are the best, and you have my gratitude for providing such great service!
Ive been here twice now and everything has been good. The service has been friendly, only thing is drinks do take a little bit. The ceviche  was great as well as the charbroiled oysters.

Wow! I am really impressed with Sakana Sushi! The fish was so fresh!! The presentation was amazing!!! I have never had a roll with so many different flavors, colors, and sauces without it being overwhelming. I didn't even want to use soy sauce (it's that good) I HIGHLY HIGHLY HIGHLY recommend this place!! I will be definitely become a regular here!! Best sushi place in town!! You will not be disappointed! The box basil crab roll is definitely something you should start with ;)

The Ramen with the Black Garlic Oil is delicious, very creamy and savory! I asked for extra broth which is $4.50 extra, well worth it! For all the unlucky souls with the flu, this is very healing and warms the tummy very well! I had leftovers today, it is even better the next day!  Great addition to SB!!!

I cannot attest to the food, although those in our party that ordered some seemed to have gobbled it up. I find the setup kind of strange. Beer at one bar and cocktails at a different bar. That makes it difficult to buy a round for your friends if you're into that. I didn't order anything fancy - I didn't want to mix it up too much from what I had already been drinking due to my rules for successful drinking as a 40 something year old woman. I do not regret that at all, but do wish I could have tried some of their signature cocktails. Next time I'm in town I'll do that! The vibe is cool and I enjoyed myself.
Been here many times and each time it's great. Staff and food is better than most places I've been. Bloody Mary is delicious and their bacon is unbeatable. Can't wait to try dinner.
You shouldn't sell day old breakfast burritos. The tortilla was hard, no exaggeration. Not at all what I was expecting, very disappointing.

If you're in Santa Barbara and want a breakfast burrito, come here. I am hard-pressed to recommend another spot. I'm pretty obsessed with breakfast burritos, and haven't found another spot in the area that can do it better.  Next time I'm back, I'll get a picture and post it. Wish I had one, but it was so good that I didn't remember to take a photo.

Came for the Mayweather and McGregor fight on Saturday. Antoine had a table set aside and gladly paid the cover charge. The delicious beers, the platter I ordered (chicken, tri tip, and shrimp with rice, beans and tortillas) was great.. but most importantly was the service. Everyone kept coming by and asking how the night was, can I get you anything, and also grabbing the used plates and glasses right away. It was a great night and glad I ran into Antoine to set it up. Thanks you guys.  -Dan

Luna Grill is Middle Eastern food the way Panda Express is Chinese food. The menu is a hodgepodge of Lebanese, Greek, Jordanian, Israel, Afghani, and Palestinian favorites, but none of Luna's items truly represent the diverse food cultures they are borrowed from. Every dish is made with American audiences in mind, and this is the kind of place one could see at a mall (okay maybe a high-end mall). The spicy hummus is not spicy, the kababs or gyro not really flavorful, and the salads cater more to post-yoga moms than voracious eaters. Truly unimpressed.

What can I say? Great view, great food, great service. In this case, the reviews are true. This place is awesome. We came early on a Sunday morning and there was no wait. There are a few options for seating but pretty much everyone gets a view. We opted for outside but behind the glass enclosure. The heaters were perfect and kept us warm. We were able to take in the most wonderful views of the oceans (saw several groups of dolphins). The food was so good, well seasoned, and came out hot. Service was on point. Everyone was kind and friendly. After our meal we walked right out to the beach for a stroll. Great spot. Come early for a peaceful and less crowded experience.

They have it all / great food, great service, great drinks, and great location. My fav is the lobster mac and cheese, oyster shooter, and old fashion.  Everyone's nice and it's a relaxing nice place to be.

We ordered two burgers & fries on the phone for takeout. My husband got the baron Von burger but it didn't come with mustard as described. I ordered the pb& j burger but it didn't have any peanut butter, which I think is key to a pb &pjs burger, and also no bacon as listed on the description. I had asked to make sure the baron burger had no mayo but I'm not sure how that translated to no anything. Then our garlic fries were plain, no garlic. I basically got a thick bland patty of meat on a brioche bun with a thick layer of jelly and bland fries. Not quite what I expected.
Cocktails were great with very fresh and tasty food. Wonderful service tidy environment. Would love to visit again.

One of my favorite spots in Santa Barbara.  This location is off the main State Street area with plenty of parking,  as it is in the shopping center. It does, however,  have ample and beautiful outdoor seating.    I had the oatmeal,  which was very smooth and delicious.  My husband greatly enjoyed his fried egg sandwich. The peach tea I had was just OK. The macarons were out of this world! I will definitely return when in the area.
JJ's is great! Juan has done a great job and the wait staff is attentive. Best prices in town and really healthy portions of everything. Overall a great experience.

Great food and fast and friendly service! I've always passed by this place but never bothered to eat here (kind of thought they were closed because it always looked so dark inside???) anyways the brealfast here is yummy and at super affordable prices. (Weekday bfast special is only $5.50!!)

I've loved CPK for years. This location seems to be hit or miss. Sometime good, other times not so much. Had a rather negative experience the other night.  My salad was good although it was devoid of the Monterey Jack Cheese in the Barbecue Chicken salad. The problem was with the waiter who was annoyed when I mentioned this. He had an negative attitude toward me which was undeserved...sort of gave me a lecture. I could go into more detail, but I'm not going to in this venue. Quite uncomfortable for me. Btw when I arrived there was no host available...I patiently waited as I know places are short of help because of COVID... Finally I walked toward the dining area...someone finally said that they'd be with me shortly.  A few minutes later I was seated. The same thing happened to two others as I was leaving. They waited a while and finally said, 'Can we be seated please?'  Over all the waiter was a downer!! Left in a bad mood because of it.   Not an enjoyable experience! Won't be returning to this location!

Great location.  Excellent service.  Good people watching spot if seated outside.  The Tower of Onions was really delicious, you must try it!  Burgers and sandwiches very good too.  Reasonable prices for Santa Barbara tourist area.  Great selection of alcohol too!

I really enjoy the food here. Everything is good quality and delicious, however it's a little pricey. I usually get something to go here before work every week and have alway been satisfied. Don't expect a nice sit down or fancy restaurant. It's more like a fancy  fast food situation. Pasta, sandwiches and garlic bread are all delicious, and portions have been just right for me.

Their service is very poor. The food is good. I love their bahn mi, but it isn't good enough that I wait 30 mins for it. I will give them one last chance though, hopefully they're better staffed by then.

Menu list is simple, but this is the perfect comfort food right here. Rice with grilled teriyaki chicken and cabbage with a buffet of toppings that include fire cucumbers, spicy mayo, and jalapeños. Portions are generous and service is quick. Hits the spot!

Their Kamamoto oysters were delicious, as well as their Jalapeno Yellowtail. The food was so fresh, they were all very tasty. They also have a very wide selection of sake. I would like to come back here to try more of their sashimi. Yum!

Mole Chicken Poblano & Chili Verde were ordered, along with some OJ. 35 minutes later got 2 x Chicken Poblano Moles & no OJ.  Another 15 minutes later, got the correct food but no OJ.  Had to speak to 2 different table waiters/1 waitress (and one of them twice!) to eventually get the Orange Juice.  Food was decent when both meals eventually came out.
The chocolate moose cake is the best. The best thing about this cake is you can eat a lot and doesn't feel full and taste really good.

so as we were riding our bikes through downtown SB, we made this place our last stop before we call it a day. we got there just as they were closing and begged our way in for the last order. they politely let us in and let us order. carnitas, lengua, & fish tacos.  1. tacos are huge 2. lengua is hands down the best   i'm just gonna throw it out there and say, this is the must place to go for mexican food if you're in the SB area. forget the rest.
Everything is excellent sometimes the kitten can look and be a little disorganized but the food is all way flavorful and well worth it

I came here after going wine tasting with one of my friends from back home. We both decided to get the five course tasting menu and split everything. Stand outs for the night were the pan fried octopus, foie gras & doughnuts, duck duo, the AMAZING lamb belly, and the pork belly. The rest were either just okay or were misses. Service was very attentive and on top of their game. Will be coming again for the lamb belly.

Santa Barbara is the perfect place to eat alfresco. This Italian restaurant is no exception.  They have many tables set up outdoors, with red table cloth and umbrellas, and socially distanced.   Complimentary bread and butter is served. The coffee isn't that good and it's lukewarm, even the refill was lukewarm. I ordered the vegetarian cheeseburger, which comes with a side of french fries. Interesting. Can't tell what the vegetarian patty is made out of.  Otherwise, the meal was all good. I enjoy eating lunch outdoors.   Service was friendly, but just ok.

This is not just the best Mexican I have had in Santa Barbara...this is the best I have had anywhere. I dont know what it is but every bite has so much flavor...especially their meat. I want to try everything on the menu. Location is great, great patio seeting, very authentic feel.

I ordered their albacore sandwich last night in Goleta. Tonight I got the same from SB and it was smaller and not as good. I can't find a way to write them because their website covers all locations except local.

This was a very tasty brunch place, but it was a bit of an odd experience in some ways.  But before I get into the nit-picks, if you go there for anything but the various Benedict entrees you are a fool! :P  Absolutely best eggs Benedict in the world.  Now to the nit-picks.  If management is listening, you might consider changing a few minor things to bump this review (and future ones too) up to 5 stars!  First, if you have more than 4 people attending make sure to avoid this location.  4 is the very most you can fit in the tables (inside or out).  I haven't been to the downtown location, but this one is very compact.  If anyone is really big it might be uncomfortable.  Second, while the food is amazing and the decor is well done, you might find their style of service a little off-putting.  For the price and looking at the uniforms, I expected table service.  If you want water, you have to pour it yourself.  They don't take your order, they give you a sticky note with your table number which you take to the register and order/pay there.  They will deliver non-water drinks and your meal, and were pleasant about it.  Honestly, if they could have just taken my order and saved me the trouble of waiting in a Saturday brunch line, that would have been solidly worth the menu prices.  Third, don't order the coffee.  It starts off very nice tasting, but they don't exactly have their espresso dialed in yet.  It finishes very bitter, even with milk and syrup flavor to compensate.  Espresso is more than just an expensive grinder and a good espresso machine.  There is a complex science/art to getting it to match the quality of the food.  Fourth, the furniture is flimsy as all get-out.  It won't collapse on you, but it will flex and have you guessing.

I'm pretty confused as to why this place only has 3.5 stars. Essentially you get the whole package with this place.. The restaurant is extremely clean inside with neat decor, the location is unbeatable with amazing views, and the food was nothing short of outstanding. I came for brunch and ordered the Boathouse Omelette.. I'm a hot sauce person and at most other places I'd have a generous serving of Tapatio/Sriracha/something else with a little kick with every bite. I did ask for Tapatio but only used it with one bite, the omelette was so flavorful on its own that I didn't want to ruin it.   The bloody mary was also delicious, as was the OJ, which I could tell from the first sip was freshly squeezed! Though you can't expect to spend $10 bucks here, I spent a very fair price for what I got, and am so very happy I came! Definitely check this place out for a nice weekend breakfast/brunch with friends and family! I can't speak for the lunch or dinner options, but I'm sure they're excellent as well.. I can't wait to try those as well. Until next time!   ALSO.. I'm not sure if they take reservations, but there was a bit of a wait during prime weekend brunch hours. However, it wasn't a big deal because by the time we were seated we were hungry enough, had a great view, and experienced fairly quick service. I wish I had a complaint, but I don't. 5 Stars!
Ordered their barbacoa, al pastor, and asada street tacos. Lots of choices for salsa.  Long line even for those who did take out. The line kept getting longer after we arrived.

This by far one of my favorite rib restaurant. There signature BBQ sauce is sweet and savory. The portions are huge and can easily be shared. The salad bar is always fresh and has a good variety of items to make a yummy salad.

We went here for Valentine's Day, and I was extremely impressed by their 'set' menu, which had so many options it didn't seem limited at all. Everything was delicious and they gave larger portions than other restaurants that are as expensive. I really recommend for a date night!

pulpo, smoked salmon, acorn fed pork and churros checked again~ broccoli: it contains mixed ta. The sauce is little bit sour, sweet and spicy. The 'flower' is pretty crispy. It think that before it fried it might dive the flour, which enhances the crispy taste. Wagyu steak carpaccio: it's pretty thin. An unknown a dip of green sauce on each slides. With the small marinated olive, it adds an extra flavor of sour and increases the multi layers of the taste. Pan con chocolate: sweetness lovers' top choice

This place is like an IHOP. The food was mediocre, but the service was stellar. I'm a Santa Barbara local and I guess I just prefer the whole 'mom and pop' feel. I could name 3 restaurants off the top of my head that give bigger portions for the same price, with the feel of being in a diner.  Some things I forgot to add in the original review: the mini muffins were stale and cold. My eggs Benedict was sloppy almost, like I found one or two potatoes in my hollandaise? I didn't even order potatoes? The place wasn't even packed and it took the waiter 20 minutes after he took our empty plates to give us our check. Don't get me wrong, he was so sweet but just slow as heck. All in all, will not be returning.
This place had good food and pretty decent service. The location is great and it is fairly inexpensive so if you are looking for a quick food fix then this is your place.

The food here is very fresh and tasty.  I have to give them 5 stars for that and the friendly service.  Once I placed a to go order and an item was missing; the staff were very understanding and comped my order next time.  Great place!

I hadn't been here since pre-pandemic. I remembered  it to be great food. The tacos were a little crispy and a little soft, the cheese was yummy and melty and the flavors were different and enticing. I'm not sure what happened - but I found it to be different now. I had the Cauliflower taco, Al Pastor Taco and fried plantains. The tortillas for the tacos were sub-par, I was so bummed! I actually didn't even enjoy the al pastor taco - I found it to be bland and I wasn't offered any salsa or anything to put on it. The cauliflower taco was ok - the cauliflower was lightly breaded and fried and there were a couple pieces of radish and some other inconsequential  accoutrements. I really wanted more than this for 5.5 for one tortilla and 3 pieces of fried cauliflower. The fried plantains tasted good, they were cut a little thick, this ended up tasting more like a dessert since the plantains were smothered in a white cream sauce. It wasn't what I expected but it was interesting. And the guacamole was about 1 large avocado - very small portion and just ok. I also had a hibiscus lemongrass - it tasted like a regular Jamaica to me, nothing special.  The price of everything is up and I understand that you get less for your money everywhere but I was disappointed. I will go back and try again because I want to like this place again. I think you should try it and see what you think. The flavors have potential and maybe it was just an off Sunday.

This place is great! Everything from the atmosphere to the service! The food was also amazing. I wanted to try everything! I ended up getting tri tip sandwich with onion rings (I will get this again for sure). The food came super quick, all drinks were refilled multiple times. Our server, I forgot her name, was very attentive, sweet and knowledgeable of the menu. The atmosphere is retro chic I would say, super fun! Even though we had to wait, Danny at the front made  the experiance so enjoyable. Definitely worth worth a try!
Good for a hot dog.  $6 meal special is reasonable for SB, but regular prices are high.  This place is clean.  Service is good but not super fast.

TACO TUESDAY - Helping local merchants survive. This was a great meal, $3.00 tacos, loaded with carne asada n chips n salsa. Can't go wrong for a great meal. Help support locals n have a good meal too
Great bakery with awesome, unique breakfast and lunch options. Exceptional service as well in a prime location on State Street. Definitely worth a stop, as it is well worth it.

Lovely place, so glad we finally went. We've stayed at hotel around the corner so many times, and have walked past this restaurant many times. Family members had stopped by previously for drinks, and we all decided it was time to make a reservation.  The service was wonderful, attentive but not overbearing. The olive bread and fried olives are so good, we had to stop before we filled ourselves up too much! The various choices made by our group were rigatoni, lasagne, salmon special, roast chicken, scallops. All received high praise. Tuan cone appetizer also got rave review.  Oh yea, we also had desserts. The phyllo crisp, creme brulee, olive oil cake, and ice cream trio were exceptional.   I have seen that this place rates #1 with the locals; I see why this is so.

This place is a Santa Barbara must try...at some point. The reason mostly is the outdoor patio. The outdoor patio really makes you feel like you are on vacation with tons of foliage and a big overhang. I had been here a couple of times when I used to live in Santa Barbara, but never felt the absolute urge to go. Why? The food is just fine, but nothing out of the ordinary. Since I now come to SB on vacation there are just so many other good places that I feel are must eats. This time, my boyfriend had urged us, saying we always walk by this place and never go, please! Who can say no to that? And so we went.  The ambiance was wonderful as expected, the service just fine, and the food, typical. Maybe I just order the bland things but every time my food is just kind of bland, and this time so was my boyfriends. I had the fish tacos, which with salsa was fine, but the fish could use more seasoning. My boyfriend had a sandwich (sorry can't remember which) that he too felt was just mediocre. So, if you want a cute place to go this is a must, but don't come expecting the best meal of your life.

Absolutely one of the best places for a burger and IPA TRY THE GOLDEN MONKEY a joy to get it on draft and one of the few places that have it!!!! The customizations to the burgers is much appreciated I started with some deviled Eggs that were made with some pan hefts and pickled red onions on top they were delicious than I moved on the 28 day dry aged burger and was cooked to a perfect medium absolutely perfectly the arugula and tomatoes were beyond fresh and it was finished with a balsamic glaze with some fresh cut fries with some homemade ketchup my experience was fantastic my bartender Ian was amazing and took care of my experience to the highest regard get here for happy hour from 2-6 pm and you will get to try everything!!!!    Ps osso buco riblets are to die for !!!!

For 2nd annual Restaurant Week we visited Paradise Cafe and had best grilled artichoke, steak frites, topped off by McConnell's ice cream - dream meal! Staff were friendly and attentive. Can't wait to return.

Took my beautiful wife out to Oppi'z for date night and it was really a great experience! The outdoor seating was so comfortable and with Italian music in the background we felt like we were back in Florence.  Our server, Sara, was so sweet. She guided us through the menu and catered to our every need. She was calm, professional and really made our first time there very enjoyable.  As far as the food.... It was fantastic!  Delicious 'salad in a jar' ( check it out, it's a thing), we tried the Ceci jar and the flavors were so good.  For mains we split both the  Tagliatelle pasta with bolognese sauce as well as the Black Parma Pizza with black dough. The pasta was sublime! Obviously house made and cooked to perfection. The sauce had so many delicious layers, really good. The pizza was also off the charts! Cheese that just made you happy inside right when you bite into it.   First time trying Oppi'z, we will be back! Thank you to Sara for making our evening a romantic one! Highly recommend!

Like many other reviewers, I too stumbled here when the wait at Cajun was just too damn much! BUT - no regrets. Derf's has been around for many years (you can read all about it on the back of their menu) and it's clear to see why it's an SB staple to many residents. The staff is extremely friendly and laid back. They'll bring you water right away, offer mimosas (the 'large', which is filled with about 70% champagne in a pint glass, is a steal at $6.50 if you're just heading to the beach after you eat) and will let you know if the food might take a while, which I appreciated.   While all their items seemed tempting, I stuck with a 2-egg breakfast, which came with home fries and an English muffin - so simple, so delicious. They have a raised patio and umbrellas that make for a very relaxed atmosphere for Sunday (or any morning) breakfast.

Brunch was a 2.5 star experience heavily influenced by sub-par service from disgruntled waitresses. Not a single smile was spotted from the waitstaff.  We waited over 10 minutes for someone to take our beverage orders, 10 minutes to receive coffee and OJ, another 10 minutes to place our breakfast orders, and about 25 minutes to receive our food. The actual chowdown was a quick and dirty business, although we couldn't get out of this establishment any faster. After finally flagging down our server, we sat for about 15 minutes before receiving our check. All interactions with our server were met with an uninterested and indifferent attitude.  Brunch was meant to be leisurely and time was not of the essence that Sunday morning, but the service moved (or didn't move) at a crawling speed. Our dining experience was further complicated by other factors like when we received the wrong beverages and when we had to ask 3 times for our server to bring milk to accompany our coffee.  We kicked off our meal with the fried green tomatoes plated over a corn, tomato, and cilantro salsa and accompanied by a buttermilk, ranch-like dressing. The fried crust on the tomatoes had a light and refreshing texture, which paired well with the spanish flavors from the salsa and the creamy dip.  The wild mushroom, asparagus, and black truffle cheese scramble came with medium eggs and a good portion of vegetables. The cheese was cheddar, which gave the milder scramble a bit more punch in flavor. The baby green salad was topped with shredded carrots and pine nuts, which I thought was a welcomed touch to what might otherwise be a boring side salad. The side of wheat toast was a bit dry and overly toasted.  The smoked bacon, spinach, tomato, onion, and gouda cheese scramble came in a similar fashion as the truffle scramble, along with a side of roasted potatoes. The potatoes were neither particularly soft nor crispy, and struck a middle ground in terms of texture. We chose the cornbread for this dish, which was moist and came with a honey butter.  Lastly, the dungeness crab cake and potato hash plate, another hearty dish, was topped with poached eggs and an avocado and tomato mixture. This dish came with toast as well. Coffee was decent and rewarmed by busy busboys. The OJ I didn't sample.  Overall, the food was good, almost great, but the poor service unfortunately overshadowed this dining experience.

The pizza was good.....you should be able to make some simple pepperoni and veggie thin crust pizzas. What sucked about the whole experience is that the driver never knocked on our door.....he or she just dropped the pizzas off and left. To top it off... they dropped them off on the neighbors doorstep not ours. Our pizzas were cold and we had to reheat them in the oven. I hope this doesn't happen to anyone else moving forward. This was just last night 7-29-20. Anyone who orders pizza for delivery should have the person knock on the door to inform them their pizza is there.....not a good look BOGO!!

While I did enjoy our dinner at Yume I couldn't help but feel that the quality of the sushi was not up to par. It was off somehow. My son enjoyed his katsu meal and we really enjoyed the steak rolls. The sushi was just not the quality I expected according to the rating here on Yelp.

As we were walking down State and looking for someplace nice to have a beer or two we came across this place. Looked very clean and inviting. It was 8:00 on a  Tuesday night and this place was HOPPIN!!!  After looking over the beer menu, we decided to go with 2 beer flights. Each flight is 5 beers, each in a glass equivalent to 1 1/2 to 2 shots. Our bartender decided to make each glass of beer different instead of two identical flights. I thought this was very clever and thoughtful of him.  After he poured each glass and put it all together, he then told my husband what each one was....that was a problem. There is no way anyone could remember what all  ten were. It would have been nice if there was some way to label these. Some of the beers did not need explanation of what they were (darks, fruity wheats) but the others did.   Would I come back? Probably.
Try the BBQ chicken pizza.  It's amazing.  The place itself is plain; clean and frienly.  Just like you'd expect from a little pizzeria.

One of my favorite SB spots. Very unassuming with an awesome lunch and late night menu.   I've tried the fried rice with tri-tip (wonderful)., the crab melt--the house made English muffin bread is so soft and good. I've also had the bahn-mi, which was one of the best things I've ever tasted. The baguette was PERFECT with a chewy soft interior and crisp exterior. The roast beef was so fresh and awesome and whatever aioli they put on it was simply amazing. I want to try everything on the menu, so I'm going to keep coming back. They also have creative house made teas (and sodas?). I tried the grapefruit green tea one day and it was incredible.

great place for brunch, tucked away in the private and rustic la arvada in scenic sb. staff is great, food is better. come for bottomless mimosas and get sloshed. braised short rib benedict and fig burger are incredible.
My favorite place in SB, hands down. Service seems a lot better.  Best hummus ever!!! It's just very expensive.  Gotta make more $$$ to eat her more often.

For a sushi joint in a coastal city with a gorgeous, lighted setting, this place was shockingly inadequate.  Our evening was saved only by the company of good friends. We were there to celebrate a birthday, and the five of us were seated in an upstairs corner.  The sake and beer we ordered came quickly - no complaints there.   Then a busboy came by with a tray of tall glasses of ice cold water. He placed one down, and then the entire tray came down. The water spilled quickly over half the table and - above an apparently slanted floor or table, no less - the ice water spilled into my lap.   I jumped up, and my friends immediately had their cloth napkins for me. Alas, no busboy. In fact, we never saw him again. No waiter either. She came around a couple minutes later, as I was still wiping down the chair with a cloth napkin that didn't collect water. She said, 'Oh, that won't do. You need a towel.' You think??? I was in such a fluster, I didn't realize she thought we had spilled the water because she said, 'Oh, I thought you guys did it.' Nice.   And then? Nothing. I mean, seriously? Not a diva, but the waitresses were acting like nothing happened! It's very hard to believe this happens often and is tolerated. A second waitress came by to take our order and, when we complained, said she would speak to the manager. She returned and said a dessert would be comped. I don't care about free desserts. I can buy mochi for cheap at 99 Ranch and green tea ice cream anywhere. What really would've been wonderful would have been a visit by the manager and a customer service acknowledgement to the bday boy himself for starting his night off wrong.   The food started coming. We eat a lot of sushi. A LOT. I have had better nigiri elsewhere. My bf and I ordered only that, and from the specials side. We don't do rolls. If you like rolls, this place has potential. Our friends ordered a few, and they looked delicious.*  *Except for the one roll I partook in. The halibut cooler appetizer barely held together. It was bland and, simply put, needed more 'fillers.'   Nigiri-wise:   Jalapeno yellowtail: You can't go wrong. So many places do it, and in so many different ways. As long as you have fresh fish and a deft sushi hand, it's solid. This one passed.   Ginger bluefin tuna: Don't know. I ordered it and 15 minutes later, the waitress returned to tell me they were out of bluefin.   Seared hotate: Warm, topped with nothing but a few sprinkles of chives. So, basically the taste of char.   Seared kobe beef: So one of our favorites is an off-menu sushi item at Sushi Roku with wagyu beef. The meat is like butter under uni. If you didn't have teeth, you wouldn't need them. I know it's not fair to compare, but Arigato's kobe was so subpar...it was 'cooked' thoroughly to a medium (no red!) and was so hard to chew. Like a piece of beef in a beef/broccoli Chinese dish.   We were still hungry, but we gave up.   Our ravenous uptake of Mad Dogs a little later was a scene to behold.

Honestly, eat here only for the view only because you can not beat it and it is what makes this restaurant so wonderful!   The Harbor Restaurant is located on the Pier in the touristy part of SB. Anywhere you sit at this restaurant, you have breathtaking views of the ocean and the beach. This is why I love California.   For food, I ordered the Mahi Mahi Tacos and a Margarita. The boy ordered one of the Chicken Dishes (can't remember the exact name) and a mixed drink. My Tacos were wonderful and I ate every bite -- which is a good sign since I'm really picky about fish dishes. My boyfriend said his chicken was really good too and ate the whole thing. Both our drinks were great too and topped off the meal.  Our server was wonderful and attentive. She seemed happy to be at work. I like this.   The only downside is this place is really pricey. Our two meals, two drinks, tax and tip came to close to $60. Jaw dropping expensive for the portion and quality of food. Honestly, you're probably paying for the view or else I couldn't justify paying so much to eat here since I could get the same meal for half the price elsewhere.   Overall, come try this place out for the views alone. And get a drink or food as well if your heart desires.   Cheers!

Located inside the Canary . There is 75 min free parking in the nearby lot . I came for brunch with my boyfriend on a Saturday . There was no wait . I ordered the Pork Belly and Eggs . The flavor was there but the pork was a bit dry . My boyfriend ordered the Lox and Bagel . Pretty basic option . We did find the wrapping paper for the salmon mixed in the salmon so that was unfortunate . Overall, I would say this was overpriced for the quality of food . There are better brunch options on State street for sure .
Tasty cheeseburger.  14 bucks for cheeseburger and fries.  Grass fed and aged meat.  Not a huge burger but definitely quality.

I've been dying to try this place since 9 years ago when I lived in Santa Barbara; so I was ecstatic when I finally got the chance to!  The price for the quality of food is definitely priced right on.  The fact that the restaurant still standing strong and tall is a sign of not over charging the locals and visitors where you wouldn't and can't afford to come back at least a few times a way.    Ordered:  1. Savory Foie Gras - Wow!  This is one of the must order dish!  The fatty foie gras was seared perfectly with a little bite of sea salt right on top.  The soft livery pairs well with the cruton on the bottom, which made it a perfect few bites.  This dish is good for one or good for sharing; anymore than that would be too much since the dish is extremely rich.  2. Forest Mushroom Ragot - another must order dish if you are a mushroom person.  Perfectly salty, perfectly flavored sated in better paired with a little bite of some green puree; yummy!   3. Scallop Trio - My favorite one is the middle one... fave bean with sourdough crouton - there was one that I wasn't so hot on cause it was just way too sweet for a savory dish.  I think this is a dish you can do without.    4. Duck Breast - the portion is huge!  We had our dish split in half, yet neither one of us could finish it.  The breast meat was seared medium rare with a perfect seared crunchy skin.  The meat was tender and juicy where the skin was crispy and crunchy.  Ooo so yummy!  Tip: Make reservation or you won't be getting a setting.

The manager here is an angel.  I have seen him working so hard for so many years and always has a great attitude.  His attitude trickles down to all the staff and overall makes this my favorite place to eat out in town.  The food and everything else is great but the manager is what seems to bring it all together for me.

The princess is my favorite drink here:) so refreshing and perfect for summer hydration. The owner does such a good job at picking polite hard working staff and is amazingly sweet and accommodating as well! So happy Boochie's is right around the corner from my work! I wish the were open for dinner too! The ambiance is amazing for lunch time breaks so peaceful and serene , the owner has hit the nail on the head ! I love it here and can feel the good energy they spread :) Thanks Boochie's !
Horrendous services, mediocre food, ridiculous prices. You're paying for a view.

This place has great staff and the platanos were yummy! The Cubano had wonderful bread that was perfectly pressed with melted cheese. I wish the drinks at the affiliated Shaker Mill had been a little tastier; I think their recipes could use  some work. But overall, it had a good vibe. My kids loved the ice cream:)

I was running errands in Sb and went to The Waterline on the recommendation of a friend. Super cool spot, lots of friendly staff to help you with menu options. I went with the popular rockfish tacos! So good! Great local wines and beers. I chose a rose' after talking with the knowledgeable  bartender. They also support local artists so after eating, I cruised around to check out the current installation. One featured artist is David J. Diamant. His show is Sunset Surfers. Not sure why this is reported closed. It is definitely OPEN! March 11, 2020.

I stayed in Santa Barbara over the weekend. We grabbed some breakfast burritos here for a quick brekkie (as the Aussies would say). The burrito didn't appear massive, but it was so filling. I couldn't finish mine, so I deferred to my boyfriend. They were good though and satisfying enough for what we were looking for. I would check it out if you need something quick and easy to grab and go.
Had dinner here again the other night...who am I kidding! This place deserves 5 stars. Food is ridiculously good.
Amoung the freshest and most delicious sushi we've had anywhere. The fish is pristine, clean and fresh. The service is personal and friendly. Favorite sushi place in Santa Barbara!

This place is not only warm & inviting, its reasonably priced & very good. When you walk in, it appears to be bar with some high type tables. If you don't take a walk to the back, you'd never notice the booths by the fireplace & just beyond those, a room decorated in an Italian villa feel. So glad my wife took a walk back & waved me over.   So we were seated and ordered. Oh btw, this was around  6:30pm & the place was very crowded! After waiting maybe 15 minutes, out came our meals. We had been eating rich foods for the last 3 days, so wanted something not so rich & had the paninis, my wife the eggplant & I had the chicken Cobb. Both delicious.   Service was good, staff friendly & the restaurant was very nice.   In my book a 5 Star.

This place is wonderful. I recently tried this place with my boyfriend before a show at the Arlington theater. First off, the assortment of muffins they give you are freshly baked and so yummy. My boyfriend ordered the blackened red fish and he was 100% in love with it, he could not stop saying how good it was. I had the Barbecue shrimp, which was equally delish! In the middle of dinner they passed out music lyrics and everyone joined in singing Louis Armstrong, 'What a wonderful world' This put a smile on my face - how can you not love that song? We ordered the key lime pie to go...yum! This restaurant was wonderful and makes me want to finally make a trip down to New Orleans soon.
We've been coming here for years si de it opened and our favorites are the queso fundido, the Norteño stack and corriente and la gringa... but honestly, anything you get here, you'll love. Enjoy!
Some girlfriends and I went here for a birthday dinner and the food, service, environment were all amazing. I wish I would have taken pictures because the paella was beautiful.

Highs: -uni was pretty fresh  Lows: -The Chirashi Bowl i ordered looked nothing like the ones in the pictures on Yelp. I saw only about 4 different kinds of sashimi with about two pieces each. 85% of the bowl was just the rice. Not only was I disappointed about the lack of variety of sashimi and the EXTREMELY SMALL PORTIONS, the sashimi wasn't too fresh and the rice was a bit mushy -poor service- i had to wait forever for the waitress to come to my table so I could ask for a check. There seemed to be many waitresses for the size of the place but none were very attentive/helpful

We only visited the ice cream section of the Market but it's definitely worth mentioning that they offer a dog-friendly ice cream called 'chilly dog.' After a day of wine tasting, it's a great treat for a doggie's good behavior :)

Love this spot. Natural wines that I've never heard of and would probably never taste, healthy good food with great flavor combos and a cozy inviting atmosphere. Don't get too attached to any wine because they switch up the menu a lot, which is amazing but also little sad but overall amazing. Seriously had some amazing wines here, some local and also a lot from France etc  Their menu is really nomnom with different variations. We see the chef at the farmers market every week so you know it's quality.  We love the smoked holy shiitake mushroom dish and all things with all the breads, but the soups are also delish. Dang it the salads are yummy too. Shoot, so are the olives and cheeseboard. So everything is good.. I guess I coulda also said that.   Overall, it's just a gem of a spot on upper state.  Love to people watch from the window bar seats and get my glug on. I mean, I put the Lou in Glou-Glou.

I have a coupon that says 'Buy one at menu price, receive one pizza at equal or lesser value FREE expires 12/31/16 Valid at all locations, delivery rates may apply.' I called to order and was first denied and told to throw the coupon away. I asked to speak with the supervisor who again gave me the same answer. Finally they spoke to a somewhat reasonable manager who said they would accept the code but only for carry out when it specifically says delivery is accepted? I am at a business where we were about to order 6 pizzas for my employees, but now am unable to due to bad business practices.

We originally went to a different restaurant for lunch but it was closed, but we found this place.  It's a fish market & restaurant.  I ordered battered fish sandwich and my husband ordered salmon poke bowl.  Salmon poke bowl had too much creamy sauce mixed with salmon but it tasted good.  My sandwich was very good.  I asked for the tarter sauce on the side (simply because I don't like to have too much sauce on it) and she served everything (but fish) on the side.  If I live near by, I would love to buy some fresh fish here!

This is the best pizza in Santa Barbara and some of the best pizza I've had. If you are lucky enough to sit and watch Brendan make your pie, you in for a show. They really care about the product and you can tell by fresh ingredients and perfect crust. I understand they long ferment the dough which makes a thin but stable slice. The sauce is sweet but only in the way a tomato is sweet. The cheese is perfect and there is a great bite to the crust. I could go on and on about but I can really just say go here and you won't be disappointed. Rachel is also awesome and she got a well-deserved high-five at the end of our meal. Watch the video. At Instagram as @gt

We came for breakfast on a Sunday morning.  It is a small and very busy place.  Nothing fancy. The service was fabulous.  We received chips and salsa while waiting​ for our breakfast burritos. We ordered the breakfast burrito with sausage and the bean and rice breakfast burritos.  They were quite large and full of flavor.  I could not finish the whole burrito and saved half for lunch. The prices are also very reasonable!

Back home in Florida, I loved a bar called The Midnight, which was a simple beer-and-wine place with prices and a vibe so great that I'd wanna be there all the time, perfectly happy to join the fun even without alcohol. Finally, Santa Barbara has something like it with Crush Bar. The idea is straightforward: give people a place to hang out without pressure, without excessive fanciness, without the noise of a busy club nor the awkwardness of a quiet winery. But they know that a hangout place needs its own ambiance, and they've got that covered too. Its overt description as an LGBTQ+ friendly place, in addition to the owners' and workers' unapologetic support for Black lives and social justice, makes me feel safe here when I just want a happy hour drink alone as I read a book. The look is classy and there's plenty of space. Plus the little things: when I mentioned that it was my anniversary with my partner, as well, a server surprised us with a cupcake sampling! Which reminds me, regarding the food, the ranch dip is so so so good, I don't understand how. I spoke to Brianna (spelling??) today about their upcoming karaoke event and I'm so excited for it. So yeah, job well done!!

My first experience at Los Arroyos was alright. The atmosphere has a nice feel where it best suits for small families and a go to  restaurant. It holds a family ambiance. The utensils and napkins came in a timely manner. The table wasn't set up yet so we had to wait in our seats for utensils. Los Arroyos is a nice restaurant. Food was good but nothing that I haven't tried elsewhere. I prefer their Goleta location.
I remember coming here after basketball game championships since I was a kid. This place is a true santa barbara staple and I have many fond memories here. Love the atmosphere good food and beer!
Came by for the pizza and beer and we enjoyed ourselves a lot. The place was noisy, but the pizza was pretty good. Service was ok.

This is a pretty generic, Orange-Countyish brewpub. Kind of sterile, and the beers that we had were pretty bland. TVs playing and generic looking guys watching sports. Not my cup of tea. Also had that 'wet dishtowel' smell.

We were across town at Hendry's Beach at 5 PM, with tickets for an event downtown at 7. DH made a half-hearted effort to talk me into going to some other Mexican restaurant more convenient, quicker, blah blah. I said NO. Although we waited about a half-hour to order and a half-hour for food (beginning at 5:30 on Friday evening), he turned to me at the end of the delicious meal and said, 'Next time we're arguing over where to go for Mexican in SB, just remind me of this.' IT'S ALWAYS WORTH IT.

I have had numerous bad experiences here. The sandwiches are totally great, but the service is some of the worst I've ever experienced. Whenever we order (call ahead) we are told it'll be 20 minutes or so, but we don't get our sandwiches until, like, 45 minutes after we place the order. When we arrive and our order isn't ready, we have asked 'how much longer' and we are told (in a very hostile tone!) that there are 'many people in front of us' and they never actually give an estimate. One time, we ordered the 'Herbert Hoover' and were aggressively told that they don't have that on their menu and that we were looking at the wrong menu. When we showed her the menu on our phone that we got from the Ike's Santa Barbara website menu link, she continued arguing with us that we were looking at the wrong menu. We finally asked her if it was possible to make the 'Herbert Hoover' and she finally said 'yea, that's on our menu but it's called the Michael Jordan' (or whatever it's called... I don't remember because she was so mean that I wasn't even listening anymore). She could have handled the situation differently, but she was looking for a fight. Honestly, I could tell more stories, but bottom line I am never eating here again and I suggest that you don't eat here either.

Horrible service! Nobody say a word, just sitting and waiting, and then we left!  This is a place where food is amazing, but let you know, we payed $70 fir breakfast last time, me and my husband. So its not cheap! I want a proper service for this price! What do you think??
this place rocks!  the longest i have waited for my tacos here is maybe 1 minute.  I've been to mexico so many times, and this place reminds me of the taco places in mexico.

The food was beautifully prepared, perfectly executed. The menu in general could be a little more refined for the price... cauliflower?  Nah. I want oysters but not at $4 each. I couldn't see the food, too dark at the bar at least, the bowl was black so I could not appreciate the whole experience. Food need to be visually stimulating too and I'm sure it was but the bar is too dark. The problem is not the food, it's the lighting. Cocktails were ok, loved the bell pepper infused gin. Could be a little stronger. Santa Barbara is cocktail 'savvy', people can take exotic and unusual cocktails but they need also to be visually appealing. It's the Instagram age, people want to take photos! Some bars have cocktails with edible orchids and unique decoration in it. You can do a lot better...I want to be surprised and blown away by my cocktail!  Decor is beautiful and intimate, chic, it's perfect for romantic dates and winter nights but a little boring. Too showroom-y... what about some local funky art? Something exciting on the walls or tables? it lacks character. Looks too 'world market'. Loved the uniforms, staff was nice, food was delicious so I'll definitely recommend it.
Fantastic service , kitchen ON POINT , unbeatable view from the upstairs patio. Thoughtfully planned out menu, fresh fresh seafood. Will be here often!

Dope little spot... Santa Barbara being my home town, I rarely come back, so this is a new joint for me. All I can say is that this joint is a good spot. Check it out and enjoy the food. Bomb burger and fries, cool servers with quality service... check this place out.

Last month picked up a dozen bagels for co-workers. Had no expectations having only been here once for a donut months ago. The line was long and it's a little awkward when you check out as there isn't much communication between the guy picking out your bagels and the cashier.. people are polite and take appropriate turns. Selected one of each bagel type (go big or go home!) didn't personally sample each type but the poppyseed bagel I had was soft and chewy. Which is preferred. The bagels are kinda large and you definitely  feel satisfied after finishing one. No  cream cheese for me! Coworkers also liked their choice of bagel. Will definitely return for my next bagel run for work.

What a surprise! I expected little and was utterly impressed. From the attentive service, to the tasty food, there is nothing I can say that will not elevate Opal's reputation. They do it all very well! Incredibly impressed!!!!! Keep it up!

Delicious!  Had breakfast here this morning. 5 of us plus our pooch.  Popo had ham and eggs. Thought it was delicious. Tender ham with over easy eggs.  Wife had bacon, cheese and avocado omelet. She loved it! Loaded and I mean loaded with pieces of crispy chewy bacon.  Daughter had the BLTA. She thought it was delicious. Nice cuts of bacon, ripe avocado and crisp greens on a nicely toasted piece of sour dough bread.  Son and I had the eggs Benedict.  Perfectly poached egg on top of a slice of ham on top of an English muffin all covered with a very tasty hollandaise sauce. Very yummy!  Pooch had a burger patty and loved it.  Very nice place for breakfast!!

My review of this cleverly named sausage joint most likely will be a bit biased, due to the fact that the majority of my visits are after the bars close. So my judgement may or may not be a wee bit distorted.   One time I remember going there and getting a hot dog. I don't remember if I liked it, and I'm not sure if that's a good thing or a bad thing. I think it's a good sign if you're blacked out and you remember what you ate.  Recently, I went there and got their sliders. $5 for three. Yes, I was blacked out. Yes, I remembered my sliders.   If I was able to give half a star, I would because they have Big Buck Hunter there.
This place is good but why is it so pricey?  I paid $18 for a lunch salad in a fancy 'food court'.  And this is without a drink or any extras because everything is priced a la carte.

Well, I've never left anything resembling a negative review on a business before but this place is consistently disappointing- even when one is trying to avoid it. Want to get a nice cup of coffee and an ice cream in the early hours of the day on a Sunday? I hope you're ready for some club music and screaming sports fans echoing throughout the public market.  I have been twice though before I got fed up with it. I was ignored while it was almost empty because the guy was talking to his friend, so we just ended up interrupting and got an annoyed look for it. The next time, the guy (a different guy) was flirting with some girl and wouldn't take our order for about 10 minutes; there were like 8 people waiting. Oh, and nobody came and checked up on us either time, so you have to go back to the bar to order again, which is fine and kind of standard at some places, but they also didn't bus our glasses for the empties?? I never get into these situations going out since I worked in the service industry throughout college and I get how unreasonable customers can be, but this place cracks me up it's so bad. Good rotating tap though so 2 stars.

Loved every minute of our stay! The staff is very friendly and approachable. The property is immaculate and right on the coast. Every detail has been thought of, including a sangria toast to the sunset. We enjoyed the wine tasting, which we were pleasantly surprised with an enthusiastic and knowledgeable host. The Bacara bar and Bistro had great atmosphere and service as well. This is definitely where we will stay again when we are in SB!

This was a really fun experience and the food was great! Very simple and straightforward spicy Cajun seafood. Our server, Cody, was great and super helpful. I had a lb of shrimp and a lb of king crab and added the corn and potatoes. We got Cajun, garlic butter and lemon pepper sauces and they were so good! Prepare to get messy but leave full and happy! We laughed a lot about the getting messy part which made it even more fun. Highly recommend for the experience.

First visit and we will definitely be back. We got the pork belly bahn mi, chef's plate (2oz. ribs, 2oz, brisket, 2oz. pulled pork with coleslaw, potato salad, and bbq beans), collard greens, the Kielbasa, and the jalapeño corn bread. Ended up being about $50 for what amounts to about two nights of food for two people- great deal!   That bahn mi sandwich was AMAZING. The smokiness in combination with the creamy and crunchy veggies against what I think was some pate was just killer. Think it sat in the box just a tad too long while prepping the other items because the bottom piece of bread was a little soggy, but man was it good.  The corn bread is the only good piece of corn bread I've had in town.. perfect with the honey butter.  Chef's plate had good portions for the price. Brisket was a little dry to me, but all the meats had great flavor and excellent bark. Kind of bummed that we ended up with the bean caviar instead of the other beans. The bean caviar was super tasty, we just wanted to try the warm ones. The sauces (4 variations- mustard, beer, coffee & spicy)- I hope they start selling them because we'd definitely buy!   The greens and the kielbasa were perfect. Nice snap on the kielbasa and a really unusual smokiness in the greens. Probably sautéed with a little bit of fat from the meats.   Other than a few minor things, totally crushed it! Can't wait to try more.
