A brief update to say the second visit was just as great as the first. Tonight Oscar served us and he was wonderful, very attentive. Breana came by to say hi too!   I seriously can't get enough of their popcorn. It's served like bread or chips would be prior to the meal.   You have got to try the Whipped Sheep's Feta and pistachio dip. We had the chili oil put on the side, since I can't eat it. It's delicious without, I imagine it's just as delicious with. It's creamy with just the right amount of salt.

Last time Lived here all my life.  Counted on Chase to make mothers day dinner special. Ordered 1 day in advance. 45 min after scheduled delivery mom not eating and restaurants says 'super busy'. There are plenty of local places to support. Not going to Chase anymore.
A very pleasant experience having lunch outdoors at The Honor Bar.  Salads are particularly good.  Great wine selection and friendly service.
Always delicious. Love their Thai coconut soup and lettuce wrap. Steamed buns hit the spot as well. I'd give a higher rating if I could!
I LOVE THIS PLACE! It's a really cute place and general sanders is my favorite! I think it's a little pricey but everything on the menu is really fresh and good

A great place to get brews and food in Santa Barbara. I had the seared ahi salad which was fresh, flavorful and fantastic. Great beer selection, good service. A bit on the pricey side, but not outrageous. Would definitely go back to this place again.
Smoothies that are fresh, tasty, creative and wholesome. Enjoyed everything we tried, and still felt great after.

Great food  Tried chicken mole  2 thighs moist sauce not sweet  Great homemade corn tortillas Generous portions  Clean environment with  wonderful art My son had the mixed seafood fajitas which had a generous portion of shrimp, breaded fish, octopus and scallop.  Good happy hour and choice of desserts if u have room
They give a lot a lot of foood for your money ' worth the price for what you get ! Try It authentic and yummy! Taco joint ! Small and always has a line but that usually means something right?

I went to where my GPS told me to go and couldn't find this place I was bummed. I really love street art but unfortunately didn't have the luck of finding this place. Theres another yelper who said the wall had been demolished :(

Got MONTEZOOMAS  REVENGE TWO WEEKS IN A ROW AT THIS PLACE. Something is wrong here with the food handling.  The decor is excellent & the staff are great. The food has to be contaminated. DONT EAT HERE,!

I came in on my last day in town before I moved to use a gift card that I had been given long ago. I was sad that I hadn't come in sooner; I definitely would've been a regular!  The seating is both indoors and out with a nice modern decor. The service was fast and friendly.  The burgers were excellent and quite large! Everything was extremely tasty and cooked to perfection. My only wish was for a more varied menu in terms of burger toppings/ buns (most sounded similar to me).

So just gotta say, this is a fun place to eat good Korean bbq. Service was a little spotty, but friendly and helpful when they remembered us.  The only thing I have an issue with was the hot green tea we ordered was late, and dinner was nearly over when the green tea arrived. It was cold. The servers had forgotten to bring it. The food was amazing, we had the beef brisket,  and garlic chicken.   Something for lost in translation and they brought so much food,... it was made clear when the bill arrived and we realized our dinner order was somehow doubled. Oh well, our first time but I plan on coming back one day. Just not soon.  Next time we will be wiser and more vocal about what we order. Make them repeat back to us what we want.
Totally worth coming here, love the food and staff!

Loved the service and location in front of the beach. But loved the delicious food. Not sure why the name was changed from Sambos to Chad's. Being a born Santa Barbrin...I grew up with Sambos. Prefer that name. But the food and service were amazing for brunch. Must go there!! Umm yes must do the mimosa flight. Makes it more fun for those brunchers.

Came here after craving dessert. We had a hard time finding parking but finally decided on the parking structure.  We walked in to a good atmosphere. Nice music, warm heater, nice lighting.  We got our menus from Clarisa, who sat us in the corner.  She was very nice and attentive.  I ordered the Brittany and the girlfriend ordered the Nutella.  They were both to die for.  We love this place.   We will definitely be returning.

Funny, funky little place. The sashimi was fresh enough but completely butchered in the cutting process. It was like the the chef used a swiss army knife. The scallops on the dynamite roll had a a slightly off flavor -- not that fresh. Based on this (because freshness is the most important thing when it comes to sushi) I would give it 2.5 starts but that isn't an option on Yelp. Service was fine. I have high expectations and other options for sushi. I wanted to like Shintori because it's convenient, but I'll have to pass.

I'm torn between a 4 and 5 star rating but from what I hear, the owner seemed to work real hard and built (or at least decorated) the restaurant from scratch. The first thing I noticed was that the men's restroom had cute little pineapples hand painted on all four walls! I mean they probably used a stencil but still, that's a lotta work! So let's digress from... bathroom talk.  The cashier greeted me and answered my questions. I was eyeing the duck and lamb tacos and he only reinforced my decision. I somehow forgot what a paratha was and he had to explain. Honestly I was second guessing my decision in eating here... $9.50 for a taco? seriously??? It sounded absurdly expensive. I bit the bullet and ordered a paratha lamb taco $9.50 and a corn duck taco $4.   Let me quote the menu: Recado rojo lamb shoulder, tzatziki, eggplant salsa. AND five-spice confit duck, hot mustard pickled cucumber, radish, sweet soy, sesame. Wow. I was a bit impatient about the food but it came out after maybe 15 mins? I was not disappointed. Both my tacos were heavy and PACKED.   The paratha with the lamb was amazing. The lamb wasn't too gamey. It was packed with cucumbers, arugla, and eggplant. It wasn't overly flavored which is how I like my food but I suppose everything worked well together... I was super hungry and I wolfed the thing down so... maybe I need to go back for a second opinion. But I love eggplant, I love arugla, I love meat... I didn't know what recado rojo was but I guess its a mix of seasonings / spices. I was hoping more for a curry style lamb but It was still good. The duck taco was alright. It was also packed and I couldn't even pick it up without stuff falling out. I hate radishes so minus points there. It was too bitter. ugh. But everything else tasted good. I tried the duck by itself and it was amazing. I think the amount of toppings and veggies that they put on the tacos kind of overpowers the meat flavors. boooo. I really enjoyed everything though and I feel like $14 wasn't a bad price to pay for everything.  I'd definitely come back and try their other tacos.   Oh lastly, related to my review of Flavor of India. Oh my lord. The water tasted so bad! I don't know what's up with SB's tap but jeez. Make sure you chug the water so you don't taste the overpowering pool flavors.

I have been coming here for many years. I have been here several times for my birthday, a time or two for a family event: birthdays, baptisms, anniversaries. You get the picture. So when i was invited to a friends bd dinner at the Tee Off, i jumped at the chance. I/We always start with drinks and Schrooms though i think that the Tee has changed the presentation of these. But: the schroomers are still my favorite among all of the sauteed mushrooms i have eaten all over the world. Yes right here in Loud family SB! Service wise: the Tee Off has always been dependent on whether the very experienced wait staff is having a good night or not. Yet the service has always been professional if it has not always been friendly or personal. I think that this is the major issue about the Tee Off and has always been. The Ladies of the Tee Off tend to as a general rule segregate their tables into categories: the tourists, the veterans, the others. I have gotten great, personal service here and ok efficient service from a couple of servers. But: i am smart enough to ignore any bs and just enjoy the company that i am with as well as the good to great food they serve here. We started at the Bar and i have to say that the bartenders (Matt) here have always been friendly, enthusiastic and personal. Martinis and garlic bread? Yes! We started with the famous Tee Off's plate of sautéed Mushrooms and they are delicious as always: chocked full of big fat small creminis', wine, veal demi-glace . Always delicious and they never change. We all ordered Prime Rib and shared the large cut, med rare. It arrived exactly as ordered with a big fat Baked Potato plus sour dough rolls. We both had a salad with Blue cheese. The greens are crisp and cold and the dressing is jammed packed with real blue cheese chunks. The Tee Off does not skimp on the accoutrements or the primary entrees, appetizers or desserts as well as the drinks. Oh, the drinks! Heavy pours are the going thing here and have always been as far as i know. The food here is and has always been first rate: prime for the Beef and top choice for the pork and chicken. This is food that you can write home about. The Chef in charge has his foot and hand on all that goes out to the customer and the final products show this firm grip. The Tee Off bar opens at 4 with the restaurant at 5 most days. We arrived early, had drinks and then the food at a booth in front. Though for me either dining room is a good place to people watch. You can make reservations if you like. We had a primo time here and the server was friendly and pleasant and more to the point, efficient. Cheers to the Tee Off and Happy New Year.

Pros: We love the happy hour with friends, fresh food, delicious cocktail concoctions, and fairly priced too. Cons:  Though it has outdoor seating, and with so much space 'al fresco' sadly, the owners didn't designate an area for customers with pets. (one of the reasons we like Islands next door).

SO HAPPY I FINALLY CAME IN! Walked by this place so many times, always wanted to try it but never really got around to it till I went with a friend, it was her favorite place. Food was absolutely amazing, as were the drinks, and the atmosphere was really nice. Only weird thing was one of the servers had a tiny crop top bra thing on with a bunch of chains which seemed a little out of place.

Was going to give 4 stars but then I remembered, I really don't like tacos all that well and these I enjoyed (salmon tacos). Good number of salmon chunks, and a good amount of guacamole. Sauces weren't all that spicy I was able to manage.   Great view,  service was slower than I expected. But food wade up for it

Lunch buffet for just about $10. So worth it and the food is delicious. The buffet itself has a variety of meat and vegetarian dishes so it's perfect for anyone that has dietary restrictions, or groups with meat eaters and non meat eaters. You have veggies options, rice dishes, and even dessert (rice pudding was amazing). I was particularly fond of a green sauce found in the condiment section of the buffet, I asked a man what it was and he said it was a blend of cilantro, greens, spices, and a bit of mint--so good! Besides the delicious food, I also really appreciated the super friendly staff that is seriously the most attentive staff at a restaurant, just about ever. Definitely recommend this place and I'm bummed I didn't take a picture of my food

Just had one of the tastiest meals I have ever had. We sat at the bar and started with the special fresh cucumber and gin cocktail and a Hobo Cabernet from Alexander Valley. Appetizers of refreshing ceviche full of tasty herbs and lime and (I never thought I would be saying this) umami bomb Brussels sprouts with a carmelized sugar fish sauce with Korean pepper that we devoured. I had one of the specials: squid and pork belly with Adzuki beans. It had a Fresno pepper verde sauce over it that was magical with the squid. D had the Local Black cod off the regular menu that had a browned butter sauce with miso and cooked perfectly. Peter and Johnny, the chefs, were very gracious and we enjoyed watching them work their artistry.

Fantastic and fun place! I'm so happy to have this restaurant in Santa Barbara. This restaurant has it all--the food, the drinks, and the  ambience. We tired the grilled vegetable Paella and the tortilla and all of it was wonderful. The drinks were refreshing and tasty. Kudos to the wait staff who were all friendly, knowledgeable and attentive. Can't wait to return!

Best burger!  We live in SB and had just finished our daily walk down along the shoreline and harbor.  Thought we'd try out the new reopened restaurant 'Salty on the Beach'.   This is the upstairs restaurant.  The Kobe beef burger was cooked to perfection with amazing flavor.  The best I've had.  We had a choice of regular fries or upgraded fries.  The upgraded were so good. Yum!  I think this is now my favorite burger in our town.  The waitstaff were super friendly, and the view of the harbor from upstairs is awesome.  Definitely will return.

Our experience at Oku was great!   My parents came to visit and we were looking for a nice dinner near the water so we could have a nice view. We booked Oku through Resy.  My dad and husband went to park the car while my mother and I shared a drink at the bar while we waited. The staff was friendly and welcoming as soon as we arrived.   We were seated on the wine/ private room which was nice because we could still feel part of the rest of the restaurant while also having a nice area so we could talk more intimately.   The wine and beer selection was great. Our waitress, Sol was amazing! We ordered so much and my parents kept asking her questions. Sol was polite and attentive. She answered all our questions while delivers waters and drinks and helping make sure our table was clean for each round of sushi that came. She was also very friendly and got to know us a little during our visit.  The sushi was fresh and tasted amazing. The staff was well informed of the differences in the pieces. We even ordered fresh wasabi that they grated at our table. My mom wanted something other than sushi so she got the steak which was really good. We shared the scallops as an appetizer. Those were some of the best ones I had ever had.   The space itself is very beautiful. It's a perfect space for friends, family, or even a date. I'd like to sit on the roof next time. We will be coming back!

Tried this place due to it's dog-friendly policy, ended up with a relaxed lunch in the faux-French backyard ambiance. Espresso martini was an 'eye-opener,' but my wife's spicy bloody mary made me jealous. Cheese plate excellent, & top notch attention to detail with all the food. Got the blue cheese burger which is apparently what the place is known for, didn't blow me away-& reason for holding back from 5star-but that's my own fault (not a huge fan of blue cheese on a burger-but often try signature dishes anyway despite this). Waitress, Holly, was helpful, enthusiastic, & added to the experience. Upped the Gordo index with the Bread Pudding for dessert, was a nice send-off, but not really a must.

We brunched with La Paloma on Saturday, and it was amazing. We started with the Avocado Tostada and Farmer's Market Fruit Salad - both super fresh and flavorful. The Burnt End Bowl had the most tender tri-tip, and was well balanced with the rice and pinquinto beans. Would definitely recommend visiting this downtown gem for brunch.

What a lovely spot!  We had their ceviche with cocktails and a generous beer pour.  The sea bass was beautiful and their homemade tartar sauce was delicious.  We were so wrapped up in conversation it took unusually long for us to decide what to eat and our servers were very nice about it.  Excellent service and comfortable outdoor dining.  Very happy we finally got around to trying this place, with its ample seating, parking, great service and food.

I have been eating at this establishment for years now.  Food is good and they have a great selection of beer.    Niko the bartender who decided to wait on our table was terrible.  He was busy hanging out with his friends at the bar.  When we finally got our check I asked him if he could split the check for us.  He said 'I don't know what you ordered' and walked away.  An appropriate tip was left for his poor service.    As we're leaving, he yells at us 'thanks a lot, I will remember you guys!'  I can only assume that means he's the type of server that spits in your food.  I guess I wont be going back.

For a place that focuses on hamburgers, I was disappointed that their burger was so bland, dense, and unseasoned.  The wait staff seemed lost and the service was slow.  They did make some good fries and the root beer float I had was in the damn good category.  However, if you are looking for a burger, I would recommend going elsewhere.

Thanks for the comment, Richard and Tina. I may have been a bit harsh. I ate a soup, which I can't remember now, which was overly sweet, and a scallop salad, which failed to balance its sweetness as well. I ate alone, and was seated toward the corner of the back room, fairly isolated. The servers did not make it over very often, which unnecessarily prolonged my stay. But thanks for your earnest efforts and your feedback.

Can't say enough great things! The new owners make  The best cakes. They are so friendly and accommodating. I've called for last minute cakes and they always make it happen! Cupcakes are amazing, perfect size and sweetness with pretty icing ! My family is obsessed with the white cake with whipped cream frosting and strawberries, we love it! I would not order cake from anyone else in town! The cakes are always fresh and beautiful! So yummy! Thank you !!!!

Barbareno is hands down my favorite dinner I've ever had in Santa Barbara. The food was really delicious, the service was spectacular and I like the tent set up they have outside during these pandemic times. I would 100% recommend this place to anyone visiting Santa Barbara. I ordered the pork chop and my husband ordered the tri tip and I have to say I had a bite and it was the best tri tip I've ever had. I'll definitely be back the next time we come up for a weekend trip from LA.
Huge portions, delicious chicken fried steak, mmmm gravy and awesome Bloody Mary.  Great little hole in the wall
Once again, Oliver's hits it out of the park. Food, service and atmosphere are top notch

Loved this place - went after a concert and was the perfect way to end the night - great food and great ambiance -  But I'm giving this place 5 stars because of our server - Edrick - rather than just take our order and move on - he was engaging and entertaining and made our dinner more enjoyable - after our order he recommended the ribblets  - I was a little unsure at first, but these things were amazing. make sure to save room for desert and get the Rum Pudding was just amazing!!! -

Locally owned and creative sandwiches wraps and salads. I've enjoyed cold sandwiches, paninis and salads here. Great for lunch.  I happily fed my whole work team here last Friday and we received incredibly happy and courteous assistance from Owen. He was great in the phone and had everything ready to go in 20 minutes.  My favorite thing so far has been the Crispy Chicken Cobb Salad made with tasty fried chicken, a wonderful egg salad, and a zesty dressing.  Don't forget the dill pickle popcorn!

Very good Mexican food, my wife and I were very impressed. I got the Chile Relleno burrito with bean and rice. The chili relleno tasted so fresh and had a little crunch so they didn't cook it too long. Wife ordered a chile verde burrito which was very good. Both burritos had plenty of filling and worth the money. We also ordered potato taquitos. You don't see potato taquitos very often at fast food Mexican restaurants. They were very good, plenty of potatoes, seasoned perfectly and you get three to an order.

Easily the best acai bowl spot in Santa Barbara!  It was tough choosing which bowl I wanted to try and it was a close race between the Island Bowl and the Dragon Bowl. Ultimately, I decided to go with the Dragon Bowl, which contains banana, mango, pitaya, and kiwi along with the honey, coconut shavings, and granola as the fixins'.   First off, this bowl was humongous. I was definitely struggling to finish everything. There was a lot of hearty chunks of banana and kiwi inside so I definitely started getting full. Although the acai was good and not too sweet, the texture was almost glue-like rather than similar to a smoothie. I wasn't sure if that was the way that it was supposed to be but the texture was too thick for my liking. Aside from the acai's texture being off-putting, the fruit was fresh and sweet.   Here's a tip, but not really a tip: I've experienced in the past, such as at Blenders, where the acai was way too sweet because of the excessive honey drizzle. I would recommend asking for a light drizzle since the acai and fresh fruit will balance it out.

Still really enjoy it here. So professional and courteous. Quick and friendly service and they accept requests around dietary restrictions. Not the healthiest place in town but good burger and salad as well as ambiance.

So I always get the same thing when I go here and I enjoy it and it makes me happy - chicken korma. rice. naan. I have no complaints about this, however service is sort of a joke inside. They ask you the same questions multiple times and get confused and forget things. I went once and my sister ordered a mango lassi and they just never brought it. She decided she didn't really want it anyway, so it was fine. Another time my friend ordered a salad that again, just never came. The next time we saw our waiter he brought our food so we just let it go.   So yes, I eat here occasionally, and I like it. Don't get your hopes up too high though.

Absolutely the best ramen in town. Their noodle bowls are to die for! I would wholeheartedly recommend the chicken soba, or the veggie udon. They also have wonderful service. This is a really good place for a date. Probably come here once or twice a month at least! Service is amazing there and they take allergies and health restrictions very seriously.

I'll preface this by saying I hate writing bad reviews. I rarely write them. We work in food service so we are extra understanding of staffing shortages and covid challenges, but tonight was just really awful - they weren't even trying. We had a little time to kill before a show at the Lobero but there weren't many restaurants open and Kai was mostly empty, so I figured it would be quick and I remembered it being pretty good the last times I ate here 15ish years ago. I have never felt so unwelcome sitting at a sushi bar. We were the only ones sitting up there and ordered 2 rolls from the chef within 2-3 min of sitting down, figuring that's all we'd have time to eat before heading back to the theater. The server came and took our drink order but never came back to ask if we wanted anything else off the menu or to refill drinks. It took over 30 minutes for the first roll to come out and another 10 minutes for the second one.  The server never brought us napkins, chopsticks, soy sauce bowls, etc, so once we finally had our first roll we had to ask her to bring stuff so we could eat. Immediately after, I flagged her down to ask if she could go ahead and run my card so we could make a quick exit since I knew we were now on track to be late getting back to our show. I left a 20% gratuity hoping the experience would get better and finish off well. The rainbow roll tasted fine, but the fish was cut really poorly and the whole roll was falling apart so it was really hard to eat. The king cobra roll was just not good and super overpriced. We realized well after ordering that it was 20.95, the most expensive roll on the menu, which would have been fine since it said it had 'crab meat'  but it turned out to just be imitation crab, super disappointing and again rolled and cut so poorly that it was impossible to eat without making a mess. The two sushi chefs were totally apathetic and had zero sense of urgency about getting food out, and they weren't even wearing masks (I wish I'd noticed this before we ordered as I would have left). The total before tip was 44.00 for two rolls and a Diet Coke. I felt totally ripped off and bummed about the experience overall both while sitting there and after we left. I will never spend my money here again. Our sushi go-tos are Edomasa and Teru (newly opened on State and Victoria by a longtime chef of Sakana), both of which take pride in the quality of food they serve and the service they provide. There are a ton of sushi places in Santa Barbara and within walking distance from this place that you'd probably be better off eating at.

My sister came into town & we were brain storming where to go for dinner.   I could not believe she has never been to Jills!!!  She was born and raised in Santa Barbara!  She said she was not that hungry but ended up eating the entire chicken sandwich, and couldnt stop talking about how much she enjoyed her dinner.  The waiter and server were so nice and funny too.  I love coming for their breakfast burritos too.

Loved everything about our stay here!  The service is impeccable, the bathrooms have heated floors and luxe amenities, we had our own walk-in closet, and the hotel provided the fluffiest robes I've ever encountered.  I loved visiting the turtle pond.  You can pick up turtle food from the valet and feed the turtles, which my husband and I did everyday.  The hotel is very close to downtown Santa Barbara and the funk zone, but it's located on a hill, which makes it feel nice and secluded.  One of our fav perks of this hotel was the free e-bike rentals.  The e-bikes made it super easy to ride down to State Street and the beach for a quick little drink and a bite at Rusty's Pizza.  Getting the bikes back up to the hotel was a little tough though, given the incline of the hills, so don't drink too much!  I also really liked the car service, which we took into town for dinner.    We didn't eat at the hotel, as we were a bit burnt out on fine dining, but the Las Casitas cocktails we grabbed from the bar were some of the best cocktails I've had.
The food is good but the views even better it's a really nice little spot I like it a lot

This is my third time here and is becoming one of my favorite healthy options. I'm always interested in checking out new places, which is what initially brought me in. At first glance it didn't seem like a place I would enjoy because it seemed to healthy to be good, but I was completely wrong.   Their staff recognized I didn't know what half of the menu items were and talked me into a quinoa burger. Now I've never eaten a veggie burger before coming to this place, and I've just had my second one today. I've never even wanted to try one, but I'm so glad I did. Not only was it really flavorful, but I was satiated without feeling lethargic...like when I eat some traditional burgers.   Even if you're like me, and don't always choose the healthiest food options, this is a great place to try new things. Don't worry they'll give you some solid recommendations!

The atmosphere was warm and inviting. My dinner buddy had the Halibut and LOVED it my Ahi sesame Tuna was great. Portions are wonderful and everything was very fresh. We both enjoyed the artichoke soup. I am not going to run back to Holdren's but I am happy to return if the occasion arises.

Came here to celebrate my friends 21st! The reservation process was a bit of a hassle. You can call ahead to make a reservation but then you must call again the day of to secure your reservation. However I think they got confused and thought that my friends reservation was a different person, so we ended up having to make two reservations. It all worked out though.  The service was incredible! We got seated right when we arrived and chips and salsa were immediately brought to our table. They were very attentive to getting our orders as well. I brought flowers for my friend and they brought over a little vase with water that she could put the flowers in. Couldn't ask for friendlier service really! They were also very helpful in recommending drinks.  The food was absolutely delicious. Full of a rich profile of flavors. I ordered the Tacos Salmon and the salmon was so juicy and paired perfectly with the grilled pineapple. It is on the more expensive side but you're paying for the atmosphere and the service as well so I felt it was fair.   I'm definitely coming back here soon! If visiting or you live in the area and haven't been here yet, you have to stop by. Even if it's just to get a cocktail from their long list of drinks!

It's been said many times that these are the best tacos in Santa Barbara because...it's true.  Everything is just, hands down, amazing.  And the Norteno taco?  *Italian chef hand kiss* Delicious!    Located in Public Market with so many amazing dining options, it's pretty telling that I continue to go back to Corazon. And I will continue to do so. So get into line, even when it reaches The Garden, order seriously anything on the menu, find a seat, and enjoy!  You'll be back, I guarantee it.   Five stars.

New fun restaurant with excellent spicy flavor.  Do your taste buds a favor and go have lunch or dinner out on State Street.  Yes it downtown but parking is getting better.  We had spicy jerk chicken and the Eggplant veggie tower!

The best Mexican food in town. Authentic recipes made fresh to order. Don't skip the #6 or the Veggie Tamale special. Fresh guacamole is a must and if you like pork be sure to try the #16 La Super Rica Especial. Cash only!
We just tried Craft Ramen for the first time and thought it was very good.  Flavorful Tonkatsu broth, quality pork belly and proper noodles.  We'll be back.

I am too old to put up with poor customer service.   I ordered online for pickup. I received an email with a 20 minute estimate. When I arrived I stood near the counter/register.  About 10-15 minutes after I arrived I began to notice that customers who had arrived after me were already getting their food. I asked the lady behind the counter, who was busy on her phone, about my order. As it turns out, my order was ready before I had arrived. She even said that she'd noticed I'd been waiting for awhile. (I was the only one standing around. Every one else was seated and eating).   By the time I got to eating the food was luke warm, the bun was soggy, and the top beef patty was sliding off. The onion was almost 1/2 inch thick. Good thing I like onions. A few more minutes and the fries would have been soggy too.   On the positive side, they do have a really nice patio for 'dine in,' as well as patio heaters.   Based on my first experience I doubt I'll come back. Sorry, but like I said, I'm too old for poor customer service.

I was not super impressed by this place. It's pretty expensive for one. The food was made with fresh, basic, few ingredients. The Mexican was very watered down California-vegan-hippie Mexican, which was fine but didn't taste like Mexican food. If my hypothetical friend Suzie who, say, has white girl 'dreads' went to Whole Foods and made her son Apple dinner, this might be what she'd come up with. I was a little taken aback by the Buddha statue decor. I guess I'm not used to Santa Barbara yet.  My partner got overcooked fish tacos with disintegrating tortillas, but he very much enjoyed his smoothie (my milkshake was par for the course).

Had dinner with a friend before the Foreigner Concert at the bowl. We were the first to walk in and order right when they opened at 5 p.m. I ordered the Salmon and Farro which was a bit dry with a glass of the only Chardonnay they poured. The wine came out right away and the server apologized for the wine having sediment and was cloudy. He said it would settle but it didn't. I took two sips and sent it back for and better looking glass of wine. The woman who replaced the glass of $13.00  Less than 5 oz. Pour had an attitude about the replacement. Worse yet, it took 40 + minutes to get our meals and two customers that were behind us got their meals first. Don't think I'll be back. I even gave them a 20% tip before I even knew what the service would be like.
3 words: awesome vegan cupcakes.  (my kid had a non-vegan cupcake & gave it a thumbs up, too)

We went for the first time to Jane on July 16, our ten-year wedding anniversary. What a nice place!  It is comfortable and clean, with friendly staff, and excellent food. It's on the pricier end of things, especially if you order wine and dessert, like we did. Still, a great place to have a celebration!

came here not knowing it was in the mall. :(  That was a disapointment but we sat at the bar and service was pretty good.  The boys burger was good and my nachos were just ok.  Beer selection was pretty good and the place was clean and bright.

This is some unique food so you have to be ready for something a little out of the ordinary.  It's all vegetarian but some really fun unique things every time I've been there.  My niece is a vegetarian and every time she is in town she and her parents have to visit this spot!

'OMG people. Its called The Eggo - sunny side egg, shaved parm, lemon sauce, romaine, tomato, ON SPINACH SUNDRIED TOMATO WAFFLES- GAH! I added carmelized onions and avocado . . .'  This was from a couple of years ago when Honey B was atop Antioch University on  Anacapa Street. It has since closed down, but the chef does pop ups and perhaps has plans to reopen in the future - if she does, we'll soon be regulars again. All of her food is wonderful, super healthy, filling, satisfying, full of deliciousness. We constantly rave about Honey B, and her cookbook has a lot of the recipes from that restaurant.   Check out her website to see what she's up to now: https://www.thehoneybkitchen.com

A cute little hole-in-the-wall place that serves what else but Brioche and Croissants.  They have a few little tables outside and the food is very fresh and wonderful.    I did find them to be a little pricey for what you get.     A couple days ago there was a huge mudslide so they are closed this week.  Really makes me crave them.   When they re-open I will be there for sure.     I hear there is a Renaud's opening across the street in a couple of months.  Is there room for two pastry places on Coast Village Road?

It can get very busy, but it's worth a wait! The best item, without question, is the chicken wings. Just wow. Probably the best wings in Santa Barbara, definitely some of the very best wings I've had. Ever. They are perfect. That alone makes Finney's worth a visit.
my Experience was great! Good service wonderful food  and Exceptionally good glass of wine Chalk hill Merlot

Amazing service as you would expect at the Four Seasons. My sister was visiting and splurged on a weekend at the hotel and I joined her for breakfast. I had the ricotta berry toast (seasonal) and green juice which were both lovely.

LOUSY SERVICE and delicious food. A beautiful Country French restaurant in a beautiful setting with very tasty food and LOUSY service and management.  Who likes to be told when they sit down that the restaurant is slammed and that they better order quick because there's a bunch of big parties ahead of them? We were rushed to order, had to ask for service continually and although the food was delicious, the lousy service was hanging over our dining experience.  We never saw the same staff person twice which made for a very scattered service experience.  The restaurant needs better management and more staff. This seems to be consistent with our historical experience at this restaurant. Great food and really lousy service and management.

Disgusting!  I couldn't even drink more than 3 sips before tossing it in the trash.  I ordered a nonfat vanilla latte and watched the barista make it.  It was sloppily done.  He poured the milk first with a lot of foam (lattes shouldn't have that much foam) and then proceeded to dump the espresso shot over the milk.  The espresso itself wasn't very good and the drink tasted slightly burned, oh my... go across the street instead and get your coffee at the Santa Barbara Roasting Company located in the Paseo Nuevo mall.
Great attitude, fast service and delicious food. Prices are reasonable and the atmosphere is kind fun. I recommend all to stop on by and give this place a try!

Once again, the atmosphere is casual yet classy! The Cajun Martini is wonderful! The homemade muffins are delectable. I love coming here because they never disappoint. Ordered seafood Gumbo and Cajun Crabcakes!

The Oyster Shot with Ghost Chili Vodka is a must try, I will go back for this alone as it has some excellent kick. My kiddo was a fan of the Shark Attack. (Main course was okay but it could have been an off day.) Service was excellent and view of ocean to match!

We ordered tacos to go yesterday, via their online ordering site.  My wife is a vegetarian, so we each had one each of the three types of veggie tacos.  They were great, very flavorful and the home-made tortillas held up and did not crack.  We liked them so much that we might come back again tonight.  The horchata was great too.  Can't wait to explore the rest of the menu.

This is my favorite empanada place! I went often during fall and now I go back once a week during spring during my lunch hour since it's half a block away from my school. The staff is really friendly, the prices are great, and the food is amazing.

What?! 3/5 stars ONLY??  I'm disappointed to see why it's only received average ratings when I believe this restaurant can do better than that.  Sure, the decor is kinda old-fashioned for an Asian restaurant but there is always ample seating.   One of the things I really enjoyed here is their immediate and friendly service :) They always checked up on my friends and I.   I had ordered the pumpkin curry and it was delicious and savory. Our waitress even hooked it up on extra rice because my two friends ordered the pineapple curry and red curry? It was currylicious up in here lol  Only reason they are away from 5 stars is because their parking is very limited but other than that, I would love to come back again for Thai food :)

The worst so disappointed  Ordered the chicken with rare tritips  See the picture My dog wouldn't eat it Pre sliced pre cooked and then thrown on the grill to make sure it was so overdone and inedible And of course you get home and what can you do besides cry and regret
Fair pricing and their pad thai tasted very good. When I called and asked about gluten free options, they were very helpful and answered all my questions.

So glad this place opened. The food and atmosphere are awesome and it's in a great location. Some of the best margaritas and Mexican food in Santa Barbara for sure. My only knock on the place is that the service can sometimes be a little slow. Otherwise this place is solid and is always good.

Top dining experience in a while, and we're locals!   I always mean to go to Petit Valentien and I don't frequent it as often as I mean to since it's off of the main drag. But, WOW, our dinner was so fantastic we are planning our next return. Friday night, April 2nd, we enjoyed the most wonderful service and server. Our server was graceful, welcoming and efficient. I should have asked her name.  We ordered wine, kale caesar, crab cakes, salmon and sea bass. Everything was beyond a 10. COVID-19 safety protocols were clear and we felt safe and comfortable. Beautiful ambience, lighting and music volume. This is a great spot for a dinner date or to enjoy and share smaller plates with friends. The cherry on top, so to speak, was the NY cheesecake special, pic included.  Can't wait to go back!!!

Yes, make a reservation and then go have a lovely time. Indoors with their spacious dining room and bar area, or outdoors with the very comfy patio, Loquita will make you feel very welcome.   At a time when some restaurants have seen their service suffer with an inexperienced or depleted workforce, Loquita appears to be unscathed. Their professional staff helps make a joyful experience reach memorable heights.  And now, they have Michelin Bib Gourmand, too, so it may be a little tougher to get in. But, try you must.   As I was between destinations, and soon to be back on the road, I opted for Encanta, a n/a beverage that is sparkly and flavorful with a hint of sweetness. Find Encanta under 'Refrescos' on the menu. If you really want to go sweet, they do have horshata, or even limonada - both of which I'm sure will deliver a tasty sugar rush. I look forward to staying in Santa Barbara sooner than later, so I might imbibe with their cocktails or wine.  With so much to choose from on the menu, we asked our server about a few dishes. We had some good instincts, and were also steered in a good direction. Under Bocaditos, we loved the Croqueta de Queso (Mahon fritter, corn, shishito peppers, pimentos aioli, yazu salt). There's also a Croqueta de Jamon, but since we also had some Charcuterie, we dialed back the pork a bit in favor of the cheese. Really, you can't go wrong here. Both the Croqueta and the Charcuterie provided much joy.  Pulpo, under the Tapas menu, is delicious. (Black garlic aioli, lemon vingaigrette, potato puree, pickled red onion) Easy to love, perfect to share. Just keep finding and exploring, and you'll likely be blown away here.  Yes, the Churros are so worth getting. I'm sure the other desserts would be too, but we had to try these. Choose three of the four sauces and enjoy. If you can eat the whole order, well, that's more that I can say. Good for 2-3 people.   So happy to have found this treasure. So grateful to the Yelpers with their great reviews that caught my eye - even if maybe all the pretty pix on IG would have gotten me curious and hungry, too.
We went there with friends tonight. All our meals were cooked to perfection...cozy quaint place that we just discovered... Great place!

Literally makes frozen grocery store pizza sound good.  The thin crust was about as appetizing and chewing on cooked cardboard.    I ordered a Margherita pizza and I had to say double the topping or I would have had a sauce cracker. The hi-light was the water and a side ceased salad.    I would only recommend to come here if you need to have AC on a warm day.  The staff was very friendly so it did have that going for them.

Inconsistent hit-and-miss cooking left me puzzled about the high rating on Yelp and wait to get a table.  HIT: Mexican food like the green rancheros. The hit hit high. The rancheros were a soulfully satisfying mix of black beans and tomatillo sauce on crunchy corn tortillas and fried eggs. The four grilled shrimp were a nice addition, but not needed given the successful flavors from the sauce.  MISS: Southern food like the biscuit sampler and shrimp and grits.  As stated on the menu, the biscuit duo of 'warm maple bacon' wasn't warm but cold and the 'flaky buttermilk' was tougher than the maple bacon. The 'flaky' biscuit was so dense that it was hard to cut with a knife and impossible to pull-open with fingers. Even desolate roadside diners don't serve biscuits that are hard, dry, and cold like these.  The shrimp and grits were also a failure. Not rich, creamy, or cheesy, the grits were watery and flaccid with occasional pea-sized lumps of starchy blobs. Of the two poached eggs, the yolk had set on one while the other was barely cooked where the yolk and parts of the egg whites were raw. The kitchen needs an egg timer. Another indication of sloppiness were the rogue pieces of chard from another dish that meandered into the plate.  Barring the multiple execution problems, the recipe was unbalanced. The flavors from the oily combination of andouille sausage, red bell peppers, and grilled onions overtook the other ingredients. This was not southern shrimp and grits but a Spanish/Italian dish of sausage and peppers.

First time visiting this new spot at East Beach. The space was nicely remodeled and as always the views were amazing. Servers provided great customer service and were friendly.   As far as the food. Seems to need a little work. Appetizer came out within minutes and tasted like it was pre-made then warmed up.   Lots of options on the menu, so will be back to try a few more items   IF YOU SERVE BREAKFAST THEY WILL COME!!!

We recently returned to the Palace for a holiday dinner with friends and found the food and service to be excellent.  My husband and I both ordered the filet mignon (mine grilled and his stuffed) and we agreed that these were some of the most delicious steaks ever.  The libations at our table were split between Cajun martinis and the Caribbean rum punch - both were amazing.   The manager was gracious, and the service was very attentive and pleasant.   We all agreed that this was the perfect dining experience!
5 of us arrived, HUNGRY   Beet salad with chicken eas very good Black & Blue Burger was quite tasty Red pepper pizza disappeared Mandarin orange salad was tasty Tomato-basil soup was delicious

It was another beautiful Santa Barbara day, and I decided to meet a few friends for happy hour. We sat outside on the patio, the view is like no other overlooking the harbor and the beautiful scenery Santa Barbara is famous for. Happy hour was fun outside on their patio, the service was great and the drinks were smooth and mild. I felt like there wasn't enough happy hour food options to choose from. But none the less our food we ordered was very tasty and was quickly served.

Excellent local spot with great coffee to boot! Love that this coffee is made in house.   I usually get an iced coffee with splenda and half and half. Nothing too fancy but it hits the spot! I also tried my husband's light roast which was excellent.   SBRC has a great swag section as well. If I didn't have a Keurig I would grab some of their bags of coffee to go!  Lastly, the staff was super friendly and nice. I'm sure it's not easy to be in a customer facing role amidst COVID-19 but they did a great job!
So excited for the unique tacos with amazing flavor. Great addition to Santa Barbara. Great variety in many as well. Grateful for this gem!!

I really wanted to like this place. Have gone now a total of 3 times. More than likely won't be back. The pastrami dog is really good. But all 3 times I've gotten soggy tater tots. Twice I've gone at night. The service is lackluster at night. Unsupervised teenagers who don't have much pep in their step. Not friendly. Second time I went the hot dog box was put in the bag upside down. This 3rd time I went was in the daytime, service is much better! Everyone friendly and says thank you. But again, soggy tater tots. Literally threw 2/3 of the bag away. Not satisfied, and don't feel like I got food worth the $10 I paid. And never once have I seen Dave there... maybe that's the problem?

The chilaquiles are delicious and the tomatillo salsa that come on them has a great balance of spicy and tangy! The portions are huge, there's always a long wait but if you don't have time to wait for a table you can go to the bar, its self seating!
Used to love the tropical guava, but it lacked the flavor it usually does. think i will pass in the future for this price point.

This is my new FAVORITE restaurant in Santa Barbara. I've been in several times and decided I wanted to celebrate my birthday there yesterday (5/16). Every experience has been amazing, from the food, drinks and service. BUT yesterday was exceptional! I don't know if they just know how to make a birthday girl feel special, but the service was impeccable- everyone was just so nice. The drinks were awesome, and the food was so good. Thank you Santo Mezcal for making my birthday one to remember!

Ordered the fried chicken sandwich with fries and they were BOMB!  Good sized sandwich , chicken was crispy and so were the fries, just how I like them. A perfect mix of sweet pickles, thick cut bacon and that buffalo sauce gave it a good tangy taste.

I love Lure. A lot. Whenever I want to go somewhere fun, not too pricey, with a nice atmosphere, I immediately think of Lure. My boyfriend and I like to sit at the Oyster bar because the dudes who work behind it are hilarious and always seem to provide top notch service. I am an avid fan of clam chowder and used to refuse to eat it anywhere other than Brophy's but I would say Lure's New England clam chowder is amazing and I can finally eat it somewhere other than Brophy's. We have gotten a variety of different menu items and have only had great experiences with everything. Their Key Lime pie is dynamite and I will literally get a piece to go if I have eaten my fill of seafood.

Sitting outside for breakfast on Shoreline Drive across from the harbor gives breakfast a nice atmosphere with plenty of views!  A pretty extensive menu with lots of choices.  I chose the 1957 Breakfast Sandwich on a Bagel with Scrambled Eggs, Bacon, Red Onion and American Cheese on an English Muffin. Served with Home Potatoes. The Best Half choose: HUEVOS RANCHEROS SPECIAL Two Eggs, Fried Corn Tortilla, Black Beans, Cheddar Cheese, Chorizo, Jalapeno, Sour Cream, Salsa and Guacamole. Served with Home Potatoes she ordered a Cappuccino and she said it was delicious! The coffee was good too  Really large portions so bring your appetite. Only take away is it's a little noisy on the Highway but traffic is intermittent! Clifford our server and his team were excellent in attending our needs!

Soooooo good!!! I had the ahi tuna burger. The bread was delicious, fish was fresh and avocado topping was perfect. Cute little spot with fair prices. Needed a healthy meal after all the fatty meals on vacation besides the fries I added on this meal....delicious!!!! Def recommend.

After searching for what felt like eons, my family, fiance, and I finally found the Riviera Mansion. It was such a blessing finding this venue and Bree. Everything leading up to our big day was smooth and easy.  Bree was extremely professional, friendly, quick to respond when we had questions, and encouraging of our vision. Day of was amazing!!!! Absolutely beautiful. The staff was excellent (chef - accommodating of dietary needs and special orders, serving staff - attentive, fun, funny, helpful), the food out of this world (custom biscuits and gravy/fried chicken/filet mignon), and our guest are still talking about how much fun they had. We flipped the space by the huge, gorgeous tree for ceremony and dinner, had happy hour on the patio across from the park, and had drinks/dancing inside. I was a bit worried about the transitions from space to space, but the timeline went flawlessly. There are also two great spaces to get ready in - for both sides of the bridal party. My husband and his groomsmen had a great time playing pool before the ceremony and there was more than enough space to have a party of 7 bridesmaids get ready in the bridal quarters. We absolutely made the right choice picking the mansion for our wedding. We could not be happier!!! Thank you!!!!!!

This place was recommended to me by my hairdresser so I figured I'd give it a try.  I'm glad I did because it's now become one the places I frequent for take-out.  Based on reviews, I went with the sesame chicken and green beans.  It was such a hit with the family, I haven't dared to stray from it yet.

I've missed the brunch experience- but ordering pickup through DoorDash for now will have to do! Pick up was super quick and easy, I just walked up to the ordering window and they finished making the food about 10 minutes before estimated ready time!  Rollex: everything breakfasty/brunchy in a wrap! Eggs, bacon, avocado, and a tomato sauce. The flatbread it was on reminded me of a flour tortilla, and it had a light layer of grease on the outside (so wash your hands and be prepared to get your hands a little messy!) The Rollex was very enjoyable, my favorite out of the two things we ordered. I split it with my boyfriend, but would probably be quite content with the portion size if I ate it all by myself.  Like-a-tiger: a healthier approach to an omlette, filled with zucchini, mushroom, onions, and spinach! I was a bit disappointed with how small the actual omlette/egg portion was (about the size of my hand), but it tasted good. The potatoes were a bit soft instead of crisp even though we ate in the car right after picking up the food, but I liked how they sautéed onions with it as well. Saving the best portion of this entree for last- the tomatoes!!! They were sweet yet somehow savory at the same time, and gave little bursts of flavor whenever I ate one.
Everything is delicious there!!!!!!! The best gluten free food I've had. The crust they make is unreal. I Highly recommend this place!!!

Miso Disappointed!  I've been going to Poke Mee since they opened, and I was delighted to hear that something similar opened up right near my office for a convenient lunch option.  NOPE!  The rice is tough and clumpy, the fish is in unreasonably large pieces which include sinew and bones, the sauces are bland and oddly creamy.  I feel like I just ate a big bowl of milky garbage harvested from the dumpster of a proper Japanese restaurant. Just in case the owners are as dense as their rice, let me clarify by saying that you're serving up the stuff that other restaurants would throw away.
clam chowder is good. fish and chips are tasty.  place is packed so service can be scarce....but once you grab their attention, theyre awesome!
They need air conditioning... The dining area is currently 93 degrees.... Other than that the food is always good and staff is very friendly.

This place is great. Above all, Mikey, the best bartender is there. He goes above and beyond with customer service, his enthusiasm and fantastic energy makes every customer feel very well taken care of. His hard work ethic is very admirable and they are very lucky to have him there. I would definitely return to this establishment for the food as well as amazing service.
Great location with a good vibe. Only had the Ginger Pimms cocktail but I will be back.  Very nice and relaxing atmosphere.

Ahh man, I really wanted to like this place, but man I was the only customer and I felt like I was treated as the millionth customer after fifteen hour shift. To be honest, I've had friendlier interactions with the homeless, and that's during a manic attack. The food? I got a tuna poke bowl, it was okay. Why three stars? Easy: good parking, good soundtrack, and friendly service close by next door.

We celebrated my Mom's birthday at Ca' Dario on a weekday, and their patio was lovely and welcoming! Our kind server, Angel, made our dining experience wonderfully festive and fun! We enjoyed the pasta dishes and the divine rack of lamb best, with some wonderful Chardonnay! Thank you, Ca' Dario, for helping our family celebrate Mom well!!

This place is a hidden gem for amazing food and service.  Funny to go to best western hotel for lunch however you will be so happy with the food.  Group of 10 of us and every one of us loved our food.  Onion rings were the best.  The burger was better than anything at gourmet restaurant.
Great pre concert place to dine next to the Lobero Theater. Particularly enjoyed the lamb so succulent. Excellent service and choices of their own wines. Will go again.

Alessia got her croissant right; the outside crunch is there and the center is soft and chewy, which all I really care about. My go to order will always be a plain croissant with jam, so based off this point alone, Alessia's Patisserie has earned their extra star in my book.   I strive to keep my reviews unbiased and uninfluenced by anyone- including an establishment offering me free goods or services.  So after the the owner reached out here on Yelp, I decided to go back to Alessia's on my own to try the same pastries that I had before giving the allowance for the possibility of damage during transit of my birthday treats from my first review (like maybe they got dried out in the car or something) which were dry and too sweet.   When I took a look at the bakery case I realized that I would've chosen completely different pastries to begin with as I am not a fan of Pear ( never actually seen a pear cake before) or pumpkin ( just kind of over it) ! So when I I figured out what was in mine I decided to go for what I like instead.   Although I do find the desert pastries at Alessia patisserie to be a bit too sweet and in my opinion, 'avant-garde ', with twists and reinventions on the classics that sometimes undermine the original versions rather than enhance them- I will say- everything is top-notch in quality.   For example, I appreciate Chef Jordan Pilarski's take on a lemon chiffon tart. It is different from the traditional lemon chiffon tart you would find at a French patisserie because the meringue top is not flame torched  and the lemon curd is slightly gelatinous making it bouncier than the traditional creamy lemon custard version. This was actually good! It reminded me of a high class version of those  Jell-O pudding pies with Cool Whip that I was never allowed to have as a kid. It tasted lighter than the Lemon Tarts I've had elsewhere. I also really enjoyed the raspberry center. It was still overly sweet for my taste, but the acidity from the lemon balanced out the sweetness like lemonade. Out of everything I tried, this tart was my favorite and not surprisingly, as it was pretty traditional apart from the custard texture, soft chiffon, and raspberry center.   I also really enjoyed the chiffon top, as it was not toasted which made it light and airy. The raspberry was a bit off center but maybe consistency and tradition aren't Alessia's aim. Either way,  there's no denying the flavor was delicious.   I also tried an intricately designed eclair with cranberry in it. The presentation on this was beautiful, as with all of the pastries at Alessia's . Once again I felt that the flavor was just a little too convoluted for me with orange zest overpowering the other flavors but as far as execution the pastry was perfect.  Finally, I see a lot of reviews commenting on the price being high. I believe my pastries were all between $12-15 for a single item. The owner has responded that the prices reflect higher than average wages for her staff which I hear is true! I personally feel there is no limit to good quality and service and I do believe that the pastries are a rare indulgence so I don't mind paying higher than average prices for them. I am curious as to the etiquette for tipping and if it is included, I saw no indication.   My only recommendation would be that they adjust the boxes to fit the size of the pastries or vise versa and implement a 'crutch' ( I made my own- see photo) to prevent sliding because they do get pretty jumbled during transition.

One of our favorite places to grab breakfast! They have a wide selection of donuts and their bagels are always tasty. I especially enjoy their homemade cream cheese flavors - I haven't had anything like them! Their vegetable cream had chopped up veggies in it and their strawberry cream cheese had jam. Definitely stop by to try some delicious bagel and cream cheese combo and donuts!

Yesterday I stopped in after my dr. Appt its been awhile since ive been in there glad to see there still there when I worked in santa Barbara I stopped in all the time almost after work always leave happy with my purchase

Unfortunately, I have been here 3 times now and I have not been wowed once. There was a time when they had a really fun bartender who did tricks so this was fun to watch. The food is not very good. I am sad to write that because I know it has been around for a while and is a family restaurant but I am out of chances with them.
Great food in a nice environment. Definitely will be going there many times in the future.

If I could give Zaytoon 10 stars I would. Our waitress Nikki was a ray of sunshine and extremely knowledgeable when we had questions on wine pairings. Our family appreciated that they seem to take pride in making each dish perfectly seasoned with the best quality ingredients. The restaurant is a clean and a beautiful setting with well manicured foliage.

We visited Loquita on a busy Friday night with a 6pm reservation. We were graciously welcomed by the hostess team and quickly seated at a cozy and comfortable table on the outside patio. My wife ordered a craft cocktail and I ordered one of their Gin & Tonics, both of which were outstanding and paired perfectly with our first course of Pintxos.   The Tapas plates are intended for sharing, so we ordered up a nice variety of dishes including the Octopus (Pulpo), Carpaccio de Carne, Tuna Tartare, Albondigas, and a wonderful seasonal salad special. Everything was outstanding. Flavors were phenomenal, presentation was great, and service was spot-on. We consider ourselves to be Octopus connoisseurs (it's a dish we try whenever we find it on a menu) and this version is the best we've ever tried. Order two!  The General Manager Skyler is a very gracious host leading this great team of professionals who all make for a very memorable dining experience. We highly recommend a visit to Loquita. If you are a tourist visiting Santa Barbara and have only one dinner out - make it Loquita and you won't regret it!

We got the 2 meals for $20 special at Embermill and it was amazing! Each meal is packaged separately and ready to eat. We got the Jerk chicken and it was so tasty. It came with sides of cabbage, rice, beans, and carrots. There was so much food that neither of us were able to completely finish. The Jamaican beer tastes like a mixture between Heineken and Corona and was a nice addition to the meal. This place is definitely worth it! Pick ups for their meal during Covid-19 hours are from 2-8PM. You should definitely give them a try!

Eureka! is located in Santa Barbara's Paseo Nuevo outdoor shopping center.  Its kind of on the periphery near the iconic tiled stairway.  The wife and I decided to come here when we visited Santa Barbara last month, hoping for a 'quick drink ' to slake our thirsts from walking and shopping all day before our dinner date at nearby Pascucci's....  Turns out getting a 'quick' anything here is a bit of a challenge!  So what I really like bout this location is it's open layout.  The walls/doors that line the location roll up, creating an airy, light-filled spot.  This is especially enjoyable on many of the U.S. Riviera's perfect days.  But what brings it down a couple-three stars...indeed, what both this AND our local location in Redlands suffers from, is decent service.  As mentioned in other reviews.  The service here is slooooooooowwwwwww....!  The wife and I sat down and she ordered a glass of white wine and I ordered just a tasting sampler of a beer I was interested in trying.  25 long minutes later they were delivered to us, with the waiter saying 'Sorry... the bar is backed up a bit'..  And yet I saw those who came in and ordered long AFTER us be delivered their drinks long BEFORE us..  *le siiiiiiiiigh*  Slow, spotty service isn't just an issue with this location.  When we visited our location in Redlands, their service was slow too...even though they had plenty of wait staff!  We didn't have any food this time, as we were just there for some drinks...which we did eventually enjoy.  As I said, the location and layout is great.  But they really need to focus on service to the customer so they can up their game.  Businesses come and go in Santa Barbara..such service can cause it to go..quickly.  Hopefully additional visits will have better results.  Be wary when visiting here.
They actually are not open until 5am if you go to their store early morning be mindful of that. Good service, odd set up for dealing with traffic inside when it is busy.
I cannot agree more that this is a tourist trap. Way too pricey with what they are serving. Go further and try Jeannines or Anderson.
Amazing food. Thai curry noodles are the best. Also good vegan options and good service.

Very nice coffee, omelette and also, as highlighted in other reviews, excellent almond croissants!  The place is a little off the main shopping road and quite small so you may struggle to get a seat at peak times but definitely worth a visit.

If you're looking for a late night spot to eat, this spot really nails it if you're out of options. Their kitchen closes at 11 PM and they have desserts along with hot meal options, vegan included! A bunch of tv's and of course the bar crowd but it's pretty laid back if it's any other days  besides Thursday through Saturday with a DJ. There's also some tables with a fire pit.

Rose café is one of my favorite spots in town. They have the best chilaquiles I've ever had and enchiladas and everything on the menu is good   You can't go wrong with anything you order on the menu. The margaritas are really good and the staff is super friendly and I love the outdoor dining area.

We had to check out this bakery while in SB. I love snickerdoodles and needs to try theirs. It was just a little dry. The sugar cookie however, was delicious. I love heart shaped cookies and the frosting was soft. Highly recommend the heart shaped cookie!

This place is great. It has that traditional deli vibe.  I am  huge fan of their specials! I am Greek and am very pleased with their gyros on Fridays. Today is Wednesday and I tricked myself into thinking it was Friday because I was thinking about those gyros. The grocery selection is also rad, but It is pricey so bring your bank roll with your empty belly.

I suggested this place based on the reviews and the fact that it was tapas style. Sharing is caring and the more people in the party, the more sharing there shall be! I made a reservation via open table, which is über easy and convenient. When we got there there were plenty of open tables, but they may have been reserved because we were seated in the upstairs dining area.   People were raving about the blackberry margarita so I knew I had to try it. It was meh. It was strong, but nothing special in my opinion. That's okay because the meal made up for it. We had the probaditas de ceviche, pork belly quesadilla, pulpo a la plancha, and the empanada de camarón. My favorites were the pork belly quesadilla and the ceviche sampler. I would get them again in a heart beat!  The ceviche sampler consisted of one made with yellowfin tuna topped with chile aioli, a white fish with jicama and pineapple, and red snapper with sweet potato and a lime ginger sauce. There were other ingredients, but those were the standout flavors in each dish. The flavors and textures really complemented one another and I liked all three. If I had to choose a favorite though I'd pick the yellow fin tuna ceviche. It was just so fresh. Add some plantain chips and you have a full meal! The white fish ceviche was topped with a tomatillo-serrano sauce so it had quite a kick to it. The pork belly in the quesadilla was so tender and bursting with flavor! Love!  That's all I gotta say. The pulpo was the most tender octopus I've ever had. And I really enjoyed the creamy sauce that accompanied it. It was a mixture of yogurt, Peruvian olive aioli, and pistachio salsa verde. I was expecting a lot more from the shrimp empanada, but it was just ok. It had two kinds of cheeses with a mango habanero glaze. Maybe it was the small portion of shrimp inside, but it was my least favorite dish.   Can't forget about dessert! We all shared Sandra's signature dessert, which was cheesecake with a strawberry compote. Absolutely delicious! I think we were all expecting it to be really dense and rich. It was quite the opposite. It kind of reminded me of an Asian style cheesecake. It was light and fluffy and the strawrberry sauce wasn't too sweet.  Service was fantastic from beginning to end. I was so stuffed afterwards, but it was a great dining experience with wonderful friends!
They have really good seafood, best I've had in Santa Barbara in a while. The ceviche is sooo good and same with the quesadillas and chicken tacos

Have you ever wondered what it's like to be sipping on mojito, munching on amazing pulled pork, and listening to Cuban street music? Look no further! Unless you are looking for street music, then you have to look further. Seriously though, you should go try their sandwiches.
What happened, Cantwell's? You used to be the go to for big tasty sandwiches? The market is still great but the quality and size of your sandwiches are the same as a supermarket deli. Very sad!

One of my must go place every time I am in Santa Barbara.  Just a solid, good, comfort food for super fresh seafood with great service and a nice selection of beers.  They serve a pretty good mitchelada and Bloody Mary too. Depending on the season but if they ever have the spider crab, you must get it.  It's taste similar to the fresh Alaskan king, not the frozen legs kind, sweet and delicate. I wonder if they ever have Fresh Alaskan King that's the ultimate best crab in the world!!!!  But the box crab and the rock crab is also delicious. If you are an Uni lover the Uni here is wriggling fresh, it's practically still moving while you eating it...I know sound weird but it's the best when you can have live urchin...they served it up on top of a piece of sashimi scallop and lime. The seafood linguine is also delish...we have been here quite a bit and had not have a bad meal.   Not to mention the giant onion ring, crispy and sweet just a great paring to the steam shell fish. They also have fresh abalone if you are in the mood for one. My eyes tend to be always bigger than my stomach hence all the food just for 2 but how can I help myself when the food is amazing right, you should have seen the people next to us they were so inspired they all each order the spiny lobster too, I should get commission from the restaurant...lol for helping them sell the food....so eat up those sweet thing you won't regret it!!!!

My friend and I came here around 10pm a couple of days ago for happy hour. It was my first time ever eating at this Eureka location and I don't think I will be coming back.   The service here was absolutely terrible, which is why I am giving this review 2 stars. The food was very average as well, about 3 stars. The waitress at the front table was attentive and kind, she immediately sat us down when we walked in. I understand that I came at 10pm but I didn't realize how dark and dimly lit the restaurant would be. I know that Eureka has more of a 'bar atmosphere' during happy hour but geez please turn on at least one or two lights, I felt like I was eating in absolute darkness. In addition to that, the music was overbearingly loud, it was SO LOUD that my friend and I debated eating outside halfway through our meal. I couldn't even hear myself talk. After we sat down, we waited about 15 minutes before any waiter came to our table. All of the waiters (about 4-6 people) were standing near the back of the restaurant talking, no one brought us water or came by our table until I finally flagged someone down. When the waiter finally came, he literally paid no attention whatsoever to what we ordered and had to come back to our table 3 times to double check . The entire time we spoke, he didn't write anything down and kept forgetting what was being said. We ordered 2 items from the happy hour menu and another 2 items from the main menu. During the time we waited for our food to arrive, another party of 5 people sat down right next to our table and the waitress attending to them was their friend........which is great and all......but the waitress literally stood at that table for a good 20 minutes just giggling and laughing with her friends. When my friend and I asked for more water, the waitress completely disregarded our request and our waiter never checked up on us after the food was brought out. I understand that working the night shift is exhausting and that most of the waiters/waitresses here are college students but lets be real, you're not getting paid to sit there and chat with your friends, you do that on your own time. The manager was in the restaurant as well and I'm surprised that she didn't say anything to that waitress. The only person who check up on my table was the manager and she was very sweet. Overall, my first time here wasn't that great and the food is definitely not to die for. I don't think I will be coming back, I still tipped 18% though for poor service and I am starting to regret it.

Wow. This is the type of gorgeous place where the food matches the beautiful atmosphere! The service was excellent, all of our dishes where amazing! The food is truly well done. You can share all kinds of dishes. All great! The elegant atmosphere, with the twinkling lights is romantic, but funky enough where you don't have to be on a date. We ate with two other couples and loved every minute of it.   The only downside was the extremely loud belly dancing music. We loved the belly dancer, and get that it's a Middle Eastern restaurant, but once the music was turned on in the middle of our dinner, we had to shout at one another just to have a conversation or say, 'Pass the tabouli!' . That loud music was a bit much during our dinner. Either turn the music down or wait until after dinner service. All the tables around us felt the same way.   That was the only downside. But, we will be back!

OK, so this place may deserve more than 3 stars, but I'll have to go back to be sure. We grabbed a dog for our toddler and he certainly scarfed it down. I almost got the last bite from him, but he snatched it out of my hand. Heck, I'm gonna bump it up to 4 stars just for that. I'm a fan of any place that can satisfy a cranky, hungry toddler!
Salads are creative, fresh, and perfectly dressed.   The poblano/shrimp quesadilla with house made tortilla is fabulous.   Salmon perfectly cooked.   Fun and international wines list.

This place is one of our SB favorites.  Their breakfasts are fantastic.  This time, we had an early dinner before going to a concert at UCSB.  We sat inside, because it was still a bit nippy, but the HH crowd was outside with the heat lamps.  We decided to order starters and share.  My husband ordered the crab cakes.  We shared mussels.  I ordered the lobster truffle macaroni, which was a real treat.

Great experience. Romi ( sp?),the Sommelier ,is a fantastic part of the equation and worth going just to discuss wines and learn from her. Rest of team super attentive. Its a great night and like the new layout.  One request: bring back the larger portion of short ribs as the smaller portion seem to have changed the character.

This was honestly one of the best breakfasts I've ever had in my life! The build a hash was perfect, and everything about it was delicious. The toast was amazing and the jam they serve is incredible. Not to mention the impeccable service.  This is easily the best breakfast spot in Santa Barbara.

Been eating their sandwiches since I was a kid.  Thankfully, it is still open and wonderful, and one of the best sandwiches in town.  Get the old school Italian sub that they do. They've perfected it.

You guys can do better! This is a sad pizza. Called in our order; told it would be 25-30 minutes. Received it closer to an hour later. Likely understaffed. Packed waiting area with a Sunday afternoon crowd. Confused about the seating/eating area around the corner where the tables weren't spaced apart and not really supposed to be there with our current lockdown? Other dishes were okay. Good Cesar dressing! We will likely come back once restrictions ease up. Have had more positive experiences dining in.

3.5 Star. Great service and nice setting, however some of the dishes seemed a bit off. We LOVED the scallop trio. Interesting concept of scallop thoughtfully prepared three ways. If this was my entree, I would have been quite happy.  My husband got the duck, which he enjoyed thoroughly. I got the lamb which was highly touted by the waiter. I was disappointed with the sides, the white poleta was too heavy on the palette paired with the lamb. 1 piece of lamb was super chewy. Overall, my lamb dish was just OK.  We got a frozen chocolate mousse dessert with peanut nougat. Interesting dessert, but didn't really stand out.   I preferred our night out at Toma's instead. Maybe I just chose the wrong dishes?
This place is a old classical place. Food was kind of okay. By the way, this was my first time. They need to update the place.

Okay, this is my third review of the Upper State St Spudnuts. I have been coming here everyday since 2010. Other than vacations, I have probably missed less than 7 days in 10 years. My standing order: authentic NY-style garlic bagel (cream cheese and jelly), homemade blueberry muffin (the best in California) and fresh Kona coffee. Don't know what I would do if we didn't have this Spudnuts in Santa Barbara. Joy, Khan and Adolpho are always great.   As others have said, they have great boba drinks, all kinds of sandwiches (people love the egg sandwiches), freshly squeezed juices and, of course, delicious donuts, croissants, fritters, twists, etc. If you come by when I am there, you may also get a very friendly greeting by my golden retriever, Milo, in the outdoor patio.

After reading the reviews for this restaurant we were looking forward to trying this restaurant after a day of sightseeing.  The layout is small and was not very accommodating for a group of four. Upon ordering our meal at the cashier we were told to sit wherever we wanted but excluding the large reserved table the only available seats for 4 were two small circular tables along a tight little corner.  The tables had not been bused so were littered with crumbs and food stains on the table cloths. We ordered penne arrabiata,  spaghetti carbonara and angel hair pasta.  While I did ask that the penne arrabiata be made not too spicy it was bland and lacked flavor.  We thought the spaghetti carbonara was dry and needed more sauce/cream.  The restaurant did not seem very interested in addressing our request. Overall, we were not pleased with the taste of the food, service or ambiance of the restaurant.  There are plenty of better options to dine at in Santa Barbara.

The Santa Barbara's Independent $7 burrito week motivated me to try something different and newer. The concept sounded cool, the Yelp score seemed promising, and the images of food appeared delicious. I called in my order and was told the order would be ready in about an hour. I arrived 50 minutes into the hour, paid the bill, and then continued to wait for another 45 minutes outside the restaurant. Friday night during burrito week, I suppose should be a little busier than usual but that was quite a wait. My hunger and anticipation climbed. Upon waiting, My concerns were the little amount of employees working, poor customer service, multiple disgruntled customers ahead of me, an overall lack of organization and management. I finally received my order, specifically named off each item to confirm that they were all in the bag and the employee confirmed it was all there. Disappointed, when I opened the bag to find my chips and guac were missing. I went back and waited for them as they looked around confused. Finally, after a long experience of waiting, I took photos for my Yelp review and dug in like it was my first meal in weeks. The $7 chips and Guacamole were undeserving of a photo, and mediocre at best, still optimistic though. Their signature Quesotacos were small, but pretty good. I liked the birria burrito the best. In general, I thought the food was good but not great and I would say not entirely worth the wait and hassle, that I experienced. I suggest some management to get the restaurant and employees organized and efficient. The food has potential for growth and again pretty good as is. Unfortunately, I'm not eager to head back anytime soon.
Just had dim sum it tasted like reheated frozen dim sum from trader joes. They wouldn't take more than 3 credit cards from our party of 9.

I had the ceviche and salmon tacos this evening for dinner at their new location on upper De La Vina.  My oh my was it yummy.  It had a great kick to it.  It had a lot of fish and veggies in it.  I was digging it for sure.  Especially with their chips and salsa.  They definitely know how to make great food! Keep it up!

I really wanted to like this place. It has a great vibe, is locally owned and operated, they use ceramic cups, and the coffee is good. The problem is that charge a whopping $1.00 upcharge for sustainable and cruelty-free plant milk. Since none of us drinks cow milk, we were lucky to walk across the street to the newly opened coffee shop that also had excellent coffee, but no penalty fee for us environmentally conscious consumers. If Caje decides to drop this upcharge, we would not only be delighted to return, but to spread the news far and wide!
Surf & Turf burger was a tasty way to kick off SB Indy burger week! #sbindyburgerweek

Okay I come here a lot- happy hour deals with my dad and what not, but today was exceptional!  I have a mango allergy (I usually tell staff, but I didn't think of it today) and I ordered the 'locals menu,' fresh salmon. It was tasting great; too great, I thought! Asked my dad to see if the sauce tasted like mango- he agreed that it did. We asked our male server, I didn't catch his name. He went back to the kitchen, and our server Kim literally came running out to tell us it was mango free! She was so concerned, and took such great care.  Secondly, I ordered a tequila sunrise but they were out of orange juice. I got a tequila with some pineapple juice instead, and she took it off the bill.   All of our food was great, but that's not even the point- our servers, especially Kim took the time to make sure we were enjoying our food/drinks and that I was safe. What's the point of going out if the service isn't excellent?!   Keep going like this, y'all! Great dinner and great staff. Way to go, management. :)

Classic diner breakfast that did not disappoint. Perfectly cooked poached eggs on good wheat bread with crispy bacon did the trick on a New Year's day morning. Strong fresh coffee fueled the day. Friendly waitress Nicole was welcoming and efficient.

The food is delicious, creative and generous portions. But the takeout ordering process has not been smooth the last two times we tried. One time I called in the order and another time my husband went in person to place and pickup. Both times we experienced snappy, unfriendly service. For the Polenta Fries, we asked for no chili or at least on the side. The cashier appeared afraid to ask the cook. Seemed like a simple request to us. We would like to return and hope that service improves. They used to be so friendly back when we could dine in.
If you want a casual lunch with great Brazilian food, free wifi, and super friendly staff, this is the place to go!
